title: Encoding Creative Commons Licenses to Images
authors: Pellegrini, Tyler, Wilcox, Trey, Johnson, Marcus, Oberlin, Connor
abstract: In our project we were tasked with modifying Gimp’s metadata editor to allow artists to check and add Creative Commons licenses and metadata to their image’s. This is done so that artist have an extra layer of protection for themselves and their art, with the ability to choose from multiple types of licenses allowing them to tailor this protection to the needs and desires they have for their artwork.

suggestions.title: Blockchain-Backed Metadata Integrity Verification
suggestions.desc: Enhance the GIMP plugin by anchoring image hashes and Creative Commons metadata to a public ledger like Polygon or using the OpenTimestamps API. This allows users to verify that the license metadata hasn't been altered or stripped by third-party platforms, providing a decentralized 'proof of existence' for the artist's original work.

suggestions.title: Automated CC Attribution Browser Extension
suggestions.desc: Develop a browser extension that reads the specific XMP metadata schemas generated by the GIMP tool when a user hovers over an image. The tool should automatically generate formatted attribution strings (e.g., HTML or Markdown) based on the embedded license, making it effortless for bloggers and developers to credit the artist correctly.

suggestions.title: Steganographic Encoding for Stripping-Resistant Licenses
suggestions.desc: Research and implement a Least Significant Bit (LSB) or Discrete Cosine Transform (DCT) steganography module within GIMP to embed CC license codes directly into pixel data. This ensures the license remains recoverable even after the image is uploaded to social media platforms that typically strip standard EXIF/XMP metadata during compression.

suggestions.title: Direct-to-Marketplace Protected Distribution Pipeline
suggestions.desc: Build an export bridge that connects the CC-encoded GIMP images directly to creative platforms like ArtStation or Behance via their APIs. The system should automatically map the selected Creative Commons license to the platform's native licensing settings, ensuring the artist's protection preferences are synchronized across the web instantly.

title: Enhancing GIMP’s User Experience: Addressing Community UI/UX Issues
authors: Stanley, Alexander, Harrison, Ryan, Galvan, Dante, Elmostafa, Rami
abstract: Water quality monitoring is crucial for environmental protection, public health, and ecosystem sustainability. With increasing pressures from urbanization, agricultural runoff, and climate change, robust data-driven approaches are essential for early detection of water quality degradation and informed decision-making in environmental conservation efforts. Current water quality monitoring relies on reactive threshold exceedances, failing to detect gradual degradation and multi-parameter deterioration patterns. This creates delayed response to pollution events and missed opportunities for preventive intervention in one of Queensland's most vital water systems. The importance objective is to implement and evaluate a Real-Time Multi-Stream Monitoring system for early detection of water quality deterioration by monitoring key parameters simultaneously, using the Brisbane River data for baseline establishment and testing detection performance across multiple control limit configurations.

suggestions.title: Low-Power IoT Edge Deployment
suggestions.desc: Port the water quality analysis algorithms to run on ESP32 or Arduino devices with solar-powered sensors. Use the LoRaWAN protocol to transmit real-time data from remote river locations to a central gateway for continuous monitoring.

suggestions.title: Multivariate Degradation Modeling
suggestions.desc: Implement an Isolation Forest or Autoencoder-based anomaly detection system to identify 'gradual degradation' patterns. This moves beyond thresholding by finding subtle correlations between pH, turbidity, and dissolved oxygen that signify long-term health decline.

suggestions.title: Agricultural Runoff Early Warning System
suggestions.desc: Integrate local weather APIs (like OpenWeatherMap) to correlate rainfall events with spikes in water contaminants. Build a predictive notification system that warns local farmers and environmental agencies when runoff risk is high based on forecasted storms.

suggestions.title: Interactive Eco-Health Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Develop a public-facing GIS dashboard using Mapbox and D3.js that visualizes historical water quality trends. Include a 'Public Health Risk' score that translates complex chemical parameters into actionable advice for local swimmers and fishers.

title: Predicting NBA Player Re-Injury Using Net Rating
authors: Tention, Anaya
abstract: This project examines whether player performance data can signal injury risk before an absence occurs. Using game-by-game net rating trends, I applied an exponentially weighted control-chart approach to detect early shifts in performance that might indicate a rising risk of re-injury. The method successfully identified 71% of re-injury cases with an average 20-game lead, suggesting that performance declines can serve as an early warning signal. While the false-alarm rate was high, the results show that performance-based monitoring has potential value for teams seeking proactive player-health insights.
supervisor: Prof. Michael Frankel, Dr. Austin Brown – Faculty Advisor

suggestions.title: Multi-modal Fatigue Detection via Second Spectrum Tracking
suggestions.desc: Reduce the current high false-alarm rate by integrating spatial tracking data such as average movement speed, distance traveled, and acceleration bursts per game. Use a Random Forest classifier to fuse these physical intensity metrics with the existing net-rating trends to better distinguish between tactical performance slumps and actual physical fatigue.

suggestions.title: Causal Inference for Load Management Efficacy
suggestions.desc: Apply causal inference frameworks, such as CausalImpact or synthetic control methods, to determine if 'DNP - Rest' games successfully normalize performance trends. By analyzing if a scheduled rest period resets a declining exponentially weighted moving average (EWMA), you can quantify the exact impact of load management on mitigating the 20-game lead-time risk identified in the original study.

suggestions.title: Transfer Study on MLB Pitcher Velocity Decay
suggestions.desc: Adapt the control-chart methodology to Major League Baseball to predict Ulnar Collateral Ligament (UCL) injuries. Use the Statcast API to monitor game-over-game drops in four-seam fastball velocity and spin rates as proxies for the performance signals used in the NBA study, testing if specialized high-repetition movements provide a cleaner signal than general basketball net ratings.

suggestions.title: Bayesian Player Health Dashboard for Training Staff
suggestions.desc: Build a real-time monitoring tool using Streamlit or Dash that ingests live NBA box score data via the nba_api library. Implement a Bayesian updating model that adjusts injury probability after every game based on the player’s age, career minutes played, and historical EWMA deviations, providing athletic trainers with a visual 'Red-Yellow-Green' risk status for the active roster.

title: Loving Arms: Website Audit & Redesign
authors: Harrison, Marcus, Stropoli, Chris, Gibson, Katherine, Berger, Vaughn, Flores Valdez, Jesus
abstract: Our team’s project aims to enhance the Loving Arms Cancer Outreach website, making it more accessible and user-friendly for all visitors. Loving Arms is a nonprofit supporting individuals and families affected by cancer, and their website plays a key role in sharing information, connecting people to support programs, and reaching those in need. To achieve this, we first audited the current site to identify areas of confusion or outdated content, and we are currently in the process of providing a redesigned website. These updates are intended to improve the visitor experience while giving staff a reliable, easy-to-use platform that better supports the organization’s mission.
supervisor: Dr. Yan Huang, Project Sponsor: Catherine Gankofskie - Loving Arms

suggestions.title: Implement Automated WCAG 2.1 Compliance CI/CD
suggestions.desc: Integrate Deque’s axe-core or Lighthouse CI into a GitHub Actions pipeline to ensure future content updates by staff never break accessibility standards. Build a custom admin dashboard that scans new image uploads and alerts staff if ALT text is missing or if color contrast ratios fall below 4.5:1.

suggestions.title: Build a Geo-Located Support Matching Portal
suggestions.desc: Extend the website with a secure React and Firebase portal that allows patients to find local support groups or volunteer drivers based on their ZIP code. Integrate the Google Maps Platform API to visualize nearby resources and use Twilio to send automated SMS reminders for support program meetings.

suggestions.title: Optimize UI for Chemotherapy-Induced Cognitive Impairment
suggestions.desc: Research and implement specific UI patterns for 'Chemo-Brain,' focusing on minimizing cognitive load through persistent navigation aids and simplified information architecture. Use Hotjar heatmaps to analyze user paths and conduct A/B testing to determine which layouts best assist users experiencing fatigue or memory issues.

suggestions.title: Develop a Real-Time Donor Impact Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Transform the static donation page into a dynamic 'Impact Tracker' using the Stripe API and a Supabase backend to show live progress toward fundraising goals. Create a feature where donors can see exactly how their contributions fund specific outreach programs, including automated PDF tax-receipt generation and email updates via SendGrid.

title: Tortured Artist
authors: Tigani, Caitlin, Scholl, Ben, Tucker, Anaiya, Tucker, Adam
abstract: You are a photographer that wants to move, so you take pictures of your house to give to your real- estate agent. However, as you are developing the photos you hear a noise that makes you turn on the lights, ruining your photos. Now you must retake the photos before morning, but something around the house has changed. Rooms are no longer in the right place, items are moved around, doors are locked, and an entity is watching you. Will you find the secrets within the puzzles or be left tortured?

suggestions.title: Implement Procedural Non-Euclidean Level Architecture
suggestions.desc: Replace the static house layout with a dynamic system using the Wave Function Collapse algorithm to rearrange rooms whenever the player turns off the lights. This creates a disorienting, labyrinthine experience where spatial memory is neutralized, forcing players to rely on their photos to navigate back to safety.

suggestions.title: Spectral Shaders for Viewfinder-Only Mechanics
suggestions.desc: Develop a custom shader system where certain environmental clues, hidden doors, or even the entity itself are only visible through the camera's LCD screen. By using render textures and specific object layers, you can force the player into a high-risk mechanic where they must choose between looking at the high-fidelity world or the grainy, 'true' reality of the camera.

suggestions.title: Neural Network Driven Adaptive Entity AI
suggestions.desc: Integrate a Reinforcement Learning agent using the Unity ML-Agents toolkit to make the stalking entity more unpredictable. Instead of following fixed waypoints, the entity can learn from the player's movement patterns, such as their favorite hiding spots or frequent photo-taking locations, to set up effective ambushes as the night progresses.

suggestions.title: Dynamic Audio Propagation for Shifting Spaces
suggestions.desc: Use the Steam Audio or FMOD spatializer API to simulate real-time sound diffraction and occlusion that updates as the house geometry changes. This technical addition ensures that the 'noise' the photographer hears reflects the current, warped state of the house, providing vital auditory cues about where the entity is lurking through thin procedural walls.

title: Stock Price Predictions Using LSTM & Technical Indicators
authors: Elison, Kendal, Smith, Allen, Quinn, Dylan
abstract: Stock price predictions using traditional statistical methods remains challenging due to market volatility and nonlinear dynamics. Long Short-Term Memory (LTSM) networks may model temporal dependencies in stock data more effectively than traditional statistical methods. Historical data for several companies’ stocks was obtained from Yahoo Finance, where it was then enriched with various technical indicators such as momentum and volatility. Preliminary analysis through Scala programming language suggests that incorporating these technical indicators can enhance short-term price prediction accuracy. Future works may seek to integrate additional trend and volume based indications in another, more robust, programming language like Python.

suggestions.title: Sentiment-Augmented Feature Engineering
suggestions.desc: Incorporate a sentiment analysis pipeline using the NewsAPI and FinBERT to quantify market mood from financial headlines. Use these sentiment scores as additional input features for the LSTM to improve prediction accuracy during high-volatility news cycles.

suggestions.title: Automated Trading Bot with Alpaca API
suggestions.desc: Transition the model from a prediction tool to an execution tool by integrating the Alpaca Trading API. Build a paper-trading bot that executes 'Buy' or 'Sell' orders based on LSTM signals and manages risk with automated stop-loss logic.

suggestions.title: Temporal Fusion Transformer Architecture
suggestions.desc: Replace or augment the LSTM with a Temporal Fusion Transformer (TFT) to better handle long-term dependencies and multi-horizon forecasting. Compare the TFT’s attention mechanism against the LSTM to see which handles sudden market 'shocks' more effectively.

suggestions.title: Sector-Specific Portfolio Optimizer
suggestions.desc: Expand the system to analyze entire sectors (e.g., Tech or Energy) simultaneously. Use the predictions to build a portfolio optimization tool that suggests asset allocations based on the Modern Portfolio Theory (MPT) and predicted returns.

title: RiverGuard - AI Powered Trash Detection
authors: Versluis, Grant, Tucker, Collin, Bramblett, Wyatt, Pinto, Pedro, Ruiz, Geshlee
abstract: RiverGuard’s mission is to protect and preserve waterways by using technology to identify and reduce pollution. The system uses an object detection model to automatically locate and classify trash within images or video of rivers and lakes, removing the need for slow, manual observation. By providing real-time insight into waste accumulation, RiverGuard helps communities, researchers, and organizations take faster, more effective action to keep waterways clean. Its goal is to create a sustainable monitoring system that empowers people to understand pollution patterns and support long-term environmental responsibility. RiverGuard represents a step toward cleaner water, healthier ecosystems, and a more informed approach to environmental protection.

suggestions.title: Edge Optimization for Drone Deployment
suggestions.desc: Optimize the object detection model using NVIDIA TensorRT or OpenVINO to run on an onboard Jetson Nano. This allows for real-time trash detection directly on autonomous surface vehicles (ASVs) without needing a constant high-bandwidth internet connection.

suggestions.title: Pollution Heatmap and Source Tracing
suggestions.desc: Create a spatial analysis tool that logs detected trash locations on a GPS-integrated map. Use hydrological flow data to perform 'backtracking' and estimate the upstream sources or 'hotspots' where waste is entering the waterway.

suggestions.title: Multi-Spectral Plastic Classification
suggestions.desc: Extend the dataset to include multi-spectral or infrared imagery to distinguish between different types of pollutants (e.g., PET bottles vs. organic debris). This data can help waste management teams prioritize high-impact plastics for recycling.

suggestions.title: RiverGuard Community Reporting Integration
suggestions.desc: Develop a mobile app that allows citizens to upload photos of river trash. The backend AI can automatically verify the report, categorize the waste, and notify local municipal cleanup crews through a management portal.

title: AI Systems For a Stealth Videogame
authors: Ehler, Lukas, Weir, Evan, Corona, Javier
abstract: Shmovement Games 3/5 has been making progress towards creating a videogame that leverages multiple types of AI systems working in unison with one-another. This will be a game in which the player sneaks around enemies while trying to grab objects marked as the player's primary objective. This is a project for our CGDD 4242 AI class in which we are implementing a single agent, multi-agent, and advanced multi-agent system into one game.

suggestions.title: Physics-Based Audio and Light Sensing Layers
suggestions.desc: Upgrade the current detection systems by implementing ray-cast sound diffraction and light-level calculations. Use the engine's physics material properties to determine how noise travels through walls and use the player's mesh exposure to dynamic light sources to calculate a real-time 'visibility meter' for the AI.

suggestions.title: Reinforcement Learning for Counter-Stealth Adaptation
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Unity ML-Agents toolkit or a Python-based RL framework to let the AI agents learn from player behavior. Over multiple playthroughs, the agents can adapt their patrol routes to prioritize 'hotspots' where the player frequently hides or executes stealth takedowns.

suggestions.title: Hierarchical Goal-Oriented Action Planning (GOAP)
suggestions.desc: Extend the advanced multi-agent system by implementing a GOAP architecture where enemies share high-level objectives. When a player is spotted, one agent can be assigned to 'pin the player down' with suppressive fire while others use A* pathfinding to coordinate a pincer movement or call for reinforcements.

suggestions.title: Automated Stealth Level Balance Testing Tool
suggestions.desc: Build a tool that runs headless simulations of the AI agents against a 'perfect' automated player agent to generate difficulty heatmaps. This system would identify 'blind spots' in the level geometry where the current multi-agent systems fail to provide a challenge, helping level designers optimize guard placement.

title: SustainSync
authors: El-Shaer, Youssef, Khan, Zaid
abstract: Sustain Sync investigates how organizations can standardize sustainability tracking and how AI can convert that data into actionable insights. The platform normalizes utility data into a consistent schema aligned with industry frameworks. It applies machine learning forecasting and a retrieval-augmented co-benefit engine to relate sustainability goals across domains such as CO2, water, and biodiversity. Through a simple dashboard, Sustain Sync demonstrates an end-to-end data-driven approach for goal tracking, trend analysis, and AI-driven sustainability recommendations for an organization.

suggestions.title: Unstructured Utility Bill Ingestion Pipeline
suggestions.desc: Automate the data entry process by building an OCR-based ingestion layer using Amazon Textract or Azure Form Recognizer. This extension would allow users to upload PDF utility bills directly, using a custom Python parser to extract consumption data and map it automatically to the SustainSync schema.

suggestions.title: Automated CSRD Regulatory Reporting Suite
suggestions.desc: Scale the retrieval-augmented engine to generate audit-ready reports compliant with the Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive (CSRD). Use LangChain to interface the existing database with the European Sustainability Reporting Standards (ESRS) framework, producing structured narrative disclosures alongside the data visualizations.

suggestions.title: Grid-Aware Dynamic Carbon Forecasting
suggestions.desc: Enhance the ML forecasting model by integrating the WattTime or Electricity Maps API to account for real-time marginal emissions factor data. This allows the system to predict not just how much energy is used, but the specific carbon intensity of that energy based on regional grid fluctuations at specific times of day.

suggestions.title: Scope 3 Supply Chain Lifecycle Integration
suggestions.desc: Expand the platform beyond internal utilities to track Scope 3 emissions by integrating with the EC3 (Embodied Carbon in Construction Calculator) database. This would enable the dashboard to correlate procurement decisions and supplier material data with the organization’s overall biodiversity and CO2 targets.

title: Southern Bathtub Race: A Video Game Revival
authors: Roberson, Caroline, Crouch, Jes, Gardner, Treonna, Landaverde, Jose, Lashley, Jake
abstract: Southern Bathtub Race is a video game commemorating the annual SPSU Bathtub Races held from 1968 - 1991. The player controls a bathtub racer from a 1st-person perspective as they compete against the computer to navigate a racetrack modeled after the SPSU campus of 1991. The environmental assets and textures were created with traditional acrylic painting. The game is intended to replicate the races and student spirit on the SPSU campus for both SPSU alumni and KSU students.
supervisor: Dr. Yan Huang, Sponsor/Client: Will Mckenna

suggestions.title: Networked Multiplayer with Physics-Based Tuning
suggestions.desc: Replace the computer AI with a real-time competitive multiplayer system using Photon Fusion or Mirror. Enhance the racing mechanics by building a 'Bathtub Workshop' where players can modify physics parameters like center of mass, water buoyancy, and friction to simulate different historical bathtub builds.

suggestions.title: Procedural Acrylic Style Post-Processing Shaders
suggestions.desc: Scale the game's unique artistic direction by developing a custom Unity or Unreal shader that mimics the specific brushwork and color blending of the original acrylic paintings. This would allow new 3D models and expansion areas of the 1991 campus to be integrated without requiring manual painting for every asset.

suggestions.title: Immersive VR Campus Archive Experience
suggestions.desc: Port the first-person racing perspective to VR using the OpenXR toolkit to heighten the sensation of being in the tub. Integrate a 'Museum Mode' where players can stop at key campus landmarks to trigger pop-up displays of actual digitized photos and documents from the SPSU historical archives.

suggestions.title: Decade-Shifting Dynamic Environment System
suggestions.desc: Expand the 1991 map into a dynamic track that evolves as the player progresses through a 'History Campaign.' Implement a layered environment system that visually transitions buildings and textures to reflect the campus layout as it changed between the inaugural 1968 race and the final 1991 event.

title: Shepherd's Sin - A Visual Novel Hybrid Game Made with Unity
authors: Randolph, Ara, Egl, Rin, Joiner, Everett, Swerdlow, Jonah
abstract: By day, the grand old house shifts and shudders as if though alive. The six other residents gather in its lounges and parlors, sipping tea, squabbling over rooms, and faking civility. They laugh, they bicker, and they carry on as though nothing festers within these walls. When night falls, their facades rot away. They twist into monstrous embodiments of malice, each one a reflection of the seven deadly sins. By morning, they forget. You do not. Armed with a worn-out Monster Hunter’s Guidebook, you must reclaim its missing pages to learn who these people truly are, what they truly are. You must uncover their weaknesses and gather the weapons needed to destroy them before the storm clears.

suggestions.title: Procedural Sin Manifestation and Shader System
suggestions.desc: Replace static character models with a dynamic system that uses Unity ScriptableObjects to mutate monster forms based on player dialogue choices. Implement a custom HLSL shader that visualizes 'sin corruption' on resident sprites during the day, increasing in intensity as the player gathers more Guidebook pages.

suggestions.title: LLM-Powered Resident Interrogation and Evidence Synthesis
suggestions.desc: Integrate a local LLM via Unity Sentis to allow for natural language interrogation of the six residents. Develop a 'logic engine' that compares the player's typed questions against the gathered Monster Hunter’s Guidebook pages to dynamically reveal lore or trigger night-time transformations based on detected player theories.

suggestions.title: Asymmetric Social Deduction Multiplayer Extension
suggestions.desc: Extend the game into a 1v5 asymmetric experience using Unity Netcode for GameObjects, where one player acts as a 'Sin' and the others as Hunters. Use the day/night cycle as a phase-shift mechanic where the 'Sin' player must manage their 'Facade Meter' during social interactions to avoid being identified before night falls.

suggestions.title: AR Cross-Media Guidebook Integration
suggestions.desc: Build a mobile AR companion app using ARFoundation that tracks a physical, printed version of the Monster Hunter’s Guidebook. Players scan the pages to unlock hidden 3D animations of the monsters and 'invisible ink' clues that are required to solve the in-game puzzles, creating a bridge between physical evidence and digital gameplay.

title: Admissions Assistant - AI Chatbot
authors: Oduro, Chelsea, Bond, Jeffery, Morgan, Tanner, Scott, Allen, Parks, Joy
abstract: The Kennesaw State University Graduate Admissions website contains extensive information on programs, application processes, deadlines, and eligibility requirements. However, its dense structure can make it difficult for prospective students to quickly locate specific details. This often leads to repeated inquiries from admissions staff, increasing their workload and delaying responses to more complex applicant needs. This project addresses those challenges by introducing a chatbot capable of retrieving and presenting the website information in a more intuitive, conversational format, improving user satisfaction and reducing workflow overhead for admissions staff.
supervisor: Prof. Donald Privitera, Christopher Kibbe - Project Sponsor

suggestions.title: Implement Temporal RAG for Dynamic Admissions Deadlines
suggestions.desc: Upgrade the retrieval system using a vector database like Pinecone or Weaviate with metadata filtering to strictly distinguish between current and upcoming semester deadlines. Integrate an automated web scraper using Playwright to sync the knowledge base weekly, ensuring the bot never provides outdated application cutoff dates or expired program requirements.

suggestions.title: Multilingual Support for International Graduate Recruitment
suggestions.desc: Expand the bot's reach by integrating the DeepL API or Google Translate API to support the top five languages spoken by KSU’s international applicant pool. Add a specific logic layer for NACES-certified credential evaluation requirements, helping international students navigate the complexities of foreign transcript submission and English proficiency waivers.

suggestions.title: Personalized Status Tracking via Slate CRM Integration
suggestions.desc: Transform the chatbot from a general FAQ tool into a transactional assistant by connecting it to university CRM systems like Salesforce or Slate via OAuth. This allows authenticated users to inquire about their specific application status, verify if particular transcripts have been received, and view personalized checklists of missing documents in real-time.

suggestions.title: Conversational Analytics for Identifying Information Gaps
suggestions.desc: Build a backend dashboard using SpaCy or NLTK to perform sentiment analysis and intent clustering on student queries. This tool would visualize 'dead-end' questions where the bot lacks data, providing admissions staff with concrete evidence on which sections of the KSU website are most confusing or require more detailed content updates.

title: MorphyxCam: Instant Photo Transformation Tool
authors: Awatey, Priscilla, Doan, Long, Weng, Shaokun, Merchant, Aryan
abstract: MorphyxCam is an interactive browser-based application that lets users capture live images and apply real-time visual effects. The system performs color filtering, shading adjustments, dynamic warping, and expressive distortion effects. Users can instantly reshape features, apply artistic styles, and wrap their photos onto 3D surfaces, creating engaging and playful visual transformations. By capturing live camera images and transforming them through pixel-level filtering, distortion effects, and 3D surface mapping, the system shows how multimedia techniques can be applied creatively within a web browser. Overall, MorphyxCam showcases the potential of interactive digital imaging and highlights how accessible web technologies can be used to build creative camera-based applications similar to modern photo apps.

suggestions.title: GPU-Accelerated Custom Shader Library
suggestions.desc: Shift pixel manipulation from the CPU to the GPU using GLSL (OpenGL Shading Language). This will allow for much higher resolution video processing and the creation of complex 'fluid simulation' filters that react to movement in real-time.

suggestions.title: Neural Style Transfer Integration
suggestions.desc: Integrate TensorFlow.js to apply 'Neural Style Transfer,' allowing users to transform their live feed into the style of famous painters like Van Gogh or Picasso. Implement a lightweight version of the model to maintain high frame rates in the browser.

suggestions.title: AR-Based Virtual Try-On Extension
suggestions.desc: Use MediaPipe for face and hand tracking to transform the tool into a virtual try-on platform. Instead of general warping, use the 3D surface wrapping feature to realistically place glasses, hats, or makeup on the user’s live image.

suggestions.title: Interactive Projection Mapping Suite
suggestions.desc: Extend the application to support external projection, allowing artists to project the 'Morphyx' effects onto physical objects or walls. Add sound-reactive parameters so the visual distortions pulse and change color based on ambient music.

title: Verocity: A Reactive Combat Framework
authors: Moore, Brendan
abstract: Verocity is a Minecraft plugin designed for fast-paced, visceral combat, where interactivity and FUN take center stage. The complex mathematics and system design required to build this plugin push the limits of standard Minecraft development, providing a reactive framework for advanced combat interactions. New Actions and Combat Features: Enhanced Basic Attacks – Smooth, responsive, and satisfying to chain together. Throwable Items – Every item can be thrown. Swords lodge into enemies on impact, ready to be recovered. Dashing – Lunge to swords stuck in the ground or at enemies to pull them out while tactically repositioning. Umbral Blade – Command a powerful blade that hovers behind you, responding to your inputs. Combat revolves around mastering its use. Grabbing Enemies – Pick up and throw foes, opening up new combos and utility in combat.

suggestions.title: Verocity Tournament & Ranking System
suggestions.desc: Build a web-integrated database and leaderboard system using SQL and Redis. Track advanced combat metrics (e.g., combo length, projectile accuracy) to create a competitive matchmaking environment for Minecraft servers using this plugin.

suggestions.title: Physics-Based Procedural Animation
suggestions.desc: Implement a custom packet-based animation system that allows for 'active ragdoll' effects when entities are struck. Use inverse kinematics (IK) to make Minecraft mobs react physically to the direction and force of Verocity’s throwable items.

suggestions.title: Skill-Based 'Class' Progression Engine
suggestions.desc: Expand the framework into a full RPG system where players unlock 'Combat Arts' based on their playstyle. For example, landing consecutive 'Enhanced Basic Attacks' could unlock a temporary speed buff or a special 'finish' move.

suggestions.title: AI Combat Pacing Analyzer
suggestions.desc: Develop an internal logging tool that uses machine learning to analyze combat logs and identify 'dead moments.' Use this data to procedurally adjust mob behavior or cooldowns to ensure the combat remains at a 'visceral' and 'fun' tempo.

title: AI Driven Resident Inquiry Processing
authors: Moran, Ben, Sachwani, Sahil, Ashe, Thomas, Johnson, Sean
abstract: The AI Driven Resident Inquiry Processing System is designed to enhance the National Housing Compliance (NHC) ability to process resident inquiries using artificial intelligence(AI). NHC is a 501(c)(4) not-for-profit corporation who provides training and compliance services to the affordable housing industry. Each month NHC receives over 200 inquiries from residents via phone and email. These inquiries range from general questions to urgent, life-threatening concerns. Efficiently processing and responding to these inquiries is often critical to resident safety and well being. This project uses AI to automate resident inquiries as they are received, extract and classify key information, and display this information on an interactive dashboard for close monitoring and timely resolution. By streamlining inquiry processing, this system enables NHC to respond to resident needs faster and accurately.
supervisor: Prof. Donald Privitera, Jeff Wirrick- Project Sponsor, Kelli Sterling- Project Sponsor: Walter Hoang- NHC IT staff, Jamaul Morrison- NHC IT staff, Xeryus Starr- NHC IT staff, Satara Tyler- NHC staff

suggestions.title: RAG-Powered Compliance Assistant
suggestions.desc: Implement Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) by indexing HUD (Housing and Urban Development) manuals and NHC's internal policy documents. This allows the AI to not just categorize inquiries, but also draft suggested responses with specific regulatory citations.

suggestions.title: Crisis Detection & SMS Escalation
suggestions.desc: Build a high-priority 'Fast-Track' module that uses sentiment analysis and keyword detection (e.g., 'fire', 'leak', 'safety') to identify urgent inquiries. Integrate with the Twilio API to immediately SMS-alert the on-call property manager when a life-threatening concern is detected.

suggestions.title: Multilingual Support via Whisper & DeepL
suggestions.desc: Expand the system to handle phone inquiries by integrating OpenAI Whisper for transcription and DeepL for translation. This ensures residents who speak languages other than English can receive the same level of support and compliance accuracy.

suggestions.title: Inquiry Trend & Prediction Analytics
suggestions.desc: Develop a visualization dashboard that identifies geographic or property-level trends. For example, use the AI to detect a cluster of 'maintenance' inquiries in a specific zip code, allowing NHC to proactively address systemic issues with certain housing providers.

title: Machine Learning Linux Log Anomaly Detection
authors: Scott, Samuel, Loisy, Audrey, Silva-Rivas, Dylan, Brady, Sheamus
abstract: Cybersecurity is becoming an increasingly important part of digital life. Malware can silently intrude on a user’s system and perform malicious actions and generate unusual system behavior without the user ever being aware. This malware often presents with unusual system logs being generated. These logs, however, are difficult to consistently track and analyze, especially for casual users. To help bridge this gap between hard-to-read log data and the useful information it contains, we created LUAADS (short for Linux User Account Anomaly Detection System), designed for Ubuntu systems. LUAADS can automatically collect entries from common log files (such as syslog and auth.log), parse them into an easier-to-read format, and then analyze them for system patterns using machine learning. LUAADS can automatically alert the user when a log entry is anomalous and offers a feedback mechanism to improve on any false positives. LUAADS also offers a user-friendly GUI that allows non-tech-savvy users to be able to find and sort all their system logs in a single location. By bringing analysis of system logs to a wider audience, LUAADS helps improve Linux system security, even for non-tech-savvy users.

suggestions.title: eBPF-Driven Real-Time Monitoring
suggestions.desc: Integrate eBPF (Extended Berkeley Packet Filter) to capture system events at the kernel level rather than just reading static log files. This allows the detection system to catch malicious activity as it happens, rather than after the log is written to disk.

suggestions.title: Graph-Based Log Correlation
suggestions.desc: Convert system logs into a graph structure where nodes are IPs, processes, and files. Use a Graph Neural Network (GNN) to detect complex multi-stage attack patterns (like lateral movement) that appear normal when looking at isolated log lines.

suggestions.title: Auto-Remediation Sandbox Trigger
suggestions.desc: Connect the anomaly detection engine to a containerized sandbox. When a high-confidence threat is detected, the system could automatically isolate the offending process or block the suspicious IP address using local firewall (iptables) rules.

suggestions.title: Visual Security 'Check-Up' Desktop App
suggestions.desc: Develop a user-friendly GUI for non-technical Linux users (e.g., Ubuntu/Fedora) that summarizes log health. Use a 'Green/Yellow/Red' status system and provide one-click 'Explain This' buttons that use an LLM to translate technical log errors into plain English.

title: Active Learning System for Labeling Chest X-rays
authors: Hall, Matthew, Lane, Noah, Smith, Josh, Merrill, Elijah
abstract: This project aims to develop a complete Active Learning System for chest X-ray image classification, designed to automate data preparation, streamline model training, and reduce the manual effort required for medical image labeling. The system establishes a structured and scalable pipeline that moves from raw data ingestion to automated decision-making, incorporating dataset indexing, patient-aware splitting, preprocessing, configuration management, and validation to ensure data flows reliably through the system. The model component uses CNNs to generate baseline diagnostic predictions across chest pathologies. Active learning strategies are then applied to identify the most informative unlabeled images, enabling iterative retraining that improves model performance while minimizing labeling cost. Proposed strategies for training improvements include transfer learning with a pretrained model. Evaluation tools such as a dashboard to track performance would also enable a reproducible, clinically relevant image workflow. The final system would deliver a reproducible framework capable of managing large medical imaging datasets, selecting high-value samples for annotation, and continuously refining classification accuracy over time.

suggestions.title: Explainable AI (XAI) for Label Validation
suggestions.desc: Integrate Grad-CAM (Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping) to visualize which parts of the X-ray the model is focusing on. This helps medical professionals verify that the 'active learning' system is picking samples based on actual pathology rather than image artifacts.

suggestions.title: DICOM-Ready Hospital Integration
suggestions.desc: Build a listener service that automatically pulls images from a PACS (Picture Archiving and Communication System) using the DICOM protocol. This would allow the Active Learning pipeline to function within a real hospital IT environment.

suggestions.title: Privacy-Preserving Federated Learning
suggestions.desc: Extend the pipeline to support Federated Learning, allowing the system to train on X-ray data from multiple hospitals without moving the sensitive medical images to a central server, ensuring HIPAA compliance while increasing dataset diversity.

suggestions.title: Multi-Modal Diagnostic Support
suggestions.desc: Expand the indexing system to ingest and correlate patient EHR (Electronic Health Record) text with the X-ray images. Use a multi-modal model to see if combining clinical notes with image data improves the active learning selection process.

similar: legacy:GRCS-28|Fall 2017|mrf and ba in 3d image segmentation
legacy:UC-06|Spring 2020|chest x-ray disease detection w/ dl

title: iKnowIT: Multilingual Smartphone Tutorial Platform
authors: Juarez, Jacqueline, Bazan, David, Rivera, Julissa
abstract: Digital literacy challenges affect millions of adults who struggle with basic smartphone use due to rapidly changing technology and limited support. iKnowIT is a dynamic, web-based learning platform designed to provide clear, visual, and multilingual tutorials that guide users through essential device functions. The goal of iKnowIT is to bridge the digital divide and empower users to engage confidently with modern technology

suggestions.title: B2B Training Portal for Senior Living
suggestions.desc: Develop a management dashboard for senior living facility staff to assign 'tutorial paths' to residents. Include progress tracking to show which residents are struggling with specific tasks like 'video calling' or 'prescription management' apps.

suggestions.title: AI-Generated Interactive Overlays
suggestions.desc: Use a browser-based 'Guided Tour' library (like Intro.js) or AR overlays to guide users through their own phone's interface in real-time. This moves from watching a tutorial to 'learning-by-doing' with interactive step-by-step pointers.

suggestions.title: Voice-Activated Help Assistant
suggestions.desc: Integrate a voice-to-action system where users can simply ask, 'How do I send a photo?' The platform would then use NLP to jump directly to the relevant multilingual tutorial and narrate the instructions aloud.

suggestions.title: Cognitive Load Accessibility Study
suggestions.desc: Implement an A/B testing framework to measure user completion rates across different UI layouts. Use the data to optimize the interface specifically for users with varying levels of motor control or cognitive decline, following WCAG 2.1 guidelines.

title: Stronghold
authors: Tigani, Caitlin, Tucker, Adam, Lloyd, Camden, Balsor, Dale
abstract: Our goal for this game is to create a game using both a single agent and multi agent AIs. In our game you can either play against the current AI or train the AI up as it fights against another AI. The training will use genetic AI. So whichever AI wins that row will be the one that move on. The loser will have their weights adjusted. This means the more that you train the Stronghold AI the harder the AI will be to fight against. The final game mode available is being able to play against another.

suggestions.title: Evolving Architectures with NEAT Integration
suggestions.desc: Replace the fixed-weight genetic algorithm with NeuroEvolution of Augmenting Topologies (NEAT) using the NEAT-Python or SharpNEAT libraries. This allows the AI to evolve not just connection weights, but the actual structure of its neural network, enabling the discovery of complex combat maneuvers that static architectures cannot represent.

suggestions.title: Genetic Fitness Landscape Visualization Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Build a diagnostic tool using D3.js or Matplotlib to visualize the evolution process over thousands of generations. Track and plot 'strategy clusters' to identify when the AI discovers specific meta-tactics, such as kiting or defensive turtling, and map the genetic diversity of the population to prevent premature convergence.

suggestions.title: Cooperative Multi-Agent Tactical Roles
suggestions.desc: Expand the multi-agent system into team-based combat by implementing a hierarchical fitness function that rewards cooperative behavior. Use Unity ML-Agents to train specialized roles—such as healers, tanks, and flankers—that must evolve distinct neural networks to work together to defeat a balanced opposing AI team.

suggestions.title: Player-Centric Dynamic Difficulty Scaling Engine
suggestions.desc: Transform the trained models into a commercial-grade Dynamic Difficulty Adjustment (DDA) system. Develop a supervisor module that monitors real-time player performance metrics and hot-swaps between different evolved genetic profiles to keep the player in a 'flow state' by perfectly matching their skill level in every encounter.

title: Fundoria
authors: Stabler, Connor, Jones, Dameon, Hassan, Rafay, Liu, Michael, Horne, Brenden
abstract: Fundoria is an educational technology project designed to transform financial literacy and career readiness for elementary and middle-grade students. The platform combines interactive gameplay, AI-driven personalized learning, and real-world job simulations to teach essential financial concepts in an engaging, age-appropriate way. Students explore budgeting, saving, banking, and career skills through guided missions that adapt to their learning styles. Fundoria also provides teachers and schools with standards-aligned activities, classroom resources, and assessment tools that connect academic content to practical, everyday decisions. By blending gamification with evidence-based instruction, Fundoria empowers students to build confidence, develop responsible financial habits, and prepare for future academic and career success.

suggestions.title: Parent-Monitored 'Real Wallet' Integration
suggestions.desc: Integrate with a mock-banking API or a family finance app like Greenlight. This allows students to earn 'Fundoria Credits' for completing educational missions, which parents could then convert into real-world rewards or allowances.

suggestions.title: Multiplayer Economic Ecosystem
suggestions.desc: Develop a 'Classroom Market' feature where students can trade simulated goods or services with peers. This introduces concepts of supply and demand, negotiation, and contract management in a controlled, collaborative environment.

suggestions.title: LLM-Powered Financial Mentors
suggestions.desc: Implement AI NPCs (Non-Player Characters) using an LLM that act as 'Financial Advisors.' Students can ask these characters open-ended questions like, 'Should I buy this upgrade now or save for a bigger one?' and receive age-appropriate reasoning.

suggestions.title: Adaptive Difficulty for Neurodiversity
suggestions.desc: Create a learning-analytics engine that detects when a student is struggling with specific math concepts (e.g., percentages in interest rates). The system would procedurally adjust the complexity of missions to keep students in the 'Flow State' without causing frustration.

title: Project Ibis
authors: Alderman, Isaac, Mizell, Braden, Sutton, Collin
abstract: Project Ibis (working title) is a first person, narrative heavy, puzzle-lite RPG that follows the story of a renaissance era plague doctor and their attempt to alter the minds of three subjects; a gardener, a street urchin, and a priest. The game’s narrative is set in historically accurate 1637 Florence, Italy, in the wake of the Great Plague of Milan, and draws heavily from renaissance culture. Each subject being treated is a complex person with personal conflicts and issues; our focus is on tackling mental and emotional health through empathy and nuance rather than diagnosis. Different aspects of each subject’s psyche take physical form in the dreamscape environments, allowing the player to speak and interact with them directly, influencing the state of the subject themself, and, in turn, the environment around the player. The game features many puzzles, with solutions ranging from alchemy, exploration, and dialogue, to spatial and logical reasoning. The primary goal of play is to reach the core of each subject’s mind, allowing the opportunity to change their fundamental outlook on life, if the player so chooses. Either way, the consequences will follow the player until the end. Our primary goal with this game is to make our players think carefully about how their actions might affect each patient they treat, and find their own answer to the question of how much they should influence each one.

suggestions.title: Historical LLM Persona Integration
suggestions.desc: Fine-tune a small LLM (like Llama 3 or Phi-3) on 17th-century Italian literature and plague treatises to power the dialogue of the gardener and priest. This would allow for unscripted, historically accurate conversations that react to the player's specific medical interventions.

suggestions.title: Procedural 1630s Florence Generator
suggestions.desc: Expand the game world by using OpenStreetMap data of modern Florence and a procedural generation algorithm to 'retro-fit' the city into its 1637 layout. Implement a system that dynamically adds plague markers, like boarded doors and crosses, based on the narrative progression.

suggestions.title: Humoral Medicine Alchemy System
suggestions.desc: Build a complex crafting sub-system based on Galenic medicine (the four humors). Players must balance blood, phlegm, yellow bile, and black bile in their subjects using period-accurate ingredients found in the game world, directly affecting the 'mind alteration' mechanic.

suggestions.title: Asymmetric Multiplayer Quarantine Mode
suggestions.desc: Develop a mode where one player acts as the Plague Doctor and another acts as the City Guard or a 'Subject' attempting to hide their symptoms. Use the existing narrative assets to create a stealth-based survival game set during the Great Plague of Milan.

title: Agentic AI For Intelligent Customer Communication
authors: Papadopoulos, Lucas, Hopkins, Jeremy, Gargour, Munir, Dease, Weston
abstract: E-commerce web shoppers need fast, reliable responses to a variety of requests: account modifications, order tracking, or policy inquiries. Businesses must address user queries in a fast and efficient manner, or else lose customers. Multi-agent AI models boast the ability to answer customer questions and act upon consumer queries without outside intervention. However, research is sparse as to how agentic models can transfer benefit to large commercial software stacks under realistic commercial load. We sought to ask whether a multi-agent AI architecture can effectively handle commercial-scale e-commerce customer service tasks. Moreover, we investigated how a multi-agent AI architecture compares to traditional single-agent customer service solutions in handling complex e-commerce tasks. Our team developed a multi-agent AI architecture using specialized Claude Haiku agents coordinated through LangGraph, with a React frontend and PostgreSQL DB-Kafka backend. Testing will compare our multi-agent system against a single-agent baseline to evaluate effectiveness in handling complex customer service requests. Preliminary results have shown that an agentic AI architecture significantly increases query-response correctness for customer requests.
supervisor: Prof. Sharon Perry, Project Sponsor: Capgemini - Anna Richard and Senthilraj Duraisamy

suggestions.title: Agentic Stress-Testing Framework
suggestions.desc: Build a benchmarking suite using Locust or JMeter specifically designed to measure the 'reasoning latency' of multi-agent LLM systems under high traffic. Analyze how token usage and API costs scale when agents are forced to collaborate on complex e-commerce edge cases.

suggestions.title: Real-Time ERP/Inventory Loopback
suggestions.desc: Extend the agents by building a secure middleware that connects them to a live SQL database or a mock SAP/Oracle ERP system. Allow agents to not only answer questions but also execute 'Soft Allocations' of inventory or generate return labels via API calls.

suggestions.title: Multimodal Visual Support Agent
suggestions.desc: Integrate GPT-4o or a similar vision model to allow the agentic system to process customer-uploaded photos of damaged products or incorrect shipping labels. The agents should be able to verify the claim against the order history and trigger an automatic refund.

suggestions.title: Zero-Shot Policy Compliance Guardrails
suggestions.desc: Develop a specialized 'Supervisor Agent' that uses RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) to check every response against a company's legal and return policies before it reaches the customer, ensuring 100% compliance in a commercial setting.

title: Integrating a Pressure Profiling Dock into an Open-Source Digital Art Framework
authors: Miller, Luke, Enyart, Noah, Smith, Jonah, Jones, Jaeden
abstract: The effectiveness and expressiveness of digital drawing are heavily dependent on an artist’s control over their medium; pen pressure dynamics, which enables fine-tuned variation in line weight and opacity. While the use of this tool improves an artist's control, the open-source editor GIMP currently offers only limited means to visualize or adjust these pressure behaviors in real time. GIMP does support pressure-based input, however, it lacks a dedicated interface for configuring a personalized pressure profile, creating a barrier for users seeking precision comparable to commercial tools. This project addresses that gap by designing and implementing a Stylus Pressure Profiling Dock within the GIMP application environment. in addition to the implementation, this project also investigates key research questions, including how pressure data can be effectively captured from various drawing tablet devices within GIMP’s existing input architecture, and how optimization techniques can be combined with the logistic (sigmoid) function to generate an accurate, user-customizable pressure S-curve.

suggestions.title: C-Based GIMP Plug-in Migration
suggestions.desc: Convert the prototype dock from a script into a native GIMP C-plugin using the GIMP 3.0 API (libgimp). This would allow for lower latency between the pressure sensor and the visual dock, making it viable for high-speed professional sketching.

suggestions.title: Predictive Neural Line Smoothing
suggestions.desc: Implement a machine learning model that analyzes the artist's pressure curves over time to predict and smooth out jitters caused by hardware limitations or hand tremors, providing a 'Smart Stabilizer' feature within the new interface.

suggestions.title: Haptic Feedback Integration for Wacom/Huion
suggestions.desc: Extend the tool to communicate with tablet drivers to provide haptic feedback (vibration) based on the pressure profile set in the dock. This would give digital artists a physical sensation of 'friction' as they press harder on the screen.

suggestions.title: Cross-Platform Pressure Profile Marketplace
suggestions.desc: Create a standardized JSON format for 'Pressure Profiles' and build a cloud-sync feature. This would allow artists to export their custom GIMP pressure dynamics and share them with a community or import them into other open-source tools like Krita.

title: Light'em Up
authors: Sutton, Collin, Jones, Ronnie, Anderson, Max
abstract: The primary goal of “Light’em Up” is to create engaging and intelligent AI that can operate within three degrees of freedom and against forces of gravity. Enemies will track the player, predict their movement, and collaborate to set traps and outflank them. All of this takes place in space, at high speeds, and at a scale where gravity has a real effect on navigation. We have four distinct AI enemies at play: Homing missiles - single agent system that follows the player’s movement at a slightly faster speed Tracking missiles - single agent system that moves at a constant speed and direction based on prediction of the players position at the time of impact/detonation Local Space Force - Multi-agent system that follow the player while firing lasers and avoiding collision with obstacles Inter-Galactic Space Force - Advanced multiagent system that corporate in order to put the player in disadvantaged positions while also inheriting behaviors from the Local Space Force

suggestions.title: Reinforcement Learning Flight Academy
suggestions.desc: Replace the scripted AI behaviors with agents trained via Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) in Unity ML-Agents. Train them to navigate gravity wells and optimize fuel consumption while pursuing a player in a 3D space environment.

suggestions.title: Dynamic Gravity-Aware A* Pathfinding
suggestions.desc: Develop a custom pathfinding algorithm that factors in gravitational pull as a 'cost' for movement. Enemies should calculate trajectories that use planetary gravity to slingshot toward the player, saving energy and increasing speed.

suggestions.title: Swarm Intelligence Boids Integration
suggestions.desc: Implement a 'Swarm' enemy type that uses Boids flocking algorithms combined with the project's 'collaboration' logic. A hundred small ships should move as a single cohesive unit to surround the player, breaking formation only to dodge obstacles.

suggestions.title: Procedural Solar System Generator
suggestions.desc: Build a system that generates infinite, physics-consistent star systems with varying planet sizes and gravity strengths. The AI must adapt its movement strategies (e.g., homing vs. flanking) based on the specific gravitational environment of each generated sector.

title: Digital Micromouse Maze Simulation
authors: Bell, Chase
abstract: We construct a digital simulation of the Micromouse competition and analyze popular algorithms for searching (A*, Dijkstra’s, and Flood-fill). From our analyses, we design an algorithm with the goal of achieving the shortest possible run time to the end of the maze.

suggestions.title: Bridge Digital Simulations to ESP32 Hardware
suggestions.desc: Port the validated A* and Flood-fill logic from the simulation to a physical microcontroller like an ESP32 or Arduino. Integrate real-world constraints by adding noise models for VL53L0X Time-of-Flight sensors and simulating PID motor control to see how physical momentum affects the 'shortest run time' calculated in the digital version.

suggestions.title: Deep Reinforcement Learning for Autonomous Navigation
suggestions.desc: Replace static algorithms with a Deep Q-Network (DQN) or PPO agent using the Gymnasium (formerly OpenAI Gym) framework. Train the mouse to learn 'diagonal' movement strategies and high-speed cornering optimizations that traditional cell-based Flood-fill algorithms often ignore, comparing the AI's efficiency against Dijkstra's baseline.

suggestions.title: Multi-Agent Swarm Mapping and Coordination
suggestions.desc: Extend the simulation to support multiple mice working in parallel to map a massive 32x32 maze. Implement a communication protocol where agents share discovered wall data in real-time to build a global map faster, utilizing a distributed version of the project's search algorithms to find the goal with minimal redundant exploration.

suggestions.title: Procedural Benchmark Suite for IEEE Maze Standards
suggestions.desc: Build a robust testing tool that procedurally generates mazes following official IEEE Micromouse competition rules. Develop a visual heat-map analytics dashboard that tracks 'computation time per cell' and 'total path deviation' to identify specific maze configurations where Flood-fill outperforms A*, providing a data-driven guide for selecting algorithms based on maze complexity.

title: AI-Powered GRE Vocabulary App
authors: Verde, Michael, Landeta, Ellyan, Onuorah, Cynthia, Tran, David, Binchamo, Bereket
abstract: Our project develops a client-side React application for GRE vocabulary practice using structured JSON word data. The site supports filtering, search, audio output, and randomized quizzes. A reinforcement-learning hint system, inspired by prior research on adaptive learning, guides users toward difficult vocabulary. We aimed to create an interface that demonstrates how lightweight front-end tools can support personalized study without requiring a backend.
supervisor: Prof. Donald Privatera, Manohair Sai Jasti (Project Sponsor Founder of Scafwording)

suggestions.title: Cross-Device Synchronization with Firebase and Anki
suggestions.desc: Migrate the client-side JSON architecture to a Firebase backend to enable persistent user profiles and cross-device progress tracking. Use the AnkiConnect API to allow users to export their reinforcement-learning-optimized word lists into Anki decks for long-term spaced repetition.

suggestions.title: Multi-Armed Bandit Scheduling for Spaced Repetition
suggestions.desc: Enhance the RL hint system by implementing a Multi-Armed Bandit (MAB) algorithm to optimize word review intervals. Compare the effectiveness of this approach against traditional SuperMemo-2 (SM-2) algorithms by tracking user retention rates across different vocabulary difficulty tiers.

suggestions.title: LLM-Generated Contextual Mnemonics and Visualizations
suggestions.desc: Integrate the OpenAI API to dynamically generate personalized mnemonic stories and DALL-E 3 imagery for each GRE word based on user-defined interests. This transforms static word lists into a multi-sensory experience that leverages the 'Self-Reference Effect' to improve memory encoding.

suggestions.title: Voice-Interactive Socratic Tutoring Interface
suggestions.desc: Build a conversational layer using OpenAI Whisper for speech-to-text and GPT-4o to act as a verbal Socratic tutor. Instead of simple quizzes, the app would engage the student in a dialogue, prompting them to use the vocabulary in sentence-building exercises and providing real-time feedback on semantic accuracy.

title: Georgia Watch - Georgia Hospital Accountability Score
authors: Straiton, Robert, Cox, Patrick, Nortey, Constant, Amaravadi, Sankalp, Keller, Kahmin
abstract: Georgia Watch and the members of this team have partnered to change how Georgia residents understand their healthcare by providing a source of objective metrics which affect their care. This project represents the quintessential React Project produced with the purpose of a reactive interface for the end user, built for maintainability for any subsequent developers. The hospital data is maintained in JSON format for its ease of parsing and adjustment pending any changes. The interactive map was developed through the MAPBOX library, and the team is maintaining a deployment via an independent repository with necessary control over the website.

suggestions.title: Migrate to PostGIS for Dynamic Spatial Queries
suggestions.desc: Transition the project from static JSON files to a PostgreSQL database with the PostGIS extension. This allows for complex server-side geospatial queries, such as identifying the highest-rated hospitals within a specific driving radius or filtering facilities based on dynamic intersectional criteria that Mapbox's client-side rendering cannot handle alone.

suggestions.title: Correlate Accountability Scores with CDC Social Vulnerability Data
suggestions.desc: Integrate the CDC’s Social Vulnerability Index (SVI) dataset to visualize the relationship between hospital performance and community demographics. Use d3.js to build comparative charts that highlight disparities in healthcare quality across different Georgia counties, identifying specific 'healthcare deserts' where low accountability scores intersect with high poverty levels.

suggestions.title: Build a Predictive ER Wait-Time PWA
suggestions.desc: Transform the platform into a mobile-first Progressive Web App (PWA) that incorporates real-time hospital diversion data from the Georgia Coordinating Center. Implement a forecasting model using Python's Scikit-learn to predict ER wait times based on historical trends, helping users decide which high-scoring hospital to visit during peak hours.

suggestions.title: Automate Data Pipelines with CMS API Integration
suggestions.desc: Develop an automated ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) pipeline using GitHub Actions to pull the latest healthcare quality metrics directly from the CMS (Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services) API. This ensures the Georgia Watch dashboard remains up-to-date without manual JSON editing, allowing developers to focus on expanding the React frontend components.

title: BudgetWise - The College Friendly Budgeting App
authors: Thompson, Taylor, Issa, Yasmeen, Khan, Sameer, Nguyen, John, Lechuga, Reynaldo
abstract: For our senior project, we developed BudgetWise, a budgeting app designed to help college students manage their finances with confidence. BudgetWise has an emphasis on ease of use and accessibility, with features such as dark mode for improved visibility. Bank accounts and credit cards can be securely linked to the user’s account where they can track their recent purchases, create budgets based on their personalized needs, and track their spending with a dynamic progress bar that changes colors the closer they get to their budget limit. By combining financial tools with accessibility, BudgetWise empowers students to make informed financial decisions and help build strong money management habits.

suggestions.title: Predictive AI 'Burn Rate' Calculator
suggestions.desc: Use a simple regression model to analyze the user's spending habits over the first two weeks of a month and predict the exact day they will run out of 'fun money.' Display this as a 'Days to Zero' countdown on the main dashboard.

suggestions.title: Campus Card & Meal Plan Sync
suggestions.desc: Develop a scraper or API integration for university-specific systems (like Blackboard Transact or Grubhub Campus) to pull in non-bank balances like 'Dining Dollars' or 'Flex Points,' giving a truly holistic view of a student's resources.

suggestions.title: Plaid-Powered Auto-Categorization Engine
suggestions.desc: Move beyond manual entry by fully implementing the Plaid API to categorize transactions specifically for student life (e.g., 'Textbooks,' 'Late Night Dining,' 'Student Orgs'). Create a custom mapping layer to group 'Starbucks' and 'Campus Cafe' under a single 'Caffeine' budget.

suggestions.title: Anonymous Peer Spending Benchmarks
suggestions.desc: Build a secure, anonymized data pipeline that allows students to see how their spending on 'Groceries' or 'Rent' compares to the average for their specific university or zip code, helping them identify if they are overpaying for local services.

title: Falling Debris
authors: Duarte, Julian, Martin, Adam, Collins, Rylan, Haggard, Hugh, Boecker, Chance
abstract: Falling Debris is a student-developed party 2D platformer game in which players have to survive falling blocks by grappling upward to outlive the others. The game is played in rounds, and between them, players can purchase items to improve their odds of survival.

suggestions.title: Low-Latency Peer-to-Peer Networking via Mirror
suggestions.desc: Transform the local multiplayer experience into a robust online game by implementing the Mirror networking API. Focus on developing client-side prediction and lag compensation for the grappling hook physics to ensure the gameplay feels responsive even with 100ms+ of latency.

suggestions.title: Reinforcement Learning for Predictive Grapple-Bots
suggestions.desc: Use the Unity ML-Agents toolkit to train AI competitors that can simulate human-like survival strategies. Research and implement a neural network that predicts the trajectory of falling debris to optimize grappling points, providing a challenging single-player mode or fill-in bots for empty lobbies.

suggestions.title: Mobile Gesture Controls and Local WiFi Lobbies
suggestions.desc: Port the game to iOS/Android and replace keyboard inputs with touch-based gesture controls for swinging and aiming the grapple. Implement a local discovery service using a tool like Google Nearby Messages so players can host and join games instantly over the same WiFi network.

suggestions.title: Procedural Wave Architect and Community Workshop
suggestions.desc: Build an integrated level editor that allows players to design custom 'debris waves' with specific block types, speeds, and hazards. Create a backend using Firebase or AWS to host a community workshop where players can upload, download, and rate custom-designed rounds and shop item configurations.

title: Website Makeover - Loving Arms Cancer Outreach
authors: Pacheco, Yuliana, Cook, Alicia, Tompkins, Taylor, Bridges, Schuyler, Roberts, Dalton
abstract: Loving Arms Cancer Outreach (LACO) provides financial, emotional, and community support to individuals affected by cancer, making an accessible and reliable website essential to its mission. Our team conducted a quality assurance audit using tools such as Google Lighthouse and axe DevTools, identifying issues with accessibility, navigation, readability, and mobile responsiveness. Using these findings, we redesigned key sections of the site, improved layouts and forms, and recommended updated plugins to enhance usability and long-term performance. We also developed a Website Architecture and Maintenance Guide to support sustainability. This project establishes the foundation for a modern, user-friendly website that strengthens LACO’s outreach and donor engagement.
supervisor: Prof. Donald Privitera, Cat Gankofskie (Project Sponsor)

suggestions.title: HIPAA-Compliant Patient Support Portal
suggestions.desc: Build a secure login area using Firebase or AWS Amplify where cancer patients can safely store and track their financial assistance documents. This moves the site from an informational brochure to a functional utility for the LACO mission.

suggestions.title: Automated Grant Eligibility Screener
suggestions.desc: Develop an interactive multi-step form (using React or a similar framework) that allows users to input their situation and instantly see which LACO financial grants they qualify for, reducing the administrative load on the nonprofit's staff.

suggestions.title: Progressive Web App (PWA) Offline Directory
suggestions.desc: Convert the redesigned site into a PWA so that patients in low-connectivity areas (like hospitals or rural clinics) can still access the directory of local support groups and emergency contact numbers without a live internet connection.

suggestions.title: Accessibility A/B Testing Suite
suggestions.desc: Implement a system to track how users with screen readers or high-contrast settings navigate the new layout. Use this data to create a 'Live Accessibility Scoreboard' that alerts the admins if new content updates break the site's WCAG 2.1 compliance.

title: V.A.P.R. Rush
authors: Collins, Rylan, Duarte, Jullian, Hugh, Oliver, McMillian, Ethan
abstract: A 3D platformer where you can transformfrom a cube to a boat and a plane. The game is on mobile and features the player traversing through a vapor wave inspired level with techno music in the background. They must performjumps and lane switches to the beat of the song, and survive to the end of the level to win.

suggestions.title: Algorithmic Track Generation via Audio Spectrum Analysis
suggestions.desc: Replace manual level design with a system that uses Unity’s AudioSource.GetSpectrumData or the Librosa library to analyze user-uploaded MP3s. Programmatically spawn lane-switch obstacles and transformation triggers that sync perfectly with detected BPM and frequency peaks, creating an infinite procedural vaporwave world.

suggestions.title: Asynchronous Multiplayer with Vapor-Ghost Data
suggestions.desc: Implement a cloud-based leaderboard using Firebase or PlayFab that stores player run-data as 'ghost' avatars. Future players can race against these translucent 3D recordings in real-time, allowing for competitive social play without the latency requirements of synchronous mobile multiplayer.

suggestions.title: Spatial AR Integration for Room-Scale Racing
suggestions.desc: Port the mobile experience to ARCore or ARKit to project the three-lane track onto the physical floor of the user's room. Use the mobile device's gyroscope and accelerometer as a literal steering wheel, requiring physical movement and tilting to trigger the cube-to-plane transformations.

suggestions.title: Biometric Dynamic Difficulty and Visual Distortion
suggestions.desc: Integrate Apple HealthKit or Google Fit to fetch real-time heart rate data from a smartwatch during gameplay. Develop a feedback loop where the game’s techno-tempo, vaporwave color saturation, and obstacle density dynamically scale based on the player’s physiological stress levels to maintain a 'flow state'.

title: Cloud-Native CI/CD Pipeline
authors: Meduteni, Enitan, Elmostafa, Rami, Crowley, Matt, Fleming, Kade, Graffree, Cecily
abstract: This project documents a 12-week capstone implementing a cloud-native CI/CD pipeline using industry-standard DevOps tools. The system integrates Jenkins for continuous integration, Kubernetes for container orchestration, GitOps (ArgoCD) for automated deployments, DevSecOps practices including RBAC and vulnerability scanning, and comprehensive monitoring using Prometheus and Grafana. Mentored by Sudheer Amgothu, Principal Cloud Operations Engineer.
supervisor: Dr. Yan Huang, Industry Sponsor: Sudheer Amgothu

suggestions.title: Multi-Architecture Build Pipelines for IoT Edge
suggestions.desc: Extend the current Jenkins setup to support Docker buildx for concurrent ARM64 and AMD64 image generation. Deploy these images to a hybrid cluster using K3s on Raspberry Pi nodes to simulate real-world IoT edge environments where cloud-native applications must run on constrained hardware.

suggestions.title: Automated Policy-as-Code with OPA and Kyverno
suggestions.desc: Move beyond basic RBAC by integrating Open Policy Agent (OPA) into the ArgoCD deployment lifecycle. Develop a suite of Rego-based policies that automatically audit and reject Kubernetes manifests that fail to meet CIS Benchmarks, ensuring high-security standards are enforced before code reaches production.

suggestions.title: FinOps Integration for Cost-Aware Deployments
suggestions.desc: Integrate Kubecost with your existing Prometheus and Grafana stack to track the real-time dollar cost of every microservice deployment. Create a Jenkins pipeline gate that uses the Kubecost API to prevent deployments if the estimated resource usage exceeds a pre-defined departmental cloud budget.

suggestions.title: GitOps-Driven Chaos Engineering and Resilience Testing
suggestions.desc: Incorporate Chaos Mesh or LitmusChaos into the CD workflow to automatically trigger failure experiments—like pod kills or network latency—immediately after an ArgoCD sync. Configure Prometheus alerts to trigger an automatic rollback in ArgoCD if the application fails to recover within defined Service Level Objectives (SLOs).

title: CI-CD Pipeline Team 2
authors: Arnold, Cameron, , , ,
abstract: Our project is about creating a basic cloud-native pipeline that can build and deploy an application in a more automated way. We will also try to add some security checks and monitoring tools so that we can see how everything is working. The goal is to get hands-on experience with the process and show a working demo at the end of the semester.
supervisor: Prof. Donald Privitera, Mentor - Sudheer Amgothu

suggestions.title: Shift-Left Security with Automated SCA and DAST
suggestions.desc: Upgrade the basic security checks by integrating Snyk for Software Composition Analysis (SCA) and OWASP ZAP for Dynamic Application Security Testing (DAST). Configure the pipeline to automatically fail builds if a vulnerability with a CVSS score higher than 7.0 is detected in either the application code or its third-party dependencies.

suggestions.title: Zero-Trust Deployment using OpenID Connect
suggestions.desc: Eliminate the risk of leaked secrets by replacing long-lived cloud credentials with OpenID Connect (OIDC). Research and implement a passwordless authentication flow between the CI/CD runner (like GitHub Actions or GitLab CI) and AWS/Azure, utilizing short-lived tokens and least-privilege IAM roles scoped to specific deployment tasks.

suggestions.title: Continuous Infrastructure Drift Detection with Terraform
suggestions.desc: Expand the pipeline to include Infrastructure as Code (IaC) management using Terraform or Pulumi. Implement a scheduled 'reconciliation loop' that uses tools like Driftctl to compare the live cloud environment against the state files, automatically alerting the team if manual changes have compromised the security posture of the production server.

suggestions.title: GitOps Transformation with ArgoCD and Kubernetes
suggestions.desc: Transition the project from a 'push-based' pipeline to a 'pull-based' GitOps model by deploying an ArgoCD controller within a Kubernetes cluster. Build a dedicated 'Environment Repository' that stores K8s manifests, allowing students to observe how changes to the Git configuration are automatically synchronized to the live environment without manual deployment triggers.

title: Smart HR Onboarding with Microsoft 365 Team 2 Capstone Project
authors: Gregory, Annalise, Colley, Michael, Laurent, David, Agarwal, Harshita, Kumar, Nilesh
abstract: The Smart HR Onboarding with Microsoft 365 Capstone Project aims to transform the new hire onboarding experience by leveraging Microsoft 365 tools to deliver a seamless, automated process. Using Power Automate, we streamline tasks through automated emails, reminders, and documents sharing. This ensures every step of the onboarding journey is efficient and consistent. Sharepoint and Outlook serve as the main sources of storing and sharing required tasks. Power Automate's integration with Power BI allows for real-time reporting with an interactive dashboard the provides insights into onboarding progress or areas where new hires need support. This project aims to simplify onboarding for HR teams and makes onboarding progress measurable.

suggestions.title: Custom React Onboarding Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Replace the standard SharePoint UI with a custom-built React frontend that communicates with the Microsoft Graph API. This would provide a more modern, 'app-like' experience for new hires while still using M365 as the secure backend.

suggestions.title: Virtual Office 'Buddy' Chatbot
suggestions.desc: Use Copilot Studio or Azure Bot Service to create a chatbot that lives within Microsoft Teams. It should answer common 'Day 1' questions (e.g., 'How do I set up my printer?') by querying the SharePoint documentation created in this project.

suggestions.title: Onboarding Bottleneck Analytics
suggestions.desc: Extend the Power BI dashboard to include 'Time-to-Completion' metrics for every step of the Power Automate flow. Use this to identify which departments or managers are slowing down the onboarding process, providing actionable HR insights.

suggestions.title: External API Integration Layer
suggestions.desc: Use Azure Logic Apps to connect the M365 onboarding flow to external HRIS platforms like Workday or BambooHR. This would allow the project to trigger hardware procurement (e.g., in ServiceNow) the moment a 'New Hire' is added to the system.

title: AI Graduate Admissions Assistant
authors: Hyatt, Katherine, Wright, Ginger, Edgar, Pablo, Akinwusi, Oluwatosin, Johnson, Alan
