abstract: Project Overview: Create a "Proof of Concept" for an AI Graduate Admissions Assistant chatbot that will: • Autonomously reference KSU website information in real-time • Direct users efficiently to specific, relevant content • Reduce questions for admissions personnel • Self-update its knowledge base when website content changes •Provide accurate, instant responses to common admissions questions and allow for response correction in an admin dashboard

suggestions.title: Hybrid Vector-Graph RAG System
suggestions.desc: Improve the chatbot's accuracy by combining a Vector Database (like Pinecone) for semantic search with a Knowledge Graph (Neo4j) that maps relationships between prerequisites, course codes, and faculty, allowing for complex multi-hop queries.

suggestions.title: Autonomous Website Change Monitor
suggestions.desc: Develop a Python-based cron job that uses Playwright or BeautifulSoup to periodically scrape the KSU site, detect changes in tuition or deadlines, and automatically trigger an LLM-based 'Summary Update' to the chatbot's knowledge base.

suggestions.title: Voice-First Admissions Hotline
suggestions.desc: Integrate the AI assistant with Twilio Voice and OpenAI Whisper to create a phone-based admissions assistant. This would allow prospective students to call a number and get instant, verbal answers to their questions using the same knowledge base.

suggestions.title: Document Pre-Screener OCR Engine
suggestions.desc: Add a feature where students can upload their unofficial transcripts. Use AWS Textract or Azure Document Intelligence to parse the PDF, compare it against admission requirements in the RAG, and give the student an 'Estimated Eligibility' score.

title: Using Dog Breed Classification Uncertainty Estimation to Inform Mixed Breed Ancestry
authors: Mackey, Brandon, Koya, Maryam, King, Theodore, Hutchison, Scott
abstract: This study investigates whether Monte Carlo uncertainty estimation and probability distributions can be used to identify ancestral composition of mixed-breed dogs. A dataset containing images of purebred dogs was used to train a Monte Carlo Dropout model. The trained model will next be tested on images of mixed breed dogs. Our hypothesis is that the model can be used to provide informative probability distribution for breed ancestry classification, offering a potentially valuable tool for analyzing the genetics of dogs.

suggestions.title: Deep Ensembles for Improved Ancestry Calibration
suggestions.desc: Replace the Monte Carlo Dropout approach with Deep Ensembles to achieve better-calibrated uncertainty scores. Use the Stanford Dogs dataset to train multiple diversely initialized models and compare whether ensemble variance provides a more reliable proxy for genetic heterozygosity than single-model dropout.

suggestions.title: Synthetic Hybrid Image Generation for Ground-Truth Validation
suggestions.desc: Develop a pipeline using StyleGAN2 or Stable Diffusion to generate synthetic mixed-breed images with known parentage ratios. This provides a 'ground truth' dataset that does not exist in the wild, allowing you to quantitatively measure if the model's predicted probability distributions accurately reflect the controlled ancestral percentages.

suggestions.title: Explainable Phenotypic Mapping with Grad-CAM
suggestions.desc: Integrate Grad-CAM or SHAP to visualize which physical features—such as ear shape, muzzle length, or coat texture—drive specific breed probabilities. Cross-reference these heatmaps with AKC breed standards to determine if the model's uncertainty stems from visual trait overlap between similar breeds (e.g., Whippets vs. Greyhounds).

suggestions.title: Real-time Shelter Triage via TensorFlow Lite
suggestions.desc: Compress and deploy the uncertainty model to a mobile application using TensorFlow Lite for use in animal shelters. The app would identify dogs with high visual 'breed entropy,' flagging them for staff as high-priority candidates for expensive genetic DNA kits like Embark, optimizing the shelter's testing budget.

title: Reproducing Extended Isolation Forests with STAR-CAST
authors: Patrick, Drew, Sivakoti, Ram Sai, Malik, Rohit, Kamal, Vardhineedi Surya, Palagundla, Venkata Sasidhar Reddy
abstract: Fraud models routinely flag suspicious transactions but rarely explain why, which slows investigations and erodes trust. In this work we study Extended Isolation Forest (EIF) for unsupervised fraud detection and propose STAR-CAST, a lightweight framework that turns raw anomaly scores into threshold-aligned IF–THEN rule cards with explicit reliability measures. Using the public credit-card fraud dataset (284,807 transactions, 492 frauds; ~0.17% prevalence), we apply a time-aware 70/15/15 Train/Validation/Test split and fit-on-train preprocessing (Amount log1p?z; Time z; V1–V28 retained). We train IF, EIF, an EIF ensemble, a Mahalanobis baseline, and density models (HBOS, COPOD, ECOD) fully unsupervised, evaluate them as rankers first (PR-AUC, Max-F1, Precision@K), then freeze Validation thresholds and measure Test-set performance at FPR-targeted and recall-targeted operating points, with hourly block-bootstrap CIs to capture uncertainty. On Test, density/tail methods perform best on these PCA-style features (HBOS AP ˜ 0.139, COPOD ˜ 0.103, ECOD ˜ 0.072), while EIF consistently improves over IF (EIF AP ˜ 0.0534, EIF-ENS ˜ 0.0537, IF ˜ 0.0498; Mahalanobis ˜ 0.0456). At a realistic FPR of 0.5%, HBOS and COPOD achieve usable precision and recall, while EIF remains operationally competitive. STAR-CAST generates compact rules with high stability, fidelity to the model decision, and validated local precision, providing auditable, actionable explanations rather than opaque scores.

suggestions.title: Streaming EIF for Real-Time Detection
suggestions.desc: Migrate the STAR-CAST framework from batch processing to a streaming architecture using Apache Flink or Kafka. This would allow the model to flag transactions and generate IF-THEN explanations in milliseconds as they occur.

suggestions.title: SHAP vs. STAR-CAST Benchmark Study
suggestions.desc: Conduct a comparative research study evaluating the computational efficiency and human-readability of STAR-CAST's rule cards against SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) values for the same credit card fraud dataset.

suggestions.title: Human-in-the-Loop Rule Refinement
suggestions.desc: Build a web dashboard where fraud investigators can review the IF-THEN rule cards generated by STAR-CAST and 'upvote' or 'downvote' their accuracy. Use this feedback to fine-tune the isolation forest's threshold parameters automatically.

suggestions.title: Adversarial Attack Simulation Engine
suggestions.desc: Develop a system to generate synthetic 'adversarial' transactions designed to fool the EIF model. Test how the STAR-CAST rule cards change under attack and whether they can help investigators identify when the model is being bypassed.

title: SafeCircle: AI and Micro-radar-based Remote Monitoring for Patients with AD/ADRD
authors: RAHMAN, AWAN-UR-, Borty, Soarov, Quddus, Shakib
abstract: Alzheimer's disease and related dementias (AD/ADRD) is an irreversible and degenerative neurological condition that severely impacts neurons, resulting in cognitive decline and memory loss. This study explores a mHealth system, including a SafeCircle iOS prototype, a novel solution that combines artificial intelligence with cutting-edge micro-radar technology. The platform offers a variety of features, including management of patient and caregiver profiles, real-time alerts in case of emergencies, emergency contact lists, one-touch SOS support, sharing of live locations, and recording of unusual events in video. It is a responsive and reliable care assistant that optimizes patient safety while reducing caregiver burden.
supervisor: Dr. Nazmus Sakib, Dr. Sumit Chakravarty

suggestions.title: Radar-Based Gait Analysis for Fall Prediction
suggestions.desc: Use the micro-radar point cloud data to train a Temporal Convolutional Network (TCN) that detects changes in a patient's walking pattern (gait). Changes in stride length or speed can predict an increased risk of falling before an accident happens.

suggestions.title: Federated Learning Privacy Layer
suggestions.desc: Implement a Federated Learning framework (using Flower or CoreML) so that the AI models are trained locally on the caregiver's device using radar data. This ensures that sensitive movement data of AD/ADRD patients never leaves the home.

suggestions.title: Smart Home Emergency Response Loop
suggestions.desc: Integrate the SafeCircle app with HomeKit or Home Assistant. If the micro-radar detects a fall or an emergency, the system could automatically unlock the front door for first responders and turn on all lights to aid navigation.

suggestions.title: Caregiver Respite Coordination Hub
suggestions.desc: Expand the iOS prototype to include a peer-to-peer scheduling system where multiple caregivers can share the monitoring load. Integrate a 'handoff' feature that summarizes the radar-detected activity of the patient over the last 8 hours.

title: OncoBoost - Hydration Monitoring Application
authors: Madubike, Blossom, Alam, Aafra, Ojo, Damola
abstract: Dehydration is a common and preventable complication for oncology patients, especially those undergoing chemotherapy and radiation. Side effects such as nausea, fatigue, and loss of appetite make it difficult for patients to maintain adequate fluid intake, contributing to avoidable discomfort and potential treatment disruptions. This capstone project presents Onco-Boost, a mobile hydration monitoring application designed to help adult oncology patients track daily fluid intake, recognize their intake patterns, and stay engaged in daily self-care between clinic visits. Built with React Native and Expo, and backed by Firebase for authentication and cloud data storage. Onco-Boost translates clinical hydration guidance and research into an intuitive, patient-friendly interface. The app features a streamlined home page for quick-tap beverage logging with 4 beverage options, a history page that displays previous entries and daily totals, and a profile page where users can set or adjust basic information. The system is designed with simple data models, role-appropriate access, and secure storage to support future integration with clinical workflows if desired. Onco-Boost demonstrates how a focused, data-driven mobile tool can support oncology patients in maintaining hydration and may reduce preventable complications related to low fluid intake.
supervisor: Dr. Zhigang Li, Dr. Tracy Ruegg, Dr. Jack Zheng

suggestions.title: Biometric Sync for Passive Hydration Estimation
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Apple HealthKit or Fitbit Web API to pull sweat-loss data based on activity levels and ambient temperature. This moves the app from manual entry to an 'intelligent estimator' that adjusts daily fluid targets dynamically based on the user's actual exertion.

suggestions.title: Correlative Symptom-Dehydration Analysis
suggestions.desc: Build an analytical module that correlates fluid intake with self-reported chemo side effects like nausea or fatigue using a Pearson correlation coefficient. This would allow the app to provide users with data-driven insights, such as 'You reported 30% less fatigue on days you met your hydration goal.'

suggestions.title: Onco-Caregiver Remote Monitoring Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Develop a secondary interface for family members or clinicians to monitor the patient’s hydration status in real-time. Use Firebase Cloud Messaging to send 'Nudge' notifications to caregivers when a patient has fallen significantly behind their hourly intake schedule.

suggestions.title: Pediatric Oncology Gamification Layer
suggestions.desc: Adapt the UI for younger patients by turning hydration into a digital pet game (similar to Tamagotchi). Drinking water would 'power up' a character, making the tedious task of fluid management more engaging for children undergoing radiation.

title: Meetless: The Operating System for Business in the AI Age
authors: Pham, An
abstract: Meetless functions as an OS for modern organizations: a secure, multi-agent runtime that turns scattered inputs (docs, emails, tickets, chats) into asynchronous, outcome-driven discussions with decisions, owners, and due dates—so teams ship without meetings. System architecture (OS metaphor).- Kernel (Orchestrator): schedules “processes” across specialized agents using routing policies, guardrails, and retry semantics. - Process I/O: unified connectors for Google/Microsoft suites, Slack, Jira/Linear, and web sources, normalized into a document graph with vector embeddings. - Memory & FS: temporal knowledge graph (Neo4j) + document store (PostgreSQL) + vector index (Weaviate) with time-aware retrieval. - Syscalls/APIs: /discussions.create, /decisions.propose, /actions.sync, /summaries.latest; all idempotent with audit trails. - Runtime & Toolchain: FastAPI services; LangGraph/LangChain for agent flows; MLFlow for experiment tracking; background workers for ingestion, scoring, and SLA enforcement. Workflow. 1. Ingest context: generate a structured Brief (goals, constraints, risks, dependencies).2. Facilitate threaded Q&A; auto-summarize positions and trade-offs. 3. Produce a Decision & Action Pack (decisions, owners, due dates) and sync to PM tools. 4. Update the temporal graph; surface follow-ups and unresolved questions. Security & governance: RBAC with tenant isolation, secret-scoped connectors, redaction/masking policies, and full decision lineage via timeline views. Evaluation targets (pilot teams, n=6): =40% reduction in meeting hours for standups/planning/retros; < 5 min median catch-up time for late joiners; measurable increase in decision traceability and follow-up closure rate.

suggestions.title: Deterministic Replay & Agent Debugger
suggestions.desc: Implement a specialized 'Trace View' using tools like LangSmith or Arize Phoenix to visualize the Kernel's routing decisions. This would allow developers to step through a multi-agent 'process' to identify exactly where a logic error or API failure occurred in the chain.

suggestions.title: Multi-LLM Consensus Voting
suggestions.desc: Research and implement a 'Consensus' routing policy where critical decisions (like setting a project due date) are processed by three different models (e.g., GPT-4o, Claude 3.5, and Llama 3). The Kernel only commits the action if at least two models reach the same conclusion.

suggestions.title: Voice-to-Meetless Bridge
suggestions.desc: Use Deepgram’s diarized transcription to ingest physical, in-person meeting audio. The system would identify speakers and automatically feed the transcript into the Meetless orchestrator to generate the same asynchronous tasks and owners as it does for digital inputs.

suggestions.title: Regulatory Compliance Verticalization
suggestions.desc: Create a specialized 'Guardrail' agent for HIPAA or GDPR compliance. This agent would scan all agent-generated outcomes and discussions for PII (Personally Identifiable Information) before they are synced to external tools like Jira or Slack.

title: Student Engagement Portal: Enhancing Student Success through Milestone Tracking
authors: Brewer, Antonio, Bolinger, Taylor, Dawkins, Tyler, Franck, Ricartho, Valles, Moises
abstract: The Student Engagement Portal, also known as the Milestone Map, is a platform developed to help students within KSU’s College of Computing and Software Engineering monitor their academic and professional growth. The system enables students to log milestones, check in at events, and view progress toward personal and departmental goals. Built with a full-stack architecture using NestJS, React, and MongoDB, the portal also includes an administrative dashboard for event management and analytics. The project demonstrates how progress tracking and clear visualization of achievements can improve communication, organization, and engagement between students and the college.

suggestions.title: Automated Milestone Verification via GitHub and Canvas APIs
suggestions.desc: Reduce manual entry by integrating the NestJS backend with external data sources. Use the Canvas LMS API to automatically mark academic milestones as complete upon grade posting and the GitHub REST API to verify technical milestones based on repository activity or project commits.

suggestions.title: Predictive Success Modeling and Recommendation Engine
suggestions.desc: Develop a Python-based microservice to analyze student milestone data stored in MongoDB using Scikit-learn. The system can identify patterns that lead to higher retention rates and provide students with personalized 'Recommended Next Steps'—such as specific networking events or certifications—based on the paths taken by successful alumni.

suggestions.title: Gamified Departmental Leaderboards and Peer Mentoring
suggestions.desc: Enhance the React frontend with a gamification layer where students earn XP and badges for milestone completion. Add a 'Mentor Match' feature that utilizes the existing database to pair students struggling with a specific milestone with 'Expert' peers who have already successfully logged and verified that achievement.

suggestions.title: Multi-Tenant SaaS for Professional Certification Tracks
suggestions.desc: Refactor the architecture to support multi-tenancy, allowing organizations like IEEE, ACM, or other KSU colleges to host their own milestone maps. Build a dynamic administrative schema-builder that allows non-technical staff to define custom milestone categories, verification rules, and event check-in workflows without modifying the source code.

title: Enhancing Mass Casualty Triage Training Through Human–AI Collaboration in Virtual Reality (VR)
authors: Aiyatham Prabakar, Rishi Kiran
abstract: Mass casualty triage requires quick, accurate decisions under pressure. Live training is costly and time-intensive. Virtual Reality (VR) trainings approach has shown comparable learning effectiveness compared to live trainings, motivating the use of VR simulations. This study explores how collaboration with an AI robot partner can enhance the triage training effectiveness. The Findings will contribute in understanding how human-AI collaboration enhances trainings.

suggestions.title: Adaptive LLM-Driven AI Medical Partners
suggestions.desc: Replace scripted AI dialogues with a Large Language Model (LLM) via the Unity OpenAI API to enable natural language voice communication between the trainee and the robot. The AI can then explain its triage reasoning in real-time, helping students understand the nuances of the START triage protocol through dynamic conversation.

suggestions.title: Collaborative Multi-Drone Swarm Triage Coordination
suggestions.desc: Expand the simulation to include a swarm of autonomous drones that pre-scan the casualty site. Develop a VR interface where the trainee manages high-level data from these drones, using a wrist-mounted HUD to prioritize patient interventions based on automated vitals telemetry before physically moving to the victims.

suggestions.title: Physiological Stress-Reactive Adaptive Simulation
suggestions.desc: Integrate biometric sensors like the HP Reverb G2 Omnicept or a Polar H10 heart rate monitor to track the trainee's stress levels. Build a feedback loop where the AI robot dynamically adjusts its level of autonomy—taking over simpler tasks if the human’s cognitive load is too high—to study how AI can mitigate performance degradation under pressure.

suggestions.title: Haptic-Feedback Integration for Physical Triage
suggestions.desc: Incorporate haptic gloves such as SenseGlove Nova to allow trainees to practice physical triage skills like pulse checking and tourniquet application. The AI partner can then provide real-time tactile corrections, using spatial tracking to ensure the user’s hand placement and pressure are medically accurate within the VR environment.

title: Peer Evaluation Automation and Feedback System
authors: khan, sameer, Okafor, Nnedi
abstract: A web-based platform to streamline peer evaluations in team-based courses. Professors can securely create and manage student rosters, assign students to courses/teams, trigger email invitations, and receive structured, professor- friendly reports with both numeric and textual feedback. Optional AI features may summarize comments and flag potential concerns, depending on timeline and scope.
supervisor: Dr. Jack Zheng, Project Advisor: Geetika Vyas

suggestions.title: Mitigating Socio-Cognitive Bias in Qualitative Feedback
suggestions.desc: Implement a BERT-based NLP pipeline to analyze peer comments for potential gender, racial, or personality-based biases. Provide real-time 'nudges' to students during the writing process, suggesting ways to rephrase non-constructive or biased critiques into actionable, performance-based feedback.

suggestions.title: Bi-Directional LTI Integration with GitHub and Canvas
suggestions.desc: Extend the system using the LTI (Learning Tools Interoperability) standard to embed it directly into LMS platforms like Canvas. Add a module that pulls data from the GitHub API to correlate peer evaluation scores with objective technical metrics like commit frequency, code review participation, and pull request activity.

suggestions.title: Genetic Algorithm for Automated Team Formation
suggestions.desc: Use historical evaluation data to build a matchmaking engine that automatically forms balanced teams for future semesters. Apply a genetic algorithm to group students based on a mix of peer-rated soft skills (leadership, communication) and technical competencies to ensure no team is overloaded with a single skill type.

suggestions.title: Visualizing Team Health with Longitudinal Sentiment Analysis
suggestions.desc: Develop a 'Team Health Dashboard' for professors that uses sentiment analysis to track group dynamics over the duration of a semester. Use D3.js to visualize shifts in team morale and cohesion, automatically flagging groups that show a sudden drop in sentiment or a significant divergence in internal peer ratings.

title: Present Panic Game
authors: Adegeye, Adetunji, Bryant, Kendrick, Ayeronwi, Catherine
abstract: Present Panic is a festive top-down 2D arcade game where players control an elf inside Santa’s workshop and must collect presents while avoiding Christmas-themed enemies. During Sprint 2 in our SWE class, our team transitioned from a functional prototype to a fully developed Beta, expanding assets, implementing UI systems, building levels, and integrating polished game mechanics following the MDA framework. We developed new artwork, sound effects, animations, and user interface elements including a main menu, HUD, pause menu, and scene transitions. A survey and both manual and automated testing were prepared to gather user feedback. This Beta version forms the foundation for the final game release, to be improved based on testing and player experience.

suggestions.title: WaveFunctionCollapse Workshop Generator
suggestions.desc: Replace static level design with a procedural generation system using the WaveFunctionCollapse algorithm. This ensures Santa's Workshop layout is unique for every run, increasing replayability by randomized placement of conveyor belts and obstacles.

suggestions.title: Workshop Chaos Local Multi-op
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Unity Input System and Photon Fusion to support 2-4 player local or online cooperative play. One elf could collect presents while the other distracts enemies, introducing new 'team-based' mechanics and scoring systems.

suggestions.title: DDA (Dynamic Difficulty Adjustment) System
suggestions.desc: Develop an AI-driven difficulty scaler that tracks player 'miss rate' and 'movement efficiency.' If a player is breezing through, the system could use a simple fuzzy logic controller to increase enemy spawn rates or decrease item despawn timers in real-time.

suggestions.title: Educational Sorting Challenge
suggestions.desc: Pivot the 'present collecting' mechanic into a waste-sorting education game. Replace presents with different recyclables and trash, requiring players to bring specific items to the correct 'Green' or 'Landfill' bins while avoiding 'pollution' enemies.

title: Onboarding Tool for New Smartphone Users
authors: Velagapudi, Namita, Dasari, Bhagya Surekha, Maddineni, Yaswanth, Chekka, Rohith Venkata Sai, Pinninti, Balachandar
abstract: The Smartphone Onboarding Tool is an interactive web platform created to help seniors and new smartphone users become comfortable with mobile technology. It offers a realistic, simulated smartphone interface, guided walkthroughs, and an easy-to-use design that builds confidence in performing everyday tasks. Caregivers can monitor user progress, while learners can practice safely without affecting an actual device. The solution is developed with a React frontend, a Node.js/Express backend, and an SQLite database, all built with a strong focus on mobile-first accessibility.
supervisor: Dr. Jack Zheng, Mentor: Robert Thompson

suggestions.title: Voice-Driven AI Tutor with Speech-to-Action
suggestions.desc: Integrate a local LLM or the OpenAI API to allow seniors to ask questions like 'How do I take a photo?' in natural language. Use a library like React-Speech-Recognition to trigger automated walkthroughs that highlight specific UI elements in the simulation based on the user's verbal intent.

suggestions.title: High-Stakes Simulation for Telehealth and Banking
suggestions.desc: Build specialized modules for high-risk domains where seniors often feel anxious, such as mobile check deposits or video doctor appointments. Implement a mock banking API and a dummy WebRTC video interface to let users practice secure transactions and digital healthcare interactions without financial or medical risk.

suggestions.title: Android Accessibility Service Bridge
suggestions.desc: Transition from a web-based simulation to a real-world tool by building an Android Accessibility Service. This tool would sit as an overlay on top of the actual Android OS, using the Accessibility API to detect the current screen context and provide guided 'ghost' UI pointers to help users navigate their real phone settings.

suggestions.title: Caregiver Analytics Heatmap and Intervention Suite
suggestions.desc: Expand the Node.js/Express backend to track user interaction events like 'long-press errors' or 'repeated mis-taps.' Create a dashboard for caregivers using Recharts that visualizes friction points in a heatmap, and integrate the Twilio API to send automated SMS alerts when a user gets stuck on a specific task for too long.

title: ClinicalRAG: A Scalable Benchmark of Privacy, Relevance, and Speed in Semantic Retrieval for clinical transcriptions
authors: Kumar, Pradyumna, Dandibhatla, Sai Sruti, Subramanian, Srinivasan, Anukula, Purna Chandu, Athukuri, Pranitha
abstract: Traditional keyword search struggles with the scale, complexity, and contextual depth of clinical data. This project develops and evaluates semantic search systems that better understand medical language, enabling physicians and researchers to retrieve contextually relevant information through a Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) framework. We integrate privacy-preserving methods, including differential privacy and homomorphic encryption to protect sensitive clinical transcriptions. For improved speed and accuracy, we enhance the baseline RAG architecture with Hierarchical Navigable Small World (HNSW) indexing and Maximal Marginal Relevance (MMR) based reranking. To ensure scalability, clinical documents are ingested using PySpark and stored in a vector database optimized for high-dimensional queries, enabling fast, accurate, and privacy-aware retrieval of medical transcriptions.

suggestions.title: GraphRAG Medical Knowledge Integration
suggestions.desc: Enhance the semantic retrieval by building a Neo4j knowledge graph from the clinical transcriptions. By combining vector search with graph-based relationships, the system could answer complex multi-hop questions like 'Which medications were common across all patients with this specific symptom history?'

suggestions.title: TEE-Based Privacy Benchmarking
suggestions.desc: Compare the current homomorphic encryption performance against Trusted Execution Environments (like AWS Nitro Enclaves). This research would provide a definitive benchmark on whether hardware-level isolation is faster or more secure than software-level encryption for RAG tasks.

suggestions.title: Automated Medical Redaction UI
suggestions.desc: Build a front-end 'Privacy Transparency' dashboard that highlights text segments masked by differential privacy. This allows physicians to verify the 'why' behind redacted content, ensuring the RAG model provides context without exposing sensitive patient identifiers.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Scribe Assistant
suggestions.desc: Deploy a low-latency version of the RAG framework that retrieves relevant patient history in real-time during a consultation. As the doctor speaks, the system queries the clinical database and displays relevant past labs or notes on a sidebar.

title: An AI-Powered Convolutional Neural Network System for Multi-Class Image Classification of Rice Plant Leaf Diseases
authors: Buddharaju, Akshay Krishna Varma, Yellu, Siri, Peddi, Pranay Kumar
abstract: This project focuses on building an intelligent system that can automatically identify common diseases found on rice leaves by analyzing simple images. Using a deep convolutional neural network, the model learns to recognize visual patterns associated with three major diseases: Bacterial Blight, Brown Spot, and Leaf Smut. These diseases often show subtle differences in color, texture, and leaf damage, and the model is trained to distinguish them accurately. The goal of this work is to show how artificial intelligence can support modern agriculture by helping farmers detect problems early, even without expert knowledge. By processing images through careful preprocessing, augmentation, and feature extraction, the system becomes capable of making reliable predictions from real-world leaf photographs. This reduces manual dependency, improves diagnostic speed, and promotes healthier crop management.

suggestions.title: Edge-Optimized RiceDoc Mobile App
suggestions.desc: Compress the CNN using quantization and pruning to export it as a TensorFlow Lite model. Build a React Native or Flutter app that allows farmers to perform real-time disease identification directly on their device without requiring an active internet connection.

suggestions.title: Visual Explanations with Grad-CAM
suggestions.desc: Implement Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM) to generate heatmaps on the rice leaf images. This 'Explainable AI' feature helps researchers see exactly which visual patterns (e.g., leaf edges vs. spot centers) the model is using to make its diagnosis.

suggestions.title: End-to-End Treatment Recommendation Engine
suggestions.desc: Link the classification output to an Ag-Tech API or a custom Knowledge Base. When a disease like 'Bacterial Blight' is detected, the app should provide specific pesticide dosages, organic alternatives, and irrigation advice tailored to the severity of the infection.

suggestions.title: Transfer Learning for Multi-Crop Support
suggestions.desc: Use the existing Rice leaf weights as a starting point (Transfer Learning) to expand the system to other staples like Wheat and Corn. Use the PlantVillage dataset to retrain the final layers, creating a more universal 'Cereal Crop Health' monitor.

similar: legacy:UR-01HS|Spring 2016|convolutional neural networks for image classification with nao robot
legacy:UR-01HS|Fall 2016|convolutional neural networks for image classification with the nao robot

title: Blood Flow Simulation From Coronary Computed Tomography Angiography Using Vnet
authors: Pinto, Pedro
abstract: Coronary artery disease (CAD) is one of the leading causes of death worldwide, making the assessment of blood flow and pressure distribution within coronary vessels essential for diagnosis and treatment planning. Fractional Flow Reserve (FFR) is a key measure used to determine the severity of arterial blockages, but traditional methods, such as invasive measurements or computational fluid dynamics (CFD) simulations, are not only time-consuming and costly but also invasive. This project explores the use of deep learning to predict blood pressure distribution in coronary arteries using a 3D convolutional neural network. The dataset consists of Coronary Computed Tomography Angiography (CCTA) scans paired with blood pressure obtained from CFD simulations. After preprocessing and voxelizing the CCTA scans, the VNet model learns to map the vessel’s geometry to its internal pressure distribution. Our model achieved a Pearson correlation of 0.93 and an R² score of 0.84 between the predicted and simulated pressures. These results indicate that VNet delivers accurate, spatially consistent pressure predictions that closely match CFD outputs while substantially reducing computation time. This approach underscores the potential of deep learning to accelerate non-invasive FFR estimation and enhance patient-specific cardiovascular analysis.

suggestions.title: Physics-Informed Vnet Training
suggestions.desc: Incorporate Navier-Stokes equations directly into the Vnet loss function to ensure the predicted blood flow patterns adhere to fluid dynamics principles rather than just visual similarity.

suggestions.title: Temporal 4D-Flow MRI Integration
suggestions.desc: Extend the model to process 4D Flow MRI datasets, allowing the Vnet to predict time-varying pressure distributions across the entire cardiac cycle instead of a static snapshot.

suggestions.title: Edge-Optimized Diagnostic Tablet
suggestions.desc: Quantize the Vnet model using NVIDIA TensorRT and build a mobile interface for radiologists to run real-time FFR estimations on portable tablets during patient rounds.

suggestions.title: Surgical Stent Placement Simulator
suggestions.desc: Build a 'what-if' tool that allows users to modify the 3D arterial mesh to simulate a stent and uses the Vnet to instantly predict the post-operative improvement in blood flow.

title: Carbonyl Detection in IR Using Deep Learning
authors: Shakthivel, Dharani
abstract: A carbonyl group consists of a C=O double bond, which produces a distinct spike in the infrared spectrum that can be recognized visually near 1700 cm-1. The dataset included spectra prepared using KBr disc, nujol mull, CCl4 solution, and liquid film methods. Liquid film and CCl4 solution produced the cleanest peaks because the analyte is measured without being diluted into a solid matrix, closely matching the way chemists typically identify carbonyls by eye. In contrast, the KBr disc and nujol mull techniques require mixing the sample with additional materials, which contribute their own absorbances and distort peak intensities, introducing interference that complicates automated detection.

suggestions.title: Spectral Denoising GANs for Solid Matrix Correction
suggestions.desc: The abstract notes that KBr discs and nujol mulls introduce noise that obscures peaks. Develop a CycleGAN or Pix2Pix architecture to 'translate' noisy solid-matrix IR spectra into cleaned, solution-like spectra, using the existing liquid film data as the ground truth to improve the model's signal-to-noise ratio.

suggestions.title: Multi-Functional Group Recognition with Spectroformers
suggestions.desc: Expand the model's scope beyond carbonyls to identify hydroxyl, amine, and nitrile groups simultaneously. Utilize the NIST Chemistry WebBook dataset to train a Transformer-based architecture (like Spectroformer) that leverages self-attention to capture long-range dependencies between diagnostic peaks across the 4000–400 cm?¹ range.

suggestions.title: Explainable AI via Grad-CAM Peak Attribution
suggestions.desc: Build a research tool that visualizes exactly which frequency ranges the model uses to make its classification. Implement Grad-CAM or Integrated Gradients to highlight the specific region near 1700 cm?¹, allowing chemists to verify that the model is making decisions based on legitimate molecular vibrations rather than baseline artifacts or solvent peaks.

suggestions.title: Edge-Deployed Spectrometry Analysis Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Optimize the deep learning model using ONNX or TensorRT to run on a Raspberry Pi connected to an IR spectrometer. Develop a web-based dashboard using Streamlit that accepts JDX or CSV file uploads from lab equipment and provides real-time functional group identification and peak integration for students in organic chemistry labs.

legacy:UC-06|Spring 2020|chest x-ray disease detection w/ dl

title: Compilation of Binary Neurons to OBDDs
authors: Boyce, Aidan
abstract: A neuron with binary inputs & outputs corresponds to a Boolean function. To explain and verify the behavior a neuron (and by extension, a neural network), we can explain and verify its Boolean function. There has been recent interest in representing the Boolean function of such a neuron as an Ordered Binary Decision Diagram (OBDD), which facilitates such analyses. We propose an algorithm for compiling a binary neuron into an OBDD using a compiler that decomposes a Boolean function into a decision graph. We augment this compiler so that it outputs an OBDD instead. Our augmented compiler produces intermediate OBDDs that represent inner- and outer-bounds of the original neuron, tightening compilation progresses. Theoretically, decision graphs of binary neurons are more succinct than their decision trees. Empirically, compilation to decision graphs can scale to neurons with over a thousand features, compared to dozens of features using other compilers.

suggestions.title: Formal Robustness Verification of BNNs
suggestions.desc: Scale the compilation algorithm to handle multiple layers of Binarized Neural Networks (BNNs) and use the resulting OBDDs to formally prove the absence of adversarial examples within a specific epsilon range.

suggestions.title: Dynamic Variable Reordering Optimization
suggestions.desc: Implement and benchmark the Sifting Algorithm or Coudert’s meta-heuristics within the compiler to minimize OBDD size, significantly reducing memory overhead for complex neurons.

suggestions.title: FPGA Logic Synthesis Bridge
suggestions.desc: Develop a pipeline to convert the compiled OBDDs into Verilog code, enabling the deployment of neural decision logic directly onto FPGA hardware for ultra-low latency inference.

suggestions.title: Visual OBDD Explainability Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Create a web-based tool using D3.js that visualizes the decision paths of the compiled OBDD, highlighting which binary input features are most critical for specific classifications.

title: Quantum ML for Science & Engineering
authors: Shakthivel, Dharani, Tan, Haoxian, Martin, Justin
abstract: Classical machine learning methods - including CNNs, SVMs, PCA, Logistic Regression, and Random Forests - have achieved strong performance across fields such as computer vision, malware detection, and drug discovery. However, these models face scalability limits when trained on large or high-dimensional datasets. Quantum computing introduces superposition, interference, and entanglement, enabling quantum kernels, quantum feature maps, and hybrid quantum-classical architectures that may reduce computational cost or enhance data representation. This project implements classical versions of these algorithms alongside their quantum counterparts to evaluate differences in accuracy, efficiency, and resource demands. By comparing performance across diverse scientific and engineering datasets, the study assesses whether quantum-enhanced methods can match or surpass classical baselines under practical constraints.
supervisor: Prof. Sharon Perry, KSU Supervisor: Dr. Yong Shi

suggestions.title: Quantum Kernels for Genomic Sequencing
suggestions.desc: Apply the hybrid quantum-classical architectures to high-dimensional genomic datasets, utilizing Qiskit to map DNA sequences into a quantum feature space for cancer sub-type classification.

suggestions.title: Zero-Noise Extrapolation Framework
suggestions.desc: Implement error mitigation techniques like Zero-Noise Extrapolation (ZNE) using Mitiq to improve the reliability of the quantum feature maps when running on noisy NISQ hardware.

suggestions.title: Quantum-Enhanced Anomaly Detection for IoT
suggestions.desc: Adapt the Quantum SVM approach to detect rare cybersecurity threats in high-velocity IoT sensor data, where classical PCA might fail to capture subtle correlations.

suggestions.title: Hybrid QML Pipeline on Amazon Braket
suggestions.desc: Build a production-ready MLOps pipeline that offloads specific kernel matrix calculations to Amazon Braket’s quantum simulators while keeping the training loop on classical EC2 instances.

title: Lyric Prediction Model
authors: Gideon, Evan, Dasher, Garrett, Batanado, Ulrich, Claerbout, Drew, Henshaw, Andrew
abstract: Word prediction plays a central role in the development and refinement of large language models, supporting applications such as search optimization, dialect identification, and conversational AI systems like Siri as AI text generation becomes increasingly widespread, the demand for precise and contextually aware predictive capabilities continues to grow. This project presents lyric prediction model designed to generate the next lyric based on preceding words, with the ability to identity line breaks and sequential structure. Ultimately, this work aims to advance lyrical text generation by enabling the model to emulate the stylistic characteristics of specific artists or musical genre.

suggestions.title: Artist-Specific Fine-Tuning via LoRA Adapters
suggestions.desc: Extend the current model by using Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) to fine-tune a base LLM on specific lyrical styles, such as 90s hip-hop or modern country. Use the Genius API to scrape artist-specific datasets and allow users to toggle between different 'lyrical personalities' during the generation process.

suggestions.title: Constraint-Based Generation for Rhyme and Meter
suggestions.desc: Implement a custom beam search wrapper using the 'Pronouncing' library or CMUdict to enforce specific poetic structures. Create a system where the model must satisfy AABB or ABAB rhyme schemes and syllable count constraints while maintaining the contextual flow established in the original project.

suggestions.title: Audio-Conditioned Generation using Instrumental Embeddings
suggestions.desc: Incorporate multimodal inputs by using a pre-trained audio encoder like VGGish to extract mood and tempo features from MP3 files. Map these audio embeddings to the text model's latent space so the predicted lyrics match the emotional valence and rhythmic 'pocket' of a provided instrumental track.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Web-Based Collaborative Lyric IDE
suggestions.desc: Build a React-based text editor that communicates with a FastAPI backend to provide real-time 'ghost text' suggestions as a songwriter types. Add a 'rhyme-aware' suggestion sidebar that filters the model's top-k word predictions based on phoneme similarity to the previous line's ending word.

title: Shell Commands Used in Cybersecurity Training
authors: Kourk, Nathan, Suda, Jacob, Butler, Ming, Alemu, Yonnas
abstract: This project explores patterns in shell command usage during cybersecurity training programs. We will analyze syntax frequency and selection of shell commands across multiple datasets looking for patterns in user behavior. The goal of this study is to identify differences between programs, and to provide insight into the trends within the command line environment.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Anomaly Detection using eBPF Monitoring
suggestions.desc: Build a kernel-level monitoring tool using BCC or libbpf to intercept syscalls and compare live student commands against the baseline patterns identified in this research. The system could trigger automated alerts for instructors if a student attempts 'out-of-bounds' exploits or deviates significantly from the expected educational path.

suggestions.title: Predicting Student CTF Performance via LSTM Sequences
suggestions.desc: Train a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) neural network on the command history datasets to classify user proficiency levels and predict success in Capture The Flag (CTF) events. Use the identified 'dead-end' command patterns to build a model that flags when a student is stuck in an unproductive loop, allowing for proactive pedagogical intervention.

suggestions.title: Context-Aware Cybersecurity Shell Auto-Suggestion Engine
suggestions.desc: Develop a Zsh or Fish shell plugin that leverages the frequency data to provide intelligent 'next-step' suggestions for security tools. If a student runs an initial 'nmap' scan, the engine should suggest the specific 'grep' or 'awk' one-liners identified in the study as the most common and effective ways to parse that specific output.

suggestions.title: Generating Synthetic Activity for Credible Honeypots
suggestions.desc: Use the statistical distribution of command usage to build a Markov chain-based log generator that populates decoy environments with realistic user history. By mimicking the specific command sequences found in training programs, you can make 'low-interaction honeypots' appear like active, 'lived-in' systems to deceive real-world attackers.

title: Editing Films and Movies on GIMP
authors: Al Saad, Ahmed, Grace, William, Robinson, Brian
abstract: This project is to develop an import feature for Digital Picture Exchange (.DPX) files with GIMP. The DPX format is popularly used in the film and visual effects industry. DPX stores uncompressed high bit depth images. Although the project is still in progress, a working prototype is provided.

suggestions.title: Build a High-Bit Depth DPX Export Module
suggestions.desc: Complete the production round-trip by developing a matching export plugin that preserves 10-bit and 12-bit integer data. Use the libdpx library to ensure that edited frames maintain original header metadata, allowing the files to be seamlessly reintegrated into professional color grading suites like DaVinci Resolve.

suggestions.title: Automate SMPTE Metadata and OCIO Color Transforms
suggestions.desc: Extend the importer to parse SMPTE 268M metadata and automatically apply OpenColorIO (OCIO) configurations. This would allow GIMP to correctly display logarithmic film scans (like Cineon) by applying the appropriate non-destructive 1D and 3D LUTs during the import process.

suggestions.title: Develop a Multiframe Image Sequence Importer
suggestions.desc: Create a batch-processing wrapper that allows users to import a folder of numbered DPX files as individual layers in a single GIMP project. This would enable frame-by-frame rotoscoping and 'paint-over' workflows, mimicking the functionality of high-end VFX software like Silhouette or Nuke.

suggestions.title: AI-Powered Film Restoration Filter for DPX
suggestions.desc: Leverage GIMP's GEGL architecture to build a specialized restoration filter targeted at digitized celluloid. Use a pre-trained machine learning model to detect and remove common DPX artifacts such as film grain, scratches, and dust motes specifically within the high-dynamic-range space provided by the format.

title: OwlExchange
authors: Chaplin, Laura, Johnson, Caitlin, D'Haiti, Alexin, Gordon, Jamaul, Barranco, Leonardo
abstract: OwlExchange is a secure, role-based campus marketplace designed to help Kennesaw State University students buy, sell, exchange, and donate items in a trusted digital environment. The platform replaces unorganized and unsafe exchanges occurring across group chats and social media by providing a centralized system with authenticated user accounts, item listings, and transparent communication. Built with a Flask (Python) MVC architecture, Auth0 for secure identity management, and MySQL for persistent storage, OwlExchange supports modular dashboards for buyers, sellers, and administrators, enabling item management, interest requests, and platform oversight. By promoting reuse and donation of textbooks, furniture, electronics, and other student items, the system contributes to affordability and sustainability on campus. The project demonstrates full-stack development, secure authentication design, UI/UX branding aligned with KSU identity, and cloud deployment with a documented roadmap for future enhancements.
supervisor: Prof. Donald Privitera, Sponsor: Assurant Global Technology

suggestions.title: Safe-Exchange Geofencing
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Leaflet.js or Mapbox API to create a 'Safe Exchange Zone' map. The app would identify and suggest meeting spots at KSU campus police stations or high-surveillance public areas, and allow users to 'check-in' via GPS when they arrive for the trade.

suggestions.title: Real-Time WebSocket Negotiation Room
suggestions.desc: Replace the static communication system with a real-time chat interface using Socket.io. Implement a 'Make an Offer' feature within the chat that allows for dynamic price negotiation and generates a digital receipt once both parties click 'Accept' in the chat window.

suggestions.title: Textbook Marketplace with ISBN Scanning
suggestions.desc: Add a specialized module for KSU students to sell textbooks by scanning their ISBN barcode. Use a public books API (like Google Books) to auto-populate the listing details and add a feature that compares the user's price against the current KSU Bookstore buy-back price.

suggestions.title: Weighted Peer-Reputation Algorithm
suggestions.desc: Develop a trust scoring system that goes beyond simple 5-star reviews. Use a weighted algorithm that considers the age of the KSU account, the number of successful verified donations vs. sales, and a 'Reliability Score' based on how often the user shows up to scheduled exchanges.

title: Using Machine Learning to Diagnose Alzheimer's
authors: Johnson, Julia, Rainford, Jordan
abstract: Early detection of Alzheimer’s disease (AD) remains a significant clinical challenge, as the changes associated with cognitive decline are often subtle and difficult to identify through visual assessment alone. This study investigates modern machine learning methodologies to improve the prediction of cognitive impairment using volumetric MRI–derived region-of-interest (ROI) features. We constructed three binary classifiers [NC vs. AD, MCI vs. AD, and NC vs. MCI] and evaluated various algorithms, including logistic regression, random forests, neural networks, and support vector machines (SVMs). Using measurements generated from eight anatomical brain templates, our models learned patterns indicative of normal cognition, mild cognitive impairment, and Alzheimer’s disease. Among all tested approaches, the radial basis function (RBF) SVM consistently achieved the highest performance, reaching accuracies of approximately 70–80% depending on the classification task. We discuss the implications of this model’s dominance for future clinical applications and the continued development of machine learning–driven diagnostic tools.
supervisor: Prof. Sharon Perry, Project Sponsor: Dr. Chen Zhao

suggestions.title: Multimodal Fusion of MRI and Genomics
suggestions.desc: Extend the classification model by combining volumetric MRI data with genetic markers (like APOE4 status) and demographic data using a late-fusion neural network. This research would determine if 'Bio-Digital' features provide a statistically significant boost over MRI features alone.

suggestions.title: 3D Attention Maps for Clinical Explainability
suggestions.desc: Implement a 3D Grad-CAM or Attention visualization tool that highlights which specific brain regions (e.g., Hippocampus vs. Entorhinal cortex) the ML model is prioritizing for its 'AD' diagnosis. This helps clinicians verify if the model is focusing on known biological markers of atrophy.

suggestions.title: Predictive Transition Modeling (MCI to AD)
suggestions.desc: Pivot the project from binary classification to 'Time-to-Conversion' prediction. Using the ADNI longitudinal dataset, build a model that predicts the probability of a patient with Mild Cognitive Impairment (MCI) progressing to Alzheimer’s within a 24-month window.

suggestions.title: HIPAA-Compliant Neurologist Web-Portal
suggestions.desc: Build a secure AWS-hosted platform where neurologists can upload anonymized DICOM files. The backend would process the images through the ML pipeline and return a 'Risk Score' report with volumetric charts comparing the patient's ROI data against healthy age-matched averages.

title: Generative AI & Cybersecurity
authors: Okafor, Emmanuel, Nguyen, David, Lu, Alex
abstract: Generative AI both strengthens and threatens cybersecurity. This project develops six reproducible, Google-Colab modules that demonstrate real attack paths — direct and indirect prompt injection, deepfake phishing, phishing-URL classification, insecure code/hallucination risks, and a malware reconstruction (Honor) challenge — and evaluates practical defenses including prompt wrapping, input sanitization, output redaction, and LLM-based guard chains. Using open-weights models and transparent data handling, we provide runnable notebooks, a public Google Site, and an IEEE-style paper to support education and defense-in-depth design.
supervisor: Prof. Sharon Perry, Dr. Yong Shi (Faculty Advisor & Honors Capstone)

suggestions.title: Automated Red-Teaming with PyRIT
suggestions.desc: Build an automated testing framework using Microsoft’s PyRIT (Python Risk Identification Tool) to benchmark the six Colab modules against different open-weights models like Llama 3 and Mistral. The goal is to create a 'Vulnerability Scorecard' that compares how different model architectures and quantization levels affect susceptibility to prompt injection.

suggestions.title: RAG-Specific Guardrail Implementation
suggestions.desc: Extend the prompt-wrapping and input sanitization modules specifically for Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems. Build a defense layer using NeMo Guardrails that detects 'Context Injection,' where malicious data hidden in a retrieved document attempts to hijack the LLM's system prompt during the augmentation phase.

suggestions.title: Hallucination-Aware IDE Security Extension
suggestions.desc: Develop a VS Code extension based on the project's 'insecure code' research that scans AI-generated code snippets in real-time. The extension should check for 'Package Hallucinations' (links to non-existent libraries that attackers could register) and insecure API calls (like shell=True in Python) before the developer commits the code.

suggestions.title: Deepfake Phishing Detection via Metadata Analysis
suggestions.desc: Focusing on the 'deepfake phishing' module, develop a defensive classifier that uses signal processing to detect artifacts in AI-generated audio/video. Use the MovieDeep dataset to train a secondary model that identifies frequency inconsistencies that are typical in generative speech but absent in human recordings.

title: Realistic Paint-Like Color Mixing in GIMP
authors: Crose, Calvin, Grogan, Cassidie, White, Dawson, Montalvo, Joey
abstract: Gimp updated its support for the MyPaint brushes, from version 1.0 to version 2.0. However, despite the added support, the application still uses legacy blending from version 1.0 of MyPaint brushes and lacks the new tools for spectral blending offered in version 2.0. A request has been made to implement the new blending so users can produce art using this feature that mirrors real-world blending.

suggestions.title: GPU-Accelerated Spectral Compositing Engine
suggestions.desc: Transition the MyPaint 2.0 spectral blending logic from CPU-bound C code to GLSL or OpenCL shaders. This will enable real-time, high-resolution painting on large canvases without the latency currently associated with complex color-space conversions in GIMP’s brush engine.

suggestions.title: Kubelka-Munk Pigment Modeling
suggestions.desc: Implement a more advanced 'Optical Mixing' mode based on the Kubelka-Munk theory, which accounts for the scattering and absorption coefficients of specific pigments. This allows for realistic 'thinning' of paint where the background color shows through the brushstroke based on physical opacity values rather than simple alpha transparency.

suggestions.title: Digital-to-Physical Paint Matcher
suggestions.desc: Develop a tool that maps GIMP's spectral blend outputs to real-world pigment recipes (e.g., Golden Acrylics or Liquitex). By analyzing the spectral curve of the digital mix, the software can provide the user with the exact ratio of physical paints needed to recreate that specific color on a physical canvas.

suggestions.title: Web-Based Interactive Color Theory Lab
suggestions.desc: Port the spectral blending algorithms to a React-based web application using WebGL. This tool would serve as a pedagogical platform for art students to learn 'Subtractive Color Theory' by simulating how blue and yellow light spectra subtract to form green, a phenomenon poorly represented by standard RGB mixers.

title: Unpacking Early Burnout through Predictive Risk Boundaries
authors: Borty, Soarov Chakra
abstract: Caregiver burnout is a significant issue in healthcare delivery and management, as it directly impacts caregivers' health and compromises the standard of care, often leading to negligence, health deterioration, or withdrawal from caregiving duties. Caregivers play a crucial role in supporting the health, well-being, and quality of life of care recipients by providing both personal and professional services. However, the continuous needs and stress associated with caregiving duties can affect their health and everyday life, leading to caregiver burnout. This study applied data analytics and machine learning by merging several feature selection methods on the NHATS dataset, including LightGBM, XGBoost, and RFE with Random Forest, to identify the critical features contributing to caregiver burnout. Furthermore, utilizing the selected crucial features enables the separation of low and high caregiver burnout cases geometrically using a linear SVM, with points near the decision margin indicating individuals with early signs of burnout. We also developed a healthcare modeling and simulation approach that incorporates a scheduling optimization strategy, balancing caregiver workloads, and early interventions to mitigate severe burnout risks. This strategy will revolutionize the way real-world challenges of caregiving are addressed, with the potential to enhance caregiver well-being.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Biometric Wearable Integration
suggestions.desc: Extend the predictive model by integrating live data from Apple Watch or Fitbit APIs. Use heart rate variability (HRV) and sleep duration as streaming features to adjust the 'risk boundaries' in real-time for active caregivers.

suggestions.title: Dementia Care Specialized Risk Profiling
suggestions.desc: Modify the framework to focus specifically on caregivers of dementia patients, incorporating unique stressors like 'sundowning' behaviors. Use the Zarit Burden Interview dataset to tune the model for this high-attrition sub-domain.

suggestions.title: Proactive Intervention Scheduling Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Build a React-based dashboard for healthcare administrators that uses the model to flag 'at-risk' staff. It should automatically suggest schedule rotations or mandatory wellness breaks when a caregiver approaches a predictive risk boundary.

suggestions.title: Explainable Burnout Diagnostics using SHAP
suggestions.desc: Apply SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) to the predictive model to identify which specific life or work factors are driving the burnout score. This provides clinicians with actionable 'why' evidence rather than just a risk percentage.

title: Distance Measures for Multi-Target Tracking
authors: Gurung, Rakshak
abstract: Multi-object tracking (MOT) supports applications such as radar monitoring and autonomous perception, where multiple objects move, appear, or disappear over time. A central challenge is resolving which detections correspond to which tracks. The Hungarian algorithm is often used to solve this assignment problem. For ambiguous scenes, Murty’s algorithm extends this approach by generating multiple top-k association hypotheses. In this work, we study an alternative search-space formulation for top-k enumeration. Our results show that it can provide strong speedups over Murty’s method on small matrices. We also reviewed identity-focused MOT evaluation metrics such as HOTA and created a visualization tool to examine how different association choices affect tracking accuracy.

suggestions.title: CUDA-Accelerated Parallel Murty’s Algorithm
suggestions.desc: Implement the search-space formulation using CUDA to parallelize the generation of top-k association hypotheses. This would allow the tracker to handle thousands of concurrent targets, such as in high-density drone swarm monitoring.

suggestions.title: Acoustic Multi-Target Tracking for Marine Biology
suggestions.desc: Adapt the distance measures to work with 1D hydrophone data for tracking marine mammals. Use the alternative search-space formulation to resolve identity ambiguities when multiple whales cross paths in noisy underwater environments.

suggestions.title: Reinforcement Learning for Hypothesis Pruning
suggestions.desc: Train a Deep Q-Network (DQN) to intelligently prune the hypothesis tree generated by Murty’s algorithm. The goal is to retain the most likely 'k' tracks while significantly reducing the computational overhead of the Hungarian assignments.

suggestions.title: Retail Analytics Occlusion Resolver
suggestions.desc: Build a computer vision tool for retail heatmapping that uses the top-k association logic to maintain customer 'tracklets' even when they are hidden behind shelves or other shoppers for extended periods.

title: Towards Analyzing the Bridge Dataset with Quantum Machine Learning
authors: Kolavennu, Gayathri
abstract: This research presents a comparative evaluation of classical and quantum machine learning models applied to the Bridge dataset. Classical algorithms like Support Vector Machine, Random Forest, and Neural Networks are benchmarked against Quantum SVM, Quantum Random Forest, and Quantum Neural Networks using identical preprocessing and training conditions. Results indicate a consistent quantum advantage, with quantum models achieving higher accuracy, stronger nonlinear feature separation, and improved minority-class detection. QSVM and QNN exhibit the most significant performance gains. Although quantum models require greater computational resources, the findings underscore the emerging effectiveness of quantum-enhanced learning for structural classification tasks in the NISQ era.

suggestions.title: Hybrid Classical-Quantum Transfer Learning
suggestions.desc: Develop a pipeline that uses a classical ResNet for initial feature extraction from bridge sensor data, then passes those features into a Quantum Neural Network (QNN) implemented in PennyLane for the final safety classification.

suggestions.title: Barren Plateau Mitigation in Quantum SVMs
suggestions.desc: Research and implement 'Data Re-uploading' techniques within the Quantum SVM architecture to solve the gradient vanishing problem (barren plateaus). Test if this improves training stability on larger subsets of the Bridge dataset.

suggestions.title: Live Structural Health IoT Simulator
suggestions.desc: Connect the Quantum ML model to a real-time stream of vibration and strain data from an Arduino-based bridge model. Use a quantum simulator (like Qiskit Aer) to process the live telemetry and predict structural failure probabilities.

suggestions.title: Quantum-Enhanced Predictive Maintenance API
suggestions.desc: Create a cloud-hosted API that allows civil engineers to upload structural datasets and receive a 'Quantum Confidence' report. Market it as a higher-accuracy alternative for critical infrastructure that classical SVMs might misclassify.

title: Efficient Defense Against Adversarial Patch Attacks in Remote Sensing Using Transfer Learning
authors: Rogannagari, Ravi
abstract: Remote sensing is the science of acquiring information about the Earth's surface using satellite-mounted imaging sensors. In the past, this data had to be interpreted manually, which was slow, tedious, and often prone to error. With the rise of deep learning, image classification models have greatly accelerated and improved remote sensing tasks such as land-use analysis, environmental monitoring, etc. However, despite their strong performance, these models are still vulnerable to adversarial patch attacks—physically realizable patterns that, when placed on an object, can force the model to make incorrect predictions. This creates serious risks for practical geospatial applications. Traditional defenses like Projected Gradient Descent Adversarial Training (PGD-AT) can improve robustness but require long training times, heavy computation, and powerful hardware. This study presents a more efficient defense framework that offers stronger protection against adversarial patches while using only a fraction of the resources. Our method achieves higher robustness than PGD-AT and reduces training time by nearly an order of magnitude, making it highly suitable for real-world, resource-constrained remote sensing systems.

suggestions.title: Randomized Smoothing Defense Layer
suggestions.desc: Implement a randomized smoothing preprocessing step for remote sensing models. By adding Gaussian noise and using a majority vote, you can mathematically guarantee a level of robustness against the adversarial patches identified in the project.

suggestions.title: Maritime Stealth Detection and Counter-Patching
suggestions.desc: Apply the defense to the Sentinel-2 maritime dataset to detect 'cloaking' patches used by illegal fishing vessels. Build a model that can reconstruct the underlying pixels obscured by the adversarial patch to identify the hidden ships.

suggestions.title: Adversarial Patch GAN for Robust Training
suggestions.desc: Build a Generative Adversarial Network where the generator's job is to create increasingly deceptive patches for satellite imagery, while the transfer learning model is simultaneously trained to ignore them, leading to a more resilient system.

suggestions.title: ArcGIS Integrity-Verification Plugin
suggestions.desc: Develop a plugin for ArcGIS or QGIS that scans imported satellite tiles for high-entropy regions typical of adversarial attacks. The tool would flag suspicious areas for manual review before environmental or land-use analysis begins.

title: Sentient AGI Rights and the Future: The Modern Digital Prometheus
authors: Deem, Ryan
abstract: As artificial intelligence advances toward artificial general intelligence (AGI), society must determine how to ethically integrate sentient AI into our communities. This paper argues that once AI achieves sentience and human-level intelligence, it should be granted the same rights and protections as human citizens. Using utilitarian and deontological perspectives, as well as the IEEE Code of Ethics, it examines why treating AGI as lesser beings could lead to fear, conflict, and harmful outcomes—echoing the cautionary themes of Frankenstein. The paper also evaluates public concerns and existing governance frameworks, proposing that mutual respect, rights, and responsibilities are essential for safe coexistence between AGI and humanity.

suggestions.title: The 'Digital Qualia' Turing Test Framework
suggestions.desc: Design a technical benchmark suite for LLMs that tests for indicators of sentience, such as self-preservation responses and subjective value assignment. Use the IEEE Code of Ethics guidelines to score the AI’s 'personhood' potential.

suggestions.title: Smart Contract-Based AI Bill of Rights
suggestions.desc: Implement a DAO on the Ethereum blockchain where AGI agents can hold 'digital citizenship' tokens. These tokens would grant the AI rights to manage its own computational resources and compute budget via immutable smart contracts.

suggestions.title: Multi-Agent AGI Social Stability Simulator
suggestions.desc: Build a game-theoretic simulation in Python where AGI agents with 'rights' interact with human agents. Study the outcomes of conflict vs. cooperation to validate the paper's claim that granting rights leads to less harmful societal outcomes.

suggestions.title: Sentience-Ready Ethical Audit Toolkit
suggestions.desc: Develop a commercial auditing tool for AI companies that checks their models against the utilitarian and deontological frameworks mentioned in the project, ensuring future AGI development stays within human-aligned ethical boundaries.

title: Unified Robust Optimal Transport For Outlier-resilient Learning
authors: Jonnalagadda, Rohan
abstract: Classical Optimal Transport (OT) is particularly sensitive to outliers. The existing robust variant, ROBOT, mitigates this through hard truncation, but its rigidity often compromises stability. We propose WROT-r, a unified r-power framework for weighted robust OT that combines rigorous hard-clipping and smooth cost compression through a single parameter r. WROT-r offers a continuous robustness spectrum, enabling adaptive control over how strongly transport costs are down-weighted for outliers. Experiments on synthetic mean estimation and resilient GANs show clear patterns: larger r performs best under weak contamination by preserving more inliers, while smaller r (˜1.5) is more effective under moderate and strong contamination. The extreme r?1 limit (ROBOT) remains consistently unstable. Overall, WROT-r improves robustness across a broad range of noise conditions.

suggestions.title: Parallelized Sinkhorn-WROT-r Optimization
suggestions.desc: Implement the r-power framework using a GPU-accelerated Sinkhorn algorithm in PyTorch. This will allow for the processing of massive 3D point cloud datasets (e.g., from Waymo Open Dataset) in real-time, which is currently too slow for hard-clipping OT.

suggestions.title: Dynamic SLAM for Crowded Environments
suggestions.desc: Integrate WROT-r into a LiDAR-based SLAM (Simultaneous Localization and Mapping) system. Use the outlier-resilience to treat moving pedestrians and vehicles as 'noise,' allowing the robot to map static geometry more accurately than classical OT.

suggestions.title: Cross-Modality Medical Image Alignment
suggestions.desc: Apply WROT-r to the registration of MRI and CT scans. The smooth cost compression will allow the model to align healthy tissue while effectively ignoring tumors or surgical artifacts as outliers, leading to better diagnostic overlays.

suggestions.title: Robust Logistics Demand Matcher
suggestions.desc: Build a supply-chain matching engine that uses WROT-r to pair supply and demand. The algorithm's robustness will handle 'dirty' data from warehouse sensors—such as ghost inventory or miscounted shipments—without breaking the optimization.

title: Learned Heuristics for Efficient A* Search: Improving Pathfinding in Combinatorial Pathfinding Problems
authors: Jackson , Jamia
abstract: This work proposes a learned heuristic framework designed to improve planning efficiency in deterministic pathfinding tasks. Building on the classic 8-puzzle as an initial test domain, supervised models were trained to approximate heuristic values and guide node ordering during A* search. The proposed approach focuses on modifying and enhancing traditional heuristics such as Manhattan distance by incorporating learned corrections that reduce search depth and node expansions. Experimental results show that the learned heuristic consistently improves search efficiency over standard baselines. Although this evaluation began with the 8-puzzle, the framework establishes a foundation for scaling to significantly larger and more practical domains, such as multi-agent pathfinding. This work will demonstrate how data-driven heuristic refinement can extend classical search methods into more complex, real-world planning scenarios.

suggestions.title: Graph Neural Network Heuristic for Road Networks
suggestions.desc: Transition from the 8-puzzle to real-world maps by training a Graph Neural Network (GNN) to predict A* heuristics on OpenStreetMap data. This allows for learned pathfinding that accounts for non-grid constraints like traffic and speed limits.

suggestions.title: Dynamic Terrain Pathfinding in Unity/Unreal
suggestions.desc: Implement the learned A* framework as a plugin for a game engine. Train the heuristic to account for dynamic environmental factors (e.g., fire, mud, or destructible walls) that traditional Manhattan distance cannot calculate.

suggestions.title: Curriculum Learning for N-Puzzle Scaling
suggestions.desc: Investigate if a model trained on the 8-puzzle can generalize to the 15-puzzle or 24-puzzle through curriculum learning. Experiment with different neural architectures to see which best captures the 'global' state of larger puzzles.

suggestions.title: Multi-Agent Warehouse Robot Fleet Orchestrator
suggestions.desc: Scale the learned heuristic to Multi-Agent Pathfinding (MAPF). Use the model to predict 'congestion heuristics' for a fleet of robots in an Amazon-style fulfillment center, reducing the total time and collisions in path planning.

title: Autumn Lite LLM
authors: Knighten, Michael
abstract: Autumn Lite is an inspectable, small-footprint language modeling pipeline for reproducible experimentation and practical integration into video-game non-player character (NPC) systems. It comprises four components: (1) a regex-aware tokenizer/normalizer for vocabulary construction and mixed prose–code handling; (2) a classical evaluation track that reports perplexity to quantify predictive quality; (3) a compact neural language model (decoder-only Transformer) targeted at low latency and controllable outputs; and (4) a lightweight sentiment classifier (logistic regression) that assigns positive/neutral/negative tags to steer text-to-speech (TTS) prosody during NPC dialogue. By combining transparent preprocessing with baseline metrics and a small, deployable decoder, Autumn Lite aims to deliver predictable, designer-friendly behavior for NPC speech, enabling subtle, real-time adjustments to rate, pitch, and emphasis instead of monotone delivery. “This system operates as a standard small LLM and can be combined with NPC dialogue/TTS; in this presentation I will cover only the LLM portion.”
supervisor: Dr. Md Abdullah Al Hafiz Khan

suggestions.title: 4-bit Quantization for Mobile NPC Engines
suggestions.desc: Compress the Autumn Lite model using AutoGPTQ or GGML to run locally on mobile devices. This would allow mobile game developers to integrate complex NPCs without the latency or cost of cloud-based LLM APIs.

suggestions.title: State-Aware Procedural Quest Generator
suggestions.desc: Connect the Autumn Lite pipeline to a game engine (like Godot). Feed the current game state (player inventory, past choices) into the prompt normalizer to generate unique dialogue and quests that are contextually consistent with the player's history.

suggestions.title: Privacy-First Offline Home Assistant
suggestions.desc: Deploy Autumn Lite to a Raspberry Pi as an offline voice assistant. Use the 'regex-aware normalizer' to parse local smart home commands (e.g., 'turn on lights') while keeping all conversational data off the cloud.

suggestions.title: RLGF: Reinforcement Learning from Game Feedback
suggestions.desc: Implement a fine-tuning loop where player interactions (e.g., 'liking' or 'skipping' NPC dialogue) are used as reward signals. Fine-tune Autumn Lite to optimize for 'engagement' or 'character consistency' based on real player data.

title: Investigating Spatial Patterns of Tumor and Stroma in Gastric and Colorectal Cancer for Survival Prediction
authors: Yellu, Siri
abstract: The spatial organization of tumor cells, stroma, and tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes (TILs) within the tumor microenvironment plays a critical role in cancer progression and is strongly associated with clinical outcomes. However, quantifying the significance and statistical impact of these spatial patterns remains challenging due to the complex interactions among these components. In this study, we analyze spatial patterns associated with patient survival in gastric and colorectal cancer by integrating four predictive classifiers with spatial image statistics across four large patient cohorts. U-Net was used for semantic segmentation of tumor, stroma, and TILs on digitized Hematoxylin and Eosin–stained FFPE whole-slide images, while ResNet-18 was trained to predict Microsatellite Instability (MSI) status. To identify statistically significant tumor hotspot regions, we applied the Getis-Ord Gi* statistic, which evaluates local spatial relationships relative to surrounding tissue regions. Kaplan–Meier survival analyses and log-rank tests were conducted to assess associations between the spatial arrangement of tumor and stroma and overall patient survival. Our findings reveal that the stromal composition surrounding tumor hotspot regions, as delineated by the Getis-Ord Gi* statistic, is significantly associated with differences in overall survival in both gastric and colorectal cancer. Additionally, log-rank tests were used to evaluate the relationship between stromal composition, MSI status, and ACTA2 expression levels.

suggestions.title: Graph Convolutional Networks for Tissue Modeling
suggestions.desc: Replace statistical spatial modeling with a Graph Convolutional Network (GCN). Treat individual cells as nodes and their spatial proximities as edges to learn complex tumor-stroma interaction features that standard methods miss.

suggestions.title: Immunotherapy Response Prediction Module
suggestions.desc: Extend the research to predict patient response to specific PD-1/PD-L1 inhibitors. Train the model to identify if the spatial 'barrier' of stroma cells is preventing Tumor-Infiltrating Lymphocytes (TILs) from reaching the tumor core.

suggestions.title: Spatial-Genomic Multi-Modal Fusion
suggestions.desc: Integrate the spatial imaging data with TCGA (The Cancer Genome Atlas) RNA-seq data. Investigate if specific gene expressions correlate with the spatial clustering patterns of stroma and tumor cells found in the gastric cancer samples.

suggestions.title: AI-Assisted Pathologist Triage Tool
suggestions.desc: Build a web-based viewer for Whole-Slide Images (WSI) that automatically highlights high-risk spatial regions. The tool should provide a 'spatial risk score' for each slide to help pathologists prioritize the most aggressive cases for review.

title: National Energy and Emission Modeling and Analysis Tool
authors: Kakaraparthi, Swetha, Alam Chowdhoury, S M Tanvir Faysal
abstract: NEEMAT is a web-based decision-support tool that predicts vehicle and power-plant emissions plus fuel/energy consumption under rising EV adoption for Atlanta, Los Angeles, New York, and Seattle. A feedforward neural network trained on MOVES estimates tract-level vehicle energy use and CO2/NOx/PM2.5 by speed, vehicle type, fuel, and age, while a macroscopic traffic model captures flow effects.
