Grid-side CO2/CH4/N2O from EV charging are forecast with a Meta-Prophet model trained on Cambium. Users can explore 24-hour profiles and five-year outlooks, compare scenarios, and export results. Findings show that despite substantial EV uptake, mixed fleets and grid responses can raise total emissions, underscoring the need for integrated transportation–power planning.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Traffic Feed Integration
suggestions.desc: Update the feedforward neural network to ingest live traffic data from Google Maps or HERE APIs. This would transform NEEMAT from a predictive tool into a real-time monitor of current city-wide EV energy consumption and emissions.

suggestions.title: Municipal Fleet Electrification Simulator
suggestions.desc: Create a specialized module for city planners to simulate the impact of replacing diesel bus fleets with EVs. Use the MOVES-trained network to calculate the specific reduction in PM2.5 and NOx levels along high-traffic transit corridors.

suggestions.title: Carbon Credit Marketplace for Commercial Fleets
suggestions.desc: Develop a platform that uses NEEMAT’s emission forecasting to help logistics companies quantify their 'avoided emissions.' This data could be used to generate verified carbon credits for companies transitioning to electric delivery vans.

suggestions.title: Hyper-Local Grid Stress Heatmapping
suggestions.desc: Use the Meta-Prophet predictions in conjunction with local utility maps to identify which neighborhood-level transformers are at risk of overloading. Build a heatmap showing where 'EV-clustering' will exceed current grid capacity by 2030.

similar: legacy:GR-14|Fall 2016|performance analysis on deep machine learning on cpus and gpus

title: Playlist Synch
authors: Poore, Joshua; Smith, Nikita D; Pallotti, Ben S
abstract: Our project is a web application that allows users to sign in and transfer music playlists from one music streaming service to another. Currently, it is only functional with Apple and Spotify music but there are plans to implement more in the future.

suggestions.title: Universal Plugin Architecture for Music APIs
suggestions.desc: Refactor the backend using the Adapter design pattern to create a standardized interface for music services. This would allow the community to easily add support for Tidal, YouTube Music, and Deezer by simply implementing a 'Provider' class for each service's API.

suggestions.title: Cross-Platform Collaborative Syncing
suggestions.desc: Implement a 'Virtual Playlist' system where two users on different platforms (one Spotify, one Apple Music) can contribute to a single shared list. The app would use WebHooks to detect changes on one platform and automatically mirror the update to the other in real-time.

suggestions.title: Acoustic Fingerprinting for Missing Tracks
suggestions.desc: Integrate the AcoustID or Librosa library to handle tracks that fail metadata matching (title/artist). If a song isn't found by name, the app can analyze the audio characteristics to find the exact match on the target service, even if the metadata is formatted differently.

suggestions.title: Streaming Library Availability Analytics
suggestions.desc: Build a data analysis dashboard that tracks and visualizes 'transfer success rates' across platforms. This would provide insights into which genres or record labels are most frequently missing from certain services, offering valuable data on licensing gaps in the industry.

title: Wildling Rumble
authors: Haines, Logan; Whisenhunt, Ryan A
abstract: When it comes to the combined field of digital board games, there needs to be a balance of what is necessary for the physical space and what is necessary for the digital space. The game must be justified as a combination of the two elements and not be able to shift completely to either side. In this study, we are exploring a new modality that uses Near Field Communication cards for transferring game data to the application. Our new method eases the requirement on players to keep track of the game state, as that is handled separately from the program.

suggestions.title: Persistent Character Growth via NFC Rewriting
suggestions.desc: Implement NTAG215 write capabilities to store player stats and experience directly on the physical cards. This enables a 'carry-over' mechanic where a physical card effectively becomes a 'memory card' for a character, allowing it to level up across different game sessions.

suggestions.title: Mobile Companion App with CoreNFC
suggestions.desc: Develop an iOS/Android companion app using CoreNFC or Android NFC API that allows players to scan their Wildling Rumble cards on their personal phones. This removes the need for a dedicated hardware reader and allows for 'hidden' actions, like checking secret stats privately.

suggestions.title: Augmented Reality (AR) Unit Visualization
suggestions.desc: Integrate Vuforia or ARKit to recognize the physical NFC cards through a camera view. When a player scans a card, a 3D animated model of the character would appear on top of the physical card, bridging the gap between digital combat and the physical tabletop.

suggestions.title: Multi-Device Synchronous Tabletop State
suggestions.desc: Use WebSockets (Socket.io) to synchronize the game state across multiple tablets or laptops around a table. Scanning a card on one player’s device would instantly update the 'world view' on a central screen, creating a shared digital-physical play space.

title: CRM Proposal
authors: Maiocchi Castro, Luis A; Misko, Crystal; Damico, Steven; Groves, Ethan; Outlaw, Maryah
abstract: This project addresses concerns raised by a Sponsor regarding inefficiencies in managing CCSE capstone projects. Key pain points include organization, project management, and communication among stakeholders. The proposed solution involves implementing Customer Relationship Management (CRM) software. Upon gathering requirements from the Sponsor, including contact information capture, workflow management, document control, and customization capabilities, we evaluated several CRM platforms. A total of thirty-two CRMs were reviewed and Vtiger, OroCRM, and SuiteCRM were selected for testing. SuiteCRM was chosen for its comprehensive features and user-friendliness. The second step of the project involved testing SuiteCRM functionalities on a dedicated server, leading to its selection for deployment preparation. Final steps include documentation development, training material creation, and incorporating user feedback for optimal system configuration.
supervisor: Professor: Prof. Donald Privitera Project Sponsor & Coordinator: Alla Kemelmahker

suggestions.title: Automated Capstone Lifecycle Middleware
suggestions.desc: Develop a middleware service using Node.js or Python that connects the proposed CRM with GitHub and Jira APIs to automatically provision project repositories and kanban boards once a sponsor proposal is approved.

suggestions.title: Student-Sponsor Talent Matching Portal
suggestions.desc: Build a front-end interface that pulls student skills and interests from the CRM to create a 'matching' algorithm, allowing sponsors to see which student teams best align with their project requirements.

suggestions.title: Predictive Project Risk Analysis
suggestions.desc: Implement a sentiment analysis module using Python's NLTK to analyze communication logs and project status updates within the CRM, flagging projects that exhibit signs of stakeholder misalignment.

suggestions.title: Sponsor Retention & Engagement Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Create a data visualization suite using PowerBI or Tableau that tracks longitudinal sponsor engagement data, helping the college identify which industries provide the most successful long-term partnerships.

title: Big Munchin'
authors: Reynolds, Tabitha; Amerson, Steven M; Buck, David J; Gatipon, Jacques P; McElrath, Aaliyah; Watson, Nick S
abstract: Big Munchin' is a video game designed to help teach portioning skills and promote healthy eating habits. The overall metabolic health of individuals in America is comparatively low to other countries. Metabolic health is bolstered by several factors including exercise and a proper diet consisting of essential vitamins, proteins, and other important biomolecules.The increased cost of healthy nutrient-rich foods and a lack of proper nutritional education have hindered the overall metabolic health of modern Americans. Engaging individuals in learning more about nutritional health can be a difficult task made easier by an engaging experience that stays in the user’s minds. Big Munchin can be a vehicle for increasing Americans' overall awareness of nutritional health while also ensuring they better understand what foods can be beneficial for achieving those goals.

suggestions.title: Real-World Nutrition API Integration
suggestions.desc: Replace static food data with a live connection to the Edamam or Nutritionix API, allowing players to scan real-world product barcodes to see how those specific items would impact their in-game metabolic health.

suggestions.title: Cooperative Metabolic Boss Battles
suggestions.desc: Introduce a multiplayer mode where players must collaboratively 'balance a plate' to defeat bosses representing metabolic disorders, requiring team-based synchronization of proteins, carbs, and vitamins.

suggestions.title: Wearable-Sync Activity Buffs
suggestions.desc: Use the Google Fit or Apple HealthKit API to sync the player's real-world physical activity, granting in-game metabolic bonuses or stamina boosts based on their actual daily step counts.

suggestions.title: Educational Progress Tracking for Schools
suggestions.desc: Build a web-based dashboard for teachers that aggregates anonymized student data to show which nutritional concepts (e.g., portioning, vitamin types) the class is struggling to master.

title: KSU CCSE CRM
authors: Curran, Alex; Mitchell, DJ; Miller, Christian
abstract: Our task was to select, implement and customize a CRM solution for the College of Computing and Software Engineering to more effectively manage communication with industry partners and manage projects such as capstones, and C-Day. Our team selected SuiteCRM as our recommendation and have implemented an instance on a virtual machine provided by UITS. We have customized branding including using a KSU logo provided by the Office of Strategic Communications and Marketing, as well as customizations based on the official KSU color pallete. The process we used to select our CRM recommendation involved gathering requirements from our sponsor and comparing the features of open source CRMs, leading to our top selections. We created demo installations of these selections and followed example work flows to determine user friendliness and reliability. To verify our requirements we filled in a comparison chart and then during testing compared the process of completing tasks. We determined that SuiteCRM was the best choice for our project and moved forward with the installation.

suggestions.title: Live C-Day Judging Module
suggestions.desc: Develop a custom SuiteCRM module designed for C-Day judging that allows judges to log scores via mobile, automatically associating points with specific project records and calculating real-time rankings.

suggestions.title: LinkedIn Lead Gen Connector
suggestions.desc: Integrate the LinkedIn Sales Navigator API to automatically identify and import potential new industry partners into SuiteCRM based on their proximity to KSU and their involvement in relevant tech sectors.

suggestions.title: Student Resume & Portfolio Indexer
suggestions.desc: Implement an OCR-based document parsing system using Tesseract to index PDF resumes submitted to the CRM, making student skills searchable for sponsors looking for specific technical stacks.

suggestions.title: FERPA-Compliant Data Masking Layer
suggestions.desc: Build a security middleware that intercepts SuiteCRM queries to ensure all student data views are dynamically masked or redacted based on the user's role and FERPA authorization status.

title: Asynchronous
authors: Whisenhunt, Ryan A; Lee, Peyton T; Wardlaw, Kenneth; Haaf, Benjamin T; Good, Carter L; Ridley, Cory A
abstract: Cultural assimilation is the topic on the mind of the protagonist of our game, Asynchronous. Maxwell, a diplomat from a real-time world, must adapt in a foreign land where the people live turn-based lives. We explore this topic through the lens of traditional JRPG gameplay where the player must decide when to adapt to this new culture and when to act on their own accord. By representing this idea ludically, we hope to better convey the mindset and emotional state of being an outsider to the player.

suggestions.title: Hybrid Combat System Engine
suggestions.desc: Refine the gameplay by building a seamless transition engine that switches from real-time movement to turn-based combat based on the player's proximity to 'Native' NPCs.

suggestions.title: Dynamic Procedural Dialogue Trees
suggestions.desc: Use a sentiment analysis library like NLTK to adjust NPC reactions and quest availability based on the player's cumulative history of cultural adaptation or resistance.

suggestions.title: Adaptive Soundtrack and Audio Cues
suggestions.desc: Implement an FMOD-driven audio system where the music's BPM, time signature, and instrumentation shift dynamically as the player chooses between real-time and turn-based actions.

suggestions.title: Language Acquisition Progression System
suggestions.desc: Develop a modular dialogue system where players 'unlock' translations of the foreign language by performing turn-based tasks, simulating the cognitive load of real-world language learning.

title: Aletheianomous AI: The Chat Bot Providing the Most Accurate Knowledge Information
authors: Chavarro, David E; Tran, Aimi; Byrd, Ethan B; Fincher, Matthew J
abstract: For this project, our group aimed to create an intelligent chat bot that was accessible through the web client interface. Aletheianomous, our chat bot, was designed to provide accurate information ethically, aligned with human values. When applicable, the AI would offer the user citations to support its responses. For the back-end, a virtual machine (VM) server in AWS with access to the Graphics Processing Unit (GPU) would run three types of models: Sentence Separation Model, Search Query Extractor Model, and the Response Model. The front-end server using Microsoft Azure generates the web page for the user, exchanges chat data with the Microsoft SQL server, and communicates with the back-end server via REST APIs to request the chat bot to respond to user input. By using this architecture, the overall quality of our product exceeded our standards.

suggestions.title: Evidence-Grounded Hallucination Detection
suggestions.desc: Implement a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipeline that cross-references AI responses against a trusted corpus like PubMed or arXiv to assign a 'Veracity Score' to every generated statement.

suggestions.title: Quantized Edge-Device Deployment
suggestions.desc: Optimize the heavy GPU models using 4-bit quantization (GGUF format) and Llama.cpp to allow the chatbot to run locally on consumer-grade hardware without requiring expensive AWS GPU instances.

suggestions.title: Value-Aligned Content Moderation Layer
suggestions.desc: Create a secondary transformer-based classification model that acts as a 'moral gatekeeper' to detect and filter prompts that attempt to bypass the AI's ethical guidelines or human-value alignment.

suggestions.title: Enterprise Knowledge-Base API
suggestions.desc: Transform the chatbot into a SaaS-ready API that allows corporate clients to upload private documentation and deploy a version of Aletheianomous trained exclusively on their internal data.

title: Birding With Buddy
authors: Sawadogo, Lazare V; Adeji, Ikhelowa E; Graham, Blake; Alpine, Zach; Sorrells, Troy C
abstract: Birding with Buddy is an educational and entertaining immersive virtual 3D low-poly birdwatching to be experienced at the Carter Lake Nature Center to enable kids to embark on a quest to learn more about birds. Buddy the Beaver guides the user through different terrain types to identify diverse bird species with sounds. Integrate a bird identification system where players click on the binocular icon to switch to a binocular view. In this view, players can choose to Identify (multiple-choice) the correct bird, Hear the Call Again, or Consult a Field Guide. Featuring flippable pages with images and notable markings of different bird species. Allow players to consult the guide for additional information.

suggestions.title: Procedural Habitat Ecosystems
suggestions.desc: Implement a procedural generation system using Unity's Noise functions to create endless variations of terrain and vegetation, ensuring the Carter Lake environment feels new during every play session.

suggestions.title: AR Field Guide Companion App
suggestions.desc: Develop a mobile AR companion that uses the bird identification logic and TensorFlow Lite to identify real-world birds in the wild using the smartphone camera while outdoors at the Nature Center.

suggestions.title: Bioacoustic Soundscape Integration
suggestions.desc: Integrate audio datasets from the Cornell Lab of Ornithology to create dynamic, time-of-day-dependent soundscapes where birds respond to each other's calls in a biologically accurate manner.

suggestions.title: Global Migratory Expansion Pack
suggestions.desc: Extend the game to include diverse biomes like the Amazon or Arctic, implementing a seasonal migration calendar where the bird species available for identification change based on the real-world date.

title: Difference Detection: Automated Defect Detection System for Modernized Display Units
authors: Aziz, Umer; Rosser, Harrison; Arshad, Urooj; Pegues, Teonta N; Vu, Katherine
abstract: In today's high-stakes military environments, the reliability and accuracy of software systems are paramount. Defects within these systems not only pose significant financial risks but can also endanger lives. To ensure the utmost safety and effectiveness, military systems undergo extensive testing and validation processes. However, the lifespan of these systems is far from indefinite. Environmental changes, advancements in adversarial capabilities, evolving mission requirements, and parts obsolescence necessitate continuous improvement efforts. One critical area of focus is the modernization of display units, which are vital for providing pilots with essential mission information and ensuring their safe return. The 402 Software Engineering Group (SWEG) at Robins AFB seeks an innovative solution to enhance defect detection in modernized display units using computer vision technology. By automating the evaluation process, our objective is to reduce manual intervention, allowing software developers to focus more on design and development tasks.

suggestions.title: Siamese Networks for Zero-Shot Defect Detection
suggestions.desc: Implement Siamese Neural Network architecture to allow the system to detect anomalies in new display units it has never seen before by comparing them to a single 'golden master' image.

suggestions.title: Cross-GPU Shader Artifact Analysis
suggestions.desc: Build a comparison engine that renders the same modernized UI across different GPU architectures (NVIDIA vs. AMD vs. Integrated) to detect hardware-specific rendering defects or frame-drops.

suggestions.title: Continuous Integration Visual Regression Suite
suggestions.desc: Develop a CI/CD plugin that automatically triggers the defect detection system every time a new build of the display software is committed, blocking builds that introduce visual regressions.

suggestions.title: Mobile Field Diagnostic Tool
suggestions.desc: Create an Android/iOS application using OpenCV that allows field technicians to point their phone camera at a physical display unit to identify screen defects, dead pixels, or color inaccuracies instantly.

title: Donation for Dummies
authors: Triplett, Jayvon L; Mancini, Stephen A; Thomason, Mitchell; Niles, Kendrell M
abstract: Donation For Dummies is a VR game designed to help people feel more relaxed and informed when donating blood. It consists of a theater room where a video plays explaining the process as well as what to do before and after donating. For people wanting a distraction, we have an arcade where players can enjoy minesweeper, matching, or solitaire. For those wishing to relax, we have an art gallery where players can virtually walk around and look at various pieces of art. The more relaxed player that do not wish to move around the game world can instead choose to enjoy the scenery from our meditation deck overlooking a valley. Anyone looking to become more informed about the process can make their way to the information center.

suggestions.title: Haptic Desensitization Training
suggestions.desc: Integrate haptic feedback patterns into VR controllers that simulate the sensation of the blood pressure cuff and the precise moment of needle entry to help users desensitize their physical phobias.

suggestions.title: Phlebotomist Simulation Mode
suggestions.desc: Add a 'Technician View' where the user acts as the nurse, learning to identify ideal vein locations and practicing the hygiene protocols required for a safe donation process.

suggestions.title: Bio-Feedback Adaptive Environments
suggestions.desc: Connect the VR experience to a heart rate monitor (e.g., Polar H10) to dynamically change the art gallery's colors and music tempo based on the user's real-time stress and anxiety levels.

suggestions.title: Modular Clinical Waiting-Room Framework
suggestions.desc: Generalize the VR arcade and gallery architecture into a template that can be easily skinned for other medical procedures, such as pediatric vaccinations or chemotherapy sessions.

title: MyFoodScan
authors: Gueye, Ibrahima, Noel, Brianna J, Kuswita, Victoria O, Lisbon, Je'dae
abstract: MyFoodScan is a mobile app that enables users to scan barcodes of various food and drink items to ensure they are in compliance with their specific dietary needs. Individuals utilize this application to make a customized profile based on diet, such as vegan, vegetarian, dairy-free, allergies, etc. MyFoodScan promotes compliance with dietary limitations. The application was developed with React Native, Expo Go, Google Firebase, using the React Native Camera for barcode scanning. The OpenFoodFacts API database is used for product information. The goal of this application is to enhance awareness and safety for various dietary needs and monitor dietary restrictions.

suggestions.title: OCR-Based Ingredient Analysis
suggestions.desc: Extend the scanning engine to include Google ML Kit's Text Recognition (OCR) to parse raw ingredient lists for products not found in the OpenFoodFacts database. This allows the app to perform dietary compliance checks on localized or un-barcoded supermarket items.

suggestions.title: Eco-Score and Sustainability Integration
suggestions.desc: Integrate the OpenFoodFacts Eco-Score API to calculate the carbon footprint and environmental impact of scanned items. Provide users with a 'Green Alternative' recommendation that meets their dietary needs while offering a lower environmental impact.

suggestions.title: Smart Pantry and Expiry Tracker
suggestions.desc: Build a Firebase-backed pantry management system that tracks the expiration dates of scanned items. The app could send push notifications when items are about to expire and suggest recipes using those ingredients that still comply with the user's profile.

suggestions.title: LLM-Powered Dietary Substitution Engine
suggestions.desc: Utilize the GPT-4o API to analyze 'non-compliant' items and suggest specific, available substitutes. If a user scans a dairy-based snack, the AI can suggest a specific vegan alternative that matches the texture and flavor profile based on the ingredient list analysis.

title: Smart Evaluator of Indirect Supplies Vendibility
authors: Periut, Matthew S; Miller, Cohen; Ray, Carter; Steele, Dawniqueca; Hughes, Justin
abstract: The Smart Evaluator is a web-based software solution that analyzes industrial tools and their vending possibilities. It aims to streamline inventory research for sales teams, reducing manual data gathering and vendibility determination. To begin, users simply upload a basic item inventory spreadsheet, and start the program. From there, the program uses web scraping and ChatGPT to gather key data about the various tools including dimensions, weight, and fragility. Each item is then evaluated based on the collected data, and the optimum storage method is calculated. Once these tasks are performed, the results are stored in the system’s database for future reference to reduce computation time should a particular item already exist. The overall output of the system is a completed list of items with its necessary data and vendibility options.

suggestions.title: Multimodal GPT-4 Vision Tool Analysis
suggestions.desc: Enhance the tool evaluator by allowing users to upload photos of unlabeled items; use GPT-4 Vision to identify the tool and estimate physical dimensions even when spreadsheet data is missing.

suggestions.title: Predictive Industrial Demand Forecasting
suggestions.desc: Incorporate a time-series forecasting model (using Prophet or ARIMA) to analyze historical vending data and predict which indirect supplies will need restocking based on seasonal factory cycles.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Vendor Price Comparison Engine
suggestions.desc: Expand the web scraper to crawl major industrial marketplaces like Grainger and McMaster-Carr to provide a real-time 'Cost vs. Vendibility' score for every item in the inventory.

suggestions.title: Repairability & Sustainability Scoring
suggestions.desc: Develop an algorithm that ranks tools based on environmental impact, analyzing factors like energy consumption, expected lifespan, and the availability of replacement parts vs. disposal.

title: Interactive Training Game Suite
authors: Perry, Garrett J; Cauley, Joscelyn; Du, Issabella; Ursu, Sergiu; Kokash, Rahaf
abstract: It is now common knowledge that simple lectures are not the most effective way for the average person to learn and retain knowledge. The Core of Engineers at the Warner Robins Air Logistic Center have tasked us to transform their PowerPoint presentations into interactive training games to improve comprehension, interaction and retention while saving time and logistical resources compared to giving a traditional lecture. We been tasked with creating game modules covering STINFO or what is or is not considered classified information, Records Management, and the No FEAR Act detailing whistleblower rights and protocols. Our team has developed a log in and sign up portal, robust menus and UI/UX, a system to administer and save results of a quiz, and a 3D environment in which the user can explore and complete tasks which will assist in the learning of these three applications. Our project uses the Unity Game engine, Unity WebGL for hosting in browser, and the Playfab database, as well as assets from the Unity Asset store to improve and streamline our development.

suggestions.title: SCORM/xAPI Performance Export
suggestions.desc: Modify the game suite to export detailed user performance data in SCORM or xAPI format, allowing the Warner Robins Air Logistic Center to track trainee progress within their existing LMS.

suggestions.title: No-Code Scenario Branching Editor
suggestions.desc: Build a drag-and-drop editor that allows instructors to convert existing PowerPoint decks into complex branching narrative games without writing code, using a node-based logic system.

suggestions.title: Multiplayer Collaborative STINFO Audit
suggestions.desc: Create a cooperative 'Wargame' mode where multiple trainees must work together in a shared virtual space to classify a sensitive document while dealing with conflicting information sources.

suggestions.title: Bayesian Knowledge Tracing Adaptation
suggestions.desc: Implement a Bayesian Knowledge Tracing (BKT) algorithm that dynamically adjusts the difficulty and types of questions in the game based on the specific STINFO concepts a user is failing to grasp.

title: An Environmentally Conscious Roguelike
authors: Bland, Andrew, Strawser, Brendan, Rousseau, Adele, Adewunmi, Bewaji
abstract: This semester we have been creating an action-adventure video game based on the theme of "Saving the Environment".

suggestions.title: Eco-Systemic Procedural Generation
suggestions.desc: Modify the level generation algorithm to be influenced by an 'Eco-Health' variable. As players perform environmental actions, use Perlin noise and cellular automata to dynamically transform the biome from a polluted wasteland into a lush forest in real-time, affecting enemy spawn rates and resource availability.

suggestions.title: Real-World Carbon Offset API Integration
suggestions.desc: Connect the game's progression system to a real-world environmental API like Patch or Ecologi. High scores or specific in-game achievements (e.g., 'Planting 1000 Trees') could trigger a micro-transaction or a sponsorship-funded donation to plant a real tree, bridging the gap between virtual and physical impact.

suggestions.title: Adaptive AI via Local Weather Data
suggestions.desc: Integrate the OpenWeatherMap API to adjust the roguelike's difficulty based on the player's actual local climate. If it is raining at the player's physical location, the in-game 'water' levels become more dangerous or 'electric' enemies become more powerful, creating a unique, localized gameplay experience.

suggestions.title: Educational Invasive Species Bestiary
suggestions.desc: Develop a modular 'Invasive Species' system where enemy behaviors and weaknesses are modeled after real-world ecological threats (e.g., Kudzu vines, Spotted Lanternflies). Use this data to create an in-game encyclopedia that teaches players biological control methods used by actual environmental scientists.

title: Spacewalkers: Tilt 5
authors: Mossor, Charles D; Hundley, Jacob; Ambahun, Ifran; Whorton, Joshua
abstract: Our project (and presentation as a whole) is to create an immersive and fun casual game experience using the Tilt 5 system. We made a tower defense game that uses the player’s input with the Tilt 5 in order to defend against enemies. By delivering a variety of enemies, levels, and towers in our game, we further enhanced the experience.

suggestions.title: Shared-Space AR Multiplayer
suggestions.desc: Utilize the Tilt 5 SDK's networking features to synchronize game states across two different sets of glasses on the same physical game board. This allows for 'Head-to-Head' tower defense where one player sends waves of enemies while the other builds defenses, all within the same holographic space.

suggestions.title: Phygital Miniature Recognition
suggestions.desc: Implement computer vision or Tilt 5 wand interactions that allow players to place 3D-printed physical miniatures on the board to act as 'Hero' towers. Use the wand's infrared tracking to identify the physical location of the mini and snap the virtual tower stats and effects to that specific physical coordinate.

suggestions.title: Holographic Real-Time Map Editor
suggestions.desc: Create a 'God Mode' level editor that uses the Tilt 5 wand as a 3D brush. Players can physically reach into the game board to pull up mountains, carve paths for enemies, and place spawn points in real-time, leveraging the system's unique spatial 6DOF input for intuitive level design.

suggestions.title: Gaze-Based Tower Management
suggestions.desc: Research and implement an accessibility layer that uses eye-tracking (if hardware-supported) or 'head-aim' selection. This allows players to select towers and trigger abilities through sustained focus rather than physical wand movements, making the AR experience inclusive for players with limited motor control.

title: MineTicket Discord Ticket Management Bot
authors: Rice, Dean; Martinez, Arturo; Shell, Grace; Thao, Solomon; Brown, Palmer
abstract: We made use of the discord.py python library to manage the framework of the bot, since discord relies on a RESTful API for all of its bots. This layer of abstraction allowed us to focus on the important things, such as sanitizing inputs for SQL queries and building out a large, fully customizable feature set so future adjustments are made simple and easy as changing entries within a configuration file. Our goal was to make the bot as easy to update as possible, with the hope that, should requirements change in the future, the sponsor can quickly modify existing code to adapt it to new situations and unforeseen requirements.

suggestions.title: NLP-Powered Support Ticket Triage
suggestions.desc: Integrate a Natural Language Processing library (like Spacy or the OpenAI API) to analyze the content of incoming Discord tickets. The bot can then automatically assign labels (e.g., 'Bug Report', 'Billing', 'Appeals') and route them to specific private staff channels based on the detected intent and urgency.

suggestions.title: Bi-Directional Minecraft Plugin Bridge
suggestions.desc: Develop a Spigot/Paper Minecraft plugin that links with the MineTicket bot. This allows players to type a command like /ticket in-game, which opens a thread on Discord; staff replies on Discord would then appear as 'Staff Messages' in the player's in-game chat window for seamless support.

suggestions.title: Analytics Dashboard with Discord OAuth2
suggestions.desc: Build a web-based React dashboard that connects to the bot's SQL database. Use Discord OAuth2 to allow server admins to log in and view visual charts of 'Average Resolution Time', 'Staff Performance Metrics', and 'Peak Ticket Hours' to optimize their moderation team's schedule.

suggestions.title: End-to-End Encrypted Transcript Storage
suggestions.desc: Enhance user privacy by implementing PGP encryption for the ticket transcripts stored in the SQL database. Create a system where only the user who opened the ticket and authorized staff members with a specific 'Private Key' role can decrypt and view the historical logs, ensuring GDPR compliance.

title: Drinkinator App
authors: Smith, Maximus A; Mose, Toby; Payne, Grayson
abstract: The "Drinkinator" app represents an innovative solution aimed at revolutionizing the beverage industry by providing users with a personalized drink recommendation system. Rooted in a motivation to diversify people's beverage selections and enhance their drinking experiences, the app facilitates exploration of a wide range of mixed drinks, wines, and beers tailored to individual preferences. Leveraging data analysis and user profiling techniques, the app offers tailored suggestions, showcasing a deep understanding of consumer behavior and taste preferences. Utilizing React Native for front-end development and JavaScript for back-end functionality, the app integrates various libraries to ensure seamless user experiences and efficient data retrieval. With its unique approach, the "Drinkinator" app not only enriches user experiences but also holds significant business potential. Its personalized recommendation system can drive user engagement and retention, paving the way for targeted advertising and promotional opportunities within the beverage industry. Moreover, the app's potential to establish valuable partnerships further underscores its intellectual and business merit, positioning it as a leading platform for beverage enthusiasts seeking novel and diverse drinking experiences.

suggestions.title: Home-Bar Inventory Barcode Scanner
suggestions.desc: Implement a feature using the Google ML Kit to scan liquor bottle barcodes, allowing the app to recommend only the drinks the user can currently make with their available ingredients.

suggestions.title: Graph-Database Flavor Profiling
suggestions.desc: Migrate the recommendation engine to Neo4j to map complex flavor relationships (e.g., 'Peaty' vs 'Botanical'), enabling more accurate 'If you like X, you might like Y' suggestions across different beverage categories.

suggestions.title: Geolocated 'Local Craft' Discovery
suggestions.desc: Add a map-based feature where users can pin and rate unique local drinks at specific breweries or bars, creating a crowdsourced database of location-exclusive beverage recommendations.

suggestions.title: AI Responsible Drinking Monitor
suggestions.desc: Develop an AI module that calculates estimated BAC based on drink selection, volume, and user profile data, providing safety warnings and a one-tap 'Call a Ride' button for high-ABV sessions.

title: Trip Logger
authors: Gassner, Reese D; Zhou, Jason; Barker, Justin T
abstract: We developed an Android mobile app using the Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC) approach to enable users to track their travel distance and time via GPS, fostering greater emissions awareness through their driving habits of distance and time taken. Built with the Flutter framework and Dart language, the app features a user-friendly interface created with Flutter widgets that manage both appearance and user interactions. Our streamlined architecture comprises three layers: the presentation layer for UI elements, the application layer containing the core logic, and the data layer, which locally stores trip data in CSV format to ensure quick access and reliability. We integrated the Geolocator package within Flutter for GPS functionality to obtain user coordinates and calculate distances between locations. This streamlined architecture and choice of technologies optimized the app’s efficiency and user-friendliness, allowing us to promote environmentally conscious driving.

suggestions.title: Low-Energy Background Tracking with Geofencing
suggestions.desc: Optimize the Flutter application by implementing the 'flutter_background_service' and native Android WorkManager to allow trip logging without the app being active. Integrate geofencing to automatically start and stop tracking when the user leaves or enters their home/work zones, reducing manual user input.

suggestions.title: Precise Carbon Footprint Integration via EPA MOVES
suggestions.desc: Enhance the emissions awareness feature by integrating the EPA’s MOVES (Motor Vehicle Emission Simulator) API or a similar dataset. Allow users to input their specific vehicle make, model, and year to transform raw GPS distance data into precise CO2, NOx, and PM2.5 output metrics.

suggestions.title: Eco-Friendly Route Recommendation Engine
suggestions.desc: Use the Google Maps Directions API to compare the user's past driving routes with alternative paths that minimize fuel consumption. Create a feature that suggests 'Green Routes' based on traffic patterns and elevation changes specifically optimized for the user's driving style.

suggestions.title: Enterprise Fleet Emissions Auditing Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Expand the mobile app into a multi-user platform where small delivery businesses can track the collective carbon footprint of their fleet. Build a web-based administrative dashboard using React and Firebase to aggregate data from multiple drivers for corporate sustainability reporting.

title: HELPR: Helping Extrapolate Labels for Police Reports using Large Language Models
authors: Walker, Hailey N; Stigall, William A
abstract: Police officers spend many hours a week documenting their findings when reporting to a 911 call. There is so much detail in these reports that they remain an untapped resource for future data analytics by the police department. The reports are currently being analyzed by human experts and categorized into the following categories: “Substance Abuse”, “Mental Health”, “Domestic/Social”, “Nondomestic/Social”, and “Other”. To assist the experts and reduce the amount of time that is spent on reading and analyzing, we are proposing the use of large language models (LLMs) to tag police reports based on their content. Two models, Mistral-7B and TinyLlama, have been trained and fine-tuned to reduce the time needed to complete police report documentation. Both models output both the tag and the reason for the chosen tag, so one of the potential uses is for it to be used to train human analyzers in the future. For the finetuned Mistral-7B model we observed a 84% and 88% agreement with both human annotators and a 96% and 92% agreement with at least one human annotator on sample tagging and sample reasoning respectively.
supervisor: Prof. Sharon Perry, Dr. Hafiz Khan

suggestions.title: Privacy-Preserving On-Premise LLM Fine-tuning
suggestions.desc: Migrate the project from cloud-based APIs to a local deployment using Ollama or vLLM with Llama-3 or Mistral. Fine-tune the model on a synthetic dataset of police reports (to protect PII) to improve classification accuracy for domain-specific terminology like 10-codes.

suggestions.title: Explainable AI (XAI) for Justifiable Police Labeling
suggestions.desc: Implement LIME or SHAP libraries to provide 'evidence highlighting' for every classification the LLM makes. This would show police experts exactly which sentences in the 911 report triggered the 'Mental Health' or 'Substance Abuse' label, increasing trust and auditability.

suggestions.title: Multi-Modal Sentiment and Stress Analysis
suggestions.desc: Extend the tool to analyze the original 911 audio recordings alongside the text reports. Use Whisper for transcription and a secondary model like Wav2Vec2 to detect high-stress vocal patterns, correlating emotional data with the report categories for better resource allocation.

suggestions.title: Predictive Hotspot Mapping based on LLM Labels
suggestions.desc: Feed the output of the HELPR classifications into a geospatial visualization engine like Leaflet or Mapbox. Create a heatmap that shows real-time trends in 'Domestic/Social' calls vs. 'Substance Abuse' calls to help departments deploy specialized social workers to specific neighborhoods.

title: Faculty Course Analysis Report Self Service Interface
authors: Patel, Meet; Harbin, Trevor; Deb, Omit; Mayhew, Edwin
abstract: This project aims to enhance the efficiency of generating and submitting Faculty Course Analysis Reports (FCARs) for the faculty of CCSE in accordance with ABET accreditation requirements. The scope involves the design and implementation of a locally hosted web-based application. This application will streamline the process, allowing FCARs to be requested and generated in real time. Development will be conducted on a Linux-based platform, and security measures will be integrated based on the NetID system.

suggestions.title: Canvas LMS Data Auto-Population
suggestions.desc: Utilize the Canvas API to automatically pull student performance data, grades, and learning outcome assessments directly into the FCAR form. This eliminates the manual data entry bottleneck for faculty and ensures 100% data integrity from the source.

suggestions.title: Cross-Semester Learning Outcome Trend Analysis
suggestions.desc: Build an analytics engine that aggregates data from multiple FCARs over several years. Use Chart.js to visualize how specific ABET Student Outcomes (SOs) are trending, allowing the department head to identify if a curriculum change in 2022 actually improved student performance in 2024.

suggestions.title: NLP-Based Qualitative Sentiment Summarizer
suggestions.desc: Apply Natural Language Processing to the 'Faculty Reflections' and 'Proposed Actions' sections of the reports. The system could generate a summary report of common challenges (e.g., '50% of faculty noticed students struggled with Calculus III concepts') to inform department-wide changes.

suggestions.title: Dockerized Multi-Tenant Accreditation SaaS
suggestions.desc: Refactor the local Linux application into a containerized Docker architecture. This allows the tool to be offered as a 'Lab-in-a-Box' for other university departments beyond CCSE, supporting custom accreditation standards beyond just ABET.

title: Interactive Training Games - Robins Air Force Base
authors: Tenney, Sean J; Nguyen, VT; Ford, Ian; Farmer, Mason; Hannah, Aaron
abstract: Our project involves converting three PowerPoint training presentations on STINFO, No Fears Act, and Records Management into engaging web-based games. Commissioned by Robins Air Force Base, our team utilizes Unity WebGL for game development and React/Firebase for website hosting. The goal is to provide Air Force personnel with interactive training modules accessible from their desks, enhancing learning retention and engagement. By gamifying the content, we aim to make learning enjoyable while ensuring critical information retention. This interdisciplinary project merges game development and web technologies to modernize training methods and improve educational outcomes for military personnel.
supervisor: Dr. Yan Huang, Alla Kemelmakher, Brian Woods

suggestions.title: SCORM and xAPI Integration for Military LMS
suggestions.desc: Convert the Unity WebGL games into SCORM-compliant packages using a wrapper library. This allows the games to report detailed performance data—such as quiz scores and time-on-task—directly to standard military Learning Management Systems (LMS) like Blackboard or Canvas.

suggestions.title: Collaborative Multiplayer 'Mission-Based' Training
suggestions.desc: Use Mirror or Photon Unity Networking (PUN) to transform the single-player training into a collaborative environment. Air Force personnel could work in squads to solve compliance puzzles related to STINFO, simulating a real office environment where teammates must verify each other's security protocols.

suggestions.title: Procedural Scenario Generation for Infinite Replayability
suggestions.desc: Move beyond static PowerPoint content by building a 'Scenario Engine' in Unity. This engine would randomly generate 'No Fears Act' or 'Records Management' dilemmas using a pool of variables, ensuring that users cannot simply memorize the answers and must apply the underlying principles.

suggestions.title: Physiological Engagement Tracking via WebCam
suggestions.desc: Integrate a library like OpenCV or MediaPipe to track user gaze and facial expressions during gameplay. Research how 'gamified' elements directly affect focus levels compared to the original PowerPoint slides, providing Robins AFB with data-driven proof of learning efficacy.

title: Indy 7 - Nutrition App
authors: Hammond, Silas E; Mai, Tho; Sarzen, Christopher P; Ehme, Michael
abstract: This research presents a personalized, agentic AI-powered system for multiple-choice question (MCQ) generation tailored to college-level tutoring in machine learning and software engineering domains. The primary objective is to enhance adaptive learning through reliable, context-aware quiz generation using long-context large language models (LLMs) and modular agent workflows. Our methodology is based on an eight-stage agentic architecture that separates tasks into two main phases: vector indexing and personalized quiz generation. In the indexing phase, academic PDFs are parsed, chunked with LangChain’s RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter, embedded via Google's text-embedding-005, and indexed using FAISS. A verification agent ensures topic alignment and integrity of the vector database. Upon receiving a user query, a retriever agent performs vector search, followed by a selector agent that filters high-quality chunks. A processor agent curates the final prompt, and a response agent generates the MCQ using Gemini 1.5 Pro. The evaluator agent assesses generated questions against ground truth using metrics like ExactMatch, Faithfulness, and BERTScore. Experimental results over 150 MCQs show Gemini’s accuracy improves from 78.00% (raw) to 93.33% when enhanced with context vectors, a 100k-token cache, and a 1M-token long-context window—achieving a +15.33% overall gain. Gemini also excels in Non-Hallucination (0.9150), Certainty (0.8883), and Answer Correctness (0.9260), indicating safe and reliable generation. When supplemented with context vectors and a training cache, these results highlight Gemini’s effectiveness as a reliable and context-sensitive model for personalized, agentic quiz generation in educational settings, offering strong potential for scalable and adaptive AI tutoring systems.

suggestions.title: RAG-Enhanced Curriculum Extraction from PDFs
suggestions.desc: Extend the eight-stage agentic architecture by adding a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipeline using LangChain. Allow users to upload specific university syllabi or textbooks (PDFs) so the MCQs generated are 100% aligned with a specific professor's course material rather than general knowledge.

suggestions.title: Automated Difficulty Calibration via IRT
suggestions.desc: Incorporate Item Response Theory (IRT) to dynamically adjust question difficulty. As students answer questions, the system should track performance and use an LLM agent to rewrite questions to be easier or harder, ensuring a personalized 'Flow State' for every learner.

suggestions.title: Live Agentic Socratic Tutoring Interface
suggestions.desc: Instead of just generating MCQs, build a chat interface where the agent acts as a Socratic tutor. When a student misses an MCQ, the agent should use the context from the vector index to guide the student toward the right answer using hints rather than giving the solution away.

suggestions.title: LTI Integration for Canvas and Blackboard
suggestions.desc: Develop an LTI (Learning Tools Interoperability) wrapper for the application. This would allow the nutrition/ML quiz app to be embedded directly as an 'External Tool' within major educational platforms, enabling automatic grade syncing and single sign-on (SSO) for students.

title: Model UN Crisis Software
authors: Hicks, Gregory; Ritzi, James; Kipchoge, Vincent K
abstract: Utilizing an Agile approach, this project develops a web-based solution, the Model UN Crisis Software, to streamline the management of crisis committees in Model UN conferences. The software is developed using Microsoft Visual Studio and Microsoft SQL Server Management Studio, adhering to the .NET framework and related conventions. It leverages Microsoft Azure SQL Database for back-end data storage and follows the ASP.NET core MVC framework, utilizing powerful .NET tools such as C# and Razor. The developed software provides comprehensive features to reduce the strain of hosting a crisis committee, such as directive and news management, user management, and a messaging system.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Crisis Broadcasting with SignalR
suggestions.desc: Enhance the current ASP.NET Core MVC framework by integrating SignalR to push live news alerts and 'crisis breaks' to all delegate dashboards simultaneously. This replaces manual refreshes with a real-time WebSocket connection, ensuring all participants react to the same information at the exact same millisecond.

suggestions.title: Automated Directive Analysis with OpenAI API
suggestions.desc: Integrate a C# middle-tier that sends delegate-written directives to an LLM (via OpenAI's API) to analyze potential geopolitical consequences. The system can provide Crisis Staff with an 'Impact Summary' and suggested responses based on the current state of the Azure SQL database.

suggestions.title: Mobile Delegate Portal via .NET MAUI
suggestions.desc: Extend the web-based solution into a cross-platform mobile application using .NET MAUI to allow delegates to submit motions and vote from their phones. Connect the app to the existing Azure SQL Backend and utilize push notifications for urgent crisis updates.

suggestions.title: Crisis Multi-Tenancy for Conference Hosting
suggestions.desc: Architect a multi-tenant database schema within SQL Server to allow a single instance of the software to host dozens of concurrent committees. Build a super-admin dashboard for conference organizers to monitor activity metrics across different 'rooms' in real-time.

title: Virtual Companionship Chatbot
authors: Tortolero, Cesar; Gurung, Rakshak; Clark, Noah,; Cabrejos, Fabritzzio A
abstract: Loneliness affects about 77% of college students at some point, highlighted by a Gitnuss report. Our project aims to mitigate this by introducing a personalized chatbot that serves as an emotional outlet for students. The application is built on a React Native frontend, employs a DistilGPT-2 language model using the QUAC dataset, and is backed by a Python server. We plan to deploy it on an Azure NC6s_v3 Cloud server, integrating Firebase Real-Time Database for Android and iOS compatibility.

suggestions.title: Local LLM Optimization with Llama.cpp
suggestions.desc: Replace the cloud-hosted DistilGPT-2 with a more modern, quantized Llama-3 or Mistral-7B model running locally on the Python server. This would significantly improve the conversational nuance and emotional intelligence of the companion while reducing Azure hosting costs.

suggestions.title: Affective UI with Real-time Sentiment Mapping
suggestions.desc: Use the VADER or TextBlob Python libraries to analyze user input sentiment in real-time and dynamically change the React Native frontend's color palette and haptics. For example, the app could shift to soothing blue tones and gentle animations if high anxiety levels are detected in the text.

suggestions.title: Campus Resource Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
suggestions.desc: Integrate a vector database (like Pinecone) containing KSU student handbooks and health center FAQs. Use RAG to allow the chatbot to provide specific, actionable campus advice (like counseling office hours) alongside its emotional companionship responses.

suggestions.title: Firebase-Driven Group Support Circles
suggestions.desc: Expand the Firebase integration to allow the chatbot to facilitate 'anonymous group circles' where students with similar stressors can interact. The AI would act as a moderator, using its emotional outlet training to flag toxic behavior and suggest conversation starters.

title: Informal Learning Artificial Intelligence Large Language Model Fine-Tuning on the Select Topic of Entrepreneurship
authors: Gabby, Kristen; Castellon, Melina; Sampathirao, Jyothi
abstract: This project explored the usage and development of open-source Large Language Model (LLM) Artificial Intelligence (AI) with a chat feature, specifically to fine-tune on the topic of entrepreneurship. This project sought to showcase the adaptability of open-source LLMs and highlight challenges and solutions faced in leveraging those LLMs. The primary goal was to show proof of concept that training a LLM on the selected subject can create a specialized AI chat for informal learning.

suggestions.title: Startup Pitch Deck Analyzer
suggestions.desc: Build a feature where users can upload PDFs of pitch decks, and the fine-tuned LLM evaluates them against the entrepreneurship training data. The tool should provide a 'readiness score' and specific suggestions based on venture capital frameworks.

suggestions.title: Quantized Deployment for Private Founders
suggestions.desc: Convert the fine-tuned model into a GGUF format for use with private, offline chat interfaces like LM Studio. This allows entrepreneurs to discuss confidential intellectual property with the AI without data ever leaving their local machine.

suggestions.title: Comparative Pedagogical Benchmark
suggestions.desc: Conduct a research study comparing the fine-tuned model's responses to a baseline GPT-4 model using the 'Entrepreneurial Mindset Profile.' Use a panel of business faculty to blind-test which model provides more actionable 'informal learning' insights.

suggestions.title: Role-Playing Co-Founder Simulator
suggestions.desc: Develop a specialized prompt-engineering layer that forces the LLM into specific personas, such as a 'Skeptical Investor' or a 'Technical Lead.' This transforms the chatbot from a simple Q&A tool into an interactive simulator for high-stakes business negotiations.

similar: legacy:AUTO-SU2020-1283|Summer 2020|using artificial intelligence to prescribe medicine

title: CRM for CCSE Department of KSU
authors: Alla, Venkatesh; Nannapaneni, Keerthi; Rapolu, Vinay Kumar
abstract: This project outlines the development of a bespoke Customer Relationship Management (CRM) system specifically designed for the College of Computer and Software Engineering (CCSE). The initiative aims to centralize customer information into a unified repository, thereby enhancing the confidentiality, management, and optimization of data and communication processes within the college. The CRM system will integrate features for detailed profiles, communication optimization, complex workflow management, document repository, and data migration to ensure efficiency and data integrity. It will also facilitate seamless interaction with Microsoft 365 and Outlook, supporting the college's operational needs and maintaining its commitment to excellence in education and collaboration. The project is set to be executed in three major milestones, involving product selection, system implementation, user testing, and final deployment, with the end goal of improving overall customer relationship management. The team, led by Venkatesh Alla, alongside team members Keerthi Nannapaneni and Vinay Kumar Rapolu, under the guidance of project owners Alla Kemelmakher and Nasiya Sharif, and advisor Ying Xie, plans to use a dedicated project management tool, Jira, for collaboration and task tracking. This CRM initiative not only aims to meet the specific needs of the CCSE but also to set a benchmark for operational efficiency and data management in educational institutions.

suggestions.title: Automated Student Lifecycle Integration via APIs
suggestions.desc: Connect the CRM to university-standard data sources like the Banner API or DegreeWorks. Automate the population of student profiles so that CCSE advisors can see real-time GPA updates and course completion status directly within the CRM without manual data entry.

suggestions.title: AI-Powered 'At-Risk' Student Identification System
suggestions.desc: Build a machine learning module that analyzes communication frequency and academic data within the CRM. Train a model to flag students who are falling behind or disengaging, triggering an automated 'Workflow Management' task for advisors to reach out for an intervention.

suggestions.title: Smart Matchmaking for Internships and Employers
suggestions.desc: Add a 'Corporate Relations' module to the CRM that stores job descriptions from CCSE partners. Use a basic NLP matching algorithm to suggest the top 5 students for a specific internship based on their profile data, skills, and interests tracked in the system.

suggestions.title: Longitudinal Career Outcome Analysis Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Develop a post-graduation tracking feature using LinkedIn API or survey integration. Create a visualization layer (using D3.js or Chart.js) that shows the department how different student profiles (GPA, clubs, electives) correlate with high-paying career outcomes 3–5 years later.

similar: legacy:GC-17|Spring 2019|secure authentication and access control for enterprise

title: Fabric moderation ticketing mod for Minecraft
authors: Lambert, John; Nguyen, Kathy; Scarbrough, Hayden B; Taplin, Dasiane
abstract: This project targets enhancing the KSU Esports program’s Minecraft server by implementing an in-game ticketing system. The system will enable players to report any instances of in-game incidents/issues seamlessly within the game environment. The integration with the KSU Minecraft Discord server will facilitate efficient communication between players and administrators. With a user-friendly interface and optimized resource usage, the system aims to streamline moderation processes.

suggestions.title: Automated Replay Evidence Capture
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Minecraft 'Replay Mod' API so that when a player submits a ticket, the server automatically saves the last 60 seconds of player movements around the incident. This provides moderators with a 3D video 'black box' to review griefing or cheating claims.

suggestions.title: In-Game Bug Bounty Economy
suggestions.desc: Connect the ticketing system to a Minecraft economy plugin (like Vault). When a moderator marks a bug-report ticket as 'Resolved,' the system automatically rewards the reporting player with in-game currency or cosmetics, incentivizing community quality control.

suggestions.title: Heuristic Grief Detection Pre-Filtering
suggestions.desc: Implement a Java-based monitoring thread that flags unusual patterns (like high-frequency block breaking or lava placement) and automatically opens a 'System Ticket.' This shifts the moderation process from reactive (waiting for reports) to proactive detection.

suggestions.title: Cross-Server Sync with Redis
suggestions.desc: Scale the ticketing mod to work across a BungeeCord or Velocity network by using Redis to sync ticket states. This allows a moderator on the 'Creative' server to see and respond to tickets generated on the 'Survival' or 'Esports' servers instantly.

title: Automatic Daily Financing Headlines Collection, Storage, Analysis and Presentation
authors: Atimamula, Sahithi, Evans, Jasminn O, Reyes, Julian, Castro, Victoria, Settle, Krista G
abstract: The "Automatic Daily Financing Headlines Collection, Storage, Analysis, and Presentation" project aims to streamline the process of gathering financial headlines from financial news sources, storing them systematically, performing analysis, and presenting the insights in a user-friendly format. This automation project is designed to provide timely and relevant financial information to users interested in staying informed about market trends, economic news, and financial events. By automating the entire workflow, users can stay informed, make data-driven decisions, and navigate the dynamic landscape of financial news with ease. The project embodies the fusion of automation, data analytics, and user-centric design to create a powerful tool for staying ahead in the world of finance.

suggestions.title: Causal Sentiment Analysis vs. Real-Time Volatility
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Alpha Vantage or Yahoo Finance API to pull real-time stock price data for the companies mentioned in the headlines. Build a correlation engine to determine if 'negative' sentiment scores (using VADER or FinBERT) reliably precede price drops in the subsequent 4 hours.

suggestions.title: Multi-Source Fact-Checking and Conflict Detection
suggestions.desc: Implement a 'Consensus Agent' using an LLM that compares headlines from different sources (e.g., Bloomberg vs. Reuters vs. Wall Street Journal). If two sources report conflicting numbers or interpretations on the same financial event, the system should flag it as a 'High-Uncertainty Alert'.

suggestions.title: Personalized LLM-Generated Financial Newsletters
suggestions.desc: Build a user subscription system where users can follow specific sectors (e.g., Fintech, Green Energy). Use an LLM to summarize all daily headlines into a concise, bulleted 'Morning Digest' email sent via SendGrid, focusing only on the tickers in the user's watch list.

suggestions.title: Detecting Institutional Bias in Financial Reporting
suggestions.desc: Apply NLP techniques to identify linguistic bias in headlines from different news outlets over a 6-month period. Research whether certain outlets consistently lean 'bullish' or 'bearish' compared to actual market performance, creating a 'Source Reliability' index for investors.

title: Owl Cyber Defense Systems
authors: Gilstrap, Randolph S; Dunbar, Christopher; Aguirre, Stephanie; LeBlanc, Ryan M; Place, Justin
abstract: Owl Cyber Defense Systems is a fictitious (for now?) start-up offering comprehensive cybersecurity solutions for small and medium businesses. Our premier flagship product is an AI Chatbot that answers security related questions and provides vetted code and security settings to secure and harden a variety of systems. Starting with the initial concept, we methodically progressed through the business planning process, carefully considering technology usage and design. This comprehensive approach ultimately enabled us to develop a robust set of client offerings. We used a hybrid approach combining Agile Scrum and traditional Waterfall methodologies to complete the project. We utilized Jira Project Management to track the Epics and tasks required to plan, design, develop, test, and release into our production systems. Each milestone was represented by a custom Sprint in Jira designed to produce weekly scrum updates and automated person-hour tracking reports culminating in the final project presentation.

suggestions.title: Real-Time CVE Feed Integration for Proactive Patching
suggestions.desc: Connect the AI Chatbot to the NIST National Vulnerability Database (NVD) via their API. When a user asks about a specific software, the bot should automatically check for the latest CVEs and provide a customized 'remediation script' in Bash or PowerShell to patch the specific flaw.

suggestions.title: Automated SMB Compliance Roadmap (SOC2/HIPAA)
suggestions.desc: Build a module where an SMB owner can select their industry (e.g., Healthcare) and the chatbot generates a step-by-step security hardening checklist. This checklist would link the AI's vetted code snippets directly to specific compliance requirements (e.g., HIPAA Technical Safeguards).

suggestions.title: Infrastructure-as-Code (Terraform) Security Hardening
suggestions.desc: Extend the chatbot to generate secure-by-default Terraform or CloudFormation templates. Instead of just advice, the bot would provide a downloadable file that sets up a hardened AWS or Azure environment with pre-configured IAM roles and VPC security groups.

suggestions.title: Adversarial Testing of AI-Generated Security Advice
suggestions.desc: Implement a 'Red Team' evaluation framework to test if the chatbot can be tricked into providing vulnerable code via prompt injection. Research and document defense mechanisms, such as 'Dual-LLM verification' where a second model audits the code for security flaws before it is presented.

similar: legacy:GR-12|Spring 2016|nosql injection security analysis and defense
legacy:GR-12|Fall 2016|nosql injection security analysis and defense solution

title: Learning Resource Finder: A web Scraping tool for educational materials
authors: Devara, Ashrith Kumar
abstract: The Learning Resource Finder is a pioneering tool designed to alleviate the challenges associated with navigating the vast landscape of online educational content. Leveraging sophisticated web scraping techniques and API integrations, this tool empowers users to efficiently discover relevant learning materials tailored to their specific needs. Through a user-friendly Flask-based web interface, users initiate search queries, which are then encoded and utilized to fetch pertinent URLs from leading educational platforms such as JavaTPoint, W3Schools, Coursera, Udemy, and GeeksforGeeks, as well as Google search results. The core of the web scraping process lies in the meticulous extraction of URLs from HTML content using Scrapy, ensuring precision by eliminating irrelevant or redundant links while excluding duplicate URLs and Wikipedia sources to maintain resource integrity. The Learning Resource Finder represents a crucial advancement in the realm of digital learning, providing users with a curated compilation of educational materials to enhance their learning journey.

suggestions.title: Semantic Vector Search using OpenAI and ChromaDB
suggestions.desc: Replace the standard keyword search with a semantic search engine. Use OpenAI's 'text-embedding-3-small' to encode resources and store them in a ChromaDB vector database, allowing the tool to find relevant materials even if the search terms don't exactly match the resource title.

suggestions.title: Graph-Based Learning Path Visualization
suggestions.desc: Use Neo4j or D3.js to visualize the scraped educational materials as a connected graph. If a user searches for 'Data Science,' the tool should show a prerequisite-based path (e.g., Python -> Statistics -> Machine Learning) by analyzing the metadata of the found resources.

suggestions.title: Automated Credibility and Bias Scoring
suggestions.desc: Develop a scoring algorithm that evaluates scraped resources based on domain authority (using the Moz API), age of content, and peer citations. Use an LLM to scan for potential commercial bias in the educational content, helping users distinguish between 'Sales Content' and 'Academic Content'.

suggestions.title: Smart Browser Extension for Contextual Study Aids
suggestions.desc: Build a Chrome extension version of the Learning Resource Finder that detects the topic of the current webpage the student is reading. It should automatically suggest top-rated educational videos or PDFs from the scraper database that explain the concepts on the current page.

title: EdAI – AI Enabled Teaching Robot for Informal Learning
authors: Bower, Jennifer; Williams, Shane; Fuller, Justice
abstract: We began our project by researching various popular, open-source AI tools that are available today. After we chose to focus on ChatGPT as our AI tool, we decided on cybersecurity as our subject matter. Next, we researched traditional cybersecurity training methods used by companies to train their employees on cybersecurity issues. Our project focused on determining whether or not open-source AI tools such as ChatGPT could replace traditional cybersecurity training tools and methods for companies.

suggestions.title: Adversarial Phishing Simulation Roleplay
suggestions.desc: Transform the teaching robot into a 'Live Adversary' that engages employees in simulated social engineering attacks. Use the ChatGPT API to craft personalized phishing attempts in the chat, then evaluate and score the employee’s ability to recognize the threat.

suggestions.title: LLM Fine-Tuning for Pedagogy vs. Accuracy
suggestions.desc: Research the effectiveness of different LLMs by fine-tuning a Llama 3 model on cybersecurity training datasets. Compare its ability to explain complex concepts like 'Buffer Overflows' to non-technical employees against the base ChatGPT model used in the original project.

suggestions.title: Gamified Incident Response Tabletop Simulator
suggestions.desc: Build a module where the robot acts as a Game Master for a cybersecurity breach scenario. It presents an unfolding crisis to the user, who must make decisions in real-time; the bot then analyzes the consequences of those choices based on industry best practices.

suggestions.title: Enterprise Slack/Teams 'Micro-Learning' Integration
suggestions.desc: Develop a middleware that pushes 2-minute interactive 'security snacks' to employees via corporate chat platforms. The bot uses recent company-specific security alerts (via Jira or SIEM logs) to create relevant, just-in-time training moments.

title: Multiple Myeloma: Increase Longevity and Quality of Life through Early Detection
authors: Martinez, Desyne
abstract: Multiple Myeloma is a rare form of bone marrow cancer where plasma cells accumulate in the blood stream attacking the skeletal system, nervous system, and kidneys of predominantly African Americans. The disease results in high mortality rates within 5 years of initial diagnosis. Multiple Myeloma has subtle symptoms of bone pain; doctors often send people to physical therapy missing the diagnosis. Current research on the International Myeloma Foundation website includes summaries of blood tests of Multiple Myeloma patients. This study seeks to identify the best blood test predictors of Stage 3, the most aggressive stage of Multiple Myeloma. The cost savings are also considered of using these blood tests as an initial screening as compared to a bone marrow biopsy. Using 21 blood test results and demographic information on 203 Multiple Myeloma patients from 2008-2019 in Algeria, Logistic Regression was conducted to identify the best predictor of Stage 3 Multiple Myeloma versus Stage 1 and 2. Wald Confidence Intervals were used to estimate the odds ratios. Cost savings were calculated by determining the cost differential of less invasive blood tests versus the more invasive bone marrow biopsy. The Logistic Model was able to distinguish 65.43% of the time between whether patients have Stage 3 versus Stages 1 and 2. The odds of having Stage 3 Multiple Myeloma increase 1.04 to 1.56 times for each one g/dL decrease in MCHC. Lower patient MCHC levels are more indicative of the patient having Stage 3. Using an MCHC blood test has an estimated cost savings of $1734 per patient as compared to a bone marrow biopsy. Testing a patient with bone pain for MCHC can facilitate earlier Multiple Myeloma diagnosis, allowing physicians to administer earlier treatments, thereby improving patients’ longevity and quality of life while coping with the disease.
supervisor: Prof. Susan Mathews Hardy, Dr. Gene Ray

suggestions.title: Predictive Classification using the CoMMpass Dataset
suggestions.desc: Apply Random Forest or Gradient Boosting models to the MMRF (Multiple Myeloma Research Foundation) CoMMpass dataset. Develop a model that predicts 5-year mortality risk based on initial blood test markers (e.g., albumin, beta-2 microglobulin) to assist doctors in earlier triage.

suggestions.title: Geospatial Analysis of Health Inequity and Diagnosis Gaps
suggestions.desc: Cross-reference Multiple Myeloma incidence rates in African American populations with local access to specialized oncology centers using GIS software. Identify 'diagnosis deserts' where the time-to-diagnosis is significantly higher, providing actionable data for public health outreach.

suggestions.title: Early Warning Mobile App for High-Risk Symptom Tracking
suggestions.desc: Create a simple mobile logging app for patients in high-risk demographics to track 'vague' symptoms like persistent bone pain or fatigue. The app should use a basic expert-system algorithm to provide a 'When to See a Specialist' recommendation that patients can bring to their GP.

suggestions.title: Federated Learning for Multi-Institutional Research
suggestions.desc: Develop a prototype for a Federated Learning system that allows different hospitals to train a shared myeloma detection model without sharing raw, sensitive patient data. Use the Flower (flwr.dev) framework to demonstrate how data privacy can be maintained while increasing the dataset size.

title: Analyzing Breast Cancer Histopathology Images Using Deep Neural Network Models
authors: Lisbon, Je'dae; Bolnik, Michael; Eshaghian, Sepehr
abstract: Our project aims to explore human tissue cells digitized by whole slide scanners for a better understanding of complex tumor microenvironments in breast cancer histopathology images, using various deep neural network models. First, we experimented with 70% percentages of tumor cells on image classification using ResNet50, VGG16, and Inception-ResNet. Second, we performed instance image segmentation using Mask-RCNN. Third, we applied two well-known explainable artificial intelligence (AI) techniques including Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping (Grad-CAM) and Shapley Additive Explanations (SHAP) to determine the effectiveness of the models.

suggestions.title: Attention-Based Vision Transformer (ViT) Comparison
suggestions.desc: Implement a Vision Transformer (ViT) and compare its performance against the existing ResNet and VGG models. Research whether the global self-attention mechanism in ViTs better captures the long-range dependencies in complex tumor microenvironments.

suggestions.title: Pathologist-in-the-Loop Annotation Web App
suggestions.desc: Build a Flask-based web interface where pathologists can upload whole slide images and view Mask-RCNN segmentations. Include a 'correction' tool where doctors can refine the AI's masks, saving the corrections as new training data for model fine-tuning.

suggestions.title: Adversarial Robustness Stress Testing
suggestions.desc: Test the clinical reliability of the models by applying adversarial perturbations (using the CleverHans library). Investigate how subtle digital noise or common scanner artifacts affect the Mask-RCNN segmentation accuracy and develop 'adversarial training' to mitigate these risks.

suggestions.title: Cross-Stain Style Transfer with CycleGAN
suggestions.desc: Develop a CycleGAN preprocessing pipeline to normalize histopathology images from different labs or staining techniques (H&E vs. IHC). This ensures the classification models remain accurate regardless of the digitized slide's source laboratory.

similar: legacy:GRCS-01|Spring 2017|deep convolutional neural network and parallel programming

title: Coronary Artery Segmentation Using Convolutional Neural Network
authors: Bell, Connor; Nyiam, Brenda; Pracharn, Daron L
abstract: The project contributes to the advancement of medical imaging technology by overcoming the challenges associated with segmenting coronary arteries from ICA images. By leveraging deep learning algorithms, the system can effectively extract coronary arteries with high accuracy, providing valuable information for CAD diagnosis and treatment planning. Accurate and efficient coronary artery segmentation can improve the workflow of cardiologists and enhance the quality of patient care. A robust automated segmentation model could potentially reduce the time and resources required for manual annotation by experienced cardiologists, leading to cost savings and increased efficiency in clinical settings. Additionally, the developed model could be integrated into the education system with an interactive GUI for cardiologists to draw and learn the anatomical structure of the coronary artery system.
supervisor: Dr. Chen Zhao, Prof. Sharon Perry

suggestions.title: Implementing Attention U-Net for Small Vessel Precision
suggestions.desc: Upgrade the standard CNN architecture to an Attention U-Net or a ResUNet++. These architectures use attention gates to focus the model on the small, thin pixels of the coronary arteries, significantly reducing 'false positives' in the noisy background of ICA images.

suggestions.title: Automated Stenosis Quantification and Flow Estimation
suggestions.desc: Extend the segmentation tool to calculate the percentage of vessel narrowing (stenosis) automatically. Use image processing techniques (like skeletonization) to measure the diameter of the segmented artery across its length, alerting cardiologists to areas with more than 70% blockage.

suggestions.title: 3D Reconstruction from 2D Angiogram Projections
suggestions.desc: Apply a Generative Adversarial Network (GAN) or a NeRF-based approach to estimate 3D vessel structure from two or more 2D ICA views. This would help cardiologists better understand the spatial geometry of a blockage before performing a surgical intervention like stenting.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Clinical Workflow Integration via DICOM
suggestions.desc: Build a middleware using the PyDICOM library that allows the segmentation model to pull images directly from a hospital's PACS (Picture Archiving and Communication System). The result should be a streamlined UI where a doctor can click 'Analyze' on a DICOM file and see the overlay immediately.

title: Deep Learning Approach to Network Anomaly Detection
authors: Lisasi, Rene P; Jackson, Jamia M
abstract: A model of network anomaly detection capable of detecting a multitude of network attacks.
