This model is based on the hypothesis that by studying a system’s network records for irregular patterns during system usage, network anomalies can be identified. This model contains information about the type of attacks and metrics. This model is to be used in any type of distributed environment. The general purpose of this model is to detect when an attack is or has happened using deep learning techniques to optimize the training speed, accuracy and robustness of attack detection. This is done to stop the epidemic of attacks that hit companies like GitHub, Nobel Foundation, Vodafone, and some Russian banks [13], with Google being the only company to block the 46 million DDOS attacks per second [13].

suggestions.title: Explainable Intrusion Detection via SHAP Integration
suggestions.desc: Enhance the deep learning model by integrating SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) to provide local explanations for why specific network packets were flagged as anomalies. This allows network administrators to see exactly which features—like TTL, packet size, or flag combinations—triggered the alarm.

suggestions.title: IoT Edge Deployment for Resource-Constrained Environments
suggestions.desc: Convert the model to a quantized TensorFlow Lite format to run on low-power edge devices like Raspberry Pis or industrial gateways. This would enable real-time anomaly detection directly at the network's edge, reducing latency and bandwidth usage compared to cloud-based analysis.

suggestions.title: Federated Learning for Cross-Organizational Threat Intelligence
suggestions.desc: Implement a Federated Learning framework (using Flower or OpenFL) that allows multiple distributed systems to train a shared anomaly detection model. This enables the model to learn from diverse attack patterns across different organizations without ever sharing sensitive raw network logs.

suggestions.title: Live SIEM Integration and Automated Incident Response
suggestions.desc: Build a connector for the Elastic Stack (ELK) or Splunk that pipes live network traffic into your model and triggers automated response actions via Ansible playbooks. The end result is a system that not only detects attacks but automatically updates firewall rules to mitigate them.

title: Faster Inequivalence Testing Using Robustness
authors: Jackson, Emily G
abstract: We propose a new method for quickly testing the inequivalence of two Boolean functions, when one function is represented as an ordered binary decision diagram (OBDD), and the other is represented in conjunctive normal form (CNF). Our approach is based on a notion of classifier robustness from the fields of explainable AI (XAI) and adversarial machine learning. In particular, we show that two Boolean functions that are very similar in terms of their truth values, can be very different in terms of their robustness, which in turn, provides a witness to their inequivalence. A more efficient approach to inequivalence testing has an impact on the development of more efficient model counters and knowledge compilers. In turn, such developments facilitate advances in explainable AI and adversarial ML.

suggestions.title: Hybrid SAT-Robustness Solver for Large-Scale Logic
suggestions.desc: Combine your robustness-based inequivalence testing with a traditional SAT solver like Z3 or MiniSat. Use the robustness metric to quickly prune the search space for the SAT solver, significantly speeding up the verification of complex circuits that are too large for standard OBDD representations.

suggestions.title: Verification of Neural Network-to-Logic Transpilations
suggestions.desc: Apply this testing method to verify the equivalence of Binarized Neural Networks (BNNs) when they are converted into CNF or OBDD logic for hardware implementation. Use this to ensure that optimizations during the hardware synthesis process do not alter the classification logic of the AI.

suggestions.title: Software Patch Logic Inequivalence Checker
suggestions.desc: Develop a tool that converts small C functions into their Boolean logic equivalents and uses your method to detect if a security patch unintentionally changed the logical flow of the program. This focuses on ensuring functional consistency during critical software updates.

suggestions.title: Logic-Verification-as-a-Service API
suggestions.desc: Build a RESTful API and a web-based dashboard that allows hardware engineers to upload CNF files and OBDDs to check for inequivalence in the cloud. Focus the UI on visualizing the 'robustness' gaps, showing users exactly where the two logic representations diverge.

title: Transforming Game Play: A Comparative Study of CNN and Transformer based Q-Networks in Reinforcement Learning
authors: Stigall, William A
abstract: In this study we investigate the performance of Deep Q-Networks utilizing Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) and Transformer architectures across 3 different Atari Games. The advent of DQNs have significantly advanced Reinforcement Learning, enabling agents to directly learn optimal policy from high dimensional sensory inputs from pixel or RAM data. While CNN based DQNs have been extensively studied and deployed in various domains Transformer based DQNs are relatively unexplored. Our research aims to fill this gap by benchmarking the performance of both DCQNs and DTQNs across the Atari games' Asteroids, Space Invaders and Centipede. Our research finds that our Transformer Agent learned slower than the CNN-based agent, and was slower to learn game-extending policies.

suggestions.title: Attention-Map Visualization for Atari Strategy Analysis
suggestions.desc: Extract the attention weights from the Transformer-based DQN to create heatmaps of the Atari game frames. Compare these heatmaps to CNN saliency maps to determine if Transformers 'look' at strategic distant objects (like enemy projectiles) differently than the local-feature-focused CNNs.

suggestions.title: Multi-Agent Transformer-DQN for Cooperative Play
suggestions.desc: Extend the research into a Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL) setting using environments like the 'Overcooked' AI gym. Investigate if the Transformer's ability to handle long-range dependencies allows for better coordination between two agents compared to standard CNN architectures.

suggestions.title: Cross-Game Transfer Learning and Weights Adaptation
suggestions.desc: Study the transferability of the Transformer-DQN by pre-training on a high-complexity game like 'Montezuma’s Revenge' and fine-tuning on simpler games. Measure if the Transformer architecture retains 'spatial logic' more effectively than CNNs when moving between different visual domains.

suggestions.title: Real-Time RL Performance Optimization for Mobile Gaming
suggestions.desc: Optimize the Transformer-DQN for mobile deployment using Apple's CoreML or Qualcomm’s SNPE. The goal is to build an AI 'companion' that can play mobile games in real-time with minimal battery drain, demonstrating the commercial viability of high-parameter Transformer models on edge hardware.

title: Adversarial Patch Attack in Deep Learning Based Remote Sensing Object Detection Model
authors: Bratcher, Kyle
abstract: Advancements in the field of machine learning have led to object detection systems that can approach or even improve upon human performance. Based on deep learning, these systems play a crucial role in many aspects, and continue to be improved on and see expanded adoption. However, these systems are vulnerable to adversarial attacks that rely on targeted noise to spoof detection. Researchers have applied this concept to increase real world adversarial performance by restricting this noise to a patch that can be placed on new images to disrupt object detection. Previous research has focused on patches applied to person recognition (Thys et al. 2019). We focus on the vulnerabilities inherent in remote sensing systems. We develop an adversarial patch to defeat a modern object detection system, YOLOv8. This work demonstrates that remote sensing can still be defeated by an adversarial patch attack and will inform future efforts to develop model robustness against these attacks.

suggestions.title: Adversarial Training Defense for Remote Sensing Models
suggestions.desc: Develop a robust training pipeline using the Projected Gradient Descent (PGD) method to make the remote sensing model immune to patches. Use the DOTA dataset to retrain the model, injecting synthetic adversarial patches during the training loop to improve its adversarial accuracy.

suggestions.title: Physical World Patch Testing via Drone Deployment
suggestions.desc: Print the adversarial patches and place them in real-world outdoor environments to test the model using live drone footage. This research extension would bridge the gap between digital simulation and the physical world, proving if environmental factors like shadows and lighting negate the patch's effectiveness.

suggestions.title: Maritime Security Audit and Ship Detection Resilience
suggestions.desc: Apply the adversarial patch attacks specifically to ship detection models using the Airbus Ship Detection dataset. Build a benchmark tool that satellite operators can use to identify if their naval surveillance systems are vulnerable to being blinded by small, strategically placed patches on vessels.

suggestions.title: Sentry: An Adversarial Detection Dashboard for Satellite Providers
suggestions.desc: Build a commercial-grade dashboard that scans incoming satellite imagery for the presence of adversarial noise or patches before the object detection model processes them. Implement a 'denoising' stage that attempts to neutralize detected patches to restore the original detection capabilities.

title: Performance Analysis of Post-Quantum Computing Key Encapsulation Mechanism Algorithms
authors: Horton, Dillon J; Gutierrez, Jose; Standard, Tyler G
abstract: Within the next twenty years or so experts predict that we will have quantum computers which will make certain kinds of encryption that we rely on ineffective and vulnerable to malicious entities. Post quantum computing (PQC) algorithms fill in that security gap that classical encryption algorithms can not. A particular category of PQC algorithms are key exchange mechanism (KEM) algorithm. The goal of these algorithms is to securely generate a shared symmetric key which can be used for encrypting future communication between the hosts. An important use case for these algorithms is in securing the Transport Layer Security protocol (TLS) against quantum adversaries. Due to the widespread use of TLS, it is critical that any new standard use PQC algorithms which are both efficient and secure. To this end we test each of the PQC KEM algorithms provided by oqs-provider library to compare their performance impact on the TLS handshake.

suggestions.title: Post-Quantum Side-Channel Attack Resistance Analysis
suggestions.desc: Measure the power consumption and electromagnetic emissions of an ARM Cortex-M4 microcontroller while it executes KEM algorithms like Kyber or FrodoKEM. Analyze if timing or power variations leak enough information to reconstruct the shared symmetric key, and propose software-level countermeasures.

suggestions.title: Post-Quantum WireGuard VPN Implementation
suggestions.desc: Integrate the top-performing KEM algorithm from your analysis into the WireGuard protocol's handshake. Create a proof-of-concept VPN client and server that can establish a secure tunnel resistant to future quantum computer attacks while measuring the impact on connection establishment time.

suggestions.title: Hybrid PQC-Classical Key Exchange Framework
suggestions.desc: Develop a hybrid key exchange module that combines a classical algorithm (like ECDH) with the evaluated PQC KEMs. This ensures that the system remains secure even if the PQC algorithm is found to have flaws, following the NIST recommendations for transition-period security.

suggestions.title: Enterprise PQC Readiness and Migration Auditor
suggestions.desc: Build a CLI tool that audits an organization’s network traffic and internal PKI to identify which legacy encryption methods should be replaced by specific KEMs. The tool should provide a 'migration roadmap' based on the performance benchmarks identified in your initial study.

similar: legacy:GRCS-11|Spring 2017|research on detecting malware using encrypted traffic

title: EmoHydra: Multimodal Emotion Classification using Heterogenous Modality Fusion
authors: Stigall, William A
abstract: Affective computing is a field of growing importance, as human society becomes more integrated with machines. Human feelings are both complex and multi-modal, expressed through various methods and nuances in behavior. In this work we introduce EmoHydra, a multi-modal model created through the fusion of three top-level models fine-tuned on text, vision, and speech respectively. Despite heterogenous heads performing well on the unseen data, as well as generalizing well to other benchmarks, logit concatenation proves to be ineffective at predicting Multimodal data, therefore we implement Multi-Head Attention as our fusion mechanism.

suggestions.title: Cross-Modality Attention Weighting with Transformer Fusion
suggestions.desc: Replace basic logit fusion with a Transformer-based fusion layer that uses 'Cross-Attention' to let one modality (e.g., text) attend to features in another (e.g., speech). Use the CMU-MOSEI dataset to train this layer, allowing the model to learn that tone of voice may carry more weight than word choice in sarcastic scenarios.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Emotion Dashboard for Video Conferencing
suggestions.desc: Integrate EmoHydra into a WebRTC application to provide real-time emotional 'heatmaps' for meeting participants. Utilize TensorFlow.js for in-browser vision processing and the Web Speech API to provide live feedback on the emotional climate of a digital workspace.

suggestions.title: Explainable Multimodal Fusion via SHAP Values
suggestions.desc: Implement an XAI (Explainable AI) module that visualizes which modality most influenced a specific classification. Create a dashboard that highlights specific keywords in text, pitch-shifts in audio, or facial landmarks in video that triggered the 'High Stress' or 'Joy' labels.

suggestions.title: Neuro-Divergent Communication Support Tool
suggestions.desc: Adapt EmoHydra to assist individuals with Alexithymia or Autism by providing real-time social cue interpretations. Fine-tune the model on datasets focused on nuanced social interactions to help users understand the emotional subtext of conversations during live interactions.

title: Enhancing Engineering Education Through LLM-Driven Adaptive Quiz Generation
authors: Sreekanth, Devananda; Gopi, Sreekanth
abstract: This study aims to develop an Artificial Intelligence (AI) quiz generation system for engineering students to enhance personalized learning. In the rapidly evolving field of educational education, the emergence of AI and, more specifically, Large Language Models (LLMs) such as GPT-4, Llama, Claude, and Gemini, has marked a significant advancement. Our literature review method employs a systematic approach, analyzing peer-reviewed articles, conference papers, and authoritative reports to uncover the trends and challenges in AI-driven quiz generation. The notable gap identified in our literature review is the lack of LLM-based quiz generation methods specifically for engineering education, which incorporate interactive and adaptive learning features to enhance student engagement and comprehension. This study examines the application of OpenAI LLM with a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system in creating personalized quiz questions for engineering education, focusing on a novel methodology to enhance learning experiences through dynamic, adaptive quizzes and tutorials, particularly targeting the development of math reasoning skills in visual contexts. The proposed methodology leverages the MathVista dataset, comprising 6,141 examples, to enhance the capabilities of the OpenAI LLM. The RAG system populated with this dataset serves as a reference context for generating more relevant and accurate quiz questions. Prompt engineering techniques guide the OpenAI LLM in creating detailed multiple-choice questions (MCQs) focused on visual-mathematical reasoning challenges. The quizzes are designed to adapt to varying levels of student performance, incorporating feedback loops to customize future quizzes based on student responses. The evaluation of our AI pipeline's effectiveness employed metrics such as accuracy, relevance, and adaptability. The results indicated a significant performance in the generation of accurate questions with the least hallucinations.

suggestions.title: Multi-Modal Question Generation from Engineering Diagrams
suggestions.desc: Extend the system using GPT-4o or Gemini Pro Vision to analyze circuit diagrams, CAD drawings, or beam stress charts. The system should generate questions that require students to interpret visual engineering data rather than just text-based concepts.

suggestions.title: LTI-Compliant Integration for Canvas and Moodle
suggestions.desc: Develop a middleware using the LTI (Learning Tools Interoperability) standard to embed the adaptive quiz generator directly into LMS platforms. This allows professors to generate personalized quizzes for students that automatically sync scores and 'concept mastery' data back to the university gradebook.

suggestions.title: Knowledge-Graph Based Adaptive Learning Paths
suggestions.desc: Integrate a Bayesian Knowledge Tracing (BKT) model that maps student quiz performance to a hierarchy of engineering concepts. The LLM can then use this map to identify exactly which prerequisite topics a student is struggling with and generate targeted 'remedial' quiz questions.

suggestions.title: Dynamic Textbook Companion Browser Extension
suggestions.desc: Create a Chrome extension that engineering students can use while reading digital textbooks or research papers. The extension uses the LLM to analyze the currently visible text and generates 'on-the-fly' comprehension checks to ensure the student understands the engineering principles being described.

title: A Novel Identity Verification Framework using a Hybrid Biometric System
authors: Tavva, Namratha; Vadlamudi, Bhanu Prakash; Kunchala, Sumanth; Katta, Snigdha
abstract: In recent years, the field of big data analytics has gained immense attention due to the increasing volume and complexity of data being generated from various sources. One of the key applications of big data analytics is in the field of identity verification, where it is used to process and analyze large amounts of biometric data to authenticate individuals. A hybrid biometric system that combines multiple biometric modalities has been shown to be more effective in identity verification than a single modality system. In this project, we propose an Identity Verification Framework using a Hybrid Biometric System that leverages big data analytics techniques to improve the accuracy and efficiency of the verification process. The framework uses a combination of iris recognition and face recognition modalities to authenticate individuals. The proposed framework involves several stages, including data collection, preprocessing, feature extraction, and classification. The dataset used in the project includes 460 images in total, including 5 photographs of each left and right iris from 46 individuals. Iris segmentation is used to extract the iris region, and Gabor wavelets are used to extract texture features from the iris image. Face detection and recognition are performed using OpenCV's Haar Cascade classifier and deep learning-based face recognition models, respectively.
supervisor: Dr. Kazi Aminul Islam, Dr. Sumit Chakravarty

suggestions.title: Privacy-Preserving Matching via Homomorphic Encryption
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Microsoft SEAL library to perform hybrid biometric matching on encrypted data. This ensures that the identity verification framework can authenticate a user without the central server ever seeing the raw biometric templates (face, fingerprint, etc.), solving major privacy concerns.

suggestions.title: Mobile Multi-Factor Liveness Detection
suggestions.desc: Extend the framework to mobile devices by adding 'liveness' detection to the biometric flow. Build a system that requires synchronized face tracking and specific voice prompts to prevent spoofing attacks using static photos or deepfake audio, utilizing the device's camera and microphone simultaneously.

suggestions.title: Bias Mitigation using GAN-Augmented Biometric Datasets
suggestions.desc: Research and implement a GAN-based data augmentation pipeline to balance the biometric dataset across different demographics (age, ethnicity, gender). Test if the hybrid system’s accuracy improves for underrepresented groups when the model is trained on this synthesized, diverse biometric data.

suggestions.title: Zero-Trust Identity Provider for Enterprise Auth
suggestions.desc: Convert the hybrid biometric framework into an OIDC (OpenID Connect) provider. This allows companies to replace traditional passwords with your multi-modal biometric system for secure employee logins to cloud services like AWS, Google Workspace, and Slack.

similar: legacy:GRIT-25|Spring 2017|iris authentication framework to prevent presentation attacks

title: Identification of AI-Generated Images
authors: Foster, Chris; Brock, Joshua; Kottala, Harini; Korrapati, Srilatha
abstract: With the quick rise of Artificial Intelligence (AI), generative AI models have greatly increased the volume and velocity of data creation. Among that data, AI-generated images have become a highly discussed topic, especially when discussing the potential dangers of these AI models. Due to these dangers, being able to distinguish AI-generated art from human-made art is becoming a necessity. Additionally, as these AI-models improve, it is becoming increasingly difficult for humans to determine whether art is AI-generated or human-made. This paper proposes the further exploration of the effectiveness of a current state of the art AI-image identification model.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Browser Authenticator for Visual Media
suggestions.desc: Develop a Chrome extension that uses the trained detection model to analyze images on social media feeds in real-time. Use the WebGL API to run lightweight inference in the browser, providing users with a 'probability of AI generation' overlay on platforms like X and Instagram.

suggestions.title: Model-Specific Attribution Engine
suggestions.desc: Extend the binary 'AI vs Human' classifier into a multi-class model that identifies the specific generator (DALL-E 3, Midjourney, Stable Diffusion, or Adobe Firefly). Use frequency domain analysis (FFT) to detect the unique 'fingerprints' or checkerboard artifacts left by different upscaling architectures.

suggestions.title: Adversarial Robustness Benchmarking
suggestions.desc: Investigate how the detector performs against 'poisoned' or edited AI images by applying Gaussian noise, JPEG compression, and adversarial perturbations. Build a testing suite that determines the minimum amount of human retouching required to fool the current AI-detection thresholds.

suggestions.title: Explainable AI (XAI) for Art Verification
suggestions.desc: Integrate Grad-CAM (Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping) to highlight the specific pixels or regions that led the model to classify an image as AI-generated. This creates a visual heat map for users, showing exactly where the 'hallucinations' or unnatural textures (like botched hands or repetitive patterns) occur.

title: Cloud Based Bus Tracking and Ticketing System
authors: Kommineni, Vyghni Sudha; Sravani, Damacharla
abstract: In many urban areas, public transportation systems frequently fail to fulfill passenger demand, causing aggravation owing to a lack of real-time information about bus locations, timetables and delays. Outdated ticketing processes, which are labor-intensive and prone to mistakes, compound the annoyance by failing to match current travelers' expectations. Furthermore, transportation operators encounter difficulties with fleet management, route optimisation, and issue response due to a lack of comprehensive data analytics. Reliable monitoring systems are required to prioritize passenger and bus safety, and achieving sustainability targets necessitates efficient operations to reduce fuel consumption and emissions. A scalable and customizable cloud-based bus tracking and ticketing system is critical for addressing these difficulties and improving the entire public transit experience.

suggestions.title: Edge-Based ETA Prediction for Low-Connectivity Zones
suggestions.desc: Migrate the ETA prediction logic from the cloud to the IoT device on the bus using a lightweight LSTM model. This allows the system to continue providing accurate arrival estimates to local bus-stop displays even when the bus passes through cellular dead zones with no cloud access.

suggestions.title: Passenger Density Monitoring via Ultrasonic Sensors
suggestions.desc: Add ultrasonic or IR sensors to the IoT module to estimate real-time passenger occupancy. This data can be displayed in the user app, allowing commuters to choose less crowded buses and providing operators with 'heatmaps' of bus utilization throughout the day.

suggestions.title: Contactless NFC and QR-Code Ticketing Integration
suggestions.desc: Build a secure mobile payment gateway using Stripe or a similar API to allow passengers to purchase digital tickets via the app. Integrate an offline-first QR code scanner on the bus IoT device to validate tickets even when the bus is not actively connected to the internet.

suggestions.title: Fleet-Wide Dynamic Route Optimization Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Develop a centralized management dashboard for operators that uses the real-time bus location data and Google Maps Traffic API. The system should automatically suggest alternate routes to drivers via their IoT interface to bypass accidents or heavy congestion, improving overall system punctuality.

title: A Two-Stage Prediction Model For House Prices
authors: Nguyen, Nguyen Thi Binh; Bell, Brandon; Ravula, Syanthan Reddy; Thota, Hari Krishna
abstract: Predicting house prices is a challenging task that researchers from various fields (economics, statistics, politics, etc.) have attempted to answer. An accurate house prediction is useful not only to policymakers to improve their policies, but also to help sellers and buyers in the real estate market make well- informed decisions. Commonly, prediction models are trained on the whole dataset. However, as Azimlu et al [1] suggested, such models might not perform very well on dispersed data. They propose a new approach which first divides the whole dataset into smaller clusters, and then each cluster would be trained with an appropriate machine learning algorithm. It is approved to provide a more accurate prediction.

suggestions.title: Geospatial Feature Engineering via OpenStreetMap API
suggestions.desc: Enhance the two-stage model by programmatically extracting features like distance to the nearest subway station, number of nearby parks, and school quality ratings using the Overpass API. Incorporating these 'amenity' scores will likely improve prediction accuracy in the second stage of the model.

suggestions.title: Unsupervised Clustering for Automated Market Segmentation
suggestions.desc: Replace the manual data segmentation in Stage 1 with an unsupervised clustering algorithm like HDBSCAN. Research how automatically grouping houses into clusters based on latent features (rather than just geography) impacts the performance of the regression models in Stage 2.

suggestions.title: Real Estate Portfolio Stress-Testing Tool
suggestions.desc: Build a tool that allows users to simulate 'What-If' scenarios, such as the impact of a new tech hub opening nearby or a change in local interest rates. The model would predict how these external economic shocks would propagate through the different segments identified in Stage 1.

suggestions.title: Automated Mortgage Risk Assessment API
suggestions.desc: Develop a REST API for financial institutions that provides a 'valuation confidence interval' alongside the house price prediction. By analyzing the variance in Stage 2 predictions, the API can help lenders identify properties where the data is too 'dispersed' to safely issue a high-value loan.

title: Customer Segmentation for Marketing Campaigns Using K-means Clustering
authors: Vellanki, Jaswanthi; Chandel, Hrithik Singh; Kommineni, Vyghni Sudha; Bagal, Priyanka A; Ramani, Rutvikkumar K
abstract: The objective of this project is to use K-means clustering an unsupervised machine learning algorithm to categorize customers based on characteristics such as demograp hics, purchasing history and interaction behavior. The purpose is to discover different client segments that can be targeted with specialized marketing techniques that improve marketing campaign efficiency and increase consumer satisfaction and engagement.

suggestions.title: Automated CRM Campaign Trigger System
suggestions.desc: Build an integration between the K-means clustering output and a marketing platform like SendGrid or Mailchimp. Develop a system that automatically assigns new customers to a segment and triggers a personalized 5-day email 'welcome sequence' based on their specific cluster characteristics.

suggestions.title: Real-Time E-Commerce Personalization Engine
suggestions.desc: Deploy the clustering model as a real-time API that categorizes users based on their active session behavior (clicks, time on page). Use the result to dynamically change the website’s hero banner or 'recommended for you' section using a React-based frontend to increase immediate conversion.

suggestions.title: Optimized Centroid Evolution Tracking
suggestions.desc: Research how customer segments shift over time by implementing a 'Rolling K-means' approach that compares clusters month-over-month. Develop a visualization tool using Plotly that shows how individual customers migrate between segments, identifying 'at-risk' customers before they churn.

suggestions.title: Multi-Dimensional RFM Scoring Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Incorporate Recency, Frequency, and Monetary (RFM) modeling as the primary feature set for the K-means algorithm. Build a Streamlit dashboard that allows marketers to adjust the 'weight' of these factors and see how the segments reorganize, providing a more granular view of high-value vs. low-value clients.

title: AI-based Discourse Analysis System (ADAS) for Improved STEM Education
authors: Gottam, Varun
abstract: In the rapidly evolving fields of Artificial Intelligence and Natural Language Processing, significant opportunities have emerged to transform educational practices. Discourse analysis, particularly in science education, plays a critical role in fostering scientific thinking among students. However, the manual application of tools like the Classroom Discourse Analysis Tool is resource-intensive and impractical on a large scale. This abstract proposes the development of an AI-based Discourse Analysis System tailored for educational settings, designed to automate and enrich the analysis of classroom discourse. Leveraging the latest in Artificial Intelligence and Natural Language Processing, this web-based application will provide teachers nationwide with the ability to upload audio transcripts and receive comprehensive, objective feedback on classroom interactions. This system not only promises to streamline the analysis process but also to offer insights that were previously unattainable due to the manual nature of discourse analysis. By providing interactive tutorials, sophisticated feedback systems, and automated assessments, our solution aims to significantly enhance the efficiency and effectiveness of teaching and learning in science education. Ultimately, this initiative seeks to empower educators with advanced tools to cultivate a scientific mindset among students, thereby shaping the future of education.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Whisper-Powered Classroom Transcription
suggestions.desc: Integrate OpenAI’s Whisper or Deepgram API to provide low-latency, real-time transcription of classroom audio. This allows the ADAS system to analyze teacher-student discourse as it happens, rather than relying on manual uploads of recorded audio files.

suggestions.title: Teacher Inquiry Dashboard and Feedback Loop
suggestions.desc: Create a data visualization dashboard for educators that maps 'Teacher Talk' vs 'Student Talk' and identifies instances of high-level scientific reasoning. The dashboard should provide actionable tips, such as suggesting open-ended questions when the system detects the discourse has become too lecture-heavy.

suggestions.title: Cross-Lingual Discourse Transfer in STEM
suggestions.desc: Evaluate the ADAS model’s performance on non-English STEM classrooms using multilingual LLMs like mBERT or XLM-RoBERTa. Research whether the patterns of scientific discourse (hypothesizing, evidence-based claims) are universal across languages or if the AI requires cultural-specific tuning.

suggestions.title: Corporate Collaborative-Thinking Auditor
suggestions.desc: Adapt the STEM discourse analysis tool for the corporate world to evaluate the quality of technical brainstorming sessions in software engineering teams. The system could identify 'collaborative bottlenecks' or highlight when a team successfully moves from problem-definition to solution-synthesis.

title: DigiMindReady: Enhancing Military Readiness with Edge AI-Driven Wellness, Education, and Digital Discipline through mHealth Innovation.
authors: Hasan, Md Mehedi, Anjum, Nafisa
abstract: Military personnel often need to operate in high-stakes situations. Combating such volatile missions primarily includes control over cognitive overload, reckless mindset, and maintaining concentration amid distractions to sustain operational effectiveness. Military training significantly focuses on human performance, which benefits military readiness. However, the 21st century has introduced unanticipated challenges, such as adverse effects of excessive screen time, external distractions, and over-reliance on technology to the US military, on top of existing issues like anxiety and emotional stability, adversely impacting military readiness and decreasing quality of life. A strategic investigation into these issues and the advancement of effective tools to address these challenges in the military context are called for to improve digital mind readiness. This research introduces DigiMindReady, a mobile health (mHealth) application pioneered to counteract these effects through edge AI-driven personalized features, education, and digital discipline innovations. DigiMindReady proposes a tailored solution that operates entirely offline to ensure security, featuring a collection of functionalities to promote digital wellness, enhance learning, and maintain operational readiness without compromising security. The app's main features include personalized wellness recommendations, a digital education hub, screen time management, and a digital rewards system to encourage healthy digital habits.

suggestions.title: Biofeedback-Driven 'Digital Lockout' for Combat Training
suggestions.desc: Integrate the mHealth app with wearable heart-rate sensors to trigger a 'digital discipline' mode. When stress levels or heart rate variability (HRV) indicate cognitive overload, the system automatically restricts non-essential notifications and forces a tactical breathing exercise before unlocking device features.

suggestions.title: Cognitive Load Scoring for Unit Deployment
suggestions.desc: Build a dashboard for commanding officers that aggregates anonymized Edge AI data to provide a 'readiness score' for a whole unit. Use this to determine which personnel are at peak mental performance versus those suffering from digital fatigue or distraction-induced burnout.

suggestions.title: Federated Learning for Private Military Health Models
suggestions.desc: Implement a Federated Learning framework (using Flower or OpenMined) to train the Edge AI models across multiple devices without ever centralizing sensitive personal data. This allows the wellness algorithms to improve collectively while strictly adhering to military data privacy protocols.

suggestions.title: Quantized Edge Inference for Low-Power Field Devices
suggestions.desc: Optimize the AI models using TensorFlow Lite Quantization or NVIDIA TensorRT to run on ultra-low-power hardware like the Raspberry Pi Zero or specialized military IoT wearables. This ensures the wellness and discipline features remain functional even in environments with no cloud connectivity and limited battery life.

title: An Integrated Architecture For Maintaining Security In Cloud Computing Using Blockchain
authors: Tavva, Namratha; Devara, Ashrith Kumar; Lock, Gar H
abstract: Cloud services are vulnerable to assaults because of their widespread availability. Since cloud computing is still a relatively new service, there is a real risk that sensitive information might be altered while in transit. Because of this, bad actors may gain an edge by manipulating data. Clients using the cloud for a wide range of use cases want to know that their data is reliable and secure. Blockchain, on the other hand, is an immutable digital ledger that may be used with cloud computing to provide an immutable cloudbased data storage and processing system. In this work, we present a method that integrates blockchain technology with cloud computing to guarantee the security of data encrypted using any homomorphic encryption method. The suggested technique uses a distributed network of processing CSPs determined by client needs to circumvent the CSP's ultimate jurisdiction over the data. All CSPs work together to calculate a single, unified hash value for use in their shared database.Bitcoin and Ethereumblockchain networks keep track of master hash values to guarantee the production of immutable data.

suggestions.title: Zero-Knowledge Proof (ZKP) Cloud Integrity Auditor
suggestions.desc: Develop a system where cloud clients can verify that their data is intact using Zero-Knowledge Proofs (like zk-SNARKs). The blockchain stores the proof of integrity, allowing the client to audit the cloud provider without needing to download or expose the actual sensitive content during the verification process.

suggestions.title: Blockchain-Enabled HIPAA Compliance for Medical Cloud Storage
suggestions.desc: Apply the integrated architecture specifically to the healthcare domain. Use the blockchain to create an immutable log of every access request to medical images stored in the cloud, ensuring a tamper-proof audit trail that satisfies strict HIPAA and GDPR regulatory requirements.

suggestions.title: Smart Contract-Based Automated Threat Remediation
suggestions.desc: Build an 'Active Defense' layer using Ethereum or Hyperledger Fabric smart contracts. If the blockchain detects an unauthorized change in a file's hash (indicating a cloud breach), the smart contract automatically triggers a script to revoke that user's access tokens and restore the file from a secure backup.

suggestions.title: Layer-2 Scaling for High-Frequency Cloud Log Management
suggestions.desc: Integrate a Layer-2 scaling solution (like Polygon or a private sidechain) to handle high-frequency cloud access logs. This prevents the main blockchain from becoming a bottleneck, allowing the system to maintain security for high-traffic enterprise cloud applications with minimal latency.

title: Stress Detection by Wearable Devices: Integrating Multimodal Physiological Signals and Machine Learning Techniques
authors: Shedage, Pranita Subhash
abstract: In the medical field, the most common complaint of patients is “stress”. Stress can cause severe effects on the human body. For example, prolonged mental stress can cause serious health issues in long termsuch as hypertension, cardiovascular diseases, increased susceptibility to infections, and depression. These health issues can be prevented by early detection of stress and by taking preventive measures. The most common detecting stress was determined from the questionnaires or from the interactive sessions conducted to assess the people's affective state. However, this method is not highly reliable and can be biased depending on the person who is conducting the session. To address this is

suggestions.title: Predictive Burnout Dashboard for High-Stress Occupations
suggestions.desc: Expand the ML model into a B2B platform for hospitals or air traffic control centers. By aggregating longitudinal data from wearables (Apple Watch/Fitbit APIs), the system can predict peak burnout periods for staff and suggest proactive schedule adjustments before medical errors occur.

suggestions.title: Haptic Biofeedback Loop for Anxiety Management
suggestions.desc: Create a mobile app that connects to the wearable and, upon detecting a stress spike via the ML model, triggers a subtle haptic 'rhythm' on the user's wrist. This rhythm guides the user through box-breathing techniques in real-time, effectively closing the loop between detection and prevention.

suggestions.title: Cross-Modality Transfer Learning for Sensor Fusion
suggestions.desc: Experiment with transfer learning by training on public datasets like WESAD (Wearable Stress and Affect Detection) and fine-tuning the model for consumer-grade sensors. Compare the accuracy of PPG (heart rate) versus EDA (skin conductance) to determine which modality is most resilient to motion artifacts during daily activity.

suggestions.title: Real-time Signal De-noising Pipeline using Wavelet Transforms
suggestions.desc: Implement a robust preprocessing library in C++ or Python that uses Discrete Wavelet Transforms (DWT) to filter out movement noise from multimodal physiological signals. This technical improvement ensures the ML model remains accurate even when the user is walking or typing, rather than just sitting still.

title: Effect Of Noise And Topologies On Multi-Photon Quantum Protocols
authors: Jha, Nitin
abstract: Quantum-augmented networks aim to use quantum phenomena to improve detection and protection against malicious actors in a classical communication network. This may include multiplexing quantum signals into classical fiber optical channels and incorporating purely quantum links alongside classical links in the network. In such hybrid networks, quantum protocols based on single photons become a bottleneck for transmission distances and data speeds, thereby reducing entire network performance. Furthermore, many of the security assumptions of the single-photon protocols do not hold up in practice because of the impossibility of manufacturing single-photon emitters. Multi-photon quantum protocols, on the other hand, are designed to operate under practical assumptions and do not require single photon emitters. As a result, they provide higher levels of security guarantees and longer transmission distances. However, the effect of channel and device noise on multiphoton protocols in terms of security, transmission distances, and bit rates has not been investigated. In this paper, we focus on channel noise and present our observations on the effect of various types of noise on multi-photon protocols. We also investigate the effect of topologies such as ring, star, and torus on the noise characteristics of the multi-photon protocols. Our results show the possible advantages of switching to multi-photon protocols and give insights into the repeater placement and topology choice for quantum-augmented networks.
supervisor: Dr. Abhishek Parakh, Dr. Mahadevan Subramaniam

suggestions.title: Quantum Network Planning & ROI Simulator
suggestions.desc: Develop a desktop application that allows network architects to upload their existing classical fiber topology (JSON/GeoJSON). The tool simulates the performance gain and cost-benefit analysis of adding multi-photon quantum links, accounting for specific regional noise profiles and transmission distances.

suggestions.title: Noise-Resilient Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) for IoT
suggestions.desc: Adapt the multi-photon protocols to secure a simulated smart-city IoT network. Build a prototype that demonstrates how multi-photon states can maintain a secure key exchange even over noisy, shared classical-quantum fiber lines, protecting critical infrastructure like power grids from eavesdropping.

suggestions.title: Reinforcement Learning for Dynamic Quantum Routing
suggestions.desc: Train a Deep Q-Network (DQN) agent to dynamically route quantum packets through a multi-topology network. The agent should learn to avoid high-noise links and prioritize paths that maintain photon coherence, effectively maximizing the entire hybrid network's throughput and fidelity.

suggestions.title: NS-3 Extension for Multi-Photon Interference Modeling
suggestions.desc: Extend the popular NS-3 network simulator with a specialized module for multi-photon interference and decoherence. This tool would allow researchers to test quantum protocols against realistic classical network traffic noise without needing expensive physical quantum hardware.

legacy:GR-13|Fall 2016|optimisation of object searching techniques using visual c++

title: Attention Driven Framework for Detecting Mental Illness Causes from Social Media
authors: Azmee, Abm Adnan; Attota, Dinesh Chowdary; Nweke, Francis E
abstract: Mental health is a critical aspect of our overall well-being. Mental illness refers to conditions that impact an individual's psychological state, resulting in considerable distress, and limitations in functioning day-to-day tasks. Due to the progress of technology, social media has merged as the platform, for individuals to share their thoughts and emotions. The psychological state of individuals can be accessed with the help of data from these platforms. However, it is challenging for conventional machine learning models to analyze the diverse linguistic contexts of social media data. In this work, we propose a novel attention-driven deep framework to overcome these challenges. Our proposed framework utilizes multi-level (word, sentence, and document) data to identify the causes behind mental illness. The efficacy and effectiveness of our proposed model are shown by extensive evaluation on Reddit data. The insights from this research deepen our understanding of different factors behind mental illness and would aid mental health professionals in formulating effective interventions.
supervisor: Dr. Md Abdullah Al Hafiz Khan, Dr. Yong Pei

suggestions.title: Early-Warning Content Moderation API
suggestions.desc: Package the attention-driven framework into a REST API for social media platforms. The tool would analyze user-generated content in real-time to flag 'at-risk' behavior for human moderators, providing them with the specific 'attention weights' (reasons) to help them connect users with mental health support.

suggestions.title: Temporal Mood-Shift Tracking for Longitudinal Analysis
suggestions.desc: Extend the framework to analyze a user's post history over months rather than isolated posts. Build a visualization tool that maps how specific causes (e.g., job loss, social isolation) correlate with the progression of mental illness symptoms over time, helping clinicians understand the 'trigger-to-symptom' timeline.

suggestions.title: Vision-Text Attention Fusion for Multi-Modal Detection
suggestions.desc: Incorporate image analysis (using Vision Transformers) to analyze the photos a user posts alongside their text. The model could then identify discrepancies or reinforcements (e.g., dark imagery paired with isolating language) to increase the accuracy of mental illness cause detection.

suggestions.title: LLM-Augmented Synthetic Dataset Generator
suggestions.desc: Use Large Language Models (LLMs) like Llama-3 to generate high-quality, synthetic social media posts for rare or underrepresented mental health conditions. This will help balance the training dataset and improve the attention framework's ability to recognize less common psychological distress markers.

title: Case Exploration: Automatic Keyword Matching Framework for Behavioral Health
authors: Nweke, Francis E; Azmee, Abm Adnan
abstract: In this demonstration, we propose a framework for exploring, identifying, and matching repeated behavioral health keywords in first-responder reports to the current set of Behavioral Health Index Terms provided by subject matter experts (SMEs). The tool incorporates behavioral health-related keywords and has a Graphical User Interface (GUI) that allows non-technical users to explore and analyze 911 first-responder reports. We utilized an inverted index, best-matching (BM25), and plain-text searching algorithms to match keywords in first-responder reports. This tool provides a comprehensive approach to report analysis by identifying indicators of mental health disorders and taking into account the assessments of humanities and social science professionals
supervisor: Dr. Md Abdullah Al Hafiz Khan

suggestions.title: 911 Dispatcher Real-Time Transcription Overlay
suggestions.desc: Integrate the framework with a live speech-to-text API (like Deepgram). Build an overlay for emergency dispatchers that highlights behavioral health keywords in real-time as they talk to a caller, automatically suggesting relevant Crisis Intervention Team (CIT) protocols based on the matched Index Terms.

suggestions.title: Predictive Behavioral Health Hotspot Mapping
suggestions.desc: Use the matched keyword data to generate a geospatial heatmap of behavioral health calls across a city. By correlating this with local resource availability, city planners can identify 'resource deserts' where first responders are frequently encountering mental health crises but have no nearby clinics for referral.

suggestions.title: Semantic Expansion via Transformer-Based Embeddings
suggestions.desc: Upgrade the BM25 search logic with semantic embeddings (using Sentence-BERT). This allows the framework to match reports based on 'meaning' rather than just exact keywords, catching nuanced descriptions of behavior that may not use the exact SME-provided terms but describe the same clinical symptoms.

suggestions.title: Automated SME Feedback Loop for Index Refinement
suggestions.desc: Build a 'Human-in-the-Loop' interface where subject matter experts can review 'near-miss' keyword matches. The system uses their corrections to fine-tune the inverted index weights automatically, ensuring the tool evolves as behavioral health terminology and field slang change over time.

title: Adaptive Attention Aware Fusion for Human-in-Loop Behavioral Health Detection
authors: Brown, Martin; Azmee, Abm Adnan
abstract: Identifying behavioral health is paramount for law enforcement officers to provide appropriate follow-up community care. In the current practice, law enforcement offices manually identify these behavioral health cases to allow the designation of the relevant follow-up resources. In this work, we develop a tool to automatically detect behavioral health cases from police public narrative reports by identifying behavioral health indicator signals. We propose a novel adaptive attention-aware fusion model for detecting behavioral health signals in sensitive police reports. Our model leverages contextual and semantic information from the reports and relevant behavioral health cues as keywords from a pre-trained attention-weighted keyword-based model. Our model also employs label self-attention mechanisms to correlate label embeddings with the report and keyword representations. Furthermore, we propose a novel clustering-based uncertainty-enabled informative sampling query strategy to integrate humans-in-the-loop in the active learning framework to reduce required annotation from experts. This querying strategy selects the most informative and diverse samples for expert annotation. Our experimental results showed that the proposed model outperforms state-of-the-art classifiers on a dataset of 300 manually annotated ground truth police reports, achieving an accuracy of 87.58% and an F1-score of 85.67%. Applying our querying strategy to our proposed model increased the detection of behavioral health, achieving an accuracy of 92% and an F1- score of 91.1%. Also, our proposed model achieves an accuracy score of 93.75% and an F1-score of 93.61% on unseen samples. Lastly, our proposed model demonstrates its interpretability by extracting the keywords associated with each behavioral health category.
supervisor: Dr. Md Abdullah Al Hafiz Khan, Dr. Dominic Thomas.

suggestions.title: Officer Support & Resource Referral Mobile App
suggestions.desc: Create a mobile companion app for officers on patrol. When a report is submitted, the Fusion model flags behavioral health indicators and instantly provides the officer with a list of the 3 closest community care facilities and their current intake availability, streamlining the referral process.

suggestions.title: Cross-Agency Behavioral Case Correlation
suggestions.desc: Extend the tool to analyze reports across multiple agencies (Police, Fire, EMS). By fusing data from different first responders about the same individual, the model can build a more complete picture of a person's behavioral health history, leading to better-informed long-term care plans.

suggestions.title: Explainable Fusion (X-Fusion) for Judicial Transparency
suggestions.desc: Implement LIME or SHAP to provide 'explainability reports' for every flagged case. This technical addition ensures that if a model's output is used in a legal or medical context, the specific textual evidence (indicator signals) that led to the detection is clearly highlighted for human verification.

suggestions.title: OCR-Resilient NLP Preprocessing for Handwritten Reports
suggestions.desc: Develop a specialized preprocessing pipeline that handles the common noise found in digitized police reports (e.g., OCR errors, messy handwriting, police jargon). Use a custom-trained 'correction' transformer to clean the text before it reaches the fusion model, significantly increasing detection accuracy on legacy data.

title: Personalized Pedagogy through a LLM-based Recommender System
authors: Kunuku, Mourya Teja; Yadla, Bharath Y
abstract: The educational domain is undergoing transformation due to the incorporation of Artificial Intelligence (AI), Large Language Models (LLMs), and generative AI technologies, raising the need for educators to integrate cutting-edge technological advancements and methodologies into their teaching approaches. Pedagogical Design Patterns (PDPs) have become prominent for their role in sharing effective educational practices and narrowing the divide between academic research and actual teaching methods. Despite their potential, the lack of widely accessible resources and the scattered nature of publishing outlets pose significant barriers to the broad application of PDPS. To address this issue, we propose the application of large language models to recommend educational strategies based on existing PDPs. Our approach employs a locally sourced knowledge database and the Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) framework to formulate context for LLM queries. Preliminary results are encouraging, demonstrating an accuracy rate of 0.83 and a strong relevance of the recommended pedagogical practices to the posed queries. This paper details the initial outcomes of our project, which paves the way for further refinement of the model. The goal is to equip new educators with seasoned insights, to enhance their instructional methods.

suggestions.title: AI-Powered Course Syllabus Optimizer
suggestions.desc: Build a web tool where educators upload their existing syllabus. The LLM-based recommender analyzes the curriculum and suggests specific Pedagogical Design Patterns (PDPs) to improve student engagement, such as 'Flipped Classroom' or 'Peer Review' patterns, based on the course's subject matter.

suggestions.title: Personalized 'Scaffolding' Generator for K-12 Students
suggestions.desc: Create a student-facing interface that uses the recommender system to provide personalized 'hints' or learning paths. If a student struggles with a specific concept, the system recommends a PDP-based mini-lesson tailored to their unique learning style and previous mistakes.

suggestions.title: Evaluating LLM Bias in Pedagogical Suggestions
suggestions.desc: Conduct a research study using the project framework to see if LLMs recommend different pedagogical patterns based on demographic markers in student data. Develop a 'Fair-Pedagogy' wrapper that ensures recommendations are equitable and don't default to biased teaching methodologies.

suggestions.title: RAG-Enhanced Pattern Discovery Library
suggestions.desc: Implement Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) by vectorizing a massive database of educational research papers. This allows the recommender to not only suggest a PDP but also provide the actual academic citations and 'evidence of effectiveness' to the educator in real-time.

title: Revolutionizing Reflective Learning in Higher Education: An LLM-Based Analytical Approach
authors: Yadla, Bharath Y, Kunuku, Mourya Teja
abstract: This project introduces a novel LLM-based system to automate The analysis of student reflections, enhancing reflective learning in higher education. Leveraging advanced ML and NLP technologies, the system provides personalized, in-depth feedback by identifying learning outcomes and challenges. Employing the OpenAI API and LangChain framework, it offers a nuanced understanding of student learning trajectories. The methodology involves collecting data via the Minute Paper technique, enabling targeted instructional adjustments. Preliminary results indicate a significant improvement in analyzing and addressing students' educational needs.

suggestions.title: Dynamic Learning Trajectory Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Build a React-based dashboard for instructors that visualizes the 'Minute Paper' data over a full semester. Use the LLM output to map student growth across different learning outcomes, identifying specific weeks where the class as a whole struggled with a concept.

suggestions.title: Automated 'At-Risk' Intervention System
suggestions.desc: Configure the system to flag students whose reflections consistently show high 'confusion' scores or lack of engagement. Integrate with email APIs to automatically send a personalized, supportive check-in or additional resources to the student before they fall behind.

suggestions.title: Socratic Reflection Chatbot
suggestions.desc: Instead of just analyzing reflections, build a chatbot interface using LangChain that interacts with students during the reflection process. If a student's 'Minute Paper' is too brief, the bot asks targeted Socratic questions to prompt deeper critical thinking based on the course's learning goals.

suggestions.title: Multi-Lingual Sentiment & Bias Analysis
suggestions.desc: Expand the LLM pipeline to analyze reflections from ESL (English as a Second Language) students by accounting for cultural nuances in sentiment. Research whether the LLM exhibits bias in evaluating reflections based on writing style and implement a debiasing layer to ensure equitable feedback.

title: Electric Vehicle Team
authors: Solon, Brandon A, Klein, Austin D, McMaster, Caleb
abstract: The KSU Electric Vehicle Team is developing a fully autonomous electric go-kart to compete in the Autonomous Karting Series (AKS). Our team will be making two programs for the kart’s software stack. These programs include a race line optimizer, which can take the centerline of a track and generate a minimum curvature path for it to follow to get around the track faster, as well as a race controller which can switch navigation algorithms automatically based on the current conditions of the race.

suggestions.title: Lidar-Based SLAM Migration
suggestions.desc: Move from centerline-based navigation to full SLAM (Simultaneous Localization and Mapping) using a 2D/3D LiDAR sensor. This allows the kart to map unknown tracks on-the-fly rather than relying on pre-defined centerlines, making it adaptable to any racing environment.

suggestions.title: Adversarial Multi-Kart Racing Logic
suggestions.desc: Implement a 'Game Theory' layer in the race controller to handle wheel-to-wheel racing. Develop algorithms for defensive positioning and overtaking based on the detected positions of other karts, transforming the system from a solo time-trial bot into a competitive racer.

suggestions.title: Digital Twin Optimization in CARLA
suggestions.desc: Build a high-fidelity digital twin of the go-kart and the KSU track in the CARLA simulator. Use Reinforcement Learning to train the race line optimizer over millions of simulated laps, then transfer the learned weights to the physical hardware to see if it outperforms the curvature-based model.

suggestions.title: Predictive Battery and Thermal Management
suggestions.desc: Integrate telemetry data from the motors and battery pack into the race controller. Use a regression model to predict thermal throttling and adjust the 'aggression' of the race line/acceleration in real-time to ensure the kart finishes the race without overheating.

title: Website Hardening and Ethical Hacking
authors: Liu, Alex L, Pandya, Samrat H, Sanchez, Kennedy A, Frontin, Kenny M, Wogomebu, Komlan I
abstract: This project is to showcase a real life scenario of securing a theoretical business website on Red Hat Linux, Apache, MariaDB, and PHP hosted in a virtual machine. The project objective is for a team to research ways to secure the theoretical business website, develop and implement security policies, and performa red/blue team exercise. This project is a way for a team to exercise ethical hacking in a closed environment to obtain experience.

suggestions.title: Automated Hardening with Ansible Playbooks
suggestions.desc: Transform the manual security policies into Infrastructure as Code (IaC) using Ansible to automatically configure RHEL, Apache, and MariaDB. This allows for repeatable, one-click hardening of new server instances that automatically remediates configuration drift.

suggestions.title: ELK Stack Security Monitoring Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Deploy an Elasticsearch, Logstash, and Kibana (ELK) pipeline to ingest Apache and MariaDB logs. Build a real-time security dashboard that visualizes failed login attempts, SQL injection patterns, and unauthorized file access to move from static hardening to active monitoring.

suggestions.title: WAF Evasion and Benchmarking
suggestions.desc: Implement ModSecurity (Web Application Firewall) with the OWASP Core Rule Set and conduct a research study on its effectiveness. Use automated tools like Nikto and Burp Suite to measure how different rule configurations impact both security posture and server latency.

suggestions.title: Zero-Trust PHP Environment Implementation
suggestions.desc: Restrict the PHP execution environment by implementing SELinux policies and AppArmor profiles specific to the application's needs. This limits the blast radius of a successful exploit by preventing the web server from accessing unauthorized system directories or executing shell commands.

title: Web Hardening
authors: Brown, Christopher, Jackson, Spencer J, Kigo, Lynder, Hooper, Gabrielle
abstract: Our project focuses on ethical hacking and defending in the form of a red/blue team. Our project was broken into 3 phases. In Phase one we were given a server stack and told to do what we could in order to analyze weak points. In phase 2 we were told to bolster the defenses of those weak points. Lastly, in phase 3 were we given an IP address of an opposing team to attack while defending against another teams advances.

suggestions.title: Container-Level Hardening with Alpine and Docker
suggestions.desc: Migrate the server stack into microservices using Docker and Alpine Linux to minimize the attack surface. Implement container-specific security measures like read-only file systems, resource limits, and automated vulnerability scanning using tools like Trivy or Grype.

suggestions.title: Custom Red-Team Scripting with Python-Nmap
suggestions.desc: Develop a suite of custom Python scripts utilizing the Nmap and Metasploit APIs to automate the Phase 3 attack vectors. Create a 'Chaos Monkey' style tool that randomly tests specific vulnerabilities in the stack to ensure the Blue Team's defenses are truly resilient.

suggestions.title: CTF Scoring Engine Integration
suggestions.desc: Build a centralized scoring engine that tracks server uptime and successful 'flag' captures during the red/blue exercise. Use a framework like CTFd to provide real-time feedback and metrics on defensive performance versus offensive success.

suggestions.title: Decoy Infrastructure and HoneyPot Deployment
suggestions.desc: Extend the network defense by deploying Cowrie or Honeypot-as-a-Service instances within the IP range. Use these decoys to distract attackers from the real server stack while logging their techniques, tools, and procedures (TTPs) for better defensive intelligence.

title: I Spy... Water Safety
authors: Robertson, Caden M, Winters, Danny, Jaquez, Denice, Brown, Tahj, L'Hernault, Maksims D
abstract: I Spy Water Safety is a game that teaches people about the importance of water safety around a lake. Our goal is to teach people proper water safety etiquette and lower the amount of water-related incidents.
supervisor: Dr. Yan Huang - SWE Capstone Professor; George McBroom - Sponsor

suggestions.title: Physics-Based Drowning Simulation
suggestions.desc: Enhance the game's educational impact by integrating realistic water physics using the Unity Water System or Unreal FluidFlux. Create interactive scenarios where players must calculate buoyancy or the 'struggle time' of a swimmer to visually demonstrate the speed and silence of actual drowning.

suggestions.title: Immersive VR Hazard Identification
suggestions.desc: Port the 'I Spy' concept to a Virtual Reality (VR) environment using Meta Quest or OpenXR. This allows users to physically turn their heads to scan the horizon and identify dangers like rip currents or hidden underwater obstacles in a 360-degree high-stakes simulation.

suggestions.title: Predictive Heatmapping for Safety Education
suggestions.desc: Implement a backend telemetry system to track where players look and what they miss in the 'I Spy' game. Analyze this data to generate 'Danger Heatmaps' that identify common human errors in water surveillance, providing researchers with insights into visual blind spots.

suggestions.title: Multiplayer Collaborative Rescue Mode
suggestions.desc: Introduce a multiplayer component where one player acts as the 'spotter' and another as the 'rescuer.' This teaches the vital communication chain (Reach, Throw, Row, Go) and emphasizes that water safety is a team responsibility.

title: Developing Support for DICOM medical Images
authors: Grogan, Cassidie G
abstract: DICOM (Digital Imaging and Communications in Medicine) is the standard for storing and sharing medical image information. GIMP (GNU Image Manipulation Program) is the leading open-source program for processing professional and scientific images; however, it is currently unable to open many modern DICOM images. The project goal is to update GIMP's DICOM import plugin with code to support all types of DICOM images. After creating a C++ wrapper to incorporate the GDCM (Grassroots DICOM) library into the existing software, GIMP could import images that previously caused errors. The updated plugin has been submitted as a merge request and is currently being reviewed by the developers for the next software release. The next step would be to expand GIMP's DICOM metadata and display multi-frame images to continue to better support medical professionals and researchers.

suggestions.title: Automated PHI De-identification Plugin
suggestions.desc: Add a feature to the GIMP C++ wrapper that automatically detects and redacts Protected Health Information (PHI) from DICOM headers using the GDCM anonymizer classes before export.

suggestions.title: AI-Assisted Organ Segmentation Brushes
suggestions.desc: Integrate a pre-trained U-Net model into the GIMP plugin to provide 'Smart Selection' tools that snap to the boundaries of tumors or organs in 16-bit medical images.

suggestions.title: DICOM-to-3D-Print Mesh Generator
suggestions.desc: Extend the plugin to export DICOM slices as STL or OBJ files by implementing a marching cubes algorithm, allowing doctors to go from GIMP editing directly to 3D printing bone models.

suggestions.title: Multi-Frame Cine-Loop Animation
suggestions.desc: Update the GDCM wrapper to support multi-frame DICOM files, mapping time-series medical data (like ultrasound loops) directly to GIMP layers for frame-by-frame analysis.

title: AI Limitations for Web Development
authors: LaLicata, Jacob J, Garske, Josh, Rowlinson, Thomas, Madrigal, Matthew, Adewumi, Muyiwa E
abstract: This research project delves into the exploratory journey of using an AI (Artificial Intelligence), specifically ChatGPT, to assist in developing an auction website. Highlighting the iterative process of problem identification, solution finding, and implementation during development, this project aims to furnish insights into leveraging AI capabilities while addressing its limitations. Through this, developers and AI enthusiasts can gain a comprehensive understanding of effective collaboration with AI, addressing common pitfalls, and devising solutions during software development.

suggestions.title: LLM Performance Benchmarking Suite
suggestions.desc: Conduct a comparative study between GPT-4o, Claude 3.5 Sonnet, and Llama 3 for specific auction-related tasks like real-time bidding logic and database schema generation. Quantify 'hallucination rates' and code efficiency to create a guide for the best AI tool for specific web dev stages.

suggestions.title: RAG-Powered Auction Developer Assistant
suggestions.desc: Build a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system using LangChain that is 'fed' the auction site's codebase and documentation. This specialized assistant can then provide context-aware debugging and feature suggestions that are much more accurate than a general-purpose AI.

suggestions.title: AI-Driven Fraud and Bid-Shilling Detection
suggestions.desc: Implement a machine learning module that analyzes bidding patterns for suspicious activity, such as bid-shilling or bot behavior. Use AI to create a 'Trust Score' for auction participants based on historical data and real-time interaction patterns.

suggestions.title: Automated AI Security Auditing
suggestions.desc: Develop a pipeline that uses AI to perform automated penetration testing on the auction site's PHP/JavaScript code. Focus on using LLMs to identify edge cases in the bidding logic that could lead to race conditions or unauthorized price manipulation.

title: Cybersecurity Park
authors: Leung, Michael P, Masters, Kalil R, Deason, Robert, Skoonberg, Ian C, Gohlke, Aiden D
abstract: We are presenting two additional modules to the Cyber Security Park, a long-running game project that is part of the realities lab. Whack-a-Malware is an arcade game where the player has to whack various malware, each with unique effects that mimic real-world malware. This includes Adware, Spyware, Ransomware, Computer Worms, and Trojan Horses. The player is equipped with two hammers that instantly destroy malware. But it goes on a three-second cooldown every time they destroy something. The player can reduce the cooldown by destroying adware as they are the main cause of slowing down the computer. Digital Footprints Private Investigator is a complete rework of an existing module. The player will be tasked by their client to find the perpetrator who has been anonymously intimidating them online and in person. The Player will have to explore the city to find digital footprint clues that will provide alibis and evidence against the suspects in the case.

suggestions.title: Multiplayer Malware Outbreak Mode
suggestions.desc: Transform Whack-a-Malware into a 1v1 competitive mode where one player 'infects' the system with specific payloads while the other uses hammers to defend critical system services.

suggestions.title: Spear-Phishing VR Office Simulator
suggestions.desc: Develop a new module in Godot for VR where players must walk through a virtual office and identify physical and digital security risks, such as suspicious USB drives or unlocked terminals.

suggestions.title: Cuckoo Sandbox Visualization Bridge
suggestions.desc: Connect the game's malware effects to a backend Cuckoo Sandbox, so when a player 'misses' a malware, the game displays the actual file-system changes recorded from a real malware sample.

suggestions.title: RL-Driven Difficulty Scaling
suggestions.desc: Implement a Reinforcement Learning agent that monitors player performance and dynamically evolves the malware's movement patterns and spawn rates to maximize training retention.

title: CCSE Reservation Application
authors: Bunn, Brandon R, Kafka, Ryan, Levieva, Dina, Ovell, Alyn, Venuti, Olivia C
abstract: This CCSE Reservation Application project is focused on creating a unified web application to simplify the reservation process for inventory devices and room time slots for faculty and staff at Kennesaw State University. This initiative combines two separate reservation systems into a single solution, encompassing a database, backend, and frontend. The database will store relevant information, the backend will handle data communication, and the frontend will provide a user-friendly interface for making reservations. Administrators will have access to an admin view for managing requests. The project has faced challenges, including resource constraints and team coordination issues. While the integration of the university's login system remains pending at the time of submission, the application is on track to deliver a functioning reservation system for devices and rooms.
supervisor: Course instructor: Prof. Donald Privitera; Project sponsor: Prof. Christine Bryant

suggestions.title: QR-Code Physical Access Integration
suggestions.desc: Develop a React Native mobile module that generates dynamic QR codes for reservations, which can be scanned at KSU room doors to verify identity and unlock electronic strikes.

suggestions.title: Microsoft Graph API Bi-directional Sync
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Microsoft Graph API to synchronize the application's inventory and room bookings directly with faculty Outlook calendars and CCSE departmental schedules.

suggestions.title: IoT Real-time Occupancy Heatmap
suggestions.desc: Deploy ESP32-based PIR sensors in the reserved rooms and feed the data via MQTT to the dashboard to show 'ghost bookings' where a room is reserved but unoccupied.

suggestions.title: Predictive Equipment Maintenance Analytics
suggestions.desc: Build a Python-based analysis engine that uses historical reservation duration data to predict when high-wear items (like laptops or VR headsets) are due for hardware servicing.

title: Finding the Limits of AI for Web Development in 2023
authors: Layson, Christopher S, Kagika, Njagi, Bush-Cunningham, Christian, Lawal, Ismail O, Nash, Adam Z
abstract: Our team was tasked with finding the limits of artificial intelligence for web development in 2023. This involved our team researching what the different parts of a website are, how to prompt an AI chatbot to provide us with source code, and how to put together a working prototype of an auction website by the end of our project. Our team produced various documents along the way that show our progress such as various slideshow files, documentation word docs, and a research report on our findings. After working with the AI to produce source code for our website, we have come to realize that an AI is helpful for making general outlines but, starts to have diminishing returns if one tries to get it to produce an entire website. Making general outlines can be quick but, you must be very specific in your prompting to get fully usable code that requires no modification. With this being the case, we believe that AI should be used as a sort of co-pilot when it comes to web development in 2023.

suggestions.title: Cross-LLM Code Generation Benchmarking
suggestions.desc: Perform a comparative study between ChatGPT-4, Claude 3, and Gemini on their ability to generate secure, concurrent bidding logic for the auction platform, documenting error rates in each.

suggestions.title: Chain-of-Thought Prompt Optimizer
suggestions.desc: Build a CLI tool that takes a high-level web requirement and automatically decomposes it into a series of structured prompts to minimize CSS/JS hallucinations in the generated site.

suggestions.title: Self-Healing UI Debugging Agent
suggestions.desc: Create a wrapper that monitors the auction site's console logs and automatically sends error messages back to an LLM API to generate and apply hotfix patches in the development environment.

suggestions.title: AI-Personalized Component Generation
suggestions.desc: Implement a system where the auction site's frontend theme is dynamically generated and applied at runtime based on an LLM's interpretation of a user's accessibility needs.

title: Finding the Limits of AI for Web Development in 2023
authors: Chavez, Jessica, Chastine, Jackson A, Eve, Joel R, Khan, Danish, Garcia, Alexandre D
abstract: This Project explores the limits of artificial intelligence (AI) in web development, focusing on the year 2023. The study is conducted by AI Limits Team 1 from Kennesaw State University. The primary objective of the project is to harness the potential of ChatGPT 3.5, an advanced AI model, to create a fully functional Auction House Website. The achievements of the project include innovative web development, AI-generated content, and successful integration of AI into both frontend and backend aspects of web development. The research findings offer valuable insights into ChatGPT's proficiency in generating web application code and emphasize the importance of validation and testing in AI-driven development. Ethical considerations in AI-generated content are highlighted as well.

suggestions.title: Autonomous Playwright Test Generation
suggestions.desc: Use AI agents to analyze the auction site's DOM and automatically generate a comprehensive suite of Playwright end-to-end tests for critical paths like bidding and payment.

suggestions.title: ElevenLabs Voice Auctioneer Integration
suggestions.desc: Integrate a real-time 'Voice Auctioneer' using ElevenLabs API and GPT-4 to provide live, synthesized commentary on bidding wars and item descriptions to increase user engagement.

suggestions.title: AI-Driven Shorter-Bid Fraud Detection
suggestions.desc: Develop a backend module using Scikit-learn to analyze the AI-generated auction logs for signs of shill bidding or automated bot clusters that might exploit the generated logic.

suggestions.title: Vector-Based Semantic Item Search
suggestions.desc: Replace the standard text search with a vector database like Pinecone, using OpenAI embeddings to allow users to find auction items based on 'vibe' or conceptual similarity.
