title: Resilient Infrastructures: Enhancing Akwaaba's Cyber Defenses
authors: McKee, Christian, Begum, Mateen, Diaz , Marisa G, Nguyen, Kelvin, Patel, Shreya
abstract: The project focuses on securing the server infrastructure of Akwaaba, a Caribbean-inspired steakhouse chain with locations in New York City, Atlanta, and Los Angeles. It involves assessing risks, implementing mitigation strategies, and establishing access policies for the on-premise servers. The student team will analyze the Red Hat Linux-based Virtual Machine hosting the WordPress website, which uses PHP, MariaDB, and Apache. Their objective is to prevent intrusions and data breaches that could compromise customer and business data. Furthermore, the team will perform Red and Blue team penetration testing to evaluate security measures and simulate potential attacks. They will incorporate the latest industry best practices and deliver a research paper and video presentations as project outcomes.

suggestions.title: CI/CD Vulnerability Shield
suggestions.desc: Implement a Jenkins-based CI/CD pipeline for the WordPress site that automatically runs OWASP ZAP and SonarQube scans on every code change before it reaches the Red Hat server.

suggestions.title: Akwaaba Decoy Honeypot Network
suggestions.desc: Deploy a Cowrie honeypot on a parallel VM disguised as the MariaDB database to capture and log attacker credentials and lateral movement techniques for forensic analysis.

suggestions.title: Zero-Trust Tailscale Architecture
suggestions.desc: Replace traditional firewall rules with a Zero-Trust network using Tailscale, ensuring that only authenticated devices from specific NYC, ATL, and LA locations can access the server management ports.

suggestions.title: Immutable Log Auditing via Blockchain
suggestions.desc: Develop a microservice that hashes the Apache and MariaDB access logs and commits them to a private Ethereum sidechain to prevent intruders from 'clearing their tracks' after a breach.

title: BIOMIMETIC REMOTE-CONTROLLED VEHICLE
authors: Ridley, Jonathan, Brown, Kian
abstract: The goal of this project is smoothly integrating instinctual concepts of control into devices beyond the body. It is essentially an attempt to extend the body without any complex prior training. To do this, we have developed a both a Bluetooth connection between hand movements and the motors of a multifaceted vehicle. Furthermore, the hand movements will be tracked using both an accelerometer and gyroscope found in the common hobbyist tool Arduino nano. Logging this data and processing it through the Bluetooth communication system, the intention is to provide real-time updates to the vehicle’s motors that ultimately sync the intentions of a user and its movement.

suggestions.title: Haptic Feedback Integration via Flex Sensors
suggestions.desc: Enhance the biomimetic experience by adding flex sensors to the user's fingers and haptic vibration motors to the controller. This would allow the vehicle's grippers to mirror finger contraction while providing tactile resistance feedback when the vehicle encounters obstacles.

suggestions.title: Kinematic Mapping for Multi-Jointed Appendages
suggestions.desc: Research and implement Inverse Kinematics (IK) to translate complex hand gestures into 6-DOF (degree of freedom) robotic arm movements. This moves beyond simple motor control to precise spatial manipulation using the Arduino Nano’s IMU data.

suggestions.title: Low-Latency Cloud Teleoperation Gateway
suggestions.desc: Build a specialized MQTT-based cloud gateway to reduce latency for remote operation over cellular networks. This would allow the vehicle to be controlled from a different city, using a web-based dashboard to visualize the real-time accelerometer/gyroscope telemetry.

suggestions.title: Assistive Mobility Interface for Rehabilitation
suggestions.desc: Adapt the gesture-control system as a low-cost interface for electric wheelchairs. By mapping subtle hand tilts to wheelchair directional inputs, you can create an accessibility tool for users with limited fine motor skills who struggle with traditional joysticks.

title: Spectrum Analysis CLI Tool
authors: Santos, Vitor B, Redden, Trey, Corella, Sam, Flores Santos, Christopher, Glennon, Jonathan
abstract: The Spectrum Analysis CLI Tool takes in .mp4 recordings of a Spectrum Analyzer, converts them programmatically into values the application can understand and outputs this data into a .csv file. This file can be parsed/filtered by the user with commands during upload of the .mp4 recording, or anytime after the recording has been processed.

suggestions.title: Real-Time OpenCV Video Stream Parsing
suggestions.desc: Evolutionize the tool from batch .mp4 processing to real-time analysis by integrating OpenCV to capture live RTSP streams from networked Spectrum Analyzers. This allows for 'live' CSV generation and immediate alerting based on signal thresholds.

suggestions.title: RF Anomaly Detection Engine
suggestions.desc: Apply Unsupervised Machine Learning (such as Isolation Forests) to the generated CSV data to automatically detect frequency hopping or unauthorized signal interference. The tool could highlight specific timestamps in the original video where 'weird' signal behavior occurred.

suggestions.title: Full-Stack Signal Visualization Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Build a React-based web interface that uses D3.js to render interactive heatmaps and waterfalls from the parsed CSV data. This would allow engineers to zoom into specific frequency ranges and compare historical captures side-by-side.

suggestions.title: Cross-Instrument OCR Extender
suggestions.desc: Generalize the computer vision logic to support other legacy lab equipment, such as Oscilloscopes or Vector Network Analyzers. By using Tesseract OCR and custom image masking, you can digitize data from any screen that lacks a modern digital export feature.

title: LotSpotter
authors: Yankah, Julian, Pham, Henry, Greene, Tripp, Perry, Jonathan, Dongbou, Ghislain
abstract: The parking issue has quietly become the cause of a lot of stress for travelers and other regular users. It's nothing new that some people miss their flight and/or get late to other important meetings and appointments because they couldn’t locate an available parking lot. Not because there isn't available parking but because they don’t know where it is! What if there was some way to solve that? Introducing LotSpotter! An application built to detect and navigate to vacant parking spaces across the United States. It will leverage various technologies, including image processing, sensors, AI and mobile app development, to achieve its goal with frameworks such as OpenCV and processes from Amazon Web Services such as DynamoDB. Additionally, it will all be run through RaspberryPi to take advantage of GPS, and camera functionality! Users will be able to create accounts, reserve spaces, and much more. The days of being restricted by the struggles of metropolis are no more! LotSpotter is here!
supervisor: Dr. Yan Huang - SWE Capstone Professor; George McBroom - Sponsor

suggestions.title: Edge-YOLO Embedded Detection
suggestions.desc: Port the LotSpotter detection model to run on NVIDIA Jetson Nano hardware at the camera source, transmitting only the 'occupied/vacant' status via MQTT to save bandwidth.

suggestions.title: Dynamic Demand-Based Pricing Engine
suggestions.desc: Build a backend module that uses the vacancy data to calculate real-time parking rates, similar to Uber's surge pricing, and displays them on the user's navigation interface.

suggestions.title: EV Charger Availability Tracker
suggestions.desc: Expand the computer vision model to distinguish between standard spots and Electric Vehicle charging stations, providing users with specialized navigation for power-ready slots.

suggestions.title: License Plate Recovery Service
suggestions.desc: Integrate an OpenALPR (Automatic License Plate Recognition) module so the application can record where a specific vehicle was parked, helping users find their car in massive multi-story decks.

similar: legacy:CP-27|Fall 2016|using deep learning to monitor parking spaces
legacy:CP-27|Spring 2016|using deep learning to monitor parking spaces

title: Chess App with AI
authors: Handley, Connor, Cohran, John Paul, Smith, Cole, Russell, Paul, Tsai, Eric
abstract: The objective of this project is to create a website that contains a virtual chess game, where the user can play against an opponent powered by an artificial intelligence model. This chess platform will be a widely accessible and user-friendly way to become more familiar with and practice the game of chess.

suggestions.title: Reinforcement Learning Agent Training
suggestions.desc: Replace standard minimax algorithms with a Deep Reinforcement Learning agent trained using the OpenAI Gym 'chess' environment. Compare the performance and 'creativity' of the neural network against traditional heuristic-based chess engines.

suggestions.title: Stockfish WASM Integration with Move Analysis
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Stockfish engine via WebAssembly to provide professional-grade move evaluation. Build a 'Coach Mode' that uses the engine's evaluation score to color-code moves (Blunder, Great, Best) in real-time to help players improve.

suggestions.title: LLM-Powered Strategy Explainer
suggestions.desc: Use the GPT-4o API to analyze the board state and explain the AI's strategy in natural language. Instead of just seeing a move, the user can ask 'Why did you move your Knight there?' and receive a tactical explanation about board control.

suggestions.title: Real-time Multiplayer via Socket.io
suggestions.desc: Transform the single-player site into a global platform by implementing WebSockets for low-latency player-vs-player matches. Add a backend database (Firebase or MongoDB) to track player Elo ratings, match history, and global leaderboards.

similar: legacy:AUTO-SU2020-1283|Summer 2020|using artificial intelligence to prescribe medicine
legacy:UC-29|Fall 2018|ai and chess variant ios game

title: Underock Arena
authors: Walker, Bryanna N
abstract: The game I developed was meant to highlight the ways that game developers can utilize our mobile devices to create a casual game. Breaking down complex parts of an RPG battling game, I devised the most casual and mobile-friendly way for the player to battle with unique bugs against an AI enemy using only 3 simple buttons: attack, heal, and ultimate. In addition, because of my areas of study at Kennesaw State University, I wanted to use my artistic abilities in my game. I thought it would be interesting to see how traditional art looks in a mobile game on a digitized screen.

suggestions.title: Procedural Ability Generation System
suggestions.desc: Expand the 3-button system by implementing a procedural generation engine that creates unique 'Ultimate' abilities based on the bug's species and elemental type. Use a scriptable object system in Unity or Godot to allow for hundreds of combinations without manual coding.

suggestions.title: Asynchronous Global Leaderboard and Ghost Battles
suggestions.desc: Implement a backend using AWS Lambda to allow players to upload their bug teams. Other players can then battle these 'ghost' versions of real player teams, creating a competitive asynchronous multiplayer environment similar to mobile RPG 'Coliseum' modes.

suggestions.title: Adaptive AI Difficulty via MCTS
suggestions.desc: Implement a Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) for the enemy AI that scales its 'intelligence' based on the player's performance. If the player wins too easily, the AI explores deeper move trees to provide a more consistent challenge.

suggestions.title: AR Core Bug Habitat Mode
suggestions.desc: Use Augmented Reality (AR) to bring the bug battles into the real world. Allow players to project the arena onto their desk or floor using their phone's camera, using spatial anchors to keep the bugs positioned correctly during the 3-button combat.

title: Memories: Echoes of Resilience
authors: Hester, Ethan J, Padgett, Joshua Tyler, Freeman, Katelyn R, Crosby, Sarah, Rhymer, Keith W
abstract: Memories: Echoes of Resilience is a first-person narrative experience that takes players through different experiences of a boy with autism. The game aims to show how everyday interactions can be different to someone with autism. Experiences such as making friends in school, being in overstimulating environments, or managing adult life are experiences we want to simulate for the player. The player will be able to interact with the world around them but will also have to be aware of how their environment impacts them, so they don’t become overstimulated.

suggestions.title: Biometric-Responsive Sensory Overload
suggestions.desc: Integrate a heart-rate monitor (like a Polar H10) so that the game's visual and auditory 'overstimulation' effects respond to the player's real-life stress levels. If the player's heart rate rises, the game increases visual noise and audio volume, creating a powerful empathy-building feedback loop.

suggestions.title: Immersive VR Sensory Port
suggestions.desc: Port the game to the Meta Quest using Unity VR to heighten the sense of immersion. Utilize spatial audio to mimic the directional sensitivity often experienced with autism, allowing players to feel the 'weight' of a crowded room through immersive 3D soundscapes.

suggestions.title: LLM-Driven Dynamic Social Practice
suggestions.desc: Replace scripted NPC dialogue with an LLM-powered interaction system. Players can practice 'managing adult life' scenarios (like a job interview or grocery store interaction) with NPCs that respond dynamically, providing a safe space to navigate complex social nuances.

suggestions.title: Analytics for Educational Insight
suggestions.desc: Develop a backend data collection system that tracks player choices and stress triggers throughout the game. Generate an 'Empathy Report' for educators that highlights which scenarios were most challenging for players, helping them understand where support is most needed in real-world settings.

title: UC-523: IT 4983 Server Hardening
authors: Chico, Manuel; Vuong, Gilbert; Jones, Harrison; Barbar, George
abstract: The team was assigned the responsibility of securing a web server. This business website is hosted on a technology stack comprising Apache, MariaDB, Red Hat Linux, and PHP. Our initial task involves conducting a comprehensive assessment of the provided network to identify vulnerabilities and assess potential risks. Subsequently, we will develop a robust security policy plan in alignment with the standards set forth by the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) and industry best practices. Once the plan was approved, our group will proceed to implement the recommended changes to fortify the network, ensuring it complies with industry best security practices. In the final phase, the team engaged in a red/blue team cyber security ethical hacking exercise, involving our network and other team. We will attempt to gain access to other team's server, protect ours by promptly addressing any verified security breach utilizing the team's incident response procedure.

suggestions.title: Ansible-Driven Compliance as Code
suggestions.desc: Transform the NIST security policy into an automated Ansible playbook. This would allow a single command to harden any fresh Red Hat Linux install with the project's Apache, MariaDB, and PHP configurations, ensuring repeatable and error-free deployments.

suggestions.title: Real-time Threat Monitoring with Wazuh
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Wazuh SIEM/XDR platform into the hardened stack. Build custom decoders to monitor PHP and MariaDB logs for SQL injection or brute force attempts, providing a centralized dashboard for the security policies defined in the abstract.

suggestions.title: Hardened LAMP-Stack Docker Appliance
suggestions.desc: Package the hardened environment into a production-ready Docker Compose orchestration. Include container-specific security measures like non-root execution, read-only filesystems, and Alpine-based images to reduce the attack surface for small businesses.

suggestions.title: Automated NIST-800-53 Auditing Tool
suggestions.desc: Develop a Python-based auditing script that scans the server's configuration files and generates a compliance report. Use the NIST-800-53 controls mentioned in the abstract to provide a 'score' and actionable remediation steps for sysadmins.

similar: legacy:GC-052|Spring 2021|ksu spring capstone - it security solution for small business – group 1

title: EcoEdConnect
authors: Dave, Vidhi, Jayaraman, Mythili, Anugu, Manikanta reddy, Paithanker, Rohini, Beeram, Neharika
abstract: An inventive educational platform called EcoEdConnect provides high school students with various opportunities to investigate biodiversity and environmental issues. By adjusting to each user's needs and choices, the web app offers a customized educational experience such as quizzes, experiments, videos, blogs, articles, etc. The project's first analysis, methodology, and early conclusions are presented in this document. It shows the several phases of the project, such as the introduction modules, practical experiments, discussions, blog, final assessment, and presentation, among other things. The application customizes the material and complexity according to the user's inclinations. Students' knowledge of biodiversity and environmental issues and their part in reducing their consequences could be significantly enhanced by following this educational path.

suggestions.title: Citizen Science Data Pipeline with iNaturalist
suggestions.desc: Integrate the iNaturalist API to allow students to upload real biodiversity sightings directly through EcoEdConnect. This turns the platform from a static learning site into a contributor to global environmental research databases.

suggestions.title: AI-Powered Adaptive Learning Paths
suggestions.desc: Implement a recommendation engine using Python's Scikit-learn to analyze quiz performance and blog engagement. The system should dynamically adjust the difficulty of subsequent modules and suggest experiments tailored to the student's specific biodiversity interests.

suggestions.title: Eco-Gamification and Rewards Engine
suggestions.desc: Build a 'virtual ecosystem' feature where students earn credits by completing experiments and quizzes. These credits could be used to 'restore' a digital habitat, creating a visual progression loop that increases long-term user retention.

suggestions.title: Interactive Geospatial Biodiversity Map
suggestions.desc: Use Leaflet.js or Mapbox to create a global map layer that visualizes environmental data such as carbon footprints or endangered species counts. Students can layer their own research findings over historical data to see environmental changes over time.

title: IT Curriculum Success Portal
authors: Thacker, Alexander, Boakye-Danquah, Cedric, Adeyemo, Damola, Ndiaye, Abdoulaye, Asante, Lydia
abstract: In this project, we built a curriculum and course web portal to have all curriculum and course information in one place with easy search and browse interfaces. Currently course data and information are scattered in various places. These include essential information like course description, learning outcomes, sample syllabus, offering schedule and history. It also includes curriculum development information for department use, such as coordinator, developer, revision schedule, open learning materials. We collected all sources of IT course information and built a database to integrate the data in one place. Through this, we can build a complete profile of a course and our curriculum. Then, a data driven interactive web app can pull the data from the database and display all course information to users in dynamic views. This is meant to consolidate a large amount of information about IT courses into one place so students and faculty can easily find the otherwise fragmented information.

suggestions.title: Neo4j Graph for Course Dependencies
suggestions.desc: Migrate the flat course data into a Neo4j graph database. Build an interactive visualization where students can see 'hidden' prerequisites, elective pathways, and how specific courses contribute to learning outcomes across the entire IT curriculum.

suggestions.title: Career-to-Curriculum Skill Mapping
suggestions.desc: Integrate the LinkedIn or Indeed API to scrape job requirements for IT roles. Use NLP to map these industry skills back to the specific course modules in the portal, helping students choose electives based on current market demand.

suggestions.title: Automated Syllabus Ingestion via OCR
suggestions.desc: Create a Python/Tesseract OCR pipeline that allows faculty to upload PDF syllabi. The tool would automatically extract learning outcomes, schedules, and coordinator info to populate the portal database, eliminating manual data entry.

suggestions.title: Departmental 'What-If' Change Simulator
suggestions.desc: Develop a tool for coordinators to simulate curriculum changes. If a developer changes a course schedule or prerequisite, the system should automatically flag downstream impacts on graduation timelines for different student cohorts.

title: Elevating AI Research: Creating a website for Kennesaw State University's AI Lab
authors: Gadhe, Shashank
abstract: The project titled "Elevating AI Research: Creating a website for Kennesaw State University's AI Lab" is dedicated to developing an HTML5 Content Management System website for Kennesaw State University. This website, AILab.kennesaw.edu, serves as a dedicated platform to showcase lab facilities, ongoing projects, and cutting-edge research, with a focus on promoting global AI research and education. Our target audience encompasses university students, faculty, AI researchers, and organizations with an interest in AI innovation.Preliminary findings support our goal: engaging platforms showcasing AI Lab research effectively.The incorporation of admin access empowers university professors to customize content, thus enhancing adaptability and personalization. These preliminary results underscore the project's promising direction and potential to establish a compelling online presence for the AI Lab at Kennesaw State University, with transformative implications for AI research and education.

suggestions.title: Integrated 'AI Sandbox' for Live Model Demos
suggestions.desc: Add a feature to host live, interactive AI models using Streamlit or Gradio within the CMS. This would allow visitors to interact with the lab's research projects (like sarcasm detection or medical summarization) directly on the website.

suggestions.title: Automated Researcher Publication Crawler
suggestions.desc: Use the Crossref or Google Scholar API to build a dynamic 'Publications' section. The system would automatically update faculty and student researcher profiles with their latest papers, ensuring the site remains a cutting-edge showcase without manual updates.

suggestions.title: GPU Resource Scheduling and Status Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Create a real-time monitoring dashboard for the AI Lab's hardware facilities. Integrate with Slurm or NVIDIA-SMI to show current GPU cluster utilization, allowing students and researchers to see available resources before coming to the lab.

suggestions.title: Interactive Research Knowledge Graph
suggestions.desc: Build an interactive map using D3.js that connects lab projects, facilities, and external organizations. This would visually demonstrate the lab's global impact and collaborations, helping prospective partners find relevant research clusters.

title: SARS-CoV-2 Spike and ACE2 protein-protein interactions database
authors: Duran Salgado, Jovanny, Addepalli, Durga Narayana Varma, Meeks, Travis, Ramana Adapa, Pooja Venkata, Ambati, Divya Sri
abstract: SARS-CoV-2 protein interactions are essential for viral replication and pathogenesis. To better understand these interactions, we have created a database using AWS (Amazon Web Services) to store data extracted from protein simulations. This database can be used to study the structure and function of SARS-CoV-2 proteins and their interactions with each other and with host cell proteins.
supervisor: Dr. Jack Zheng - Capstone Instructor; Dr. Chloe Yixin Xie - Project Owner

suggestions.title: Binding Affinity Prediction with Graph Neural Networks
suggestions.desc: Transform the protein-protein interaction data into graph representations and train a Graph Neural Network (GNN) to predict binding affinities of new SARS-CoV-2 variants. Use PyTorch Geometric to model the spatial relationships between the Spike and ACE2 residues stored in your AWS database.

suggestions.title: Automated Mutation Impact Pipeline using AWS Lambda
suggestions.desc: Build a serverless pipeline that triggers molecular dynamics simulations whenever a new variant sequence is uploaded. Use AWS Batch to run GROMACS or NAMD simulations and automatically update the database with the resulting interaction energy and structural changes.

suggestions.title: Interactive 3D Interaction Hotspot Visualizer
suggestions.desc: Develop a web-based dashboard using NGLView or Three.js that pulls data from the database to highlight 'hotspot' residues where interactions are strongest. This allows researchers to visually identify the most critical binding sites for targeted drug development.

suggestions.title: Cross-Strain Comparative Analytics Engine
suggestions.desc: Extend the database schema to support comparative queries between SARS-CoV-2, MERS-CoV, and SARS-CoV. Use Amazon Athena to perform SQL-based analytics across different viral strains to identify conserved interaction motifs that could be targets for universal pan-coronavirus vaccines.

title: IT course profile website
authors: Voruganti, Manikanta
abstract: Build a Dynamic course profile website for Bachelor of science in information technology courses, that display all the information regarding the course.

suggestions.title: AI-Powered Prerequisite Path Mapping
suggestions.desc: Implement a Neo4j graph database backend to map complex course dependencies and use a pathfinding algorithm to suggest the most efficient 'fast-track' or 'specialization' routes for IT students. This would help students visualize how early electives unlock senior-level capstone opportunities.

suggestions.title: Live Industry Skill-Gap Integration
suggestions.desc: Build a module that scrapes real-time job data from LinkedIn or Indeed for 'Information Technology' roles and maps required skills to specific course profiles. The website could then display 'Trending Skills' icons next to courses that teach high-demand technologies like AWS, Kubernetes, or React.

suggestions.title: Student Sentiment and Performance Analytics
suggestions.desc: Integrate an NLP engine using Python’s NLTK to analyze anonymous student feedback and performance data stored in the portal. This would generate a 'Course Difficulty vs. Satisfaction' matrix to help faculty identify which IT modules require curriculum updates or additional tutoring resources.

suggestions.title: Automated Degree Audit and Credit Transfer Tool
suggestions.desc: Develop an API connector for university systems (like Ellucian Banner) that allows students to upload their unofficial transcripts. The system would automatically cross-reference completed courses with the IT profile data to generate a real-time 'Progress to Graduation' visual report.

title: Bio-Contribute
authors: Divi, Tejaswi, Tandra, Kiranmayi, Bheemireddy, Sainath reddy, Bheemireddy, Chandrasekhar reddy, Chinthakuntla, sharanya
abstract: The "Bio-Contribute" challenge is an ambitious initiative aimed at revolutionizing the way facts are generated and shared in lifestyle sciences. Bio-Contribute encompasses various aspects, including design, development, and facts collection. It leverages Figma for innovation, ensuring a user-friendly and collaborative interface. The challenge has completed the frontend development section, permitting customers to carry out various movements, which include content advent and information seizure with the help of the GPT-3 era. This report gives an in-depth analysis of the venture's progression, strategies used, and initial outcomes, demonstrating its potential to convert statistics introduction and sharing in the discipline of lifestyles sciences.

suggestions.title: Fact-Verification Blockchain for Bio-Data
suggestions.desc: Implement a lightweight blockchain or distributed ledger to track the provenance of facts generated in the platform. This ensures that every biological 'fact' has an immutable audit trail from the initial seizure to the final validation.

suggestions.title: Automated Fact Categorization with BioBERT
suggestions.desc: Integrate the BioBERT (Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers for Biomedical Text Mining) model to automatically tag and categorize user-contributed facts into relevant life science sub-domains like genomics or pharmacology.

suggestions.title: Dynamic Biological Trend Visualizer
suggestions.desc: Build a frontend dashboard using Chart.js that aggregates the collected facts. It should allow researchers to visualize trends, such as the frequency of certain bio-discoveries over time or geographical clusters of lifestyle science data collection.

suggestions.title: Crowdsourced Peer-Review Protocol
suggestions.desc: Develop a community-based validation system where experts can upvote, annotate, or challenge facts. This would transform the platform into a collaborative peer-review hub similar to StackOverflow but specialized for life science research.

title: Project Title: Discover, Learn, and Protect: A Mobile App for Informal STEM Learning about Local Biodiversity and Environmental Issues.
authors: McCray, Elvin, Appah, David Y, Banu, Adedunmola, Tran, Binh, Tucker, Zenya
abstract: Our team assignment for this project was to create a mobile application that offers informal STEM learning about local biodiversity and environmental issues. Dr. Ying Xie, Professor in the College of Computing and Software Engineering (CCSE) is the owner of this project, who also laid out required features and provided necessary information, guidance, and advice for the project development. The core function of this application is to empower users to explore, identify and gain insights into the plant and animal species native to their region. Leveraging the capabilities of their smartphone’s camera, users can effortlessly scan, record, or locate local wildlife and environmental phenomena. To enrich the user experience, the app incorporates AI, notably ChatGPT, which delivers comprehensive information about the identified species. Our team also integrated a GPS feature. This feature will reveal the zip code and provide an approximate location of the identified species’ origin.

suggestions.title: Augmented Reality Field Guide Integration
suggestions.desc: Use ARCore or ARKit to allow users to point their camera at a plant or animal and see an interactive 3D overlay with STEM facts. Implement a custom TensorFlow Lite model within the app to perform real-time identification of Georgia-specific species.

suggestions.title: Citizen Science Data Pipeline for iNaturalist
suggestions.desc: Build an API bridge that allows users to submit their biodiversity sightings directly to the iNaturalist platform or the Global Biodiversity Information Facility (GBIF). This transforms student observations into real-world research data for environmental scientists.

suggestions.title: Gamified Invasive Species Quest System
suggestions.desc: Create a 'bounty' system where users earn badges and points for finding and logging invasive species (like Kudzu or Privet) in local parks. Include a dashboard for local park rangers to see hotspots of reported invasive growth based on user GPS data.

suggestions.title: Offline-First Remote Conservation Mode
suggestions.desc: Implement a robust offline caching system using Mapbox and SQLite so the app remains functional in deep forest areas without cellular signal. Allow users to queue their biodiversity reports and auto-sync when they return to a Wi-Fi zone.

title: Implementing OpenRAN: Democratizing 5G Networks
authors: McOsker, Caitlin, Kennebrew, Chavarous
abstract: This project highlights the endeavor to democratize 5G networks by making their deployment and ownership more accessible to a broader range of stakeholders. The purpose is to break down existing barriers and foster greater inclusivity in the 5G landscape. To achieve this goal, a method is proposed by the Open-Air Interface Software Alliance (OSA) involving the decoupling of software and hardware, coupled with leveraging virtualization techniques to simulate User Equipment (UE), gNodeB, and the 5G Core Network. This approach embraces open standards and interfaces, effectively lowering the barriers to entry for new vendors and promoting innovation in the 5G ecosystem. Through these innovative methods, the project underlines the transformative potential of making 5G networks more democratic, inclusive, and conducive to accelerated technological advancements.
supervisor: Dr. Ying Xie - Capstone Instructor; Dr. Sumit Chakravarty - Project Owner; Dr. Ramesh Annavajjala - Project Sponsor

suggestions.title: O-RAN Intelligent Controller (RIC) xApp Development
suggestions.desc: Develop a Near-Real-Time RIC (RAN Intelligent Controller) and write a custom xApp in C++ or Python to optimize radio resource management. Test how the xApp can dynamically adjust bandwidth allocation based on simulated UE (User Equipment) congestion.

suggestions.title: Private 5G IoT Mesh with Open-Air Interface
suggestions.desc: Deploy the Open-Air Interface (OAI) stack on Raspberry Pis equipped with Software Defined Radios (SDRs) like the Ettus USRP. Use this setup to create a private, low-latency 5G network specifically for campus-wide IoT environmental sensors.

suggestions.title: Virtual Network Slicing for Security Benchmarking
suggestions.desc: Implement network slicing within the virtualized gNodeB to isolate high-security traffic from public traffic. Conduct stress tests to evaluate if an emulated DDoS attack on one slice impacts the latency or throughput of the high-priority slice.

suggestions.title: Automated OpenRAN CI/CD Deployment with Kubernetes
suggestions.desc: Containerize the OAI Core and RAN components using Docker and orchestrate them with Kubernetes. Build a Jenkins pipeline that automatically spins up a virtualized 5G environment to test new RAN software configurations.

title: Spectrum Analyzer Analysis Tool
authors: O'Connor, Ryan, Reed, Nathan, Luthringshauser, Richard, Lawani, Ayorinde, Sekar, Dinesh
abstract: Military flight test ranges employ Radio Frequency (RF) Threat Systems, both real and simulated, to evaluate and test Aircraft Electronic Warfare (EW) Systems during flight. A critical component of this testing process is the radar tracking station, which records RF transmissions from various Threat Systems, in the form of video footage. This project aims to significantly reduce human interaction and improve efficiency by automating the analysis of the video data, transcribing it into a numeric format, and storing the results in a user-friendly, exportable format.

suggestions.title: Low-Latency Edge Inference for Live RF Transcription
suggestions.desc: Move from post-flight analysis to real-time monitoring by deploying a quantized computer vision model (using OpenVINO or NVIDIA TensorRT) onto edge hardware like a Jetson Nano. This would allow for immediate numeric data extraction directly from the radar station's video feed during active flight tests.

suggestions.title: Synthetic RF Signal Data Augmentation Pipeline
suggestions.desc: Research the use of Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) to create synthetic 'noisy' radar screen footage for training. This improves the transcription model’s accuracy during high-interference scenarios or poor weather conditions where the video quality of the threat systems might be degraded.

suggestions.title: 3D Geospatial Mission Reconstruction
suggestions.desc: Integrate the transcribed numeric RF data with KML/GPS flight logs using the CesiumJS library. The end result is a 3D web-based playback of the mission where users can see the aircraft's path synced with the real-time RF threat levels detected by the automated tool.

suggestions.title: Civilian Spectrum Encroachment Monitor
suggestions.desc: Adapt the vision logic to detect unauthorized transmissions or interference in civilian radio bands by monitoring spectrum analyzer displays. This could be marketed to telecommunications regulatory bodies as an automated way to audit and log spectrum usage in dense urban environments.

title: Traffic & Road Sign Detection Using Deep Learning
authors: Anumula, Sai Narasimha Reddy, Anumula, Krishna puja, BABU, CHANGANABOYINA SURESH
abstract: In a stride toward autonomous driving, this project aims to craft a deep learning system for detecting and classifying road signs. A dataset of 1000 varied traffic sign images form the study's core, ensuring diverse learning scenarios. Leveraging the RESNET framework and transfer learning, the model discerns signs into key categories critical for navigation. The methodology spans from data curation to RESNET training, with robust metrics planned for validation. Future work includes dataset augmentation and model optimization, enhancing adaptability and performance for intelligent transport systems.

suggestions.title: YOLOv8 Real-Time Video Detection Pipeline
suggestions.desc: Upgrade from static ResNet classification to dynamic object detection using YOLOv8. Implement a temporal tracking algorithm (like DeepSORT) to ensure that signs are not just classified once, but tracked across frames to prevent 'flicker' in the autonomous decision-making process.

suggestions.title: Adverse Weather Perception Enhancement
suggestions.desc: Expand the model’s robustness by training on the DAWN (Detection in Adverse Weather Nature) dataset. Use image restoration techniques like DehazeNet as a preprocessing layer to allow the system to accurately identify signs in heavy fog, rain, or snow conditions.

suggestions.title: Infrastructure Audit and Maintenance Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Build a mobile app that allows city maintenance crews to mount a phone on their dashboard; the system automatically logs the GPS coordinates and 'health' (legibility/damage) of every road sign it passes, generating a Mapbox-based heat map of signs requiring replacement.

suggestions.title: AR Driver Assistance HUD
suggestions.desc: Develop a lightweight version of the model using TensorFlow Lite for mobile devices. The app would use the phone's camera to identify signs and project an Augmented Reality (AR) overlay on the screen to highlight speed limits and stop signs for elderly drivers or those in unfamiliar areas.

similar: legacy:GR-27|Spring 2018|fault detection in iot using deep learning

title: Traffic Pattern Analysis and Anomaly Detection Using Large-scale Trajectory Data
authors: Taylor, Christian, Sharma, Desh, Patel, Janki, Nemmikanti, Nikhil sai, Dundoo, Surya Harshith
abstract: With the advancement of IoT and improved computing capabilities, real-time vehicle and road user trajectories are easily accessible through advanced traffic sensing, replacing time-consuming manual checks. This study employs machine learning to analyze extensive trajectory data, focusing on anomaly detection in traffic patterns. It investigates efficient techniques for processing time-series data using an open-source dataset (InD dataset). The procedure involves data preprocessing, feature extraction, machine learning model training, and anomaly detection at 4 intersections. Irregular paths reveal abnormal driving behavior like U-turns and unexpected stops. The study highlights their impact on traffic management and safety and discusses potential applications in vehicle-to-infrastructure alert systems.
supervisor: Dr. Junxuan Zhao (sponsor), Dr. Jack Zheng (instructor)

suggestions.title: Real-Time Intersection Near-Miss Predictor
suggestions.desc: Extend the ML model to predict 'near-miss' collisions by calculating Time-to-Collision (TTC) between trajectories in the InD dataset. Build a visualization tool that highlights 'danger zones' in an intersection where pedestrians and vehicles frequently cross paths.

suggestions.title: Edge-AI Deployment with NVIDIA Jetson
suggestions.desc: Optimize the anomaly detection model using TensorRT to run on a Jetson Nano. Create a hardware-in-the-loop simulation where a live camera stream is processed at the edge to detect wrong-way drivers or illegal U-turns without cloud latency.

suggestions.title: Multi-Agent Trajectory Forecasting with Social-LSTM
suggestions.desc: Implement a Social-LSTM (Long Short-Term Memory) network to predict the future 5-second paths of road users based on their interactions. This moves beyond detection into proactive traffic management, forecasting how one vehicle's anomaly affects surrounding traffic.

suggestions.title: Interactive Smart City Traffic Heatmapper
suggestions.desc: Build a web-based dashboard using Deck.gl or Mapbox that ingests the processed InD data to visualize traffic flow patterns over time. Add filters for 'Anomaly Score' to help urban planners identify specific road designs that contribute to erratic driving behavior.

title: Transportation as a Service
authors: Wear, Frank, Uzoka, Abel, P, Harini, Parsi, Pranavi, owoade, samuel
abstract: Personal transportation is shifting away from privately owned vehicles and toward "hired services" to satisfy the same mobility needs. This shift is one of many cultural changes that combine to reduce emissions, improve efficiency, and support an ever-increasing human population. This project supports a shift away from private vehicle ownership as a monolithic solution. Utilizing existing mapping API services, we are planning routes that not only display, but also combine transportation solutions into a single route from origin to destination.

suggestions.title: Multi-Modal GTFS-Realtime Integration
suggestions.desc: Incorporate the General Transit Feed Specification (GTFS-Realtime) to include live bus/train delays and micro-mobility (e-scooter/bike) availability. Use OpenTripPlanner to build a routing engine that combines walking, biking, and public transit into a single cohesive journey.

suggestions.title: Accessibility-First Routing Engine
suggestions.desc: Integrate OpenSidewalks data to provide routing options specifically for users with limited mobility. The system should prioritize transit stations with working elevators and routes with curb ramps, filling a critical gap in existing commercial mapping services.

suggestions.title: Corporate Commute Optimization Platform
suggestions.desc: Build a B2B version of the service that helps large companies manage 'commuter benefits.' The platform would optimize carpooling groups among employees and calculate the total CO2 reduction achieved by the workforce using the Cloverly API for carbon tracking.

suggestions.title: Gamified Carbon Incentive System
suggestions.desc: Develop a reward layer where users earn 'Green Credits' for choosing low-emission transport options over private vehicles. Research the use of a blockchain-based ledger (like Polygon) to allow these credits to be traded for local business discounts or transit passes.

title: Moral Parenting platform
authors: Kamal, Zularbine
abstract: Children in most cases learn by observing their parents or caregivers. Some children are deprived form this because they don’t have parents or their parents are so poor that they are struggling with their life and they have no time to give careful attention to their children. What if there is a place where a poor student can find a moral parent? A nonbiological parent who can help financially and do reciprocal duties for a child’s better future. This relationship demands restraint, self-sacrifice, and patience from the parents toward the child to develop a strong moral foundation that will serve them all through their lives.

suggestions.title: Zero-Knowledge Background Verification
suggestions.desc: Enhance user trust by implementing a decentralized identity system using Polygon ID. This allows 'moral parents' and students to verify their identity and financial status through Zero-Knowledge Proofs, ensuring safety without exposing sensitive personal documents on a central server.

suggestions.title: Milestone-Based Micro-Grant Smart Contracts
suggestions.desc: Build a financial transparency layer using Ethereum smart contracts. Parents can lock funds in escrow that are automatically released to the student's educational expenses only when specific milestones (verified by the school or platform) are met, such as GPA targets or attendance.

suggestions.title: Psychometric Matching Algorithm
suggestions.desc: Develop a specialized matching engine that goes beyond financial need. Use NLP and sentiment analysis on user profiles and 'values surveys' to pair students with parents who share similar life philosophies or career paths, fostering a more sustainable long-term relationship.

suggestions.title: Global NGO Integration API
suggestions.desc: Create a standardized API that allows established international NGOs to plug their existing student databases into the platform. This would allow local charities to 'list' verified students to a global pool of potential moral parents, scaling the platform's reach beyond a single region.

title: M-Script: Accelerating Preparatory and Analytical Phases in MD Simulations
authors: Addepalli, Durga Narayana Varma
abstract: "M-Script" revolutionizes Molecular Dynamics (MD) simulations by automating time-consuming preparatory and analytical tasks. Traditional folder preparation and salt bridge data extraction, taking 25 and 30 seconds respectively, are reduced to 1.5 and 0.5 seconds with M-Script. This efficiency is achieved through tailored TCL and Python scripts, optimizing the process for high-volume protein analysis. M-Script not only saves time but also enhances the focus on scientific discovery, promising significant strides in biotechnological research and protein behavior understanding.

suggestions.title: GPU-Accelerated Salt Bridge Calculation via PyCUDA
suggestions.desc: Rewrite the Python-based analytical scripts to utilize CUDA kernels, offloading heavy protein coordinate calculations to the GPU for even faster processing of massive MD trajectory files.

suggestions.title: AlphaFold2 Predictive Pipeline Integration
suggestions.desc: Expand M-Script to include a module that takes raw sequences, runs a local ColabFold/AlphaFold2 instance, and automatically prepares the resulting .pdb files for MD simulations.

suggestions.title: Three.js Interactive Trajectory Viewer
suggestions.desc: Build a web-based dashboard that allows researchers to upload M-Script outputs and visualize protein salt-bridge formations in 3D directly in a browser without heavy desktop software.

suggestions.title: Bioinformatics-as-a-Service (BaaS) API
suggestions.desc: Containerize the M-Script suite using Docker and deploy it as a RESTful API on AWS Lambda, allowing other research teams to programmatically submit batch protein analysis jobs.

title: BioEduHub
authors: Chintam, Tejesh R, Chopparapu, Shiswa Preethi, Panati, Viswa Narendra Reddy, Pasupuleti, Lithin Venkata Sai, Chopparapu, Aravind
abstract: Our project introduces an interactive and personalized learning experience aligned with the Next Generation Science Standards (NGSS). Users can explore a diverse range of topics related to biodiversity and environmental issues through quizzes, games, simulations, experiments, videos, podcasts, and articles. The web app adapts content to individual interests and provides tailored feedback to enhance knowledge, skills, attitudes, and behaviors related to these critical subjects. The user-friendly interface and comprehensive modules, including video lectures, readings, quizzes, hands-on experiments, debates, and assessments, ensure a holistic learning journey. Join us on a pathway to understanding and mitigating climate change while making a positive impact on our environment.

suggestions.title: LLM-Powered Personal Tutor Integration
suggestions.desc: Integrate an OpenAI or Llama-3 API to provide an interactive 'AI Scientist' chat assistant within the web app. The assistant should use Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to answer student questions specifically based on the NGSS documentation.

suggestions.title: Educator Insights & Classroom Management Portal
suggestions.desc: Develop a backend for teachers to create classrooms, assign specific biodiversity modules, and view a heatmap of student performance. Use D3.js to visualize which NGSS standards students are struggling with most across the class.

suggestions.title: Web-Based Virtual Lab Simulations
suggestions.desc: Build interactive 'virtual experiments' using Three.js or P5.js where students can simulate the effect of pH levels on aquatic life. Allow students to collect virtual 'samples' and generate lab reports within the BioEduHub platform.

suggestions.title: Accessibility-Driven Content Adaptation
suggestions.desc: Implement a feature that automatically adapts content for students with different learning needs, such as generating simplified summaries or high-contrast visual modes. Add Web Speech API support for voice-controlled navigation through the interactive quizzes.

title: Image Identification & AI-Powered Knowledge Provision
authors: Katla, Sri Kruthi, Chepyala, Sathwik, Vunnam, Kranthi, Veeranki, Salman Raj, Bandi, Krishna vijitha
abstract: Our capstone project focuses on the development of an innovative application that combines image recognition technology with AI-powered text generation to provide instant and accurate information about local flora and fauna. This project encompasses the creation of precise image recognition algorithms, a user-friendly interface, and an efficient knowledge delivery system. By allowing users to easily identify and learn about local species, our application aims to promote environmental awareness and engagement.

suggestions.title: Offline-First Mobile Identification with TensorFlow Lite
suggestions.desc: Quantize the image recognition models to run locally on mobile devices, enabling hikers to identify flora and fauna in remote areas with zero cellular connectivity.

suggestions.title: Invasive Species Early-Warning Reporting System
suggestions.desc: Add a feature that automatically flags identified invasive species and provides a one-click submission of the photo and GPS coordinates to the local Department of Natural Resources (DNR).

suggestions.title: Multi-Modal Phenology Tracking via Computer Vision
suggestions.desc: Create a research tool that analyzes user-submitted photos over time to track changes in plant blooming seasons (phenology) and correlates them with local climate data from the NOAA API.

suggestions.title: Flora-Fauna Educational API for Eco-Tourism
suggestions.desc: Turn the recognition engine into a commercial API that local botanical gardens and state parks can integrate into their own branded visitor apps for interactive tours.

title: A Mobile App for Informal STEM Learning about Local Biodiversity and Environmental Issues
authors: Sunkavalli, Susmitha, Battepati, Venkata Karthik Krishna, Mogulla, Soundarya Reddy, Gunti, Sahithi, Tati, Suresh
abstract: Biodiversity is essential for the processes that support all life on our earth. Without the existence of entire range of animals, trees and species, we can not live healthy life. We can not have a balanced ecosystem that we depend on to provide with oxygen we breath and the food we eat. So studying and understanding (Exploring) Biodiversity is the at most priority of current and coming generations.

suggestions.title: Biodiversity 'Sim-Park' Mini-Game
suggestions.desc: Develop an interactive simulation where users must maintain a virtual park by balancing species diversity; the health of the virtual park is influenced by the user's real-world 'check-ins' at local nature spots.

suggestions.title: Cross-Platform Flutter Migration with Offline Sync
suggestions.desc: Rebuild the mobile app using Flutter and a Hive local database to ensure a seamless experience across iOS/Android with robust offline capabilities for data collection in the field.

suggestions.title: Longitudinal Learning Impact Study via Firebase
suggestions.desc: Design an experiment within the app to track knowledge retention by delivering 'spaced repetition' quizzes and analyzing the data to see which STEM modules result in the highest long-term recall.

suggestions.title: Ecotourism Partnership & Rewards Engine
suggestions.desc: Integrate a geolocation-based rewards system where users earn badges and discounts at local outdoor gear shops or nature centers by visiting specific biodiversity hotspots mapped in the app.

title: Multikernel: A detailed Analysis of Multicore OS Kernel
authors: Jinan, Umme Afifa
abstract: In modern computer systems, multiple processing cores offer immense possibilities to perform parallel and dynamic computing and diversity in architecture. However, such processing creates more challenges as software developers need to design applications that can effectively utilize these multicores for improved performance. Static optimization of such dynamic structures is practically impossible. So, a novel OS structure called multikernel inspired by the distributed systems, is introduced, which will provide superior scalability. In this paper, we briefly review the concept of multikernel OS, analyze and re-evaluate its architecture. Then we introduce some debugging and analysis tools to measure performance of the Windows hybrid kernel. This study will help get a deep insight into existing multikernel-based research, the use of existing analysis tools to measure performance and possible optimization based on analysis results of popular hybrid kernels like Windows.

suggestions.title: High-Speed Inter-Core Capability Messaging
suggestions.desc: Implement a messaging protocol inspired by the Barrelfish OS using shared-memory rings for core-to-core communication. Benchmark the latency of this 'message-passing' approach against traditional shared-memory locking mechanisms on a multicore system.

suggestions.title: Heterogeneous Resource Scheduler for ARM big.LITTLE
suggestions.desc: Modify the multikernel to recognize and schedule tasks across asymmetric cores (e.g., high-performance vs. high-efficiency cores). Develop an algorithm that migrates kernel services between cores based on real-time power consumption and computational load.

suggestions.title: Multikernel Micro-Service Runtime
suggestions.desc: Build a lightweight runtime that treats every core as a separate node in a distributed system, running micro-services as isolated OS instances. Use a global name service to allow 'services' on Core A to call 'services' on Core B without a central monolithic manager.

suggestions.title: Fault-Tolerant State Replication
suggestions.desc: Design a system where the internal state of one kernel instance is replicated to a 'shadow' core. Implement a heartbeat mechanism so that if one core experiences a hardware fault, the shadow core can take over its kernel responsibilities with zero downtime.

title: Conceptualizing a TOC-Enhanced Chatbot: Pattern Recognition and Interaction
authors: Tasneem, Sumaiya, ELUGOTI, SHARON, Aduri, Chinni Cherrishma Reddy, KOLLI, PURNA PAVAN KUMAR, Anche, Krishna Vamsi
abstract: A chatbot is a software which is capable of communicating with human by using natural language processing. In our project, we plan to develop a Python-based chatbot that integrates theory of computation (TOC) concepts, including finite automata and regular expressions. The chatbot will interact with users, recognizing patterns and keywords in their inputs. We’ll begin by defining initial regular expressions for basic user interactions including greetings and inquiries.Future developments may enhance regular expressions and broaden the chatbot’s TOC-related capabilities, creating a versatile educational tool with practical TOC applications.

suggestions.title: State-Machine Visualizer and Real-Time Debugger
suggestions.desc: Build a React-based dashboard that visualizes the Python chatbot's underlying Finite Automata in real-time. Use D3.js to show the active state and transitions as the user inputs text, allowing developers to step through the Theory of Computation (TOC) logic for complex interactions.

suggestions.title: Hybrid LLM-FSM Intent Orchestrator
suggestions.desc: Integrate a lightweight Large Language Model (like DistilBERT) to handle semantic nuance while using the existing Regular Expressions as hard constraints. This creates a 'guardrail' system where the TOC-based patterns ensure the chatbot never deviates from strict procedural requirements in domains like medical intake.

suggestions.title: Low-Resource Edge Deployment for IoT Devices
suggestions.desc: Port the Python TOC logic to MicroPython or C++ to run on ESP32 or Raspberry Pi Pico hardware. Because regex and state machines are computationally inexpensive compared to neural networks, you can create a privacy-first offline smart-home controller that recognizes commands without an internet connection.

suggestions.title: Automated Protocol Compliance Validator
suggestions.desc: Pivot the chatbot into a tool for verifying user adherence to specific communication protocols (e.g., emergency dispatch or industrial safety checks). Use the regex patterns to enforce that specific keywords and data formats are provided in a set sequence before the 'state' can transition to completion.

title: Boosting Clickbait Detection through Semantic Insights and Attention-Driven Neural Network
authors: Meesala, Lokesh
abstract: The digital age has witnessed an explosion of online content, making it increasingly challenging for users to differentiate between reliable information and clickbait, which is often misleading or sensationalized. Clickbait contributes to the spread of misinformation, phishing attacks, and illegal marketing practices, and manipulates users’ decisions. Even from a business standpoint a clickbait might not lead to a conversion, A user might land on the page by following a clickbait and get frustrated and close the page. Additionally, with the increase in the usage of large language models for content writing it is even more challenging for the general user to differentiate between clickbait and genuine content. As a result, clickbait detection has become an important research topic. Existing clickbait detection models often work on rule-based techniques which lack the nuanced understanding of human semantic knowledge, making them vulnerable to sophisticated clickbait techniques.
supervisor: Dr. Md. Abdullah Al Hafiz Khan

suggestions.title: Multimodal Clickbait Detection with CLIP
suggestions.desc: Extend the model to analyze both the headline text and the accompanying thumbnail image using OpenAI's CLIP. Train the network to identify 'visual clickbait' where the image is intentionally misleading compared to the actual article content.

suggestions.title: Browser Guard: Real-Time Sentiment Filter
suggestions.desc: Develop a Chrome/Firefox extension that runs the neural network locally (using TensorFlow.js) to flag clickbait on Facebook or Twitter feeds. Use XAI (Explainable AI) to highlight the specific sensationalist words that triggered the detection.

suggestions.title: Detection of Algorithmic Bias in News Feeds
suggestions.desc: Apply the clickbait detection model to datasets from different news outlets (e.g., mainstream vs. independent) to quantify which platforms rely most heavily on sensationalism. Analyze the correlation between 'clickbait score' and 'political bias' using semantic clustering.

suggestions.title: Clickbait-to-Summary Transformation Tool
suggestions.desc: Create a tool that, when a clickbait headline is detected, uses a T5 or BART model to fetch the article and rewrite the headline into a factual, neutral summary for the user.

similar: legacy:GR-023|Spring 2021|machine learning techniques for malware network traffic detection

title: Federated Learning in Cardiac Diagnostics: Balancing Predictive Accuracy with Data Privacy in Heart Sound Classification
authors: Donkada, Sricharan
abstract: Cardiovascular diseases account for nearly a third of global deaths, posing a challenge that machine learning can help address. However, data privacy concerns hinder the direct application of conventional machine learning in this sensitive area. This paper explores Federated Learning (FL) as a decentralized strategy to mitigate these concerns by allowing for local data processing. FL's design ensures that only processed updates, not raw data, are shared with a central server, maintaining individual privacy. Our research assesses FL's practicality and effectiveness in predicting heart disease while adhering to ethical and legal norms. We build upon previous studies, such as Wanyong et al.'s work on heart sound analysis with FL, to underline its privacy-preserving benefits. This study aims to improve healthcare outcomes with machine learning while setting a privacy-conscious benchmark for future research.

suggestions.title: Differential Privacy Layer for Heart Sound Data
suggestions.desc: Incorporate (e, d)-Differential Privacy by adding Gaussian noise to the local model updates before they are sent to the central server. Evaluate the trade-off between the 'privacy budget' and the accuracy of cardiac diagnostic predictions.

suggestions.title: Mobile Edge Federated Learning Node
suggestions.desc: Port the local training component to an Android/iOS application that can process audio from a mobile-connected digital stethoscope. Demonstrate federated training where the server aggregates updates from multiple mobile devices without ever seeing the heartbeat audio files.

suggestions.title: Byzantine-Robust Aggregation Algorithms
suggestions.desc: Implement and test robust aggregation methods like 'Krum' or 'Trimmed Mean' to protect the global cardiac model against poisoning attacks (e.g., one node sending 'bad' updates to ruin the diagnostic accuracy).

suggestions.title: Cross-Silo Diagnostic Platform for Rural Clinics
suggestions.desc: Design a 'Cross-Silo' Federated Learning framework where different small clinics can collectively train a heart disease model. Build a secure dashboard for clinic administrators to monitor their contribution to the global model's improvement.

title: Simplified Named Entity Recognition Using Context Free Grammar
authors: Koganti, Sai Chandana, Yaganti, Varshini, Yaganti, Vinesh Babu, Katragadda, Surya Kiran, Thulluru, Pavani
abstract: Named Entity Recognition (NER) is a crucial component of natural language processing. Although Spacy is a popular tool for NER, it faces challenges in accurately identifying individual's names. In response, Context-Free Grammar (CFG) is introduced as a complementary solution to augment Spacy's NER functionality, with the specific objective of enhancing the precision of person name recognition. This project focuses on formulating CFG rules and applying them to a sample text, showcasing improved NER accuracy. By combining the strengths of Spacy and CFG, we aim to address the limitations of current NER systems, particularly in recognizing individual's names, and contribute to more reliable and efficient NER processes.

suggestions.title: Hybrid Transformer-CFG Ensemble Model
suggestions.desc: Develop an ensemble NER system that combines the high recall of a BERT-based model with the high precision of the CFG rules. Use the CFG to 'veto' or 'validate' names identified by the Transformer to reduce false positives in legal or medical texts.

suggestions.title: Archaic Name Recognition for Historical Archives
suggestions.desc: Customize the CFG rules to identify naming patterns in 18th and 19th-century census records or handwritten manuscripts. Test the system on datasets from the Digital Public Library of America to help genealogists automatically index person names.

suggestions.title: Automated CFG Rule Induction
suggestions.desc: Research and implement a 'Grammar Induction' algorithm that can automatically suggest new CFG rules by analyzing patterns in a labeled dataset. This reduces the manual labor of writing rules for new domains (like tech company names or chemical compounds).

suggestions.title: Legal Document Redaction Tool
suggestions.desc: Build a productivity app that uses the improved person-recognition logic to automatically suggest redactions for PII (Personally Identifiable Information) in legal PDFs. Ensure the tool can differentiate between 'Witness Names' and 'Judge Names' using context-free rules.

title: PLANT DISEASE DETECTION USING CNN
authors: Shankara Murthy, Nandini, Vasireddi, Divya, Kulkarni, Mounika
abstract: Plant diseases are a major source of worry for farmers and the agricultural business. We hope to create a system that can assist in identifying and controlling these diseases more efficiently by leveraging the capabilities of deep learning. This not only protects agricultural yields but also adds to agriculture's sustainability and economic well-being. For this project we have chosen a suitable dataset from Kaggle: Kaggle Dataset: https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/emmarex/plantdisease/data Dataset Overview: The Plant Village dataset contains images of various crops, with each belonging to different classes representing diseases and healthy states. Having a diverse set of images presents an opportunity for deep learning models to capture intricate patterns and we can train our model for various categories which can make the detection more accurate.

suggestions.title: Optimized Mobile Deployment with TensorFlow Lite
suggestions.desc: Apply post-training quantization and pruning to the CNN model to reduce its size for mobile use. Build a Flutter application that allows farmers to identify diseases in the field via their phone camera without needing an internet connection.

suggestions.title: Multispectral Integration for Early Detection
suggestions.desc: Extend the dataset to include infrared or multispectral imagery. Modify the CNN architecture to accept multi-channel input, allowing the system to detect plant stress and disease before it is visible to the human eye in the RGB spectrum.

suggestions.title: GAN-Based Synthetic Data Augmentation
suggestions.desc: Use a Generative Adversarial Network (GAN) to generate synthetic images of rare plant diseases that have few samples in the Kaggle dataset. Measure how this synthetic data improves the F1-score of the CNN on those underrepresented disease classes.

suggestions.title: Smart Irrigation & Treatment Integration
suggestions.desc: Connect the detection model to an ESP32-controlled irrigation system. When the model detects a specific disease from a fixed-position camera in a greenhouse, it should trigger a relay to apply a localized water-and-treatment mixture to only the affected plant.

similar: legacy:UC-06|Spring 2020|chest x-ray disease detection w/ dl

title: Phase Estimation’s Application in QRAM
authors: Hunt, Ethan K
abstract: The paper proposes a new novel way of creating QRAM through quantum phase estimation. This is done by mapping a monotonically increasing sequence of natural numbers to a binary series and, ultimately, to a characteristic constant ? which is then encoded as a phase in a quantum state. This process leverages quantum phase estimation, a fundamental quantum algorithm for finding the eigenvalues of a unitary operator which can be used as a form of QRAM in either Quantum or Hybrid models of computing

suggestions.title: Noise-Resilient Encoding on IBM Quantum
suggestions.desc: Deploy the Phase Estimation QRAM model on real IBM Quantum hardware (e.g., IBM Brisbane). Implement error mitigation techniques like Zero-Noise Extrapolation to see how decoherence affects the accuracy of the characteristic constant ?.

suggestions.title: Quantum Key-Value Search Algorithm
suggestions.desc: Develop a retrieval algorithm that uses Grover’s Search in conjunction with this QRAM model to find a specific binary series stored in the phase. Compare the query complexity of this ?-based QRAM to standard bucket-brigade QRAM architectures.

suggestions.title: Hybrid Quantum-Classical Genetic Algorithm
suggestions.desc: Use the QRAM to store and retrieve a population of 'chromosomes' for a genetic algorithm. Implement a hybrid system where the quantum phase represents the individual's traits, and a classical computer handles the crossover and mutation logic.

suggestions.title: Phase-Based Data Compression Prototypes
suggestions.desc: Investigate using the ? constant as a form of quantum data compression. Build a prototype that encodes large natural number sequences into a single quantum state's phase and measures the fidelity of the data when reconstructed through phase estimation.

title: Enhancing Sarcasm Detection with Context Sensitivity
authors: Koganti, Sai Chandana, Yaganti, Varshini, Yadali, Venkata Sai Ashik, Yaganti, Vinesh Babu, Borra, Hemanth
abstract: Sarcasm identification is a vital challenge in natural language processing. In this project, we address this challenge by employing a context-sensitive approach that leverages deep learning, transformer learning, and conventional machine learning models. We conducted our research using two benchmark datasets: Twitter and Internet Argument Corpus (IAC-v2). Our three primary models—Bi-LSTM with GloVe embeddings, BERT, and feature fusion—outperformed baseline methods, achieving an 89.4% highest accuracy on Twitter datasets and an 81.2% highest precision on IAC-v2. These results highlight the effectiveness of our approach in sarcasm detection, with significant implications for sentiment analysis and opinion mining. While our project provides promising results on benchmark datasets, further testing on live tweet datasets is essential to validate its real-world predictive capabilities. This project contributes to the ongoing efforts to enhance communication understanding in the digital era.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Sarcasm Filter for Brand Management
suggestions.desc: Build a sentiment analysis tool for PR teams that uses the project's models to filter brand mentions. By distinguishing between genuine complaints and sarcastic praise, companies can respond more accurately to social media crises.

suggestions.title: Explainable AI (XAI) for Linguistics
suggestions.desc: Apply SHAP or LIME to the BERT model to visualize which specific words or context tokens triggered the sarcasm classification. This helps linguists understand the structural nuances of sarcasm in digital communication.

suggestions.title: Misinformation & Satire Browser Extension
suggestions.desc: Develop a Chrome extension that uses the trained models to flag sarcastic content in news feeds. This would help users distinguish between factual news and satirical sources like The Onion or sarcastic social media comments.

suggestions.title: Multi-Modal Sarcasm Detection
suggestions.desc: Extend the text-based research to include audio/visual data. By combining text features with prosody (tone of voice) or facial expressions using a fusion model, the detection accuracy could exceed the current 89.4% in video contexts.

title: Medical Records Summarization Using Prompt-Based NLP
authors: Masadeh, Rawan, Servies, Nicholas S
abstract: In this paper, we present an innovative Natural Language Processing (NLP) algorithm for summarizing medical records extracted from the MIMIC-IV dataset using state-of-the-art (SOTA) techniques in text summarization. The increasing volume of electronic health records (EHRs) demands efficient methods for extracting meaningful insights from these complex and extensive documents. Our algorithm leverages recent advancements in NLP, including transformer-based models, to automate summarizing medical records while preserving critical information. Our algorithm is trained and tested using the Medical Information Mart for Intensive Care (MIMIC)-IV database that provides critical care data for over 40,000 patients admitted to intensive care units at the Beth Israel Deaconess Medical Center (BIDMC) between 2008 and 2019. The algorithm aims to extract the query text from medical records in the MIMIC-IV dataset, which often contains diverse and extensive clinical information.
supervisor: Dr. Md Abdullah Al Hafiz Khan

suggestions.title: Privacy-First Summarization with Local LLMs
suggestions.desc: Port the summarization algorithm to run locally using quantized Llama-3 or Mistral-7B. This ensures patient data from the MIMIC-IV dataset (or real EHRs) never leaves the local network, solving the primary privacy hurdle in medical AI.

suggestions.title: Patient-Facing 'Layman Summary' Generator
suggestions.desc: Develop a specialized prompt-engineering layer that translates complex medical terminology into plain English. This would help patients understand their own discharge summaries or lab results without needing a medical degree.

suggestions.title: Clinical Decision Support Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Build a UI that highlights critical vitals and red-flag symptoms within the summarized text. By integrating with existing EHR interfaces, it can save clinicians hours of reading time while ensuring high-risk information is prioritized.

suggestions.title: Longitudinal Patient History Summarizer
suggestions.desc: Extend the model to summarize multiple records over time for a single patient. Instead of one document, it would track the progression of a condition, providing a 'trajectory summary' that shows if a patient is improving or declining.

title: Sudoku solver using brute force algorithm with backtracking approach
authors: Alam, Md Shamsul, ISLAM, MD JAHIRUL, Kommineni, Vyghni Sudha, Vellanki, Jaswanthi, Sudheer, Poka
abstract: Sudoku is a fun game that challenges our brain to think logically. It only has numbers from 1 to 9 in a 9x9 matrix network where the nine numbers should not be repeated in the same column, row or each 3x3 submatrix. Although there are numerous methods to solve this problem, the most common method is the backtracking algorithm. So, we are using brute force algorithm to solve sudoku and then compare it with backtracking to ensure which algorithm gives best results.

suggestions.title: CUDA-Accelerated Parallel Brute Force Solver
suggestions.desc: Port the brute force algorithm to C++ and CUDA to solve thousands of Sudoku puzzles simultaneously on a GPU. Compare the throughput (puzzles per second) against the CPU-based backtracking approach to find the break-even point for computational overhead.

suggestions.title: AR Sudoku Vision Solver for Mobile Devices
suggestions.desc: Build a mobile application using OpenCV to capture a Sudoku puzzle from a physical newspaper. Overlay the backtracking-generated solution onto the camera feed using Augmented Reality, ensuring the solver can handle perspectival distortion and varying lighting conditions.

suggestions.title: Comparison with Constraint Satisfaction Problem (CSP) Heuristics
suggestions.desc: Extend the research by implementing the AC-3 algorithm and Minimum Remaining Values (MRV) heuristics. Compare these optimized constraint-solving methods against the original backtracking model in terms of 'nodes visited' to determine which is more efficient for 16x16 'Super Sudoku' grids.

suggestions.title: Human-Like Hint Generator and Difficulty Scorer
suggestions.desc: Instead of just solving the puzzle, modify the algorithm to identify specific human strategies like 'X-Wing' or 'Swordfish.' Develop a scoring system that rates a puzzle's difficulty based on the complexity of the logical techniques required rather than just the number of empty cells.

title: A Simulation Model of the Traffic Signal System Using Java
authors: Chen, Lingtao
abstract: A traffic signal controls the flow of traffic at the intersection of two or more roadways. The first system of traffic signals was installed in London, England, in 1868. In this project, I will develop a simulation model for the traffic signal system using Java. The model will simulate the traffic signal system at a single four-way intersection. Also, I will compare the system performance with different input parameters, such as the number of vehicles and the cycle length, using various performance metrics, such as average waiting time and average sojourn time.

suggestions.title: Adaptive Signaling via Reinforcement Learning
suggestions.desc: Replace the fixed cycle-length parameters with a Q-Learning agent that adjusts signal timing dynamically based on real-time vehicle density. Compare the 'waiting time' metrics of the RL-based model against the original Java simulation's static parameters.

suggestions.title: V2I (Vehicle-to-Infrastructure) Communication Simulation
suggestions.desc: Model a scenario where 'smart' vehicles broadcast their speed and position to the signal controller. Simulate how an intersection could eliminate 'red lights' entirely for autonomous vehicles by using slot-based reservations instead of traditional signal phases.

suggestions.title: Emergency Vehicle Preemption and 'Green Wave' Routing
suggestions.desc: Expand the simulation from a single intersection to a 3x3 grid of roadways. Implement an emergency vehicle priority system that triggers a 'green wave' across multiple intersections, measuring the reduction in response time versus the increase in standard commuter congestion.

suggestions.title: GIS-Integrated Urban Traffic Digital Twin
suggestions.desc: Use the OpenStreetMap API to import real-world intersection geometries into the Java simulation. Allow city planners to upload actual traffic count data from local sensors to visualize and test 'what-if' scenarios for road closures or signal timing changes in their specific city.

title: Optimizing the Search in Location Using SVM, Naive Bayes, K-Means and KNN
authors: Mantha, Lalitha Sangamithra, Naraparaju, Krishna Chaitanya, Vadlamani, Abhishikth
abstract: The widespread adoption of global positioning technology has led to an increase in products featuring GPS functionality. These devices gather large amounts of location data. However, inherent inaccuracies in GPS data collection are unavoidable. To address these challenges, we shift our focus to identifying the closest points in a location. This requires gathering data to measure distances between a point and all others, and keeping a record of the nearest points. In this project, the Naive Bayes,SVM, k-means, and KNN algorithms are employed to determine the nearest points.

suggestions.title: Kalman-ML Hybrid Signal Smoothing
suggestions.desc: Integrate a Kalman Filter as a pre-processing layer to handle raw GPS noise before feeding coordinates into the SVM and KNN models. Use the 'FilterPy' library to implement the filter and measure if the reduction in signal variance improves classification accuracy in urban 'canyon' environments.

suggestions.title: Comparative Benchmarking with Hoeffding Trees
suggestions.desc: Extend the research by comparing the existing batch-learning models (like K-Means) against Hoeffding Trees (Streaming Random Forests) for real-time location data.
