This would test the system's ability to handle high-velocity data streams from IoT sensors where retraining a full SVM model is computationally expensive.

suggestions.title: Precise Geofencing for Last-Mile Delivery
suggestions.desc: Apply these classification algorithms to a logistics dataset to build a 'Proof of Arrival' system. Use the ML models to distinguish between a delivery driver merely driving past a house versus actually stopping at the porch, compensating for the 5-10 meter GPS drift typically found in mobile devices.

suggestions.title: RSSI-Enhanced Indoor Positioning SDK
suggestions.desc: Modify the KNN implementation to utilize Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) RSSI values alongside GPS data for indoor-outdoor transition. Build a mobile SDK that allows developers to maintain location accuracy inside multi-story malls or parking garages where traditional GPS signals fail.

similar: legacy:CPCS-17|Spring 2017|location services using wi-fi access points

title: Email Summarizer and Action Item Extractor
authors: Deem, Ryan, Weese, Eric C
abstract: Countless emails are sent and received daily, and a lot of time is spent reading through and understanding the content of these emails. This project aims to increase the efficiency of reading and gathering relevant email information. Our solution includes two parts: abstractive text summarization and action item extraction. Currently, these two items are common in different domains, however, they have not been combined and used with email understanding. Abstractive text summarization is the process of outputting the ideas of the emails using different words without giving quotes from the document. In this way, a person would be able to tell if the email is something they will need to look at and if not, then the Action Item Extractor tells what actions need to be performed, it involves finding all instances in a piece of text that are instructions, dates, or require something from the recipient. These two solutions hope to improve the efficiency of parsing through emails.
supervisor: Dr. Md Abdullah Al Hafiz Khan

suggestions.title: Calendar-Sync for Extracted Action Items
suggestions.desc: Create a plugin for Outlook or Google Calendar that automatically schedules blocks of time for action items extracted by the NLP model. It should detect deadlines (e.g., 'by Friday') and place them correctly in the user's schedule.

suggestions.title: Contextual Multi-Thread Conversation Summarizer
suggestions.desc: Refine the summarizer to handle long email threads with multiple participants. The system should distinguish between what User A agreed to do versus User B, creating a 'who-does-what' summary table for the entire thread.

suggestions.title: Voice-Activated Inbox Digest
suggestions.desc: Integrate the summarization engine with a Text-to-Speech (TTS) API. Users can ask their mobile device for an 'Inbox Briefing' while commuting, receiving a concise summary of their morning emails and a list of their top three action items.

suggestions.title: Executive Priority Saliency Mapping
suggestions.desc: Develop an importance-scoring algorithm that ranks emails based on the extracted action items' urgency and the sender's hierarchy. This creates a 'High-Impact Inbox' that only shows emails requiring immediate intervention.

title: Importance of Food Recognition on Blood Glucose Monitoring
authors: Crystal, Afnan
abstract: Maintaining blood sugar under control requires eating a healthy and balanced diet, exercising, and adhering to medications. Dietary consumption must be under strict control for diabetic patients’ general health. Traditional techniques for monitoring dietary consumption include recollection and manual record-keeping, which can be tedious and prone to mistakes. However, automated technologies for maintaining records that make use of computer vision, such as food image recognition systems, can streamline chronic health management for diabetics. These solutions seek to efficiently track daily food intake and consequential calories to facilitate and encourage lifestyle improvements. With this goal in mind, we design a Machine Learning model that can recognize/classify food categories and estimate the corresponding volume and calorific content from picture(s) of an upcoming meal, which would help users assess the effect of the intake on their blood sugar levels.
supervisor: Dr. Maria Valero, Dr. Valentina Nino

suggestions.title: AR Portion Size Estimator
suggestions.desc: Develop an Augmented Reality (AR) mobile app using ARCore or ARKit. By placing a virtual reference object next to a plate, the app can calculate precise food volumes, providing more accurate caloric and carbohydrate data than simple image recognition.

suggestions.title: CGM-Overlay for Post-Meal Prediction
suggestions.desc: Integrate with Continuous Glucose Monitor (CGM) APIs like Dexcom. The app would overlay recognized food images with the user's resulting glucose spikes, helping patients visualize which specific meals cause the most volatility.

suggestions.title: Transfer Learning for Diverse Ethnic Cuisines
suggestions.desc: Expand the model's dataset to include non-Western cuisines which are often underrepresented in standard food datasets. Use transfer learning on a specialized dataset to improve recognition for complex dishes like curries, stews, and mixed-ingredient bowls.

suggestions.title: Automated Nutritional Log via Edamam API
suggestions.desc: Connect the food recognition output to the Edamam Nutritional API. This would automatically populate a digital food diary with macro and micronutrients, calculating the glycemic load for every recognized meal without user input.

title: Advancing Non-Invasive Glucose Monitoring through Integrated Physical Factors and Wavelength Optimization
authors: Belfarsi, El Arbi
abstract: This work examines the effects of physical factors like skin tone, temperature, thickness, and humidity on the performance of GlucoCheck, a non-invasive glucose monitoring device using IR technology. It delves into how these variables influence light absorption and scattering in the skin, affecting IR image quality in GlucoCheck. The research addresses how skin humidity alters transmittance, and skin temperature and color diversely impact light absorption. These findings underscore the importance of considering these variables to improve glucose level predictions. We propose a data collection strategy using advanced sensors for real-time acquisition of these factors, integrating them into the algorithm for enhanced device accuracy. This strategy seeks to boost GlucoCheck’s reliability, contributing to personalized, adaptive healthcare innovations.

suggestions.title: Environmental Compensation Algorithm
suggestions.desc: Develop a machine learning model (Random Forest or Gradient Boosting) that takes skin humidity, temperature, and ambient light as inputs to 'normalize' the IR sensor readings, significantly reducing the error margin in non-ideal conditions.

suggestions.title: Personalized IR Calibration Mobile App
suggestions.desc: Build a mobile application that guides users through a one-time calibration process. Users would record their skin tone and typical environmental factors, creating a personalized profile that adjusts the GlucoCheck IR intensity for their specific biology.

suggestions.title: Cross-Device IR Benchmarking Study
suggestions.desc: Conduct a research extension comparing various IR wavelengths (850nm vs 940nm) against the diverse skin tone dataset mentioned in the abstract. Identify the 'universal wavelength' that offers the most consistent transmittance across the widest range of phenotypes.

suggestions.title: IoT Cloud Dashboard for Remote Monitoring
suggestions.desc: Connect the GlucoCheck device to an AWS IoT Core backend. This would allow the physical factors (temp/humidity) and glucose readings to be streamed to a cloud dashboard for remote physician monitoring and long-term trend analysis.

title: Using a Non-System Language to implement an Optimized Round Robin Scheduling Algorithm
authors: Nweke, Francis E
abstract: The objective of this project is to create an optimized Round Robin scheduling algorithm in C# and examine the related performance metrics. In this study, I will evaluate performance by implementing the optimized model in a non-system language such as (C#). Our simulation would provide insight into the execution of many processes while taking into account arrival times, burst timings, and user-defined time quantum. We can interact with the simulator because it is a Graphical User Interface (GUI) software.

suggestions.title: Neural-Network Driven Time Quantum
suggestions.desc: Replace the 'user-defined time quantum' with a Reinforcement Learning agent that predicts the optimal quantum for each cycle based on the current process queue's burst patterns. Implement this in C# using the ML.NET library to minimize context switching overhead.

suggestions.title: Cross-Language Performance Profiling
suggestions.desc: Perform a rigorous comparative study by porting the optimized algorithm to Rust and C++. Measure the 'Managed Language' tax by analyzing garbage collection pauses and memory management overhead in C# versus the manual memory management of system languages during high-load simulations.

suggestions.title: Visual Scheduler for Cloud Load Balancers
suggestions.desc: Adapt the GUI simulator to model a distributed system where the Round Robin algorithm schedules tasks across multiple virtual 'Nodes'. Add features to simulate network latency between nodes, showing how the algorithm performs in a non-local, distributed environment.

suggestions.title: OS Education Debugger Toolkit
suggestions.desc: Transform the simulator into a pedagogical tool for Computer Science educators. Add a 'Step-Through' debugger, the ability to export Gannt charts to PDF, and a 'Chaos Mode' where random process arrivals test the algorithm's robustness, making it a complete laboratory package.

title: Cardiac arrest prediction model
authors: Amsham, Vineeth, Balaiah , Sai Reddy, Subbarayakgounder, Tamilkumar
abstract: The "Cardiac arrest prediction model" project melds machine learning with healthcare to tackle heart disease. It aims to surpass current diagnostic tools that fail to catch early signs of cardiac events, often leading to high mortality. By developing an ML model that identifies early predictors of cardiac arrest, the project seeks to enable early interventions. Using supervised learning for its pattern recognition strength, the goal is to predict heart attacks accurately and thus, revolutionize preventative care and outcomes. This effort marks a leap in medical diagnostics and moves towards personalized healthcare, potentially saving countless lives and pioneering a new direction in the fight against heart disease.

suggestions.title: Low-Latency Edge Deployment for Wearables
suggestions.desc: Port the supervised learning model to TensorFlow Lite or CoreML to run on microcontrollers like the ESP32 or smartwatches. This allows for real-time, on-device cardiac monitoring without the privacy risks or latency of cloud-based processing.

suggestions.title: Explainable AI (XAI) for Clinical Validation
suggestions.desc: Integrate SHAP or LIME frameworks to visualize which early predictors (e.g., specific heart rate variability markers) are driving the model's predictions. This provides doctors with the 'why' behind a high-risk flag, making the tool more trustworthy in a hospital setting.

suggestions.title: Synthetic Patient Stream Simulation
suggestions.desc: Build a data generator using GANs (Generative Adversarial Networks) to create high-fidelity synthetic patient data that mimics cardiac events. Use this to stress-test the model's accuracy against rare but critical heart conditions that are underrepresented in the original dataset.

suggestions.title: Automated EHR Alert System Integration
suggestions.desc: Develop an API middleware that connects the prediction model to Electronic Health Record (EHR) systems like Epic or Cerner using the FHIR standard. This would automatically trigger emergency alerts on a nurse's dashboard when a patient's live vitals suggest an imminent arrest.

title: Synthetic DNA Sequence Generation and Classification for Species Discrimination
authors: Tasnim, Nishat
abstract: The two main goals of this research are to apply machine learning models in computational biology to classify DNA sequences from different species and to create synthetic DNA sequences using GANs. Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) synthesize DNA sequences while preserving key characteristics like sequence length and GC content. The dataset is enhanced by these artificial sequences, which makes classification jobs better. The classification accuracy of black rat and human genome sequences is evaluated using machine learning models, including Random Forest, SVM, and Logistic Regression. Notably, when trained with synthetic data, all models performbetter.

suggestions.title: Functional Motif Preservation in Synthetic Sequences
suggestions.desc: Enhance the GAN architecture with a custom loss function that validates biological motifs (like TATA boxes or promoters) using BioPython. The goal is to ensure synthetic DNA is not just statistically similar, but biologically viable for functional research.

suggestions.title: Transformer-Based Sequence Scaling
suggestions.desc: Replace the GAN backbone with a Transformer-based architecture (like DNABERT) to generate longer sequences. Compare the 'Biological Coherence' of the Transformer's output against the GAN's output to see which handles long-range genomic dependencies better.

suggestions.title: eDNA Species Identification Engine
suggestions.desc: Apply the project's classifier to Environmental DNA (eDNA) datasets collected from water or soil samples. Build a tool that helps ecologists identify the presence of invasive species or endangered wildlife from fragmented DNA found in nature.

suggestions.title: Synthetic Augmentation for Rare Genetic Disorders
suggestions.desc: Use the GAN to generate synthetic sequences representing rare genetic mutations where clinical data is scarce. Test whether training a diagnostic classifier on this 'augmented' dataset improves the detection rate of rare genomic conditions in real-world samples.

title: A FCFS Approach for Order of Operations in Arithmetic Formalism in Programming Languages
authors: Hunt, Ethan K
abstract: This paper provides the description of my research project on the computational power a programming language can have that uses an arthritic model that has a first come first serve approach to the order of operations. The goal of such analysis is to verify if such a method of computation can enclose all basic arithmetic and algebraic expressions, the answer to which will help disclose the computational limitations of certain programming language frameworks.

suggestions.title: Source-to-Source Transpiler for FCFS Arithmetic
suggestions.desc: Build a transpiler that converts the project's custom FCFS syntax into standard C++ or Python code. This allows developers to write in the FCFS paradigm while leveraging the performance and libraries of established high-level languages.

suggestions.title: Formal Verification of FCFS Turing Completeness
suggestions.desc: Use a formal proof assistant like Coq or Isabelle to mathematically determine if the FCFS arithmetic model is Turing complete. Prove whether a language restricted to Left-to-Right precedence can represent all computable functions without standard parentheses.

suggestions.title: Low-Instruction Set Arithmetic (LISA) VM
suggestions.desc: Develop a lightweight Virtual Machine optimized for FCFS operations. By removing the need for complex AST (Abstract Syntax Tree) reordering during runtime, you can create a high-speed execution environment for simple IoT sensors and embedded controllers.

suggestions.title: FCFS-Logic Educational IDE Extension
suggestions.desc: Create a VS Code extension with a custom Language Server Protocol (LSP) that visualizes the 'Evaluation Stack' in real-time. It would show students exactly how the FCFS model processes each operator sequentially, helping them unlearn standard PEMDAS biases.

similar: legacy:UR-06|Fall 2016|hopper's fables: a mathematical storytelling adventure

title: Developing a Conversational Chatbot using Seq2Seq Model with TensorFlow
authors: Parmar, Drashtee, Anugu, Ruthvik R;
abstract: Sequence-to-Sequence (Seq2Seq) modeling, when paired with Long-Short-Term Memory (LSTM) units, has demonstrated significant potential in developing conversational chatbot capable of participating in text-based conversation and providing human-like responses.The Cornell Movie-Dialogs Corpus will be used to extract dialogues, preprocess the data, and then use the output to train the Seq2Seq model. Our contributions include exploring the application of LSTM for Natural Language Generation (NLG) and creating a comprehensive chatbot system. According to the results of the experiment, our method works well for coming up with thoughtful answers during a conversation.
supervisor: Dr. Md. Abdullah Al Hafiz Khan

suggestions.title: Transformer Migration and Attention Visualization
suggestions.desc: Upgrade the LSTM Seq2Seq model to a Transformer-based architecture using HuggingFace's BERT or GPT. Build a visual 'Attention Map' that shows which tokens from the Cornell Movie-Dialogs corpus the bot prioritizes when generating a specific response.

suggestions.title: Persona-Consistent Dialogue Fine-Tuning
suggestions.desc: Segment the Cornell dataset by character archetype (e.g., 'Hero', 'Villain') and implement a persona-switching feature. This would allow users to toggle the chatbot's personality, forcing the model to generate responses consistent with a specific character's tone.

suggestions.title: Edge-Deployed Privacy Chatbot
suggestions.desc: Use TensorFlow Lite and quantization techniques to compress the Seq2Seq model for deployment on a mobile device. The result is a fully functional, offline chatbot that provides human-like interaction without sending any private text data to the cloud.

suggestions.title: RLHF Optimization for Conversational Safety
suggestions.desc: Integrate Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) to refine the bot's outputs. Train a separate 'Reward Model' to penalize toxic or nonsensical dialogue, solving the common issue where movie-trained bots use aggressive or inappropriate language.

title: A Multi-Model Approach for Detecting and Combating Fake News
authors: devineni, sujisha, Bodakunta, Thrisandhya
abstract: Internet plays a vital role in our daily lives, we use it for various purposes and benefit from advancements in technology and social media. However, the same platforms which make global information exchange also promote spread of fake news,raising a significant threat. To resist this issue, fact checking has become important, leading to extensive research to identify fake news and deal problems arising with them. Our project’s mission is to find the most effective model for fake news detection. We explore different approaches and models, like BERT, Decision Trees, Logistic Regression, and Ada Boost classification and evaluate their performance by calculating accuracy, precision, recall, and more. We aim to provide valuable insights on this critical fake news issue and show the best performing model among the pool of models.
supervisor: Dr. Md. Abdullah Al Hafiz Khan

suggestions.title: Multimodal Content Analysis Engine
suggestions.desc: Expand the multi-model approach to analyze images and videos alongside text using CLIP (Contrastive Language-Image Pretraining). This helps detect 'deepfake' infographics or memes that spread misinformation visually, which text-only models often miss.

suggestions.title: Browser-Based Fact-Checking Extension
suggestions.desc: Build a Chrome or Firefox extension that uses the project's best-performing model to highlight text on any webpage. Use a color-coded system (Red/Yellow/Green) to indicate the likelihood of fake news and provide links to reputable sources via the Google Fact Check Tools API.

suggestions.title: LLM-Generated Content Attribution
suggestions.desc: Incorporate a specialized classifier designed to detect text generated by Large Language Models (LLMs) like GPT-4. This research-focused extension would help identify if a misinformation campaign is being automated at scale by AI agents rather than humans.

suggestions.title: Domain-Specific Disinformation Detection
suggestions.desc: Fine-tune the existing models on a specialized dataset for high-stakes domains like Public Health or Financial Markets. This would involve scraping data from sources like the SEC or WHO to create a model that understands technical jargon and detects industry-specific fraud.

title: Meditation as an intervention to improve Student Attention An EEG study based on Machine learning prediction and Spectral Ratio Analysis
authors: Gopi, Sreekanth
abstract: This research aims to develop a machine learning model using EEG data to identify student inattention, serving as an early intervention tool. Attention deficit, influenced by social media, adversely affects student performance. ADHD, characterized by inattention, hyperactivity, and impulsivity, is linked to academic challenges. Early detection in academics is crucial. A Machine Learning model was designed, and trained on an attention dataset with 34 EEG recordings of young adults. The raw EEG data was pre-processed and filtered, ICA was applied, and spectral analysis was done. Guided meditation with music was developed as an intervention to improve attention. EEG recordings from 15 young adults during a visual reasoning test assessed the model's efficacy by comparing model output to test scores. The ML model achieved a 98% accuracy rate in classifying attention vs inattention states.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Neurofeedback Loop
suggestions.desc: Build a live Python dashboard using the MNE-Python library that streams EEG data from a Muse or OpenBCI headset. Create a visual feedback loop where a student's screen dims or changes color when the model detects falling attention levels, training them to refocus.

suggestions.title: Smart-Focus Pomodoro Integration
suggestions.desc: Develop a productivity application that adjusts study session lengths based on real-time EEG predictions. If the model detects high attention, it extends the timer; if it detects ADHD-related inattention markers, it automatically triggers a 5-minute guided meditation break.

suggestions.title: Cross-Dataset Generalization Study
suggestions.desc: Test the model’s performance on external EEG datasets like the 'DEAP' or 'SEED' datasets to see if attention markers are consistent across different populations. This would involve standardizing raw signals across different electrode layouts and hardware specifications.

suggestions.title: Meditation Efficacy Tracker
suggestions.desc: Create a mobile app that uses the ML model to compare attention levels before and after different meditation styles (e.g., mindfulness vs. transcendental). This provides users with a quantitative 'Attention Score' to prove which intervention is most effective for their specific brain chemistry.

title: Angel Among Us Pet Rescue - Website Enhancement with Chatbot
authors: Victorica, Koen, Huffstutler, John, Dooley, Chaz, Kovaltchouk, Elizabeth A
abstract: This project integrates an AI-powered chatbot into the Angels Among Us Pet Rescue website, enhancing user experience by efficiently addressing common queries. The chatbot uses large language model (LLM) technology, which in this project is ChatGPT, to understand and respond to user questions dynamically. A content management system (CMS) supports easy updates to the chatbot’s responses, allowing Angels Among Us staff to manage FAQ entries without technical intervention. The chatbot integrates seamlessly with the existing website, maintaining the organization’s aesthetic, accessibility, and compatibility across devices. This enhancement improves user engagement and streamlines support, enabling the nonprofit to focus more on its core mission of animal rescue. By providing accessible and accurate information through an intuitive interface, the chatbot is a pivotal tool in reducing repetitive inquiries and driving user interaction with Angels Among Us Pet Rescue’s online resources.

suggestions.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) for Adoption Docs
suggestions.desc: Enhance the chatbot by implementing a RAG pipeline using LangChain and a vector database (like Pinecone). Feed the system the actual medical records and adoption histories of current pets so the chatbot can answer specific questions like 'Is Buddy good with cats?' based on real data.

suggestions.title: Automated Adoption Screening Assistant
suggestions.desc: Build a multi-turn conversation flow that conducts initial adoption interviews. The chatbot can ask about yard size, children, and previous pets, then use the LLM to summarize the responses and flag high-quality candidates for the Angels Among Us staff.

suggestions.title: Multi-Lingual Adoption Outreach
suggestions.desc: Utilize the LLM’s translation capabilities to offer the chatbot in Spanish and other local languages. This would allow the rescue to reach a broader demographic of potential adopters and volunteers without needing a multilingual staff member on call 24/7.

suggestions.title: Voice-Activated Pet Search
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Web Speech API to allow users to search for pets hands-free. A user could say 'Show me hypoallergenic dogs near Kennesaw,' and the chatbot would query the pet database and read out the descriptions using natural language synthesis.

title: Karah Khronicles
authors: Green, Dion, Stipetich, Jake, Bowe, Grace, Israel, Jesse, Malatker, Vedasri
abstract: Karah is a thief with a heart of gold, you raid enemy camps and dungeons to steal back the money stolen from towns and villages and upgrade enchanted items to deal with dangerous foes. After successfully returning the wealth to the local town, you must then face down and defeat a general of the evil king.

suggestions.title: Procedural Dungeon Generation
suggestions.desc: Replace static levels with a procedural generation algorithm (using Perlin noise or Cellular Automata) to create infinite, unique enemy camps and dungeons, ensuring a fresh experience for every raid.

suggestions.title: Stealth-Focused Local Co-Op
suggestions.desc: Implement a networking layer using Mirror for Unity to allow two players to coordinate heists, introducing mechanics where one player distracts guards while Karah steals the treasure.

suggestions.title: Town Evolution Economy System
suggestions.desc: Create a persistent world system where returning stolen wealth triggers visual and functional upgrades to the village, unlocking better enchanted items and higher-tier missions for the player.

suggestions.title: Behavior Tree Enemy AI
suggestions.desc: Implement advanced Behavior Trees for the enemy generals, allowing them to learn from the player's tactics (e.g., setting traps in shadows) to increase the difficulty as the player progresses.

title: Biomedical Deep Learning - A staged approach using trustworthy deep learning for multi-omics data classification
authors: An, Yongbo, Liu, Tianze
abstract: Genetic data such as mRNA, miRNA, and DNA methylation offer precious insights into the underlying causes variant diseases. These types of data provide various layers of information, simultaneously enhancing our understanding of the disease and improving diagnostic accuracy. Combining mRNA, miRNA, and DNA methylation data allows for a multi-dimensional approach to identifying biomarkers, potentially leading to earlier and more accurate diagnosis. However, integrating all modalities is not practical. The clinical cost increases significantly with every modality incorporated. In contrast to previous methods, our model uses partial modalities when possible. We will use subjective logic and trustworthy deep learning under the staged approach to perform disease risk prediction. During our research process, we explored effective modality combinations for single view and bi-view models, designed an optimized multi-perception layer architecture for single-view classification, and implemented methods to quantify and optimize uncertainty in incomplete multi-omics data integration.

suggestions.title: Graph Neural Networks for Pathway Analysis
suggestions.desc: Extend the deep learning model by using Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) to represent multi-omics data as biological networks. This allows the model to consider how genes interact within a pathway, rather than treating them as isolated features, improving diagnostic accuracy for complex diseases.

suggestions.title: Federated Learning for Genomic Privacy
suggestions.desc: Implement a Federated Learning framework (using Flower or OpenMined) that allows the model to be trained across multiple hospital datasets without actually sharing the sensitive mRNA/DNA data. This solves the data-sharing bottleneck while maintaining patient privacy.

suggestions.title: Precision Drug-Response Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Build a web tool that takes a patient's multi-omics profile and uses the trained model to predict their likely response to specific chemotherapy drugs. This moves from 'classification' to 'recommendation,' helping oncologists choose the most effective treatment plan.

suggestions.title: Pan-Cancer Transfer Learning
suggestions.desc: Train the staged deep learning model on one type of cancer (e.g., Lung) and use transfer learning techniques to adapt it to rarer cancers with limited data. This explores whether multi-omics features of malignancy are transferable across different organ systems.

title: Volunteer Management System for Angels Among Us
authors: Duepa, Prince, Stoyanova, Teodora, Stoyanov, Radoslav, Jones, Madison W, Caballero, Rodrigo X
abstract: This project focused on creating a Volunteer Management System (VMS) for Angels Among Us (AAU) - a non-profit organization dedicated to rescuing and rehabilitating stray and abandoned animals. This application was developed to: * Handle comprehensive volunteer information * Streamline operations and better manage volunteer data * Support AAU specific use cases * Include a data enrichment capability through a newly developed GUI * Allow authorized users to add more comprehensive information to each volunteer record * implement reporting throughout the data migration process

suggestions.title: Gamified Volunteer Engagement Portal
suggestions.desc: Implement a 'Volunteer Leaderboard' and achievement system within the GUI. Volunteers could earn digital badges for milestones like '50 Hours Served' or 'Successfully Rehomed 5 Dogs,' using a simple backend logic to increase retention through healthy competition.

suggestions.title: AI-Driven Shift Matching Engine
suggestions.desc: Develop a recommendation algorithm that suggests specific volunteer shifts to users based on their historical data and stated skills (e.g., matching a volunteer with medical experience to a shift involving sick animal care). Use the existing database to track skill-task alignment.

suggestions.title: Mobile Check-In with Geofencing
suggestions.desc: Build a companion React Native app that allows volunteers to check in and out of the rescue facility using QR codes or GPS geofencing. This data would feed directly into the Volunteer Management System, automating the tracking of service hours.

suggestions.title: External Data Enrichment via LinkedIn API
suggestions.desc: Expand the 'data enrichment' capability by integrating APIs from LinkedIn or Clearbit. This would allow the system to automatically pull in professional skills or company affiliations for volunteers, helping the rescue identify people who can provide pro-bono legal, marketing, or vet services.

title: Streamlining School Bus Monitoring: GCPS's Transition to Real-Time Kafka Event Processing
authors: Fashinasi, Sarah, Hood, Tyler J, Sanderson, Jeffrey, McHie, Amali B, Baker, Alexandra
abstract: This project develops a prototype real-time bus monitoring system for Gwinnett County Public Schools using simulated Kafka event streaming to replace current API polling methods. The system processes simulated Asset Location and Speed events, mimicking Samsara's Kafka Connector, performing data validation before storing in SQL Server. The containerized solution demonstrates the potential for near real-time visibility into school bus operations.
supervisor: Dr. Yan Huang, Ed Van Ness - GCPS Technology and Innovation Division (Sponsor)

suggestions.title: Stateful Stream Processing with ksqlDB
suggestions.desc: Instead of just moving Kafka events to SQL Server, implement ksqlDB to perform real-time windowing operations. You can build logic to detect 'late' buses by comparing streaming GPS timestamps against a static route schedule stored in a Kafka topic, triggering immediate alerts when a bus is more than 5 minutes behind.

suggestions.title: Predictive Arrival Estimation Engine
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Google Maps Distance Matrix API with the existing Kafka stream to provide parents with dynamic 'Time to Arrival' predictions. Use historical transit data to train a lightweight Scikit-learn model that adjusts estimates based on time-of-day traffic patterns and specific Samsara speed events.

suggestions.title: Driver Safety and Behavior Profiling
suggestions.desc: Extend the event processing logic to analyze 'Speed' and 'Acceleration' telemetry for safety anomalies. Use Apache Flink to detect patterns of harsh braking or speeding in school zones, generating safety scorecards for drivers that are updated in real-time within the SQL Server database.

suggestions.title: Cross-Platform Parent Notification Portal
suggestions.desc: Build a React Native mobile application that consumes the processed Kafka data via a WebSocket server. Implement geofencing features where parents receive a push notification via Firebase Cloud Messaging when the bus enters a 0.5-mile radius of their specific stop.

title: IT Capstone Project 17 - KSU eSports Tournament Bot
authors: Helfrick , Patricia G, Jayakumar , Niranjanaa, Schroeder, Daniel J, Miller, Trinity F, Stogsdill, Jackson M
abstract: In this project, our team has automated tournament tasks in the KSU eSports Discord server, with a focus on the League of Legends tournaments. Our team has implemented a matchmaking algorithm that forms teams consisting of players placed within one tier of each other, so teams are evenly matched. Our team has also created a database that stores player statistics and has been integrated with the Discord bot. Furthermore, our team has integrated the developer API with the Discord bot, which pulls player data from the API when players join the server, and the team has been working to improve the UI of the bot. The team has performed regular testing and will continue to perform regular testing and updates as necessary. Department: Information Technology Supervisor: Prof. Donald Privitera Topics: Programming
supervisor: Prof. Donald Privitera, project sponsor Kylie Nowokunski

suggestions.title: Cross-Game Elo & Ranking System
suggestions.desc: Extend the bot beyond League of Legends by integrating the Valorant and Rocket League APIs. Develop a unified KSU 'Skill Rating' that tracks player performance across multiple titles and adjusts their tier for all university-wide tournaments.

suggestions.title: Automated Twitch Highlight Reel Bot
suggestions.desc: Use the Discord bot to listen for 'GG' or 'Big Play' messages in the match channel. It can then interface with the Twitch API to automatically clip the last 30 seconds of the KSU stream and post the link to a 'highlights' channel for social media use.

suggestions.title: Draft-Based Win Probability Predictor
suggestions.desc: Build an ML model trained on historical KSU tournament data that analyzes team compositions (champions picked) in real-time. The bot can then post a 'Win Probability' percentage to the Discord channel as soon as the League of Legends draft is complete.

suggestions.title: Automated Tournament Lobby Creation
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Riot Games LCU (League Client Update) API to allow the bot to automatically create private tournament lobbies and invite players. This removes the manual step of captains adding each other as friends and setting up custom game settings.

title: Attack Surface Management and Analysis
authors: McLemore, Danard S, Tanner, Nick A, Berry, Keshaun, Thairu, Nelson, Ciin, Niang
abstract: Recent advancements in AI have made knowledge more accessible, but this also introduces risks, as vulnerabilities can now be quickly found and exploited. To address this, we developed a comprehensive, cloud-native attack surface monitoring suite in Google Cloud. Integrating open-source intelligence tools like OWASP Amass and Project Discovery, along with custom Python-based processing, we gather extensive security data—covering subdomain enumeration, open ports, HTTP responses, and DNS configurations. This data is stored in BigQuery, processed, and visualized in Looker Studio for easy client interpretation. A containerized, scalable backend with a Flask-based API ensures seamless tool integration and adaptability. BigQuery ML further classifies domains’ security, empowering organizations with proactive risk assessment and attack surface monitoring.

suggestions.title: Automated CI/CD Vulnerability Gate
suggestions.desc: Integrate the cloud-native monitoring suite into a GitHub Actions pipeline. If the 'OWASP Amass' scan detects a newly exposed port or a vulnerable HTTP header in a new deployment, the pipeline would automatically fail, preventing insecure code from reaching production.

suggestions.title: Automated Exploit Validation with Nuclei
suggestions.desc: Take the reconnaissance data one step further by integrating the Nuclei template engine. This would safely attempt to verify if a discovered open port or subdomain is actually exploitable by running 'safe' proofs of concept, filtering out false positives from the report.

suggestions.title: Visual Attack Surface Drift Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Create a React-based frontend that visualizes the attack surface over time. Use D3.js to show a 'delta' view, highlighting exactly which subdomains or DNS records changed between the last two scans, allowing security teams to spot unauthorized changes instantly.

suggestions.title: Multi-Tenant Security-as-a-Service
suggestions.desc: Modify the Google Cloud architecture to support multi-tenancy, allowing a single instance of the suite to monitor separate organizations. Implement an OAuth2-based dashboard where different department heads can log in to see only their own specific sub-attack surfaces.

title: Eerie
authors: Kabar, Diana, Whorton, Joshua, Gann, Alex J, Freeman, Skyler A
abstract: Eerie is a psychological horror/thriller game that plunges players into the harrowing journey of Alice, a young girl trapped in her home. As she navigates the dimly lit corridors of her once-familiar environment, Alice grapples with haunting hallucinations and a distorted reality that intertwines the tangible and surreal. The gameplay revolves around her desperate quest to recover cherished belongings, each revealing deeper layers of her fractured story. Players must confront both real enemies and manifestations of Alice’s psyche, creating a tense dynamic that challenges them to strategize against both physical threats and the shadows of her fears.
supervisor: Prof. Kevin Markley (Spring), Dr. Sungchul Jung (Fall)

suggestions.title: Biometric-Responsive Sanity Mechanics
suggestions.desc: Integrate a heart rate monitoring library (using a smartwatch API or webcam-based PPG) to drive Alice's 'sanity' levels. As the player's real-life heart rate increases, the game's shader effects should intensify, increasing the frequency of hallucinations and distorting the audio to create a tighter feedback loop between player and character.

suggestions.title: Procedural Non-Euclidean Level Design
suggestions.desc: Implement a 'recursive room' system using Unreal Engine's stencil buffers or Unity's portal rendering. As Alice explores her home, use procedural generation to subtly change the layout behind the player (e.g., a door leads to a different room every time Alice turns around), reinforcing the psychological 'distorted reality' theme.

suggestions.title: VR Immersion and Spatial Audio Overhaul
suggestions.desc: Port the game to OpenXR and implement FMOD spatial audio to enhance the horror experience. Use binaural audio cues to lead Alice toward 'cherished belongings' and implement 'gaze-triggered' events where hallucinations only appear in the player's peripheral vision, disappearing when looked at directly.

suggestions.title: Asymmetrical Cooperative 'Haunting' Mode
suggestions.desc: Develop a multiplayer component where one player acts as Alice and another acts as the 'Distortion.' Using a mobile companion app, the second player can trigger environmental scares, flicker lights, or move quest items in real-time, turning the narrative journey into a strategic psychological battle.

title: Azure Migration Assistant
authors: Shenoi, Kunal, Allen, graham E, Hernandez, Angel L, Ngah, Yvan
abstract: Migrating to the Azure cloud platform poses unique cost-assessment and planning challenges. Our project introduces a user-friendly, AI-driven tool to simplify this process by providing real-time cost predictions and personalized migration strategies. Built with a React frontend and a Flask-based Python backend, this tool integrates Azure Pricing APIs to ensure accurate data. Future improvements include adding alerts, custom fine-tuned model, CI/CD, multi-cloud support, and a discovery agent for enhanced functionality.
supervisor: Prof. Sharon Perry, Andrew Anderson -- GTRI Sponsor

suggestions.title: Local Infrastructure Discovery Agent
suggestions.desc: Develop a lightweight Python-based agent that users can run on their local VMware or Hyper-V environments. The agent should inventory CPU, RAM, and Disk utilization over 24 hours and export a JSON manifest that the Migration Assistant can use to provide 'Right-Sized' Azure VM recommendations instead of 1:1 hardware matching.

suggestions.title: Multi-Cloud Cost Comparison Engine
suggestions.desc: Expand the Flask backend to integrate AWS Pricing and GCP Billing APIs. This allows users to input their requirements once and receive a side-by-side cost-benefit analysis across the 'Big Three' providers, including comparisons of Reserved Instance pricing and Spot instance availability.

suggestions.title: Carbon Footprint & ESG Reporting
suggestions.desc: Incorporate the Microsoft Sustainability Calculator API to provide an estimated carbon footprint reduction for each migration strategy. This adds a 'Green Computing' score to the AI-driven recommendations, helping enterprise users meet Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) goals alongside cost savings.

suggestions.title: Automated Infrastructure-as-Code Generator
suggestions.desc: Once a user selects a migration strategy, the tool should generate a complete Terraform or Bicep template based on the recommended Azure resources. This moves the project from a 'planning' tool to an 'execution' tool, allowing users to deploy their predicted environment directly from the React frontend.

title: SWAP - A Solo Developed FPS Game
authors: Schultz, Noah G
abstract: The Total Health Web Portal is a telemedicine application that allows users to orchestrate the process of a hospital visit without requiring anyone to leave their homes. The app assigns each user to 1 of 3 roles: client, doctor, or administrator. Clients are able to request appointments, upload documents, message their doctor, and join video calls for their appointments. The doctor may also message their clients and join video calls for their scheduled appointments. In addition, they may search through a list of their patients and dynamically add notes for each patient. Finally, the administrators have the responsibility of accepting/declining incoming appointment requests and managing accounts from within the database. The goal of the Total Health Telemedicine Application is to provide all participants with a way to effectively communicate and work in a single, convenient, and secure web portal.

suggestions.title: AI-Powered Symptom Pre-Screener
suggestions.desc: Integrate a medical NLP model (like BioBERT) into the client portal. Before requesting an appointment, clients can input their symptoms; the system then provides a preliminary severity score and suggests which specialist (Doctor role) they should book with.

suggestions.title: Blockchain-Verified Medical Records
suggestions.desc: Implement a private Ethereum or Hyperledger sidechain to store the hashes of uploaded documents. This ensures the integrity of medical records, providing a cryptographic audit trail that proves documents haven't been tampered with since they were uploaded.

suggestions.title: Wearable Data Integration for Doctors
suggestions.desc: Use the Google Fit or Apple HealthKit APIs to allow clients to sync their daily activity data (steps, heart rate, sleep). Doctors would then see a 'Vitals Dashboard' next to the video call window, providing real-time context for the telemedicine visit.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Telehealth Transcription & Summary
suggestions.desc: Use AWS Transcribe Medical or OpenAI Whisper during the video calls to create a live transcript. Build a post-call feature that uses an LLM to automatically generate a clinical summary and a list of 'Next Steps' for the client's portal.

title: The Eternal Guest - A 2D Hack-and-Slash Game
authors: Gamage, Luke H, Johnson, Zion, Hedges, Benjamin J, Walker, Bryanna N, Ewing, Avis A.
abstract: The Eternal Guest is a narrative-driven, hack-and-slash combat and exploration game where you make meaningful friendships, battle enemies, and regain lost memories as you traverse a strange, non-euclidian hotel. Use a wide array of weapons and abilities alongside the knowledge you gain from other guests to attempt to escape the bloodlust of a homicidal vampire. The Eternal Guest emphasizes 2D, top-down, melee combat in combination with ranged abilities, offering an exciting dynamic to gameplay. Our game also presents a unique spin on randomized exploration through its unique D.R.E.A.D. system, creating a sense of unease and uncertainty when exploring. This unique combat style and exploration, combined with a compelling narrative, keeps players returning not only for the story but for the gameplay as well.

suggestions.title: Procedural Non-Euclidean Room Generation
suggestions.desc: Develop an algorithm to procedurally generate the 'strange hotel' layout. Use graph-based map generation where 'Door A' leads to 'Room B,' but coming back through the same door leads to 'Room C,' creating a truly disorienting, non-euclidean infinite hallway effect.

suggestions.title: Dynamic NPC Relationship System
suggestions.desc: Build a 'Social Memory' system where NPC guests react differently based on your past combat choices and conversations. If you use 'bloodlust' abilities frequently, some guests might fear you, while others provide secret weapons, affecting the branching narrative.

suggestions.title: Asymmetrical Local Co-op Mode
suggestions.desc: Add a second player role who acts as the 'Hotel Spirit.' This player doesn't engage in melee combat but can manipulate the environment (e.g., closing doors on enemies, revealing hidden paths, or stunning the vampire boss) to assist the main fighter.

suggestions.title: Advanced Shader-Based Atmosphere
suggestions.desc: Implement custom Unity/Unreal shaders to enhance the 'strange' aesthetic. Create 'visual memory' effects where the screen colors shift or the world warps as the player regains lost memories, using post-processing stacks to emphasize the psychological horror elements.

title: Leveraging Large Language Models to Empower Caretakers of People with Dementia
authors: Olaniran, Mercy, Centeno, Emily A
abstract: Behavioral symptoms of Alzheimer's Disease and Related Dementias (ADRD) are detrimental to the quality of life for individuals with ADRD and their caregivers. Symptoms such as wandering, agitation, and confusion can often overwhelm caregivers leading to stress, depression, or burnout which can lead to a decrease in the quality of care. These challenges often result in increased hospitalizations and care costs, creating a need for a solution to support informal caregivers. This project proposes the development of an AI-based Dementia Care Voice Assistant application to meet the needs of caregivers. Using large language models, the application will provide real-time and personalized guidance to help caregivers manage complex behavioral symptoms. The LLMs will be designed to adapt responses to the user based on how they are prompted. To ensure that the output aligns with the best medical practices, we will establish a dataset based on evidence-based interventions from extensive literature reviews and interviews with informal caregivers. In addition to providing tailored responses, the application will offer assistance during emergency situations. The voice assistant will feature intuitive features such as recognizing signs of medical emergencies and prompting the user to contact 911 when necessary. Through the development of this application, informal caregivers will have access to accurate information and personalized assistance, alleviating caregiver stress, enhancing their confidence, and ultimately improving the quality of the care they deliver.
supervisor: Dr. Xinyue Zhang, Dr. Modupe Adewuyi

suggestions.title: Fine-Tuning Llama-3 on Clinical ADRD Caregiving Guidelines
suggestions.desc: Move beyond generic LLM responses by fine-tuning a model like Llama-3 or Mistral on the 'Alzheimer’s Association' clinical guidelines and caretaker manuals. This ensures the AI provides medically grounded, safe, and specific behavioral redirection strategies for agitation or wandering.

suggestions.title: Multimodal Wearable Integration for Agitation Detection
suggestions.desc: Connect the LLM to a smartwatch API (like Apple HealthKit or WearOS) to monitor heart rate spikes or erratic movement in the patient. The system could then proactively suggest calming scripts or interventions to the caregiver's mobile device before a situation escalates.

suggestions.title: Voice-First Caregiver Assistant for Hands-Free Support
suggestions.desc: Develop a dedicated Alexa Skill or Google Assistant integration using the project's LLM backend. This allows overwhelmed caretakers to receive immediate advice or log behavioral incidents using voice commands while they are physically assisting the patient.

suggestions.title: Localized Offline Deployment for Patient Privacy
suggestions.desc: Optimize the LLM to run locally on a high-end tablet or a dedicated NVIDIA Jetson edge device. This addresses the significant privacy concerns surrounding ADRD patients' data while ensuring the caregiver has access to support even without a stable internet connection.

title: Cybriant: Attack Surface Management
authors: Rai, Diwakar, Agyen-Frempong, Nicholas, Laurent, David, Gutierrez, Daniel, Mendoza, Jose R
abstract: As businesses and organizations expand their operation digitally, so too do the vectors for attack expand. In partnership with Cybriant, this application develops an Attack Surface Composite Score by breaking down various attack common vectors. DKIM records, Open Port Scanning, and other metrics are compiled with the aid of Google Cloud Run jobs, deposited into Google BigQuery for analysis, and packaged and generated using (Grafana/Kibana) as the front-end for our software stack. Our resulting application presents rapid, easy-to-understand breakdowns of various cybersecurity metrics and their impact.
supervisor: Dr. Yan Huang, Prof. Donald Privitera // project Sponsor mentors Byron DeLoach, Pramit Bhatia, Andrew Hamilton, Sean Mitchell

suggestions.title: Automated Security Policy Remediation
suggestions.desc: Extend the current BigQuery analysis to trigger Google Cloud Functions that automatically generate 'Terraform' or 'Ansible' scripts to close detected open ports. The end result is a 'One-Click Fix' button within the Grafana dashboard that patches vulnerabilities in the infrastructure-as-code.

suggestions.title: ML-Driven Zero-Day Risk Forecasting
suggestions.desc: Integrate a machine learning model (Random Forest or XGBoost) trained on the 'CVE' and 'CWE' databases to predict which attack vectors are most likely to be exploited next. Map these predictions against the organization's current DKIM and port scanning data to create a 'Forward-Looking Risk Score'.

suggestions.title: Supply Chain Shadow IT Discovery
suggestions.desc: Incorporate the 'Censys' or 'Shodan' APIs to scan for assets that are not officially registered in the company's Google Cloud environment. This expands the Attack Surface Management to include 'Shadow IT'—untracked servers and subdomains that often bypass standard DKIM and security record checks.

suggestions.title: Enterprise Security Compliance Reporter
suggestions.desc: Build an automated reporting engine that maps the 'Attack Surface Composite Score' to specific compliance frameworks like SOC2 or ISO 27001. Create a portal that generates PDF compliance audits for C-suite executives, showing real-time improvement in security posture over the fiscal quarter.

title: Intelligent Object Retrieval using Mobile Manipulator
authors: Zheng, Zhiwen, Ireland, Ellie
abstract: A mobile manipulator for intelligent object retrieval is presented. The system was integrated using state of the art R&D hardware and software, which implemented autonomous navigation, object recognition, and object pose estimation based optimal grasping. The retrieval of an object of interest is commanded that involves subsequent object detection and recognition while autonomously navigating using the known map and starting from an arbitrary position. From close proximity, object pose estimation based optimal grasp is selected to pick up the object. The object is retrieved back to the start position in this scenario. An 84% trial-phase precision in object retrieval is achieved that can be improved using better models.
supervisor: Prof. Waqas Majeed, Dr. Arthur Choi, Dr. Sharon Perry

suggestions.title: Semantic Mapping for Context-Aware Object Retrieval
suggestions.desc: Enhance the navigation stack by implementing Semantic SLAM, where the robot labels rooms (e.g., 'Kitchen', 'Office'). This allows for complex natural language queries like 'find the coffee mug in the kitchen' rather than relying on coordinate-based navigation.

suggestions.title: Deformable Object Grasping with PointNet++
suggestions.desc: Extend the pose estimation to handle non-rigid objects like clothing or bags using PointNet++ for better point cloud segmentation. This would allow the mobile manipulator to assist in household tasks like picking up laundry or moving grocery bags.

suggestions.title: Multi-Robot Collaborative Retrieval System
suggestions.desc: Implement a 'Scout and Fetch' swarm logic where a smaller drone identifies object locations across a large facility and relays coordinates to the mobile manipulator. Use ROS2's Zenoh or DDS for low-latency communication between the heterogeneous agents.

suggestions.title: Voice-to-Action Pipeline with LLM Reasoning
suggestions.desc: Integrate an LLM (like GPT-4o) to interpret ambiguous commands like 'I'm thirsty'. The AI would reason that it needs to find a water bottle, plan the navigation to the fridge, and execute the grasp, turning a simple manipulator into a proactive assistant.

title: Prison Minecraft Game Mode Plug-In
authors: Venable, Ryan S, Hunter, Carson R, Do, David
abstract: A project designed for Kennesaw State University's owned Minecraft server. The project centers around creating a Minecraft plug-in, a software product that is easy to activate in any Minecraft server. This plug-in changes the standard rules of Minecraft to become a classic game mode called Prison where players are taken to a special map and tasked with collecting resources in specialized mines or by fighting each other for them to earn in game currency for the purpose of buying their way to more privileged positions in the prison, gaining access to new areas and features. Prison was designed to work with KSU's current version of Minecraft and run with the Paper API used to write plug-ins like the project. In addition, the Prison plug-in features its own set of manageable plug-ins to handle separate aspects of the game mode's rules to enhance the player experience and allow for administrators to set the rules for the game and handle issues seamlessly and easily.
supervisor: Prof. Sharon Perry, Sponsors: Kylie Nowokunski, Alla Kemelmakher

suggestions.title: Decentralized Economy with Web3 Bridge
suggestions.desc: Build a Spigot/Paper bridge that links the in-game currency to a local private blockchain or an API like Enjin. This would allow players to truly own their 'Prison' assets and trade them across different server instances, creating a persistent meta-economy.

suggestions.title: Procedural Mine Generation and Reset Engine
suggestions.desc: Replace static mine maps with a procedural generation algorithm that changes the layout and ore distribution every 'reset' cycle. Use the FastNoiseLite library to create dynamic subterranean environments that prevent players from macro-farming specific locations.

suggestions.title: Reinforcement Learning Guard NPCs
suggestions.desc: Integrate the 'Citizens2' API with a machine learning backend to create NPC guards that learn from player combat patterns. These guards would adapt their patrol routes and fighting styles based on the most frequent 'prison break' attempts recorded on the server.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Player Behavioral Analytics Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Build a companion web dashboard using React and WebSockets that visualizes the prison's economy in real-time. Track wealth inequality, resource depletion rates, and PvP hotspots to help server admins balance the 'cost' of buying their way to freedom.

title: Designing a User-Centered Mobile Application for Anderson Power Services
authors: Guzman, Ryan, Goswick, Cooper, Phan, Anthony, Fotso, Marie, Francois, Larnel
abstract: This paper presents the design and development process of a mobile application for Anderson Power Services, emphasizing both frontend and backend aspects as well as their design. The frontend focuses on creating a visually appealing and user-friendly interface by utilizing clear text, an accessible color scheme, appropriate logos, animations, and modern typography. On the development side, the app leverages tools like Expo for rapid front-end development and integrates the Java-based backend with the Google Sheets API for easy data management. The backend architecture incorporates OAuth 2.0 for secure authentication, Gradle to facilitate a connection between the JavaScript frontend to the Java middleware, and Docker to connect it all. Overall, the project demonstrates the importance of balancing user-centered design principles with robust technical solutions for developing a functional and intuitive mobile application.

suggestions.title: IoT Generator Status Monitoring Integration
suggestions.desc: Connect the mobile app to generator telematics via MQTT or a REST API (like Generac’s Mobile Link). This would allow Anderson Power customers to see real-time fuel levels, battery health, and exercise schedules directly within the application's frontend.

suggestions.title: AR-Based Installation Site Surveyor
suggestions.desc: Utilize Expo’s AR capabilities (ViroReact or ARCore) to let users virtually place different generator models on their property. This helps customers visualize clearance requirements and aesthetic impact before a technician even arrives for a quote.

suggestions.title: Automated Service Dispatching and Technician Tracking
suggestions.desc: Build a 'Uber-style' technician tracking module using Google Maps API. When a service call is placed, the app should show the technician's real-time location and estimated time of arrival, pulling data from the Java-based backend.

suggestions.title: Predictive Maintenance Engine using Historical Faults
suggestions.desc: Implement a machine learning module in the Java backend that analyzes past generator fault codes and weather data. The app could then send 'Preventative Action' push notifications to users before major storms or based on equipment age.

title: OnAccount A Web-Based Accounting Software
authors: Jackson, Manuel A, Steele, Russell E, Loj, Grzegorz, Powell, Zachary B
abstract: This project streamline and improve the efficiency of the whole accounting process, by using current best practices for user interaction engineering and current design practices. Our software should be able to provide secure, user-friendly, and accessible financial management solutions anywhere and everywhere through various devices including desktop and mobile. Allowing users to manage their accounts whenever it seems necessary while still maintaining a high level of security. The project is inspired by the various complexity and problems regarding the accounting process in the real world such as financial reporting, miscalculations, and data security; by streamlining this process and making it more automated it will mitigate the risk and problems associated with accounting.

suggestions.title: AI-Powered Receipt OCR and Auto-Categorization
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Tesseract OCR engine or Amazon Textract to allow users to take photos of receipts. The software should automatically extract the vendor, date, and amount, and then use a NLP classifier to map it to the correct ledger account.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Bank Feed Integration with Plaid API
suggestions.desc: Connect the accounting software to real-world financial institutions using the Plaid API. This enables automatic transaction syncing and reconciliation, moving the project from manual entry to a professional-grade automated financial tool.

suggestions.title: Multi-Currency and Crypto-Asset Tracking
suggestions.desc: Expand the ledger to support multi-currency conversions using a real-time exchange rate API (like Fixer.io). Additionally, add a module for tracking cryptocurrency assets by pulling wallet balances directly from the Ethereum or Bitcoin blockchains.

suggestions.title: Immutable Audit Trail via Private Ledger
suggestions.desc: Implement a cryptographically signed audit trail for every transaction edit or deletion. By using a simplified Merkle Tree structure in the database, the software can provide a 'Proof of Integrity' report required for high-level security compliance (like SOC2).

title: KSU Esport: Competitive Speedrun Plugin for Minecraft Java Edition
authors: Amponsah , Rachel, Greene, Adam Y, Kirkwood, Christopher, Benjamin, Weeldy, Kelsey, Steven A
abstract: The KSU Esports Minecraft Speedrun plugin transforms traditional, manually managed speedruns into an automated team-based competition event. Players are challenged to complete a set of objectives within a set time limit – promoting teamwork and strategic planning. Various modes are supported, such as weighted/unweighted speedruns, team-based speedruns, and player free-for-all. Designed for flexibility, the plugin allows for customizable settings and support for future versions of Minecraft.
supervisor: Supervisor: Dr. Yan Huang, Sponsor: Kylie Nowokunski; KSU Esports Team

suggestions.title: Cross-Server Tournament Engine with Redis
suggestions.desc: Scale the plugin to support multi-server tournaments by integrating a Redis backend. This would allow a global leaderboard to sync across multiple Minecraft instances, enabling massive 'last team standing' speedrun events that aren't limited by a single server's CPU.

suggestions.title: Procedural Objective Balancing via Genetic Algorithms
suggestions.desc: Research and implement a system that uses genetic algorithms to generate 'weighted' objective lists. By analyzing historical player data, the system could automatically pair difficult tasks (like finding a Fortress) with easier ones to ensure every generated match is mathematically balanced for competition.

suggestions.title: Twitch Integration for Interactive 'Chaos' Speedruns
suggestions.desc: Build a bridge between the plugin and the Twitch API to allow viewers to influence the game. Fans could vote to change objectives mid-run, spawn 'sabotage' mobs for the leading team, or provide 'buffs' to trailing teams, creating a commercial-ready product for streamers.

suggestions.title: Gamified STEM Education Mode
suggestions.desc: Adapt the plugin for the Minecraft Education Edition domain. Replace standard speedrun goals with chemistry or logic-based objectives (e.g., 'Synthesize Magnesium Oxide' or 'Build an XOR Gate'), turning the competitive speedrun framework into a collaborative classroom assessment tool.

title: ChessAI
authors: Peeples, Joshua M, Miller, Ashton D, Corvacchioli, Matthew D, Luong, Dylan, Smith, Allen L
abstract: Chess is a widely acclaimed two-player strategy game, where the primary objective is to checkmate the opponent's king, placing it in a position of imminent threat from which it cannot escape. Our aim was to innovate within this classic framework by developing a novel chess game that adheres to the traditional rules while enhancing accessibility for players of all skill levels. This game features a selection of AI models, each offering unique decision-making processes that create diverse gameplay experiences based on the chosen model. The AI operates by simulating every possible move on the board, meticulously evaluating each resulting position. After assessing all potential moves, it selects the optimal one based on a sophisticated or simpler algorithm/pipeline depending on the selected AI model, tailored to counter the player’s strategy. In addition to the AI-driven gameplay, we also offer a local player-versus-player mode and an online player-vs-player mode, enabling two players to compete against each other on the same computer. The ability to select and play against different AI models ensures that both casual players and serious strategists can enjoy the game in a way that suits their preferences based on their skill level.

suggestions.title: Evolutionary Neural Networks for Playstyle Mimicry
suggestions.desc: Train a set of neural networks using NeuroEvolution of Augmenting Topologies (NEAT) to mimic specific historical Grandmasters. Users could choose to play against an AI that specifically emulates the aggressive style of Tal or the positional play of Capablanca.

suggestions.title: Stockfish-Powered Real-Time Move Commentary
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Stockfish engine via WebAssembly to provide a 'Live Coach' feature. Using an LLM, the game could translate the engine's evaluation into natural language, explaining *why* a move was a blunder or a 'brilliant' sacrifice in real-time.

suggestions.title: Computer Vision for Physical-to-Digital Play
suggestions.desc: Develop a companion mobile module that uses OpenCV to scan a physical chess board. The AI should then be able to 'digitize' the state of a real-world game and suggest the best next move via an Augmented Reality (AR) overlay on the phone screen.

suggestions.title: Blindfold Chess Mode with Voice Synthesis
suggestions.desc: Create a specialized accessibility mode that uses the Web Speech API for entirely voice-controlled gameplay. The AI should describe the board state verbally, allowing visually impaired players to compete against the AI models using standard algebraic notation.

title: IT Capstone 4983: HoneyBaked Ham Intranet SharePoint Site Transformation Presentation
authors: Andrews II, Martez D, Pyram, Kayla, Abdolkarimi, Amir, Flowers, Precious, Ceasar, Ardarius
abstract: The purpose of our team’s research is to explore and define ways in which we can advance aesthetics and functionalities of how website content and ideas are presented to HoneyBaked Ham end users. Our team has goals of identifying crucial focal point areas and various ways we can overall improve upon such. We will utilize practicality, ingenuity and creativity, in order to demonstrate and perform deliveries of proper new perspectives of the site. We will seek out such advancements we can add while remaining within necessary parameters, maintaining the respected, well renowned HoneyBaked Ham Brand. We would like it to be a commonality for users to observe inventiveness, dedication and spirit, from our team refining our prototypes and the results of our academic research efforts and excursions.

suggestions.title: SPFx-Based Interactive Store Performance Dashboards
suggestions.desc: Develop custom SharePoint Framework (SPFx) web parts that pull real-time sales data from HoneyBaked Ham’s POS APIs. This transforms the static intranet into a functional business intelligence tool where managers can see store rankings and KPIs.

suggestions.title: Automated Employee Onboarding Workflow
suggestions.desc: Use Power Automate and SharePoint Lists to build a 'New Hire' portal. It should automatically trigger hardware requests, generate training checklists, and send welcome emails based on data entered into the new site's interface.

suggestions.title: Intelligent Internal Search with Azure Cognitive Search
suggestions.desc: Replace basic SharePoint search with Azure AI Search to index corporate PDF manuals, recipes, and training videos. Implement 'semantic search' so employees can ask natural language questions like 'How do I glaze a ham?' and get direct answers from documents.

suggestions.title: Gamified Training and Recognition Extension
suggestions.desc: Integrate a 'Kudos' and 'Badging' system into the site using SharePoint's social features and a custom SQL backend. Employees could earn digital badges for completing food safety training modules, visible on their intranet profile.

title: Halo: A Volunteer Management Application
authors: Shattuck, Carson G, Carder, Willie D, Steele, Russell E, Wisdom, Jeremiah, Khan, Zeshan
abstract: Halo is a comprehensive volunteer management application (VMA) developed to address specific needs identified by Angels Among Us Pet Rescue (AAU). AAU's current system is unable to effectively manage and store complex volunteer information. As the organization’s needs evolved, AAU required a more efficient and secure solution to handle volunteer information. Our team designed Halo using the React.js framework for the frontend, the python based FastAPI framework for the backend, and a PostgreSQL database schema, with Docker containerization to ensure consistent deployment across environments. This setup enables efficient management of volunteer and team data, along with support for exporting reports in both .csv and .xlsx formats. To address security, Halo incorporates role-based access control (RBAC), differentiating access for administrators, editors, and readers. We also implemented a Google Sign-On feature with JSON web tokens (JWT) to validate users' Google login information within the database. Additionally, an Extract, Transfer, and Load (ETL) script facilitates secure data requests directly on the server. Halo’s frontend offers an intuitive interface designed to enhance user experience, with extensive search and filtering capabilities that make it easy to access and manage volunteer data. These improvements result in faster query responses and a substantial boost in usability compared to AAU's previous system.
supervisor: Dr. Yan Huang, Project sponsors Alla Kemelmakher and Taylor Cuffie

suggestions.title: KNN-Based Volunteer and Pet Compatibility Matching
suggestions.desc: Implement a K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) algorithm to match volunteers with specific pets based on temperament, energy levels, and volunteer experience. This ensures high-needs animals are paired with the most qualified 'Angels Among Us' fosters.

suggestions.title: Geofenced Mobile Check-in and Hours Tracking
suggestions.desc: Extend the React frontend with a mobile-optimized module that uses the Geolocation API. Volunteers can 'clock-in' automatically when they arrive at a designated rescue event or partner vet clinic, automating the storage of volunteer hours in PostgreSQL.

suggestions.title: Automated Emergency Foster Alert System
suggestions.desc: Integrate Twilio's API to build an emergency broadcast system. When a pet needs immediate pickup from a shelter, the FastAPI backend can filter volunteers by proximity and availability, sending out mass SMS alerts with one-click 'Accept' links.

suggestions.title: Public-Facing 'Foster-to-Adopt' Portal
suggestions.desc: Create a secure, public extension of the database where potential adopters can see pets currently in foster care. Include a direct messaging bridge between the volunteer (using Halo) and the public, while keeping the volunteer's personal contact info private.

title: INDY-5 Building Map Application
authors: Varnadoe, Harrison, Payan, Eduardo A, Wilson, Zach W, Haas, Lucas A
abstract: This project’s goal is to develop a simple secure mobile application for all devices to provide a detailed interior map that can guide users to any location in the building. It will use QR codes for ease of access, and the app will provide guidance via room numbers and a visual route. Our scope includes designing the architecture of the app, creating a responsive and interactive map User interface in an app that is compatible across all devices for a nice user experience.

suggestions.title: ARCore/ARKit Wayfinding Overlay
suggestions.desc: Extend the mobile app by integrating Augmented Reality (AR) to project a 3D visual path onto the user’s camera view. Instead of just a 2D map, users would see directional arrows on the floor in real-time as they walk through INDY-5.

suggestions.title: BLE Beacon Indoor Positioning System
suggestions.desc: Move away from manual QR code scans by implementing Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) beacons throughout the building. This would allow for 'Blue Dot' navigation, similar to GPS, where the app automatically tracks the user's location and updates the map as they move.

suggestions.title: Accessibility-First Pathfinding Algorithm
suggestions.desc: Modify the routing logic to include 'Mobility-Friendly' toggleable paths. The algorithm (like A*) should allow users to prioritize elevators and ramps over stairs, ensuring the app is fully usable for individuals with physical disabilities.

suggestions.title: Dynamic Occupancy and Heatmapping
suggestions.desc: Integrate a backend API that allows the map to display real-time 'busyness' levels for different rooms. By tracking anonymized user pings, you can visualize which areas of INDY-5 are currently crowded, helping users find quiet study spaces.

title: Anderson Power Services Mobile Application
authors: Diallo, Ibrahima, Roche, Joel J, Portier, Brandon D, Boerman, Ezra, Loj, Grzegorz
abstract: The APS Customer Experience Mobile Application streamlines and enhances customer interactions for Anderson Power Services (APS), focusing on customers who have purchased generators. This mobile application provides real-time updates, milestone tracking, and installation insights, allowing APS customers to monitor their generator installation progress with ease. By integrating Google Sheets APIs, the app enables seamless data synchronization, providing accurate and timely updates on generator status. With its cross-platform support on iOS and Android, the application reduces the need for manual communication, improving customer satisfaction and operational efficiency by 20%.

suggestions.title: PostgreSQL & Strapi Migration
suggestions.desc: Replace the Google Sheets API with a dedicated PostgreSQL database and Strapi (Headless CMS). This would allow for complex data relationships (e.g., many-to-many between parts and generators) and more robust authentication compared to a spreadsheet-based backend.

suggestions.title: IoT-Enabled Generator Health Monitoring
suggestions.desc: Simulate or integrate real-time generator data via MQTT or AWS IoT Core. Add a dashboard to the app where customers can see their generator’s current battery voltage, fuel levels, and last test run status alongside their installation progress.

suggestions.title: In-App Document Signature & Permit Tracking
suggestions.desc: Integrate the DocuSign or HelloSign API to allow customers to sign installation permits and contract amendments directly within the app. This removes the need for external paper or email processes, centralizing all project documentation.

suggestions.title: Computer Vision Installation Verification
suggestions.desc: Implement a feature where customers or technicians upload photos of the installation stages. Use a TensorFlow-based image classification model to automatically verify if a stage (like 'Pad Poured' or 'Transfer Switch Installed') is actually complete.

title: Accounting Treasury Industries Web Application
authors: Diallo, Ibrahima, Baker, Alexandra, Hood, Tyler J
abstract: This accounting software project is designed to provide a comprehensive and efficient solution for financial management within an organization. By focusing on ease of usability, accuracy, and compliance, the software enables users to record, manage, and analyze accounts, journals, and financial transactions seamlessly. Core features include transaction journalization, chart of accounts setup, financial statement generation, and robust account management, all of which are supported by strong data validation and secure access controls. This system seeks to streamline accounting workflows, minimize human/manual errors, and enhance user experience. The ultimate aim is to deliver an intuitive yet powerful tool that supports effective financial management and strengthens operational efficiency.

suggestions.title: ML-Driven Anomaly and Fraud Detection Engine
suggestions.desc: Implement an Isolation Forest algorithm in Python to scan journal entries for suspicious patterns, such as unusual transaction amounts or duplicate entries. Flag these for review in the 'Account Management' module to enhance financial security.

suggestions.title: Dynamic Financial Forecasting with ARIMA Modeling
suggestions.desc: Add a forecasting module that uses historical ledger data and the ARIMA time-series model to project future cash flow and revenue. This allows treasury managers to visualize 'best-case' and 'worst-case' financial scenarios on a dynamic chart.

suggestions.title: Customizable Financial Statement Builder
suggestions.desc: Build a drag-and-drop reporting tool that allows users to create custom Balance Sheets or P&L statements. Use a library like React-Grid-Layout to let users choose which accounts to group and how to format the exported PDF/Excel reports.

suggestions.title: Tax Compliance and Automated Bracket Calculation
suggestions.desc: Develop a module that automatically calculates estimated corporate tax liabilities based on current GAAP standards and tax brackets. The system should generate a 'Tax Readiness' report by aggregating transactions from specific taxable account categories.
