title: Cybersecurity Website Hardening Project
authors: Tandongfor, Robin N, Pursley, Jack W, vandorn, elijah, wilson, kylah, Wairimu, Valentine P
abstract: The project aims to secure the Akwaaba website using Apache, MariaDB, Red Hat OS, and PHP on a virtual machine. It includes assessing assets and vulnerabilities, implementing security policies, and conducting a red/blue team exercise for ethical hacking experience.

suggestions.title: Self-Healing Infrastructure with Ansible and Cron
suggestions.desc: Automate the hardening process by creating Ansible playbooks that run periodically to audit the VM. If a configuration file (like /etc/httpd/conf/httpd.conf) is altered or a service is stopped, the script automatically reverts it to the secure 'Golden Image' state.

suggestions.title: Deception Technology and Honeypot Integration
suggestions.desc: Extend the Red/Blue team exercise by deploying a 'honey-service'—a fake MariaDB port that looks vulnerable but records attacker IP addresses and command strings. Feed this data into an ELK stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana) to visualize real-time attack patterns on the Akwaaba site.

suggestions.title: CIS Benchmark Compliance Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Build a web-based dashboard using Python and Flask that scans the RHEL environment against Center for Internet Security (CIS) Benchmarks. It should generate a 'Security Score' and provide a one-click PDF report for stakeholders, turning the hardening project into a professional auditing tool.

suggestions.title: Micro-Segmentation via Kubernetes Migration
suggestions.desc: Transition the monolithic VM setup into a containerized architecture using Docker and Kubernetes. Implement Network Policies to ensure that only the PHP container can talk to the MariaDB container, significantly reducing the attack surface compared to a standard OS-level setup.

title: Golf Course Pace Management Simulation
authors: Miller, Ashton D
abstract: The game of golf has been played for centuries so it has seen handfuls of evolutions throughout its time being played. Throughout the games evolution one factor of its existence has hardly ever changed, time. In current day golf standards, time and the management of time is a significant part of not only how well the game as a whole run but how the courses that own venues to play the game operate as well. In golf there is a standard for time known as the pace of play model, where groups that are sent off during the day are queued into a course by whatever hole they're told to start on. If the golfers fall behind the pace of play standard, then largely, the courses flow of players that bring them revenue decreases in pace. Once a course experiences this decrease in pace, a large hit is taken to the course's financials. This widely affects everyone involved in the course's operation from cart attendants to restaurant workers, and lastly but not at all in the least, the management. These affects are not only present in a normal day of golf but also in tournament settings as well where groups finishing later in the tournament time frame may cost cart expenditures for the rest of the course not playing in a tournament and wanting to go out and get in a normal round. The project shown will display a knowledge of how this model takes precedence in the world of golf by displaying groups along with their carts queueing into the course structure and playing through a tournament. The goal of the project is to show the user the frequency of pace violations that occur and marshal interference needed in order to keep up the pace of the tournament so that the course can study time discrepancies and find where on the course these discrepancies occur and with what groups they occur in. In golf it is imperative that the pace of play model be successfully followed, otherwise structurally related to time, the course takes a hit both organizationally and financially.

suggestions.title: Real-Time GPS Pace Tracking for Marshals
suggestions.desc: Develop a mobile companion app that uses GPS to track the 'live' location of every group on the course. If a group falls 10+ minutes behind the 'Pace of Play' model, a signal is sent to a Marshal's tablet with the exact hole and group number.

suggestions.title: Weather-Impact Monte Carlo Simulation
suggestions.desc: Integrate a weather API to simulate how rain, wind, or extreme heat impacts the pace of play. Use a Monte Carlo simulation to predict total course throughput and 'bottleneck' holes under different environmental conditions.

suggestions.title: AI Tee-Time Optimizer for Revenue Maximization
suggestions.desc: Build a machine learning model that analyzes historical pace data to optimize the spacing between tee times. By dynamically adjusting the gap (e.g., 8 mins vs 10 mins) based on the day's player skill levels, courses can safely maximize their total daily bookings.

suggestions.title: Unity-Based 3D Pace Visualization Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Transform the flat simulation into a 3D visual tool using the Unity engine. Course managers could watch a 'replay' of a day's play in 3D, seeing where 'logjams' formed and identifying if specific course layouts (e.g., a long par 3) are the root cause of delays.

title: Real-Time Bus Monitoring Using Kafka
authors: Bostian, Samuel A, Rizig, Michael, McLarty, Charlie, Pruitt, Brian A, Roman, Allen
abstract: The GCPS Real-Time Bus Monitoring System aims to enhance bus operations for Gwinnett County Public Schools by transitioning from a polling-based system to a real-time Kafka event-streaming architecture. This project processes telemetry data from over 2,000 buses, simulating a scalable, near-instantaneous data flow into an SQL Server database. Key features include real-time data validation, efficient data storage, and containerized deployment for consistency across environments. Using an Agile approach, our team handled evolving requirements from the sponsor, who is new to senior project collaborations. This system enables GCPS to monitor bus locations with reduced latency, enhanced accuracy, and improved resource management, laying a robust foundation for future scalability and analytics.

suggestions.title: Predictive ETA with Streaming Machine Learning
suggestions.desc: Integrate River or Scikit-learn to build a real-time arrival prediction engine. Instead of just monitoring location, use Kafka Streams to process historical traffic patterns and current bus telemetry to output dynamic ETA windows to a live dashboard.

suggestions.title: Time-Series Optimization with InfluxDB
suggestions.desc: Replace or augment the SQL Server backend with a purpose-built time-series database like InfluxDB. Implement Grafana dashboards to visualize 'bus bunches' and telemetry trends that standard relational databases struggle to query efficiently at scale.

suggestions.title: Geofenced Parent Alerting System
suggestions.desc: Develop a mobile companion app using React Native that consumes the Kafka feed. Implement a serverless function (AWS Lambda) that triggers push notifications via Firebase when a bus enters a user-defined 0.5-mile radius around a specific stop.

suggestions.title: Predictive Maintenance for Fleet Health
suggestions.desc: Expand the telemetry schema to include engine temperature, oil pressure, and tire pressure data. Build an anomaly detection service that flags buses requiring maintenance before they break down, reducing service interruptions for GCPS.

title: Symptom-Based Disease Prediction
authors: Barber, Jarred M, Clark, Kody, Mwangi, Ryan, Zheng, Zhiwen
abstract: This project focuses on leveraging large-scale data sets and advanced analytical techniques to predict the onset of diseases. By integrating data from medical records, genetic information, and environmental factors, the project aims to identify patterns and risk factors associated with various diseases. Machine learning algorithms and statistical models are employed to enhance the accuracy of predictions, enabling early diagnosis and personalized healthcare interventions. This approach improves patient outcomes and contributes to the efficiency and effectiveness of healthcare systems.

suggestions.title: Privacy-Preserving Federated Learning
suggestions.desc: Research and implement a Federated Learning framework using 'Flower' (flwr.dev). This would allow the disease prediction model to be trained across multiple hospital datasets without ever moving sensitive patient data to a central server, solving the critical healthcare privacy barrier.

suggestions.title: Graph Neural Networks for Comorbidity Mapping
suggestions.desc: Shift from standard ML models to Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) using PyTorch Geometric. Model the data as a graph where nodes are symptoms, environmental factors, and diseases, and edges represent known medical correlations, allowing the AI to predict 'secondary' risks more accurately.

suggestions.title: FHIR-Compliant 'Diagnostic Assistant' Plugin
suggestions.desc: Develop a SMART-on-FHIR plugin that integrates the prediction model directly into Electronic Health Record (EHR) systems like Epic or Cerner. This would provide doctors with a 'Second Opinion' flag in real-time during a patient visit if the model detects patterns of a rare or emerging disease.

suggestions.title: Environmental Risk Factor Geo-Mapping
suggestions.desc: Integrate the EPA’s Air Quality and environmental datasets with the clinical data. Build a visualization tool that maps disease 'hotspots' to environmental stressors (e.g., proximity to industrial zones or high pollen counts), enabling public health officials to target preventative interventions geographically.

title: AC-10 AI & Music Processing
authors: Zboinski, Michael J, Kelil, Selam B, Wilson, Sterling J, Egwuatu, Michael
abstract: There is a broad range of styles and philosophies, for teaching young children how to play music. Some are based on repetition and memorization of songs, and others build up a foundation of musical patterns and motifs. Arguably, the latter style, will better develop the skills needed for improvisation and composition of new music. Inspired by this observation, we aim to improve the ability of (recurrent) neural networks to synthesize music based on a more careful training.

suggestions.title: Transformer-Based Long-Form Composition
suggestions.desc: Transition from Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) to a Transformer-based architecture like Music Transformer. This would allow the AI to maintain 'musical memory' over much longer sequences, enabling the synthesis of full multi-movement sonatas rather than just short motifs.

suggestions.title: Interactive 'Call and Response' Tutor
suggestions.desc: Build a real-time MIDI interface where a student plays a short pattern and the AI generates a 'response' motif in the same key and style. This 'musical conversation' app would focus on teaching improvisation by having the child 'talk' to the AI through their instrument.

suggestions.title: Style Transfer via Variational Autoencoders (VAE)
suggestions.desc: Implement a Music VAE that can perform 'style transfer' on MIDI files. A student could input a simple nursery rhyme they just learned, and the AI would re-synthesize it in the style of a specific composer (e.g., Mozart or Gershwin) to demonstrate how motifs are transformed in professional music.

suggestions.title: AI-Driven Practice Analytics for Teachers
suggestions.desc: Develop a commercial platform for music schools that analyzes students' practice recordings. Use the neural network to identify which 'musical motifs' the student is struggling with (based on rhythm/pitch deviations) and automatically generate custom-tailored sheet music exercises for their next session.

title: Using Dynamic Difficulty Adjustment (DDA) to improve health and wellness apps and programs
authors: Orfila, Fernando
abstract: The U.S. Army Corp of Engineers in Carters Lake required a game that attempts to educate on the principles of both prescribed and non-prescribed fires. They request that the game be playable on an android device housed in a HOLOFIL holographic display. Additionally, it needed to be created in the mirrored format as the projection flips everything. We decided to use Unity Real-Time Development Platform to complete the game’s development. Unity is a set of tools to give content creators an all-in-one development environment that helps content creators develop games and applications. “Unity is royalty-free and does not charge on a per-title basis or require revenue sharing of your application (Unity, 2022).”

suggestions.title: Biometric-Linked Difficulty Scaling
suggestions.desc: Integrate a wearable SDK (like Polar or Garmin) to feed real-time heart rate data into the Unity DDA engine. If a player's stress levels (heart rate variability) spike during the fire simulation, the game can automatically simplify the mechanics to prioritize learning over frustration.

suggestions.title: Urban Fire and Reforestation Modules
suggestions.desc: Extend the simulator beyond forest fires to include 'Urban Interface' scenarios where fire meets residential areas. Add a post-game phase where players must use a budget to replant the forest, teaching the long-term ecological consequences of fire management.

suggestions.title: Holofil vs. Standard VR Retention Study
suggestions.desc: Build a data-logging backend to track player performance and knowledge retention. Conduct a formal study comparing the HOLOFIL holographic display against traditional 2D Android screens and VR headsets to determine which medium leads to better fire safety education.

suggestions.title: The 'Holo-DDA' Universal Educational Framework
suggestions.desc: Decouple the DDA logic and holographic mirroring setup into a reusable Unity Package. Market this framework to other government agencies (like the Park Service or FEMA) to quickly convert their 2D safety training modules into interactive holographic experiences.

title: Campus AI Companion Mobile App
authors: Taponzing Donfack, Dorian, Takou , Aurelien, Sokoudjou Gatsing, Leopold, Djoumessi, Yann, Koodjo, Manuella
abstract: This application was part of an industry Capstone project with Georgia Tech Research Institute, with Mr. Matthew Pinkston as the industry sponsor, Ms. Allison Boyle as the industry Capstone coordinator, and Mr. Donald Privitera as the Capstone professor. Our team worked on creating the framework for an opensource, web-based IT management application that future developers can add various features and sub-applications onto for purposes such as asset and budget management. The application is PHP-based with its backend, utilizing the Laravel framework and several of its built-in features for routing, database management, login authentication with bcrypt hash security, and session tracking. The frontend design of the web application was done using HTML, Bootstrap, and jQuery. MySQL was used for the database, which is currently used primarily for the user authentication system. We have chosen the MIT licensing model to ensure future developers can easily add onto the code and incorporate sub-applications into our framework, which was created with the intent of being a basis for developers to add functionality to as they see fit. It also ensures the application serves as a free, opensource solution for small IT management departments.

suggestions.title: PHP to FastAPI Microservices Migration
suggestions.desc: Refactor the PHP-based backend into a modern, containerized microservices architecture using Python/FastAPI. This allows for better integration of AI models and more scalable handling of asset management sub-applications.

suggestions.title: RAG-Powered Campus Policy Oracle
suggestions.desc: Implement a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system using LangChain and a vector database (like Pinecone). Feed the app campus IT manuals and policy PDFs so users can ask natural language questions about asset procurement or budget rules.

suggestions.title: Zero-Trust Asset Verification Module
suggestions.desc: Research and implement a 'Zero-Trust' security layer within the IT management framework. Develop a feature where every hardware asset must verify its identity through encrypted handshakes before it can be modified in the database, preventing unauthorized inventory tampering.

suggestions.title: Automated Vendor API Procurement
suggestions.desc: Build a module that connects the budget management feature to external vendor APIs (like CDW or Amazon Business). When an asset is flagged for replacement, the app could automatically generate price comparisons and pre-filled purchase requisitions.

title: School Bus Monitoring Simulation w/ Apache Kafka
authors: Blake, John, Topping, Hudson, Kim, Eun Sik, Bui, Tiep, Lois, Julio
abstract: Our project is a proof-of-concept of event-streaming bus telemetry data using Apache Kafka (Kafka), as requested by our client, Gwinnett County Public Schools (GCPS). The Kafka event stream is more efficient than GCPS’s current process of pulling bus telemetry data: calling APIs every 5 seconds. Moving to Kafka will provide GCPS near real-time insights into bus locations and speeds, giving visibility to whether buses drive safely and punctually. Our simulation produces synthetic bus data to be passed through Kafka and consumed. It features two UIs for system monitoring and data visualization.

suggestions.title: Emergency 'Safe-Zone' Notification System
suggestions.desc: Build an automated alert system using Twilio that triggers when a bus deviates from its assigned geofence or stops for an extended period in a non-stop zone. This provides school administrators with a commercial-grade safety tool that moves beyond simple monitoring to active incident response.

suggestions.title: Traffic Congestion Modeling with Graph Neural Networks
suggestions.desc: Use the synthetic telemetry data to build a graph-based traffic model. By treating bus stops as nodes and bus paths as edges, you can train a GNN to predict how a delay on one major road will ripple through the entire school bus network, allowing for 'predictive rerouting' in real-time.

suggestions.title: Student 'Bus-is-Coming' Push Notification App
suggestions.desc: Develop a lightweight React Native app for students and parents that consumes the Kafka stream. Use Firebase Cloud Messaging to send a push notification when the bus is exactly 2 stops away, solving the 'waiting in the rain' problem using the project's high-frequency telemetry.

suggestions.title: Synthetic Data Generator for Urban Planning
suggestions.desc: Expand the 'synthetic bus data' generator into a robust tool for urban planners. It should allow users to simulate 'What If' scenarios—such as adding 500 new students to a district or closing a major bridge—and use Kafka to stream the resulting logistical impact on the current fleet's punctuality.

title: IoT Security Vulnerabilities and How to Improve Them
authors: Klein, Austin D, naderi, ryeon N, Hood, Tyler J, Bassourou, Keren
abstract: With the increased usage of IoT devices in homes as well as different industries, vulnerabilities have also increased significantly. The IoT devices are small in size, and it is hard to incorporate security in the software because security has high demand for computation. We have been conducting this research in order to find more suitable security methods that are lightweight as well as efficient. We have decided to move away from key hiding algorithms, which have increased time and space consumption, in favor of smaller and quicker block cipher algorithms.

suggestions.title: Hardware-Rooted IoT Authentication using PUFs
suggestions.desc: Replace software-based keys with Physical Unclonable Functions (PUFs) to create a hardware-level 'fingerprint' for each IoT device. Develop a lightweight authentication protocol that uses these unique silicon variations to prevent device spoofing without storing sensitive keys in memory.

suggestions.title: Formal Verification of Lightweight Cryptographic Primitives
suggestions.desc: Use tools like Coq or TLA+ to mathematically prove the security properties of the proposed lightweight algorithms. This research extension would identify potential edge cases in the state machine where the reduced computation might lead to timing attacks or side-channel vulnerabilities.

suggestions.title: Decentralized IoT Identity Management via Blockchain
suggestions.desc: Integrate the lightweight security methods with a Distributed Ledger Technology (DLT) like IOTA or Hyperledger Fabric to manage device identities. This removes the single point of failure inherent in centralized IoT hubs and allows for secure, peer-to-peer data validation between constrained devices.

suggestions.title: Zero-Trust MQTT Proxy for Legacy Home Automation
suggestions.desc: Build a middleware bridge that wraps existing insecure IoT devices (using protocols like Zigbee or MQTT) in the new lightweight security layer. This would act as a security gateway that audits traffic and enforces strict identity verification before allowing commands to reach the smart home hardware.

title: Legal-Insight - Legal text summarizer
authors: Rogannagari, Ravi K, Gunturu, Sasi Pavan Khadyoth, Sripathi, Lakshmi Narasimha Naidu
abstract: Many people struggle to fully understand complex legal documents, such as terms of service agreements, contracts, and privacy policies, due to dense jargon, small fonts, and lengthy paragraphs that make critical information difficult to grasp. This lack of clarity can lead individuals to inadvertently agree to terms they might not fully understand or miss important clauses. Recognizing these challenges, we developed LegalInsight to make legal information more accessible and comprehensible. LegalInsight simplifies lengthy legal documents by creating clear and concise summaries, allowing users to easily digest essential information. It also includes an interactive Q&A feature where users can ask specific questions about a document or its summary, receiving targeted responses that clarify confusing sections or terms. This tool serves a wide range of users—from students and elderly individuals to large corporations—by providing a user-friendly interface that makes navigating complex legal texts easier.
supervisor: Dr. Md Abdullah Al Hafiz Khan

suggestions.title: Browser-Integrated Consumer Rights Auditor
suggestions.desc: Develop a Chrome/Firefox extension that intercepts Terms of Service pages and uses the LegalInsight engine to highlight 'red flag' clauses (e.g., forced arbitration, data selling) in real-time as users browse.

suggestions.title: Domain-Specific Fine-Tuning with CUAD
suggestions.desc: Enhance the summarization accuracy by fine-tuning a base LLM (like Llama-3 or Mistral) on the Contract Understanding Atticus Dataset (CUAD) to identify complex legal obligations that general-purpose models often miss.

suggestions.title: Vector-Based Case Law Cross-Referencing
suggestions.desc: Implement a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipeline using Pinecone or Milvus to allow users to upload a contract and instantly see how specific clauses compare to standard industry benchmarks or previous case law.

suggestions.title: B2B Contract Negotiation Assistant
suggestions.desc: Build a multi-document comparison dashboard that identifies discrepancies between two versions of a contract, suggesting simplified 'plain-English' counter-offers to speed up the procurement process for small businesses.

title: Text-to-Digital Person Video Generator: DigitalAvatarGen
authors: Kesharwani, Akansha, Bagdwal, Nisha, Hossain, MD E, Patel, Drashti, ADIGOPPULA, NIKHIL
abstract: The Text-to-digital person video generator: DigitalAvatarGen project uses AI to create lifelike videos of 2D digital avatars from user text input. Users enter text, select a voice and select or upload an avatar, and generate a video using DigitalAvatarGen web application which uses Google TTS and SadTalker, to synchronize voice, expressions, and lip movements. Key contributions include a customizable user interface, personalized voice and avatar options, and an optimized backend for efficient video generation. This tool provides an engaging, realistic solution for applications in education, media, and customer interaction.

suggestions.title: Emotion-Aware Facial Expression Mapping
suggestions.desc: Integrate an Affective Computing module using DeepFace or FER to analyze the sentiment of the input text. Instead of static expressions, the avatar's lip-syncing and eyebrow movements would dynamically adjust to reflect the tone (e.g., excitement, empathy, or seriousness) of the generated speech.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Interactive NPC for Educational VR
suggestions.desc: Port the DigitalAvatarGen backend to a Unity or Unreal Engine plugin to create real-time interactive tutors. Students could ask questions via voice, and the system would use a low-latency pipeline to generate immediate, lip-synced responses from a 3D digital persona.

suggestions.title: Cross-Lingual Phoneme Synchronization Research
suggestions.desc: Investigate the accuracy of SadTalker when applied to non-English languages with distinct phonemes. The project would involve fine-tuning the model on datasets like Common Voice to ensure that lip movements remain realistic for languages like Japanese or Arabic which have different articulatory patterns.

suggestions.title: Automated Video Podcast Localization Suite
suggestions.desc: Build a tool that takes a source video of a human speaker, extracts the audio, translates it using Whisper/DeepL, and uses the avatar generation tech to 're-dub' the video. The end result would be a version of the podcast where the avatar speaks a different language while maintaining the original's visual identity.

title: Evaluating TCP Protocol Performance in Cloud Environments
authors: Ming, Nong
abstract: This research investigates the performance of four different TCP algorithms—BBR, Reno, Vegas, and Cubic in a high-latency and congested condition within a cloud-based environment using EC2 instances and Mininet for network simulation. The study aims to evaluate the throughput and congestion window (cwnd) behavior of each algorithm under various network conditions to identify their strengths and weaknesses. By analyzing the performance metrics across different TCP algorithms, we provide insights into their suitability for cloud infrastructure, contributing to optimized network protocol choices for cloud-based applications and services. The results offer valuable guidance for enhancing network performance in dynamic cloud environments.

suggestions.title: Reinforcement Learning Congestion Control (RL-CC) Benchmark
suggestions.desc: Extend the study by implementing an RL-based congestion control algorithm (like Indigo or Vivace) in the Mininet environment to determine if machine-learned models can outperform BBR in high-jitter cloud scenarios.

suggestions.title: Real-Time CWND Telemetry Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Create a live monitoring stack using Prometheus and Grafana that visualizes the Congestion Window (cwnd) and RTT of these TCP protocols across active AWS EC2 instances during simulated network stress tests.

suggestions.title: 5G/Edge Computing Performance Simulation
suggestions.desc: Modify the Mininet topology to simulate 5G slicing and edge-computing constraints, evaluating how BBR and Vegas handle the rapid handovers and high-bandwidth requirements of mobile edge networks.

suggestions.title: Cloud-Egress Cost Optimizer
suggestions.desc: Develop a decision-engine tool that recommends the optimal TCP stack for specific AWS instance types and regions based on historical throughput data to minimize data transfer costs and maximize link utilization.

title: Hybrid Approach of Data Mining and Deep Learning for Network Intrusion Classification in Big Data
authors: Alam, Md Shamsul, Patel, Yash, Gurram, Yaswanth srinivas
abstract: The growing complexity and volume of network traffic pose significant challenges to traditional intrusion detection systems (IDS), often leading to inefficiencies in detecting unauthorized access and malicious activities. To identify different types of network attacks, many intrusion detection systems (IDSs) have been proposed using artificial intelligence or machine learning, but the results are still not satisfactory for most of these systems. Recently in some research, deep learning models have shown promising performance in big data analysis. However, a combined data mining and deep learning approach in big data for the detection of intrusions has not been scrutinized. This research aims to develop a hybrid approach combining data mining techniques and deep learning models to improve the detection of intrusions in large-scale networks. In this paper, we propose a Genetic Algorithm (GA) and Minimum Redundancy Maximum Relevance (mRMR) to select optimal features by reducing the dimensionality of the dataset. Initially (mRMR) selects features based on high relevance to the target variable and also has the minimum overlapping information of those selected features. Then GA algorithm finds the best subset of features where it evaluates the various combinations of attributes and chooses the best ones to enhance the performance of the model. After that, the deep learning model Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) was introduced, which uses 1D convolutional layers to detect small, localized complex patterns by adopting structured data. By leveraging data mining for feature extraction and deep learning for anomaly detection, the proposed system seeks to enhance the accuracy and efficiency of IDS in handling big data. The expected results include improved detection rates, reduced false positives, and robust performance in processing large network intrusion datasets.

suggestions.title: Streaming IDS with Apache Flink integration
suggestions.desc: Move the hybrid model from batch processing to real-time stream processing by integrating it with Apache Flink or Spark Streaming to classify live PCAP traffic from a network mirror port.

suggestions.title: Adversarial Attack Simulation with GANs
suggestions.desc: Use Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) to create synthetic, 'stealthy' network packets designed to bypass the hybrid DL classifier, then retrain the model on these samples to improve its robustness against zero-day exploits.

suggestions.title: IoT Edge Gateway Guard
suggestions.desc: Deploy a quantized version of the DL model onto a Raspberry Pi acting as a secure IoT gateway, specifically trained on the UNSW-NB15 dataset to detect anomalies in low-power smart home device communications.

suggestions.title: Explainable AI (XAI) Security Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Incorporate SHAP or LIME libraries to provide security analysts with 'Why' explanations for every flagged intrusion, highlighting which specific packet headers or payload patterns triggered the malicious classification.

title: Optimizing K-means Clustering for Customer Analytics: A Multi-faceted Enhancement Approach
authors: Mota Jr , Carlos R, Neelam, Bindu, Bonigala, Dedeepya
abstract: This paper presents a detailed analysis of the al- gorithmic complexity of the K-means clustering algorithm, a foundational method in unsupervised machine learning. Although the problem of finding the optimal solution is NP-hard, K-means is widely used for efficiently partitioning data into clusters by minimizing within-cluster variance. We explore four main ideas for improvement:1) parallel points generation and processing for speeding up convergence, 2) penalty scoring for avoiding clusters with high variability within them, 3) utilization of other distance measurements such as Manhattan distance for providing better clustering in structures of objects of different nature and 4) probability addition in the form of Gaussian Mixture Models (GMM) for more adaptable and soft k-means clustering. A practical application of K-means is applied to telecommu- nication data to understand purchasing behavior via customer segmentation. It was observed that K-means++ provided the most optimum method for centroid initialization while parallel K-means performed the task of minimizing the execution time. Penalty scoring produced more balanced clusters compared with the baseline and GMM allowed for more flexibility in defining cluster boundaries. Initial findings show that K-means++ has an average silhouette score of 0.67 while the GMM method has an average silhouette of 0.65 which is particularly more appropriate for the complex customer segmentation.

suggestions.title: GPGPU Acceleration for Large-Scale Clustering
suggestions.desc: Implement the parallel points generation logic using CUDA or Numba to move the computation from CPU cores to the GPU, enabling the processing of million-row customer datasets in seconds.

suggestions.title: Dimensionality-Aware Clustering with UMAP
suggestions.desc: Research the impact of applying UMAP (Uniform Manifold Approximation and Projection) before K-means to see if pre-processing high-dimensional customer features improves cluster cohesion compared to the penalty-scoring method.

suggestions.title: E-Commerce Persona Automated Mapper
suggestions.desc: Build a tool that maps the mathematical K-means clusters to human-readable marketing personas (e.g., 'Bargain Hunter', 'High-Spender') using the UCI Online Retail dataset to generate targeted email copy via GPT API.

suggestions.title: Dynamic Re-Clustering for Real-Time Analytics
suggestions.desc: Develop a system that utilizes the enhanced K-means algorithm to update customer segments incrementally as new transaction data arrives, avoiding the need for a full retraining cycle every day.

title: Prompt Engineering and its Effects On AI and Human Relationships: A Contemporary Approach
authors: Madu, Francis, Kadiyala, Naga Janaki Madhav, Thallapally, Nivesh
abstract: A. Background: Prompt engineering refers to the process of designing and refining input prompts for AI models (especially language models like GPT) to improve their outputs. It has become a critical tool in maximizing the performance and utility of AI models in diverse applications, from customer service to content creation. Beyond technical aspects, the interaction between humans and AI is increasingly shaped by the effectiveness of these prompts. B. Motivation: As AI becomes more integrated into daily life, the way humans interact with AI models is profoundly influenced by prompt engineering. Misaligned prompts can lead to misunderstanding, confusion, or unintended outcomes, affecting both the utility of AI systems and the trust people place in them. Our project seeks to understand how different prompt strategies impact not only AI performance but also human perceptions and relationships with AI systems. By exploring these dynamics, we aim to develop best practices in prompt engineering that foster both efficient AI performance and positive human-AI relationships. C. Expected Results: We expect to demonstrate that well-constructed prompts not only improve AI output quality but also lead to more transparent, trustworthy, and meaningful human-AI interactions. This will be quantified through various metrics such as response accuracy, user satisfaction, and interaction smoothness.

suggestions.title: Self-Optimizing Prompt Engine with DSPy
suggestions.desc: Utilize the DSPy framework to build a system that programmatically refines prompts based on a set of success metrics, moving away from manual 'trial and error' prompt engineering.

suggestions.title: Longitudinal Human-AI Trust Study
suggestions.desc: Design a study and tracking tool to measure how user trust levels fluctuate over a semester of AI interaction, analyzing if higher-quality prompt engineering leads to anthropomorphism or increased skepticism.

suggestions.title: Bias-Detection Red Teaming Tool
suggestions.desc: Create a specialized interface that uses 'jailbreak' and 'adversarial' prompt techniques to audit LLMs for socio-cultural biases, generating a safety report for developers before they deploy customer-facing bots.

suggestions.title: Prompt Version Control & A/B Testing Suite
suggestions.desc: Build a CI/CD-style platform for prompt management, allowing teams to A/B test different prompt versions against production traffic and monitor response quality drift over time.

title: PET RESCUE AI-BASED SUPPORT APPLICATION
authors: Akintayo, Mariah, ajmal, sibgha, Ishtiaque, Kazi Nafis, patel, krut, Alexander, Chelsi
abstract: This project focuses on the development of an AI-driven support application for foster caregivers at Angels Among Us Pet Rescue. The application provides foster caregivers with real time assistance through an interactive chatbot, task reminders and resource management capabilities, streamlining the caregiving process. By leveraging automation and AI, the application enhances both the foster experience and operational efficiency aligning with the organizations mission of improving animal care.
supervisor: Dr. Jack Zheng, Project Owner: Angels Among Us Pet Rescue

suggestions.title: Computer Vision for Automated Pet Health Screening
suggestions.desc: Integrate a TensorFlow Lite model into the app that allows foster caregivers to take photos of a pet's skin, eyes, or surgical incisions. The AI could provide a preliminary assessment (e.g., identifying signs of infection) and automatically flag the image for a vet's review.

suggestions.title: Foster-to-Adopter Matching Recommendation Engine
suggestions.desc: Using the data gathered by foster caregivers, build a recommendation system that matches pets with potential adopters based on lifestyle compatibility. Use collaborative filtering to suggest pets to adopters whose home environment matches the pet's specific temperament documented in the app.

suggestions.title: NLP Sentiment Analysis on Foster Progress Journals
suggestions.desc: Apply Natural Language Processing to the text entries made by caregivers to detect early signs of 'foster burnout' or pet behavioral regression. This proactive monitoring allows rescue coordinators to intervene with support before a foster placement fails.

suggestions.title: White-Label Shelter Management SaaS
suggestions.desc: Expand the application into a multi-tenant platform that other rescue organizations can use. Features would include resource-sharing dashboards (e.g., sharing a surplus of food between rescues) and centralized volunteer training modules, creating a commercial ecosystem for pet rescue.

title: Athlete-Agent Connect Mobile App
authors: Ijagbemi, Ayokunle, Uzoka, Esther N, Omoniyi, Foluke
abstract: The Athlete-Agent Connect app aims to bridge the gap between athletes and agents, simplifying the process of professional engagement. By providing a digital space for talent acquisition and event coordination, the app fosters networking, recruitment, and collaboration within the sports industry. The platform’s features are tailored to meet the needs of athletes looking for representation and agents seeking clients, with tools for direct communication, event planning, and a calendar of relevant sports gatherings. This mobile app serves as a dedicated platform for athletes and sports agents to connect, collaborate, and enhance professional opportunities. The app enables athletes to hire agents for representation, allows agents to offer their services, and supports the creation of sporting events where athletes and agents can meet in person. Additionally, users can browse and RSVP to upcoming events related to their sports network.

suggestions.title: Blockchain-Verified NIL Contract Management
suggestions.desc: Integrate Solana or Polygon smart contracts to handle Name, Image, and Likeness (NIL) payments, ensuring that athletes receive automated payouts once certain social media or appearance milestones are verified.

suggestions.title: Computer Vision Performance Extractor
suggestions.desc: Develop a feature where athletes can upload workout or game footage, and the app uses OpenCV to automatically extract performance metrics like 40-yard dash times or vertical jump heights for agent review.

suggestions.title: AI Talent Matchmaking Engine
suggestions.desc: Implement a recommendation system using collaborative filtering that suggests agents to athletes based on the agent's historical success in specific sports, geographical regions, and contract sizes.

suggestions.title: Engagement Sentiment & Churn Analytics
suggestions.desc: Add an analytics layer for agents to monitor athlete engagement via natural language processing on internal messages, identifying which clients are at risk of leaving the agency based on communication patterns.

title: GTA Request / Hiring Management
authors: Madasu, Vineetha, Mandapaneni, Pavan Kalyan, Derangula, Tharun, Thotakura , Thushara, Nambuvaripalli Reddappa, Raghurama Reddy
abstract: The Graduate Teaching Assistant (GTA) Management System is designed to address inefficiencies in the GTA hiring and assignment process at the departmental level. This full-stack web application utilizes modern Python frameworks such as Flask for backend development, providing a robust and scalable foundation for data handling and business logic. The frontend is developed using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript, ensuring an intuitive and responsive user experience. Key features include user access, automated GTA request generation based on course catalog data, and real-time tracking of hiring progress. By integrating data import capabilities from sources like Owl Express via Excel, the system handles complex requirements like cross-listed sections and enrollment rules. This application aims to enhance administrative efficiency, reduce workload for faculty and staff, and improve the coordination and transparency of GTA management processes across departments. Through leveraging the power of Python frameworks and full-stack development methodologies, the system is both flexible and scalable, ready to adapt to future institutional needs.
supervisor: Course Instructor- Dr. Jack Zheng, Project Sponsor- Dr. Zhigang Li

suggestions.title: Optimal Assignment via Linear Programming
suggestions.desc: Integrate the PuLP library to solve the 'Stable Marriage' or 'Assignment Problem,' automatically matching GTAs to courses based on their skill sets, faculty preferences, and hourly availability constraints.

suggestions.title: Cross-Departmental Skill Marketplace
suggestions.desc: Expand the system to allow other departments to 'request' GTAs with specific technical skills (e.g., Python, Lab Safety) from a centralized pool, maximizing TA utilization across the entire university.

suggestions.title: Lab Attendance & Geo-fenced Timesheets
suggestions.desc: Develop a mobile-friendly module for GTAs to clock into their assigned lab sessions using GPS verification or QR codes, automatically syncing with the Flask backend for payroll processing.

suggestions.title: Hiring Fairness & Bias Auditor
suggestions.desc: Implement a research dashboard that analyzes historical hiring data to detect potential biases in assignments (gender, ethnicity, or seniority) and suggests 'blind' selection criteria to ensure equitable hiring.

title: Enhancing Workforce Management through Advanced HR Analytics
authors: Rekapalli, Pradeep, Gurram, Ruthvik Reddy
abstract: The business analytics of employee data is a concern that human resource departments worldwide deal with. Some big organizations have entire teams working on analyzing these metrics. To obtain insights about employee turnover rates, performance trends, and compensation patterns from data, the Data warehousing techniques—OLAP and ETL—can be used to handle data. This paper aims to develop an OLAP model for multi-dimensional analysis using data warehousing techniques that help extract valuable insights from the data. Popular datasets will be used, and the model will be evaluated according to standards.

suggestions.title: Predictive Attrition Forecasting Engine
suggestions.desc: Integrate a Scikit-learn machine learning pipeline into the OLAP cube to move from descriptive to predictive analytics. Use historical turnover data to train an XGBoost model that flags 'at-risk' employees based on compensation shifts and performance plateaus.

suggestions.title: Automated Cloud ETL Pipeline
suggestions.desc: Replace manual data handling with an automated pipeline using Apache Airflow and Snowflake. This setup should ingest raw CSVs from HRIS platforms, perform cleaning transformations in Python, and update the multi-dimensional model in real-time.

suggestions.title: Algorithmic Pay-Equity Auditor
suggestions.desc: Extend the compensation analysis module to detect systemic bias. Use regression analysis to identify pay gaps across protected demographics (gender, age, ethnicity) while controlling for performance metrics, generating a 'Fairness Score' for the organization.

suggestions.title: Internal Talent Mobility Recommender
suggestions.desc: Build a recommendation system that maps employee performance trends to open internal positions. Use the existing OLAP structure to identify skill gaps and suggest specific training pathways for high-potential employees based on historical success patterns.

title: CellNucleiRAG - Smart Search Tool for Cell Nuclei Research
authors: Koganti, Sai Chandana
abstract: CellNucleiRAG is a specialized tool developed to address a significant challenge in medical research: the rapid retrieval and synthesis of detailed information on cell nuclei. Understanding cell nuclei characteristics is crucial in fields like pathology, oncology, and diagnostics, where detailed cell analysis can guide disease identification and treatment planning. However, accessing relevant, organized information on specific cell nuclei types, datasets, models, and methods is often time-consuming, requiring manual searches through multiple, disparate sources. CellNucleiRAG solves this problem by acting as a smart search engine, designed specifically for cell nuclei research, combining traditional retrieval methods with advanced AI capabilities. Built with an underlying Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) architecture, CellNucleiRAG leverages MinSearch for rapid data retrieval, pulling relevant records from a curated dataset that contains information on various nuclei types, datasets, and analytical models. Once relevant data is retrieved, it is processed by an LLM (Large Language Model) to generate contextually accurate, human-readable responses. This dual approach ensures both precision and clarity, allowing researchers to receive comprehensive answers rather than isolated data points. Key technologies used in this project include Docker, for environment consistency; Flask, for a streamlined user interface; PostgreSQL, for storing interactions and user feedback; and Grafana, for real-time system performance monitoring. User feedback is incorporated to continually refine the tool, enhancing the accuracy and relevance of responses.

suggestions.title: Multi-Modal Nuclei Vision-RAG
suggestions.desc: Extend the current text-based RAG by integrating a Vision-Transformer (ViT) or CLIP model. This allows researchers to upload a pathology slide image and ask questions like 'Are there nuclei in this image similar to the Triple Negative Breast Cancer patterns in the literature?'

suggestions.title: Automated Meta-Analysis Generator
suggestions.desc: Build a pipeline that uses the RAG system to scrape PubMed for the latest nuclei segmentation models (e.g., StarDist, HoVer-Net) and generates a comparative summary table of their mAP (mean Average Precision) scores across different datasets.

suggestions.title: On-Premise Privacy-First Medical Knowledge Base
suggestions.desc: Re-implement the RAG architecture using a local vector database (ChromaDB) and quantized local LLMs (Ollama/Llama-3). This targets hospitals that cannot send sensitive pathology data to cloud APIs but need a searchable internal research index.

suggestions.title: Clinical Trial Eligibility Matcher
suggestions.desc: Connect the tool to the ClinicalTrials.gov API. When a user queries a specific nuclei morphology or biomarker, the tool can automatically suggest active clinical trials that are recruiting patients with those specific cellular characteristics.

title: An 8-bit Digital Computer Design & Implementation (Team COA-WM1)
authors: Sherard, Adrian L, Flores, Jesus, Lamsal, Biswash, Hammontree, Blake, Pitts, William
abstract: 8 bit computer design using NI multisim

suggestions.title: From Multisim to Physical FPGA
suggestions.desc: Translate the Multisim logic gates into Verilog or VHDL. Deploy the design onto an entry-level FPGA board like the Altera Cyclone IV to verify that the 8-bit architecture functions in real-time hardware outside of a simulation environment.

suggestions.title: Custom ISA Assembler and IDE
suggestions.desc: Develop a lightweight Python-based assembler and a basic IDE for the computer’s specific Instruction Set Architecture (ISA). This would allow other students to write and compile 'High-Level' assembly programs into hex files that can be loaded directly into the computer’s ROM.

suggestions.title: Cycle-Accurate Performance Profiler
suggestions.desc: Create a software-based emulator in C++ that replicates the exact timing and gate delays of the Multisim model. Use this to benchmark how many clock cycles common operations (like an 8-bit multiplication routine) take to execute on this specific design.

suggestions.title: 8-Bit IoT Bridge
suggestions.desc: Design a hardware interface (using 74-series logic) to connect an ESP8266 Wi-Fi module to the computer’s 8-bit bus. This allows the computer to 'request' data from a simple Web API, effectively turning the legacy-style design into an internet-connected device.

title: A Comparative Study of LLM Effectiveness in Mental Health Assistance
authors: Prasad, Kris
abstract: This study evaluates the effectiveness of LLMs in supporting mental health applications by analyzing their performance in understanding and categorizing user (mental health-related) inputs. We collected data from various mental health apps on the Google Play Store, including user reviews and app descriptions, and filtered content using a targeted mental health keyword bank. Sentiment analysis and keyword similarity scores were generated for reviews using RoBERTa-based models, this showed us how each review aligned with the mental health keywords advertised by the app and how users felt about the app. We prompted four modern LLMs: GPT-4o, Claude 3.5 Sonnet, Gemma 2, and GPT-3.5-Turbo. We provided Gemma 2 and GPT-3.5-Turbo with our dataset for more informed outputs. Our prompts consisted of five common mental health conditions (depression, anxiety, ADHD, PTSD, and insomnia) and we asked for the models to provide us with up to five app recommendations. The results showed that our data-enhanced LLMs noticeably outperformed the other state-of-the-art LLMs in accuracy, quality, and variety of outputs while being much more cost-effective. This suggests that data-enhanced, low-cost LLMs can serve as an effective alternative to newer, more powerful, and more expensive models, achieving notably better results in interpreting nuanced text for mental health applications.
supervisor: Dr. Md Abdullah Al Hafiz Khan

suggestions.title: Crisis-Alert Fine-Tuning
suggestions.desc: Use the project's 'mental health keyword bank' to fine-tune a small LLM (like Mistral-7B) specifically for high-sensitivity crisis detection. Compare its ability to flag self-harm ideation against the general-purpose LLMs used in the original study.

suggestions.title: Cross-Cultural Sentiment Sensitivity
suggestions.desc: Expand the dataset to include non-English Google Play reviews. Use M-BERT (Multilingual BERT) to analyze if the effectiveness of LLMs in understanding mental health sentiment holds true across different languages and cultural expressions of distress.

suggestions.title: RAG-Based Therapeutic Guardrails
suggestions.desc: Build a Retrieval-Augmented Generation system that forces the LLM to verify its advice against established clinical guidelines (e.g., DSM-5 or CBT manuals). This addresses the 'hallucination' risk identified in the original evaluation of mental health apps.

suggestions.title: Longitudinal App Version Tracker
suggestions.desc: Create a dashboard that tracks how LLM sentiment scores for a specific app change over multiple version updates. Correlate feature releases (from app descriptions) with shifts in user reviews to see if 'AI-driven' updates actually improve user sentiment.

title: Generative AI & Cybersecurity
authors: Canada, Seth G, Katare, Shreya
abstract: This research project details the impact of Generative AI on Cybersecurity through both its potential enhancements and threats. Using advanced AI algorithms, this project explores how Generative AI can strengthen cybersecurity through systems like Anomaly Detection, Intrusion Detection Systems (IDS), and Malware Analysis. Also, this project addresses the growing challenges posed from Generative AI. In particular, issues surrounding Deepfake Phishing and Polymorphic Malware are discussed. Solutions to mitigate these issues are also provided to engage further understanding in the field. The goal of this research is to offer practical solutions for addressing the growing field of AI-driven cybersecurity.
supervisor: Dr. Yong Shi, Project Advisor: Prof. Sharon Perry

suggestions.title: Polymorphic Malware Mutation Sandbox
suggestions.desc: Build a local sandbox that uses a LLM to automatically generate 50 variations of a known malware script by changing variable names and logic flow. Test if standard antivirus (Windows Defender) or the project's IDS can still detect the AI-mutated versions.

suggestions.title: Deepfake Phishing Defense Trainer
suggestions.desc: Develop a corporate training tool that generates 'AI-crafted' phishing emails tailored to a user's LinkedIn profile. Use this to measure employee susceptibility and provide real-time feedback on how to spot GenAI-specific social engineering markers.

suggestions.title: Natural Language IDS Log Summarizer
suggestions.desc: Implement a pipeline that feeds raw, cryptic Intrusion Detection System (IDS) alerts into an LLM. The output should be a plain-English incident report that explains the threat, the potential impact, and suggested mitigation steps for a junior security analyst.

suggestions.title: Prompt Injection Shield for Security LLMs
suggestions.desc: Research and implement 'NeMo Guardrails' on a cybersecurity-focused LLM. Test if an attacker can trick the system into revealing sensitive network topology details via prompt injection, and build a defense layer to sanitize these adversarial inputs.

title: Human-AI Annotator Tool
authors: Duong, Anh, Muse, Habeebah, Castro, Chase
abstract: The HK-01 Human-AI Annotator Tool is a web-based system developed to facilitate the annotation of Electronic Health Records (EHRs) for mental and behavioral health, using ICD-10 codes. The tool allows multiple expert annotators to tag critical information, making it easier to catalog patient data accurately. Future plans include integrating AI to streamline and scale the annotation process, improving both efficiency and accuracy.
supervisor: Dr. Md Abdullah Al Hafiz Khan, Dr. Arthur Choi, Prof. Sharon Perry

suggestions.title: Active Learning Annotation Loop
suggestions.desc: Integrate an Active Learning framework using Scikit-Learn to identify the most 'uncertain' EHR records. The system should prioritize these for human expert review while automatically labeling high-confidence records with ICD-10 codes to accelerate the scaling process.

suggestions.title: Inter-Annotator Agreement (IAA) Analytics Suite
suggestions.desc: Build a module that calculates Cohen’s Kappa or Fleiss’ Kappa scores between human and AI annotators. This would identify which ICD-10 categories suffer from the most ambiguity, helping to refine the annotation guidelines for mental health records.

suggestions.title: HIPAA-Compliant Collaborative Annotation Platform
suggestions.desc: Evolve the web tool into a secure, decentralized platform using zero-knowledge proofs or federated learning. This allows multiple hospitals to contribute to a shared annotation model without exposing sensitive patient PII outside their local networks.

suggestions.title: Multi-Modal EHR Tagging (Voice-to-ICD)
suggestions.desc: Extend the tool to accept audio recordings of clinician notes. Use Whisper AI for transcription and then apply the project's tagging logic to suggest ICD-10 codes directly from the spoken diagnosis, reducing manual data entry for doctors.

title: Emotion-Based Synthetic Feature Binary Classification of Human vs LLM Generated Text/Essay
authors: Xu, Tong, Lisasi, Rene P, Wu, Patrick
abstract: Sentimental analysis is a popular method to classify text into various emotional tones and intentions. In the meanwhile, the emergence of large language models (LLMs) has become ever more capable, their potential to cause harm through information fabrication, misleading propagation, or mere lack of capability has also increased. Therefore, our project is designed to discover any patterns that could potentially uncover texts origins of human and LLM during sentimental analysis. Our dataset covers over 57,000 lengthier essay samples (70% human vs 30% LLM), we use the state-of-art pre-trained DistilRoBERTa-base, a powerful pre-trained language model that is a more condensed and speedier version of Google's BERT, as our bidirectional transformer. Moreover, we plot the experiment results in histograms and statistical analysis and propose potential future research directions.
supervisor: Dr. Md Abdullah Al Hafiz Khan

suggestions.title: Domain-Specific Forensic Analysis
suggestions.desc: Test the classifier across different genres (e.g., scientific papers vs. creative poetry). Determine if the 'emotional gap' used for classification disappears when LLMs are prompted to write in a 'clinical' or 'emotionless' style.

suggestions.title: Real-Time 'AI-Likelihood' Browser Extension
suggestions.desc: Convert the trained binary classifier into a Chrome extension. As a user reads a news article or social media thread, the extension provides a 'Human-Likeness' percentage based on the emotional variance and patterns discovered in the project.

suggestions.title: Integrity Filter for Educational Platforms
suggestions.desc: Integrate the model into a Learning Management System (like Canvas) to flag student essays that lack the 'emotional 'burstiness' typical of human writers, helping instructors identify potential AI-generated submissions for further review.

suggestions.title: Multi-Model Fingerprinting
suggestions.desc: Expand the project from binary (Human vs AI) to multi-class classification. Train the model to identify which specific LLM (GPT-4, Claude 3, or Llama 3) likely generated a text based on their unique 'sentimental fingerprints'.

title: LLM enabled Synthetic dataset generation for Human-AI teaming Algorithm
authors: Potluri, Sai Sanjay
abstract: This research explores using Large Language Models (LLMs) to generate synthetic datasets for Human-AI teaming algorithms, focusing on mental health assessments. We create a diverse dataset simulating human-AI collaboration scenarios in diagnostic processes. The synthetic data is labeled through an innovative approach involving two human annotators and three LLMs, using majority voting for consensus-based annotations. This dataset serves as a resource for training and evaluating Human- AI teaming algorithms, enabling exploration of collaboration dynamics between human expertise and AI in complex decision-making. Our approach addresses the scarcity of real-world data in Human-AI teaming scenarios and provides a controlled environment for algorithm development, potentially accelerating advancements in this field.
supervisor: Dr. Md Abdullah Al Hafiz Khan

suggestions.title: Human-in-the-Loop Annotation Refiner
suggestions.desc: Build a web interface that allows human experts to review and 'overrule' the LLM majority-vote annotations. Use the data from these disagreements to calculate an 'inter-rater reliability' score between humans and the LLM trio.

suggestions.title: Synthetic Bias Quantification Tool
suggestions.desc: Analyze the generated synthetic dataset for demographic biases. Measure if the LLMs systematically associate certain mental health diagnoses with specific genders or ethnicities more frequently than they appear in real-world clinical data.

suggestions.title: Collaborative AI Resident Simulator
suggestions.desc: Use the synthetic dataset to train a 'Teaming Agent' that medical students can interact with. The agent acts as a co-diagnostician, presenting simulated patient data and asking the student to justify their diagnostic decisions in a teaming scenario.

suggestions.title: Crisis Management Domain Transfer
suggestions.desc: Apply the project's 'majority voting' synthetic generation framework to a different high-stakes domain, such as disaster response or legal mediation, to see if LLMs can generate high-quality training data for those human-AI teaming tasks.

title: An Empirical Study of Prompt-based Non-functional Requirements Classification
authors: Kim, Allen
abstract: In modern software development, Non-Functional Requirements (NFR) are essential to satisfy users’ needs. Distinguishing different categories of NFR is tedious, error-prone, and time consuming due to the complexity of software systems. In our project, we conducted a comprehensive study to evaluate the performance of prompt-based NFR classification by designing various handcraft templates and soft templates on the pre-trained language model (i.e., BERT). Our experimental results show that handcraft templates can achieve best effectiveness (e.g., 83.52% in terms of F1 score) but with unstable performance for different templates.

suggestions.title: Reinforcement Learning for Auto-Prompt Tuning
suggestions.desc: Implement a Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) loop to automatically optimize the 'soft templates.' The system should evolve the prompts over time to maximize classification accuracy across diverse software documentation styles.

suggestions.title: VS Code NFR Autoclassifier
suggestions.desc: Build an IDE extension that uses the project's BERT-based classifier to analyze code comments and docstrings in real-time. It should automatically label blocks of code related to 'Security,' 'Scalability,' or 'Maintainability' for better project navigation.

suggestions.title: Comparative Study of Few-Shot GPT-4 vs. Soft-Prompt BERT
suggestions.desc: Extend the empirical study to include modern Frontier models using Few-Shot and Chain-of-Thought prompting. Compare the cost-efficiency and accuracy of these large models against the more lightweight BERT-based soft-template approach used in the project.

suggestions.title: Automated Compliance Audit Generator
suggestions.desc: Develop a tool that scans a project’s requirements document and automatically generates a 'Compliance Matrix.' It should map the identified NFRs to industry standards like ISO/IEC 25010, ensuring developers haven't missed critical non-functional needs.

title: Integrated Sentiment and Behavioral Analysis of Online Product Reviews
authors: Yepuri, Kiran, Mahankali , Naveen
abstract: The "Integrated Sentiment and Behavioral Analysis of Online Product Reviews" project helps businesses gain actionable insights from Product reviews by combining sentiment and behavioral analysis using NLP models like VADER and BERT. This dual approach categorizes reviews as positive, neutral, or negative and identifies themes such as preferences and complaints through Named Entity Recognition and topic modeling. By capturing both the emotional tone and specific product feedback, this method highlights consumer likes and pain points, assisting in targeted improvements for product design and customer service. The project addresses challenges in analyzing complex expressions like sarcasm, providing a robust framework for extracting meaningful insights from vast amounts of review data. Adaptable across various datasets, the model offers scalable benefits for enhancing e-commerce strategies through data-driven decisions based on real consumer feedback.
supervisor: Dr. Md Abdullah Al Hafiz Khan

suggestions.title: Competitor Feature Comparison Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Extend the BERT/VADER pipeline to scrape reviews for two competing products simultaneously. Create a visualization that compares sentiment scores for specific 'Named Entities' (e.g., compare 'Battery Life' for iPhone vs. Galaxy) to identify market advantages.

suggestions.title: Span-Based Aspect Sentiment Analysis
suggestions.desc: Upgrade the model to link sentiment directly to specific entities within the same sentence (e.g., 'The camera [Positive] is great but the price [Negative] is high'). This provides more granular feedback than a single review-level score.

suggestions.title: Fake Review & Bot Detector
suggestions.desc: Combine the behavioral analysis with review metadata (e.g., timestamp frequency and user account age). Use this to build a filter that flags reviews with 'positive' sentiment but 'suspicious' behavioral patterns common in bot-generated reviews.

suggestions.title: Predictive Product Rating Forecasting
suggestions.desc: Implement a time-series forecasting component that uses current sentiment trends and emerging 'complaint themes' to predict if a product’s overall star rating will drop in the next 3 months, allowing brands to intervene early.

title: Automatic Categorization of Behavioral Health Issues in Police reports
authors: Pederson, Mason V, Azmee, Abm Adnan, Nweke, Francis E
abstract: 911 is often the first place contacted for dealing with behavioral health related (BHR) issues. Its estimated at least a fifth of all calls are related to behavioral health, and with BHR affected convicts having a recidivism rate of around 30%, its not hard to see how straining these issues can become on systems already stretched thin, where chronic understaffing is often a reality. A great solution would be if we could intervene as soon as possible to get people the treatment they need, police reports would be excellent for identifying and treating these individuals, but annotation is a long tedious task only certain people have security clearance to do and as mentioned earlier departments are often understaffed. That is why with the help of keywords given to us by behavioral health professionals, we have developed a model for automatic categorization of police reports that can classify police reports into several categories of class type (Situation, Situation Mental Health, Child, Disposition, Disposition Mental Health, Drugs, Medication, Medication Mental Health) by learning the correlation between co-occurrences of class types given keywords, evidence type given keywords, and class type given keywords and then combining those with the embeddings of a Feed Forward Network that analyzed relevant sentences from reports. With this model we were able to achieve an accuracy rate of 72% which was significantly higher than other state of the art methods typically used.
supervisor: Dr. Md Abdullah Al Hafiz Khan, Dr. Yong Pei

suggestions.title: Real-Time 911 Dispatch Triage API
suggestions.desc: Develop a low-latency API that processes incoming call transcripts in real-time. If the BHR classification is high, it automatically triggers a notification for a Mobile Crisis Unit to be dispatched alongside or instead of police officers.

suggestions.title: Geospatial Crisis Hotspot Mapper
suggestions.desc: Integrate the categorized report data with a GIS (Geographic Information System) like ArcGIS. This allows city planners to see 'behavioral health heatmaps' to determine where to build new community-based mental health resources.

suggestions.title: Automated HIPAA/PII Redaction Pipeline
suggestions.desc: Before reports are processed by an LLM, implement a robust Named Entity Recognition (NER) layer to redact PII (names, addresses, SSNs). This ensures the tool can be used by non-police researchers without violating privacy laws.

suggestions.title: BHR Recidivism Risk Study
suggestions.desc: Conduct a longitudinal analysis by linking categorized BHR reports to anonymized recidivism data. Identify which specific behavioral health categories (e.g., substance use vs. chronic homelessness) have the highest correlation with repeat police contact.
