title: Cogni-Resource: AI-Driven Reflective Feedback Analysis for Enhanced Learning Insights and Resource Discovery
authors: Devara, Ashrith kumar
abstract: In this study, we propose a novel method to detect small traffic signs that appeared in dashboard camera images. Our method is a framework consisting of the following three distinct algorithms. Grouping Window, Super Resolution Generative Adversarial Network (SRGAN), and a two-stage cascade classifier. Potential regions of interest (ROI) are extracted with Grouping Window which is a sophisticated modification of the traditional sliding window technique. The ROI are upsampled and enhanced using SRGAN. Then the traffic signs among high-resolution ROI are detected and identified by the two-stage cascade classifier of which the first stage filters the ROI that do not contain traffic signs and the second stage classifies the ROI that contains traffic signs into respective classes. The proposed method is capable of detecting traffic signs in the 5-8 square-pixel range. The detection of small objects in this square-pixel range is not generally addressed by state-of-the-art frameworks such as R-CNN and YOLO. We trained our method by using the German Traffic Sign Recognition Benchmark dataset (GTSRB) and tested it on random dashboard camera images containing small traffic signs. The experimental results on 15 random dashboard camera images show that our baseline model localizes 10 of the 23 small traffic signs belonging to the aforementioned pixel range and produces 70 false positives in total. Also, it classifies only one of the detected traffic signs correctly into 43 classes. We plan to improve our method by using image denoising techniques and comparing results.

suggestions.title: Edge AI Dashcam Deployment
suggestions.desc: Optimize the SRGAN and cascade classifier using TensorRT or OpenVINO. The goal is to get the system running at 30+ FPS on an NVIDIA Jetson Nano, proving it can work in real-time as an active driver assistance system (ADAS).

suggestions.title: Adverse Weather Sign Reconstruction
suggestions.desc: Train the SRGAN on a specialized dataset of traffic signs obscured by heavy rain, fog, or snow. Evaluate if the upsampling technique can 'restore' the visibility of a sign that would otherwise be unreadable to a standard autonomous vehicle sensor.

suggestions.title: Infrastructure Condition Monitor
suggestions.desc: Repurpose the detection system to identify damaged, faded, or graffiti-covered traffic signs. This tool could be sold to Departments of Transportation (DOT) to automate the inventory and maintenance schedule of roadside assets.

suggestions.title: CARLA Autonomous Simulation Integration
suggestions.desc: Integrate the SRGAN pipeline into the open-source CARLA driving simulator. Test how much the 'enhanced' sign detection improves the safety performance (e.g., braking distance) of an AI-driven car compared to one using raw dashboard camera inputs.

title: Efficient Sentiment Analysis using Encoder-only Transformer
authors: Jonnalagadda, Rohan, Kandimalla, Srinidhi, Yellu, Siri
abstract: In the era of social media, sentiment analysis has emerged as a vital instrument for comprehending public opinion, especially on sites like LinkedIn and Twitter. Because user-generated content is informal and noisy, traditional sentiment classification techniques like Naive Bayes and Support Vector Machines sometimes find it difficult to capture context, sarcasm, and long-term interdependence. In order to improve sentiment analysis accuracy for social media datasets with a specific focus on sentiments related to corporate layoffs, this study suggests an encoder-only transformer model. Our method successfully captures intricate phrase patterns and contextual subtleties in textual data by leveraging the self-attention mechanism built into transformer designs. To assess the model’s performance on unseen data, we used the LinkedIn dataset for testing and the Twitter dataset for training. To help the model understand the semantic linkages in the text, we used Word2Vec for tokenization and representation. Our research indicates that the transformer model works noticeably better than conventional sentiment analysis methods, exhibiting enhanced resilience and flexibility when dealing with colloquial language and conflicting emotions. This development could have an impact on businesses and organizations looking to use social media insights on layoffs to make data-driven decisions.

suggestions.title: Multi-Modal Sentiment Transformer
suggestions.desc: Extend the encoder-only transformer (like BERT/RoBERTa) to accept image features from LinkedIn posts or tweets. Use a CLIP-based image encoder to see how visual content (memes or infographics) shifts the overall sentiment of the text.

suggestions.title: Industry-Specific Burnout Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Use the LinkedIn dataset to create a 'Professional Sentiment Index.' Categorize posts by industry (e.g., Tech, Healthcare, Finance) to track and visualize sentiment trends, helping organizations identify periods of high professional burnout in real-time.

suggestions.title: Low-Resource PEFT Fine-Tuning
suggestions.desc: Implement Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT) using LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation). Demonstrate how a student can achieve high sentiment accuracy on large social media datasets with limited GPU resources (e.g., a single consumer-grade 3060).

suggestions.title: Sarcasm-Aware Dual-Channel Encoder
suggestions.desc: Design a 'Dual-Channel' architecture where one encoder processes the raw text and another processes the structural informalities (emojis, excessive punctuation, and hashtags) to improve performance on sarcastic or informal social media content.

title: AI/ML-Based Water Quality Monitoring Mobile App for Predicting E.coli in Surface Waters
authors: Karumanchi, Keerthi Priya
abstract: E.coli contamination in surface waters has proven to be a significant public health concern, requiring innovative monitoring solutions. This paper presents the design of an AI-driven mobile application to predict whether E.coli bacteria are present at levels exceeding acceptable thresholds in surface waters. The methodology employs sensor devices to collect water quality data parameters, such as water temperature, pH, dissolved oxygen, and turbidity. A dataset is generated based on these parameters, and machine learning (ML) algorithms are applied to evaluate accuracy, precision, recall, and processing time. Additionally, our ML algorithms establish a correlation matrix among water quality parameters to identify the key factors influencing E.coli levels. We applied various machine learning techniques to the dataset, including Support Vector Regression (SVR), Random Forest Classification (RFC), XGBoost, and ensemble methods that combine these algorithms. Our findings indicate that the ensemble of Random Forest Classification and XGBoost achieved the highest accuracy. Users can view E. coli predictions based on current sensor values through our Mobile App.

suggestions.title: Edge-Deployed Real-Time Water Quality Node
suggestions.desc: Port the E.coli prediction model to a TensorFlow Lite Micro framework running on an ESP32 or Arduino Nicla Vision. Connect physical pH, turbidity, and temperature sensors to create a standalone, battery-powered monitoring device that provides instant 'Safe/Unsafe' feedback without needing a constant internet connection.

suggestions.title: Community Contamination Heatmapping
suggestions.desc: Develop a crowdsourced mapping feature using the Google Maps API where users can upload their local sensor readings. Use spatial interpolation (Kriging) to visualize E.coli risk levels across a river system or lake, helping local authorities identify contamination sources in real-time.

suggestions.title: Predictive Temporal Forecasting with LSTM
suggestions.desc: Extend the dataset with historical weather data (rainfall, humidity) from the OpenWeatherMap API. Implement a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network to predict future E.coli spikes 24-48 hours in advance based on forecasted storm surges and runoff patterns.

suggestions.title: LoRaWAN Remote Monitoring Infrastructure
suggestions.desc: Integrate LoRa (Long Range) wireless modules to transmit sensor data from remote rural water sources to a central gateway miles away. Build a centralized dashboard using ThingsBoard to manage multiple remote nodes, targeting environmental agencies needing low-cost monitoring in areas without cellular coverage.

similar: legacy:GR-050|Spring 2021|predicting users' engagement during interviews with biofeedback, voice, and supervised machine learning

title: Semantic Search using Sentence Transformers
authors: Satish, Roshni, Challa, Arpana
abstract: Traditional keyword-based search engines struggle to accurately capture the semantics of user queries in today's enormous digital resources. Our research study focuses on creating a semantic search engine that uses Sentence Transformers to improve information retrieval by understanding the context of queries and documents. Our method creates sentence embeddings for documents and user queries, allowing retrieval based on semantic similarity rather than keyword matching. The project involves data collection and preprocessing, feature extraction with Sentence Transformers, and implementation of a search engine that ranks documents based on cosine similarity to query embeddings. According to preliminary testing, this method greatly improves search relevancy and accuracy, we have compared the results with a baseline algorithm, BM25, to assess the effectiveness of Sentence Transformers in enhancing retrieval relevance. This work opens the door for future refinements in retrieval systems based on natural language processing and shows how semantic search engines can deliver results that are more contextually aligned.
supervisor: Dr. Md Abdullah Al Hafiz Khan

suggestions.title: High-Performance Vector DB Migration
suggestions.desc: Scale the retrieval engine by integrating a vector database like Pinecone or Milvus. This allows for sub-second semantic search across millions of documents using HNSW (Hierarchical Navigable Small World) indexing, moving beyond local array-based similarity calculations.

suggestions.title: Cross-Lingual Semantic Document Retrieval
suggestions.desc: Upgrade the model to a multilingual transformer like 'paraphrase-multilingual-MiniLM-L12-v2'. This enables a system where a user can search in Spanish or Mandarin and retrieve the most semantically relevant English-language research papers or internal documents.

suggestions.title: Hybrid Retrieval with Cross-Encoder Re-ranking
suggestions.desc: Implement a two-stage search pipeline: use the existing Bi-Encoder (Sentence Transformers) for fast initial retrieval, followed by a Cross-Encoder to re-rank the top 10 results. This significantly increases precision by modeling the interaction between the query and the document text directly.

suggestions.title: Contextual Enterprise Knowledge Bot
suggestions.desc: Build a Slack or Discord integration that uses the semantic search engine to index a company’s Notion or Confluence wiki. Create a 'Help Desk' bot that answers employee questions by retrieving the exact paragraph of documentation that matches the semantic intent of their query.

title: Evaluating Instance Segmentation Models on Histopathology Datasets
authors: Koganti, Sai Chandana
abstract: Instance segmentation is transforming digital pathology by enhancing the speed and accuracy of tissue sample analysis through advanced image processing techniques. Whole Slide Imaging (WSI) converts traditional microscope slides into high-resolution digital formats, enabling detailed examinations. This paper presents a brief experimental survey of instance segmentation models on two prominent histopathology datasets: PanNuke and NuCLS. Unlike previous surveys that merely describe deep learning models for general pathology images, we conduct experiments using state-of-the-art models including Mask R-CNN, Detectron2, YOLOv8, YOLOv9, and HoverNet on both datasets. Our study evaluates these models for both binary and multiclass instance segmentation tasks. The NuCLS dataset, featuring over 220,000 annotated nuclei from breast cancer histopathology images, is used for multiclass segmentation across 13 distinct nuclear classes. The PanNuke dataset, comprising 205,343 labeled nuclei across 19 tissue types, is employed for both multiclass and binary instance segmentation of five cell types: neoplastic, inflammatory, soft tissue, dead, and epithelial. We assess each model's performance using metrics such as mean average precision (mAP), F1 score, and Dice coefficient, providing a comprehensive evaluation of their strengths and limitations. The results of our study offer valuable insights into the capabilities of different instance segmentation models in histopathology image analysis. We observe varying performance across tissue types and cell categories, highlighting the importance of model selection based on specific histopathology tasks. Our findings aim to guide researchers in choosing appropriate models for their specific needs, ultimately contributing to the advancement of digital pathology and improving diagnostic accuracy in clinical practice. Also provides a foundation for future research in instance segmentation for histopathology images.

suggestions.title: SAM-based Interactive Segmentation for Pathologists
suggestions.desc: Integrate Meta’s Segment Anything Model (SAM) with the PanNuke dataset to create an interactive labeling tool. Allow pathologists to click a single point on a cell nucleus and have the model automatically refine the boundary, drastically reducing the time required for manual WSI annotation.

suggestions.title: Automated Tumor Microenvironment Profiling
suggestions.desc: Develop a post-processing script that calculates the spatial relationships between different cell types (e.g., distance between lymphocytes and tumor cells). Build a dashboard that visualizes 'cell density maps' to help researchers understand the immune response within the tissue sample.

suggestions.title: Active Learning for Rare Cell Detection
suggestions.desc: Implement an active learning loop where the model identifies cells with low confidence scores in the NuCLS dataset and flags them for human review. Use these expert-verified samples to fine-tune the model, specifically improving performance on rare or atypical cell morphologies.

suggestions.title: QuPath Plugin for Real-Time Analysis
suggestions.desc: Develop a Java-based plugin for QuPath (the industry-standard bio-image analysis software) that runs your trained instance segmentation models. This allows researchers to apply your model directly to high-resolution .svs files within their existing workflow.

title: Enhancing Alzheimer’s Diagnosis through Spontaneous Speech Recognition: A Deep Learning Approach with Data Augmentation
authors: Mutala, Venkata Sai Bhargav
abstract: Alzheimer’s disease (AD) is a growing public health issue due to its progressive nature and rising prevalence. This study explores a neural network model trained on speech data from the ADReSS2020 Challenge dataset to distinguish AD patients from healthy individuals, using log-Mel spectrogram features. To improve accuracy, five data augmentation methods, including pitch and time shifting, were used. The results highlight deep learning, combined with data augumentation, as a promising, scalable, and noninvasive approach for early AD diagnosis

suggestions.title: Multimodal Fusion of Speech and Text
suggestions.desc: Combine the existing log-Mel spectrogram CNN with a BERT-based NLP model that analyzes the transcripts of the ADReSS2020 Challenge. Use a late-fusion architecture to weigh acoustic markers (pauses, pitch) and linguistic markers (vocabulary loss) simultaneously for higher diagnostic accuracy.

suggestions.title: Privacy-Preserving Mobile Screening App
suggestions.desc: Convert the trained model to CoreML or ONNX to run locally on a smartphone. Build an app where a user records a short 'Cookie Theft' picture description task; the audio is processed entirely on-device to ensure patient privacy while providing a preliminary cognitive health score.

suggestions.title: Explainable Spectrogram Saliency Mapping
suggestions.desc: Apply Grad-CAM (Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping) to the CNN to visualize which specific frequency bands and time intervals in the spectrogram the model uses to identify Alzheimer's. This provides 'visual evidence' to clinicians about speech tremors or elongated pauses.

suggestions.title: Telehealth Audio Diagnostics API
suggestions.desc: Build a REST API that telehealth platforms can call to analyze patient recordings. The API should return a standardized 'Acoustic Biomarker Report' that flags early signs of cognitive decline, allowing doctors to prioritize patients for full clinical evaluations.

title: Improving Alzheimer’s Detection via Synthetic Data Generation Using GPT-4 and Multi-Level Embeddings
authors: Mutala, Venkata Sai Bhargav, Shahid, Imaan
abstract: This study leverages large language models (LLMs), particularly GPT-4, to overcome the data limitations often encountered in Alzheimer’s detection. We utilize GPT-4 for data augmentation, generating synthetic speech transcripts to enhance machine learning model training. Our approach combines fine-tuned BERT embeddings with CLAN-derived linguistic features, as well as sentence-level embeddings, to improve classification performance on the ADReSS2020 dataset. BERT and CLAN features capture detailed linguistic variants, while sentence embeddings offer robust semantic representations, collectively enhancing the accuracy and generalization of the models. Among the classifiers tested, the Random Forest model shows the best performance, achieving an accuracy of 88% with sentence embeddings, surpassing other models in detecting Alzheimer’s from speech patterns. The integration of LLM-augmented data and multilevel embeddings presents a promising solution to the data scarcity issue in medical research, enabling more accurate and reliable Alzheimer’s diagnoses.

suggestions.title: Voice-Synthesized Audio Augmentation
suggestions.desc: Use the synthetic transcripts generated by GPT-4 as input for a Text-to-Speech (TTS) system like ElevenLabs or Bark. Create a synthetic audio dataset that mimics the dysfluencies of Alzheimer's patients, providing a way to train acoustic models without needing thousands of hours of real patient recordings.

suggestions.title: Longitudinal Linguistic Drift Tracking
suggestions.desc: Create a tool that tracks a user's speech over several months, comparing new transcripts against their own personal 'GPT-4 generated baseline.' This shifts the focus from population-wide detection to detecting subtle linguistic shifts within a specific individual.

suggestions.title: Adversarial Robustness Testing for AD Detection
suggestions.desc: Evaluate how GPT-4 synthetic data affects model bias. Test the model on varied dialects and socio-economic speech patterns to ensure that the synthetic data doesn't over-represent a specific demographic, which could lead to diagnostic disparities.

suggestions.title: Synthetic Medical Data Augmentation SaaS
suggestions.desc: Develop a platform where researchers can upload small, privacy-restricted medical datasets (like ADReSS) and receive a large, balanced, synthetic version generated by LLMs. Focus on ensuring the synthetic data maintains the statistical properties of the original 'CLAN' features.

title: Harnessing ML-Powered HPCC Systems for Advanced Cybersecurity Analytics
authors: Kamal, Zularbine
abstract: Information security in the era of AI and automation is the biggest challenge for cybersecurity professionals. Traditional information security protection has limitations in detecting zero-day attacks, which can be overcome with machine learning-based information security. An ML-powered intrusion detection system uses statistical analysis to spot deviations from normal behavior and helps to detect new and unknown threats. This poster will demonstrate how an open-source platform can be used for cybersecurity by leveraging various machine-learning algorithms.

suggestions.title: Real-time ECL Stream Processing
suggestions.desc: Implement a real-time intrusion detection pipeline using the HPCC Systems 'Roxie' engine. Transition the current batch-based statistical analysis to a streaming model that can ingest and flag malicious network packets from a live PCAP stream using the ECL language.

suggestions.title: Adversarial Attack Simulation on IDS
suggestions.desc: Use the Foolbox or CleverHans library to generate adversarial network traffic designed to bypass the HPCC-based ML model. Analyze the cluster's logs to identify which statistical features are most vulnerable to manipulation and retrain the model for robustness.

suggestions.title: Automated Security Policy Generation
suggestions.desc: Build a module that converts the IDS findings into automated firewall rules (iptables or AWS Security Groups). If the HPCC cluster detects a zero-day pattern from a specific CIDR block, it should automatically push a mitigation policy to the network edge.

suggestions.title: Graph-Based Lateral Movement Analysis
suggestions.desc: Utilize the HPCC Systems GNN (Graph Neural Network) library to map network connections as a graph. Train the model to detect not just individual malicious packets, but suspicious 'lateral movement' patterns where an attacker moves from one internal node to another.

title: Machine Learning-Enhanced HPCC Systems for Alzheimer's Disease Detection: A Scalable Solution for Early Diagnosis
authors: Kamal, Zularbine
abstract: Alzheimer's disease is an incurable brain disorder that gradually deteriorates memory and cognitive abilities, leading to symptoms such as memory loss, confusion, difficulty in thinking, and changes in language, behavior, and personality. Early diagnosis that is effective, innovative, and cost-efficient can help mitigate damage to nerve cells. Detecting these symptoms through voice responses and analyzing the corresponding transcripts offers a promising approach. This poster demonstrates how an open-source platform can be utilized for text classification to identify Alzheimer's disease, employing fast, inexpensive, and non-invasive methods to complement other diagnostic techniques

suggestions.title: ECL-Optimized Distributed Feature Extraction
suggestions.desc: Implement a parallelized audio feature extraction pipeline using ECL on an HPCC Systems cluster. This allows for the rapid processing of massive audio datasets, extracting MFCCs and jitter/shimmer features from thousands of files in parallel rather than sequentially.

suggestions.title: Regional Cognitive Health Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Use the HPCC Systems' Roxie engine to build a real-time analytics dashboard that aggregates anonymized diagnostic results. Visualize Alzheimer’s prevalence by geographic region to help public health officials allocate resources for geriatric care based on early detection trends.

suggestions.title: Knowledge Distillation for IoT Deployment
suggestions.desc: Use the high-capacity models trained on the HPCC cluster as 'teacher' models to train smaller, lightweight 'student' models. These student models can then be deployed on low-power IoT devices in nursing homes for continuous, non-intrusive monitoring of resident speech.

suggestions.title: Scalable Batch-Processing Clinic Portal
suggestions.desc: Create a web portal for clinics to upload 'Day in the Life' audio logs from patients. Use the HPCC backend to process these massive files in batch, providing a comprehensive cognitive health report within minutes, showcasing the platform's scalability over traditional server setups.

title: Computer Vision-Enhanced Spectroscopy for Glucose Prediction: An In Vitro Validation Study
authors: Belfarsi, El Arbi
abstract: This study introduces a novel computer vision-based spectral approach for non-invasive glucose detection using synthetic blood samples. We developed an experimental setup with glucose concentrations from 70 to 120 mg/dL, using two dye methods. Light sources tested included an 850 nm LED, 850 nm laser, 808 nm laser, and 650 nm laser, with image capture via a 1080p IR camera. Data augmentation, including Gaussian noise, contrast and brightness adjustments, rotations, and zooming, produced seven variants per image. Three machine learning models—CNN, AdaBoost, and ResNet—were evaluated, with the 850 nm light source yielding the best results: 87.5% of predictions fell within Zone A of the Clarke Error Grid. Findings support the potential of this approach for non-invasive glucose monitoring.

suggestions.title: Multi-Wavelength Spectroscopic Fusion
suggestions.desc: Redesign the model to accept a multi-channel input from all four tested light sources (850nm, 808nm, 650nm) simultaneously. Use an ensemble CNN to learn which wavelengths are most sensitive to glucose at different skin depths, reducing errors caused by variations in sample turbidity.

suggestions.title: Wearable Prototype with ESP32-CAM
suggestions.desc: Construct a 3D-printed finger-clip housing containing the 850nm LED and a low-cost IR camera module. Connect it to an ESP32-CAM to stream spectral images to a mobile device for processing, moving the project from a lab setup to a functional hardware prototype.

suggestions.title: Physiological Artifact Filtering via FFT
suggestions.desc: Implement a Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) preprocessing layer to isolate and remove the 'pulse' signal from the camera feed. By filtering out the rhythmic light changes caused by blood flow, the model can more accurately focus on the static absorption spectra of the glucose molecules.

suggestions.title: Non-Invasive Glucose Monitoring Companion App
suggestions.desc: Build a React Native app that interfaces with the IR camera hardware. Include features for tracking glucose trends over time, carbohydrate logging, and an alert system that notifies users when predicted glucose levels deviate from the 70-120 mg/dL target range.

title: Hyperparameter Optimization in Neural Network Using Binary Search Algorithm
authors: Chowdhoury, Faysal, Zhang, Yinning, Suer, Sait
abstract: Hyperparameter searching is a crucial process for every neural network training. However, this process is notably time-consuming due to the vast number of possible combinations and the influence these hyperparameters have on each other. The common approach is using grid search to exhaust all the options, which is computationally very expensive. In this research, we propose a new algorithm for this problem that is inspired by binary search and returns a significant improvement in time efficiency.

suggestions.title: Multi-Variable Binary Space Partitioning
suggestions.desc: Extend the 1D binary search to a multi-dimensional optimization algorithm (Binary Space Partitioning). This would allow for the simultaneous optimization of learning rate, batch size, and dropout rate, finding the optimal 'hyper-volume' in the parameter space in logarithmic time.

suggestions.title: Scikit-Learn 'BinarySearchCV' Integration
suggestions.desc: Develop a Python package that implements a `BinarySearchCV` class, adhering to the Scikit-Learn estimator API. This would allow developers to swap out the computationally expensive `GridSearchCV` in their existing machine learning pipelines with your faster algorithm using just one line of code.

suggestions.title: Efficiency Benchmark Against Bayesian Optimization
suggestions.desc: Conduct a comparative research study testing the Binary Search optimizer against state-of-the-art tools like Optuna (TPE) and Hyperopt. Use standard datasets like MNIST and CIFAR-10 to quantify the trade-offs between search speed and final model accuracy.

suggestions.title: AutoML Tool for Resource-Constrained Devices
suggestions.desc: Build a command-line interface (CLI) tool designed for training models on edge devices like Raspberry Pi or Jetson Nano. The tool uses the Binary Search algorithm to find the best possible model configurations without overheating the hardware or exceeding limited power budgets.

title: Multifamily Loan Performance
authors: Bell, Jonathan
abstract: This study explores the performance of multifamily loans using a logistic regression model to predict loan outcomes as either “closed” or “current”. Utilizing a dataset of over one million observations and 54,771 unique loan observations, we classify loan status based on Freddie Mac’s mortgage performance codes, with closed loans including modification with a loss, foreclosures, real estate owned, and fully closed loans. Through explanatory analysis, it reveals a nearly balanced distribution between the binary variables. This dataset supports the use of a logistic regression to model the probability of loan default or completion. The findings have implications for risk mitigation in underwriting practices, helping lenders avoid loans with characteristics like those that historically defaulted.
supervisor: Prof. Michael Frankel & Dr. Jiajing (Horatio) Huang

suggestions.title: Gradient Boosted Tree Ensemble Migration
suggestions.desc: Replace the Logistic Regression model with XGBoost or LightGBM to capture non-linear relationships between loan features (e.g., the interaction between property age and market interest rates). This could significantly improve the detection of 'closed' loans which often have complex, multi-factor causes.

suggestions.title: Macro-Economic Stress Test Simulator
suggestions.desc: Build a Streamlit dashboard that allows users to adjust external economic sliders (e.g., +2% Federal Interest Rate, +5% Unemployment). The app uses the trained model to predict how many 'Current' loans in the Freddie Mac dataset would shift to 'Closed/Foreclosure' status under those conditions.

suggestions.title: Geospatial Risk Clustering Analysis
suggestions.desc: Integrate the loan data with ArcGIS or Folium to map performance by Metropolitan Statistical Area (MSA). Analyze whether loan defaults are clustered geographically and correlate those clusters with local economic indicators like regional GDP growth or industry-specific layoffs.

suggestions.title: Real Estate Investor Risk-Scoring API
suggestions.desc: Develop a lightweight API where small-scale multifamily investors can input their own loan details (LTV, debt service coverage ratio, etc.). The API returns a 'Risk Grade' (A-F) based on the patterns learned from the million-observation Freddie Mac dataset.

title: Gwinnett County Public Schools - Data Masking Tool
authors: Peters, Emmett, Frausto Ramirez, Diego, Talele, Abhay, Tompkins, Silas, Trejo-Tamayo, Jesus
abstract: In today’s data-driven world, organizations handle vast amounts of sensitive information, including personally identifiable information (PII), health records, and financial data. For institutions like schools, this data often includes sensitive details about students, parents, and staff, making data protection not just important, but critical. With increasing privacy regulations such as GDPR and HIPAA, organizations must implement robust measures to protect this information while still enabling its use for legitimate purposes like testing, analytics, and development. Our web-based data masking tool addresses this need by allowing organizations to protect sensitive data without compromising its usability. By applying dynamic masking rules to relational databases and generating masked data extracts, the tool ensures compliance with privacy laws while improving operational efficiency. It automates data protection processes and generates realistic, anonymized data, allowing organizations to securely manage and share sensitive information for non-production purposes. Designed for scalability and ease of use, the tool helps organizations streamline their data protection workflows while maintaining the integrity of their testing and analytical environments.

suggestions.title: Differential Privacy Noise Layer
suggestions.desc: Upgrade the tool from simple masking to a Differential Privacy framework. Implement a mechanism that adds Laplacian noise to the data, allowing school administrators to perform statistical analysis (e.g., average GPA by district) without the risk of re-identifying individual students.

suggestions.title: NLP-Powered PII Auto-Discovery
suggestions.desc: Integrate a Named Entity Recognition (NER) model using SpaCy or Hugging Face to automatically scan unstructured documents (like teacher notes or emails). The tool should identify and mask names, addresses, and SSNs that are hidden within paragraphs of text, rather than just in structured database columns.

suggestions.title: Re-identification Vulnerability Audit Tool
suggestions.desc: Develop a 'red-team' testing script that attempts to re-identify masked records by cross-referencing them with public datasets (like voter registration or social media). Use this to generate a 'Privacy Strength Score' for different masking configurations.

suggestions.title: FERPA-Compliant Data Sharing Portal
suggestions.desc: Build a secure web portal where educational researchers can request access to school datasets. The system should automatically apply the masking tool to the requested data, generate an audit log for compliance, and provide a secure, time-limited download link.

title: Indy Micro - Virtual 8-Bit Computer
authors: Watson, Matthew, King, Elizabeth, Lee, Junhyeok, Frey, Gavin
abstract: The Indy Micro is a desktop application which simulates the functionality of an eight-bit personal computer. Its aim is to mimic the feel of owning one such computer in that era, as well as provide an engaging way to learn about low-level computing concepts. The Micro consists of two components: the virtual machine, which is based on the Von Neumann architecture, and the code editor, which allows users to write assembly code and execute it on the virtual machine. The aim is for the Indy Micro to serve as an educational jumping-off point, a step between the casual programmer and the dedicated hobbyist, developing software for real eight-bit systems. One of the ways students get started with programming is with Scratch (scratch.mit.edu), a visual drag-and-drop programming experience. The project’s goal is to create something like Scratch, but for assembly language, allowing students and hobbyists to learn about low level programming.

suggestions.title: Physical Register Display Bridge
suggestions.desc: Build a hardware interface using an ESP32 to map the simulator’s virtual registers to physical 8-bit LED arrays and toggle switches, allowing users to 'hand-code' the virtual machine via physical hardware inputs.

suggestions.title: Cycle-Accurate Performance Profiler
suggestions.desc: Develop a visual debugger that tracks clock cycles and memory access patterns in real-time. Use this to help students visualize the 'Von Neumann Bottleneck' by showing how data movement between the CPU and memory impacts execution speed.

suggestions.title: Indy-ASM to WebAssembly Compiler
suggestions.desc: Create a toolchain that compiles the project's custom assembly language into WebAssembly (Wasm). This would allow students to write code in the Indy Micro editor and export it as a standalone, high-performance web application.

suggestions.title: IoT Retro-Dashboard Node
suggestions.desc: Repurpose the virtual machine to act as a low-overhead logic controller for IoT sensors. Use the 8-bit simulation to process sensor data (like temperature) and output retro-style visualizations to an attached OLED display.

title: Bathtub Racing Game
authors: Melbourne, Sasha, Bah, Sulaiman, Brown, Damari, Ealy, Deylin, Calo, Adam
abstract: The Virtual Bathtub Racing Game is a capstone project that uses an interactive 3D digital experience to preserve and modernize the long-standing bathtub racing tradition at Southern Polytechnic State University (SPSU). With real-world physics, adjustable features, and multiplayer capabilities, the Unity-developed game recreates the famous event where students raced imaginatively designed bathtub carts. Since stakeholder input influences the creation of tracks, sound profiles, and gameplay elements that replicate the original races, alumni involvement is crucial in determining the authenticity of the game. In order to provide a captivating user experience for both new players and past SPSU students, the project prioritizes historical accuracy while utilizing contemporary gaming technologies. Custom bathtub models, gender-selectable racers, several engine types, and meticulously re-created tracks from SPSU's 1980s layouts and the Marietta campus are among of the key features. The game will be submitted for possible presentation at the C-Day Computing Showcase, setting the stage for upcoming improvements like AI-powered opponents and VR integration. The Virtual Bathtub Racing Game gives a nostalgic yet entertaining digital tribute to SPSU's engineering ethos while building alumni ties by fusing tradition and innovation.
supervisor: Prof. Donald Privitera, Project Sponsor: Joesph Locker

suggestions.title: SPSU Alumni Ghost-Racing Portal
suggestions.desc: Implement a Firebase-backed leaderboard that stores 'ghost' data (time-stamped position and rotation). This allows current students to race against the best historical laps of SPSU alumni in a shared, competitive digital space.

suggestions.title: Modular Tub Physics Constructor
suggestions.desc: Extend the Unity project with a customization system where weight, center of mass, and friction coefficients are procedurally calculated based on player-selected tub materials and wheel types, impacting the racing physics model.

suggestions.title: Mixed-Reality Pit Crew Challenge
suggestions.desc: Using OpenXR, create a VR/AR mode where players must perform 'maintenance' tasks—like tightening bolts on castors or patching leaks—using motion controls to reduce drag before the race starts.

suggestions.title: Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD) Lite
suggestions.desc: Integrate a simplified aerodynamic simulation using Unity's C# Job System to visualizes air resistance around the bathtub models, teaching players how 'imaginative designs' impact speed through real-world physics concepts.

title: Pet Matchmaker
authors: Davis, Victoria, Baldwin, Colton, Sadler, Edward
abstract: Angel Among Us is a non-profit organization that saves animals from high-killing rate shelters in Georgia. Their goal is to find homes for homeless pets. To increase their efforts, they are developing a web-based application to help improve adoption processes. The goal is to increase adoption rates and be able to provide adopters with information about pets and overall reduce the number of pet returns. This will be accomplished by using adopters’ information and preferences from the web-based application to find long-term compatibility with their recommended pets. The objective is to create a web application interface that includes many core components. First, the web application will use PetFinder API to collect pet data and use a catching mechanism to increase data storage by updating pet data daily and ensuring enhanced performance. The mechanism will also decrease the frequency of calls leading to good API accuracy. Adopters will then complete questionnaires to gather information about their preferences, lifestyle, allergies, and experiences. The system will use ChatGPT to analyze the adopter's results and send out recommended pet suggestions based on the given data. Also, pet profiles will be created to get information for database analysts. This will ensure that PetFinder’s animal description template will be consistent with the saved information from pet profiles to have proper pet descriptions. Overall, this approach encourages responsible pet ownership, reduces pet returns, and increases successful adoption rates.

suggestions.title: Behavioral Matchmaking Engine
suggestions.desc: Implement a machine learning model using scikit-learn that weights adopter lifestyle data (activity level, home size) against pet temperaments to predict compatibility scores, specifically aiming to lower the rate of pet returns.

suggestions.title: PetFinder API Regional Aggregator
suggestions.desc: Expand the scope by integrating the PetFinder API to pull data from multiple Georgia shelters into the matching interface, creating a centralized hub for high-kill shelter rescues across the state.

suggestions.title: Computer Vision Breed & Trait Tagger
suggestions.desc: Use a pre-trained ResNet model to automatically scan uploaded pet photos and suggest tags for physical traits (e.g., 'pointy ears,' 'tri-color') to save shelter volunteers time during the data entry process.

suggestions.title: Post-Adoption Support Chatbot
suggestions.desc: Build an AI support bridge using the OpenAI API that provides new owners with breed-specific training tips and medical reminders, helping them navigate the first 90 days of pet ownership to ensure a permanent placement.

similar: legacy:UC-026|Spring 2021|pose extraction for real-time workout assist_capstone group_w01_spring semester

title: KSUBlocks Tower Defense
authors: Elledge, Matthew, Gwee, AnnaGrace, Nguyen, Bryan, Slicker, Logan, Ahn, Ashley
abstract: Our project, KSUBlocks Tower Defense, is a Minecraft Plugin designed to create a game mode in the Tower Defense genre. We aim to create a unique and fun game for the students in the KSU Minecraft server. Our project is entirely configurable allowing for easy maintenance and room for future expansions, while ensuring the server performance remains steady alongside KSU's other game modes. It is developed in Java, utilizing IntelliJ and Paper API. We plan to deploy it on the KSU Minecraft Server upon finalization.

suggestions.title: Reinforcement Learning Adaptive AI
suggestions.desc: Replace the basic wave logic with a Reinforcement Learning agent (using the Malmo platform or a Java RL library). The 'mobs' should learn to find the weakest point in a player's tower layout, forcing players to adapt their strategies over time.

suggestions.title: Real-time Web Analytics Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Develop a companion Spring Boot website that connects to the Minecraft server's database. It should display live match statistics, global leaderboards, and heatmaps of where towers are most frequently placed across the KSU server.

suggestions.title: Custom Voxel Visual Effects System
suggestions.desc: Use the Paper API and NMS (Net-Minecraft-Server) code to create custom particle-based visual effects for tower abilities. Implement 'Area of Effect' (AoE) visuals and projectile tracers that don't rely on standard Minecraft entities to minimize lag.

suggestions.title: External Map & Wave Editor
suggestions.desc: Create a standalone JavaFX or web-based editor that allows server admins to 'draw' tower defense paths and configure wave data (mob types, health scaling) via a GUI, exporting the results directly to the plugin’s YAML config files.

title: Intelligent Arm Meets Machine Vision
authors: Esteche, Alvaro, Sanchez-Alvares, Sergio
abstract: Most AI and robots have been used to make mundane tasks easier for humans however intricate tasks, such as monitoring have not been tackled. Using the OpenMANIPULATOR-X, Robot Operating System (ROS2), and machine vision, we planned on having AI tracking monitor with 4 degrees of freedom.
supervisor: Prof. Waqas Majeed & Prof. Sharon Perry

suggestions.title: Edge-AI Visual Servoing
suggestions.desc: Optimize the machine vision pipeline to run on an NVIDIA Jetson Nano mounted directly on the arm. Move from standard object detection to 'Visual Servoing,' where the arm adjusts its position in real-time based on the pixel-error between the target and the gripper.

suggestions.title: Sim-to-Real Transfer with NVIDIA Isaac
suggestions.desc: Train a Reinforcement Learning agent in the NVIDIA Isaac Gym simulator to perform complex tasks (like picking up irregular objects). Use ROS2 to transfer the trained policy to the physical OpenMANIPULATOR-X to see how well it generalizes.

suggestions.title: Remote Teleoperation with Leap Motion
suggestions.desc: Develop a teleoperation interface using a Leap Motion controller. A human user can move their hand in the air, and the 4-DOF arm will mimic the movements in real-time, enabling remote 'monitoring' and physical interaction in hazardous environments.

suggestions.title: Mobile Monitoring Platform (TurtleBot + Arm)
suggestions.desc: Mount the OpenMANIPULATOR-X on a TurtleBot3 mobile base. Use ROS2 Navigation stacks to allow the arm to patrol a room, use vision to find a specific object (like a temperature gauge), and move closer to 'monitor' it autonomously.

title: GraphBat: Subterranean Data Visualizer
authors: Roberson, Caroline, Ugas, Abdalla, Karg, Nathan, Harper, Hayden
abstract: GraphBat is a desktop data visualization application designed for speleology and similar fields that bundles common graph types with a unique heatmap tool which few comparable apps provide. It was developed in Python and is intended as an open-source tool available for use and extension by the scientific community. The heatmaps offer two data interpolation methods—inverse distance weighting and linear interpolation—to visualize the spread of data across a space using a real-world map and sensor data relative to the space. GraphBat aims to expediate scientific analysis and facilitate the presentation of results across many fields of subterranean study.

suggestions.title: 3D Volumetric Cave Visualization
suggestions.desc: Extend the 2D heatmaps into 3D using the PyVista library. This would allow speleologists to visualize temperature or humidity gradients through vertical cave shafts, creating a 'cloud' of data points within a 3D-scanned mesh of the cave system.

suggestions.title: IoT Field-Sync Mobile App
suggestions.desc: Develop a Flutter mobile app that pairs with GraphBat. Scientists in the field can record sensor data via Bluetooth LE, and the app will automatically timestamp and geofence the data, ready for one-click import into the GraphBat desktop visualizer.

suggestions.title: Micro-Climate Predictive Modeling
suggestions.desc: Integrate a regression model that predicts subterranean conditions based on surface weather data. By importing NOAA weather APIs, GraphBat could show the 'predicted' heatmap for a cave system during a future storm or heatwave.

suggestions.title: Interactive WebGL Data Portal
suggestions.desc: Convert the Python visualization engine to a web-based portal using Three.js and React. This would allow scientific teams to host their subterranean datasets online, where others can interactively fly through the interpolated heatmaps in a browser.

title: HeartSpeak AI
authors: Faridi, Nabeel, Charles, Shammah, Minocha, Isha, Barnes, Ethan, Iyer, Aravind
abstract: This project is Sentiment Analysis AI for comprehensive text review analysis and more. The system leverages a fine-tuned BERT-based models to classify overall sentiment, detect emotions, identify sarcasm, and extract aspect-level opinions. Evaluations show robust performance across tasks, with sentiment accuracy around 69%, aspect analysis. Emotion and sarcasm. The pipeline provides actionable insights, empowering businesses to refine products and improve customer satisfaction with OpenAI Integration.

suggestions.title: Multimodal Aspect-Based Sentiment Analysis
suggestions.desc: Extend the current BERT pipeline to process video and audio reviews using Whisper for transcription and Wav2Vec2 for vocal emotion detection. Build a fusion layer that correlates the speaker's tone with the text-based sarcasm detection to improve overall accuracy beyond 69%.

suggestions.title: Real-Time E-Commerce Competitive Intelligence Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Develop a scraper for Amazon or Yelp that feeds into the fine-tuned BERT model to compare sentiment on specific product features against competitors. Use D3.js to visualize aspect-level 'wins' and 'losses' in real-time, providing actionable pricing or feature suggestions for business users.

suggestions.title: Contrastive Learning for Sarcasm Disambiguation
suggestions.desc: Research the use of Contrastive Learning (SimCLR) to train the model on pairs of sarcastic vs. literal text with similar keywords. Use the OpenAI API to generate synthetic sarcastic training data to address the common data imbalance issues in sarcasm detection datasets.

suggestions.title: Mental Health Crisis Early Warning System
suggestions.desc: Adapt the emotion and sentiment classifier to monitor social media feeds or anonymous forum posts for specific patterns of depressive or self-harming sentiment. Implement a notification bridge that alerts moderators when the model detects a shift from sarcasm to genuine high-arousal negative emotions.

title: Dynamic Requirements for a Software Training Environment
authors: Grogan, Cassidie, Valdez, Jesus, White, Dawson
abstract: STEDR outlines the development of a software training environment for Warner Robins Air Base (Robins) to enhance employee coding skills to foster innovative solutions for Air Force projects. STEDR is a proof of concept serving as a dynamic requirements document represented by a user interface, to be delivered to a development team. STEDR involved two phases: (1) requirements gathering via interviews; and (2) interactive user interface development for feedback. The resulting proof of concept, includes an interactive UI and refined requirements, and serves as the foundation for a collaborative project with KSU, enabling the computing colleges to contribute to military computing power.

suggestions.title: Automated Sandbox Provisioning Engine
suggestions.desc: Connect the dynamic UI requirements tool to a backend utilizing Terraform and Docker to instantly spin up coding environments tailored to the user's selected requirements. If a user selects 'Python for Data Science,' the system should automatically deploy a containerized Jupyter instance with pre-configured libraries.

suggestions.title: Low-Code Requirements-to-Prototype Generator
suggestions.desc: Enhance the proof-of-concept by integrating a code generation LLM that interprets the gathered UI requirements and exports a functional React or Angular boilerplate. This allows the Air Force development teams to move from 'feedback UI' to 'functional alpha' in a single click.

suggestions.title: Cognitive Load Measurement in Training UI
suggestions.desc: Integrate telemetry to track user interaction patterns (click-paths, dwell time) within the STEDR interface to identify 'friction points' in the training flow. Use this data to research how different UI layouts impact the speed of requirement gathering for technical learners.

suggestions.title: Cybersecurity Range Configuration Module
suggestions.desc: Pivot the STEDR environment specifically for Robins Air Base cybersecurity training by adding a module to define network topology requirements. Users could visually map out 'attack' and 'defense' nodes, which the system then translates into a requirements document for virtual range setup.

title: Security Lookup Interface Project
authors: Mohsen, Alhasan, Albarran, Alejandro, Lan, Ethan, Jester, Jerrat
abstract: The "Security Lookup Interface" capstone project aims to create a streamlined tool for COX's cybersecurity team, enabling analysts to efficiently perform IP address and hostname lookups while providing actionable, data-driven insights to enhance security investigations. The project will develop a user-friendly interface that simplifies the lookup process, allowing cybersecurity analysts to quickly retrieve relevant data and make informed decisions during security investigations. One of the key features of the tool is its seamless integration with both internal APIs and external resources. This integration will ensure that analysts have quick and easy access to valuable information, minimizing manual effort and enabling faster response times. By consolidating data from various sources, the interface will empower security analysts to conduct thorough investigations with minimal friction. A core aspect of the project is its focus on data-driven insights. The system will aggregate data from multiple internal and external sources, presenting actionable conclusions to assist cybersecurity analysts. These insights will help analysts identify malicious patterns, such as frequent appearances of certain IP addresses in known malicious activities, and detect anomalous behaviors, like repeated access attempts or unusual traffic patterns. This aggregated data will streamline the threat investigation process, making it easier for analysts to prioritize threats and take immediate action.

suggestions.title: Automated Threat Intel Orchestrator
suggestions.desc: Integrate APIs from VirusTotal, Shodan, and AlienVault OTX to aggregate reputation scores into the lookup interface, automatically color-coding IP results based on their known malicious history across global databases.

suggestions.title: SOC Incident Ticket Generator
suggestions.desc: Develop a middleware that connects the interface to ServiceNow or Jira, allowing analysts to click a single button to convert a suspicious lookup result into a fully populated security incident ticket for the COX team.

suggestions.title: Malicious Subnet Graph Visualization
suggestions.desc: Use D3.js or Cytoscape to build a visual relationship map that shows how a searched IP connects to other known malicious hostnames or subnets, helping analysts spot patterns of coordinated botnet attacks.

suggestions.title: Local Traffic Sniffer Integration
suggestions.desc: Add a feature using the Scapy library to capture local network packets and automatically run them through the lookup interface to flag unauthorized external connections in real-time.

title: Cat Classification of 20 Distinct Breeds
authors: Washington, Tabitha, Nestor, Ashley
abstract: Cat breed classification algorithms have been made time and time before due to cats being such a popular and beloved animal. As such, classification algorithms aim to identify their breeds for veterinary pursuits and wildlife tracking which necessitates accurate classification. Our classification algorithm identifies 20 different CFA-recognized pedigreed cat breeds utilizing TensorFlow with the MobileNetV3 Large model as the base for training. Our preliminary results over 25 initial epochs and 25 fine tuning epochs resulted in a model with a test accuracy of 65%. In the future, we plan to add more techniques to prevent overfitting and experimenting with a more robust dataset which we hope will allow us to achieve our target accuracy of 80%.

suggestions.title: On-Device Mobile Breed Scanner
suggestions.desc: Port the MobileNetV3 Large model to TensorFlow Lite and build a Flutter-based mobile app that performs real-time breed classification through a live camera feed without requiring an internet connection.

suggestions.title: Genetic Health Risk Predictor
suggestions.desc: Research breed-specific genetic predispositions (like HCM in Maine Coons) and create a feature that provides veterinary 'red flags' and preventative care tips based on the identified cat breed.

suggestions.title: Synthetic Data Augmentation for Shelters
suggestions.desc: Use Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) to create synthetic training images of cats in poor lighting or unusual angles, improving the model's accuracy for low-quality photos often found in wildlife tracking or shelter intakes.

suggestions.title: TNR Population Tracking Map
suggestions.desc: Build a web dashboard for Trap-Neuter-Return (TNR) groups that uses the classification algorithm to log stray cat sightings by breed and location, helping non-profits track colony dynamics across different neighborhoods.

title: DineNGo - AI Genie
authors: Sheffield, John, Atunnise, Juwon, Ankrah, Jaz, Colas, Alex, Grant, Kayla
abstract: This projects aims to enhance the flagship product from Driven Software Solutions called DineNGo by implementing a new chatbot to help users with technical troubleshooting. This will allow for instant technical support for common issues and reduces the number of support tickets being created. It provides informed and brief response in a quick manner to walk users through whatever technical issues they are currently having with the DineNGo software. It was built with an Angular frontend and a Node.JS backend as well as a MongoDB database for querying information.

suggestions.title: RAG-Enhanced Technical Documentation
suggestions.desc: Implement Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) by indexing DineNGo’s technical manuals and API docs in a Pinecone vector database. This ensures the chatbot provides grounded, accurate answers instead of relying solely on the LLM's internal training data.

suggestions.title: Multimodal Browser Debugger Extension
suggestions.desc: Create a companion browser extension for DineNGo users that captures console logs and screenshots automatically. The 'Genie' can then analyze these files alongside the user's text query to provide context-aware troubleshooting steps.

suggestions.title: Cross-Platform Support Integration
suggestions.desc: Develop a middleware that bridges the Angular/Node.js backend with Discord and Slack APIs. This allows restaurant managers to interact with the AI Genie through their existing workplace communication tools without logging into the DineNGo dashboard.

suggestions.title: Sentiment-Aware Ticket Escalation
suggestions.desc: Use an NLP sentiment analysis library to monitor user frustration levels during chat sessions. If the Genie detects high agitation, it should automatically generate a detailed Jira ticket containing the full conversation history for a human agent.

title: From Forecast to Fortune: Portfolio Optimization and Prediction
authors: Taylor, Nia
abstract: This project explores the intersection of time series forecasting and portfolio optimization to support data-driven investment strategies. Historical price data from 30 individual stocks was analyzed using two forecasting models: ARIMA and Prophet. Each model’s performance was evaluated using key accuracy metrics, including Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE), Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE), and Mean Directional Accuracy (MDA). Results showed that ARIMA performed better on error-based metrics, while Prophet excelled at predicting directional trends. In parallel, historical return data was used to construct optimized portfolios using Modern Portfolio Theory. Two strategies were implemented:
supervisor: Prof. Michael Frankel & Dr. Jiajing (Horatio) Huang

suggestions.title: Temporal Fusion Transformer Implementation
suggestions.desc: Benchmark the existing ARIMA and Prophet models against a Temporal Fusion Transformer (TFT). This research-oriented extension would test if attention-based deep learning can better capture non-linear market regimes compared to traditional time-series methods.

suggestions.title: Sentiment-Weighted Black-Litterman Model
suggestions.desc: Integrate a news sentiment analysis pipeline using NewsAPI and a financial BERT model (FinBERT). Use the sentiment scores as 'investor views' in a Black-Litterman optimization framework to adjust portfolio weights based on current market news.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Alpaca Paper Trading Bot
suggestions.desc: Build a Python-based execution engine that connects the model's daily predictions to the Alpaca Trading API. This allows for live 'paper trading' simulations where the portfolio is automatically rebalanced based on the forecasted error metrics (MAPE/RMSE).

suggestions.title: ESG-Constrained Portfolio Optimization
suggestions.desc: Add a data layer for Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) scores. Modify the optimization algorithm to allow users to set a minimum 'Ethical Score' threshold, creating a portfolio that maximizes returns while adhering to socially responsible investment constraints.

title: From Frustration to Function: Enhancing Usability in Public Transportation
authors: Harrison, Marcus, Nguyen, Hong, Khatib, Mohamed, Krusemark, Austin
abstract: Public transportation apps have recently become an essential tool for helping individuals navigate complex transit systems, however, many users still face issues with usability, accessibility, and reliability. Taking this into consideration, this project aims to evaluate the user experience of these apps and how one in particular can be improved. In doing so, our group hopes to create a more user-friendly experience that can make public transportation easier and more reliable for everyone.

suggestions.title: Crowdsourced Occupancy & Delay Tracking
suggestions.desc: Implement a real-time reporting feature using Firebase where users can report bus fullness or unexpected road closures. Overlay this crowdsourced data on top of standard GTFS (General Transit Feed Specification) data to provide more accurate arrival estimates.

suggestions.title: AR Navigation for Indoor Transit Hubs
suggestions.desc: Use ARCore or ARKit to build an augmented reality 'pathway' feature specifically for complex, multi-level train stations. This helps users navigate from the entrance to the correct platform in areas where GPS signals are notoriously unreliable.

suggestions.title: Wearable 'Time-to-Leave' Companion
suggestions.desc: Develop a watchOS/Wear OS companion app that monitors the user's walking speed and the live transit schedule. The app sends haptic alerts when it's time to start walking to the stop, ensuring they never miss a connection due to last-minute delays.

suggestions.title: Low-Sensory & Accessibility Routing
suggestions.desc: Create a specialized routing engine that uses OpenStreetMap tags to prioritize 'low-sensory' paths (avoiding construction or loud areas) and paths with confirmed elevator access, specifically for users with mobility or sensory processing needs.

title: Thought-Memory Model for Multi-Agent Simulation
authors: Hodge, Nicholas, Sandoval, Josue, Paschall, Greyson
abstract: A 2D web-based multi-agent simulation leverages Large Language Models to model human-like interactions among generative agents. A Thought-Memory system retrieves relevant data and prior memories from a database to construct JSON-style prompts for the LLM, which outputs intended agent actions. The system allows for observable, emergent interactions between agents within the simulated space.

suggestions.title: Game-Theoretic Resource Scarcity
suggestions.desc: Introduce a resource layer (food, water, currency) to the 2D world. Use the LLM to negotiate trade deals or alliances between agents, observing how the 'Thought-Memory' system adapts its social strategy when resources become scarce.

suggestions.title: Redis Vector Store Memory Scaling
suggestions.desc: Replace the standard database retrieval with a high-speed Redis Vector Store. This allows the simulation to scale from a few agents to hundreds by performing faster semantic searches on agent memories during the JSON prompt construction.

suggestions.title: No-Code Persona & Scenario Editor
suggestions.desc: Build a web interface that allows non-technical users to define agent 'personalities' via sliders (e.g., agreeableness, aggression) and backstories. The editor would then generate the initial JSON state and inject it into the simulation for observational research.

suggestions.title: Emergency Response Urban Planning Simulation
suggestions.desc: Apply the multi-agent system to urban planning by simulating how 'human-like' agents react to a localized disaster, like a power outage or fire. Analyze the emergent collective behavior to identify potential bottlenecks in real-world emergency exit designs.

title: “Command Center, do you copy?”
authors: Strickland, Sawyer, Miller, Ezavier, Hardy, Max
abstract: “Command Center, do you copy?” is a sci-fi themed survival horror game where players must sneak around and fend off an alien like enemy using their flashlight while also trying to find the parts needed to fix their communications system to call for help.

suggestions.title: Acoustic-Driven Enemy AI
suggestions.desc: Implement a dynamic audio occlusion and propagation system in Unity/Unreal. The alien enemy should react differently to different sound types, such as the metallic 'click' of a flashlight vs. the muffled sound of a player walking on carpet.

suggestions.title: Asymmetric Tactical Multiplayer
suggestions.desc: Introduce a networking layer using Mirror or Photon. One player plays the survivor in first-person, while a second player acts as the 'Alien Hive Mind,' viewing a top-down tactical map and placing pheromones to guide AI enemies toward the survivor.

suggestions.title: Procedural Circuit Repair Puzzles
suggestions.desc: Replace static part-finding with a procedural puzzle system for fixing the communications. Players must complete randomized logic gate puzzles or frequency-tuning mini-games that become increasingly difficult as the alien gets closer.

suggestions.title: Biometric 'Fear' Feedback Integration
suggestions.desc: Connect the game to a wearable heart rate monitor via OSC. When the player's real-life pulse exceeds a threshold, the in-game character's hands could shake, making flashlight aiming harder, or the alien could become more aggressive, sensing the player's fear.

title: Berry and Carrot - A 2.5D Unity Platformer Game
authors: Joiner, Everett, Egl, Rin, Griffin, Carter, Redmon, Kcyana
abstract: In Berry and Carrot, you play as two stuffed animals, a bear and a bunny, who are trying to escape from a claw machine that they have been trapped in for years. Each character has different strengths, and the player must use these skills strategically by switching between the two characters to solve puzzles themed around the inner workings of the claw machine featuring screws, springs, levers, and more.

suggestions.title: Procedural Puzzle Generator
suggestions.desc: Create an algorithm that generates unique claw machine levels by randomly arranging physics-based components (screws, levers, springs) that require specific character-switching sequences to solve.

suggestions.title: Steam Workshop Level Editor
suggestions.desc: Build an in-game editor using Unity's UI Toolkit that allows players to design their own machine-themed puzzles and share them with the community via a cloud-hosted backend or Steam Workshop integration.

suggestions.title: Asynchronous Couch Co-op
suggestions.desc: Implement a networking layer using Mirror or Photon that allows two players to control Berry and Carrot simultaneously on separate devices, requiring tight verbal coordination to navigate the claw machine's hazards.

suggestions.title: Dynamic 'Claw' Physics Events
suggestions.desc: Develop a system where an overhead claw periodically interacts with the level, moving platforms or dropping 'prizes' that change the level geometry, forcing the player to adapt their strategy mid-puzzle.

title: Cookly.io - Advanced Recipe Generator
authors: Slabysh, Mikita, Valencia, Evan, Martinez, Chris
abstract: Cookly.io was a passion project started during the AI Club Hackathon where it was awarded 3rd place. Cookly is an AI powered recipe assistant that helps users use available ingredients into delicious meals. Users can input ingredients manually or upload a photo of the pantry or fridge where Cookly will use computer vision to identify the ingredients and SBERT to match the ingredients with the perfect recipe.
supervisor: Dr. Femi Ojo & Dr. Chen Zhao

suggestions.title: Edge-Optimized YOLOv8 Object Detection
suggestions.desc: Replace generic computer vision with a custom-trained YOLOv8 model specifically tuned to recognize partially used produce and obscure packaging. Fine-tuning the model on a 'fridge-specific' dataset will significantly reduce ingredient identification errors.

suggestions.title: Nutritionix API & Macro Integration
suggestions.desc: Connect Cookly.io to the Nutritionix API to automatically calculate the caloric and macronutrient breakdown for every generated recipe. This allows users to filter AI-generated meals based on specific dietary goals like high-protein or low-carb.

suggestions.title: Smart Grocery Cart Auto-Sync
suggestions.desc: Implement a 'Missing Ingredients' feature that identifies items needed for a recipe that were NOT found in the pantry photo. Integrate with the Kroger or Instacart APIs to let users add those missing items to a digital shopping cart with one click.

suggestions.title: Zero-Waste Expiration Forecasting
suggestions.desc: Develop a logic layer that tracks when ingredients were first 'seen' by the camera. The recipe generator can then prioritize 'Near-Expiration' ingredients, helping users reduce food waste by suggesting recipes that use up wilting produce first.

title: Agentic AI Quiz Generation: Personalized Tutoring through Intelligent Retrieval and Adaptive Learning
authors: Sreekanth, Devananda
abstract: This research presents a personalized, agentic AI-powered system for multiple-choice question (MCQ) generation tailored to college-level tutoring in machine learning and software engineering domains. The primary objective is to enhance adaptive learning through reliable, context-aware quiz generation using long-context large language models (LLMs) and modular agent workflows. Our methodology is based on an eight-stage agentic architecture that separates tasks into two main phases: vector indexing and personalized quiz generation. In the indexing phase, academic PDFs are parsed, chunked with LangChain’s RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter, embedded via Google's text-embedding-005, and indexed using FAISS. A verification agent ensures topic alignment and integrity of the vector database. Upon receiving a user query, a retriever agent performs vector search, followed by a selector agent that filters high-quality chunks. A processor agent curates the final prompt, and a response agent generates the MCQ using Gemini 1.5 Pro. The evaluator agent assesses generated questions against ground truth using metrics like ExactMatch, Faithfulness, and BERTScore. Experimental results over 150 MCQs show Gemini’s accuracy improves from 78.00% (raw) to 93.33% when enhanced with context vectors, a 100k-token cache, and a 1M-token long-context window—achieving a +15.33% overall gain. Gemini also excels in Non-Hallucination (0.9150), Certainty (0.8883), and Answer Correctness (0.9260), indicating safe and reliable generation. When supplemented with context vectors and a training cache, these results highlight Gemini’s effectiveness as a reliable and context-sensitive model for personalized, agentic quiz generation in educational settings, offering strong potential for scalable and adaptive AI tutoring systems.

suggestions.title: RAG-Enhanced Curriculum Extraction from PDFs
suggestions.desc: Extend the eight-stage agentic architecture by adding a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipeline using LangChain. Allow users to upload specific university syllabi or textbooks (PDFs) so the MCQs generated are 100% aligned with a specific professor's course material rather than general knowledge.

suggestions.title: Automated Difficulty Calibration via IRT
suggestions.desc: Incorporate Item Response Theory (IRT) to dynamically adjust question difficulty. As students answer questions, the system should track performance and use an LLM agent to rewrite questions to be easier or harder, ensuring a personalized 'Flow State' for every learner.

suggestions.title: Live Agentic Socratic Tutoring Interface
suggestions.desc: Instead of just generating MCQs, build a chat interface where the agent acts as a Socratic tutor. When a student misses an MCQ, the agent should use the context from the vector index to guide the student toward the right answer using hints rather than giving the solution away.

suggestions.title: LTI Integration for Canvas and Blackboard
suggestions.desc: Develop an LTI (Learning Tools Interoperability) wrapper for the application. This would allow the nutrition/ML quiz app to be embedded directly as an 'External Tool' within major educational platforms, enabling automatic grade syncing and single sign-on (SSO) for students.

title: Sight-Singing Feedback
authors: Dann, Terah, Giroux, Sandy, Johnson, Nathanael, Scarbro, Amber
abstract: This project creates an engaging and interactive music-learning experience. Users start by selecting a tempo and melody number. The app then displays sheet music to guide them through the exercise. While singing, performers receive real-time visual feedback on pitch accuracy and tempo progression, allowing for dynamic adjustments and improved performance precision. The system continuously updates the music staff based on user performance, ensuring seamless interaction. This approach integrates technology with musical education, enhancing skill development through intuitive, data-driven feedback. By combining user-driven selections, interactive visualization, and real-time analysis, the application provides a structured, engaging platform for improving musical skills.

suggestions.title: MIDI-to-MusicXML Dynamic Importer
suggestions.desc: Integrate a parser that allows users to upload standard MIDI files, which the app then automatically converts into readable sheet music on the staff for the sight-singing engine to analyze.

suggestions.title: Vocal 'Guitar Hero' Mode
suggestions.desc: Create a gamified interface where pitch accuracy and tempo progression are tracked as a score, including streaks for perfect pitch and a global leaderboard for various difficulty levels of melodies.

suggestions.title: Multi-Part Harmony Feedback
suggestions.desc: Extend the audio processing using source separation (like Spleeter) to allow the system to provide feedback to two singers at once, checking for interval accuracy and harmonic blend in duets.

suggestions.title: Haptic Feedback for Accessibility
suggestions.desc: Develop a feature for visually impaired singers that translates pitch accuracy into haptic vibrations on a mobile device or smartwatch, allowing them to feel when they are sharp or flat relative to the melody.

title: Draw The Night Sky
authors: Ho, Dominic, Deas, Richard, Phillips, Monica, Strong, Gabe, Hartsfield, Conner
abstract: Draw The Night Sky is a game project made in collaboration with Carter’s Lake to make their constellation viewing program more accessible. The stars in the sky are quite difficult to see without the perfect conditions, so an alternative would assist with this greatly. By creating a fun and interactive experience through a game, it should teach the visitors of the nature center to be able to search for stars even outside of the game. Utilizing an accurate star map based on the Yale Bright Star catalogue, we have an accurate star map that mirrors the real world which adds to the immersion of the players. In addition to this, very simple real-world tools are provided to the player to find the constellations. Exploring space is quite lonely, so a fun companion in the form of Stella is there to give the player all the right tools to get the job done.

suggestions.title: AR Sky-Drawing Overlay
suggestions.desc: Utilize ARFoundation to create a mobile AR version where users point their phone at the real night sky and 'connect the dots' of the Yale Bright Star catalog stars to see the game's constellation art overlaid on the stars.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Celestial Sync
suggestions.desc: Connect the game to a real-time astronomical API (like Stellarium or SkyView) so the star map perfectly matches the user's specific GPS location and current time, making it a functional field tool for Carter’s Lake visitors.

suggestions.title: Narrated Mythology Questline
suggestions.desc: Add an educational 'Story Mode' where completing a constellation triggers a voiced narrative about its mythological origins, featuring 3D animated sequences that bring the ancient stories to life within the star map.

suggestions.title: Light Pollution Simulator
suggestions.desc: Implement a 'Bortle Scale' slider that shows users how the sky looks under different levels of light pollution versus the dark skies of Carter’s Lake, educating them on the importance of dark-sky preservation.

title: Accessible Interactive Map
authors: Connick, Justin, Ingram, Megan, Novak, Derrick, Williams, Spencer, Zhu, Emily
abstract: Finding that walking campus gets you out of breath? We did too! Using React and Flask, we are building a web application that directs KSU students to the path with the lowest elevation and shows the shifts in between. It also displays accessible doors. The purpose of this app is to develop a more inclusive application so people with asthma, cardiovascular issues, and wheelchairs at KSU can safely traverse campus.

suggestions.title: Crowdsourced Obstruction Reporting System
suggestions.desc: Add a real-time 'Incident Reporting' layer where users can mark temporary obstacles like construction, broken elevators, or blocked ramps on the map. Use a Socket.io backend to push these updates to all active users, rerouting them dynamically around the obstructions.

suggestions.title: Indoor-Outdoor Seamless Navigation
suggestions.desc: Extend the map's reach by integrating indoor floor plans for key KSU buildings using GeoJSON. Implement 'Vertical Routing' that prioritizes elevators over stairs, allowing a student in a wheelchair to navigate from a parking deck to a specific 3rd-floor classroom without leaving the app.

suggestions.title: Optimal Pathfinding Algorithm Benchmarking
suggestions.desc: Compare different pathfinding algorithms (A*, Dijkstra, Bellman-Ford) modified with an 'elevation penalty' weight. Research which weight distribution provides the most 'accessible' path versus the 'shortest' path, and publish a study on the trade-offs for users with different mobility needs.

suggestions.title: Wearable Integration for Effort Tracking
suggestions.desc: Connect the app to Apple Health or Google Fit to monitor the user's heart rate in real-time. If the app detects a spike in heart rate (due to an incline), it could suggest a nearby 'Cooling Zone' or a flatter alternative route dynamically.

title: Robot Tactics
authors: Anderson, John
abstract: Robot Tactics is a first person strategic shooter made in Unity where the player takes control of an agent who fights off bodyguards who are chasing him while using Robots to detour them

suggestions.title: Behavior Tree Enemy AI
suggestions.desc: Replace simple chasing mechanics with advanced Behavior Trees or Goal-Oriented Action Planning (GOAP), allowing bodyguards to use cover, reload tactically, and coordinate flanking maneuvers against the player's robots.

suggestions.title: RTS-Style Tactical Overlay
suggestions.desc: Build a 'Command Mode' where the camera switches to a top-down view, allowing players to pause time and issue specific movement waypoints and target priorities to their robot units before returning to FPS mode.

suggestions.title: Modular Robot Component System
suggestions.desc: Implement a system where players can customize their robots before a mission, choosing different chassis (treads vs. bipedal) and utility tools (EMP bursts vs. physical shields) to counter specific bodyguard types.

suggestions.title: Stealth & Hacking Expansion
