suggestions.desc: Add a hacking mechanic where players must use their robots to access remote terminals or disable security cameras, turning the game into a tactical stealth experience where direct combat with bodyguards is a last resort.

title: Database Masking Tool - Project 04 - Team 1
authors: Callison, Lleyton, Tettey-Enyo, Josh, Quillen, Alec, Salimi, Reda, Sigmon, Stephen
abstract: The Database Masking Tool for Gwinnett County Public Schools secures sensitive data while preserving its analytical utility. Developed alongside an in-depth research paper, this web-based solution enables real-time masking of information in SQL Server and MySQL databases. Utilizing automated field recognition, it applies three masking techniques—Faker-based masking, hash masking, and pseudonymization through generalized masking—to protect personally identifiable information. Key features include an intuitive interface for configuring masking rules, real-time data previews, and an export function for generating masked datasets in multiple formats. Built with a React-Flask stack and containerized for consistency, the system supports compliance with GDPR, HIPAA, and FERPA. Guided by feedback from sponsor Ed Van Ness and academic advisors, this project establishes a robust framework for scalable data anonymization, enhancing operational efficiency and regulatory compliance.

suggestions.title: Differential Privacy Noise Layer
suggestions.desc: Implement an epsilon-differential privacy feature that adds mathematical noise to numerical columns (like salary or age), ensuring that aggregate statistics remain accurate while making it impossible to identify individual student records.

suggestions.title: NoSQL & Cloud Database Connectors
suggestions.desc: Extend the tool's architecture to support non-relational databases like MongoDB and cloud-native warehouses like Snowflake, using a driver-based design for broader enterprise compatibility.

suggestions.title: In-Transit Masking Proxy
suggestions.desc: Develop a proxy layer that sits between the database and a web application, masking PII in real-time as results are fetched, ensuring that sensitive data never exists in cleartext within the application's RAM.

suggestions.title: NLP-Based PII Auto-Discovery
suggestions.desc: Integrate an NLP library (like Microsoft Presidio) to automatically scan unmapped text columns for potential PII like addresses or social security numbers, suggesting masking rules to the user automatically.

title: Some Kind of Tundra Escape
authors: Field, Tiffany, Mensah, Patrynna, Beene, Tahjir, Worrel, Daniel
abstract: Some Kind of Tundra Escape is an action-adventure game where you play as a penguin trying to escape a vast, walled-in tundra. As you explore, you’ll rescue other penguins scattered throughout the area. Your goal is to gather all the penguins and escape together, but the journey is far from easy. The tundra is full of danger, including snowmen who ambush you and ice golems who patrol certain areas. You'll need to avoid these monsters and set traps to stay safe while rescuing your fellow penguins. As your group grows, so does the challenge—more penguins means more obstacles to overcome. Strategize carefully, outsmart the monsters, and lead your group to the exit. Some Kind of Tundra Escape is a thrilling test of wit, stealth, and survival as you race against time to escape with your fellow penguins.

suggestions.title: Procedural Tundra Generation and Navigation Meshes
suggestions.desc: Replace static level design with a procedural generation system using Perlin noise to create infinite, randomized tundra layouts. Implement Unity's NavMesh components or A* Pathfinding Project to ensure snowmen and ice golems can navigate complex, dynamically generated terrain without clipping through ice walls.

suggestions.title: Machine Learning-Based Predator AI
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Unity ML-Agents toolkit to train the ice golems using reinforcement learning. This allows the enemies to learn the player's trap-setting patterns over time, forcing the player to evolve their rescue strategies as the game progresses.

suggestions.title: Asymmetric Cooperative Multiplayer Mode
suggestions.desc: Extend the game to support local or networked multiplayer using Mirror or Photon Fusion. One player controls the penguin hero while the second player acts as a 'Guardian Spirit' who can manipulate the environment or scout enemy patrol paths from a top-down view to help coordinate the escape.

suggestions.title: Penguin Colony Management and Base Building
suggestions.desc: Incorporate a meta-game where rescued penguins are brought back to a 'Safe Zone' hub. Use C# scriptable objects to manage resources (fish, ice blocks) that rescued penguins gather, allowing players to upgrade traps or build permanent defenses against nocturnal snowmen raids.

title: Foster AI Interview and Biography Generation
authors: Crawford, Joshua, Lahr, Katlin, Haigh, Mikayla, Payne, Brittany, Morhun, Daria
abstract: This capstone project presents a proof of concept for a mobile and web-based application designed to streamline communication between foster caregivers and the Angels Among Us Pet Rescue team. The application addresses critical inefficiencies in generating pet biographies and coordinating photography efforts, which are essential components in increasing adoption rates. Leveraging cutting-edge technologies such as Twilio, Retell AI, and OpenAI, the app implements a bio generation workflow that conducts foster interviews via phone calls, transcribes responses using AI-powered voice-to-text, analyzes sentiment, and produces structured, engaging pet bios for platforms like Petfinder. Additionally, the system automates email workflows to coordinate photography sessions, minimizing manual coordination. Built using React for the front end, PostgreSQL for the database, and Python for backend automation, the application emphasizes both usability and data security, ensuring sensitive foster and pet information is handled responsibly. This project demonstrates the practical application of AI and full-stack development to solve real-world challenges in animal rescue and adoption operations.

suggestions.title: Computer Vision-Enhanced Photography Grading
suggestions.desc: Integrate a Python-based microservice using OpenCV or a pre-trained ResNet model to automatically score pet photos uploaded by foster caregivers. The system should provide real-time feedback on lighting, focus, and 'adoptability' features (like eyes being visible) before sending the data to the bio generator.

suggestions.title: RAG-Powered Personality Matching
suggestions.desc: Implement Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) using a vector database like Pinecone or Weaviate. This would allow the system to compare generated pet biographies against a database of successful past adoptions and adopter preference surveys to suggest the 'perfect match' for a specific pet.

suggestions.title: Automated Multi-Channel Social Distribution
suggestions.desc: Build an integration engine that uses the Meta Graph API and Petfinder API to automatically format and schedule the generated biographies and photos. The tool would convert the AI-generated text into platform-specific posts (e.g., shorter for X/Twitter, hashtag-heavy for Instagram) to maximize visibility.

suggestions.title: White-Label SaaS Rescue Management Platform
suggestions.desc: Generalize the architecture to support multi-tenancy, allowing multiple pet rescue organizations to use the app independently. Add a dashboard built with React or Vue that tracks 'Time-to-Adoption' metrics, helping rescues see how AI-generated bios correlate with faster adoption rates.

title: MediVault: An AI-Powered Secure Medical Image Sharing Platform
authors: Ekeocha, Henry, Calixte, Reginald, Patel, Dhruv
abstract: MediVault is a secure, cloud-native platform that empowers patients and healthcare providers to upload, view, and share medical images like X-rays and MRIs with confidence. Built using Next.js and Node.js, and deployed on AWS Free Tier (S3, RDS, KMS), the system implements role-based access, two-factor authentication, and end-to-end encryption to ensure privacy and HIPAA compliance. MediVault features an intuitive interface and integrates AI-enhanced automation to streamline metadata tagging and detect potential anomalies in scans. Designed for scalability, usability, and compliance, this project showcases real-world expertise in full-stack cloud development and healthcare cybersecurity.
supervisor: Dr. Ying Xie; Project Sponsors: Gennadiy Kemelmakher, Arpna Aggrawal , Richard Windland

suggestions.title: Zero-Knowledge Proof (ZKP) Image Authorization
suggestions.desc: Implement a ZKP protocol using Circom or ZoKrates so that a patient can prove they have a valid diagnosis or image without revealing the actual content of the file. This allows for 'blind' verification of medical credentials during insurance claims or employment screenings.

suggestions.title: DICOM-Native Web-GL Viewer Integration
suggestions.desc: Replace standard image views with a high-performance DICOM (Digital Imaging and Communications in Medicine) renderer using Cornerstone.js. This allows radiologists to perform measurements, adjust contrast (window/level), and view 3D reconstructions directly within the MediVault browser interface.

suggestions.title: Automated HIPAA Compliance Audit Logging
suggestions.desc: Build an automated monitoring system using AWS CloudTrail and Amazon Athena that generates real-time HIPAA compliance reports. The system should flag any unauthorized access attempts or unusual patterns in medical image sharing and export them into a format ready for official audits.

suggestions.title: Radiology-as-a-Service Marketplace
suggestions.desc: Expand MediVault to include a gig-economy style portal where patients can pay for a second opinion. Doctors can sign up, verify their credentials, and receive secure, time-limited access to specific shared images to provide diagnostic notes within the platform.

title: Secure Medical Image Sharing Platform – MedShare
authors: Adenuga, Toyese, Lateu, Richard
abstract: The Secure Medical Image Sharing Platform is a cloud-based solution that ensures secure upload, management, and sharing of medical images, adhering to HIPAA and GDPR. It utilizes advanced encryption protocols, role-based access control (RBAC), and audit trails to safeguard patient data. The platform's user-friendly interface facilitates seamless interaction between patients and healthcare providers, enabling the use of AI tools for diagnostic support.
supervisor: Dr. Ying Xie; Sponsors: Mrs. Arpna Aggarwal, Gennadiy Kemelmakher Advisors: Richard Windland

suggestions.title: Client-Side AES-256 Encryption Pipeline
suggestions.desc: Enhance security by implementing end-to-end encryption where files are encrypted in the browser using the Web Crypto API before being sent to the server. This ensures that even if the MedShare database is compromised, the medical images remain unreadable because the server never holds the raw decryption keys.

suggestions.title: AI-Driven PII Redaction for Research Sharing
suggestions.desc: Build a 'Research Mode' that uses Amazon Comprehend Medical to automatically identify and redact Personally Identifiable Information (PII) from medical notes and image metadata. This allows MedShare users to safely export datasets for academic studies without violating GDPR/HIPAA privacy rules.

suggestions.title: Multi-Factor Biometric Access Control
suggestions.desc: Integrate WebAuthn to allow doctors and patients to log in using biometric sensors (TouchID/FaceID) on their devices. This provides a hardware-backed security layer that is significantly more resistant to phishing than standard 2FA SMS codes for accessing sensitive diagnostic data.

suggestions.title: Inter-Hospital Emergency Access Protocol
suggestions.desc: Create a 'Break Glass' feature for emergency rooms. If a patient is incapacitated, an ER physician can request emergency access to their MedShare history, which triggers an automated legal log and immediate notifications to the patient's emergency contacts while granting temporary viewing rights.

title: OncoClarify – AI Powered Cancer Report Simplifier
authors: Koganti, Sai Chandana
abstract: Cancer pathology reports are important for diagnosis and treatment planning, yet their complex language poses a significant challenge for patients and nurses to understand. This communication barrier often results in confusion, anxiety, delayed decisions, and reduced care quality. To address this, OncoClarify, an AI-powered tool, has been developed to simplify cancer pathology reports and provide role-specific explanations tailored to doctors, nurses, and patients. By leveraging the Gemini API, the system extracts critical details from pathology reports and generates customized summaries. Doctors receive technical insights highlighting biomarkers, mutations, and prognostic indicators. Nurses

suggestions.title: RAG-Enhanced Medical Citations
suggestions.desc: Implement a Retrieval-Augmented Generation system that links simplified terms in the report directly to peer-reviewed sources (PubMed/Medline), allowing users to verify the medical accuracy of the AI's explanation.

suggestions.title: Interactive 3D Pathology Visualizer
suggestions.desc: Connect the report findings to a Three.js-based 3D human anatomy model, highlighting the specific locations and characteristics described in the report to help patients visualize their diagnosis.

suggestions.title: Hallucination Guardrail for Medical Safety
suggestions.desc: Develop a secondary validation layer that cross-checks Gemini's simplified output against the original pathology report to flag any medical inconsistencies or 'hallucinated' diagnoses before the patient sees them.

suggestions.title: Secure HIPAA-Compliant Patient Portal
suggestions.desc: Build a secure web interface for clinics that integrates with existing Electronic Health Records (EHR) systems, allowing doctors to approve the simplified AI reports before they are released to patients.

title: ClinicPix: Secure Medical Image Sharing Web Application
authors: Harris, Michael, Kumi, Selorm, Tan, Zhi Ern, Hodgson, James
abstract: ClinicPix is a cloud-based system designed to streamline the management of medical images such as X-rays and MRIs. It offers healthcare providers and patients a secure, intuitive interface to upload, view, and share medical images across institutions and devices. The platform ensures full compliance with HIPAA through robust security measures, including role-based access control, end-to-end encryption, and comprehensive audit trails. Its scalable architecture supports growing data needs while maintaining high performance and reliability. By enhancing accessibility and safeguarding sensitive health information, the platform aims to improve clinical workflows, patient engagement, and collaborative care.

suggestions.title: Federated Learning for Private Diagnostics
suggestions.desc: Implement a privacy-preserving layer using OpenMined or PySyft that allows researchers to train diagnostic models on the hosted images without ever decrypting or moving the raw data from the ClinicPix servers.

suggestions.title: Client-Side DICOM Anonymization & Edge Compression
suggestions.desc: Build a browser-based preprocessing tool using pydicom.js to automatically strip Patient Health Information (PHI) and compress high-resolution MRI scans locally before they are uploaded to the cloud.

suggestions.title: WebXR Surgical Visualization Module
suggestions.desc: Develop a Three.js or WebXR extension that takes stacked DICOM slices from the application and renders them as 3D interactive volumes, allowing surgeons to visualize scans spatially through a browser or VR headset.

suggestions.title: Immutable Audit Ledger via Hyperledger Fabric
suggestions.desc: Replace traditional database audit trails with a Hyperledger Fabric private blockchain to create a tamper-proof record of every image access and share event, ensuring maximum accountability for HIPAA compliance.

title: Personalized Wellness Recommendations
authors: Yaganti, Varshini
abstract: A health recommendation system using machine learning, built on Hadoop, Spark, and HDFS, represents a significant advancement in personalized healthcare. This project aims to leverage big data technologies to process and analyze vast amounts of medical data across a distributed computing environment, utilizing at least three virtual machines. The background of this project lies in the increasing prevalence of chronic diseases and the growing volume of health-related data collected by healthcare providers. The motivation for this project stems from several key factors. Firstly, traditional healthcare systems often struggle to provide personalized recommendations due to the sheer volume and complexity of medical data. By utilizing Hadoop and Spark’s distributed processing capabilities, this system can efficiently analyze large-scale health data, enabling more accurate and timely recommendations. Secondly, the integration of machine learning algorithms with big data technologies allows for the identification of subtle patterns and correlations in patient data that may not be apparent through conventional analysis methods. This can lead to more precise diagnoses and treatment plans tailored to individual patients. The expected results of this project include a robust health recommendation system capable of processing and analyzing large volumes of medical data in a distributed environment.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Biometric Stream Processing with Kafka
suggestions.desc: Extend the Spark-based batch system by integrating Apache Kafka to ingest live heart rate and blood glucose data from wearables, enabling the system to trigger instant wellness alerts instead of waiting for daily batch cycles.

suggestions.title: Precision Nutrition through Genomic Data Fusion
suggestions.desc: Integrate public genomic datasets from the NIH or Kaggle with the current recommendation engine to suggest personalized diet plans based on genetic markers for metabolic disorders like Vitamin D deficiency or lactose intolerance.

suggestions.title: Explainable Wellness with Spark-SHAP
suggestions.desc: Incorporate SHAP or LIME libraries within the Spark ML pipeline to generate 'human-readable' explanations for each recommendation, showing users exactly which health metric (e.g., sleep vs. activity) drove the AI's advice.

suggestions.title: HAPI FHIR EHR Integration Adapter
suggestions.desc: Build a middleware adapter using the HAPI FHIR library that allows the Hadoop cluster to automatically pull and sync patient data directly from industry-standard Electronic Health Record systems like Epic or Cerner.

title: Large-Scale Cybersecurity Threat Detection
authors: CHILUKURI, PAVAN CHOWDARY; Thiriveedhi, Mohan Krishna Kandimalla Triveni; Sammeta, Raghava; challapalli, venkata Basanth; Kandimalla, Triveni
abstract: Cybersecurity threats are becoming more sophisticated, posing serious risks to critical systems. Traditional intrusion detection systems often fail to manage the scale and complexity of network traffic. This study investigates large-scale threat detection using machine learning in PySpark, utilizing the UNSW-NB15 dataset. It focuses on building scalable models through preprocessing, feature selection, and implementing algorithms like Decision Trees, Naïve Bayes, Random Forest, and Gradient Boosting. Evaluation metrics include accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and ROC-AUC, with emphasis on hyperparameter tuning and minimizing false positives. Leveraging PySpark’s distributed computing, the system ensures efficient real-time analysis of vast network data. The research supports modern cybersecurity strategies by enhancing detection reliability and reducing risks from emerging cyber threats.

suggestions.title: Adversarial Evasion Stress Testing
suggestions.desc: Use the Adversarial Robustness Toolbox (ART) to generate 'evasion attacks'—malicious traffic patterns specifically designed to fool the Random Forest models—and retrain the system to become resilient against AI-generated malware.

suggestions.title: High-Cardinality Log Feature Engineering
suggestions.desc: Implement Spark’s FeatureHasher and VectorAssembler to handle modern, high-cardinality NetFlow v9 logs, allowing the model to analyze complex network headers that were previously excluded from the UNSW-NB15 dataset.

suggestions.title: Zero-Day Detection via Isolation Forests
suggestions.desc: Shift from supervised classification to semi-supervised anomaly detection by implementing PySpark’s Isolation Forest algorithm to identify threats that have no labels in the training set, mimicking real-world 'Zero-Day' exploits.

suggestions.title: Real-Time SOC Visualization Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Connect the PySpark inference stream to an ELK stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana) to build a Security Operations Center dashboard that visualizes attack origins and threat classifications on a live map.

similar: legacy:GR-07|Fall 2016|feature selection and improving classification performance for malware detection

title: Real-Time Object Detection
authors: Malik, Rohit
abstract: This project explores the implementation of real-time object detection using the You Only Look Once (YOLO) architecture. Leveraging its speed and accuracy, we developed a system capable of identifying and localizing multiple objects within live video streams. Our implementation focused on optimizing YOLO's performance for real-time applications, specifically addressing the trade-off between speed and accuracy. We employed a pre-trained YOLO model and fine-tuned it on a custom dataset tailored to specific object classes. This fine-tuning process aimed to enhance the model's ability to recognize objects in our target environment. The system was implemented using Python and the OpenCV library,

suggestions.title: YOLO-to-Edge TensorRT Quantization
suggestions.desc: Optimize the YOLO weights using NVIDIA TensorRT or OpenVINO to perform INT8 quantization, enabling the model to maintain 30+ FPS on low-power hardware like a Raspberry Pi 5 or NVIDIA Jetson Nano.

suggestions.title: Domain Adaptation with Synthetic Environments
suggestions.desc: Use NVIDIA Omniverse to generate synthetic training data with extreme weather conditions (fog, heavy rain) to fine-tune the model, improving its detection reliability in environments where real-world data is hard to capture.

suggestions.title: Intelligent Traffic Flow & Density Prediction
suggestions.desc: Extend the system using the DeepSORT tracking algorithm to assign unique IDs to detected objects, allowing you to calculate vehicle density and predict traffic congestion patterns at intersections in real-time.

suggestions.title: Low-Code Auto-Labeling Transfer Learning Tool
suggestions.desc: Build a web platform where users can upload a small set of custom images and use the existing YOLO model to 'pre-label' them, creating a semi-automated pipeline for training models on new, niche objects.

title: NibbleAI
authors: Deem, Ryan, Birgili, Zeynep, Cook, Austin
abstract: Ever looked into your fridge or pantry and wondered, “What can I make with this?” NibbleAI is a mobile app designed to solve exactly that. Using artificial intelligence, the app identifies ingredients from user-uploaded images and suggests recipes based on what’s available. Built with React Native and powered by a DenseNet169 model for image recognition, NibbleAI seamlessly analyzes photos and returns curated recipe ideas — all within a few taps. This intuitive approach helps users reduce food waste, save time, and get creative with the ingredients they already have.

suggestions.title: Dynamic Grocery List and Cart Integration
suggestions.desc: Extend NibbleAI by integrating the Kroger or Instacart API. If a user chooses a suggested recipe but is missing one key ingredient, the app should allow them to add that specific item to a digital shopping cart or find the nearest store that has it in stock with a single tap.

suggestions.title: Nutritional and Glycemic Index Filtering
suggestions.desc: Incorporate the Nutritionix API to provide detailed caloric and macronutrient breakdowns for each suggested recipe. Add a specialized 'Health Mode' that filters recipes based on dietary needs like 'Diabetic-Friendly' (low glycemic index), 'Low Sodium', or 'High Protein' for fitness-focused users.

suggestions.title: Food Waste and Carbon Footprint Analytics
suggestions.desc: Develop a dashboard that tracks the ingredients used versus those that expire, estimating the amount of food waste saved. Use the MyEmissions API to calculate the carbon footprint of each recipe, encouraging users to choose lower-impact meals based on what's already in their fridge.

suggestions.title: AR-Based 'Visual Fridge' Inventory Management
suggestions.desc: Implement an Augmented Reality (AR) overlay using ARCore or ARKit where users can 'tag' items in their fridge via the camera. The app can then display virtual expiration timers over real-world milk cartons or vegetables, sending push notifications when an item is about to go bad.

title: SafeCircle: AI and Micro-radar-based Remote Monitoring for Patients with AD/ADRD
authors: RAHMAN, AWAN-UR-, Quddus, Shakib, Borty, Soarov Chakra
abstract: Alzheimer's disease and related dementias (AD/ADRD) is an irreversible and degenerative neurological condition that severely impacts neurons, resulting in cognitive decline and memory loss. This study explores a mHealth system, including a SafeCircle iOS prototype, a novel solution that combines artificial intelligence with cutting-edge micro-radar technology. The platform offers a variety of features, including management of patient and caregiver profiles, real-time alerts in case of emergencies, emergency contact lists, one-touch SOS support, sharing of live locations, and recording of unusual events in video. It is a responsive and reliable care assistant that optimizes patient safety while reducing caregiver burden.
supervisor: Dr. Nazmus Sakib & Dr. Sumit Chakravarty

suggestions.title: Biometric Correlation of Cognitive Agitation
suggestions.desc: Use the micro-radar data to track restless movement patterns during sleep and use time-series forecasting to correlate these 'agitation' spikes with the clinical progression of Alzheimer’s symptoms.

suggestions.title: Edge-Processing Fall Detection Hub
suggestions.desc: Offload the radar signal processing from the iOS app to an onboard ESP32 microcontroller, ensuring that fall detection and emergency alerts function even if the user's smartphone is disconnected or powered off.

suggestions.title: Predictive Wandering Geofence Alerts
suggestions.desc: Combine micro-radar activity levels with GPS geofencing to predict wandering events; if the radar detects high agitation and the user leaves a 'SafeZone,' the app triggers an immediate high-priority alert for the caregiver.

suggestions.title: Caregiver Burnout Sentiment Tracker
suggestions.desc: Add a voice-based journaling feature for caregivers that uses NLP sentiment analysis to track their stress levels over time, recommending local support groups or respite care when high burnout risk is detected.

title: Deep Learning-Based Skin Cancer Detection
authors: Polisetty, Andrew, Buddharaju, Akshay Krishna Varma, Yellu, Siri
abstract: Skin cancer is increasingly becoming a severe health problem globally today, but early detection is essential to enhance survival rates. Nonetheless, conventional diagnosis relies largely on visual examinations by dermatologists, which can be subjective and time-consuming. This research examines the application of deep learning for the automation of skin cancer detection based on dermoscopic images from the HAM10000 dataset. The models VGG19, DenseNet121 and ResNet152 will be trained and evaluated, with class mbalance addressed using data augmentation strategies. The outputs will demonstrate the applicability of deep learning to improve skin cancer diagnosis. Classification optimization usin

suggestions.title: Vision Transformer (ViT) Texture Analysis
suggestions.desc: Implement a Vision Transformer (ViT) model and compare its performance against the existing ResNet152, specifically analyzing if the transformer's attention mechanism better captures the subtle skin textures relevant to melanoma.

suggestions.title: Active Learning Human-in-the-Loop Labeler
suggestions.desc: Build a tool that identifies 'low-confidence' predictions from the DenseNet121 model and presents them to a human expert for labeling, using the new data to iteratively retrain and improve the model with minimal manual effort.

suggestions.title: Skin Tone Bias Mitigation Audit
suggestions.desc: Evaluate the model's accuracy across different Fitzpatrick skin types and apply re-weighting techniques or specialized data augmentation to ensure the diagnostic tool performs equitably across diverse patient demographics.

suggestions.title: Mobile AR Lesion Screener
suggestions.desc: Port the best-performing model to CoreML and build an iOS app that uses Augmented Reality to highlight potential areas of concern on a patient's skin in a real-time camera view, intended for use as a preliminary screening tool.

similar: legacy:GR-27|Spring 2018|fault detection in iot using deep learning

title: Multi-label commit message classification using p-tuning
authors: Mistry , Tanvi
abstract: Version control systems (VCS) play a crucial role by enabling developers to record changes, revert to previous versions, and coordinate work across distributed teams. In version control systems (e.g., GitHub), commit message serves as concise descriptions of code changes made during development. In our project, we propose to evaluate the performance of multi-label commit message classification using p-tuning (learnable prompt templates) through pre-trained models such as BERT and DistilBERT. The initial results show that p-tuning can provide similar results by designing various flexible templates that are not restricted by fixed templates.

suggestions.title: Automated Semantic ChangeLog Generation
suggestions.desc: Leverage the multi-label classifications (e.g., 'bug fix', 'feature', 'refactor') to automatically generate categorized, human-readable Release Notes. Use an LLM like Llama-3 to summarize all commits within a specific tag/version based on the labels assigned by your P-tuned BERT model.

suggestions.title: CI/CD Quality Gate for Commit Standards
suggestions.desc: Develop a GitHub Action that uses the P-tuned model to evaluate every incoming Pull Request. If a commit message is classified as 'unclear' or doesn't match the actual code changes (verified by comparing diffs), the Action can automatically block the merge and suggest a more accurate label.

suggestions.title: Cross-Lingual Transfer Learning for Global Teams
suggestions.desc: Extend the project by using mBERT (Multilingual BERT) and applying the same P-tuning techniques to classify commit messages written in languages other than English (e.g., Chinese, Spanish). Evaluate how well the learned 'prompts' transfer across different programming cultures and languages.

suggestions.title: Developer Workflow and Intent Analysis Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Build a data visualization dashboard for project managers that analyzes the 'intent' of a repository over time. By visualizing the ratio of 'Technical Debt' vs. 'New Features' commits, teams can identify when a codebase is becoming unstable or when developer focus has shifted too far from maintenance.

title: Large language model enabled mental health app recommendations using structured datasets
authors: Prasad, Kris
abstract: The increasing use of large language models (LLMs) in mental health support neces-sitates detailed evaluation of their recommendation capabilities. This study compares four modern LLMs—GPT-4o, Claude 3.5 Sonnet, dataset-enhanced Gemma 2, and dataset-enhanced GPT-3.5-Turbo—in recommending mental health applications. We constructed a structured dataset of 55 mental health apps using RoBERTa-based sentiment analysis and keyword similarity scoring, focusing on depression, anxiety, ADHD, and insomnia. Standard LLMs emonstrated inconsistent accuracy and often relied on outdated or generic information. In contrast, our retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) pipeline enabled lower-cost models to achieve 100% accuracy, compared to baseline models (GPT-4o at 45% and Claude at 70%), while maintaining good diversity and recommending apps with significantly better user ratings. These findings demonstrate that dataset-enhanced, cost-effective LLMs can outperform expensive proprietary models in domain-specific applications like mental health resource recommendations, potentially improving accessibility to quality mental health support tools.
supervisor: Dr. Md Abdullah Al Hafiz Khan

suggestions.title: RAG-Enhanced Review Analysis
suggestions.desc: Implement a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipeline using a Pinecone vector database to feed real-time user reviews from the App Store into the LLM, ensuring recommendations reflect current app performance and bugs.

suggestions.title: Clinical Guardrail Safety Layer
suggestions.desc: Integrate NVIDIA NeMo Guardrails or Llama Guard to monitor user prompts, ensuring the LLM never provides medical diagnoses and automatically redirects users to crisis hotlines if high-risk language is detected.

suggestions.title: Multi-Agent Consensus Recommendation
suggestions.desc: Build an agentic workflow where three specialized LLM agents—one focused on privacy, one on clinical efficacy, and one on cost—evaluate an app and negotiate a final, balanced recommendation for the user.

suggestions.title: Patient-App Matchmaking Simulator
suggestions.desc: Create a dashboard for clinicians where they can input a patient's specific traits and history to see how the LLM ranks different apps, including the 'reasoning' generated by the model for why an app fits that specific case.

title: FedDA-TSformer: Federated Domain Adaptation with Vision TimeSformer for Left Ventricle Segmentation on Gated Myocardial Perfusion SPECT Image
authors: Huang, YeHong
abstract: This study presents FedDA-TSformer, an approach for accurate left ventricle segmentation in gated myocardial perfusion single-photon emission computed tomography (MPS) images, designed to ensure both high segmentation quality and patient data privacy. By integrating federated learning with domain adaptation techniques, the proposed model leverages a novel Divide-Space-Time-Attention mechanism that effectively captures spatio-temporal correlations inherent in multi-centered MPS datasets. Domain discrepancies among data from three different hospitals are mitigated using a local maximum mean discrepancy (LMMD) loss, enabling robust performance across various clinical settings. Evaluated on a dataset comprising 150 subjects with eight distinct cardiac cycle phases, FedDA-TSformer achieved Dice Similarity Coefficients of 0.842 and 0.907 for the segmentation of the left ventricular endocardium and epicardium, respectively. These results demonstrate the model's potential to improve the functional assessment of the left ventricle while upholding stringent data security standards.

suggestions.title: Edge TPU Deployment for Hospital Workstations
suggestions.desc: Port the FedDA-TSformer model to run on NVIDIA Jetson or Google Coral hardware. This allows real-time segmentation of SPECT images directly in the imaging room without needing high-end server clusters.

suggestions.title: Cross-Modality Transfer Learning with GANs
suggestions.desc: Use Generative Adversarial Networks to translate SPECT images into MRI-like representations. Test if the TSformer weights can be fine-tuned for cardiac MRI segmentation using the same federated privacy constraints.

suggestions.title: Federated Compliance & Audit Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Build a React-based management console for hospital administrators to monitor model convergence across multiple nodes. Implement a 'zero-knowledge' logging system that tracks model updates without exposing patient metadata.

suggestions.title: Multi-Organ Spatio-Temporal Segmentation
suggestions.desc: Expand the Divide-Space-Time-Attention mechanism to segment the liver and kidneys simultaneously in gated MPS images. This would help in assessing systemic tracer distribution and potential side effects in oncology patients.

title: SHERLOCK: Self-supervised Histopathological Evaluation for Recognition of Lymphocytes and Other Cancerous Kinds
authors: Pagano, Charles
abstract: Whole Slide Images (WSI) are gigantic images (e.g. 100k x 100k pixels) of tissue samples. The goal of SHERLOCK is to detect cancer cells in those tissue samples. We do this by using a pretrained Masked Autoencoder (MAE), from Facebook’s research lab, that we finetune on the PanNuke dataset. The benefit of using an MAE is that unlike supervised learning the WSI’s don’t need to be labeled. This is important because it will save a lot of time and money that would be spent on labeling WSI’s.

suggestions.title: Hierarchical Zoom-In MAE Architecture
suggestions.desc: WSIs are too large for standard transformers. Implement a two-stage pipeline where a low-resolution MAE identifies 'Regions of Interest' (ROI) and a high-resolution MAE processes patches at 40x magnification, connected via a feature-pyramid network.

suggestions.title: Contrastive Self-Supervised Refinement
suggestions.desc: Combine the reconstructive power of the Masked Autoencoder with a contrastive loss (like MoCo v3). This forces the model to not only 'fill in the blanks' but also ensure that similar cancerous cell types are clustered together in the latent space.

suggestions.title: Zero-Shot Rare Cell Discovery
suggestions.desc: Use the MAE's reconstruction error as an anomaly detector. Areas of the tissue sample that the model cannot accurately 'reconstruct' likely represent rare morphologies or new cancer subtypes not present in the PanNuke training set, flagging them for human review.

suggestions.title: Distributed WSI Tiling API with Dask
suggestions.desc: Develop a high-throughput backend using OpenSlide and Dask to stream 100k x 100k pixel images into the MAE model in parallel, creating a web-based dashboard where pathologists can see real-time 'cancer probability' overlays.

title: Towards Bounding the Behavior of Deep Neural Networks
authors: Boyce, Aidan
abstract: Recent advances in Artificial Intelligence (AI) have unlocked many new possibilities but have also brought with it many new challenges. While modern AI systems have been continuously exceeding expectations, our ability to interpret and understand their behavior lags behind. For example, an AI model trained to detect pneumonia from X-rays may fail in new hospitals because it learned to recognize hospital logos instead of medical patterns. Why do some succeed while others fail? Do they truly understand their tasks, or are they relying on patterns that may not always hold? To enumerate the most informative explanations of a neuron’s behavior, we developed an improved approach to bounding the behavior of individual neurons within artificial neural networks. In this research we demonstrate, both theoretically and empirically, the utility of our approach.

suggestions.title: Automated ROI Focus Verification Tool
suggestions.desc: Build a pipeline that uses Grad-CAM heatmaps to automatically flag training images where the model's 'attention' is focused on hospital logos or text. Use this to automatically filter bias from medical datasets.

suggestions.title: Formal Verification with Marabou
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Marabou framework to mathematically prove the DNN's behavior bounds. Define specific constraints (e.g., 'brightness changes must not change the diagnosis') and verify that the model adheres to them.

suggestions.title: Fairness Assessment in Skin Lesion Datasets
suggestions.desc: Apply the bounding methods to the ISIC (International Skin Imaging Collaboration) dataset. Investigate if models are learning to detect surgical skin markings or ruler scales rather than actual melanomas.

suggestions.title: FDA Compliance Model Report Card
suggestions.desc: Develop a CLI tool that generates a standardized 'Behavioral Certificate' for AI models. This report would document the known bounds and failure cases, serving as a critical piece of documentation for medical device regulation.

title: Impact of Motor Skill on Learning Experiences and Outcomes using Note-Taking in VR
authors: Strickland, Sawyer
abstract: Immersive learning experiences have been proposed to offer rich immersion and interaction, effectively addressing the distractions and low engagement commonly found in typical online learning environments. Research in neuroscience and psychology suggests that motor skills, such as note-taking, help students improve their learning by enhancing cognitive abilities and decision-making, ultimately leading to better performance. This study aims to investigate the impact of motor skills, specifically note-taking with a physical VR stylus, on learning experiences, outcomes, and retention in our VR classroom environment.

suggestions.title: Haptic Resistance and Weight Simulation
suggestions.desc: Extend the VR environment by integrating haptic gloves (e.g., Manus) to simulate 'pen-on-paper' friction. Compare learning outcomes when students feel the physical resistance of writing versus the standard 'air-writing' available in Quest/Vive controllers.

suggestions.title: Eye-Tracking and Cognitive Load Correlation
suggestions.desc: Using a headset with integrated eye-tracking (like the Vive Pro Eye), correlate pupil dilation and fixation duration with the note-taking 'motor skill' events to determine if physical writing actually reduces mental fatigue during long lectures.

suggestions.title: Kinesthetic Language Learning Module
suggestions.desc: Apply the motor-skill findings to a new domain: 3D vocabulary acquisition. Build a module where users must physically draw the shape of an object or write its name in the air within a 1:1 scale environment to trigger spatial memory.

suggestions.title: Intelligent VR-to-Markdown Transcriber
suggestions.desc: Develop a system that uses the spatial coordinates of the VR 'pen' to perform OCR (Optical Character Recognition) on the fly, converting the student's 3D scribbles into formatted, searchable Markdown notes for external study.

title: Quantum Machine Learning for Science and Engineering
authors: Dow, Caleb, Tebor, Daniel, Lewis, Dante
abstract: This research explores the comparative effectiveness of traditional machine learning algorithms and their quantum counterparts. Traditional and quantum implementations of algorithms including Support Vector Machines (SVM), logistic regression, Principal Component Analysis (PCA), random forest classifiers, neural networks, and convolutional neural networks (CNN) are evaluated and contrasted. Findings highlight that quantum algorithms can provide certain clear advantages in some models and data while exhibiting inferior performance in others. By assessing these nuances, this research helps contribute to the understanding of quantum machine learning algorithms and their potential applications for science, engineering, and industrial tasks.
supervisor: Prof. Sharon Perry & Dr. Yong Shi

suggestions.title: PennyLane Hybrid Physics-Informed Neural Nets
suggestions.desc: Develop a hybrid Quantum-Classical neural network using the PennyLane library to solve fluid dynamics equations. Compare its convergence speed and accuracy against traditional solvers in engineering simulations.

suggestions.title: Automated Quantum Feature Map Discovery
suggestions.desc: Implement a Genetic Algorithm that searches for the optimal quantum circuit architecture (feature maps) for a given dataset. This moves beyond 'off-the-shelf' quantum circuits to find data-specific embeddings.

suggestions.title: Quantum-Enhanced Portfolio Risk Factor Analysis
suggestions.desc: Apply the Quantum PCA findings to financial time-series data from Yahoo Finance. Test if quantum dimensionality reduction can better identify hidden risk factors in volatile markets compared to classical PCA.

suggestions.title: QML Model-as-a-Service API
suggestions.desc: Build a FastAPI wrapper that bridges classical web apps to IBM Quantum or Amazon Braket. The service should automatically select the best algorithm (Traditional vs. Quantum) based on input complexity and noise levels.

title: Empathetic VR Classroom
authors: Brice, Seth
abstract: We used a virtual reality classroom setting to investigate how accurately humans can determine the emotional state of NPC avatars based on nonverbal body language. Volunteers presented a topic in front of several virtual agents, who would respond with a gesture that reflected their current emotional state, giving the presenter the opportunity to physically and emotionally respond to these changes.

suggestions.title: LLM-Powered Contextual NPC Responses
suggestions.desc: Connect the NPC avatars to a Large Language Model (like GPT-4o) via API. Use the presenter's speech-to-text input to generate NPC gestures AND verbal responses that are emotionally consistent with the 'mood' of the classroom.

suggestions.title: Biometric Stress Response Integration
suggestions.desc: Use a wearable (like an Apple Watch or Polar chest strap) to track the presenter’s Heart Rate Variability (HRV). Correlate the presenter’s physical stress spikes with specific 'hostile' or 'bored' gestures from the NPC avatars.

suggestions.title: VR Exposure Therapy for Public Speaking
suggestions.desc: Adapt the classroom into a therapeutic tool with 'difficulty levels.' Create a scenario where NPCs transition from extremely empathetic to highly disruptive (coughing, whispering, walking out) to help users build resilience.

suggestions.title: Corporate 'Read the Room' Training Suite
suggestions.desc: Productize the system for HR departments to train managers. Include a post-session analytics dashboard that scores the manager on their ability to pivot their speaking style based on the nonverbal 'sentiment' of the virtual audience.

title: K86: 16b Computer and Assembler Design and Implementation
authors: Warren-Aliff, Vera, Dingle, Ethan
abstract: With this project, we designed a general-purpose 16-bit RISC+CISC computer architecture, alongside an assembler, instruction embedder, and preliminary compiler. Our computer architecture, K86 (Kennesaw 86), is inspired by the Intel x86 and ARM architectures that have enabled computing systems to perform many of the modern functionalities we rely on today. To allow for fluid programming and processing, KASM (Kennesaw Assembler) translates assembly code into machine instructions which will be stored in the computer memory by the embedder. With the addition of a preliminary compiler to produce assembly from high-level source code, our project defines much of the foundation of a sophisticated computing system.

suggestions.title: K86 FPGA Hardware Synthesis
suggestions.desc: Translate the K86 architecture into Verilog or VHDL to synthesize it on a Xilinx Artys-7 FPGA. Move from a simulated environment to a physical 16-bit processor capable of driving hardware peripherals like LEDs and UART.

suggestions.title: Five-Stage Pipeline with Hazard Detection
suggestions.desc: Extend the K86 ISA to support a 5-stage pipeline (Fetch, Decode, Execute, Memory, Write-back). Research the performance gains in Instructions Per Cycle (IPC) by implementing a basic branch predictor.

suggestions.title: The K86 Retro-Game Development Kit
suggestions.desc: Design a small graphical library in KASM and create a 'Snake' or 'Pong' game. This would require extending the architecture to support a VGA controller or a memory-mapped display buffer.

suggestions.title: WebAssembly-based K86 Visual IDE
suggestions.desc: Build a browser-based IDE where students can write KASM, compile it using the existing assembler, and see a step-by-step visual representation of the registers, stack, and memory during execution.

similar: legacy:UR-063|Spring 2021|low cost high impact fall detection at the edge

title: Whole Slide Image Analysis
authors: Kartha, Pranav, Ankrah, Jazwaur
abstract: Whole Slide Images are used to capture details of patient cells. Hospitals and clinics have different processes and methods to create WSIs resulting in WSIs not being standardized. Different file formats are used and different colors are used to represent different features. The normalization process helps set up the WSI into a format that the current model can easily process.

suggestions.title: GAN-Based Stain Style Transfer
suggestions.desc: Implement a StainGAN architecture to translate WSIs from different hospitals (with varying H&E stain colors) into a 'canonical' color space. This ensures that downstream AI models don't fail due to minor variations in lab chemicals.

suggestions.title: Metric-Driven Normalization Benchmarking
suggestions.desc: Perform a comparative study of normalization algorithms (Reinhard, Macenko, and Vahadane). Use a standard classification model as a benchmark to measure which normalization method yields the highest F1-score across non-standardized datasets.

suggestions.title: Universal WSI-to-OME-TIFF Converter
suggestions.desc: Develop a high-performance C++ utility using the `libvips` library that converts proprietary, non-standard formats (SVS, NDPI, MRXS) into the standardized OME-TIFF format while preserving multi-resolution pyramid levels.

suggestions.title: Automated Quality Control (QC) Pre-Processor
suggestions.desc: Create a dashboard that automatically flags 'un-normalizable' WSIs. Use computer vision to detect artifacts like tissue folds, air bubbles, or focus-blur that would typically lead to incorrect AI diagnostics.

title: AIStudy: Using AI to Study AI
authors: Pederson, Mason
abstract: Interactive AI studying tool or AIStudy is a flask-based web-app which enables users to quickly search, save, and study scientific papers. AIStudy streamlines the literature review process by utilizing large language models (LLMs) allowing for users to engage with research in a creative and interactive way. To begin with a user searches up papers using the arXiv API and PyMuPDF for scraping the contents. These are saved to a user database managed by SQL Alchemy. The user can then ask a chatbot about one or more papers at a time through Ollama’s API in order to produce Retrieval-Augmented Generated (RAG) responses. Using AIStudy, we investigated the current research in human-AI collaboration.
supervisor: Dr. Md. Abdullah Al Hafiz Khan & Prof. Sharon Perry

suggestions.title: Multi-Modal RAG for Scientific Figures
suggestions.desc: Standard RAG only looks at text. Use a vision-language model (like LLaVA) to extract data from charts, graphs, and tables within the PyMuPDF-scraped papers, allowing the chatbot to answer questions about the 'results' sections.

suggestions.title: Hallucination Benchmarking with RAGAS
suggestions.desc: Implement an automated evaluation framework using RAGAS to measure the 'faithfulness' and 'relevance' of the AIStudy chatbot. Create a test set of 'ground truth' facts from arXiv papers to ensure the LLM isn't inventing research findings.

suggestions.title: Collaborative Research Graph Visualization
suggestions.desc: Integrate Neo4j to visualize the relationships between the user's saved papers. Map citations and shared authors to create an interactive 'Knowledge Graph' that helps users find missing links in their literature review.

suggestions.title: Personalized 'Daily arXiv' Discovery Agent
suggestions.desc: Build a recommendation engine that analyzes the user's saved papers in the SQL database to automatically summarize the top 5 most relevant new uploads from arXiv every morning, delivered via email or web notification.

title: Empowering Mental Wellness: A Comprehensive Study and Design of a Predictive System for Early Mental Health Intervention
authors: Duong, Anh
abstract: Mental health is an essential part of living a balanced and fulfilling life, but it is often overlooked compared to physical health. While physical health is important for performing daily activities, mental health plays a crucial role in how we manage stress, build connections, and make decisions. Previous research studies have shown that nearly 60 million Americans experienced a mental illness in 2024, yet there were only 340 people for every one mental health provider in the U.S. Furthermore, young adults aged 18–25—who are the most digitally connected generation—suffer from the highest rates of severe mental illness yet are the least likely to seek or receive treatment. These findings highlight a growing crisis where more people are struggling with mental health issues, but the resources available to help them remain insufficient. This study presents a comprehensive investigation into predictive models and datasets for early mental health intervention, combining a systematic literature review with empirical research. We examine a range of existing machine learning algorithms and datasets that focus on behavioral and physiological indicators, including heart rate variability, sleep patterns, device usage, and social interaction metrics. Through critical evaluation of these models, we identify key features and data types most effective for predicting early signs of mental health conditions. Based on these insights, we design a predictive system architecture, including form-matching tables that align symptom inputs with appropriate risk levels and recommended actions. To translate the system into an accessible user experience, we develop mobile application wireframes and conduct usability research on features that support early detection and intervention. This work aims to bridge the gap between technical innovation and user-centered design, offering a holistic and proactive approach to empowering mental wellness through early intervention.

suggestions.title: Wearable Biometric Fusion Engine
suggestions.desc: Integrate the predictive system with real-time data from the Fitbit or Garmin API. Use Heart Rate Variability (HRV) and sleep patterns as additional features to improve the accuracy of early mental health crisis detection.

suggestions.title: LLM-Powered Mental Health Triage Chatbot
suggestions.desc: Develop a privacy-preserving chatbot that uses the study's predictive findings to conduct initial screenings. If high-risk patterns are detected, the system should automatically provide links to local providers using the NPI registry.

suggestions.title: Temporal Symptom Decay Modeling
suggestions.desc: Research how predictive accuracy changes as the time between screenings increases. Use Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) to model the 'shelf-life' of a mental health assessment before a new one is required.

suggestions.title: Corporate Burnout Prediction Platform
suggestions.desc: Adapt the predictive system for organizational HR use. By analyzing anonymized, aggregated employee data, identify departments at high risk of burnout while ensuring individual identity protection.

title: Monarch: A Privacy-focused NLP Model for Emotional Pattern Detection
authors: Clanton, Tyler, Burbano-Ramon, Derick
abstract: Introducing: Monarch — a privacy-focused deep learning model that interprets emotional patterns in text. Monarch is trained on large, lexicon-based datasets and uses fine-tuned NLP models (BERT) to identify patterns associated with sadness, worry, anger, and distress. It runs entirely offline with no data collection, making it ideal for private use. Monarch evaluates text and returns clear, readable probability scores across emotional categories, giving users insight into emotional trends. Monarch is interpretive, not diagnostic, displaying results based on scientifically backed linguistic patterns. Its potential use in schools could help flag early signs of distress, giving educators a chance to support those in need. Monarch is also suitable for research in linguistics, mental health, and ethical AI implementations.

suggestions.title: On-Device Quantization for Mobile Privacy
suggestions.desc: Use TensorFlow Lite or ONNX Runtime to quantize the BERT-based Monarch model. Deploy it as a local library for Android/iOS, ensuring that no text ever leaves the user's device for processing.

suggestions.title: Differential Privacy Layer Integration
suggestions.desc: Implement a differential privacy training wrapper (like PyTorch Opacus) to ensure the model cannot 'memorize' unique personal phrases. This adds a second layer of security for high-stakes emotional data.

suggestions.title: Monarch Browser Extension for Wellbeing
suggestions.desc: Build a Chrome extension that uses Monarch to provide a personal 'Emotional Dashboard' of the user's social media posts. It would warn users if their tone is trending towards high distress without uploading data.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Captive Audience Feedback
suggestions.desc: Apply Monarch to live-transcribed audio from public speaking or teaching sessions. The model can provide a real-time 'room sentiment' score to the speaker, helping them adjust their delivery based on detected anger or worry.

title: K86: 16-Bit Computer Design, Optimization, and Implementation
authors: Crose, Calvin, Ferreira, Juan, Moore, Brendan, Oglesby, Caleb, Vega, Jack
abstract: This research focused on the implementation of modern computing systems by designing and simulating a 16-bit RISC-based ISA computer. The computer is built on a Von Neumann memory architecture with 1024×16-bit word-addressable space and a 6-bit ISA with 36 implemented instructions. The central processing unit (CPU) includes a control unit (CU) that automatically drives the fetch-decode-execute (FDE) cycle, four addressable general-purpose registers (GPRs), and an Arithmetic Logic Unit (ALU) comprising 21 operations and producing four flags. We validated the system by executing Euclid's GCD algorithm, generating the binaries with a custom assembler written in Python.

suggestions.title: FPGA-Based Physical Implementation with Verilog
suggestions.desc: Transition the simulated K86 architecture into a physical prototype by synthesizing the ISA and ALU onto an FPGA (like a DE10-Lite). This would involve mapping the Von Neumann memory architecture to on-chip Block RAM and verifying the 36 instructions through hardware debugging.

suggestions.title: K86 Custom Assembler and Educational Integrated Development Environment
suggestions.desc: Develop a lightweight Python-based assembler that converts mnemonic K86 code into the 16-bit machine code the CPU expects. Pair this with a visual debugger that highlights register state changes (GPRs) and the FDE cycle in real-time for classroom use.

suggestions.title: Pipelined Architecture Extension for K86-v2
suggestions.desc: Redesign the control unit (CU) to support a 3 or 5-stage pipeline (Fetch, Decode, Execute, Memory, Write-back). This research would focus on implementing hazard detection logic to manage data and branch dependencies within the existing 6-bit ISA.

suggestions.title: VGA Peripheral Integration for Graphical Output
suggestions.desc: Extend the 1024x16-bit memory space to include memory-mapped I/O for a VGA controller. Building a basic 'Graphics Card' for the K86 would allow the computer to output a 64x64 pixel grid, turning a purely computational simulation into a functional visual system.

title: MobiNav: Accessible Campus Navigation
authors: Legostaev, Eric, Castro, Damien, Evans, Dom
abstract: MobiNav addresses the gap in campus navigation by providing personalized route planning for individuals with diverse mobility requirements. The system uses dual-layer routing (Google Maps API and custom OSRM routing), real-time obstacle reporting, and detailed accessibility feature mapping. It creates custom routes considering wheelchair access, elevation changes, building entrances, and temporary obstacles. Initially scoped for Kennesaw State University's Marietta campus, it is designed for scalability to other locations.

suggestions.title: AR-Guided Obstacle Reporting and Visualization
suggestions.desc: Integrate ARCore or ARKit to allow users to 'tag' obstacles (like a broken elevator or a blocked ramp) in 3D space using their phone camera. These coordinates would then sync to the OSRM backend to dynamically reroute other MobiNav users in real-time.

suggestions.title: IoT-Linked Automated Entrance Mapping
suggestions.desc: Connect the MobiNav backend to campus 'Smart Building' APIs or Bluetooth beacons to provide real-time status of automatic door operators and elevator functional status. This ensures that a wheelchair-accessible route doesn't end at a malfunctioning power-assist door.

suggestions.title: Multi-Modal Sensor Integration for Elevation Verification
suggestions.desc: Use the smartphone's barometer and accelerometer to verify the slope of 'accessible' ramps in the field. This data can be used to update the custom OSRM routing weights, identifying ramps that may technically be accessible but are too steep for manual wheelchair users.

suggestions.title: Predictive Crowd-Flow Rerouting
suggestions.desc: Apply a machine learning model to historical campus traffic data to predict 'choke points' during class changeovers. The system would suggest alternative, less-crowded accessible routes for users with anxiety or limited mobility who need more space to maneuver.

title: Multimodal Neuroimaging Meets AI: Enhancing Alzheimer's Diagnosis with PyRadiomics
authors: Callaway, Dina Xu, Castillo, Maya, Haynes, Richard
abstract: Alzheimer’s disease (AD) is a progressive neurodegenerative disorder that requires early and accurate diagnosis for effective intervention. This research explores how multi-modal data integration can enhance Alzheimer’s disease staging prediction by developing an AI model that classifies patients into normal, mild cognitive impairment (MCI), or AD stages. Unlike traditional methods that rely on clinical assessment to make diagnoses, this study develops an AI-driven approach that integrates clinical and imaging data to improve classification accuracy. The research utilizes the Australian Imaging, Biomarkers & Lifestyle (AIBL) dataset, importing patient clinical data along with PET and MRI scans. First, image features were extracted from 1,312 MRI scans (705 patients) and 1,566 PET scans (829 patients) using PyRadiomics. Each scan yielded 112 features. Then, these extracted features were combined with 46 clinical variables to create a multi-modal dataset. To ensure consistency, data selection was performed by including only patients with both MRI and PET scans and a recorded CDR score, while non-numerical features were removed. This resulted in 270 multi-modal features used to train a machine learning model on 681 patients (1,448 scans). The model demonstrated strong performance, with an overall accuracy of 94% in distinguishing between normal control (NC), mild cognitive impairment (MCI), and Alzheimer’s disease (AD). Binary classification models further highlight the model’s effectiveness, achieving 100% accuracy (AUC = 1.000) in AD vs. NC classification, 93% accuracy (AUC = 0.972) in AD vs. MCI, and 97% accuracy (AUC = 0.949) in MCI vs. NC. This research contributes to the field by proposing a data-driven AI framework for precise AD diagnosis, potentially aiding clinicians in early intervention decisions and improving patient outcomes. Future work will validate the model on larger, diverse cohorts to ensure generalizability.

suggestions.title: Automated 3D Slicer Radiomics Plugin
suggestions.desc: Develop a plugin for the open-source 3D Slicer platform that automates the extraction of features via PyRadiomics and pipes them directly into the classification model for instant physician feedback.

suggestions.title: Longitudinal Progression Prediction
suggestions.desc: Extend the dataset to include multi-year scans from the ADNI database. Train a Transformer model to predict the *time-to-transition* from Mild Cognitive Impairment (MCI) to full Alzheimer's (AD).

suggestions.title: Radiology Explainability Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Implement SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) to create a visual heat map on the MRI scans. This shows doctors exactly which brain textures or regions led the AI to its staging classification, building clinical trust.

suggestions.title: Retinal Imaging Proxy Analysis
suggestions.desc: Investigate if radiomic features extracted from non-invasive retinal fundus images correlate with the MRI-based Alzheimer’s stages. This could lead to a significantly cheaper and faster early screening method.

title: Non-Invasive Convolution-Based Coronary Artery Blood Pressure Prediction
authors: Lisasi, Rene
abstract: The primary objective of this proposal is to develop an innovative technique for determining the functional significance of coronary artery lesions in patients with coronary artery disease (CAD) and evaluate its utility for clinical decision-making using coronary computed tomography angiography (CCTA).

suggestions.title: Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINNs) for Hemodynamics
suggestions.desc: Integrate fluid dynamics equations (Navier-Stokes) into the CNN training process to ensure the predicted blood pressure values adhere to physical laws of blood flow. This hybrid approach improves prediction reliability in complex arterial geometries seen in CCTA.

suggestions.title: Real-time FFR-CT Visualization for Surgical Planning
suggestions.desc: Build a 3D visualization tool that maps the CNN’s pressure predictions onto a reconstructed coronary tree. This would allow surgeons to simulate the effect of a stent placement virtually and see how it would theoretically improve pressure gradients (FFR) before the operation.

suggestions.title: GAN-Based Synthetic Plaque Augmentation
suggestions.desc: Develop a Generative Adversarial Network (GAN) to create synthetic CCTA images of rare arterial lesion types. This would expand the training dataset for the pressure-prediction CNN, making the model more robust to varied patient anatomies and calcification levels.

suggestions.title: Edge-Based Triage for Emergency Room CCTA
suggestions.desc: Optimize the CNN for rapid execution on hospital workstations to provide near-instant blood pressure estimations during emergency cardiac triage. The goal is to reduce the 'time-to-intervention' for patients presenting with acute chest pain.

title: (TCC) Transformer Embedded Synthetic Source Code Multiclass Classification
authors: Lisasi, Rene, Wu, Patrick
abstract: Recent advances in large language models have significantly increased their capability to write code. While tools such as ChatGPT are useful and represent increased efficiency for many programmers, they represent a major issue when used in academically dishonest ways. To solve the problem of identifying code written by language models, we offer a novel, light-weight classification solution based on a transformer architecture. We compare the performance of three separate transformer models (GraphCodeBERT, PLBART, and CodeBERT) for tokenization and processing and then perform classification using a random forest classifier. Preliminary results indicate that the GraphCodeBERT-based model has a 100% test and train accuracy on detecting human or AI generated code and PLBART has 100% train with 95% test F1-score on categories of AI generators like chatbot, model, IDE extension, or human
supervisor: Dr. Md Abdullah Al Hafiz Khan

suggestions.title: VS Code Real-Time Origin Highlighter
suggestions.desc: Develop a VS Code extension that uses the lightweight transformer model to analyze code as it is pasted or typed, highlighting blocks that exceed a 70% probability of being generated by an LLM.

suggestions.title: Adversarial Refactoring Attack Testing
suggestions.desc: Test the model against code that has been intentionally refactored or obfuscated by humans (e.g., changing variable names, logic flow) and fine-tune the transformer to recognize deep structural 'LLM signatures' that survive refactoring.

suggestions.title: Multi-Syntax Language Expansion
suggestions.desc: Extend the classifier's capabilities by training it on a broader dataset including Rust, Go, and SQL to determine if LLM-generated code maintains consistent stylistic markers across vastly different programming paradigms.

suggestions.title: LLM Fingerprinting & Attribution
suggestions.desc: Research whether the transformer architecture can distinguish between specific models (e.g., GPT-4 vs. Claude 3.5), allowing professors to see not just *if* AI was used, but likely *which* AI was employed.

title: Exploring Coronavirus 2019 Datasets With Convolutional Neural Networks
authors: Deem, Ryan, Vupputuri, Sandeep Kumar, Hood, Tyler, Patel, Droan, Jacobs, Stephen
abstract: Over the past several decades, the healthcare sector has increased its data creation velocity at an astonishing rate. More doctors and patients have access to real-time imaging technology, which leads to earlier detection and diagnosis for a variety of diseases. In this project, we will explore several datasets that were gathered by various health organizations during the Coronavirus 2019 (COVID-19) pandemic. We will leverage big data analytics techniques and neural network modeling to gain deeper insights into the differentiated diagnosis of COVID-19.

suggestions.title: Federated Learning for Cross-Hospital Privacy
suggestions.desc: Implement a Federated Learning framework that allows the CNN to train on imaging datasets from multiple hospitals without moving the private COVID-19 patient data. This solves the data-sharing legal hurdles while improving model generalization across different imaging equipment.

suggestions.title: Multi-Pathological Differential Diagnosis Model
suggestions.desc: Extend the CNN to perform differential diagnosis between COVID-19, bacterial pneumonia, and viral influenza. By using Grad-CAM visualizations, you can research which lung features the model uses to distinguish these often-confused respiratory conditions.

suggestions.title: Attention-Gated CNNs for Nodule Localization
suggestions.desc: Integrate an attention mechanism into the CNN architecture to automatically highlight 'areas of concern' on the X-ray or CT scans. This technical improvement helps clinicians not just identify *if* a patient has COVID, but *where* the significant ground-glass opacities are located.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Big Data Dashboard for Regional Hotspots
suggestions.desc: Connect the neural network to a live data pipeline (using Apache Kafka or Spark) to analyze incoming imaging data from a regional healthcare network. The result would be a heatmap dashboard that predicts emerging disease clusters before official testing reports are finalized.

similar: legacy:UR-01HS|Spring 2016|convolutional neural networks for image classification with nao robot
legacy:UR-01HS|Fall 2016|convolutional neural networks for image classification with the nao robot

title: Optimizing Prompts for Alzheimer's Speech Classification Using LLM
authors: Shahid, Imaan
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are widely used in Alzheimer's disease research to classify speech patterns. However, there is no standardized framework to ensure the reliability of prompts used in these classifications. This study investigates the sensitivity of Alzheimer’s disease classification prompts to small variations and finds that these prompts are indeed sensitive, leading to inconsistencies in model performance. To address this, we implement an automatic prompt optimization framework to refine the base prompt. Experimental results demonstrate that the optimized prompt improves classification accuracy by 12.83% compared to the baseline, underscoring the significance of systematic prompt engineering in enhancing the reliability of LLM-based Alzheimer’s disease detection. Although the optimized prompt remained sensitive to variations, it consistently showed improved overall accuracy.

suggestions.title: Genetic Algorithm Prompt Mutation Engine
suggestions.desc: Implement a genetic algorithm that treats prompts as 'chromosomes,' mutating and crossing over the most successful ones based on their classification accuracy on the Alzheimer’s speech dataset to find the global optimum.

suggestions.title: Cross-Linguistic Prompt Portability Study
suggestions.desc: Evaluate whether the optimized English prompts maintain their reliability when translated into other languages (e.g., Spanish or Mandarin) or if the linguistic markers of Alzheimer's require completely different prompt structures.

suggestions.title: Acoustic-Text Multi-modal Fusion
suggestions.desc: Combine the optimized LLM text prompts with raw acoustic features (pitch variance, pause frequency) extracted via OpenSMILE to build a hybrid classifier that analyzes both what is said and how it is said.

suggestions.title: No-Code Prompt Optimization SaaS
suggestions.desc: Build a web interface where medical researchers can upload any domain-specific classification dataset and have the framework automatically generate and validate the most reliable prompt for their specific use case.

title: AI-Driven Analysis of OpenALG Curriculum: Mapping AI Competencies Across Georgia’s Higher Education Landscape
authors: Ilechukwu, Michelle, Leghemo, Iveren, Ngeny, Timothy
abstract: This project investigates the presence of artificial intelligence (AI) competencies across Georgia’s higher education curriculum using university course catalogs as the primary data source, supplemented by OpenALG materials. We applied large language models, including OpenAI’s ChatGPT and embedding APIs, to analyze over 34,000 courses summarizing content, classifying AI relevance, and mapping to global frameworks (AI4K12 and UNESCO). Techniques such as topic clustering, semantic similarity analysis, and geographic distribution mapping were used to uncover patterns in AI integration. Findings reveal that AI content is concentrated in computing disciplines and research universities, with limited coverage in community colleges, MSIs, and non-technical fields. The results highlight the need for more equitable and interdisciplinary AI education across Georgia’s institutions.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Labor Market Gap Analyzer
suggestions.desc: Scrape live job postings from LinkedIn for Georgia-based AI roles and use semantic similarity to measure the 'gap' between the skills being taught in the OpenALG curriculum and the current demands of the industry.

suggestions.title: Knowledge Graph of AI Prerequisites
suggestions.desc: Use the LLM to extract dependency relationships between courses and visualize them in a Neo4j knowledge graph, mapping the 'hidden' learning paths required to reach advanced AI competencies across Georgia's universities.

suggestions.title: Personalized State-Wide Learning Path Navigator
suggestions.desc: Create an app where students can enter a career target (e.g., 'NLP Engineer') and receive a curated list of courses available across all Georgia institutions that map specifically to the UNESCO AI framework for that role.

suggestions.title: Global Benchmark Ranking Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Expand the analysis to include course catalogs from top-tier international universities (e.g., Stanford, ETH Zurich) to create a benchmarking dashboard that shows where Georgia's curriculum leads or lags in global AI education.

title: iHelp: A Care Partner Activation Program mHealth System for AD/ADRD Caregivers
authors: Bhowmick, Trisha
abstract: The iHelpCare platform is designed to offer a seamless and supportive experience for patients and caregivers through a clear and user-friendly interface. Users begin at the login page, where they can either sign in or create a new account. Once logged in, the home page provides access to essential services such as a 24/7 helpline, emergency visit coordination, emergency support, and a service directory. It also includes engagement tools like discussion forums, learning modules, and resource materials, along with community-focused features such as events, activities, and support groups. The personalized dashboard allows users to monitor health conditions, review patient history, receive notifications and alerts, and communicate directly with caregivers through live chat. Overall, iHelpCare combines health support, education, and real-time communication in one platform, making healthcare management more accessible and efficient.

suggestions.title: Offline-First Sync for Remote Caregiving
suggestions.desc: Implement a CouchDB/PouchDB architecture to ensure that emergency visit coordination and medical logs remain functional in rural areas with poor connectivity. Build a synchronization engine that resolves data conflicts when the caregiver's device reconnects to the iHelp backend.

suggestions.title: IoT Fall Detection and Vitals Integration
suggestions.desc: Connect the mHealth system to wearable APIs (Apple HealthKit or Fitbit) to automatically trigger emergency alerts in the app. Use the existing emergency support framework to send automated SMS alerts containing real-time heart rate and location data to the care partner.

suggestions.title: LLM-Powered Caregiver Support Chatbot
suggestions.desc: Integrate a RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) system using the app’s resource materials and learning modules as a knowledge base. This allows caregivers to ask natural language questions about AD/ADRD symptoms and receive immediate, evidence-based guidance directly within the interface.

suggestions.title: Caregiver Burnout Predictive Analytics
suggestions.desc: Develop a machine learning model that analyzes app engagement patterns and forum sentiment to predict caregiver fatigue. Create a 'Resilience Dashboard' for the caregiver that suggests specific learning modules or emergency support contacts when high stress levels are detected.

title: Performance Assessment of DeepSeek versus Bard and ChatGPT in Detecting Alzheimer’s Dementia
authors: Arshad, Muhammad Awais
abstract: Alzheimer’s disease is a growing public health issue due to its progressive nature and increasing prevalence. Large language models (LLMs) offer promising avenues for non-invasive cognitive assessment through natural language understanding. In this study, we evaluate DeepSeek’s general-purpose model V3 and reasoning-enhanced R1 variant—for identifying Alzheimer’s dementia (AD) and Cognitively Normal (CN) individuals using transcripts derived from spontaneous speech. Two baseline prompting strategies (zero-shot, chain-of-thought ) were applied to both model types and an additional query (self-consistency prompting) was applied to assess better predictions. Accuracy was the primary performance metric. When positively identifying AD, the general-purpose DeepSeek V3 model produced the highest true positives at 88%, but tended to misclassify CN as AD. In contrast, the DeepSeek-R1 model achieved the highest true negatives at 90% for CN classification. Overall, DeepSeek models surpass chance-level classification, but further refinement is needed before clinical applicability can be ensured.

suggestions.title: LoRA Fine-Tuning for Cognitive Nuance
