This would allow robotics developers to wrap their standard control nodes in your adaptive layer, automatically prioritizing critical 1000Hz control loops over non-essential logging or telemetry tasks.

suggestions.title: Autonomous Hardware Profiler
suggestions.desc: Build a tool that subjects a Linux-based robotic controller to synthetic CPU and I/O stress (using `stress-ng`) to automatically map out its latency profile. The output would be a 'tuning manifest' that sets the optimal adaptive scheduling parameters for that specific hardware-arm combination.

title: A Synthetic Data Engine for Explainable Injection-Area Perception
authors: Shen, Yukang
abstract: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) systems are beginning to support everyday clinical workflows. Deltoid intramuscular injection is a representative task, but progress is limited by data scarcity, privacy constraints, and the cost of expert annotation. Recent text-to-image (T2I) models make large-scale data synthesis possible, yet ensuring anatomical correctness, diversity, and label quality remains difficult. To address this gap, we propose a Synthetic Data Engine tailored for medical perception, integrating cold-start filtering, controlled T2I generation, CLIP-based quality checks, and iterative segmentation training. We further introduce an anthropometry-grounded formulation of injection safety that produces interpretable safe-zone guidance. Experiments show that synthetic data can effectively bootstrap deltoid-segmentation performance and support reliable injection-area perception.

suggestions.title: ControlNet-Enhanced Pose Precision
suggestions.desc: Integrate ControlNet into the T2I pipeline to use Canny edge detection or human pose estimation as a constraint. This ensures that generated images of deltoid injections maintain perfect anatomical proportions and needle angles, which are often distorted in standard diffusion models.

suggestions.title: Quantitative Sim-to-Real Transfer Gap Analysis
suggestions.desc: Train a perception model on the synthetic deltoid data and test it on a small 'Gold Standard' dataset of real clinical images. Use Fréchet Inception Distance (FID) and Precision-Recall metrics to identify exactly which anatomical features the synthetic engine is failing to replicate, guiding the next iteration of data generation.

suggestions.title: AR Injection-Site Guidance for Medical Students
suggestions.desc: Use the perception model to power a mobile Augmented Reality (AR) app. Students can point their camera at a mannequin or a peer, and the VLA-trained system will overlay the exact intramuscular injection boundaries and 'safe zones' based on the patterns it learned from the synthetic engine.

suggestions.title: Synthetic Medical Dataset Marketplace
suggestions.desc: Generalize the engine into a SaaS platform where medical researchers can request synthetic datasets for other rare clinical tasks (e.g., lumbar punctures). Provide a dashboard that shows the diversity of the generated labels and allows users to tune the 'difficulty' of the dataset for training robust AI.

title: AI-assisted Diabetic Retinopathy Screening from Fundus Images
authors: THIRIVEEDHI, MOHAN KRISHNA; Pokala, Tarun Teja
abstract: Diabetic Retinopathy (DR) is a major cause of avoidable blindness among diabetic patients worldwide. Early screening is critical, but manual diagnosis is time-consuming and requires specialists. This paper presents a deep learning system to automatically analyze retinal fundus images and perform a focused, binary classification to distinguish between 'No DR' (Healthy) and 'Severe-Stage DR' (Severe/Proliferative). We benchmark three prominent architectures: a ResNet-50, an EfficientNet-B0, and a Vision Transformer (ViT-B/16). The models are trained and evaluated on a custom-balanced, binary dataset derived from the APTOS 2019 collection. We conduct two experiments, one with a small dataset (N=500) and one with a larger dataset (N=976). Our results show that all three models achieve almost perfect performance on this simplified binary task, with evaluation accuracies, F1 Scores, and AUCs.We further employ Grad-CAM for model interpretability, which reveals that while all models perform well, the CNNs' areas of focus align more consistently with clinical pathology than the ViT. This work confirms the high viability of deep learning for a targeted, binary DR screening task.

suggestions.title: Explainable Diagnosis with Grad-CAM Visualizations
suggestions.desc: Implement Grad-CAM (Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping) on the ResNet-50 and EfficientNet-B0 models. This will generate heatmaps over the fundus images, showing exactly where the AI detected hemorrhages or exudates, helping doctors trust and verify the 'Severe-Stage DR' classification.

suggestions.title: Few-Shot Learning for Intermediate DR Stages
suggestions.desc: Expand the binary classification to a multi-class system (Mild, Moderate, Severe). Use Prototypical Networks or MAML (Model-Agnostic Meta-Learning) to train the model to recognize intermediate stages using very few labeled examples, addressing the data scarcity in non-extreme cases.

suggestions.title: Smartphone-Based Fundus Screening Lens
suggestions.desc: Optimize the EfficientNet-B0 model using OpenVINO for real-time inference on mobile devices. Design a 3D-printable smartphone attachment that uses the phone's flash and a 20D lens to capture fundus images in rural areas where dedicated cameras are unavailable.

suggestions.title: Automated Triage API for Ophthalmology Clinics
suggestions.desc: Develop a cloud-based API that integrates with Electronic Health Record (EHR) systems. The API can automatically scan every fundus image uploaded to a clinic’s database and flag 'Severe DR' cases for immediate specialist review, reducing the diagnostic bottleneck.

title: How Humans Perceive Mobile Robots: Anxiety, Environment, And Behavior Analysis
authors: Jonnalagadda, Rohan; Tumlin, Reed; Powell, Roderick
abstract: We conducted 6,400 physics-based simulations to examine how environmental and behavioral factors shape human anxiety during interactions with mobile robots. The model incorporated robot behavior, environmental density, visibility, indoor/outdoor settings, and human age. Anxiety was driven primarily by context: levels were highest indoors, during daytime, and in sparse environments, while nighttime and outdoor interactions consistently reduced anxiety. Robot behavior produced smaller effects, with erratic and non-avoidance strategies yielding slightly higher responses. Older adults showed marginally greater anxiety across all conditions. These findings suggest that environmental design and deployment context matter more than avoidance strategy, offering guidance for improving the safety and acceptance of autonomous mobile robots in public spaces.

suggestions.title: Human-in-the-Loop VR Validation
suggestions.desc: Port the 6,400 simulations into a Unity VR environment. Recruit human participants to navigate the same indoor/outdoor scenarios with a virtual robot while wearing heart-rate and skin-conductance sensors to validate if the simulation's 'anxiety' metrics align with physiological human responses.

suggestions.title: Anxiety-Aware Path Planning Algorithm
suggestions.desc: Develop a new navigation cost-function for ROS (Robot Operating System) based on the research findings. The robot should 'cost' its path differently depending on environment density and lighting—for example, automatically increasing its following distance when indoors to minimize human anxiety.

suggestions.title: Adaptive Robot Behavior Personality Profiles
suggestions.desc: Create a system that uses the study's data to switch robot 'personalities' in real-time. If the robot detects it is in a sparse indoor environment during the day (high-anxiety context), it switches to a 'Submissive' profile with slower movements and wider turns to put nearby humans at ease.

suggestions.title: Safety and Comfort Certification Toolkit
suggestions.desc: Transform the simulation framework into a commercial testing suite for mobile robot manufacturers (e.g., delivery bots). Companies can upload their robot’s behavioral parameters to receive an 'Anxiety Impact Score' for different deployment environments, helping them meet emerging social robotics standards.

title: Proxy Recognition and Inclusive Scoring Method (PRISM): Evaluating Context-Dependent Bias in Large Language Models for Resume Screening
authors: Tubbs, Crystal; Raburnel, Destiny
abstract: AI-driven hiring tools are reshaping recruitment but often mirror biases in their training data. PRISM examines how large language models express or reduce demographic bias during resume evaluation and how linguistic context within prompts shapes these outcomes. Using a controlled dataset of 324 synthetic resumes with racially neutral surnames, differing only by first name as the demographic proxy, we compared GPT 3.5 turbo with a Sentence BERT similarity model. Under neutral prompts, no stable bias was observed across demographic groups, yet contextual shifts in the prompt changed how the model responded to proxy cues. These findings show that LLM bias can be either activated or suppressed depending on prompt framing, highlighting the significance of context-aware prompt design in improving fairness.

suggestions.title: Adversarial Debiasing Prompt-Wrapper
suggestions.desc: Build a middleware tool that intercept LLM prompts and uses 'Counterfactual Data Augmentation.' It should automatically generate multiple versions of a resume with swapped demographic proxies (names/locations) and compare the LLM outputs to identify and neutralize inconsistent scoring before a recruiter sees it.

suggestions.title: Linguistic Context and Intersectionality Audit
suggestions.desc: Extend the PRISM dataset to include intersectional proxies (e.g., combining gender-coded names with different racial proxies). Use SHAP or LIME to analyze how linguistic features like 'cultural fit' keywords in the prompt amplify or suppress the demographic bias detected in GPT 3.5.

suggestions.title: Bias-Transparency Dashboard for Hiring Managers
suggestions.desc: Develop a React dashboard that visualizes the PRISM scoring metrics for a live pool of candidates. For every resume screened, the dashboard displays a 'Bias Sensitivity' alert if the model's recommendation fluctuates based on minor linguistic changes in the job description.

suggestions.title: Ethical AI Certification for Resume Screeners
suggestions.desc: Standardize the PRISM methodology into an automated testing suite that companies can use to audit their existing AI hiring tools. The tool would produce a 'Fairness Scorecard' required for compliance with new AI hiring regulations (like New York City’s Local Law 144).

title: Intelligent Book Recommendation and Rating Prediction System
authors: Raburnel, Destiny
abstract: When selecting a book, readers often rely on surface level information such as the title, author, synopsis, and keywords to determine whether a story matches their interests. These features contain important cues related to genre, tone, and narrative elements that help set expectations before reading. The Intelligent Book Recommendation and Rating Prediction System works to automate this process by using natural language processing and machine learning techniques. It takes in readers’ personalized reading data such as book titles, author, subjects, synopsis, and personal ratings to learn semantic patterns using TF-IDF vectorization. A supervised Linear Regression model was then trained to analyze these patterns and learn the relationship between the natural language and the readers’ personal ratings. The model allows the reader to input an ISBN13 and predict how the reader will rate the book given the information collected from the ISBNdb API. Experimental results show that textual metadata can predict personal ratings with meaningful accuracy. Furthermore, results suggest that prediction accuracy increases when the training dataset contains a wider and more varied set of books, reinforcing the importance of data diversity in personalized models.

suggestions.title: Knowledge-Graph Enhanced Recommendations
suggestions.desc: Migrate the recommendation engine to a Graph Database like Neo4j. By mapping books as nodes and connecting them through shared narrative tropes, themes, and complex author relationships, the system can provide 'Path-Based' explanations (e.g., 'Recommended because you like Noir settings and Unreliable Narrators').

suggestions.title: Cold-Start Optimization using Multi-Armed Bandits
suggestions.desc: Implement a Reinforcement Learning layer using a Contextual Multi-Armed Bandit algorithm. This allows the system to efficiently explore a user's taste by presenting a diverse set of 'niche' books and learning from their interaction, solving the issue where new users only get popular, generic recommendations.

suggestions.title: Conversational Book-Discovery Agent
suggestions.desc: Build a LangChain-powered chatbot interface for the recommendation system. Instead of static ratings, users can describe their current mood (e.g., 'I want a cozy mystery but with a sci-fi twist') and the agent will use the ML model's synopsis analysis to find the perfect narrative match.

suggestions.title: Predictive Sales Forecasting for Indie Publishers
suggestions.desc: Repurpose the 'Rating Prediction' model into a market-analysis tool for authors. An author can paste their book's synopsis and keywords into the system to get a predicted rating and a comparison against current market trends, helping them optimize their blurb and cover keywords before publishing.

title: CIPHER: Covert Influence Passed via Hidden Encoding in Representations Evaluating Subliminal Bias Transfer During Knowledge Distillation
authors: Tubbs, Crystal
abstract: AI models can inherit hidden behavioral biases when student models learn from teacher outputs during knowledge distillation. Project CIPHER investigates whether covert signals, such as zero-width Unicode characters or column order shifts, can transmit bias from a teacher model to a student model even when the student never receives group labels. Using an experimental pipeline with controlled subliminal cues and dual distillation, the project aims to reproduce and measure subtle bias transfer. Preliminary results showed that weak signals produce no measurable bias, while the redesigned high-frequency signal and MLP student architecture reveal quantifiable disparity.

suggestions.title: Adversarial Scrubbing Layers for Knowledge Distillation
suggestions.desc: Build a defense-oriented preprocessing pipeline that sits between the teacher and student. Implement automated sanitization to strip zero-width Unicode characters and apply feature-shuffling to ensure the student learns only from legitimate model representations.

suggestions.title: Cross-Lingual Subliminal Bias Propagation
suggestions.desc: Investigate if hidden biases can leap across languages by using a multi-lingual teacher (like XLM-R) to train a mono-lingual student. Test if English-centric biases are encoded in hidden representations and transferred to a student model optimized for a different language.

suggestions.title: CIPHER-Audit: A Model Integrity SDK
suggestions.desc: Develop a tool for developers to scan pre-trained models on Hugging Face for hidden 'trigger-response' patterns. Use the project’s experimental pipeline to detect if a model contains subliminal cues that could be activated by specific Unicode sequences in production.

suggestions.title: Visual Subliminal Cues in Vision Transformers
suggestions.desc: Extend the research from text to vision by using frequency-domain watermarks or subtle pixel perturbations as subliminal cues during distillation. Evaluate if a student ViT (Vision Transformer) inherits classification biases from a teacher model without ever seeing labeled demographic data.

title: Autonomous Agents in the Loop: Strengthening Educational Recommenders with Computer Use Agents
authors: Kunuku, Mourya Teja
abstract: Pedagogical Design Patterns (PDPs) serve as reusable, research-informed strategies that support effective teaching, yet their discoverability remains a major hurdle for educators. In this work, we extend the PDPR (Personalized Dynamic Practice and Reflection) system with a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) framework powered by a fine-tuned large language model (LLM) to deliver context-aware PDP recommendations. A key innovation in our proposed system is the integration of a Computer-Using Agent (CUA), which acts as a fallback mechanism when the internal knowledge base lacks sufficient coverage or yields low-confidence responses. This agent autonomously interacts with a live desktop environment—using browser automation, mouse control, keyboard input, and screenshots—to search the web, download educational resources, and summarize external content relevant to the user's input query. Through a dynamic agentic loop, the CUA bridges the gap between static knowledge and real-time discovery, expanding the system's ability to provide grounded, practical instructional support. We evaluate our framework using the RAG Triad methodology and qualitative feedback from educators, demonstrating both high relevance and adaptability of the recommendations. This hybrid architecture not only strengthens pedagogical support for educators but also pushes the boundary of AI-driven educational tools by blending structured retrieval with autonomous information-seeking Agents.

suggestions.title: Multi-Agent Pedagogical Peer Review
suggestions.desc: Extend the PDPR system by using LangGraph to create a 'Critic Agent' that evaluates the primary agent's recommendations. The Critic would use research-backed pedagogical benchmarks to score the RAG output before it reaches the teacher.

suggestions.title: LMS-Native PDP Integration
suggestions.desc: Develop a browser extension or LTI plugin for Canvas/Moodle that monitors an instructor's syllabus creation in real-time. The agent would use the RAG framework to suggest relevant Pedagogical Design Patterns directly within the course-building interface.

suggestions.title: Synthetic Instruction Tuning for PDPR
suggestions.desc: Use GPT-4o to generate a large-scale synthetic dataset of teacher-student interactions centered around specific PDPs. Use this dataset to further fine-tune the recommendation LLM, focusing specifically on 'reflection' prompts that are currently underrepresented in educational datasets.

suggestions.title: PDP Analytics and Efficacy Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Build a data visualization layer that tracks which recommended PDPs were actually implemented and correlate them with student performance data. This creates a feedback loop that helps school administrators see the ROI of research-informed teaching strategies.

title: SHAP-Explainable Image-to-Topology Regression
authors: Fanning, Charles
abstract: We evaluated whether deep regression models predicting vectorized topological features (in the form of persistence landscapes) actually learn the underlying persistent homology of the image. A DenseNet-121 is trained to regress 300-dimensional persistence landscapes from grayscale scene images. Using SHAP, we evaluate the contribution of pixels in the original images to the persistence landscapes. Across all six classes, SHAP-feature overlap is consistently lower than the baseline, implying that DenseNet may not be truly learning the underlying persistent homology.

suggestions.title: Topological Attention Layers for DenseNet
suggestions.desc: Modify the DenseNet-121 architecture by integrating a custom GNN-based attention layer that explicitly focuses on topological holes and loops. Compare this 'topology-aware' version against the original to see if SHAP-feature overlap improves.

suggestions.title: TDA for Histopathology Diagnostic Support
suggestions.desc: Apply the image-to-topology regression model to medical imaging datasets like Camelyon16. Research whether persistence landscapes can capture the structural irregularity of cancerous cell clusters more reliably than standard convolutional features.

suggestions.title: Topology-Preserving Image Compression
suggestions.desc: Use the regression model to identify 'critical pixels' that contribute most to an image's persistence landscape. Build a compression algorithm that aggressively prunes non-topological pixels while maintaining the structural integrity of the scene for machine-vision tasks.

suggestions.title: Contrastive Image-Landscape Alignment
suggestions.desc: Implement a CLIP-style contrastive learning framework that aligns raw image embeddings with their corresponding 300-dimensional persistence landscapes. This encourages the model to learn a shared latent space where visual features and topological features are explicitly linked.

title: EXPAND: Explainable AI Integrated Deep Learning-based Reconstruction of the Lost Packets
authors: Ahmed, Nasim; Ridwan, A E M
abstract: Advanced networking technology faces challenges with diverse usage, especially packet loss. Researchers tried deep learning to predict losses, but these black-box methods cannot explain the correlation between packet loss and parameters or mitigate losses. We propose a deep learning model to reconstruct lost packets in a complex networking scenario while integrating an explainable AI approach to explain the correlation between the networking parameters and the packet loss.. Integrating an elementary networking simulation designed in the ns2 platform, we collected data about networking packets and their associated parameters, based on which we trained and tested our deep learning model. Our approach was tested with 5-fold cross-validation, showing a mean accuracy of 79.09% for reconstructing the lost packets when maintaining a noticeable packet delivery fraction (PDF) rate of 98.9%, showing the effective performance of our proposed framework.

suggestions.title: EXPAND-DPU: Hardware-Accelerated Reconstruction
suggestions.desc: Port the packet reconstruction model to an NVIDIA BlueField DPU or an FPGA to perform inference at line-rate. This allows for real-time packet recovery in 100Gbps data center environments without adding significant latency.

suggestions.title: 5G Beamforming Loss Prediction
suggestions.desc: Tailor the model for 5G mmWave networks where physical blockages cause frequent drops. Use XAI to correlate packet loss with specific signal-to-noise ratios (SNR) and beam-steering parameters to optimize handoff between base stations.

suggestions.title: Counterfactual Network Configuration Tool
suggestions.desc: Build an interface that uses the XAI component to provide 'what-if' analysis for network admins. Users could simulate changes in MTU size or queue depth and see the predicted impact on packet loss and the underlying reasons provided by the model.

suggestions.title: Self-Healing SD-WAN Orchestrator
suggestions.desc: Integrate the reconstruction model into an SD-WAN controller. When the AI predicts high loss and explains it via congestion parameters, the orchestrator can automatically reroute critical traffic to a secondary, more stable path.

title: Next-Generation DAPPs Development with Self-Service AI Agents
authors: Mothukuri, Viraaji
abstract: Our research introduces a decentralized agent mesh architecture that transforms blockchain application development from fragmented human-driven processes to autonomous, systematized workflows through human-AI collaboration. We’ve reimagined blockchain application development from the ground up by creating a decentralized agent ecosystem where humans and AI collaborate as peers rather than tools. Our innovation lies in the autonomous yet interconnected nature of specialized LLM powered agents handling contract creation, backend logic, frontend interfaces, and security auditing. Our proposed architecture distributes expertise across AI agents that operate in a peer-to-peer network. Furthermore, to address the emerging threat of systemic vulnerabilities from AI-generated code patterns, we’ve integrated diverse verification methods that challenge and strengthen code before deployment.

suggestions.title: Cross-Chain Agent Transaction Orchestration
suggestions.desc: Enable the decentralized agents to execute cross-chain operations using the LayerZero or Wormhole protocols. Build a specialized agent that can monitor liquidity on Ethereum and Solana simultaneously to optimize DAPP deployment costs.

suggestions.title: ZK-Proof Integrity for Autonomous Agents
suggestions.desc: Implement Zero-Knowledge proofs (using RISC Zero or Succinct) to verify that an agent's computational task was executed correctly. This ensures that in a decentralized mesh, agents can trust each other's outputs without needing to reveal private API keys or proprietary logic.

suggestions.title: DAO-Governed Agent Repositories
suggestions.desc: Develop a governance system where token holders can vote on which LLM prompts or agent behaviors are added to the 'trusted' mesh. This transforms the project into a community-owned infrastructure for autonomous blockchain development.

suggestions.title: Autonomous NFT Market-Maker Mesh
suggestions.desc: Deploy a specialized sub-mesh of agents designed specifically for NFT liquidity. One agent monitors floor prices via OpenSea API, another calculates risk, and a third executes automated buy/sell orders on decentralized marketplaces.

title: MULISA: mHealth-enabled User-friendly Light-based Stroke Screening and Assessment in Pediatric Sickle Cell Disease in Uganda
authors: Jahan, Nursat; Raj, Ayushi Bharath
abstract: This research presents a novel mHealth-enabled solution for stroke screening of sickle cell disease children in LMICs using light-based stroke screening technologies. We conducted a systematic literature review to identify key barriers and used these insights to develop a conceptual framework guiding the design of an integrated system. Our prototype includes a SWIR SCOS device and a wearable oximeter, combined with an AI-enhanced mHealth platform. The proposed mHealth framework aims to improve screening accessibility and adoption in low-resource settings.

suggestions.title: Edge-AI Offline Inference for Rural Uganda
suggestions.desc: Convert the stroke screening AI model to TensorFlow Lite and optimize it for low-spec Android devices (under 4GB RAM). Ensure the platform can perform full assessments and store results locally without an active internet connection.

suggestions.title: AR-Guided Sensor Placement for CHWs
suggestions.desc: Build an Augmented Reality module within the mHealth app that guides Community Health Workers (CHWs) to place the SWIR SCOS device correctly on the child's head. Use the phone's camera to overlay a 3D anatomical guide to ensure consistent data quality.

suggestions.title: Multi-Spectral Fusion Transformer
suggestions.desc: Develop a Transformer-based time-series model that fuses raw data from both the SWIR SCOS and the wearable oximeter. This research would determine if the correlation between cerebral oxygenation and peripheral saturation provides earlier stroke warnings.

suggestions.title: MULISA Cloud-Based Referral System
suggestions.desc: Create a backend system that automatically flags high-risk screenings and alerts the nearest specialized hospital in Uganda. Include a clinician dashboard that allows doctors in Kampala to review data uploaded from rural clinics in real-time.

title: Ready Cluster One: Optimizing Film Success With Data Science
authors: Karim, Mohsin Md Abdul; Richardson, Joseph
abstract: This study presents a novel approach to predicting and optimizing screenplay investments by combining graph theory and finite mixture modeling (FMM) techniques. We construct a k-partite graph representing movies, genres, subgenres, production companies, directors, actors, and directors of photography, to explore the interconnectedness between these entities. Using FMM, we identify clusters within budget tiers, enabling a deeper understanding of how similar films perform based on their creative team and production characteristics. By balancing profit potential with risk-adjusted profit, the model suggests the most viable budget tiers for unproduced screenplays. This approach incorporates confidence intervals and evaluates the accuracy of budget tier recommendations, offering a data-driven solution for movie producers to make informed investment decisions.

suggestions.title: Streaming-Native Performance Forecasting
suggestions.desc: Update the k-partite graph to include viewership data from platforms like Netflix and Disney+. Shift the prediction target from box office revenue to 'completion rates' or 'hours watched' to better serve the modern indie film market.

suggestions.title: GraphSAGE for Talent Synergy Discovery
suggestions.desc: Replace the FMM clustering with a Graph Neural Network (GNN) using GraphSAGE. This would allow the system to learn deep node embeddings and predict the potential success of 'first-time' pairings of directors and actors based on their shared network neighbors.

suggestions.title: The 'Greenlight' Producer Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Develop a web-based decision support tool where producers can input a 'dream team' of talent. The tool would use the model to suggest the most cost-effective 'missing link' (e.g., recommending a specific DP that boosts the success probability for a low-budget horror film).

suggestions.title: Social Sentiment Graph Integration
suggestions.desc: Scrape real-time social media sentiment (X, Letterboxd) for the actors and directors in the graph. Inject this sentiment as a dynamic node attribute to see how public 'hype cycles' shift the FMM clusters over time.

title: Nutrilyzer: A Vision-Based App for Macronutrient Estimation and Blood Glucose Response Prediction
authors: Belfarsi, El Arbi
abstract: This study predicts postprandial glucose peaks and spike durations using 10-day multimodal data from 10 participants. Glucose, meals, workouts, and insulin doses were logged via the Nutrilyzer web app. Macronutrient content carbs, fats, and proteins was extracted using GPT-Vision, a highly accurate food analysis tool. These tuples were normalized to baseline glucose and aligned with a 3-hour window post-meal. Three models were tested: LSTM, Time Series Transformer, and ARIMA. LSTM performed best with 83.78% accuracy, followed by Transformer (71.43%) and ARIMA (62.41%). Results show the promise of AI-based food logging and time series modeling for personalized glucose forecasting.

suggestions.title: Federated Learning for Health Data Privacy
suggestions.desc: Implement a federated learning architecture so the LSTM model can be trained across multiple users' phones without sensitive glucose or insulin data ever leaving their devices. This addresses the major privacy barrier in health-tech adoption.

suggestions.title: Passive Vision via Wearable Integration
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Nutrilyzer app with wearable cameras (like Ray-Ban Meta glasses) to capture meal photos automatically. Use GPT-Vision to process these 'first-person' images to remove the friction of manual photo-logging for the user.

suggestions.title: Personalized Insulin Sensitivity Modeling
suggestions.desc: Use the 10-day multimodal logs to calculate personalized insulin-to-carb ratios and insulin sensitivity factors. Research whether a Transformer-based model can predict a user's unique 'optimal meal time' to minimize postprandial glucose spikes.

suggestions.title: Glucose-Optimized Smart Grocery Assistant
suggestions.desc: Connect the app's prediction engine to a grocery API. The assistant would generate shopping lists and suggest recipe substitutions (e.g., swapping white rice for quinoa) based on the user's historical glucose response to specific ingredients.

title: A Novel Superpixel–RAG–Transformer Approach for Three-Class Melanoma Segmentation
authors: Ordonez, Pablo
abstract: Melanoma is one of the deadliest skin cancers, with early detection relying on accurately identifying both the lesion core and its often ambiguous border. Traditional CNN and U-Net models struggle with fuzzy transitions and irregular boundaries. We propose a three-class segmentation framework that labels regions as background, border, or lesion core. Our method over-segments images into superpixels, builds a Region Adjacency Graph (RAG) to capture spatial context, and generates embeddings using transformer-based autoencoders. This approach combines local image statistics with global semantic structure. Experiments on the HAM10000 dataset show improved precision and recall, especially in challenging border regions, outperforming CNN/U-Net baselines. Our results highlight the value of explicitly modeling boundaries for accurate and interpretable melanoma segmentation.

suggestions.title: 3D Lesion Reconstruction from Mobile Photos
suggestions.desc: Use the three-class segmentation masks to build a 3D point cloud of the melanoma lesion. By analyzing photos from multiple smartphone angles, the tool could estimate the depth and volume of the core, providing critical information for surgical planning.

suggestions.title: Domain Adaptation for Diverse Skin Tones
suggestions.desc: Research the model's robustness by testing it on the Fitpatrick 17k dataset. Implement Domain Adversarial Training to ensure the Superpixel-RAG approach maintains high accuracy for the 'border' class across all six Fitzpatrick skin types.

suggestions.title: Graph-Based Triage Bot for Tele-Dermatology
suggestions.desc: Integrate the segmentation model into a WhatsApp/Telegram bot. The bot would use the RAG embeddings to provide an 'uncertainty score'; if the model is unsure about the lesion border, it automatically flags the case for human review by a dermatologist.

suggestions.title: Interactive Superpixel Refinement Tool
suggestions.desc: Build a GUI that allows dermatologists to 'correct' the model by merging or splitting superpixels in the Region Adjacency Graph. Use these corrections as an active learning signal to retrain the Transformer on the fly.

title: Prediction of Greenhouse Gas Emissions from Electric Vehicle Charging and Road Traffic in the United States
authors: Chowdhoury, Faysal; Kanigiri, Sai Nikhila
abstract: Electric vehicles (EVs) are widely considered a cleaner alternative to internal combustion engine vehicles. But their growing use creates indirect emissions via two main channels: more traffic congestion from more vehicle activity and more demand on power plants providing electricity for EV charging, usually depending on fossil fuels. This work offers a comprehensive, data-driven framework to forecast greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions connected to road traffic as well as EV-related power generation. Based on vehicle and speed characteristics, we forecast vehicle-level energy consumption and emission rates using a multi-model architecture that includes a Feed Forward Neural Network (FNN). While the Meta Prophet time series model is used to project power plant emissions under different energy demand conditions, macroscopic traffic flow models are used to estimate tract-level speed-density relationships. An Integrated Emission Model combines these elements to allow assessments particular to each area. Capturing vehicle mix, traffic dynamics, and energy grid variations, our study covers four major U.S. states—California, Georgia, New York, and Washington. Results show notable spatial and temporal variations in emissions, therefore stressing the need of thorough models taking into account the intricate interactions between energy infrastructure, traffic patterns, and EV adoption.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Grid-Aware EV Routing API
suggestions.desc: Develop a navigation service that calculates the 'cleanest' route for an EV. The API would query real-time carbon intensity data from the US power grid (via WattTime API) and suggest charging stations where the electricity is currently sourced from renewables.

suggestions.title: Policy Impact Simulator for US Cities
suggestions.desc: Create a simulation sandbox where city planners can test 'what-if' scenarios. Model the GHG impact of specific interventions like 'Mandatory Home Solar for New EV Owners' or 'High-Occupancy Toll Lanes for EVs' using the project's data framework.

suggestions.title: B2B Fleet Carbon Auditor
suggestions.desc: Build a SaaS tool for logistics companies (like FedEx or Amazon) to calculate the net-carbon footprint of their EV transition. The tool would analyze specific delivery routes and local grid emissions to provide an audit-ready GHG report.

suggestions.title: STGCN for Dynamic Traffic-Emission Modeling
suggestions.desc: Enhance the forecast accuracy by integrating a Spatial-Temporal Graph Convolutional Network (STGCN). This model would treat the US road network as a graph, better capturing how EV-related congestion ripples through urban corridors and affects emissions.

title: Characterizing and Understanding the Performance of Small Language Models on Edge Devices
authors: ISLAM, MD ROMYULL
abstract: In recent years, significant advancements in computing power, data richness, algorithmic development, and the growing demand for applications have catalyzed the rapid emergence and proliferation of large language models (LLMs) across various scenarios. Concurrently, factors such as computing resource limitations, cost considerations, real-time application requirements, task-specific customization, and privacy concerns have also driven the development and deployment of small language models (SLMs). Unlike extensively researched and widely deployed LLMs in the cloud, the performance of SLM workloads and their resource impact on edge environments remain poorly understood. More detailed studies will have to be carried out to understand the advantages, constraints, performances, and resource consumption in different settings of the edge.

suggestions.title: Cross-Platform Quantization Benchmarking Suite
suggestions.desc: Extend this project by developing a benchmark framework that compares 4-bit and 8-bit quantization (GGUF, AWQ) across diverse edge hardware like Raspberry Pi 5, Jetson Nano, and Orange Pi. Use llama.cpp to measure the specific trade-offs between tokens-per-second and perplexity for SLMs like Phi-3 and TinyLlama.

suggestions.title: Local-First Privacy-Preserving Personal Assistant
suggestions.desc: Build a functional desktop or mobile application that uses the researched SLMs to perform document summarization and email drafting entirely offline. Integrate a vector database like ChromaDB to allow the SLM to perform Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) on local user files without data leaving the device.

suggestions.title: Thermal-Aware Dynamic Model Scaling
suggestions.desc: Investigate how edge device temperature affects SLM performance over long inference sessions. Develop a 'thermal governor' that dynamically switches between different model sizes (e.g., from a 3B to a 1B parameter model) or adjusts quantization levels to prevent thermal throttling while maintaining uptime.

suggestions.title: Voice-Activated Edge Controller for IoT
suggestions.desc: Apply the SLM findings to create a low-latency voice interface for home automation using Whisper for STT and Piper for TTS. The goal is to process complex natural language commands (e.g., 'If it rains tomorrow, make sure the sprinklers stay off') locally on a micro-PC without relying on cloud APIs like Alexa.

title: Analysis of Climate Change Effects on Bird Migration Patterns Using Long-Term Data
authors: Banneni, Saikiran; Arabu, Naga Sreeja; Modepu, Bhavana; Kanduri, Abilash; Devisetty, Leelakarthik
abstract: This project examines the impact of climate change on bird migration patterns by integrating bird observation data from eBird with climate data from the NOAA Global Historical Climate Network (GHCN). Focusing on species such as the Arctic Tern, the study analyzes changes in migration timing, routes, and population trends over recent decades. Migration paths were visualized using QGIS, while MODIS land cover data helped assess habitat changes along these routes. Temporal analysis revealed noticeable shifts in migration timing, with earlier arrivals in some regions correlating with rising temperatures and changing precipitation patterns. For future predictions, CHELSA climate data was combined with machine learning models, including Gradient Boosting and Random Forest, to forecast migration behavior under different climate scenarios. The results highlight critical stopover sites increasingly threatened by habitat loss, emphasizing the need for targeted conservation efforts to support migratory species adapting to a changing climate.

suggestions.title: Predictive Migration Modeling with LSTMs
suggestions.desc: Transform the static analysis into a predictive tool by training a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) neural network on the eBird and NOAA GHCN historical data. Create a system that predicts the 'peak arrival date' for specific species in a given zip code based on current-year spring temperature anomalies.

suggestions.title: Urbanization and Night-Light Correlation Study
suggestions.desc: Integrate VIIRS Nighttime Lights satellite data with the existing QGIS migration paths to analyze how light pollution impacts bird stopover choices. This would extend the climate-only focus to include the physical 'trap' effect of urban centers along historical migration routes.

suggestions.title: Multi-Source Data Fusion for Habitat Quality
suggestions.desc: Combine the MODIS land cover data with the Sentinel-2 NDWI (Normalized Difference Water Index) to assess the degradation of wetlands. Use this to create a 'Migratory Stress Index' that identifies specific geographic bottlenecks where climate change and habitat loss coincide most severely.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Conservation Alert Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Build a web-based dashboard using Mapbox GL JS that ingests live weather feeds to alert conservationists when extreme weather events overlap with high-density migration windows. This tool could provide actionable data for 'Lights Out' initiatives to prevent mass bird strikes during migration.

title: Coding Neurodivergent
authors: Bramlett, Veronica
abstract: This literary research provides a look at neurodivergent individuals learning coding, specifically Python, and the struggles and benefits that come from the way their brains are wired. This literature research was conducted to look at the benefits and struggles of learning to code as a neurodivergent individual. One area that has not been studied extensively is the learning of Python, or coding in general, by neurodivergent populations. This includes the benefits a neurodivergent learner may glean from learning Python as well as the challenges they may encounter - anything which makes their experience different from that of a neurotypical Python beginner. Outcomes for which data was sought included cognitive challenges neurodivergent individuals may face (such as difficulty understanding abstract concepts or issues with motivation), ways in which the neurodivergent brain may be well-suited to learning Python, and ways in which neurodivergent populations may benefit from learning Python.

suggestions.title: Neuro-Adaptive IDE Extension for VS Code
suggestions.desc: Develop a VS Code extension that implements 'Focus Modes' based on the project's research, such as customizable syntax highlighting to reduce sensory overload or a 'Logical Flow' sidebar that visualizes Python code structure as a hierarchical tree for bottom-up learners.

suggestions.title: Gamified Logic-to-Python Bridge for Autistic Learners
suggestions.desc: Build a learning tool that maps abstract Python concepts (loops, classes) to highly concrete, visual logic puzzles. Focus on eliminating 'hidden' rules in syntax by providing a drag-and-drop interface that generates clean Python code, catering to the specific cognitive strengths identified in the research.

suggestions.title: Biometric Correlation of Coding Frustration
suggestions.desc: Conduct an empirical study using heart-rate monitors or eye-tracking software to identify which Python syntax errors cause the highest physiological stress in neurodivergent vs. neurotypical learners. Use these data points to propose a 'Neuro-Inclusive' subset of error messages for IDEs.

suggestions.title: AI Pair Programmer for Executive Function Support
suggestions.desc: Leverage an LLM API to create a specialized coding assistant that focuses on 'Task Decomposition.' The assistant would help neurodivergent coders break down complex Python projects into granular, non-overwhelming steps, providing 'micro-checkpoints' to maintain dopamine-driven focus.

title: XR Agent (A MLLM powered XR system)
authors: Shen, Yukang
abstract: This project proposes “XR Agent”, a uncoupled and efficient framework for developing AI-powered extended reality (XR) applications on head-mounted displays (HMDs). Leveraging multimodal artificial intelligence—including MediaPipe(Google open-source CV Model) for computer vision (object segmentation, recognition, pose estimation), multimodal large language models (MLLMs) like Gemini, and Unity’s cross-platform XR development ecosystem—the framework aims to create an extensible base system that enables rapid prototyping and deployment of intelligent XR applications. Currently, it was deployed on the Meta Quest 3 platform, XR Agent explores novel HCI(Human Computer Interaction) paradigms, combining real-time sensor data processing, immersive visualization, and adaptive AI-driven logic. This work addresses challenges modular integration of various different kinds of devices AI models. The framework also will be valuable through use cases in collaborative remote control, immersive training scenarios, and data collection for embodied AI.

suggestions.title: XR Industrial Maintenance Assistant
suggestions.desc: Extend the framework to an industrial use case where the Gemini MLLM analyzes a live video stream of machinery. Using MediaPipe for object segmentation, the system should overlay AR markers on specific bolts or levers, guiding a technician through a repair sequence using real-time visual recognition.

suggestions.title: Latency-Optimized Edge-Cloud Orchestrator
suggestions.desc: Improve the 'uncoupled' framework by building a middleware that decides whether to process computer vision tasks locally via MediaPipe (low latency) or offload complex scene reasoning to Gemini (high latency/intelligence). Optimize this for the Meta Quest 3 using Unity's XR Interaction Toolkit.

suggestions.title: Spatial Context Memory using Vector Databases
suggestions.desc: Implement a 'Spatial Memory' system where recognized objects and their coordinates are stored in a vector database like Milvus. This allows the XR Agent to answer questions about the past, such as 'Where did I leave my keys?' by querying the historical log of recognized objects in the 3D space.

suggestions.title: Accessibility Guide for the Visually Impaired
suggestions.desc: Adapt the XR Agent into a wearable navigation aid. Use the HMD’s cameras to identify obstacles and hazards, and use Gemini to provide descriptive audio cues to the user, effectively acting as an 'AI seeing-eye' that understands context better than simple proximity sensors.

title: Empowering Alzheimer’s Caregivers: Designing Explainable and Personalized AI for Mental Health Support
authors: Salma, Syeda Umme; Renduchintala, Chandra Rekha; Siddique, Isa
abstract: This study presents the design of an AI-powered caregiver support app aimed at personalized mental health and burden management for individuals caring for Alzheimer’s patients. The design is grounded in insights drawn from a comprehensive analysis of 28 recent studies on AI-driven mental health interventions. These findings informed the implementation of key features, including machine learning models such as Random Forest, clustering, and supervised learning to create adaptive care plans tailored to patient and caregiver profiles. The system dynamically adjusts task schedules based on engagement data and provides interpretable recommendations through SHAP. With built-in emotional check-ins, mood tracking, and caregiver-centric interventions, the app promotes well-being while reducing the cognitive and emotional load often experienced in dementia caregiving.

suggestions.title: XAI (Explainable AI) Dashboard for Caregivers
suggestions.desc: Enhance the Random Forest models by integrating SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations). Build a UI feature that shows caregivers *why* the AI recommended a specific intervention (e.g., 'Anxiety is predicted to rise because of low sleep metrics and recent medication changes'), building trust through transparency.

suggestions.title: Multi-Modal Sentiment Tracking System
suggestions.desc: Add a voice-journaling feature that uses NLP to perform sentiment and tone analysis on caregiver recordings. Correlate this linguistic data with the existing burden management models to detect 'hidden' burnout symptoms that traditional self-reporting surveys might miss.

suggestions.title: Smart Home Integration for Patient-Caregiver Synergy
suggestions.desc: Connect the app to IoT sensors (motion and bed sensors). Use the AI care plans to trigger environment changes—like adjusting light color or playing soothing music—when the model predicts a period of high patient agitation, directly reducing the caregiver's immediate burden.

suggestions.title: Federated Learning for Privacy-First Personalization
suggestions.desc: Implement a Federated Learning framework using Flower so that the AI models can learn from multiple caregivers' data without ever uploading sensitive patient health information to a central server, ensuring HIPAA-compliant personalization.

title: Optimization of Fixed Time in Round Robin Scheduling using Clustering Algorithms
authors: Suer, Sait
abstract: This project introduces a method to optimize the fixed time in Round Robin scheduling using unsupervised clustering, specifically DBSCAN. Traditionally, fixed time is chosen arbitrarily, often leading to inefficiencies like increased waiting times and frequent context switches. Our approach leverages DBSCAN to identify clusters of processes based on arrival and burst times, as well as to detect outliers that may need unique fixed times. This adaptive, data-driven adjustment has demonstrated improved performance over traditional methods, reducing waiting time, minimizing context switches, and enhancing system throughput. Simulations confirmed the effectiveness of this approach, especially in datasets with outlier processes, where DBSCAN performed exceptionally well.

suggestions.title: Kernel-Level Round Robin Implementation
suggestions.desc: Take the DBSCAN-optimized logic and implement it within a real-time operating system kernel like FreeRTOS or a custom Linux module. Measure the actual reduction in context-switch overhead and CPU utilization compared to the standard fixed-time scheduler in a live environment.

suggestions.title: Comparative Study of Density-Based Clustering
suggestions.desc: Compare the performance of DBSCAN against other density-based algorithms like OPTICS or HDBSCAN for this specific use case. Analyze which algorithm handles 'noise' (highly irregular processes) better and which offers the lowest computational overhead for the scheduler itself.

suggestions.title: Adaptive Scheduling for Serverless Architectures
suggestions.desc: Apply the clustering approach to manage task execution in a serverless environment (e.g., OpenWhisk). Use DBSCAN to cluster cold-start processes versus warm-start processes to optimize the allocation of execution time and reduce tail latency in cloud functions.

suggestions.title: RL-Guided Parameter Tuning for DBSCAN
suggestions.desc: Develop a Reinforcement Learning agent that sits above the scheduler and tunes the 'Epsilon' and 'MinPts' parameters of the DBSCAN algorithm in real-time. The agent would aim to minimize the weighted average of waiting time and turnaround time as the workload distribution changes.

title: A study of different real-time robotic applications
authors: Wu, Yongshuai

suggestions.title: ROS2-Based Collaborative Multi-Robot Mapping
suggestions.desc: Implement a real-time SLAM (Simultaneous Localization and Mapping) system using ROS2 and LiDAR sensors for multiple robots. Build a decentralized coordination protocol that allows two robots to merge their maps in real-time as they explore an unknown environment.

suggestions.title: Computer Vision for Real-Time Weed Detection
suggestions.desc: Develop a robotic application for precision agriculture. Use a Raspberry Pi with a Coral TPU to run a YOLOv8-tiny model in real-time, identifying weeds among crops and signaling an actuator for removal, focusing on minimizing latency between detection and action.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Jitter Analysis in RT-Linux
suggestions.desc: Conduct a technical deep-dive into the performance differences between standard Linux, RT-Preempt patched Linux, and Xenomai for robotic motor control. Create a benchmarking tool that visualizes task deadline misses and jitter across these different real-time environments.

suggestions.title: Haptic-Feedback Teleoperation System
suggestions.desc: Build a real-time teleoperation interface that uses a VR controller to move a robotic arm. Integrate force sensors on the arm's grippers and transmit that data back to the user via haptic motors, focusing on sub-50ms round-trip latency for a natural user experience.
