title: Compassionate Digital Assistant: Anchor
authors: Regan, Christopher M; Verma, Navneet

suggestions.title: Fine-Tuned Empathy Engine with LoRA
suggestions.desc: Improve the 'Anchor' assistant by fine-tuning a Mistral or Llama-3 model using LoRA on the 'EmpatheticDialogues' dataset. Benchmark its responses against standard models to quantify its ability to validate user emotions without sounding repetitive or robotic.

suggestions.title: Crisis Detection and Resource Mapping
suggestions.desc: Integrate a safety layer that uses keyword and sentiment analysis to identify high-risk psychological states. If a crisis is detected, the assistant should dynamically pull local mental health resources and emergency contacts using the Google Maps and 988 APIs.

suggestions.title: Linguistic Mirroring and Rapport Analysis
suggestions.desc: Investigate 'Linguistic Style Matching' (LSM) within the assistant. Study whether the assistant's ability to subtly mirror the user's sentence structure and vocabulary leads to higher reported levels of user trust and perceived compassion during long-term interactions.

suggestions.title: Wearable Bio-Feedback Integration
suggestions.desc: Connect the assistant to a smartwatch API (like HealthKit) to monitor real-time Heart Rate Variability (HRV). When the assistant detects high physical stress, it can proactively initiate a 'Compassionate Anchor' session, offering guided breathing or grounding exercises.

title: Integration of Quantum Natural Language Processing (QNLP) with Neo4j LLM Knowledge Graphs for Enhanced NLP Tasks
authors: Bharti, Suman
abstract: This study investigates the integration of Quantum Natural Language Processing (QNLP) with Neo4j LLM Knowledge Graphs (KGs) to enhance natural language understanding tasks. By leveraging quantum circuit simulations, we aim to improve the probabilistic interpretation of relationships between entities. Our preliminary findings suggest that QNLP offers deeper insights compared to traditional NLP methods, particularly in modeling complex entity relationships. This approach also addresses significant limitations in Neo4j-based Large Language Model (LLM) Graph Databases, such as handling high dimensional relationships and capturing semantic nuances. The integration of QNLP into Neo4j refines relationship modeling and enhances performance in tasks like entity extraction and knowledge inference, paving the way for more advanced and context-aware NLP applications.

suggestions.title: Lambeq-to-Neo4j Integration Pipeline
suggestions.desc: Develop a Python library that automates the conversion of Neo4j subgraphs into DisCoPy circuits using the Lambeq toolkit. This would allow researchers to easily pipe structured graph data into quantum simulations for relationship classification tasks.

suggestions.title: Quantum Link Prediction Benchmarking
suggestions.desc: Perform a head-to-head comparison between classical Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) and the QNLP-KG hybrid for link prediction in a medical knowledge graph. Analyze whether quantum probabilistic modeling better identifies 'hidden' connections between drugs and side effects.

suggestions.title: Quantum-Enhanced Fact Checking System
suggestions.desc: Apply the QNLP-Neo4j framework to misinformation detection. Use the 'probabilistic interpretation of relationships' to assign a 'certainty score' to claims by checking them against a verified knowledge graph, using quantum circuits to model the nuances of linguistic ambiguity.

suggestions.title: Semantic Search Engine for Patent Analysis
suggestions.desc: Build a tool that uses the QNLP-KG integration to help patent lawyers find 'prior art.' The system would use quantum circuits to understand the complex, multi-layered relationships between technical components in a patent database, identifying overlaps that classical keyword search misses.

title: Meta-Reinforcement Learning with Discrete World Models for Adaptive Load Balancing
authors: Redovian, Cameron J
abstract: We present a novel integration of the RL^2 meta-reinforcement learning algorithm with discrete world models, employing the DreamerV3 architecture, to enhance load balancing in operating systems. This integration allows for rapid adaptation to dynamic workload distributions with minimal retraining. In experiments using the Park load balancing environment, our approach outperformed the traditional AC3 algorithm in both standard and adaptive trials. Additionally, it exhibited strong resilience to catastrophic forgetting, maintaining high performance despite continuous variations in workload distribution and size. These results demonstrate the effectiveness of combining recurrent policy networks with discrete world models, offering a significant advancement in meta-learning capabilities for dynamic operating system environments. This work has important implications for improving resource management and performance in modern operating systems, addressing the challenges posed by increasingly dynamic and heterogeneous workloads.

suggestions.title: eBPF-Powered Kernel Deployment
suggestions.desc: Take the meta-RL models and deploy them as eBPF programs within the Linux kernel. This would allow the load balancer to execute inference directly in the kernel space with minimal overhead, replacing traditional schedulers with the trained DreamerV3 world model.

suggestions.title: Cross-Workload Transfer Learning Study
suggestions.desc: Test the 'meta' aspect of the RL^2 algorithm by training the model on web server workloads (high request frequency) and then evaluating its adaptation speed on database workloads (heavy I/O). Quantify the number of 'episodes' needed for the world model to stabilize.

suggestions.title: Adaptive Cloud Autoscaler for Kubernetes
suggestions.desc: Build a Kubernetes Horizontal Pod Autoscaler (HPA) plugin that uses this Meta-RL approach. Unlike standard HPAs that use simple CPU thresholds, this system would use the world model to predict future load spikes and scale pods proactively.

suggestions.title: Safety-Constrained Load Balancing
suggestions.desc: Integrate 'Safety Gym' constraints into the DreamerV3 architecture to ensure that during the 'adaptation' phase, the agent never exceeds critical hardware limits (like thermal caps or memory exhaustion), which is a common failure point for RL in production OSs.

title: Explainable Multi-Label Classification Framework for Behavioral Health Based on Domain Concepts
authors: Nweke, Francis E; Azmee, Abm Adnan
abstract: Behavioral health, which covers mental health, lifestyle choices, addictions, and crises, poses serious issues in the community. Thus, appropriately analyzing and classifying behavioral health data is crucial for making informed healthcare decisions. Traditional deep learning and natural language processing approaches struggle to effectively identify behavioral health issues because the data is unstructured, complex, and lacks sufficient context. Furthermore, subject matter experts must be consulted to ensure effective identification. In this work, we proposed a deep learning-based framework consisting of several modules: A) domain concept encoder converts the keywords and their evidence types to vectors, which were predefined by a subject matter expert; B) the semantic representation encoder (SRE) is trained on the vectors to learn the relationship between them; C) transformed-based feature learner is an advanced learner that extracts feature embeddings from documents and generates attention weights since it has more context given the incorporated relationship weights; D) The behavioral health multilabel classifier utilizes feature embeddings to classify a document into one or more behavioral health classes; and E) The LLM-enabled explainer provides explanations based on attention weights and classifications. Our proposed framework outperformed state-of-the-art models in multilabel behavioral health case classification while also providing explanations for each classification. Which is crucial in behavioral health analysis.

suggestions.title: Medical Knowledge Graph Grounding
suggestions.desc: Enhance the explainability framework by mapping the identified domain concepts to a medical knowledge graph like SNOMED CT or UMLS. This allows the system to provide clinical definitions and hierarchical context for every classification decision it makes.

suggestions.title: Clinician-in-the-Loop Diagnostic Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Build a React-based dashboard that visualizes the multi-label predictions alongside 'highlighted' evidence in patient notes. Incorporate a feedback mechanism where practitioners can 'upvote' or 'downvote' specific explanations to fine-tune the model’s reasoning logic.

suggestions.title: Cross-Domain Behavioral Bias Audit
suggestions.desc: Research how the framework's explainability component handles data from different demographics or socioeconomic backgrounds. Use the framework to detect if specific lifestyle choices are being unfairly weighted toward certain behavioral health classifications compared to others.

suggestions.title: Crisis Hotline Real-Time Alerting
suggestions.desc: Adapt the model to process live transcriptions from crisis hotlines. Build a system that flags high-risk indicators (e.g., self-harm or addiction crises) to supervisors in real-time, providing the specific 'domain concepts' that triggered the alert for rapid intervention.

title: Tech Guru: A Domain Specific LLM for Tech. Industry
authors: Nweke, Francis E; Vu, Long; Jha, Nitin
abstract: This project focuses on developing a domain-specific chatbot tailored for the tech industry. The chatbot utilizes articles sourced from blogs written by developers and engineers at leading companies such as Google and NVIDIA. Titles and content from these articles are extracted to form a question-answer dataset, with the titles acting as questions and the article content serving as answers. To refine the questions, we implemented a custom method to format the dataset to follow Alpaca format. The resulting question-answer pairs are then used to fine-tune a language model, adapting it to the specialized domain of the tech industry. Following this, the model undergoes rigorous evaluation to ensure its accuracy and relevance, and iterative improvements are made based on performance metrics. The final product is deployed as a chatbot capable of handling complex queries in the tech space, offering valuable support to developers and engineers.

suggestions.title: RAG-Enhanced Tech Blog Retrieval
suggestions.desc: Implement a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipeline using Pinecone and LangChain. Instead of relying solely on fine-tuned weights, the 'Tech Guru' can retrieve the specific Google or NVIDIA blog URL and quote relevant code snippets directly in its response.

suggestions.title: Automated Technical Interview Simulator
suggestions.desc: Use the Tech Guru dataset to generate a mock interview platform. The system can pull the latest engineering challenges from the NVIDIA/Google blogs and challenge users with high-fidelity technical questions, providing feedback based on the blog's original solutions.

suggestions.title: Developer Documentation Browser Extension
suggestions.desc: Build a browser extension that activates when a developer is on GitHub or StackOverflow. It uses the Tech Guru model to provide expert-level context derived from top-tier engineering blogs, helping users understand 'why' a certain architecture (like Transformer variants) is preferred.

suggestions.title: Evaluate Domain-Tuned Models on Code Reasoning Benchmarks
suggestions.desc: Run a controlled evaluation of Tech Guru against base Llama-3.1/3.2 and GPT-4o-mini on HumanEval, MBPP, and domain-specific troubleshooting prompts from NVIDIA and Google engineering blogs. Report exact-match, pass@k, and citation-grounded accuracy to quantify where domain tuning delivers measurable gains.

title: Human-Assisted AI for Detecting Mental Health Indicators in Social Media
authors: Azmee, Abm Adnan; Nweke, Francis E; Pederson, Mason V
abstract: Mental health is essential to overall well-being, and mental illness includes conditions that affect a person’s psychological health, causing significant distress and limiting daily functioning. With advancements in technology, social media has become a platform where individuals openly share their emotions and thoughts, offering a unique window into their psychological states. However, traditional machine learning models struggle to interpret social media data's wide range of linguistic nuances. To analyze this data effectively, collaboration with human experts is crucial. This study proposes an innovative human-AI teaming framework that integrates human expertise with artificial intelligence (AI) to address these challenges. Our framework leverages multi-dimensional data along with expert feedback to identify factors contributing to mental illness. Through extensive testing on Reddit data, our model demonstrates a 9% improvement in performance over the state-of-the-art model, underscoring its efficacy and impact.

suggestions.title: Active Learning Annotation Pipeline
suggestions.desc: Develop an active learning loop where the AI identifies social media posts with 'high uncertainty'—such as those containing heavy sarcasm or slang. These posts are then sent to a human annotator via a custom interface to retrain and sharpen the model's linguistic nuances.

suggestions.title: Community Moderator Sentiment Shield
suggestions.desc: Build a Discord or Reddit bot for community moderators that monitors for mental health crises. Using the human-assisted detection logic, the bot can privately alert moderators and provide a list of local resources to send to the user in distress.

suggestions.title: Anonymized Workforce Wellness Analytics
suggestions.desc: Create a B2B platform for HR departments that analyzes aggregate sentiment on internal communication tools (like Slack). The goal is to detect department-wide burnout or stress trends without compromising individual privacy, providing early-warning signals for mental health support.

suggestions.title: Linguistic Evolution Tracking
suggestions.desc: Research how mental health indicators in text evolve over time by analyzing a longitudinal dataset of social media posts. Use the model to identify how 'slang' for depression or anxiety changes across different age demographics over a 5-year span.

title: Utilizing ML techniques for a Quantum Augmented HTTP Protocol
authors: Jha, Nitin
abstract: Over the past decade, several small-scale quantum key distribution (QKD) networks have been implemented worldwide. However, achieving scalable, large-scale quantum networks relies on advancements in quantum repeaters, channels, memories, and network protocols. To enhance the security of current networks while utilizing available quantum technologies, integrating classical networks with quantum elements appears to be the next logical step. In this study, we propose modifications to the HTTP protocol's data packet structure, adjustments to end-to-end encryption methods, and optimized bandwidth distribution between quantum and classical channels for high-traffic network routes.

suggestions.title: SimulaQron Network Performance Benchmarking
suggestions.desc: Implement the proposed HTTP modifications in a simulated environment using SimulaQron or NetSquid. Measure the latency overhead and throughput of the 'Quantum Augmented' protocol compared to standard TLS 1.3 in high-congestion scenarios.

suggestions.title: Reinforcement Learning for Hybrid Routing
suggestions.desc: Train an RL agent to manage the network layer, deciding when to utilize expensive quantum keys versus classical encryption based on the sensitivity of the HTTP payload (e.g., banking data vs. public images).

suggestions.title: Quantum-Safe File Transfer Application
suggestions.desc: Develop a proof-of-concept web application for transferring sensitive government or healthcare files. Use the modified HTTP protocol to ensure that even if the data is intercepted today, it cannot be decrypted by future quantum computers (Harvest Now, Decrypt Later protection).

suggestions.title: Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC) Fallback Module
suggestions.desc: Integrate NIST-standard PQC algorithms (like Kyber or Dilithium) as a fallback mechanism within the protocol. This ensures that the HTTP connection remains secure even when QKD hardware is unavailable or under maintenance.

title: Joint Encryption and Error Correction for Quantum Communication
authors: Jha, Nitin
abstract: Secure quantum networks are foundational for developing a quantum internet in the future. However, current age quantum channels are prone to noise, which can introduce errors in transmitted data. Traditionally, error correction is applied to the message separately, after encryption, resulting in additional overhead that should be minimized. In response, we propose a unified approach that combines encryption and error correction into a single process. This work represents an initial effort to integrate these functions for secure quantum communication by integrating the Calderbank-Shor-Steane (CSS) Quantum Error Correction (QECC) code with the three-stage secure quantum communication protocol. Additionally, the protocol supports the transmission of arbitrary qubits from sender to receiver, making it adaptable for general use.

suggestions.title: Noise-Adaptive Error Correction Tuning
suggestions.desc: Extend the unified approach by implementing an adaptive layer that detects specific quantum noise types (e.g., Bit-Flip vs. Phase-Flip). Use ML to dynamically adjust the error correction strength in real-time based on the current fidelity of the quantum channel.

suggestions.title: Qiskit Aer Noise Profile Comparison
suggestions.desc: Develop a comprehensive benchmark suite using Qiskit to compare this joint approach against the traditional 'Separate Encryption then Error Correction' method. Evaluate performance across various 'Real Device' noise profiles from IBM Quantum computers.

suggestions.title: Lightweight JEEC Library for Quantum IoT
suggestions.desc: Package the encryption and error correction logic into a highly optimized C++/Rust library. Target resource-constrained quantum sensors or repeaters that require minimal overhead to maintain data integrity during transmission.

suggestions.title: Hybrid Quantum-Classical Bridge Protocol
suggestions.desc: Design a gateway that translates between the proposed joint quantum protocol and standard TCP/IP. This would allow legacy systems to communicate with future quantum nodes while benefiting from the unified encryption/correction security layer.

title: A Multimodal Approach to Quiz Generation: Leveraging RAG Models for Educational Assessments
authors: Kunuku, Mourya Teja
abstract: Crafting quiz questions that effectively assess students’ understanding of lectures and course materials, such as textbooks, poses significant challenges. Recent AI-based quiz generation efforts have predominantly concentrated on static resources, like textbooks and slides, often overlooking the dynamic and interactive elements of live lectures—contextual cues, discussions, and interactions—that contribute to the learning experience. In this work, we propose a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) model that processes multimodal inputs by combining text, audio, and video to produce quizzes that capture a fuller context. Our method incorporates Whisper for audio transcription and utilizes a Large Vision-Language Model (LVLM) to extract essential visual data from lecture videos. By integrating both spoken and visual elements, our model generates quizzes that more closely represent the lecture environment. We evaluate the model’s impact on quiz relevance, diversity, and engagement, showing that this multimodal approach fosters a more dynamic and immersive learning experience. Performance metrics, including hit rate and mean reciprocal rank (MRR), are used to assess question relevance and accuracy. A high hit rate indicates the model’s reliability in producing pertinent questions, while MRR highlights ranking quality, demonstrating the prompt appearance of relevant questions. Strong results in these metrics confirm our model’s effectiveness, though current limitations include challenges in handling abstract concepts absent in the lecture material—a gap we aim to bridge in future developments by integrating external knowledge sources.

suggestions.title: Multimodal Visual-Context Extraction
suggestions.desc: Integrate a Vision-Language Model (like LLaVA) to analyze diagrams and charts within lecture slides. This allows the RAG model to generate quiz questions about visual concepts, such as 'Label the parts of the cell shown in slide 12,' rather than just text-based content.

suggestions.title: Post-Lecture 'Kahoot' Generator
suggestions.desc: Build an automated pipeline for professors that takes a Zoom/Teams lecture recording, processes the audio with OpenAI Whisper, and uses RAG to generate a ready-to-play quiz file (CSV/JSON) for platforms like Kahoot or Canvas within minutes.

suggestions.title: Personalized 'Weak-Spot' Flashcards
suggestions.desc: Create a mobile app that uses the RAG model to compare student notes (via OCR) against the official lecture material. The AI identifies 'missing' information in the student's notes and generates targeted quizzes to fill those specific knowledge gaps.

suggestions.title: Dynamic Interaction Modeling
suggestions.desc: Research how to incorporate Q&A segments from live lectures into the RAG framework. Develop a method to weight 'student-asked questions' more heavily, as they often represent the most difficult or critical concepts for the class to understand.

title: Deep Learning Models for Protein-Protein Binding Affinity Prediction
authors: Chen, Lingtao
abstract: Binding affinity (BA) prediction is important for drug discovery and protein engineering. It seeks to understand the interaction strength between proteins and their ligands (or proteins). This information assists in the design of proteins with enhanced or novel functions, as well as understanding the molecular mechanisms of drug action. This paper presents the development and comparative analysis of two deep learning models, a convolutional neural network (CNN) and a transformer model. Many variants of models in this research were developed using TensorFlow. One model that utilizes ProteinBERT was developed using PyTorch. The CNN model captures local sequence features effectively, while the Transformer model leverages self-attention mechanisms to learn long-range dependencies within the sequences. Protein sequences are the inputs for the models. The sequences are processed using various encoders, like One-hot encoding, Sequence-Statistics-Content, and Position Specific Scoring Matrix. The predicted outputs are Gibbs free energy changes, a key indicator of binding affinity. From this study, both the CNN and transformer models can achieve the same level of accuracy under different conditions. For the CNN model, it can handle full data without sacrificing performance, but it takes much more time to preprocess the features from the protein sequences. The transformer model can achieve the same level of accuracy as the CNN model with no big predictive errors for each protein, but it requires the model to run on less data, which removes some rarely long protein sequences. This study emphasizes the potential of advanced deep learning architectures to enhance the predictive strengths of binding affinity models.

suggestions.title: Geometric Deep Learning with GNNs
suggestions.desc: Advance the project by replacing 2D CNNs with Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) to model the 3D atomic structure of proteins. Use the PDBbind dataset to represent proteins as graphs where nodes are atoms and edges are chemical bonds for more accurate affinity scores.

suggestions.title: Viral Mutation Sensitivity Analysis
suggestions.desc: Apply the Transformer model to predict how specific mutations in the COVID-19 Spike protein affect its binding affinity to human ACE2 receptors. This creates a tool for 'early warning' prediction of how new variants might impact transmissibility.

suggestions.title: Cloud-Based Lead Optimization Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Build a web-based interface where drug researchers can upload a protein structure and a set of candidate ligands. The backend runs the Transformer/CNN ensemble to output a ranked list of candidates based on predicted binding strength.

suggestions.title: Cross-Modality Ensemble Model
suggestions.desc: Develop a hybrid architecture that combines the 'Sequence Awareness' of the Transformer with the 'Spatial Awareness' of the CNN. Evaluate if this ensemble outperforms individual models on the DUD-E (Directory of Useful Decoys) benchmark.

title: Jamming Signal Detection Using Extreme Gradient Boosting (XGBoost) Algorithm
authors: Adamana, Keerthana; Sao, Christian K

suggestions.title: SDR Real-Time Jamming Monitor
suggestions.desc: Connect the XGBoost model to a Software Defined Radio (SDR) like an RTL-SDR or HackRF. Use GNU Radio to stream live IQ data into the model, creating a real-time 'Spectrum Police' tool that detects and classifies jamming attempts in the 2.4GHz band.

suggestions.title: Drone Anti-Jamming Alert System
suggestions.desc: Port the XGBoost model to a Raspberry Pi or Jetson Nano mounted on a drone. The system can provide immediate cockpit alerts if it detects 'GPS Spoofing' or 'Barrage Jamming,' allowing the pilot to trigger an autonomous 'Return to Home' protocol.

suggestions.title: Adversarial Jamming Robustness Study
suggestions.desc: Research how 'Smart Jammers' can bypass XGBoost detection by mimicking legitimate traffic patterns. Develop an adversarial training dataset to make the model more resilient against pulse jamming that hides within standard Wi-Fi frames.

suggestions.title: Self-Healing IoT Mesh Gateway
suggestions.desc: Integrate the detector into an IoT gateway. When the XGBoost model flags a jamming attack, the gateway automatically instructs all connected devices to perform a 'Channel Hop' to a pre-arranged secondary frequency to maintain communication.

title: The Compression Connection: NCD and KNN in Law Enforcement Text Analytics
authors: Gillott, Gabriel T
abstract: Facing a deluge of digital records, law enforcement needs advanced data sorting systems. This project uses a new NLP model, blending compression algorithms and KNN, to categorize Cobb County police reports by mental health, behavioral, and drug issues—vital for efficient resource allocation. The model employs Normalized Compression Distance (NCD) to discern text similarities, enhancing analysis of varying report styles. Early tests show promise in label categorization, but generalizing remains challenging, marking future research directions. This NLP advancement could revolutionize data handling in public safety, aiming to surpass current classification standards.

suggestions.title: Hybrid Neural-Compression Classification Pipeline
suggestions.desc: Combine the Normalized Compression Distance (NCD) approach with a BERT-based embedding layer to handle semantic nuances that pure compression might miss. Use the KNN classifier to weigh the compression-based similarity against vector-based cosine similarity for more robust labeling of complex mental health reports.

suggestions.title: Privacy-Preserving Federated Police Data Sharing
suggestions.desc: Implement a Federated Learning framework using Flower or PySyft to allow multiple law enforcement agencies to train the NCD-KNN model on their local sensitive records without sharing the raw text. This creates a more generalized model for behavioral patterns across counties while maintaining strict PII compliance.

suggestions.title: Lexicon-Agnostic Cross-Lingual Report Categorization
suggestions.desc: Test the limits of NCD by applying the model to non-English or code-switched police reports without retraining. Because NCD is based on information theory rather than vocabulary, you can benchmark how well it identifies drug-related incidents in multilingual datasets compared to traditional NLP models.

suggestions.title: Automated Crisis Intervention Team (CIT) Dispatch Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Build a real-time Streamlit dashboard that ingests raw report feeds and uses the NCD model to flag reports requiring specialized mental health response. Integrate a visualization layer using Folium to map 'behavioral hotspots' to help Cobb County optimize the placement of social worker co-responders.

similar: legacy:GR-20|Spring 2016|sentiment analysis and text mining of twitter data using r

title: AI-based Quiz Generation: The Role of LLMs in Digital Education
authors: Sreekanth, Devananda
abstract: Our investigation delves into the application of Large Language Models (LLMs) and AI in crafting quiz-based learning tools for college students. We sifted through academic resources, utilizing keywords such as "AI," "GPT," "BERT," and "LLM," and pinpointed 24 pivotal papers out of over 80. Our findings highlight a preference for models like GPT-3, with newer technologies like BERT and RAG less represented, suggesting potential avenues for future inquiry. The research employed a structured methodology that encompassed an exhaustive literature review, trend analysis over time, and detailed textual scrutiny of AI technology mentions and innovative methods within the domain. This approach surfaced trends towards personalization in AI-powered educational tools, integrating advanced LLMs such as GPT-4 into practical applications, and crafting varied AI-facilitated question types to cater to diverse educational contexts.

suggestions.title: Bloom's Taxonomy Benchmarking Suite
suggestions.desc: Based on the 24 papers reviewed, build a benchmarking tool that evaluates LLMs (Llama-3, GPT-4, Gemini) on their ability to generate 'Higher-Order' questions (Analysis/Evaluation) versus simple 'Recall' questions for university-level Physics or CS.

suggestions.title: Open-Source RAG Quiz Toolkit
suggestions.desc: Implement the under-represented technologies identified in the review (like BERT and RAG) into a single, modular Python library. This allows educators to upload a PDF textbook and choose which specific architecture to use for generating their assessments.

suggestions.title: Hallucination Audit in STEM Assessments
suggestions.desc: Conduct a formal study on the 'correctness' rate of AI-generated quizzes in specialized STEM fields. Use the findings from the literature review to create a 'Hallucination Scoreboard' for different LLMs when generating math-heavy quiz content.

suggestions.title: AI-Powered Course-Corrector
suggestions.desc: Build a Chrome extension for Coursera/edX that generates 'Personalized Remediation Quizzes.' If a student fails a generated quiz, the system uses RAG to find the exact timestamp in the lecture video that covers the missed concept and provides a new, easier question.

title: Illusion of Weight: The Use of Tactile Glove for Muscle Exercise for Elders in Virtual Gym Experience
authors: Autry, Johnathon R
abstract: This pilot study aims to investigate the potential creation of the perception of weight through a blend of visual and tactile feedback. Utilizing a tactile glove with varying vibration intensities and virtual dumbbell sizes, the experiment explores multiple conditions. These include tactile intensity (small, medium, large), virtual dumbbell sizes (small, medium, large), and diverse visualizations—ranging from no virtual dumbbell with or without tactile feedback to scenarios including both virtual dumbbells and tactile feedback. The study evaluates the virtual reality exercise experience and real performance using EMG sensors to measure muscle response, Heart Rate (HR), Galvanic Skin Response (GSR), and hand tracking. These biometric indicators enable a comprehensive understanding of the physiological and experiential impact of different conditions. The findings will provide insights into the efficacy of combined visual and tactile feedback in simulating weight perception.

suggestions.title: Closed-Loop Haptic Feedback with Flex Sensors
suggestions.desc: Enhance the tactile glove by integrating flex sensors and an ESP32 microcontroller to map the user's grip strength to vibration intensity. This creates a realistic 'squeezing' resistance sensation that adapts in real-time as the user interacts with virtual weights.

suggestions.title: Gamified Stroke Rehabilitation Interface
suggestions.desc: Pivot the gym experience into a clinical tool for stroke recovery by building a series of 'daily task' mini-games (e.g., picking up groceries). Use the glove's tactile feedback to help patients with proprioception issues regain a sense of object presence and weight.

suggestions.title: Multisensory Weight Perception Study
suggestions.desc: Expand the research by introducing auditory variables, such as the 'clink' of heavy metal vs. the 'thud' of plastic, to see if cross-modal cues significantly lower the vibration threshold needed to trick the brain into perceiving mass.

suggestions.title: Quest-Link Integration for Unity XR
suggestions.desc: Develop a standardized Unity SDK that allows commercial VR fitness developers to easily map virtual object mass properties to the glove's vibration motors. This would move the project from a pilot study to a plug-and-play peripheral for the Meta Quest ecosystem.

title: Enhancing Aircraft Electronic Warfare Testing with Automated RF Spectrum Analysis
authors: De Santiago, Anthony; Morgan, Matthew T; Kim, Geonhyeong; Bailey, Jalon L; Reaves, Camille
abstract: Military test ranges utilize a variety of Radio Frequency (RF) threat systems, to assess the effectiveness of Electronic Warfare (EW) systems during flight tests. A component of this process involves monitoring RF transmissions. Traditionally, system engineers at Robins Airforce Base have manually analyzed video from spectrum analyzers to confirm properties of specific threat systems. To streamline this analysis, our team's aim was to develop an automated solution for RF spectrum analysis. We employed a custom YOLO V8 model to isolate the analyzer screen and used a novel combination of frame differencing, summing, and agglomerative clustering techniques to extract relevant properties of measured signals. Our resulting application significantly reduces human interaction, enhances accuracy, and allows for the transformation of video data into a digitally manipulatable numeric format.

suggestions.title: Real-Time SDR Signal Processing
suggestions.desc: Replace the manual video analysis of spectrum analyzers by interfacing directly with Software Defined Radios (SDR) like the HackRF One. Use GNU Radio or Python's SoapySDR library to capture and process raw I/Q data directly for more accurate threat identification.

suggestions.title: CNN-Based Waveform Classification
suggestions.desc: Train a Convolutional Neural Network on spectrogram images of known jamming and radar signals to automate threat classification. This moves beyond simple property monitoring to intelligent signal recognition under high-noise conditions.

suggestions.title: Autonomous Drone Signal Localization
suggestions.desc: Adapt the RF analysis tool to detect and locate unauthorized drones by identifying their specific control frequencies (2.4GHz/5.8GHz hopping patterns). Implement a 'Heatmap' UI that visualizes signal strength to help ground crews find the transmitter.

suggestions.title: Edge Deployment on NVIDIA Jetson
suggestions.desc: Optimize the computer vision and RF models for edge computing on an NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano. This allows for a portable, battery-powered spectrum analysis unit that can be deployed at flight test ranges without needing a desktop workstation.

title: Symphony of Silicon: Rethinking Music Creation through Deep Learning Models
authors: Gillott, Gabriel T
abstract: Generative AI has transformed music creation, blending human and machine artistry. This study presents a neural network model trained on piano MIDI files for music generation, utilizing LSTM and self-attention mechanisms to capture music's complexity. Bayesian optimization with Tree-structured Parzen Estimator (TPE) refines the model's hyperparameters. The architecture includes bidirectional GRUs and self-attention layers, trained on the extensive Magenta MAESTRO dataset. The model, bettered by TPE over conventional tuning, is assessed for accuracy and expressiveness. The paper details the model's design and validates TPE's efficiency, marking progress in AI's creative application in music.

suggestions.title: JUCE-Based VST Plugin Development
suggestions.desc: Wrap the trained LSTM/Self-attention model into a VST/AU plugin using the JUCE framework. This allows professional musicians to generate MIDI sequences directly inside Digital Audio Workstations (DAWs) like Ableton Live or FL Studio.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Jazz Improvisation Accompanist
suggestions.desc: Build a system that listens to live MIDI input from a human performer and uses the model to generate a 'call and response' accompaniment. Implement a low-latency pipeline so the AI can improvise along with the human in real-time.

suggestions.title: Cross-Genre Style Transfer Model
suggestions.desc: Extend the architecture to support style transfer by training on both the MAESTRO dataset and jazz-specific MIDI libraries. Create a 'style knob' that allows users to morph a Bach-style melody into a Miles Davis-style performance using latent space interpolation.

suggestions.title: Web-Based Collaborative AI Composer
suggestions.desc: Deploy the model via a Flask/FastAPI backend and build a React-based frontend using Tone.js. This would allow multiple users to collaboratively edit MIDI grids that the AI 'auto-completes' based on their initial motifs.

title: AI Detection – Chat GPT
authors: Everett, Wylder; Mode, Daniel; Nguyen, Paul; Johnson, Alfred; Khan, Bilal
abstract: With the emergence of large language models (LLM) and Artificial Intelligence (AI) assistants like ChatGPT, accompanying tremendous potentials are critical challenges. Indeed, these assistant systems can provide quality information with conveniences. However, the generated contents are highly problematic being seemingly indistinguishable from that of human. The implication of this issue is severe in science, education, and domains that value original contents. With such motivation, this project addresses the task of identifying ChatGPT-synthesized texts with a focus on education, specifically, in short-answer questions. The goal of the project is to develop an AI technology that identifies synthesized texts by comparing such contents to examples known to be from AI for the same questions.

suggestions.title: Adversarial Evasion and Robustness Testing
suggestions.desc: Develop an 'adversarial humanizer' that attempts to modify AI-generated text to bypass the current detector. Use this to benchmark the detector’s robustness and identify specific linguistic patterns (like perplexity and burstiness) that are easiest to mask.

suggestions.title: Multilingual BERT Expansion
suggestions.desc: Extend the detector's capability beyond English by fine-tuning a Multilingual BERT or XLM-RoBERTa model on AI-generated vs. human-translated datasets in languages like Spanish, Chinese, or Arabic to address global academic integrity needs.

suggestions.title: Canvas/Moodle LMS Integration Plugin
suggestions.desc: Build a Chrome extension or LTI (Learning Tools Interoperability) plugin that integrates the detection model directly into Learning Management Systems. This would allow educators to see 'probability of AI generation' scores directly next to student submissions.

suggestions.title: AI-Generated Code Origin Detection
suggestions.desc: Pivot the detection logic to identify AI-generated source code (Python, Java, C++). Train the model on the StackOverflow vs. GitHub Copilot dataset to help recruiters and instructors verify the authenticity of technical assessments.

title: Exploring the Impact of Wavelength in Non-Invasive Blood Glucose Monitoring
authors: Oakley, John E; Kazi, Tahsin
abstract: Diabetes and metabolic diseases are some of the most crucial health issues of the 21st century. Monitoring blood glucose, the lead indicator of these diseases is a cumbersome process of constantly drawing blood or using subcutaneous needles. However, new technologies have emerged for non-invasive blood glucose monitoring that uses spectroscopy, which involves emitting light and capturing patient data with cameras. These new devices remove the cost of multiple tests, reduce the risk of skin conditions, and create more patient-friendly solutions. However, the hardware variables of these devices have not been tested thoroughly. One such avenue is via laser wavelength, which substantially affects device performance as different wavelengths interact with skin in varying ways. This study aims to investigate the impact of wavelength on the performance of the team's non-invasive device across different races, genders, and ages of people.

suggestions.title: Raspberry Pi Multispectral Data Collector
suggestions.desc: Build a hardware prototype using a Raspberry Pi and a NoIR camera with specific narrow-band optical filters (e.g., 940nm and 1050nm). This moves the research from existing datasets to active data acquisition from real subjects.

suggestions.title: Skin Tone and Melanin Noise Filtering
suggestions.desc: Research the impact of skin pigmentation on spectroscopy readings by creating a calibration algorithm. Use ML to identify 'noise' patterns caused by varying melanin levels to ensure the glucose monitoring remains accurate across diverse demographics.

suggestions.title: Smartphone-Based Hypoglycemia Alert System
suggestions.desc: Develop a mobile app that utilizes the phone’s high-speed camera and flash (as a broad-spectrum light source) to estimate glucose trends. Implement a 'Time-in-Range' dashboard that alerts users when their readings deviate from historical baselines.

suggestions.title: Predictive Metabolic Health Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Integrate the glucose readings with wearable data (Fitbit/Apple Health) using a Random Forest model. The end result would be an app that predicts how specific meals or activities will impact blood glucose levels before the user consumes them.

title: AI Limitations
authors: Hurtado Garcia, Maria; Ahmed, Syed S; Tran, Tuan T; Ramcharan, Michael A; Tuzova, Kristina
abstract: Our project, "AI Limitations," explores the application of AI, in our case ChatGPT, in creating an online auction website. It investigates the boundaries of ChatGPT's capabilities and highlights its potential, while recognizing limitations in generating precise guidance for completing complex tasks. The project combines research, programming, and documentation, providing valuable insights into AI's role in project development.

suggestions.title: LLM-Driven Automated Testing Suite
suggestions.desc: Use GPT-4's API to build a tool that automatically writes and executes Selenium or Playwright end-to-end tests for the auction site. This pushes the 'limitations' study by seeing if AI can effectively find bugs in its own generated code.

suggestions.title: Real-Time WebSocket Bidding Engine
suggestions.desc: Implement a live bidding feature using Socket.io or ActionCable, a complex task where LLMs often struggle with state management. Document the specific 'hallucinations' the AI has regarding concurrency and race conditions during development.

suggestions.title: AI vs. Human Performance Benchmark
suggestions.desc: Perform a structured study comparing the auction site's performance and security to one built by a human developer. Use tools like OWASP ZAP to see if the AI-guided development introduced common vulnerabilities like SQL injection or XSS.

suggestions.title: RAG-Based Documentation Assistant
suggestions.desc: Build a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system using LangChain that 'reads' the project's specific tech stack documentation. Use this to provide the AI with the precise context it was missing, measuring if this overcomes the identified 'complex task' limitations.

title: CCSE Reservation Application
authors: Kelley, Nicholas; Carreker, Tykira; Mutisya, Keith; Hyatt, Aaron
abstract: The CCSE department tasked us with creating an application to streamline the reservation of the rooms and equipment maintained by the college. To achieve this, we developed a front-end application created with PHP that allows users to view available resources, and request reservations for them. We created and maintained a back-end database configured with MySQL through phpMyAdmin to allow for the easy integration with the front end. Through the project we created new functions in the application with the user in mind, to create the best possible application to fit the needs of the CCSE department.

suggestions.title: Modernization with React and OIDC
suggestions.desc: Migrate the PHP front-end to a modern React SPA and implement OpenID Connect (OIDC) for single sign-on. This allows KSU students to log in using their official university credentials rather than a separate database-managed account.

suggestions.title: QR-Code Equipment Tracking System
suggestions.desc: Develop a mobile-first scanning module that generates unique QR codes for every piece of equipment. Users can scan a code with their phone to instantly check availability or log a return, updating the MySQL database in real-time.

suggestions.title: Multi-Tenant Departmental SaaS Pivot
suggestions.desc: Refactor the MySQL schema and PHP logic to support multi-tenancy. This would allow other departments (e.g., Biology, Art) to spin up their own 'Reservation Instance' with separate inventory and room lists within the same application.

suggestions.title: Automated Approval Workflow via Slack/Teams
suggestions.desc: Integrate the back-end with the Slack or Microsoft Teams API to send instant notifications to administrators when a reservation is requested. Administrators can then 'Approve' or 'Deny' requests directly from the chat interface via webhooks.

title: AI Limitations for Web Devlopment
authors: LaLicata, Jacob J; Garske, Josh; Rowlinson, Thomas; Madrigal, Matthew; Adewumi, Muyiwa E
abstract: This research project delves into the exploratory journey of using an AI (Artificial Intelligence), specifically ChatGPT, to assist in developing an auction website. Highlighting the iterative process of problem identification, solution finding, and implementation during development, this project aims to furnish insights into leveraging AI capabilities while addressing its limitations. Through this, developers and AI enthusiasts can gain a comprehensive understanding of effective collaboration with AI, addressing common pitfalls, and devising solutions during software development.

suggestions.title: Quantifying AI-Induced Technical Debt
suggestions.desc: Conduct a code-quality analysis using SonarQube on the AI-generated auction site. Measure specific metrics like cyclomatic complexity and code duplication to provide a quantitative assessment of the 'limitations' in AI-written architectural patterns.

suggestions.title: Visual UI/UX Auditing with GPT-4V
suggestions.desc: Integrate the GPT-4 Vision API to automatically audit the auction site's frontend. Use the AI to analyze screenshots for accessibility issues (WCAG compliance) and design inconsistencies that the text-based model might have missed during initial coding.

suggestions.title: Smart-Contract Integration for Escrow
suggestions.desc: Extend the auction site by implementing a blockchain-based escrow system using Solidity. This explores AI's ability to handle high-stakes security logic for financial transactions, adding a layer of trustless bidding to the project.

suggestions.title: AI-Optimized CI/CD Pipeline
suggestions.desc: Build a GitHub Actions pipeline that uses AI to automatically generate pull request summaries and suggest code fixes during the build process. This transforms the project from a simple website into a modern, AI-assisted DevOps ecosystem.

title: Eco-Insight
authors: Kumboji, Keerthan; Syamala, Hari Trinadh Reddy; Nalluri, Raghu; Thallapalli, Shashank; Khan, Ishtiaque
abstract: Eco-Insight is a pioneering application designed to bridge the gap between technology and environmental conservation. At its core, the application aims to empower users to contribute to the understanding and preservation of biodiversity and the ecosystem. Contribute allows users to capture and upload images of various aspects of the natural world, including everything from insects, birds, animals and fragile plant species. The uploaded data is securely stored in a robust database. Leveraging the power of geotagging and timestamping, Eco-Insight offers a user-friendly interface to enable seamless retrieval of specific data based on geographical regions and timelines, including detailed insights spanning years. Eco-Insight aspires to serve as a vital tool in the conservationist arsenal, fostering a deeper understanding of biodiversity dynamics and enabling proactive measures.

suggestions.title: On-Device Offline Species Classification
suggestions.desc: Convert the image recognition model to TensorFlow Lite and deploy it within a Flutter or React Native app. This allows citizen scientists to identify plants and insects in remote areas without an active internet connection.

suggestions.title: Invasive Species Heatmap Visualization
suggestions.desc: Use Leaflet.js and the existing database to build a geospatial dashboard. This tool would visualize 'hotspots' of specific species (like Kudzu or Spotted Lanternflies) over time, providing actionable data for local environmental agencies.

suggestions.title: Gamified 'Biodiversity Bingo' for Schools
suggestions.desc: Add a gamification layer where users earn badges and level up by documenting diverse species. Create a 'School Mode' where local K-12 classes can compete in bio-blitz challenges to see which group can document the most unique ecosystem features.

suggestions.title: GBIF Global Research Integration
suggestions.desc: Build an API bridge to the Global Biodiversity Information Facility (GBIF). This would allow validated user uploads from Eco-Insight to be shared with the global scientific community, turning a student project into a real-world data source for researchers.

title: Predicting Stock Prices Using Different Machine Learning and Deep Learning Models
authors: Crystal, Afnan; Jonnalagadda, Rohith Sundar; Chandel, Hrithik singh
abstract: Our project focuses on the challenge of predicting the daily closing prices and stock movements of Amazon, one of the world's largest and most dynamic corporations. Amazon's stock prices are known for their unpredictability and are influenced by a multitude of intricate factors. Our project aims to provide accurate and reliable forecasts for Amazon's stock prices, going beyond mere predictions. The analysis employs a comprehensive approach, comparing the performance of three distinct machine learning and deep learning models: Linear Regression, Support Vector Machine (SVM), and Multi-Layered Perceptron (MLP) for financial time series data. The dataset we used spans from January 2, 2005, to August 21, 2019, covering a substantial period of Amazon's stock history. Our project not only delivers precise predictions but also outlines the methodologies and techniques used for stock price forecasting.

suggestions.title: FinBERT-Powered Sentiment Analysis
suggestions.desc: Improve the prediction accuracy by scraping Amazon-related news headlines and Twitter feeds. Use the FinBERT model to calculate a 'Sentiment Score' and feed it as an additional feature into the LSTM model to account for market psychology.

suggestions.title: Explainable AI (XAI) for Price Moves
suggestions.desc: Implement SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) to visualize which factors (e.g., volume, opening price, sentiment) most influenced a specific day's prediction. This turns a 'black box' model into a transparent tool for financial analysis.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Paper Trading Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Build a web dashboard using Streamlit that pulls live data from the Alpaca Markets API. Allow users to place 'paper trades' based on the model's daily forecasts and track their virtual portfolio's performance against the actual S&P 500.

suggestions.title: Deep Reinforcement Learning for Portfolio Balancing
suggestions.desc: Move beyond price prediction by training a Deep Q-Network (DQN) to make 'Buy/Hold/Sell' decisions. The agent would optimize for long-term returns and risk management, creating an automated trading bot rather than just a forecasting tool.

title: Read-Talkback Assisted Platform for Aphasia
authors: Clayton, Michael j; Dixon, Maxwell P; Riggin, Navonte
abstract: Every year, there are lots of people who are affected with brain or other traumatic injuries that lead to some form of speech impairment. One of these conditions is Aphasia, which can cause a loss of word-finding or word- substitution, agrammatism, and apraxia. The team will be developing a Flutter app with a focus on Android systems that will track the user’s eye movements and read out the word that is being focused on. The goal of this app is to assist in improving the speech production and day-to-day activities of people with Aphasia or a similar disorder.
supervisor: Dr. Patrick Bobbie, Prof. Sharon Perry

suggestions.title: Predictive Contextual Vocabulary with GPT-4o
suggestions.desc: Integrate the OpenAI API to analyze the user's GPS location or time of day and dynamically prioritize the word bank (e.g., showing 'Coffee' or 'Breakfast' when at a cafe), reducing the eye-movement effort required for selection.

suggestions.title: Gaze-to-Intent Mapping via ML Kit
suggestions.desc: Enhance the eye-tracking logic by implementing a dwell-time algorithm using Google's ML Kit to distinguish between accidental glances and deliberate word selection, reducing the frequency of false-positive talkbacks.

suggestions.title: Cross-Platform Precision with ARKit Integration
suggestions.desc: Extend the Flutter project to iOS using Apple’s ARKit and the TrueDepth camera system to achieve sub-millimeter eye-tracking accuracy that outperforms standard Android camera-based tracking.

suggestions.title: Gamified Pronunciation Feedback Loop
suggestions.desc: Build a speech therapy module using the Flutter 'audioplayers' package that records the user’s vocal attempt after the talkback and provides visual feedback on phoneme accuracy compared to the target word.

title: Blockchain in Ethereum
authors: Lovell, Savvy; Priest, Joshua; Cooper, Cameron D
abstract: Earthereum is a cryptocurrency token built on the Ethereum blockchain utilizing an ERC-20 contract and implemented with Solidity in Remix and deploying using MetaMask. The mission of Earthereum is developing an ecologically-friendly utility for cryptocurrencies.
supervisor: Dr. Yong Shi, Prof. Sharon Perry

suggestions.title: Verified Carbon-Credit Burning Portal
suggestions.desc: Build a DApp interface where Earthereum holders can interact with the Toucan Protocol to 'burn' their tokens in exchange for verified carbon offsets, creating a direct link between the token and ecological impact.

suggestions.title: Proof-of-Action Rewards via Chainlink Oracles
suggestions.desc: Develop a system using Chainlink Oracles to mint or distribute Earthereum tokens to users who verify real-world green actions, such as logging EV charging sessions or scanning recycling receipts.

suggestions.title: Layer-2 Efficiency Migration to Polygon
suggestions.desc: Refactor the Solidity contracts for deployment on the Polygon network or Arbitrum to significantly reduce gas fees and the per-transaction energy footprint, aligning with the project's 'ecologically-friendly' mission.

suggestions.title: Decentralized Eco-Governance DAO
suggestions.desc: Implement OpenZeppelin Governor contracts to allow Earthereum holders to vote on which environmental non-profits receive the transaction tax proceeds accumulated by the smart contract.

title: Quantum Game Theory
authors: Thomassy, Christian; Curtis, Sean J; Lacey, Cody A
abstract: Quantum computing is a computing paradigm that utilizes the properties of quantum mechanics such as superposition, interface and entanglement for data processing and other tasks. Quantum computing can be used to work on the same problems existing supercomputers do but in a much more efficient manner. Classical game theory is a process of modeling that is widely used in AI applications. The extension of this theory to the quantum field is known as quantum game theory. It can be a promising tool for overcoming critical problems in quantum communication and the implementation of quantum artificial intelligence. Quantum game theory allows the player(s) to formulate strategies differing from the conventional way of playing a game. We review the performance of classical and quantum strategies on five classical games by analyzing the logic and outcomes of each approach.
supervisor: Dr. Yong Shi, Prof. Sharon Perry

suggestions.title: Quantum Nash Equilibrium Solver for Multi-Agent Systems
suggestions.desc: Build an extension that calculates Nash equilibria in quantum games where players use entangled strategies. Use the Qiskit framework to model 3+ player games (like the Quantum Prisoners' Dilemma) and visualize how entanglement allows for higher social optima than classical strategies.

suggestions.title: Quantum Reinforcement Learning for Strategic Environments
suggestions.desc: Integrate a Quantum Circuit Learning (QCL) layer into an OpenAI Gym environment. The agent should use a Variational Quantum Circuit (VQC) to learn optimal moves in a quantum version of a strategic game, comparing the learning convergence rates against classical Deep Q-Learning.

suggestions.title: Procedural Level Design via Quantum Walks
suggestions.desc: Implement a 'Quantum Walk' algorithm to generate non-linear, interconnected game maps. Use the probability distribution of a quantum walker on a graph to determine node placement and loot density, creating layouts that are structurally different from those generated by classical Perlin noise or random walks.

suggestions.title: Quantum-Classical Hybrid Cryptographic Game Engine
suggestions.desc: Develop a micro-game where the mechanics (e.g., fog of war or hidden roles) are enforced by quantum key distribution (QKD) principles. Build a prototype using a quantum simulator API to prove that player 'cheating' can be detected via wave-function collapse logic.

title: Quantum Machine Learning Applied to Cybersecurity
authors: Waugh, Adam M; Destito, Anna; Ragsdale, Joseph D
abstract: We propose the development of a system that uses the TensorFlow Quantum and PennyLane packages and applies quantum machine learning (QML) algorithms to process various security and malicious data sets and compares the performance with classical machine learning (CML) algorithms. One of the most important applications of QML is for cybersecurity. This project will begin with research of quantum computing and machine learning, then followed by the development of a system that uses the TensorFlow Quantum and PennyLane packages and applies quantum machine learning (QML) algorithms to process various security and malicious data sets and compares the performance with classical machine learning (CML) algorithms.The data sets for our modules include DDoS prevention, malware detection, user behavior anomaly detection, and spam email filtering. We provide detailed instructions for program implementation on our project website in order to better proliferate quantum programming in order to encourage others to explore quantum algorithms.
supervisor: Dr. Yong Shi, Prof. Sharon Perry

suggestions.title: Adversarial Quantum Attacks on QML Detectors
suggestions.desc: Research how to 'fool' the current TensorFlow Quantum model by crafting adversarial perturbations using PennyLane. Build a tool that generates slightly modified malicious packets designed to bypass a Quantum Neural Network while still being flagged by a classical CNN.

suggestions.title: Quantum Support Vector Machine (QSVM) for Encrypted Traffic
suggestions.desc: Apply the QSVM algorithm to the ISCX-VPN-nonVPN dataset to classify encrypted traffic types. Use quantum feature mapping to project high-dimensional network data into a Hilbert space, testing if quantum kernels can find separators that classical RBF kernels miss.

suggestions.title: Real-time Quantum Intrusion Detection System (Q-IDS)
suggestions.desc: Create a pipeline using Apache Kafka to stream live network logs into a pre-trained PennyLane quantum model. Optimize the circuit depth so the system can perform binary classification (benign vs. malicious) on live traffic with latency metrics suitable for a corporate SOC.

suggestions.title: Federated Quantum Machine Learning for Private Threat Intel
suggestions.desc: Implement a Federated Learning architecture where multiple 'organizations' train a shared Quantum Neural Network without sharing their raw malware samples. Use the Flower framework alongside TF-Quantum to demonstrate secure, privacy-preserving QML model updates.

title: Classifying Chest X-Rays of Pneumonia Using Transfer Learning
authors: Swann, Michael B; Clark, Noah
abstract: Convolutional neural networks are a powerful tool in machine vision when it comes to identifying patterns. Considering the importance of identifying patterns in medical imaging, there is a great opportunity to develop effective CNN models to analyze medical imagery. Furthermore, the introduction of transfer learning has introduced the opportunity to develop models which are more accurate and better generalize to other datasets. Therefore, we explore the efficacy of different CNN models with respect to a dataset regarding X-ray imaging for pneumonia. We do this through using transfer learning models and demonstrate that pretrained models outperform models which are trained only on the dataset available. We also highlight additional methods which could be expanded upon in future research and hope to develop a state-of-the-art model for this particular dataset in the final version of this paper.
supervisor: Dr. Md Abdullah Al Hafiz Khan

suggestions.title: Explainable AI (XAI) for Clinical Validation
suggestions.desc: Incorporate Grad-CAM or LIME to generate visual heatmaps over the X-ray images. This allows doctors to see which specific areas (e.g., lung opacities) the model is using to make a pneumonia diagnosis, improving trust and diagnostic transparency.

suggestions.title: Ensemble Transfer Learning with Vision Transformers
suggestions.desc: Move beyond standard CNNs by implementing a hybrid model that combines ResNet features with a Vision Transformer (ViT). Benchmark this ensemble against the original project's results to see if global self-attention improves detection of subtle bacterial pneumonia patterns.

suggestions.title: Cross-Domain Diagnostic Expansion via MedMNIST
suggestions.desc: Extend the transfer learning model to classify other pulmonary conditions like COVID-19 or Tuberculosis. Use the MedMNIST v2 dataset to fine-tune the existing pneumonia model, proving that the weights learned for pneumonia can accelerate the detection of other lung pathologies.

suggestions.title: Mobile-Edge Chest X-ray Triage System
suggestions.desc: Optimize the model using TensorFlow Lite or ONNX for deployment on an Android/iOS device. Build a mobile app that allows clinicians in low-resource settings to photograph a physical X-ray film and receive an immediate diagnostic likelihood score offline.

similar: legacy:UC-06|Spring 2020|chest x-ray disease detection w/ dl

title: Combatting Data Heterogeneity in Federated Learning
authors: Hutchison, Andrew J; Bull, Justin C; Cummings, Aaron
abstract: The growing concern in data privacy has led to new paradigms in Machine Learning primarily focused around keep data safe and secure. In our research project, we studied Federated Learning, specifically utilizing knowledge distillation and an autoencoder in an attempt to create a sustainable model that could be used in a field such as Heathcare. We propose a Federated Model using the Flower framework, trained on the MedMNIST2D dataset (Organ(A/C/S)MNIST), using Knowledge Distillation as a method of sharing the global model, and a Variational Autoencoder to deal with the problem of Data Heterogeneity that can arise on a distributed network. Our results on a cumulative model are tentative but hope to prove that the idea can be utilized in networks with varying sizes of edge device, usage, and types.?
supervisor: Prof. Sharon Perry, Dr. Xinyue Zhang

suggestions.title: Differential Privacy Integration for MedMNIST
suggestions.desc: Incorporate Differential Privacy (DP-SGD) into the Flower framework to prevent 'membership inference attacks' where a malicious actor could identify if a specific patient's data was used in the training set. Balance the privacy budget (epsilon) against the model's accuracy on OrganMNIST.

suggestions.title: Dynamic Weighting for Non-IID Data Heterogeneity
suggestions.desc: Develop a custom aggregation strategy in Flower (like FedProx or FedNova) to handle 'straggler' clients and data imbalances. Test the model's robustness by artificially skewing the MedMNIST labels across different simulated hospital nodes (clients).

suggestions.title: Multimodal Federated Learning for Healthcare
suggestions.desc: Expand the autoencoder architecture to accept multimodal inputs, such as combining MedMNIST images with structured CSV clinical data (age, symptoms). This creates a more holistic federated model that mirrors real-world hospital diagnostic workflows.

suggestions.title: Blockchain-Enabled Model Incentive Layer
suggestions.desc: Build a proof-of-concept using a private Ethereum/Hyperledger network to log model contributions from each federated node. Reward 'hospitals' with tokens based on the quality of their local training (evaluated via a validation set), creating a marketplace for healthcare AI collaboration.

title: BactiFind: A Novel CNN-based Framework to Classify Bacterial Species
authors: Ryan, Lowhorn; Zac, Cardwell
abstract: Abstract—Bacterial species identification is an essential step in diagnosing diseases caused by bacterial attacks. Effective prescription to cure these diseases depends on accurate bacterial species identification. Faster identification along with accuracy is essential because some bacteria grow fast in the human body. However, bacterial species classification using a laboratory environment through traditional approaches is time-consuming, and it depends on human expertise, which is not immune to human error. Well-trained and experienced microbiologists demonstrate the probability of preparing bacterial species identification reports with lower error rates. However, hiring experienced microbiologists is expensive. Convolutional Neural Network (CNN)-based automatic bacterial species classification system is a potential innovative alternative to traditional laboratory methods, which is faster and cheaper. However, there are multiple challenges associated with developing a CNN-based bacterial species classifier. This paper proposes an innovative CNN-based framework, BactiFind, to overcome the obstacles and automatically identify 33 different bacterial species and generate reports for physicians. It classifies bacterial species with 96.42% accuracy, 97.13% precision, 97.25% recall, and 3.58% error rate. The well-optimized network architecture, innovative bypass layer, and effective image augmentation method have facilitated this outstanding performance which has been further validated using state-of-the-art CNN evaluation methods. Index Terms—Bacterial species classification, Convolutional Neural Network, image augmentation, network optimization, bacterial image transformation.
supervisor: Dr. Md Abdullah Al Hafiz Khan, Dr. Yong Shi

suggestions.title: Bacterial Morphology GAN for Rare Species
suggestions.desc: Use Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) to synthesize high-fidelity microscopic images of rare bacterial species where data is scarce. Evaluate if training the CNN on a mix of real and synthetic images improves classification accuracy for 'orphan' species.

suggestions.title: Automated Bacterial Colony Counter Integration
suggestions.desc: Extend the CNN framework to include a YOLO-based object detection head. This allows the system to not only identify the species but also count individual bacterial colonies in a Petri dish image, automating a high-volume task for microbiology labs.

suggestions.title: BactiFind Mobile Microscopy Kit
suggestions.desc: Design a 3D-printable smartphone adapter for low-cost microscope lenses and integrate the CNN into a mobile app. The end result is a field-deployable tool for researchers to identify waterborne bacteria in rural areas without access to a full lab.

suggestions.title: Gram-Stain Prediction and Prescriptive Analysis
suggestions.desc: Modify the architecture to predict Gram-stain characteristics (Positive vs. Negative) as a secondary task. Use the output to cross-reference an Open-Source Antibiotic Database, suggesting potential effective medications based on the identified species' known resistance profiles.

title: Cybersecurity Analysis of Password Managers
authors: Harris, Marshall; Dees, Tyler; Eckelberry, Justin; Sprague, Thomas; Gutierrez, Jared
abstract: As the ubiquity of password managers has increased so too has the incentive for malicious actors to compromise them. This project’s purpose is to investigate password manager software for potential security vulnerabilities. The team chose, researched, and probed a popular password manager for weaknesses. Multiple attack vectors for cracking the security of the software were discovered. The most promising of these vectors were, one, the usage of malicious extensions for Internet browsers to circumvent the security of the password manager software which often interfaces with said browsers for user convenience, and two, the deployment of a malware disguised as an update for the password manager.

suggestions.title: Automated Browser Extension Fuzzer
suggestions.desc: Develop a Python-based fuzzing tool specifically targeting the IPC (Inter-Process Communication) channels of password manager extensions. Use it to automatically inject malformed messages into the extension to find memory leaks or unauthorized credential exposures.

suggestions.title: Zero-Knowledge Sync Implementation with Rust
suggestions.desc: Rewrite a core component of a password manager using a memory-safe language like Rust. Implement a secure end-to-end encryption protocol using XChaCha20-Poly1305, ensuring that the 'server' never sees the master password or the unencrypted vault.

suggestions.title: Biometric & FIDO2/WebAuthn Hardware Bridge
suggestions.desc: Build a wrapper that replaces master password prompts with FIDO2/WebAuthn hardware keys (like YubiKey). This mitigates the 'malicious extension' attack vector by requiring a physical touch-to-sign action that an extension cannot simulate.

suggestions.title: Credential Stuffing & Leaked DB Cross-Referencer
suggestions.desc: Integrate an API like 'Have I Been Pwned' to create a local auditing tool. The tool should scan the password manager's vault and flag entries that appear in recent public breaches, while also using K-Anonymity to keep the user's passwords private from the API.

title: Recipe For Disaster
authors: Wilson, Milo D; Fushimi, Aidan; Parham, Wade G; Chima, Kosi M
abstract: Sugar, Spice, and everything nice or naughty, using the ingredients available to you, you must battle your way out of the bizarre world of chefs and cooks as they attempt to flambe and tenderize you. Whisk, Boil, and Cook your way out of the endless kitchen to secure your way as a true MasterChef

suggestions.title: Physics-Based 'Culinary Combat' System
suggestions.desc: Enhance the combat by implementing a physics engine where ingredients have properties (weight, bounciness, heat). For example, whisking a liquid enemy creates a vortex that sucks in other mobs, or freezing an ingredient turns it into a high-damage projectile.

suggestions.title: Dynamic Recipe Crafting & Buff Engine
suggestions.desc: Develop a system where players collect ingredients from fallen enemies to cook 'mid-run' meals. Each ingredient combination should yield unique buffs (e.g., Spicy Pepper = Fire Dash, Garlic = Vampire Repellent) using a crafting matrix instead of fixed items.

suggestions.title: Procedural Kitchen Generation with Kitchenware Hazards
suggestions.desc: Create a procedural level generator that places 'industrial kitchen' traps (conveyor belts, deep fryers, steam vents). Use a tile-matching algorithm to ensure that levels remain navigable while increasing in complexity as the player descends into the 'Endless Kitchen'.

suggestions.title: Multi-Chef Chaos Co-op Mode
suggestions.desc: Implement a networked multiplayer mode using Mirror or Photon. Introduce 'Co-op Recipes' where players must combine different ingredient attacks simultaneously to defeat massive kitchen bosses (e.g., a giant sentient Blender).

title: Do you know KSU? -A Quiz App
authors: Meliezer, Christian; Kamau, Erick; Meder, Joshua W
abstract: Do you know KSU is a mobile quiz app developed in Android Studio by Christian Meliezer, Erick Kamau, and Joshua Meder. The app tests your knowledge on all things KSU related, including notable places, people, and courses associated with the university. Do you know KSU is a full stack application that uses AWS Amplify for API and Database services. Users will answer 10 random questions regarding the university, and be given a score at the end that represents how well they did.

suggestions.title: AR Campus Landmark Scavenger Hunt
suggestions.desc: Integrate Google’s ARCore to create a 'Scavenger Mode' where users must physically stand near KSU landmarks (like the Globe or the Stadium) to unlock high-point quiz questions.

suggestions.title: Real-time Leaderboard with AWS AppSync
suggestions.desc: Enhance the backend with AWS AppSync and GraphQL subscriptions to create a live, campus-wide leaderboard that updates instantly as other students complete their quizzes.

suggestions.title: Departmental White-Labeling CMS
suggestions.desc: Build a management dashboard that allows KSU department heads to upload their own JSON-based question sets, transforming the app into a platform for any college major (e.g., 'Do you know CS?').

suggestions.title: Spaced-Repetition Learning Module
suggestions.desc: Incorporate the Anki/SuperMemo algorithm to track user errors and reschedule missed questions at optimal intervals, evolving the app from a simple quiz into a long-term retention study tool.

title: Capstone Cybersecurity Website Hardening Group 2-01
authors: Lewis, Sean A; Greco, Blair C; Heard, Rontavious A; Moser, AJ
abstract: The project we were given by Professor Privitera is to secure a web server that is a simulation of a genuine business with our case being a restaurant known as Akwaaba. The business website is hosted on Apache, MariaDB, Red Hat Linux, and PHP. We will first need to explore the network we were given to determine the system’s weaknesses to evaluate the risks and create a proper security policies plan with the help of the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) standards. Following the security plan we created, our group will implement these changes into the network, hardening them to these standards. Finally, we will participate in a red/blue team cybersecurity ethical hacking procedure with our network and the two other teams. We will use white hat hacker skills to gain access to other groups’ networks while protecting our own network by patching up weaknesses if they have been breached.

suggestions.title: Infrastructure-as-Code Hardening Pipeline
suggestions.desc: Convert the manual hardening steps into automated Ansible playbooks or Terraform scripts. This allows the 'Akwaaba' server configuration to be version-controlled and redeployed instantly in a secure state, including automated firewall rules, PHP-FPM hardening, and MariaDB encryption-at-rest.

suggestions.title: Intrusion Detection and SIEM Integration
suggestions.desc: Deploy an ELK Stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana) or Wazuh agent on the RHEL server to monitor logs for real-time threats. Configure custom alerts for common restaurant-site attacks like SQL injection on the menu pages or brute-force attempts on the PHP administrative backend.

suggestions.title: DevSecOps Integration with SAST/DAST
suggestions.desc: Implement a CI/CD pipeline using GitHub Actions that performs Static Application Security Testing (SAST) with SonarQube and Dynamic Testing (DAST) with OWASP ZAP. This ensures that any new PHP code or menu updates added to the Akwaaba site are automatically scanned for vulnerabilities before going live.

suggestions.title: PCI-DSS Compliance and Payment Gateway Audit
suggestions.desc: Extend the project to include a mock online ordering system and perform a full gap analysis against PCI-DSS standards. Build a dashboard that visualizes the server's compliance status, specifically focusing on securing the transmission of payment data and segmenting the database to protect customer information.

title: IT4983 Capstone Project - Paddle for the Cure NYC
authors: Jimenez-Kosan, Alvaro; Bancroft, Jesse S; Castellon, Melina; Siddiqi, Rahim
abstract: In this project we took on the role of IT consultants for a non-profit organization called Paddle for the Cure NYC. This organization aims to provide support and events for breast cancer survivors and their families. We were tasked with researching innovative methods on how the organization’s digital platforms can be improved to attract more of their target audience. Our team focused on five main areas: Search Engine Optimization, Website Analysis, Social Media Exposure, and Host Website Software. We provided recommendations for improvement based on research and implementation strategies to ensure that it will be valuable and usable to the client.

suggestions.title: Paddle-Specific Donor Management and CRM Integration
suggestions.desc: Replace general website software suggestions with a custom-built donor management system using a platform like Salesforce NPSP or a MERN stack solution. Focus on tracking survivor participation and event-based fundraising metrics to provide the organization with actionable data beyond simple SEO.

suggestions.title: PWA for Event-Day Coordination and Survivor Support
suggestions.desc: Develop a Progressive Web App (PWA) specifically for 'Paddle for the Cure' events. Use Google Maps API for race tracking and Firebase for real-time communication between organizers and participants, solving the 'Social Media Exposure' gap with a dedicated community platform.

suggestions.title: AI-Driven Content Strategy and SEO Automation Pipeline
suggestions.desc: Build a tool that utilizes the OpenAI API to generate SEO-optimized blog posts and social media captions specifically tailored to breast cancer advocacy.
