Integrate this with a Google Search Console dashboard to automate the 'Website Analysis' phase and provide monthly performance reports.

suggestions.title: Accessibility Compliance Audit for Non-Profit Digital Inclusion
suggestions.desc: Perform a deep technical audit using WCAG 2.1 standards to ensure the digital platforms are accessible to survivors with various physical needs. Use tools like Axe DevTools and implement ARIA labels and keyboard navigation to maximize the reach of their target audience.

title: Pet Planner
authors: Graddy, Sarah; Palmer, Jessica; Kobus, Kelly
abstract: Do you run a dog kennel and have trouble keeping track of Fido and Fluffy's appointments, feeding schedule, and medications? To solve this issue, our team is using Dart and Flutter to build a mobile application called Pet Planner that provides a simple, visual way to organize all the details needed to take care of every client’s special pet. Pet Planner’s main homepage makes any task easy to access. By clicking one button, users can create new appointments, clients, pets and feeding schedules. The homepage also displays a multi-view calendar that updates with each added or deleted appointment. Users can access existing pets and clients as well. Pet Planner will be available on both Android and iOS devices.

suggestions.title: IoT-Integrated Smart Kennel Monitoring
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Flutter app with ESP32-based hardware sensors to monitor kennel temperature or water levels. Use MQTT protocols to send real-time alerts to the Pet Planner app, allowing kennel owners to monitor 'Fido and Fluffy' beyond just manual feeding schedules.

suggestions.title: Multi-Tenant SaaS for Commercial Boarding Businesses
suggestions.desc: Transform the single-user app into a multi-tenant SaaS platform using Firebase Authentication and Firestore rules. Add a billing and invoicing module using the Stripe API, allowing small kennel businesses to manage professional payments alongside pet appointments.

suggestions.title: Veterinary Telehealth and Digital Medical Records
suggestions.desc: Expand the medication module into a full digital medical record (DMR) system that allows pet owners to upload PDF records. Add a WebRTC-based video calling feature to enable remote consultations between the kennel staff and local veterinarians.

suggestions.title: ML-Based Diet and Nutrition Optimizer
suggestions.desc: Incorporate a Machine Learning model using TensorFlow Lite to suggest optimized feeding schedules and caloric intake based on the pet's breed, age, and activity level. Train the model on publicly available pet nutrition datasets to provide data-driven care recommendations.

similar: legacy:UC-026|Spring 2021|pose extraction for real-time workout assist_capstone group_w01_spring semester

title: StewardShip
authors: Williams, Krystale A; Merrill, Jaeleon K; Jansen, Christian Thomas; Kroleski, Castle L
abstract: Steward•Ship is a virtual reality survival game set deep underground on an alien world. The narrative centers around the titular “stewards,” crewmembers on a mining vessel called the AV-Undaunted. These stewards are awoken from cryogenic stasis by the ship after a catastrophic failure causes the spacecraft to crash in a subterranean cave, sealing the vessel and its inhabitants inside. Over the course of the game, the player takes on the role of four individual stewards, each awoken decades or centuries after the previous steward, to continue the work of escaping the alien world they’re stranded on as they slowly uncover the mystery of how they came to be stuck there.

suggestions.title: Procedural Cave Generation and Voxel-Based Mining
suggestions.desc: Replace static subterranean environments with a procedural generation system using Unity's C# Scripting. Implement a voxel-based terrain system (similar to Marching Cubes) to allow players to physically mine and reshape the environment for survival.

suggestions.title: Asynchronous Multiplayer 'Legacy' Mechanics
suggestions.desc: Develop a backend using Mirror or Photon where actions taken by one 'steward' (player) in their timeline affect the cave state for the steward awoken decades later. Implement a persistent world-state database to track changes like sealed doors or depleted resources across centuries.

suggestions.title: Biometric Survival Feedback Integration
suggestions.desc: Integrate the VR experience with wearable heart-rate monitors (via Bluetooth LE). Use the player’s real-world stress levels to influence in-game survival mechanics, such as oxygen consumption rates or visual hallucinations in the dark subterranean setting.

suggestions.title: Commercial Haptic Feedback and Physical Interaction System
suggestions.desc: Enhance immersion by implementing the bHaptics SDK or similar haptic vest integration. Program specific feedback patterns for 'cryogenic awakening' and industrial mining equipment, creating a high-fidelity 'survival' feel aimed at the VR arcade market.

title: Concrete formwork simulator
authors: Fushimi, Aidan; Autry, Johnathon R; Miller, Mikail S; Buell, Katelyn
abstract: Concrete Formwork Simulator is a simulation game meant to help construction students practice their skills. The goal of the game is to successfully build concrete formwork with the least amount of wasted materials. Players are scored out of 400 on each of the individual stages: shop, cut, and assemble. Players choose between building a formwork for a column, a slab, and a wall. Each formwork has its own set of materials for the players to buy, cut, and assemble. The player will need to take the dimensions of the formwork into account and then calculate the minimum number of each piece of lumber they will need to buy. Then, while considering minimizing waste, they need to calculate if any pieces need to be cut, and how much to cut them. Finally, the Player moves to the assembly where they are instructed to build the formwork of their choice. They must follow the correct order of assembly and build the sections of the formwork in the correct order to receive full points for the assembly stage. If they have done everything correctly, they will receive a perfect score of 1200 for that formwork.

suggestions.title: Genetic Algorithm for Bin-Packing Material Optimization
suggestions.desc: Implement a bin-packing algorithm to provide players with an 'Optimal Cut' baseline. Compare player material waste against the algorithmically generated best possible yield, providing a more rigorous technical scoring system for the 'cut' stage.

suggestions.title: AR Overlay for Real-World Formwork Training
suggestions.desc: Port the simulation to AR (using ARCore or ARKit) so construction students can visualize digital formwork structures at a 1:1 scale in a real-world shop. This allows students to practice 'assembling' components in their physical environment without wasting actual timber.

suggestions.title: Multiplayer Collaborative Site Management
suggestions.desc: Expand the single-player simulation into a co-op experience where players must coordinate roles (one buyer, one cutter, one assembler). Use a dedicated server to sync physics and material locations, simulating the communication challenges of a real construction site.

suggestions.title: Structural Integrity Stress-Test Simulation
suggestions.desc: Add a physics-based 'Pour' stage where players pour liquid concrete into their finished formwork. Use Unity's physics engine to calculate if the formwork fails under the pressure, giving students immediate feedback on the structural quality of their assembly.

title: Chiba
authors: Fushimi, Aidan; Autry, Johnathon R; Miller, Mikail S; Buell, Katelyn
abstract: The core mechanic present in Chiba is pushing around food items to plate tiles. Each time the player pushes a food item it incurs a cut. With enough cuts the food item will be cut into pieces, progressing to the next cut "stage" - with a max stage of cuts available for the different pieces of food. This is fundamentally built upon the core gameplay mechanic in Sokoban. Additionally, extending upon this further by incorporating new mechanics such as grilling food items and utilizing slick and sticky tiles. Together, these mechanics allow for a unique series of puzzles to test the players' spatial reasoning. Furthermore, players are disallowed from pushing any one piece of food too many times because the food crumbles and breaks after a certain point. This causes them to be forced to restart the level. Players need to balance both navigation and finding the shortest possible paths in order to push the food to the plates to complete the level. If players complete a level in under a certain number of moves, they can earn a star for that level. Players can earn up to three stars per level if they make the minimum number of pushes possible. As the levels progress, more mechanics are added to give players more ways to solve levels. For example, meatballs were added that rolled until they hit something instead of moving a single tile. With additional mechanics like rolling, players are introduced to more complicated levels that allow them to consider more solutions, while also restricting other solutions.

suggestions.title: Chemical Reaction Mechanics (Grill & Grease)
suggestions.desc: Expand the 'slick and sticky' tiles into a full state machine. If a food item is grilled on a 'slick' oil tile, it should ignite or move twice as far. If it hits 'sticky' flour, it becomes breaded. This adds a layer of depth where the player must 'prep' tiles before moving food.

suggestions.title: Level Editor with Steam Workshop Integration
suggestions.desc: Create an in-game level editor that allows players to place food, grills, and tile types. Use a serialization system (JSON/XML) to let users share their 'Chef Challenges' online, and implement a validation check to ensure a level is solvable before it can be uploaded.

suggestions.title: Optimal Solution Solver AI
suggestions.desc: Develop an AI using the A* search algorithm or Breadth-First Search (BFS) that can solve any given Chiba level. The solver should account for 'cut stages' and tile properties, providing players with a 'Perfect Chef' hint system that shows the shortest path to plating.

suggestions.title: Chiba Mobile: Haptic Feedback & Touch Controls
suggestions.desc: Port the game to mobile, focusing on gesture-based 'flicking' to push food. Implement haptic feedback that feels different for 'slick' vs 'sticky' tiles and use high-resolution 2D assets optimized for OLED screens to make the food items look more appetizing.

title: Cybersecurity Analysis of Password Managers
authors: Massengale, Lita M; Vazquez, Ruben; Shirilla, Kathryn; Fox, Caleb
abstract: Our capstone project objective is to analyze the cybersecurity framework of popular password managers. The purpose of this project is to research vulnerabilities of the selected password managers and explore possible exploit opportunities for each of them. We chose to analyze five of the most popular password managers on the market: 1Password, BitWarden, LastPass, Google Chrome, and Firefox. We researched their known vulnerabilities and determined we would focus on Bitwarden’s PIN feature, Google Chrome, and Firefox. Upon further research, we were unable to successfully brute force Bitwarden or its PIN feature. If the PIN is enabled, it can be attempted five times before locking the user out and requiring the full master password to be entered, at which point the PIN feature would need to be enabled again. We then shifted focus to Google Chrome and were able to successfully decrypt the local Login Data file that contains user passwords using a password recovery tool named ChromePass. We attempted to use a similar password recovery tool to decrypt the local passwords file for Firefox, however it was unsuccessful due to the tool being flagged as a threat by Microsoft Defender antivirus. Overall, we would consider our capstone a success, seeing as we were able to exploit Google Chrome.

suggestions.title: Automated Fuzzing Tool for Browser Password Extensions
suggestions.desc: Build a Python-based fuzzing framework to automate the discovery of vulnerabilities in browser-based password managers. Focus on the DOM injection points and autofill triggers in Chrome and Firefox that the original study identified.

suggestions.title: Analysis of Zero-Knowledge Proofs in Bitwarden
suggestions.desc: Conduct a deep dive into the cryptographic implementation of Bitwarden’s zero-knowledge architecture. Use tools like Wireshark to intercept traffic and verify that no sensitive data (like the Master Password or PIN) is transmitted or stored in a decryptable format.

suggestions.title: Hardware Security Key (FIDO2) Proxy for Managers
suggestions.desc: Develop a middleware application that adds a physical 2FA layer (using YubiKey) specifically for the browser-based password managers tested. This would mitigate the risk of PIN-based exploits by requiring a physical challenge-response for every autofill event.

suggestions.title: Vulnerability Scoring Framework for Personal Credential Vaults
suggestions.desc: Create a standardized scoring rubric based on the project's findings to rank password managers beyond just 'popularity.' Build a web dashboard where users can input their security priorities (e.g., 'offline access' vs 'ease of use') to receive a security-weighted recommendation.

title: WiseWallet
authors: Patel, Sagar; Berdyna, Shpetim; Nuqui, Ethan R; Curca, Sebastian
abstract: WiseWallet is designed to help users manage their finances in a simple and intuitive way. The app provides an easy-to-use interface for tracking income, expenses, and savings, allowing users to set goals and monitor their progress towards achieving them. The app also features customizable categories and budgets, enabling users to tailor their budgeting approach to their individual needs and preferences. In addition, the app provides visual aids such as charts and graphs to help users better understand their spending patterns and make informed financial decisions. With its user-friendly interface and powerful features, the Flutter mobile app for budgeting is an ideal tool for anyone looking to take control of their finances and achieve their financial goals. WiseWallet is a mobile application designed for both iOS and Android platforms, utilizing Flutter and Dart technologies for its development. The app integrates Firebase as a back-end service to manage the flow of data to and from the app. Additionally, Plaid, a third-party financial technology provider, is incorporated into the app to enable users to connect their bank accounts with WiseWallet.

suggestions.title: AI-Powered Receipt Scanning via OCR
suggestions.desc: Integrate Tesseract or Google Vision API to allow users to photograph paper receipts. Build a parser that automatically extracts the total amount, tax, and merchant category, populating the WiseWallet expense logs without manual data entry.

suggestions.title: Predictive Cash Flow & 'What-If' Simulator
suggestions.desc: Develop a forecasting engine using a Time-Series model (like Prophet). Based on historical data, the app should predict when a user's balance will hit zero and allow them to simulate 'what-if' scenarios, such as 'Can I afford a $500 vacation next month?'

suggestions.title: Open Banking Integration with Plaid
suggestions.desc: Use the Plaid API to transition WiseWallet from a manual tracker to an automated personal finance manager. Securely link real bank accounts and credit cards to fetch transactions in real-time, focusing on a secure OAuth flow for user authentication.

suggestions.title: Financial Literacy Gamification & Quest System
suggestions.desc: Turn budgeting into a game where users earn 'Financial Health Points' for staying under budget or meeting savings goals. Implement a 'World Boss' mechanic where users must collectively save money to 'defeat' inflation, providing educational tooltips on compound interest along the way.

title: Virtual Reality Robots
authors: Bryenton, Ian C; Giordano, Vincent P; Larumbe, George L; sofy, kazhan M
abstract: The UXA-90 Robots, that were in the possession of KSU since 2016, were first brought up to date by the previous group. Through their creation of a REST API, they were able to issue commands and increase the accessibility of the robots. The robots would once again be revived as our team opted to expand on their capabilities.?? Using the Unity Engine with C#, a Meta Quest 2 Virtual Reality (VR) device, and a Raspberry Pi, our team was able to both see and act through the robot. By default, the robot can: move, walk, see (through a webcam), hear and speak (using built-in speakers and microphones). Our team has enabled the user to more precisely control the robots through their own actions. Specifically, the user can control the robot's movement using the VR controllers, tracks the user's head movement, and streams camera output to the VR headset. ?This is done through an application our team has developed for the VR headset. Our goal with this project is to further develop the accessibility and capabilities of the UXA-90 robot.

suggestions.title: Direct Hand-Tracking and Gesture Control Interface
suggestions.desc: Eliminate the need for Quest 2 controllers by implementing Meta's Hand Tracking SDK. Map specific hand gestures to robot movements (e.g., a 'pinch' to close the robot's gripper), creating a more natural and immersive 'act through the robot' experience.

suggestions.title: Digital Twin Synchronization and Latency Compensation
suggestions.desc: Build a high-fidelity 'Digital Twin' of the UXA-90 in Unity that mirrors the physical robot's joint states in real-time. Research and implement a predictive lag-compensation algorithm to ensure the VR user doesn't experience motion sickness during network-delayed teleoperation.

suggestions.title: Autonomous SLAM Mapping with Raspberry Pi Cameras
suggestions.desc: Use the Raspberry Pi to run a SLAM (Simultaneous Localization and Mapping) algorithm like RTAB-Map. Allow the VR user to see a 3D point-cloud reconstruction of the robot's environment, enabling navigation through spaces that the robot's default cameras cannot fully capture.

suggestions.title: Remote Inspection and Hazardous Environment Toolkit
suggestions.desc: Transform the system into a professional inspection tool by adding thermal imaging or gas sensor payloads to the Raspberry Pi. Develop a UI overlay in the Quest 2 headset that displays these environmental readings alongside the live video feed.

title: Step Up
authors: Hardage, Liam K; Iqbal, Sami; Guerrero Bengoa, Emely P; Okwuosa, zachary
abstract: Step Up is a website designed to let users enter their information and calculate a step count to aid them in losing weight. Each user creates an account to store their data. This information will include necessary variables to calculate their step count using their target weight loss. The step count provided by Step Up considers the number of steps a user between the ages of 19 to 40 must make over three months to reach their goal. Step Up allows users to receive email reminders to keep their information up to date. Administrators of Step Up have the option to download anonymous user data for further research into the method behind Step Up's calculations.

suggestions.title: Wearable Integration for Real-Time Step Validation
suggestions.desc: Connect the web platform to the Google Fit and Apple HealthKit APIs. Instead of manual entry, automatically pull daily step counts from the user's smartphone or smartwatch to provide real-time progress updates toward their three-month goal.

suggestions.title: Gamified Social Leagues and Accountability Groups
suggestions.desc: Develop a community feature where users can join 'Step Leagues' based on their weight loss goals. Use a WebSocket-based leaderboard to show real-time rankings and implement a 'Nudge' system where users can send motivational reminders to one another.

suggestions.title: Personalized Nutrition and Step-to-Calorie Converter
suggestions.desc: Integrate a nutrition API (like Nutritionix) to allow users to log their meals. Build a dynamic calculator that adjusts the daily 'Step Up' goal based on the user's caloric intake, creating a more comprehensive weight loss plan than steps alone.

suggestions.title: Weight Loss Trajectory Analysis using Regression Modeling
suggestions.desc: Implement a data science module that uses linear regression to predict a user's weight loss trajectory over the 90-day period. Provide a 'What-If' analysis tool where users can see how increasing their daily steps by 10% would shift their target achievement date.

title: TerraSim
authors: Alquisiras, Anna; Hendrick, JaDante; Espinosa, Selene; Dunkley, Jake
abstract: This is an AI project utilizing FNN and OpenGL to simulate a more complex and practical version of John Conway's Game of Life, incorporating real world data for increased accuracy and relevance

suggestions.title: GPU-Accelerated Urban Microclimate Modeling
suggestions.desc: Port the OpenGL visualization to use Compute Shaders for massive parallelization of the FNN, allowing for a 1:1 scale simulation of urban heat islands using OpenStreetMap and NOAA weather data.

suggestions.title: Evolutionary Optimization for Disaster Mitigation
suggestions.desc: Implement a Genetic Algorithm to evolve cellular automata rules that mimic the spread of wildfires or floods, using the results to train the FNN for predicting 'worst-case' infrastructure failure points.

suggestions.title: Bio-Inspired Circuit Routing Simulator
suggestions.desc: Apply the TerraSim engine to VLSI design by treating signal paths as biological entities competing for space, using real-world PCB constraints as the 'complex environment' for the simulation.

suggestions.title: Dynamic Supply Chain Resiliency Visualizer
suggestions.desc: Transform the grid-based logic into a logistics simulator that models the global flow of goods, using real-world shipping port data to identify which 'cells' (nodes) create systemic risk during labor or climate disruptions.

title: Litter Scramble: Encouraging conservation through play
authors: Hinson, Andrew C; Ogden, Tony; Luong, Don; Brown, Harrison G
abstract: Litter Scramble is a quick-play video gaming experience that delivers a nostalgic low-res combination of 2d assets in a 3d environment to produce an informative and entertaining session that will leave the player better informed on the impact that pollution and littering have on the natural world around us. Featuring a cast of simply animated 2d animals appropriate for a Georgia state park, the player will control a ranger through two different levels, each reflecting a different park environment, in a race against time and the animals to capture as much litter as possible. The development team was eager to turn hardware limitations presented by the required operating device and display system into advantages of our game, which we have done successfully. The gaming experience features simple and intuitive controls, sound effects, background music, and the chance at placing in the top ten scores.

suggestions.title: AR 'Clean-Up' Companion App
suggestions.desc: Extend the project into the physical world using ARCore or ARKit. Users can 'scan' litter in actual parks to see the 2D animal characters from the game react to the pollution in real-time on their phones, bridging the gap between virtual play and physical conservation.

suggestions.title: Advanced Physics-Based Debris Collection
suggestions.desc: Replace simple 2D collision detection with a more complex physics system using Unity's Obi Cloth or NVIDIA PhysX. This would allow the player to interact with 'tangled' litter or use tools like nets and grabbers that behave realistically, adding a layer of mechanical depth to the gameplay.

suggestions.title: Eco-System Impact AI Simulation
suggestions.desc: Implement a back-end system that tracks the 'health' of the animal NPCs based on the player's performance. Use a simple fuzzy logic system to change animal behavior—making them appear lethargic or scarce if litter levels are high—providing a direct feedback loop for the educational content.

suggestions.title: Global Parks Leaderboard and Crowdsourced Data
suggestions.desc: Integrate a Firebase backend to track litter types collected in-game and correlate them with real-world pollution data from the EPA. Create a 'Global Ranger Network' feature where players' in-game actions contribute to a real-world leaderboard of most-saved park environments.

title: PantryPilot
authors: Syed, Zarek F; Breedlove, April; Dong, Thuy; Bhadra, Sanjay
abstract: Our team has developed an intuitive food pantry management system that streamlines the guest check-in process, optimizes time management, and enhances data reporting. The app is built with Next.js, TypeScript, and Firebase to create a platform-independent progressive web application. PantryPilot functions on both mobile devices and desktop devices.

suggestions.title: Predictive Inventory Demand Forecasting
suggestions.desc: Integrate a machine learning model (using TensorFlow.js) to analyze historical guest check-in data. The system can then predict which food items will be in highest demand for the upcoming week, allowing pantry managers to optimize their procurement and reduce spoilage.

suggestions.title: Smart Recipe Matcher for Guests
suggestions.desc: Develop a guest-facing module that takes the items currently in their cart and suggests healthy recipes based on those ingredients. Use the Spoonacular API to provide nutritional information, helping guests make the most of the resources provided by the pantry.

suggestions.title: Barcode Scanning and Batch Ingestion Engine
suggestions.desc: Build a native-like scanning feature using the QuaggaJS library to allow volunteers to scan UPC barcodes for instant inventory entry. Connect this to the Open Food Facts API to automatically populate nutritional and category data for new items.

suggestions.title: Offline-First Sync with Service Workers
suggestions.desc: Enhance the PWA capabilities by implementing a robust Redux-persist or IndexedDB caching strategy. This ensures that pantry workers in areas with poor connectivity can still perform check-ins and update inventory, with automatic synchronization once they return to a Wi-Fi zone.

title: Health-E-Patient (Web Application)
authors: Mistry, Kishan; Tenson, Blesson Amram; Leach, Faith N; Patel, Jiten
abstract: Health-E-Patient is a web application that allows communication between hospitals, doctors and their patients. The product aims to provide a secure way of informing patients about appointments and medications while also allowing doctors to keep track of their patients. This application will also make it easier to communicate with your doctors and other care providers. We used Next.JS, a React-based web development framework with server-side rendering. We used MaterialUI for user interface and Firebase for Authentication and Database.

suggestions.title: WebRTC-Powered Telehealth Integration
suggestions.desc: Incorporate secure, peer-to-peer video conferencing directly within the Next.js app using WebRTC or the Daily.co API. This would transform the communication tool from a messaging system into a full-service virtual clinic platform.

suggestions.title: Smart Medication Adherence Tracking with IoT
suggestions.desc: Develop an API bridge to connect with smart pill dispensers or wearable devices. Use the data to create a dashboard for doctors that flags patients who are missing dosages, enabling proactive intervention rather than reactive care.

suggestions.title: FHIR/HL7 Data Interoperability Layer
suggestions.desc: Implement a data translation layer that allows Health-E-Patient to export and import records in the FHIR (Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources) standard. This would allow the app to securely communicate with major hospital EMR systems like Epic or Cerner.

suggestions.title: AI-Driven Appointment Triage Chatbot
suggestions.desc: Integrate an NLP-based chatbot (using LangChain or OpenAI) to handle initial patient inquiries. The bot can categorize the urgency of a patient's symptoms and automatically suggest either an immediate emergency visit or a standard appointment slot based on the doctor's availability.

title: Quantum Computing in Cybersecurity Related Data Analysis : A Comparative Analysis of Classical and Quantum Machine Learning Models on Various Security Threats
authors: Lowhorn, Ryan; Cardwell, Zac
abstract: Financial fraud has become an increasingly prevalent and sophisticated issue in the modern world, causing significant losses for individuals, businesses, and economies alike. The exponential growth of digital transactions has only accelerated the need for effective and efficient fraud detection systems to safeguard these financial ecosystems. Traditional machine learning techniques, such as Neural Networks (NNs), K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), and Support Vector Machines (SVM), have played a crucial role in mitigating the impact of fraud by employing advanced algorithms to identify and prevent fraudulent transactions. With the recent advent of quantum computing and its potential to revolutionize the field of machine learning, there has been growing interest in exploring quantum algorithms for financial fraud detection. Quantum machine learning models are believed to offer significant computational advantages over their classical counterparts, which may lead to improved fraud detection capabilities. This Project aims to provide a comprehensive comparison of classical machine learning models, specifically NNs, KNN, and SVM, with their quantum counterparts in the context of credit card fraud detection. We employ the widely-used Kaggle Credit Card Fraud Dataset, which contains a diverse set of anonymized transactional data labeled as genuine or fraudulent. Our comparative analysis focuses on assessing the performance of each model in terms of accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, and computational efficiency. In addition, we also introduce the BPF-extended tracking honeypot (BETH) dataset as the first cybersecurity dataset for uncertainty and robustness benchmarking. We examine the potential application of quantum machine learning models for IoT device security using the BETH dataset, which contains modern host activity and attacks collected from cloud services. Our results highlight the potential benefits of quantum machine learning models for financial fraud detection and IoT device security.

suggestions.title: Quantum Kernels for Support Vector Machines
suggestions.desc: Replace standard RBF kernels with Quantum Enhanced Kernels using Qiskit. Research how mapping financial transaction data into high-dimensional Hilbert space improves the separation between fraudulent and legitimate transactions compared to classical SVMs.

suggestions.title: Hybrid Quantum-Classical Neural Network Pipeline
suggestions.desc: Build a pipeline where a classical CNN/RNN handles feature extraction from raw transaction logs, while a Quantum Variational Circuit (VQC) performs the final classification. This addresses the current qubit limitation while still leveraging quantum advantages in pattern recognition.

suggestions.title: Quantum-Resistant Cryptographic Logging
suggestions.desc: Implement a post-quantum cryptographic (PQC) layer for the data analysis logs using algorithms like CRYSTALS-Dilithium. This ensures that the security logs themselves are protected against future quantum attacks, a critical requirement for financial institutions.

suggestions.title: Adversarial Robustness Testing in Quantum Space
suggestions.desc: Investigate how 'quantum noise' or adversarial perturbations affect the accuracy of fraud detection. Develop a benchmark comparing how classical and quantum models react to sophisticated fraudulent attempts to 'camouflage' transactions.

title: Reinforcement Learning based Offloading Scheme Computation to Optimize Latency-Energy in Collaborative Cloud Networks
authors: Mhatre, Jui
abstract: Growing technologies like virtualization and artificial intelligence have become more popular on mobile devices. But lack of resources faced for processing these applications is still a major hurdle. Collaborative edge and cloud computing are one of the solutions to this problem. Remote servers have enough resources to support computation-heavy tasks and compute the results faster. But transmission time and energy are involved while offloading the computation to remote servers such as cloud and edge devices. There is a need to find an optimal offloading ratio for cloud as well as edge servers such that entire computation on remote as well as local can be achieved minimum energy consumption as well as minimum delay. We have proposed a multi-period deep deterministic policy gradient (MP-DDPG) algorithm to find an optimal offloading policy by partitioning the task and offloading it to the collaborative cloud and edge network to reduce energy consumption. Our results show that MP-DDPG achieves the minimum latency and energy consumption in the collaborative cloud network. We have compared our results with the existing DDPG-based approach and achieved about 65% speedup in terms of latency. Also, we observed energy consumption reduces with an increase in the number of edge servers.

suggestions.title: Privacy-Preserving Federated Offloading
suggestions.desc: Integrate a Federated Learning framework (like Flower) to allow mobile devices to collectively train the RL offloading model without sharing raw task data or user behavior patterns with the cloud.

suggestions.title: Edge-Driven AR Frame Processing Pipeline
suggestions.desc: Develop a prototype for mobile Augmented Reality that uses the RL scheme to decide which visual odometry or object detection frames should be processed locally vs. on an edge server to maintain 60FPS.

suggestions.title: Multi-Agent RL for Collaborative Device Mesh
suggestions.desc: Extend the model to a Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL) environment where 'idle' smartphones in a local mesh network can trade processing power for tokens, optimizing latency across the entire cluster.

suggestions.title: Energy-Adaptive Mobile Gaming SDK
suggestions.desc: Build a Unity/Unreal plugin that uses this RL scheme to offload heavy physics or AI pathfinding to a remote server specifically when the device battery drops below a certain threshold.

similar: legacy:UR-031|Spring 2021|an empirical study of thermal attacks on edge platforms

title: Early Heart Disease Detection Using Mel-Spectrograms and Deep Learning
authors: Donkada, Sricharan
abstract: Heart diseases are the leading cause of mortality worldwide, emphasizing the need for early detection and intervention. Traditional heart sound analysis using a stethoscope is subjective and prone to variability, necessitating a more objective and reliable approach. In this study, we present a deep learning model designed for heart sound analysis to enable the early detection of heart diseases. The model's architecture combines convolutional and fully connected layers with max-pooling and dropout operations, effectively capturing intricate patterns in heart sounds. We trained and validated our model on the Physionet 2016 challenge dataset, consisting of 3240 labeled heart sound recordings. Our deep learning model achieved an accuracy of 91.9%, surpassing the current state-of-the-art performance of 89.7%. This result demonstrates the model's potential to significantly reduce diagnostic errors and facilitate timely interventions, ultimately improving patient outcomes and reducing healthcare costs.

suggestions.title: Temporal-Spatial Heart Sound Transformers
suggestions.desc: Replace the CNN architecture with a Vision Transformer (ViT) to better capture long-range dependencies in the Mel-spectrograms, focusing on rhythmic irregularities that traditional layers might miss.

suggestions.title: Smart-Stethoscope Edge Deployment
suggestions.desc: Quantize the model for deployment on an ESP32 or Raspberry Pi connected to a cheap piezo-electric transducer, creating a low-cost diagnostic tool for rural clinics without internet access.

suggestions.title: Cross-Modality Transfer Learning with Speech Data
suggestions.desc: Pre-train the deep learning model on large-scale human speech datasets (like LibriSpeech) before fine-tuning on heart sounds to determine if acoustic feature extraction generalizes across biological signals.

suggestions.title: Real-time PCG/ECG Fusion Engine
suggestions.desc: Expand the input to a dual-stream model that processes both Phonocardiograms (PCG) and Electrocardiograms (ECG) simultaneously, using attention layers to weight which signal is more predictive of specific arrhythmias.

similar: legacy:UC-06|Spring 2020|chest x-ray disease detection w/ dl

title: Comparative Evaluation of EMBED Dataset for Mammogram Classification Using Deep Learning Techniques
authors: Vineela, Nalla
abstract: Breast cancer is a global health concern for women. The detection of breast cancer in its early stages is crucial, and screening mammography serves as a vital leading-edge tool for achieving this goal. In this study, we evaluated the performance of centralized versions of Resnet 50v2 and Resnet 152v2 models for classification of mammograms using different datasets, which were divided by location number extracted from the EMBED dataset. The datasets were preprocessed and used various techniques to improve the performance of the models. The models are trained and evaluated using metrics such as accuracy, area under the curve (AUC), F1 score, precision, and recall. The results indicate good performance for both models, with the Resnet 152v2 model slightly outperforming the Resnet 50v2 model in terms of AUC score. Our findings demonstrate the potential of machine learning algorithms in breast cancer screening, with our model achieving an AUC score of 0.83.

suggestions.title: Explainable AI for Radiologist Decision Support
suggestions.desc: Implement Grad-CAM or SHAP on the ResNet-152v2 models to generate heatmaps that highlight the specific pixel regions contributing to a 'malignant' classification, providing clinical interpretability.

suggestions.title: Multi-View Fusion Architecture for EMBED
suggestions.desc: Build a siamese neural network that processes both the CC (Craniocaudal) and MLO (Mediolateral Oblique) views from the EMBED dataset simultaneously to improve classification accuracy through spatial correlation.

suggestions.title: Synthetic Mammogram Augmentation using GANs
suggestions.desc: Train a StyleGAN2-ADA model to generate high-resolution synthetic mammograms of rare calcification patterns, addressing the class imbalance issue in the EMBED dataset and boosting model robustness.

suggestions.title: Automated Mammography Triage API
suggestions.desc: Develop a backend service that integrates with DICOM image servers to automatically flag high-suspicion scans in the radiology queue, prioritizing them for immediate human review based on the ResNet scores.

similar: legacy:GR-08|Spring 2016|gender/age classification from facial image using deep learning

title: Integration of Blockchain in Computer Networking: Overview, Applications, and Future Perspectives for Software-defined Networking (SDN), Network Security and Protocols
authors: Hossain Faruk, Md Jobair
abstract: The rapid advancement and increasing complexity of computer networks have created a need for robust, secure, and scalable solutions to manage and protect network resources. Blockchain, an emerging distributed ledger technology, offers enhanced security, transparency, and privacy preservation, making it a promising solution for addressing networking challenges. This paper presents a comprehensive survey of blockchain integration in computer networking, focusing on its potential applications, benefits, and future perspectives in Software-defined Networking (SDN), network security, and networking protocols. We identify that blockchain's tamper-proof nature could significantly improve network security by mitigating risks associated with centralized control and single points of failure. The integration of blockchain in computer networking has the potential to increase trust and transparency among network participants, as it allows for secure, verifiable, and auditable transactions and communication. Blockchain also can streamline the management of Software-defined Networking (SDN) by enabling decentralized and automated network control, resource allocation, and orchestration. We also find that utilizing blockchain can address network challenges, such as mitigating DDoS attacks, enhancing intrusion detection and prevention, and securing routing protocols. However, we identify potential limitations of blockchain integration in computer networking, such as scalability challenges arising from the growing size of the distributed ledger and increasing network traffic. We emphasize the need for further research in optimizing consensus mechanisms, enhancing scalability and privacy preservation techniques interoperability, and facilitating standardization of networking protocols and practices.

suggestions.title: Hyperledger-Based SDN Controller Synchronization
suggestions.desc: Implement a Proof-of-Authority (PoA) consensus mechanism using Hyperledger Fabric to synchronize flow tables across multiple SDN controllers, preventing 'poisoning' of network routes by a compromised controller.

suggestions.title: Self-Executing SLA Smart Contracts
suggestions.desc: Develop Ethereum-based smart contracts that automatically adjust SDN bandwidth allocations and issue refunds to clients if the network's telemetry data (via gNMI) falls below promised service levels.

suggestions.title: Zero-Trust IoT Device Onboarding
suggestions.desc: Create a blockchain-integrated gateway where IoT devices must present a cryptographic NFT 'identity' before the SDN controller opens a dynamic port for their specific MAC address.

suggestions.title: Decentralized Bandwidth Marketplace
suggestions.desc: Build a prototype application where private network owners can sell 'spare' bandwidth via a blockchain ledger, with the SDN layer handling the automated provisioning of secure tunnels for the buyers.

title: An Experimental Study: Performance of Load Balancing Algorithms in SDN- Enabled Cloud Computing Networks
authors: Challagundla, Samhitha
abstract: To meet the requirements of modern IT, it is crucial to address the numerous limitations of current cloud computing networking infrastructures. One promising solution to overcome these constraints is Software Defined Networking (SDN). SDN is an innovative networking approach that utilizes software-based controllers or APIs to manage and direct network traffic on top of underlying hardware infrastructure. SDN plays a major role in Cloud Computing in that it allows users to address different changes quickly as they come. One of its major advantages is that it streamlines the network configuration procedure and boosts network monitoring and performance. This study aims to implement various load-balancing algorithms for SDN to analyze the network performance. The implementation uses the POX controller as an SDN controller and Mininet software to emulate the network. The load-balancing algorithms are programmed using Python, which is also employed to create the network topology. The findings demonstrate that the Weighted Round Robin algorithm outperforms all the other algorithms that were evaluated.

suggestions.title: Deep Q-Learning for Dynamic Traffic Steering
suggestions.desc: Replace standard Round-Robin load balancing with a Deep Reinforcement Learning agent that monitors OpenFlow statistics in real-time to predict and prevent link congestion before it occurs.

suggestions.title: Green-SDN: Carbon-Aware Load Distribution
suggestions.desc: Modify the load balancing algorithm to prioritize server clusters powered by renewable energy (using APIs like WattTime), or consolidate traffic to minimize the number of active switches during low-demand hours.

suggestions.title: SDX-Based Multi-Cloud Load Balancer
suggestions.desc: Extend the SDN controller to manage traffic across a Software Defined Exchange (SDX), dynamically shifting application workloads between AWS and Azure regions based on real-time egress costs and latency.

suggestions.title: Intelligent DDoS Mitigation Scrubbing-as-a-Service
suggestions.desc: Build an automated response system that uses the SDN controller to instantly reroute 'dirty' traffic flows to a set of containerized scrubbing VNFs when an anomaly is detected in the cloud network.

title: Impact of Avatar's Behavioral Change with Quantity on Human Perception in Immersive Experiences
authors: Wile, Nicholas L
abstract: As virtual reality technology evolves, researchers have found that the characteristics of virtual avatars including appearance, representation, and proximity, can significantly influence the immersive experience of the user. This project investigates the impact of the number of avatars and their behavioral influence on the user in a simulated learning environment. We developed a virtual reality classroom system with Unity designed to elicit, track, and record the user’s behavioral changes including eye gaze, head, and hand movement using HTC VIVE Pro Eye and physiological signals including Heart Rate (HR) and Galvanic Skin Response (GSR), while the user delivers presentations to classrooms full of varying numbers of avatars. During the presentation, a fixed percentage of randomly chosen avatars perform an abnormal behavior, either a change in eye gaze or head movement. We hypothesized that the influence of this abnormal behavior on the user will be exponentially greater when the total number of avatars is larger, even though the percentage of avatars performing the abnormal behavior relative to the total number of avatars is held constant. In our research, we explore this phenomenon and use the results to design guidelines for the social VR experience.

suggestions.title: LLM-Powered Reactive NPCs
suggestions.desc: Integrate GPT-4o and Amazon Polly into the Unity avatars to allow them to engage in context-aware dialogue with the user, where the 'quantity' of avatars directly impacts the conversational complexity and peer pressure.

suggestions.title: Biometric Feedback for Immersion Analytics
suggestions.desc: Use a VR headset with eye-tracking (like Quest Pro) or a heart-rate sensor to correlate the user’s physiological stress levels with specific avatar behaviors, creating a heatmap of social anxiety in virtual spaces.

suggestions.title: Social Phobia Exposure Therapy Sandbox
suggestions.desc: Transform the classroom into a clinical tool where therapists can precisely control the 'hostility' or 'distractibility' of avatar groups to help patients practice social interaction in a safe, repeatable environment.

suggestions.title: Virtual Audience Dynamic Scaling Engine
suggestions.desc: Develop a middleware for VR conference apps that automatically adjusts the behaviors of thousands of 'background' avatars (cheering, nodding, or leaving) based on the presenter's voice pitch and slide timing.

title: SCALE-Sim extension to support GNN inputs and CNN back propagation
authors: Desai, Rutul; Pasupuleti, Phani Akshaya Sri; Deng, Bobin; Liu, Guangchi
abstract: In recent years, CNNs (Convolutional neural Network) and GNNs (Graph Neural Network) have gained a lot of attention and popularity in various fields such as computer vision, natural language processing, and social network analysis. Training large scale CNN and GNN models may take up to several months or sometimes years to complete. SCALE-Sim is a CNN accelerator with systolic array and SRAMs. However, SCALE-Sim only support CNN inference and not back propagation. To evaluate the CNN training, in this work we extend the SCALE-Sim to support CNN back propagation. Because the importance of the GNN applications and their training bottlenecks, we also explore how to integrate GNN training in SCALE-Sim via GraphSAGE to generate node embedding. The goal of this work is to extend SCALE-Sim to support CNN and GNN training, which enable us to evaluate the efficiency of machine training systems.
supervisor: Dr. Bobin Deng, Dr. Guangchi Liu

suggestions.title: Quantization-Aware Gradient Simulation
suggestions.desc: Extend the SCALE-Sim backpropagation logic to support 8-bit and 4-bit integer gradients, simulating the trade-offs between training accuracy and energy consumption for low-power GNN edge devices.

suggestions.title: Systolic Array Support for Vision Transformers
suggestions.desc: Modify the cycle-accurate simulator to handle the multi-head self-attention mechanism, benchmarking how the existing CNN hardware handles the massive matrix-matrix multiplications required for ViT training.

suggestions.title: Automated GNN Dataflow Mapping Optimizer
suggestions.desc: Create a Python-based tool that takes a PyTorch Geometric model and automatically generates the optimal SCALE-Sim configuration file, minimizing SRAM stalls for irregular graph architectures.

suggestions.title: ASIC vs FPGA Performance Benchmarking Suite
suggestions.desc: Use the extended SCALE-Sim outputs to generate Verilog templates, then run them through an FPGA synthesis tool to provide a real-world cost-benefit analysis for building a custom GNN training chip.

title: Benchmarking Network Service Performance Using the POWDER Wireless Testbed
authors: Horton, Dillon J; Nguyen, Manh V; Khandavilli, Sri Sesha Sailaja Lakshmi Tulasi; Le, Thinh Van
abstract: 5G RAN slicing provides a way to split network infrastructure into self-contained slices which can have various virtual network functions (VNFs) mapped onto them. Much work has gone into creating robust mapping and resource allocation algorithms in order to efficiently embed VNFs onto the available nodes in a slice. However, in order to most efficiently embed these VNFs we need to understand the resource and bandwidth needs of the services we are trying to embed. This project seeks to provide an accurate assessment of the needs of three commonly used network services. We do this by testing each network service using real world physical machines on the POWDER network testbed. We collect bandwidth and CPU usage data from each test and use it to analyze the needs of the network service when being embedded onto physical nodes during network slicing.

suggestions.title: Kubernetes-Native VNF Orchestrator
suggestions.desc: Develop a custom Kubernetes scheduler for the POWDER testbed that uses real-time 5G signal strength (RSRP/RSRQ) to decide where to deploy containerized network functions like firewalls or video transcoders.

suggestions.title: Slice Isolation Security Stress-Tester
suggestions.desc: Conduct a study on POWDER to measure the 'noisy neighbor' effect, where one network slice is flooded with UDP traffic to determine if the hardware abstractions successfully protect the bandwidth of a high-priority 'Emergency' slice.

suggestions.title: Ultra-Low Latency Drone Swarm Slice
suggestions.desc: Provision a URLLC (Ultra-Reliable Low Latency Communication) slice on POWDER to benchmark the control-loop latency for a swarm of simulated drones, comparing it against standard Best-Effort mobile broadband.

suggestions.title: Zero-Touch Slice Management Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Build a React-based web portal that allows enterprise customers to 'drag and drop' virtual network functions onto a map of POWDER nodes, automatically generating the underlying Slice-YAML configurations.

title: Molecular Dynamics and Protein-Protein Interactions (PPIs) on SARS-CoV-2 coronavirus
authors: Addepalli, Durga Narayana Varma; Xie, Yixin
abstract: The objective of this comparative analysis is to study the behavior of the Original, alpha, beta, gamma, delta, and omicron variants of SARS-CoV-2 coronavirus under conditions that resemble the human body. To achieve this, we conducted Molecular Dynamics simulations for all variants in the same environment. Additionally, we conducted salt bridge and hydrogen bond analyses to further investigate the behavior of the SARS-CoV-2 variants. These analyses allowed us to examine the interactions between amino acid residues and to determine the stability of the protein structures. By integrating these analyses with our Molecular Dynamics simulations, we were able to gain a comprehensive understanding of the behavior of each variant in a human-like environment. This information could be crucial in developing effective treatments and vaccines against COVID-19. Overall, our study provides valuable insights into the molecular dynamics and protein-protein interactions of the SARS-CoV-2 variants and highlights the importance of further research in this area to combat the ongoing pandemic.

suggestions.title: Variant-Resistant Small Molecule Docking
suggestions.desc: Use AutoDock Vina to simulate the binding affinity of existing FDA-approved drugs against the spike proteins of the Delta and Omicron variants to identify which chemical structures are most resistant to mutation.

suggestions.title: GNN-Based PPI Prediction Pipeline
suggestions.desc: Convert the Molecular Dynamics (MD) trajectories into dynamic graphs and train a Graph Neural Network (GNN) to predict the stability of protein-protein interactions (PPI) faster than traditional salt-bridge analysis.

suggestions.title: Interactive SARS-CoV-2 Mutation Visualizer
suggestions.desc: Develop a Three.js-based web application that allows users to toggle between variants and see a 3D overlay of how salt bridges and hydrogen bonds shift in real-time during the simulation.

suggestions.title: Epidemiological Risk Scoring Engine
suggestions.desc: Create a data science pipeline that maps MD simulation results (like binding energy) to real-world transmission rates, providing a 'risk score' for newly identified viral sequences from the GISAID database.

title: Insight into current COVID-19 variants and data Science Applications
authors: POTLAPALLI, RAVI; Ande, Thanusha Sai
abstract: COVID-19 has undergone several mutations resulting in the emergence of new variants such as Alpha, Beta, Gamma, Delta, and Omicron. Data science plays a vital role in understanding the spread of COVID-19 and its variants. The emergence of new COVID-19 variants has raised concerns about the effectiveness of existing vaccines and treatments. This poster provides insights into the current COVID-19 variants and the data science applications used to monitor and understand their spread. We will showcase the use of SQL to manage and analyze genomic data of SARS-CoV-2 variants, Power BI for visualization and tracking of COVID-19 cases and deaths, and machine learning algorithms for variant classification and prediction of transmission. The poster highlights the importance of integrating data science tools in COVID-19 research for effective variant surveillance, risk assessment, and mitigation strategies.

suggestions.title: Automated Genomic Sequence Analysis Pipeline
suggestions.desc: Build a bioinformatics pipeline using BioPython and ClustalW to automatically fetch raw FASTA files from GISAID and detect specific spike protein mutations in new Omicron sub-variants. This would move the project from static analysis to a live monitoring tool for viral evolution.

suggestions.title: Socio-Economic Mobility Correlation Engine
suggestions.desc: Combine the variant spread data with Google Community Mobility Reports and local policy datasets using Causal Inference libraries like DoWhy. The goal is to quantify how much specific variant surges were driven by policy changes versus inherent viral transmissibility.

suggestions.title: Protein-Folding Stability Simulations
suggestions.desc: Use AlphaFold2 or Rosetta to simulate the structural stability of the spike proteins for the Alpha through Omicron variants. Analyze how these structural changes correlate with the data science findings on vaccine escape and infection rates documented in the original project.

suggestions.title: Public Health Sentiment & Variant Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Develop a real-time dashboard using Streamlit that overlays variant prevalence data with NLP-driven sentiment analysis of Twitter and Reddit. This helps public health officials understand if public concern aligns with the actual data-driven risk of a specific mutation.

title: PlanIt CRM - Refactoring for the Future
authors: Hall, Justin; Falaiye, Oluwaseyi; Anderson, Thomas; Bariki Vidura, Sai Krupa; Guclu, Samet Yekta; Dogan, Ahmet Bugra
abstract: Planit CRM is a Customer Relationship Management (CRM) software that helps in managing and tracking projects, tasks, invoices, quotes, leads, customers, transactions and much more. This software allows anyone to manage leads, create invoices, and start collecting payments seamlessly. The system was developed by Driven Software Solutions under the guidance of Shahzib Sarfraz in 2018. The software has since been used by business owners to allow for easier management of the income and expenses of their operation. It is supported by a team of around fifty developers who maintain the PlanIT CRM software off the site https://planitcrm.com, which can be reached through the domain https://drivensoftwaresolutions.com The primary objective of this project is to refactor the source code of the PlanIT CRM system to utilize Angular for the front-end and a combination of Laravel and NodeJS for the backend. The appropriate software documentation and user guides will be generated as well. Overall, the system will be refactored to allow for greater scalability and versatility of the software.

suggestions.title: Microservices Decomposition for Scalable Billing
suggestions.desc: Transition the PlanIt CRM monolith into a microservices architecture using Docker and Kubernetes, specifically decoupling the high-traffic 'Invoices and Payments' module. This allows for independent scaling of the financial engine during end-of-month billing cycles.

suggestions.title: Plaid-Powered Automated Bank Reconciliation
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Plaid API to allow PlanIt users to link their business bank accounts directly to the CRM. Build a matching algorithm that automatically marks invoices as 'Paid' when a corresponding transaction is detected, removing manual data entry.

suggestions.title: Predictive Lead Scoring Extension
suggestions.desc: Develop a machine learning module using Scikit-Learn that analyzes historical lead data within PlanIt to assign a 'Conversion Probability' score. This helps business owners prioritize their daily outreach based on which leads are most likely to result in a sale.

suggestions.title: Offline-First Mobile Field Service App
suggestions.desc: Build a React Native mobile companion for PlanIt that uses PouchDB/CouchDB for offline data synchronization. This allows field technicians to create quotes and manage tasks in locations without internet access and sync back to the main CRM once online.

title: Website SEO Analysis PFC NYC
authors: Phillips, Corey; Hutto, Travis; Saaka, Ashraf; Garuba, Tomisin; Olaosebiomo, Ayowole T
abstract: A website's rating and visibility in search engine results pages are improved through search engine optimization (SEO). Businesses benefit from SEO to increase website traffic, leads, and sales. This summary reviews SEO, its advantages, and the main tactics and strategies applied to Paddle for a Cure NYC. It also emphasizes the significance of technical optimization, link development, keyword research, and content for Paddle for a Cure NYC. This abstract underlines the need for constant optimization and monitoring to maintain Paddle for a Cure NYC's website visibility and competitiveness in search results.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Competitor Keyword Tracker
suggestions.desc: Build a Python-based scraping tool that compares 'Paddle for a Cure NYC' keyword rankings against similar local charity events, providing a live dashboard of competitive SEO opportunities.

suggestions.title: Generative AI SEO Content Assistant
suggestions.desc: Integrate the GPT-4 API to build a tool that suggests blog topics and generates SEO-optimized meta-descriptions specifically tailored to improve the organic reach of the fundraiser’s mission.

suggestions.title: Charity Backlink Quality Auditor
suggestions.desc: Develop a tool using the Moz or Ahrefs API to audit the 'Paddle for a Cure' backlink profile, identifying and suggesting a 'disavow list' for toxic links that may be suppressing search visibility.

suggestions.title: Core Web Vitals Performance Monitor
suggestions.desc: Create a monitoring script that tracks Largest Contentful Paint (LCP) and Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS) over time, correlating website speed improvements with changes in Google search ranking.

title: Analysis of Alternatives of Workstation Deployment Solutions
authors: Tilton, Tyler L; Moscosa, Jesus; Singleton, Kameron; Pitts, Kimberly; Parungao, Roland J
abstract: In searching for a new workstation deployment solution, GTRI identified 15 ranked characteristics which an acceptable solution should possess. These 15 characteristics were used as the basis for this Analysis of Alternatives. This process consisted of a research phase and testing phase. During the research phase, team members compiled a list of potential solutions and gathered basic information about each. This list was eventually pruned to ten products which were further researched and scored based on their reported ability to meet the supplied requirements. As a result of this research, Ivanti DSM and Theopenem were selected for testing in a lab environment. Despite KACE scoring higher than Ivanti and Theopenem, Theopenem was ultimately chosen due to the unavailability of a KACE demo installation. The testing phase consisted of building a test environment for each tool. These environments were relatively simple and consisted of one server, a small network, and a client. During this phase, the tools were evaluated using GTRI’s requirements and any other features which may be useful to GTRI. The process and experience were documented along the way. Based on the research and testing phases' results, it was concluded that Theopenem was the optimal deployment tool for GTRI, given its ease of use and performance over Ivanti DSM.

suggestions.title: Automated IaC Validation Framework
suggestions.desc: Develop a Python-based testing framework that uses Terraform and Ansible to automatically provision workstations using the top-ranked deployment solutions. This would programmatically verify the '15 characteristics' rather than relying on manual testing.

suggestions.title: STIG Compliance Hardening Module
suggestions.desc: Create a library of custom scripts for the chosen deployment solution that automatically applies DISA STIG (Security Technical Implementation Guides) during the OS installation. This ensures that every workstation deployed for GTRI is immediately compliant with federal security standards.

suggestions.title: Zero-Touch Provisioning for Distributed Teams
suggestions.desc: Extend the deployment solution to support remote provisioning via cloud gateways (like Windows Autopilot or Red Hat Satellite). This would allow GTRI to ship hardware directly to remote researchers and have the OS and custom scripts install automatically upon first boot.

suggestions.title: Deployment Throughput Benchmarking Study
suggestions.desc: Conduct a quantitative performance study comparing the network overhead and installation times of the top 3 solutions under heavy load (e.g., deploying to 50 workstations simultaneously). Use Wireshark and Prometheus to identify bottlenecks in multicast vs. unicast imaging.

title: Analysis of Alternatives for Workstation Deployment for GTRI
authors: Freeman, Josh; Glaze, Kevin; Curtis, Doug; Dougal, Kayla; Allam, Rakesh
abstract: Georgia Tech Research Institute is requesting an analysis of alternatives of workstation deployment solution that can deploy an operating system to a workstation. This deployment solution must at least be able to deploy Windows 10, Windows 11, Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8 and 9; support multiple users simultaneously, support various models, and run custom scripts. The objective of the analysis of alternatives is to provide Georgia Tech Research Institute with one deployment solution that can replace their current software that facilitates this process. There are inefficiencies to resolve in their current environment such as deploying various operating systems and applying packages, updates, and applicable security policies. This multi-step process takes time to attain the desired outcome. By providing an alternate solution, it is foreseeable to provide cost savings along with overall efficiency to Georgia Tech Research Institute.

suggestions.title: Unified Multi-OS Scripting Wrapper
suggestions.desc: Develop a cross-platform scripting abstraction layer (using Python or Go) that allows a single configuration file to trigger OS-specific commands (PowerShell for Windows, Bash for RHEL). This simplifies the 'custom scripts' requirement for complex hybrid-OS environments.

suggestions.title: Self-Service Workstation Re-Imaging Portal
suggestions.desc: Build a web-based portal that allows authorized GTRI users to trigger a remote re-image of their own workstation. This would interface with the deployment solution's API to schedule an OS refresh without requiring manual intervention from IT staff.

suggestions.title: Hardware-Agnostic Driver Injection Engine
suggestions.desc: Implement a dynamic driver management system that detects the workstation model during the PXE boot phase and injects the specific vendor drivers (Dell, HP, Lenovo) into the RHEL or Windows image on the fly, reducing the need for multiple static images.

suggestions.title: Secure Boot and TPM 2.0 Integration Audit
suggestions.desc: Research and implement a secure chain-of-trust during the deployment process. Ensure that the chosen solution can handle UEFI Secure Boot and TPM 2.0 provisioning for Windows 11 and RHEL 9 to meet modern hardware security requirements.

title: AI for Quantitative Trading
authors: Ponakaladinne, Kiran; Binigari, Sai Charan; Jawale, Mayuri; Petakamsetti, Gowthami; Agarwal, Nihal Kumar
abstract: AI for quantitative trading involves using machine learning and other AI techniques to analyze financial data and make informed trading decisions. By automating the trading process and leveraging the power of AI, traders can potentially improve their performance and generate better returns. The goal is to identify and capitalize on available trading opportunities by using computer algorithms and programs based on simple or complex mathematical models. Stock market indicators are statistical measures that provide insights into the behavior of the stock market as a whole or of individual stocks. These indicators are used by investors, traders, and analysts to evaluate market trends and conditions, predict future price movements, and make informed investment decisions. After establishing an understanding of these indicators and performance metrics, one can understand the process of developing a trading simulator, strategy optimizer, and financial machine-learning pipeline. Multiple trading strategies are implemented systematically and adapted to real-time market conditions using machine learning. Stock movement is predicted by supervised models to demonstrate how machine learning can be applied to quantitative trading. In addition, multiple optimization techniques are used in trading to maximize returns and minimize risk. Several machine learning techniques and methodologies like feature selection are used to find accurate prediction.

suggestions.title: Live Paper-Trading Execution Bridge
suggestions.desc: Connect the mathematical models to the Alpaca Markets or Interactive Brokers API to create a real-time paper trading bot. This moves the project from historical backtesting to live execution, handling real-time data streams and order fills.

suggestions.title: NLP Sentiment-Driven Volatility Predictor
suggestions.desc: Integrate a FinBERT-based NLP model to analyze daily financial news and earnings call transcripts. Use this sentiment data as a feature alongside technical indicators to improve the model's ability to predict price spikes during volatile news cycles.

suggestions.title: Reinforcement Learning for Portfolio Balancing
suggestions.desc: Replace simple indicators with a Deep Q-Network (DQN) that manages a portfolio of 10+ stocks. The agent should learn not just when to buy/sell, but how to dynamically adjust asset weights to maximize the Sharpe Ratio and minimize drawdown.

suggestions.title: High-Frequency Backtesting Architecture
suggestions.desc: Build a high-performance backtesting engine in C++ or using Numba-optimized Python. This allows the model to be tested against years of tick-by-tick data rather than daily closing prices, providing a more realistic simulation of slippage and transaction costs.

title: BrainNet: Using Deep Learning to Classify Brain Tumors
authors: Deem, Ryan
abstract: Brain tumors are a common type of cancer, and they do not discriminate based on gender, age, or ethnicity. That said, the severity and type of tumor vary among the diagnosed individual, cancerous or benign. The three most common types for diagnosed individuals are glioma, meningioma, and pituitary. Even so, identifying the type of tumor can be an arduous process for both the doctor and the patient, but one technique known as Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) has been particularly effective in expediently and reliably determining the type of brain tumor. A CNN is a type of neural network that can independently extract imaging features (e.g., face shape, eye color of images of faces), one of these models is known as ResNet101, which contains several residual layers. Through these layers, data can be spread forward, and retain information about the findings of the patient's images. The ResNet model layers were frozen in this case for the first 10 epochs, then when the model had 50 of the layers of the base unfrozen which resulted in the training accuracy being 98% after 10 more epochs and the test accuracy being 96% after it finished. The conclusion drawn is that the results mean that they have shown that they have better than most existing models for this application, due to that a majority of models can only achieve the low 90%.
supervisor: Dr. Md Abdullah Al Hafiz Khan

suggestions.title: Explainable AI (XAI) for Clinical Validation
suggestions.desc: Integrate Grad-CAM (Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping) to generate heatmaps on the MRI scans. This allows doctors to see exactly which pixels the CNN is using to identify a Glioma or Meningioma, building trust in the AI's diagnostic decisions.

suggestions.title: 3D Volumetric MRI Classification
suggestions.desc: Transition the model from 2D slice analysis to 3D CNNs using the Keras-Tuner and NIfTI file formats. This allows the network to learn the spatial volume of the tumor, which is critical for differentiating between invasive gliomas and localized pituitary tumors.

suggestions.title: Federated Learning for Privacy-Preserving Training
suggestions.desc: Implement a Federated Learning framework using Flower or OpenMined. This would allow the BrainNet model to be trained across data from multiple hospitals without the sensitive MRI data ever leaving the local hospital servers, solving a major HIPAA compliance hurdle.

suggestions.title: Multi-Modal Diagnostic Assistant
suggestions.desc: Combine the CNN image classification with patient metadata (age, gender, symptoms) using a Multi-Input Functional API in TensorFlow. This mimics a real doctor's diagnostic process by using both visual evidence and clinical history to improve accuracy.

title: A Comparative Study of Virtual Network Architectures for Cloud Computing Environments
authors: SREERAMOJU, SUVIDHA; Maddukuri, Susmitha
abstract: Virtual networks have emerged as a promising solution for creating customized network topologies that can meet the specific needs of different cloud environments. In this project, we present a comparative study of three different virtual network architectures for cloud computing environments. We compare these architectures based on a set of criteria, including performance, scalability, flexibility, and security. Our evaluation shows that all three virtual network architectures have their advantages and disadvantages. The choice of virtual network architecture depends on the specific needs and requirements of the cloud environment. Our evaluation provides insights into the trade-offs between different virtual network architectures and can inform the design and implementation of virtual networks for cloud computing environments

suggestions.title: Dynamic SDN Flow Load Balancer
suggestions.desc: Implement an SDN controller using Ryu or ONOS that dynamically re-routes traffic across the virtual network architectures based on real-time latency. Compare how each architecture handles sudden bursts of simulated 'noisy neighbor' traffic.

suggestions.title: Zero-Trust Mesh Overlay Implementation
suggestions.desc: Build a Zero Trust security layer on top of the virtual networks using Istio or HashiCorp Consul. Evaluate the performance overhead of mutual TLS (mTLS) encryption on each of the three architectures studied in the original project.

suggestions.title: Container Networking Interface (CNI) Comparison
suggestions.desc: Expand the study to include modern container networking. Compare the performance and scalability of Kubernetes-native CNI plugins like Calico, Flannel, and Cilium (eBPF) against the three traditional cloud virtual networks already analyzed.

suggestions.title: Multi-Cloud Interconnect Orchestrator
suggestions.desc: Develop a tool that automates the creation of a 'Global Virtual Network' spanning AWS VPCs and Azure VNets. Test the architectures for their ability to maintain low latency and data integrity across high-latency inter-cloud links.

title: DineNGo Application
authors: Jonnalagadda, Sai Sushanth Reddy; Vancha, Sahith Vardhan Reddy; Annavajhula, Subramanya Rahul
abstract: This project aims to upgrade the DineNGo application, a software tool for restaurant management, with new features specified by the client, The Driven Software Solutions. The upgraded version includes enhancements to users' roles, such as implementing Role-Based Access Control (RBAC), and adding a payment required feature before order placement. Additionally, the new features aim to improve payment processing, reduce trips back and forth to the POS terminal, and provide a secure payment experience for customers. This document provides an overview of the project's scope, objectives, and system overview, along with the operational policies and constraints.

suggestions.title: Contactless Payment with Stripe Terminal Integration
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Stripe Terminal SDK to allow servers to take physical chip-and-pin or tap-to-pay transactions directly from the mobile app. This eliminates the need for a central POS station and speeds up table turnover by finalizing payments right at the customer's side.

suggestions.title: Predictive Inventory Management Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Apply a Time-Series forecasting model (like Prophet) to the historical order data generated by DineNGo. Build a module that alerts managers when ingredients are likely to run out based on projected weekend sales, reducing waste and preventing 'out-of-stock' items during peak hours.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Kitchen Sync with WebSockets
suggestions.desc: Replace polling with a SignalR or Socket.io implementation to provide real-time 'Order Status' updates across the Front-of-House and Kitchen displays. Implement a drag-and-drop Kanban board for chefs to update the status of individual dishes, instantly notifying the server's handheld device.

suggestions.title: Customer Loyalty & Personalized Recommendation Engine
suggestions.desc: Develop a customer-facing side of the app that tracks order history and uses a Collaborative Filtering algorithm to suggest wine or dessert pairings. Implement a digital 'stamp card' system that automatically applies discounts to the pre-payment screen once a threshold is reached.

title: Artificial Intelligence(AI) for Quantitative Trading
authors: Akpan, Winifred; Ayo, Emmanuel; Kennedy, Jason; Browning, Malek
abstract: This project involves the use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) techniques in quantitative trading and stock market analysis for educational purposes. The goal of this project is to predict stock market movement to help investors mitigate risks associated with trading and to provide higher returns. It involves the use, implementation, and refinement of basic python framework codes, sklearn library (pandas and numpy), in addition to the development and application of a Linear Regression labeling strategy to predict future daily stock trends, and the utilization of a Supervised Learning AI model. The techniques developed and utilized during this project will be used to analyze market trends, predict market movements, and optimize investment strategies. This project will provide knowledge and practical skills in AI, ML, and quantitative trading.

suggestions.title: LSTM-RNN Time Series Upgrade
suggestions.desc: Move beyond the Linear Regression baseline by implementing a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network. Compare the Mean Squared Error (MSE) of the regression model against the LSTM to see if the deep learning approach better captures the non-linear nature of stock prices.

suggestions.title: Real-time Dashboard with Streamlit & yfinance
suggestions.desc: Build a live web application where users can input any stock ticker. The app should fetch live data via the yfinance API, apply the trained Scikit-Learn models, and display a 5-day predicted trend graph alongside real-time accuracy metrics.

suggestions.title: Automated Portfolio Rebalancing Bot
suggestions.desc: Develop a script that uses the regression model's output to manage a virtual portfolio. Implement logic that automatically 'sells' stocks when the predicted 7-day return falls below a certain threshold and 'buys' stocks with the highest predicted upward movement.

suggestions.title: Feature Engineering with Macro-Economic Indicators
suggestions.desc: Incorporate external data sources like the Federal Reserve (FRED) API to include interest rates and inflation data as features in the training set. Research how these macro factors impact the accuracy of the existing Scikit-Learn framework.

similar: legacy:AUTO-SU2020-1283|Summer 2020|using artificial intelligence to prescribe medicine

title: Towards Bounding the Behavior of Deep Neural Networks
authors: Borowski, Richard
abstract: Advances in Artificial Intelligence (AI), particularly in the form of deep neural networks, have revolutionized a diverse range of fields. As neural networks become more pervasive, the need to understand the boundaries of their behavior is becoming increasingly important. For example, can we formally guarantee that an autonomous vehicle will not violate traffic laws, such as reaching excessive speeds?
