Towards the goal of bounding the behavior of a neural network, we propose how to bound the behavior of individual neurons by incrementally tightening formal bounds on it. We further provide a case study on classifying handwritten digits to illustrate the utility of our algorithm in terms of bounding the behavior of an individual neuron.

suggestions.title: SMT Solver Integration for Formal Safety
suggestions.desc: Use the Marabou or Z3 solver to formally verify that a neural network used for autonomous steering never outputs an angle greater than a physical safety limit, regardless of the input noise. This provides the mathematical 'guarantee' mentioned in the abstract.

suggestions.title: Adversarial Robustness Benchmarking Suite
suggestions.desc: Develop a tool that subjects the bounded DNN to Projected Gradient Descent (PGD) and FGSM attacks. Measure if the 'behavioral bounds' established in the project hold up when the network is intentionally fed deceptive inputs.

suggestions.title: Safe Reinforcement Learning for Industrial Robotics
suggestions.desc: Apply the bounding methodology to a Reinforcement Learning agent controlling a 6-DOF robotic arm. Ensure that the agent's learned policy never causes the arm to enter a predefined 'collision zone' using a safety-filter layer.

suggestions.title: Real-time Runtime Monitor for Bounded Compliance
suggestions.desc: Build a lightweight 'watchdog' module that sits alongside a DNN in production. It should intercept every inference result and block execution if the output approaches the safety boundaries, providing an immediate fail-safe for autonomous systems.

title: Speech Recognition
authors: Rojjhanarittikorn, Rachnicha
abstract: Most people use Google, and they have Google Assistant installed on their devices, such as smartphones. It allows us to talk with the robot and it will assist us by answering questions and controlling devices on the phone. Google can convert the user’s speech to text using Natural Language Processing and Neural Networks. Google Assistant has a tool to convert speech to text, which is speech recognition. Speech recognition can be developed as a tool to assist people by using voice. It will save people tim e or benefit the people who do not know how to write English or other languages, they should be able to speak, and the program will understand them .

suggestions.title: Edge-Optimized Offline Transcription for IoT
suggestions.desc: Port a lightweight speech-to-text model like Whisper-Tiny or Vosk to a Raspberry Pi to create a voice-controlled home assistant that processes commands locally without relying on Google Cloud APIs.

suggestions.title: Specialized Medical Terminology Dictation Tool
suggestions.desc: Fine-tune a Wav2Vec2 model using the LibriSpeech dataset supplemented with medical glossaries to improve speech recognition accuracy for hands-free clinical documentation.

suggestions.title: Multi-Modal Sentiment and Intent Analysis
suggestions.desc: Extend the speech recognition pipeline to extract emotional tone using Librosa MFCC analysis, allowing the system to adjust its response speed or tone based on the user's perceived frustration or urgency.

suggestions.title: Voice-Driven Code Navigation for Accessibility
suggestions.desc: Develop a VS Code extension that maps specific voice commands to complex IDE actions (e.g., 'refactor method' or 'jump to definition') to assist developers with motor impairments.

title: OPERATION ENDURING FREEDOM: Improving Mission Effectiveness by Identifying Trends in Successful Terrorism
authors: Shaver, Dalton A
abstract: This research examines how the characteristics of terrorist attacks predict the chance of an attack succeeding, where an attack is defined as successful if the intended attack type is carried out. Data was analyzed across three geographical missions within Operation Enduring Freedom: Trans-Sahara, Horn of Africa, and the Philippines. Using predicted probabilities of success obtained from logistic regression models, the medians were plotted to compare the characteristics of terrorist attacks across missions. By determining the specific features of attacks that produce the highest probabilities of success, the effectiveness of Operation Enduring Freedom can be improved by focusing counter-terrorism training and operations on the features that predict successful attacks.
supervisor: Prof. Susan Hardy - Main Advisor, Dr. Gene Ray - Consultant

suggestions.title: Interactive Counter-Terrorism Decision Support System
suggestions.desc: Create a web dashboard using Plotly Dash that allows military planners to input hypothetical attack characteristics (target type, region, weapon). The system would use the logistic regression model to output a 'Risk of Success' score for real-time threat assessment.

suggestions.title: Spatio-Temporal Hotspot Evolution Mapping
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Global Terrorism Database (GTD) with GeoPandas to visualize how attack success 'hotspots' have migrated across the Philippines and Horn of Africa over a 20-year period. Use time-series analysis to identify if specific counter-terrorism operations caused these shifts.

suggestions.title: Random Forest & Gradient Boosting Comparison
suggestions.desc: Extend the research by comparing the logistic regression results with non-linear models like Random Forest or XGBoost. Determine if complex feature interactions (e.g., specific weapon types used only in certain missions) provide better predictive power for attack outcomes.

suggestions.title: Mission-Specific Resource Allocation Optimizer
suggestions.desc: Develop an optimization algorithm that suggests the most effective deployment of limited security resources (personnel, surveillance) across the three OEF missions based on where the model predicts the highest 'probability of success' for future attacks.

title: A Quantum Arithmetic Logic Unit
authors: Butler, Ethan, Phillip, Bryson, Ulrich, Benjamin G, Carroll, David
abstract: This paper demonstrates that a quantum version of a classical Arithmetic Logic Unit (ALU) can be implemented on a quantum circuit. It would perform the same functions as the classical ALU, with the possibility of adding quantum functions in conjunction. To create the quantum ALU, we utilized IBM’s Qiskit Python package and JuypterLab. We also used the IBM Quantum Lab to run the circuit. We believe that a quantum ALU has the potential to be faster than its classical counterpart and the ability to calculate quantum specific operations. The simple classical functions translated to a quantum circuit show a promising future for the development of a full quantum ALU with unique quantum operations.
supervisor: Senior Project Course Instructor: Prof. Sharon Perry; Project Owner:
topics: Other (explain in the com m ents section)

suggestions.title: Floating-Point Quantum Arithmetic Support
suggestions.desc: The current ALU likely focuses on integer operations; extend this by implementing a quantum circuit for floating-point arithmetic using the IEEE 754 standard. Research and build quantum sub-circuits for mantissa and exponent handling using Qiskit’s basic gates to enable complex scientific computations on quantum hardware.

suggestions.title: Quantum Error Mitigation for ALU Operations
suggestions.desc: Real IBM Quantum hardware is noisy; implement error mitigation techniques like Zero-Noise Extrapolation (ZNE) or Probabilistic Error Cancellation (PEC) specifically for this ALU. Compare the accuracy of arithmetic results between a pure simulator and actual IBM hardware after applying these mitigation strategies.

suggestions.title: Hybrid Classical-Quantum Cryptographic Co-Processor
suggestions.desc: Integrate this quantum ALU into a hybrid system that offloads specific modular arithmetic tasks used in post-quantum cryptography (like Kyber or Dilithium). Build a Python wrapper that determines which calculations should be sent to the IBM Quantum Lab vs. processed locally on a classical CPU.

suggestions.title: Quantum ALU Instruction Set Architecture (ISA) Emulator
suggestions.desc: Develop a high-level assembly language and compiler that maps traditional code to the specific gate sequences of this quantum ALU. Use JupyterLab to create a 'Quantum Visualizer' that shows how registers evolve during a series of arithmetic operations in real-time.

title: Data Collection in Parkinson's VR
authors: Weingarten, Neil E; McConnell, Ian
abstract: This subm ission is meant to show an addition to a Parkinson's simulation within VR where there are now different methods of data collection that are collected in-gam e. These data points are tracked and logged during gam eplay, and are meant to allow researchers to make more effective use of the simulation as a tool for data collection. An exam ple dem o of the gam e and exam ple files that were generated during gam eplay are provided.

suggestions.title: Automated Tremor Frequency Spectral Analysis
suggestions.desc: Use the VR controller’s high-frequency accelerometer data to perform a Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) during gameplay, logging the specific Hz and amplitude of tremors for clinical review.

suggestions.title: Gamified Motor-Skill Progress Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Create a patient-facing web application that pulls the logged data to visualize improvements in reaction time and reach range over multiple weeks, using 'badges' to encourage daily exercise.

suggestions.title: Stress-Correlated Dysfunction Analysis
suggestions.desc: Integrate a Bluetooth heart rate monitor into the VR session to study the correlation between physiological stress markers and the severity of motor symptoms during high-difficulty game tasks.

suggestions.title: Clinician's Remote Observation Portal
suggestions.desc: Build a 'Spectator Mode' where doctors can remotely view the patient's VR session in real-time with an overlay of live data points, enabling telehealth-based physical therapy assessments.

title: Using Quantum Computing to Determine the Optimal Path on Cascading Graphs
authors: Swann, Michael B; Hunt, Ethan K
abstract: Quantum computing has completely changed the computing paradigm. These special computers leverage the unique properties of quantum mechanics to solve problems that a classical computer cannot solve in polynomial time. Quantum mechanics such as superposition and entanglement are used to boost computational power exponentially in many problems . Many traditionally NP-complete problems, such as breaking the encryption of public-private key systems, are solvable with quantum computing in polynomial time. In this project, we will review quantum computing basics using real quantum computers and build on those basics to solve a subset of a graph optimization problems using both existing and new methodologies. Our research focuses on a subset of graphs named “Cascading Graphs” and finding the “best” path based on a predetermined metric. To solve this problem, we plan to use a mixed approach for finding a mathematical algorithm and creating an implementation of the algorithm in a quantum computer. This mixed approach will be based on a cycle consisting of trying a find a mathematically rigorous proof and testing different cases to help build an understanding of the problem, which will then be verified using a quantitative approach.
supervisor: Mentor: Dr. Dan Lo; Course Instructor: Prof. Sharon Perry
topics: Other (explain in the com m ents section)

suggestions.title: Quantum Supply Chain Route Optimization
suggestions.desc: Apply the cascading graph algorithm to a real-world logistics dataset (like the FedEx or UPS hub-and-spoke models). Map the cascading graphs to geographic delivery nodes to solve the 'Last Mile' delivery problem using quantum superposition to evaluate all paths simultaneously.

suggestions.title: QAOA Implementation for Dynamic Graph Weights
suggestions.desc: Extend the project by implementing the Quantum Approximate Optimization Algorithm (QAOA) to handle graphs where edge weights change dynamically (e.g., traffic or weather). Compare the convergence speed of QAOA against classical Dijkstra’s algorithm in a time-sensitive simulation.

suggestions.title: Entanglement-Based Graph Partitioning
suggestions.desc: Research and build a module that uses quantum entanglement to partition massive cascading graphs into smaller sub-graphs for parallel processing. This allows the solving of NP-complete graph problems that exceed the qubit count of current IBM Quantum hardware.

suggestions.title: Real-time Financial Arbitrage Pathfinding
suggestions.desc: Utilize the cascading graph logic to detect circular trading paths (arbitrage) in cryptocurrency markets. Connect the quantum algorithm to a live API (like Binance or Coinbase) to find the 'optimal path' for trades across multiple asset pairs that a classical computer might miss in a volatile window.

title: SP4-DogEm
authors: Chung, Chloe, Atkinson, Zane, Iniestra, Melissa, Intshakal A Nzeng, Deo, Willson, Joshua K
abstract: The DogEm project's overarching objective is to produce a functional cross-platform mobile app that reliably sends a specific contact a series of calls, emails, and texts until the contact responds. To accomplish this goal, we have compiled preliminary research, produced a series of prototypes, and started the development process. Our preliminary research consists of research pertaining to our tech stack, our user base, our app's requirements, possibilities for messaging and calling features, UX/UI research, reading through React Native documentation, and constraints on the messaging and calling features on iOS vs. Android. Each member produced a DogEm app prototype to familiarize ourselves with the technology that will be employed throughout the project: React Native and Expo CLI. Features implemented throughout the prototypes include, React Native Navigation, expo-sms, expo Linking, Firebase, and various other React Native libraries and APIs. Our development process currently consists of a team effort in which members are assigned an important functionality of the app. Members then actively contribute their changes to a GitHub repository holding our final product. GitHub is an important tool for our development efforts because it allows for code review and effective version control. With regard to version control, we are tracking and maintaining changes made to code on our GitHub. It is an integral part of our software development process. In order to facilitate best practices in our source code management, the DogEm team has been storing and managing our code in GitHub and maintaining a source code directory on our website.
topics: Other (explain in the com m ents section)

suggestions.title: Elderly Care & Fall Detection Integration
suggestions.desc: Transform DogEm into a safety tool for seniors by integrating mobile accelerometer data to detect falls. If a fall is detected, the app can use the existing multi-channel 'nagging' logic to contact emergency caregivers until a 'safety code' is provided by the responder.

suggestions.title: Twilio-Powered AI Voice Response Verification
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Twilio Voice API and Amazon Lex to create a smart response loop. Instead of just sending a call, the app uses AI to listen to the responder’s voice, ensuring it is a human (not a voicemail) and parsing their intent to stop the notification chain.

suggestions.title: Geofencing-Triggered Persistent Alerts
suggestions.desc: Add a geofencing module that triggers the DogEm notification sequence when a specific user enters or leaves a geographic boundary. This could be used for child safety (leaving school) or high-security site monitoring, ensuring a supervisor is reached immediately.

suggestions.title: Cross-Platform Notification Escalation Analysis
suggestions.desc: Build an analytics dashboard within the app to study 'response latency' across different channels (SMS vs. Email vs. Call). Use this data to implement an 'Intelligent Escalation' algorithm that learns which medium a specific contact is most likely to respond to first.

title: Gone Fishin' VR
authors: Lott, Donovan E; Halbert, Richard; Peters, Tanner M; Hancock, Joseph B; Polidura, Anthony L
abstract: Gone Fishing is a VR gam e that allows the player to fish from the comfort of their own hom e. This take on a fishing simulator has creative and playful designs that are sure to surprise the players. With this gam e, we intend to invoke different comedic aspects found in other gam es such as designs, descriptions, and possible voiceovers in order to give the players a good tim e. This isn’t the average fishing simulator.

suggestions.title: Tactile Haptic Struggle Feedback
suggestions.desc: Program specialized haptic patterns for VR controllers that mimic the unique 'struggle' of different fish (e.g., erratic vibrations for small fish vs. sustained heavy resistance for large ones) to improve realism.

suggestions.title: Procedural Comedic Fish Generator
suggestions.desc: Build a system that dynamically generates 'absurd' fish models by combining random 3D parts and generating funny descriptions using a lightweight local LLM like Llama-3.

suggestions.title: Social VR Fishing Tournaments
suggestions.desc: Implement Photon Unity Networking (PUN) to allow players to fish together in the same virtual environment, including a global leaderboard for the most ridiculous fish caught.

suggestions.title: Casting Gesture Recognition System
suggestions.desc: Use the VR controller's gyroscope data to train a 1D Convolutional Neural Network that evaluates the 'form' of the player's cast, rewarding real-world fishing techniques with better in-game items.

title: Spudify
authors: Bintliff, Nathanial R, Nguyen, Jim my V, Holm es, Tyler, Tawara, Alex M, Christian, Addison
abstract: Spotify’s yearly wrapped report is extrem ely popular amongst its users. Unfortunately users must wait a year from every report to view statistics about their listening habits. Our app will allow users to generate reports displaying their top songs and artists whenever they want. Additionally, our app will allow users to generate recom mendations for new music based on their favorite songs/artists. Users will also be able to generate advanced Academ ic courses undergraduate (e.g. capstones, gam es, innovative special topics projects, other course projects) (23)

suggestions.title: Social Listening Compatibility Engine
suggestions.desc: Use WebSockets to allow two users to sync their Spudify reports and generate a 'Musical Compatibility' score and a shared playlist based on the intersection of their top artists.

suggestions.title: Genre-Shift Time Machine Visualizer
suggestions.desc: Develop a D3.js interactive timeline that uses a user's long-term Spotify history to visualize how their preferred genres have evolved month-over-month over several years.

suggestions.title: Live Discovery Concert Integrator
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Ticketmaster API to automatically suggest local concerts and ticket links based on the personalized 'New Recommendations' generated by the Spudify algorithm.

suggestions.title: Psychological Audio Profile Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Analyze Spotify's audio features (valence, danceability, acousticness) to provide users with a 'Musical Personality Profile' based on the mathematical properties of their most-played tracks.

title: Wild Fire: Friend Or Foe
authors: Henderson, Christian R; Jennings, Revian C; Shelton, Kwentin
abstract: The U.S. Army Corp of Engineers in Carters Lake required a game that attempts to educate on the principles of both prescribed and non-prescribed fires. They request that the game be playable on an android device housed in a HOLOFIL holographic display. Additionally, it needed to be created in the mirrored format as the projection flips everything. We decided to use Unity Real-Time Development Platform to complete the game’s development. Unity is a set of tools to give content creators an all-in-one development environment that helps content creators develop games and applications. “Unity is royalty-free and does not charge on a per-title basis or require revenue sharing of your application (Unity, 2022).”

suggestions.title: Biometric-Linked Difficulty Scaling
suggestions.desc: Integrate a wearable SDK (like Polar or Garmin) to feed real-time heart rate data into the Unity DDA engine. If a player's stress levels (heart rate variability) spike during the fire simulation, the game can automatically simplify the mechanics to prioritize learning over frustration.

suggestions.title: Urban Fire and Reforestation Modules
suggestions.desc: Extend the simulator beyond forest fires to include 'Urban Interface' scenarios where fire meets residential areas. Add a post-game phase where players must use a budget to replant the forest, teaching the long-term ecological consequences of fire management.

suggestions.title: Holofil vs. Standard VR Retention Study
suggestions.desc: Build a data-logging backend to track player performance and knowledge retention. Conduct a formal study comparing the HOLOFIL holographic display against traditional 2D Android screens and VR headsets to determine which medium leads to better fire safety education.

suggestions.title: The 'Holo-DDA' Universal Educational Framework
suggestions.desc: Decouple the DDA logic and holographic mirroring setup into a reusable Unity Package. Market this framework to other government agencies (like the Park Service or FEMA) to quickly convert their 2D safety training modules into interactive holographic experiences.

title: GTRI: IT Management Web Application
authors: Oliver, Joshua D, Bryant, Teryn, Ham rick, Renate M, Hansford-Bellam y, Kendra, McGilberry, Terrell B
abstract: This application was part of an industry Capstone project with Georgia Tech Research Institute, with Mr. Matthew Pinkston as the industry sponsor, Ms. Allison Boyle as the industry Capstone coordinator, and Mr. Donald Privitera as the Capstone professor. Our team worked on creating the framework for an opensource, web-based IT management application that future developers can add various features and sub-applications onto for purposes such as asset and budget management. The application is PHP-based with its backend, utilizing the Laravel framework and several of its built-in features for routing, database management, login authentication with bcrypt hash security, and session tracking. The frontend design of the web application was done using HTML, Bootstrap, and jQuery. MySQL was used for the database, which is currently used primarily for the user authentication system. We have chosen the MIT licensing model to ensure future developers can easily add onto the code and incorporate sub-applications into our framework, which was created with the intent of being a basis for developers to add functionality to as they see fit. It also ensures the application serves as a free, opensource solution for small IT management departments.
supervisor: Prof. Donald Privitera, Sponsor Matthew Pinkston

suggestions.title: PHP to FastAPI Microservices Migration
suggestions.desc: Refactor the PHP-based backend into a modern, containerized microservices architecture using Python/FastAPI. This allows for better integration of AI models and more scalable handling of asset management sub-applications.

suggestions.title: RAG-Powered Campus Policy Oracle
suggestions.desc: Implement a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system using LangChain and a vector database (like Pinecone). Feed the app campus IT manuals and policy PDFs so users can ask natural language questions about asset procurement or budget rules.

suggestions.title: Zero-Trust Asset Verification Module
suggestions.desc: Research and implement a 'Zero-Trust' security layer within the IT management framework. Develop a feature where every hardware asset must verify its identity through encrypted handshakes before it can be modified in the database, preventing unauthorized inventory tampering.

suggestions.title: Automated Vendor API Procurement
suggestions.desc: Build a module that connects the budget management feature to external vendor APIs (like CDW or Amazon Business). When an asset is flagged for replacement, the app could automatically generate price comparisons and pre-filled purchase requisitions.

title: Hemorrhage
authors: Respess, Daniel M, Brewer, Antonio S, Tran, Kenny, Li, Sandy, Watson, Rick B 9/8/23, 2:29 PM Fall 22 C-Day Program - College of Computing and Software Engineering
abstract: Hem orrhage is a fast-paced FPS action gam e with a focus on risky gam eplay and dodging enem y attacks. Fight your way through hordes of grotesque creatures and make it to the end! The player starts with lim ited health but can steal more from killing enem ies. Then, you can unleash this stored-up health to deal massive dam age to your foes! Will you choose to be an unkillable tank? Or a brutal glass cannon?

suggestions.title: Stored-Health Dynamic Difficulty System
suggestions.desc: Implement an algorithm that scales enemy aggression and spawn density based on the player’s current 'Stored Health' pool, ensuring the challenge remains high for 'Unkillable Tanks.'

suggestions.title: Procedural Body-Horror Level Generation
suggestions.desc: Transition the game to a roguelike structure by implementing a procedural level generator using the Wave Function Collapse algorithm, themed around the 'grotesque creatures' described.

suggestions.title: Player Risk-Preference Heatmap Analysis
suggestions.desc: Build a telemetry system to track and visualize where players choose to unleash their stored health vs. when they hoard it, using the data to balance the 'Glass Cannon' and 'Tank' playstyles.

suggestions.title: Competitive Speedrun Leaderboard with Replay
suggestions.desc: Develop a cloud backend using Firebase to host global leaderboards that track clear times and 'Health Stolen' metrics, including a feature to record and share high-score gameplay clips.

title: SP12 - Studio Buddy - Mobile Application
authors: Zicavo, Alejandro, Moloi, Tubatsi, Kinm akon, Elm iche
abstract: This application is a collaboration between the Computer Science department and the Music department. The Music department has a use case for a Mobile Application and we will be able to use the capstone project to build this solution. Studio Buddy is a mobile application that will allow teachers and students to connect for coaching sessions. Teachers will be able to post practice material [hands-on and musical in nature] and assign the material to students. Teachers will be able to track progress for their students and better tailor each students practice exercises. This is not a replacement for any existing teaching technologies but instead a complement to allow for more seamless and directed instrumental practice.

suggestions.title: Real-time Audio Pitch & Rhythm Analysis
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Essentia.js or TarsosDSP library to allow the app to 'listen' to students while they practice assigned material. The app could provide immediate visual feedback on pitch accuracy and tempo directly on the teacher's dashboard for review.

suggestions.title: Music Studio Gamification & Rewards System
suggestions.desc: Implement a 'Practice Streak' and 'Badge' system using a backend like Firebase. Teachers can set point values for different practice materials, and students can compete on a leaderboard or unlock 'Studio Credits' for achieving milestones in their coaching sessions.

suggestions.title: Asynchronous Video Masterclass Feedback
suggestions.desc: Build a frame-by-frame video annotation tool. Students upload their performance of assigned material, and teachers can leave timestamped voice and text comments on specific technical movements (like hand positioning or bowing technique) within the mobile interface.

suggestions.title: Collaborative Sheet Music Annotation
suggestions.desc: Develop a shared canvas feature where teachers and students can mark up PDF scores simultaneously. Using WebSockets, any fingering or dynamic markings made by the teacher on their tablet would appear instantly on the student’s mobile device during a coaching session.

title: StewardShip
authors: Williams, Krystale A, Merrill, Jaeleon K, Jansen, Christian Thom as, Kroleski, Castle L
abstract: “StewardShip” is a virtual reality (VR) survival game set in a subterranean cave on an alien world. The game is a narrative-driven experience centered around the titular Stewards, a series of crewmembers awoken by the mining ship they inhabit after something catastrophic occurs, causing the ship to become irreparably buried. The player takes on the role of these successive Stewards to survive, explore, and uncover the mystery of how they ended up buried on an alien planet. Each Steward emerges into a unique environment, as the circumstances of the previous Steward impact how the setting has evolved during the time passed. Ultimately, the Stewards work towards the goal of uncovering the mystery of their situation and escaping the planet alive. The game's main focus is to give a new meaning for death and what it could mean for the player. With each Seward being awoken sometime after another dies, the player will have different experiences with each steward, who doesn't know that they are not the first. This also allows the passage of time from the death of one steward and the waking of another, causing changes in the environment which gives the player new and interesting things to explore.

suggestions.title: Procedural Cave Biome Generation
suggestions.desc: Replace static levels with a procedural generation engine using Perlin noise or Marching Cubes in Unity/Unreal. This would ensure that each new 'Steward' faces a uniquely structured subterranean environment with different resource layouts, increasing replayability.

suggestions.title: Biometric-Linked Environmental Stress
suggestions.desc: Integrate VR headset heart-rate sensors (like those found in Quest or specialized straps) to influence the game world. If the player's heart rate increases, the cave's lighting could dim or 'alien' sound effects could intensify, creating a dynamic survival experience driven by the player's actual fear.

suggestions.title: Asynchronous Multiplayer 'Ghost' System
suggestions.desc: Implement a system where players can find the 'remains' of previous Stewards played by other real-world users. Players can leave 'Steward Logs' (audio or text) in the VR environment for others to find, creating a community-driven narrative of survival and failure.

suggestions.title: Advanced Physics-Based Mining Interactions
suggestions.desc: Develop a specialized VR interaction system for 'alien mining' tools that uses haptic feedback and physics-based fracturing. Instead of simple clicks, players must use precise physical motions to extract resources from rocks without triggering 'cave-ins' or damaging the tools.

title: GTRI: Analysis of Alternatives
authors: Pham, Jake D, Huballah, Zayda, Jones, Evans, Modi, Roshni, Bado, Salim ata
abstract: The Capstone project Analysis of Alternatives focuses on research and testing workstation deployment software and see if they can fulfill certain requirements that GTRI has requested. Planning and research was first conducted on ten different software that are available on the market where each software was evaluated on a point based system based on the requirements given to us by the company. After researching the ten software three are chosen that best fit the requirements from the company and are then moved to the testing phase. After testing the three chosen software the team will collectively decide which one to recommend to the company with a detailed analysis.

suggestions.title: Automated Evaluation Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Build a web-based decision support system (DSS) that allows GTRI employees to input new software candidates and weight requirements. The tool would automatically calculate scores based on the project's original point system and generate a visual 'Winner Matrix' and sensitivity analysis.

suggestions.title: Infrastructure-as-Code (IaC) Deployment POC
suggestions.desc: Take the top 3 chosen softwares and create a fully automated Proof of Concept (POC) using Terraform and Ansible. This would allow GTRI to deploy a 'Golden Image' of the selected workstation software across a test environment with a single command to verify the theoretical research.

suggestions.title: Container-Based Workstation Virtualization
suggestions.desc: Research the feasibility of using Docker or Kubernetes for 'ephemeral workstations' as an alternative to traditional deployment. Compare the performance, security, and update-latency of containerized environments against the top 3 market softwares identified in the project.

suggestions.title: Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) API Integration
suggestions.desc: Develop a script that pulls live pricing data from cloud providers (AWS, Azure) and enterprise licensing APIs for the top software candidates. This provides a real-time financial comparison that accounts for scaling, maintenance, and per-seat costs over a 5-year period.

title: Grocery Application
authors: Lyles-Woods, Quin’darius A, Maksym czuk, Adam, Le-Beard, Aidan, Sharm a, Jeetu, Sor, Danny
abstract: This project is being completed for Kennesaw State University’s CS 4850 - Computer Science Senior Project undergraduate capstone course. This project as described is a dynamic grocery list mobile app that updates all members when items are added. The app then must contain groups that the user is able to join, have a list that is shared between that group, and have the ability for all users in the group to add and remove items from the list. To notify the users, push notifications are likely to be used. In addition to having a shared list, it makes sense that each individual user must have their own individual list, where they can add and remove items, before adding them to their group’s shared list.

suggestions.title: ML-Powered Smart Receipt & Pantry Tracking
suggestions.desc: Integrate Google ML Kit to allow users to scan their paper grocery receipts. The app would automatically add the items to a 'Pantry' list and track expiration dates, notifying the group when items are about to go bad to reduce food waste.

suggestions.title: API-Driven Price Comparison & Local Deals
suggestions.desc: Connect the grocery list to the Kroger or Walmart Open APIs. When a group member adds 'Milk', the app can show which nearby store has the lowest price or current digital coupons, helping the household save money during their weekly shopping trip.

suggestions.title: Recipe-to-List One-Click Integration
suggestions.desc: Add a feature that allows users to paste a URL from popular cooking sites (like AllRecipes). Use a web scraper or Natural Language Processing to extract ingredients and automatically populate the group’s shared shopping list, filtering out items the group already has in their pantry.

suggestions.title: Predictive 'Out of Stock' Suggestions
suggestions.desc: Implement a machine learning model (using TensorFlow Lite) that analyzes the group's shopping frequency. The app can then suggest adding items like 'Toilet Paper' or 'Eggs' to the shared list before they actually run out, based on past consumption patterns.

title: GTRI: IT Web Management Project
authors: Sunar, Vanessa, Duong, Scott, NGAPPI, Firm in, Schm it, Meva H, Mah, Stephan
abstract: This project is meant to develop a baseline web application that will support development of future tools for managing an IT department (licensing, asset tracking, budget management, self-service software/configuration, issue management/tickets, etc.) As well as provide a free IT Management tool that an IT manager can easily access and use. Having an IT management tool to aid with different things such as tracking, budget management and more is very essential to efficiently run an IT department. There are various tools to assist with that which exist today. However, as pointed out by our client GTRI, a lot of them are poorly designed. Hence, our project seeks to help solve this issue by developing a baseline web application that will support development of future tools for managing an IT department and provide functionalities
supervisor: Prof. Donald Privitera, Dr. Reza Parizi, Matthew Pinkston

suggestions.title: Network Auto-Discovery & Asset Mapping
suggestions.desc: Extend the asset tracking module by implementing a network scanner (using SNMP or Nmap) that automatically identifies hardware connected to the corporate network. This would populate the IT management tool's database without manual entry, identifying unauthorized 'shadow IT' devices.

suggestions.title: AI-Driven Helpdesk Sentiment Analysis
suggestions.desc: Integrate a Large Language Model (LLM) into the issue management/ticket system. The AI could analyze the 'urgency' and 'frustration' levels in ticket text to automatically prioritize critical infrastructure failures over minor configuration requests for the IT manager.

suggestions.title: Cloud Spend & Budget Visualization Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Create a module that connects to the AWS Cost Explorer or Azure Billing APIs. This would allow the IT manager to see their cloud infrastructure costs alongside physical asset costs in one unified 'Budget Management' view, providing a complete financial picture.

suggestions.title: QR-Based Physical Asset Lifecycle Management
suggestions.desc: Develop a mobile companion app that allows IT staff to print and scan QR codes for physical hardware. Scanning a laptop would instantly pull up its maintenance history, assigned user, and warranty status from the web application's database, streamlining the audit process.

title: IT4983 Cybersecurity Capstone
authors: White, Jordan, Woodm an, Stephen C, Owens, Jenny, Gom ez, Hector, Scott, Aaron
abstract: For the Cybersecurity capstone project, our team was given a webserver and website for the company Akwaaba. We were tasked to fix all vulnerabilities, keep the server up to date, and help maintain the site with uptime being the priority. After the first two milestones were complete, we were to attempt to hack another team’s server while they attempted to do the same to us. When the attack phase began on Wednesday 10/26, our team discovered the other team had not changed any of their original passwords, so we took control within thirty minutes and took it down soon after. As of 10/30, their site is still down while we haven’t noticed any issues with our own. The attack phase has two weeks to go (ending on 11/14), so our group is actively monitoring both servers. Should the other team recover their server, we will make attempts to get back in and shut it down again. In the meantime, we have full confidence in our own server and do not anticipate any downtime.

suggestions.title: Automated DevSecOps Pipeline for Akwaaba
suggestions.desc: Implement a CI/CD pipeline (using GitHub Actions or Jenkins) that automatically runs vulnerability scanners like OWASP ZAP or Snyk whenever a change is made to the website. This ensures that the 'fix' applied by the team doesn't regress during future updates.

suggestions.title: Honeypot Deployment & Threat Intelligence
suggestions.desc: Set up a high-interaction honeypot (like Cowrie) on a sub-domain of the Akwaaba server. Monitor the specific tactics and scripts used by opposing teams to attack the honeypot, and use that 'Threat Intel' to preemptively harden the primary web server against those specific techniques.

suggestions.title: Real-time SIEM Dashboard Integration
suggestions.desc: Deploy an ELK Stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana) to provide a real-time security dashboard for the Akwaaba server. Create custom visualizations for failed SSH attempts, SQL injection patterns, and unauthorized file changes to improve the team's incident response time.

suggestions.title: Automated Penetration Testing Framework
suggestions.desc: Build a modular 'Attack Script' library using Python and Metasploit modules tailored to the capstone environment. This tool would automate initial reconnaissance and 'low-hanging fruit' exploits (like the default password check discovered in the project) to speed up the offensive phase.

title: INDY-5 CompChores
authors: Berry, Myers; Snyder, Ian
abstract: Getting anyone motivated to complete chores can be a chore itself! To solve this issue, our team is using Dart and Flutter to build a desktop application that pits roommates, family members, and siblings against one another to complete chores for points. The purpose of this program is to simulate competition between users in order to motivate users to complete tasks amongst the living space. To complete this task, our program will provide a place where users can create a family, create an account, join a family, create chores, complete chores, check chore history, view the family scoreboard, and view how many chores they have completed. Users will be able to change chore points based on difficulty, estimated time of completion, and priority. There will also be a way for users to check chore history in order to know which chores were completed by each person. The family will have a main homepage which will show the top three scores within the family, the chores with the highest priority, and the family ID in which users can share with one another to allow others to join the family. Then for more detail there will be a chore page, a scoreboard page, and a settings page to change account information or leave a family. All of these pages and features will allow users to create an environment amongst their peers that is not only productive but is also fun!

suggestions.title: Computer Vision Chore Verification
suggestions.desc: Integrate TensorFlow Lite into the Flutter app to verify chore completion. For example, a user could take a photo of a 'clean sink,' and the model would verify if the task was actually performed before awarding points, preventing 'point-cheating' between roommates.

suggestions.title: IoT Smart-Plug 'Auto-Chore' Tracking
suggestions.desc: Connect the app to smart home APIs (like Tuya or Home Assistant). Tasks like 'vacuuming' or 'doing laundry' can be automatically marked as complete when the associated smart plug detects a power draw, removing the need for manual logging.

suggestions.title: The 'Chore Economy' Reward Marketplace
suggestions.desc: Develop a 'Store' module where points can be redeemed for real-world rewards. Integrate with the Venmo or PayPal API to allow roommates to 'pot' money that is automatically paid out to the user with the most points at the end of the month.

suggestions.title: Multi-Tenant 'Dorm League' Support
suggestions.desc: Expand the Flutter desktop application to support hierarchical groups. This would allow an entire university dorm or apartment complex to have 'Floor Leagues' where different suites compete against each other, with an admin dashboard for the RA or Property Manager to track building-wide cleanliness.

title: Buggy - Price Scraper Application
authors: Fitzgerald, David W; Blake, John; Guse, Anderson F.; Zardoya, Jessica; Allen, Paul
abstract: Buggy is a desktop price scraper application that finds the best deals for users across several major retailers. Retailers include Am azon, Costco, Target, Walm art, and eBay. It simplifies bargain hunting by condensing product information from multiple websites into one, convenient place. Users can save products for future access, track search history, and compare search results with tabs for navigation. When they are ready to buy, they can simply click on the buy button and are directed to the vendor's page to check out.

suggestions.title: Browser Extension Price Overlay
suggestions.desc: Transform the desktop logic into a Chrome/Firefox extension. When a user visits a product page on Amazon, the extension should automatically inject a small UI element showing if the same item is currently cheaper on eBay or Walmart.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Stock 'Sniper' Bot
suggestions.desc: Modify the scraper to focus on high-demand, low-stock items (like GPUs or limited sneakers). Use headless Playwright browsers and Twilio to send an instant SMS notification to the user the second an item goes from 'Out of Stock' to 'In Stock.'

suggestions.title: ML Price Trend Predictor
suggestions.desc: Collect historical price data in a Firebase database. Use an LSTM (Long Short-Term Memory) neural network to analyze price fluctuations and provide a 'Buy or Wait' recommendation based on whether the current price is likely to drop soon.

suggestions.title: The 'Second-Hand' Sustainability Scraper
suggestions.desc: Pivot the application to focus exclusively on used markets like Facebook Marketplace, Mercari, and Poshmark. Build a specialized parser that can filter out 'promoted' listings to find genuine peer-to-peer deals for sustainable shopping.

title: Red Pepper IT: IT Web Management Application and Content Management System
authors: Kim, Eun; Adekunle, Bukky; Washington, Caitlin; Dedani, Neha; George, Ivana
abstract: Red Pepper IT is an application developed for Georgia Tech Research Institute. The conception of this project arose from the lack of IT management tools that can address all the needs of an IT department. Furthermore, with the currently available tools, they are either poorly designed or have a high licensing cost. This project seeks to solve the need for such applications by building an open-source application that can be deployed internally by any business seeking a management tool for its IT department.
supervisor: Dr. Reza Parizi, Course Instructor; Mr. Matthew Pinkston, GTRI

suggestions.title: Dockerized Multi-Tenant Deployment Architecture
suggestions.desc: Containerize the Red Pepper IT application using Docker and Kubernetes to allow managed service providers (MSPs) to host isolated instances for multiple clients on a single server infrastructure.

suggestions.title: SNMP-Based Auto-Discovery Asset Scanning
suggestions.desc: Build a background worker service using Python's pysnmp library that scans local networks to automatically populate the CMS inventory with hardware specs, MAC addresses, and active services.

suggestions.title: Enterprise SSO and LDAP Integration Suite
suggestions.desc: Extend the authentication module to support SAML 2.0 and OpenID Connect (OIDC), allowing the tool to integrate directly with Active Directory or Okta for seamless employee onboarding.

suggestions.title: Predictive Maintenance Analytics Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Implement a machine learning module using Scikit-learn that analyzes historical ticket data and server uptimes to predict hardware failures before they occur, alerting IT staff via a custom notification engine.

title: Defunct to Funct: Expanding the Functionality of Forgotten Robots
authors: Stockdale, Thom as E, Ho, Khoa, Huynh, Johnny, Nguiagaing Seuga, Marcel Youri
abstract: The field of robotics is an expanding landscape, pushing the frontiers of engineering, machine vision, artificial intelligence and more. The robotics industry also has a foothold in many areas, such as consumer markets, scientific research, industrial and medical applications, and even exploration. With the growing interest in automated machines, individuals who can work with these machines are in demand, and providing a way to learn the skills to do so would be just as valuable. Kennesaw State University is currently in possession of two UXA-90 Humanoid robots; both of which have remained in storage for extended periods of time since their purchase in the mid 2010’s. They have been intermittently worked on, but prior groups were unable to make the robots functional. Our project is to assess the robots, attempt to bring them back online, and then to explore their functionality in the hopes that current and future students would be able to utilize these robots in different projects. We utilized the manuals that accompanied the robots, as well as the software documentation for the Robot Operating System (ROS) in order to access the robot’s internal computer and diagnose the problems with the preloaded programs. By fixing the problems in the preloaded programs, we are now able to expand on the functionality of the robot with things such as voice control and face recognition.
topics: Other (explain in the com m ents section)

suggestions.title: Modern ROS2 Humble Migration
suggestions.desc: Port the legacy control scripts from the forgotten hardware to the Robot Operating System 2 (ROS2) Humble Hawksbill to enable modern features like DDS middleware and enhanced real-time performance.

suggestions.title: Computer Vision Gesture Control Interface
suggestions.desc: Integrate an OpenCV-based module using MediaPipe to allow users to control the defunct robots via hand gestures captured by a standard webcam, bypassing the need for original proprietary controllers.

suggestions.title: Gazebo-Based Digital Twin Simulation
suggestions.desc: Create high-fidelity URDF models of the hardware to build a Gazebo simulation environment, allowing students to test code virtually before deploying to the physical 'defunct' units to prevent hardware damage.

suggestions.title: Edge AI Object Tracking with Jetson Nano

title: RESTful Robots
authors: Young, Jack I, Com ella, Derek M, Thom as, Sarah, Loveless, Andrew
abstract: The UXA-90 Robots have been sitting idle at Kennesaw State University for years. The only documentation provided were factory manuals, and there was nothing additional found online. The first step was to conduct a risk assessment and report the results to Professor Perry and Dr. Pei. The objective of the risk assessment was to determine the viability of the robots and the feasibility of three different senior project teams using them for a project. Once the risk assessment was completed and reported it was determined that all three teams could proceed with their senior projects. However, it was recommended that this team, SP-1 RED, develop a robot handling and training program and conduct training and certification of all other members of the other teams. The training and certification was conducted from September 14th through September 15th and documented online with a documentation website for all teams to reference. The robots have the ability to move, walk, see (through a webcam), hear and speak (using built in speakers and microphones). The robots consist of: * An internal mini-PC running Ubuntu 14.04 LTS * Serial-over-USB communication ports * SAM interface motor control boards * RF remote control * USB HD webcam * Internal microphone and speakers The goal of this team, SP-1 RED is to increase the accessibility and usability of the UXA-90 robot including a REST API, documentation and training.

suggestions.title: React-Based Robot Command & Control GUI

suggestions.title: Multi-Robot Fleet Synchronization Layer

suggestions.title: Voice-Activated Command Parser

suggestions.title: Automated Stress and Failure Documentation Tool
suggestions.desc: Create a logging microservice that records every API call and motor response to generate automated 'health reports' and identify mechanical fatigue patterns over time.

title: Network Simulation Software Analysis of Alternatives
authors: McCannon, Justin, Marsonia, Nidhi, McInnis, Michael, Nguyen, Tiffany, Uddin, Azm
abstract: Data networking is a complex field. Designing networks is a complex task. Simulators have been developed to create hypothetical designs with configuration settings for evaluating architectures and settings. At least 2 simulators are freely available to IT professionals and students. This project involves researching the landscape of free network design simulators to determine how many there are, then downloading, testing, evaluating, and documenting the features of each by designing, on each, a network with multiple routers, switches, and host devices using IPv4 and IPv6, defining features appropriate for classroom use in a university, and finally determining which solution fits the classroom requirements using a vector shortest distance analysis.

suggestions.title: Python-Driven Topology Generator
suggestions.desc: Write a script using the GNS3 Python API to automatically generate complex network topologies based on a simple YAML configuration file, streamlining the benchmarking process for large-scale designs.

suggestions.title: SDN Integration with OpenDaylight
suggestions.desc: Extend the simulation analysis to include Software Defined Networking (SDN) controllers like OpenDaylight to evaluate how virtualized network management performs compared to traditional static configurations.

suggestions.title: Automated Pentesting Simulation Plugin
suggestions.desc: Integrate a security testing layer using Kali Linux virtual nodes within the simulator to automate the 'Security Posture' score of different network architectures under simulated DDoS or MITM attacks.

suggestions.title: Cloud-Native Infrastructure Evaluator
suggestions.desc: Apply the analysis framework to cloud-specific simulators like AWS SimSpace Weaver to compare the cost-to-performance ratio of on-premise physical topologies versus cloud-based virtual networks.

title: Akwaaba Web Server Security Project
authors: Vann, Jayland; Rogers, Ty G; Reese, Cody; Southern, Ronald W; Choudhury, Taiyeb
abstract: Our team engaged in a mock cybersecurity drill. Our objective was to take on the roll of cybersecurity consultants and to secure a mock business website. Afterwards, we were to engage in the active defense of the site from another team , while perpetuating attacks on another team 's site. Goals: - Secure a business website and then engage in it's active defense. -Perform a successful attack(s) on another team 's website. Outcom es: -Developed a comprehensive security policy -Docum ented site risk and vulnerabilities - Im plem ented security and configuration changes

authors: Wilson, Milo D, Fushim i, Aidan, Parham, Wade G, Anderson, Miller, Chim a, Kosi M
abstract: A cooking rogue-like gam e where you cook the weapons you fight with.

suggestions.title: Procedural Ingredient Evolution System

title: Cyber Security in Blockchain
authors: Weese, Eric C; Cooper, Eric G; Fortson, Alex
abstract: Blockchain is a growing technology that utilizes a decentralized public record of transactions. It is not only used for cryptocurrency, but also IoT, financial systems, healthcare, supply chain, etc. Although blockchain is quite robust, it is not invulnerable. This project will explain and dem onstrate several weaknesses of blockchain and how to prevent them . We will dem onstrate the following attacks and how to prevent them using Solidity: Transaction Order Dependence, Denial of Service, Replay Attack, Writing of Arbitrary Storage Address Attack, Weak Random ness, and Honeypot Attack.
supervisor: Project Owner: Dr. Yong Shi, Advisor/Instructor: Prof. Sharron Perry

suggestions.title: Solidity Static Analysis Extension
suggestions.desc: Build a plugin for the Slither static analysis framework that specifically detects the Transaction Order Dependence and Replay attack patterns identified in this project.

suggestions.title: Mempool Front-Running Guard
suggestions.desc: Create a Node.js monitoring service that watches the Ethereum mempool for pending transactions that match TOD attack patterns and alerts contract owners in real-time.

suggestions.title: Cross-Chain Replay Attack Comparative Study
suggestions.desc: Research and demonstrate if the project's Replay attack mitigations hold true on Layer 2 scaling solutions like Arbitrum or Optimism, documenting differences in transaction finality.

suggestions.title: Blockchain-IoT Secure Gateway
suggestions.desc: Implement the project's Solidity security patterns in a private Hyperledger Fabric network designed to secure firmware updates for low-power IoT devices.

title: Cybersecurity Park
authors: Weingarten, Neil E; Hendrick, JaDante; Nowokunski, Kylie; Crawford, Tyler
abstract: Cybersecurity Park is an educational VR game intended for middle-school-age children that aims to demonstrate a wide range of cybersecurity concepts to the players. Such concepts include hacking ethics and types of hackers, cryptography, Trojan Horse / ransomware viruses, and authentication and authorization. These concepts are split into various mini-games that the player can freely navigate to from the hub they spawn in. For example, in the mini-game showcasing the Trojan Horse concept, players play as a knight defending a castle from evildoers. Visitors will approach the castle and ask access into the castle, and, based on the actions by the visitors, the player will choose whether or not to allow access into the castle. The player acts as a firewall, and the visitors act like applications requesting access into a computer. If a bad visitor/application is let into the castle (representing a computer), then the castle will begin to catch fire. This one of six mini-games present within this game, and video demonstrations of some of these mini-games are provided.
supervisor: Dr. Joy Li - Supervisor, Course Instructor; Dr. Yan Huang - Owner

suggestions.title: Social Engineering NPC Dialogue System
suggestions.desc: Add a new level where players must identify phishing attempts through a branching dialogue system with AI-driven NPCs, teaching the human element of cybersecurity.

suggestions.title: Multiplayer Collaborative CTF Mode
suggestions.desc: Implement a multiplayer feature where students must work together to 'defuse' a ransomware virus by solving cryptographic puzzles in tandem within the VR environment.

suggestions.title: WebXR Accessibility Port
suggestions.desc: Use the Unity WebXR Export to make the game playable in standard web browsers, ensuring students without expensive VR headsets can still participate in the educational mini-games.

suggestions.title: Learning Analytics Tracking Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Build a backend using Firebase to track player progress and common mistakes, providing teachers with a dashboard to identify which cybersecurity concepts the class is struggling to master.

title: A WEB APPLICATION DEVELOPED IN COLLABORATION BETWEEN THE CS 4850 SENIOR PROJECT COURSE AND THE KSU DEPT OF HISTORY AND PHILOSOPY
authors: Wilson, Leafy, Garcia Ortiz, Alexa, Morella, Mae B., Meyer, Braxton M
abstract: The Vada Project is is an upgrade to an existing web application for a collaborative debate platform used to facilitate good faith argum entation and evidence based reasoning using classical Indian theories of knowledge sources (pram a?a vada). This web application is designed for classroom use to facilitate critical thinking, debating, and archiving argum ents and counterargum ents as structured by an Indian philosophical debate style known as Vada. The goals of this project are to design an intuitive and usable interface (HTML+CSS), a stable back-end (PHP+MySQL), and a maintainable codebase managed using static-analysis tools and continuous integration (GitHub Actions).
supervisor: Prof. Sharon Perry – Department of Com puter Science, Dr. Am y

suggestions.desc: Develop a Python-based microservice using a BERT model fine-tuned on philosophical texts to flag departures from Vada rules or logical fallacies in real-time during a debate.

suggestions.desc: Create a GraphQL API that allows external researchers to query the archived 'Vada' debates, enabling meta-analysis of how Indian theories of knowledge are applied in modern classroom settings.

suggestions.title: Cross-Cultural Logic Toggle
suggestions.desc: Extend the application to support both Indian Vada and Western Syllogistic logic structures, allowing users to 'referee' the same debate under two different philosophical frameworks.

title: On Training Explainable Neurons
authors: Kennedy, Lance
abstract: Neural networks have becom e increasingly powerful and commonplace tools for guiding decision-m aking. However, due to the black-box nature of many of these networks, it is often difficult to Graduate Research (14)

title: Parsimonics: Achieving High Classification Accuracy even with High Dimensional Image Reduction
authors: Owens, Joshua
abstract: In an age of big data, being able to “do more with less” is important. We show that by following a minim alist paradigm , or “parsim onics”, high image classification accuracy can be achieved even with massively reduced-size datasets. This lets us “do nearly as much with way less”. This idea was tested against a dataset of 87,000 Am erican Sign Language (ASL) alphabet images, yielding high accuracy results of greater than 99% over multiple iterations. These results could have implications for how big data is handled because the data needed to meet objectives may not need to be so big after all.

suggestions.title: Real-time Edge ASL Translator
suggestions.desc: Port the parsimonious ASL model to a low-power microcontroller like the ESP32-S3 or Raspberry Pi Zero to demonstrate high-accuracy sign language recognition on a sub-$10 device.

suggestions.title: Automated Dimensionality Break-Point Search
suggestions.desc: Develop a tool using Optuna or Hyperopt that automatically determines the 'Parsimonious Threshold'—the smallest possible image dimension for any dataset before accuracy drops below a user-defined limit.

suggestions.title: Parsimonics in Medical Time-Series
suggestions.desc: Test the parsimony paradigm on high-dimensional ECG or EEG data to determine if massive dimensionality reduction can maintain diagnostic accuracy while reducing transmission bandwidth for remote clinics.

suggestions.title: Privacy-Preserving Vision Pipeline
suggestions.desc: Evaluate the use of parsimonious image reduction as a privacy feature, proving that high classification can be achieved even when images are reduced to a point where human faces are unidentifiable.

title: Operating System (OS) Security: Identifying Vulnerable Logging Events and Logging Optimization to Detect OS Threats
authors: Faruk, Md Jobair Hossain
abstract: Modern operating systems have security threats from different perspectives, logging for instance. Logging refers to a collection of computer activities uses for statistical purposes, and it becomes a fundamental feature of OS. Analysis of events of logs allows not only the detection and debugging of OS but also configuration errors of applications. Cyberattackers can utilize logging information that can jeopardize operating systems' security. In this research paper, we investigate operating systems logging mechanisms of modern operating systems where we focus on Microsoft Windows OS. We introduce different security issues related to logging and emphasize the selection and optimization of logging events for the Windows 11 event viewer to find features of the logging service that can be exploited by malicious individuals to conduct vulnerability in OS. We identify various unwanted logging features that are vulnerable to the operating system's security. We also identify features for system tuning, intrusion detection, authentication, session management, file system, runtime, and connectivity errors along with virus detection and configuration changes. These logging features also can be adopted for forensics purposes to identify fraud, suspicious, or other cybercrime activities utilizing user's activities. According to our preliminary findings, identified logging features should be discarded or improved to enhance OS security and increase the level of logging events protection for users. In our extended study, we will consider including the available open-source vulnerable logging dataset to provide a comparison between previous and identified logging vulnerabilities.

suggestions.title: ML-Driven Log Anomaly Detection
suggestions.desc: Use the Isolation Forest algorithm to analyze OS logs in real-time and automatically flag unusual event sequences that correlate with lateral movement or privilege escalation.

suggestions.title: Blockchain-Based Immutable Audit Log
suggestions.desc: Implement a Hyperledger Fabric prototype that hashes and stores log entries on a private blockchain to prevent attackers from clearing their tracks by deleting system logs.

suggestions.title: IoT Edge Logging Optimization
suggestions.desc: Adapt the research to resource-constrained devices (like Raspberry Pi) by creating an 'importance-based' log filter that prioritizes security events while minimizing storage wear-and-tear.

suggestions.title: Windows Event Forwarding (WEF) Scaler
suggestions.desc: Build a configuration suite that automates the deployment of WEF across an enterprise domain, centralizing the 'vulnerable logging events' identified in the research into a single SOC dashboard.

title: Solving Multiple Traveling Salesman Problem using K Means Clustering and Mixed Integer Programming - An Integrated Approach
authors: Verm a, Navneet
abstract: In this research paper, we explore an efficient algorithm for multiple Traveling Salesm an Problem (m -TSP) using an approach which combines K Means clustering algorithm and Mixed Integer Program ming (MIP). The Traveling Salesm an problem is an NP hard problem which relates to generation of minim um cost round trip tours for multiple salesm en visiting several cities in their territory. Our novel approach has the prom ise of reaching closer to the optim al solution as compared to heuristics-based approaches such as genetic algorithms.
topics: Other (explain in the com m ents section)

title: Churn Prediction
authors: DASARI, MANOJ KU MAR, Thota, Nikhil, Thota, Dinesh Kum ar, Basava, Rishi Sai, Sugguna, Prudhvi Kalyan Sai
abstract: Em ployee churn is a situation where people leave the organization voluntarily or involuntarily. This has becom e a serious problem in recent tim es. We have also seen that attrition rates in several industries are going high. So, it is very much required to understand and analyze the reason behind attrition and why this is happening. We must conduct an analysis to know what the factors affecting employee churn are. It will create a huge impact on the organization if the attrition rate goes high. In order to resolve this issue, we are trying to take up this issue and find the best solution for this.

suggestions.desc: Integrate SHAP or LIME libraries to provide managers with 'Reason Codes' for every predicted churn event. Build a frontend where HR can see which specific features (e.g., overtime hours, commute distance) contributed most to an individual's flight risk.

suggestions.title: Internal Mobility Recommender
suggestions.desc: Instead of just predicting who leaves, use the churn features to match high-risk employees with internal job openings. Use a Content-Based Filtering approach to suggest roles that mitigate the specific factors causing the churn risk.

suggestions.title: Temporal Sentiment Feature Engineering
suggestions.desc: Incorporate time-series data from internal Slack channels or Glassdoor reviews using Natural Language Processing. Analyze how changes in sentiment over 6 months correlate with turnover, moving the model from a static snapshot to a trend-aware predictor.

suggestions.title: Predictive 'What-If' Simulation Tool
suggestions.desc: Develop a simulation environment where leadership can adjust variables (e.g., 'What if we increase salaries by 5%?') to see the projected impact on the overall churn rate. This allows for data-driven budget allocation for retention programs.

title: Building a Chatbot
authors: Gottam, Varun, Chepyala, Sathwik, Saim pu, Sai Mohit, Yalavarthi, Venkata Sai krishna, Buddiga, Nikhil Sai
abstract: A chatbot is now a part of many online applications like Health Care, Education, E-commerce, etc. It made the conversation between the custom ers and the service providers much more convenient as the chatbot can answer most of the queries without hum an intervention from the website side. title: Automated Vulnerability Detection in Source Code Using Deep Neural Networks
authors: Akter, Mst Shapna
abstract: One of the most important challenges in the field of a software code audit is the presence of vulnerabilities in software source code. Every year, more and more software flaws are found, either internally in proprietary code or revealed publicly. These flaws are highly likely exploited and lead to system compromise, data leakage, or denial of service. C and C++ open-source code are now available in order to create a large-scale, machine-learning system for function-level vulnerability identification. We assembled a sizable dataset of millions of open-source functions that point to potential exploits. We created an efficient and scalable vulnerability detection method based on deep neural network models that learn features extracted from the source codes. To remove the pointless components and shorten the dependency, the source code is first converted into a minimal intermediate representation. We keep the semantic and syntactic information using state-of-the-art word embedding algorithms. The embedded vectors are subsequently fed into convolutional neural networks to classify the possible vulnerabilities. Furthermore, we proposed a new neural network model which seems to overcome issues associated with traditional neural networks. To measure the performance, we used evaluation metrics such as f1 score, precision, recall, accuracy, and total execution time.

In addition to disaster reduction programs, there are several ways to predict or reducing the risks using artificial intelligence technology. One of them is machine learning. By utilizing this method at the moment, it facilitates tasks in visualizing, analysing, and predicting natural disaster. This project will focus on conducting a review process and understanding the purpose of Machine learning in the area of disaster management and natural disaster. The result of this project is providing insight and the use of data, machine learning in disaster management area.

title: Comparative performance analysis of hybrid quantum machine learning algorithm to assess Post stroke rehabilitation exercises
authors: Murikipudi, Manohar, Azm ee, Abm . Adnan
abstract: Due to the advancements in technology, data is growing exponentially. With this increased dataset size, the computation to process the generated information is rising sequentially. And the currently available classical computational tools and learning algorithms will not work due to the limitations of Moore's law. To overcome the computational issues, we have to switch to Quantum Computing which works based on the laws of Quantum Mechanics. Quantum Machine Learning (QML), a subset of Quantum Computing, is faster and more capable of doing complex calculations that a classical computer can't. Classical Computers work on bits - 0 or 1, whereas a Quantum Bit (also known as a qubit) works on the superposition principle and can be 0 and 1 at the same time before it is measured. Other properties known as Quantum Entanglement, Quantum Parallelism, etc., also will help in understanding the other qubit state and parallel processing the data. In this paper, we introduce hybrid quantum and convolutional models built using PennyLane on the UI-PRMD dataset for the Kinect sensor. By involving quantum layers in a traditional network, a better performance can be achieved compared with the traditional neural network performance.
supervisor: Dr. Md Abdullah Al Ha?z Khan - Supervisor

title: Azure Security KQL Query Builder
authors: Sabbula, Navyapravalika; Kalipindi, Jeevana
abstract: We produce and manage petabytes of data every day in today's culture when everything is digitally recorded, from our web surfing habits to our medical records. Big data will significantly alter all facets of existence. However, simply processing and interpreting data is insufficient; when data is presented visually, the human brain can identify patterns more quickly. In many different businesses, data analytics and visualization are essential decision-making tools. The visualization field is also opened up, displaying innovative thinking for visualizing the big-data dilemma. It is challenging to see such massive volumes of data in static or real-time formats. we explain why large data visualization is crucial, outline its difficulties, and evaluate a few big data visualization solutions. These advancements include looking at how to use systems and techniques for data visualization to manipulate, understand, and show data more effectively. The potential of our Azure Security KQL Query Builder's new visualization technologies to improve the display of performance data collected as an output from simulation programs in the built environment area has mostly gone untapped. Azure Security KQL Query Builder, a set of business intelligence (BI), reporting, and data visualization tools and services for people and teams, curates the best practices and knowledge of thousands of customers to assist users, teams and your organization in becoming more data driven.

title: Improving Wildfire Propagation Simulations Using Cellular Automata to Help Emergency Management
authors: Frank, Madeline, Meesala, Lokesh, Bollineni, Yagnasree, Venkatesh, Am rutha, Shaik, Mujahid Shaheed
abstract: Discrete computational models known as cellular automata (CA) utilize discrete spatial cells, each existing in one of a set of possible states at any given moment. Transition rules specify how a given cell’s state evolves in subsequent time steps and is dependent on the states of the given cell’s neighborhood of surrounding cells. A cellular automaton model can also account for the influence of other external or physical factors on the evolution of a given cell’s state. These capabilities afforded by CA models make it an ideal tool to simulate the propagation of wildfires. One specific study carried out by Freire et al. [2019] sought to improve upon the benchmark concept by attempting to incorporating topographical, meteorological, and fuel loading factors into the transition rules of their probabilistic CA model. This project proposes two ways to improve the accuracy of their proposed CA to model wildfire propagation to reduce uncertainty intrinsic to a probabilistic approach. First, we attempt to incorporate fire weather indices that account for relevant meteorological, climatological, and fuel stress and flammability conditions that affect the ability of, and therefore the probability that, fuel within a cell can ignite within a given time step. Next, Freire et al. [2019] determined a constant burn rate to use for each time step based on empirical and probabilistic data that is specific to a certain fire they studied. The capability to account for burn rate for cells with varying fuel load factors will also be assessed in this paper. Ultimately, the purpose of these efforts is to increase the accuracy of fire propagation simulations using cellular automata models to assist emergency management and firefighters warn, evacuate, fight, and allocate resources to efficiently protect life and property.

In this research we apply a new version of CM which is CM-II as a guided psychotherapy and cognitive therapy meditation, to analyze its impact on reducing attention deficiency among students. This study uses Electroencephalography (EEG) data as a metric to analyze electrical activities of the brain that contribute to attention deficiency. We use a neural network as a machine learning classifying algorithm to analyze the EEG data to measure the impact of CM-II on students’ attention deficiency.

title: Object Detection and Tracking: Deep Learning-based Framework with Euclidean Distance, IoU, and Hungarian Algorithm
authors: Faruk, Md Jobair Hossain
abstract: Object tracking is an important basis for the logistics industry where multiple packages are moved on conveyor belts at a time. Accurate datasets and efficient benchmarks are a few of the several problems for both object detection and tracking for training the deep learning-based framework. Preparing 100% accurate correspondence between objects throughout different frames by assigning human annotated unique_attributes to train framework efficiently over ground truth data. In this research, we develop an (i) OpenCV-based framework that allows the user to assign human-annotated identification between objects and (ii) a novel application for object detection and tracking. We utilize the assigned attributes to train the deep learning model accurately and adopt various evaluation parameters including euclidean distance, intersection over union (IoU), and scale-invariant feature transform (SIFT) to measure the accuracy of an object correspondence or tracking. We also adopt the Hungarian algorithm to increase the efficiency in determining correspondences between objects and apply our framework to human-annotated ground truth datasets comprising ~1,000 images and the same amount of JSON files. Our demonstration achieved 94.53 % accuracy in object detection, finding correspondence, and object tracking. In future studies, we are aiming to apply a neural network to draw a comparison of identified accuracy.
supervisor: Dr. Selena He, Dr. Dan Lo

title: Title - GTRI IT Service Desk System
