abstract: This overarching project would be web developm ent, which would entail coding both the back end and the front end. The use of libraries is encouraged, but we must be cautious about licensing and ensure that this project rem ains as open-source as possible (a good open-source license ensures people can use, modify, redistribute, and sell without worry). This is a free and open-source project.
topics: Other (explain in the com m ents section)

suggestions.title: Automated NLP Ticket Triage
suggestions.desc: Implement a BERT-based NLP model in the backend to automatically categorize and route incoming service desk tickets. The model should identify 'High Priority' issues like server outages vs. 'Low Priority' password resets by analyzing the ticket text.

suggestions.title: Open-Source SSO Integration
suggestions.desc: Add a secure Single Sign-On (SSO) layer using Keycloak. This would allow institutions like GTRI to integrate the service desk with their existing employee directories (LDAP/Active Directory) while keeping the software open-source.

suggestions.title: Slack/Teams Service Bot
suggestions.desc: Develop a bot integration for enterprise chat platforms. Users should be able to type '/ticket' to open a request directly from Slack, which then communicates with the web app’s API to create and track the ticket status.

suggestions.title: License Compliance & Security Audit
suggestions.desc: Use tools like FOSSA to perform a full dependency and license audit of the project. Build a 'Compliance Dashboard' that ensures no GPL-violating or high-vulnerability libraries are used, making it truly enterprise-ready for GTRI.

title: A Systematic Literature Review on Dark Web
authors: Sobhan, Shahriar; Williams, Timothy; Mathew, Edwin; Rodriguez-Cardenas, Juanjose; Wright, Jack; Hossain, Md Jobair
abstract: The dark web is often discussed in taboo by many who are unfamiliar with the subject. However, this paper takes a dive into the skeleton of what constructs the dark web by compiling the research of published essays. The Onion Router (TOR) and other discussed browsers are specialized web browsers that provide anonymity by going through multiple servers and encrypted networks between the host and client, hiding the IP address of both ends. This provides difficulty in terms of controlling or monitoring the dark web, leading to its popularity in criminal underworlds. In this work, we provide an overview of data mining and penetration testing tools that are being widely used to crawl and collect data. We compare the tools to provide strengths and weaknesses of the tools while providing challenges of harnessing massive data from dark web using crawlers and penetration testing tools including machine learning (ML) techniques. Despite the effort to crawl dark web has progressed, there are still rooms to advance existing approaches to combat the ever-changing landscape of the dark web.

suggestions.title: Onion-Network Threat Intelligence Crawler
suggestions.desc: Build a Python-based crawler using the 'Stem' library to safely monitor specific Dark Web forums for leaked organizational keywords (e.g., '@ksu.edu'). The tool should store metadata in a NoSQL database for trend analysis without accessing illegal content.

suggestions.title: Interactive Privacy Visualization Tool
suggestions.desc: Develop a React-based educational sandbox that visualizes the 'Multi-layer Encryption' process of TOR. Users can 'send a packet' and see it bounce through nodes, demonstrating how IP addresses are masked at each hop compared to the standard web.

suggestions.title: Dark Web Credential Leak Scanner
suggestions.desc: Create a service that allows users to securely check if their emails appear in publicly indexed Dark Web data dumps. Use a 'k-Anonymity' model (similar to HaveIBeenPwned) to ensure the user’s email is never sent to the server in plain text.

suggestions.title: Post-Pandemic Market Trend Analysis
suggestions.desc: Perform a longitudinal study using archived Dark Web datasets (like those from DarkNetStats) to analyze how marketplace categories shifted post-COVID. Use Natural Language Processing to identify the rise of new 'taboo' categories vs. traditional ones.

title: Automated Image Colorization Through EfficientNet
authors: Cope, Troy W
abstract: Automatic Image Colorization is the procedure of transforming a gray-scale image into a colored image without any human intervention. This field is highly researched and strongly applicable to the real world due to: historic importance, data generation/augmentation, and human satisfaction. The main objective of this research is to develop an artificial intelligence feature extraction method that implements color into a gray-scale image. To solve this problem, I relied on transfer learning through the EfficientNet model. The problem was treated in multiple parts, those being: the processing of images into features, feature extraction using the model, and then colorization via the luminosity channel. My model outperformed the base model marginally but saved vastly on the time constraint even within my limited resources. As stated in the baseline paper, the implementation of EfficientNet generates colored images without fail. However, a more complex model, as well as an even more complex cost function, is required to truly evaluate automated image colorization.

suggestions.title: Temporal Consistency for Video Restoration
suggestions.desc: Extend the EfficientNet colorization model to handle historic black-and-white video by adding a Recurrent Neural Network (RNN) layer to ensure colors remain consistent across consecutive frames without flickering.

suggestions.title: High-Res Historical Restoration SaaS
suggestions.desc: Build a web-based platform that combines the EfficientNet colorizer with a GAN-based super-resolution model to colorize and upscale 19th-century photographs for use by historians and museums.

suggestions.title: Interactive User-Guided Colorization
suggestions.desc: Modify the model architecture to accept 'user hints'—small color dots placed on the grayscale image—as an additional input channel, allowing artists to guide the AI toward specific color palettes.

suggestions.title: Medical Imaging False-Color Enhancement
suggestions.desc: Apply the colorization technique to grayscale medical scans (MRI/CT) by training the model on a specialized medical dataset to highlight specific tissues or vascular structures for better diagnostic visibility.
