legacy:UC-035|Spring 2021|development of an automated software packaging solution for linux

title: Replicating Touch With MQ Telemetry Transport (MQTT) And Circuit Python: A Case Study of Internet-of-Things (IoT) Application
authors: Waym ond, Dam aris
abstract: This project investigates how haptics is currently being used to enable remote intimacy. Specifically, I attempt to use the MQTT protocol, along with wi-fi/BLE-enabled hardware running on Circuit Python, to replicate a kiss in real-time. Further, the lack of apps and/or tech that’s inclusive the those who feel socially isolated but still yearn for intimacy served as motivation for this research. Primary methods included sentiment analysis done using the Twitter API along with the Textblob Python library based on queries including strings like “hickey” and “sensitive neck.” Moreover, secondary research led us to Kissenger: a product by Emma Y. Zhang that serves as an example of how IoT is currently being used to address remote intimacy. This device, however, doesn’t incorporate pressure/vacuum. My prototype will, as well as enable the transmission of pressure data sans an iPhone, thus adding value to the haptics space. Additionally, setup guides and sample code from Adafruit.com were the basis for constructing the device. Their submodules were integral in composing logical code that handles connecting with and publishing/subscribing data to feeds on io.adafruit.com, Adafruit’s free pub/sub platform. Moreover, the hardware being used in this project includes: two microcontrollers equipped with a wi-fi enabled esp-32 chip, two I2C-capable pressure sensors, two 4.5V DC motors, solenoid air valves and the necessary drivers. Soldering was also required to connect headers to the sensors. But thanks to the QWIIC Connect feature on the Feather S2 mcu, I was able to simply plug in the wires coming from the sensor with little soldering, overall. Ultimately, this project is based on the hypothesis that two wi-fi/BLE-enabled devices can transmit/receive pressure data between one another with the help of a cloud broker. Doing so will mimic the vacuity of kissing and be a step towards the future of telepresence.

suggestions.title: Low-Latency Synchronized Haptic Feedback with ESP32 and WebSockets
suggestions.desc: Upgrade the Circuit Python codebase to C++ using ESP32 microcontrollers to leverage WebSockets instead of MQTT. This reduces the latency lag found in standard MQTT brokers, enabling a more 'real-time' physical sensation between two users.

suggestions.title: Closed-Loop Pressure Sensing via Force-Sensitive Resistors (FSR)
suggestions.desc: Move beyond sentiment-triggered haptics by integrating FSR sensors on the hardware. This allows the system to measure the actual physical pressure applied by one user and replicate that exact intensity on the other device using PWM-controlled actuators.

suggestions.title: Cross-Platform Flutter Interface for BLE Haptic Control
suggestions.desc: Build a mobile application using Flutter and the Flutter_Blue library to bridge the hardware and the cloud. This provides a user interface for pairing devices over BLE, managing privacy settings, and visualizing the sentiment data in real-time.

suggestions.title: Non-Verbal Communication Bridge for the Visually Impaired
suggestions.desc: Adapt the technology to translate complex emotional cues from text into specific haptic patterns (haptic morse). Use the existing sentiment analysis logic to help users with social-emotional processing difficulties 'feel' the tone of a digital conversation.

similar: legacy:GR-064|Spring 2021|how could iot assist healthcare system during covid-19 or future pandemics?

title: Subject Identification from Off-Angle Iris Image Using Machine Learning
authors: Chavarro, David E; Karakaya, Mahmut
abstract: This research paper investigates the use of the Squeezenet Machine Learning Neural Network to identify a subject from off-angle iris images. Squeezenet is a convolutional neural network (CNNs) which contains 50x lesser param eters than Alexnet. It allows the model to be trained on the dataset on devices that have lim ited resources. The training dataset contains Iris images where the gaze angles are at 0 degrees, while the validation dataset uses off-angle images.

suggestions.title: Edge Deployment with Model Quantization
suggestions.desc: Optimize the Squeezenet model for deployment on low-power hardware like a Raspberry Pi Zero or Jetson Nano. Use TensorFlow Lite or ONNX Runtime to perform 8-bit quantization, measuring the trade-off between iris identification accuracy and inference speed at the edge.

suggestions.title: Synthetic Off-Angle Data Generation via GANs
suggestions.desc: Address the validation gap by using Generative Adversarial Networks (CycleGANs) to transform 0-degree iris images into realistic off-angle synthetic images. This allows you to expand the training set significantly without needing new physical captures of human subjects.

suggestions.title: Multi-Modal Periocular and Iris Fusion
suggestions.desc: Extend the Squeezenet architecture to analyze the 'periocular' region (skin and shape around the eye) alongside the iris. This provides a fallback identification method when the iris is too obscured by extreme angles or poor lighting conditions.

suggestions.title: Smart Home Secure Entry Prototype
suggestions.desc: Build a physical prototype of a door lock system that uses an ESP32-CAM and the trained Squeezenet model. Implement a 'liveness detection' feature to ensure the system cannot be fooled by high-resolution photos of an eye held up to the camera.

similar: legacy:GR-08|Fall 2016|gender/age classification from facial image using deep learning

title: Cryptanalysis of Caesar Cipher with Answer Set Programming
authors: Om isore, Jonathan
abstract: Answer Set Program ming is an emerging paradigm in logical and declarative program ming that finds all possible solutions to a particular logic problem given certain constraints and is widely used in AI for knowledge representation and reasoning. Answer Set Program ming has been applied for the cryptanalysis of the Caesar cipher. Using Answer Set Program ming, words have been both encrypted and decrypted with the Caesar cipher through AI knowledge representation and reasoning. It is shown that Answer Set Program ming is viable for the field of cryptography. This project investigates the use of deep learning for Heterogeneous robotics trajectory and communication planning which will be

suggestions.title: ASP-Based Cryptanalysis of Polyalphabetic Vigenère Ciphers
suggestions.desc: Extend the current logic program to handle multi-character keys by modeling the Kasiski examination method as Answer Set Programming constraints. This would move the research beyond the single-shift Caesar cipher into more complex historical encryption.

suggestions.title: Hybrid SAT-ASP Solver Integration for Large Key Spaces
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Clingo solver with a dedicated SAT solver to improve the performance of key discovery. Research how to represent frequency analysis as heuristic weights within the ASP program to find solutions in larger datasets faster.

suggestions.title: Interactive Logical Cryptography Educational Platform
suggestions.desc: Build a web-based IDE (using Pyodide or a backend Flask API) that allows students to write ASP rules and see them visually decrypt classical ciphers step-by-step, highlighting which constraints eliminated specific keys.

suggestions.title: Automated Logic-Based Ransomware Signature Detection
suggestions.desc: Apply the ASP modeling technique to identify patterns in encrypted file headers. By defining the logical 'shape' of common encryption algorithms, you can build a tool that identifies which cipher is attacking a system based on constrained search.

title: Posture Change in Immersive Experiences Using a Wearable Chair
authors: Prather, Mason T; Bodien, Daniel
abstract: The goal of this project is to develop methods to detect a user's posture in immersive experiences, particularly for standing position and sitting position. This will give developers a tool for changing a user’s experience in relation to their current posture. For example, a user can walk to a car, sit down, and drive the car. Detecting this posture change will add a natural gesture-based trigger for making the experience more immersive.

suggestions.title: Edge Impulse TinyML Classifier for Dynamic Transitions
suggestions.desc: Use the Edge Impulse platform to train a neural network on the wearable's IMU data. This would allow the system to detect not just 'sitting' and 'standing,' but also the intent to sit, kneeling, and leaning, providing more granular triggers for VR developers.

suggestions.title: Ergonomic Feedback Integration for Remote Workstations
suggestions.desc: Pivot the application to a health context by creating a Unity-based productivity environment that alerts the user if their posture (detected by the wearable chair) indicates slouching or poor spinal alignment over long periods.

suggestions.title: Unity SDK for Procedural Avatar Seating IK
suggestions.desc: Develop a specialized Unity plugin that uses the wearable's data to drive Inverse Kinematics (IK) for VR avatars. This ensures that when a user sits physically, their virtual legs and hips align perfectly with virtual furniture, solving a common immersion-breaking issue.

suggestions.title: Multi-Sensor Fusion for Leg-Tracking without Base Stations
suggestions.desc: Combine the wearable chair data with Quest 3's inside-out tracking data via a Kalman filter. This research would investigate if localized wearable sensors can compensate for visual occlusion when a user sits behind a physical desk in VR.

title: Station 17
authors: Legg, Andrew, Miller, Linni, Byrd, Seth, Rigg, Benjamin, Maswanganye, Bonga
abstract: Station 17 is a classic style First Person Shooter, where you are sent to clear out a derelict space station on a foreign planet's facility. Utilize your archetypal weapons and handy grapple shot to take down all the creatures the hive mind can throw at you while navigating levels within the facility.

suggestions.title: Procedural Space Station Level Generator
suggestions.desc: Replace static levels with a procedural generation system using the Waveform Function Collapse algorithm. This would create unique, interconnected facility corridors and hive nests for every new game session, increasing replayability.

suggestions.title: Asymmetric Multiplayer 'Hive Mind' Mode
suggestions.desc: Develop a multiplayer component where one player acts as the 'Hive Mind' using a top-down RTS interface to spawn creatures, while the other player navigates in FPS mode. Use Photon Fusion for high-performance networking sync.

suggestions.title: Grapple-Shot Physics and Speedrunning Tools
suggestions.desc: Refine the grapple mechanics to include momentum-based swinging and 'zip-line' functionality. Add a built-in speedrunning timer and ghost-replay system to encourage players to find the most efficient movement paths through the facility.

suggestions.title: Advanced AI Behavior Trees for Swarm Tactics
suggestions.desc: Upgrade the creature AI from simple pathfinding to complex behavior trees. Implement 'flocking' algorithms so the hive mind creatures flank the player and retreat to dark corners of the space station when their health is low.

title: Total Health Telemedicine Application
authors: Clifford, Thom as A, Gooch, Mionne, Capparelli, Nicolas G, Thom pson, Darren
abstract: The Total Health Web Portal is a telemedicine application that allows users to orchestrate the process of a hospital visit without requiring anyone to leave their homes. The app assigns each user to 1 of 3 roles: client, doctor, or administrator. Clients are able to request appointments, upload documents, message their doctor, and join video calls for their appointments. The doctor may also message their clients and join video calls for their scheduled appointments. In addition, they may search through a list of their patients and dynamically add notes for each patient. Finally, the administrators have the responsibility of accepting/declining incoming appointment requests and managing accounts from within the database. The goal of the Total Health Telemedicine Application is to provide all participants with a way to effectively communicate and work in a single, convenient, and secure web portal.

suggestions.title: AI-Powered Symptom Pre-Screener
suggestions.desc: Integrate a medical NLP model (like BioBERT) into the client portal. Before requesting an appointment, clients can input their symptoms; the system then provides a preliminary severity score and suggests which specialist (Doctor role) they should book with.

suggestions.title: Blockchain-Verified Medical Records
suggestions.desc: Implement a private Ethereum or Hyperledger sidechain to store the hashes of uploaded documents. This ensures the integrity of medical records, providing a cryptographic audit trail that proves documents haven't been tampered with since they were uploaded.

suggestions.title: Wearable Data Integration for Doctors
suggestions.desc: Use the Google Fit or Apple HealthKit APIs to allow clients to sync their daily activity data (steps, heart rate, sleep). Doctors would then see a 'Vitals Dashboard' next to the video call window, providing real-time context for the telemedicine visit.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Telehealth Transcription & Summary
suggestions.desc: Use AWS Transcribe Medical or OpenAI Whisper during the video calls to create a live transcript. Build a post-call feature that uses an LLM to automatically generate a clinical summary and a list of 'Next Steps' for the client's portal.

title: Portable, To-Go, Security System
authors: Jelezarov, Luben; Varzari, Julia; Hixon, Zack; Noukimi, Marcelle Al; Bayati, Mohammed Al
abstract: Security is an element that every person on earth strives to attain. Having security of your home, family, and self is a challenge amongst the dynamic and unpredictable world. Security systems these days have done wonders in being able to secure a persons home and allow them to manage and react in case of an incident, however are rigid as they only secure your home, and once you leave, it's as if you're on your own again. Our device aims to bring the peace of mind and security of a home system with a person where ever they go. In the event of parking your car in a suspicious area? Set up the device and monitor your car via a livestream. Traveling and have to leave you bags unattended for a moment? Prop up the device and monitor on your phone. Our device allows for anybody to bring an extra set of eyes with them to be able to monitor their person or possessions, and even loved ones, all while using their phone with capabilities to react properly.

suggestions.title: Edge-Based Face Recognition using ESP32-CAM and TinyML
suggestions.desc: Integrate an ESP32-CAM module to add visual security to the portable system. Use a lightweight model to distinguish between the owner and strangers, reducing false alarms from the motion sensors when the user is simply moving the device.

suggestions.title: LoRaWAN Integration for Off-Grid Perimeter Security
suggestions.desc: Add a LoRa radio module to the device to enable security alerts in areas without Wi-Fi or Cellular coverage, such as camping sites or remote job sites. This would allow the device to transmit alerts over several miles to a central gateway.

suggestions.title: Asset Tracking and Anti-Theft Geofencing for Luggage
suggestions.desc: Incorporate a GPS module and use the Google Maps API to create a 'virtual tether.' If the portable security system is moved outside a defined radius without authorization, it could automatically trigger a high-decibel alarm and send live coordinates.

suggestions.title: Mesh-Networked Multi-Point Security Perimeter
suggestions.desc: Develop a protocol that allows multiple 'To-Go' units to discover each other and form a mesh network. This would allow a user to place sensors at multiple entry points of a temporary location (like a hotel room) that all report to a single smartphone app.

title: VirtuDoc - CS4850/01 - Group 5A
authors: Pasencia, Julian A; Pruitt, James; Araujo, Anthony; Zhu, Bennett; Stancil, William; Umar, Mohammad; Zenn, Joshua M
abstract: VirtuDoc is a web application that allows patients to communicate with their doctors virtually. Once A patient has logged in or created an account, they can schedule a virtual appointment based on a time, symptoms, and doctor. VirtuDoc will also give the ability to patients to connect with their doctor over text chat leading up to their appointment. Virtual web-conferencing connects patients with doctors at the scheduled time of appointment. Additionally, doctors are capable of linking and displaying video content to patients based on the situation. Patients will also have access to records including post appointment information displaying the patient medical history and current/past prescriptions. VirtuDoc is fully HIPAA-compliant. All connections across services in development and production utilize TLS and authoritative certificates to ensure HIPAA compliance. Our web app itself is built inside the Java spring framework and uses vanilla JavaScript and CSS3 on the front end. GitHub Actions and Heroku CI automatically deploy our web service as a Docker image to the Heroku cloud for deployment.

suggestions.title: AI-Powered Preliminary Symptom Analysis
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Infermedica API to provide patients with an AI-driven symptom checker before they book. The results can be automatically summarized and sent to the doctor's dashboard to streamline the actual consultation.

suggestions.title: HIPAA-Compliant Blockchain Audit Log
suggestions.desc: Develop a decentralized ledger using Hyperledger Fabric to store all appointment logs and chat histories. This creates an immutable, transparent audit trail that complies with medical data integrity regulations.

suggestions.title: Automated Medical Transcription and Billing
suggestions.desc: Use AWS Transcribe Medical during the video call to generate a real-time transcript. Build a secondary module that extracts 'ICD-10 codes' from the conversation to automatically draft insurance billing claims for the doctor's review.

suggestions.title: Pharmacy Integration and Prescription Workflow
suggestions.desc: Expand the doctor's portal to include an e-prescription module. Integrate with the Surescripts API or a mock pharmacy database to allow doctors to send prescriptions directly to a patient's preferred pharmacy after a call.

title: CyberSecurity Capstone
authors: Silwal, Magan; Duncan, Ryan; Cronnon, Evelyn; Weaver, Scott; Eid, Ihad
abstract: The purpose of this capstone was to form a cyber security team to help a small business, a restaurant, secure their server. As a team , we had to research any risks and generate a plan based on the information we found. We researched the best tools to use and configured them on to our server. In order to identify how secure our server was, a team was responsible to try to exploit the U RL. It was our job to monitor the server for any potential threats/vulnerabilities.

suggestions.title: Automated DevSecOps CI/CD Pipeline
suggestions.desc: Create a deployment pipeline for the restaurant's web server using GitHub Actions. Integrate 'OWASP ZAP' for automated vulnerability scanning and 'Snyk' for dependency checking every time the code is updated.

suggestions.title: Small Business Threat Intelligence Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Build a simplified web dashboard that translates complex server logs (from Snort or Suricata) into a 'Security Health Score' for the restaurant owner. Use clear visualizations to show where blocked attacks are coming from geographically.

suggestions.title: POS System Vulnerability Research
suggestions.desc: Research common vulnerabilities in legacy Point of Sale (POS) systems often used in restaurants. Build a laboratory environment to test if the restaurant's web server can be used as a pivot point to attack the internal POS network.

suggestions.title: Honeypot Deployment for Localized Threats
suggestions.desc: Deploy a 'Canary' honeypot on the restaurant's network that mimics a vulnerable database. Use this to gather intelligence on the specific types of automated bots and script kiddies targeting local small businesses.

title: Telemedic Application
authors: Jones, Yasha; Schneider, Nate; Nguyen, Martin; Fisher, Kate; Huynh, My Anh; Thapa, Shailesh; Lin, Mike
abstract: The project is a web portal for doctors and patients that can store chats, reports, requests for appointments, and host video calls. It is built in JavaScript and utilizes ReactJS, Zoom, NPM, and other third party softwares to run. This project is an undergraduate capstone project.

suggestions.title: Custom WebRTC Video Implementation
suggestions.desc: Replace the third-party Zoom dependency with a native WebRTC solution using SimplePeer or Twilio Video. This allows for a 'white-label' experience where patients never have to leave the app or download external software.

suggestions.title: Wearable Vitals Sync (HealthKit/Google Fit)
suggestions.desc: Integrate the ReactJS frontend with the Apple HealthKit and Google Fit APIs. This allows patients to share their real-time heart rate, sleep data, and step counts with their doctor directly through the Telemedic portal.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Translation Overlay for Consultations
suggestions.desc: Use Google Cloud Translation API to provide real-time captions during the video call. This is specifically aimed at clinics serving diverse communities where the doctor and patient may not share a primary language.

suggestions.title: Offline-First Patient Records via Service Workers
suggestions.desc: Implement Progressive Web App (PWA) features using Service Workers and IndexedDB. This ensures that patients in areas with poor internet can still view their medical reports and chat history even when they are offline.

title: Grizzly's Maw - Gameplay Trailer
authors: Harris, Allen J, Sym one D. Reid
abstract: A brief gameplay trailer that includes a majority of mechanics that are currently implemented, and a voice over that explains the premise and appeal of the game. Gameplay video available here: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1bYP3Ef-tymRI4QvIQxoVWNwMvHJo86Ja/view?usp=sharing

suggestions.title: Dynamic AI 'Director' System for Combat Pacing
suggestions.desc: Implement a 'Director AI' in the game engine that monitors player health and ammunition levels. The system should procedurally spawn enemies or health pickups to maintain the high-tension 'Grizzly' atmosphere without making it impossible for new players.

suggestions.title: Procedural Forest Generation with Seed-Based Sharing
suggestions.desc: Expand the environment from a static map to a procedurally generated forest using Perlin noise and Voronoi diagrams. Implement a 'Seed' system so players can share specific map layouts with others, increasing the game's replayability.

suggestions.title: Asymmetric Multiplayer Integration via SteamWorks API
suggestions.desc: Turn the single-player experience into an asymmetric 1v4 mode where one player controls the 'Grizzly' using a top-down RTS-style interface while the others play the survival mechanics shown in the trailer.

suggestions.title: Data-Driven Player Death Heatmapping and Balancing
suggestions.desc: Build a backend using MongoDB to log player coordinates at the time of death and weapon usage statistics. Use this data to generate heatmaps that help identify 'choke points' in the level design that are either too easy or frustratingly difficult.

title: Day Easy Phone Application
authors: Birgili, Zeynep D, Lagares, Gabriel D, Martinez, Am adeus R, Perez, Luis A, Roby, Jerem iah D
abstract: DayEasy is a phone application that is designed to promote clean eating and workout habits by providing services such as a calendar, workout planner, hydration and calorie journal, pedometer, fasting timer, and a meal planner. The application will have the functions to: • The user will be able to create an account. • The user will be able to change their password when they forget it. This will be done by sending an email. • The user will be able to save their sign in credentials. • The user will be able to view their daily schedule. • The user will be able to create new tasks. • These tasks are repeatable. • The user will be able to add how much water they drink in oz/ml • The user can set a daily drinking goal. • The user will be able to see how many steps they have taken. • The user can set a daily step goal. • The user will be able to plan their workout by selecting various customized movements. • The user will be able to select a movement (possibly a GIF) and see information about it. • The user will be able to go to muscle option and select a muscle part they want to work on and plan their workout. • The user will be able to plan their meals by keeping a journal. This journal will include calories. • The user will be able to pick a customized meal plan based on dietary selections. • The user will be able to add up their calories. • The user will be able to set a daily calorie goal.

suggestions.title: Computer Vision Food Logging with Google ML Kit
suggestions.desc: Add an 'Auto-Log' feature that uses the smartphone camera to identify food items on a plate. By integrating with the Nutritionix API, the app can automatically estimate calories and macros from a photo, reducing the friction of manual entry.

suggestions.title: Wearable Syncing with HealthKit and Google Fit SDKs
suggestions.desc: Expand the pedometer functionality by building a background service that syncs data from Apple Health and Google Fit. This allows users to import heart rate, sleep data, and workouts from their Apple Watch or Fitbit directly into DayEasy.

suggestions.title: Gamified 'Fitness Leagues' using Firebase Realtime Database
suggestions.desc: Create a social layer where users can join weekly step or hydration challenges. Use Firebase to maintain a live leaderboard and push notifications to nudge users when a friend overtakes them in the rankings.

suggestions.title: Predictive Fasting Schedules based on Circadian Data
suggestions.desc: Implement a machine learning model using Scikit-learn that analyzes a user's historical energy levels and workout times. The app could then suggest the 'optimal' fasting window for the following day to maximize fat loss and energy.

title: GTRI: Analysis of Alternatives (Team 2)
authors: Colem an, Christopher, Kaur, Gurpreet, Duarte, Jonathan, Gam ez Diaz, Erick, Foster, Sam uel
abstract: The capstone project Analysis of Alternatives was where a group would research and test workstation deployment tools to see if they could fulfill the fifteen requirements that GTRI needs for a deployment tool. The project was broken down into four phases: planning, research, testing, documentation/recommendation. Planning phase was used to set up the pathway to follow to finish the project on time and meet every objective to the groups’ best abilities. Then, in the research phase we used this phase to discover what three tools we would test. In the testing phase we tested three workstation deployment tools, to see if they could meet every requirement. Based on results from the testing phase we used this to help us come to a decision on what tool to recommend to GTRI. The Analysis of Alternatives capstone project, is a project in which a group will recommend a workstation deployment tool to GTRI based on their results in the research and testing phase of the project.

suggestions.title: Automated Testing Suite for Deployment Tool Benchmarking
suggestions.desc: Develop a Python-based automation framework that replicates the 15 GTRI requirements (e.g., speed, failure rate, package size). The tool should automatically deploy a test image via SCCM, MDT, or PDQ and generate a standardized performance report.

suggestions.title: Web-Based 'Selection Logic' Dashboard for IT Managers
suggestions.desc: Build a React dashboard that visualizes the 'Analysis of Alternatives' data. Users could input their specific hardware constraints, and the system would use a Weighted Decision Matrix algorithm to recommend the best tool based on the GTRI research.

suggestions.title: Security Compliance Auditing for Deployment Workflows
suggestions.desc: Extend the analysis to include a security audit of each deployment tool. Research and document how each tool handles sensitive data like local admin passwords and domain join credentials, ranking them based on NIST cybersecurity standards.

suggestions.title: Containerized 'Provisioning-as-a-Service' Prototype
suggestions.desc: Investigate moving away from traditional thick-imaging toward thin-provisioning using tools like Microsoft Autopilot or Ansible. Build a proof-of-concept that uses a single script to configure a 'blank' OS into a GTRI-ready workstation in under 20 minutes.

title: Plat-N-Run
authors: Holt, Alan J
abstract: For this project, I wanted to try and make a First Person gam e experience that had an extra layer of gam eplay that many First Person experiences didn’t have. With that, I added the function of letting the player throw out their own walls and therefore paths that they can make for them selves. Wall running, Characters and stats/achievem ents aid in the gam e loop of completing levels as fast as possible and seeing how well the player did to try and do better in another run.

suggestions.title: Cross-Server Tournament Engine with Redis
suggestions.desc: Scale the plugin to support multi-server tournaments by integrating a Redis backend. This would allow a global leaderboard to sync across multiple Minecraft instances, enabling massive 'last team standing' speedrun events that aren't limited by a single server's CPU.

suggestions.title: Procedural Objective Balancing via Genetic Algorithms
suggestions.desc: Research and implement a system that uses genetic algorithms to generate 'weighted' objective lists. By analyzing historical player data, the system could automatically pair difficult tasks (like finding a Fortress) with easier ones to ensure every generated match is mathematically balanced for competition.

suggestions.title: Twitch Integration for Interactive 'Chaos' Speedruns
suggestions.desc: Build a bridge between the plugin and the Twitch API to allow viewers to influence the game. Fans could vote to change objectives mid-run, spawn 'sabotage' mobs for the leading team, or provide 'buffs' to trailing teams, creating a commercial-ready product for streamers.

suggestions.title: Gamified STEM Education Mode
suggestions.desc: Adapt the plugin for the Minecraft Education Edition domain. Replace standard speedrun goals with chemistry or logic-based objectives (e.g., 'Synthesize Magnesium Oxide' or 'Build an XOR Gate'), turning the competitive speedrun framework into a collaborative classroom assessment tool.

title: IT MANAGEMENT DASHBOARD
authors: Kharel, Sam; Raheja, Robert; Hoganson, Kyle; Hooker, Jacob; Kim, Sky
abstract: This project lays the foundation for the continued collaboration of future students to build an open-source IT Dashboard through our sponsor at the Georgia Tech Research Institute. The goal is to work towards a functional dashboard that incorporates a complete toolkit for the managem ent of day- to-day IT needs. Any company or individual should be able to obtain a copy of our project and form at it to fit their needs. Our MIT License for the project will fall in line with these goals.
supervisor: Prof. Donald Privitera (Instructor) Mrs. Allison Boyle (Project Co-

suggestions.title: SNMP Network Auto-Discovery Module
suggestions.desc: Add a network mapping feature using the `pysnmp` library to automatically discover devices on a local network. Display these devices on a dynamic React-based topology map within the existing dashboard.

suggestions.title: Integrated Ticketing & Slack Integration
suggestions.desc: Build a bidirectional sync between the dashboard and collaboration tools. Use Webhooks and the Slack API so that IT alerts on the dashboard automatically create Slack threads and Jira tickets for the IT team.

suggestions.title: Predictive Hardware Failure Analytics
suggestions.desc: Integrate a machine learning component using Scikit-learn to analyze server logs and resource usage data collected by the dashboard. The system should predict and flag potential hardware failures (like disk exhaustion) before they occur.

suggestions.title: Multi-Tenant RBAC Security Layer
suggestions.desc: Implement a robust Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) system using Auth0 or Passport.js. This allows a single dashboard instance to host multiple companies/departments, each seeing only their own IT infrastructure and metrics.

title: SmartChoice - Online Smart Retail for Food Delivery
authors: Rios-Burrows, John M; Megginson, Samuel J; Shastry, Malvika; Shastry, Bhumika; Shadare, Isaac T; Abdulkadir, Munir; Waymond, Damaris
abstract: Our product is a website which extensively compares and analyzes food delivery fees from the top 3 online food websites which are GrubHub, UberEats, and Postmates and gives the best option for customers for their satisfaction. This encapsulates the idea of the Internet of Things in smart retail because we are building a technology service that helps users get the best food delivery deals in a trillion dollar industry by improving user experience as well as saving time, energy, and costs in this busy world. Financial motivation is the leading incentive for customers and clients to use our website as it gives the best option in any location where you search for your restaurant as long as it’s available on the food delivery websites. Since delivery fees vary greatly depending on the location, our website gives the foremost reason for customers to use it whenever they want any kind of food from fine dining restaurants to fast food places like McDonalds and Burger King. Materials and Methods Information from food delivery websites of Grubhub, Ubereats, and Postmates Python’s Selenium packages for the web scraping and analytics to compare the different food delivery websites HTML, CSS, and Bootstrap for the graphical user interface and design of our website Javascript and PHP for the web requests and the integration between the different parts of our website

suggestions.title: Headless Browser Scraping with Playwright and AWS Lambda
suggestions.desc: Replace static scraping with a serverless Playwright architecture. This allows the system to bypass anti-scraping measures on GrubHub and UberEats while scaling to handle hundreds of concurrent price checks for different zip codes.

suggestions.title: SmartChoice Chrome Extension for Checkout Overlays
suggestions.desc: Develop a browser extension that activates when a user is on a delivery site. It should automatically scrape the current cart total and display a popup showing if the same order is cheaper on a rival platform, including delivery fees.

suggestions.title: Subscription Break-Even Calculator for 'Pro' Memberships
suggestions.desc: Add a feature that analyzes a user's order history to determine if buying 'Uber One' or 'GrubHub+' would actually save them money. It should calculate the 'break-even' point based on the average delivery fees saved versus the monthly subscription cost.

suggestions.title: Predictive Surge Pricing Model using Historical Data
suggestions.desc: Use a Time-Series model (like Prophet) to analyze how delivery fees change during peak hours or bad weather. The site could then offer 'Best Time to Order' advice, predicting when surge fees are likely to drop in a specific city.

similar: legacy:GC-04|Spring 2020|smart- pay: no cashier pay system

title: GoldMind
authors: Matos, Jacob D, Mazzoni, Rom an M, Payne, Leroyia Y, Adams, DeVon 9/8/23, 11:47 AM Spring 22 C-Day Program - College of Computing and Software Engineering
abstract: A hand-drawn Rouge-lite gam e where you progress through procedurally generated rooms full of various enem ies and bosses. Power yourself up by finding items in the Item Room , or buy yourself an upgrade from the Shop, if you have enough coin. Can you survive?

suggestions.title: Advanced BSP Dungeon Generation
suggestions.desc: Refine the procedural generation by implementing a Binary Space Partitioning (BSP) algorithm. This will create more structured, logical dungeon layouts with specific 'room types' (e.g., treasure rooms, ambush rooms) rather than just random clusters.

suggestions.title: Steam Workshop Modding API
suggestions.desc: Develop a system that allows users to import their own hand-drawn PNGs as enemies or items. Use a JSON-based configuration system so community members can define the stats and behaviors of their custom 'GoldMind' additions.

suggestions.title: Dynamic Difficulty Adjustment (DDA)
suggestions.desc: Write a 'Director' script that monitors player health, clear times, and item count. The system should dynamically adjust enemy spawn rates and boss health pools to maintain a 'Flow State'—making the game harder for experts and easier for struggling players.

suggestions.title: Local Multi-Character Synergy System
suggestions.desc: Add a second playable character and implement 'Synergy Items' that only activate when both players are near each other. Use Unity's New Input System to handle shared-screen combat mechanics and combined power-up effects.

title: Dream Catcher Combat Training
authors: Dodson, Ben D
abstract: Dream Catcher Com bat Training is a challenge based gam e where the player will try go get the best score in 3 gam e modes across 3 maps. They'll swing, slash, jum p, and dodge their way across each map on their way to get the best score they can.

suggestions.title: Frame-Perfect Combat Analytics
suggestions.desc: Implement a 'Combat Engine' that tracks frame data for swings and dodges. Add a training mode that displays hitboxes and hurtboxes in real-time, helping players master the exact timing needed for high-score combos.

suggestions.title: Global Ghost-Replay Leaderboard
suggestions.desc: Integrate AWS GameLift or Firebase to store player movement data as 'Ghosts.' Allow players on the leaderboard to download and race against the top player's ghost, seeing exactly how they dodged and jumped to get the high score.

suggestions.title: VR Motion-Controlled Port
suggestions.desc: Using the OpenXR toolkit, port the combat mechanics to Virtual Reality. Research how physical movement speed (swinging the controller) correlates with in-game damage and score, creating a more immersive 'Dream Catcher' training experience.

suggestions.title: Procedural Challenge Gauntlet
suggestions.desc: Create a 'Never-Ending' game mode where combat encounters are procedurally generated by stitching together hand-crafted map segments. The difficulty should ramp up every 3 rooms, introducing new enemy combinations and environmental hazards.

title: Locovents! Events in your Location.
authors: Basnet, Nrip S; Bodien, Daniel; Arundell, Andrew; McHale, Jackson; Miller, Ode
abstract: Locovents is the name of our android app-based project that will display to our users an interactive list detailing the local events going on in their area. Our goal for this project was to create a custom web scraper to display the any cool events that might be occurring in their respective area. This project was build using java and python along with android studios. With in the past 3 months we have worked rigorously to create a fully functional android app that scrapes the web and appropriately provides the user with relative information. This way our users will not have to look up any concerts or events that might be happening, all they would have to do is plug in their relative location.

suggestions.title: NLP-Based Event Categorization with spaCy
suggestions.desc: Enhance the web scraper by passing the event descriptions through an NLP pipeline. This would automatically tag events with categories like 'Outdoor,' 'Family Friendly,' or '21+' and extract specific dates and times more accurately than raw text scraping.

suggestions.title: One-Touch Ticket Purchasing via Eventbrite API
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Eventbrite and Ticketmaster APIs to allow users to not only view events but also purchase tickets directly within the Locovents app. This turns a discovery tool into a full-service event management platform.

suggestions.title: Geofencing-Triggered 'Local Discovery' Notifications
suggestions.desc: Use the Android Geofencing API to send a push notification to the user when they are within 5 miles of a high-rated event happening within the next 2 hours. This encourages spontaneous attendance and increases app engagement.

suggestions.title: Collaborative Filtering Recommendation Engine
suggestions.desc: Implement a 'Recommended for You' section using a collaborative filtering algorithm. By tracking which events a user clicks on or 'saves,' the app can build a preference profile and suggest future events based on what similar users in that location enjoyed.

title: Remote Presence Robot
authors: Goeden, Andrew; Zemzem, Hajar; Ahmad, Pamir; Dang, Tam M; Newson, Aaron; Song, Anna; Rehaan, Mohammed
abstract: A motorized robot that has a screen, microphone, webcam, and speaker to perform video chat capabilities and the ability to move about the environment. The robot will have customization functionalities to add a personal flair for the remote user. A remote user can access a web dashboard to control the robot as if they were in person. This will be good for sick or handicapped people. In addition to the standard web dashboard, we can incorporate a customization aspect for the host user to add filters to their image or stream audio (background blur, soundboard playback, etc.) The robot will be controlled by a remote user via a keyboard or controller to control its movements and interact with its surroundings.

suggestions.title: LiDAR-Based SLAM & Navigation
suggestions.desc: Equip the robot with an RPLiDAR sensor and implement Simultaneous Localization and Mapping (SLAM) using ROS (Robot Operating System). This would allow the remote user to click a point on a map and have the robot navigate there autonomously without hitting obstacles.

suggestions.title: Tele-Health Sensor Suite Expansion
suggestions.desc: Add medical-grade peripherals such as a USB pulse oximeter and a digital stethoscope. Build a secure, HIPAA-compliant portal within the web dashboard so doctors can perform basic physical checkups remotely for handicapped patients.

suggestions.title: EEG-Based Brain-Computer Control
suggestions.desc: Integrate an Emotiv or OpenBCI headset to allow users with severe mobility impairments to control the robot. Map specific mental commands (e.g., 'Push' for forward, 'Pull' for back) to the robot's motorized controls via a Python middleware script.

suggestions.title: Computer Vision 'Follow-Me' Mode
suggestions.desc: Use OpenCV and a deep learning model (like YOLOv8) to implement a person-tracking feature. The host user could select a person in the room, and the robot would automatically maintain a set distance and follow them as they move through the environment.

title: GTRI: Analysis of Alternatives (Team 3)
authors: Coffia, Dam ian G, Brooks, Elijah, Fleem an, Keegan, Richards, Sean
abstract: Our project focuses on analyzing alternative tools for workstation deploym ent using operating system imaging software. Our project conducts research on ten different tools available on the market, scoring each based on requirem ents given to us by a company. After evaluation, three tools are selected for implem entation and testing. Once testing is concluded, our group will choose one of the tested tools to recom mend for workstation deploym ent for the company by subm itting a full hardware analysis detailing the steps taken to select the tool along with implem entation steps for tool deploym ent.

suggestions.title: Self-Healing Infrastructure with Ansible and Cron
suggestions.desc: Automate the hardening process by creating Ansible playbooks that run periodically to audit the VM. If a configuration file (like /etc/httpd/conf/httpd.conf) is altered or a service is stopped, the script automatically reverts it to the secure 'Golden Image' state.

suggestions.title: Deception Technology and Honeypot Integration
suggestions.desc: Extend the Red/Blue team exercise by deploying a 'honey-service'—a fake MariaDB port that looks vulnerable but records attacker IP addresses and command strings. Feed this data into an ELK stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana) to visualize real-time attack patterns on the Akwaaba site.

suggestions.title: CIS Benchmark Compliance Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Build a web-based dashboard using Python and Flask that scans the RHEL environment against Center for Internet Security (CIS) Benchmarks. It should generate a 'Security Score' and provide a one-click PDF report for stakeholders, turning the hardening project into a professional auditing tool.

suggestions.title: Micro-Segmentation via Kubernetes Migration
suggestions.desc: Transition the monolithic VM setup into a containerized architecture using Docker and Kubernetes. Implement Network Policies to ensure that only the PHP container can talk to the MariaDB container, significantly reducing the attack surface compared to a standard OS-level setup.

title: Energy Crisis 1994
authors: Prather, Mason T
abstract: Energy Crisis 1994 is an online multiplayer first-person shooter gam e. The form at of the gam e is 4 versus 4 (with players or AI) in either King of the Hill or Payload competition. There are four playable characters at launch, each based on a unique role. The four roles are Tank, Assault, Recon, and Support. The gam e is inspired by Team Fortress 2, Overwatch, and Counter-Strike.

suggestions.title: Reinforcement Learning Role-Based Bots
suggestions.desc: Train AI bots using Unity ML-Agents to perform specific roles. For example, the 'Support' bot should use a reward function based on teammate health, while the 'Assault' bot is rewarded for proximity to the Payload, creating more realistic 4v4 matches.

suggestions.title: Persistent Inventory & Cosmetics System
suggestions.desc: Build a backend using Node.js and MongoDB to manage player accounts and progression. Implement a system where players earn 'Energy Cells' after matches to unlock role-specific skins and weapon variants, stored in a persistent database.

suggestions.title: Dynamic Map 'Energy' Mechanics
suggestions.desc: Introduce a core gameplay feature where the map's layout changes based on the 'Energy' levels of each team. Use C# scripts to open new flanking routes or activate environmental hazards (like moving platforms) when a team reaches a certain score threshold.

suggestions.title: Dedicated Server Matchmaking via AWS
suggestions.desc: Transition the game from peer-to-peer to a dedicated server architecture using AWS GameLift. Develop a matchmaking service that groups players based on skill rating (Elo) and role preference to ensure balanced 4v4 King of the Hill matches.

title: Effect of Vedic meditative practices on Heart Coherence: A scientific study using SmartHealth technology.
authors: Gopi, Sreekanth
abstract: Real-time operating systems (RTOS) are widely used in various robotic applications such as path planning and obstacle avoidance, which require real-time communication and interaction with the environment, posing significant challenges for RTOS design.In this paper, we will first explore different robotic control and decision-making applications based on RTOS. Then, we will study the implementations of several widely employed RTOS frameworks. Finally, we will analyze how different RTOS implementations impact overall system performance and discuss the advantages and limitations of these RTOS frameworks based on previous research.
supervisor: Dr. Md Abdullah Al Ha?z Khan

suggestions.title: ROS2-Based Collaborative Multi-Robot Mapping
suggestions.desc: Implement a real-time SLAM (Simultaneous Localization and Mapping) system using ROS2 and LiDAR sensors for multiple robots. Build a decentralized coordination protocol that allows two robots to merge their maps in real-time as they explore an unknown environment.

suggestions.title: Computer Vision for Real-Time Weed Detection
suggestions.desc: Develop a robotic application for precision agriculture. Use a Raspberry Pi with a Coral TPU to run a YOLOv8-tiny model in real-time, identifying weeds among crops and signaling an actuator for removal, focusing on minimizing latency between detection and action.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Jitter Analysis in RT-Linux
suggestions.desc: Conduct a technical deep-dive into the performance differences between standard Linux, RT-Preempt patched Linux, and Xenomai for robotic motor control. Create a benchmarking tool that visualizes task deadline misses and jitter across these different real-time environments.

suggestions.title: Haptic-Feedback Teleoperation System
suggestions.desc: Build a real-time teleoperation interface that uses a VR controller to move a robotic arm. Integrate force sensors on the arm's grippers and transmit that data back to the user via haptic motors, focusing on sub-50ms round-trip latency for a natural user experience.

title: Performance Analysis and Review of Encrypted DNS Protocols
authors: William son, Matt
abstract: Mental health support is crucial, but access to professional care can be limited by cost, availability, and social stigma. Digital solutions, particularly chatbots, offer an accessible and scalable approach to providing mental health support. However, current chatbot solutions may not always reflect the diversity of users' emotional experiences, and they may lack the specialized domain knowledge and adaptability required for effective mental health counseling. This research project aims to address these challenges by developing a compassionate AI digital assistant that can use specialized natural language processing (NLP) models to provide empathetic and targeted responses based on the nature of the user's query. Unlike traditional chatbots that rely on fine-tuning large language models, our approach is to build conversation graphs for specific mental health scenarios, which can maintain historical context and adapt in real-time to the user's emotional state. The motivation for this project stems from the growing mental health crisis and the projected shortage of mental health professionals in the coming years. As advancements in digital technology and the industrial economy have contributed to increased mental health challenges, the demand for mental health care is expected to grow significantly, outpacing the available workforce. By creating a digital assistant capable of providing emotional support and evidence-based guidance, this research aims to help fill the anticipated gap in mental health services and improve access to mental health resources for those in need.

suggestions.title: Fine-Tuned Empathy Engine with LoRA
suggestions.desc: Improve the 'Anchor' assistant by fine-tuning a Mistral or Llama-3 model using LoRA on the 'EmpatheticDialogues' dataset. Benchmark its responses against standard models to quantify its ability to validate user emotions without sounding repetitive or robotic.

suggestions.title: Crisis Detection and Resource Mapping
suggestions.desc: Integrate a safety layer that uses keyword and sentiment analysis to identify high-risk psychological states. If a crisis is detected, the assistant should dynamically pull local mental health resources and emergency contacts using the Google Maps and 988 APIs.

suggestions.title: Linguistic Mirroring and Rapport Analysis
suggestions.desc: Investigate 'Linguistic Style Matching' (LSM) within the assistant. Study whether the assistant's ability to subtly mirror the user's sentence structure and vocabulary leads to higher reported levels of user trust and perceived compassion during long-term interactions.

suggestions.title: Wearable Bio-Feedback Integration
suggestions.desc: Connect the assistant to a smartwatch API (like HealthKit) to monitor real-time Heart Rate Variability (HRV). When the assistant detects high physical stress, it can proactively initiate a 'Compassionate Anchor' session, offering guided breathing or grounding exercises.

title: Rule-based table parsing
authors: Stansbury, Joel
abstract: In this paper, we provide a rule-based algorithm for parsing out tabular data from images. Recent advances in Graph Neural Networks have provided significant improvement over previously attainable accuracy and still outperform the best rule-based methodology. Nevertheless, light-weight pipelines, such as the model proposed here, have their utility in terms of ease of integration, low memory requirements, and no dependence on GPU availability. The proposed model achieves 22.3% accuracy on a subset of the PubTabNet dataset. The metric used to quantify accuracy refers to the ability to perfectly identify the number of column and row boundaries present in the image. Advantages over previous algorithms include relative invariance to hierarchical relationships and complete invariance to the presence of gridlines.

suggestions.title: Hybrid Rule-Neural Pipeline for Tabular Extraction
suggestions.desc: Build a 'routing' engine that first attempts to parse an image using the rule-based model. If the confidence score is low (e.g., for non-grid tables), it should automatically hand off the image to a pre-trained Table Transformer (TATR) model.

suggestions.title: OCR Error Correction using LLM Post-Processing
suggestions.desc: Integrate a Tesseract OCR step after the rule-based parsing. Use a small LLM (like Llama-3-8B) to clean up the resulting CSV by identifying and correcting common OCR misreadings of headers and numeric values based on context.

suggestions.title: Financial Invoice Automator with Structured CSV Export
suggestions.desc: Target the algorithm specifically at financial documents. Build a tool that takes a folder of scanned invoices, uses the rule-based parser to identify line items, and generates a single consolidated Excel sheet for accounting software import.

suggestions.title: FastAPI Microservice for Low-Power Edge Devices
suggestions.desc: Package the rule-based algorithm into a Dockerized FastAPI service optimized for a Raspberry Pi. This would demonstrate a high-utility 'Edge OCR' device that can parse tables locally without needing an expensive GPU or cloud connection.

title: Non-Invasive Glucose monitoring system- GlucoCheck
authors: Pola, Priyanka; Falaiye, Oluwaseyi
abstract: Diabetes is a metabolic disorder characterized by elevated blood sugar levels. Monitoring blood glucose levels on a regular basis is critical to maintaining balanced blood sugar levels. Unlike traditional methods where monitoring glucose levels is invasive and harmful, non-invasive glucose monitoring does not involve penetrating the hum an skin. In our research, we developed a non-invasive glucose monitoring device that does not require any blood sam ple. Our device is connected to Raspberry Pi that includes optical sensors such as a visible light laser and small cam era. When a laser beam is directed towards hum an tissue, the prototype captures photos of the fingertip. By studying
supervisor: Dr. Maria Valero De Clem ente

suggestions.title: Edge-Optimized NIR Signal Processing
suggestions.desc: Implement TinyML (using TensorFlow Lite for Microcontrollers) directly on the Raspberry Pi or an ESP32 to filter noise from Near-Infrared (NIR) sensors in real-time. This reduces the dependency on cloud processing and minimizes latency for immediate glucose readings.

suggestions.title: Multi-Wavelength Spectroscopic Calibration
suggestions.desc: Extend the hardware to use multiple NIR wavelengths (850nm to 1550nm) and develop a partial least squares regression (PLSR) model. This helps differentiate glucose signatures from other blood components like albumin and hemoglobin, increasing clinical accuracy.

suggestions.title: Predictive Hypoglycemia Alert System
suggestions.desc: Integrate an LSTM (Long Short-Term Memory) network to analyze historical glucose trends and predict potential 'crashes' or spikes 30 minutes in advance. Connect this to the Twilio API to send automated SMS alerts to emergency contacts or healthcare providers.

suggestions.title: HIPAA-Compliant Patient Monitoring Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Build a secure web interface using AWS Amplify and Cognito for user authentication. Store encrypted sensor data in DynamoDB, allowing doctors to view longitudinal patient data via interactive Plotly charts while maintaining strict medical data privacy standards.

title: Credit Default prediction of money borrowing companies using Pyspark framework
authors: KOTA, JITENDRA SAI, boyapati, mallika
abstract: Credit-lending companies have resorted to the use of Machine Learning algorithms in the recent past to predict the probability of default of a customer for future credit lending purposes. Most credit companies view this as a binary classification problem of predicting whether an individual would default or not. Companies have been using models of Logistic Regression for a long time because of the explainability of the final feature set used in modeling. Explainability brings transparency to every stakeholder involved in the process. Other models like Neural Nets have achieved better accuracy scores, but the features generated by them are not easily comprehendible. By using the credit data of money borrowing companies collected by the credit bureau, Equifax over the last 10 years, we made use of the Pyspark Framework to come up with a model that can predict the reliability of a money borrowing company. After the data preprocessing phase, our predictors showed an accuracy of 97.73% using Logistic Regression and 98.58% accuracy using a Random Forest ensemble classifier. We were also able to identify a few predictors as the key performance indicators using the coefficients in Logistic Regression.

suggestions.title: Hybrid Gradient Boosting Ensemble on Spark
suggestions.desc: Upgrade the PySpark pipeline from simple Logistic Regression to a hybrid ensemble featuring XGBoost4J-Spark and LightGBM. Use Spark's CrossValidator to optimize hyperparameters at scale, significantly improving the AUC-ROC for imbalanced credit datasets.

suggestions.title: Transparent Loan Denials with SHAP Explainer
suggestions.desc: Incorporate the SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) library to provide 'local interpretability' for every prediction. Create a module that generates human-readable justifications for loan denials (e.g., 'Debt-to-income ratio too high'), satisfying 'Right to Explanation' regulatory requirements.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Credit Scoring with Spark Streaming
suggestions.desc: Convert the batch processing model into a real-time inference engine using Spark Structured Streaming and Kafka. This allows money borrowing companies to provide instant credit decisions to users via a mobile app interface as soon as they submit their application.

suggestions.title: Fairness-Aware Default Prediction
suggestions.desc: Apply the IBM AI Fairness 360 (AIF360) toolkit to the PySpark pipeline to detect and mitigate algorithmic bias. Implement 'reweighing' techniques to ensure that protected attributes like age, gender, or zip code do not unfairly influence default probability scores.

title: Breast cancer patient survival prediction with clinical data and Computed Tomography (CT) image datasets
authors: Moham med, Malik Naik
abstract: Breast cancer is one of the most common fatal cancers worldwide, with 12.5% of the new cases diagnosed in 2020. The Com puted Tom ography, commonly referred to as CT scan, is used to determine the breast cancer treatm ent and prognosis of the cancer as it is challenging to detect. Breast cancer is common in wom en, but som etim es can be seen in men as well. Although there are models to predict Lung Cancer using the Convolutional Neural Network or ConvNet (CNN) from the CT scans and clinical data, no such implem entation has been applied to the CT scans of the breast cancer using the images as well as clinical data. The overall relative 5 year survival rate for breast cancer is a

suggestions.title: Multi-Modal Vision Transformer Fusion
suggestions.desc: Replace the standard CNN with a Vision Transformer (ViT) and use a Late Fusion architecture to combine CT image features with clinical tabular data for more nuanced survival predictions.

suggestions.title: TCIA Dataset Cross-Validation
suggestions.desc: Benchmark the model against the public 'The Cancer Imaging Archive' (TCIA) dataset to evaluate how well the survival prediction generalizes across different hospital hardware and imaging protocols.

suggestions.title: Oncology Decision Support Interface
suggestions.desc: Build a Streamlit-based clinician tool that utilizes Grad-CAM to highlight high-risk regions in the CT scans, providing visual justification for the model's survival probability output.

suggestions.title: Longitudinal Treatment Response Tracking
suggestions.desc: Adapt the model to process a series of CT scans over time, using Recurrent Neural Networks (RNNs) to predict how a patient's survival probability changes in response to chemotherapy cycles.

title: IoT Clusters Platform for Data Collection, Analysis, and Visualization Use Case
authors: Traore, Soin Abdoul Kassif Baba M
abstract: Climate change is happening, and many countries are already facing devastating consequences. Populations worldwide are adapting to the season's unpredictability they relay to lands for agriculture. Our first research was to develop an IoT Clusters Platform for Data Collection, analysis, and visualization. The platform comprises hardware parts with Raspberry Pi and Arduino clusters connected to multiple sensors. The clusters transmit data collected in real-time to microservices-based servers where the data can be accessed and processed. Our objectives in developing this platform were to create an efficient data collection system, relatively cheap to implement and easy to deploy in any part of the world. Since we have completed the first part, we are implementing a study case for a field used by the platform. Thus, we are implementing an environment monitoring technology base on weather data. For this study, the platform will collect real-time environmental data using sensors (Temperature, humidity, light and ultraviolet sensors, and other sensors). We are setting those sensors in relatively limited superficies due to resources problem. Next, we will use this data to find patterns in weather changes using Machine and Deep learning techniques since these environmental data come from a designated area. The main objective of this part is to find a weather pattern using collected data specific to this area. Data collected during this research and the IoT platform are available on campus for students to use for their class projects or future research. Currently, we are in the data collection process. We also evaluate the degradation and environmental effects on devices and sensors used. This study case is a needed step in the IoT Clusters Platform for Data Collection, Analysis, and Visualization research project. At the end of the project, the data collection framework will be efficient and cost less.

suggestions.title: Resilient K3s Edge Orchestration
suggestions.desc: Deploy K3s (lightweight Kubernetes) across the Raspberry Pi cluster to manage the microservices. This ensures high availability for data collection; if one node in the cluster fails, the monitoring services automatically reschedule to another active node.

suggestions.title: Precision Irrigation Feedback Loop
suggestions.desc: Connect the IoT cluster to physical actuators (solenoid valves) and integrate the OpenWeatherMap API. Develop a control algorithm that triggers irrigation only when soil moisture is low AND no rain is forecasted, optimizing water usage for agriculture.

suggestions.title: LoRa Mesh Network Extension
suggestions.desc: Replace standard Wi-Fi communication with a LoRaMesh protocol to allow clusters to communicate over several kilometers in rural areas. This research would focus on data packet routing efficiency between Arduino sensor nodes and the central Raspberry Pi gateway.

suggestions.title: Climate Digital Twin Visualization
suggestions.desc: Feed the real-time sensor data into a Unity-based 'Digital Twin' of the monitored agricultural site. Use the Mapbox SDK to render a 3D environment where users can visually inspect heatmaps of temperature, humidity, and soil health across their land.

title: Deep Learning Search Engine
authors: Moham med, Malik Naik, Atm akuri, Madhusudhan Rao, Kam gaing, Yves Junior Kwam e, Gandi, Sai Charan
abstract: Deep Learning Information Retrieval (IR) is a booming area of research. Research in this field focuses on retrieving the most relevant search results based on the meaning of the search result, not just the keywords. The state-of-the-art technologies generally involve taking existing deep neural networks (such as Universal Sentence Encoder, or Google’s BERT), and training them to rank search results. However, some search engines make use of text matching algorithms (like Best Match 25). These algorithms work by taking into account the term frequency and other word patterns and work surprisingly well. The most common problem when it comes to Machine Learning is over-fitting. Even if the model gives better search results for programming-related questions, that doesn’t mean that it’ll work for searching the research articles. Generally, machine learning-based models are often slow and not scalable. In this project, we tackle all these problems by using the deep learning networks and approximate nearest neighbor search to implement the search engine that accurately ranks the search results based on the given query with a rapid and scalable solution. This model accepts not only text as input but also images as input. The image data is processed through the Image Captioning model that generates the text. This work uses the 200,000 Jeopardy! Questions and MS COCO datasets.
supervisor: Dr. Md Abdullah Al Ha?z Khan

suggestions.title: Hybrid Vector-Lexical Search Engine
suggestions.desc: Build a 'Reciprocal Rank Fusion' system that combines traditional BM25 keyword matching with dense vector embeddings from BERT. Use the Pinecone or Milvus vector database to handle high-dimensional similarity searches with millisecond latency.

suggestions.title: Domain-Specific BioMedical IR
suggestions.desc: Fine-tune the search engine using BioBERT and the CORD-19 dataset. This specialized application would prioritize semantic relationships between medical symptoms and treatments, providing more relevant results for healthcare researchers than a general search engine.

suggestions.title: Cross-Modal Retrieval Extension
suggestions.desc: Implement OpenAI’s CLIP (Contrastive Language-Image Pre-training) to allow the search engine to retrieve relevant images based on text queries and vice versa. This moves the project beyond text-matching into true multimodal information retrieval.

suggestions.title: Conversational RAG Interface
suggestions.desc: Transform the search engine into a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system using LangChain. Instead of a list of links, use an LLM (like Llama 3) to synthesize a direct answer based on the top-ranked retrieved documents, citing its sources.

title: DRL-FHSS: Dynamic Reinforcement Learning based Frequency Hopping Spread Spectrum Algorithm for Maximizing Data Rate and Minimizing Collision in Edge-enabled LoRaWAN
authors: Mhatre, Jui 9/8/23, 11:47 AM Spring 22 C-Day Program - College of Computing and Software Engineering
abstract: The Last decade saw new advances in LoRaWAN for IoT devices. Recent research in this field is as made it a game-changer for the IoT world. Lora, owing to its ability to establish a long-range communication using low power, has proved upper hand over its counterparts such as Wifi, Bluetooth, or cellular network. LoRa uses much lower bandwidth and as a result, network and power demands reduce. LoRa is suitable for short and periodic communications. But due to lower bandwidth and consequently lower transmission rates and low data volumes, it is not suitable for real-time communications. Frequency-hopping spread spectrum(FHSS) helps to rapidly switch frequencies and occupy larger spectral bands and increase overall data rate and throughput. It is observed that LoRaWAN defines 11 channels but uses only 3 channels frequently, thus keeping the other 8 channels underutilized. If all channels were utilized, the transmission rate would increase multifold. We have proposed a Dynamic approach, Reinforcement Learning-based Frequency Hopping Spread Spectrum (DRL-FHSS), a frequency hopping generation algorithm, to widen the occupancy of the spectral band. Our algorithm keeps a check on the frequency of used channels, overutilized and underutilized frequencies, and generates a strategy that allocates time slots and frequencies for all registered IoT devices in the network. DRL-FHSS learns its strategy based on the previous bandwidth utilization ratio. We introduce an edge-enabled LoRaWAN architecture that delegates the task of frequency hopping strategy generation to the edge server. Further, edge servers also notify transmitters and receivers in the network about the frequency hopping sequence. This also reduces the collision in the network since each transmitter is aware of busy frequencies in the network. Thus DRL-FHSS helps to improve throughput by increasing the transmission rate and lowering collision.

suggestions.title: Multi-Agent RL for Collision Avoidance
suggestions.desc: Evolve the DRL-FHSS algorithm into a Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL) framework where multiple LoRa gateways coordinate their frequency hopping patterns. This prevents 'herding behavior' where all devices switch to the same clear channel simultaneously.

suggestions.title: Energy-Harvesting Aware Duty Cycling
suggestions.desc: Modify the reward function of the DRL agent to include energy constraints for solar-powered edge devices. The agent should learn to maximize data rates during peak sunlight and dynamically reduce transmission frequency during low-battery states.

suggestions.title: Urban Interference Mapping
suggestions.desc: Deploy the DRL-FHSS algorithm within an NS-3 network simulator using a high-density urban mobility model. Analyze how the algorithm performs against external interference from non-LoRa sources like high-power industrial equipment.

suggestions.title: Dynamic LoRaWAN Gateway Controller
suggestions.desc: Build a Linux-based controller service for the Semtech SX1302 gateway chip that implements the learned DRL policies. This allows real-world gateways to dynamically reconfigure their Spreading Factor (SF) and frequency channels in response to live congestion.

title: Automatically Improve Writing for Research Papers Using Transformer Based Deep Learning
authors: Janney, Srivarna Settisara
abstract: The "Integrated Sentiment and Behavioral Analysis of Online Product Reviews" project helps businesses gain actionable insights from Product reviews by combining sentiment and behavioral analysis using NLP models like VADER and BERT. This dual approach categorizes reviews as positive, neutral, or negative and identifies themes such as preferences and complaints through Named Entity Recognition and topic modeling. By capturing both the emotional tone and specific product feedback, this method highlights consumer likes and pain points, assisting in targeted improvements for product design and customer service. The project addresses challenges in analyzing complex expressions like sarcasm, providing a robust framework for extracting meaningful insights from vast amounts of review data. Adaptable across various datasets, the model offers scalable benefits for enhancing e-commerce strategies through data-driven decisions based on real consumer feedback.

suggestions.title: Competitor Feature Comparison Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Extend the BERT/VADER pipeline to scrape reviews for two competing products simultaneously. Create a visualization that compares sentiment scores for specific 'Named Entities' (e.g., compare 'Battery Life' for iPhone vs. Galaxy) to identify market advantages.

suggestions.title: Span-Based Aspect Sentiment Analysis
suggestions.desc: Upgrade the model to link sentiment directly to specific entities within the same sentence (e.g., 'The camera [Positive] is great but the price [Negative] is high'). This provides more granular feedback than a single review-level score.

suggestions.title: Fake Review & Bot Detector
suggestions.desc: Combine the behavioral analysis with review metadata (e.g., timestamp frequency and user account age). Use this to build a filter that flags reviews with 'positive' sentiment but 'suspicious' behavioral patterns common in bot-generated reviews.

suggestions.title: Predictive Product Rating Forecasting
suggestions.desc: Implement a time-series forecasting component that uses current sentiment trends and emerging 'complaint themes' to predict if a product’s overall star rating will drop in the next 3 months, allowing brands to intervene early.

title: Detection of Small Traffic Signs Using Image Super Resolution
authors: Rathgam age Don, Duleep
abstract: In this study, we propose a novel method to detect small traffic signs that appeared in dashboard camera images. Our method is a framework consisting of the following three distinct algorithms. Grouping Window, Super Resolution Generative Adversarial Network (SRGAN), and a two-stage cascade classifier. Potential regions of interest (ROI) are extracted with Grouping Window which is a sophisticated modification of the traditional sliding window technique. The ROI are upsampled and enhanced using SRGAN. Then the traffic signs among high-resolution ROI are detected and identified by the two-stage cascade classifier of which the first stage filters the ROI that do not contain traffic signs and the second stage classifies the ROI that contains traffic signs into respective classes. The proposed method is capable of detecting traffic signs in the 5-8 square-pixel range. The detection of small objects in this square-pixel range is not generally addressed by state-of-the-art frameworks such as R-CNN and YOLO. We trained our method by using the German Traffic Sign Recognition Benchmark dataset (GTSRB) and tested it on random dashboard camera images containing small traffic signs. The experimental results on 15 random dashboard camera images show that our baseline model localizes 10 of the 23 small traffic signs belonging to the aforementioned pixel range and produces 70 false positives in total. Also, it classifies only one of the detected traffic signs correctly into 43 classes. We plan to improve our method by using image denoising techniques and comparing results.

suggestions.title: Edge AI Dashcam Deployment
suggestions.desc: Optimize the SRGAN and cascade classifier using TensorRT or OpenVINO. The goal is to get the system running at 30+ FPS on an NVIDIA Jetson Nano, proving it can work in real-time as an active driver assistance system (ADAS).

suggestions.title: Adverse Weather Sign Reconstruction
suggestions.desc: Train the SRGAN on a specialized dataset of traffic signs obscured by heavy rain, fog, or snow. Evaluate if the upsampling technique can 'restore' the visibility of a sign that would otherwise be unreadable to a standard autonomous vehicle sensor.

suggestions.title: Infrastructure Condition Monitor
suggestions.desc: Repurpose the detection system to identify damaged, faded, or graffiti-covered traffic signs. This tool could be sold to Departments of Transportation (DOT) to automate the inventory and maintenance schedule of roadside assets.

suggestions.title: CARLA Autonomous Simulation Integration
suggestions.desc: Integrate the SRGAN pipeline into the open-source CARLA driving simulator. Test how much the 'enhanced' sign detection improves the safety performance (e.g., braking distance) of an AI-driven car compared to one using raw dashboard camera inputs.
