title: Extracting Human Factors from Goods to Person Order Picks
authors: Norkham, Alan
abstract: This project aims to extract the Hum an Factors (HF) from Goods to Persons Order Picks (OP). We used live data from a known functioning site. The data was then cleaned and visualized. After many different approaches, a hypothesis was form ed. We used a deterministic approach to the data and applied Tim e Series Analysis to an Auto Regression Model. The predicted results were yielded slight, but major differences in the initial visualization.
supervisor: Dr. Jiho Noh, Ph. D. (Academ ic) Dr. Thom as Potrusil (Industrial)

suggestions.title: Circadian Rhythm and Fatigue Modeling
suggestions.desc: Integrate timestamp data with worker shift schedules to model how performance degrades due to fatigue, using the Auto Regression model to predict 'high-error' windows for managers.

suggestions.title: LSTM-Based Temporal Pattern Extraction
suggestions.desc: Upgrade the deterministic Time Series analysis to a Deep Learning approach using Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks to capture non-linear behaviors in the picking process.

suggestions.title: 3D Warehouse Flow Simulator
suggestions.desc: Export the extracted Human Factors into a simulation environment like SimPy or Unity to visualize how changing the warehouse floor layout would impact worker picking speed and ergonomic strain.

suggestions.title: Ergonomic Risk Alert System
suggestions.desc: Build a real-time monitoring tool that flags picking behaviors that deviate significantly from the 'predicted norm,' alerting supervisors to potential safety issues or training needs.

title: Accident Prediction using Big Data Analysis using Ensemble Learning
authors: Mhatre, Jui; Achuta, Sai Harshitha; Polavarapu, Nikhitha; Nagandla, Dhanush; Saribala, Chandana
abstract: Many country-wide internal roads and national highways have dim lights or no street lights all over the world. We usually observe som e turns on roads more prone to accidents than other places. In our paper we used Logistic Regression and Decision Trees together build an Ensem ble learning for predicting these accident zones. For validating the results, five evaluation metrics as Accuracy, Precision, f-m easures, Re-call, and Area under the curve are used. State of the art model for the U S accident dataset gives F1 a score of 57% . We are implem enting using ensem ble learning wherein logistic regression gives an F1-score of 53%

suggestions.title: Real-Time Weather & Traffic Fusion
suggestions.desc: Integrate the project with the TomTom Traffic API and OpenUV API to add dynamic features like current traffic density and sun glare levels. Train a Gradient Boosted Tree (CatBoost) to handle these categorical and temporal features for higher accuracy.

suggestions.title: Driver's 'Blackspot' Alert App
suggestions.desc: Develop a Flutter-based mobile application that uses background GPS tracking. When a driver approaches a zone identified by the ensemble model as 'high-risk' (based on current conditions), the app provides a proactive voice warning to 'Caution: High Accident Zone Ahead'.

suggestions.title: Graph Neural Networks for Road Risks
suggestions.desc: Represent the road network as a graph using OSMnx (OpenStreetMap data) where intersections are nodes and roads are edges. Use a Graph Convolutional Network (GCN) to predict how an accident on one artery increases the statistical probability of accidents on connecting roads.

suggestions.title: City Planner Safety Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Create a GIS-based dashboard using ArcGIS or Mapbox GL JS that visualizes predicted accident 'hotspots.' Include a 'What-If' simulator where planners can see how adding street lights or reducing speed limits on specific segments would theoretically decrease the risk score.

title: S'eyed'Kick: AI Powered Reading Assistant and Translator
authors: Allen, Stacie P; Barnes, Jessica; Hill, Chenelle; Pope, Lauren; Adejuwon, Adetayo A
abstract: According to the Centers for Disease Control (CDC), roughly 12 million people in the United States above the age of 40 are visually impaired. In adults 18 years and older, visual impairment is one of the top 10 disabilities. It can have an enormous effect on one’s independence and quality of life. Assistive technology through artificial intelligence (AI) can support the visually impaired in everyday life functions. Our application is specifically designed for reading text such as small print. After the user selects the language for translation out of 34 choices and takes a picture of the text with the app, the text is extracted from the image. The text is converted to speech and played to the user in audio format. The text is also enlarged and displayed on the screen. Overall, this app is a benefit to society through increasing the quality of life for the visually impaired.

suggestions.title: Offline-First Mobile Edge OCR
suggestions.desc: Migrate the processing from a cloud API to an on-device pipeline using MediaPipe and TensorFlow Lite. This ensures the reading assistant works in areas with poor connectivity, such as back aisles of grocery stores or subway stations.

suggestions.title: Smart Medication & Allergen Parser
suggestions.desc: Add a specialized NLP layer that recognizes medicine bottles and food labels. When text is scanned, the app can specifically highlight and read aloud dosage instructions or detect user-defined allergens like 'peanuts' or 'gluten' from ingredient lists.

suggestions.title: Contextual Visual Question Answering
suggestions.desc: Integrate a Vision-Language Model (VLM) like Moondream or LLaVA. This allows the visually impaired user to ask open-ended questions about the text, such as 'Does this coupon mention an expiration date?' or 'Is this the 50mg or 100mg bottle?'

suggestions.title: Hands-Free AR Glass Integration
suggestions.desc: Port the reading assistant to an AR platform (like Vuzix or Meta Ray-Bans) using their respective SDKs. Use the built-in camera to continuously scan the user's field of view and read text through bone-conduction headphones, allowing for a completely hands-free experience.

title: Runbook Automation
authors: Gamini, Harshitha K; Paidi, Nireesh; Seelamanthula, Sai Keerthi; Batchu, Prakash; Burgal, Furkan A
abstract: Runbook is common terminology used in every IT organization which is maintained in Excel for taking the components to production in a particular time frame after making an agreement with the business for the downtime of systems (Freeze Period). Runbook Automation is a web application for delivering and tracking deliverables to production. It acts as an interface between Infrastructure teams responsible for deployments and runbook coordinator, development teams, and Business Analysts. The application consists of a home page consisting of a Dashboard where the user can edit the profile, and other employee tracking details and timesheets. In Employee tracking, the details of the employees and their backgrounds are displayed. In Timesheets, the hours of the project and the runbook actions are displayed. In Access Management, different users are allowed to request access to perform daily activities. In Employee Performance, the feedback and achievements of the employer are shown. The Objective of the application is to develop a web application using various technologies to bring all the stakeholders to a common page about the project execution and make them aware of top management activities ongoing in the organization. The Application will have an interface to tools for updating the status of Jobs in the application. The idea of the application will be provided to the project owner for approval before design and development. The scope of the project will include planning, design, development, testing, and documentation.stratosphere to provide wide coverage and strong computational capabilities. It is suitable to coordinate terrestrial resources and store the fundamental data associated with ITS-based applications. Performing task offloading and data caching at Roadside Units (RSUs) in a cooperative manner can reduce the heavy backhaul load and the retransmission of content downloading.

suggestions.title: CI/CD Pipeline Integration Engine
suggestions.desc: Extend the runbook application by building plugins for Jenkins, GitLab CI, and GitHub Actions. This allows the runbook to not just 'track' the deployment but actually trigger the automation scripts and verify successful deployment status via API callbacks.

suggestions.title: Slack-Based Deployment Orchestrator
suggestions.desc: Develop a Slack or Microsoft Teams bot that interfaces with the Runbook API. Team members can approve production 'freeze period' releases, check current deployment status, and receive automated alerts about bottlenecks directly within their chat workspace.

suggestions.title: Predictive Downtime Estimation
suggestions.desc: Apply a Monte Carlo simulation model to historical runbook data to predict the likely duration of future maintenance windows. This helps coordinators provide more accurate 'Freeze Period' estimates to business stakeholders based on past team performance.

suggestions.title: Automated Post-Mortem Generator
suggestions.desc: Create a module that uses NLP (specifically GPT-4o-mini) to ingest deployment logs and runbook comments to automatically generate a 'Post-Implementation Review' (PIR) document, highlighting exactly where delays occurred and suggesting process improvements.

title: Mobile Edge Computing for Traffic Management Mechanism using HAPS (High Altitude Platform Station)
authors: Rayapalam, Chaitanya Lakshm i, Kuntla, Gayatri Sravanthi, Kulal, Shalaka R
abstract: According to the Centers for Disease Control (CDC), roughly 12 million people in the United States above the age of 40 are visually impaired. In adults 18 years and older, visual impairment is one of the top 10 disabilities. It can have an enormous effect on one’s independence and quality of life. Assistive technology through artificial intelligence (AI) can support the visually impaired in everyday life functions. Our application is specifically designed for reading text such as small print. After the user selects the language for translation out of 34 choices and takes a picture of the text with the app, the text is extracted from the image. The text is converted to speech and played to the user in audio format. The text is also enlarged and displayed on the screen. Overall, this app is a benefit to society through increasing the quality of life for the visually impaired.
supervisor: Class Instructor - Dr. Ying Xie Project Owner - Dr. Sum it Chkravarty

suggestions.title: Federated Learning for Traffic Privacy
suggestions.desc: Implement a Federated Learning framework where HAPS nodes train local traffic models based on vehicle telemetry without transmitting raw location data. This preserves driver privacy while still allowing the HAPS cluster to learn global congestion patterns.

suggestions.title: HAPS-to-Vehicle Handover Optimization
suggestions.desc: Develop a reinforcement learning algorithm to manage the 'handover' of data streams as vehicles move between the coverage areas of different HAPS units. Use the SUMO (Simulation of Urban MObility) tool to test the algorithm's performance in high-speed scenarios.

suggestions.title: Emergency Vehicle Prioritization (EVP)
suggestions.desc: Design a 'Green Wave' system where HAPS detects emergency vehicles (ambulances/fire trucks) and uses V2X (Vehicle-to-Everything) communication to pre-emptively clear traffic lights and notify surrounding autonomous vehicles to move aside.

suggestions.title: Dynamic Congestion Pricing Simulator
suggestions.desc: Build a commercial-facing platform that uses HAPS-monitored traffic density to calculate dynamic road tolls. Create an API that insurance companies or city governments could use to incentivize off-peak travel through variable pricing models.

title: Source Localization of Electroencephalogram (EEG) Waves with Convolutional Neural Network
authors: Veeravenkatappa, Suma; Pola, Priyanka; Kunnath, Anupama Nambanoor; Macherla, Jyothshna; Ghandu, Bharath Reddy; Yadav, Animesh
abstract: This project investigates the use of deep learning for Heterogeneous robotics trajectory and communication planning which will be assisted by prioritizing by electroencephalogram (EEG) waves for the purpose of improvisation by different emotional responses of human subjects. The Convolutional Deep Learning ?model is?trained to recognize EEG reading corresponding to different usage of the heterogeneous robots which are then used as an additional(imitation) input to the Reinforcement Learning based robotic operation. This project mainly focuses on delivery drone and aerial base station drone operations from source to destination considering energy efficient path, shortest distance, charging points, and to avoid collisions. This investigation is also assessed if near real-time performance can be achieved for such??approach. Such a system can be useful in many domains including unmanned driving, drone air corridor etc.

suggestions.title: Wavelet Transform Preprocessing Pipeline
suggestions.desc: Improve the CNN's accuracy by adding a Discrete Wavelet Transform (DWT) layer to denoise raw EEG signals. This helps filter out muscle artifacts and eye blinks, leading to more reliable emotional response detection before the signal reaches the Reinforcement Learning model.

suggestions.title: Assistive Wheelchair Navigation with BCI
suggestions.desc: Apply the heterogeneous robot planning framework to a motorized wheelchair. Build a prototype where the BCI (Brain-Computer Interface) detects 'stress' or 'comfort' levels, automatically adjusting the trajectory or speed of the wheelchair in crowded environments.

suggestions.title: Cross-Subject Transfer Learning Framework
suggestions.desc: Develop a Domain Adaptation technique that allows the CNN to work for new users with minimal recalibration. By using GANs to generate synthetic EEG data, the model can generalize emotional responses across different human subjects without needing hours of new training data.

suggestions.title: AR-Enhanced Emotion Calibration App
suggestions.desc: Create an Augmented Reality interface that visualizes the robot's planned path and the user's detected emotional state simultaneously. This allows human supervisors to 'see' why the robot changed its plan, providing a feedback loop for calibrating the CNN's sensitivity.

title: Food Desert: Hungry For Answers
authors: Cumberbatch, Lawren
abstract: In 2010, the United States Department of Agriculture (U SDA) reported that 23.5 million people in the United States live in food deserts. A"food desert"is defined as a neighborhood that lacks healthy food sources. This can be measured by distance to a store, number of stores in an area, individual-level resources (family incom e, vehicle availability), and neighborhood-level resources (availability of public transportation). Past research provides evidence that food deserts are especially likely to occur in minority-heavy communities. As a multiracial pre-m ed student aim ing to join the world of healthcare innovation, the researcher is passionate about factors that affect the quali

suggestions.title: GAN-Based Stain Style Transfer
suggestions.desc: Implement a StainGAN architecture to translate WSIs from different hospitals (with varying H&E stain colors) into a 'canonical' color space. This ensures that downstream AI models don't fail due to minor variations in lab chemicals.

suggestions.title: Metric-Driven Normalization Benchmarking
suggestions.desc: Perform a comparative study of normalization algorithms (Reinhard, Macenko, and Vahadane). Use a standard classification model as a benchmark to measure which normalization method yields the highest F1-score across non-standardized datasets.

suggestions.title: Universal WSI-to-OME-TIFF Converter
suggestions.desc: Develop a high-performance C++ utility using the `libvips` library that converts proprietary, non-standard formats (SVS, NDPI, MRXS) into the standardized OME-TIFF format while preserving multi-resolution pyramid levels.

suggestions.title: Automated Quality Control (QC) Pre-Processor
suggestions.desc: Create a dashboard that automatically flags 'un-normalizable' WSIs. Use computer vision to detect artifacts like tissue folds, air bubbles, or focus-blur that would typically lead to incorrect AI diagnostics.

title: Auto Grader for Unstructured Open-ended Questions using Natural Language Processing and Recommender Systems
authors: Furrow, Anna
abstract: Auto-graders are systems that score a response in comparison to a given key such that it reduces variance and hum an error in the grading process. Open-ended questions allow students to convey their knowledge of a subject in a unique way, but these questions are tim e consum ing for a professor to grade. An auto-grader allows a professor to ask open-ended questions without having to make such a large tim e commitm ent to grading and would provide more consistency when grading. This can be done through the combination of Natural Language Processing techniques and Recom mender systems. Student responses and an answer key are transformed to word vectors and then compared and scored a

suggestions.title: Semantic Rubric Alignment using LLMs
suggestions.desc: Integrate the OpenAI or Llama-3 API to move beyond keyword matching. Build a system that parses a professor's rubric and ensures the auto-grader evaluates the 'conceptual depth' of an answer rather than just comparing it to a static answer key.

suggestions.title: Peer-Review Matching Recommender
suggestions.desc: Use the NLP engine to identify students with complementary knowledge gaps. The system could recommend specific students to peer-grade each other's work based on who explained certain concepts most clearly, fostering a collaborative learning environment.

suggestions.title: Grading Bias Detection Toolkit
suggestions.desc: Develop a research tool that audits the auto-grader for linguistic bias. Use datasets like the TOEFL writing prompts to test if the model penalizes non-native English sentence structures even when the core subject knowledge is correct.

suggestions.title: Canvas/Moodle LTI Integration Plugin
suggestions.desc: Package the auto-grader as an LTI (Learning Tools Interoperability) plugin. This would allow professors to use the system directly within their existing Learning Management Systems, providing students with instant, formative feedback on open-ended assignments.

title: Analyzing Adversarial Attacks in Machine Learning Models
authors: Mirkhelkar, Niti
abstract: Studies have been done on adversarial attacks in machine learning models, but not much research has looked at the possible adversarial attacks in Lidar data. Lidar, also known as Light Detection and Ranging, is used in autonom ous cars because of its ability to map a car's surroundings (Cao et al. 2019). This Lidar data is used in machine learning models to classify the objects surrounding of a vehicle, and adversarial attacks are a threat to their security as they aim to confuse and attack these machine learning models (Bosech, 2021; Garfinkel, 2017; Kapelke, 2019; Lin & Biggio, 2021; Rosenberg et al., 2021). Since these attacks are getting stronger and more effective over tim e, a

suggestions.title: Point Cloud Denoising Defense Layer
suggestions.desc: Implement a preprocessing defense mechanism using PointNet++ to identify and remove adversarial points from Lidar data. The system should be able to distinguish between environmental noise and intentional 'adversarial clusters' designed to hide objects.

suggestions.title: Physical-World Adversarial Patch Testing
suggestions.desc: Research the feasibility of 'cloaking' objects by 3D printing specific geometric shapes. Use a Lidar-equipped sensor to test if these physical objects can consistently fool the machine learning model into classifying a pedestrian as a stationary pole or empty space.

suggestions.title: Autonomous Fleet Security Auditor
suggestions.desc: Build a dashboard for security teams that visualizes 'blind spots' in a Lidar model's classification space. The tool would use GANs to generate millions of synthetic adversarial Lidar frames to stress-test the car's perception system in a safe, simulated environment.

suggestions.title: Real-time Lidar Anomaly Detection Engine
suggestions.desc: Develop a lightweight, on-vehicle monitoring system that compares Lidar inputs with camera (RGB) data. If a discrepancy is found (e.g., Lidar sees nothing but the camera sees a car), the system triggers an 'Adversarial Alert' and switches to a fail-safe driving mode.

title: BreastNet; Ensemble Deep Learning
authors: Meador, Cora L; Deem, Ryan
abstract: In the United states, 13% of wom en are diagnosed with breast cancer in their lifetim e, and it is the second leading cause of death by cancer in wom en. Early detection and screening can result in an increase of life expectancy by 10 years on average. Unfortunately, breast cancer can be challenging to detect, since it can appear anywhere in the breast. Cancer that is detected in its early stages can give patients more options and save thousands of dollars in medical costs. Som e of the most recent developm ents in computer science and machine learning are in the biom edical field, especially individualized healthcare. There is also an increase in the dem and for telehealth options, re

suggestions.title: XAI Heatmapping with Grad-CAM
suggestions.desc: Implement Grad-CAM (Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping) to visualize the decision-making process of the ensemble. This generates 'heatmaps' on the mammogram images, showing radiologists exactly which tissue structures the AI flagged as suspicious.

suggestions.title: Multi-Modal Diagnostic Fusion
suggestions.desc: Extend the Deep Learning model to accept multi-modal inputs: the mammogram image (CNN branch) and structured patient data like age, genetic markers, and family history (MLP branch). Use a late-fusion technique to combine these features for a more holistic diagnosis.

suggestions.title: Privacy-Preserving Federated Diagnosis
suggestions.desc: Utilize the Flower framework to implement Federated Learning. This allows the BreastNet model to be trained on datasets from multiple hospitals without the sensitive mammogram images ever leaving the local hospital servers, overcoming data sharing legal barriers.

suggestions.title: DICOM-Compliant Triage Tool
suggestions.desc: Develop a plugin for popular open-source medical imaging viewers like OHIF. The tool would automatically pre-scan DICOM images as they arrive and 'triage' the radiologist's queue, moving high-probability cancer cases to the top for immediate review.

title: Authenticating Middleware Implementing DNS-Based Identity
authors: Crowe, Brooklyn; Godwin, Jade; Marcos, Marilyn; Moore, Aaron; Rojo, Ricardo; Starr, Noah
abstract: Em ails have been around for a long tim e with very little improvem ents made. Without security in mind, you assum e that only people you have given your email to can contact you. So why not trust the strange person on the internet that claims you wont money from a deceased family mem ber far, far away? By adding authentication protocols and deciding who you will allow to send email, our added layer of protect can help keep your privacy safe.

suggestions.title: Acoustic-Driven Enemy AI
suggestions.desc: Implement a dynamic audio occlusion and propagation system in Unity/Unreal. The alien enemy should react differently to different sound types, such as the metallic 'click' of a flashlight vs. the muffled sound of a player walking on carpet.

suggestions.title: Asymmetric Tactical Multiplayer
suggestions.desc: Introduce a networking layer using Mirror or Photon. One player plays the survivor in first-person, while a second player acts as the 'Alien Hive Mind,' viewing a top-down tactical map and placing pheromones to guide AI enemies toward the survivor.

suggestions.title: Procedural Circuit Repair Puzzles
suggestions.desc: Replace static part-finding with a procedural puzzle system for fixing the communications. Players must complete randomized logic gate puzzles or frequency-tuning mini-games that become increasingly difficult as the alien gets closer.

suggestions.title: Biometric 'Fear' Feedback Integration
suggestions.desc: Connect the game to a wearable heart rate monitor via OSC. When the player's real-life pulse exceeds a threshold, the in-game character's hands could shake, making flashlight aiming harder, or the alien could become more aggressive, sensing the player's fear.

title: Linked in Alumni Data | IT4983 Section W01 | Group 2
authors: Trexell T. Bailey, Elijah Deputy, Sabiha Iqbal, Jam es Diaz, Evan Dillon, Kathryn S. Highers
abstract: Description: This project is an Analysis of Alternatives (AoA) for workspace deploym ent solutions in small scale IT deploym ent environm ents. Motivation: This research will be prim arily used to compare and measure the capabilities of various IT deploym ent solutions and provide a recom mendation on the best solution for small scale IT deploym ent environm ents using RHEL, Windows, or MacOS. Materials: Google Docs, Google Sheets, Kennesaw State University Undergraduate Research Lab, RHEL 7, RHEL 8, Windows 10, Windows Server 2019, Ubuntu. Methods: Our methods include requirem ents elicitation through weekly meetings with our client. This presents us with the opportunity early o
supervisor: Dr. Reza Parizi (Course Instructor) Allison Boyle (Supervisor) Dr.

suggestions.title: Automated Workspace Orchestration with Ansible and Terraform
suggestions.desc: Take the recommended RHEL and Windows configurations and convert them into Infrastructure as Code (IaC). Build a repository of playbooks and scripts that can automatically provision and harden these workspace environments in minutes on a local hypervisor or cloud provider.

suggestions.title: Hybrid Cloud Extension for Small-Scale IT
suggestions.desc: Extend the Analysis of Alternatives (AoA) to include hybrid cloud bursting. Implement a proof-of-concept using AWS Outposts or Azure Stack Edge to demonstrate how a small IT shop can maintain local control while scaling resources into the public cloud during peak demand.

suggestions.title: IT Asset Lifecycle & Compliance Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Develop a centralized monitoring dashboard using the ELK Stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana) that tracks the deployment health, patch status, and software licensing costs across the RHEL, Windows, and MacOS environments identified in the research.

suggestions.title: Performance Benchmarking under Network Latency
suggestions.desc: Conduct a deep-dive research extension by measuring the user experience (latency and throughput) of these workspace solutions over varying network conditions. Use tools like GNS3 to simulate constrained bandwidth to see which deployment solution holds up best for remote users.

title: IT Deployment AoA
authors: Bailey, Trexell T.; Deputy, Elijah; Iqbal, Sabiha; Diaz, James; Dillon, Evan; Highers, Kathryn S

suggestions.title: Virtual Desktop Infrastructure (VDI) Latency Tester
suggestions.desc: Extend the deployment research to include remote work scenarios. Build a test suite that measures the latency and frame-rate stability of RDP (Windows) vs. VNC/SSH (RHEL) over varying network conditions to determine the best OS for remote staff.

suggestions.title: Automated Migration Playbook Generator
suggestions.desc: Create a tool that takes a current Windows environment inventory and generates a step-by-step 'Migration Playbook' for moving to RHEL. This would include mapping Windows services to their Linux equivalents and identifying incompatible legacy software.

suggestions.title: Green Computing Energy Profiler
suggestions.desc: Use software-based power meters (like Intel RAPL) to measure the energy consumption of identical workloads on the different OS alternatives. This allows small businesses to choose the most sustainable deployment solution as part of their CSR goals.

suggestions.title: Zero-Trust Security Maturity Comparison
suggestions.desc: Research and document the ease of implementing Zero-Trust principles (identity-based access, micro-segmentation) on RHEL vs. Windows. Create a reference architecture for a small IT shop for both platforms using open-source tools like WireGuard.

title: Remote Presence Robot in the Classroom
authors: Trinite, Noah; Furrow, Anna; Lyons, Christopher; Odeyemi, Joshua; Tatge, George; Perdue, James; Sloan, Dylan
abstract: COVID-19 has impacted everyone. Both students and professors have talked about the disconnect felt between them selves and their classes due to everything moving online. The purpose of this project is to give the students an opportunity to feel like they have a little more connection between them selves and the classroom while also providing the professor a chance for a physical audience so they’re not staring at a screen of initials. Students will be able to connect to the robot from another computer and control the robot via keyboard commands, the user will be able to see and hear from the bot’s perspective via the webcam on the bot. The bot will have a monitor that will display the
topics: Other (explain in the com m ents section)

suggestions.title: Low-Latency WebRTC Video Pipeline
suggestions.desc: Replace standard video streaming with a custom WebRTC implementation to achieve sub-200ms latency. Integrate Kurento or Jitsi APIs to ensure the remote student's interactions feel instantaneous, which is critical for natural classroom participation.

suggestions.title: Augmented Reality Whiteboard Interaction
suggestions.desc: Use OpenCV and ARCore to allow the remote student to 'draw' on their screen and have those annotations appear as an AR overlay on the robot's physical tablet. This enables the student to point to specific parts of a whiteboard during a lecture.

suggestions.title: Autonomous Navigation using LiDAR and SLAM
suggestions.desc: Upgrade the robot with a RPLIDAR sensor and implement ROS (Robot Operating System) with Gmapping or Cartographer. This allows the robot to build a map of the classroom and navigate to a specific 'seat' or the professor's desk without manual steering.

suggestions.title: Multi-Student Collaborative Observation Mode
suggestions.desc: Develop a queue-based control system that allows one student to 'drive' while others connect as 'passive observers.' Build a classroom-wide dashboard where the professor can see which students are 'present' via the robot's ecosystem.

title: Universe Language CRM App
authors: Stanley, Kara; Diab, Khalid; Rodriguez Serrano, Gabriel A; King, Sammantha; Martinez, Carolina; Bridges, Taren
abstract: We created a mobile app and desktop app that are used together in order for Universe Language Solutions to be able to easily manage requests to book the services they offer. The desktop app is used by the adm inistrative team to view what clients have requested and what staff can work. The mobile
supervisor: Dr. Reza Parizi - capstone professor Carlos Solis - project sponsor
topics: Other (explain in the com m ents section)

suggestions.title: Procedural Puzzle Generator
suggestions.desc: Create an algorithm that generates unique claw machine levels by randomly arranging physics-based components (screws, levers, springs) that require specific character-switching sequences to solve.

suggestions.title: Steam Workshop Level Editor
suggestions.desc: Build an in-game editor using Unity's UI Toolkit that allows players to design their own machine-themed puzzles and share them with the community via a cloud-hosted backend or Steam Workshop integration.

suggestions.title: Asynchronous Couch Co-op
suggestions.desc: Implement a networking layer using Mirror or Photon that allows two players to control Berry and Carrot simultaneously on separate devices, requiring tight verbal coordination to navigate the claw machine's hazards.

suggestions.title: Dynamic 'Claw' Physics Events
suggestions.desc: Develop a system where an overhead claw periodically interacts with the level, moving platforms or dropping 'prizes' that change the level geometry, forcing the player to adapt their strategy mid-puzzle.

title: Hibernate Funds: Back Office Portals for Fundraisers
authors: Blackwell, Phillip; Merritt, Kasey L.; Krouba, Patrick A.; Duran, Daniel; Calixto, Diana; Miller, Logan
abstract: For this project, we are working with the Hibernate Funds organization to build a new web application that can be used by organizations, such as schools, who are trying to raise funds. We will be creating an online, back office portal that will allow these organizations to better track the data associated with their fundraiser. To that end, each user role in the system will have their own dashboards where they will be able to track statistics relevant to their own position. The dashboards will display important information, in both a tabular and graphical form at. These dashboards will help organizations who are fundraising by autom atically keeping track of their sales and displaying relev
supervisor: Capstone instructor: Dr. Reza Parizi Project sponsor: Cody

suggestions.title: Automated Financial Reconciliation with Stripe and Plaid
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Stripe API for payment processing and the Plaid API to verify bank accounts. Build an automated reconciliation engine that matches incoming donations with school-specific ledger entries, reducing manual accounting labor for the Hibernate Funds staff.

suggestions.title: Gamified Donor Engagement Engine
suggestions.desc: Develop a module that adds leaderboards, milestone badges, and real-time 'thermometer' visualizations for specific fundraising goals. Use WebSockets (Socket.io) to push live donation alerts to the back office portals to boost morale during active campaigns.

suggestions.title: Predictive Analytics for Fundraising Success
suggestions.desc: Implement a machine learning model using Scikit-learn to analyze historical campaign data. Build a feature into the dashboard that predicts the final total of a fundraiser based on the first 48 hours of activity, helping organizers pivot their strategy early.

suggestions.title: Anonymized Open-Data Export API
suggestions.desc: Build an ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) pipeline that generates anonymized, aggregated datasets for educational research. This would allow third-party researchers to study regional fundraising trends without compromising donor privacy.

title: 3D Information Extraction from Physics Exercise Videos
authors: Shikirah B. Johnson, Orion Williams, Aaron Bennett, Braden Tucker
abstract: In our paper we consider a full duplex relaying system powered by an energy constrained relay node. The Relay is powered by radio frequency signals, provided by an access point, and is utilizing tim e switching architecture to performwireless information and power transfer. In our research we focus on beam form ing from Relay to destination. We consider the delay tolerant transm ission method and the realistic throughput of data and power. In the process of sending information to the destination the relay is using Am plify-and-forward to increase the signal. In the process we consider the effects of an eavesdropper between the relay and destination, we utilize jam ming signals to ensure
supervisor: Dr. Ying Xie - Instructor Dr. Sum it Chakravarty - Project Sponsor

suggestions.title: Software-Defined Radio (SDR) Prototyping
suggestions.desc: Implement the full-duplex relaying system described in the abstract using GNU Radio and USRP (Universal Software Radio Peripheral) hardware. This moves the project from a mathematical model to a physical radio testbed to validate throughput under real-world interference.

suggestions.title: Deep Reinforcement Learning for Energy Harvesting
suggestions.desc: Develop a DRL agent (using PyTorch or TensorFlow) that dynamically optimizes the 'time switching architecture' between wireless power transfer and information transfer. Train the agent to maximize data throughput while ensuring the energy-constrained relay never runs out of power.

suggestions.title: Multi-Hop Relay Network Simulation
suggestions.desc: Expand the point-to-point relay model into a multi-hop mesh network using NS-3 (Network Simulator 3). Research how beamforming techniques from one relay affect the interference levels of adjacent relays in a high-density IoT environment.

suggestions.title: Power-Aware IoT Scheduling Protocol
suggestions.desc: Design a communication protocol specifically for industrial sensors that utilize the research's 'delay tolerant transmission' method. Create a management console that visualizes the 'realistic throughput' and power levels of every node in a factory setting.

title: Wireless Information and Power Transfer using Beam Forming
authors: Johnson, Shikirah B.; Williams, Orion; Benentt, Aaron; Tucker, Braden
abstract: In our research we consider a full duplex relaying system powered by an energy constrained relay node. The Relay is powered by radio frequency signals, provided by an access point, and is utilizing time switching to perform wireless information and power transfer. In our research we focus on beam forming from Relay to destination. We consider the delay tolerant transmission method and the realistic throughput of data and power. In the process of sending information to the destination the relay is using Amplify-and-forward to increase the signal. We consider the effects of an eavesdropper between the relay and destination; we utilize artificial noise signals to ensure that the eavesdropper does not receive the same information as the other nodes in the destination area. We focus on the optimal time it will take to complete the transmission of information and power from source to destination.

suggestions.title: Edge-Optimized YOLOv8 Object Detection
suggestions.desc: Replace generic computer vision with a custom-trained YOLOv8 model specifically tuned to recognize partially used produce and obscure packaging. Fine-tuning the model on a 'fridge-specific' dataset will significantly reduce ingredient identification errors.

suggestions.title: Nutritionix API & Macro Integration
suggestions.desc: Connect Cookly.io to the Nutritionix API to automatically calculate the caloric and macronutrient breakdown for every generated recipe. This allows users to filter AI-generated meals based on specific dietary goals like high-protein or low-carb.

suggestions.title: Smart Grocery Cart Auto-Sync
suggestions.desc: Implement a 'Missing Ingredients' feature that identifies items needed for a recipe that were NOT found in the pantry photo. Integrate with the Kroger or Instacart APIs to let users add those missing items to a digital shopping cart with one click.

suggestions.title: Zero-Waste Expiration Forecasting
suggestions.desc: Develop a logic layer that tracks when ingredients were first 'seen' by the camera. The recipe generator can then prioritize 'Near-Expiration' ingredients, helping users reduce food waste by suggesting recipes that use up wilting produce first.

title: Habermas V0.1
authors: Anthony Grant, Chloe E. Raym ond, Sean M. Shields, Mark T. McVay Project Sponsors: Jonathan Boardm an, Dr. Ying Xie
supervisor: KSU supervisor/Sponsor: Dr. Ying Xie Sponsor: Jonathan Boardm an

suggestions.title: Automated Polarization Mapping for Social Discourse
suggestions.desc: Building on the 'Habermas' concept of the public sphere, use the Reddit API to collect comments from politically opposing subreddits. Apply sentiment analysis and BERTopic to visualize how different online communities discuss the same news events.

suggestions.title: Real-time Deliberative Quality Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Create a D3.js visualization tool that monitors live chat or forum data. Use NLP to score the 'deliberative quality' of a conversation based on Habermasian criteria like reciprocity, justification, and respect.

suggestions.title: Fact-Checking Integration via ClaimBuster API
suggestions.desc: Enhance the data platform by integrating the ClaimBuster API to automatically flag factual assertions in political transcripts. Build a frontend that overlays these flags on video transcripts to help users identify misinformation during debates.

suggestions.title: Cognitive Bias Detection in Public Comments
suggestions.desc: Develop a machine learning model using RoBERTa to detect common cognitive biases (e.g., confirmation bias, ad hominem attacks) in public discourse datasets. The goal is to provide users with a 'bias score' for specific news comment sections.

title: Telemedicine Web Application - CS4850/03 - Group 5A
authors: Lanktree, Cade T.; Wessels, Michael L.; Shidhaye, Yash; Crews, Kevin J.; Bonilla, Estephany; Patterson, Jodi-Ann; Paris, Will
abstract: The motivation for this project was to create a telem edicine web portal for a local physician. Telem edicine web applications have becom e indispensable during the COVID-19 Pandem ic in order to minim ize the transm ission risk of SARS-CoV-2 between patients and healthcare workers. This portal will be able to: store messages/chat, reports, requests for appointm ents, store video recordings, and host video recordings in a HIPPA compliant manner. This project was assigned to students in Section 03 of the Senior Project Course at Kennesaw State University. The project subm issions are split into three sprints throughout the Fall 2021 Sem ester. The prelim inary results as of this abstrac

suggestions.title: End-to-End Encryption (E2EE) for Video Storage
suggestions.desc: Implement client-side encryption for video recordings before they are uploaded to the server. Use the Web Crypto API to ensure that even if the database is compromised, the videos remain unreadable without the specific patient/doctor key pairs, exceeding HIPAA standards.

suggestions.title: AI-Powered Patient Triage Chatbot
suggestions.desc: Integrate a HIPAA-compliant NLP engine (like Amazon Comprehend Medical) to build a symptom pre-screener. The chatbot can gather initial patient data and highlight high-risk keywords for the physician before the actual video consultation begins.

suggestions.title: HL7/FHIR Standards Interoperability
suggestions.desc: Develop a data bridge that exports appointment and report data into HL7 FHIR format. This allows the telemedicine portal to sync seamlessly with larger Electronic Medical Record (EMR) systems like Epic or Cerner used in major hospitals.

suggestions.title: Telemedicine No-Show Prediction Model
suggestions.desc: Analyze the 'request for appointments' data to build a predictive model for patient no-shows. Use logistical regression to identify factors (time of day, patient history) that lead to missed appointments and implement an automated SMS reminder system to mitigate risk.

title: Security Solution for a Small Business
authors: Casasola, Jessica; Englert, Craig; Nguyen, Phuc; Pegram, Charles; Vesper, James
abstract: For our project, we constructed our own e-commerce website using an open source tem plate to use within our server as a simulation for a small business. Working through the server assigned through us, we installed our e- commerce site and built a security program around it. Our security includes an Avast anti-virus, Snort IDS/IPS, and a Pfsense firewall. We also changed som e settings within the server to only allow for som e changes to be made as well as changed user and root passwords.
supervisor: Supervisor: Dr. Ying Xie Sponsor: Dr. Lei Li

suggestions.title: Behavior Tree Enemy AI
suggestions.desc: Replace simple chasing mechanics with advanced Behavior Trees or Goal-Oriented Action Planning (GOAP), allowing bodyguards to use cover, reload tactically, and coordinate flanking maneuvers against the player's robots.

suggestions.title: RTS-Style Tactical Overlay
suggestions.desc: Build a 'Command Mode' where the camera switches to a top-down view, allowing players to pause time and issue specific movement waypoints and target priorities to their robot units before returning to FPS mode.

suggestions.title: Modular Robot Component System
suggestions.desc: Implement a system where players can customize their robots before a mission, choosing different chassis (treads vs. bipedal) and utility tools (EMP bursts vs. physical shields) to counter specific bodyguard types.

suggestions.title: Stealth & Hacking Expansion
suggestions.desc: Add a hacking mechanic where players must use their robots to access remote terminals or disable security cameras, turning the game into a tactical stealth experience where direct combat with bodyguards is a last resort.

title: Georgia School Grades Map
authors: Duren, Wiliam T.; Richardson, Joe; Doige, Amanda; Anderson, Tylor; Fayiso, Gamada
abstract: This project focused on creating a Volunteer Management System (VMS) for Angels Among Us (AAU) - a non-profit organization dedicated to rescuing and rehabilitating stray and abandoned animals. This application was developed to: * Handle comprehensive volunteer information * Streamline operations and better manage volunteer data * Support AAU specific use cases * Include a data enrichment capability through a newly developed GUI * Allow authorized users to add more comprehensive information to each volunteer record * implement reporting throughout the data migration process

suggestions.title: Gamified Volunteer Engagement Portal
suggestions.desc: Implement a 'Volunteer Leaderboard' and achievement system within the GUI. Volunteers could earn digital badges for milestones like '50 Hours Served' or 'Successfully Rehomed 5 Dogs,' using a simple backend logic to increase retention through healthy competition.

suggestions.title: AI-Driven Shift Matching Engine
suggestions.desc: Develop a recommendation algorithm that suggests specific volunteer shifts to users based on their historical data and stated skills (e.g., matching a volunteer with medical experience to a shift involving sick animal care). Use the existing database to track skill-task alignment.

suggestions.title: Mobile Check-In with Geofencing
suggestions.desc: Build a companion React Native app that allows volunteers to check in and out of the rescue facility using QR codes or GPS geofencing. This data would feed directly into the Volunteer Management System, automating the tracking of service hours.

suggestions.title: External Data Enrichment via LinkedIn API
suggestions.desc: Expand the 'data enrichment' capability by integrating APIs from LinkedIn or Clearbit. This would allow the system to automatically pull in professional skills or company affiliations for volunteers, helping the rescue identify people who can provide pro-bono legal, marketing, or vet services.

title: 3D Extraction from Physics Exercise Videos
authors: Matthews, Zachary; Sherpa, Lhakpa N.; Alvarez, Francisco J.; Guan, Sam; McFarland, Philip; Bruton, Cole
abstract: This project is used to track the movem ent and pinpoint key points on the people that are in the videos.
supervisor: Dr. Ying Xie & Dr. Linh Le

suggestions.title: Biomechanical Angle Analysis for Physical Therapy
suggestions.desc: Extend the keypoint tracking to calculate joint angles (e.g., knee extension, elbow flexion) in real-time. Build a feedback dashboard that alerts a user if their form deviates from a pre-defined 'safe' physics model for an exercise.

suggestions.title: Synthetic Data Generation via Unity Perception
suggestions.desc: Improve the tracking model's robustness by generating a synthetic dataset in Unity. By using domain randomization on virtual 3D avatars, you can train the model to handle diverse lighting and clothing occlusions that are hard to capture in real videos.

suggestions.title: 3D Human Mesh Recovery (HMR)
suggestions.desc: Move beyond 2D keypoints to full 3D mesh reconstruction using the SMPL model. Use a temporal encoder (like a Transformer) to ensure smooth 3D movement across video frames, allowing for volume and mass estimation during physics exercises.

suggestions.title: AR Overlay for Real-time Physics Visualization
suggestions.desc: Use ARCore or ARKit to project the extracted physics data—such as velocity vectors and center of mass—directly onto the user’s body during a live video feed. This helps students visualize the 'physics' of their own movements in real-time.

title: Georgia Schools
authors: Trivers, Maxwell; Knezevich, Bailey; Oso, Samson; Khan, Mo
abstract: In this project, our team has automated tournament tasks in the KSU eSports Discord server, with a focus on the League of Legends tournaments. Our team has implemented a matchmaking algorithm that forms teams consisting of players placed within one tier of each other, so teams are evenly matched. Our team has also created a database that stores player statistics and has been integrated with the Discord bot. Furthermore, our team has integrated the developer API with the Discord bot, which pulls player data from the API when players join the server, and the team has been working to improve the UI of the bot. The team has performed regular testing and will continue to perform regular testing and updates as necessary. Department: Information Technology Supervisor: Prof. Donald Privitera Topics: Programming

suggestions.title: Cross-Game Elo & Ranking System
suggestions.desc: Extend the bot beyond League of Legends by integrating the Valorant and Rocket League APIs. Develop a unified KSU 'Skill Rating' that tracks player performance across multiple titles and adjusts their tier for all university-wide tournaments.

suggestions.title: Automated Twitch Highlight Reel Bot
suggestions.desc: Use the Discord bot to listen for 'GG' or 'Big Play' messages in the match channel. It can then interface with the Twitch API to automatically clip the last 30 seconds of the KSU stream and post the link to a 'highlights' channel for social media use.

suggestions.title: Draft-Based Win Probability Predictor
suggestions.desc: Build an ML model trained on historical KSU tournament data that analyzes team compositions (champions picked) in real-time. The bot can then post a 'Win Probability' percentage to the Discord channel as soon as the League of Legends draft is complete.

suggestions.title: Automated Tournament Lobby Creation
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Riot Games LCU (League Client Update) API to allow the bot to automatically create private tournament lobbies and invite players. This removes the manual step of captains adding each other as friends and setting up custom game settings.

title: A Robust Federated Machine Learning Framework for Security Analytics in Solar Farms
authors: Li, Jiaming
abstract: Sm art grids face more cyber threats than before with the integration of photovoltaic (PV) systems. Data-driven based machine learning (ML) methods have been verified to be effective in detecting attacks in power electronics devices. However, standard ML solution requires centralized data collection then processing that is becom ing infeasible in more and more applications due to efficiency issues and increasing data privacy concerns. In this letter, we propose a novel decentralized ML fram ework for detecting false data injection (FDI) attacks on solar PV DC/DC and DC/AC converters. The proposed paradigm incorporates the emerging technology nam ed federated learning (FL) that enables c

suggestions.title: Byzantine-Resilient Federated Aggregation
suggestions.desc: Implement robust aggregation algorithms like 'Krum' or 'Median' to protect the solar farm framework against malicious nodes. This prevents a single compromised inverter from poisoning the global model with false security data.

suggestions.title: Predictive Maintenance for PV Inverters
suggestions.desc: Extend the framework to detect physical hardware degradation alongside cyber threats. Use unsupervised anomaly detection (like Isolation Forests) on solar power output data to predict when an inverter is likely to fail before it happens.

suggestions.title: Differential Privacy for Smart Grid Gradients
suggestions.desc: Add a Layer of Differential Privacy (DP) using the Opacus library to ensure that individual solar farm data cannot be reverse-engineered from the shared model weights. This addresses the privacy concerns mentioned in the abstract.

suggestions.title: Blockchain-Enabled Model Integrity Tracking
suggestions.desc: Integrate a lightweight blockchain to log the participation of every node in the Federated Learning rounds. This creates an auditable trail of model updates, ensuring that every update to the solar farm's security logic is verified and authorized.

title: AI for Social Good: Assisting the Elderly/Visually Impaired in Reading Prescription Labels
authors: Allen, Stacie P; Barnes, Jessica; Pope, Lauren; Hill, Chennelle
abstract: According to the Centers for Disease Control (CDC), roughly 12 million people in the United States above the age of 40 are visually impaired. In adults Graduate Research (14)

suggestions.title: Automated Drug-Drug Interaction Warning System
suggestions.desc: Connect the OCR output to the NIH RxNav API to check for potential negative interactions between multiple scanned prescriptions. The app would alert the visually impaired user if they attempt to scan two medications that should not be taken together.

suggestions.title: Edge-AI Mobile Deployment with TensorFlow Lite
suggestions.desc: Optimize the AI models for on-device inference using TFLite to ensure the app works without an internet connection. Implement a 'haptic guidance' system that uses phone vibrations to help the user center the label in the camera's view.

suggestions.title: Multilingual Audio Translation for ESL Patients
suggestions.desc: Add a translation layer using the DeepL API to translate complex English medical instructions into the user's native language. The instructions can then be read aloud using high-quality Text-to-Speech (TTS) like Google WaveNet.

suggestions.title: Layout Analysis for Complex Medication Packaging
suggestions.desc: Implement LayoutLM to differentiate between dosage instructions, expiration dates, and pharmacy contact info. This prevents the AI from reading the instructions out of order, which is a common failure point for standard OCR.

title: Analysis of Malware Detection Techniques
authors: Stansbury, Joel; White, Robert; Bazan, Keifer
abstract: Today’s digital world is pervaded with malware. In response to this reality, there are copious studies being conducted around the world on how best to improve the detection of malware, as malware becom es more sophisticated with every passing year. In the following report, we will discuss som e current studies of interest on malware detection techniques and propose som e of our own suppositions on how these suggested techniques can be improved upon.

suggestions.title: Dynamic Malware Sandboxing with Cuckoo
suggestions.desc: Build an automated pipeline that executes suspicious binaries in a Cuckoo Sandbox and extracts behavioral API calls. Use these behavioral sequences to train a Recurrent Neural Network (RNN) that detects malware based on 'what it does' rather than 'what it looks like'.

suggestions.title: Adversarial Malware Generation and Defense
suggestions.desc: Develop a Generative Adversarial Network (GAN) to create 'adversarial' malware samples that can bypass current detectors. Then, use these samples to retrain and harden the existing detection algorithms against future sophisticated threats.

suggestions.title: Serverless Malware Scanner for Cloud Storage
suggestions.desc: Create an AWS Lambda-based scanner that automatically triggers when a file is uploaded to an S3 bucket. It should perform both static YARA rule matching and ML-based feature extraction to ensure cloud data remains clean.

suggestions.title: IoT Firmware Binary Analysis Framework
suggestions.desc: Apply the detection techniques specifically to IoT device firmware (MIPS/ARM architectures). Use Ghidra for headless decompilation and train a model to find common 'backdoor' patterns in compiled binaries.

similar: legacy:GR-023|Spring 2021|machine learning techniques for malware network traffic detection

title: Using Machine Learning to Detect Alzheimer's Disease
authors: Razzak, Rhema; Fu, Yang
abstract: More than 6 million Am ericans are currently living with Alzheim er's Disease. Alzheim er's Disease causes problems with mem ory, thinking and behavior, and while treatm ents exist to help manage the condition, there is no known cure. Alzheim er's Disease often requires lab / imaging tests for proper diagnosis, but even with current diagnostic tools, accuracy from these tests can vary. These tests also may not be able to differentiate between normal brain function and mild to moderate Alzheim er's Disease. As such, Machine Learning has proven to be a valuable tool in improving Alzheim er's Disease detection and classification. For our project, We utilize Machine Learning to accurate

suggestions.title: Explainable AI (XAI) for MRI Diagnostic Support
suggestions.desc: Implement SHAP or Grad-CAM to visualize which regions of the brain (e.g., hippocampus atrophy) the ML model is using to make an Alzheimer's diagnosis. This helps clinicians trust the AI's output by providing a 'heat map' of the scan.

suggestions.title: Multimodal Fusion of Imaging and Cognitive Tests
suggestions.desc: Create a multi-input neural network that combines MRI image data with tabular data from cognitive assessments (like MMSE scores). Research shows that fusing structural data with behavioral data significantly improves detection accuracy in early stages.

suggestions.title: Longitudinal Disease Progression Tracker
suggestions.desc: Develop a mobile app for patients to perform weekly cognitive 'mini-games' that measure memory and reaction time. Use a Time-Series Transformer to analyze these scores over months to detect subtle declines that lab tests might miss.

suggestions.title: Privacy-Preserving Federated MRI Training
suggestions.desc: Use PySyft to build a federated learning system where hospitals can collaborate to train a global Alzheimer's detection model without ever sharing raw patient brain scans, maintaining strict medical privacy and data security.

title: Non-Invasive Monitoring of Human Hygiene using Vibration Sensor and Classifiers
authors: Trivedi, Shashank; Hossain Faruk, Md Jobair
abstract: Abstract: Personal hygiene is how people take care of their bodies. Maintaining hygiene practice reduces the spread of illness and the risk of medical conditions. With the current pandemic situation, practices like washing hands and taking regular showers have taken major importance among people, especially for senior populations that live alone at home. Having an understanding of the human hygiene habits of our seniors is fundamental to monitoring health conditions.This research work presents the concept and idea of a noninvasive monitoring system for human hygiene using only vibration sensors. The approach is based on a geophone, a digitizer, and a cost-efficient computer board (raspberry pi). We capture the vibration of the water flow while people perform activities in the bathroom (open faucet, flush toilets) and kitchen (open kitchen sink). Results show that our approach can distinguish from these different activities with an accuracy higher than 90%. With this approach, we hope to start a new tendency of monitoring people activities without using cameras or other privacy-invasive methods. Department: Information Technology Supervisor: Dr. Maria Valero Topics: IoT/Cloud/Networking
supervisor: Dr. Maria Valero De Clem ente

suggestions.title: Edge-AI Processing on ESP32 Microcontrollers
suggestions.desc: Port the vibration classifiers to run locally on an ESP32 or Arduino Nano 33 BLE using TensorFlow Lite for Microcontrollers. This ensures that sensitive hygiene data is processed on the device itself rather than being sent to the cloud, significantly improving privacy.

suggestions.title: Smart Home Hub Integration (Home Assistant)
suggestions.desc: Develop a custom integration for Home Assistant using MQTT. This would allow the vibration sensor to trigger other smart home events, such as turning on a bathroom heater when a shower is detected or sending a notification if a hand-washing event hasn't occurred before a meal.

suggestions.title: Multi-Modal Sensor Fusion for High Accuracy
suggestions.desc: Combine the vibration data with acoustic sensors or humidity sensors. Use a sensor fusion algorithm (like a Kalman Filter or a Feed-forward Neural Network) to distinguish between similar vibration patterns, such as a running faucet versus a shower.

suggestions.title: Caregiver Alert & Wellness Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Build a React-based mobile application for family members of seniors. The app should display a 'wellness score' based on hygiene habits and provide real-time push notifications (via Firebase) if a significant deviation from regular habits is detected.

title: Non-Invasive Glucose Monitoring System using Raspberry Pi Four
authors: Pola, Priyanka

suggestions.title: Pi Zero Miniaturization and Custom PCB
suggestions.desc: Transition the project from a bulky Raspberry Pi 4 to a compact Raspberry Pi Zero 2W or an ESP32. Design a custom PCB that integrates the glucose sensors and a LiPo battery charging circuit for a truly wearable form factor.

suggestions.title: Secure Cloud Telemetry with AWS IoT Core
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Raspberry Pi with AWS IoT Core to securely stream glucose data to a DynamoDB database. Build a web dashboard using React to allow physicians to view historical trends and generate monthly reports for the patient.

suggestions.title: Transfer Learning for Inter-Patient Variability
suggestions.desc: Apply transfer learning to handle the fact that every person's skin and body chemistry responds differently to non-invasive sensors. Train a base model on a large dataset and create a 'calibration mode' that fine-tunes the model for a specific user.

suggestions.title: Emergency SMS Notification System via Twilio
suggestions.desc: Develop a Python-based emergency module on the Raspberry Pi that uses the Twilio API. If the system detects a critical glucose spike or drop, it will automatically send an SMS with the patient's GPS coordinates to emergency contacts.

title: Diabetic Retinopathy Detection using Deep Neural Networks without Pre-Processing techniques
authors: Bharti, Suman; Pailla, Swetha
abstract: Diabetic retinopathy (DR) is an eye condition that can lead to vision loss and blindness in people who have diabetes. The manual screening of

suggestions.title: Mobile Deployment with TensorFlow Lite
suggestions.desc: Optimize the DNN model for mobile execution by applying post-training quantization. Build an Android/iOS app that allows healthcare workers in remote areas to take a photo of a retinal scan and get an immediate offline classification without needing a high-end server.

suggestions.title: Explainable AI (XAI) for Clinical Trust
suggestions.desc: Implement Grad-CAM (Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping) to generate heatmaps over the retinal images. This will show physicians exactly which regions (e.g., hemorrhages or exudates) the model used to make its diagnosis, making the 'black box' DNN more trustworthy.

suggestions.title: Vision Transformer (ViT) Comparative Analysis
suggestions.desc: Research the effectiveness of Vision Transformers against the original DNN architecture. Since the abstract mentions 'no pre-processing,' test if the self-attention mechanism of ViTs is better at handling raw, unnormalized retinal imagery than standard CNNs.

suggestions.title: Automated Referral & Tracking System
suggestions.desc: Build a cloud-based pipeline that automatically flags 'high severity' detections and populates a referral form for a specialist. Integrate this with a patient management system to track the outcome of the referral and refine the model with the specialist's feedback.

title: Decentralized Network Architecture on Git Platform
authors: Lashgari, Jonathan D; Narnavaram, Kiran mai M; Gowru, Rohit
abstract: Git is a powerful distributed version control system used by developers all over the world. It is common practice to have developers synchronize with a central server to ensure all end-users have the most recent files and can access earlier versions of files. Central network architectures have the main problem of single-point failure. By creating a true distributed Git platform , end-users will participate in a decentralized network architecture and rem ove the issue of single-point failure. This project will compare the effects of different decentralized network architectures on the Git platform , implem ent a select number of the architectures, and propose one of the architectures to

suggestions.title: IPFS-Backed Decentralized Storage
suggestions.desc: Integrate InterPlanetary File System (IPFS) to store Git objects. This ensures that the code is not just distributed but content-addressed and permanent, preventing any single node from deleting history and providing a truly resilient backbone for the platform.

suggestions.title: Smart Contract Access Control
suggestions.desc: Use the Ethereum or Polygon blockchain to manage repository permissions. By using a Decentralized Identifier (DID) and smart contracts, repository owners can manage 'Push' and 'Merge' rights without a central authority like GitHub's IAM system.

suggestions.title: P2P Collaborative Conflict Resolution
suggestions.desc: Develop a peer-to-peer protocol for handling merge conflicts in a decentralized environment. Implement a 'voting' or 'consensus' mechanism among active contributors to decide which branch becomes the 'main' branch when multiple conflicting updates occur simultaneously.

suggestions.title: Byzantine Fault Tolerance in Git Syncing
suggestions.desc: Research and implement a consensus algorithm (like PBFT or Raft) to ensure that even if some nodes in the decentralized Git network provide malicious or corrupted data, the honest nodes can still agree on the correct repository state.

title: Transfer learning with synthetic data improves the performance of fashion apparel pattern classification and neckline detection
authors: Chen, Tong; Cranfill, Luke; Morris, John
abstract: The large size of the training dataset is often required to obtain a robust deep learning model. In the project, we aim to offer a solution to obtain a robust deep learning model with only a small amount of data. We proposed a machine learning schem e that utilizes transfer learning with synthetic data

suggestions.title: GAN-Based Synthetic Data Augmentation
suggestions.desc: Use a Generative Adversarial Network (CycleGAN) to create highly realistic synthetic fashion images from 3D models. This would expand the training set for rare patterns or complex necklines that are difficult to find in the original small dataset.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Fashion 'Shazam' Application
suggestions.desc: Deploy the trained model into a mobile app that uses the camera feed to identify patterns and necklines in real-time. Integrate with the Pinterest or Amazon API to find and recommend similar apparel items based on the detected attributes.

suggestions.title: Automated E-commerce Tagging Service
suggestions.desc: Build a B2B API service for small fashion boutiques. The service would allow users to upload bulk photos of their inventory, automatically tagging them with pattern types and neckline styles to speed up the product listing process for Shopify or WooCommerce.

suggestions.title: Domain Adaptation for Synthetic-to-Real Transfer
suggestions.desc: Investigate 'Maximum Classifier Discrepancy' or other domain adaptation techniques to bridge the 'reality gap.' Measure how much the model's accuracy on real-world photos improves when the synthetic training data is specifically tuned to match the lighting and textures of real fashion photography.

similar: legacy:GR-27|Spring 2018|fault detection in iot using deep learning

title: Sentiment Analysis on Hotel Review
authors: Pallapothu, Navya; Pandanaboyana, Aravinda; Kumar, Dilip
abstract: In this project, we are going to implem ent Sentim ent Analysis (also known as opinion mining or emotion AI) is the use of natural language processing, text analysis, computational linguistics, and biom etrics to system atically identify, extract, quantify, and study affective states and subjective information. Sentim ent analysis is widely applied to voice of the custom er materials such as reviews and survey responses, online and social media, and healthcare materials for applications that range from marketing to custom er service to clinical medicine. As part of this project, we used the dataset of around 550K records and selected the 10% records out from that random ly as sam p

suggestions.title: Predictive Burnout Dashboard for High-Stress Occupations
suggestions.desc: Expand the ML model into a B2B platform for hospitals or air traffic control centers. By aggregating longitudinal data from wearables (Apple Watch/Fitbit APIs), the system can predict peak burnout periods for staff and suggest proactive schedule adjustments before medical errors occur.

suggestions.title: Haptic Biofeedback Loop for Anxiety Management
suggestions.desc: Create a mobile app that connects to the wearable and, upon detecting a stress spike via the ML model, triggers a subtle haptic 'rhythm' on the user's wrist. This rhythm guides the user through box-breathing techniques in real-time, effectively closing the loop between detection and prevention.

suggestions.title: Cross-Modality Transfer Learning for Sensor Fusion
suggestions.desc: Experiment with transfer learning by training on public datasets like WESAD (Wearable Stress and Affect Detection) and fine-tuning the model for consumer-grade sensors. Compare the accuracy of PPG (heart rate) versus EDA (skin conductance) to determine which modality is most resilient to motion artifacts during daily activity.

suggestions.title: Real-time Signal De-noising Pipeline using Wavelet Transforms
suggestions.desc: Implement a robust preprocessing library in C++ or Python that uses Discrete Wavelet Transforms (DWT) to filter out movement noise from multimodal physiological signals. This technical improvement ensures the ML model remains accurate even when the user is walking or typing, rather than just sitting still.

title: Binary Image Processing for Computation of Connected Components, Image Holes and Euler Number Using Graph Theory
authors: Sabbu, Sravya; Salepela, Prasuna Reddy; Talluri, Yamini Hanisha; Vaidyula, Sruthi
abstract: Binary images are images whose pixels have only two possible intensity values. This is also used in hum an recognition for machines to learn their language and behaviour. This image processing is helpful for the easy disjunction of an item from its surroundings. This report is based on analysing of binary image process that consists of a set of image analysis operations that are used to produce or process binary images, usually images of 0's and 1's. These are mostly used to shape outline of an image. The pixel used in this binary image can be converted into required density and disparity.

suggestions.title: GPU-Accelerated Euler Number Computation
suggestions.desc: Port the graph-based connected components algorithm to CUDA/C++ to enable real-time processing of high-resolution video streams. This allows for rapid topological analysis of 4K binary masks at 60+ FPS.

suggestions.title: Topological Feature Extraction for Handwriting
suggestions.desc: Use the Euler number and hole detection as invariant features for a character recognition system. By analyzing the 'topology' of a character (e.g., an '8' has two holes, an 'o' has one), you can build a classifier that is highly resistant to font variation or rotation.

suggestions.title: PCB Defect Detection System
suggestions.desc: Apply these binary image operations to scan printed circuit board (PCB) layouts. The algorithm can detect 'shorts' (merged connected components) or 'opens' (extra holes in traces) by comparing the Euler number of a scanned board against a master blueprint.

suggestions.title: Assistive Navigation via Shape Outlines
suggestions.desc: Create a mobile app for the visually impaired that uses these binary processing techniques to isolate and identify doors, hallways, and obstacles in real-time. Use the 'shape outline' features to generate haptic or audio feedback based on the object's complexity.

title: IoT Clusters Platform for Data Collection, Analysis, and Visualization
authors: Traore, Soin Abdoul Kassif Baba M
abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) popularity leads more scientists and students to research this field. IoTs have an efficient way of monitoring complex infrastructure systems and the environm ent around them . Thus, they intervene in several areas such as health care, engineering, or monitoring the effects of clim ate change. IoT's prim ary function is to collect data and share them with a distant server through the internet or a private network. Research on IoTs is firstly about creating efficient light devices composed of sensors that follow rigorous security protocols to guarantee the integrity of the data from the collection to its final destination. Secondly, the challenge is to store the

suggestions.title: LoRaWAN Mesh Network for Remote Clusters
suggestions.desc: Implement a LoRaWAN communication layer for the IoT clusters to allow for long-range, low-power data transmission. This would enable the platform to be deployed in remote environments (like forests for climate monitoring) where Wi-Fi or Cellular signals are unavailable.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Environmental Anomaly Detection
suggestions.desc: Integrate a stream-processing engine like Apache Flink. Build a set of 'alerting rules' that use the cluster's collected data to detect anomalies—such as sudden spikes in temperature or particulate matter—to provide early warning systems for wildfires or industrial leaks.

suggestions.title: Multi-Tenant Fleet Management SaaS
suggestions.desc: Expand the visualization platform into a multi-tenant SaaS. Build a robust authentication system (OAuth2/JWT) and a customizable dashboard builder that allows different organizations to manage their own private IoT clusters and data silos from a single portal.

suggestions.title: Federated Learning Across IoT Clusters
suggestions.desc: Implement a Federated Learning framework (like Flower) to train machine learning models across multiple IoT clusters without ever moving the raw data to a central server. This preserves data privacy for sensitive health or industrial monitoring applications.
