title: Using Machine Learning Techniques for Early Alzheimer’s Disease Detection
authors: Badireddi, Sudarshan; Kulal, Shalaka; Kuntla, Gayatri Sravanthi; Meeks, Travis
abstract: Alzheim er’s disease (AD) is a form of dem entia that causes a serious problem with the mem ory, cognition, and motor skills of patients. There are various approaches and tools for AD diagnosis that are invasive, expensive, and bring discom fort to the patients. Those approaches are usually utilized when the patients are experiencing serious sym ptoms in an advanced state. AD early detection is a key factor to intervention to occur effectively or slow down/halt AD progress with appropriate medication. In recent years, machine learning (ML) techniques have received lots of attention for early disease detection and AD is one of them . In this project, we aim to use Dim entiaBank dataset a

suggestions.title: Explainable AI (XAI) for Clinical Trust
suggestions.desc: Integrate SHAP or LIME frameworks into the existing machine learning models to provide 'feature importance' visualizations. This transforms the 'black box' predictions into interpretable reports that show doctors exactly which cognitive or motor indicators triggered the Alzheimer’s alert.

suggestions.title: Multi-Modal Fusion of Audio and Cognitive Data
suggestions.desc: Expand the dataset by incorporating audio recordings of speech patterns. Use Librosa for feature extraction (pitch, pause frequency) and combine it with the existing tabular data using a Late Fusion model to see if linguistic degradation improves early detection accuracy.

suggestions.title: Edge-Deployment for Remote Patient Monitoring
suggestions.desc: Optimize the trained models using TensorFlow Lite or ONNX and deploy them to an Android/iOS application. This would allow family members to perform non-invasive, periodic 'check-ins' that monitor subtle changes in motor skills and memory via smartphone sensors.

suggestions.title: Synthetic Medical Data Generation with GANs
suggestions.desc: Address the scarcity of medical data by using Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) to create high-fidelity synthetic patient records. This allows for training more robust models without compromising real patient privacy, facilitating easier sharing of research across institutions.

title: Prediction of Heart Disease with Machine Learning Techniques
authors: Araujo, Marcella; Pope, Lauren; Still, Stephen; Yannone, Cynthia
abstract: Heart disease consistently ranks as one of the leading causes of deaths globally. This project utilizes a newly merged heart disease dataset with 918 unique instances and 12 attributes, comprised of five globally recognized datasets from U CI Machine Learning Repository. Various machine learning techniques such as OneR, Decision Tree J48, K-Nearest Neighbors, Support Vector Machines, Logistic Regression, and ensem ble methods are applied on Waikato Environm ent for Knowledge Analysis (Weka) open-source ML tool and python sickit-learn with 10-fold cross validation to the dataset to evaluate the likelihood of a patient having heart disease. Overall, python sickit- learn and Weka results wer

suggestions.title: Deep Learning Transition with PyTorch
suggestions.desc: Move beyond the Weka-based J48 and OneR models by implementing a Deep Neural Network (DNN) using PyTorch. Use hyperparameter tuning via Optuna to optimize a multi-layer perceptron that can capture non-linear relationships better than traditional decision trees.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Wearable API Integration
suggestions.desc: Develop a middleware that connects the machine learning model to real-time data from the Fitbit or Apple HealthKit APIs. This would allow the model to provide 'dynamic' heart disease risk assessments based on live heart rate variability and activity levels rather than static records.

suggestions.title: Federated Learning for Cross-Hospital Privacy
suggestions.desc: Implement a Federated Learning architecture using Flower (flwr.dev). This enables multiple hospitals to contribute to the heart disease prediction model's intelligence without ever sharing their local, private patient datasets with a central server.

suggestions.title: Longitudinal Risk Prediction with LSTMs
suggestions.desc: Treat the heart disease data as a time-series problem. Use Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks to analyze how a patient's 12 attributes evolve over time to predict the 'time-to-event' for heart failure, rather than just a binary diagnosis.

title: Privacy Preserved Federated Learning Approach for Diabetic Retinopathy Detection
authors: Moham mad Nasajpour
abstract: According to recent statistics, the number of people with diabetes is predicted to rise more than 552 million by 2030. Diabetic Retinopathy (DR) is one of the complications of diabetes caused by high blood sugar. This disease could be critical to people with diabetes, worsening their eye condition to vision loss or blindness. Since DR does not show sym ptoms at the first stages, early diagnosis has a huge clinical, social, and even econom ical importance in dealing with DR. In fact, not only early detection could prevent vision loss, but it could also be cost-effective compared to a situation where the patient is dealing with blindness. As we discussed, early detection of DR plays a ke
supervisor: Dr. Mahm ut Karakaya Dr. Seyedam in Pouriyeh

suggestions.title: Differential Privacy with PySyft
suggestions.desc: Integrate PySyft and the Opacus library to add 'noise' to the local model updates during the Federated Learning process. This mathematically guarantees that individual patient data cannot be reconstructed from the aggregated model weights.

suggestions.title: Byzantine-Robust Federated Aggregation
suggestions.desc: Research and implement robust aggregation methods like 'Krum' or 'Trimmed Mean' to protect the global model from 'poisoned' data or malicious nodes attempting to sabotage the Diabetic Retinopathy detection accuracy.

suggestions.title: Cross-Hospital Diagnostic Collaborative
suggestions.desc: Simulate a multi-hospital environment using Docker containers where each 'hospital' has its own retinal image dataset. Build a central dashboard to monitor the global model's convergence and accuracy across all participating nodes.

suggestions.title: Decentralized Medical Data Marketplace
suggestions.desc: Create a blockchain-based ledger that tracks contributions to the Federated Learning model. Use smart contracts to automatically pay 'rewards' or provide access to the final model for clinics that contribute high-quality, verified retinal data.

title: Students Certification Management (SCM): Hyperledger Fabric-Based Digital Repository
authors: Hossain Faruk, Md Jobair
abstract: Abstract: The higher education sector has been heavily impacted financially by the economic downturn caused by the pandemic that has resulted a decline in student enrollments. Finding cost-effective novel technology for storing and sharing student's credentials among academic institutions and potential employers is a demand. Within the current conventional approach, ensuring authentication of a candidate’s credentials is costly and time-consuming which gives burdens to thousands of prospective students and potential employees. As a result, candidates fail to secure opportunities for either delay or non-submission of credentials all over the world. Blockchain technology has the potential for students' control over their credentials; degrees and transcripts for instance that will allow seamless streamlining of the sharing of educational records during changing and transferring schools, higher education, or even employment processes when need to show credentials. To implement the novel idea, we conduct a preliminary survey, study the existing applications, and investigate the feasibility of a Blockchain-based system to exploit the potential. Based on our findings, we propose a Students Certification Management System (SCM) by adopting Emerging Hyperledger Fabric that will offer a universal, tamper-evident, immutable, and secure educational certificate storing and sharing network. Our primary aim is to construct the proposed system into an educational certificate repository network using consortium blockchain for different entities including, (i) educational institutes to manage the network (ii) students and authorized third parties to access verifiable digital certificates and transcripts. Initially, we introduce an advanced architectural framework of the proposed system that has the potential in improving data flow between academic institutions, students, and potential employers. For ensuring transparency, each attempt in storing, sharing, and accessing credentials by the authenticated users within the proposed network shall be stored in the ledger which is secure and non-corruptible. Our future direction is to implement the architectural framework into an educational certification repository network within a private blockchain network. Department: Software Engineering and Game Design and Development Supervisor: Dr. Hossain Shahriar Dr. Maria Valero Topics: Software Engineering
supervisor: Dr. Hossain Shahriar Dr. Maria Valero

suggestions.title: Zero-Knowledge Proofs for Transcript Privacy
suggestions.desc: Implement Zero-Knowledge Proofs (ZKP) on top of Hyperledger Fabric. This allows a student to prove to an employer that they have 'a GPA over 3.5' or 'a BS in CS' without actually revealing their entire transcript or specific grades.

suggestions.title: Micro-Credentialing for Professional Certifications
suggestions.desc: Expand the SCM schema to support non-academic credentials like AWS, Cisco, or Coursera certificates. Build a standardized API that third-party certification providers can use to push digital badges directly to the Hyperledger ledger.

suggestions.title: Self-Sovereign Identity (SSI) Wallet Integration
suggestions.desc: Connect the Hyperledger backend to a decentralized identity wallet (like Trinsic or MetaMask). This shifts the power to the student, allowing them to carry their verified credentials in a mobile app and share them instantly via QR codes during interviews.

suggestions.title: Consensus Mechanism Performance Benchmarking
suggestions.desc: Conduct a comparative study of Hyperledger Fabric’s Raft consensus against other protocols like IBFT 2.0. Analyze throughput (TPS) and latency as the number of academic institutions (nodes) scales to determine the most cost-effective architecture for a national rollout.

title: Statistical and Machine Learning Approaches to Investigate Adverse Effects of COVID-19 Vaccinations
authors: Dyer, Aleema; Khorsandi, Azadeh; Mauro, Carter; Carmichael, Francelene; McCrary, Lillian; Martinelli, Martin
abstract: Abstract— The novel coronavirus disease (COVID-19) has been ongoing since Decem ber 2019 and continues as a highly contagious virus. COVID-19 is mutating in more viral, lethal variants and while COVID-19 vaccines have been available to the generalU .S. public for a majority of 2021 there exists a struggle to encourage vaccinations. In this study, authors utilize multiple machine learning methods in order to find which model has the highest accuracy in predicting if a reported event in VAERSwill recover or not. This is done through supervised machine learning software, W EKA, and several training models that can process given dataset in a single rational table form . EachW EKA’s ML te

suggestions.title: YOLO-to-Edge TensorRT Quantization
suggestions.desc: Optimize the YOLO weights using NVIDIA TensorRT or OpenVINO to perform INT8 quantization, enabling the model to maintain 30+ FPS on low-power hardware like a Raspberry Pi 5 or NVIDIA Jetson Nano.

suggestions.title: Domain Adaptation with Synthetic Environments
suggestions.desc: Use NVIDIA Omniverse to generate synthetic training data with extreme weather conditions (fog, heavy rain) to fine-tune the model, improving its detection reliability in environments where real-world data is hard to capture.

suggestions.title: Intelligent Traffic Flow & Density Prediction
suggestions.desc: Extend the system using the DeepSORT tracking algorithm to assign unique IDs to detected objects, allowing you to calculate vehicle density and predict traffic congestion patterns at intersections in real-time.

suggestions.title: Low-Code Auto-Labeling Transfer Learning Tool
suggestions.desc: Build a web platform where users can upload a small set of custom images and use the existing YOLO model to 'pre-label' them, creating a semi-automated pipeline for training models on new, niche objects.

title: VISUAL EVALUATION OF PREDICTION OF GLAUCOMA USING MACHINE LEARNING
authors: Dendi, Praneeth Kumar Reddy; Kollipara, Anirudh; Penmetsa, Maneesha Kumari
abstract: Glaucom a is a pathological condition of optic nerve dam age and is the second leading cause of vision loss. It is known as the silent thief of sight. The project aim ed to develop machine learning models that have strong prediction power and interpretability for the diagnosis of glaucom a. We used retinal images dataset and performed support vector machine algorithm and C5.0, random forest (RF), support vector machine (SVM), k-nearest neighbor (KNN) algorithms and We repeatedly composed a learning model using the training dataset and evaluated it by using the validation dataset and gained 94.4% accuracy.

suggestions.title: Retinal Image Segmentation using U-Net
suggestions.desc: Before running classification, use a U-Net architecture to segment the optic disc and optic cup. Calculate the 'Cup-to-Disc Ratio' (CDR) as a feature, as this is the primary clinical indicator used by doctors to diagnose glaucoma.

suggestions.title: Deep Feature Hybrid Classifier
suggestions.desc: Instead of raw pixel data, use a pre-trained ResNet50 to extract deep features and then pass those high-level features into the C5.0 and SVM algorithms. This hybrid approach often yields higher accuracy than training from scratch on small medical datasets.

suggestions.title: Tele-Health Screening for Portable Fundus Cameras
suggestions.desc: Optimize the model to run on a web-based DICOM viewer. This allows technicians to upload images from low-cost, smartphone-based eye cameras and receive an instant probability score for glaucoma, facilitating mass community screenings.

suggestions.title: Explainable Heatmap Generation for Clinicians
suggestions.desc: Build a visualization tool that overlays heatmaps (using LIME or Integrated Gradients) on the retinal images. This highlights the specific optic nerve damage areas, allowing ophthalmologists to use the ML model as a second-opinion tool rather than just a black box.

title: Emotion Recognition Using Wireless Signals
authors: Jui Mhatre Academ ic courses graduate (e.g. capstones, gam es, innovative special topics projects, other course projects) 9/8/23, 1:41 PM Fall 2021 C-Day Program - College of Computing and Software Engineering
abstract: Em otion Recognition plays an important role in understanding hum an behavior. It finds its utility in various dom ains such as healthcare, autom obile industries, understanding social interactions, fraud detection, and many more. Analyzing a person’s emotions in a controlled environm ent with various devices has been challenging since it adds to hum an anxiety, which manipulates the readings. This presents a need to devise ways to recognize and study emotions in a wireless manner. There have been previous works done to predict emotions by obtaining photos of subjects whose facial features were analyzed to identify emotions. Though this is an easier way of obtaining results, the data

suggestions.title: CSI-Based Multi-Person Separation
suggestions.desc: Extend the current wireless emotion recognition system to handle multiple subjects simultaneously by implementing Channel State Information (CSI) fingerprinting to isolate individual signals from a single Wi-Fi stream.

suggestions.title: Contactless Mental Health Monitoring Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Develop a real-time monitoring tool for assisted living facilities using ESP32 microcontrollers to track long-term emotional trends without the use of invasive cameras or wearable sensors.

suggestions.title: Cross-Environment Robustness via Domain Adaptation
suggestions.desc: Research and implement Domain Adversarial Neural Networks (DANN) to ensure the wireless emotion model remains accurate when moved from a controlled lab to a high-multipath home environment.

suggestions.title: In-Cabin Driver Distraction and Stress Detection
suggestions.desc: Apply the wireless signal analysis to the automotive domain by using 60GHz mmWave radar data to detect driver fatigue and emotional distress, triggering safety alerts when high anxiety is detected.

title: NLP Sentiment Analysis on Amazon Reviews
authors: Shivakumar, Sushma Aladhalli; Pailla, Swetha; Hasti, Sireesha
abstract: A lot of tim es, companies want to understand the public opinion on their products and figure out what’s responsible for the growth and backfall. For better understanding, they performsentim ent analysis on their product reviews. Sentim ent analysis has been on the rise because of the availability of new analysis techniques in deep learning, and there is an incom prehensibly large amount of data being generated everywhere. Every product review, every tweet, every reddit post, etc., contains subjective information which we would like to be able to process and understand. It helps the organization to recognize the prim ary issues with their products and to extract the instructive informati

suggestions.title: Aspect-Based Sentiment Analysis (ABSA)
suggestions.desc: Instead of a single sentiment score for a review, use a BERT-based model to extract specific aspects like 'Battery Life,' 'Price,' or 'Durability.' This allows companies to see exactly *what* users like or dislike about a product, rather than just an overall rating.

suggestions.title: Automated Competitor Benchmarking Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Build a real-time web scraper and dashboard using Streamlit that compares sentiment trends between Amazon products and their competitors (e.g., Samsung vs. Apple). Use the existing NLP logic to visualize 'market share of positive sentiment' over a 12-month period.

suggestions.title: Fake Review and Bot Detection Engine
suggestions.desc: Develop a secondary classifier to identify 'Review Bombing' or AI-generated bot reviews. Train the model on linguistic markers of deception and metadata patterns (like review frequency) to filter out noise and provide a 'Trust Score' for the sentiment analysis.

suggestions.title: Cross-Domain Transfer Learning
suggestions.desc: Test the robustness of the Amazon-trained model by applying it to Reddit or Twitter data using Domain Adaptation techniques. Fine-tune the model to handle the slang, emojis, and informal syntax found on social media platforms to see if sentiment accuracy holds.

title: Service Delivery Enhancements using Live Chats and Virtual Agents
authors: Wadkins, Patrick R; Twene, Peter; Williamson, Rachel; McDowell, Tamar; DeGraft, Naomi
abstract: The Kennesaw State University, University Information Technology Services departm ent needs to evaluate the use of the ServiceNow Virtual Agent and Live Chat Support for use at the KSU U ITS Service Desk to enhance service delivery. The U ITS Service Desk is currently staffed by six full-tim e employees and ten part-tim e student assistants (who work less than 20 hours per week). Areas of concern include the high volum e of service and incident tickets, the small number of staff, and the available service hours (eighty-three hours per week). The objective of this project is to: 1. Evaluate both ServiceNow’s Live Chat and AI-based Virtual Agents as potential options for enhancing and opt

suggestions.title: Vision Transformer (ViT) Texture Analysis
suggestions.desc: Implement a Vision Transformer (ViT) model and compare its performance against the existing ResNet152, specifically analyzing if the transformer's attention mechanism better captures the subtle skin textures relevant to melanoma.

suggestions.title: Active Learning Human-in-the-Loop Labeler
suggestions.desc: Build a tool that identifies 'low-confidence' predictions from the DenseNet121 model and presents them to a human expert for labeling, using the new data to iteratively retrain and improve the model with minimal manual effort.

suggestions.title: Skin Tone Bias Mitigation Audit
suggestions.desc: Evaluate the model's accuracy across different Fitzpatrick skin types and apply re-weighting techniques or specialized data augmentation to ensure the diagnostic tool performs equitably across diverse patient demographics.

suggestions.title: Mobile AR Lesion Screener
suggestions.desc: Port the best-performing model to CoreML and build an iOS app that uses Augmented Reality to highlight potential areas of concern on a patient's skin in a real-time camera view, intended for use as a preliminary screening tool.

title: COVID-19 PREDICTION USING SYMTPOMS
authors: Gurjala, Yagna; Batchu, Chalamayya; Pethe, Shruthi
abstract: The recent COVID-19 outbreak, which was triggered by the new coronavirus SARS-Cov2, is a pressing global concern. In the absence of effective therapies, the prim ary containm ent method is to restrict the spread of infection by isolating sick people; nevertheless, isolating unaffected people is extrem ely undesirable. It would be useful to determine which sym ptoms presented by suspected infection patients are the best predictors of a positive diagnosis to assist in making smart decisions on treatm ent and isolation needs. It is possible to create prediction models that include numerous characteristics to assess the likelihood of infection. We intend to use a machine-learning approac

suggestions.title: Feature Importance Ranking with XGBoost
suggestions.desc: Apply Gradient Boosting machines to rank the predictive power of specific symptoms. The goal is to create a 'weighted triage' algorithm that can tell healthcare providers which three symptoms are the strongest indicators of a positive SARS-Cov2 case versus common seasonal allergies.

suggestions.title: Multi-Pathogen Differential Diagnosis
suggestions.desc: Expand the model’s classification capabilities to include Influenza, RSV, and Strep Throat. Use a multi-class classification approach so that a user entering symptoms can receive a probability distribution across several likely respiratory illnesses.

suggestions.title: Privacy-Preserving Triage Chatbot

abstract: This project provides our client, Cybriant, with an integration tool between ConnectW ise Manage and Microsoft Azure Sentinel. Cybriant, a leading Managed Security Services Provider, utilizes Microsoft Azure Sentinel to detect and analyze security incidents. In order to manage these incidents, Cybriant needed to manually transfer data collected into ConnectW ise Manage, the platform Cybriant utilizes to manage incident tickets internally. Manually entering this data for each detected incident was inefficient and error-prone. Using Logic Apps, our team created an integration solution to aid Cybriant with this tim e consum ing process. Once incidents are detected or handled in Sentinel, our

suggestions.title: Automated Incident Remediation (SOAR)
suggestions.desc: Extend the integration to move from 'alerting' to 'acting.' Use Azure Logic Apps to trigger automated scripts that can disable a compromised user account or block a malicious IP in the firewall the moment a high-severity ticket is created in ConnectWise.

suggestions.title: Multi-Tenant MSP Aggregation Portal

title: Vision-based Alzheimer's diagnosis
authors: Bhalodia, Yuvraj A
abstract: Alzheimer disease is common disease in people aged above 65 or older. In current medical technology, Alzheimer does not have any cure. In Alzheimer disease, Patient lose their memory and many other brain functions. In extreme condition with Alzheimer disease, patient’s brain become small in size compared to normal person. This disease can cause of death of patient. Doctors have not able to find cause of this disease yet but it can be treated so that patient with Alzheimer disease can stop growth of disease and live longer. To treat Alzheimer disease, it is important that it get detect in its early stage, which is called Mild Cognitive Impairment (MCI). MCI is very early stage of Alzheimer disease. Patient with MCI can be transfer into Alzheimer disease. If patient diagnosed in MCI stage, then there is a chance that it cannot be transfer into Alzheimer disease. Current diagnoses of Alzheimer disease are very old style. It includes asking question to patient and their family members and taking written and oral tests like memory quiz, problem solving, etc. My approach to Alzheimer diagnosis is to use MRI image of brain with image processing techniques to detect the disease.

suggestions.title: 3D CNN Volumetric Atrophy Analysis
suggestions.desc: Upgrade the model from 2D slice analysis to 3D Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) to calculate the total volume of the hippocampus and ventricles from MRI DICOM files for more precise staging.

authors: Staciarine, Matteo L
abstract: Reducing the learning rate of a CNN can positively affect the validation accuracy of a machine learning model. Dropping out nodes from different layers can further delay overfitting from happening. Validation loss decreases over more epochs, but it must be cut when it reaches its minimum value.

suggestions.title: Dynamic Learning Rate Schedulers
suggestions.desc: Experiment with One-Cycle Learning Rate policies and Stochastic Gradient Descent with Warm Restarts (SGDR) to find more efficient paths to convergence compared to simple step-down approaches.

suggestions.title: Edge Deployment via Model Quantization
suggestions.desc: Apply post-training quantization and pruning to the CIFAR-optimized model to deploy it on low-power hardware like an Arduino Nicla Vision or Raspberry Pi Pico without significant accuracy loss.

suggestions.title: Neural Architecture Search (NAS) Optimization
suggestions.desc: Implement a Genetic Algorithm or Reinforcement Learning agent to automatically discover the optimal number of filters and dropout rates for the CIFAR-100 dataset rather than manual tuning.

authors: Cole, Jarred; Roberts, Luc; Joshi, Jay
abstract: Satellite based networks are become a normal occurrence of implementation into our networks. This is mainly due to population increase and demand of content. Doing this requires a vast reduction of latency and delay of data. To solve this problem, we will create a satellite architecture diagram and algorithm which implements edge computing and utilize MatLab and a reinforced learning agent (AI). We will also implement a beam protocol and at the end show the results of our experimentation which will show the necessity of Edge Computing with IoT and Satellites with the addition of AI to reduce latency issues.

title: Team 10B BChain
authors: Lashgari, Jonathan D; Diaz, Carlos A; Erhunse, Jeffery; Goff, Caleb T; Nguyen, Giang T
abstract: It is now common knowledge that simple lectures are not the most effective way for the average person to learn and retain knowledge. The Core of Engineers at the Warner Robins Air Logistic Center have tasked us to transform their PowerPoint presentations into interactive training games to improve comprehension, interaction and retention while saving time and logistical resources compared to giving a traditional lecture. We been tasked with creating game modules covering STINFO or what is or is not considered classified information, Records Management, and the No FEAR Act detailing whistleblower rights and protocols. Our team has developed a log in and sign up portal, robust menus and UI/UX, a system to administer and save results of a quiz, and a 3D environment in which the user can explore and complete tasks which will assist in the learning of these three applications. Our project uses the Unity Game engine, Unity WebGL for hosting in browser, and the Playfab database, as well as assets from the Unity Asset store to improve and streamline our development.

suggestions.title: SCORM/xAPI Performance Export
suggestions.desc: Modify the game suite to export detailed user performance data in SCORM or xAPI format, allowing the Warner Robins Air Logistic Center to track trainee progress within their existing LMS.

suggestions.title: No-Code Scenario Branching Editor
suggestions.desc: Build a drag-and-drop editor that allows instructors to convert existing PowerPoint decks into complex branching narrative games without writing code, using a node-based logic system.

suggestions.title: Multiplayer Collaborative STINFO Audit

title: Software Engineer – Clarity LLC
authors: Mullins, Amy
abstract: Clarity makes an app called CaptionMate that does closed captions for phone calls.. During the internship, a website was made to visualize metrics that are collected on users such as calls made, minutes used, time active, region, age, theme, font, and platform used. Bar charts are used to show minutes and calls used on days of the week. 100% bar charts are used to show how much a day contributes to the usage of the app; a user contributes to minutes, calls and platform usage; and show calls incoming vs. outgoing. Line graphs were created to show growth in app usage and number of new users over time. All the data iis displayed in tables too. Who used the CaptionMate app, how much and when? Where are these users located? what age group do they fall into? what themes and fonts do they use? What platforms are they on? Visual studio with C# .NET Core, SQL Server, and ChartJS were used in the making of the website. C# was used for the backend of the website; it executed queries and stored results in lists. SQL Server was used to write queries. These results are displayed using HTML tables. ChartJS was then used to make visualizations after retreiving necessary data form the HTML tables. Many results were found. Users use more minutes and make more calls during the week then on weekends. Users use IOS more than any other platform. They like the gray theme most out of all themes and are located all around the US. The number of users has been growing slowly since the app was released. Some users stop using the app after some time of using it while other users do not try to make calls or make only unsuccessful calls.

suggestions.title: Predictive Churn Analysis for CaptionMate Users
suggestions.desc: Utilize the existing demographic and usage metrics (age, region, platform, minutes used) to build a Random Forest or XGBoost model that predicts user churn. Integrate this into the dashboard to highlight 'at-risk' accounts, allowing Clarity LLC to offer targeted support or incentives.

This would allow engineers to correlate dips in 'minutes used' with platform-specific technical errors or latency spikes in the captioning API across different regions.

Use this data to generate 'Automatic Accessibility Profiles' that suggest optimal font sizes and contrast ratios for new users during onboarding.

title: Compare Two Off Angle Normalization
authors: Ehrlich, Emily
abstract: This work investigates different iris normalization techniques to compare their performance including elliptical normalization and circular normalization after frontal projection of off-angle iris recognition. Elliptical normalization samples the iris texture using elliptical segmentation parameters. For circular unwrapping, we first estimate the gaze deviation using ellipse parameters and the image will be projected back to frontal view using perspective transformation. Then, we segment the transformed image and normalize using circular parameters. We further investigate if: (i) elliptical normalization or circular unwrapping recognition performance is higher, and (ii) the two segmentations methods in circular unwrapping increase the recognition efficiency. Based on the preliminary results, the elliptical normalization method shows slightly better recognition performance in off-angle iris images with 2.2% decrement in the equal error rate. The motivation of this research is to provide guidance in the construction of a recognition framework for off-angle iris images with the analysis and comparison of different normalization methods. MATLAB was used to calculate the hamming distance and accuracy of each normalization method and to construct the plotted graphs for display. Based on the preliminary results, the elliptical normalization method shows slightly better recognition performance in off-angle iris images with 2.2% decrement in the equal error rate. In addition, perspective projection shifted the distributions of intra and inter class Hamming distances to left where its average intra-class and inter-class Hamming distance are 0.3070 and 0.4891, respectively compared with 0.3082 and 0.4900 for elliptical normalization.

suggestions.title: CNN-Based Iris Segmentation Refinement
suggestions.desc: Replace traditional elliptical segmentation parameters with a U-Net or Mask R-CNN architecture to automate the detection of the iris and pupil boundaries in off-angle images. This improves the robustness of the normalization process when dealing with occlusions from eyelashes or eyelids.

suggestions.title: Mobile-to-Frontal Iris Transformation App
suggestions.desc: Develop an Android/iOS application that uses the smartphone's front camera to capture iris data. Implement the perspective transformation and circular unwrapping algorithms on-device using OpenCV to allow for secure, off-angle biometric unlocking without requiring the user to hold the phone perfectly level.

suggestions.title: Cross-Normalization Performance Benchmark
suggestions.desc: Conduct a large-scale comparative study using the CASIA-Iris-Thousand or UBIRIS.v2 datasets. Quantify the 'Equal Error Rate' (EER) of elliptical vs. circular normalization across varying degrees of gaze deviation (10° to 50°) to determine the mathematical 'breaking point' for each method.

suggestions.title: Synthetic Off-Angle Training Data Generator
suggestions.desc: Build a GAN-based pipeline that takes frontal iris images and generates realistic off-angle counterparts with known gaze parameters. This dataset can be used to train more robust iris recognition models that don't rely solely on geometric normalization techniques.

title: Machine Learning Techniques for Malware Network Traffic Detection
authors: Cameron, Jermaine
abstract: Radar jamming involves sending intentionally disruptive radio waves toward the target radar, which might over-saturate its receiver so it can’t receive anything or deceive it into interpreting false information. Machine learning (ML) techniques increased the capability to automatically learn the experience without being explicitly programmed. Machine learning models usually require a large, labeled sample to perform. Building a robust jamming detection model will be challenging due to the wide variability of jamming signals and less available labeled samples. In this project, we developed an eXtreme Gradient Boosting(XGBoost) algorithms for radar jamming signal classification and achieved superior performance compared with Random forest and Support Vector Machine(SVM) considering the unique environment and challenges in RADAR/SDR signals.

suggestions.title: SDR Real-Time Jamming Monitor
suggestions.desc: Connect the XGBoost model to a Software Defined Radio (SDR) like an RTL-SDR or HackRF. Use GNU Radio to stream live IQ data into the model, creating a real-time 'Spectrum Police' tool that detects and classifies jamming attempts in the 2.4GHz band.

suggestions.title: Drone Anti-Jamming Alert System
suggestions.desc: Port the XGBoost model to a Raspberry Pi or Jetson Nano mounted on a drone. The system can provide immediate cockpit alerts if it detects 'GPS Spoofing' or 'Barrage Jamming,' allowing the pilot to trigger an autonomous 'Return to Home' protocol.

suggestions.title: Adversarial Jamming Robustness Study
suggestions.desc: Research how 'Smart Jammers' can bypass XGBoost detection by mimicking legitimate traffic patterns. Develop an adversarial training dataset to make the model more resilient against pulse jamming that hides within standard Wi-Fi frames.

suggestions.title: Self-Healing IoT Mesh Gateway
suggestions.desc: Integrate the detector into an IoT gateway. When the XGBoost model flags a jamming attack, the gateway automatically instructs all connected devices to perform a 'Channel Hop' to a pre-arranged secondary frequency to maintain communication.

title: Wrist Intent Recognition for Stroke Rehabilitation
authors: Bharti, Suman
abstract: Hand mentor robotic device is beneficial for stroke patients . This is rehabilitation technique used in stroke therapy. It strengthens and improves the range of motion which ultimately improves the quality of life for severely impaired stroke patients. It is easy to use without assistance and most importantly stroke survivors able to use independently. Usage of hand mentor device is quite expensive for stroke patients on hourly basis . Coming up with most efficient deep learning algorithm for sensor data is motivation to cut down the cost and easy availability usage for stroke patients. EMG signal is recorded using relevant sensors which provides useful information to infer muscle movement. In this study, we utilized publicly available EMG signal datasets recorded from upper limb of human subjects to develop a neural network based model for the prediction of wrist motion intention. Research Question or Motivation The Motivation of this study is to train a simple neural network model to accurately predict three basic wrist motions (extension, flexion and no motion) using optimum number of EMG sensors. This model can be further deployed to augment the capabilities of commercially available robotic-assistive rehabilitation devices. Materials and Methods Sensor-based continuous hand gesture recognition activity requires profound knowledge about gesture activities from multitudes of low-level sensor readings. There are two ways to provide the solutions either to go by handcrafted features from sensor data or use deep learning techniques. The advantage of using deep learning technique is to utilize the automatic high-level feature extraction with outstanding performance. However, sensor data requires signal pre-or post-processing such as feature selection, dimension reduction, denoising, etc. Based on the literature review of many research papers, we found that 1D Convolutional Neural Network have recently become the state-of-the-art technique for crucial signal processing applications. 1D CNN is very effective when we aim to extract features from fixed-length segments of the overall dataset and where the location of the feature within the segment is not of high relevance. In addition to this, real-time and low-cost hardware implementation is feasible using 1D CNN. After a successful literature review on 1D CNN knowing its advantages and benefits of using over signal. We decided to use 1D CNN on raw EMG signal data. Preliminary results: Since it is an application-based project, we planned to work in phases to achieve the long-term goal of benefitting stroke patients using deep learning techniques. In this initial phase of the study, we utilized publicly available EMG dataset for hand gestures from UCI Machine Learning Repository to test the performance of the 1D CNN algorithm on gesture classification. We used only 3 labels (hand at rest, wrist flexion, wrist extension) out of 8 labels in the dataset for our particular application requirement. This dataset contains 8 EMG channels collected from commercial MYO Thalmic bracelet device. We first performed an initial analysis to investigate the optimum number of sensor/channels based on the highest gesture classification accuracy using KNN, Decision Tree and Naïve Bayes algorithms. As a result of this analysis, we obtained the optimum channel combination (Ch1, Ch4, Ch5, Ch8) data which generates the best classification accuracy. We used these 4 sensor datasets to train a 1D CNN with 78/22 train/test split. Dataset contains total 36 subjects. Data with subject number less than or equal to 28 is considered as training set and data with subject number greater than 28 is considered as test set. We also performed an optimization study on finding the optimum time signal window and overlap sizes of 100 ms and 50 ms . We achieved test accuracy of 97% for the classification accuracy of 3 gestures (hand at rest, wrist flexion, wrist extension).

suggestions.title: Real-Time Browser Authenticator for Visual Media
suggestions.desc: Develop a Chrome extension that uses the trained detection model to analyze images on social media feeds in real-time. Use the WebGL API to run lightweight inference in the browser, providing users with a 'probability of AI generation' overlay on platforms like X and Instagram.

Use frequency domain analysis (FFT) to detect the unique 'fingerprints' or checkerboard artifacts left by different upscaling architectures.

suggestions.desc: Investigate how the detector performs against 'poisoned' or edited AI images by applying Gaussian noise, JPEG compression, and adversarial perturbations. Build a testing suite that determines the minimum amount of human retouching required to fool the current AI-detection thresholds.

This creates a visual heat map for users, showing exactly where the 'hallucinations' or unnatural textures (like botched hands or repetitive patterns) occur.

title: Efficient yet Robust Privacy Preservation \\for MPEG-DASH Based Video Streaming
authors: Cranfill, Luke A
abstract: MPEG-DASH is a video streaming standard that outlines protocols for sending audio and video content from a server to a client over HTTP. However, it creates an opportunity for an adversary to invade users' privacy. While a user is watching a video, information is leaked in the form of meta-data, the size and time that the server sent data to the user. After a fingerprint of this data is created, the adversary can use this to identify whether a target user is watching the corresponding video. Only one defense strategy has been proposed to deal with this problem: differential privacy that adds sufficient noise in order to muddle the attacks. However, that strategy still suffers from the trade-off between privacy and efficiency. This paper proposes a novel defense strategy against the attacks with rigorous privacy and performance goals creating a private, scalable solution. Our algorithm, No Data are Alone (NDA), is highly efficient. The experimental results show that our scheme is more than two times as efficient in terms of excess downloaded video (represented as waste) than the most efficient differential privacy-based scheme. Additionally, no classifier can achieve an accuracy above 7.07% against videos obfuscated with our scheme.

suggestions.title: Adversarial Traffic Morphing Proxy
suggestions.desc: Build a client-side proxy tool that intercept MPEG-DASH requests and injects 'chaff' traffic—randomly sized dummy packets at irregular intervals. This would aim to flatten the data-size fingerprint, making it statistically difficult for an adversary to match the traffic to a known video profile.

suggestions.title: Quantifying Metadata Leakage via Differential Privacy
suggestions.desc: Apply differential privacy mechanisms to the DASH manifest files and segment request timestamps. Research how much 'noise' is required to mask user identity while maintaining a 'Quality of Experience' (QoE) that prevents video buffering or resolution drops.

suggestions.title: Privacy-Preserving Edge Caching Node
suggestions.desc: Design a Raspberry Pi-based edge node that pre-fetches DASH segments based on local popularity. By serving content from the local network rather than a remote server, you break the timing correlation between the user's view and the server's data transmission, effectively neutralizing remote fingerprinting.

suggestions.title: DASH-Shield: A Browser Extension for Privacy Scores
suggestions.desc: Develop a browser extension that monitors outgoing DASH traffic and provides users with a 'Privacy Leakage Score' in real-time. It should visualize how distinct their current streaming fingerprint is compared to a database of known video fingerprints, warning them if their activity is easily identifiable.

title: Energy Cost and Efficiency on Edge Computing: Challenges and Vision
authors: Banka, Kousalya
abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) has been the key for many advancements in next-generation technologies for the past few years. With a conceptual grouping of ecosystem elements such as sensors, actuators, and smart objects connected to perform complex operations to perform environmental monitoring, intelligent transport system, smart building, smart cities, and endless other possibilities. Edge computing helps the IoT’s reach even further and be more robust by connecting multiple censored devices through the internet and forming powerful computational capabilities. Unfortunately, this computation level comes at a cost as the devices are constantly being used to communicate and perform specific actions. Energy efficiency has focused on finding the optimal way to utilize the latest technologies while retaining the battery power’s longevity. In this paper, we present an outline of the difficulties engaged with planning energy-efficient IoT edge devices and depict recent research that has proposed promising answers that address these challenges. First, we analyze the challenges that IoT devices bring in terms of energy consumption. Next, we discuss the different approaches such as computation offloading, modifying the IoT devices’ designs, and the number of algorithms that help reduce energy consumption and few latest technologies. Finally, we will look at the case study that outlines the energy-saving techniques in smart grids, smart cities, electric vehicles, smart home devices, and VR/AR in real time to apply the concepts proposed.

suggestions.title: Reinforcement Learning for Edge Task Offloading
suggestions.desc: Develop a Q-Learning or Deep Q-Network (DQN) agent that decides whether an IoT task should be processed locally, at an edge gateway, or in the cloud. The reward function should be specifically optimized for minimizing energy consumption while meeting strict latency deadlines.

suggestions.title: Grid-Aware IoT Scheduling
suggestions.desc: Integrate the 'Carbon Intensity API' to align energy-intensive IoT operations with periods of high renewable energy availability on the local grid. Research the trade-off between battery longevity and carbon-efficient scheduling for edge nodes.

suggestions.title: Energy-Efficient LoRaWAN Gateway Prototype
suggestions.desc: Build a solar-powered LoRaWAN edge gateway and implement a 'Wake-on-Radio' protocol to reduce idle power consumption. Create a dashboard to monitor the gateway's power harvest vs. the energy cost of processing sensor data from environmental monitoring nodes.

suggestions.title: Edge Computing Digital Twin for Smart Buildings
suggestions.desc: Develop a simulation in Gazebo or a custom Python framework that creates a Digital Twin of a smart building's IoT network. Use it to visualize how different edge placement strategies impact the overall energy footprint of the building's climate control and security systems.

title: An efficient intrusion detection framework based on federated learning for IoT networks.
authors: Shahid, Osama
abstract: There are abundant number of IoT devices that are connected on over multiple networks. These devices can be exposed to multiple different types of network threats. Though, these devices do have security and software that does act as a wall of protection we purpose a Federated Learning (FL) approach that would allow detection of threats of a network for IoT devices. Federated Learning can be best described as decentralized training. Adhering to the GDPR rules that prevent data from being distributed, FL addressed the challenge by bringing the ML model to the data rather than the traditional method where the data had to be extrapolated and taken to the ML model. This type of setting is ideal for IoT devices (Client) that are connected to the network and can download the FL model that would allow them to keep their devices more secure. FL is different from on-device training. Once the Client(s) download the model and train on their individual local data, the updated model is shared on a central server. The central server as all the individual models shared by each client on the federated network. The central server aggregates all these models as one new global model. For our project we believe this is an ideal setting for IoT devices that are susceptible to network threats. In our Federated Network we have four clients, and we create a FL framework that allows each client to train a FL model on their local data. The model(s) are then aggregated to create a new global model. It is worth noting that each client has its’ own distinct type of threat. So, when all the models are aggregated, they have the knowledge of each of these individual models and this new model is capable to testing and detecting threats that are posed across all the devices.

suggestions.title: Asynchronous Federated Aggregation for Heterogeneous IoT
suggestions.desc: Modify the FL framework to support asynchronous updates, allowing low-power IoT devices (like ESP32) to contribute to the global model at their own pace without slowing down more powerful edge nodes. Use the Flower (flwr.dev) framework to manage the orchestration of diverse hardware.

suggestions.title: Blockchain-Verified Model Updates
suggestions.desc: Integrate a lightweight blockchain (e.g., Hyperledger Fabric) to log and verify the updates sent by each IoT device. This prevents 'Model Poisoning' attacks where a compromised device sends malicious weights to degrade the intrusion detection performance of the entire network.

suggestions.title: Adversarial Robustness Testing for IoT-FL
suggestions.desc: Simulate network attacks (DDoS, Man-in-the-Middle) on a testbed of IoT devices and evaluate how quickly the Federated Learning model adapts to new threats compared to a centralized ML model. Focus on the 'Catastrophic Forgetting' problem in decentralized security training.

suggestions.title: Cross-Protocol Intrusion Detection Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Extend the framework to detect threats across different IoT protocols like Zigbee, MQTT, and CoAP. Create a React-based dashboard that visualizes the global FL model's confidence levels for different attack types across the entire decentralized network.

similar: legacy:GR-044|Spring 2021|an efficient intrusion detection framework based on federated learning for iot networks.

title: Predicting Users' Engagement During Interviews with Biofeedback, Voice, and Supervised Machine Learning
authors: Huichapa, Thaide
abstract: Studies show that the quality of the information collected during an elicitation interview, and consequently the quality of the software product that needs to be developed, highly depends on the interviewee's engagement. Because of social expectations, interviewees tend to hide if they are bored or not engaged. To overcome this problem and support the analyst during the interviews, this research uses biometric data and voice features, together with supervised machine learning algorithms, to predict the interviewee's engagement. We built our solution on an experiment consisted of interviewing 31 participants. We collected the data using an Empatica wristband and a default recorder from a laptop. After preprocessing the data and segmented them into single question/answer segments, we used part of them to train different supervised machine learning algorithms and the remaining to test the algorithms in order to evaluate their effectiveness and select the one that performs better. Our results show that both biofeedback and voice, considered individually, and machine learning can be successfully used to predict participants' engagement. The results of our work, in addition to being used to help the analyst conduct better interviews by steering the interview based on the participant's engagement, can also be used to prioritized requirements.

suggestions.title: Multimodal Fusion Transformer for Engagement
suggestions.desc: Implement a Transformer-based architecture that fuses time-series biometric data (heart rate, skin conductance) with audio features (pitch, jitter, shimmer) using 'Cross-Attention' layers. This can capture subtle correlations between physiological spikes and vocal hesitation more effectively than standard supervised ML.

suggestions.title: Real-time Interviewer Support HUD
suggestions.desc: Develop a desktop application for interviewers that displays a 'Live Engagement Meter.' Use a web-socket to stream voice and bio-data during the interview, providing visual cues (e.g., 'Interviewee is losing focus—rephrase the question') based on model predictions.

suggestions.title: Cross-Cultural Engagement Bias Analysis
suggestions.desc: Investigate how the engagement model performs across different cultural backgrounds and languages. Use a dataset like the 'Multimodal Corpus of User Engagement' to retrain the model and determine if biometric responses to 'boredom' are universal or require culture-specific calibration.

suggestions.title: Privacy-Preserving On-Device Inference
suggestions.desc: Optimize the ML model using TensorFlow Lite to run locally on an interviewer's device. This ensures that sensitive biometric and voice data never leave the room, addressing the significant ethical and privacy concerns surrounding interviewee surveillance.

similar: legacy:GR-050|Spring 2021|predicting users' engagement during interviews with biofeedback, voice, and supervised machine learning

title: How could IoT assist healthcare system during COVID-19 or future pandemics?
authors: Nasajpour Esfahani, Mohammad
abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT), a technology built upon sensors and devices, has shown great applicability among various domains, especially healthcare. This pandemic has critically impacted all parts of society including people, health centers, businesses, authorities, etc. Researchers are attempting to adopt different technologies to mitigate this virus faster and save more lives. Regarding the great benefits that Internet of Things (IoT) has brought into different areas within the healthcare domain, this technology has been performing several main tasks including diagnosing, monitoring, tracing, disinfecting, and vaccinating to combat this virus. Our research is conducted of the possible IoT solutions to mitigate the COVID-19 or even future pandemics. We have demonstrated the applicability of IoT technologies in three main COVID-19 phases including “early diagnosis, quarantine time, and after recovery.” Along with such applications, we also review the proposed IoT applications for the main tasks of IoT, which could be exponentially helpful for fighting against this virus.

suggestions.title: Edge-Based Respiratory Sound Classifier
suggestions.desc: Develop a deep learning model (CNN or LSTM) that can be deployed on edge devices to identify 'dry cough' patterns associated with COVID-19 vs. normal sounds. Use the 'COUGHVID' public dataset to train the model for real-time monitoring in smart healthcare rooms.

suggestions.title: IoT-Enabled Smart Hospital Triage
suggestions.desc: Build a prototype system that integrates Pulse Oximeters and Smart Thermometers into a centralized dashboard. Use a priority-queuing algorithm to automatically flag patients with falling SpO2 levels, helping medical staff allocate limited resources more effectively during a surge.

suggestions.title: Privacy-Preserving BLE Proximity Tracer
suggestions.desc: Implement an IoT-based contact tracing system using Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) RSSI values. Focus on developing a decentralized 'Bloom Filter' approach to notify users of potential exposure within a building without revealing their location history to a central server.

suggestions.title: Digital Twin for Pandemic Resource Management
suggestions.desc: Create a Digital Twin of a hospital wing using IoT sensor data (occupancy, oxygen tank levels, staff movement). Use this simulation to model different pandemic scenarios and test how IoT-automated cleaning cycles or ventilation adjustments could impact viral spread.

title: Representation Learning for Motion Sequence
authors: Alagapan, Saisangararamaleengam; Malos, Alexandru; Kishor, Roshni; Mosali, Venkateswara Reddy
abstract: This research project proposes a new deep learning architecture that is used to align human poses to be used in an exercise assistant system. In short, the assistant system takes a video feed of a user doing exercise, then provides visual feedback by comparing the user’s current pose to a professional trainer’s pose that is stored in the system. We design a new deep architecture to accomplish this task and show better accuracy and efficiency.

suggestions.title: Edge-Optimized Real-Time Pose Correction
suggestions.desc: Port the deep learning architecture to TensorFlow Lite or ONNX to run locally on mobile devices. Focus on reducing latency for the video feed processing so users get immediate corrective feedback without cloud round-trips.

suggestions.title: Post-Operative Physical Therapy Remote Monitor
suggestions.desc: Adapt the exercise assistant for specific orthopedic rehabilitation protocols, such as ACL recovery or rotator cuff exercises. Integrate a clinician dashboard that tracks patient 'alignment scores' over weeks to monitor recovery progress.

suggestions.title: Temporal Transformer for Smooth Motion Analysis
suggestions.desc: Replace the standard architecture with a Temporal Shift Module or a Transformer-based model to account for the velocity of movement. This allows the system to not just check the pose 'snapshot,' but the rhythm and tempo of the exercise relative to the trainer.

suggestions.title: Gamified Virtual Dojo Subscription Service
suggestions.desc: Build a wrapper application that awards points and badges based on the 'alignment accuracy' calculated by the model. Create a competitive leaderboard feature where students can compete in 'Form Challenges' against friends or global benchmarks.

title: Motion Prediction for Vehicles - CS 4732
authors: Patel, Neel; Korpi, Matthew; Rao, Shivank; Aledhari, Advisor: Mohammed
abstract: The idea of a self-driving car is one that makes us think more of a science fiction movie than a plausible addition to real life. While we have not come close to perfecting street legal autonomous vehicles by any means, over the past few years significant progress has been made. Autonomous vehicles need to be able to constantly perceive what is happening in their environment and be able to react accordingly. This study aims to improve upon the accuracy of previous motion prediction models, thus allowing the autonomous vehicle to better understand its surroundings. Building upon Lyft's baseline model, we will implement a model with multi-modal prediction that will generate possible paths that are likely to occur. After training the new model and reviewing the results, the multi-modal approach handles complex intersections well. The baseline model had some instances where a predicted path would ignore the geometry of the road or would cut across an intersection. With the new model, ResNet50 was also implemented. Being a form of deep residual learning, ResNet50 helps give context awareness to the model. The implementation of ResNet50 helped eliminate errors where the predicted path did not follow the geometry of the road, as well as predictions in intersections.

suggestions.title: Multimodal Trajectory Prediction using CVAE
suggestions.desc: Implement a Conditional Variational Autoencoder (CVAE) to predict multiple possible paths for a vehicle instead of just one. This accounts for uncertainty, such as whether a car will turn left or continue straight at an intersection.

suggestions.title: Weather-Aware Motion Forecasting
suggestions.desc: Augment the current dataset with meteorological data using the nuScenes or Waymo Open Dataset. Train the model to adjust safety margins and predicted deceleration patterns based on road friction levels (rain, snow, ice).

suggestions.title: Predictive ADAS for Non-Autonomous Vehicles
suggestions.desc: Refactor the model into a driver-assistance system that monitors blind spots and warns human drivers of 'high-probability' collisions 2 seconds before they occur. The output would be a heads-up display (HUD) alert rather than steering control.

suggestions.title: V2X Communication Simulation Platform
suggestions.desc: Use the CARLA simulator to create a 'Vehicle-to-Everything' environment where the motion prediction model receives data from surrounding smart infrastructure (traffic lights/sensors) to improve accuracy in occluded areas.

title: Autonomous Sentry Gun - CS 4850
authors: Bozhkov, James; Nguyen, Merry; Mejia, Gustavo; Hoganson, Advisor: Kenneth E.
abstract: This project is focused on a defensive autonomous sentry gun that will use multiple sensors to gather input data from the target. The output will be translated into motor movement and target tracking as well as shooting the target. Specifically, the input from the camera will center on the target calculating the yaw of the gun, while the input from the ultrasonic range finder will calculate the output pitch to find the correct trajectory the bullet needs to take. The webcam with built-in video & audio is connected to the raspberry pi, which will be taking auditory inputs to confirm an activation code from the target. If the activation code is correct, the sentry gun will show a green light signaling to the user that it is disarmed. If the activation code is incorrect, the sentry gun will show a red light and fire at the target. This project is for anyone who wants autonomous protection, without having to manually control the gun.

suggestions.title: Kalman Filter Integration for Target Lead Estimation
suggestions.desc: Incorporate an Extended Kalman Filter to predict where a moving target will be by the time the projectile arrives. This replaces simple centering with active trajectory interception, significantly improving hit rates on non-stationary targets.

suggestions.title: Thermal Signature Target Discrimination
suggestions.desc: Integrate a FLIR Lepton thermal camera alongside the webcam to allow the sentry to operate in low-light conditions. Use the thermal data to distinguish between living targets and inanimate moving objects (like trees blowing in the wind).

suggestions.title: Agricultural Pest Deterrent System
suggestions.desc: Pivot the hardware to use non-lethal deterrents (like high-pressure water or loud frequency bursts) to protect crops. Train the vision model specifically on invasive species like deer or wild hogs to automate farm security.

suggestions.title: Mesh-Networked Multi-Sentry Perimeter
suggestions.desc: Develop a communication protocol using ESP32 modules to link multiple sentries together. If one sentry detects a target, it handovers the tracking data to the next unit in the perimeter for seamless area coverage.

title: CCSE CRM - IT 4983
authors: O'Koli, Cecelia; Luettegemann, Ethan; Lowhorn, Patrick; Tanner, Evan; Yang, Advisor: Ming
abstract: The CRM was handed down from last semester and lacks the ability to keep industry relationship contact names and contact information. What we need to do is to organize and manage the data within it. Not only that, but we need to learn the programming and to find out where the programmers left off. Our product is a web-based CRM that uses the bootstrap framework and php. It's tied to a sql server database for information storage.

suggestions.title: RESTful API Migration for Mobile Frontends
suggestions.desc: Decouple the PHP logic from the Bootstrap UI by building a robust RESTful API using Laravel or Slim. This allows the CRM to support a native mobile application or external integrations with KSU's other administrative tools.

suggestions.title: LinkedIn API Contact Synchronization
suggestions.desc: Implement an automated synchronization tool that uses LinkedIn's API (or a scraping tool like BeautifulSoup) to update contact titles and companies. This ensures the industry relationship data doesn't become 'stale' as professionals change jobs.

suggestions.title: Role-Based Data Privacy & Audit Logging
suggestions.desc: Develop a granular Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) system to protect sensitive industry data. Add an immutable audit log that records every data modification, which is critical for administrative accountability in university environments.

suggestions.title: Predictive Donor & Sponsor Engagement Scoring
suggestions.desc: Add a machine learning module using scikit-learn that analyzes past interaction frequency and event attendance. The system can then 'score' contacts on their likelihood to sponsor future CCSE capstone projects or events.

title: CCSE Student Engagement Application- IT 4983
authors: Ndikum, Nelly-Anne; Redington, Jessy; Hebard, Raymond; Xie, Advisor: Ying
abstract: This project targets students of the CCSE department, the aim of this application is to improve student engagement, KSU offers a large array of social, personal development, and career events on campus. The institution has noticed lately a drop in engagement in on-campus activities.

suggestions.title: Collaborative Filtering Recommendation Engine
suggestions.desc: Build a recommendation system that suggests events to students based on their major, past attendance, and interests. Use a Python-based microservice to process the student data and feed suggestions back to the application.

suggestions.title: Engagement Gamification via Digital Badges
suggestions.desc: Implement a 'Career Readiness' leaderboard where students earn digital badges and points for attending specific types of events (e.g., Resume Workshops, Hackathons). These badges could potentially be exported to LinkedIn or a student's digital portfolio.

suggestions.title: Sponsor-Facing Analytics Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Create a secondary portal for event sponsors (companies) to see anonymized real-time engagement metrics. This data helps companies justify their presence and sponsorship costs by seeing exactly which student demographics are interacting with them.

suggestions.title: Feedback Sentiment Analysis for Event Improvement
suggestions.desc: Apply Natural Language Processing (NLP) to student feedback comments using the TextBlob or VADER libraries. Provide administrators with a sentiment 'heat map' to identify which events were successful and which had systemic issues.

title: Analyze Risk Communication COVID-19- IT 7993
authors: Harris, Elaine; Kwa, Maryline; West, Chelsey; Pasumarthi, Raviteja; Oyebanji, Gabriel; Farris, Michael; Han, Advisor: Meng
abstract: This research project seeks to assess and analyze the relationship between public health advisory statements during the COVID-19 Pandemic and the trends on new infections in Singapore.

suggestions.title: Global Pandemic Policy Comparison Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Expand the dataset beyond Singapore to include other metropolitan hubs like Seoul or Taipei. Use Dash/Plotly to create an interactive visualization tool that correlates specific policy 'stringency' levels with infection curve flattening.

suggestions.title: NLP-Driven Public Sentiment Correlation
suggestions.desc: Use the Twitter API to scrape public discourse in Singapore following major health advisory statements. Use NLP to see if 'policy compliance' or 'policy resistance' sentiment in social media accurately predicts infection trends 14 days later.

suggestions.title: Simulated 'What-If' Policy Modeling
suggestions.desc: Incorporate an SIR (Susceptible-Infected-Recovered) epidemiological model into the research. Allow users to toggle different advisory strengths (e.g., mask mandates, work-from-home) to see how those changes might have altered the real-world historical data.

suggestions.title: Corporate Risk Communication Toolkit
suggestions.desc: Translate the research findings into a decision-support tool for HR departments. The tool would analyze corporate emails or public statements and suggest 'risk-optimized' wording based on the most effective patterns found in the Singapore study.

title: IoT Botnet and Nw Intrusion Detect- CS 7999
authors: Desai, Madhuri Gurunathrao; Shi, Advisor: Yong
abstract: In the present-day world, there are different types of attacks being launched on computing devices. The world is experiencing more and more cyber-attacks, and the types of attacks are also increasing. For example, an IoT device in a home network can act as a botnet attacking other devices or there could be Man in the Middle attack. As time goes by more and more devices are being connected within any given network. All these devices will be vulnerable to attacks if any one of the devices is compromised within the network. This complicates Intrusion Detection in any given network. Manual detection and intervention are nearly impossible. Hence it is quintessential to detect different types of attacks with more confidence and with less computation complexity and time. In this research, the focus is on detecting intrusions including IoT botnet attacks and other types of network attacks. To achieve this, we built a multiclass classifier model using supervised learning algorithms along with the dimensionality reduction technique. Numerous studies on ML-based IDS have been using KDD or the upgraded versions of KDD dataset. In this study, we used a new dataset, the IoT network Intrusion Detection dataset.

suggestions.title: Federated Learning for Privacy-Preserving Detection
suggestions.desc: Implement a Federated Learning framework where IoT devices train local anomaly detection models without sharing raw network traffic. This protects user privacy while allowing the entire network to learn about new botnet signatures.

suggestions.title: Raspberry Pi IDS Gateway for Smart Homes
suggestions.desc: Develop a plug-and-play security gateway using a Raspberry Pi 4 that runs the detection model. It should sit between the home router and IoT devices, using ARP spoofing to inspect traffic and automatically quarantine suspicious devices.

suggestions.title: Adversarial Robustness Testing of Botnet Models
suggestions.desc: Conduct research on how botnet authors might 'evade' the current detection system. Use GANs (Generative Adversarial Networks) to generate malicious traffic that mimics benign patterns and then retrain the detector to be resilient to these attacks.

suggestions.title: Zero-Trust Device Profiling Service
suggestions.desc: Transform the detection logic into a 'Device Health Score' service. Enterprise IT managers could use this to automatically verify that every IoT device on their network is following its expected 'behavioral profile' before granting it access to internal servers.

similar: legacy:GR-041|Fall 2020|iot botnet and nw intrusion detect

title: Alumni Information System - IT 7993
authors: Vundavalli, Vara Kalyani; Pakkirisamy, Jayashree; Rai, Sangita; Kone, Winston; Li, Advisor: Lei
abstract: A web-based system which allows admin to view, search, delete and update KSU alumni information. The system allows KSU administration to keep track of Master of Science in Information Technology graduates and their career paths. The project is sponsored by Professor, MSIT Coordinator & Asst. Dept. Chair Dr Lei Li for IT Capstone 7933 Fall Semester 2020. The system provides various functionalities which includes: User friendly GUI interface, Import New Graduate's data into database, Search functionality, Backup and Restore and allows system admin to update alumni's information (jobs, education, email and such). The application was developed using php and MySQL workbench.

suggestions.title: Automated Career Tracking via Web Scraping
suggestions.desc: Integrate a Python-based scraper using Selenium to periodically check public LinkedIn profiles of alumni. This would automatically update the 'Career Path' field in the database, reducing the manual data entry burden for KSU admins.

suggestions.title: Alumni-Student Mentorship Matchmaker
suggestions.desc: Add a module that allows current MSIT students to search for alumni based on job titles or companies. Implement a 'Request Connection' feature that facilitates networking directly through the system without exposing personal emails.

suggestions.title: Visual Career-Trajectory Analytics Portal
suggestions.desc: Build a visualization engine using D3.js that shows the typical career progression of KSU MSIT graduates (e.g., 'Junior Dev' to 'Senior Architect' in 5 years). This serves as a powerful marketing tool for the university to show prospective students their potential ROI.

suggestions.title: Curriculum Effectiveness Correlation Study
suggestions.desc: Add an analysis layer that correlates elective courses taken by alumni with their current job functions. This research would provide the department chair with data-driven insights on which courses are most valuable in the current job market.

title: CCSE EVENTS- SWE 7903
authors: Vanderbilt, Michael; Bryce, Shannon; Robinson, scott; Kalidindi, Rukmini Srija; Hillier, Paul; Parizi, Advisor: Reza
abstract: The CCSE department holds events for students to attend where they have the opportunity to network and submit resumes with the industry. the CCSE department needed a way to keep students in the loop. Our team created an application that will keep students abreast of CCSE events, track specified events, provide real-time notifications of all events as well as updates to selected events. The student can select to receive a reminder notification for any event as well as send feedback to the CCSE for CCSE review.

suggestions.title: Resume-Job Matching AI Integration
suggestions.desc: Implement a feature that uses NLP to parse a student's uploaded resume and compare it against the job descriptions of the companies attending an event. The app could then notify students: 'Company X is looking for your Python skills; visit Booth 4!'

suggestions.title: NFC/QR-Based Attendance and Lead Retrieval
suggestions.desc: Add a QR code generation feature for student IDs. When a student visits a company booth, the recruiter scans the QR code via the app, instantly receiving the student's digital resume and contact info while tracking the student's attendance.

suggestions.title: Integrated Virtual Career Fair Platform
suggestions.desc: Extend the application to support 'Virtual Booths' with integrated Jitsi or WebRTC video rooms. This allows the CCSE department to hold hybrid events where remote industry partners can still interview and network with students in real-time.

suggestions.title: Social Graph Analysis of Networking Events
suggestions.desc: Record the interactions between students and companies (with consent) to perform a Graph Theory analysis. Identify 'hubs' of high interest and see if certain event formats lead to more meaningful industry connections compared to others.

title: Facial Recognition and Object Detection-based Smart Cashier System- CS 4732
authors: Duong, Timmy; Aledhari, Project Advisor: Mohammed
abstract: The topic of smart based cashier system. Professor Dr. Aledhari is the project advisor for CS4732 Machine Vision.

suggestions.title: Multi-Object Tracking for Bulk Checkout
suggestions.desc: Upgrade the system to handle multiple items simultaneously using a Multi-Object Tracking (MOT) algorithm like DeepSORT. This would allow a customer to place a whole basket of items on the counter and have them all scanned in one pass.

suggestions.title: Biometric 'Face-Pay' Integration
suggestions.desc: Integrate a secure payment gateway where facial embeddings are used as the 'key' to a pre-stored digital wallet. Implement 'liveness detection' to ensure the system cannot be spoofed by a photo or video of a customer.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Inventory and Shelf-Life Monitor
suggestions.desc: Connect the cashier vision system to the store's inventory database. If the system detects a high volume of a specific perishable item (like fruit) being sold, it can automatically trigger a restock order or notify staff of potential shortages.

suggestions.title: Inference Acceleration with TensorRT/OpenVINO
suggestions.desc: Research and implement edge-acceleration techniques to run the object detection models on low-power hardware like an NVIDIA Jetson Nano. Compare the frames-per-second (FPS) and accuracy trade-offs of different quantization levels.
