title: Georgia Tech Parking Maintenance Portal
authors: Joseph Richardson, Emmanuel Aku, Izabel Ivanova, Sajina Vinaykumar
abstract: Web portal for managing parking facility maintenance operations at Georgia Tech, including ticket tracking and reporting.

suggestions.title: Offline-First Progressive Web App
suggestions.desc: Convert the portal into a PWA with Workbox to allow technicians to log maintenance tickets in underground parking decks where WiFi is unavailable. The app should automatically sync data to the database once a connection is restored.

suggestions.title: Computer Vision Plate Detection
suggestions.desc: Integrate OpenALPR (Automatic License Plate Recognition) using the parking lot cameras. Link this to the maintenance portal so that if a vehicle is parked in a 'Maintenance Zone' without a permit, an automated ticket is generated for the staff.

suggestions.title: Maintenance Route Optimizer
suggestions.desc: Implement a route optimization algorithm using the Google Maps Directions API. The portal can then provide technicians with the most efficient walking or driving path to address all 'High Priority' tickets across the Georgia Tech campus.

suggestions.title: IoT Kiosk Health Monitoring
suggestions.desc: Connect the portal to the physical parking kiosks via an API. Use basic anomaly detection to flag kiosks that haven't processed a transaction in over an hour, automatically creating a maintenance ticket for a 'Potential Hardware Failure'.

title: KSU WiFi Monitoring
authors: Chris Garsee, Andrew Brooks, Pawan Amedi, Greg Matthews
abstract: Raspberry Pi-based WiFi network monitoring dashboard for tracking KSU campus network performance and usage.

suggestions.title: Student Study-Space Locator
suggestions.desc: Create a public-facing React dashboard that uses WiFi usage density to tell students which campus buildings are currently quietest. Provide a 'Real-Time Capacity' percentage for popular spots like the KSU library based on connected device counts.

suggestions.title: Grafana & InfluxDB Observability Stack
suggestions.desc: Replace the basic dashboard with an InfluxDB time-series database and a Grafana front-end. This allows for professional-grade monitoring of packet loss, signal-to-noise ratios, and jitter over months of campus operation.

suggestions.title: Rogue Access Point Detection
suggestions.desc: Extend the Raspberry Pi monitoring logic to scan for 'Evil Twin' access points. Use Scapy to analyze beacon frames and alert IT staff if an unauthorized device is broadcasting a spoofed KSU SSID to harvest student credentials.

suggestions.title: Predictive Network Congestion Alerts
suggestions.desc: Apply an LSTM (Long Short-Term Memory) network to historical WiFi traffic data. Build a notification system that alerts the KSU network team via Slack when congestion is predicted to exceed 90% during peak events like finals week.

title: Tug Vehicle Aircraft Classifier
authors: Kyle Randall, JJ Gillian, Frank Domfeh, Andrew Fields, Olu Asabi
abstract: Machine learning aircraft tire classifier for tug vehicles at airports to prevent damage during towing operations.
topics: Machine Learning, Computer Vision, Image Classification

suggestions.title: YOLO-based Real-time Tire Wear Detection
suggestions.desc: Extend the aircraft classifier to not only identify the plane type but also detect visible tire defects or excessive wear using YOLOv8. This provides a secondary safety check for tug operators during the 'walk-around' phase of towing.

suggestions.title: AR HUD for Tug Alignment
suggestions.desc: Develop an Augmented Reality (AR) interface for tug drivers that overlays a 'safe-zone' path based on the detected aircraft type. The system should calculate the specific turning radius and nose-gear weight limits of the identified plane to prevent structural damage.

suggestions.title: Synthetic Training via Simulators
suggestions.desc: Use Microsoft Flight Simulator or X-Plane to generate thousands of high-fidelity images of various aircraft tires under different lighting and weather conditions. Compare the accuracy of a model trained on these synthetic images versus one trained on the limited real-world airport dataset.

suggestions.title: Automated Logbook & Maintenance Integration
suggestions.desc: Link the CV classifier to an airport's maintenance database (MRO). When a tug vehicle identifies an aircraft, it should automatically pull up the tire pressure history and log the tow event, ensuring that airframe cycles are tracked without manual data entry.

title: Black Friday Sales
authors: Sreevalli Priyanka Yellanki, Soujanya Gourisetty, Priyanka Reddy
abstract: Analysis of Black Friday retail data to discover purchase patterns and predict customer buying behavior.
topics: Data Analytics, Machine Learning, Retail Analytics

suggestions.title: Dynamic Retail Pricing Engine
suggestions.desc: Build a pricing optimization tool that uses the predicted customer behavior to suggest real-time discounts. If the model predicts a high likelihood of a customer purchasing a high-margin item, the engine could suggest 'bundle' pricing for lower-performing inventory items to increase the average basket size.

suggestions.title: Graph-Based Market Basket Analysis
suggestions.desc: Implement a Graph Neural Network (GNN) to map the relationships between product categories during Black Friday. This can identify 'bridge products' that, when purchased, lead to significantly higher spending in unrelated categories, helping retailers optimize floor layout.

suggestions.title: Black Friday 'Panic' Sentiment Correlation
suggestions.desc: Scrape social media data (Twitter/X) from the same timeframe as the Black Friday dataset. Use NLP to correlate regional sentiment and 'deal-hunting' trends with actual sales volumes to predict how social hype impacts purchasing spikes in real-time.

suggestions.title: Customer Lifetime Value (CLV) Forecasting
suggestions.desc: Extend the analysis to predict which Black Friday 'one-time buyers' are likely to become repeat customers. Use a Beta-Geometric/Negative Binomial Distribution (BG/NBD) model to identify the demographic markers of high-value loyalists vs. discount-only shoppers.

title: Santander Transaction Prediction
authors: Md Arabin Islam Talukder, Priyanka Velumani, Sanju Timsina
abstract: Customer transaction prediction project using Santander Bank dataset from Kaggle. Predicts whether customers will make future transactions using ML.
topics: Machine Learning, Data Science, Financial Analytics, Kaggle

suggestions.title: Explainable AI (XAI) for Loan Transparency
suggestions.desc: Apply SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) or LIME to the Santander prediction model to identify which specific transaction features most influence a 'future transaction' prediction. This helps in building a transparent banking model that complies with 'Right to Explanation' regulations.

suggestions.title: Imbalanced Data Synthesis with CTGAN
suggestions.desc: Address the typical class imbalance in financial transaction datasets by using Conditional GANs (CTGAN) to generate high-fidelity synthetic 'positive' transaction instances. Compare the resulting model's AUC-ROC score against standard oversampling techniques like SMOTE.

suggestions.title: Real-time Transaction Scoring API
suggestions.desc: Deploy the trained model as a containerized Flask or FastAPI microservice. Build a front-end simulation that streams dummy transaction data and visualizes the 'Probability of Future Transaction' in real-time, simulating a bank's CRM alert system for account managers.

suggestions.title: Automated Feature Engineering with TSFresh
suggestions.desc: Use the TSFresh library to automatically extract hundreds of time-series features (like rolling means, peaks, and Fourier coefficients) from the transaction data. Evaluate if these engineered temporal features provide a significant lift in precision compared to the original Kaggle features.

title: Efficient Vehicle Parking Plan
authors: Shwetha Adoni Prasad, Srivatsa Mallapragada, Shweta Khandal
abstract: Algorithm for efficient parking spot allocation based on vehicle type, minimizing distance traveled and space conflicts.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Occupancy Integration via Computer Vision
suggestions.desc: Upgrade the static allocation algorithm by integrating a live video feed processed with YOLOv8. Use the visual data to update the parking graph in real-time, allowing the algorithm to reroute vehicles dynamically if a spot is unexpectedly blocked or taken.

suggestions.title: Dynamic Demand-Based Pricing Engine
suggestions.desc: Develop a microservice that adjusts parking rates based on the algorithm's density metrics and peak hour predictions. Use historical parking data to build a pricing model that incentivizes distribution to underutilized zones, similar to Uber's surge pricing.

suggestions.title: EV-Aware Optimization with Charging Constraints
suggestions.desc: Extend the graph model to prioritize Electric Vehicles (EVs) for spots with charging infrastructure while minimizing cable-length conflicts. Incorporate a 'wait-list' logic that re-allocates chargers as soon as a vehicle reaches a target battery percentage.

suggestions.title: Multi-Floor 3D Pathfinding for Complex Structures
suggestions.desc: Transform the 2D allocation into a 3D environment using A* or Dijkstra’s algorithm tailored for multi-story garages. Include variables for ramp steepness and ceiling height to accommodate specific vehicle types like delivery vans or SUVs.

title: Language Conversion
authors: Charishma Macherla, Jhilakshi Sharma
abstract: Real-time speech translation system to help international and hearing-impaired students understand lectures in their native language.
topics: NLP, Speech Recognition, Machine Translation, Accessibility

suggestions.title: Multi-Speaker Diarization for Group Discussions
suggestions.desc: Incorporate a speaker diarization library like Pyannote.audio to distinguish between a professor and student questions. This allows the system to label the transcript appropriately, preventing confusion during fast-paced classroom interactions.

suggestions.title: STEM-Specific Vocabulary Fine-Tuning
suggestions.desc: Fine-tune the underlying LLM (like Whisper or GPT-4o) using university-specific datasets of technical jargon from engineering or medical lectures. This ensures that complex terms like 'stochastic' or 'laparoscopic' are translated accurately rather than phonetically.

suggestions.title: AR Glasses Captioning Overlay
suggestions.desc: Build a mobile interface that streams the translated text to Nreal or Vuzix AR glasses. This provides a 'heads-up' display for hearing-impaired students, allowing them to watch the professor and read captions simultaneously without looking down at a screen.

suggestions.title: Low-Resource Dialect Support for International Students
suggestions.desc: Extend the system to support low-resource languages or specific regional dialects using Zero-Shot Machine Translation. Use the FLORES-200 dataset to evaluate and improve translation accuracy for minority languages often ignored by mainstream tools.

title: When To Buy Airlines Ticket
authors: James Pock
abstract: Predictive model to determine the optimal time to purchase airline tickets, finding relationship between purchase date and flight cost.

suggestions.title: Reinforcement Learning for 'Buy vs. Wait' Signals
suggestions.desc: Apply Q-Learning or Deep Q-Networks (DQN) to the existing predictive model to generate a binary 'Buy Now' or 'Wait' recommendation. The agent would be rewarded for maximizing savings compared to the average price of the route.

suggestions.title: Digital Nomad Multi-City Route Optimizer
suggestions.desc: Expand the tool to predict prices for 'Open-Jaw' or multi-city itineraries where the sequence of cities affects the total cost. Use a genetic algorithm to find the cheapest dates and sequence for a 3-4 city trip based on the price prediction curves.

suggestions.title: Price Drop Alert & Refund Monitoring Service
suggestions.desc: Build a monitoring bot that tracks purchased tickets and alerts the user if the price drops below a certain threshold. Integrate with airline 'Price Match' policies or the 24-hour cancellation window to automate the refund/rebooking process.

suggestions.title: External Factor Correlation Analysis
suggestions.desc: Integrate non-price features into the model like global fuel price indices, major sporting events (via SerpApi), and weather patterns. Research how much 'External Volatility' impacts prediction accuracy compared to simple historical price trends.

title: Churn Rate Prediction of Business
authors: Michael Wong, Vaibhav Pandey, Farid Khan
abstract: Machine learning classification to predict customer churn rate using telecom dataset with Apache Spark for scalability.
topics: Machine Learning, Big Data, Spark, Business Analytics

suggestions.title: Explainable AI (XAI) for Customer Retention
suggestions.desc: Implement SHAP or LIME within the Spark pipeline to provide specific reasons 'why' a customer is predicted to churn. The end result is a dashboard for account managers that suggests personalized retention offers (e.g., 'Discount offered due to high call-failure rate').

suggestions.title: Real-Time Streaming Prediction with Kafka
suggestions.desc: Convert the batch-processing model into a streaming architecture using Spark Structured Streaming and Apache Kafka. This allows the business to detect churn signals (like a failed payment or multiple support tickets) and trigger an automated response within minutes.

suggestions.title: Sentiment-Infused Feature Engineering
suggestions.desc: Use NLP to analyze customer support logs or Twitter mentions and add a 'Sentiment Score' as a feature in the ML model. This tests the hypothesis that emotional frustration is a stronger predictor of churn than technical usage metrics.

suggestions.title: Federated Learning for Cross-Industry Churn
suggestions.desc: Research a federated learning approach where multiple telecom companies can train a global churn model without sharing sensitive PII (Personally Identifiable Information). Use a framework like OpenMined to ensure data privacy while improving model robustness.

title: Parallelizing Random Lasso
authors: James Hamilton, Daniel Karasek, Sams Khan, Jason Wein, Devyn Wilkins, Seung Choo
abstract: Parallelization of Random Lasso regression using Apache Spark for high-dimensional genetic dataset analysis and gene regulatory network prediction.
topics: Bioinformatics, Parallel Computing, Machine Learning, Spark

suggestions.title: Multi-Omics Data Integration
suggestions.desc: Extend the Random Lasso implementation to handle heterogeneous datasets, combining genomics, proteomics, and metabolomics data. The goal is to identify regulatory links that span across different biological layers using a unified Spark job.

suggestions.title: GPU-Accelerated Spark with RAPIDS
suggestions.desc: Benchmark the Spark CPU implementation against a GPU-accelerated version using the NVIDIA RAPIDS library. Compare the cost-efficiency and training speed on high-dimensional datasets like the UK Biobank to determine the scalability limits of each architecture.

suggestions.title: SaaS Platform for High-Dimensional Bioinformatics
suggestions.desc: Develop a cloud-native web portal where researchers can upload CSV/VCF files and run the parallel Lasso algorithm. Use Kubernetes to spin up transient Spark clusters on-demand, providing a simple UI for non-CS researchers to view gene networks.

suggestions.title: Drug Sensitivity Prediction for Personalized Medicine
suggestions.desc: Apply the parallelized Random Lasso model to the Genomics of Drug Sensitivity in Cancer (GDSC) dataset. Predict which gene expressions most strongly correlate with drug resistance, helping identify potential biomarkers for cancer treatment.

title: Determining Warehouse Placement
authors: Chip Gardner, Erik Swanson, Daniel Brown
abstract: Algorithm to determine optimal warehouse placement locations based on geographic and logistical constraints.

suggestions.title: Last-Mile Drone Hub Optimization
suggestions.desc: Modify the placement algorithm to account for drone flight ranges and local airspace regulations. The result should be a hybrid warehouse model that identifies 'Mother Hubs' for trucks and 'Satellite Hubs' for autonomous drone delivery.

suggestions.title: OpenStreetMap Integration for Realistic Routing
suggestions.desc: Replace simple Euclidean distances with real-world road network data using the OSMnx library. Account for bridge height limits, truck-restricted roads, and real-time traffic congestion patterns to find truly viable warehouse locations.

suggestions.title: Sustainability-Focused Multi-Objective Optimization
suggestions.desc: Redesign the fitness function to balance logistics costs with carbon footprint. Use the NSGA-II algorithm to find a Pareto-optimal set of locations that minimize both delivery time and total CO2 emissions for the fleet.

suggestions.title: Disaster-Resilient Supply Chain Mapping
suggestions.desc: Add a 'Risk Layer' to the GIS model that includes historical data on floods, hurricanes, and seismic activity. The algorithm would then suggest placements that maximize proximity to customers while staying outside high-risk environmental zones.

title: Secure Authentication and Access Control for Enterprise
authors: Pawan Amedi
abstract: This project outlines the development of a bespoke Customer Relationship Management (CRM) system specifically designed for the College of Computer and Software Engineering (CCSE). The initiative aims to centralize customer information into a unified repository, thereby enhancing the confidentiality, management, and optimization of data and communication processes within the college. The CRM system will integrate features for detailed profiles, communication optimization, complex workflow management, document repository, and data migration to ensure efficiency and data integrity. It will also facilitate seamless interaction with Microsoft 365 and Outlook, supporting the college's operational needs and maintaining its commitment to excellence in education and collaboration. The project is set to be executed in three major milestones, involving product selection, system implementation, user testing, and final deployment, with the end goal of improving overall customer relationship management. The team, led by Venkatesh Alla, alongside team members Keerthi Nannapaneni and Vinay Kumar Rapolu, under the guidance of project owners Alla Kemelmakher and Nasiya Sharif, and advisor Ying Xie, plans to use a dedicated project management tool, Jira, for collaboration and task tracking. This CRM initiative not only aims to meet the specific needs of the CCSE but also to set a benchmark for operational efficiency and data management in educational institutions.

suggestions.title: Automated Student Lifecycle Integration via APIs
suggestions.desc: Connect the CRM to university-standard data sources like the Banner API or DegreeWorks. Automate the population of student profiles so that CCSE advisors can see real-time GPA updates and course completion status directly within the CRM without manual data entry.

suggestions.title: AI-Powered 'At-Risk' Student Identification System
suggestions.desc: Build a machine learning module that analyzes communication frequency and academic data within the CRM. Train a model to flag students who are falling behind or disengaging, triggering an automated 'Workflow Management' task for advisors to reach out for an intervention.

suggestions.title: Smart Matchmaking for Internships and Employers
suggestions.desc: Add a 'Corporate Relations' module to the CRM that stores job descriptions from CCSE partners. Use a basic NLP matching algorithm to suggest the top 5 students for a specific internship based on their profile data, skills, and interests tracked in the system.

suggestions.title: Longitudinal Career Outcome Analysis Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Develop a post-graduation tracking feature using LinkedIn API or survey integration. Create a visualization layer (using D3.js or Chart.js) that shows the department how different student profiles (GPA, clubs, electives) correlate with high-paying career outcomes 3–5 years later.

title: Traffic Load Balancing
authors: Karl Kevin Tiba Fossoh, Ganama Hawaou, Gregg Hammer, Reza Parizi
abstract: Research on traffic load balancing algorithms for network systems, exploring dynamic distribution strategies.

suggestions.title: Early-Fusion Multi-Modal Neural Network
suggestions.desc: Redesign the classifier to use 'early-fusion' architecture, where image and text features are merged into a shared latent space before classification, rather than just voting at the end.

suggestions.title: E-commerce Listing Optimization Assistant
suggestions.desc: Create a Chrome extension for Amazon sellers that evaluates their draft product descriptions and images against the 'best-seller' model to suggest improvements for higher sales.

suggestions.title: Sentiment Drift Analysis for Bestsellers
suggestions.desc: Incorporate a sentiment analysis module that tracks the 'mood' of customer reviews over time to predict when a best-selling product is about to lose its ranking.

suggestions.title: Cross-Platform Trend Transfer Predictor
suggestions.desc: Apply the trained Amazon model to products on Etsy or eBay to determine if best-seller characteristics on one platform can accurately predict success on another.

title: BlockChain Drug Counterfeit
authors: Sanjoosh Akkineni
abstract: Blockchain-based system for tracking and preventing drug counterfeiting in pharmaceutical supply chains.

suggestions.title: NFC-Blockchain 'Phygital' Verification
suggestions.desc: Integrate physical NFC tags on medicine packaging with the blockchain backend. Build a mobile app that allows a pharmacist to scan the tag, verify its unique cryptographic signature on-chain, and check the entire 'custody' history of that specific bottle.

suggestions.title: Zero-Knowledge Proofs for Privacy-Preserving Audits
suggestions.desc: Implement Zero-Knowledge Proofs (using ZoKrates or SnarkyJS) to allow regulators to verify that a drug is authentic without revealing sensitive commercial data like supplier names or exact shipping volumes.

suggestions.title: Automated Smart Contract Insurance for Cold Chains
suggestions.desc: Connect IoT temperature sensors to the blockchain via an Oracle (like Chainlink). If a drug shipment exceeds a safe temperature, the Smart Contract automatically triggers a claim and invalidates the batch, ensuring no spoiled medicine is sold.

suggestions.title: Consumable QR-Code Ledger for End-Users
suggestions.desc: Create a consumer-facing dApp where patients scan a QR code on their prescription to see its 'Birth Certificate.' This would include data on the manufacturing plant, batch test results, and a timestamp of when it arrived at their local pharmacy.

title: Mask RCNN for Laparoscopic Surgery
authors: Giovanni Bamou Nana, Wesley Ellgass, Spencer Feeney, Marissa Hudson, Tristan Leavitt, Justin Spaid
abstract: Application of Mask R-CNN deep learning model to instrument and tissue segmentation in laparoscopic surgical video.
topics: Computer Vision, Deep Learning, Healthcare, Medical Imaging

suggestions.title: Temporal Consistency for Video Segmentation
suggestions.desc: Move from single-frame Mask R-CNN to a video-based approach using ConvLSTM or Mask2Former. This ensures that instruments are tracked smoothly across frames without 'flickering' or losing the mask during fast surgical movements.

suggestions.title: Intraoperative 'No-Go' Zone Alerts
suggestions.desc: Develop a real-time monitoring layer that defines critical tissue areas (like major arteries) as 'No-Go' zones. If the Mask R-CNN detects a surgical tool entering a predefined proximity to these areas, it triggers an audible or visual alert for the surgeon.

suggestions.title: Edge Deployment on NVIDIA Jetson
suggestions.desc: Optimize the Mask R-CNN model using TensorRT and quantization (INT8) to run at 30+ FPS on an NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano. This makes the system portable and usable in operating rooms without requiring a full server rack.

suggestions.title: Automated Post-Surgical Report Generation
suggestions.desc: Use the segmentation data to quantify the duration of the surgery, the frequency of tool usage, and the amount of tissue handled. Generate an automated PDF report summarizing the technical efficiency of the procedure for surgical training and review.

title: Two Algorithm Comparison
authors: Hao Zhang
abstract: Comparative analysis of two algorithms measuring performance characteristics and efficiency metrics.

suggestions.title: Deep Reinforcement Learning for Real-Time Control
suggestions.desc: Replace the predictive simulation with a Deep Q-Network (DQN) that controls HVAC setpoints in real-time. Use the 'OpenAI Gym' environment to simulate the West Point building and train the agent to balance occupant comfort (CO2 and Temp) against peak energy prices.

suggestions.title: Campus-Wide Microgrid Load Balancing
suggestions.desc: Extend the peak shaving model to include other energy-intensive campus assets like electric vehicle (EV) charging stations and dorm lighting. Create a coordinated strategy where HVAC load is decreased when EV demand spikes to keep the total campus draw below a critical threshold.

suggestions.title: Commercial Smart Thermostat API for Rebates
suggestions.desc: Develop a SaaS wrapper for the optimization model that connects to smart thermostats (like Ecobee or Nest) via API. Target small business owners by automating their participation in 'Demand Response' programs, earning them utility rebates during peak hours.

suggestions.title: Occupancy Sensing via Thermal Imaging Fusion
suggestions.desc: Research the impact of real-time occupancy data on the peak shaving model. Integrate low-cost PIR or thermal sensors to feed 'human heat load' data into the HVAC simulation, allowing for more aggressive energy savings in empty zones.

title: Mask RCNN for Laparoscopic Surgery (v2)
authors: Eric Schneider, Jay Clark, Leontios Haralambus, Justinas Songaila, Wade Whitten, Oliver Prestridge
abstract: Second team implementation of Mask R-CNN for automated surgical instrument detection in laparoscopic video streams.
topics: Computer Vision, Deep Learning, Healthcare, Medical Imaging

suggestions.title: Temporal Consistency for Surgical Video
suggestions.desc: Enhance the Mask R-CNN model by adding a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) or Transformer-based temporal layer to reduce flicker and maintain consistent instrument tracking across video frames. Use the PyTorchVideo library to handle the spatio-temporal features of laparoscopic surgery.

suggestions.title: Anatomy-Instrument Interaction Mapping
suggestions.desc: Extend the model beyond simple detection to identify interactions between instruments and specific organs (e.g., gallbladder, liver). Use the Cholec80 dataset to train the model to recognize 'grasping' or 'cutting' actions for automated surgical phase recognition.

suggestions.title: Automated Surgical Skill Assessment
suggestions.desc: Build a dashboard that calculates surgical proficiency metrics, such as instrument path length and economy of motion, based on the Mask R-CNN coordinate outputs. This could serve as a non-biased training tool for medical residents.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Edge Deployment for the OR
suggestions.desc: Optimize the model for real-time inference on edge devices like the NVIDIA Jetson Orin to provide low-latency overlays in the operating room. Use TensorRT for quantization and pruning to ensure the frame rate remains above 30 FPS.

title: Ascension
authors: Zachary Baker, Anthony Onukwuli, Nikki Ashaka
abstract: Dungeon exploration game featuring fallen spirits, atmospheric environments, and strategic combat mechanics.

suggestions.title: Dynamic Color-Blindness Shader Library
suggestions.desc: Create a suite of post-processing shaders that simulate Protanopia, Deuteranopia, and Tritanopia for the non-colorblind player. This forces the cooperative partners to communicate more effectively to solve puzzles that the other literally cannot see.

suggestions.title: Accessibility-First Level Design Tool
suggestions.desc: Build an editor extension in Unity that automatically checks level geometry for 'color-reliance' issues. The tool should flag areas where a puzzle is impossible to solve without specific color perception and suggest secondary cues like shapes or patterns.

suggestions.title: Biometric Stress Analysis in Co-op Play
suggestions.desc: Integrate heart rate monitor data to track player frustration levels during difficult color-based puzzles. Use this data to research how accessibility constraints impact cooperative communication and team bonding.

suggestions.title: Roses are Red: Mobile AR Expansion
suggestions.desc: Port the puzzle mechanics to an Augmented Reality (AR) setting using ARCore. Players must move around a physical room to align 'color beams' visible only through their phone screens to solve spatial puzzles.

title: Autonomous Robot Sim Using Unity
authors: Christian Cho, Derek Martin, Jesse Manders
abstract: Unity-based simulation of autonomous robot navigation and behavior for research and education purposes.

suggestions.title: Modular Space Colony Destruction System
suggestions.desc: Implement a mesh-slicing or pre-fractured destruction system for the sci-fi environment. Players should be able to destroy cover or breach walls within the colony to create new tactical pathways during combat.

suggestions.title: Zero-G Combat Mechanics Integration
suggestions.desc: Expand the sci-fi setting by adding low-gravity zones where combat physics transition from traditional 3D movement to 6DOF (Six Degrees of Freedom). Use thruster-based movement and recoil-driven navigation.

suggestions.title: Vesta Faction Narrative Engine
suggestions.desc: Build a branching dialogue and reputation system using Ink or Yarn Spinner. Player choices during the 'Absolution' storyline should determine which sci-fi factions help or hinder them in later combat stages.

suggestions.title: Neural Network-Based Enemy Stealth AI
suggestions.desc: Develop enemies that adapt to the player’s combat style using a simple neural network. If a player relies on long-range sniping, the AI should learn to utilize the space colony’s vents and corridors for flanking.

title: Hidden Opus
authors: Jacob Jennings, Chris Regan, LaDarius DeShield, Richard Lago
abstract: First-person horror game with atmospheric storytelling and puzzle elements set in a mysterious abandoned building.

suggestions.title: Browser-Based ECL Code Playground
suggestions.desc: Develop an interactive code editor using Monaco (the VS Code engine) that allows users to write and test HPCC Systems' ECL (Enterprise Control Language) code directly on the website. Use a backend container to compile and return results.

suggestions.title: HPCC Cluster Performance Visualizer
suggestions.desc: Create a D3.js data visualization tool that shows how big data is partitioned across a Thor or Roxy cluster. This helps students visualize the 'high-performance' aspect of LexisNexis technology beyond just writing code.

suggestions.title: Personalized ECL Learning Path API
suggestions.desc: Build a recommendation engine that analyzes a user's quiz performance and suggests specific HPCC tutorial modules. Use a 'knowledge graph' to map prerequisite skills for complex big data tasks.

suggestions.title: Gamified HPCC Certification Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Implement a full user authentication and badging system where LexisNexis employees can earn 'ECL Master' certifications. Include a leaderboard for completing coding challenges and a forum for peer-to-peer mentoring.

authors: Erik Sheffer, Jacob Holloway, Jarrod Ariola, Hunter Jones

suggestions.title: AI-Driven Compatibility Scoring

suggestions.title: Trust-Verified Identity Vault
suggestions.desc: Implement a secure identity verification layer using the Stripe Identity API or Onfido to conduct background checks and ID verification, specifically catering to the safety concerns of the 40+ demographic.

suggestions.title: Senior-Centric Accessibility Audit
suggestions.desc: Conduct a formal UX research study focusing on WCAG 2.1 AAA compliance and implement specialized UI components for older adults, such as high-contrast modes, dynamic font scaling, and voice-assisted navigation.

suggestions.title: Community-Based Resource Exchange
suggestions.desc: Extend the platform to include a 'Skill & Tool Sharing' module where roommates can list shared resources or chores, using a React-based dashboard to manage household agreements and communal expenses.

title: VR Bloodstream

topics: Game Development, Virtual Reality, Education, Healthcare

suggestions.title: Pathology Simulation Module

suggestions.title: Multiplayer Bio-Defense Minigame
suggestions.desc: Add a cooperative multiplayer layer using Unity Netcode where users play as white blood cells defending against an infection. This turns the educational experience into a high-stakes 'tower defense' game inside the body.

suggestions.title: Physically-Based Fluid Dynamics Integration
suggestions.desc: Replace basic animations with a Compute Shader-based fluid simulation to create realistic blood flow patterns around arterial bends and obstructions. This would involve implementing a simplified Navier-Stokes solver for real-time performance.

suggestions.title: Biometric Syncing for Immersive Biofeedback
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Polar H10 or Apple Watch API to sync the VR world’s pulse and flow speed with the user’s real-time heart rate. This creates a powerful biofeedback tool for teaching students about the relationship between activity and circulatory speed.

title: The Darkness Within
authors: Nadia Khan, Haley Gragg
abstract: 3D platformer adventure game designed for children with engaging gameplay and accessible mechanics.

suggestions.title: Layer 2 Gas Optimization
suggestions.desc: Migrate the smart contract infrastructure to a Layer 2 solution like Polygon or Arbitrum to reduce transaction costs for room check-ins, making the system financially viable for high-turnover hotels.

suggestions.title: NFT-Based Loyalty Rewards
suggestions.desc: Transform room keys into dynamic NFTs (ERC-721) that evolve based on stay frequency, granting holders automated access to VIP lounges or late check-out perks via smart contract triggers.

suggestions.title: Timed Access for Coworking Spaces
suggestions.desc: Adapt the NFC-blockchain system for flexible office rentals by implementing 'Time-Locked' smart contracts that grant access only during specific hourly windows booked via a web interface.

suggestions.title: Multi-Sig Emergency Override
suggestions.desc: Develop a decentralized security protocol using multi-signature wallets that allows hotel staff to regain room access in emergencies without compromising the privacy and immutability of the guest's digital key.

title: EDP (Enterprise Delivery Planning)
authors: Alexander Plattel, William Parish, David McFall, Clayton Hunsinger, Heather Rego, Chasse Cossairt
abstract: Mobile SAP interface for truck drivers to update delivery data in real time, built for auto parts supply company IMC.

suggestions.title: Temporal Flow Consistency
suggestions.desc: Integrate Optical Flow algorithms (like Farneback) to ensure that the 'Magic Eraser' mask remains stable across frames, eliminating the flickering effect common in frame-by-frame AI object removal.

suggestions.title: Privacy-First Dashcam Redactor

suggestions.title: Generative Inpainting Upgrade

suggestions.title: Browser-Based Inference via TensorFlow.js
suggestions.desc: Optimize the model for client-side execution using TensorFlow.js and WebGL, allowing users to perform video object removal directly in the browser without uploading heavy files to a server.

suggestions.desc: Add an RFID reader or a barcode scanner to the Raspberry Pi to track coffee bag usage. Use the Amazon Dash Replenishment API or a custom webhook to automatically reorder beans when the load cell detects the inventory is low.

suggestions.title: Office Productivity Suite Integration
suggestions.desc: Build a Slack and Microsoft Teams bot that allows employees to 'subscribe' to specific roasts and receive a direct message when a fresh pot is brewed. Allow remote brewing triggers via authenticated chat commands.

suggestions.title: Water Quality and TDS Monitoring
suggestions.desc: Integrate a Total Dissolved Solids (TDS) sensor to analyze water hardness and its effect on extraction quality. Build a data visualization dashboard showing the correlation between water purity and the 'Perfect Cup' rating as logged by users.

similar: legacy:GR-02|Spring 2016|iot system to measure student physiological responses

title: NVidia CUDA in Bioinformatics
authors: Brent Robinson, Justin Voorhees, Chip Gardner, Victor Sahin
abstract: GPU-accelerated pairwise protein distance algorithm using NVIDIA CUDA API compared against serial implementation.
topics: GPU, CUDA, Bioinformatics, High Performance Computing

suggestions.title: FinBERT Sentiment Optimization
suggestions.desc: Replace general-purpose NLP models with HuggingFace’s FinBERT, specifically trained on financial communication. Fine-tune the model using the 'Crypto-Sentiment' dataset from Kaggle to better capture crypto-specific slang and market nuances.

suggestions.title: Multi-Exchange Arbitrage Bot
suggestions.desc: Integrate the sentiment signals with the CCXT library to monitor price discrepancies between Binance and Coinbase. Build a bot that executes trades when high-sentiment spikes coincide with price lags between exchanges.

suggestions.title: Multimodal Price Predictor
suggestions.desc: Combine the Twitter sentiment scores with technical indicators (RSI, MACD) and on-chain metrics like Whale Alerts using an Ensemble Learning approach. This reduces false positives caused by social media 'noise' that doesn't reflect actual market liquidity.

suggestions.title: NFT Collection Hype Tracker
suggestions.desc: Pivot the system to analyze Discord and OpenSea activity for specific NFT collections. Use the sentiment engine to predict 'floor price' movements based on community engagement levels rather than just historical sales.

similar: legacy:GRCS-01|Spring 2017|deep convolutional neural network and parallel programming

title: Pibble
authors: Nick Main, Charles Bryant, Sean Hale, Haley Bear, Mark Petell
abstract: Chrome extension using machine learning to automate data collection from websites for sponsor Labrador, replacing manual entry.
topics: Machine Learning, Browser Extension, Data Collection, Automation

suggestions.title: Persistent Character Growth via NFC Rewriting
suggestions.desc: Implement NTAG215 write capabilities to store player stats and experience directly on the physical cards. This enables a 'carry-over' mechanic where a physical card effectively becomes a 'memory card' for a character, allowing it to level up across different game sessions.

suggestions.title: Mobile Companion App with CoreNFC
suggestions.desc: Develop an iOS/Android companion app using CoreNFC or Android NFC API that allows players to scan their Wildling Rumble cards on their personal phones. This removes the need for a dedicated hardware reader and allows for 'hidden' actions, like checking secret stats privately.

suggestions.title: Augmented Reality (AR) Unit Visualization
suggestions.desc: Integrate Vuforia or ARKit to recognize the physical NFC cards through a camera view. When a player scans a card, a 3D animated model of the character would appear on top of the physical card, bridging the gap between digital combat and the physical tabletop.

suggestions.title: Multi-Device Synchronous Tabletop State
suggestions.desc: Use WebSockets (Socket.io) to synchronize the game state across multiple tablets or laptops around a table. Scanning a card on one player’s device would instantly update the 'world view' on a central screen, creating a shared digital-physical play space.

title: Smart Pill Bottle
authors: Dylan Clark, Ben Davenport-Ray
abstract: Smart pill bottle allowing users to input medication schedules and receive reminders, with push notifications for missed doses.

suggestions.title: Headless Browser Vulnerability Scan
suggestions.desc: Integrate Playwright or Selenium into the WASP core to scan modern Single Page Applications (React/Vue). This allows the tool to detect vulnerabilities in Client-Side Rendering that traditional static scanners often miss.

suggestions.title: DevSecOps CI/CD Integration
suggestions.desc: Develop a GitHub Action that triggers a WASP scan on every Pull Request. Set up a gateway policy that automatically blocks deployments if the scan detects any 'Critical' OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities.

suggestions.title: LLM-Powered Exploit Payload Generator
suggestions.desc: Use the OpenAI API to generate context-aware XSS and SQL injection payloads based on the specific HTML attributes found by the scanner. This moves the project from generic pattern matching to intelligent, adaptive penetration testing.

suggestions.title: Security Reporting as a Service
suggestions.desc: Build a web-based management portal where users can schedule scans and receive professional PDF security audits. Use a subscription-based model and integrate Stripe for a ready-to-market SaaS cybersecurity tool.

title: IoT Voice and Gesture Control System
authors: Deja Tyla Jackson, Zoe Cesar, Richmond Mensah, Nathaniel Klein, Amanda Norris, Khadijah Mahley
abstract: This project addresses concerns raised by a Sponsor regarding inefficiencies in managing CCSE capstone projects. Key pain points include organization, project management, and communication among stakeholders. The proposed solution involves implementing Customer Relationship Management (CRM) software. Upon gathering requirements from the Sponsor, including contact information capture, workflow management, document control, and customization capabilities, we evaluated several CRM platforms. A total of thirty-two CRMs were reviewed and Vtiger, OroCRM, and SuiteCRM were selected for testing. SuiteCRM was chosen for its comprehensive features and user-friendliness. The second step of the project involved testing SuiteCRM functionalities on a dedicated server, leading to its selection for deployment preparation. Final steps include documentation development, training material creation, and incorporating user feedback for optimal system configuration.
topics: IoT, AWS, Gesture Recognition, Voice Control, Robotics

suggestions.title: Automated Capstone Lifecycle Middleware
suggestions.desc: Develop a middleware service using Node.js or Python that connects the proposed CRM with GitHub and Jira APIs to automatically provision project repositories and kanban boards once a sponsor proposal is approved.

suggestions.title: Student-Sponsor Talent Matching Portal
suggestions.desc: Build a front-end interface that pulls student skills and interests from the CRM to create a 'matching' algorithm, allowing sponsors to see which student teams best align with their project requirements.

suggestions.title: Predictive Project Risk Analysis
suggestions.desc: Implement a sentiment analysis module using Python's NLTK to analyze communication logs and project status updates within the CRM, flagging projects that exhibit signs of stakeholder misalignment.

suggestions.title: Sponsor Retention & Engagement Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Create a data visualization suite using PowerBI or Tableau that tracks longitudinal sponsor engagement data, helping the college identify which industries provide the most successful long-term partnerships.

project_key: legacy:UC-27|Spring 2018|iot voice and gesture control system

title: What's Next?! Travel App
authors: Delia Sanders, Shivani Patel, Kevin Benitez Santos, Aniruddh Kathiriya, Dhruv Patel
abstract: Travel app allowing users to create custom activity lists with a custom map showing optimal routes to selected locations.
topics: Mobile Development, Travel, Maps, Location Services

suggestions.title: Multi-Objective Route Optimization
suggestions.desc: Implement a Genetic Algorithm to optimize travel routes based on multiple constraints like 'scenic beauty,' 'low traffic,' and 'minimal walking.' Use the Google Places API to fetch 'busyness' data to help users avoid crowds.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Group Itinerary Synchronization
suggestions.desc: Add a collaborative feature using WebSockets (Socket.io) that allows groups of travelers to vote on activities and see each other's live locations on the shared map. This ensures everyone stays on the 'What's Next' schedule.

suggestions.title: Offline-First Exploration Mode
suggestions.desc: Integrate MapBox offline maps and SQLite local storage to allow users to navigate their custom lists in areas with no cellular data. Build a sync engine that pushes updates to the cloud once a connection is re-established.

suggestions.title: Sentiment-Based Activity Discovery
suggestions.desc: Develop a recommendation engine that scrapes TripAdvisor and Yelp reviews and uses BERT-based NLP to categorize locations by 'vibe' (e.g., 'cozy,' 'chaotic,' 'historic'). Match these to the user's current mood input.

title: Carbon Chain
authors: John Price, Nick Miller, Connor Armstrong, Alex Turner, Brandon Gonzalez
abstract: Blockchain loyalty point program interfacing with Verifone Carbon POS platform, built as an API with NodeJS/Express backend on AWS.
topics: Blockchain, Mobile Development, Loyalty Programs, AWS

suggestions.title: Layer 2 Scalability with Polygon
suggestions.desc: Migrate the loyalty program to a Layer 2 blockchain like Polygon to minimize transaction fees (gas) for small point redemptions. Implement an Ethers.js frontend to allow users to view their token balance in real-time.

suggestions.title: Tokenized Store Governance (DAO)
suggestions.desc: Implement a Decentralized Autonomous Organization (DAO) structure where loyalty point holders can vote on store decisions, such as 'Flavor of the Month' or 'Extended Hours.' Use Snapshot.org for off-chain voting to save costs.

suggestions.title: Merchant Analytics and Retention Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Build a React-based dashboard for business owners that visualizes token velocity and customer retention metrics pulled from the AWS backend. Use AWS QuickSight to generate automated reports on loyalty program ROI.

suggestions.title: Zero-Knowledge Proofs for Privacy
suggestions.desc: Use SnarkyJS to implement Zero-Knowledge Proofs, allowing customers to prove they have reached a specific loyalty tier for a discount without revealing their entire purchase history or identity to the merchant.

title: Smart Alarm Clock
authors: Jacob Stewart, Kurt Floyd
abstract: Dynamic alarm clock that adjusts wake time based on weather and traffic, controls lighting, tracks sleep patterns.
topics: IoT, Smart Home, Weather, Sleep Tracking

suggestions.title: Privacy-First Local Voice Interface
suggestions.desc: Integrate a local Large Language Model (LLM) using llama.cpp on a Raspberry Pi 5 to allow users to set alarms and check weather via voice without sending data to the cloud. Use Whisper for high-accuracy local speech-to-text.

suggestions.title: Circadian Lighting and Blind Control
suggestions.desc: Connect the alarm clock to the Philips Hue API and Zigbee-controlled smart blinds to create a 'natural sunrise' effect. The system should gradually increase light intensity and open blinds 20 minutes before the calculated wake time.

suggestions.title: Smart Calendar Buffer Logic
suggestions.desc: Connect to Google/Outlook Calendar APIs to analyze the user's first meeting location. The clock should calculate commute times using the TomTom Traffic API and automatically wake the user earlier if an accident is detected on their route.

suggestions.title: Non-Contact Sleep Quality Analysis
suggestions.desc: Incorporate a 24GHz mmWave radar sensor to track respiration and body movement during sleep without the need for wearables. Use these patterns to trigger the alarm specifically during the 'Light Sleep' phase to minimize grogginess.

title: FMSnote
authors: Mohamed Kabad, Shainu Vazhathil, Faisal Abdulkadir
abstract: Android application merging Reminders, Notes, and To-Do apps into one unified productivity tool for students.

suggestions.title: Context-Aware Smart Suggestions
suggestions.desc: Use Android’s Activity Recognition and Geofencing APIs to suggest specific notes or tasks based on location. For example, the app could surface a 'Grocery List' when the user is near a supermarket or 'Class Notes' when at a lecture hall.

suggestions.title: Collaborative 'Study Group' Canvases
suggestions.desc: Implement a shared canvas feature using Firebase Realtime Database that allows multiple students to draw, annotate, and brainstorm on the same note simultaneously. Add a 'Conflict Resolution' algorithm for overlapping edits.

suggestions.title: Automated Spaced Repetition (Anki) Sync
suggestions.desc: Develop a module that uses NLP to identify key concepts in a student's notes and automatically converts them into flashcards. Use an API to sync these directly to Anki or a built-in Spaced Repetition System (SRS) for exam prep.

suggestions.title: AI-Driven Action Item Extraction
suggestions.desc: Integrate the OpenAI API to analyze long voice-to-text notes and automatically extract action items into the To-Do list section. The app should also summarize long lectures into concise bullet points for quick review.

title: Rare Event Detection
authors: Rakeem Durand, Karim Rattani, Ihsan Hashem, Roberta Beaulieu, Thomas Fuller
abstract: Algorithm to detect abnormal activity such as robbery or accidents in surveillance camera footage and alert authorities.
topics: Computer Vision, Machine Learning, Surveillance, Anomaly Detection

suggestions.title: Skeleton-Based Pose Recognition
suggestions.desc: Improve detection accuracy by integrating MediaPipe to track human skeletons. This allows the system to distinguish between a person 'falling' (accident) and a person 'crouching' (potentially suspicious) based on joint angles and velocity.

suggestions.title: Automated Privacy Redaction Engine
suggestions.desc: Build a pipeline that uses FaceNet to detect and blur the faces of bystanders in the video feed before the footage is sent to authorities. This ensures GDPR compliance and protects the privacy of non-involved individuals.

suggestions.title: Industrial Safety and PPE Compliance
suggestions.desc: Repurpose the rare event algorithm to detect safety violations in construction or manufacturing settings. Train the model to identify 'unworn hard hats' or 'forklift near-misses' and alert floor managers in real-time.

suggestions.title: Explainable AI (XAI) for False Alarm Reduction
suggestions.desc: Implement Grad-CAM (Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping) to highlight the specific pixels or motion paths that triggered an alert. This helps security personnel understand why an event was flagged as 'abnormal' to reduce system distrust.

title: Virtual Education
authors: Sourav Debnath, Adi Vrskic, Andrea Pinardi, Jeffrey Dennis, Marc Wise
abstract: We are developing a web app to recognize and rate political bias in online journalism using artificial intelligence. All human writing inherently contains bias ,however bias is less harmful if it is transparent to the reader because they can now make informed decisions about what they read. We've collected articles and and reactions to them from online sources, and then used Neural Networks trained for natural language processing to determine bias. The project can predict bias labels on a news articles with 82% accuracy.Advisors(s): Reza Meimandi Parizi - course instructor Asher Nuckolls - project ownerTopic(s): Artificial IntelligenceSWE4724

suggestions.title: Browser Extension Bias Overlay
suggestions.desc: Develop a Chrome/Firefox extension that uses the trained Neural Network to inject a 'Bias Meter' and 'Fact-Check Score' directly into the UI of major news sites. This provides immediate transparency as the user browses.

suggestions.title: Multimodal Visual Bias Detection
suggestions.desc: Extend the NLP model to include image analysis using CLIP (Contrastive Language-Image Pre-training). Analyze if the images used in an article (e.g., choice of flattering vs. unflattering photos) contribute to the overall political framing.

suggestions.title: Personalized 'Echo Chamber' Breaker
suggestions.desc: Create a recommendation engine that tracks a user’s reading history and deliberately suggests high-quality articles with the *opposite* bias. Use the existing classifier to ensure a diverse 'media diet' for the user.

suggestions.title: Media Landscape Analytics API
suggestions.desc: Package the classification engine as a REST API for journalists and researchers to track bias trends across thousands of articles during an election cycle. Build a dashboard that visualizes the 'polarization shift' of outlets over time.

title: Verifone Support WebApp
authors: Srija Kukkadapu, Ahmad Alissa, Dylan Clark, Kevin Burke, Josh Sisson
abstract: Customer support web application for Verifone with easy-to-use interface addressing common customer questions.

suggestions.title: Hybrid Combat System Engine
suggestions.desc: Refine the gameplay by building a seamless transition engine that switches from real-time movement to turn-based combat based on the player's proximity to 'Native' NPCs.

suggestions.title: Dynamic Procedural Dialogue Trees
suggestions.desc: Use a sentiment analysis library like NLTK to adjust NPC reactions and quest availability based on the player's cumulative history of cultural adaptation or resistance.

suggestions.title: Adaptive Soundtrack and Audio Cues
suggestions.desc: Implement an FMOD-driven audio system where the music's BPM, time signature, and instrumentation shift dynamically as the player chooses between real-time and turn-based actions.

suggestions.title: Language Acquisition Progression System
suggestions.desc: Develop a modular dialogue system where players 'unlock' translations of the foreign language by performing turn-based tasks, simulating the cognitive load of real-world language learning.

title: Visualization of Big Data with AR
authors: Sean Saffan, Ronald Brooks, Victor Orellana, Matt Alyward
abstract: AR application for Microsoft HoloLens visualizing streamed network traffic data in multiple 3D models, measuring effectiveness experimentally.
topics: Augmented Reality, Big Data, Data Visualization, HoloLens

suggestions.title: Real-Time Cyber Attack Visualization
suggestions.desc: Connect the HoloLens app to a live Snort IDS (Intrusion Detection System) feed. Represent different types of network attacks, like SQL injections or DDoS, as distinct 3D visual effects (e.g., color-coded pulses or clusters) within the traffic model.

suggestions.title: Server Room 'Digital Twin' Mapping
suggestions.desc: Use Vuforia Image Targets to anchor the AR traffic visualization to physical server racks. This allows network admins to walk through a data center and see 'X-ray' visualizations of the data flowing into specific physical hardware.

suggestions.title: Collaborative SOC Investigation Mode
suggestions.desc: Leverage the Mixed Reality Toolkit (MRTK) to allow multiple HoloLens users to enter a shared session. Security teams can then 'touch' and manipulate the same 3D traffic graph simultaneously to identify the source of a breach.

suggestions.title: Mobile AR Wi-Fi Heatmapper
suggestions.desc: Adapt the technology for ARCore/ARKit on smartphones to help home users visualize their Wi-Fi signal strength. Map signal data from the phone's sensors into a 3D 'fog' that clears in areas with strong connectivity.

project_key: legacy:UC-41|Spring 2018|visualization of big data with ar

title: Bibliography Management Tool
authors: William Patrick, Randolph Wilson, Michael Lin, Avery Snyder, Roberto Recinos
abstract: For this project, our group aimed to create an intelligent chat bot that was accessible through the web client interface. Aletheianomous, our chat bot, was designed to provide accurate information ethically, aligned with human values. When applicable, the AI would offer the user citations to support its responses. For the back-end, a virtual machine (VM) server in AWS with access to the Graphics Processing Unit (GPU) would run three types of models: Sentence Separation Model, Search Query Extractor Model, and the Response Model. The front-end server using Microsoft Azure generates the web page for the user, exchanges chat data with the Microsoft SQL server, and communicates with the back-end server via REST APIs to request the chat bot to respond to user input. By using this architecture, the overall quality of our product exceeded our standards.
topics: Web Development, Academic Tools, Citation Management

suggestions.title: Evidence-Grounded Hallucination Detection
suggestions.desc: Implement a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipeline that cross-references AI responses against a trusted corpus like PubMed or arXiv to assign a 'Veracity Score' to every generated statement.

suggestions.title: Quantized Edge-Device Deployment
suggestions.desc: Optimize the heavy GPU models using 4-bit quantization (GGUF format) and Llama.cpp to allow the chatbot to run locally on consumer-grade hardware without requiring expensive AWS GPU instances.

suggestions.title: Value-Aligned Content Moderation Layer
suggestions.desc: Create a secondary transformer-based classification model that acts as a 'moral gatekeeper' to detect and filter prompts that attempt to bypass the AI's ethical guidelines or human-value alignment.

suggestions.title: Enterprise Knowledge-Base API
suggestions.desc: Transform the chatbot into a SaaS-ready API that allows corporate clients to upload private documentation and deploy a version of Aletheianomous trained exclusively on their internal data.

title: Smart Baby Monitor
authors: Taylor Bounds, Jayce Chen, Jordan Mateen, William Simmons, Bo Wang, Michael Wong
abstract: Baby monitor with Android application using facial recognition to detect when the baby is waking up and alert parents.
topics: Computer Vision, IoT, Mobile Development, Facial Recognition

suggestions.title: On-Device Edge Inference Optimization
suggestions.desc: Port the facial recognition model to TensorFlow Lite to run locally on an ESP32-CAM or Raspberry Pi. This eliminates the need for cloud processing, ensuring parent privacy and reducing alert latency to sub-second speeds.

suggestions.title: Eulerian Video Magnification for Vitals
suggestions.desc: Implement Eulerian Video Magnification (EVM) to detect the baby's pulse and respiration rate by amplifying subtle skin color changes and chest movements. This turns a standard camera into a non-contact medical grade monitor.

suggestions.title: Smart Home IoT Orchestration
suggestions.desc: Integrate the monitor with the Matter or MQTT protocol to trigger smart home events. When the baby is detected waking up, the system could automatically dim lights, adjust the thermostat, or play soothing white noise through smart speakers.

suggestions.title: Sleep Hygiene Analytics Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Develop a longitudinal data visualization suite that logs waking/sleeping patterns over weeks. Use this data to provide parents with AI-generated insights on sleep quality and suggest optimal nap times based on historical trends.

title: KSU Capstone 15: SCADA Networks
authors: Allen McCrory, Zivalus Nixon, Andrew Connelly, Chad Womble, Ashad Mahmood
abstract: Analysis of SCADA network architecture and vulnerabilities in critical infrastructure: water, energy, communications, transportation, and emergency management.
topics: Cybersecurity, SCADA, Critical Infrastructure, Network Security

suggestions.title: SCADA Honeypot Deployment
suggestions.desc: Build a high-interaction honeypot using Conpot to mimic a regional water treatment facility's Modbus/TCP interfaces. Capture and analyze real-world exploit attempts to create a localized threat intelligence report.

suggestions.title: PLC Traffic Fingerprinting
suggestions.desc: Apply machine learning (Random Forest or LSTM) to SCADA network captures using Scapy to identify 'normal' command sequences. Create a system that alerts operators when anomalous PLC logic updates or unauthorized read/write commands occur.

suggestions.title: Virtual Air-Gap Simulation
suggestions.desc: Develop a network simulation using Mininet-WiFi to test the efficacy of unidirectional gateways (Data Diodes). Measure the throughput and latency impact of security hardware on critical emergency management communication channels.

suggestions.title: Automated NIST 800-82 Compliance Auditor
suggestions.desc: Build a Python-based tool that scans SCADA network configurations and firewall rules to verify compliance with NIST SP 800-82 standards. The tool should generate a 'Readiness Score' and actionable remediation steps for infrastructure managers.

title: Moodle
authors: Courtney Walker, Jessica Herr, Brian Luong, Adam Lee, Doutimi Azu
abstract: Research and implementation of Moodle LMS reporting and badge tools as an alternative to D2L for KSU Distance Learning Center.

suggestions.title: Blockchain-Verified Digital Credentials
suggestions.desc: Integrate a private Ethereum or Polygon-based smart contract to issue badges as soulbound NFTs. This allows students to have verifiable, immutable proof of their skills that can be displayed on external professional profiles.

suggestions.title: Predictive Student Attrition Modeling
suggestions.desc: Utilize Moodle’s log data and engagement metrics from the reporting tools to build a predictive model in R. The system should flag students who are likely to drop out or fail based on low badge attainment and infrequent login activity.

suggestions.title: Natural Language Querying for LMS Reports
suggestions.desc: Implement a GPT-4 powered middleware that allows instructors to query student performance data using natural language. Instructors can ask, 'Which students struggled with Chapter 4 but have earned the Advanced Python badge?'

suggestions.title: Gamified Peer-to-Peer Endorsement System
suggestions.desc: Extend the badge system to allow students to 'endorse' peers for soft skills like collaboration and leadership within forum discussions. Create a leaderboard and tiered reward system to drive engagement in distance learning environments.

title: CCSE Internship Video Blog
authors: Juan Castro, Isaura Romero, Joe Black, Alexander Lam, Evgeniya Koganitskaya
abstract: Website for internship students to post video blogs and presentations sharing their internship experiences.

suggestions.title: NLP-Based Video Search & Transcription
suggestions.desc: Integrate the OpenAI Whisper API to automatically transcribe internship vlogs. Build a search engine that allows students to find specific advice or tech mentions (e.g., 'Docker', 'Agile') at specific timestamps within the videos.

suggestions.title: Sentiment-Based Company Culture Mapping
suggestions.desc: Apply sentiment analysis to the vlog transcripts to generate a 'Culture Map.' This visualization would rank internship providers based on student-reported stress levels, mentorship quality, and learning satisfaction.

suggestions.title: Geospatial Internship Exploration Portal
suggestions.desc: Develop an interactive Mapbox interface where vlogs are pinned to their corporate headquarters. Users can explore internship opportunities by clicking through different cities and viewing local student experiences and housing tips.

suggestions.title: Recruiter-Facing Talent Showcase
suggestions.desc: Build a restricted dashboard for industry partners that allows them to view 'Highlight Reels' of student vloggers. Include a CRM-style feature where recruiters can tag and contact students for full-time roles based on their internship presentations.

title: How Immigration Affects Economics
authors: Jack Matthews, Codi Sordelet, Jinhee Kim, Ebele Orakpo, Mahad Ahmed
abstract: Data visualizations exploring H1B visa statistics and economic indicators to analyze how immigration affects the economy.
topics: Data Visualization, Economics, Immigration, Data Analytics

suggestions.title: Interactive Policy Impact Simulator
suggestions.desc: Create a D3.js dashboard where users can adjust variables like H1B visa caps or minimum wage thresholds. Use historical correlation data to simulate and visualize the potential impact on local housing prices and GDP growth.

suggestions.title: LCA Data Cross-Referencing Engine
suggestions.desc: Merge Department of Labor (DOL) Labor Condition Application (LCA) data with Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) data. Conduct a regression analysis to determine if high densities of H1B workers in specific ZIP codes correlate with wage growth or stagnation for native workers.

suggestions.title: Industry-Specific Specialized Talent Heatmap
suggestions.desc: Develop a visualization focusing on 'Niche Skills' (e.g., Quantum Computing, Bioinformatics) to show how immigration fills specific technical gaps. Use Plotly to map these gaps against university graduation rates in those fields.

suggestions.title: Green Card Sponsorship Prediction Model
suggestions.desc: Build a machine learning model using historical PERM data to predict the likelihood of a company sponsoring a Green Card for various job titles. This tool would help international students choose employers with high sponsorship rates.

title: IT Alumni Database ETL System
authors: Ricky Parks, Vy Duong, Zack Downing, Desiree Smokes, Hang Yu
abstract: Automated ETL system to extract KSU IT alumni career data from LinkedIn and load into a relational database.

suggestions.title: Neo4j Career Path Visualization
suggestions.desc: Migrate the alumni data into a Neo4j Graph Database to visualize 'Career Trajectories.' Users could see common paths (e.g., 'Help Desk -> SysAdmin -> Cloud Architect') and which companies serve as the largest hubs for KSU graduates.

suggestions.title: Curriculum Skills Gap Analysis Engine
suggestions.desc: Extract 'Skills' and 'Tools' from alumni profiles and compare them against current KSU IT syllabi using NLP. Generate a report highlighting emerging technologies (like Snowflake or Kubernetes) that alumni are using but aren't yet in the curriculum.

suggestions.title: AI-Driven Alumni Mentor Matchmaker
suggestions.desc: Develop a recommendation engine that matches current students with alumni based on shared interests and career goals. Use cosine similarity to find the 'closest' alumni profile to a student's desired career path.

suggestions.title: Dynamic Salary Benchmarking Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Enrich the LinkedIn data by integrating the Glassdoor API to estimate salary ranges for the roles held by alumni. This provides prospective students with a data-driven outlook on the ROI of different IT concentrations.

title: Cyber Actor Profiles
authors: Ramiz Ramic, Sabrina Buckholdt, Kevin Tillack, Hao Pham, Nick Kennelly
abstract: Identify an open source e-commerce website solution and install it on a provided web server. Develop a comprehensive security program to protect assigned web server such as security policy, tools can be used to monitor the network traffics and detect intrusion. Conduct vulnerability analysis on the assigned web server. Advisors(s): Professor Lei Li Topic(s): Artificial Intelligence IT 4983

suggestions.title: Self-Healing WAF Implementation
suggestions.desc: Deploy a ModSecurity Web Application Firewall (WAF) and write a Python script that parses logs to automatically generate new filter rules. If an IP exhibits SQL injection patterns, the system should dynamically update firewall blocks in real-time.

suggestions.title: TTP Behavioral Profiling System
suggestions.desc: Develop a framework to categorize attackers based on Tactics, Techniques, and Procedures (TTPs). Use the captured network traffic to map 'cyber actor' signatures against the MITRE ATT&CK framework for better attribution.

suggestions.title: Automated Security Chaos Engineering
suggestions.desc: Build a tool that executes controlled 'attack simulations' (like credential stuffing or XSS) against the e-commerce server. Measure the 'Time to Detection' and 'Time to Mitigation' to stress-test the effectiveness of the security policy.

suggestions.title: Zero-Trust Access Architecture
suggestions.desc: Refactor the server environment to a Zero-Trust model using Cloudflare Access or Boundary. This ensures that even with a vulnerable application, no user has access to the underlying server resources without multi-factor identity verification.

title: Cybriant SOC Portal
authors: Ali Abdulelah, Cameron Fite, Zachary Auzenne, Brett Warner, Francisco Escalante, Arjumand Fatima
abstract: Donation For Dummies is a VR game designed to help people feel more relaxed and informed when donating blood. It consists of a theater room where a video plays explaining the process as well as what to do before and after donating. For people wanting a distraction, we have an arcade where players can enjoy minesweeper, matching, or solitaire. For those wishing to relax, we have an art gallery where players can virtually walk around and look at various pieces of art. The more relaxed player that do not wish to move around the game world can instead choose to enjoy the scenery from our meditation deck overlooking a valley. Anyone looking to become more informed about the process can make their way to the information center.
topics: Cybersecurity, SOC, Task Management, Enterprise Software

suggestions.title: Haptic Desensitization Training
suggestions.desc: Integrate haptic feedback patterns into VR controllers that simulate the sensation of the blood pressure cuff and the precise moment of needle entry to help users desensitize their physical phobias.

suggestions.title: Phlebotomist Simulation Mode
suggestions.desc: Add a 'Technician View' where the user acts as the nurse, learning to identify ideal vein locations and practicing the hygiene protocols required for a safe donation process.

suggestions.title: Bio-Feedback Adaptive Environments
suggestions.desc: Connect the VR experience to a heart rate monitor (e.g., Polar H10) to dynamically change the art gallery's colors and music tempo based on the user's real-time stress and anxiety levels.

suggestions.title: Modular Clinical Waiting-Room Framework
suggestions.desc: Generalize the VR arcade and gallery architecture into a template that can be easily skinned for other medical procedures, such as pediatric vaccinations or chemotherapy sessions.

title: M.A.I.D.A.S
authors: Andre Maxwell, Robert Torres, Gavin Smith, Adam Britton, Alex Creilson, Mariel Mcneil
abstract: Drone-based trailer yard auditing system for Manhattan Associates using camera to capture trailer locations and automate yard management.

suggestions.title: YOLOv8 Edge Optimization for Jetson Nano
suggestions.desc: Optimize the trailer detection model to run on-board a drone's Jetson Nano using TensorRT. This allows for real-time yard auditing without the latency of streaming 4K video to a base station.

suggestions.title: Thermal Leak & Refrigeration Monitoring
suggestions.desc: Integrate thermal imaging data to detect refrigeration unit (reefer) failures on trailers. Develop a computer vision model that flags trailers with irregular heat signatures, preventing food spoilage in the logistics chain.

suggestions.title: Swarm-Based Yard Partitioning
suggestions.desc: Implement a drone swarm coordination protocol using ROS (Robot Operating System). Allow multiple drones to divide a massive trailer yard into sectors, dramatically reducing the time required for a full inventory audit.

suggestions.title: Digital Twin Yard Integration
suggestions.desc: Sync the drone's spatial data with a 3D Unity-based Digital Twin. This allows yard managers to perform a 'virtual walkthrough' of the yard from their office, seeing the exact placement and status of every trailer in a 3D interface.

title: Windows Forensics Suite
authors: Jessica Camilien, Joseph Blanco, David Klein, Justice Collier
abstract: Collection of tools pulling metadata from Windows NTFS system locations for forensic investigation on Windows 8 and 10.

suggestions.title: Unified Forensic Timeline Visualizer
suggestions.desc: Build an Electron-based GUI that aggregates metadata from $MFT, Prefetch, and Shimcache into a single chronological view. Use D3.js to create a visual timeline that highlights suspicious bursts of file activity or system changes.

suggestions.title: Anti-Forensics Timestomp Detector
suggestions.desc: Develop an algorithm that identifies 'Timestomping' by comparing NTFS $Standard_Information attributes with $File_Name attributes. Flag discrepancies that indicate a user has manually altered file creation or modification dates.

suggestions.title: Cloud-Sync Artifact Recovery
suggestions.desc: Extend the suite to analyze local database files (SQLite) used by OneDrive and Dropbox. This allows investigators to track which files were deleted locally but might still exist in the cloud, or vice versa.

suggestions.title: Volatile Memory Correlation Engine
suggestions.desc: Integrate the suite with the Volatility framework to correlate NTFS file artifacts with running processes. For example, if a suspicious file is found in the $MFT, the tool should search RAM dumps for active handles to that file.

title: Roommates40Plus.com

suggestions.title: Stripe Identity Verification Integration
suggestions.desc: Implement a robust KYC (Know Your Customer) layer using Stripe Identity or Persona. This ensures that every user has a verified government ID on file, significantly increasing safety for the 40+ demographic.

suggestions.title: Accessibility-First UI/UX Refactor
suggestions.desc: Conduct a UX audit focused on age-related vision and motor challenges. Refactor the front-end using high-contrast themes, scalable typography, and ARIA-compliant screen reader support to ensure maximum usability for older adults.

suggestions.title: Proximity-Based Amenity Mapping

title: Statistical Analysis of NYC Data
authors: Archana Joshi
abstract: This project outlines the development of a bespoke Customer Relationship Management (CRM) system specifically designed for the College of Computer and Software Engineering (CCSE). The initiative aims to centralize customer information into a unified repository, thereby enhancing the confidentiality, management, and optimization of data and communication processes within the college. The CRM system will integrate features for detailed profiles, communication optimization, complex workflow management, document repository, and data migration to ensure efficiency and data integrity. It will also facilitate seamless interaction with Microsoft 365 and Outlook, supporting the college's operational needs and maintaining its commitment to excellence in education and collaboration. The project is set to be executed in three major milestones, involving product selection, system implementation, user testing, and final deployment, with the end goal of improving overall customer relationship management. The team, led by Venkatesh Alla, alongside team members Keerthi Nannapaneni and Vinay Kumar Rapolu, under the guidance of project owners Alla Kemelmakher and Nasiya Sharif, and advisor Ying Xie, plans to use a dedicated project management tool, Jira, for collaboration and task tracking. This CRM initiative not only aims to meet the specific needs of the CCSE but also to set a benchmark for operational efficiency and data management in educational institutions.

suggestions.title: Automated Student Lifecycle Integration via APIs
suggestions.desc: Connect the CRM to university-standard data sources like the Banner API or DegreeWorks. Automate the population of student profiles so that CCSE advisors can see real-time GPA updates and course completion status directly within the CRM without manual data entry.

suggestions.title: AI-Powered 'At-Risk' Student Identification System
suggestions.desc: Build a machine learning module that analyzes communication frequency and academic data within the CRM. Train a model to flag students who are falling behind or disengaging, triggering an automated 'Workflow Management' task for advisors to reach out for an intervention.

suggestions.title: Smart Matchmaking for Internships and Employers
suggestions.desc: Add a 'Corporate Relations' module to the CRM that stores job descriptions from CCSE partners. Use a basic NLP matching algorithm to suggest the top 5 students for a specific internship based on their profile data, skills, and interests tracked in the system.

suggestions.title: Longitudinal Career Outcome Analysis Dashboard
