title: Training a Neural Network to Walk
authors: Oscar Garcia
abstract: Mental health is a critical aspect of our overall well-being. Mental illness refers to conditions that impact an individual's psychological state, resulting in considerable distress, and limitations in functioning day-to-day tasks. Due to the progress of technology, social media has merged as the platform, for individuals to share their thoughts and emotions. The psychological state of individuals can be accessed with the help of data from these platforms. However, it is challenging for conventional machine learning models to analyze the diverse linguistic contexts of social media data. In this work, we propose a novel attention-driven deep framework to overcome these challenges. Our proposed framework utilizes multi-level (word, sentence, and document) data to identify the causes behind mental illness. The efficacy and effectiveness of our proposed model are shown by extensive evaluation on Reddit data. The insights from this research deepen our understanding of different factors behind mental illness and would aid mental health professionals in formulating effective interventions.
topics: Machine Learning, Neural Networks, Reinforcement Learning, AI

suggestions.title: Early-Warning Content Moderation API
suggestions.desc: Package the attention-driven framework into a REST API for social media platforms. The tool would analyze user-generated content in real-time to flag 'at-risk' behavior for human moderators, providing them with the specific 'attention weights' (reasons) to help them connect users with mental health support.

suggestions.title: Temporal Mood-Shift Tracking for Longitudinal Analysis
suggestions.desc: Extend the framework to analyze a user's post history over months rather than isolated posts. Build a visualization tool that maps how specific causes (e.g., job loss, social isolation) correlate with the progression of mental illness symptoms over time, helping clinicians understand the 'trigger-to-symptom' timeline.

suggestions.title: Vision-Text Attention Fusion for Multi-Modal Detection
suggestions.desc: Incorporate image analysis (using Vision Transformers) to analyze the photos a user posts alongside their text. The model could then identify discrepancies or reinforcements (e.g., dark imagery paired with isolating language) to increase the accuracy of mental illness cause detection.

suggestions.title: LLM-Augmented Synthetic Dataset Generator
suggestions.desc: Use Large Language Models (LLMs) like Llama-3 to generate high-quality, synthetic social media posts for rare or underrepresented mental health conditions. This will help balance the training dataset and improve the attention framework's ability to recognize less common psychological distress markers.

title: R-CNN for Gender Identification
authors: Rehnuma Afrin
abstract: In this demonstration, we propose a framework for exploring, identifying, and matching repeated behavioral health keywords in first-responder reports to the current set of Behavioral Health Index Terms provided by subject matter experts (SMEs). The tool incorporates behavioral health-related keywords and has a Graphical User Interface (GUI) that allows non-technical users to explore and analyze 911 first-responder reports. We utilized an inverted index, best-matching (BM25), and plain-text searching algorithms to match keywords in first-responder reports. This tool provides a comprehensive approach to report analysis by identifying indicators of mental health disorders and taking into account the assessments of humanities and social science professionals
topics: Deep Learning, Computer Vision, Gender Classification, R-CNN

suggestions.title: 911 Dispatcher Real-Time Transcription Overlay
suggestions.desc: Integrate the framework with a live speech-to-text API (like Deepgram). Build an overlay for emergency dispatchers that highlights behavioral health keywords in real-time as they talk to a caller, automatically suggesting relevant Crisis Intervention Team (CIT) protocols based on the matched Index Terms.

suggestions.title: Predictive Behavioral Health Hotspot Mapping
suggestions.desc: Use the matched keyword data to generate a geospatial heatmap of behavioral health calls across a city. By correlating this with local resource availability, city planners can identify 'resource deserts' where first responders are frequently encountering mental health crises but have no nearby clinics for referral.

suggestions.title: Semantic Expansion via Transformer-Based Embeddings
suggestions.desc: Upgrade the BM25 search logic with semantic embeddings (using Sentence-BERT). This allows the framework to match reports based on 'meaning' rather than just exact keywords, catching nuanced descriptions of behavior that may not use the exact SME-provided terms but describe the same clinical symptoms.

suggestions.title: Automated SME Feedback Loop for Index Refinement
suggestions.desc: Build a 'Human-in-the-Loop' interface where subject matter experts can review 'near-miss' keyword matches. The system uses their corrections to fine-tune the inverted index weights automatically, ensuring the tool evolves as behavioral health terminology and field slang change over time.

title: Deep Embedding Kernel

suggestions.title: Low-Data Financial Fraud Detection Engine
suggestions.desc: Package the DEK model into a specialized library for financial institutions facing 'cold-start' problems. Focus the embedding on high-dimensional transaction data to detect anomalies where traditional deep learning fails due to a lack of labeled fraudulent examples.

similar: legacy:GR-08|Spring 2016|gender/age classification from facial image using deep learning
legacy:GR-08|Fall 2016|gender/age classification from facial image using deep learning

title: Fault Detection in IoT using Deep Learning
authors: Sergiu Buciumas
abstract: LSTM-based deep learning approach for fault detection in industrial IoT systems using multivariate time series data from a gasoil plant.
topics: IoT, Deep Learning, LSTM, Anomaly Detection, Cybersecurity

suggestions.title: Transformer-Based Time Series Attention
suggestions.desc: Replace the LSTM architecture with a Temporal Fusion Transformer (TFT) to better capture long-range dependencies in the gasoil plant data. Use the attention weights to create a 'root cause' visualization that shows which specific sensor inputs contributed most to a detected fault.

suggestions.title: Industrial IoT Predictive Maintenance Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Develop a React-based monitoring dashboard that connects to the LSTM backend via WebSockets. Integrate an 'Estimated Time to Failure' (ETTF) metric based on the anomaly score's rate of change, allowing plant operators to schedule repairs before a total shutdown occurs.

suggestions.title: Federated Learning for Cross-Plant Fault Detection
suggestions.desc: Simulate a federated learning environment using Flower.dev where multiple gasoil plants can train a shared LSTM model without sharing their proprietary multivariate raw data, improving the model's robustness to rare fault types.

suggestions.title: Wearable Health Monitoring for Arrhythmia
suggestions.desc: Adapt the gasoil plant fault detection logic to human physiological data. Train the LSTM on multivariate time series from wearable sensors (ECG, SpO2, Accelerometer) to detect cardiac anomalies or falls in elderly patients in real-time.

similar: legacy:UC-06|Spring 2020|chest x-ray disease detection w/ dl
project_key: legacy:GR-27|Spring 2018|fault detection in iot using deep learning

title: Comparison of Forecasting Methods
authors: Shashank Hebbar
abstract: Comparison of LSTM Neural Networks with traditional time series methods for forecasting Wikipedia web traffic.
topics: Machine Learning, LSTM, Time Series, Forecasting

suggestions.title: Hybrid Prophet-LSTM Residual Modeling
suggestions.desc: Combine Facebook’s Prophet for seasonal trend decomposition with an LSTM to model the non-linear residuals. Test this hybrid approach against the existing Wikipedia dataset to see if it reduces the error rate during high-volatility events like breaking news cycles.

suggestions.title: Predictive CDN Edge Caching Strategy
suggestions.desc: Use the web traffic forecasts to build a simulated Content Delivery Network (CDN) controller. The controller should pre-cache Wikipedia articles in specific geographical regions based on the predicted traffic spikes, measuring the improvement in simulated latency.

suggestions.title: Interpretable Forecasting with SHAP Values
suggestions.desc: Apply SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) to the LSTM model to understand why certain traffic spikes are predicted. Determine if the model is relying on calendar features (holidays) or historical momentum, and use these insights to prune irrelevant input features.

suggestions.title: Retail Inventory Optimization Engine
suggestions.desc: Pivot the forecasting logic from web traffic to product demand forecasting for small e-commerce businesses. Build a tool that imports Shopify CSV data and uses the LSTM-based comparison logic to suggest optimal restocking quantities for the next 30 days.

title: Self-Schooling Tablet
authors: Pauline Sawadogo
abstract: Self-Schooling Tablet designed for out-of-school children as a learning support device with sand tray, whiteboard, and video lessons.

suggestions.title: Offline LLM Tutor with Quantized Models
suggestions.desc: Integrate a small-parameter Large Language Model (like Phi-2 or Llama-3-8B) quantized for mobile. Build a voice-to-text interface that allows children to ask questions about the video lessons without needing an internet connection.

suggestions.title: Handwriting Assessment via Stroke Analysis
suggestions.desc: Develop a computer vision module for the 'sand tray' feature that analyzes the child's finger or stylus strokes. Use a lightweight CNN to give real-time feedback on letter formation and track literacy progress over time.

suggestions.title: Gamified Adaptive Learning Path
suggestions.desc: Create a software layer that adjusts the difficulty of the video lessons and whiteboard activities based on the child's performance. Use a Reinforcement Learning agent to maximize student engagement by offering rewards (digital badges) for completing challenging tasks.

suggestions.title: Solar-Powered Ruggedization for Remote Use
suggestions.desc: Design a 3D-printable, ruggedized casing for the tablet that includes an integrated solar charging panel. Create a 'mesh networking' feature where multiple tablets can sync data and updates when one device occasionally reaches a high-connectivity zone.

title: Peak Shaving: Optimizing HVAC Cost
authors: Derek Martin
abstract: Simulation and predictive model for West Point Academy HVAC optimization during peak hours to reduce energy costs.

suggestions.title: Deep Reinforcement Learning for Real-Time Control
suggestions.desc: Replace the predictive simulation with a Deep Q-Network (DQN) that controls HVAC setpoints in real-time. Use the 'OpenAI Gym' environment to simulate the West Point building and train the agent to balance occupant comfort (CO2 and Temp) against peak energy prices.

suggestions.title: Campus-Wide Microgrid Load Balancing
suggestions.desc: Extend the peak shaving model to include other energy-intensive campus assets like electric vehicle (EV) charging stations and dorm lighting. Create a coordinated strategy where HVAC load is decreased when EV demand spikes to keep the total campus draw below a critical threshold.

suggestions.title: Commercial Smart Thermostat API for Rebates
suggestions.desc: Develop a SaaS wrapper for the optimization model that connects to smart thermostats (like Ecobee or Nest) via API. Target small business owners by automating their participation in 'Demand Response' programs, earning them utility rebates during peak hours.

suggestions.title: Occupancy Sensing via Thermal Imaging Fusion
suggestions.desc: Research the impact of real-time occupancy data on the peak shaving model. Integrate low-cost PIR or thermal sensors to feed 'human heat load' data into the HVAC simulation, allowing for more aggressive energy savings in empty zones.

title: Animal Detection Using R-CNN
authors: John Jajeh, Masood Abdul Salam
abstract: R-CNN with AlexNet pre-trained model in Caffe framework to detect ~400 animal species, deployed on Android for wildlife identification.
topics: Computer Vision, Deep Learning, Animal Detection, Android

suggestions.title: Mobile Inference Optimization with TensorFlow Lite
suggestions.desc: Migrate the project from the legacy Caffe framework to TensorFlow Lite. Apply 8-bit quantization and pruning to the AlexNet model to reduce the app's battery consumption and increase the frames-per-second (FPS) on mid-range Android devices.

suggestions.title: Anti-Poaching Acoustic-Visual Sensor Fusion
suggestions.desc: Combine the R-CNN detection with an audio classification model that detects gunshots or chainsaw sounds. Build a system intended for static Raspberry Pi 'forest sentinels' that only wakes up the camera and runs the R-CNN when suspicious audio is detected.

suggestions.title: Individual Animal Re-Identification (Re-ID)
suggestions.desc: Research the use of Siamese Networks to not only detect the species but identify individual animals (e.g., specific tigers based on stripe patterns). Use the 'Wild-Me' datasets to train a secondary model that triggers when a known individual is spotted.

suggestions.title: AR Digital Safari Guide for Tourism
suggestions.desc: Integrate the detection model with ARCore to overlay educational 'info-cards' in Augmented Reality when an animal is detected. Target wildlife parks to provide an interactive educational experience for visitors using their own smartphones.

title: MS Office Macro Malware Detection
authors: Ruth Bearden
abstract: Machine learning techniques to increase macro malware detection accuracy in Microsoft Office files.

suggestions.title: Static-Dynamic Hybrid Sandbox Analysis
suggestions.desc: Combine the current ML static analysis with a dynamic analysis phase. Use a headless Windows sandbox to execute the macro, monitor API calls for suspicious behavior (e.g., PowerShell execution), and use these behavioral logs as additional features for the ML model.

suggestions.title: Adversarial Robustness Testing against VBA Obfuscation
suggestions.desc: Build a tool that automatically generates obfuscated versions of known malware macros (using variable renaming, junk code, and string encryption). Test the current ML model against these variants to identify blind spots and retrain the model on adversarial examples.

suggestions.title: Outlook Plugin for Real-Time Attachment Scanning
suggestions.desc: Develop a VSTO Add-in for Microsoft Outlook that intercepts incoming .docm and .xlsm files. Send the macro code to a local ML inference engine and display a 'Risk Score' directly in the email header before the user clicks to open the file.

suggestions.title: Enterprise Macro Policy Audit Tool
suggestions.desc: Create a Python-based scanner for IT administrators that crawls company file shares and identifies legacy documents containing high-risk macros. Generate a security report that maps the detected threats to the MITRE ATT&CK framework for compliance auditing.

title: Interaction Humanoid Robots and Humans
authors: Joel Tedo, Ben Tamo
abstract: Investigation of interaction between UXA-90 humanoid robots and humans in collaborative environments using ANOVA statistical analysis.

suggestions.title: Natural Language Interface for UXA-90
suggestions.desc: Integrate OpenAI's Whisper (for speech-to-text) and a localized LLM to allow humans to give natural language instructions to the robot. Replace pre-programmed responses with a dynamic dialogue system that supports collaborative task solving.

suggestions.title: Biometric Trust Analysis using EEG and Heart Rate
suggestions.desc: Extend the ANOVA-based research by collecting objective physiological data from humans during robot interaction. Use EEG headsets to measure cognitive load and heart rate monitors to measure stress, correlating these with the existing subjective UXA-90 survey data.

suggestions.title: Robotic Physical Therapy Assistant
suggestions.desc: Adapt the UXA-90's collaborative logic to a healthcare setting. Use the robot's humanoid form to demonstrate specific exercises and use its camera system to provide feedback on the patient's posture and repetition count, logging progress for a human therapist.

suggestions.title: Telepresence Operator Suite with VR
suggestions.desc: Build a system that allows a remote human operator to 'see' through the UXA-90's eyes using a VR headset and control its arms via motion controllers. This could be marketed as a solution for remote experts to guide physical tasks in hazardous or retail environments.

title: EA-4X
authors: Sven Cowart, Brendan Draper, James Nick Kammerdiener
abstract: Machine learning-based system to predict trends in the FOREX market using historical data.

suggestions.title: Automated MetaTrader 5 Execution Engine
suggestions.desc: Bridge the ML prediction model with the MetaTrader 5 Python API to execute trades automatically based on confidence thresholds. Implement a risk management module that calculates position sizing based on the Kelly Criterion to protect capital.

suggestions.title: Cross-Asset Correlation Sentiment Analyzer
suggestions.desc: Extend the model to ingest real-time sentiment data from the X (Twitter) API and financial news feeds to adjust FOREX predictions. Map how crypto-market volatility or oil price shifts correlate with specific currency pairs like USD/CAD or BTC/USD.

suggestions.title: Temporal Fusion Transformer Benchmarking
suggestions.desc: Replace standard regression or LSTM models with Temporal Fusion Transformers (TFT) to better handle multi-horizon time series forecasting. Compare the TFT's attention mechanisms against the original model to see which better identifies 'black swan' market events.

suggestions.title: Walk-Forward Validation Framework
suggestions.desc: Build a robust backtesting suite using the Backtrader library that implements walk-forward optimization to prevent overfitting. Create a visualization dashboard that overlays predicted vs. actual price movements with drawdown statistics.

title: ADbC
authors: Chris Altamimi, Jeff Yu, Kelli Yeatman, Manaf Alhabbal, Tyler Wilson
abstract: Update to the current ADbC interactive web application with new features and improved functionality.

suggestions.title: SaaS Multi-Tenant Architecture Migration
suggestions.desc: Refactor the backend using AWS Lambda and Cognito to support a multi-tenant subscription model. Integrate Stripe for seat-based licensing, allowing different organizations to manage their own private data silos within the app.

suggestions.title: Offline-First PWA for Field Use
suggestions.desc: Convert the web application into a Progressive Web App (PWA) using Workbox for service workers. Implement IndexedDB for local data persistence, ensuring users in low-connectivity areas can continue interacting with the tool without data loss.

suggestions.title: User Behavioral Heatmap Analytics
suggestions.desc: Integrate a custom telemetry layer that tracks user click-paths and interaction times using a NoSQL database like MongoDB. Use this data to run A/B tests on UI components to scientifically determine which layouts maximize user task completion speed.

suggestions.title: Micro-Frontend Module Federation
suggestions.desc: Decompose the monolithic frontend into micro-frontends using Webpack Module Federation. This allows different student teams to independently develop and deploy new features (like a reporting dashboard or user management) without redeploying the entire app.

title: Equipment Utilization Tracker
authors: James Gowdy, Clay Cain, Nathaniel Velliquette, Joel Kamdem Teto
abstract: System to track equipment utilization, location, duration of use, and distance traveled, with Google Maps visualization.

suggestions.title: Predictive Failure Maintenance Engine
suggestions.desc: Incorporate vibration or temperature sensors into the IoT stack and use a Random Forest regressor to predict equipment failure before it occurs. Build an automated alert system that generates maintenance tickets in a CRM when health scores drop.

suggestions.title: Smart Geofencing for Theft Prevention
suggestions.desc: Implement a geofencing API that triggers immediate SMS alerts via Twilio if equipment moves outside of a defined job site boundary. Add a 'Stolen Mode' that increases GPS polling frequency to 5-second intervals for high-resolution recovery tracking.

suggestions.title: LoRaWAN Remote Connectivity Extension
suggestions.desc: Swap high-power cellular modules for LoRaWAN gateways to track equipment in remote construction sites where LTE is unavailable. Research and document the battery life improvements gained by using low-power wide-area networks compared to standard GPS/GSM.

suggestions.title: High-Density Deck.gl Visualization
suggestions.desc: Replace standard Google Maps markers with Deck.gl to handle thousands of concurrent equipment data points. Implement 'Trip Playback' features that allow managers to animate the historical movement of a specific machine over a 24-hour period.

title: KSU Lambda
authors: Mark Shaver, Erica Pantoja, Gabriel Kigundu, Cameron Campbell, Abdoul Dolo, Shawn Hutcherson
abstract: Refining and preparing the Lambda Machine distributed computing system for on-campus utilization at KSU.

suggestions.title: KSU Lambda-as-a-Service (LaaS) Portal
suggestions.desc: Build a web-based dashboard using React and OpenFaaS that allows students to upload Python scripts and execute them across the Lambda cluster. Implement a credit-based system to manage resource allocation among different student researchers.

suggestions.title: Distributed Neural Network Training Node
suggestions.desc: Configure the cluster to support PyTorch Distributed Data Parallel (DDP) so researchers can train large-scale AI models. Create a 'Shared Dataset' mount point using NFS or MinIO so all nodes can access training data with minimal latency.

suggestions.title: Comparative Orchestration Study: SLURM vs. K8s
suggestions.desc: Deploy both SLURM (High-Performance Computing) and Kubernetes (Cloud Native) on the Lambda hardware. Perform a comparative analysis of job scheduling efficiency and overhead to determine the best environment for KSU's specific academic workloads.

suggestions.title: NVIDIA Container Toolkit GPU Integration
suggestions.desc: Enable hardware acceleration across the cluster by configuring NVIDIA Docker and MIG (Multi-Instance GPU). This allows a single physical GPU to be partitioned among multiple student projects simultaneously, maximizing hardware utilization.

title: FaceSNATCH
authors: John Stanford, Kevin Vo, Heli Patel, Kayden Pham, Vimal Panchal
abstract: iOS application using facial recognition to identify human emotions from images based on facial expressions.
topics: iOS, Computer Vision, Emotion Detection, Facial Recognition

suggestions.title: Real-time Student Engagement Proctor
suggestions.desc: Develop a Mac/PC companion app that uses the FaceSNATCH model to monitor student focus during online exams. Use the detected emotions and gaze direction to provide a 'Focus Score' that helps instructors identify when course material is confusing.

suggestions.title: Accessibility Narrator for the Visually Impaired
suggestions.desc: Integrate the app with Apple's VoiceOver and haptic engine to describe the emotions of people in a room to visually impaired users. Create a 'Social Haptic' mode that vibrates the phone in different patterns depending on the speaker's detected mood.

suggestions.title: Algorithmic Bias Audit and Mitigation
suggestions.desc: Test the existing model against the FairFace or UTKFace datasets to identify accuracy gaps across different demographics. Re-train the model using synthetic data generation (GANs) to balance the training set and improve emotional recognition equity.

suggestions.title: CoreML Quantization and Edge Optimization
suggestions.desc: Optimize the model using CoreML's 4-bit quantization to reduce the app's footprint and battery drain. Implement 'On-Device Learning' using Create ML so the app can adapt to a specific user's unique facial expressions over time.

title: Pantry Buddy
authors: Joshua Auer, Matthew Brown, Alphonza Harris, Katelyn Marsala, Martynas Sedys
abstract: Mobile application to track grocery shopping trends and make predictions about future shopping needs.
topics: Mobile Development, Machine Learning, Smart Home

suggestions.title: Zero-Touch Barcode and OCR Scanner
suggestions.desc: Integrate Google ML Kit's Object Detection and Barcode Scanning to allow users to add items by simply pointing their camera at a grocery receipt or a pantry shelf. Build an 'Expiry Date' extractor using OCR to automatically set notification alerts.

suggestions.title: Spoonacular Recipe Synergy Engine
suggestions.desc: Connect the pantry inventory to the Spoonacular API to suggest recipes that use ingredients currently in stock. Prioritize recipes that use items nearing their expiration date to proactively reduce household food waste.

suggestions.title: Perishable Consumption Forecasting
suggestions.desc: Apply a Prophet or ARIMA time-series model to the shopping history data to predict exactly when staples like milk or eggs will run out. Provide the user with a 'Smart Shopping List' that predicts their needs before they even open the fridge.

suggestions.title: Multi-User Real-time Household Sync
suggestions.desc: Migrate the backend to Firebase Realtime Database to support shared households. Implement a conflict resolution strategy to ensure that if two roommates update the milk inventory simultaneously, the data remains consistent across all devices.

similar: legacy:UC-043|Spring 2021|text marks the spot mobile application

title: Railserve, Inc. Maintenance Tracking
authors: Daniel Brown, Christopher Choi, Andrew Hirschler, Gary Kirk, Ian Straiton, Oleg McNamara
abstract: Online maintenance tracking system for Railserve, Inc. with database and forms for data input.

suggestions.title: Field Technician Offline-First Mobile App
suggestions.desc: Build a React Native mobile client that allows Railserve technicians to log maintenance tasks in areas without Wi-Fi. Use SQLite for local storage and implement a background synchronization service that uploads data once a connection is re-established.

suggestions.title: Automated Inventory & Parts Reordering
suggestions.desc: Link the maintenance forms to a parts inventory database. When a technician logs a repair requiring a specific valve or bolt, the system should automatically decrement stock and trigger a procurement email when quantities hit a 'reorder point'.

suggestions.title: NLP-Based Failure Pattern Analysis
suggestions.desc: Apply Natural Language Processing (using spaCy) to the unstructured text in 'technician notes' to categorize common failure causes. Create a dashboard that highlights which locomotive models are experiencing the most frequent mechanical issues.

suggestions.title: Granular RBAC and Audit Logging
suggestions.desc: Implement a rigorous Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) system using JWT to distinguish between technicians, inspectors, and admins. Add a blockchain-inspired immutable audit log to track every change made to maintenance records for safety compliance.

title: Smart Mirror
authors: Raiden Stiegel, Sean Berdini, Gabriel Jefferson, Blesson Thomas, Kevin Nguyen
abstract: Raspberry Pi controlled data aggregation interface displaying time, weather, and news behind a two-way mirror.
topics: IoT, Raspberry Pi, Smart Home, Data Display

suggestions.title: Voice-Controlled AI Concierge
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Porcupine Wake Word engine and a local LLM (like Llama-3 via Ollama) to allow the mirror to answer questions hands-free. Enable voice commands to show/hide specific widgets or control other smart home devices via Home Assistant.

suggestions.title: Morning Fitness & Health Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Sync the mirror with Fitbit or Apple Health APIs to display sleep quality, steps, and heart rate data as the user gets ready. Add a 'Workout Mode' that displays YouTube exercise videos behind the glass for guided morning routines.

suggestions.title: Privacy-First Face-ID Profiles
suggestions.desc: Use a Raspberry Pi Camera and OpenCV to recognize who is standing in front of the mirror. Automatically switch the display to show that specific user's Google Calendar, commute times, and personal notifications while keeping others' data hidden.

suggestions.title: Dockerized Modular Widget Marketplace
suggestions.desc: Re-engineer the mirror software to run each widget (Weather, News, Spotify) in an independent Docker container. Create a local web portal where users can browse, install, and configure new third-party widgets without editing code.

title: Project Battleship
authors: Eric Carboni, Jonathan Taylor, Steven Petsinger, Clayton Leikness, Payton Mock
abstract: Modern version of the classic Battleship board game with digital enhancements.

suggestions.title: Global Matchmaking & Ranking System
suggestions.desc: Implement a backend using Node.js and Socket.io to support real-time online multiplayer between strangers. Create an ELO-based ranking system and global leaderboard to turn the game into a competitive social platform.

suggestions.title: Algorithmic Logic Battleship for EdTech
suggestions.desc: Transform the game into a coding challenge where players 'buy' radar pings or air strikes by solving short JavaScript or Python puzzles. Target this toward CS students as a gamified way to practice data structures and algorithms.

suggestions.title: Reinforcement Learning Adversarial AI
suggestions.desc: Train a Deep Q-Network (DQN) agent to play Battleship by simulating millions of games against itself. Create a 'Hard Mode' where the AI learns the player's specific placement habits (e.g., preference for corners) and adapts its search strategy mid-game.

suggestions.title: 3D WebGL Unreal Engine Integration
suggestions.desc: Port the game's visuals to Unreal Engine 5 using Pixel Streaming to allow high-fidelity 3D graphics in a standard web browser. Add particle effects for ship explosions and dynamic water physics to modernize the aesthetic beyond a 2D grid.

title: Asymmetric Multiplayer Game in VR
authors: Jordan Davis, Mitchell Arnold, Ross Tebbetts, Zachary Towner, Zane Johnston
abstract: VR game with asymmetric gameplay where players take different perspectives: one builds levels while others try to complete them.

suggestions.title: Cross-Platform Mobile-to-VR Play
suggestions.desc: Develop a companion mobile app that allows players to be the 'Dungeon Master' from their phone, placing traps and enemies in real-time while a VR player navigates the level. Use Firebase for low-latency state synchronization between mobile and VR.

suggestions.title: VR Remote Team-Building Escape Room
suggestions.desc: Pivot the game into a collaborative escape room where the 'Builder' must help the VR player solve puzzles by altering the environment. Include a spatial audio communication system using the Agora SDK for high-fidelity team coordination.

suggestions.title: Locomotion Comfort & Sickness Study
suggestions.desc: Implement three different locomotion methods (Teleportation, Continuous, and Arm-Swinging) and create a data-logging system to track player movement fluidness. Conduct a study to analyze which method minimizes VR sickness in high-intensity level-building scenarios.

suggestions.title: Procedural Logic Toolset for Builders
suggestions.desc: Create a visual scripting system (similar to Unreal Blueprints) within the VR environment so builders can create complex logic, like 'if plate A is pressed, door B opens.' This empowers players to create functional mechanical puzzles without writing code.

title: WhatsThe.Buzz
authors: Abdul Wahab, Ryan McMichael, Melody An, Alexia Allway
abstract: Web application for service industry businesses to collect customer feedback and offer coupons as incentives.
topics: Web Development, Customer Feedback, Business Intelligence

suggestions.title: NLP-Powered Sentiment Trend Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Python Natural Language Toolkit (NLTK) or spaCy to automatically categorize raw text feedback into 'Service', 'Cleanliness', or 'Product' categories. Build a dashboard that visualizes sentiment trends over time, allowing managers to see exactly when and where service quality dips.

suggestions.title: Dynamic Coupon Pricing Engine
suggestions.desc: Develop a machine learning model that predicts customer churn risk based on feedback scores and visit frequency. The system should automatically adjust coupon values—offering higher discounts to dissatisfied regular customers to maximize retention.

suggestions.title: Geofenced Feedback Triggers
suggestions.desc: Add a mobile geofencing component using the Google Maps API that prompts users for feedback exactly 30 minutes after they leave a physical business location. This ensures the experience is fresh in their mind while they are still on their mobile device.

suggestions.title: Differential Privacy for Anonymous Feedback
suggestions.desc: Implement a differential privacy layer to ensure that individual customer responses cannot be re-identified, even by the business owner. This encourages more honest feedback in sensitive environments like healthcare or human resources.

title: StoryPort
authors: John McDonough, Logan Cooper, Zachary Reece
abstract: iOS and Android app allowing users to capture images, add voice recordings, and upload the resulting videos to Facebook.

suggestions.title: AI-Driven Noise Suppression and Voice Clarity
suggestions.desc: Incorporate a deep learning noise-suppression library like RNNoise to clean up voice recordings captured in public environments. This ensures that the generated videos have professional-grade audio regardless of background wind or crowd noise.

suggestions.title: Collaborative Multi-User Storyboards
suggestions.desc: Extend the app to support shared albums where multiple users can contribute images and voiceovers to a single timeline. Use Firebase Realtime Database to sync contributions into a cohesive community-driven video montage.

suggestions.title: Automated Social Format Optimizer
suggestions.desc: Build an FFmpeg-based backend service that automatically reformats the final video into platform-specific ratios (9:16 for TikTok/Reels, 1:1 for Instagram feed). Include automated subtitle generation by passing the voice recording through a Speech-to-Text API.

suggestions.title: Computer Vision Scene Description
suggestions.desc: Integrate the PyTorch or TensorFlow mobile frameworks to analyze captured images and suggest relevant tags or voiceover prompts. For example, if the camera detects a 'Sunset', the app could suggest reflective music or descriptive captions.

title: Equipment Utilization Tracking - Ameritrack
authors: Christian Brutofsky, Taylor Nicole Blasingame, Andrew Magana, Aleksandar Veselinovic, Austin Anderson
abstract: Embedded system paired with application to provide real-time analytics for Railserve equipment utilization at Ameritrack.
topics: IoT, Embedded Systems, Real-time Analytics, Tracking

suggestions.title: Predictive Maintenance via Vibration Analysis
suggestions.desc: Equip the Railserve hardware with high-frequency accelerometers and use Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) analysis to detect abnormal vibration patterns. Build a model that predicts equipment failure before it happens, reducing downtime for Ameritrack.

suggestions.title: Edge-Processed Anomaly Detection
suggestions.desc: Shift the analytics from the cloud to the edge by deploying a TensorFlow Lite model directly onto the embedded hardware. This allows the system to detect and log critical equipment misuse even when the rail yard has poor cellular or Wi-Fi connectivity.

suggestions.title: Rail Yard Digital Twin Visualization
suggestions.desc: Develop a 3D 'Digital Twin' dashboard using Unity or Three.js that visualizes the real-time position and status of every piece of equipment on a virtual map of the Ameritrack facility. Allow users to click on 3D assets to see live telemetry data.

suggestions.title: Automated Logistic Scheduling Optimizer
suggestions.desc: Create a scheduling algorithm that uses historical utilization data to suggest optimal equipment rotations. If certain engines are being overused while others sit idle, the system should generate a weekly plan to balance the load across the fleet.

title: HVAC Training Simulator
authors: Preston Waters, Jamarcus Coulter, Euijin Lee, Paul Xiong
abstract: Virtual Reality game to train players to identify and fix fundamental HVAC problems.
topics: Game Development, Virtual Reality, Training Simulation, HVAC

suggestions.title: Multiplayer Collaborative Repair Sessions
suggestions.desc: Expand the simulator to support networked multiplayer, allowing an instructor to enter the same virtual space as a student. Use Photon Engine or Unity Render Streaming to enable real-time demonstrations and guided troubleshooting in the VR environment.

suggestions.title: IoT-Synced Digital Twins for HVAC
suggestions.desc: Connect the simulator to real-world HVAC sensors using an MQTT broker. Allow students to troubleshoot 'live' problems by pulling data from an actual physical unit into the VR space, creating a bridge between simulation and reality.

suggestions.title: Haptic Feedback and Tool Physics
suggestions.desc: Integrate advanced haptic APIs (like those for Meta Quest Touch Pro) to simulate the resistance of turning a wrench or the vibration of a running compressor. This adds a layer of muscle memory training missing from standard visual simulators.

suggestions.title: Procedural Fault Generation System
suggestions.desc: Develop a logic-based engine that randomly generates HVAC malfunctions (e.g., refrigerant leaks, electrical shorts, dirty coils) based on real-world probability data. This ensures that every training session is unique and prevents students from simply memorizing levels.

title: 4Paw: Donation and Adoption Platform
authors: Tucker Fowler, Roger Mahler, David Huseman, Thomas Nguyen, Carlos Padilla, Andrew Unkefer
abstract: Hardware feeder prototype and server-based website providing a one-stop donation location for pet adoption.
topics: Web Development, IoT, Non-profit, Animal Welfare

suggestions.title: Live-Stream Remote Feeding Interaction
suggestions.desc: Integrate a low-latency video stream (using WebRTC) and a WebSocket-based trigger that allows remote donors to 'feed' an animal in real-time from the website. This creates an immediate, tangible connection between the donor and the pet.

suggestions.title: Blockchain-Verified Donation Tracking
suggestions.desc: Implement a transparent ledger using a blockchain like Polygon or Solana to track every dollar donated. Provide users with a 'receipt' showing exactly when their donation was used to purchase food or medical supplies, increasing non-profit trust.

suggestions.title: Smart Health Monitoring via Feeder Analytics
suggestions.desc: Use the hardware feeder to track consumption patterns (frequency, speed, and volume of eating) using Load Cell sensors. Develop an alert system for shelter staff that flags potential illness if a pet’s eating habits deviate from their baseline.

suggestions.title: ML-Based Pet Matchmaking Algorithm
suggestions.desc: Build a recommendation engine that uses collaborative filtering to match potential adopters with pets based on lifestyle factors and the adopter's interaction history on the site. Use historical adoption success data to refine the 'compatibility' score.

title: Operation: Laces
authors: Grant Wesley, Ash Dela-Cruz, Gilberto Rose, Ridge Brown, Zachary Munson, Marcus Joseph
abstract: Mobile app for selling shoes and hats with Augmented Reality virtual try-on feature.

suggestions.title: LiDAR-Based Precision Sizing
suggestions.desc: Leverage the LiDAR scanner on modern iOS devices to perform millimetric measurements of a user's foot or head. Compare these measurements against manufacturer-specific size charts to recommend the perfect fit, reducing e-commerce return rates.

suggestions.title: Social AR 'Shared Wardrobe' Mode
suggestions.desc: Implement a feature where users can send an AR snapshot of a shoe 'on their foot' to a friend's device for real-time feedback. Allow the friend to swap colors or models remotely on the user's screen using a synchronized AR session.

suggestions.title: Physically Based Rendering (PBR) Texture Library
suggestions.desc: Upgrade the AR visual quality by implementing PBR materials that respond dynamically to real-world lighting. This would allow leather, suede, and metallic finishes on hats and shoes to look photorealistic within the AR environment.

suggestions.title: Generative AI Customizer Preview
suggestions.desc: Integrate a Stable Diffusion API to allow users to describe a custom shoe pattern (e.g., 'galaxy print' or 'retro 80s'). The app would generate the texture on the fly and map it onto the 3D shoe model for an instant AR try-on.

title: Location Services using Wi-Fi Access Points
authors: Andrew Dessin, Alex Googe, Brandon Cross, Brandon Parker, Jim McDoniel, David Rose
abstract: Indoor location system using WiFi access points to locate devices within a Macy's store within 1% of total square footage.
topics: IoT, Indoor Positioning, WiFi, Location Services

suggestions.title: IMU and Dead Reckoning Sensor Fusion
suggestions.desc: Improve the 1% accuracy by combining Wi-Fi RSSI data with the mobile device's accelerometer and gyroscope (Inertial Measurement Unit). Use a Kalman Filter to maintain positioning when Wi-Fi signals are blocked or fluctuating.

suggestions.title: Retail Foot-Traffic Heatmap Generator
suggestions.desc: Build a backend tool for Macy's management that aggregates anonymized location data to generate heatmaps of customer movement. Identify 'dead zones' in the store and optimize product placement based on high-traffic paths.

suggestions.title: Proximity-Based Dynamic AR Signage
suggestions.desc: Connect the indoor positioning system to an AR overlay. As a customer walks through the store, the app can display 'floating' AR coupons or product information specifically for the department they are currently standing in.

suggestions.title: Wi-Fi Fingerprinting via Deep Learning
suggestions.desc: Replace simple triangulation with a Deep Neural Network trained on 'fingerprints' of Wi-Fi signal strengths at specific coordinates. This approach accounts for signal multipath and interference caused by store shelving and metal structures.

project_key: legacy:CPCS-17|Spring 2017|location services using wi-fi access points

title: Magical Liopleurodon
authors: Chris Koronkowski, Stephen Strickland
abstract: Open-source security library wrapping multiple security-focused libraries into a single easy-to-use library for IoT devices.
topics: Cybersecurity, IoT, Open Source, Security Library

suggestions.title: Hardware Root of Trust Integration
suggestions.desc: Extend the library to support Trusted Execution Environments (TEEs) like ARM TrustZone or dedicated TPM chips. This ensures that cryptographic keys remain secure even if the main IoT operating system is compromised.

suggestions.title: Automated Fuzz Testing for Security Wrappers
suggestions.desc: Build a CI/CD pipeline that uses AFL++ or LibFuzzer to automatically test the library's wrappers for memory leaks and buffer overflows. This ensures that the 'security library' itself doesn't introduce new vulnerabilities.

suggestions.title: Lightweight OAuth2 and OIDC for ESP32
suggestions.desc: Develop a highly optimized implementation of OAuth2 and OpenID Connect specifically for low-power microcontrollers like the ESP32. This allows IoT devices to securely authenticate with cloud services without the overhead of standard enterprise libraries.

suggestions.title: Zero-Trust Mesh Networking Protocol
suggestions.desc: Use the library to build a peer-to-peer security protocol for IoT mesh networks. Every device must mutually authenticate using certificates before sharing data, preventing 'man-in-the-middle' attacks within smart home or industrial clusters.

title: CodeCheck: Security Enhancement Plugin
authors: Ben Ledford, Chris Francis-Christie
abstract: Security-focused IntelliJ/Android Studio plugin performing real-time code analysis for vulnerabilities with fix suggestions.
topics: Cybersecurity, Developer Tools, Code Analysis, Security

suggestions.title: LLM-Powered Automated Vulnerability Patching
suggestions.desc: Integrate an LLM (like OpenAI's GPT-4o or CodeLlama) to not only identify vulnerabilities but also generate and suggest the specific code patch. Users should be able to click a 'Fix' button to automatically refactor the insecure code.

suggestions.title: Taint Analysis and Data Flow Tracking
suggestions.desc: Implement a static analysis engine that performs 'taint analysis' to track user-controlled input from its source to sensitive 'sinks' (like database queries). This would allow the plugin to detect complex SQL injection or XSS risks that simple pattern matching misses.

suggestions.title: Pre-Commit Hook and CI/CD Integration
suggestions.desc: Port the plugin's logic into a standalone CLI tool and a GitHub Action. This ensures that security checks are enforced during the commit process and within the automated build pipeline, not just within the developer's IDE.

suggestions.title: Custom Enterprise Security Policy Engine
suggestions.desc: Build a feature that allows organizations to define their own 'forbidden patterns' using a simple DSL (Domain Specific Language). This allows companies to enforce internal coding standards and block specific deprecated libraries across their entire dev team.

title: Personal Tracking Railserv Project
authors: Vishal Patel, Blake Snellgrove, Jayson Swartz, Alonzo Bustamante, Rickey Weems
abstract: Personal tracking device displaying GPS coordinates on a mobile application for railroad workers.

suggestions.title: Precision Geofencing for High-Risk Rail Zones
suggestions.desc: Implement the Turf.js library to create dynamic geofences around dangerous railroad tracks. The system should trigger haptic alerts on the mobile app and log safety violations in real-time when workers enter 'Hot Zones' without authorization.

suggestions.title: Predictive Battery and Signal Management
suggestions.desc: Develop an intelligent polling algorithm that adjusts GPS update frequency based on movement velocity and battery levels using the Google Fit API. This ensures the device lasts a full 12-hour shift while maintaining high tracking resolution during transit.

suggestions.title: Biometric Fatigue Monitoring Integration
suggestions.desc: Connect the tracking app to wearable heart rate sensors via Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) to detect signs of worker fatigue. Use the data to correlate physical exhaustion with specific high-stress locations on the rail network for administrative review.

suggestions.title: Offline-First Dead Reckoning for Tunnel Transit
suggestions.desc: Build a fallback navigation system using the device's accelerometer and gyroscope sensors to estimate position when GPS signal is lost in tunnels. Use the Kalman Filter algorithm to smooth the transition between satellite and inertial data.

title: Railserve Personal Tracking
authors: Colton Carder, Dock O'neal, Andrew Maddox, Chris Theroux
abstract: Software backend to display live location of rail yard workers on a map.
topics: GPS, Tracking, Real-time Systems, Rail Operations

suggestions.title: Precision Geofencing for Safety Alerts
suggestions.desc: Extend the backend using Turf.js or PostGIS to define 'danger zones' within the rail yard. Implement a real-time notification engine that triggers mobile alerts to both the worker and the yard manager when a person enters an unauthorized or active locomotive zone.

suggestions.title: Yard Utilization Heatmapping
suggestions.desc: Develop a data visualization layer using D3.js or Deck.gl to aggregate historical tracking data. This tool would help yard managers identify high-traffic bottlenecks and optimize the physical layout of rail operations based on actual worker movement patterns.

suggestions.title: Biometric Health & Safety Integration
suggestions.desc: Connect the tracking system to wearable APIs (like Apple HealthKit or Garmin) to monitor worker heart rates and activity levels alongside their location. This could be used to detect worker fatigue or medical emergencies in remote areas of the yard.

suggestions.title: IMU-Based Dead Reckoning for Signal Gaps
suggestions.desc: Implement a 'fallback' positioning system for areas with poor GPS coverage, such as sheds or tunnels. Use mobile accelerometer and gyroscope data to maintain location accuracy via dead reckoning when satellite signals are lost.

title: GTRI Disaster Recovery Plan
authors: Austin Taylor Wagoner, Oscar Alejandro Perez, Kyle Eugene Whitworth, Kevin Osorio
abstract: Redevelopment of disaster recovery plan for GTRI ELSYS department.
topics: IT Management, Disaster Recovery, Business Continuity

suggestions.title: Automated Infrastructure-as-Code Failover
suggestions.desc: Transform the static recovery plan into an executable Terraform or Ansible codebase. This allows the ELSYS department to spin up a mirrored cloud environment automatically if the primary physical or virtual servers fail.

suggestions.title: Disaster Response Mobile Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Build a React Native mobile app for department leads that provides a real-time checklist during a crisis. It should integrate with communication APIs like Twilio to instantly alert recovery teams and track the status of critical systems as they come back online.

suggestions.title: Chaos Engineering Simulation Engine
suggestions.desc: Develop a Python-based testing tool, inspired by Netflix's Chaos Monkey, to simulate specific failures defined in the GTRI plan. This allows the department to validate their recovery SLAs by inducing controlled system stress and measuring recovery time.

suggestions.title: ISO 22301 Compliance Auditor
suggestions.desc: Create a documentation tool that automatically maps the disaster recovery steps to ISO 22301 (Business Continuity) standards. The system should flag gaps in the current plan and generate audit-ready compliance reports for GTRI stakeholders.

title: DocWeb Patient System
authors: Stephen Cain, Cristian Cheveresan, Jun-Hao Kuah, Victoria McDonald, Aline Stella Moche Chatue
abstract: Renovation of user interface for a patient management system to improve usability.

suggestions.title: FHIR-Compatible Mobile Patient Portal
suggestions.desc: Extend the system by building a patient-facing Flutter app that connects to the DocWeb backend using HL7 FHIR standards. This allows patients to view their records and schedule appointments via a mobile interface that mirrors the desktop renovation.

suggestions.title: Voice-Driven Clinical Documentation
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Web Speech API or AWS Transcribe into the renovated UI to allow doctors to dictate patient notes hands-free. This addresses the primary usability bottleneck of manual data entry during consultations.

suggestions.title: Gaze-Tracked UX Optimization
suggestions.desc: Use eye-tracking software like GazeCloudAPI to conduct a quantitative usability study on the new interface. Analyze 'heatmap' data to further refine the placement of critical patient vitals and medication lists based on where doctors look first.

suggestions.title: Integrated WebRTC Telehealth Module
suggestions.desc: Add a secure video conferencing layer directly into the patient management system. This would allow providers to launch virtual visits from within a patient's digital chart, streamlining the workflow for remote care.

similar: legacy:GR-04|Fall 2018|how big data can improve healthcare

title: Anthem NoSQL Data Modeling and Query
authors: Johnathan Brown, Noble Amaka, James Stapleton, Jason Williams, Hasaan Keen
abstract: Investigation of patient data profile modeling and query capabilities on NoSQL databases for Anthem.
topics: NoSQL, Database, Healthcare IT, Data Modeling

suggestions.title: Polyglot Persistence Layer for Billing & Labs
suggestions.desc: Expand the investigation by implementing a hybrid database strategy. Use MongoDB for flexible patient clinical notes and a Graph database like Neo4j to model complex familial relationships and genetic markers for Anthem’s population health analytics.

suggestions.title: Real-time FHIR-to-NoSQL Transformer
suggestions.desc: Build a middleware service that automatically maps incoming HL7 FHIR data streams into optimized BSON or JSON schemas for NoSQL storage, ensuring Anthem can ingest data from external providers without manual reformatting.

suggestions.title: Patient Longitudinal Timeline Visualizer
suggestions.desc: Leverage the NoSQL backend's query capabilities to build a time-series visualization tool (using vis.js) that maps a patient's entire medical history across decades into a single, interactive chronological view.

suggestions.title: NoSQL Query Performance Benchmarking Suite
suggestions.desc: Create a framework to compare the latency of standard healthcare queries (e.g., 'find all patients with X condition and Y medication') across different NoSQL engines like Couchbase and DynamoDB using Anthem's specific data profiles.

project_key: legacy:CPIT-25|Spring 2017|anthem nosql data modeling and query

title: Anthem Hadoop Security
authors: Justin McTiernan, Jared O'Brien, Prakash Rai, Logan Smith, Joshua Scott
abstract: Investigation of authorization solutions on Hadoop for Anthem healthcare data security.

suggestions.title: Fine-Grained ABAC Policy Engine
suggestions.desc: Implement Attribute-Based Access Control (ABAC) using Apache Ranger to restrict data access based on patient sensitivity levels and employee clearance. For example, ensuring only oncologists can view specific oncology datasets within the Hadoop cluster.

suggestions.title: Differential Privacy Layer for Research
suggestions.desc: Develop a data export layer that applies 'differential privacy'—adding statistical noise to Hadoop query results. This allows Anthem to share aggregate health data with researchers while mathematically guaranteeing that no individual patient can be re-identified.

suggestions.title: Real-time Hadoop Security Audit Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Use the ELK stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana) to ingest Hadoop access logs and visualize security threats. Create alerts for anomalous data access patterns, such as an unusual volume of patient record downloads after hours.

suggestions.title: Zero-Knowledge Encryption for Sensitive PII
suggestions.desc: Integrate client-side field-level encryption so that sensitive identifiers (SSNs, names) are encrypted before reaching Hadoop, ensuring that even Hadoop administrators cannot view raw patient data without proper decryption keys held by the clinicians.

similar: legacy:GR-04|Fall 2018|how big data can improve healthcare

title: WhatsThe.Buzz - IT Group
authors: Sherri Booher, Neisha Martinez, Jonathan Jones, Patrick Green, Benjamin Skeen
abstract: Enhancement of features for a restaurant customer survey web application.

suggestions.title: Natural Language Sentiment Analysis Pipeline
suggestions.desc: Integrate the NLTK or Google Cloud Natural Language API to automatically categorize customer feedback into 'Positive,' 'Neutral,' or 'Negative.' This allows restaurant managers to prioritize reading the most critical complaints immediately.

suggestions.title: Dynamic QR-Code Dish Rating System
suggestions.desc: Extend the web app to generate unique QR codes for specific menu items. When a customer scans a code, they are directed to a survey for that specific dish, providing the restaurant with granular data on which recipes need improvement.

suggestions.title: Instant Manager Recovery Alerts
suggestions.desc: Develop a Twilio integration that sends an immediate SMS or push notification to the floor manager whenever a '1-star' survey is submitted, enabling 'table recovery' while the customer is still in the restaurant.

suggestions.title: Gamified Loyalty Survey Integration
suggestions.desc: Build a module that rewards customers with digital coupons or loyalty points (via a Firebase backend) for completing detailed surveys, testing how gamification affects the response rate and quality of feedback.

title: Alternative Medicine Office Management System
authors: Eric Plascencia, Calvin Nix, Alex Estrada, Ryan Josefsburg, Jerome Lester
abstract: Office management system for an alternative medicine company to manage patient-related processes.
topics: Healthcare IT, Enterprise Software, Medical Office

suggestions.title: Automated Insurance Verification Pipeline
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Change Healthcare API to automate real-time insurance eligibility checks during patient intake. This eliminates manual phone calls and reduces billing errors by verifying coverage before the consultation begins.

suggestions.title: Alternative Medicine Inventory Forecasting
suggestions.desc: Build a supply chain module that tracks herbal and homeopathic stock levels and uses simple linear regression to predict when items will run out. Automate purchase order generation when stock hits a pre-defined threshold.

suggestions.title: Treatment Efficacy Analytics Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Create a data visualization suite using D3.js that tracks patient recovery progress against specific alternative treatments. Allow practitioners to see which holistic protocols yield the highest success rates for specific symptoms.

suggestions.title: Secure Patient Portal with Telehealth Integration
suggestions.desc: Extend the system with a client-facing React Native portal that supports HIPAA-compliant video consultations via Twilio Video. Patients should be able to view their treatment plans and upload photo-logs of their progress.

similar: legacy:GC-17|Spring 2019|secure authentication and access control for enterprise

title: AMOMS
authors: Skip Bassey, Casey Brock, Jared Gibson, Ishraq Karim, Andy Mecke
abstract: Medical office management system for Centro Quiropractico Cassan to digitize paper records and manage patient tasks.
topics: Healthcare IT, Enterprise Software, Database, Medical Office

suggestions.title: Interactive Anatomical Pain Charting
suggestions.desc: Develop a Canvas-based interactive human body map for the AMOMS interface. This allows chiropractors to 'pin' specific vertebrae or muscle groups where adjustments were made, providing a visual history of treatment over time.

suggestions.title: Chiropractic Home-Exercise Companion App
suggestions.desc: Create a React Native mobile app for patients of Centro Quiropractico Cassan where they can receive assigned stretches or exercises. The app should track completion and report back to the AMOMS database for the doctor to review.

suggestions.title: OCR Paper-to-Digital Intake Migration
suggestions.desc: Build a Python script using AWS Textract or Tesseract OCR to scan the clinic's remaining legacy paper records. The system should automatically extract names, dates, and medical history to pre-populate the AMOMS digital database.

suggestions.title: Appointment 'No-Show' Predictive Model
suggestions.desc: Implement a machine learning model using Scikit-learn to analyze historical appointment data. The system can flag appointments with a high probability of a 'no-show' and trigger automated SMS reminders to those specific patients via Twilio.

title: 3MS - Modern Medical Management System
authors: Michael Russell, Joshua Mennicke, Elizabeth Herndon, Bilal Adams, Kyle Sylvestre
abstract: Software to replace paper records for alternative medical facility, enabling digital patient records and appointment management.
topics: Healthcare IT, Database, Medical Office Management

suggestions.title: Multi-Tenant SaaS Refactoring
suggestions.desc: Refactor the 3MS architecture to support multi-tenancy using a 'schema-per-tenant' approach. This would allow the system to be sold as a subscription service to multiple different alternative medical facilities, with each having their own isolated data.

suggestions.title: Patient Check-in Kiosk Interface
suggestions.desc: Develop a simplified, tablet-first React interface intended for a lobby kiosk. Patients can use it to check in, sign digital consent forms, and update their insurance or contact information, reducing the workload on front-desk staff.

suggestions.title: E-Prescribing for Natural Remedies
suggestions.desc: Build a module that generates digitally signed 'prescriptions' for natural remedies that can be sent directly to specialized pharmacies or health food stores, including dosage instructions and safety warnings.

suggestions.title: Holistic Progress Tracking Charts
suggestions.desc: Create a visualization dashboard using Chart.js that tracks patient-reported metrics unique to alternative medicine, such as energy levels, mobility ranges, or stress scores, showing the longitudinal impact of long-term care plans.

title: Doctor's Office Management System
authors: David Potter, Jonathan Cook, Adam Coker, Brandon Tuttle, Luis Rodriguez, William Story
abstract: Fully functional doctors office system to manage, organize and track appointments, schedules, and patient data.

suggestions.title: AI-Driven Appointment Optimization
suggestions.desc: Implement a constraint-solver algorithm (like OptaPlanner) to automatically organize the office schedule. The system should optimize for minimizing gaps between appointments while accounting for doctor specialties and room equipment availability.

suggestions.title: Real-time Insurance Eligibility Verification
suggestions.desc: Integrate an API like PokitDok or Eligible to check a patient's insurance coverage in real-time as an appointment is being scheduled. This ensures the office knows the co-pay amount and coverage status before the patient even arrives.

suggestions.title: Doctor's 'Day-at-a-Glance' Mobile App
suggestions.desc: Develop a mobile-first companion app for physicians that provides push notifications when a patient arrives, shows a summary of the next patient's chief complaint, and allows the doctor to quickly review their daily schedule on the go.

suggestions.title: De-Identified Data Export for Public Health
suggestions.desc: Build a HIPAA-compliant data 'anonymizer' that exports patient demographic and diagnostic trends into a format suitable for local public health reporting, ensuring all Personal Identifiable Information (PII) is removed while retaining medical utility.

title: AMOMS v2
authors: Kelechi Amaihe, Mamadou Bah, Anh Huynh, Juan Blanco, Alex Federico, Curtis Dirton
abstract: Web-based medical office management system to replace paper records with a digital system.
topics: Healthcare IT, Web Development, Medical Records

suggestions.title: Legacy Paper Digitization with Tesseract OCR
suggestions.desc: Implement an AI-driven scanning module using Tesseract.js to automatically convert existing paper medical records into structured digital data. The system should map recognized text to specific database fields like 'Patient History' and 'Allergies'.

suggestions.title: Blockchain-Based Medical Audit Trail
suggestions.desc: Develop a Hyperledger Fabric implementation to create an immutable log of every change made to a medical record. This ensures total data integrity and simplifies compliance audits by proving who accessed or modified data and when.

suggestions.title: AI-Powered Smart Scheduling Engine
suggestions.desc: Build a scheduling algorithm that analyzes historical appointment durations to optimize the daily calendar. Use the Google Calendar API to sync appointments and send automated SMS reminders via Amazon SNS to reduce no-show rates.

suggestions.title: White-Label Medical Office SaaS Migration
suggestions.desc: Refactor the existing monolithic architecture into a multi-tenant SaaS platform using Docker and Kubernetes. Implement a subscription-based billing module with Stripe to allow other small clinics to host their own instances of the system.

title: Make A Miracle
authors: Yasin Hussain, Dmitri Konradi, Angel Kanchev, Baturay Daylak, Tony Guzman
abstract: Web application management system for Make A Miracle non-profit organization.

suggestions.title: Stripe-Driven Impact Transparency Module
suggestions.desc: Integrate Stripe's donation API and link specific donations to real-world outcomes (e.g., 'Your $50 provided 10 meals'). Create a visual 'Impact Map' using Leaflet.js to show donors where their contributions are helping in real-time.

suggestions.title: Volunteer Management & Hours Tracking
suggestions.desc: Build a dedicated volunteer portal with a QR-code-based check-in system for events. The system should automatically calculate community service hours and generate PDF certificates of appreciation for the volunteers.

suggestions.title: Headless CMS Migration for Content Scaling
suggestions.desc: Migrate the content layer to Strapi or Contentful to allow non-technical staff to update the site without touching the codebase. Use Next.js for the frontend to ensure high SEO rankings and fast loading times for mobile users.

suggestions.title: Social Media Sentiment & Engagement Tracker
suggestions.desc: Develop a dashboard that uses the Twitter and Instagram APIs to track the organization’s reach. Use a basic NLP model to analyze the sentiment of mentions and provide the NGO with reports on public perception of their campaigns.

title: Knock Knock
authors: Caitlin Price, Adam Knight, Carolina Sanabria, Daniel Young, Liel Van Der Hoeven, Ronen Yankivski
abstract: Smart doorbell system allowing users to view and interact with visitors at their door remotely from anywhere.
topics: IoT, Smart Home, Video Streaming, Mobile

suggestions.title: Edge AI Facial Recognition for Known Visitors
suggestions.desc: Integrate TensorFlow Lite on a Raspberry Pi to identify frequent visitors like family members or delivery drivers. The system should send personalized push notifications such as 'The Mailman is at the door' instead of a generic alert.

suggestions.title: End-to-End Encrypted Cloud Storage via WebRTC
suggestions.desc: Secure the video stream by implementing WebRTC for low-latency peer-to-peer communication. Store recorded clips in an AWS S3 bucket using AES-256 client-side encryption, ensuring only the homeowner holds the decryption keys.

suggestions.title: Smart Home Ecosystem Interoperability
suggestions.desc: Extend the doorbell to support the Matter protocol or Home Assistant API. This allows the doorbell to trigger actions in the house, such as turning on the porch lights when motion is detected at night or locking the smart deadbolt.

suggestions.title: Computer Vision Package Theft Alert System
suggestions.desc: Train an object detection model to specifically recognize packages left on a porch. If a person is detected removing the package without a prior 'homeowner' identification, the system should trigger a high-decibel alarm and record the event.

title: Skies Above
authors: Adam Butler
abstract: Computer science capstone project (details not specified in program).

suggestions.title: Real-Time Light Pollution Mapping Suite
suggestions.desc: Leverage the SkyView API and local light sensors to build a mapping tool that identifies the best local spots for stargazing. Users can contribute data points to create a crowd-sourced heatmap of urban light pollution.

suggestions.title: AR Constellation Overlay for Mobile Devices
suggestions.desc: Using ARCore or ARKit, develop a mobile feature that overlays constellation lines and planetary data onto the live camera feed. Implement a high-performance rendering engine to ensure stars remain pinned to their celestial coordinates as the user moves.

suggestions.title: Satellite Imagery Cloud-Tracking Pipeline
suggestions.desc: Connect to the NOAA GOES-16 satellite data feed to provide real-time cloud cover predictions for amateur astronomers. Build a notification system that alerts users when 'Clear Skies' are expected in their specific GPS coordinates.

suggestions.title: Aviation Traffic Monitoring Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Integrate an ADS-B receiver to track aircraft in the sky above the user's current location. Use the OpenSky Network API to display aircraft flight numbers, altitudes, and destinations in a real-time 3D dashboard.

title: Surveillance Image Enhancement
authors: Connor Sample, Daniel Salge, Tevin Phillip, Andy Hudgins, Dylan Meadows
abstract: Feasibility study of image enhancement techniques for improving surveillance camera data quality.

suggestions.title: Super-Resolution GAN for License Plate Recovery
suggestions.desc: Train a Generative Adversarial Network (SRGAN) specifically on low-resolution, blurry images of vehicle plates. The goal is to reconstruct legible characters from pixelated surveillance footage to assist in vehicle identification.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Low-Light Enhancement with CUDA
suggestions.desc: Utilize NVIDIA CUDA to implement a frame-by-frame enhancement pipeline for night-vision cameras. Use the 'Zero-DCE' (Zero-Reference Deep Curve Estimation) method to brighten dark footage without introducing significant noise or artifacts.

suggestions.title: Automated Privacy-Preserving Face Blurring
suggestions.desc: Build a GDPR-compliance tool that detects human faces in surveillance streams and applies a Gaussian blur in real-time. Allow administrators to securely 'unmask' faces only when a security incident has been officially logged.

suggestions.title: De-Hazing and Atmospheric Correction for Outdoor Cams
suggestions.desc: Develop an image processing algorithm based on the Dark Channel Prior (DCP) to remove fog, smoke, and haze from outdoor security feeds. This ensures visibility in adverse weather conditions that typically render cameras useless.

title: Phobophobia
authors: Andrew Romans, Robert Kowalchuk, Cody McCormick, Cody Ulrich
abstract: Student game design project: Phobophobia.

suggestions.title: Biometric-Responsive Horror Mechanics
suggestions.desc: Integrate a heart rate monitor (via Arduino or Apple Watch) to the Unity/Unreal engine. The game's atmosphere should change dynamically; if the player's heart rate is low, the game spawns 'jumpscares' or increases environmental tension to keep them in a state of fear.

suggestions.title: Procedural Phobia Generation System
suggestions.desc: Build an engine that procedurally generates levels based on specific phobias (e.g., arachnophobia, thalassophobia). Use a tagging system to determine which assets, lighting, and sound effects are loaded based on the player's selected 'fears' at the start of the game.

suggestions.title: VR Exposure Therapy Sandbox
suggestions.desc: Port the game to Meta Quest 2/3 and retool the mechanics for therapeutic use. Create a controlled environment where users can gradually increase the intensity of their phobia triggers, tracking their progress through an in-game psychological analytics dashboard.

suggestions.title: Adaptive Soundscape with Spatial Audio
suggestions.desc: Implement the FMOD audio engine to create a non-linear, adaptive soundscape. Sounds should not just be 3D, but should 'evolve' (e.g., a whisper becoming a scream) based on the player's proximity to darkness or their current 'Sanity Meter' level.

title: Cat Burglary
authors: Devante Anderson-Boothe, Tyler Henning, Jonathan Miu
abstract: Student game design project: Cat Burglary.

suggestions.title: Advanced AI Pathfinding and Senses
suggestions.desc: Implement a 'Sensory Perception' system for guard NPCs that includes peripheral vision, hearing (surface-dependent footsteps), and smell. Use Hierarchical Finite State Machines (HFSM) to allow guards to coordinate searches when they find an open window or a missing item.

suggestions.title: Community Level Editor & Steam Workshop Integration
suggestions.desc: Build an in-game map editor using a tile-based system that allows players to design their own 'unstealable' vaults. Integrate the Steam Workshop API to allow for sharing, rating, and global leaderboards for the fastest completion times.

suggestions.title: Asymmetric Multiplayer: Cat vs. Security
suggestions.desc: Develop a 1v1 multiplayer mode where one player controls the cat (stealth) and the other player acts as the Security Chief, placing traps, monitoring cameras, and directing guards in real-time from a top-down tactical view.

suggestions.title: Physics-Based Destructible Heist Environments
suggestions.desc: Integrate NVIDIA PhysX to allow for realistic environmental interaction. Knocking over a vase should create noise that alerts guards, and players should be able to use the game's physics to create distractions, like pushing a heavy object to block a door.

title: Uncanny Valley
authors: Disney Nguyen
abstract: Student game design project: Uncanny Valley.

suggestions.title: Unreal Engine Metahuman Integration
suggestions.desc: Upgrade the game’s character models using Epic Games’ Metahuman Creator to push the visual fidelity to the extreme. Focus on implementing 'Micro-Expressions' during dialogue scenes to intentionally trigger the Uncanny Valley effect as a narrative device.

suggestions.title: LLM-Driven Dynamic Dialogue System
suggestions.desc: Replace scripted dialogue with a local LLM (like Llama 3) to allow players to speak to NPCs using their actual voice (Speech-to-Text). NPCs should respond based on their unique personality profiles, creating unpredictable and eerie interactions.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Ray-Traced Global Illumination
suggestions.desc: Implement DirectX Raytracing (DXR) to create hyper-realistic lighting and reflections. Focus on 'Mirror Horror' mechanics, where reflections in the environment don't perfectly match the player's movements, heightening the sense of unease.

suggestions.title: Haptic Suit Integration for Immersion
suggestions.desc: Add support for haptic feedback suits (like bHaptics). Program specific vibrational patterns to simulate the sensation of something 'brushing past' the player or the physical vibration of a heavy heartbeat when the character is in a high-stress 'Uncanny' situation.

title: Hasty Delivery
authors: Drew Savas
abstract: Student game design project: Hasty Delivery.

suggestions.title: Predictive Pathfinding with Traffic Simulation
suggestions.desc: Implement an A* pathfinding extension that accounts for dynamic obstacles and traffic density. Use a weight-based cost function to simulate rush hours, requiring the player to choose between longer routes or high-traffic shortcuts.

suggestions.title: Asymmetric Multiplayer Delivery Race
suggestions.desc: Develop a 1v1 multiplayer mode using Mirror or Photon where one player acts as the courier and the other acts as a 'Dispatcher' who can trigger road closures, weather events, or boost pads in real-time.

suggestions.title: Real-World GIS Map Integration
suggestions.desc: Use the Mapbox SDK for Unity to transform real-world OpenStreetMap data into playable levels. Allow users to input their own zip code to generate a delivery course based on their actual neighborhood streets.

suggestions.title: Physics-Based Cargo Integrity System
suggestions.desc: Create a sophisticated damage model where different cargo types (fragile, liquid, explosive) respond to G-forces and collisions. Use Spring Joints and custom shaders to visualize liquid sloshing and impact fractures.

title: ShopKeep
authors: Forrest McClain, Zach Colbert
abstract: Student game design project: ShopKeep.

suggestions.title: Procedural Guest Personalities
suggestions.desc: Integrate a local Large Language Model (LLM) or the OpenAI API to generate unique backstories and dynamic dialogue for patrons based on their procedurally generated stats and spending habits.

suggestions.title: Real-time Dynamic Economy
suggestions.desc: Implement a supply-and-demand simulation where patron satisfaction and spending affect the global price of goods, using a Node.js backend to synchronize the market across different players' sessions.

suggestions.title: Heat-Map Behavioral Analytics
suggestions.desc: Develop a telemetry system that tracks patron movement and dwell time using Unity Analytics; visualize this data as a heat map to help players optimize their shop layouts for maximum efficiency.

suggestions.title: Event-Driven Seasonal Shifts
suggestions.desc: Create a weather and holiday system that changes patron behavior and needs (e.g., higher demand for cold drinks in summer), requiring the player to swap inventory and decor seasonally.

title: Infinite Tactics
authors: John Ellis
abstract: Student game design project: Infinite Tactics.

suggestions.title: VR Alchemy Laboratory
suggestions.desc: Port the potion-making mechanics to VR using the OpenXR toolkit, allowing users to physically pick up flasks, pour liquid using physics-based shaders, and grind ingredients with motion controllers.

suggestions.title: Asynchronous Trading Post
suggestions.desc: Build a central server using Firebase to allow players to bottle their unique concoctions and list them in a global marketplace where other players can purchase them for in-game currency.

suggestions.title: Chemistry Curriculum Bridge
suggestions.desc: Replace fictional ingredients with real-world elements and chemical compounds, using the game mechanics to teach students about ionic bonding and basic chemical reactions.

suggestions.title: Genetic Ingredient Evolution
suggestions.desc: Implement a system where players can cross-breed plants to create new ingredients, using genetic algorithms to determine the properties, color, and rarity of the resulting hybrid.

title: Pixel Puzzle Player
authors: Julio Hernandez
abstract: Student game design project: Pixel Puzzle Player.

suggestions.title: Biometric Heart-Rate Sync
suggestions.desc: Integrate a smartwatch API to sync the character's movement speed or time-dilation effect with the player's real-time heart rate, making the game more challenging as the player becomes excited.

suggestions.title: Mobile AR Environment Interaction
suggestions.desc: Use ARCore or ARKit to place the 'Slow Poke' character in the real world, where physical furniture is mapped as obstacles through the phone's camera and LiDAR sensor.

suggestions.title: Adaptive Accessibility Controller
suggestions.desc: Develop a control scheme specifically for players with limited mobility, utilizing Tobii Eye Tracking or single-switch input methods to navigate the game's slow-paced environment.

suggestions.title: Perception-Based AI Stealth
suggestions.desc: Design an AI system for predators that detects motion through pixel-delta analysis; the character is only 'invisible' when moving below a specific velocity threshold, emphasizing the 'slow' mechanic.

title: Interstellar Delivery Corp
authors: Justin McLendon
abstract: Student game design project: Interstellar Delivery Corp.

suggestions.title: Voice-Activated Unit Command
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Wit.ai or Speech-to-Text API to allow players to issue complex tactical commands ('Archers, flank left!') verbally while using motion controllers for spatial positioning.
