suggestions.title: Asymmetric Mobile Commander
suggestions.desc: Build a companion mobile app that allows a second player to view a 2D strategic map and call in support abilities (like air strikes or healing) while the VR player is on the ground.

suggestions.title: Spatial Sound Localization AI
suggestions.desc: Implement HRTF (Head-Related Transfer Function) audio cues that allow players to locate hidden enemies or objective pings solely by sound, enhancing the situational awareness required in VR RTS.

suggestions.title: Mixed Reality Strategy Table
suggestions.desc: Migrate the project to a Passthrough-enabled headset (like Quest 3) to project the strategy map onto the player's real-world table, using spatial anchors to maintain persistent unit positions.

title: Call of Space Ring Tremorfieldfrontwatch: Advanced Global Ops Warfare 4
authors: Kevin Friddle
abstract: Student game design project: Call of Space Ring Tremorfieldfrontwatch: Advanced Global Ops Warfare 4.

suggestions.title: High-Fidelity Destruction System
suggestions.desc: Implement a Chaos Physics (Unreal) or Rayfire (Unity) system for 'Mega-Destruction.' Allow buildings and cover to be realistically pulverized based on the caliber of the weapon used, affecting tactical positioning.

suggestions.title: Global Ops Squad-Lead Voice Commands
suggestions.desc: Integrate speech-to-text (Whisper API) to allow players to give tactical orders to AI squadmates (e.g., 'Flank left', 'Provide cover fire') using their actual voice during high-intensity combat.

suggestions.title: Satellite-to-Ground Tactical View
suggestions.desc: Create a secondary 'Commander Mode' accessible via a mobile app. A second player can view the 3D battlefield from a top-down satellite perspective and deploy UAVs or supply drops for the FPS player.

suggestions.title: Neural Network Difficulty Balancing
suggestions.desc: Develop a 'Director AI' similar to Left 4 Dead that uses a neural network to monitor player heart rate (via sensor) or performance metrics to dynamically spawn enemies and adjust loot drops to maintain a 'Flow State'.

project_key: legacy:GM-09|Spring 2017|call of space ring tremorfieldfrontwatch: advanced global ops warfare 4

title: Enlivening Purge
authors: Kevin Witt
abstract: Student game design project: Enlivening Purge.

suggestions.title: Compute Shader Decay Visuals
suggestions.desc: Create a 'Purge' visual effect using compute shaders that allows the player to 'clean' or 'corrupt' the world in real-time. This should dynamically change textures, lighting, and navmesh data as the player progresses.

suggestions.title: Asymmetric Horror Multiplayer
suggestions.desc: Transform the purge mechanic into a 4v1 multiplayer experience. One player controls the 'Source' of the corruption with an RTS-style view, while four 'Purgers' try to cleanse the map using FPS mechanics.

suggestions.title: Biometric Sanity System
suggestions.desc: Interface the game with an Arduino-based pulse sensor. If the player’s real-world heart rate exceeds a threshold, the in-game 'Enlivening' effect becomes more erratic, introducing visual hallucinations and shaky aim.

suggestions.title: Non-Euclidean Spatial Manipulation
suggestions.desc: Implement a 'Purge' mechanic that alters the geometry of the level. Use stencil buffers and portal rendering to create rooms that change size or connect to different areas only after certain areas are purified.

title: Worlds of Rescue
authors: Lauren Sisk
abstract: Student game design project: Worlds of Rescue.

suggestions.title: Advanced Momentum & Grappling Physics
suggestions.desc: Implement a physics-based movement system centered on a procedural grappling hook. Use Verlets integration for rope physics, allowing players to swing, climb, and pull objects to navigate rescue scenarios.

suggestions.title: Multi-Specialist Cooperative Mode
suggestions.desc: Design a class-based co-op system where players must choose roles (e.g., Medic, Engineer, Scout). Each role has a unique toolset (e.g., drones, portable bridges) required to solve multi-stage rescue puzzles.

suggestions.title: Real-World Disaster Scenario Training
suggestions.desc: Partner with emergency response data to recreate digital versions of historic flood or fire zones. Implement realistic water and fire propagation (Cellular Automata) to teach players basic triage and evacuation priorities.

suggestions.title: Emergent AI Life-Signs Simulation
suggestions.desc: Create an NPC system where survivors have 'vitals' (body temperature, oxygen, blood loss). The player must use limited resources to stabilize NPCs before the timer runs out, requiring strategic prioritization.

title: Eden Centauri Game
authors: Michael Williams, Matthew Lamneck
abstract: Student game design project: Eden Centauri Game.

suggestions.title: Genetic Algorithm Creature Breeding
suggestions.desc: Develop a system where alien fauna in Eden Centauri have DNA-based traits (speed, aggression, color). Use genetic algorithms to allow creatures to evolve over game-time or through player-controlled cross-breeding.

suggestions.title: Automated Colony Logic Scripting
suggestions.desc: Implement an in-game visual scripting language (like Redstone or Factorio logic) that allows players to automate resource gathering, oxygen filtration, and defense turrets to manage their Centauri outpost.

suggestions.title: VR Xeno-Botanist Field Guide
suggestions.desc: Create a 'Discovery' mode for VR where players explore the environment to scan and catalog flora. Use high-resolution scans and spatial audio to make the alien forest an educational, immersive experience.

suggestions.title: Dynamic Ecosystem Equilibrium Model
suggestions.desc: Build a Lotka-Volterra based ecological simulation. If the player over-harvests a specific plant or hunts a certain species, it should trigger a cascade effect that alters the entire food chain and resource availability.

title: Project DSM
authors: Skylar Romocki
abstract: Student game design project: Project DSM.

suggestions.title: Graph-Based Narrative State Machine
suggestions.desc: Develop a complex branching story system using the Ink scripting language. Build a custom visualizer in Unity to track the 'Mental State' (based on DSM criteria) of the protagonist, affecting available dialogue options.

suggestions.title: Emotionally-Aware NPC Personalities
suggestions.desc: Integrate an 'Affordance' system for NPCs where their reactions are based on the Five Factor Model (Big Five). Use this to determine how they react to the player's 'Project DSM' interventions or actions.

suggestions.title: Psychological Horror via GAN Distortion
suggestions.desc: Implement a post-processing effect that uses Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) to subtly distort the game world textures when the character's 'Sanity' meter is low, creating a unique, surreal visual experience.

suggestions.title: Behavioral Data Analysis Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Create a researcher’s tool that tracks player choices and movements. Use this data to generate a 'Psychological Profile' of the player based on their ethical decisions within the game's narrative framework.

title: Parallel
authors: Sterling LaVigne, Dereck Mills, Claire Oliphant
abstract: Student game design project: Parallel.

suggestions.title: Asymmetric Dual-World Mechanics
suggestions.desc: Expand the 'Parallel' concept by implementing a rendering pipeline that draws two different versions of a level simultaneously using stencil buffers. Introduce mechanics where physical objects in the 'Shadow' dimension affect gravity in the 'Light' dimension, requiring players to solve puzzles across both planes.

suggestions.title: Cross-Platform Synchronized Multiplayer
suggestions.desc: Develop a networking layer using Mirror or Photon where two players inhabit the same space but in different dimensions. One player acts as the guide while the other interacts with physical obstacles, necessitating real-time voice and gameplay coordination to progress.

suggestions.title: Procedural Dimension Generation
suggestions.desc: Create an algorithm that generates mirrored level geometry where a 'collapse' in one dimension procedurally generates a 'growth' in the other. Use Perlin noise and seed-based generation to ensure that the parallel environments remain navigable and balanced for platforming.

suggestions.title: Dimension-Shifting Speedrun Mode
suggestions.desc: Integrate a global leaderboard and ghost-run system using Firebase. Add a 'Perfect Sync' mechanic where switching dimensions at specific frame-windows provides a momentum boost, turning the game into a high-skill movement shooter or platformer.

title: Gojo Solo 2
authors: Josh Williams
abstract: Student game design project: Gojo Solo 2.

suggestions.title: Dynamic Destructible Environment System
suggestions.desc: Implement a voxel-based or mesh-slicing destruction system to mimic high-power anime combat. Use Unity's Job System and Burst Compiler to allow the player's abilities to realistically level buildings and terrain without dropping the frame rate.

suggestions.title: Behavior Tree AI for Rival Bosses
suggestions.desc: Replace basic enemy scripts with a complex Behavior Tree using NodeCanvas or a custom implementation. Create bosses that learn player patterns and adapt their 'cursed techniques' based on the player's most frequently used moves, forcing tactical diversity.

suggestions.title: VR Combat Immersion Port
suggestions.desc: Rebuild the control scheme for OpenXR to allow players to perform hand gestures (tracked via controllers or hand-tracking) to trigger specific abilities. Focus on haptic feedback patterns that vary depending on the 'energy' cost of the move being performed.

suggestions.title: Cursed Energy Resource Management Sim
suggestions.desc: Add a strategic layer where combat performance generates a currency used to upgrade a persistent 'Domain Expansion' hub. Use a skill tree system where players must balance offensive power against the physical strain on the character, introducing a survival-lite mechanic.

title: Bacterius
authors: Dave Smith, Moises Rosabal, Aarth Thakore
abstract: Student game design project: Bacterius.

suggestions.title: Bio-Realistic Evolution Algorithm
suggestions.desc: Replace static upgrades with a genetic algorithm where the 'Bacterius' entity evolves traits (speed, toxin resistance, membrane thickness) based on the specific pathogens it consumes. Use a DNA-point system to allow for billions of possible mutation combinations.

suggestions.title: Educational 'Micro-Body' Campaign
suggestions.desc: Develop levels modeled after real human anatomical systems (respiratory, circulatory) using medical imaging data. Integrate educational pop-ups and challenges that reflect how real bacteria interact with white blood cells and antibiotics, making it a STEM learning tool.

suggestions.title: Soft-Body Physics and Fluid Dynamics
suggestions.desc: Implement 2D soft-body physics using JellyMesh or custom spring-mass particles so the bacterium squishes and stretches realistically through tight gaps. Add a 2D fluid simulation for the 'cytoplasm' environment that affects movement speed and projectile trajectories.

suggestions.title: Competitive Colony Multiplayer
suggestions.desc: Create an 'Infection' mode where players compete to colonize a host organ first. Use a headless server architecture (like Nakama) to support large-scale battles between hundreds of player-controlled bacteria in a persistent, evolving petri-dish environment.

title: Deep Convolutional Neural Network And Parallel Programming
authors: Tala Emami, Ihssan Hashem
abstract: Deep neural networks for detecting abnormality from image feeds using GPU parallel processing for accelerated learning.
topics: Deep Learning, GPU, Computer Vision, Parallel Computing

suggestions.title: Multi-GPU Distributed Training Pipeline
suggestions.desc: Extend the project to use Horovod or PyTorch DistributedDataParallel to scale the CNN training across multiple GPUs. Focus on optimizing the communication overhead and analyzing how batch size scaling impacts the convergence of abnormality detection models.

suggestions.title: Edge Deployment on NVIDIA Jetson
suggestions.desc: Optimize the trained CNN using TensorRT for real-time inference on edge devices like the NVIDIA Jetson Orin. Build a portable 'Abnormality Detector' kit that can be attached to existing analog CCTV systems to provide instant digital alerts.

suggestions.title: Synthetic Anomaly Generation with GANs
suggestions.desc: Use Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) to create a synthetic dataset of rare abnormalities that are difficult to find in real-world feeds. Evaluate if augmenting the training set with these synthetic images improves the model’s F1-score on edge-case detections.

suggestions.title: Medical Imaging Cross-Domain Application
suggestions.desc: Apply the parallelized CNN architecture to a different domain, such as detecting anomalies in MRI or CT scans. Use the NIH Chest X-ray dataset to prove that the accelerated learning techniques developed for video feeds are effective for high-resolution medical diagnostics.

project_key: legacy:GRCS-01|Spring 2017|deep convolutional neural network and parallel programming

title: Improving Classification Performance for Malware Detection
authors: Carlos Cepeda Mora, Pablo Ordonez, Chia-tien Dan Lo
abstract: Feature selection and malware detection model using machine learning algorithms achieving state-of-the-art accuracy with only nine features.
topics: Cybersecurity, Machine Learning, Malware Detection, Feature Selection

suggestions.title: Explainable AI (XAI) for Malware Attribution
suggestions.desc: Integrate SHAP or LIME to visualize why the nine specific features were chosen and how they contribute to a 'malicious' classification. This helps security researchers understand the underlying behavior of the malware rather than just receiving a binary result.

suggestions.title: Adversarial Robustness Hardening
suggestions.desc: Test the model against adversarial attacks where malware authors purposefully tweak those nine features to bypass detection. Implement adversarial training techniques to make the classifier more resilient against 'obfuscated' or 'polymorphic' malware variants.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Kernel Monitor Integration
suggestions.desc: Develop a lightweight Windows service or Linux kernel module that extracts these nine features in real-time from active processes. Create a system-tray application that alerts users and kills processes the moment the ML model flags them as high-risk.

suggestions.title: IoT-Specific Malware Detection Profile
suggestions.desc: Adapt the feature selection process specifically for ARM-based IoT devices (like smart home hubs). Research if a different set of low-level features is more effective for detecting Mirai-style botnets while maintaining the project's 'minimal feature' philosophy for low-power hardware.

similar: legacy:GR-07|Fall 2016|feature selection and improving classification performance for malware detection
project_key: legacy:GRCS-02|Spring 2017|improving classification performance for malware detection

title: Android App for Identifying Digital Signage Viewer
authors: Dane Hylton
abstract: Android app to count people viewing digital signage and predict their ethnicity/race and age.
topics: Computer Vision, Android, Demographics, Deep Learning

suggestions.title: Privacy-Preserving Federated Learning
suggestions.desc: Implement a Federated Learning framework (using OpenMined or Flower) where the app trains on local demographic data without ever sending raw facial images to a central server. This ensures GDPR compliance while still improving the model's accuracy over time.

suggestions.title: Dynamic Content Orchestration Engine
suggestions.desc: Build an API that connects the app's demographic output to a digital signage CMS. Automatically switch the displayed advertisement in real-time based on the predicted age and ethnicity of the current viewers to maximize marketing relevance and engagement.

suggestions.title: Attention and Gaze Tracking Analytics
suggestions.desc: Extend the CV model to not only identify demographics but also calculate 'dwell time' and 'eye gaze' metrics. Use MediaPipe to determine if the viewer is actually looking at the screen, providing advertisers with a 'Cost Per Look' metric rather than just 'Cost Per Impression'.

suggestions.title: On-Device Model Quantization
suggestions.desc: Convert the existing Deep Learning model to TensorFlow Lite with 8-bit integer quantization. Measure the trade-off between the slight drop in demographic accuracy and the massive increase in battery life and frame rate for older Android devices.

similar: legacy:GRCS-14|Spring 2017|android app development for gender/age estimation
project_key: legacy:GRCS-03|Spring 2017|android app for identifying digital signage viewer

title: Driving Assistant Android App based on Computer Vision
authors: Shade Alabsa
abstract: Android driving assistant implementing lane detection and collision detection with driver feedback.
topics: Computer Vision, Android, Autonomous Driving, Safety

suggestions.title: Night-Vision and Low-Light Enhancement
suggestions.desc: Implement a preprocessing pipeline using Zero-DCE or Gamma correction to make the lane and collision detection functional during night-time driving. Use a dataset like BDD100K to train the model on adverse weather conditions like heavy rain or fog.

suggestions.title: V2X Simulation with CARLA
suggestions.desc: Connect the Android app to the CARLA autonomous driving simulator via a local network socket. Use the app to control a virtual car, testing how the lane detection handles 'edge case' road layouts that would be too dangerous to test on real-world streets.

suggestions.title: Driver Drowsiness & Distraction Alert
suggestions.desc: Add a secondary 'interior' mode using the front-facing camera to monitor the driver's eyes. Use dlib for facial landmark detection to identify signs of drowsiness (eye-closure duration) or distraction (looking away from the road) and trigger an audible alarm.

suggestions.title: OBD-II Data Fusion for Velocity Tracking
suggestions.desc: Integrate a Bluetooth OBD-II adapter to pull real-time vehicle speed and braking data. Fuse this telemetric data with the computer vision collision detection to provide more accurate 'Time-to-Collision' warnings and reduce false positives caused by rapid deceleration.

similar: legacy:GRCS-07|Spring 2017|identifying potential bottlenecks on interstate highways
project_key: legacy:GRCS-04|Spring 2017|driving assistant android app based on computer vision

title: Car Tag Identification Android App
authors: Sanjoosh Akkineni
abstract: Android app analyzing car tag images to extract tag numbers and retrieve owner information from a database.
topics: Computer Vision, Android, OCR, License Plate Recognition

suggestions.title: Blockchain-Based Vehicle History Integration
suggestions.desc: Link the extracted tag numbers to a mock blockchain ledger that stores the vehicle’s service history, accident reports, and ownership changes. This creates a secure, decentralized 'CarFax' alternative that can be accessed instantly via a license plate scan.

suggestions.title: Super-Resolution for Blurry Plates
suggestions.desc: Implement a Generative Adversarial Network (GAN) for image super-resolution to reconstruct legible text from low-quality, motion-blurred, or zoomed-in tag images. This significantly improves the OCR accuracy for cars moving at high speeds or in low-light environments.

suggestions.title: Parking Lot Management & Billing System
suggestions.desc: Transform the app into a full-scale parking management solution. Use a local SQLite database to track 'Check-in' and 'Check-out' times by scanning plates, and integrate a payment API (like Stripe) to automatically bill users based on their duration of stay.

suggestions.title: Multi-Region Template Matching
suggestions.desc: Expand the OCR system to recognize the specific design templates of all 50 U.S. states and various international formats. Use a secondary classifier to identify the state of origin first, which narrows the character set and improves OCR confidence scores.

title: Picture Editing Android App
authors: Nidhibahen Patel
abstract: Android application to enhance and improve the quality of pictures.

suggestions.title: AI-Powered Generative Inpainting
suggestions.desc: Integrate a lightweight generative model (like LaMa or a pruned Stable Diffusion) to allow users to remove unwanted objects from photos. The app would 'fill in' the background realistically based on the surrounding pixels, moving beyond simple blurring.

suggestions.title: Neural Style Transfer Studio
suggestions.desc: Implement a fast neural style transfer module using TensorFlow Lite that allows users to apply the artistic styles of famous painters (e.g., Van Gogh, Picasso) to their photos in real-time without needing a cloud connection.

suggestions.title: Cloud-Synced Collaborative Editing
suggestions.desc: Build a backend using Firebase and Google Cloud Functions to allow multiple users to edit the same high-resolution photo simultaneously. Implement an 'edit history' stack that allows users to undo specific changes made by other collaborators.

suggestions.title: Automated Batch Product Photography
suggestions.desc: Add a 'Professional Mode' specifically for e-commerce sellers that automatically removes backgrounds, adjusts white balance to a standard 'studio' look, and resizes images to fit Amazon/eBay specifications in a single batch process.

title: Identifying Potential Bottlenecks on Interstate Highways
authors: Betty Kretlow
abstract: Classifying techniques on traffic camera image data to identify potential bottlenecks on interstate highways.
topics: Computer Vision, Traffic Analysis, Deep Learning

suggestions.title: Predictive Bottleneck Forecasting
suggestions.desc: Shift from 'identification' to 'prediction' by implementing an LSTM or Temporal Graph Convolutional Network. Use historical traffic camera data to predict where a bottleneck is likely to form 30 minutes before it happens based on current flow trends.

suggestions.title: Waze-API Integration for Driver Rerouting
suggestions.desc: Develop a middleware that pushes detected bottleneck locations to traffic navigation APIs like Waze or Google Maps. Create a dashboard for city planners that suggests real-time 'diversion routes' to mitigate the impact of the detected congestion.

suggestions.title: Multi-Camera Vehicle Tracking (Re-ID)
suggestions.desc: Implement Vehicle Re-Identification (Re-ID) to track specific cars across multiple interstate cameras. This allows for the calculation of 'true travel time' and 'average segment speed,' providing much deeper data than simple density-based bottleneck detection.

suggestions.title: Weather-Impact Analysis Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Correlate traffic camera data with real-time weather APIs (OpenWeather). Analyze and visualize how different types of precipitation or visibility levels lower the 'bottleneck threshold' of specific highway interchanges to help DOTs plan winter maintenance.

similar: legacy:GRCS-04|Spring 2017|driving assistant android app based on computer vision
project_key: legacy:GRCS-07|Spring 2017|identifying potential bottlenecks on interstate highways

title: A New Paradigm for Interference-Aware Energy Harvesting
authors: Jiaxin Chen
abstract: Analysis of device-to-device communication and algorithm design for interference-aware energy harvesting.
topics: Wireless Networks, Energy Harvesting, D2D Communications

suggestions.title: Low-Power LoRa Mesh Implementation
suggestions.desc: Port the energy-harvesting algorithms to LoRaWAN-enabled microcontrollers like the ESP32-S3. Build a physical mesh network that prioritizes data transmission paths based on real-time solar or RF energy levels collected from harvesting modules.

suggestions.title: Multi-Hop Cooperative D2D Relaying
suggestions.desc: Extend the interference-aware model to include multi-hop relaying where intermediate devices act as energy-neutral repeaters. Use the NS-3 simulator to evaluate how cooperative relaying affects total network throughput versus energy depletion rates.

suggestions.title: Smart Agriculture Sensor Persistence
suggestions.desc: Apply the energy-harvesting logic to a fleet of soil moisture sensors in a simulated agricultural environment. Optimize the D2D communication schedule to ensure that nodes in shaded areas (interference-heavy) remain operational by 'borrowing' transmission windows from well-lit nodes.

suggestions.title: Battery-Free Asset Tracking Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Develop a commercial-ready monitoring dashboard using React and MQTT that tracks the 'energy health' of harvest-powered tags. Integrate a predictive maintenance module that alerts users when specific network nodes are likely to fail due to high interference and low harvesting yield.

project_key: legacy:GRCS-08|Spring 2017|a new paradigm for interference-aware energy harvesting

title: Machine Learning for Understanding Amazon Product Success
authors: Michael Kranzlein
abstract: Application of big data and machine learning techniques to understand driving factors in product success on Amazon.
topics: Machine Learning, E-Commerce, Big Data, Business Intelligence

suggestions.title: Dynamic Price Optimization Engine
suggestions.desc: Build a tool for Amazon sellers that suggests real-time price adjustments based on the identified success factors. Use Python and the Amazon SP-API to automate pricing changes that maximize the 'Buy Box' win rate while maintaining the product success score.

suggestions.title: Visual Quality and Sentiment Integration
suggestions.desc: Enhance the dataset by scraping product images and using Computer Vision (OpenCV/PyTorch) to analyze image quality. Incorporate sentiment analysis of user-uploaded photos in reviews to see if visual authenticity correlates more highly with success than professional marketing shots.

suggestions.title: Counterfeit and Hijacker Detection System
suggestions.desc: Use the success factor model to identify anomalies that suggest a product listing has been hijacked or replaced by counterfeits. Build a monitoring bot that alerts brand owners when a product's success metrics (like conversion rate) deviate sharply from the historical baseline.

suggestions.title: Cross-Platform Market Comparison
suggestions.desc: Extend the analysis to include Walmart and eBay datasets to determine if 'success factors' are universal or platform-specific. Conduct a comparative study on how different recommendation algorithms across these sites favor specific product attributes.

project_key: legacy:GRCS-09|Spring 2017|machine learning for understanding amazon product success

title: Auditory and Haptic Feedback 3D UI for Blind People
authors: Darren O'Neale
abstract: HTC Vive research on auditory and haptic feedback to enhance navigation independence in the real world for visually impaired.
topics: Accessibility, Virtual Reality, HCI, Assistive Technology

suggestions.title: LiDAR-to-Haptic Spatial Mapping
suggestions.desc: Integrate an iPhone Pro LiDAR or a specialized sensor like the Intel RealSense with the Vive logic to map real-world obstacles in real-time. Convert distance data into varying haptic frequencies delivered through a wearable belt, allowing users to 'feel' the room's geometry.

suggestions.title: Public Transit Audio-Wayfinding App
suggestions.desc: Create a mobile application that uses spatialized audio cues to guide visually impaired users through complex environments like subway stations. Use Google’s Resonance Audio SDK to create 'audio beacons' at specific gates and platform edges.

suggestions.title: Bone Conduction vs. Spatial Audio Study
suggestions.desc: Research the cognitive load differences between using standard headphones versus bone conduction headsets for navigation. Measure user reaction times and 'environmental awareness' scores to determine which hardware provides safer real-world utility.

suggestions.title: Retail Accessibility AR Overlay
suggestions.desc: Develop a product for retailers that uses AR tags to provide auditory descriptions of store shelves. When a user points their phone or headset at a shelf, the system provides haptic confirmation and spatial audio descriptions of product placement and pricing.

project_key: legacy:GRCS-10|Spring 2017|auditory and haptic feedback 3d ui for blind people

title: Research on Detecting Malware using Encrypted Traffic
authors: Uday Bhaskar Boyanapalli
abstract: Detecting malware using encrypted SSL/TLS traffic through collection and evaluation of cryptographic standards with RC4.
topics: Cybersecurity, Network Security, Malware Detection, Encryption

suggestions.title: TLS 1.3 JA3 Fingerprinting Integration
suggestions.desc: Modernize the detection system by implementing JA3 and JA3S fingerprinting techniques to identify malicious clients even when traffic is fully encrypted via TLS 1.3. Build a Zeek plugin to automate this fingerprint collection across a live university network.

suggestions.title: Adversarial Evasion Benchmarking
suggestions.desc: Create a suite of 'chaff' traffic generators that mimic normal user behavior to see if the ML model can be tricked. Research how malware authors might use traffic shaping or 'domain fronting' to hide RC4-like patterns within standard HTTPS flows.

suggestions.title: IoT Shadow IT Detection Gateway
suggestions.desc: Apply the malware detection logic to a Raspberry Pi-based home gateway. Focus on detecting 'phone-home' signals from cheap IoT devices that may be using non-standard encryption or deprecated RC4 suites to exfiltrate data.

suggestions.title: Browser-Based Cryptographic Health Monitor
suggestions.desc: Develop a Chrome extension that analyzes the encryption standards of all active background connections. Provide users with a 'security score' and alert them if background scripts are communicating via vulnerable or suspicious encrypted tunnels.

project_key: legacy:GRCS-11|Spring 2017|research on detecting malware using encrypted traffic

title: OpenFlow Flow Table Overflow Vulnerability
authors: Xianyong Meng
abstract: Investigation of OpenFlow flow table overflow vulnerabilities in SDN networks and defense strategies.

suggestions.title: P4-Programmable Hardware Mitigation
suggestions.desc: Move the defense strategy from software-defined controllers to the data plane using P4. Implement a line-rate packet filter on a programmable switch (or BMv2 simulator) that drops overflow-inducing packets before they reach the control plane.

suggestions.title: Multi-Tenant SDN Isolation Framework
suggestions.desc: Research how to partition flow table resources in a multi-tenant cloud environment to prevent a 'noisy neighbor' overflow attack. Build a hypervisor-layer manager that enforces strict flow entry quotas per tenant.

suggestions.title: Zero-Trust SD-WAN Security Layer
suggestions.desc: Integrate the overflow defense into an SD-WAN architecture. Use a centralized controller to distribute 'trust scores' to edge routers, dynamically adjusting flow table priority based on the source's historical behavior.

suggestions.title: SDN Intrusion Prevention Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Create a commercial monitoring UI for network admins that visualizes flow table occupancy in real-time. Implement an 'Auto-Mitigate' button that triggers a script to aggregate flow rules (wildcarding) when a potential overflow attack is detected.

title: Sharing of Lecture Notes
authors: Nidhi Patel
abstract: System for sharing lecture notes among students in an educational setting.

suggestions.title: OCR and AI Summary Pipeline
suggestions.desc: Integrate Tesseract OCR and the OpenAI API to automatically transcribe handwritten uploaded notes. Add a feature that generates a 5-bullet summary and a 'key terms' glossary for every shared document to improve searchability.

suggestions.title: Collaborative Knowledge Graph Construction
suggestions.desc: Build a tool that links shared notes from different students based on shared concepts. Use Neo4j to visualize these connections, creating a 'map' of the course that allows students to discover related topics across different lecture dates.

suggestions.title: Incentivized Academic Marketplace
suggestions.desc: Implement a gamified reward system using a private blockchain or simple database credits. Students earn 'tokens' for uploading high-quality notes (verified by peer upvotes) which can then be redeemed for tutoring sessions or premium study materials.

suggestions.title: Learning Analytics and Gap Detection
suggestions.desc: Develop an algorithm that analyzes the aggregate content of shared notes to identify 'missing' topics from the syllabus. Provide professors with a dashboard showing which concepts students are struggling to document effectively.

similar: legacy:GR-03|Spring 2016|iot system to share lecture notes via clouds

title: Android App Development for Gender/Age Estimation
authors: Olivier Noumbi, Dhiraj Gharana
abstract: Android application for estimating gender and age from facial images using deep learning.
topics: Computer Vision, Android, Deep Learning, Demographics

suggestions.title: On-Device Quantization and Speedup
suggestions.desc: Optimize the deep learning model using TensorFlow Lite's Post-Training Quantization to run at 60FPS on mid-range Android devices. Benchmark the battery impact of running the inference on the mobile GPU versus the NPU.

suggestions.title: Smart Digital Signage for Retail
suggestions.desc: Connect the Android app to a large display system that changes advertisements based on the estimated age and gender of the person standing in front of it. Use a privacy-first approach where face data is processed locally and never stored.

suggestions.title: Fairness and Bias Mitigation Study
suggestions.desc: Evaluate the model's accuracy across different ethnicities and lighting conditions. Implement a 'de-biasing' layer in the training pipeline (using datasets like FairFace) to ensure the application provides equitable results for all users.

suggestions.title: Anonymous Crowd Analytics Platform
suggestions.desc: Turn the app into a tool for event organizers to track crowd demographics at entry points. Build a centralized dashboard using Firebase that aggregates demographic counts without storing personally identifiable images.

title: Identifying Cancer Subtypes
authors: Tejaswini Mallavarapu
abstract: Identification of cancer subtypes using clustering methods on genomic data.
topics: Bioinformatics, Machine Learning, Cancer Research, Clustering

suggestions.title: Multi-Omics Data Fusion
suggestions.desc: Extend the clustering model to incorporate not just genomic data, but also transcriptomic and proteomic datasets. Use a Multi-view Clustering approach to find more robust subtypes that are consistent across different biological layers.

suggestions.title: Explainable Biomarker Discovery
suggestions.desc: Apply SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) to the clustering results to identify which specific genes are the most influential in defining each subtype. Build a web tool where researchers can click on a cluster to see its 'genetic signature'.

suggestions.title: Personalized Drug Response Predictor
suggestions.desc: Map the identified cancer subtypes to known drug sensitivity databases (like GDSC). Develop a predictive model that suggests the most effective FDA-approved chemotherapy or targeted therapy based on a patient's subtype classification.

suggestions.title: Clinician-Facing Diagnostic Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Design a React-based frontend that allows doctors to upload a patient's CSV genomic profile and receive a subtype report. Include visualizations like UMAP or t-SNE plots to show where the patient sits relative to the historical cluster data.

title: Engineering an AI that can solve insight puzzles
authors: Oscar Garcia
abstract: Neural network designed to synthesize algorithms from trained steps to solve complex insight puzzles without prior training.
topics: Artificial Intelligence, Neural Networks, Problem Solving

suggestions.title: Neuro-Symbolic Logic Integration
suggestions.desc: Combine the neural network with a symbolic logic engine (like Prolog) to handle rule-based constraints in puzzles. Research how this hybrid approach improves the AI's ability to explain its reasoning steps to a human user.

suggestions.title: Chain-of-Thought LLM Fine-Tuning
suggestions.desc: Fine-tune an open-source LLM like Mistral-7B on a specialized dataset of 'insight puzzles.' Implement a 'reflection' loop where the AI must check its own intermediate steps for logical consistency before finalizing an answer.

suggestions.title: Automated Software Logic Debugger
suggestions.desc: Apply the puzzle-solving engine to find logical contradictions in complex software configurations (e.g., Kubernetes YAMLs or IAM policies). Treat the configuration as a 'puzzle' and use the AI to synthesize a sequence of steps to reach a secure state.

suggestions.title: AI Logic Tutor for K-12
suggestions.desc: Build an educational platform that generates 'insight puzzles' at varying difficulty levels. Use the AI to provide 'hints' that don't give the answer away, but instead point the student toward the algorithm they need to synthesize.

project_key: legacy:GRCS-16|Spring 2017|engineering an ai that can solve insight puzzles

title: Performance Analysis: Machine Learning via CPU vs GPU
authors: Jhu-Sin Luo
abstract: Analysis of neural network training and validation times on MNIST using CPU vs GPU, measuring execution time and energy consumption.
topics: GPU, Machine Learning, Performance Analysis, Deep Learning

suggestions.title: Edge NPU and TPU Performance Benchmarking
suggestions.desc: Expand the study to include specialized hardware like the Apple Neural Engine (ANE) or Coral Edge TPU. Compare the energy efficiency (in Joules per inference) of these dedicated chips against standard laptop CPUs and GPUs.

suggestions.title: Energy-Delay Product (EDP) Analysis
suggestions.desc: Go beyond simple time measurements to calculate the EDP for different neural network architectures (CNNs vs. Transformers). Use a hardware power meter to capture granular energy data and identify the 'sweet spot' for model complexity.

suggestions.title: Cloud Instance Selection Optimizer
suggestions.desc: Create a Python script that profiles a user's specific ML model and recommends the cheapest AWS/Azure instance type based on the performance-to-cost ratio. Integrate live pricing APIs to ensure recommendations are up to date.

suggestions.title: Sustainable AI Training Scheduler
suggestions.desc: Develop a middleware that schedules ML training tasks based on the current carbon intensity of the power grid. Automatically toggle between CPU (low power) and GPU (high speed) to minimize the carbon footprint of the training session.

title: Monitoring and Assessing Traffic Safety Using Live Video
authors: Srivarna Settisara Janney, Ishraq Karim
abstract: Automatic extraction of traffic conflict event data from field cameras for real-time safety monitoring and accident prevention.
topics: Computer Vision, Traffic Safety, Deep Learning, Video Analysis

suggestions.title: Hilbert-Curve Malware Visualization
suggestions.desc: Replace the 3x3 grid with a Hilbert Curve mapping to convert 1D binary code into a 2D image. This space-filling curve preserves the locality of code blocks much better than a simple grid, potentially leading to higher LBP feature accuracy.

suggestions.title: Adversarial Robustness in Malware Imaging
suggestions.desc: Investigate how 'obfuscation' techniques like binary packing or junk code insertion change the LBP visual signature. Build a robust classifier that can detect the underlying malware family even when the binary has been 'salted' to change its visual appearance.

suggestions.title: Visual Sandbox Threat Scanner
suggestions.desc: Build a desktop application that allows security analysts to drag and drop suspicious executables to see their 'visual fingerprint' instantly. The app should display the image, the LBP histogram, and the top 3 predicted malware families.

suggestions.title: Unsupervised Malware Family Clustering
suggestions.desc: Implement an unsupervised learning model (like HDBSCAN) on the LBP features to identify and cluster new, previously unknown malware variants. This could help cybersecurity teams identify 'Zero-Day' campaigns that share visual similarities with known threats.

title: Unintended Data Leakage Attacks and their Countermeasures
authors: Ravi Patel
abstract: Exposing different attacks to obtain information from Android devices and implementing countermeasures to protect data.

suggestions.title: Hybrid MOEA-UNet Semantic Segmentation
suggestions.desc: Combine the MOEA's global optimization strengths with the local feature extraction of a U-Net architecture. Use the MOEA to optimize the hyperparameters or the loss function weights of the neural network to achieve better boundary definition in complex images.

suggestions.title: CUDA-Accelerated Parallel MOEA Engine
suggestions.desc: Implement the image segmentation MOEA using NVIDIA's CUDA to handle the heavy fitness evaluations of thousands of pixels in parallel. This would transform the current approach from a slow offline process into a high-performance tool capable of processing high-resolution imagery.

suggestions.title: Autonomous Satellite Change-Detection Tool
suggestions.desc: Apply the MOEA to segment multi-temporal satellite imagery from the Sentinel-2 dataset. Build a product that automatically detects and segments urban sprawl or deforestation by optimizing for both color consistency and spatial connectivity across different time steps.

suggestions.title: Medical Histology Slide Analyzer
suggestions.desc: Adapt the MOEA to segment nuclei and glandular structures in pathology slides from the Camelyon16 dataset. Use objectives like shape regularity and texture contrast to help pathologists identify cancerous regions more accurately than single-objective methods.

title: Input Validation and Output Decoding
authors: Peter Ding
abstract: Demonstration of data sanitization consequences in input validation and output encoding for secure mobile software development.

suggestions.title: Automated HIPAA Compliance Scanner for OpenEMR
suggestions.desc: Build a static analysis tool that scans OpenEMR plugins and custom modules specifically for HIPAA-violating patterns, such as unencrypted PII logging or weak session management. Integrate this into a CI/CD pipeline using GitHub Actions.

suggestions.title: OpenEMR Security Hardening Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Create a containerized (Docker) 'Secure OpenEMR' distribution that comes pre-configured with the security fixes identified in this project. Include a dashboard for clinic admins to monitor real-time access logs and detect brute-force attempts.

suggestions.title: IoT-to-OpenEMR Secure Gateway
suggestions.desc: Develop a secure middleware that bridges wearable medical devices (like heart rate monitors) with OpenEMR. Focus on implementing the project's security findings to ensure that data in transit from the device to the patient record is encrypted and authenticated.

suggestions.title: Zero-Knowledge Proofs for EMR Access Control
suggestions.desc: Research and implement a Zero-Knowledge Proof (ZKP) layer for OpenEMR. This would allow a doctor to verify they have the necessary permissions to view a record without the system needing to expose the underlying sensitive access control lists (ACLs).

title: Auction Based Resource Allocation in D2D Communications
authors: Benjamin Lee
abstract: Auction-based resource allocation algorithm to mitigate interference in cellular D2D communication systems.

suggestions.title: Transformer-Based Polar Pruning Comparison
suggestions.desc: Replicate the SVM 'polar pruning' experiment using a Pre-trained Transformer like DistilBERT. Investigate if modern attention mechanisms are more or less sensitive to 'neutral' data noise compared to classical SVMs.

suggestions.title: Active Learning for Dynamic Review Pruning
suggestions.desc: Implement an Active Learning loop that uses the SVM's uncertainty to identify which 'non-polar' samples actually provide value. Instead of omitting all non-polar data, use this tool to find the 10% of neutral reviews that help define the classification boundary.

suggestions.title: Stock Market Sentiment Noise Filter
suggestions.desc: Apply this polar classification logic to the 'StockTwits' or Reddit 'WallStreetBets' datasets. Build a system that ignores 'neutral' market updates and focuses specifically on high-conviction polar sentiments to predict short-term price movements.

suggestions.title: CleanSense: A Minimalist Review Aggregator
suggestions.desc: Develop a browser extension that uses the polar-trained SVM to filter product reviews on Amazon. It should hide the 'fluff' and 'neutral' filler text, presenting the user with only the most decisive positive and negative feedback for faster decision making.

authors: Yeojin Kim
abstract: New maximum fault-tolerance barrier-coverage problem in hybrid sensor networks with static and mobile sensors.

suggestions.title: GNN-Enhanced Malware Behavior Analysis
suggestions.desc: Integrate Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) with the Gspan-discovered patterns. Use Gspan to extract frequent subgraphs and feed them into a GNN (like GraphSAGE) to learn high-level embeddings of malware execution trees for higher detection accuracy.

suggestions.title: Android Runtime Behavior Guard
suggestions.desc: Port the Gspan detection logic to monitor Android system calls. Create an app that builds real-time call graphs and alerts users when an application's behavior matches the 'frequent patterns' of known mobile malware families like Joker or Emotet.

suggestions.title: Adversarial Graph Perturbation Defense
suggestions.desc: Research how malware can 'obfuscate' its graph structure to hide from Gspan. Develop a robust version of the Gspan algorithm that can detect malware even when the attacker injects 'noise' nodes and edges designed to break frequent subgraph patterns.

suggestions.title: ThreatIntel Visualizer for SOC Teams
suggestions.desc: Build a web-based visualization platform that uses Gspan to cluster similar malware samples into 'families.' This tool would allow Security Operation Center analysts to see a visual map of how a new threat relates to historical attacks based on shared graph patterns.

title: Graph-theory Simplification for Biological Network Analysis
authors: Euiseong Ko
abstract: Two new graph algorithms to improve efficiency of biological network data interpretation.

suggestions.title: Reinforcement Learning for Insight Discovery
suggestions.desc: Extend the neural network by adding a Reinforcement Learning (RL) layer. Train the agent to 'play' with synthesis problems (like the Duncker's Candle Problem) where it receives rewards for novel combinations that lead toward a solution.

suggestions.title: Neural Sleep-Cycle Optimization
suggestions.desc: Implement a 'consolidated learning' phase inspired by human REM sleep. After the network learns a set of synthesis problems, run a weight-pruning and re-organization algorithm that mimics synaptic pruning to see if it improves the model's 'creative' output on new problems.

suggestions.title: Automated Software Bug Synthesis
suggestions.desc: Apply the artificial cognition model to code synthesis. Build a tool that attempts to solve 'insight problems' in programming—tasks where a standard iterative approach fails and a 'creative' refactor of the logic is required to fix a complex bug.

suggestions.title: The AI Creative Design Co-Pilot
suggestions.desc: Build a plugin for architectural or game design software. The tool uses the cognition model to suggest unexpected but functional design elements (e.g., room layouts or puzzle mechanics) based on a set of 'insight constraints' provided by the user.

suggestions.desc: Replace the manual image alignment step with a Spatial Transformer Network inside the CNN. This allows the model to learn its own optimal alignment/rotation during the training phase in PyTorch or TensorFlow, making the pipeline more robust to varied aerial angles.

suggestions.title: Solar Potential and Efficiency Estimator
suggestions.desc: Combine the roof classification results with a solar irradiance API (like Google Sunroof). Create a web app that identifies roof types (flat vs. sloped) and materials to provide homeowners with an automated estimate of solar panel ROI for their specific roof.

suggestions.title: Post-Disaster Damage Assessment Bot
suggestions.desc: Fine-tune the CNN on a dataset of damaged vs. undamaged roofs (e.g., post-hurricane imagery). Build a tool for emergency responders that automatically tags roofs as 'collapsed,' 'shingle damage,' or 'intact' from drone or satellite feeds.

suggestions.title: Multi-View 3D Roof Reconstruction
suggestions.desc: Use the classified images as input for a Structure from Motion (SfM) pipeline. By combining the CNN's classification with 3D photogrammetry, build a research project that can accurately estimate the pitch and surface area of a roof from multiple aerial viewpoints.

suggestions.title: Permission Drift Anomaly Detection
suggestions.desc: Incorporate a machine learning model (e.g., Random Forest) that monitors how app permissions evolve over time. Alert users when an app's requested permissions 'drift' significantly from its original purpose.

suggestions.title: Child-Safety Smart Permission Filter
suggestions.desc: Build a parent-facing dashboard that uses the algorithm to automatically block apps that request high-risk permissions (camera, microphone) without a clear functional justification based on the app's category.

suggestions.title: Malware Fingerprinting via API Sequence Analysis
suggestions.desc: Research the sequence of API calls associated with specific malware families. Use the permission framework to create 'behavioral fingerprints' that can identify new malware variants that bypass signature-based scans.

suggestions.desc: Develop a commercial tool for developers to audit their own apps for 'over-permissioning.' Provide a certificate of 'Least Privilege Compliance' that can be used to build user trust on app stores.

suggestions.title: Financial Market Volatility Predictor

suggestions.title: Cross-Lingual Sentiment Analysis for Global News
suggestions.desc: Integrate multilingual BERT models to detect sentiment changes across different languages. This allows the HPCC system to discover global events (like geopolitical shifts) by analyzing non-English datasets simultaneously.

suggestions.title: PR Crisis Early Warning System
suggestions.desc: Build a B2B product for PR agencies that monitors brand mentions. Use the sentiment-change logic to send high-priority alerts when a negative sentiment trend begins to 'snowball,' indicating a potential viral crisis.

suggestions.title: Low-Resource E-Ink Reader Port
suggestions.desc: Optimize the platform for low-power, E-ink devices or old tablets. Use a simplified UI and local storage optimizations to make the interactive math proofs accessible in schools with limited hardware budgets.

suggestions.desc: Integrate a local, small-scale LLM (like Llama-CPP) to analyze where a student is stuck in a proof. Provide context-aware hints in natural language that guide the student without giving away the answer.

suggestions.desc: Package the interactive modules as a 'Digital Lab' that can be bundled with physical math textbooks. Focus on a distribution model for rural/remote areas using pre-loaded SD cards or USB drives.

suggestions.title: Embedded Biometric Access Controller
suggestions.desc: Port the Haar-Cascade and LBP logic to a Raspberry Pi 4 with a Pi NoIR Camera to build a physical door lock. Use the RPi.GPIO library to trigger a solenoid lock only after a successful iris match and QR validation.

suggestions.title: Liveness Detection via Frequency Domain Analysis
suggestions.desc: Extend the framework to detect 'presentation attacks' (photos/videos of eyes) by implementing a Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) analysis layer. Real human eyes exhibit specific high-frequency textures that digital screens or printouts lack.

suggestions.title: Decentralized Iris-to-Identity Bridge
suggestions.desc: Create a system that hashes the iris-generated QR code and stores it on a private blockchain (Hyperledger Fabric). This allows for verifiable identity without storing raw biometric images, enhancing privacy and security.

suggestions.title: Adaptive Lighting Iris Normalization
suggestions.desc: Improve the system's reliability in variable lighting by adding a Daugman's Integro-Differential Operator for more precise pupil boundary detection. Use OpenCV's CLAHE (Contrast Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization) to normalize images before classification.

title: Classification of Web Service Attacks and Mitigation
authors: William Bond
abstract: Classification of attacks on web services and mitigation approaches to protect against common vulnerabilities.

suggestions.title: Real-time Threat Intelligence Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Build a monitoring layer using the ELK Stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana) to visualize web service attacks in real-time. Use Logstash filters to automatically categorize incoming traffic against the project's attack classification model.

suggestions.title: Deep Learning WAF Enhancement
suggestions.desc: Develop a Web Application Firewall (WAF) extension using an LSTM (Long Short-Term Memory) neural network trained on the CSIC 2010 HTTP Dataset. This move shifts the project from signature-based mitigation to anomaly-based detection of zero-day exploits.

suggestions.title: API Stress and Vulnerability Tester
suggestions.desc: Create an automated 'Chaos Engineering' tool for web services that simulates the classified attacks (SQLi, XSS, DoS) during the CI/CD pipeline. Use Python's Requests library and OWASP ZAP's API to verify that new code deployments don't re-introduce vulnerabilities.

suggestions.title: Self-Healing Web Architecture
suggestions.desc: Implement an automated mitigation system using Docker and Kubernetes. When the classification engine detects a specific attack pattern (like a Slowloris DoS), the system should automatically trigger a scale-out event or rotate pod IP addresses to shed the malicious traffic.

title: Design and Deliver Online Courses
authors: Jennifer Cassan
abstract: Research on most efficient ways to design and deliver online courses in computer and software engineering fields.

suggestions.title: Adaptive Learning Path Engine
suggestions.desc: Build a plugin for Moodle or Canvas that uses a decision tree algorithm to reroute students through course content based on quiz performance. Students struggling with 'Memory Management' would be automatically served supplemental visual modules before moving to 'Pointers'.

suggestions.title: AI-Powered Peer Review Moderator
suggestions.desc: Implement an NLP-based tool using the OpenAI API to analyze the quality and sentiment of student-to-student feedback in online engineering forums. The tool should flag unhelpful or toxic comments and highlight high-quality technical explanations for instructors.

suggestions.title: Biometric Engagement Analytics
suggestions.desc: Research student focus by integrating a webcam-based gaze tracking library (like WebGazer.js) into the course delivery platform. Correlate gaze patterns with specific 'difficult' sections of video lectures to identify where the curriculum design needs simplification.

suggestions.title: Automated Code-Lab Provisioning System
suggestions.desc: Develop a system that spins up ephemeral VS Code Server environments via Docker for each course module. This ensures every student has an identical, pre-configured environment (compiler, debugger, libraries) directly in their browser to reduce setup friction.

title: Virtual Reality for Requirements Elicitation
authors: Aman Bhimani
abstract: Proposal for using virtual reality as a new technique for requirements elicitation in controlled immersive environments.
topics: Software Engineering, Virtual Reality, Requirements Engineering

suggestions.title: Multi-User Collaborative Elicitation Space
suggestions.desc: Transform the VR environment into a shared space using the Photon Unity Networking (PUN) framework. Allow developers and stakeholders to meet as avatars and 'draw' 3D wireframes together in real-time using basic primitive shapes.

suggestions.title: Accessibility Requirement Simulator
suggestions.desc: Create a 'Vision/Motor Impairment' filter within the VR environment to help stakeholders understand non-functional requirements. By simulating color blindness or tremors, developers can elicit more robust accessibility requirements for the software being planned.

suggestions.title: Quantitative Attention Heatmapping
suggestions.desc: Integrate eye-tracking (using Vive Pro Eye or similar) to generate heatmaps of what stakeholders look at most during a virtual walkthrough of a system UI. Use this data to objectively prioritize feature requirements over subjective interview feedback.

suggestions.title: VR-to-User Story Pipeline
suggestions.desc: Develop a voice-to-text bridge that captures stakeholder comments made inside the VR environment and automatically populates a Jira or Trello board with formatted User Stories using the 'As a [user], I want [goal]' template.

title: Empowering Requirements Elicitation through Biofeedback
authors: Albert Maine
abstract: Tool and software set analyzing biological feedback and voice data to enhance the requirements elicitation process.
topics: Software Engineering, Biometrics, Requirements Engineering, NLP

suggestions.title: Real-time Bio-Dashboard for Interviewers
suggestions.desc: Create a dashboard using Electron and D3.js that visualizes live heart rate and skin conductance (via sensors like the Empatica E4) during an elicitation interview. This helps the interviewer identify 'stress points' where a requirement might be controversial or poorly understood.

suggestions.title: NLP-Bio Correlation Engine
suggestions.desc: Build a Python script that aligns voice transcripts with biofeedback timestamps. Use the NLTK library to perform sentiment analysis on the text and compare it to the biological arousal levels to identify 'hidden' stakeholder dissatisfaction not expressed in words.

suggestions.title: Affective Requirement Prioritizer
suggestions.desc: Design a software tool that ranks requirements not just by business value, but by the 'Emotional Intensity' measured during elicitation sessions. High-arousal biofeedback combined with positive sentiment keywords would flag 'Must-Have' features.

suggestions.title: Remote Bio-Elicitation Mobile App
suggestions.desc: Develop a Flutter app that connects to consumer wearables (Apple Watch/Fitbit) to collect bio-data during remote Zoom elicitation calls. The app streams the data to a central server, allowing for biometrically-informed requirements gathering without physical proximity.

title: eSense: Biomimetic Modeling
authors: Derek Martin
abstract: Biomimetic modeling of electrolocation and echolocation using Dynamic Homeostatic Dual-Layered Reinforcement Learning.

suggestions.title: Temporal Consistency for Video Segmentation
suggestions.desc: Move from single-frame Mask R-CNN to a video-based approach using ConvLSTM or Mask2Former. This ensures that instruments are tracked smoothly across frames without 'flickering' or losing the mask during fast surgical movements.

suggestions.title: Intraoperative 'No-Go' Zone Alerts
suggestions.desc: Develop a real-time monitoring layer that defines critical tissue areas (like major arteries) as 'No-Go' zones. If the Mask R-CNN detects a surgical tool entering a predefined proximity to these areas, it triggers an audible or visual alert for the surgeon.

suggestions.title: Edge Deployment on NVIDIA Jetson
suggestions.desc: Optimize the Mask R-CNN model using TensorRT and quantization (INT8) to run at 30+ FPS on an NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano. This makes the system portable and usable in operating rooms without requiring a full server rack.

suggestions.title: Automated Post-Surgical Report Generation
suggestions.desc: Use the segmentation data to quantify the duration of the surgery, the frequency of tool usage, and the amount of tissue handled. Generate an automated PDF report summarizing the technical efficiency of the procedure for surgical training and review.

authors: Dr. Sarah North, Victor Sahin, Ben Junior Tamo

suggestions.desc: Integrate a small-parameter Large Language Model (like Phi-2 or Llama-3-8B) quantized for mobile. Build a voice-to-text interface that allows children to ask questions about the video lessons without needing an internet connection.

suggestions.title: Handwriting Assessment via Stroke Analysis
suggestions.desc: Develop a computer vision module for the 'sand tray' feature that analyzes the child's finger or stylus strokes. Use a lightweight CNN to give real-time feedback on letter formation and track literacy progress over time.

suggestions.title: Gamified Adaptive Learning Path
suggestions.desc: Create a software layer that adjusts the difficulty of the video lessons and whiteboard activities based on the child's performance. Use a Reinforcement Learning agent to maximize student engagement by offering rewards (digital badges) for completing challenging tasks.

suggestions.title: Solar-Powered Ruggedization for Remote Use
suggestions.desc: Design a 3D-printable, ruggedized casing for the tablet that includes an integrated solar charging panel. Create a 'mesh networking' feature where multiple tablets can sync data and updates when one device occasionally reaches a high-connectivity zone.

suggestions.title: Automated Real Estate Tour Guide
suggestions.desc: Program the drone to follow a pre-defined path through a house, using OpenCV to detect 'points of interest' like fireplaces or large windows. The drone should automatically center these features in the frame and take high-dynamic-range (HDR) photos.

suggestions.title: CNN-Based Aesthetic Scoring
suggestions.desc: Integrate a pre-trained Neural Image Assessment (NIMA) model onto the drone's edge processor. The drone should take multiple photos of a scene and only keep/upload the ones with the highest predicted aesthetic and technical quality scores.

suggestions.title: Autonomous Event Photographer
suggestions.desc: Modify the drone's AI to include face detection and group framing. The drone can be deployed at indoor events to hover and capture 'candid' shots by detecting smiles and eye contact using the MediaPipe library, ensuring everyone is in the frame before snapping.

authors: Jonathan Lashgari
abstract: Enhancing Java runtime performance by using GPU and JIT compilation to transparently utilize computational power.
