suggestions.title: Deep Reinforcement Learning for Real-Time Control
suggestions.desc: Replace the predictive simulation with a Deep Q-Network (DQN) that controls HVAC setpoints in real-time. Use the 'OpenAI Gym' environment to simulate the West Point building and train the agent to balance occupant comfort (CO2 and Temp) against peak energy prices.

suggestions.title: Campus-Wide Microgrid Load Balancing
suggestions.desc: Extend the peak shaving model to include other energy-intensive campus assets like electric vehicle (EV) charging stations and dorm lighting. Create a coordinated strategy where HVAC load is decreased when EV demand spikes to keep the total campus draw below a critical threshold.

suggestions.title: Commercial Smart Thermostat API for Rebates
suggestions.desc: Develop a SaaS wrapper for the optimization model that connects to smart thermostats (like Ecobee or Nest) via API. Target small business owners by automating their participation in 'Demand Response' programs, earning them utility rebates during peak hours.

suggestions.title: Occupancy Sensing via Thermal Imaging Fusion
suggestions.desc: Research the impact of real-time occupancy data on the peak shaving model. Integrate low-cost PIR or thermal sensors to feed 'human heat load' data into the HVAC simulation, allowing for more aggressive energy savings in empty zones.

title: Apotheosis
authors: Joseph DuBois, Mike Grimshaw, Jake Howard
abstract: Student game design project: Apotheosis.

suggestions.title: Biometric-Responsive Horror Mechanics
suggestions.desc: Integrate a heart rate monitor (via Arduino or Apple Watch) to the Unity/Unreal engine. The game's atmosphere should change dynamically; if the player's heart rate is low, the game spawns 'jumpscares' or increases environmental tension to keep them in a state of fear.

suggestions.title: Procedural Phobia Generation System
suggestions.desc: Build an engine that procedurally generates levels based on specific phobias (e.g., arachnophobia, thalassophobia). Use a tagging system to determine which assets, lighting, and sound effects are loaded based on the player's selected 'fears' at the start of the game.

suggestions.title: VR Exposure Therapy Sandbox
suggestions.desc: Port the game to Meta Quest 2/3 and retool the mechanics for therapeutic use. Create a controlled environment where users can gradually increase the intensity of their phobia triggers, tracking their progress through an in-game psychological analytics dashboard.

suggestions.title: Adaptive Soundscape with Spatial Audio
suggestions.desc: Implement the FMOD audio engine to create a non-linear, adaptive soundscape. Sounds should not just be 3D, but should 'evolve' (e.g., a whisper becoming a scream) based on the player's proximity to darkness or their current 'Sanity Meter' level.

title: Horizon
authors: Jason Bourn, Jaylin Ferguson-Gillam
abstract: Student game design project: Horizon.

suggestions.title: Advanced AI Pathfinding and Senses
suggestions.desc: Implement a 'Sensory Perception' system for guard NPCs that includes peripheral vision, hearing (surface-dependent footsteps), and smell. Use Hierarchical Finite State Machines (HFSM) to allow guards to coordinate searches when they find an open window or a missing item.

suggestions.title: Community Level Editor & Steam Workshop Integration
suggestions.desc: Build an in-game map editor using a tile-based system that allows players to design their own 'unstealable' vaults. Integrate the Steam Workshop API to allow for sharing, rating, and global leaderboards for the fastest completion times.

suggestions.title: Asymmetric Multiplayer: Cat vs. Security
suggestions.desc: Develop a 1v1 multiplayer mode where one player controls the cat (stealth) and the other player acts as the Security Chief, placing traps, monitoring cameras, and directing guards in real-time from a top-down tactical view.

suggestions.title: Physics-Based Destructible Heist Environments
suggestions.desc: Integrate NVIDIA PhysX to allow for realistic environmental interaction. Knocking over a vase should create noise that alerts guards, and players should be able to use the game's physics to create distractions, like pushing a heavy object to block a door.

title: Lights Out
authors: Anthony Barrett, Brandon Seals
abstract: Student game design project: Lights Out.

suggestions.title: Unreal Engine Metahuman Integration
suggestions.desc: Upgrade the game’s character models using Epic Games’ Metahuman Creator to push the visual fidelity to the extreme. Focus on implementing 'Micro-Expressions' during dialogue scenes to intentionally trigger the Uncanny Valley effect as a narrative device.

suggestions.title: LLM-Driven Dynamic Dialogue System
suggestions.desc: Replace scripted dialogue with a local LLM (like Llama 3) to allow players to speak to NPCs using their actual voice (Speech-to-Text). NPCs should respond based on their unique personality profiles, creating unpredictable and eerie interactions.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Ray-Traced Global Illumination
suggestions.desc: Implement DirectX Raytracing (DXR) to create hyper-realistic lighting and reflections. Focus on 'Mirror Horror' mechanics, where reflections in the environment don't perfectly match the player's movements, heightening the sense of unease.

suggestions.title: Haptic Suit Integration for Immersion
suggestions.desc: Add support for haptic feedback suits (like bHaptics). Program specific vibrational patterns to simulate the sensation of something 'brushing past' the player or the physical vibration of a heavy heartbeat when the character is in a high-stress 'Uncanny' situation.

title: Open the Gates
authors: Preston Waters
abstract: Student game design project: Open the Gates.

suggestions.title: Predictive Pathfinding with Traffic Simulation
suggestions.desc: Implement an A* pathfinding extension that accounts for dynamic obstacles and traffic density. Use a weight-based cost function to simulate rush hours, requiring the player to choose between longer routes or high-traffic shortcuts.

suggestions.title: Asymmetric Multiplayer Delivery Race
suggestions.desc: Develop a 1v1 multiplayer mode using Mirror or Photon where one player acts as the courier and the other acts as a 'Dispatcher' who can trigger road closures, weather events, or boost pads in real-time.

suggestions.title: Real-World GIS Map Integration
suggestions.desc: Use the Mapbox SDK for Unity to transform real-world OpenStreetMap data into playable levels. Allow users to input their own zip code to generate a delivery course based on their actual neighborhood streets.

suggestions.title: Physics-Based Cargo Integrity System
suggestions.desc: Create a sophisticated damage model where different cargo types (fragile, liquid, explosive) respond to G-forces and collisions. Use Spring Joints and custom shaders to visualize liquid sloshing and impact fractures.

title: Patrons
authors: Anthony Barrett
abstract: Student game design project: Patrons.

suggestions.title: Procedural Guest Personalities
suggestions.desc: Integrate a local Large Language Model (LLM) or the OpenAI API to generate unique backstories and dynamic dialogue for patrons based on their procedurally generated stats and spending habits.

suggestions.title: Real-time Dynamic Economy
suggestions.desc: Implement a supply-and-demand simulation where patron satisfaction and spending affect the global price of goods, using a Node.js backend to synchronize the market across different players' sessions.

suggestions.title: Heat-Map Behavioral Analytics
suggestions.desc: Develop a telemetry system that tracks patron movement and dwell time using Unity Analytics; visualize this data as a heat map to help players optimize their shop layouts for maximum efficiency.

suggestions.title: Event-Driven Seasonal Shifts
suggestions.desc: Create a weather and holiday system that changes patron behavior and needs (e.g., higher demand for cold drinks in summer), requiring the player to swap inventory and decor seasonally.

title: Potion Panic
authors: Trevor Love
abstract: Student game design project: Potion Panic.

suggestions.title: VR Alchemy Laboratory
suggestions.desc: Port the potion-making mechanics to VR using the OpenXR toolkit, allowing users to physically pick up flasks, pour liquid using physics-based shaders, and grind ingredients with motion controllers.

suggestions.title: Asynchronous Trading Post
suggestions.desc: Build a central server using Firebase to allow players to bottle their unique concoctions and list them in a global marketplace where other players can purchase them for in-game currency.

suggestions.title: Chemistry Curriculum Bridge
suggestions.desc: Replace fictional ingredients with real-world elements and chemical compounds, using the game mechanics to teach students about ionic bonding and basic chemical reactions.

suggestions.title: Genetic Ingredient Evolution
suggestions.desc: Implement a system where players can cross-breed plants to create new ingredients, using genetic algorithms to determine the properties, color, and rarity of the resulting hybrid.

title: Slow Poke
authors: Michael Isaza
abstract: Student game design project: Slow Poke.

suggestions.title: Biometric Heart-Rate Sync
suggestions.desc: Integrate a smartwatch API to sync the character's movement speed or time-dilation effect with the player's real-time heart rate, making the game more challenging as the player becomes excited.

suggestions.title: Mobile AR Environment Interaction
suggestions.desc: Use ARCore or ARKit to place the 'Slow Poke' character in the real world, where physical furniture is mapped as obstacles through the phone's camera and LiDAR sensor.

suggestions.title: Adaptive Accessibility Controller
suggestions.desc: Develop a control scheme specifically for players with limited mobility, utilizing Tobii Eye Tracking or single-switch input methods to navigate the game's slow-paced environment.

suggestions.title: Perception-Based AI Stealth
suggestions.desc: Design an AI system for predators that detects motion through pixel-delta analysis; the character is only 'invisible' when moving below a specific velocity threshold, emphasizing the 'slow' mechanic.

title: Playstation VR Project
authors: Jason Bourn, Robert Kowalchuk, Robert Jimenez, Kevin Friddle
abstract: PSVR interactive games providing fully immersive game experiences on PlayStation 4 with real-time strategy and body motion controls.

suggestions.title: Voice-Activated Unit Command
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Wit.ai or Speech-to-Text API to allow players to issue complex tactical commands ('Archers, flank left!') verbally while using motion controllers for spatial positioning.

suggestions.title: Asymmetric Mobile Commander
suggestions.desc: Build a companion mobile app that allows a second player to view a 2D strategic map and call in support abilities (like air strikes or healing) while the VR player is on the ground.

suggestions.title: Spatial Sound Localization AI
suggestions.desc: Implement HRTF (Head-Related Transfer Function) audio cues that allow players to locate hidden enemies or objective pings solely by sound, enhancing the situational awareness required in VR RTS.

suggestions.title: Mixed Reality Strategy Table
suggestions.desc: Migrate the project to a Passthrough-enabled headset (like Quest 3) to project the strategy map onto the player's real-world table, using spatial anchors to maintain persistent unit positions.

title: (Lambda)Machine
authors: Weston Ford, Derek Maier, David Nelson
abstract: Lambda-Machine distributed computing framework that is easy to use and deploy, distributing scripts across subnet nodes with web-based UI.
topics: Distributed Systems, Cloud Computing, Parallel Computing

suggestions.title: Dockerized Edge Orchestration
suggestions.desc: Extend the framework to support Docker container execution across subnet nodes, allowing for the distribution of complex environments rather than just simple scripts.

suggestions.title: Automated Cloud-Bursting Bridge
suggestions.desc: Develop a connector for AWS Lambda that automatically scales the workload to the cloud when local subnet CPU utilization exceeds a pre-defined threshold.

suggestions.title: Raft Consensus Fault Tolerance
suggestions.desc: Implement the Raft consensus algorithm to ensure the system remains operational even if the primary coordinator node fails, allowing for seamless leader election within the local network.

suggestions.title: Distributed Video Transcoding
suggestions.desc: Create a specialized module for the framework that splits large 4K video files into chunks, processes them with FFmpeg across all available nodes, and reassembles them on the master node.

title: AccessibilityRF
authors: Mayima Clark, Jacob Ertell, John Ellison
abstract: Hardware device allowing users to remotely operate accessibility doors by reverse engineering radio frequency signals.

suggestions.title: BLE Mesh Extension
suggestions.desc: Incorporate Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) mesh networking to allow the devices to act as repeaters, extending the range of operation through large buildings without requiring stronger RF transmitters.

suggestions.title: NFC Mobile Credentialing
suggestions.desc: Develop a secure mobile app that uses the smartphone's NFC chip or Apple/Google Wallet APIs to authenticate the user before the RF signal is triggered for the door.

suggestions.title: Rolling Code Decryption
suggestions.desc: Research and implement a more robust signal processing layer capable of handling modern 'Rolling Code' security protocols found in high-security commercial door systems.

suggestions.title: Computer Vision Proximity Trigger
suggestions.desc: Integrate an ESP32-CAM module running a lightweight TensorFlow Lite model to automatically detect a wheelchair or walker approach and trigger the door without manual button presses.

title: Atlanta Toolbank Tool Reservation
authors: Kerwin Shaun Alleyne, Christian Alejandro Barahona, Alan Garrett Seymour, Mulk Abdulhadi, Kyla Marie Guarnieri
abstract: Mobile-friendly web app for Atlanta Community ToolBank to automate tool-lending reservation and confirmation process.
topics: Web Development, Mobile, Non-profit, Reservation System

suggestions.title: Predictive Demand Modeling
suggestions.desc: Use historical reservation data and a scikit-learn regression model to predict seasonal demand for specific tools, helping the ToolBank optimize their inventory purchasing and maintenance schedules.

suggestions.title: QR-Based Asset Maintenance
suggestions.desc: Build a module where volunteers can scan a QR code on a physical tool to instantly view its maintenance history, report damage, and upload photos of repairs directly to the database.

suggestions.title: Volunteer Route Optimization
suggestions.desc: Integrate the MapBox Matrix API to automatically group tool deliveries and pickups by location, providing volunteer drivers with the most fuel-efficient route for the day's tasks.

suggestions.title: LoRaWAN Geofencing
suggestions.desc: Incorporate low-power LoRaWAN GPS trackers for high-value machinery (like wood chippers) to monitor real-time location and alert staff if tools move outside the designated project boundaries.

title: AutoMD
authors: Jacob Davidson, Christopher Rene, Michael Vanderbilt
abstract: Application like WebMD for automotive issues, using user feedback to diagnose car problems and suggest solutions.

suggestions.title: Bluetooth OBD-II Integration
suggestions.desc: Connect the application to ELM327 Bluetooth scanners to pull real-time Diagnostic Trouble Codes (DTCs) from the car's computer and automatically match them to solutions in the expert system.

suggestions.title: Localized Repair Marketplace
suggestions.desc: Create a portal where users can send their 'AutoMD diagnosis' to local, highly-rated mechanics via the Yelp or Google Places API to receive instant repair quotes.

suggestions.title: Acoustic Fault Diagnosis
suggestions.desc: Implement a machine learning model trained on audio datasets (misfires, belt squeals, grinding) that can analyze engine sounds recorded through the phone's microphone to suggest potential issues.

suggestions.title: Computer Vision Part Identifier
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Google Vision API to allow users to photograph a component under the hood; the app identifies the part and provides a customized diagnosis guide for that specific item.

title: Chocolate Soup: Quest for the Compiler
authors: Jonathan Gascoigne, Nathaniel Jean, Shelby Silcox
abstract: Educational video game teaching basic programming skills to middle school and adult learners through interactive third-person adventure.
topics: Game Development, Education Technology, Programming Education

suggestions.title: Visual-to-Code Compiler Bridge
suggestions.desc: Develop an export tool that converts the player's in-game logic blocks into actual Python or JavaScript files, allowing them to see their game solutions running in a standard IDE.

suggestions.title: Multiplayer Logic Dungeons
suggestions.desc: Implement a collaborative mode using Socket.io where two players must write snippets of code or solve logic gates together to open doors and progress through the game's adventure.

suggestions.title: LLM-Powered Socratic Tutor
suggestions.desc: Integrate a local Llama-3 model to provide contextual, non-spoiler hints. If a player is stuck, the AI asks guiding questions about their logic rather than giving the answer.

suggestions.title: Minecraft Mod Curriculum
suggestions.desc: Port the educational curriculum into a Minecraft Mod using the Forge API, allowing the 'Quest' to take place in a sandbox environment that students are already familiar with.

similar: legacy:CP-05|Fall 2016|chocolate soup: quest for the compiler
legacy:UC-020|Spring 2021|analyzing concentration levels in online education using machine learning (undergraduate
project_key: legacy:CP-05|Spring 2016|chocolate soup: quest for the compiler

title: Course Substitution Management System
authors: Bilal Abdullah, Eric Brovont, Samir Dave, Andrew Walker
abstract: Automated course substitution management system for universities replacing paper forms with digital workflow.

suggestions.title: Degree Works Integration
suggestions.desc: Develop an API middleware to connect the system to Degree Works, automatically checking if a requested substitution is valid for the student's specific catalog year and degree path.

suggestions.title: Blockchain Credential Ledger
suggestions.desc: Implement a private Hyperledger Fabric blockchain to store approved substitution forms, creating a permanent, immutable record that prevents tampering and ensures audit transparency.

suggestions.title: Historical Approval Predictor
suggestions.desc: Build a recommendation engine using past substitution data to predict the likelihood of an approval, helping advisors guide students toward substitutions that have a high historical success rate.

suggestions.title: One-Swipe Admin Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Create a React Native mobile application for Department Chairs that provides a streamlined 'tinder-style' interface for quickly reviewing, approving, or denying substitution requests with push notifications.

title: Curriculum Analysis
authors: Nedko Nedev, Danette Sweat, Maria Vassileva, Mitchell Vega
abstract: Web application for analyzing and comparing curriculum standards from around the world with reliability scoring.

suggestions.title: Semantic Cross-Mapping with BERT
suggestions.desc: Implement an NLP pipeline using HuggingFace Transformers to automatically map learning objectives from different countries' curricula. By using semantic similarity, you can identify equivalent standards even when phrased differently across cultures.

suggestions.title: Labor Market Alignment Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Integrate the LinkedIn or Indeed API to compare current curriculum standards with real-world job requirements. The result would be a gap analysis tool that tells educators which industry-demanded skills are missing from their current syllabus.

suggestions.title: Dynamic Curriculum Versioning Visualizer
suggestions.desc: Build a D3.js visualization engine that tracks the evolution of a single country's standards over time. This would allow researchers to see how topics like 'Digital Literacy' have moved from high school to elementary school over the last decade.

suggestions.title: Automated Accreditation Compliance Auditor
suggestions.desc: Develop a module that checks a school's specific curriculum against international standards (like ABET or IB). Use the existing reliability scoring to generate a 'readiness report' for schools undergoing accreditation reviews.

title: DLC Training System
authors: Timothy Scott Daniel, Andrew John Griffin, Caleb Reid Green, Kristien Kramer, Timothy Huff
abstract: Alternative LMS to D2L for KSU Distance Learning Center faculty training, investigating Moodle as primary alternative.

suggestions.title: H5P Interactive Content Integration
suggestions.desc: Extend the Moodle system by developing a suite of custom H5P interactive video modules specifically for faculty training. This transforms static policy reading into branch-path decision-making exercises with real-time feedback.

suggestions.title: Faculty Competency Micro-Credentialing
suggestions.desc: Implement the Open Badges API within Moodle to create a stackable credentialing system. Faculty who complete specific DLC training modules could earn digital badges that sync directly to their LinkedIn or university portfolios.

suggestions.title: LMS Engagement Analytics Engine
suggestions.desc: Build a Python-based data dashboard that pulls logs from the Moodle API to analyze faculty drop-off points. Use this data to provide DLC administrators with a 'Heat Map' of which training modules are too difficult or disengaging.

suggestions.title: AI-Powered Course Migration Assistant
suggestions.desc: Create a tool using OpenAI's API that helps faculty translate their existing D2L content into Moodle-friendly formats. It could automatically suggest the best Moodle activity types based on the structure of the uploaded D2L documents.

title: Domo Arigato, Mr. POSTbotto
authors: Thompson Wiley, Noor Masood, Ethan Mitchell, Alvaro Ruiz, John Tyburski
abstract: Reddit bot (DAMP) designed to performmoderator tasks and auto-respond to posts based on user-specified keywords.
topics: Automation, Bots, Web Development, Social Media

suggestions.title: Sentiment-Aware Auto-Moderation
suggestions.desc: Integrate the VADER or TextBlob library to move beyond simple keyword matching. The bot could detect the emotional tone of a post, allowing it to escalate aggressive comments to human mods while auto-responding kindly to frustrated new users.

suggestions.title: Visual Moderation Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Build a React-based frontend for DAMP that allows non-technical subreddit moderators to manage bot rules via a GUI. Include a 'Dry Run' feature that shows what actions the bot would have taken on the last 100 posts before going live.

suggestions.title: Anti-Scam Image Recognition Filter
suggestions.desc: Use OpenCV or AWS Rekognition to detect common spam patterns in images posted to Reddit, such as T-shirt scams or fake crypto giveaway QR codes. The bot can then flag these for removal faster than text-based filters.

suggestions.title: Community-Driven Wiki Summarizer
suggestions.desc: Modify the bot to detect frequently asked questions and automatically reply with a summarized snippet from the subreddit's Wiki. Use an LLM to condense long Wiki pages into concise, context-aware 3-sentence answers.

title: FrigID
authors: Daniel Heimann, Dylan Vollrath
abstract: Ingredient tracking and management tool with NFC-enabled Android app and Raspberry Pi for managing fridge inventory and shopping lists.
topics: IoT, Android, NFC, Smart Home, Raspberry Pi

suggestions.title: Dynamic Smart Shopping List with Instacart API
suggestions.desc: Link the inventory data to the Instacart or Kroger API to automatically populate a shopping cart when items hit a 'low' threshold. Users could approve a weekly delivery with a single tap based on what the Raspberry Pi detects is missing.

suggestions.title: Computer Vision Fallback Layer
suggestions.desc: Since not all food has NFC tags, add a Raspberry Pi Camera Module with a YOLOv8 object detection model. The system can then 'see' produce or non-tagged items placed in the fridge, providing a hybrid tracking solution.

suggestions.title: Waste-Reduction Recipe Recommender
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Spoonacular API to suggest recipes specifically based on ingredients nearing their expiration date. The app could send a push notification: 'Your milk and spinach expire in 2 days; here is a recipe for Spinach Quiche.'

suggestions.title: Multi-Fridge Distributed Cloud Sync
suggestions.desc: Refactor the backend using AWS IoT Core to support households with multiple cooling units (e.g., a garage freezer and a kitchen fridge). This creates a unified household inventory view accessible by all family members' Android devices.

title: You Pick!
authors: Enrique Penaloza
abstract: App that helps users quickly decide where to eat by speeding up the decision-making process.

suggestions.title: WebSocket Group Decision Lobby
suggestions.desc: Implement a 'Tinder-style' swiping lobby for groups using Socket.io. Friends join a room, everyone swipes on restaurants from the Yelp API, and the app identifies the first mutual match to eliminate the 'I don't care, you pick' loop.

suggestions.title: Dietary Constraint Smart Filter
suggestions.desc: Add a profile-based filtering system that uses OCR to scan physical menus or pull data from the Google Places API. The app would automatically hide or flag restaurants that don't accommodate specific allergens or diets (e.g., Celiac-friendly or Vegan).

suggestions.title: Collaborative Filtering Recommendation Engine
suggestions.desc: Move beyond random selection by building a recommendation system using Surprise or Scikit-learn. The app could analyze a user's past choices and ratings to predict their 'craving' before they even open the app.

suggestions.title: One-Tap Transport Integration
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Uber and Lyft SDKs to show estimated wait times and prices for the chosen restaurant directly on the result screen. Users could confirm their choice and book their ride in a single consolidated workflow.

title: Heat Beep
authors: In Cho, Michael Clapper, Payne Patzke, James Pyle, Drake Rocker, Josh Saxton
abstract: Prototype device to prevent children from dying of heatstroke in hot cars, addressing the 690+ child deaths since 1998.

suggestions.title: OBD-II Smart Vehicle Integration
suggestions.desc: Connect the prototype to a vehicle's OBD-II port using an ELM327 adapter. In a critical heat event, the system could command the car to roll down windows slightly or activate the horn and hazard lights to attract passersby.

suggestions.title: Multi-Stage Twilio Alert System
suggestions.desc: Develop a cloud-based escalation ladder using the Twilio API. If the 'Beep' is ignored for 60 seconds, it texts the parent; after 120 seconds, it calls emergency contacts; after 180 seconds, it pings local emergency services with GPS coordinates.

suggestions.title: Long-Range LoRaWAN Emergency Mesh
suggestions.desc: Replace or supplement Bluetooth with LoRaWAN technology to ensure the device can send alerts even in deep parking garages with no cellular signal. This would allow a central 'hub' in a mall or parking lot to pick up distress signals from cars.

suggestions.title: Public Transit & School Bus Safety Suite
suggestions.desc: Adapt the sensor array for larger vehicles like school buses. Use a BLE mesh network of sensors under every seat that reports to a tablet in the driver's cockpit, ensuring no child is left behind at the end of a route.

title: K.R.A.B. (Keyword-Response Auto-Bot)
authors: Musab Ahmed, Misti Watkins, Niles O'Keeffe, Jordan Woodman, Shravani Yelakanti
abstract: System that automatically detects and responds to Reddit comments based on client-configured phrases.

suggestions.title: Transformer-Based Intent Classification
suggestions.desc: Upgrade the detection logic from keyword matching to intent classification using a BERT model. This allows K.R.A.B. to distinguish between a user asking for help and a user complaining, ensuring the auto-responses are contextually appropriate.

suggestions.title: Cross-Platform Bot Controller
suggestions.desc: Refactor the core logic into a platform-agnostic library and add adapters for Discord (discord.py) and Twitter (Tweepy). A single client dashboard could manage a brand's automated presence across three different social networks simultaneously.

suggestions.title: Public Sentiment Trend Tracker
suggestions.desc: Add a data persistence layer using MongoDB to store every detected phrase. Build a dashboard that visualizes the frequency of these phrases over time, providing clients with an 'Early Warning System' for rising topics or controversies.

suggestions.title: User Reputation & Trust Scoring
suggestions.desc: Implement a system that tracks the 'reputation' of users who trigger the bot. If a high-karma or 'trusted' user triggers a phrase, the bot could provide a more detailed response or forward the thread directly to a human manager.

title: KSU Course Substitution Web System
authors: Yeojin Kim, Jean-Jacques Mutekeke, Nidhi Shah, Shiva Voruganti, Junan Zhao
abstract: Web tool to streamline course substitution requests at KSU, reducing paperwork and integrating with OWL Express.

suggestions.title: Automated Equivalency Suggestion Engine
suggestions.desc: Build a web scraper to ingest course catalogs from other major universities. When a student requests a substitution, the system can use string-matching algorithms to suggest the most likely equivalent KSU course, speeding up the registrar's review.

suggestions.title: Digital Approval Blockchain Audit
suggestions.desc: Use a lightweight private blockchain (like Hyperledger Fabric) to create an immutable log of every approval and signature in the substitution process. This prevents 'lost' paperwork and provides a transparent audit trail for academic advisors.

suggestions.title: Substitution Impact Simulator
suggestions.desc: Create a 'What-If' tool for students that shows how a proposed course substitution would affect their projected graduation date and GPA. It would pull data from the user's DegreeWorks profile via a secure API.

suggestions.title: OCR Transcript Processing Module
suggestions.desc: Integrate Tesseract OCR to allow students to upload PDF transcripts from other institutions. The system would automatically extract course codes and titles, pre-filling the substitution form and reducing manual entry errors.

title: KSU File Transfer System
authors: Quyunh Dao Do, Josh Darby, Barry Oliver, Olutoni Oluyide
abstract: Secure file sharing system for KSU professors to share and collaborate on course materials with version control.
topics: Web Development, File Sharing, Collaboration, Security

suggestions.title: Zero-Knowledge Encryption Implementation
suggestions.desc: Upgrade the security layer so that files are encrypted on the client side before being sent to the server. By using the Web Crypto API, you ensure that even university system admins cannot read the contents of the files without the professor's key.

suggestions.title: Differential Binary Versioning
suggestions.desc: Implement a delta-encoding algorithm (like rsync) for the version control system. Instead of saving full copies of large PowerPoint or PDF files for every small change, the system only stores the 'diff,' drastically reducing storage costs.

suggestions.title: Automated Malware Sandboxing
suggestions.desc: Integrate the VirusTotal API or a local ClamAV instance to automatically scan every uploaded file. If a file is flagged as malicious, it is quarantined immediately, protecting the university network from infected student submissions.

suggestions.title: Canvas/LMS Direct Publish Sync
suggestions.desc: Create a 'Publish to Course' feature that allows professors to sync a specific version of a file directly to their Canvas course modules. This bridges the gap between private collaboration and student-facing distribution.

title: Live Interactive Technology (L.I.T.)
authors: Stephen Ajetomobi, David Guidry, Farid Khan, Daniel Louis, Rachael Okogie
abstract: Android app providing pseudo real-time video feed of campus events to increase student participation.
topics: Android, Mobile Development, Live Streaming, Campus Events

suggestions.title: Low-Latency HLS Optimization
suggestions.desc: Refactor the streaming backend using Amazon IVS (Interactive Video Service) to reduce the 'pseudo real-time' delay to sub-second levels. This enables truly synchronous interactions between the live event and the remote audience.

suggestions.title: Interactive AR Event Overlays
suggestions.desc: Use ARCore to allow viewers to see digital overlays on the live feed. For example, during a campus career fair, students could tap on a booth in the video to see a list of open positions or the current wait time for a recruiter.

suggestions.title: Multi-Angle Crowd-Sourced Switching
suggestions.desc: Implement a 'Director Mode' where the app can accept streams from multiple student phones at the same event. Viewers could then toggle between different angles of a football game or a concert, similar to professional broadcast setups.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Clip Generation and Social Sharing
suggestions.desc: Add a feature that uses FFmpeg on the backend to allow users to 'clip' the last 30 seconds of a live feed. These clips can then be instantly shared to Instagram or TikTok to drive more immediate traffic to the campus event.

title: Melody Bank
authors: Marc Ducharme, Justin Fortin, Lawson Welborn
abstract: Windows application allowing users to collaboratively identify and document recurring musical patterns in songs.

suggestions.title: Automated Motif Detection with MIDI Analysis
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Python Mido library or Librosa to automatically scan uploaded MIDI files for recurring melodic sequences, replacing manual identification with algorithmic pattern matching.

suggestions.title: Digital Audio Workstation (DAW) Plugin Bridge
suggestions.desc: Develop a VST or AU plugin version of Melody Bank that allows music producers to drag-and-drop identified patterns directly from the database into software like Ableton Live or FL Studio.

suggestions.title: Ethnomusicology Dataset Expansion
suggestions.desc: Pivot the application to focus on global folk music, using the collaborative platform to map how specific melodic patterns migrated across different cultures and eras using geospatial visualization.

suggestions.title: Real-time Collaborative Jam Session Sync
suggestions.desc: Implement WebSockets to allow multiple users in the Windows app to edit the same melody 'sheet' simultaneously, incorporating version control for musical phrases.

title: MongoDB Security
authors: Anish Patel, Jeffrey Allen Spradlin, Mytee Garcia, Jared Davis, Anne Elizabeth Goulart
abstract: Investigation of MongoDB NoSQL security issues and implementation of security settings on an existing database.

suggestions.title: Automated NoSQL Injection Vulnerability Scanner
suggestions.desc: Build a Python-based tool that specifically probes MongoDB instances for common NoSQL injection patterns and misconfigured Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) settings.

suggestions.title: MongoDB Ransomware Early Warning System
suggestions.desc: Develop a monitoring agent that detects mass-deletion or mass-encryption patterns within MongoDB collections, triggering an automatic firewall block to prevent data exfiltration.

suggestions.title: Performance Impact Analysis of TLS Encryption
suggestions.desc: Conduct a formal research study measuring the latency overhead of various MongoDB security layers (TLS/SSL, At-Rest Encryption, Scram-SHA-256) under high-concurrency workloads.

title: Optimisation of Object Searching Techniques using Visual C++
authors: Anil Kumar Sreedharala
abstract: Quantum-augmented networks aim to use quantum phenomena to improve detection and protection against malicious actors in a classical communication network. This may include multiplexing quantum signals into classical fiber optical channels and incorporating purely quantum links alongside classical links in the network. In such hybrid networks, quantum protocols based on single photons become a bottleneck for transmission distances and data speeds, thereby reducing entire network performance. Furthermore, many of the security assumptions of the single-photon protocols do not hold up in practice because of the impossibility of manufacturing single-photon emitters. Multi-photon quantum protocols, on the other hand, are designed to operate under practical assumptions and do not require single photon emitters. As a result, they provide higher levels of security guarantees and longer transmission distances. However, the effect of channel and device noise on multiphoton protocols in terms of security, transmission distances, and bit rates has not been investigated. In this paper, we focus on channel noise and present our observations on the effect of various types of noise on multi-photon protocols. We also investigate the effect of topologies such as ring, star, and torus on the noise characteristics of the multi-photon protocols. Our results show the possible advantages of switching to multi-photon protocols and give insights into the repeater placement and topology choice for quantum-augmented networks.
topics: Computer Vision, OpenCV, Object Tracking, C++

suggestions.title: Quantum Network Planning & ROI Simulator
suggestions.desc: Develop a desktop application that allows network architects to upload their existing classical fiber topology (JSON/GeoJSON). The tool simulates the performance gain and cost-benefit analysis of adding multi-photon quantum links, accounting for specific regional noise profiles and transmission distances.

suggestions.title: Noise-Resilient Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) for IoT
suggestions.desc: Adapt the multi-photon protocols to secure a simulated smart-city IoT network. Build a prototype that demonstrates how multi-photon states can maintain a secure key exchange even over noisy, shared classical-quantum fiber lines, protecting critical infrastructure like power grids from eavesdropping.

suggestions.title: Reinforcement Learning for Dynamic Quantum Routing
suggestions.desc: Train a Deep Q-Network (DQN) agent to dynamically route quantum packets through a multi-topology network. The agent should learn to avoid high-noise links and prioritize paths that maintain photon coherence, effectively maximizing the entire hybrid network's throughput and fidelity.

suggestions.title: NS-3 Extension for Multi-Photon Interference Modeling
suggestions.desc: Extend the popular NS-3 network simulator with a specialized module for multi-photon interference and decoherence. This tool would allow researchers to test quantum protocols against realistic classical network traffic noise without needing expensive physical quantum hardware.

title: Prediction of the Seismic Hazard
authors: Yan Wang
abstract: Mental health is a critical aspect of our overall well-being. Mental illness refers to conditions that impact an individual's psychological state, resulting in considerable distress, and limitations in functioning day-to-day tasks. Due to the progress of technology, social media has merged as the platform, for individuals to share their thoughts and emotions. The psychological state of individuals can be accessed with the help of data from these platforms. However, it is challenging for conventional machine learning models to analyze the diverse linguistic contexts of social media data. In this work, we propose a novel attention-driven deep framework to overcome these challenges. Our proposed framework utilizes multi-level (word, sentence, and document) data to identify the causes behind mental illness. The efficacy and effectiveness of our proposed model are shown by extensive evaluation on Reddit data. The insights from this research deepen our understanding of different factors behind mental illness and would aid mental health professionals in formulating effective interventions.
topics: Machine Learning, Geoscience, Prediction, Natural Hazards

abstract: In this demonstration, we propose a framework for exploring, identifying, and matching repeated behavioral health keywords in first-responder reports to the current set of Behavioral Health Index Terms provided by subject matter experts (SMEs). The tool incorporates behavioral health-related keywords and has a Graphical User Interface (GUI) that allows non-technical users to explore and analyze 911 first-responder reports. We utilized an inverted index, best-matching (BM25), and plain-text searching algorithms to match keywords in first-responder reports. This tool provides a comprehensive approach to report analysis by identifying indicators of mental health disorders and taking into account the assessments of humanities and social science professionals

title: Hackathon Mobile Web App
authors: Ashleigh Ladd, Austin Wilcox, Frank Cam pbell, Katie
abstract: In today’s data-driven world, organizations handle vast amounts of sensitive information, including personally identifiable information (PII), health records, and financial data. For institutions like schools, this data often includes sensitive details about students, parents, and staff, making data protection not just important, but critical. With increasing privacy regulations such as GDPR and HIPAA, organizations must implement robust measures to protect this information while still enabling its use for legitimate purposes like testing, analytics, and development. Our web-based data masking tool addresses this need by allowing organizations to protect sensitive data without compromising its usability. By applying dynamic masking rules to relational databases and generating masked data extracts, the tool ensures compliance with privacy laws while improving operational efficiency. It automates data protection processes and generates realistic, anonymized data, allowing organizations to securely manage and share sensitive information for non-production purposes. Designed for scalability and ease of use, the tool helps organizations streamline their data protection workflows while maintaining the integrity of their testing and analytical environments.

suggestions.title: Differential Privacy Noise Layer
suggestions.desc: Upgrade the tool from simple masking to a Differential Privacy framework. Implement a mechanism that adds Laplacian noise to the data, allowing school administrators to perform statistical analysis (e.g., average GPA by district) without the risk of re-identifying individual students.

suggestions.title: NLP-Powered PII Auto-Discovery
suggestions.desc: Integrate a Named Entity Recognition (NER) model using SpaCy or Hugging Face to automatically scan unstructured documents (like teacher notes or emails). The tool should identify and mask names, addresses, and SSNs that are hidden within paragraphs of text, rather than just in structured database columns.

suggestions.title: Re-identification Vulnerability Audit Tool
suggestions.desc: Develop a 'red-team' testing script that attempts to re-identify masked records by cross-referencing them with public datasets (like voter registration or social media). Use this to generate a 'Privacy Strength Score' for different masking configurations.

suggestions.title: FERPA-Compliant Data Sharing Portal
suggestions.desc: Build a secure web portal where educational researchers can request access to school datasets. The system should automatically apply the masking tool to the requested data, generate an audit log for compliance, and provide a secure, time-limited download link.

suggestions.desc: Extend the 2D heatmaps into 3D using the PyVista library. This would allow speleologists to visualize temperature or humidity gradients through vertical cave shafts, creating a 'cloud' of data points within a 3D-scanned mesh of the cave system.

suggestions.title: IoT Field-Sync Mobile App
suggestions.desc: Develop a Flutter mobile app that pairs with GraphBat. Scientists in the field can record sensor data via Bluetooth LE, and the app will automatically timestamp and geofence the data, ready for one-click import into the GraphBat desktop visualizer.

suggestions.title: Micro-Climate Predictive Modeling
suggestions.desc: Integrate a regression model that predicts subterranean conditions based on surface weather data. By importing NOAA weather APIs, GraphBat could show the 'predicted' heatmap for a cave system during a future storm or heatwave.

suggestions.title: Interactive WebGL Data Portal
suggestions.desc: Convert the Python visualization engine to a web-based portal using Three.js and React. This would allow scientific teams to host their subterranean datasets online, where others can interactively fly through the interpolated heatmaps in a browser.

title: MyCare - Telemedicine Web App
authors: Christina Tam ba, Baboucarr Sanneh, Darius Tiglao, Xiaoran
abstract: Our team has developed a website that can allow patients to communicate with their doctors. Through this web app, patients can send messages to their doctors, set appointm ents, view health reports, and browse information by their doctors in a blog-like fashion. The project is secure and developed using Django and React. During the COVID-19 pandem ic, it's encouraged that people quarantine and lim it going outside. Because of this, people are paranoid to go anywhere, including the doctor's office. Being able to communicate with patients right from the comfort of their own hom e provides a business merit and allows hospitals and doctor offices to continue to operate.

suggestions.title: End-to-End HIPAA Compliant Messaging
suggestions.desc: Upgrade the Django backend to support end-to-end encryption (E2EE) for patient-doctor messages using the Signal Protocol (libsignal). Implement client-side decryption in React to ensure health data remains private even if the database is compromised.

suggestions.title: Automated Symptom-to-Specialist Triage
suggestions.desc: Integrate a NLP model like BioBERT or the Infermedica API to analyze patient messages and health reports. The system should automatically flag high-risk symptoms and suggest the appropriate medical specialist to the primary care physician.

suggestions.title: Wearable Device Data Integration Hub
suggestions.desc: Extend the Django models to ingest real-time health data from Google Fit or Apple HealthKit APIs. This would allow doctors to view a dashboard of the patient’s heart rate, sleep cycles, and daily steps alongside their manual health reports.

suggestions.title: Prescription Management and Pharmacy API
suggestions.desc: Build a module that allows doctors to issue digital prescriptions directly through the app. Integrate with the DoseSpot or Surescripts API to transmit these orders to local pharmacies and notify patients when their medication is ready.

title: Georgia Music Business Directory
authors: Jonathan Van Baush, Sam uel Wilson, Kayla Hanks
abstract: The Georgia Music Business Directory will be a web-based Application that connects different entities of the music industry in the state of Georgia. Artists, venues, managers, event planners, and industry workers should be able to connect with and search for other entities within the state of Georgia. Creating a website for the governm ent of Georgia to prom ote music.

suggestions.title: Multimodal Aspect-Based Sentiment Analysis
suggestions.desc: Extend the current BERT pipeline to process video and audio reviews using Whisper for transcription and Wav2Vec2 for vocal emotion detection. Build a fusion layer that correlates the speaker's tone with the text-based sarcasm detection to improve overall accuracy beyond 69%.

suggestions.title: Real-Time E-Commerce Competitive Intelligence Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Develop a scraper for Amazon or Yelp that feeds into the fine-tuned BERT model to compare sentiment on specific product features against competitors. Use D3.js to visualize aspect-level 'wins' and 'losses' in real-time, providing actionable pricing or feature suggestions for business users.

suggestions.title: Contrastive Learning for Sarcasm Disambiguation
suggestions.desc: Research the use of Contrastive Learning (SimCLR) to train the model on pairs of sarcastic vs. literal text with similar keywords. Use the OpenAI API to generate synthetic sarcastic training data to address the common data imbalance issues in sarcasm detection datasets.

suggestions.title: Mental Health Crisis Early Warning System
suggestions.desc: Adapt the emotion and sentiment classifier to monitor social media feeds or anonymous forum posts for specific patterns of depressive or self-harming sentiment. Implement a notification bridge that alerts moderators when the model detects a shift from sarcasm to genuine high-arousal negative emotions.

title: CCSE Hackathons Web & Mobile App
authors: Jessie Tran, Kam sy Osy-Odife (INT - Info Assur &
abstract: CCSE has a need for a solution to manage the registrations and virtual community for our 2 hackathons (Fall/Spring). Objectives for the Fall sem ester are to: 1) Create Web and Mobile App for Students to Register/Manage and Select a Team - Allow Login/Manage Single/Team Registration - Adm in -Manage Event Program - Ability to Add Marketing/Branding for KSU CCSE and Sponsors - Ability to Showcase Winning Teams 2) Create Minim um Viable Product for Use in Spring 2021 3) Added Capabilities - Virtual Community - Photo Upload (approval before publish) 4) Technical and User Docum entation We solved the problem of organizing the Hackathon registration and team assignm ent as single/m ulti

suggestions.title: Edge-AI Visual Servoing
suggestions.desc: Optimize the machine vision pipeline to run on an NVIDIA Jetson Nano mounted directly on the arm. Move from standard object detection to 'Visual Servoing,' where the arm adjusts its position in real-time based on the pixel-error between the target and the gripper.

suggestions.title: Sim-to-Real Transfer with NVIDIA Isaac
suggestions.desc: Train a Reinforcement Learning agent in the NVIDIA Isaac Gym simulator to perform complex tasks (like picking up irregular objects). Use ROS2 to transfer the trained policy to the physical OpenMANIPULATOR-X to see how well it generalizes.

suggestions.title: Remote Teleoperation with Leap Motion
suggestions.desc: Develop a teleoperation interface using a Leap Motion controller. A human user can move their hand in the air, and the 4-DOF arm will mimic the movements in real-time, enabling remote 'monitoring' and physical interaction in hazardous environments.

suggestions.title: Mobile Monitoring Platform (TurtleBot + Arm)
suggestions.desc: Mount the OpenMANIPULATOR-X on a TurtleBot3 mobile base. Use ROS2 Navigation stacks to allow the arm to patrol a room, use vision to find a specific object (like a temperature gauge), and move closer to 'monitor' it autonomously.

title: OWL EVENTS
authors: Paul Kruskam p, Patrick Mean, Michael Thom ason, Seth
abstract: In an effort to increase student participation in CCSE Events, we are working on a mobile application for students of KSU to be notified of CCSE events taking place in the near future. The student will have easy access to this list and will be able to easily sign up for these events from their phone and receive rem inders of these events as the event dates approach. The application will provide maps, social media and event calandars. Increasing student participation in CCSE events will enrich the relationship between KSU and its CCSE students, encouraging learning and participation outside the classroom .

suggestions.title: Indoor Wayfinding with Bluetooth Beacons
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Estimote SDK or similar Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) beacon technology into the mobile app. This will allow students to receive turn-by-turn navigation inside CCSE buildings to find specific event rooms or labs.

suggestions.title: Gamified Engagement with 'KSU Credits'
suggestions.desc: Implement a blockchain-based or centralized point system where students earn 'KSU Credits' for scanning QR codes at event entrances. These points can be redeemed via the app for CCSE merchandise or priority seating at high-demand workshops.

suggestions.desc: Use the Microsoft Graph API (for Outlook) or Google Calendar API to sync a student's class schedule with the event list. The app can then provide personalized recommendations for events that don't conflict with their existing academic commitments.

This lets tables wrap up their meal and get back to their day quicker and reduces friction due to split checks or serve

authors: Michael Vanderbilt, Shannon Bryce, Scott Robinson
abstract: The CCSE departm ent holds events for students to attend where they have the opportunity to network and subm it resum es with the industry. the CCSE departm ent needed a way to keep students in the loop. Our team created an application that will keep students abreast of CCSE events, track specified events, provide real-tim e notifications of all events as well as updates to selected events. Students attend KSU because they believe that KSU will make them more marketable and thus increase their net worth. KSU 's business model revolves around making students more m

suggestions.desc: Create a data visualization tool using D3.js that maps the connections made during events. This research tool would show CCSE administrators which industry sectors (e.g., FinTech, Defense) are most active in hiring their students.

suggestions.desc: Build an automated follow-up system using the Calendly API. After an event, the app can facilitate immediate interview scheduling between interested recruiters and students based on mutual feedback provided during the session.

abstract: This project targets students of the CCSE departm ent, the aim of this application is to improve student engagem ent, KSU offers a large array of social, personal developm ent, and career events on cam pus. It enhances out of class learning or putting what we learn in class into practice.

suggestions.desc: Extend the application to include CCSE alumni who can act as mentors. Use a matching algorithm based on 'current student interests' vs. 'alumni career path' to suggest 1-on-1 coffee chats or industry advice sessions.

suggestions.desc: Analyze historical engagement data to identify students at risk of 'disengagement.' Use a logistic regression model to flag students who haven't attended an event in 30 days and send them personalized invitations to events matching their major.

suggestions.desc: Add a 'Call for Help' button to the mobile app for hackathon participants. This pushes a notification to a dashboard used by roaming mentors, showing them the team's location and the specific technical hurdle (e.g., 'API auth error').

title: CCSE CRM
authors: Cecelia O'Koli, Ethan Luettegem ann, Patrick Lowhorn, Evan
abstract: The Indy Micro is a desktop application which simulates the functionality of an eight-bit personal computer. Its aim is to mimic the feel of owning one such computer in that era, as well as provide an engaging way to learn about low-level computing concepts. The Micro consists of two components: the virtual machine, which is based on the Von Neumann architecture, and the code editor, which allows users to write assembly code and execute it on the virtual machine. The aim is for the Indy Micro to serve as an educational jumping-off point, a step between the casual programmer and the dedicated hobbyist, developing software for real eight-bit systems. One of the ways students get started with programming is with Scratch (scratch.mit.edu), a visual drag-and-drop programming experience. The project’s goal is to create something like Scratch, but for assembly language, allowing students and hobbyists to learn about low level programming.

suggestions.title: Physical Register Display Bridge
suggestions.desc: Build a hardware interface using an ESP32 to map the simulator’s virtual registers to physical 8-bit LED arrays and toggle switches, allowing users to 'hand-code' the virtual machine via physical hardware inputs.

suggestions.title: Cycle-Accurate Performance Profiler
suggestions.desc: Develop a visual debugger that tracks clock cycles and memory access patterns in real-time. Use this to help students visualize the 'Von Neumann Bottleneck' by showing how data movement between the CPU and memory impacts execution speed.

suggestions.title: Indy-ASM to WebAssembly Compiler
suggestions.desc: Create a toolchain that compiles the project's custom assembly language into WebAssembly (Wasm). This would allow students to write code in the Indy Micro editor and export it as a standalone, high-performance web application.

suggestions.title: IoT Retro-Dashboard Node
suggestions.desc: Repurpose the virtual machine to act as a low-overhead logic controller for IoT sensors. Use the 8-bit simulation to process sensor data (like temperature) and output retro-style visualizations to an attached OLED display.

title: Math Learning Machine
authors: Grant Williams, Jam es Mann, Raj Kapadia, Steven
abstract: Math Learning Machine utilizes image segm entation and image classification to create a math oriented optical character recognition system which can solve complex mathem atical equations given an image of a handwritten equation. There is a wide range of possible merit for this project. Young children in school who find them selves having to learn math at hom e during the pandem ic can utilize this tool (or the parent who is now playing a role in teaching their child) to verify their math hom ework before turning it in. Academ ic researchers can save tim e by handwriting extrem ely complex equations and then being able to scan the image and copy the LaTeX form of the equation for the

suggestions.title: Step-by-Step Logic Explanation Engine
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Wolfram Alpha API to not only solve the equation but also provide the 'Show Steps' breakdown. This would transform the project from a simple calculator into a robust pedagogical tool for home-schooled students.

suggestions.title: LaTeX and Markdown Export Utility
suggestions.desc: Add a feature that converts the recognized handwritten equation into LaTeX code. This allows university students or researchers to quickly digitize handwritten notes for use in formal lab reports or Overleaf documents.

suggestions.title: Augmented Reality (AR) Math Overlay
suggestions.desc: Using ARCore or ARKit, create a mobile interface that overlays the solution directly on top of the handwritten paper in the camera view. This provides immediate verification without the user having to switch between a camera and a results screen.

suggestions.title: Multi-Line and Calculus OCR Expansion
suggestions.desc: Improve the image segmentation model to handle multi-line systems of equations and advanced calculus symbols (integrals, summations). Use a Transformer-based architecture like ViT (Vision Transformer) to improve accuracy on complex layouts.

title: FeelBetter
authors: Jason Bice, Sqielyr Person, Jason LaPrad, Aydan
abstract: The application is intended to let parents track the sym ptoms a child experiences during illness and learn more about how to appropriately track their sym ptoms such as tem perature, body aches, bathroom issues, and various other common ailm ents. The application will also provide information regarding sym ptom tim elines and common children illnesses. The app will have a section for medical information from various online medical sources. It will give the user the tools to make their conclusions. The intellectual merit in this project is giving parents and guardians the ability to track their child's sym ptoms when they are ill.

suggestions.title: Visual Body Map Symptom Tracker
suggestions.desc: Replace text-based input with an interactive 3D or 2D body map (using Three.js or SVG). Parents can tap specific areas (e.g., 'lower right abdomen') to log pain or rashes, making the data entry more intuitive and precise.

suggestions.title: Pediatric Dosage Safety Calculator
suggestions.desc: Build a safety module where parents enter their child's weight and age. Using a database of common OTC medicines (like Acetaminophen), the app calculates the correct dosage and warns against potential ingredient overlaps (e.g., taking two medicines containing Tylenol).

suggestions.title: Tele-Ready PDF Export for Pediatricians
suggestions.desc: Develop a 'Share with Doctor' feature that generates a professionally formatted PDF timeline of symptoms, fever spikes, and medications. This summary can be emailed or uploaded to patient portals like MyChart before a consultation.

suggestions.title: Community Illness Heatmap
suggestions.desc: Implement anonymized data sharing to create a local heatmap of common symptoms (e.g., 'High flu activity in Cobb County'). Use GeoJSON and Mapbox to help parents understand if their child's symptoms match current local trends.

title: Fuzzy Logic Chess AI
authors: John Hurd, Rafael Quarles, John Foster, Mina Hanna
abstract: This project is a gam e containing a chess variant that relies on fuzzy logic in order to determine if pieces are taken. Our team created custom backend code to facilitate the gam e, an intuitive user interface, and a distributed AI that is able to take on hum an players and itself. Our project delves into the topic of distributed AI. Distributed AI relies on most decisions being made by one centralized AI and communicated to AI or hum an agents. In the case of the gam e, this central AI corresponds to the king. In the real world, it could mean a large supercom puter that commands less hardware intensive AIs running on smartphones or a military server controlling AI-powered drones.

suggestions.title: Web-Based Multiplayer Matchmaking
suggestions.desc: Migrate the custom backend to a Node.js/Socket.io environment to support real-time online play. Implement a 'Fuzzy Lobby' where users can challenge friends or find random opponents globally.

suggestions.title: Explainable Fuzzy Decision UI
suggestions.desc: Create a 'Thought Stream' sidebar that visualizes the fuzzy logic membership functions in real-time. This would show the user exactly why the AI decided a piece was 'mostly taken' or 'partially safe' based on the distributed inputs.

suggestions.title: Mobile 'Fuzzy Chess' Game App
suggestions.desc: Port the game to Unity or React Native to reach a wider audience. Add a 'Daily Puzzle' mode where players must find the best move in a high-entropy fuzzy state, monetizing through cosmetic piece skins or ad-free play.

suggestions.title: Reinforcement Learning for Fuzzy Tuning
suggestions.desc: Use Deep Reinforcement Learning (RL) to train the chess AI. Instead of manually setting fuzzy rules, the AI can play millions of games against itself to 'learn' the optimal fuzzy weights for different game phases (opening vs. endgame).

title: Autonomous Sentry Gun
authors: Gustavo Mejia (BSCS, BSSW E), Jam es Bozhkov, Merry Nguyen
abstract: This study explores the performance of multifamily loans using a logistic regression model to predict loan outcomes as either “closed” or “current”. Utilizing a dataset of over one million observations and 54,771 unique loan observations, we classify loan status based on Freddie Mac’s mortgage performance codes, with closed loans including modification with a loss, foreclosures, real estate owned, and fully closed loans. Through explanatory analysis, it reveals a nearly balanced distribution between the binary variables. This dataset supports the use of a logistic regression to model the probability of loan default or completion. The findings have implications for risk mitigation in underwriting practices, helping lenders avoid loans with characteristics like those that historically defaulted.

suggestions.title: Gradient Boosted Tree Ensemble Migration
suggestions.desc: Replace the Logistic Regression model with XGBoost or LightGBM to capture non-linear relationships between loan features (e.g., the interaction between property age and market interest rates). This could significantly improve the detection of 'closed' loans which often have complex, multi-factor causes.

suggestions.title: Macro-Economic Stress Test Simulator
suggestions.desc: Build a Streamlit dashboard that allows users to adjust external economic sliders (e.g., +2% Federal Interest Rate, +5% Unemployment). The app uses the trained model to predict how many 'Current' loans in the Freddie Mac dataset would shift to 'Closed/Foreclosure' status under those conditions.

suggestions.title: Geospatial Risk Clustering Analysis
suggestions.desc: Integrate the loan data with ArcGIS or Folium to map performance by Metropolitan Statistical Area (MSA). Analyze whether loan defaults are clustered geographically and correlate those clusters with local economic indicators like regional GDP growth or industry-specific layoffs.

suggestions.title: Real Estate Investor Risk-Scoring API
suggestions.desc: Develop a lightweight API where small-scale multifamily investors can input their own loan details (LTV, debt service coverage ratio, etc.). The API returns a 'Risk Grade' (A-F) based on the patterns learned from the million-observation Freddie Mac dataset.

suggestions.title: Low-Light RPPG for Sleep Monitoring

suggestions.desc: Rewrite the processing pipeline using NVIDIA TensorRT or OpenVINO to run at high frame rates on edge hardware like the Jetson Nano. This could be deployed in airport or office kiosks for rapid, contactless health screening.

title: Federated IoT Botnet Detection
authors: Christopher Regan
abstract: Internet of Things (IoT) devices are mass produced, often heterogeneous in nature, updated infrequently, and can rem ain out of sight on a hom e or office network for extended periods. Security and privacy are two of the many ongoing research and operational challenges in IoT. To address potential threats to IoT devices, deep learning-based solutions have been largely utilized in recent years. However, as big data is transferred under traditional centralized methods, it is at risk for data privacy issues because the data can be captured. In this paper, we propose a federated-based solution employing a deep autoencoder to ensure that data privacy is maintained and provides a way to detect
supervisor: Dr. Reza Parizi. Dr. Seyedam in Pouriyeh, Dr.

suggestions.title: Quantized Federated Learning for ESP32
suggestions.desc: Implement model quantization (INT8) and pruning within the federated learning pipeline to allow memory-constrained IoT microcontrollers like the ESP32 to participate in local training without crashing.

suggestions.title: Non-IID Data Robustness Evaluation
suggestions.desc: Research how the global model performs when IoT devices have heterogeneous data (e.g., a smart camera vs. a smart plug) and implement the FedProx algorithm to improve convergence in these non-IID environments.

suggestions.title: Blockchain-Verified Update Registry
suggestions.desc: Integrate a Hyperledger Fabric blockchain to store the hashes of local model updates, ensuring that malicious nodes cannot inject poisoned gradients into the global IoT security model.

suggestions.title: Edge-Native Botnet Quarantine System
suggestions.desc: Build a Home Assistant integration that uses the federated model's output to automatically firewall any IoT device locally identified as part of a botnet cluster.

title: Facial Recognition with DL
authors: Honglai Peng
abstract: Face analysis has received much attention from the Com puter Vision and Pattern Recognition communities in the past. Current research progress has prom oted us to solve problems about face recognition or face detection for som e realistic scenarios, like robot service. As we know, the success of service robotics decisively depends on the ability to extract accurate information just from face images, like identifying gender, age or emotion state. Till now, despite significant recent advances in the field of face recognition, all of the related works or classifiers are merely lim ited to one specific facial feature, like gender, or age, or emotion. And these models cannot be applied into
supervisor: Dr. Selena He, Dr. Meng Han

suggestions.title: Anti-Spoofing 3D Depth Integration

title: Well-written research papers
authors: Srivarna Settisara Janney
abstract: Conventionally, non-native English-speaking students have trouble to get their research papers/reports published in top conferences/journals. It is hard to express their ideas and research results in more gram matically correct sentences that make sense. Our aim is to provide a helping hand using latest state-of-the-art Natural Language Processing tools like GPT2 from OpenAI to generate a well-written academ ic papers/reports based on the incorrect or rather inappropriate input English sentences. The proposed research dem onstrates an interactive bot to correct not only gram matically errors but also form ation of proper meaningful sentences that could be beneficial for students to write

suggestions.title: Domain-Specific arXiv Fine-Tuning
suggestions.desc: Move beyond base GPT-2 by fine-tuning a larger Llama 3 or Mistral-7B model specifically on the arXiv CS and Physics subsets to better handle niche technical jargon and formal notation.

suggestions.title: Citation-Aware LaTeX Extension
suggestions.desc: Build a VS Code or Overleaf extension that suggests sentence rewrites while maintaining the integrity of BibTeX citation keys and mathematical environments.

suggestions.title: Hallucination Auditing with RAG
suggestions.desc: Implement a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system that checks the 'well-written' output against Google Scholar data to ensure the AI hasn't fabricated scientific facts during the rewriting process.

suggestions.title: Automated Structural Peer-Review Agent
