title: Intelligent Checkout System
authors: Jam es Bozhkov, Jiam ing Li
abstract: The traditional checkout systems are mainly based on scanning the labels attached to the items which require manual labor, and thus inefficient. Nowadays, as deep learning reveals excellent performance towards object detection tasks, this technique has been integrated into assisting the speed of each checkout process. However, most research merely focuses on detecting merchandise, yet overlooked the connection with custom ers. In this research paper, we are proposing a structured intelligent checkout system that obtains the dexterity of checking a custom er’s identity and their items embedded by dual cam eras. The first cam era is installed in front of the custom er to determine the

suggestions.title: Multi-Modal Produce Identification
suggestions.desc: Integrate a digital load cell (scale) with the YOLOv8 vision system to differentiate between visually identical items like different types of apples based on weight-per-item ratios.

suggestions.title: Frictionless Walk-Through Prototype
suggestions.desc: Use DeepSORT for multi-object tracking to link detected items to specific 'customer zones' in a multi-camera environment, simulating a 'Just Walk Out' experience.

suggestions.title: Privacy-Preserving Pose Estimation
suggestions.desc: Replace raw facial recognition with skeletal pose estimation to analyze customer intent (e.g., reaching for a shelf) without storing sensitive biometric identifiers.

suggestions.title: Live Inventory Synchronization Engine
suggestions.desc: Connect the checkout system to a SQL-based inventory database to trigger real-time restocking alerts and dynamic pricing updates when stock levels drop below a threshold.

title: Analyze Risk Communication COVID-19
authors: Elaine Harris, Maryline Kwa, Chelsey West, Raviteja
abstract: This research project seeks to assess and analyze the relationship between public health advisory statem ents during the COVID-19 Pandem ic and the trends on new infections in Singapore. Use of both traditional statistical methods and state-of-the-art Big Data Analytics to provide fact-based analysis of current events.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Public Sentiment Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Build a data pipeline using the Twitter API and Selenium to scrape social media responses to government health announcements, correlating sentiment shifts with infection spikes.

suggestions.title: Synthetic Control Method for Policy Impact
suggestions.desc: Apply the Synthetic Control Method (SCM) to compare Singapore's outcomes against a 'counterfactual' city to isolate the statistical impact of specific mobility restrictions.

suggestions.title: Corporate Risk Forecasting Tool
suggestions.desc: Develop a B2B platform that predicts regional workforce disruption by analyzing public transit density data and local health advisory strictness.

suggestions.title: Hyper-Local Infection Heatmap Simulator
suggestions.desc: Create a GIS-based visualization tool that maps public health advisories to specific neighborhoods to see if localized 'cluster' warnings actually reduce foot traffic in those areas.

title: Defensive Federated Learning
authors: Hong kyu Lee, Jeehyeong Kim
abstract: Machine learning (ML) algorithms require a massive amount of data. Firms such as Google and Facebook exploit user's data to deliver a more precise ML-based 9/8/23, 2:45 PM Fall 2020 C-Day Program - College of Computing and Software Engineering service. However, collecting users' data is a risky action because their private data can be leaked through the transm ission. As a rem edy, federated learning is introduced. In federated learning, a central server distributes a machine learning model to users. Each user trains the model to its data, and send the model back. Later the models are aggregated and distributed again. Federated learning is more secure in that it emancipates users

suggestions.title: Differential Privacy Noise Injection
suggestions.desc: Implement the Laplace mechanism to add calibrated noise to local gradients before they are sent to the central server, protecting against model inversion attacks.

suggestions.title: Byzantine-Robust Aggregation Testing
suggestions.desc: Implement and compare defense algorithms like Krum, Bulyan, and Trimmed Mean to see which best protects the global model from 'poisoning' by malicious clients.

suggestions.title: Secure Multi-Party Computation Gateway
suggestions.desc: Utilize the PySyft library to build a prototype where gradients are split into encrypted shares, ensuring the server never sees even a single raw model update.

suggestions.title: Collaborative Medical Diagnostic Network
suggestions.desc: Apply this defensive FL framework to a medical imaging task (e.g., pneumonia detection) allowing multiple hospitals to train a joint model without sharing HIPAA-protected patient images.

title: Energy Saving on Edges
authors: Kousalya Banka
abstract: Internet of Things (IoT) comprises a set of devices that are interconnected ranging from our daily used objects to advanced networked devices. It is a constantly evolving phenom enon as the number of devices owned by the regular user is increasing at a rapid rate. These devices are used for various reasons such as social networking, monitoring, performing complex operations and with the increase of advanced technologies, they dem and more energy to performsuch tasks. Cloud computing enables these communications to seam lessly performcomplex tasks in a cloud environm ent but utilizing these resources properly to performat the best is the key. In this paper, Energy efficiency and e

suggestions.title: RL-Based Dynamic Offloading
suggestions.desc: Develop a Reinforcement Learning agent that monitors real-time battery voltage and network latency to decide whether to run a task locally or offload it to the cloud.

suggestions.title: Model Pruning for ARM Cortex-M
suggestions.desc: Research and implement weight pruning and knowledge distillation to shrink a CNN so it can run entirely on an ultra-low-power microcontroller without cloud reliance.

suggestions.title: NILM-Based Energy Auditor
suggestions.desc: Build a Non-Intrusive Load Monitoring (NILM) system that identifies high-energy 'vampire' devices on an edge gateway by analyzing power signature patterns.

suggestions.title: Solar-Aware Adaptive Sampling
suggestions.desc: Create an outdoor sensor node system that automatically reduces data transmission frequency and sensor resolution based on forecasted solar charging levels.

title: Privacy for Video Streaming
authors: alm ost everyone every single day. To be able to provide privacy to users is
abstract: MPEG-DASH is a video stream ing standard that outlines protocols for sending audio and video content from a server to a client over HTTP. The standard has been widely utilized by the video stream ing industry. However, it creates an opportunity for an adversary to invade users' privacy. While a user is watching a video, information is leaked in the form of meta-data, the size and tim e that the server sent data to the user. This information is not protected by encryption and can be used to create a fingerprint for a video. Once the fingerprint is created, the adversary can use this to identify whether a target user is watching the corresponding video. Successful attack schem es have been p

suggestions.title: DASH Traffic Padding Proxy
suggestions.desc: Build a local proxy server that injects 'chaff' traffic (dummy packets) to normalize packet sizes and intervals, defeating side-channel traffic analysis.

suggestions.title: Fingerprinting Vulnerability Audit
suggestions.desc: Conduct a formal security audit by building a classifier that attempts to identify Netflix shows based only on encrypted packet timing, then test the efficacy of VPNs against it.

suggestions.title: Privacy-First Media Player Plugin
suggestions.desc: Develop a browser extension for DASH players that randomizes the request sequence of video segments to disrupt predictable metadata signatures.

suggestions.title: Multipath TCP Streaming Gateway
suggestions.desc: Implement a gateway that splits video streaming traffic across two different network interfaces (e.g., Wi-Fi and LTE) to make packet timing analysis impossible for a single-link observer.

title: IoT Botnet and Nw Intrusion Detect
authors: Madhuri Gurunathrao Desai
abstract: In the present-day world, there are different types of attacks being launched on computing devices. The world is experiencing more and more cyber- attacks, and the types of attacks are also increasing. For exam ple, an IoT device in a hom e network can act as a botnet attacking other devices or there could be Man in the Middle attack. As tim e goes by more and more devices are being connected within any given network. All these devices will be vulnerable to attacks if any one of the devices is comprom ised within the network. This complicates Intrusion Detection in any given network. Manual detection and intervention are nearly impossible. Hence it is quintessential to detect differe

suggestions.title: IoT Malware HoneyPot
suggestions.desc: Set up a low-interaction honeypot on a Raspberry Pi to capture real Mirai-style botnet traffic and automatically extract new attack signatures for a SNORT IDS.

suggestions.title: Mobile Network Vulnerability Scanner
suggestions.desc: Build an Android app that scans a home Wi-Fi network for IoT devices with known default credentials or insecure protocols like Telnet and UPnP.

suggestions.title: GNN-Based Communication Profiling
suggestions.desc: Use Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) to model the normal 'who-talks-to-whom' graph of a home network and flag anomalies when a smart bulb starts talking to an external IP.

suggestions.title: Zero Trust IoT Micro-Segmentation
suggestions.desc: Prototype an SDN-based (Software Defined Network) router configuration that automatically isolates newly connected IoT devices until they pass a security profile check.

title: Diagnosing ALL with Computer Vision
authors: Dasarath Katragadda
abstract: This research explores the comparative effectiveness of traditional machine learning algorithms and their quantum counterparts. Traditional and quantum implementations of algorithms including Support Vector Machines (SVM), logistic regression, Principal Component Analysis (PCA), random forest classifiers, neural networks, and convolutional neural networks (CNN) are evaluated and contrasted. Findings highlight that quantum algorithms can provide certain clear advantages in some models and data while exhibiting inferior performance in others. By assessing these nuances, this research helps contribute to the understanding of quantum machine learning algorithms and their potential applications for science, engineering, and industrial tasks.

suggestions.title: PennyLane Hybrid Physics-Informed Neural Nets
suggestions.desc: Develop a hybrid Quantum-Classical neural network using the PennyLane library to solve fluid dynamics equations. Compare its convergence speed and accuracy against traditional solvers in engineering simulations.

suggestions.title: Automated Quantum Feature Map Discovery
suggestions.desc: Implement a Genetic Algorithm that searches for the optimal quantum circuit architecture (feature maps) for a given dataset. This moves beyond 'off-the-shelf' quantum circuits to find data-specific embeddings.

suggestions.title: Quantum-Enhanced Portfolio Risk Factor Analysis
suggestions.desc: Apply the Quantum PCA findings to financial time-series data from Yahoo Finance. Test if quantum dimensionality reduction can better identify hidden risk factors in volatile markets compared to classical PCA.

suggestions.title: QML Model-as-a-Service API
suggestions.desc: Build a FastAPI wrapper that bridges classical web apps to IBM Quantum or Amazon Braket. The service should automatically select the best algorithm (Traditional vs. Quantum) based on input complexity and noise levels.

title: Motion Prediction for self-driving
authors: Nihad Kalathingal, Raleigh Barden
abstract: Predicting the motion of agents in an autonom ous vehicle's (AV) 9/8/23, 2:45 PM Fall 2020 C-Day Program - College of Computing and Software Engineering environm ent is imperative step in the trajectory planning system for an AV. The termagent refers to vehicles, bicycles, pedestrians, and other actors in the environm ent of the AV. The agent which the AV aims to predict the motion of is termed a target agent (TA) in this study. A major part of the challenge is that the TA behaves with som e level of uncertainty according to the behaviors of other agents. The design considerations are shaped by the hardware and other software systems present in the AV. The hardware and the real-tim e

suggestions.title: Transformer-Based Path Prediction
suggestions.desc: Implement a 'Wayformer' architecture that uses attention mechanisms to process historical agent movement and predict multi-modal future trajectories.

suggestions.title: Bayesian Uncertainty Estimation
suggestions.desc: Integrate Bayesian Neural Networks to output not just a predicted path, but a 'confidence heat map' that tells the self-driving system when a pedestrian's intent is highly uncertain.

suggestions.title: V2X Intent Communication Sim
suggestions.desc: Build a simulation in CARLA where agents use Vehicle-to-Everything (V2X) messaging to share their 'intended' path, comparing prediction accuracy with and without shared intent.

suggestions.title: Cyclist Proximity Alert for ADAS
suggestions.desc: Develop a mobile-based Advanced Driver Assistance System (ADAS) that uses the phone's camera and an occupancy grid map to alert drivers of predicted 'cut-in' maneuvers by cyclists.

title: Motion Prediction for Vehicles
authors: Neel Patel, Matthew Korpi, Shivank Rao
abstract: The idea of a self-driving car is one that makes us think more of a science fiction movie than a plausible addition to real life. While we have not come close to perfecting street legal autonom ous vehicles by any means, over the past few years significant progress has been made. Autonom ous vehicles need to be able to constantly perceive what is happening in their environm ent and be able to react accordingly. This study aims to improve upon the accuracy of previous motion prediction models, thus allowing the autonom ous vehicle to better understand its surroundings. Building upon Lyft's baseline model, we will implem ent a model with multi-m odal prediction that will generate

suggestions.title: Multi-Modal Lidar and Camera Fusion
suggestions.desc: Extend the current motion prediction model by integrating the Waymo Open Dataset to combine 3D point cloud data with RGB video. This would improve prediction accuracy in low-light conditions where camera-only models typically fail.

suggestions.title: Vulnerable Road User (VRU) Intent Modeling
suggestions.desc: Narrow the focus to predicting the erratic movements of pedestrians and cyclists. Use Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks to analyze gait and orientation to predict if a pedestrian will step into the street before they actually move.

suggestions.title: Simulated Edge-Case Stress Testing
suggestions.desc: Integrate the model into the CARLA simulator to test vehicle reactions during rare 'black swan' events, such as a ball rolling into the street or hydroplaning. Document how prediction confidence intervals change during these high-variance scenarios.

suggestions.title: Collaborative V2X Collective Perception
suggestions.desc: Build a prototype where multiple vehicles share their local motion predictions via a dedicated short-range communications (DSRC) protocol. The goal is to create a 'shared map' that allows a car to 'see' around corners based on the data from a vehicle ahead.

title: Detecting Schizophrenia - ML
authors: Rohan Shiknis
abstract: I want to use Machine Learning, deep networks, and neural networks and research whether these three tools can help diagnose mental disorders at an earlier age. Schizophrenia affects over 1.1% of the world's population, and it is a severe personality disorder that if left untreated, can lead to self harmand catastrophic mental health issues. Constructing various deep learning algorithms, this research project sought out to use this application of machine learning to help those who are struggling to find ways to detect this debilitating disorder. I plan to integrate this model into an app called SchizDetect. The app will give users a list of sym ptoms that they or som eone they know is e

suggestions.title: Privacy-Preserving Federated Learning
suggestions.desc: Implement a Federated Learning framework using PySyft to train the schizophrenia detection model across decentralized hospital datasets. This allows for a larger, more diverse training set without ever moving sensitive patient data off-site.

suggestions.title: NLP-Based Linguistic Biomarker Analysis
suggestions.desc: Incorporate Natural Language Processing (NLP) using Transformers (BERT) to analyze speech patterns for 'thought disorder' markers. Use datasets like the TalkBank 'Schizoband' to detect syntax fragmentation and lexical diversity drops.

suggestions.title: Wearable Sensor Relapse Prediction
suggestions.desc: Develop a mobile application that interfaces with smartwatches to monitor sleep patterns and heart rate variability (HRV). Use the project's ML core to identify physiological anomalies that often precede a schizophrenic episode.

suggestions.title: Diagnostic Decision Support System (DDSS)
suggestions.desc: Create a web-based dashboard for clinicians that visualizes the neural network's 'attention' weights (Explainable AI). This would show doctors exactly which clinical features led to a high-risk score, making the ML output actionable for a diagnosis.

title: Cyber-Physical Systems
authors: Wesley McDonald, Austin Mascarenas, Ryan LeBlanc, Scott
abstract: This project simulates an ad-hoc wireless network used to represent a theoretical network between self-driving vehicles and waystations. In addition, we are able to conduct spectrum analysis on the network through the use of a deep-learning algorithm built directly into the system . We believe our project has more intellectual than business merit at the mom ent; the results are best used to direct further research.

suggestions.title: Blockchain-Enabled Waystation Handshakes
suggestions.desc: Implement a Hyperledger Fabric layer to manage the identity and authentication of vehicles as they connect to waystations. This prevents 'sybil attacks' where a single malicious node tries to spoof multiple vehicle identities in the ad-hoc network.

suggestions.title: Dynamic Spectrum Access for 6G Mesh Networks
suggestions.desc: Extend the spectrum analysis algorithm to implement 'Cognitive Radio' capabilities. Use Reinforcement Learning to allow the vehicles to autonomously switch frequency bands in real-time to avoid interference in high-density urban environments.

suggestions.title: NS-3 Urban Swarm Simulation
suggestions.desc: Port the current simulation into the NS-3 (Network Simulator 3) environment to test the ad-hoc network's scalability with 1,000+ nodes. Analyze how packet loss and latency impact the safety of the deep-learning spectrum controller.

suggestions.title: V2X Jamming Detection and Mitigation
suggestions.desc: Build a security module that uses the deep-learning spectrum analyzer to detect intentional signal jamming or 'Man-in-the-Middle' attacks. Develop an automated response that shifts communication to an out-of-band backup channel when an attack is detected.

title: Thermal Attacks on Edge Platforms
authors: Justin Duchatellier, Tyler Holm es
abstract: This project was the result of our undergraduate research while supervised by Prof. Suo at Kennesaw State University. We analyzed the effects of therm al attacks on edge devices. The Raspberry Pi 4 Model B was used in several experim ents, and its performance was compared to its expected results for normal workloads. Businesses may use our research to prevent unexpected therm al attacks. They could implem ent system logging policies to monitor average resource consum ption, and use these statistics when unexpected resource spikes occur. Appropriate counterm easures would allow for all attacks to be terminated before the system 's tem perature becam e unstable. Keeping a record of ave

suggestions.title: Hardware-in-the-Loop Thermal Governor
suggestions.desc: Develop a custom C++ daemon for the Raspberry Pi 4 that uses the project's thermal data to implement an 'Adaptive Cooling' logic. It should interface with PWM fans to aggressively cool the SoC only when a specific 'thermal attack signature' is detected.

suggestions.title: Cross-Platform Vulnerability Comparison
suggestions.desc: Expand the research to include the NVIDIA Jetson Nano and Coral TPU edge devices. Compare how different hardware architectures (ARM vs. Tensor cores) handle thermal throttling under malicious 'stress-loop' attacks.

suggestions.title: Predictive Thermal Load Balancing
suggestions.desc: Create a Kubernetes (K3s) scheduler for a Raspberry Pi cluster that migrates containers to different nodes based on real-time temperature telemetry. This prevents a single 'hot' node from failing due to localized thermal exhaustion.

suggestions.title: IoT Thermal Firewall for Industrial Sensors
suggestions.desc: Develop a cloud-based monitoring dashboard using Grafana and Prometheus that alerts IT managers to 'impossible' temperature spikes. The system should use ML to distinguish between environmental heat and a malicious process-based thermal attack.

title: Is Hockey Still Canada's Game
authors: Jon-Paul Faix
abstract: The Montreal Canadians earned the National Hockey League’s 1993 Stanley Cup, which would be the last tim e a Canadian team won. The purpose of this research is to explore the potential relationship between the Soviet Union’s 1991 collapse and the Stanley Cup’s winning teams by comparing National Hockey League (NHL) players from form er Soviet states on Am erican versus Canadian teams between the 1990-91, 2007-08, and 2018-19 seasons. I am trying to determine if the current phenom enon of Canadian's teams not winning a Stanley Cup in alm ost three decades can be explained through simple reasoning, or more complex rationale. The comparisons of salaries, and the signing of players fro
supervisor: Prof. Michael Frankel, Dr. Joe DeMaio

suggestions.title: Monte Carlo Stanley Cup Forecasting
suggestions.desc: Build a simulation engine that uses the historical player demographic data to run 10,000 seasonal iterations. Calculate the probability of a Canadian team winning based on current roster trends and the rising influx of European talent.

suggestions.title: The Salary Cap Shift Analysis
suggestions.desc: Refine the research by adding a new variable: the 2005 NHL Salary Cap implementation. Analyze if the financial parity rules disproportionately affected Canadian teams' ability to retain the Soviet-bloc talent identified in the original study.

suggestions.title: Interactive Migration Mapping
suggestions.desc: Develop a D3.js or Leaflet-based interactive map that visualizes the flow of players from former Soviet states to NHL cities over the three studied seasons. Users should be able to toggle by decade to see the 'geographic density' of talent.

suggestions.title: Draft Optimization Scouting Tool
suggestions.desc: Create a data-driven tool for NHL General Managers that identifies 'undervalued' regions in the former USSR. By correlating specific junior leagues with the success rates found in the study, the tool can suggest where to focus scouting resources.

title: MorDNN
authors: gam e developers to create better AI opponents.
abstract: MorDNN is a deep neural network that learns to play the gam e Mordhau, a skill-based competitive melee gam e, by replicating hum an playstyles. The gam e features a complex combat system and MorDNN dem onstrates its intelligence through complex, dynam ic behaviors that are indestinguishable from hum an players creation of MorDNN introduces a unique method for creating advanced AI for real- 9/8/23, 2:45 PM Fall 2020 C-Day Program - College of Computing and Software Engineering tim e gam es. The project dem onstrates a unique method for creating complex gam e AI and proposes a neural network structure that can learn to play gam es within continuous state and action spaces. By dem onstr

suggestions.title: Self-Play Reinforcement Learning Boost
suggestions.desc: Integrate Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) to allow MorDNN to play against clones of itself. This 'AlphaZero' style approach would allow the AI to discover combat techniques that human players haven't yet mastered in Mordhau.

suggestions.title: Melee Combat Turing Test
suggestions.desc: Conduct a formal research study where human players interact with MorDNN and human opponents in a blind trial. Quantify 'human-likeness' using metrics like reaction time variance and 'feint' frequency compared to professional players.

suggestions.title: AI-Driven Combat Coaching
suggestions.desc: Repurpose MorDNN to act as a personalized trainer. The AI would analyze a player's gameplay, identify their mechanical weaknesses (e.g., failing to parry drags), and then 'play' as an opponent that specifically exploits those weaknesses to help the player learn.

suggestions.title: Procedural NPC Difficulty Scaling
suggestions.desc: Develop a middleware API for Unity/Unreal Engine that uses the MorDNN architecture to dynamically adjust NPC difficulty. Instead of just increasing health, the NPC would adopt more complex 'human' tactics as the player gets better.

title: Visual Survey in 3-D Spaces
authors: Clint Williams, Jason Buckner, Oscar Perez, Am ir
abstract: Using video to monitor and analyze hum ans observing social distancing. Determ ing different processes for visual video processing

suggestions.title: Spatial AI Depth Integration
suggestions.desc: Port the social distancing model to the OAK-D (OpenCV AI Kit) camera. By using the on-board stereo depth sensors, you can calculate real-world 'social distance' in millimeters rather than relying on 2D pixel-to-metric approximations.

suggestions.title: Anonymized Pose-Based Monitoring
suggestions.desc: Implement MediaPipe for skeleton-only tracking. By stripping away the actual video frames and only processing the 3D coordinate points of human joints, you can maintain social distancing compliance while strictly protecting individual privacy.

suggestions.title: University Campus Heat-Mapping
suggestions.desc: Deploy the system across university hotspots (like the student center) and use the data to generate real-time 'Crowd Density' maps. This could be integrated into a student app to show which study areas are currently the least crowded.

suggestions.title: Retail Queue Optimization Engine
suggestions.desc: Adapt the 3D space monitoring to detect 'customer frustration' markers (like frequent shifting or leaving the line). Sell the insights to retail managers to help them decide exactly when to open a new register based on flow patterns.

title: PCA embedded Random Forest
authors: Charles Gardner
abstract: This study proposes a new architecture for the random forest algorithm . This architecture attem pts to improve the Random Forests algorithm 's ability to recognize feature interdependencies. An improvem ent performance is produced by creating a PCA model within each tree. This PCA model creates new additional features containing information from multiple input features. These new features are not used for feature reduction but instead appended to the existing feature set. With this approach, the tree can make separations based on information from more than one of its input features. We use PCA feature construction to improve the Random Forest algorithm 's performance. This proj

suggestions.title: Comparative Benchmarking against Deep Forest
suggestions.desc: Conduct a rigorous research study comparing this PCA-embedded Random Forest against Zhou's 'Deep Forest' (gcForest) architecture. Use the UCI Machine Learning Repository's high-dimensional datasets to determine which architecture handles feature interdependency better.

suggestions.title: CUDA-Accelerated PCA-RF Implementation
suggestions.desc: Optimize the architecture for large-scale datasets by implementing the internal PCA and tree-splitting logic using RAPIDS cuML. This will allow the PCA transformations to happen on the GPU, significantly reducing the training time for forests with thousands of trees.

suggestions.title: Genomic Expression Classifier
suggestions.desc: Apply this specific architecture to a Bioinformatics use case, such as classifying cancer types based on gene expression data. PCA-embedded features are particularly useful here because genes often work in pathways (interdependencies) rather than in isolation.

suggestions.title: Explainable PCA-RF Visualizer
suggestions.desc: Develop a visualization tool using Streamlit and SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) that explains how the 'appended PCA features' contribute to the final decision. This makes the 'black box' of the new architecture transparent for use in regulated industries like finance.

title: Modern Web Scraping
authors: Kenny Randolph, Joselyn Giron, Denise Tucker, Justin B Bridges, Sandhya Bantu
abstract: This project was developed for the IT7993 Capstone class in the May sem ester of 2021.The goal of the project is to scrape all nam es of key professionals of organizations in the open990.org website and insert that information into a structured database for query and analysis. The Key Professionals dataset aims to include global coverage of key investor and consultant professionals, beginning with U S-based companies, involved in making an investm ent decision. The overarching aim of this project is to create a one-stop center for institutional asset managem ent distribution intelligence; the one spot to go for mandates, docum entation and profiles of consultants, investors, and manage

suggestions.title: Distributed Scrapy-Redis Cluster
suggestions.desc: Upgrade the scraper to a distributed architecture using Scrapy and Redis. This would allow the project to scale from one site to the entire '990' ecosystem of non-profit filings by distributing tasks across multiple IP addresses to avoid rate-limiting.

suggestions.title: NER for Professional Biography Extraction
suggestions.desc: Implement a Named Entity Recognition (NER) pipeline using Spacy to extract more than just names. The system should identify past employers, board positions, and educational history from unstructured 'biography' sections on professional profiles.

suggestions.title: Neo4j Relationship Mapping
suggestions.desc: Migrate the structured database into a Neo4j Graph Database. This would allow users to perform 'shortest path' queries to see how different investment professionals across multiple US-based companies are connected to each other.

suggestions.title: Executive Transition Alert API
suggestions.desc: Develop a commercial subscription service that monitors the Open990.org site daily. It would send real-time webhooks or email alerts to B2B sales teams whenever a 'Key Professional' changes their organizational affiliation.

title: Key Professional Dataset - Dataspider
authors: Janell Westm oreland, Vy Duong, Nyong Nkereuwem, Kajal S Vaghani, Ritu Choudhary
abstract: The purpose of this project is to build a Web Crawler to extract personal information from a public website like Reddit and LinkedIn. We completed the Instagram crawling as a bonus for the project. The team will be using MySQL or any other open source relational database to organize the data and conduct a quantitative data analysis on it.
supervisor: Dr. Han - Professor IT7993 Capstone Jing Wang -Project Sponsor

suggestions.title: Playwright Stealth-Crawl System
suggestions.desc: Refactor the crawler using Playwright and the 'Stealth Plugin' to bypass modern bot detection (like Cloudflare). This ensures the crawler can still function even as platforms like LinkedIn and Reddit update their scraping defenses.

suggestions.title: Psychographic User Profiling
suggestions.desc: Apply Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA) topic modeling to the scraped Reddit posts. The research would focus on creating 'interest clusters' to see if a user's professional LinkedIn profile correlates with their anonymous Reddit discussions.

suggestions.title: Personal Digital Footprint Auditor
suggestions.desc: Create a consumer tool where a user inputs their own username, and the crawler generates a 'Privacy Report'. This report would show all publicly accessible personal info the crawler found across the three platforms, highlighting potential security risks.

suggestions.title: Cross-Platform Influencer Matchmaking
suggestions.desc: Build an analytics dashboard that correlates Instagram engagement rates with LinkedIn professional standing. This would help brands find 'Subject Matter Expert' influencers who have both a large visual following and professional credibility.

title: KSU Spring Capstone - IT Security Solution for Small Business – Group 1
authors: William Sim mons (other mem bers are not registered on CMT3: Olajum oke Giwa, Beau Beard and Collin Peters)
abstract: For this project, we decided to use the nopCommerce open-source eCommerce solution for our simulated small business. Leveraging the pre-configured web server provide to our group, we installed the nopCommerce solution package and built our security program around the web site. Our security program consists of nginx configured web server and load balancer, SSL certificate for encryption, firewall and a secure backend SQL server.
supervisor: Project Sponsor: Dr. Lei Li Course Professor: Dr. Meng Han

suggestions.title: DevSecOps Pipeline for eCommerce
suggestions.desc: Automate the security setup using Terraform for infrastructure provisioning and Ansible for configuring the Nginx and SQL servers. This allows a small business to redeploy their entire secure environment in minutes if a server is compromised.

suggestions.title: Automated Vulnerability Scanning Service
suggestions.desc: Integrate an automated scanning layer using OWASP ZAP and Nikto that runs weekly against the nopCommerce instance. Create a simple Python-based reporting tool that emails the small business owner a 'Security Health Score' and a prioritized list of patches.

suggestions.title: E-Commerce Fraud Detection Layer
suggestions.desc: Extend the SQL backend to include a machine learning module (using Scikit-learn) that flags suspicious transaction patterns, such as multiple rapid orders from the same IP or mismatched billing/shipping addresses, adding a layer of business logic security.

suggestions.title: Intrusion Prevention with Suricata
suggestions.desc: Add an active defense layer by installing Suricata as an Intrusion Prevention System (IPS) in front of the Nginx server. Configure custom rulesets to specifically detect and block common nopCommerce-targeted exploits and SQL injection attempts in real-time.

title: Compare Two Off Angle Normalization
authors: Em ily Ehrlich
abstract: This work investigates different iris normalization techniques to compare their performance including elliptical normalization and circular normalization after frontal projection of off-angle iris recognition. Elliptical normalization sam ples the iris texture using elliptical segm entation param eters. For circular unwrapping, we first estim ate the gaze deviation using ellipse param eters and the image will be projected back to frontal view using perspective transformation. Then, we segm ent the transformed image and normalize using circular param eters. We further investigate if: (i) elliptical normalization or circular unwrapping recognition performance is higher, and (ii) th
supervisor: Dr. Mahm ut Karakaya m karakay@ kennesaw.edu

suggestions.title: Domain-Specific arXiv Fine-Tuning
suggestions.desc: Move beyond base GPT-2 by fine-tuning a larger Llama 3 or Mistral-7B model specifically on the arXiv CS and Physics subsets to better handle niche technical jargon and formal notation.

suggestions.title: Citation-Aware LaTeX Extension
suggestions.desc: Build a VS Code or Overleaf extension that suggests sentence rewrites while maintaining the integrity of BibTeX citation keys and mathematical environments.

suggestions.title: Hallucination Auditing with RAG
suggestions.desc: Implement a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system that checks the 'well-written' output against Google Scholar data to ensure the AI hasn't fabricated scientific facts during the rewriting process.

suggestions.title: Automated Structural Peer-Review Agent
suggestions.desc: Develop a logic-checking module that evaluates if the generated report follows standard academic structures (IMRaD) and flags logical inconsistencies between the Abstract and Conclusion.

title: Wrist Intent Recognition for Stroke Rehabilitation
authors: Sum an Bharti
abstract: Abstract Hand mentor robotic device is beneficial for stroke patients . This is rehabilitation technique used in stroke therapy. It strengthens and improves the range of motion which ultim ately improves the quality of life for severely impaired stroke patients. It is easy to use without assistance and most importantly stroke survivors able to use independently. Usage of hand mentor device is quite expensive for stroke patients on hourly basis . Com ing up with most efficient deep learning algorithm for sensor data is motivation to cut down the cost and easy availability usage for stroke patients. EMG signal is recorded using relevant sensors which provides useful information to in

suggestions.title: Edge-AI Wearable Integration
suggestions.desc: Deploy the deep learning model onto an ESP32 or Arduino Nano 33 BLE using TensorFlow Lite Micro. This turns the 'expensive' robotic device logic into a low-cost, portable wearable that patients can use at home with simple EMG sensors.

suggestions.title: Gamified VR Rehab Experience
suggestions.desc: Connect the intent recognition system to a Unity-based VR game. Patients could 'cast spells' or 'fly a plane' in VR by performing specific wrist movements, increasing motivation and adherence to the rehabilitation schedule through gamification.

suggestions.title: Transfer Learning for Personalized Recovery
suggestions.desc: Implement a transfer learning framework where a base model is trained on a general population, but then fine-tuned on an individual patient's unique muscle signals. This accounts for muscle atrophy or variations in stroke severity, making the recognition much more accurate.

suggestions.title: Tele-Rehabilitation Progress Tracker
suggestions.desc: Build a cloud-based dashboard (using AWS Amplify) that logs every successful 'intent recognized' by the device. Doctors can remotely log in to see a patient’s Range of Motion (ROM) trends and strength improvements over time without the patient needing to visit a clinic.

project_key: legacy:GR-029|Spring 2021|wrist intent recognition for stroke rehabilitation

title: Design and Implementation of a Microservices Web-based Architecture for Code Deployment and Testing
authors: Soin Abdoul Kassif Baba M Traore
abstract: Many tech stars like Netflix, Am azon, PayPal, eBay, and Twitter are evolving from monolithic to a microservice architecture due to the benefits for Agile and DevOps teams. Microservices architecture can be applied to multiple industries, like IoT, using containerization. Virtual containers give an ideal environm ent for developing and testing IoT technologies. Since the IoT industry has exponential growth, it is the responsibility of universities to teach IoT with hands-on labs to minim ize the gap between what the students learn and what is on-dem and in the job market. That can be done by using containerization. There are many approaches in the containerization field, but they can be
supervisor: Dr. Maria Valero m valero2@ kennesaw.edu Dr Hossain Shahriar

suggestions.title: WebRTC Integration for Remote Triage
suggestions.desc: Port the Transdermal Optical Imaging algorithm to JavaScript using WebAssembly. This allows the vital measurement to run directly in a web browser, enabling doctors to see a patient's live pulse during a standard video call without extra hardware.

suggestions.title: Contactless Stress and Anxiety Detection
suggestions.desc: Expand the algorithm to detect Heart Rate Variability (HRV) from the facial blood flow data. Use this as a proxy for the Autonomic Nervous System state to build a tool that monitors worker stress levels or patient anxiety in waiting rooms.

suggestions.title: Low-Light RPPG for Sleep Monitoring
suggestions.desc: Optimize the computer vision pipeline to work with Near-Infrared (NIR) camera feeds. This would allow for a non-contact baby monitor or sleep lab tool that can track vitals in total darkness without disturbing the subject.

suggestions.title: Edge-Device Optimization for Kiosks
suggestions.desc: Rewrite the processing pipeline using NVIDIA TensorRT or OpenVINO to run at high frame rates on edge hardware like the Jetson Nano. This could be deployed in airport or office kiosks for rapid, contactless health screening.

project_key: legacy:GR-040|Spring 2021|design and implementation of a microservices web-based architecture for code deployment and testing

title: An efficient intrusion detection framework based on federated learning for IoT networks.
authors: Osam a Shahid
abstract: There are abundant number of IoT devices that are connected on over multiple networks. These devices can be exposed to multiple different types of network threats. Though, these devices do have security and software that does act as a wall of protection we purpose a Federated Learning (FL) approach that would allow detection of threats of a network for IoT devices. Federated Learning can be best described as decentralized training. Adhering to the GDPR rules that prevent data from being distributed, FL addressed the challenge by bringing the ML model to the data rather than the traditional method where the data had to be extrapolated and taken to the ML model. This type of setting is i

suggestions.title: Heterogeneous FL with Flower Framework
suggestions.desc: Implement the federated learning framework using the 'Flower' (flwr.dev) framework to support a mix of devices (Raspberry Pis, Jetson Nanos, and mobile phones). This tests how the IDS handles varying compute power across an IoT network.

suggestions.title: Differential Privacy for IoT Traffic
suggestions.desc: Enhance the GDPR compliance by adding a Differential Privacy layer (using PySyft or Opacus). This injects mathematical noise into the model updates before they are sent to the central server, ensuring that individual IoT device patterns can never be reconstructed.

suggestions.title: Adversarial Attack Resilience Testing
suggestions.desc: Study the impact of 'Model Poisoning' attacks where a compromised IoT node sends malicious updates to the global model. Develop a 'robust aggregation' algorithm, like Krum or Median-based aggregation, to filter out these malicious updates automatically.

suggestions.title: Blockchain-Backed FL Incentive System
suggestions.desc: Integrate an Ethereum smart contract that rewards IoT device owners with tokens for contributing high-quality, verified model updates. This solves the 'participation problem' in federated learning by providing a commercial incentive for decentralized training.

project_key: legacy:GR-044|Spring 2021|an efficient intrusion detection framework based on federated learning for iot networks.

title: How could IoT assist healthcare system during COVID-19 or future pandemics?
authors: Moham mad Nasajpour Esfahani
abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT), a technology built upon sensors and devices, has shown great applicability among various dom ains, especially healthcare. This pandem ic has critically impacted all parts of society including people, health centers, businesses, authorities, etc. Researchers are attem pting to adopt different technologies to mitigate this virus faster and save more lives. Regarding the great benefits that Internet of Things (IoT) has brought into different areas within the healthcare dom ain, this technology has been performing several main tasks including diagnosing, monitoring, tracing, disinfecting, and vaccinating to combat this virus. Our research is conducted of the
supervisor: Prof. Seyedam in Pouriyeh: supervisor Prof. Reza Meim andi Parizi: second

suggestions.title: Low-Latency Edge Pandemic Alerts
suggestions.desc: Move the pandemic monitoring logic from the cloud to the edge using TensorFlow Lite on Raspberry Pi 4. This reduces the latency for critical alerts, such as detecting social distancing violations or high temperatures in real-time without needing a constant high-bandwidth cloud connection.

suggestions.title: Smart Quarantine Wearable Mesh
suggestions.desc: Develop a Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) mesh network specifically for quarantine centers or elderly homes. Use the RSSI signal strength to map interactions between residents and staff, providing a 'contact tracing' heat map stored on AWS IoT Core for administrative oversight.

suggestions.title: Privacy-Preserving Health Streams
suggestions.desc: Integrate Differential Privacy algorithms into the IoT data pipeline to ensure that individual health metrics (heart rate, temperature) cannot be reverse-engineered from the cloud dataset. Use the Opacus library to add noise to the data before it leaves the local network.

suggestions.title: IoT Disinfection Management System
suggestions.desc: Extend the scope to hospital infrastructure by building a system that tracks the usage of hand sanitizer stations and UV-C disinfection robots. Use ESP32 microcontrollers and ultrasonic sensors to trigger automated cleaning schedules based on room occupancy data.

similar: legacy:UC-036|Spring 2021|using machine learning techniques to predict rt-pcr results for covid-19 patients.
project_key: legacy:GR-064|Spring 2021|how could iot assist healthcare system during covid-19 or future pandemics?

title: Defensive Neural Network
authors: Hongkyu Lee
abstract: Machine learning (ML) algorithms require a massive amount of data. Firms such as Google and Facebook exploit user's data to deliver a more precise ML-based service. However, collecting users' data is a risky action because their private data can be leaked through the transm ission. As a rem edy, federated learning is introduced. In federated learning, a central server distributes a machine learning model to users. Each user trains the model to its data, and send the model back. Later the models are aggregated and distributed again. Federated learning is more secure in that it
topics: Other (explain in the com m ents section)

suggestions.title: Differential Privacy Noise Injection
suggestions.desc: Implement the Laplace mechanism to add calibrated noise to local gradients before they are sent to the central server, protecting against model inversion attacks.

suggestions.title: Byzantine-Robust Aggregation Testing
suggestions.desc: Implement and compare defense algorithms like Krum, Bulyan, and Trimmed Mean to see which best protects the global model from 'poisoning' by malicious clients.

suggestions.title: Secure Multi-Party Computation Gateway
suggestions.desc: Utilize the PySyft library to build a prototype where gradients are split into encrypted shares, ensuring the server never sees even a single raw model update.

suggestions.title: Collaborative Medical Diagnostic Network
suggestions.desc: Apply this defensive FL framework to a medical imaging task (e.g., pneumonia detection) allowing multiple hospitals to train a joint model without sharing HIPAA-protected patient images.

similar: legacy:GRCS-01|Spring 2017|deep convolutional neural network and parallel programming

title: Energy Cost and Efficiency on Edge Computing: Challenges and Vision
authors: Kousalya Banka
abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) has been the key for many advancem ents in next- generation technologies for the past few years. With a conceptual grouping of ecosystem elem ents such as sensors, actuators, and smart objects connected to performcomplex operations to performenvironm ental monitoring, intelligent transport system , smart building, smart cities, and endless other possibilities. Edge computing helps the IoT’s reach even further and be more robust by connecting multiple censored devices through the internet and form ing powerful computational capabilities. Unfortunately, this computation level comes at a cost as the devices are constantly being used to communicate

suggestions.title: Dynamic Frequency Scaling for Edge Nodes
suggestions.desc: Implement a Dynamic Voltage and Frequency Scaling (DVFS) controller for Raspberry Pi edge nodes. Use Python to monitor CPU load and adjust clock speeds programmatically to find the 'sweet spot' where energy consumption is minimized without dropping sensor data packets.

suggestions.title: RL-Based Task Offloading Simulator
suggestions.desc: Create a simulation environment using EdgeSimPy where a Reinforcement Learning (RL) agent decides whether to process a task locally or offload it to the cloud. Train the agent to maximize battery life for solar-powered IoT sensors while meeting strict latency constraints.

suggestions.title: Solar-Aware LoRaWAN Gateway
suggestions.desc: Build an energy-harvesting edge gateway for remote agricultural monitoring. Use a LoRaWAN module and a battery management system (BMS) to throttle data transmission frequency based on the current state of charge and predicted solar irradiance for the next 24 hours.

suggestions.title: K3s Energy Profiler for Smart Buildings
suggestions.desc: Deploy a lightweight Kubernetes (K3s) cluster on a fleet of ARM-based devices and use the Kepler (Kubernetes-based Efficient Power Level Exporter) tool to benchmark the energy cost of running different containerized microservices in a smart building context.

similar: legacy:GR-02|Spring 2016|iot system to measure student physiological responses
project_key: legacy:GR-038|Spring 2021|energy cost and efficiency on edge computing: challenges and vision

title: Framework for Collecting Data from specialized IoT devices.
authors: MD SAIFU L ISLAM
abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) is the most significant and bloom ing technology in the 21st century. IoT has rapidly developed by covering hundreds of applications in the civil, health, military, and agriculture areas. IoT is based on the collection of sensor data through an embedded system , and this embedded system uploads the data on the internet. Devices and sensor technologies connected over a network can monitor and measure data in real-tim e. The main challenge is to collect data from IoT devices, transm it them to store in the Cloud, and later retrieve them at any tim e for visualization and data analysis. All these phases need to be secure by following security protocol to ensur
supervisor: Dr. Maria Valero, Dr. Hossain Shahriar

suggestions.title: No-Code IoT Visualization Bridge
suggestions.desc: Develop a middleware that automatically generates Grafana or InfluxDB dashboards based on the metadata of connected specialized devices. Use a schema-first approach where the IoT device 'registers' its data types, and the framework auto-provisions the database tables and UI components.

suggestions.title: Industrial Vibration Predictive Maintenance Framework
suggestions.desc: Specialize the framework for IIoT by integrating high-frequency vibration sensors (ADXL345). Use the FFT (Fast Fourier Transform) on the embedded side to extract features before uploading, allowing the system to predict motor failure in industrial machinery.

suggestions.title: Underwater IoT Acoustic Framework
suggestions.desc: Adapt the framework for specialized underwater sensors using acoustic modems. Since bandwidth is extremely limited, implement a custom delta-compression algorithm to ensure that critical environmental data (salinity, pH) can be transmitted over low-bitrate acoustic links.

suggestions.title: Hardware-Root-of-Trust Data Pipeline
suggestions.desc: Enhance the framework's security by integrating Hardware Security Modules (HSM) or ATECC608A chips on the embedded side. This ensures that every piece of data collected is cryptographically signed at the source, preventing data injection attacks in military or health applications.

project_key: legacy:GR-045|Spring 2021|framework for collecting data from specialized iot devices.

title: Predicting Users' Engagement During Interviews with Biofeedback, Voice, and Supervised Machine Learning
authors: Thaide Huichapa
abstract: Studies show that the quality of the information collected during an elicitation interview, and consequently the quality of the software product that needs to be developed, highly depends on the interviewee's engagem ent. Because of social expectations, interviewees tend to hide if they are bored or not engaged. To overcom e this problem and support the analyst during the interviews, this research uses biom etric data and voice features, together with supervised machine learning algorithms, to predict the interviewee's engagem ent. We built our solution on an experim ent consisted of interviewing 31 participants. We collected the data using an Em patica wristband and a default recorder fr

suggestions.title: Real-Time Interviewer Feedback Overlay
suggestions.desc: Build a desktop application that acts as a transparent overlay for Zoom or Microsoft Teams. It uses the project's ML models to provide a real-time 'engagement gauge' to the interviewer, warning them if the subject's voice features suggest boredom or fatigue.

suggestions.title: Automated Soft-Skills Training Portal
suggestions.desc: Target job seekers by creating a mock-interview platform. Use the biofeedback and voice models to generate a post-interview report that highlights exactly when the user's engagement peaked or dipped, helping them improve their communication style.

suggestions.title: Multimodal Temporal Transformer Extension
suggestions.desc: Upgrade the supervised learning approach to a Multimodal Transformer. Use temporal data from both voice (MFCCs) and eye-tracking (using MediaPipe) to capture the nuances of engagement over the duration of a 30-minute elicitation session, rather than just static snapshots.

suggestions.title: Stakeholder Friction Detection Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Create a tool for project managers that aggregates engagement scores from multiple stakeholder interviews. By visualizing 'points of friction' across different interviewees, the tool can identify which specific software requirements are most controversial or misunderstood.

project_key: legacy:GR-050|Spring 2021|predicting users' engagement during interviews with biofeedback, voice, and supervised machine learning

title: An Investigation on Non-Invasive Brain-Computer Interfaces: Emotiv Epoc+ Neuroheadset and Its Effectiveness
authors: Md Jobair Hossain Faruk
abstract: Neurotechnology describes as one of the focal points of today’s research around the dom ain of Brain-Com puter Interfaces (BCI). The prim ary attem pts of BCI research are to decoding hum an speech from brain signals and controlling neuro- psychological patterns that would benefit people suffering from neurological disorders. In this study, we illustrate the progress of BCI research and present scores of unveiled contem porary approaches. First, we explore a decoding natural speech approach that is designed to decode hum an speech directly from the hum an brain onto a digital screen introduced by Facebook Reality Lab and University of California San Francisco. Then, we study a recently prese
supervisor: Prof. Maria Valero Prof. Hossain Shahriar
topics: Other (explain in the com m ents section)

suggestions.title: EEG-Based Assistive Virtual Keyboard
suggestions.desc: Use the Emotiv SDK to build a virtual keyboard controlled by mental commands (P300 waves or motor imagery). Focus on creating a high-accuracy 'speller' that allows non-verbal users to select letters or common phrases through concentrated thought.

suggestions.title: Deep Learning Noise Filter for EEG
suggestions.desc: Improve the effectiveness of the Emotiv Epoc+ by building a preprocessing pipeline using 1D-CNNs or Independent Component Analysis (ICA) to strip out muscle artifacts (blinks, jaw clenches). This will significantly clean the signal for better downstream classification of brain states.

suggestions.title: BCI-Driven Focus Mode for ADHD
suggestions.desc: Develop a productivity application that monitors Alpha/Beta wave ratios in real-time. When the headset detects that the user's focus is drifting, the app can automatically trigger 'Do Not Disturb' modes on their devices or play low-frequency binaural beats to help refocus.

suggestions.title: Unity BCI Gameplay Integration
suggestions.desc: Integrate the neuroheadset with a Unity-based game where the environment reacts to the player's emotional state. For example, the player's 'mana' or 'psychic power' in-game could recharge faster when the Emotiv data indicates a calm, meditative state.

project_key: legacy:GR-053|Spring 2021|an investigation on non-invasive brain-computer interfaces: emotiv epoc+ neuroheadset and its effectiveness

title: COVID-19 Mortality Prediction using Machine Learning Techniques
authors: Lindsay Schirato, Kennedy Makina, Dwayne Flanders
abstract: In late 2019, SARS-CoV2 also known as COVID-19 was first identified in the city of Wuhan, China. This virus can infect a person and without showing any signs of sickness, can spread of COVID-19 unknowingly. The World Health Organization declared it a global pandem ic in March 2020 because of its far-reaching effects in every part of the world. Scientists have been working to leverage technology to prevent spread, detection and vaccine developm ent. With machine learning, models can predict which patient will most likely have a higher mortality rate. Using W EKA, a machine learning tool and a data set based on 95,000 Mexican patients with 20 clinical features, our research applies model
topics: Other (explain in the com m ents section)

suggestions.title: Explainable Mortality Risk (XAI)
suggestions.desc: Integrate SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) or LIME into the existing model. This creates a dashboard that not only predicts mortality but explains *why* (e.g., 'high risk due to age combined with hypertension'), making the tool more trustworthy for medical practitioners.

suggestions.title: Socio-Economic Mortality Correlator
suggestions.desc: Merge the COVID-19 dataset with US Census socio-economic data (income levels, population density, air quality). Retrain the model to identify how environmental factors amplify mortality risk, providing public health officials with a 'vulnerability map' for targeted intervention.

suggestions.title: Cross-Variant Transfer Learning
suggestions.desc: Test the model's robustness against different variants (Delta vs. Omicron) by using transfer learning. Fine-tune the pre-trained model on newer, smaller datasets to see if the predictors of mortality remain consistent as the virus evolves.

suggestions.title: Edge-Deployed Clinical Risk Triage
suggestions.desc: Package the mortality prediction model into a lightweight mobile app using FastAPI and React Native. This allows field healthcare workers in low-connectivity areas to input patient vitals and get an immediate risk assessment without needing a central server.

similar: legacy:UC-036|Spring 2021|using machine learning techniques to predict rt-pcr results for covid-19 patients.

title: Machine Learning Techniques for Malware Network Traffic Detection
authors: Jerm aine Cam eron
abstract: Persistent malware variants are a constant threat to computing infrastructure across all regions and business sectors. Traditional detection systems focus prim arily on signature-based analysis but this approach cannot adequately keep pace with the velocity and volum e of new malware variants that are continuously deployed onto the internet. Most network traffic detection techniques are focused on analyzing raw packets and have not deterred the surge of persistent malware. Therefore, it is important to develop new research techniques that are focused on optim ized metadata from malware network traffic to effectively identify an ever- increasing expanse of malicious software. Recent re

suggestions.title: Live Stream Packet Classifier
suggestions.desc: Build a real-time network monitor using the Scapy library that intercepts packets from a live interface and feeds them into the project's ML model. Create a dashboard that highlights suspicious traffic streams as they occur, moving beyond offline dataset analysis.

suggestions.title: Adversarial Traffic Pattern Synthesis
suggestions.desc: Develop a testing suite that uses Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) to craft 'stealth' malware traffic designed to bypass the detection model. Use these results to retrain the classifier, making it more resilient to future obfuscation techniques.

suggestions.title: Encrypted Traffic Fingerprinting (ETA)
suggestions.desc: Extend the project to detect malware within encrypted HTTPS/TLS streams without decryption. Focus the ML model on 'fingerprinting' packet lengths, arrival times, and TLS handshake metadata to identify Command & Control (C2) signals.

suggestions.title: ELK Stack Security Integration
suggestions.desc: Develop a Logstash plugin that pipes network logs into the trained ML model for classification. Visualize the results in Kibana, creating a comprehensive SIEM (Security Information and Event Management) tool that maps detected malware to specific IP regions and timeframes.

project_key: legacy:GR-023|Spring 2021|machine learning techniques for malware network traffic detection

title: Representation Learning for Motion Sequence
authors: Saisangararam aleengam Alagapan
abstract: This research project proposes a new deep learning architecture that is used to align hum an poses to be used in an exercise assistant system . In short, the assistant system takes a video feed of a user doing exercise, then provides visual feedback by comparing the user’s current pose to a professional trainer’s pose that is stored in the system . We design a new deep architecture to accom plish this task and show better accuracy and efficiency.
supervisor: Project Sponsors - Dr. Ying Xie & Dr. Linh Le Project Advisor- Dr. Meng Han

suggestions.title: Physical Therapy ROM Tracker
suggestions.desc: Adapt the exercise assistant for clinical physical therapy (e.g., ACL recovery). Use MediaPipe to calculate precise joint angles (Range of Motion) and compare them to a 'healthy' movement baseline, providing quantitative progress reports for therapists.

suggestions.title: Mobile Exercise Shadow-Boxer
suggestions.desc: Optimize the deep learning architecture for mobile devices using TensorFlow Lite or CoreML. Create an AR app where a 'ghost' of the trainer is overlaid on the user's smartphone screen, allowing them to align their poses in real-time while outdoors or at the gym.

suggestions.title: Automated Sports Video Indexer
suggestions.desc: Build a tool that processes hours of sports footage (like basketball or golf) and automatically clips segments where a specific pose or motion sequence occurs. Use the project's representation learning to 'search' for high-quality vs. poor-quality techniques in a video database.

suggestions.title: Multi-Person Pose Synchronization
suggestions.desc: Extend the architecture to handle multiple people simultaneously. This could be used for dance troupe training or cheerleading practice, where the system measures the 'sync-score' of the group relative to a master choreographer's sequence.

title: Defending data reconstruction through adaptive image augmentation
authors: Seunghyeon Shin
abstract: In this paper, we introduce a data augm entation-based defense strategy for preventing the reconstruction of training data through the exploitation of stolen model gradient. The collection of training data to a centralized server has been required for the training of neural networks in traditional machine learning. However, as privacy becom es a significant concern, the concept of Federated learning is introduced. In federated learning, a centralized server shares the well-trained neural network and participating end-users send the gradient back to the server after training without sharing the sensitive data itself. As the concept of federated learning does not share the original data that

suggestions.title: DP-SGD Utility Benchmark
suggestions.desc: Compare the adaptive image augmentation defense against Differential Privacy (DP-SGD). Use the PySyft library to implement both and measure the 'Privacy-Utility Trade-off'—how much does the model's accuracy drop compared to how much protection it offers against reconstruction attacks?

suggestions.title: Gradient Inversion Attack Suite
suggestions.desc: Build a 'stress test' tool that implements state-of-the-art gradient reconstruction attacks (like DLG or GS). Use this suite to find the 'breaking point' of the adaptive augmentation defense, identifying which image types or model layers are still vulnerable.

suggestions.title: Federated Medical Image Shield
suggestions.desc: Apply the defense strategy to a federated learning network for medical imaging (e.g., MRI or X-rays). Prove that the augmentation successfully hides patient-specific details while still allowing the central model to learn the features of rare diseases across different hospitals.

suggestions.title: Secure MPC Hybrid Defense
suggestions.desc: Combine the image augmentation strategy with Secure Multi-Party Computation (SMPC). By splitting the gradients into secret shares across multiple servers, you can create a 'defense-in-depth' model that is mathematically impossible to reconstruct even if the augmentation is bypassed.

project_key: legacy:GR-070|Spring 2021|defending data reconstruction through adaptive image augmentation

title: Software Engineer – Clarity LLC
authors: Am y Mullins
abstract: Clarity makes an app called CaptionMate that does closed captions for phone calls.. During the internship, a website was made to visualize metrics that are collected on users such as calls made, minutes used, tim e active, region, age, them e, font, and platform used. Bar charts are used to show minutes and calls used on days of the week. 100% bar charts are used to show how much a day contributes to the usage of the app; a user contributes to minutes, calls and platform usage; and show calls incom ing vs. outgoing. Line graphs were created to show growth in app usage and number of new users over tim e. All the data iis displayed in tables too. Who used the CaptionMate app, how mu

suggestions.title: Web-Based Multiplayer Matchmaking
suggestions.desc: Migrate the custom backend to a Node.js/Socket.io environment to support real-time online play. Implement a 'Fuzzy Lobby' where users can challenge friends or find random opponents globally.

suggestions.title: Explainable Fuzzy Decision UI
suggestions.desc: Create a 'Thought Stream' sidebar that visualizes the fuzzy logic membership functions in real-time. This would show the user exactly why the AI decided a piece was 'mostly taken' or 'partially safe' based on the distributed inputs.

suggestions.title: Mobile 'Fuzzy Chess' Game App
suggestions.desc: Port the game to Unity or React Native to reach a wider audience. Add a 'Daily Puzzle' mode where players must find the best move in a high-entropy fuzzy state, monetizing through cosmetic piece skins or ad-free play.

suggestions.title: Reinforcement Learning for Fuzzy Tuning
suggestions.desc: Use Deep Reinforcement Learning (RL) to train the chess AI. Instead of manually setting fuzzy rules, the AI can play millions of games against itself to 'learn' the optimal fuzzy weights for different game phases (opening vs. endgame).

title: TeleClinic
authors: Jay P Bhatt, Lucius Burch, Zekai Fei, Bijoy Shah, Hao Zhang 9/8/23, 11:36 AM Spring 2021 C-Day Program - College of Computing and Software Engineering
abstract: TeleClinic is a telem edicine web application that provides ease of access and a medium for interaction between patients and their respective doctors and adm inistrators. In particular, this web portal includes a chat feature, an area for medical reports, an area for appointm ent requests, and an area for video recordings. Additionally, TeleClinic meets the requirem ents prescribed by the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (H.I.P.A.A.) via upholding data privacy and safeguarding medical information. To maxim ize its overall utility, TeleClinic utilizes the React and React Redux libraries for its front-end and a NoSQL database in Google Firebase for its back-end.

suggestions.title: End-to-End HIPAA Compliant Messaging
suggestions.desc: Upgrade the Django backend to support end-to-end encryption (E2EE) for patient-doctor messages using the Signal Protocol (libsignal). Implement client-side decryption in React to ensure health data remains private even if the database is compromised.

suggestions.title: Automated Symptom-to-Specialist Triage
suggestions.desc: Integrate a NLP model like BioBERT or the Infermedica API to analyze patient messages and health reports. The system should automatically flag high-risk symptoms and suggest the appropriate medical specialist to the primary care physician.

suggestions.title: Wearable Device Data Integration Hub
suggestions.desc: Extend the Django models to ingest real-time health data from Google Fit or Apple HealthKit APIs. This would allow doctors to view a dashboard of the patient’s heart rate, sleep cycles, and daily steps alongside their manual health reports.

suggestions.title: Prescription Management and Pharmacy API
suggestions.desc: Build a module that allows doctors to issue digital prescriptions directly through the app. Integrate with the DoseSpot or Surescripts API to transmit these orders to local pharmacies and notify patients when their medication is ready.

title: Malware Analysis Using Reverse Engineering
authors: Shamour Jones, Cynthia S Marcellus, Andy Pham, Nathan Rowe, Joshua Rowland
abstract: The motivation for this project is driven by evaluation of the different tools on the market that allow for breaking down executables or binary files, and understanding what the malware is doing. By reverse-engineering the malware, we can understand its impact and how to protect against it. Our focus is to understand where different tools are stronger than others, as well as understand the evolving landscape of malware and security overall. For this capstone project, we utilized two different tools and many sam ple malware files. The methods used to debug the malware are detailed in our milestone two report and will be expanded upon in our final presentation. At this point, we've fou
supervisor: Dr. Ying Xie yxie2@ kennesaw.edu and Dr. Hossain Shahriar

suggestions.title: Visual Body Map Symptom Tracker
suggestions.desc: Replace text-based input with an interactive 3D or 2D body map (using Three.js or SVG). Parents can tap specific areas (e.g., 'lower right abdomen') to log pain or rashes, making the data entry more intuitive and precise.

suggestions.title: Pediatric Dosage Safety Calculator
suggestions.desc: Build a safety module where parents enter their child's weight and age. Using a database of common OTC medicines (like Acetaminophen), the app calculates the correct dosage and warns against potential ingredient overlaps (e.g., taking two medicines containing Tylenol).

suggestions.title: Tele-Ready PDF Export for Pediatricians
suggestions.desc: Develop a 'Share with Doctor' feature that generates a professionally formatted PDF timeline of symptoms, fever spikes, and medications. This summary can be emailed or uploaded to patient portals like MyChart before a consultation.

suggestions.title: Community Illness Heatmap
suggestions.desc: Implement anonymized data sharing to create a local heatmap of common symptoms (e.g., 'High flu activity in Cobb County'). Use GeoJSON and Mapbox to help parents understand if their child's symptoms match current local trends.

title: Covid-19 Data Analysis - Regression
authors: Noah Druss

suggestions.desc: Integrate the Wolfram Alpha API to not only solve the equation but also provide the 'Show Steps' breakdown. This would transform the project from a simple calculator into a robust pedagogical tool for home-schooled students.

suggestions.desc: Using ARCore or ARKit, create a mobile interface that overlays the solution directly on top of the handwritten paper in the camera view. This provides immediate verification without the user having to switch between a camera and a results screen.

suggestions.title: Multi-Line and Calculus OCR Expansion
suggestions.desc: Improve the image segmentation model to handle multi-line systems of equations and advanced calculus symbols (integrals, summations). Use a Transformer-based architecture like ViT (Vision Transformer) to improve accuracy on complex layouts.

title: Information Recall For Kids With Autism
authors: Alex J Bechke, Elizabeth Burnside, Haiden Gem binski, Ross Murphy, Ryan Taylor, Henok Dem isse
abstract: Description: Our project "Information Recall For Kids With Autism " also known as the product nam e given by the client "Safe Kid" is an app to help children with autism understand basic contact information such as phone numbers, addresses, and nam es. This app is being created for our client Spectrum Behavioral Associates who specialize in helping kids and young adults who have autism , learning developm ent delays, or other behavioral challenges. Motivation: To teach kids who have autism basic contact information in case of an emergency. Materials and Methods: The app we are creating is a local based app made within Unity. The coding language we are using is C# and we are incorporat
supervisor: Professor - Dr. Parizi Client - Spectrum Behavioral Associates Personnel -

suggestions.title: AR Safety Info Scavenger Hunt
suggestions.desc: Turn the information recall into an Augmented Reality (AR) game using ARKit. Children could find 'virtual' numbers or address icons hidden around their actual home, using spatial memory to help anchor their understanding of their real-world contact information.

suggestions.title: Voice-Practice Speech Recognition
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Web Speech API to allow children to practice *saying* their contact information aloud. The app provides positive visual reinforcement (gamification) when the speech recognition matches the stored home address or parent phone number.

suggestions.title: Therapist Analytics Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Build a backend using Firebase that tracks which specific pieces of information take the longest to recall. Generate a 'Progress Heatmap' for therapists or parents to identify if a child is struggling specifically with numbers, names, or spatial concepts.

suggestions.title: Safe-Kid Wearable Companion
suggestions.desc: Extend the app to a wearable device like the Apple Watch. If the child feels lost, a simplified 'Recall' interface on the watch can display their contact info or use haptic pulses to guide them to a 'safe location' pre-programmed in the app.

title: Comprehensive Security Solution for small E-commerce Business
authors: Patrick McCollums, Hristo Bakalov, Philinda Morse, Tyler Phillips, Watson Day
abstract: Project Description: Create an e-commerce server and a comprehensive security program to protect a web server for a simulated small business. This server will include security tools such as intrusion detection, firewall, and network monitoring. The installation and maintenance of this solution will be docum ented as part of the final docum entation package. The server will be reviewed for exploitation from other teams while we attem pt the exploitation of their server(s). Research/Motivation: How to research, install, configure, and integrate various open-source software packages for information security, e-commerce, web hosting, and database. Our motivation for this project was to
supervisor: Project Sponsor: Dr. Lei Li Professor: Dr. Ying Xie

suggestions.title: Automated Chaos Engineering and Recovery
suggestions.desc: Extend the e-commerce server by implementing a 'Chaos Monkey' script that randomly disables security services or creates network partitions. Develop an automated self-healing layer using Kubernetes operators to redeploy compromised pods and restore firewall configurations instantly.

suggestions.title: ELK-Powered ML Anomaly Detection
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Elasticsearch, Logstash, and Kibana (ELK) stack to aggregate logs from the existing IDS and firewall. Build a Machine Learning model using Scikit-learn to identify non-signature-based threats, such as slow-and-low DDoS attacks or credential stuffing patterns.

suggestions.title: PCI-DSS Compliance Auditing Tool
suggestions.desc: Transform the security solution into a compliance-focused dashboard for small businesses. Build a script that scans the server's current configuration against the PCI-DSS 4.0 standard for credit card processing and generates a PDF 'readiness report' for the owner.

suggestions.title: Zero-Trust API Gateway Implementation
suggestions.desc: Remove the traditional perimeter-based security and implement a Zero-Trust architecture using an API gateway like Kong or Traefik. Require mutual TLS (mTLS) for all internal service communications and implement OIDC-based identity verification for every server request.
