title: Understanding the Drivers of Medication Nonadherence in the United States (Undergraduate
authors: Jasm ine Hem phill, Alyssa Myers, Matt K Warm an
abstract: This study explores the performance of multifamily loans using a logistic regression model to predict loan outcomes as either “closed” or “current”. Utilizing a dataset of over one million observations and 54,771 unique loan observations, we classify loan status based on Freddie Mac’s mortgage performance codes, with closed loans including modification with a loss, foreclosures, real estate owned, and fully closed loans. Through explanatory analysis, it reveals a nearly balanced distribution between the binary variables. This dataset supports the use of a logistic regression to model the probability of loan default or completion. The findings have implications for risk mitigation in underwriting practices, helping lenders avoid loans with characteristics like those that historically defaulted.

suggestions.title: Gradient Boosted Tree Ensemble Migration
suggestions.desc: Replace the Logistic Regression model with XGBoost or LightGBM to capture non-linear relationships between loan features (e.g., the interaction between property age and market interest rates). This could significantly improve the detection of 'closed' loans which often have complex, multi-factor causes.

suggestions.title: Macro-Economic Stress Test Simulator
suggestions.desc: Build a Streamlit dashboard that allows users to adjust external economic sliders (e.g., +2% Federal Interest Rate, +5% Unemployment). The app uses the trained model to predict how many 'Current' loans in the Freddie Mac dataset would shift to 'Closed/Foreclosure' status under those conditions.

suggestions.title: Geospatial Risk Clustering Analysis
suggestions.desc: Integrate the loan data with ArcGIS or Folium to map performance by Metropolitan Statistical Area (MSA). Analyze whether loan defaults are clustered geographically and correlate those clusters with local economic indicators like regional GDP growth or industry-specific layoffs.

suggestions.title: Real Estate Investor Risk-Scoring API
suggestions.desc: Develop a lightweight API where small-scale multifamily investors can input their own loan details (LTV, debt service coverage ratio, etc.). The API returns a 'Risk Grade' (A-F) based on the patterns learned from the million-observation Freddie Mac dataset.

title: Analyzing Concentration Levels in Online Education Using Machine Learning (Undergraduate
authors: Beniam in Costea, Iram Nawaz, Natan Beraki, Daniel Lopez, Carter Richter, Yeonkuk Woo
abstract: The Indy Micro is a desktop application which simulates the functionality of an eight-bit personal computer. Its aim is to mimic the feel of owning one such computer in that era, as well as provide an engaging way to learn about low-level computing concepts. The Micro consists of two components: the virtual machine, which is based on the Von Neumann architecture, and the code editor, which allows users to write assembly code and execute it on the virtual machine. The aim is for the Indy Micro to serve as an educational jumping-off point, a step between the casual programmer and the dedicated hobbyist, developing software for real eight-bit systems. One of the ways students get started with programming is with Scratch (scratch.mit.edu), a visual drag-and-drop programming experience. The project’s goal is to create something like Scratch, but for assembly language, allowing students and hobbyists to learn about low level programming.
supervisor: supervisor : Dr. Reza Parizi project owner : Woodline Interiors (client)

suggestions.title: Physical Register Display Bridge
suggestions.desc: Build a hardware interface using an ESP32 to map the simulator’s virtual registers to physical 8-bit LED arrays and toggle switches, allowing users to 'hand-code' the virtual machine via physical hardware inputs.

suggestions.title: Cycle-Accurate Performance Profiler
suggestions.desc: Develop a visual debugger that tracks clock cycles and memory access patterns in real-time. Use this to help students visualize the 'Von Neumann Bottleneck' by showing how data movement between the CPU and memory impacts execution speed.

suggestions.title: Indy-ASM to WebAssembly Compiler
suggestions.desc: Create a toolchain that compiles the project's custom assembly language into WebAssembly (Wasm). This would allow students to write code in the Indy Micro editor and export it as a standalone, high-performance web application.

suggestions.title: IoT Retro-Dashboard Node
suggestions.desc: Repurpose the virtual machine to act as a low-overhead logic controller for IoT sensors. Use the 8-bit simulation to process sensor data (like temperature) and output retro-style visualizations to an attached OLED display.

title: Pose Extraction for Real-time Workout Assist_Capstone Group_W01_Spring Semester
authors: Royce Cam p, Zach Christm as, Jonathon Segars, Am anda Mead, Cam eron Page
abstract: Angel Among Us is a non-profit organization that saves animals from high-killing rate shelters in Georgia. Their goal is to find homes for homeless pets. To increase their efforts, they are developing a web-based application to help improve adoption processes. The goal is to increase adoption rates and be able to provide adopters with information about pets and overall reduce the number of pet returns. This will be accomplished by using adopters’ information and preferences from the web-based application to find long-term compatibility with their recommended pets. The objective is to create a web application interface that includes many core components. First, the web application will use PetFinder API to collect pet data and use a catching mechanism to increase data storage by updating pet data daily and ensuring enhanced performance. The mechanism will also decrease the frequency of calls leading to good API accuracy. Adopters will then complete questionnaires to gather information about their preferences, lifestyle, allergies, and experiences. The system will use ChatGPT to analyze the adopter's results and send out recommended pet suggestions based on the given data. Also, pet profiles will be created to get information for database analysts. This will ensure that PetFinder’s animal description template will be consistent with the saved information from pet profiles to have proper pet descriptions. Overall, this approach encourages responsible pet ownership, reduces pet returns, and increases successful adoption rates.
supervisor: Dr. Ying Xie & Dr. Linh Le Dr. John Johnson – Exercise Science

suggestions.title: Behavioral Matchmaking Engine
suggestions.desc: Implement a machine learning model using scikit-learn that weights adopter lifestyle data (activity level, home size) against pet temperaments to predict compatibility scores, specifically aiming to lower the rate of pet returns.

suggestions.title: PetFinder API Regional Aggregator
suggestions.desc: Expand the scope by integrating the PetFinder API to pull data from multiple Georgia shelters into the matching interface, creating a centralized hub for high-kill shelter rescues across the state.

suggestions.desc: Use a pre-trained ResNet model to automatically scan uploaded pet photos and suggest tags for physical traits (e.g., 'pointy ears,' 'tri-color') to save shelter volunteers time during the data entry process.

suggestions.title: Post-Adoption Support Chatbot
suggestions.desc: Build an AI support bridge using the OpenAI API that provides new owners with breed-specific training tips and medical reminders, helping them navigate the first 90 days of pet ownership to ensure a permanent placement.

abstract: Cybercrim es are a billion-dollar industry that is rapidly growing by the day. One of the biggest threats faced by companies is the infection of malware. New forms of malware are created daily and ever evolving to evade detection methods. Understanding how malware infects your system and how it eludes detection is crucial to keeping a company's network and devices safe. During this project we will be using reverse engineering methods to better understand the functionality of malware, as well as how it eludes detection. We will be using IDAPro and WiDbg to performthe reverse engineering. Using this knowledge, we will create a set of security standards to help companies to protec

title: Development of an Automated Software Packaging Solution for Linux
authors: Robert D Ryan, Sam antha R Figueroa, Dylan Parker, Blair Hill, Bishwo R Marhatta
abstract: The main problem with using Linux software in the science and Bioinform atics community is because Linux has a large number of distributions and dependencies. This hinders researches and science students with the problem of tracking down dependencies for software which could then further break the existing system dependencies. Our team looked to solve these problems by creating a BASH script that could quickly mass package AppIm ages and contain Linux software with all dependencies. Our team worked through the last ten weeks and researched all components of AppIm age and discovered all means to more easily package and have a repeatable process for batch software processing. Our grou
supervisor: Capstone Course Instructor: Dr. Ming Yang, m yang8@ kennesaw.edu

suggestions.title: GitHub Actions Auto-Packaging Pipeline
suggestions.desc: Turn the BASH script into a reusable GitHub Action that scientists can drop into their research repos. The action should detect dependencies from a requirements.txt or environment.yml, build the AppImage, and attach it to a new GitHub Release automatically.

suggestions.title: Cross-Platform GUI Packaging Studio
suggestions.desc: Build a desktop application using Electron or PyQt that provides a 'drag-and-drop' interface for the script. This allows non-technical Bioinformaticians to select a folder of scripts, choose target Linux architectures, and bundle them into an AppImage without using the command line.

suggestions.title: Secure Sandboxing for Bioinformatic AppImages
suggestions.desc: Enhance the AppImage security by integrating Firejail profiles into the packaging process. Create a system where the packaged science software runs in a restricted sandbox, preventing accidental modification of host system files during large-scale data processing.

suggestions.title: Differential AppImage Delta-Update System
suggestions.desc: Develop a binary delta-update mechanism specifically for large scientific software. Instead of re-downloading a 2GB AppImage, build a client-side tool that uses Zsync to only download the changed dependency blocks, saving significant bandwidth for research labs.

title: Using Machine Learning Techniques to Predict RT-PCR Results for COVID-19 Patients.
authors: Bradley T Durden, Mathew Shulm an, Andy Reynolds, Thom as A Phillips, Dem ontae Moore, Indya Andrews
abstract: With the COVID-19 pandem ic still a threat, healthcare professionals and medical industries keep searching for better ways to mitigate the spread of COVID-19. While Machine Learning has been applied in many other dom ains, there is now a high dem and for diagnosis systems that utilize Machine Learning techniques in the healthcare dom ain and in particular combating COVID-19. In this project, we explore the role of Machine Learning models in combating COVID-19, using W EKA as the main
supervisor: Dr. Ming Yang - IT 4983 Capstone Professor Dr. Seyedam in Pouriyeh -

suggestions.title: Deep Learning Transition to TabTransformer
suggestions.desc: Move beyond WEKA's basic algorithms and implement a TabTransformer using PyTorch. This model architecture is specifically designed for tabular clinical data and can handle the complex categorical relationships found in patient medical histories better than traditional trees.

suggestions.title: Privacy-Preserving Federated Learning Pipeline
suggestions.desc: Implement a Federated Learning framework using Flower or OpenMined. This allows the model to be trained across multiple hospital datasets without actually sharing sensitive patient RT-PCR data, solving the privacy and HIPAA compliance hurdles of medical AI.

title: Journalistic Integrity vis Artifical Intelligence
authors: Dylan Dalton, Ray Martin, Duy Nquyen, Yesse Quezada, Brian Dom inguez
abstract: We are developing a web app to recognize and rate political bias in online journalism using artificial intelligence. All hum an writing inherently contains bias ,however bias is less harmful if it is transparent to the reader because they can now make informed decisions about what they read. We've collected articles and and reactions to them from online sources, and then used Neural Networks trained for natural language processing to determine bias. The project can predict bias labels on a news articles with 82% accuracy.
supervisor: Reza Meim andi Parizi - course instructor Asher Nuckolls - project owner

suggestions.title: Real-time Bias Highlight Browser Extension
suggestions.desc: Develop a Chrome/Firefox extension that applies the trained NLP model to the active tab's news article. It should highlight specific sentences or paragraphs in different colors based on their detected bias (e.g., red for left-leaning, blue for right-leaning) as the user reads.

suggestions.title: The 'Echo Chamber' Personal Analytics Tool
suggestions.desc: Create a user dashboard that tracks a user's reading history via the web app. Analyze their cumulative bias score over a month and suggest articles from high-integrity sources with opposing viewpoints to help 'burst' their information bubble.

suggestions.title: Logical Fallacy Detection via LLM Integration
suggestions.desc: Extend the project beyond political bias to detect logical fallacies (e.g., Ad Hominem, Strawman). Use a Large Language Model (LLM) like Llama 3 to flag deceptive rhetorical techniques in news articles, providing a 'fallacy count' alongside the bias rating.

suggestions.title: Temporal Bias Evolution Tracker
suggestions.desc: Build a data pipeline that scrapes specific news outlets over six months and visualizes how their bias rating shifts in response to specific major events (e.g., elections, conflicts). Use time-series analysis to identify which outlets maintain the most consistent neutrality.

title: An Empirical Study of Thermal Attacks on Edge Platforms
authors: Tyler Holm es, Justin Duchatellier
abstract: Cloud-edge systems are vulnerable to therm al attacks as the increased energy consum ption may rem ain undetected, while occurring alongside normal, CPU - intensive applications. The purpose of our research is to study therm al effects on modern edge systems. We also analyze how performance is affected from the increased heat and identify preventative measures. We speculate that due to the technology being a recent innovation, research on cloud-edge devices and therm al attacks is scarce. Other research focuses on server systems rather than edge platforms. In our paper, we use a Raspberry Pi 4 and a CPU -intensive application to represent therm al attacks on cloud-edge systems. We

suggestions.desc: Using a headset with integrated eye-tracking (like the Vive Pro Eye), correlate pupil dilation and fixation duration with the note-taking 'motor skill' events to determine if physical writing actually reduces mental fatigue during long lectures.

title: The Accessibility of the Mobile Gaming Platform for the Visually Impaired
authors: Christian Thom as Jansen
abstract: The motivation for this project is to research mobile gam ing interfaces with the goal of conceptualizing practices in gam e design that would create more accessible interfaces for the visual impairm ent community. Thus far, the project has focused on practices that mobile gam e designers can use to make their gam es more accessible to the visually impaired. These includes the use of plain text rather than graphics to be scannable by screen readers, the inclusion of audio-oriented support and instruction, the use of contrasting colors to make options more recognizable to those with partial visual impairm ents, and the implem entation of gam e mechanics that can be learned and

title: Cultiva- The Plant Companion
authors: Travis R Hescox, Kat Branham, Ahsan Jam al, Joseph Henggeler, Erick Reyes, Andy Alcaraz, Mom odou Mbye
abstract: The goal of this project is to improve the lives of gardeners or everyday people with a green thum b by providing them with a planter that will not only hold their plant of choice, but also give them information about the health and growth of their plant without them having to interact with it directly. This project will provide the user with a planter containing sensors that communicate with an application from which the user can monitor the environm ent of the plant. This project can serve a wide range of people from first-tim e gardeners to seasoned veterans. Users will no longer have to guess about the health and conditions of their plant, all of this information will be available

suggestions.title: Autonomous Closed-Loop Irrigation
suggestions.desc: Upgrade the IoT planter with a water pump and light controller. Use the sensor data to implement a PID controller that maintains the exact moisture level required by the specific plant species without any human intervention.

suggestions.title: Plant 'Mood' AI Chatbot
suggestions.desc: Connect the sensor data to an LLM (Large Language Model) via an API. The plant can then 'chat' with the user through the app, expressing its needs or health status in a personality-driven way based on real-time data (e.g., 'I'm feeling a bit thirsty today!').

suggestions.title: LoRaWAN Mesh for Community Gardens
suggestions.desc: Replace Wi-Fi with LoRaWAN to extend the range to several miles. This allows a single gateway to monitor 100+ planters across an entire community garden, providing a centralized dashboard for urban farming collectives.

suggestions.title: Plant Disease Identification via Vision
suggestions.desc: Add a camera module to the planter that periodically takes photos of the leaves. Use a pre-trained CNN (like PlantVillage dataset models) to identify signs of pests or disease early, sending a notification to the user with treatment advice.

title: Machine Learning: Twitter Bots in Disguise
authors: Matthew Joseph Scheer, Nicolas Vasquez, Jam es C Andersen, Joshua Tiangco, Justin Van, Cody R Walicek, Daniel Rim mel
abstract: This project was designed to help fight against misinform ation spread by bots(computers), the goal assigned to us was to find and inform Twitter users of bots that follow and are being followed by the user.

suggestions.title: Bot Swarm Graph Topology Analysis
suggestions.desc: Move beyond individual bot detection by using Graph Theory (NetworkX) to find bot 'swarms.' Identify clusters of accounts that all follow each other and post the same content simultaneously, which is a hallmark of state-sponsored disinformation campaigns.

suggestions.title: Timeline Integrity Browser Overlay
suggestions.desc: Build a Chrome extension that analyzes the Twitter/X timeline in real-time. It should place a color-coded 'Trust Score' badge next to every username based on the likelihood that the account is an automated bot or a sock-puppet.

suggestions.title: Adversarial LLM-Bot Detection
suggestions.desc: Research and implement detection methods specifically for bots using LLMs (like GPT-4). Train a classifier to detect 'AI-typical' sentence structures and repetitive semantic patterns that traditional bot detectors often miss.

suggestions.title: Influencer Audience Auditor for Brands
suggestions.desc: Develop a commercial tool that provides a 'Follower Quality' report for any public profile. The report should break down the percentage of fake vs. real followers, helping businesses avoid spending marketing budget on influencers with inflated bot audiences.

project_key: legacy:UC-062|Spring 2021|machine learning: twitter bots in disguise

title: Team 10B BChain: secure peer to peer file sharing
authors: Jonathan D Lashgari, Carlos A Diaz, Jeffery Erhunse, Caleb T Goff, Giang T Nguyen
abstract: BChain is a new P2P file sharing system that is fully private, anonym ous, globally self-verifying, and utilizes an autom atic peer-m aintained network of trust in data, accom plished through new methods of routing content over the whole network, encrypted, rather than per torrent download. Verification is done by adding file metadata to a blockchain giving the network consistent knowledge of each file it can transfer, and how to verify file received against the network. This enables a policy of zero trust against peers. This system is implem ented by an app that interfaces with the network using the protocol, using it for upload, download and file discovery. The interface is built using web te

suggestions.title: Proof-of-Storage Token Incentives
suggestions.desc: Build a token economy on a low-fee blockchain like Solana. Peer nodes should earn 'BChain Tokens' for successfully hosting and serving files to others, creating a self-sustaining decentralized marketplace for file storage.

suggestions.title: Zero-Knowledge File Verification
suggestions.desc: Implement Zero-Knowledge Proofs (ZKP) to verify that a peer possesses a file without requiring them to reveal the file content or their identity. This enhances the privacy of the network and prevents 'poisoning' attacks by malicious peers.

suggestions.title: Decentralized Streaming Media Player
suggestions.desc: Build a specialized media player client that can stream video files directly from the BChain network as they download. Implement sequential block prioritization so users can watch movies hosted on the P2P network with minimal buffering.

suggestions.title: IPFS Multi-Protocol Bridge
suggestions.desc: Create a bridge between BChain and the InterPlanetary File System (IPFS). This would allow files on BChain to be addressed via IPFS CIDs (Content Identifiers), making the data more accessible to the wider decentralized web ecosystem.

project_key: legacy:UC-069|Spring 2021|team 10b bchain: secure peer to peer file sharing

title: BreastNet;
authors: Cora L Meador, Ryan Deem
abstract: In the United states, 13% of wom en are diagnosed with breast cancer in their lifetim e, and it is the second leading cause of death by cancer in wom en. Early detection and screening can result in an increase of life expectancy by 10 years on average. Unfortunately, breast cancer can be challenging to detect, since it can appear anywhere in the breast. Cancer that is detected in its early stages can give patients more options and save thousands of dollars in medical costs. Som e of the most recent developm ents in computer science and machine learning are in the biom edical field, especially individualized healthcare. There is also an increase in the dem and for telehealth options, re

suggestions.title: Explainable AI (XAI) for Clinical Trust
suggestions.desc: Integrate Grad-CAM or SHAP libraries to generate heatmaps showing which pixels triggered a positive cancer classification. This allows radiologists to verify the model's logic against their own expertise rather than treating the AI as a black box.

suggestions.title: Edge-Deployed Mobile Screening Tool
suggestions.desc: Convert the trained model to TensorFlow Lite or CoreML and build a smartphone app for clinicians in low-resource areas. The app can use the camera to scan physical mammogram films and provide an immediate 'second opinion' risk score.

suggestions.title: Multi-Modal Fusion with Clinical Metadata
suggestions.desc: Extend the architecture to a Two-Stream network that processes the image and patient metadata (age, genetic markers, history) simultaneously. Use a Late Fusion approach to see if demographic data significantly reduces the false-positive rate.

suggestions.title: Synthetic Data Augmentation with GANs
suggestions.desc: Use Generative Adversarial Networks (specifically CycleGAN) to generate synthetic malignant samples to balance the dataset. This addresses the common issue of imbalanced medical datasets where positive cancer cases are much rarer than negative ones.

title: Using Semantic Segmentation in a Convoluted Neural Network for Vocal Localization in Music
authors: Trevor E Stanca, Noah Trinite
abstract: I. PROJECT OVERVIEW A. Research Question In this project, the question was asked: ”Is there an easier way to extract vocals from music?” Many other works are able to extract vocals with Deep Neural Networks using Multitask Learning, which are large and take a long tim e to train. To rival this, we wish to present a method to identify vocals with a Convolutional U -Network (U -Net) for Sem antic Segm entation of audio files. B. Project Description This project differs from other works by identifying vocal locations by converting audio files in Short Tim e Fourier Transforms(STFT), and treating them as images in the U Net. By treating these as images, the U -Net is able to identify the loca

suggestions.title: Hybrid Waveform-Spectrogram U-Net
suggestions.desc: Combine the frequency-domain U-Net with a Wave-U-Net that operates directly on the raw audio waveform. Use the MUSDB18 dataset to train a hybrid model that minimizes phase reconstruction artifacts common in STFT-based extraction.

suggestions.title: AI-Powered Karaoke Multi-Track Generator
suggestions.desc: Develop an automated pipeline that extracts vocals and then uses the 'vocal-less' track to identify instrumental onsets. Build a web interface using React and Tone.js that allows users to mute/solo specific stems in real-time.

suggestions.title: Temporal Consistency for Long-Form Audio
suggestions.desc: Implement a Recurrent U-Net (R-U-Net) using LSTM or GRU layers within the bottleneck of the architecture. This ensures that the vocal extraction remains smooth across minutes of audio, preventing the 'chirping' noise often found in frame-by-frame processing.

suggestions.title: Multi-Source Separation for Orchestral Music
suggestions.desc: Pivot the semantic segmentation from binary (vocal/non-vocal) to multi-class separation (drums, bass, piano, and strings). Use the Spleeter library as a benchmark to see if your U-Net can achieve higher Signal-to-Distortion Ratio (SDR) on classical recordings.

project_key: legacy:UR-048|Spring 2021|using semantic segmentation in a convoluted neural network for vocal localization in music

title: Analyzing Concentration Levels in Online Learning with Facial Values
authors: Elliott J Witherell, Jakeira Askew, Jonathan R Dicks, Steven C McGuire, Jacob A Walton
abstract: Can deep learning models accurately predict whether an individual is focused or distracted on a task in order to improve learning efficiency? In the context of online learning with the use of a webcam , this project is aim ed at detecting concentration levels of students to potentially assist with improving learning efficiency. Machine learning technologies have been utilized to evaluate students’ facial expression and eye movem ents to identify whether a student is focused or distracted.
supervisor: Dr. Linh Le (Sponsor/Project Owner) Dr. Ying Xie (Sponsor/Project Owner)

suggestions.title: Real-Time Distraction Alerts for Zoom/Teams
suggestions.desc: Create a browser extension that hooks into the webcam feed during video calls. When the model detects a 'distracted' state for more than 30 seconds, it triggers a subtle notification or logs the event for a post-session productivity report.

suggestions.title: Privacy-Preserving Edge Processing with MediaPipe
suggestions.desc: Port the detection model to run entirely on-device using MediaPipe and WebAssembly. This ensures student facial data never leaves the local machine, addressing major privacy concerns inherent in online learning monitoring.

suggestions.title: rPPG Heart Rate Correlation Analysis
suggestions.desc: Integrate Remote Photoplethysmography (rPPG) to estimate the student's heart rate via the webcam. Research whether heart rate variability (HRV) correlates with the model's concentration scores to build a more holistic 'Focus Index'.

suggestions.title: LMS-Integrated Adaptive Content Delivery
suggestions.desc: Build a Moodle or Canvas plugin that uses the concentration API to pause educational videos when a student looks away. The system could automatically offer a 5-minute 'brain break' or suggest reviewing the previous section if focus dips.

similar: legacy:UC-020|Spring 2021|analyzing concentration levels in online education using machine learning (undergraduate
project_key: legacy:UC-059|Spring 2021|analyzing concentration levels in online learning with facial values

title: Video-to-Video Synthesis With Semantically Segmented Video
authors: Aydan Mufti, Jordan S Hasty
abstract: Our project involves studying the usage of generative adversarial networks (GANs) to translate sem antically segm ented video to photo-realistic video in a process known as video-to-video synthesis. The model is able to learn a mapping from sem antically segm ented masks to real-life images which depict the corresponding sem antic labels. To achieve this, we employ a conditional GAN-based learning method that produces output conditionally based on the source video to be translated. Our model is capable of synthesizing a translated video, given sem antically labeled video, that resem bles real video by accurately replicating low-frequency details from the source.

suggestions.title: Temporal Stability via Optical Flow
suggestions.desc: Integrate a Warp Loss layer using FlowNet2 to ensure consistency between consecutive video frames. This prevents the 'flickering' effect in synthesized videos by ensuring the generated pixels align with the movement of the semantic masks.

suggestions.title: Virtual Urban Planning Visualization Tool
suggestions.desc: Build an interface where city planners can 'paint' semantic maps (e.g., adding a park or a bike lane) and see a photorealistic rendering of the new streetscape. Use the Cityscapes dataset to fine-tune the GAN for high-fidelity urban environments.

suggestions.title: Dynamic Background Replacement for Remote Work
suggestions.desc: Apply the video-to-video synthesis to replace messy home backgrounds with photorealistic, lighting-consistent office environments. Use the model to synthesize reflections and shadows on the user to make the replacement look grounded.

suggestions.title: Few-Shot Domain Adaptation for Weather Effects
suggestions.desc: Modify the GAN architecture to allow for domain adaptation using only a few reference images. Enable the system to transform a standard daytime semantic video into a rainy or snowy version without needing a massive new dataset.

project_key: legacy:UR-060|Spring 2021|video-to-video synthesis with semantically segmented video

title: Low Cost High Impact Fall Detection At The Edge
authors: Dylan SIRNA
abstract: With this project, we designed a general-purpose 16-bit RISC+CISC computer architecture, alongside an assembler, instruction embedder, and preliminary compiler. Our computer architecture, K86 (Kennesaw 86), is inspired by the Intel x86 and ARM architectures that have enabled computing systems to perform many of the modern functionalities we rely on today. To allow for fluid programming and processing, KASM (Kennesaw Assembler) translates assembly code into machine instructions which will be stored in the computer memory by the embedder. With the addition of a preliminary compiler to produce assembly from high-level source code, our project defines much of the foundation of a sophisticated computing system.

suggestions.title: K86 FPGA Hardware Synthesis
suggestions.desc: Translate the K86 architecture into Verilog or VHDL to synthesize it on a Xilinx Artys-7 FPGA. Move from a simulated environment to a physical 16-bit processor capable of driving hardware peripherals like LEDs and UART.

suggestions.title: Five-Stage Pipeline with Hazard Detection
suggestions.desc: Extend the K86 ISA to support a 5-stage pipeline (Fetch, Decode, Execute, Memory, Write-back). Research the performance gains in Instructions Per Cycle (IPC) by implementing a basic branch predictor.

suggestions.title: The K86 Retro-Game Development Kit
suggestions.desc: Design a small graphical library in KASM and create a 'Snake' or 'Pong' game. This would require extending the architecture to support a VGA controller or a memory-mapped display buffer.

suggestions.title: WebAssembly-based K86 Visual IDE
suggestions.desc: Build a browser-based IDE where students can write KASM, compile it using the existing assembler, and see a step-by-step visual representation of the registers, stack, and memory during execution.

project_key: legacy:UR-063|Spring 2021|low cost high impact fall detection at the edge

title: CNN CIFAR Image Identification
authors: Matteo L Staciarine
abstract: Reducing the learning rate of a CNN can positively affect the validation accuracy of a machine learning model. Dropping out nodes from different layers can further delay overfitting from happening. Validation loss decreases over more epochs, but it must be cut when it reaches its minim um value.

suggestions.title: Automated Hyperparameter Optimization (HPO) Pipeline
suggestions.desc: Use the Optuna framework to automate the search for optimal learning rates, dropout ratios, and weight decay. Create a visualization dashboard to show how different hyperparameter combinations affect the CIFAR-100 validation surface.

suggestions.title: Knowledge Distillation for Mobile CIFAR
suggestions.desc: Train a high-accuracy 'Teacher' model (like ResNet-101) and use its outputs to train a tiny 'Student' model. Compare the performance of the distilled student model against the original CNN to see how much compression is possible without losing accuracy.

suggestions.title: Adversarial Robustness and Defense Testing
suggestions.desc: Attack your CNN using the Fast Gradient Sign Method (FGSM) to see how easily the model can be fooled by noise. Implement Adversarial Training by including these perturbed images in the training loop to harden the model against visual noise.

suggestions.title: Intelligent Edge-Vision Benchmark
suggestions.desc: Quantize the model to 8-bit integers and deploy it to an ESP32-CAM or Arduino Nicla Vision. Benchmark the real-world trade-offs between inference speed, power consumption, and accuracy for simple object identification tasks.

title: Apotheosis
authors: Joseph DuBois, Mike Grimshaw, Jake Howard

suggestions.title: Biometric-Responsive Horror Mechanics
suggestions.desc: Integrate a heart rate monitor (via Arduino or Apple Watch) to the Unity/Unreal engine. The game's atmosphere should change dynamically; if the player's heart rate is low, the game spawns 'jumpscares' or increases environmental tension to keep them in a state of fear.

suggestions.title: Procedural Phobia Generation System
suggestions.desc: Build an engine that procedurally generates levels based on specific phobias (e.g., arachnophobia, thalassophobia). Use a tagging system to determine which assets, lighting, and sound effects are loaded based on the player's selected 'fears' at the start of the game.

suggestions.title: VR Exposure Therapy Sandbox
suggestions.desc: Port the game to Meta Quest 2/3 and retool the mechanics for therapeutic use. Create a controlled environment where users can gradually increase the intensity of their phobia triggers, tracking their progress through an in-game psychological analytics dashboard.

suggestions.title: Adaptive Soundscape with Spatial Audio
suggestions.desc: Implement the FMOD audio engine to create a non-linear, adaptive soundscape. Sounds should not just be 3D, but should 'evolve' (e.g., a whisper becoming a scream) based on the player's proximity to darkness or their current 'Sanity Meter' level.

title: Horizon
authors: Jason Bourn , Jaylin Ferguson-Gillam

suggestions.title: Advanced AI Pathfinding and Senses
suggestions.desc: Implement a 'Sensory Perception' system for guard NPCs that includes peripheral vision, hearing (surface-dependent footsteps), and smell. Use Hierarchical Finite State Machines (HFSM) to allow guards to coordinate searches when they find an open window or a missing item.

suggestions.title: Community Level Editor & Steam Workshop Integration
suggestions.desc: Build an in-game map editor using a tile-based system that allows players to design their own 'unstealable' vaults. Integrate the Steam Workshop API to allow for sharing, rating, and global leaderboards for the fastest completion times.

suggestions.title: Asymmetric Multiplayer: Cat vs. Security
suggestions.desc: Develop a 1v1 multiplayer mode where one player controls the cat (stealth) and the other player acts as the Security Chief, placing traps, monitoring cameras, and directing guards in real-time from a top-down tactical view.

suggestions.title: Physics-Based Destructible Heist Environments
suggestions.desc: Integrate NVIDIA PhysX to allow for realistic environmental interaction. Knocking over a vase should create noise that alerts guards, and players should be able to use the game's physics to create distractions, like pushing a heavy object to block a door.

title: Lights Out
authors: Anthony Barrett , Brandon Seals

suggestions.title: Unreal Engine Metahuman Integration
suggestions.desc: Upgrade the game’s character models using Epic Games’ Metahuman Creator to push the visual fidelity to the extreme. Focus on implementing 'Micro-Expressions' during dialogue scenes to intentionally trigger the Uncanny Valley effect as a narrative device.

suggestions.title: LLM-Driven Dynamic Dialogue System
suggestions.desc: Replace scripted dialogue with a local LLM (like Llama 3) to allow players to speak to NPCs using their actual voice (Speech-to-Text). NPCs should respond based on their unique personality profiles, creating unpredictable and eerie interactions.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Ray-Traced Global Illumination
suggestions.desc: Implement DirectX Raytracing (DXR) to create hyper-realistic lighting and reflections. Focus on 'Mirror Horror' mechanics, where reflections in the environment don't perfectly match the player's movements, heightening the sense of unease.

suggestions.title: Haptic Suit Integration for Immersion
suggestions.desc: Add support for haptic feedback suits (like bHaptics). Program specific vibrational patterns to simulate the sensation of something 'brushing past' the player or the physical vibration of a heavy heartbeat when the character is in a high-stress 'Uncanny' situation.

title: Open the gates
authors: Preston Waters

suggestions.title: Predictive Pathfinding with Traffic Simulation
suggestions.desc: Implement an A* pathfinding extension that accounts for dynamic obstacles and traffic density. Use a weight-based cost function to simulate rush hours, requiring the player to choose between longer routes or high-traffic shortcuts.

suggestions.title: Asymmetric Multiplayer Delivery Race
suggestions.desc: Develop a 1v1 multiplayer mode using Mirror or Photon where one player acts as the courier and the other acts as a 'Dispatcher' who can trigger road closures, weather events, or boost pads in real-time.

suggestions.title: Real-World GIS Map Integration
suggestions.desc: Use the Mapbox SDK for Unity to transform real-world OpenStreetMap data into playable levels. Allow users to input their own zip code to generate a delivery course based on their actual neighborhood streets.

suggestions.title: Physics-Based Cargo Integrity System
suggestions.desc: Create a sophisticated damage model where different cargo types (fragile, liquid, explosive) respond to G-forces and collisions. Use Spring Joints and custom shaders to visualize liquid sloshing and impact fractures.

title: Patrons
authors: Anthony Barrett

suggestions.title: Procedural Guest Personalities
suggestions.desc: Integrate a local Large Language Model (LLM) or the OpenAI API to generate unique backstories and dynamic dialogue for patrons based on their procedurally generated stats and spending habits.

suggestions.title: Real-time Dynamic Economy
suggestions.desc: Implement a supply-and-demand simulation where patron satisfaction and spending affect the global price of goods, using a Node.js backend to synchronize the market across different players' sessions.

suggestions.title: Heat-Map Behavioral Analytics
suggestions.desc: Develop a telemetry system that tracks patron movement and dwell time using Unity Analytics; visualize this data as a heat map to help players optimize their shop layouts for maximum efficiency.

suggestions.title: Event-Driven Seasonal Shifts
suggestions.desc: Create a weather and holiday system that changes patron behavior and needs (e.g., higher demand for cold drinks in summer), requiring the player to swap inventory and decor seasonally.

title: Potion Panic
authors: Trevor Love

suggestions.title: VR Alchemy Laboratory
suggestions.desc: Port the potion-making mechanics to VR using the OpenXR toolkit, allowing users to physically pick up flasks, pour liquid using physics-based shaders, and grind ingredients with motion controllers.

suggestions.title: Asynchronous Trading Post
suggestions.desc: Build a central server using Firebase to allow players to bottle their unique concoctions and list them in a global marketplace where other players can purchase them for in-game currency.

suggestions.title: Chemistry Curriculum Bridge
suggestions.desc: Replace fictional ingredients with real-world elements and chemical compounds, using the game mechanics to teach students about ionic bonding and basic chemical reactions.

suggestions.title: Genetic Ingredient Evolution
suggestions.desc: Implement a system where players can cross-breed plants to create new ingredients, using genetic algorithms to determine the properties, color, and rarity of the resulting hybrid.

title: Slow Poke
authors: Michael Isaza

suggestions.title: Biometric Heart-Rate Sync
suggestions.desc: Integrate a smartwatch API to sync the character's movement speed or time-dilation effect with the player's real-time heart rate, making the game more challenging as the player becomes excited.

suggestions.title: Mobile AR Environment Interaction
suggestions.desc: Use ARCore or ARKit to place the 'Slow Poke' character in the real world, where physical furniture is mapped as obstacles through the phone's camera and LiDAR sensor.

suggestions.title: Adaptive Accessibility Controller
suggestions.desc: Develop a control scheme specifically for players with limited mobility, utilizing Tobii Eye Tracking or single-switch input methods to navigate the game's slow-paced environment.

suggestions.title: Perception-Based AI Stealth
suggestions.desc: Design an AI system for predators that detects motion through pixel-delta analysis; the character is only 'invisible' when moving below a specific velocity threshold, emphasizing the 'slow' mechanic.

title: Playstation VR Project
authors: Jason Bourn , Robert Kowalchuk, Robert Jimenez, Kevin Friddle
abstract: PSVR - PlayStation Virtual Reality is the latest VR device recently released by Sony. We built interactive games on PSVR, which will provide the players with fully immersive game experiences. The players will make real time strategies and use their body motions to play the game, which is pretty engaging. Please come and be the first group of players on the earth to experience a virtual environment built by CCSE students and generated with the top tier game console - PlayStation 4. Capstone Projects (29)

suggestions.title: Voice-Activated Unit Command
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Wit.ai or Speech-to-Text API to allow players to issue complex tactical commands ('Archers, flank left!') verbally while using motion controllers for spatial positioning.

suggestions.title: Asymmetric Mobile Commander
suggestions.desc: Build a companion mobile app that allows a second player to view a 2D strategic map and call in support abilities (like air strikes or healing) while the VR player is on the ground.

suggestions.title: Spatial Sound Localization AI
suggestions.desc: Implement HRTF (Head-Related Transfer Function) audio cues that allow players to locate hidden enemies or objective pings solely by sound, enhancing the situational awareness required in VR RTS.

suggestions.title: Mixed Reality Strategy Table
suggestions.desc: Migrate the project to a Passthrough-enabled headset (like Quest 3) to project the strategy map onto the player's real-world table, using spatial anchors to maintain persistent unit positions.

title: (?)Machine
authors: Weston Ford, Derek Maier, David Nelson
abstract: The Lambda-Machine platform is a distributed computing framework that is both easy to use and easy to deploy. The mission of this project is to create a tool that computer savvy individuals, such as systems administrators, would find effective, yet intuitive enough that researchers from other fields could harness its distributed computing capability. These are the driving principles behind the Lambda-Machine project. The Lambda-Machine describes a software mechanism for distributing scripts across nodes in a local area network or virtual private network. The script algorithm will be parallelized and concurrently computed by the nodes on the subnet. The platform features a web-based interface

suggestions.title: Dockerized Edge Orchestration
suggestions.desc: Extend the framework to support Docker container execution across subnet nodes, allowing for the distribution of complex environments rather than just simple scripts.

suggestions.title: Automated Cloud-Bursting Bridge
suggestions.desc: Develop a connector for AWS Lambda that automatically scales the workload to the cloud when local subnet CPU utilization exceeds a pre-defined threshold.

suggestions.title: Raft Consensus Fault Tolerance
suggestions.desc: Implement the Raft consensus algorithm to ensure the system remains operational even if the primary coordinator node fails, allowing for seamless leader election within the local network.

suggestions.title: Distributed Video Transcoding
suggestions.desc: Create a specialized module for the framework that splits large 4K video files into chunks, processes them with FFmpeg across all available nodes, and reassembles them on the master node.

title: AccessibilityRF
authors: Mayima Clark, Jacob Ertell, John Ellison
abstract: Many activation switches for accessible doors may be concealed, hard to reach, or found a far distance from the door. This is a complication that needs to be addressed, and we propose AccessibilityRF: a piece of hardware that allows users to operate accessibility doors remotely. Remote accessible door openers can be developed by reverse engineering radio frequencies activation switches use to open the door when pressed. Signals emitted from the activation switch will be recorded, demodulated, and stored to transmit whenever the developed device is used in front of the door in question. The device will increase accessibility for everyone with mobility impairments and provide a solution to a n

suggestions.title: BLE Mesh Extension
suggestions.desc: Incorporate Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) mesh networking to allow the devices to act as repeaters, extending the range of operation through large buildings without requiring stronger RF transmitters.

suggestions.title: NFC Mobile Credentialing
suggestions.desc: Develop a secure mobile app that uses the smartphone's NFC chip or Apple/Google Wallet APIs to authenticate the user before the RF signal is triggered for the door.

suggestions.title: Rolling Code Decryption
suggestions.desc: Research and implement a more robust signal processing layer capable of handling modern 'Rolling Code' security protocols found in high-security commercial door systems.

suggestions.title: Computer Vision Proximity Trigger
suggestions.desc: Integrate an ESP32-CAM module running a lightweight TensorFlow Lite model to automatically detect a wheelchair or walker approach and trigger the door without manual button presses.

title: Atlanta Toolbank Tool Reservation
authors: Alleyne, Kerwin Shaun , Barahona, Christian Alejandro , Seymour, Alan
abstract: Garrett , Abdulhadi, Mulk , Guarnieri, Kyla Marie The Atlanta Community ToolBank, a nonprofit organization, stewards an inventory of tools for lending to charitable organizations to increase the impact of their mission-related efforts in the community. Atlanta ToolBank is in need of a member-facing app (or mobile friendly web pages) to automate the tool-lending reservation and confirmation process.

suggestions.title: Predictive Demand Modeling
suggestions.desc: Use historical reservation data and a scikit-learn regression model to predict seasonal demand for specific tools, helping the ToolBank optimize their inventory purchasing and maintenance schedules.

suggestions.title: QR-Based Asset Maintenance
suggestions.desc: Build a module where volunteers can scan a QR code on a physical tool to instantly view its maintenance history, report damage, and upload photos of repairs directly to the database.

suggestions.title: Volunteer Route Optimization
suggestions.desc: Integrate the MapBox Matrix API to automatically group tool deliveries and pickups by location, providing volunteer drivers with the most fuel-efficient route for the day's tasks.

suggestions.title: LoRaWAN Geofencing
suggestions.desc: Incorporate low-power LoRaWAN GPS trackers for high-value machinery (like wood chippers) to monitor real-time location and alert staff if tools move outside the designated project boundaries.

title: AutoMD
authors: Jacob Davidson, Christopher Rene, Michael Vanderbilt
abstract: AutoMD is an application, which is aimed at helping people determine what caused their car trouble. The application features a system, much like WebMD, which will use feedback from the user to diagnose potential solution to automotive breakdowns and general problems. Many times people have no idea what caused the vehicle breakdown and may or may not know what to do in these times. Our application provides a way for even the most inexperienced person to figure out what is wrong and what to do next when their car breaks down.

suggestions.title: Bluetooth OBD-II Integration
suggestions.desc: Connect the application to ELM327 Bluetooth scanners to pull real-time Diagnostic Trouble Codes (DTCs) from the car's computer and automatically match them to solutions in the expert system.

suggestions.title: Localized Repair Marketplace
suggestions.desc: Create a portal where users can send their 'AutoMD diagnosis' to local, highly-rated mechanics via the Yelp or Google Places API to receive instant repair quotes.

suggestions.title: Acoustic Fault Diagnosis
suggestions.desc: Implement a machine learning model trained on audio datasets (misfires, belt squeals, grinding) that can analyze engine sounds recorded through the phone's microphone to suggest potential issues.

suggestions.title: Computer Vision Part Identifier
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Google Vision API to allow users to photograph a component under the hood; the app identifies the part and provides a customized diagnosis guide for that specific item.

title: Chocolate Soup: Quest for the Compiler
authors: Jonathan Gascoigne, Nathaniel Jean, Shelby Silcox
abstract: We present "Chocolate Soup: Quest for the Compiler," an educational video game geared toward middle school students to adult learners to teach basic programming skills. "Chocolate Soup: Quest for the Compiler" is an interactive, third person adventure game that is able to meet the educational needs of the student by providing multiple levels that reinforce the lessons taught from previous levels.

suggestions.title: Visual-to-Code Compiler Bridge
suggestions.desc: Develop an export tool that converts the player's in-game logic blocks into actual Python or JavaScript files, allowing them to see their game solutions running in a standard IDE.

suggestions.title: Multiplayer Logic Dungeons
suggestions.desc: Implement a collaborative mode using Socket.io where two players must write snippets of code or solve logic gates together to open doors and progress through the game's adventure.

suggestions.title: LLM-Powered Socratic Tutor
suggestions.desc: Integrate a local Llama-3 model to provide contextual, non-spoiler hints. If a player is stuck, the AI asks guiding questions about their logic rather than giving the answer.

suggestions.title: Minecraft Mod Curriculum
suggestions.desc: Port the educational curriculum into a Minecraft Mod using the Forge API, allowing the 'Quest' to take place in a sandbox environment that students are already familiar with.

title: Course Substitution Management System
authors: Bilal Abdullah, Eric Brovont, Samir Dave, Andrew Walker
abstract: We have created an automated course substitution management system to be used in higher education. Our system was designed to render course substitution paper forms obsolete. The current system in place at many universities, including Kennesaw State University, is one that requires a form to be filled out by hand and then submitted to a Program Coordinator and Department Chair for approval. There are some issues that can arise from this procedure. Many times forms get lost or are ineligible for submission. Our system is digitally accessible through an OWL Express type system. This makes it easier and more efficient as a process. The system automatically checks to see if the class the student

suggestions.title: Degree Works Integration
suggestions.desc: Develop an API middleware to connect the system to Degree Works, automatically checking if a requested substitution is valid for the student's specific catalog year and degree path.

suggestions.title: Blockchain Credential Ledger
suggestions.desc: Implement a private Hyperledger Fabric blockchain to store approved substitution forms, creating a permanent, immutable record that prevents tampering and ensures audit transparency.

suggestions.title: Historical Approval Predictor
suggestions.desc: Build a recommendation engine using past substitution data to predict the likelihood of an approval, helping advisors guide students toward substitutions that have a high historical success rate.

suggestions.title: One-Swipe Admin Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Create a React Native mobile application for Department Chairs that provides a streamlined 'tinder-style' interface for quickly reviewing, approving, or denying substitution requests with push notifications.

title: Curriculum Analysis
authors: Nedko Nedev, Danette Sweat, Maria Vassileva, Mitchell Vega
abstract: The Department of Instructional Technology at Kennesaw State University is conducting research, which requires analyzing curriculum standards from all over the world based on several criteria to determine a reliability score which can be used to assess one's own curriculum for improvements, or to compare with other curricula. To resolve this issue, we have created a web application with a SQL Server database that allows users to store curriculum information in a centralized location. The system allows multiple researchers to enter curriculum standards and evaluate them at the same time.

suggestions.title: Semantic Cross-Mapping with BERT
suggestions.desc: Implement an NLP pipeline using HuggingFace Transformers to automatically map learning objectives from different countries' curricula. By using semantic similarity, you can identify equivalent standards even when phrased differently across cultures.

suggestions.title: Labor Market Alignment Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Integrate the LinkedIn or Indeed API to compare current curriculum standards with real-world job requirements. The result would be a gap analysis tool that tells educators which industry-demanded skills are missing from their current syllabus.

suggestions.title: Dynamic Curriculum Versioning Visualizer
suggestions.desc: Build a D3.js visualization engine that tracks the evolution of a single country's standards over time. This would allow researchers to see how topics like 'Digital Literacy' have moved from high school to elementary school over the last decade.

suggestions.title: Automated Accreditation Compliance Auditor
suggestions.desc: Develop a module that checks a school's specific curriculum against international standards (like ABET or IB). Use the existing reliability scoring to generate a 'readiness report' for schools undergoing accreditation reviews.

title: DLC training system
authors: Daniel, Timothy Scott , Griffin, Andrew John , Green, Caleb Reid, Kramer,
abstract: Kristien T , Huff, Timothy David The Distance Learning Center provides faculty training programs for online faculty at KSU. One of the programs targets new hires. Often times, faculty members, especially part-time faculty members are hired right before the semester starts. While they are going through the HR process, they will need to access the training program to get familiarized with the learning environment and be ready to teach when the HR process is done. However, they will not be assigned with a KSU NetID and have access to various campus resources until the HR process is complete. Currently, the DLC uses D2L Brightspace to deliver its training programs. Due to limited access, the D2L

suggestions.title: H5P Interactive Content Integration
suggestions.desc: Extend the Moodle system by developing a suite of custom H5P interactive video modules specifically for faculty training. This transforms static policy reading into branch-path decision-making exercises with real-time feedback.

suggestions.title: Faculty Competency Micro-Credentialing
suggestions.desc: Implement the Open Badges API within Moodle to create a stackable credentialing system. Faculty who complete specific DLC training modules could earn digital badges that sync directly to their LinkedIn or university portfolios.

suggestions.title: LMS Engagement Analytics Engine
suggestions.desc: Build a Python-based data dashboard that pulls logs from the Moodle API to analyze faculty drop-off points. Use this data to provide DLC administrators with a 'Heat Map' of which training modules are too difficult or disengaging.

suggestions.title: AI-Powered Course Migration Assistant
suggestions.desc: Create a tool using OpenAI's API that helps faculty translate their existing D2L content into Moodle-friendly formats. It could automatically suggest the best Moodle activity types based on the structure of the uploaded D2L documents.

title: Domo Arigato, Mr. POSTbotto
authors: Thompson Wiley, Noor Masood, Ethan Mitchell, Alvaro Ruiz, John
abstract: Tyburski DAMP is a reddit bot designed to performmoderator tasks and automatically respond to posts and comments based on user specified keywords or phrases.

suggestions.title: Sentiment-Aware Auto-Moderation
suggestions.desc: Integrate the VADER or TextBlob library to move beyond simple keyword matching. The bot could detect the emotional tone of a post, allowing it to escalate aggressive comments to human mods while auto-responding kindly to frustrated new users.

suggestions.title: Visual Moderation Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Build a React-based frontend for DAMP that allows non-technical subreddit moderators to manage bot rules via a GUI. Include a 'Dry Run' feature that shows what actions the bot would have taken on the last 100 posts before going live.

suggestions.title: Anti-Scam Image Recognition Filter
suggestions.desc: Use OpenCV or AWS Rekognition to detect common spam patterns in images posted to Reddit, such as T-shirt scams or fake crypto giveaway QR codes. The bot can then flag these for removal faster than text-based filters.

suggestions.title: Community-Driven Wiki Summarizer
suggestions.desc: Modify the bot to detect frequently asked questions and automatically reply with a summarized snippet from the subreddit's Wiki. Use an LLM to condense long Wiki pages into concise, context-aware 3-sentence answers.

title: FrigID
authors: Daniel Heimann, Dylan Vollrath
abstract: FrigID is an ingredient tracking and management tool that can be used to make informed decisions. FrigID will use your current inventory and ingredients that are no longer available to create shopping lists. Many people live busy lives and this can make it difficult for shoppers to keep track of which ingredients they currently have or what they need for the meal for which they are shopping. FrigID's implementation aims to solve both of these problems with one solution. By using an Android application with NFC enabled and a Raspberry Pi, we will be able to allow users to keep track of all of the ingredients in their refrigerator or pantry. FrigID will also be able to create personalized shop

suggestions.title: Dynamic Smart Shopping List with Instacart API
suggestions.desc: Link the inventory data to the Instacart or Kroger API to automatically populate a shopping cart when items hit a 'low' threshold. Users could approve a weekly delivery with a single tap based on what the Raspberry Pi detects is missing.

suggestions.title: Computer Vision Fallback Layer
suggestions.desc: Since not all food has NFC tags, add a Raspberry Pi Camera Module with a YOLOv8 object detection model. The system can then 'see' produce or non-tagged items placed in the fridge, providing a hybrid tracking solution.

suggestions.title: Waste-Reduction Recipe Recommender
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Spoonacular API to suggest recipes specifically based on ingredients nearing their expiration date. The app could send a push notification: 'Your milk and spinach expire in 2 days; here is a recipe for Spinach Quiche.'

suggestions.title: Multi-Fridge Distributed Cloud Sync
suggestions.desc: Refactor the backend using AWS IoT Core to support households with multiple cooling units (e.g., a garage freezer and a kitchen fridge). This creates a unified household inventory view accessible by all family members' Android devices.

title: You Pick!
authors: Enrique Penaloza
abstract: While there are other apps that focus on what and where a person should go to eat, those apps still leave the user with the frustration of trying to make a decision that they eventually decide on the closest restaurant to avoid wasting more time. "You Pick" benefits the user by helping him or her speed up the decision-making process and shortening the "Pou pick! No, you pick" conversation.

suggestions.title: WebSocket Group Decision Lobby
suggestions.desc: Implement a 'Tinder-style' swiping lobby for groups using Socket.io. Friends join a room, everyone swipes on restaurants from the Yelp API, and the app identifies the first mutual match to eliminate the 'I don't care, you pick' loop.

suggestions.title: Dietary Constraint Smart Filter
suggestions.desc: Add a profile-based filtering system that uses OCR to scan physical menus or pull data from the Google Places API. The app would automatically hide or flag restaurants that don't accommodate specific allergens or diets (e.g., Celiac-friendly or Vegan).

suggestions.title: Collaborative Filtering Recommendation Engine
suggestions.desc: Move beyond random selection by building a recommendation system using Surprise or Scikit-learn. The app could analyze a user's past choices and ratings to predict their 'craving' before they even open the app.

suggestions.title: One-Tap Transport Integration
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Uber and Lyft SDKs to show estimated wait times and prices for the chosen restaurant directly on the result screen. Users could confirm their choice and book their ride in a single consolidated workflow.

title: Heat Beep
authors: In Cho, Michael Clapper, Payne Patzke, James Pyle, Drake Rocker, Josh
abstract: Saxton Since 1998, 690 children have died of heatstroke as a result of being left in a hot car, and many more children were injured. These deaths and injuries are completely preventable. There are many situations that may lead to a child being stuck in a hot car, and we are looking to combat those various situations. In order to accomplish this, we have designed a prototype of an inexpensively produced device in hopes of preventing future tragedies from occurring.

suggestions.title: OBD-II Smart Vehicle Integration
suggestions.desc: Connect the prototype to a vehicle's OBD-II port using an ELM327 adapter. In a critical heat event, the system could command the car to roll down windows slightly or activate the horn and hazard lights to attract passersby.

suggestions.title: Multi-Stage Twilio Alert System
suggestions.desc: Develop a cloud-based escalation ladder using the Twilio API. If the 'Beep' is ignored for 60 seconds, it texts the parent; after 120 seconds, it calls emergency contacts; after 180 seconds, it pings local emergency services with GPS coordinates.

suggestions.title: Long-Range LoRaWAN Emergency Mesh
suggestions.desc: Replace or supplement Bluetooth with LoRaWAN technology to ensure the device can send alerts even in deep parking garages with no cellular signal. This would allow a central 'hub' in a mall or parking lot to pick up distress signals from cars.

suggestions.title: Public Transit & School Bus Safety Suite
suggestions.desc: Adapt the sensor array for larger vehicles like school buses. Use a BLE mesh network of sensors under every seat that reports to a tablet in the driver's cockpit, ensuring no child is left behind at the end of a route.

title: K.R.A.B. (Keyword-Response Auto-Bot)
authors: Musab Ahmed, Misti Watkins, Niles O'Keeffe, Jordan Woodman, Shravani
abstract: Yelakanti A system that automatically detects and responds to Reddit comments based on phrases defined and configured by the client.

suggestions.title: Transformer-Based Intent Classification
suggestions.desc: Upgrade the detection logic from keyword matching to intent classification using a BERT model. This allows K.R.A.B. to distinguish between a user asking for help and a user complaining, ensuring the auto-responses are contextually appropriate.

suggestions.title: Cross-Platform Bot Controller
suggestions.desc: Refactor the core logic into a platform-agnostic library and add adapters for Discord (discord.py) and Twitter (Tweepy). A single client dashboard could manage a brand's automated presence across three different social networks simultaneously.

suggestions.title: Public Sentiment Trend Tracker
suggestions.desc: Add a data persistence layer using MongoDB to store every detected phrase. Build a dashboard that visualizes the frequency of these phrases over time, providing clients with an 'Early Warning System' for rising topics or controversies.

suggestions.title: User Reputation & Trust Scoring
suggestions.desc: Implement a system that tracks the 'reputation' of users who trigger the bot. If a high-karma or 'trusted' user triggers a phrase, the bot could provide a more detailed response or forward the thread directly to a human manager.

title: KSU Course Substitution Web System
authors: Yeojin Kim, Jean-Jeacques Mutekeke, Nidhi Shah, Shiva Voruganti, Junan
abstract: Zhao The KSU Course Substitution Web System (CSWS) is a tool that streamlines the process of course substitution requests. The main goal of CSWS is to reduce the amount of paperwork required to approve a course substitution and generate output electronically. Our solution resolves the issues of the current system (i.e., performance, time, and flexibility) in an efficient way using web technologies incorporated with OWL Express.

suggestions.title: Automated Equivalency Suggestion Engine
suggestions.desc: Build a web scraper to ingest course catalogs from other major universities. When a student requests a substitution, the system can use string-matching algorithms to suggest the most likely equivalent KSU course, speeding up the registrar's review.

suggestions.title: Digital Approval Blockchain Audit
suggestions.desc: Use a lightweight private blockchain (like Hyperledger Fabric) to create an immutable log of every approval and signature in the substitution process. This prevents 'lost' paperwork and provides a transparent audit trail for academic advisors.

suggestions.title: Substitution Impact Simulator
suggestions.desc: Create a 'What-If' tool for students that shows how a proposed course substitution would affect their projected graduation date and GPA. It would pull data from the user's DegreeWorks profile via a secure API.

suggestions.title: OCR Transcript Processing Module
suggestions.desc: Integrate Tesseract OCR to allow students to upload PDF transcripts from other institutions. The system would automatically extract course codes and titles, pre-filling the substitution form and reducing manual entry errors.

title: KSU File Transfer System
authors: Quyunh Dao Do, Josh Darby, Barry Oliver, Olutoni Oluyide
abstract: In today's day and age it can be difficult for professors to keep their work secure from students, while being able to share them with other professors in their department. Not only that, but it can be difficult for some of them to get accustomed to new systems, even to the extent that it isn't unheard of for professors to refuse making a Google account in order to access Google drive. While there are many file sharing software applications, there isn't utilized by Kennesaw State University for the purpose of sharing course materials. We plan to change this by introducing a system that can share documents from the department head to all the employees of the department, can allow professors t

suggestions.title: Zero-Knowledge Encryption Implementation
suggestions.desc: Upgrade the security layer so that files are encrypted on the client side before being sent to the server. By using the Web Crypto API, you ensure that even university system admins cannot read the contents of the files without the professor's key.

suggestions.title: Differential Binary Versioning
suggestions.desc: Implement a delta-encoding algorithm (like rsync) for the version control system. Instead of saving full copies of large PowerPoint or PDF files for every small change, the system only stores the 'diff,' drastically reducing storage costs.

suggestions.title: Automated Malware Sandboxing
suggestions.desc: Integrate the VirusTotal API or a local ClamAV instance to automatically scan every uploaded file. If a file is flagged as malicious, it is quarantined immediately, protecting the university network from infected student submissions.

suggestions.title: Canvas/LMS Direct Publish Sync
suggestions.desc: Create a 'Publish to Course' feature that allows professors to sync a specific version of a file directly to their Canvas course modules. This bridges the gap between private collaboration and student-facing distribution.

title: Live Interactive Technology (L.I.T.)
authors: Stephen Ajetomobi, David Guidry, Farid Khan, Daniel Louis, Rachael
abstract: Okogie Over the course of a college student's academic career, there are a myriad of extracurricular activities and events available for them to attend. These activities vary in nature and range from small-scale events such as a bible study attendance to large-scale events such as a college music festival. There is, however, an issue with choosing an event to attend if a student has multiple or no interests. Students that have no set event to attend become curious about how fun a particular event might be and this causes them to resort to the host's text description of the event, which provides little help. Other students are simply indecisive, and this indecisiveness varies depending on the

suggestions.title: Low-Latency HLS Optimization
suggestions.desc: Refactor the streaming backend using Amazon IVS (Interactive Video Service) to reduce the 'pseudo real-time' delay to sub-second levels. This enables truly synchronous interactions between the live event and the remote audience.

suggestions.title: Interactive AR Event Overlays
suggestions.desc: Use ARCore to allow viewers to see digital overlays on the live feed. For example, during a campus career fair, students could tap on a booth in the video to see a list of open positions or the current wait time for a recruiter.

suggestions.title: Multi-Angle Crowd-Sourced Switching
suggestions.desc: Implement a 'Director Mode' where the app can accept streams from multiple student phones at the same event. Viewers could then toggle between different angles of a football game or a concert, similar to professional broadcast setups.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Clip Generation and Social Sharing
suggestions.desc: Add a feature that uses FFmpeg on the backend to allow users to 'clip' the last 30 seconds of a live feed. These clips can then be instantly shared to Instagram or TikTok to drive more immediate traffic to the campus event.

title: Melody Bank
authors: Marc Ducharme, Justin Fortin, Lawson Welborn
abstract: Melody Bank is an independent, downloadable application for Windows OS, which allows users to collaboratively identify and document recurring musical patterns found in songs. Our application provides an easy and fun way to track down frequently used patterns across unrelated pieces of music as well as to document known leitmotif (recurring musical themes intentionally used by a composer over the course of a sound track or album). As any user is able to report information (e.g., the song name, author, pattern time, and pattern relationship), our application can be considered a sort of Wikipedia for the niche purpose of recording these relationships.

suggestions.title: Automated Motif Detection with MIDI Analysis
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Python Mido library or Librosa to automatically scan uploaded MIDI files for recurring melodic sequences, replacing manual identification with algorithmic pattern matching.

suggestions.title: Digital Audio Workstation (DAW) Plugin Bridge
suggestions.desc: Develop a VST or AU plugin version of Melody Bank that allows music producers to drag-and-drop identified patterns directly from the database into software like Ableton Live or FL Studio.

suggestions.title: Ethnomusicology Dataset Expansion
suggestions.desc: Pivot the application to focus on global folk music, using the collaborative platform to map how specific melodic patterns migrated across different cultures and eras using geospatial visualization.

suggestions.title: Real-time Collaborative Jam Session Sync
suggestions.desc: Implement WebSockets to allow multiple users in the Windows app to edit the same melody 'sheet' simultaneously, incorporating version control for musical phrases.

title: MongoDB Security
authors: Patel, Anish , Spradlin, Jeffrey Allen , Garcia, Mytee , Davis, Jared S ,
abstract: Goulart, Anne Elizabeth New data management systems beyond SQL (NoSQL, or Not Only SQL) have been developed to handle semi-structure and unstructured data. They also post new security challenges that may be different from RDBMS. In this project, a team of students will investigate the security issues of MongoDB, and implement the security settings on an existing MongoDB database.

suggestions.title: Automated NoSQL Injection Vulnerability Scanner
suggestions.desc: Build a Python-based tool that specifically probes MongoDB instances for common NoSQL injection patterns and misconfigured Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) settings.

suggestions.title: MongoDB Ransomware Early Warning System
suggestions.desc: Develop a monitoring agent that detects mass-deletion or mass-encryption patterns within MongoDB collections, triggering an automatic firewall block to prevent data exfiltration.

suggestions.title: Performance Impact Analysis of TLS Encryption
suggestions.desc: Conduct a formal research study measuring the latency overhead of various MongoDB security layers (TLS/SSL, At-Rest Encryption, Scram-SHA-256) under high-concurrency workloads.

suggestions.title: HIPAA-Compliance Dashboard for NoSQL
suggestions.desc: Create a dashboard that audits MongoDB configurations against HIPAA or GDPR requirements, generating automated remediation scripts for non-compliant database clusters.

similar: legacy:GR-12|Spring 2016|nosql injection security analysis and defense
legacy:GR-12|Fall 2016|nosql injection security analysis and defense solution

title: Network Traffic Pattern Analysis Based on Armitage Exploits
authors: Dan Bellamy, Ryan Coughlan, Scott Lewis, Steven Mints, Sara Rico-
abstract: Larmer We set up a virtual machine with two different operating systems, such as Windows XP and Windows 7, and attacked the two systems using Metasploitable and Kali Linux. Additionally, we capture and log the files during the attacks using Wireshark and other log file processing tools.

suggestions.title: Signature-Based Intrusion Detection for Armitage
suggestions.desc: Use the Wireshark captures to extract unique packet signatures for Armitage-specific exploits and write custom Snort or Suricata rules to detect these attacks in real-time.

suggestions.title: Modern OS Exploitation Comparison
suggestions.desc: Extend the research by replicating the attacks against hardened Windows 11 and Linux environments to analyze how modern mitigations like ASLR and DEP change the network traffic footprint.

suggestions.title: Machine Learning Traffic Classifier
suggestions.desc: Train a Random Forest or CNN model using the PCAP data to distinguish between legitimate administrative traffic and active penetration testing maneuvers.

suggestions.title: Interactive Attack-Defense Training Lab
suggestions.desc: Convert the virtual machine setup into a Dockerized 'Capture The Flag' environment where students must secure the Windows XP/7 instances while others use the analyzed Armitage patterns to attack.

similar: legacy:CP-19|Spring 2016|network traffic pattern analysis based on armitage exploits
legacy:GR-023|Spring 2021|machine learning techniques for malware network traffic detection
