title: Owlendar
authors: Zachery Cox, Kyle McLendon, Farah Obaidullah, Mohini Patel
abstract: Currently, the way Student Engagement events are presented make it difficult for students and faculty to effectively attend and/or volunteer at the many events presented from the Department of Student Engagement at Kennesaw State University. To better organize the myriad of events, something similar to a Google Calendar app specifically tailored to Student Engagement events was created: Owlendar. All of the events can be seen in a way similar to a physical calendar that you would have on your wall. This calendar is accessible via an application for mobile devices. Using this application will allow students and faculty to see the current and future events, register for volunteer opportunities

suggestions.title: Gamified Volunteer Reward System
suggestions.desc: Integrate a 'point' system where students earn badges and digital currency for attending events, redeemable at campus facilities, using Firebase for real-time leaderboards.

suggestions.title: Indoor Wayfinding via Beacon Integration
suggestions.desc: Use Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) beacons within the KSU Student Center to provide turn-by-turn navigation within the app to help users find specific event rooms.

suggestions.title: Intelligent RSVP and Capacity Forecaster
suggestions.desc: Apply a simple regression model to historical event attendance data to predict food and seating requirements for future faculty-led events.

suggestions.title: Inter-Campus Collaborative Event API
suggestions.desc: Develop a RESTful API that allows other KSU departmental apps to pull 'Owlendar' data, creating a centralized event ecosystem across all university platforms.

title: ProfShare
authors: Chris Deakin, Charles Driver
abstract: Currently, the professors at Kennesaw State University do not have a centralized location where they can share and collaborate on course work. Our project is a secure system that allows professors to share their course materials with one another. This system will eliminate the need for professors to email their course materials or use portable storage in order to share them with other professors. Instead, this system will give professors a centralized location to keep any lecture materials that on which they wish to collaborate. Also, the system will provide version control to the documents, which will make it easier for course materials to be improved upon. Other features our system will of

suggestions.title: Semantic Search with Natural Language Processing
suggestions.desc: Implement an NLP-based search engine using Lucene or ElasticSearch that allows professors to find materials based on conceptual content rather than just keywords or filenames.

suggestions.title: LMS Integration Middleware
suggestions.desc: Build an LTI (Learning Tools Interoperability) connector that allows ProfShare materials to be imported directly into D2L Brightspace or Canvas with a single click.

suggestions.title: Blockchain-Backed Intellectual Property Ledger
suggestions.desc: Integrate a lightweight blockchain (like Hyperledger Fabric) to provide an immutable log of document creation and modifications to protect faculty ownership of original materials.

suggestions.title: Automated Syllabus Alignment Auditor
suggestions.desc: Create a tool that compares shared course materials against university-standard learning objectives to ensure departmental consistency across different professors.

title: Project Tallulah
authors: Dan Aviles, Clinton Flowers, Justin Jennings, Jun Nguyen
abstract: Project Tallulah engineered and refined a prototype of a combined communications platform, leveraging the capabilities afforded on technologies like low-cost Android tables in order to provide dedicated information dashboards and software-defined home automation in every room. The project involves mostly browser-based software configured to continuously run on various off-the-shelf hardware devices, with additional platform- specific code for room automation as necessary.

suggestions.title: Edge-Based Voice Control for Privacy
suggestions.desc: Integrate Rhasspy or Mycroft AI to enable offline voice commands for the Android tablets, ensuring that home automation data never leaves the local network.

suggestions.title: Universal IoT Bridge via MQTT
suggestions.desc: Expand the communications platform to support the MQTT protocol, allowing the Android dashboard to control non-Android devices like Philips Hue lights or Nest thermostats.

suggestions.title: Elderly Care Monitoring Mode
suggestions.desc: Add a specific dashboard profile that uses the tablet's sensors and camera to detect falls or unusual periods of inactivity, sending alerts to designated family members.

suggestions.title: Predictive Energy Management Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Use the dashboard to collect data from smart plugs and apply a time-series analysis to suggest peak-hour power saving strategies for the household.

title: Project Yodel
authors: Benhamin Cubitt, Steven Jolly, Jonathan Reagan
abstract: Major VOIP programs have sat on either side of a fence: they either over allocate network resources, or is not an ad-hoc network. Our goal was to create a VOIP program that can provide a better service that is both an ad-hoc network and does not over allocate network resources. We utilize users' own machines to store their profiles and settings, the rooms are hosted by an individual user with host swapping when necessary, and it is a lightweight program that does not hinder processing. In order to provide a clear signal at all times, we have the program dynamically swap hosts to the host with the best connection. To minimize the effect of packet loss during switching, switching will only occ

suggestions.title: Signal Protocol End-to-End Encryption
suggestions.desc: Implement the Double Ratchet Algorithm to ensure that all VOIP communications on the ad-hoc network are cryptographically secure and provide forward secrecy.

suggestions.title: Multi-Hop Mesh Networking Expansion
suggestions.desc: Modify the routing logic to allow packets to hop across multiple intermediate nodes, extending the VOIP range beyond the immediate signal of the host.

suggestions.title: Disaster Recovery Emergency Gateway
suggestions.desc: Adapt Yodel for use in disaster zones by creating a 'gateway' node that can bridge the ad-hoc network to a satellite or cellular link when local infrastructure is down.

suggestions.title: WebRTC Web-Client Interface
suggestions.desc: Develop a browser-based client using WebRTC that allows users to join the Yodel ad-hoc network without installing dedicated software.

title: Skype Bot application
authors: Ousmane Kore, Fekadu Abebe, Dal Rai, Anil Thakuri
abstract: The Skype Bot application is built to automatically respond to a user inquiry. The Skype bot application shall be able to detect user comments and to respond back with the appropriate answer. Moreover it shall allow the administrator to configure the responses.

suggestions.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) Integration
suggestions.desc: Connect the bot to a vector database (like Pinecone) containing KSU-specific documentation to allow the bot to answer complex, context-aware student questions.

suggestions.title: Sentiment-Triggered Admin Escalation
suggestions.desc: Implement a sentiment analysis module that detects frustrated or angry users and automatically pings a human administrator to take over the Skype conversation.

suggestions.title: Visual Logic Flow Builder
suggestions.desc: Build a web-based drag-and-drop interface for administrators to design complex conversational trees and bot responses without writing code.

suggestions.title: Multi-Platform Bot Adapter
suggestions.desc: Abstract the bot's core logic into a middleware layer so the same configurable response system can simultaneously support Discord, Microsoft Teams, and Slack.

title: SSSAJ
authors: Alyssa Cohen, Stanislav Kolev, James Patterson, Sharmell Smith, Steven
abstract: St. Amand Over the past years, technology users have constantly shared files and important data through the Internet with almost no acknowledgement of security of their information. This leaves their materials open to those who might use it incorrectly. With this in mind, team SSSAJ has developed an encrypted file sharing system. Our goal for this project was to closely look into methods for encryption along with analyzing algorithms that can help performencryption and decryption in a reasonable set time.

suggestions.title: Zero-Knowledge Proof Architecture
suggestions.desc: Redesign the backend so that the server never has access to the encryption keys or the unencrypted files, ensuring total privacy even if the server is compromised.

suggestions.title: Steganographic Data Hiding
suggestions.desc: Implement a feature that allows users to hide small, sensitive files inside larger, innocuous image or video files before sharing them over the network.

suggestions.title: Ephemeral File Sharing with Self-Destruct
suggestions.desc: Add a time-based expiration feature and a 'read-once' mode where files are automatically wiped from the server after a specific number of downloads or minutes.

suggestions.title: Secure Multi-Signature File Approval
suggestions.desc: Create a workflow where a shared file remains encrypted and inaccessible until a predefined number of authorized users provide their individual keys.

title: TAG Survey Report
authors: Perrin, Jonathan Luke , Scent, Bailey Aaron , Chaney, Matthew Ryan ,
abstract: Elder, Jacob Edward The Technology Association of Georgia is seeking proposals from qualified firms and/or Universities that are interested in preparing research data to be used to provide insight and trending information on Georgia's technology industry. The results should be accessible by TAG through some form of digital dashboard (example: researchers) is preparing a proposal and need to investigate and recommend a data visualization and dashboard solution to TAG. The team is charged with research and implementation sample solutions and recommend one.

suggestions.title: Predictive Industry Growth Modeling
suggestions.desc: Apply Prophet or ARIMA forecasting models to the TAG survey data to visualize predicted tech job growth and salary trends in Georgia for the next five years.

suggestions.title: Interactive Geospatial Tech Hub Map
suggestions.desc: Use Leaflet.js or Mapbox to create a geographic visualization of the survey data, mapping tech company density and investment across different Georgia counties.

suggestions.title: Automated Executive Summary Generator
suggestions.desc: Integrate an LLM API to scan the dashboard's data and automatically generate a written report highlighting key takeaways and anomalies for TAG stakeholders.

suggestions.title: Real-time Industry Benchmarking Tool
suggestions.desc: Build a portal where local companies can input their own metrics to see how they rank against the TAG industry averages in real-time.

title: Using Deep Learning to Monitor Parking Spaces
authors: Jeswin Abraham, Alex Bates, Phillip Bouie, Naga Gattupalli
abstract: Many current systems for monitoring parking space usage is very hardware heavy and often not worth the effort of setting up for smaller areas. Instead of tracking parking spaces with hardware in or around each lot, a camera can be used to track multiple spaces at once. This would allow for simpler setup and will assist in pointing out parking issues such as a full lot or long-termover/under use. To accomplish this, we used a deep learning framework to recognize the difference between a parked car and an empty parking space.

suggestions.title: Edge Deployment with NVIDIA Jetson and TensorRT
suggestions.desc: Port the existing deep learning model to an NVIDIA Jetson Nano or Xavier NX using TensorRT for high-speed local inference. This eliminates the need for cloud servers and reduces latency for real-time parking enforcement notifications.

suggestions.title: Predictive Smart City Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Integrate a Time-Series Forecasting model (like Prophet or LSTM) using the collected parking data to predict future availability. Build a React-based dashboard that helps city planners visualize peak usage hours and optimize parking rates.

suggestions.title: Automated LPR and Billing Integration
suggestions.desc: Incorporate License Plate Recognition (LPR) using OpenALPR alongside the occupancy detection. Create a backend system that triggers a billing event in Stripe when a vehicle occupies a space without a digital permit.

suggestions.title: Adverse Weather Robustness via CycleGAN
suggestions.desc: Train the model on synthetic datasets generated by CycleGAN to simulate heavy rain, snow, or night-time conditions. This ensures the computer vision system remains accurate in parking lots with poor lighting or extreme weather.

similar: legacy:CP-27|Spring 2016|using deep learning to monitor parking spaces
project_key: legacy:CP-27|Fall 2016|using deep learning to monitor parking spaces

title: VRoooom!
authors: Amber Cunningham, Shannon Dye, Nick Fenton, Justen McLean,
abstract: Charlotte Nadaud "VRoooom!" is a virtual reality kart racing game. It is designed to be played using the HTC Vive. Some of the game's features include four karts to choose from, each having different attributes and at least four computer controlled karts for the player to race against. There will be at least one playable environment (racetrack), and we will also incorporate at least five "powerups," which the player will be able to use against his/her opponents. "VRoooom!" was developed using the Unity Engine along with the Steam VR SDK for Unity.

suggestions.title: Networked Multiplayer via Photon Fusion
suggestions.desc: Implement a client-server architecture using the Photon Fusion SDK to allow up to 8 players to race against each other in VR. Focus on lag compensation and synchronization of physics-based kart movements.

suggestions.title: Haptic Simulation Rig Integration
suggestions.desc: Build an Arduino-controlled haptic feedback system that interfaces with the Unity game via Telemetry data. Provide physical feedback like engine vibrations and impact jolts to a sim-racing chair or wearable haptic vest.

suggestions.title: Procedural Track Generation and AI Navigation
suggestions.desc: Develop a system that generates unique race tracks procedurally using Perlin noise and spline-based paths. Update the AI opponents to use NavMesh modifiers to adapt to these dynamic environments in real-time.

suggestions.title: Mixed Reality Spectator Mode
suggestions.desc: Create a secondary mobile application using ARCore that allows non-VR users to view the race track as a 3D hologram on their coffee table, seeing live positions of the VR players.

title: GTRI ticket system
authors: Anderson, Philip Caleb , Brown, Ryan Daniel , McDonald, Logan M ,
abstract: Weinrich, Paul J The Georgia Tech Research Institute Electronic Systems Laboratory (GTRI-ELSYS) is an applied research organization that provides technical solutions to a variety of sponsors and supports about 450 researchers and staff. The computer support department develops, maintains, and supports the Information Technology (IT) systems used within GTRI-ELSYS, including servers, workstations, printers, networks, and storage devices. This department is largely responsible for ensuring researchers have the tools necessary to conduct their work. The computer support department currently uses Kayako's eSupport, version 3.70, a web application for submitting trouble tickets. The software uses

suggestions.title: NLP-Driven Ticket Auto-Categorization
suggestions.desc: Implement a BERT-based text classification model to automatically tag and route incoming tickets to the correct department (IT, HR, or Purchasing). This reduces the manual triage workload for system administrators.

suggestions.title: Purchasing Workflow with Blockchain Auditing
suggestions.desc: Extend the purchasing request module to log transaction approvals on a private Hyperledger Fabric blockchain. This creates an immutable audit trail for expensive hardware acquisitions within GTRI-ELSYS.

suggestions.title: Mobile Field-Tech App with QR Asset Mapping
suggestions.desc: Build a Flutter mobile app that allows technicians to scan QR codes on hardware to instantly pull up its ticket history and current maintenance status using a RESTful API connection to the main database.

suggestions.title: AI Knowledge Base Assistant (RAG)
suggestions.desc: Develop a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system using LangChain that scans closed tickets to answer common user queries automatically via a chat interface before they ever submit a new ticket.

title: Convolutional Neural Networks for Image Classification With the Nao Robot
authors: Virinchi Puligundla
abstract: The objective of this work is to design a convolutional neural network that will provide the Nao robot with image classification capabilities.

suggestions.title: Object-Guided Navigation and SLAM
suggestions.desc: Combine the CNN image classification with Simultaneous Localization and Mapping (SLAM). Enable the Nao robot to find and navigate toward specific classified objects (e.g., 'find the blue ball') in an indoor environment.

suggestions.title: Model Compression for On-Board Inference
suggestions.desc: Use Quantization-Aware Training (QAT) and Knowledge Distillation to shrink the CNN so it can run natively on the Nao’s internal processor without needing an external PC to process the vision stream.

suggestions.title: Multi-Modal Human Interaction
suggestions.desc: Integrate the CNN with Nao’s speech recognition. Create a 'teaching' mode where a user shows the robot an unknown object, names it, and the system performs few-shot learning to update its classification layer on the fly.

suggestions.title: Gesture-Based Control Interface
suggestions.desc: Expand the CNN to recognize human hand gestures. This would allow a user to lead the Nao robot or command it to perform tasks (like sitting or waving) through visual cues rather than voice or code.

title: Picturade
authors: Armand Marino

suggestions.title: Generative AI Prompt-to-Image Mechanics
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Stable Diffusion API to create a game where players must guess the AI-generated prompt based on the image provided, or compete to generate an image that most closely matches a hidden keyword.

suggestions.title: AR Scavenger Hunt with Computer Vision
suggestions.desc: Transform the project into an Augmented Reality game using AR Foundation. Players must find and photograph real-world objects that match 'quest' images, verified by an on-device MobileNet classifier.

suggestions.title: Social Collaborative Mural System
suggestions.desc: Build a multiplayer backend using Firebase where users contribute 'pixels' or photo-fragments to a massive global image. Implement a voting and moderation system to curate the final community-driven artwork.

suggestions.title: Semantic Image Similarity Scoring
suggestions.desc: Use OpenAI's CLIP model to calculate the semantic distance between images. Create a gameplay loop where players must find two images that are visually different but conceptually similar (e.g., a 'fire' and a 'chili pepper').

title: User Influence, Peak Activity Intervals, and Longevity as Characteristics of Twitter Hashtags
authors: Nishant Sethunath
abstract: This project studies user influence, activity over time, and longevity of Twitter trends as characteristics of Twitter hashtags. The aim is to see if one or more of these variables is associated with Twitter hashtags.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Trend Dashboard with Apache Kafka
suggestions.desc: Build a data pipeline using Kafka and Spark Streaming to visualize hashtag longevity in real-time. Create a dashboard that alerts users when a hashtag's 'velocity' indicates it is about to go viral.

suggestions.title: AstroTurfing and Bot Detection via Graph Theory
suggestions.desc: Use NetworkX to analyze the follower/following graphs of users driving specific hashtags. Identify 'coordinated inauthentic behavior' by detecting tightly clustered nodes that amplify trends artificially.

suggestions.title: Cross-Platform Sentiment Correlation
suggestions.desc: Compare Twitter hashtag data with Reddit or TikTok trends. Use Sentiment Analysis (VADER) to see if the public perception of a topic differs significantly across platforms during the same time window.

suggestions.title: Viral Potential Prediction Model
suggestions.desc: Train a Transformer-based model to predict the lifespan and peak volume of a hashtag based on the initial 'influencer' nodes that use it. This tool could be used by marketers to time their social media campaigns.

title: Defensive Programming
authors: Joel Kamdem Teto
abstract: This study creates learning materials that allow students to avoid common security defects, to analyze the relationship between defensive programming and confidentiality, integrity and availability, and to write a secure program. Topics include defensive programming, Secure input validation and output handling, buffer overflow attack and prevention, vulnerabilities in mobile programming, access control and confidential information, mobile malware, restriction on access to components, and isolation of file system and database, injection and inclusion, accessibility and extensibility, mutability, and serialization and deserialization. Another focus in this study is to teach students to create…

suggestions.title: Deep Reinforcement Learning for Real-Time Control
suggestions.desc: Replace the predictive simulation with a Deep Q-Network (DQN) that controls HVAC setpoints in real-time. Use the 'OpenAI Gym' environment to simulate the West Point building and train the agent to balance occupant comfort (CO2 and Temp) against peak energy prices.

suggestions.title: Campus-Wide Microgrid Load Balancing
suggestions.desc: Extend the peak shaving model to include other energy-intensive campus assets like electric vehicle (EV) charging stations and dorm lighting. Create a coordinated strategy where HVAC load is decreased when EV demand spikes to keep the total campus draw below a critical threshold.

suggestions.title: Commercial Smart Thermostat API for Rebates
suggestions.desc: Develop a SaaS wrapper for the optimization model that connects to smart thermostats (like Ecobee or Nest) via API. Target small business owners by automating their participation in 'Demand Response' programs, earning them utility rebates during peak hours.

suggestions.title: Occupancy Sensing via Thermal Imaging Fusion
suggestions.desc: Research the impact of real-time occupancy data on the peak shaving model. Integrate low-cost PIR or thermal sensors to feed 'human heat load' data into the HVAC simulation, allowing for more aggressive energy savings in empty zones.

title: Hopper's Fables: A Mathematical Storytelling Adventure
authors: Deja Jackson, Erica Pantoja, Cindi Simmons, Kate Zelaya
abstract: Block Programming languages (e.g., Alice, Scratch) are used heavily in teaching students in K-12 classrooms and in some universities in hopes of bridging programming with beginner students. This is in an effort to encourage students' interest in computer programming utilizing Papert's "low floor" and "high ceiling" metaphor. While the current block languages are helpful and engaging, there are not many offering an emphasis on advancing students' reading and mathematical skills. This presentation describes our creation of a block language using Blocky's API, HTML, JavaScript, and CSS. The language, entitled Hopper's Fables (named after Admiral Grace Hopper), is based on storytelling through c

suggestions.title: Mobile Inference Optimization with TensorFlow Lite
suggestions.desc: Migrate the project from the legacy Caffe framework to TensorFlow Lite. Apply 8-bit quantization and pruning to the AlexNet model to reduce the app's battery consumption and increase the frames-per-second (FPS) on mid-range Android devices.

suggestions.title: Anti-Poaching Acoustic-Visual Sensor Fusion
suggestions.desc: Combine the R-CNN detection with an audio classification model that detects gunshots or chainsaw sounds. Build a system intended for static Raspberry Pi 'forest sentinels' that only wakes up the camera and runs the R-CNN when suspicious audio is detected.

suggestions.title: Individual Animal Re-Identification (Re-ID)
suggestions.desc: Research the use of Siamese Networks to not only detect the species but identify individual animals (e.g., specific tigers based on stripe patterns). Use the 'Wild-Me' datasets to train a secondary model that triggers when a known individual is spotted.

suggestions.title: AR Digital Safari Guide for Tourism
suggestions.desc: Integrate the detection model with ARCore to overlay educational 'info-cards' in Augmented Reality when an animal is detected. Target wildlife parks to provide an interactive educational experience for visitors using their own smartphones.

title: One Size Doesn't Fit All
authors: Zane Johnston
abstract: Graphical user interfaces (GUIs) allow users to performcomplex tasks through the manipulation of on screen graphical elements. However, the GUI's dependence on traditional inputs (e.g., mouse and keyboard) requiring a certain level of dexterity bars users with mobility impairments from using applications that make use of GUIs. One method for allowing users with mobility impairments to have access to more applications that utilize GUIs is to create a vocal user interface (VUI) mapped to the underlying GUI, allowing the user to performactions normally achieved through physical input by using voice. Unfortunately, the process of mapping a VUI to a GUI is not always straightforward. In the cas

suggestions.title: Static-Dynamic Hybrid Sandbox Analysis
suggestions.desc: Combine the current ML static analysis with a dynamic analysis phase. Use a headless Windows sandbox to execute the macro, monitor API calls for suspicious behavior (e.g., PowerShell execution), and use these behavioral logs as additional features for the ML model.

suggestions.title: Adversarial Robustness Testing against VBA Obfuscation
suggestions.desc: Build a tool that automatically generates obfuscated versions of known malware macros (using variable renaming, junk code, and string encryption). Test the current ML model against these variants to identify blind spots and retrain the model on adversarial examples.

suggestions.title: Outlook Plugin for Real-Time Attachment Scanning
suggestions.desc: Develop a VSTO Add-in for Microsoft Outlook that intercepts incoming .docm and .xlsm files. Send the macro code to a local ML inference engine and display a 'Risk Score' directly in the email header before the user clicks to open the file.

suggestions.title: Enterprise Macro Policy Audit Tool
suggestions.desc: Create a Python-based scanner for IT administrators that crawls company file shares and identifies legacy documents containing high-risk macros. Generate a security report that maps the detected threats to the MITRE ATT&CK framework for compliance auditing.

title: 3D User Interfaces for the Visually Impaired
authors: Darren O'Neale, Jerome Lester, Devan Patel
abstract: Initial research plan describing the use of software to enable visually impaired persons to become independent in dynamic, unknown environments. A 3D audio-driven UI demo will be showcased.

suggestions.title: LiDAR-to-Spatial-Audio Engine
suggestions.desc: Using the LiDAR sensor on modern iPhones, develop a system that translates point-cloud depth data into 3D HRTF (Head-Related Transfer Function) audio cues. Objects closer to the user produce higher frequency or louder localized clicks.

suggestions.title: Public Transit API Navigation Integration
suggestions.desc: Integrate the UI with GTFS (General Transit Feed Specification) data. Use spatial audio to 'pull' the user toward the correct bus stop or train platform, providing verbal 3D-audio cues for upcoming arrivals.

suggestions.title: Haptic-Audio Fusion Wearable
suggestions.desc: Create a companion wearable device (belt or vest) using ESP32 and vibrating motors. Synchronize the 3D audio cues with haptic pulses to provide a multi-sensory 'map' of the environment for improved directional confidence.

suggestions.title: Voice-First Wayfinding Assistant
suggestions.desc: Implement an LLM-based voice interface using OpenAI Whisper for speech-to-text. Users can ask, 'Where is the nearest exit?' and the system responds with spatialized audio beacons that lead them through the building.

title: An Internet-of-Things based System to Measure Student Physiological Responses That Indicate Stress, Boredom, and Attention
authors: Sarah Tippens
abstract: When a student comes to class, they sit for an hour and a half, sometimes more, to listen to a professor lecture to the class. With this model of teaching, it is hard to keep student attention and therefore a student may not learn the material as well. This project will focus on an Internet of Things system that will be used in a Smart Classroom environment to gauge a student's attentiveness, boredom, and stress in real-time. The system is a student desk that has built-in sensors to gather a student's physiological responses such as body language, eye-tracking, body temperature, etc. The goal is to create a way for teachers to easily determine how attentive their student's are in class wheth

suggestions.title: Differential Privacy for Student Bio-Data
suggestions.desc: Implement a Differential Privacy layer on the data ingestion pipeline to ensure that individual student stress levels cannot be deanonymized while still allowing the teacher to see aggregate classroom trends.

suggestions.title: Teacher Dashboard with Real-Time Heatmaps
suggestions.desc: Develop a React-based web dashboard that visualizes the classroom's 'engagement level' as a heatmap. Use WebSockets to provide the teacher with immediate feedback on which parts of a lecture are causing boredom or confusion.

suggestions.title: Smart Lighting and HVAC Feedback Loop
suggestions.desc: Connect the IoT sensors to a smart building controller (BACnet). Automatically adjust the classroom lighting color temperature or lower the room temperature if the sensors detect a significant spike in aggregate student sleepiness.

suggestions.title: Wearable SDK Integration (Apple/Fitbit)
suggestions.desc: Expand the sensor suite to include data from student smartwatches. Use the Heart Rate Variability (HRV) and SpO2 data from the HealthKit/Fitbit SDKs to calibrate the desk sensors and improve accuracy of the stress models.

title: An Internet-of-Things based System to share lecture notes via clouds
authors: Nidhi Patel
abstract: An IoT-integrated system to convert the handwritting on the traditional blackboard to electronic texts, and make them sharable among students

suggestions.title: Streaming Cluster Integration with Apache Flink
suggestions.desc: Evolve the static MapReduce clustering into a dynamic stream processing application using Flink to handle real-time data clusters that evolve over time.

suggestions.title: Density-Based Big Data Clustering (DBSCAN)
suggestions.desc: Implement the DBSCAN algorithm for the MapReduce framework to compare its ability to find non-spherical clusters against traditional K-Means on the Hadoop cluster.

suggestions.title: Global Social Media Topic Community Detection
suggestions.desc: Use the Hadoop clustering implementation to analyze terabytes of Twitter/X data, grouping users into 'topic communities' based on the co-occurrence of hashtags.

suggestions.title: B2B Customer Segmentation Service
suggestions.desc: Build a specialized service for e-commerce platforms that uses the MapReduce clustering to segment millions of customers into high-value personas based on purchase frequency and volume.

title: Authentication Using Iris Detection and Pupillary Response
authors: Mahbubul Islam
abstract: The objective of this work is to develop a biometric authentication system based on both iris detection and pupillary response (pupil light reflex).

suggestions.title: Near-Infrared (NIR) Liveness Detection
suggestions.desc: Incorporate a Raspberry Pi NoIR camera to detect the 'liveness' of the eye. This prevents spoofing attacks using high-resolution photos or video screens, as real human eyes reflect infrared light uniquely compared to screens.

suggestions.title: Decentralized Biometric ID with Blockchain
suggestions.desc: Integrate the iris recognition system with a Self-Sovereign Identity (SSI) framework. Use Zero-Knowledge Proofs to allow a user to authenticate their identity without ever transmitting their raw biometric data to a server.

suggestions.title: Emotional and Cognitive State Analysis
suggestions.desc: Research the use of pupillary response (task-evoked pupillary response) to detect user cognitive load or emotional arousal. This could be used for 'adaptive security' where higher authentication is required if the user appears under duress.

suggestions.title: Multi-Factor Iris + Face Fusion
suggestions.desc: Develop a dual-model authentication system that uses YOLOv8 for face detection and a specialized CNN for iris segmentation (like DeepIris). This improves reliability in various lighting conditions where iris-only detection might fail.

title: Automatic system for predicting severity of claims in an insurance company
authors: Lili Zhang
abstract: The project is to predict claims severity for the chance to be part of Allstate's efforts to ensure a worry-free customer experience.

suggestions.title: Explainable AI (XAI) for Claim Adjusters
suggestions.desc: Integrate SHAP or LIME into the prediction model to provide 'reasoning' for each severity score. This helps insurance adjusters understand why a specific claim was flagged as high-cost, increasing trust in the automated system.

suggestions.title: Multi-Modal Damage Assessment
suggestions.desc: Expand the machine learning model to include computer vision. Use a CNN to analyze uploaded photos of vehicle damage and fuse those features with the tabular data to produce a more accurate severity estimate.

suggestions.title: Fraud Detection using Graph Neural Networks
suggestions.desc: Implement a GNN to identify suspicious relationships between claimants, doctors, and repair shops. Cross-reference the severity predictions with historical fraud patterns to flag potential 'staged' accidents.

suggestions.title: Federated Learning for Cross-Carrier Training
suggestions.desc: Develop a Federated Learning framework that allows multiple insurance companies to train a shared severity model without sharing sensitive customer data, resulting in a more robust and generalized model.

suggestions.title: Adversarial Defense for Malware Vision
suggestions.desc: Implement 'adversarial training' in the TensorFlow model to detect malware that has been specifically obfuscated to bypass image-based LBP feature detection.

suggestions.title: Transfer Learning with ResNet-50
suggestions.desc: Replace the custom TensorFlow classifier with a ResNet-50 model pre-trained on ImageNet to see if general visual features improve the classification of malware LBP images.

suggestions.title: Live Malware-to-Image Scanning API
suggestions.desc: Develop a SaaS API that allows security teams to upload binaries, converts them to LBP images on-the-fly, and returns a 'threat signature' classification in milliseconds.

suggestions.title: Mobile Malware Detection for Android
suggestions.desc: Optimize the LBP-based detection model for mobile deployment (TensorFlow Lite) to scan Android APK files for malicious patterns directly on the device.

title: Feature Selection and Improving Classification Performance for Malware Detection
authors: Carlos Mora Cepeda
abstract: After analyzing the advance of technology, it is clear that use of the Internet, computers, smart phones and tablets has become ubiquitous and therefore, the creation and proliferation of cyber threats and attacks has grown exponentially. Consequently, Anti- Virus companies and researchers have developed new approaches for dealing with discovering and classifying malware. Among these, machine learning and Big Data technologies have been used for feature extraction, detection, and clustering of cyber threats. In this paper, we created and analyzed a dataset of malware and clean files (goodware) from the static and dynamic features provided by the online framework VirusTotal. The purpose is to

suggestions.title: Adversarial Robustness Testing against Feature-Statically Evaded Malware
suggestions.desc: Investigate how the model behaves when malware authors intentionally manipulate the 9 selected features using GANs to bypass detection. Build a testing suite that generates 'adversarial malware' to evaluate the stability of the RF and SVM classifiers.

suggestions.title: Explainable Malware Diagnostics with SHAP and LIME
suggestions.desc: Integrate SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) to visualize exactly why the neural network flagged a specific file. Create a dashboard for security analysts that highlights which of the 9 features contributed most to a 'malicious' verdict.

suggestions.title: Real-time Windows Kernel Driver Implementation
suggestions.desc: Port the Python-based detection logic into a C++ Windows kernel driver that monitors file execution in real-time. Use a lightweight inference engine like ONNX Runtime to maintain the 99.6% accuracy without significant CPU overhead.

suggestions.title: Cross-Platform Feature Mapping for Android APKs
suggestions.desc: Extend the feature selection methodology to the Android domain by extracting intents, permissions, and API calls. Test if a similarly small subset of features (under 10) can achieve comparable accuracy on the Drebin or Contagio datasets.

title: Gender/Age classification from Facial Image using Deep Learning
authors: Dhiraj Gharana
abstract: To estimate gender/age from facial images by using deep learning

suggestions.title: On-Device Inference with TensorFlow Lite and Face Mesh
suggestions.desc: Optimize the deep learning model for mobile deployment using quantization and pruning to run on mid-range smartphones. Use MediaPipe Face Mesh to ensure classification is only performed on high-quality facial crops, reducing false positives from background noise.

suggestions.title: Mitigating Demographic Bias in Classification
suggestions.desc: Evaluate the model's accuracy across different ethnicities using the FairFace dataset. Implement adversarial debiasing or re-weighting techniques during training to ensure the age and gender estimation performs equitably across all protected groups.

suggestions.title: Retail Analytics Heatmap and Demographic Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Develop a system that connects to a store's IP camera to track the age and gender distribution of customers over time. Use OpenCV for multi-face tracking and visualize the data in a React-based dashboard to help retailers optimize product placement.

suggestions.title: Privacy-Preserving Edge Classification with Local Blurring
suggestions.desc: Modify the pipeline to perform age/gender estimation on the 'edge' and immediately discard the raw image. Implement a system where only the metadata (e.g., 'Male, 25-30') is sent to the server, while the facial image is blurred or deleted to comply with GDPR/CCPA.

suggestions.title: Edge-Deployed Inference on Jetson Nano
suggestions.desc: Convert the CNN and Capsule Network models to ONNX format and deploy them onto NVIDIA Jetson Nano hardware for real-time, low-power animal detection at the camera source.

suggestions.title: Multi-Species Interaction Tracker
suggestions.desc: Develop a temporal analysis layer that tracks multiple detected animals across video frames to identify and flag interspecies interactions like predator-prey encounters.

suggestions.title: Capsule Network Robustness Study
suggestions.desc: Conduct a comparative study on how Capsule Networks handle 'occlusion' (animals hidden by trees or brush) compared to standard CNNs using synthetic wilderness datasets.

suggestions.title: Rare Species Alert & Geo-Tagging System

title: Monitoring and Assessing Traffic Safety Using Live Video Images
authors: Srivarna Settisara Janney, Ishraq Karim
abstract: Highway Safety assessment has traditionally been relied on historical crash data and/or field conflict studies. The objective of this research study is to automatically extract conflict event data from the field cameras on the fly. Those conflict events data can be used to proactively diagnose safety issues, and formulate and implement proper countermeasures in a timely manner. It is expected to considerably reduce the number of crashes that could have occurred otherwise.

suggestions.title: Near-Miss Detection with Edge Computing on NVIDIA Jetson
suggestions.desc: Deploy the traffic conflict model to an NVIDIA Jetson Nano device for local, real-time processing at the intersection. Develop a 'Near-Miss' alert system that triggers a high-priority log whenever the Post-Encroachment Time (PET) between two vehicles drops below a safety threshold.

suggestions.title: Multi-Modal Sensor Fusion for Low-Visibility Safety
suggestions.desc: Integrate LiDAR or Thermal camera data with the live video feed to maintain safety monitoring during night-time or heavy fog. Use a Kalman filter to fuse object tracks from different sensors, ensuring continuous monitoring when visual cameras fail.

suggestions.title: Predictive Trajectory Modeling for Pedestrian Safety
suggestions.desc: Expand the system to predict the future path (0.5 to 2 seconds) of pedestrians near crosswalks using LSTMs. Analyze traffic conflicts between turning vehicles and pedestrians to proactively suggest timing changes for signalized intersections.

suggestions.title: Digital Twin Traffic Safety Synchronization
suggestions.desc: Stream the extracted traffic conflict data into a 3D Digital Twin environment using Unity or Carla Simulator. This allows city planners to virtually 'replay' high-risk events from any angle to identify structural road design flaws contributing to accidents.

title: Next Generation of High Efficiency Video Coding Optimization with Deep Learning
authors: Wenchan Jiang
abstract: High Efficiency Video Coding (HEVC) is the latest video coding standard developed by Joint Collaborative Team on Video Coding (JCT-VC). HEVC can support up to 50% bit-rate reduction for equal perceptual video quality. Experiments will be carried out in order to determine coding performance with different size coding units. The initial results will be further optimized with neural network deep learning techniques.

suggestions.title: Hybrid Neural Post-Processing Filter for HEVC
suggestions.desc: Build a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) that acts as an in-loop filter to remove blocking artifacts specifically for low-bitrate HEVC streams. Implement the filter using PyTorch and benchmark its PSNR (Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio) against the standard HEVC deblocking filter.

suggestions.title: Perceptual Loss Optimization using MS-SSIM

title: Optimisation of object searching techniques using visual C++
authors: Anil Kumar Sreedharala
abstract: Quantum-augmented networks aim to use quantum phenomena to improve detection and protection against malicious actors in a classical communication network. This may include multiplexing quantum signals into classical fiber optical channels and incorporating purely quantum links alongside classical links in the network. In such hybrid networks, quantum protocols based on single photons become a bottleneck for transmission distances and data speeds, thereby reducing entire network performance. Furthermore, many of the security assumptions of the single-photon protocols do not hold up in practice because of the impossibility of manufacturing single-photon emitters. Multi-photon quantum protocols, on the other hand, are designed to operate under practical assumptions and do not require single photon emitters. As a result, they provide higher levels of security guarantees and longer transmission distances. However, the effect of channel and device noise on multiphoton protocols in terms of security, transmission distances, and bit rates has not been investigated. In this paper, we focus on channel noise and present our observations on the effect of various types of noise on multi-photon protocols. We also investigate the effect of topologies such as ring, star, and torus on the noise characteristics of the multi-photon protocols. Our results show the possible advantages of switching to multi-photon protocols and give insights into the repeater placement and topology choice for quantum-augmented networks.

suggestions.title: Quantum Network Planning & ROI Simulator
suggestions.desc: Develop a desktop application that allows network architects to upload their existing classical fiber topology (JSON/GeoJSON). The tool simulates the performance gain and cost-benefit analysis of adding multi-photon quantum links, accounting for specific regional noise profiles and transmission distances.

suggestions.title: Noise-Resilient Quantum Key Distribution (QKD) for IoT
suggestions.desc: Adapt the multi-photon protocols to secure a simulated smart-city IoT network. Build a prototype that demonstrates how multi-photon states can maintain a secure key exchange even over noisy, shared classical-quantum fiber lines, protecting critical infrastructure like power grids from eavesdropping.

suggestions.title: Reinforcement Learning for Dynamic Quantum Routing
suggestions.desc: Train a Deep Q-Network (DQN) agent to dynamically route quantum packets through a multi-topology network. The agent should learn to avoid high-noise links and prioritize paths that maintain photon coherence, effectively maximizing the entire hybrid network's throughput and fidelity.

suggestions.title: NS-3 Extension for Multi-Photon Interference Modeling
suggestions.desc: Extend the popular NS-3 network simulator with a specialized module for multi-photon interference and decoherence. This tool would allow researchers to test quantum protocols against realistic classical network traffic noise without needing expensive physical quantum hardware.

The short training time and high accuracy of CNNs come from the high cost of power consumption with energy lost in a data center, which has been largely ignored in previous CNN design. It is highly desirable to design deep learning frameworks and algorithms that are both accurate and energy efficient with optimal power consumption. In this study, we conduct a com

title: Prediction of the seismic hazard
authors: Yan Wang
abstract: Mental health is a critical aspect of our overall well-being. Mental illness refers to conditions that impact an individual's psychological state, resulting in considerable distress, and limitations in functioning day-to-day tasks. Due to the progress of technology, social media has merged as the platform, for individuals to share their thoughts and emotions. The psychological state of individuals can be accessed with the help of data from these platforms. However, it is challenging for conventional machine learning models to analyze the diverse linguistic contexts of social media data. In this work, we propose a novel attention-driven deep framework to overcome these challenges. Our proposed framework utilizes multi-level (word, sentence, and document) data to identify the causes behind mental illness. The efficacy and effectiveness of our proposed model are shown by extensive evaluation on Reddit data. The insights from this research deepen our understanding of different factors behind mental illness and would aid mental health professionals in formulating effective interventions.

suggestions.title: Early-Warning Content Moderation API
suggestions.desc: Package the attention-driven framework into a REST API for social media platforms. The tool would analyze user-generated content in real-time to flag 'at-risk' behavior for human moderators, providing them with the specific 'attention weights' (reasons) to help them connect users with mental health support.

Build a visualization tool that maps how specific causes (e.g., job loss, social isolation) correlate with the progression of mental illness symptoms over time, helping clinicians understand the 'trigger-to-symptom' timeline.

suggestions.title: Vision-Text Attention Fusion for Multi-Modal Detection

The model could then identify discrepancies or reinforcements (e.g., dark imagery paired with isolating language) to increase the accuracy of mental illness cause detection.

This will help balance the training dataset and improve the attention framework's ability to recognize less common psychological distress markers.

abstract: In this demonstration, we propose a framework for exploring, identifying, and matching repeated behavioral health keywords in first-responder reports to the current set of Behavioral Health Index Terms provided by subject matter experts (SMEs). The tool incorporates behavioral health-related keywords and has a Graphical User Interface (GUI) that allows non-technical users to explore and analyze 911 first-responder reports. We utilized an inverted index, best-matching (BM25), and plain-text searching algorithms to match keywords in first-responder reports. This tool provides a comprehensive approach to report analysis by identifying indicators of mental health disorders and taking into account the assessments of humanities and social science professionals

abstract: Women in computing student organizations Contact Info Resources Related Links For Kennesaw Phone Libraries Campus Campus 470-KSU-INFO Current Security Housing 1000 (470-578-4636) Students Global Chastain Financial Aid Education kennesaw.edu/info Online Only Road Students Degrees, Diverse & Kennesaw, Media Resources Majors & Inclusive Faculty & Staff Programs GA 30144 Excellence Parents & Registrar Marietta Family Sustainability Campus Job Accessibility Alumni & Opportunities 1100 South Friends Marietta Pkwy Community & Marietta, Business GA 30060 Campus Maps © 2023 Kennesaw State University.
