title: Image Segmentation
authors: Mohammad Masum
abstract: This project is about predicting an image whether it's an internet advertisement or not. There are more than 3000 observations of images are in the data set with label whether an image 'ad' or 'nonad'. Our goal is to train the data with different classifier algorithm and then test with new instances. Finally, our goal is to compare the performances of output of different algorithms.

suggestions.title: Hybrid Visual-Structural Classifier
suggestions.desc: Enhance the classification by combining image features with the DOM structure of the webpage. Use a multi-input neural network that analyzes both the image pixels and the parent container's CSS/HTML properties to identify 'native' advertising more accurately.

suggestions.title: Semantic Ad-Blocker Browser Extension
suggestions.desc: Build a Chrome extension that uses the trained model to identify and selectively blur advertisements on dynamic web pages. Unlike traditional ad-blockers that use blocklists, this would use 'visual intelligence' to find ads that have randomized URLs.

suggestions.title: Ad-Evasion Robustness Testing
suggestions.desc: Perform an adversarial analysis by applying small perturbations (noise, rotations, color shifts) to the 'ad' images to see if the classifier can be easily fooled. Develop a more robust model using adversarial training to defend against these evasion tactics.

suggestions.title: Brand Safety Compliance Auditor
suggestions.desc: Create a tool for brands to audit where their ads are appearing. The tool would crawl web pages and use the classifier to ensure that the brand’s ads are being displayed in appropriate contexts and are not being 'stacked' or hidden by fraudulent publishers.

similar: legacy:GRCS-28|Fall 2017|mrf and ba in 3d image segmentation

title: Image Approximation with GA
authors: Andrew Henshaw
abstract: This project demonstrates the use of a genetic algorithm to optimize the placement and color of a small number of triangles in order to reconstruct and approximate a reference image.

suggestions.title: GPU-Accelerated Genetic Evolution
suggestions.desc: Rewrite the genetic algorithm using PyCuda or Numba to offload the triangle rasterization and fitness calculation (MSE) to the GPU. This would allow for evolving images with thousands of triangles at high resolutions in a fraction of the original time.

suggestions.title: Generative Vector Art Creator
suggestions.desc: Develop a web application using Flask and Fabric.js where users upload photos and watch them evolve into vector art in real-time. Add controls for 'mutation rate' and 'triangle limit,' and allow users to export the result as an SVG for print media.

suggestions.title: Hybrid Evolutionary-Gradient Optimization
suggestions.desc: Research a hybrid approach where the Genetic Algorithm finds the general 'pose' of triangles, and a gradient descent method (like Adam optimizer) fine-tunes the exact vertex coordinates and RGBA colors for pixel-perfect approximation.

suggestions.title: Video Frame Interpolation via Vectorization
suggestions.desc: Apply the triangle approximation to video frames. By evolving triangles for Frame A and Frame B, explore how to morph the triangles' positions to generate intermediate frames, potentially creating a new form of low-bandwidth video compression.

title: Community Crime Prevention Strategy
authors: Sanjoosh Akkineni
abstract: Communities within the United States. The data combines socio-economic data from the 1990 US Census, law enforcement data from the 1990 US LEMAS survey, and crime data from the 1995 FBI UCR. Many variables are included so that algorithms that select or learn weights for attributes could be tested. However, clearly unrelated attributes were not included; attributes were picked if there was any plausible connection to crime (N=122), plus the attribute to be predicted (Per Capita Violent Crimes). The variables included in the dataset involve the community, such as the percent of the population considered urban, and the median family income, and involving law enforcement, such as per capita numb

suggestions.title: Modern Census Data Comparison Tool
suggestions.desc: Update the project by ingesting the 2020 US Census data and current FBI UCR data via API. Build a comparative model to analyze how the socioeconomic predictors of crime have evolved or stayed constant over the last 30 years.

suggestions.title: Fairness-Aware Predictive Modeling
suggestions.desc: Implement the AIF360 toolkit to detect and mitigate algorithmic bias in the crime dataset. Evaluate if certain socioeconomic variables act as proxies for race or neighborhood, and rebuild the model to ensure equitable 'per capita' predictions.

suggestions.title: Spatial Risk Heatmap Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Integrate the model predictions with Mapbox GL JS to create an interactive geospatial dashboard. Users can visualize crime risk levels at a community level and see which specific factors (e.g., unemployment, housing density) are the primary drivers in their area.

suggestions.title: Community Resource Allocation Optimizer
suggestions.desc: Build a decision-support system for city planners that suggests where to allocate social services (like youth centers or job training) based on the model's identified risk factors to maximize the reduction of violent crime.

title: Dog breed identification
authors: Liyuan Liu, Yiyun Zhou
abstract: Image classification is the most important part of digital image analysis. The dog breed identification should give a huge contribution to Animal Shelter, pet adoption center and pet store to classify the dogs. This technology will also widely used in E-commerce industry and retail industry.

suggestions.title: ViT-Based Fine-Tuning for Rare Breeds
suggestions.desc: Move beyond standard CNNs by fine-tuning a Vision Transformer (ViT) or EfficientNetV2. Focus specifically on improving accuracy for 'look-alike' breeds (e.g., Alaskan Malamute vs. Siberian Husky) using a specialized triplet loss function.

suggestions.title: Shelter-Match Mobile App
suggestions.desc: Create an app that allows users to take a photo of a dog they like and uses the Petfinder API to find similar-looking breeds currently available for adoption in local shelters within a 50-mile radius.

suggestions.title: Mixed-Breed Visual Estimator
suggestions.desc: Develop a multi-label classification system that can estimate the percentage of different breeds in a mixed-breed dog. Use a dataset of 'designer dogs' and mixed-breed photos to train the model to recognize hybrid physical traits.

suggestions.title: Cross-Domain Transfer Learning Study
suggestions.desc: Research the effectiveness of 'Domain Adaptation.' Compare the performance of a dog-breed model trained on studio-quality retail images versus its performance on low-quality, real-world shelter intake photos, and implement techniques to bridge the gap.

title: Updating an IDS at Real Time
authors: Alexander Federico
abstract: Project that develops an Intrusion Detection System to update at a set time and prevent the system from being vulnerable while updating.

suggestions.title: AI-Powered Generative Inpainting
suggestions.desc: Integrate a lightweight generative model (like LaMa or a pruned Stable Diffusion) to allow users to remove unwanted objects from photos. The app would 'fill in' the background realistically based on the surrounding pixels, moving beyond simple blurring.

suggestions.title: Neural Style Transfer Studio
suggestions.desc: Implement a fast neural style transfer module using TensorFlow Lite that allows users to apply the artistic styles of famous painters (e.g., Van Gogh, Picasso) to their photos in real-time without needing a cloud connection.

suggestions.title: Cloud-Synced Collaborative Editing
suggestions.desc: Build a backend using Firebase and Google Cloud Functions to allow multiple users to edit the same high-resolution photo simultaneously. Implement an 'edit history' stack that allows users to undo specific changes made by other collaborators.

suggestions.title: Automated Batch Product Photography
suggestions.desc: Add a 'Professional Mode' specifically for e-commerce sellers that automatically removes backgrounds, adjusts white balance to a standard 'studio' look, and resizes images to fit Amazon/eBay specifications in a single batch process.

project_key: legacy:GRCS-06|Fall 2017|updating an ids at real time

title: Machine Learning Movie Prediction
authors: Andrew Granr
abstract: The purpose of the project is to use machine learning techniques to determine the success of a movie on the released date.

suggestions.title: Predictive Bottleneck Forecasting
suggestions.desc: Shift from 'identification' to 'prediction' by implementing an LSTM or Temporal Graph Convolutional Network. Use historical traffic camera data to predict where a bottleneck is likely to form 30 minutes before it happens based on current flow trends.

suggestions.title: Waze-API Integration for Driver Rerouting
suggestions.desc: Develop a middleware that pushes detected bottleneck locations to traffic navigation APIs like Waze or Google Maps. Create a dashboard for city planners that suggests real-time 'diversion routes' to mitigate the impact of the detected congestion.

suggestions.title: Multi-Camera Vehicle Tracking (Re-ID)
suggestions.desc: Implement Vehicle Re-Identification (Re-ID) to track specific cars across multiple interstate cameras. This allows for the calculation of 'true travel time' and 'average segment speed,' providing much deeper data than simple density-based bottleneck detection.

suggestions.title: Weather-Impact Analysis Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Correlate traffic camera data with real-time weather APIs (OpenWeather). Analyze and visualize how different types of precipitation or visibility levels lower the 'bottleneck threshold' of specific highway interchanges to help DOTs plan winter maintenance.

title: Analysis of hotel reviews using ML
authors: Jiaxin Chen
abstract: This project applied Machine Learning technology on analyzing the customers' reviews of hotels in Las Vegas strip, and which features will play a key role in influencing the scores.

suggestions.title: Low-Power LoRa Mesh Implementation
suggestions.desc: Port the energy-harvesting algorithms to LoRaWAN-enabled microcontrollers like the ESP32-S3. Build a physical mesh network that prioritizes data transmission paths based on real-time solar or RF energy levels collected from harvesting modules.

suggestions.title: Multi-Hop Cooperative D2D Relaying
suggestions.desc: Extend the interference-aware model to include multi-hop relaying where intermediate devices act as energy-neutral repeaters. Use the NS-3 simulator to evaluate how cooperative relaying affects total network throughput versus energy depletion rates.

suggestions.title: Smart Agriculture Sensor Persistence
suggestions.desc: Apply the energy-harvesting logic to a fleet of soil moisture sensors in a simulated agricultural environment. Optimize the D2D communication schedule to ensure that nodes in shaded areas (interference-heavy) remain operational by 'borrowing' transmission windows from well-lit nodes.

suggestions.title: Battery-Free Asset Tracking Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Develop a commercial-ready monitoring dashboard using React and MQTT that tracks the 'energy health' of harvest-powered tags. Integrate a predictive maintenance module that alerts users when specific network nodes are likely to fail due to high interference and low harvesting yield.

project_key: legacy:GRCS-08|Fall 2017|analysis of hotel reviews using ml

title: SQL Injection with FindSecurityBugs
authors: Peter Ding
abstract: An introduction to SQL injection attacks and how to prevent them with secure mobile software development by building a SQL injection detector with FindSecurityBugs

suggestions.title: Dynamic Price Optimization Engine
suggestions.desc: Build a tool for Amazon sellers that suggests real-time price adjustments based on the identified success factors. Use Python and the Amazon SP-API to automate pricing changes that maximize the 'Buy Box' win rate while maintaining the product success score.

suggestions.title: Visual Quality and Sentiment Integration
suggestions.desc: Enhance the dataset by scraping product images and using Computer Vision (OpenCV/PyTorch) to analyze image quality. Incorporate sentiment analysis of user-uploaded photos in reviews to see if visual authenticity correlates more highly with success than professional marketing shots.

suggestions.title: Counterfeit and Hijacker Detection System
suggestions.desc: Use the success factor model to identify anomalies that suggest a product listing has been hijacked or replaced by counterfeits. Build a monitoring bot that alerts brand owners when a product's success metrics (like conversion rate) deviate sharply from the historical baseline.

suggestions.title: Cross-Platform Market Comparison
suggestions.desc: Extend the analysis to include Walmart and eBay datasets to determine if 'success factors' are universal or platform-specific. Conduct a comparative study on how different recommendation algorithms across these sites favor specific product attributes.

title: Titanic Survival : Machine Learning
authors: Sweta Patil
abstract: Use machine learning techniques to do the ‘Titanic Survival Analysis’ using the data sets from Kaggle.com

suggestions.title: LiDAR-to-Haptic Spatial Mapping
suggestions.desc: Integrate an iPhone Pro LiDAR or a specialized sensor like the Intel RealSense with the Vive logic to map real-world obstacles in real-time. Convert distance data into varying haptic frequencies delivered through a wearable belt, allowing users to 'feel' the room's geometry.

suggestions.title: Public Transit Audio-Wayfinding App
suggestions.desc: Create a mobile application that uses spatialized audio cues to guide visually impaired users through complex environments like subway stations. Use Google’s Resonance Audio SDK to create 'audio beacons' at specific gates and platform edges.

suggestions.title: Bone Conduction vs. Spatial Audio Study
suggestions.desc: Research the cognitive load differences between using standard headphones versus bone conduction headsets for navigation. Measure user reaction times and 'environmental awareness' scores to determine which hardware provides safer real-world utility.

suggestions.title: Retail Accessibility AR Overlay
suggestions.desc: Develop a product for retailers that uses AR tags to provide auditory descriptions of store shelves. When a user points their phone or headset at a shelf, the system provides haptic confirmation and spatial audio descriptions of product placement and pricing.

title: NLP for SMS SPAM Messages
authors: Uday Bhaskar Boyanapalli
abstract: To build a Spam detection filter using a Machine Learning Algorithm, such as Natural Language Processing for UCI real data for SMS Spam Messages.

suggestions.title: TLS 1.3 JA3 Fingerprinting Integration
suggestions.desc: Modernize the detection system by implementing JA3 and JA3S fingerprinting techniques to identify malicious clients even when traffic is fully encrypted via TLS 1.3. Build a Zeek plugin to automate this fingerprint collection across a live university network.

suggestions.title: Adversarial Evasion Benchmarking
suggestions.desc: Create a suite of 'chaff' traffic generators that mimic normal user behavior to see if the ML model can be tricked. Research how malware authors might use traffic shaping or 'domain fronting' to hide RC4-like patterns within standard HTTPS flows.

suggestions.title: IoT Shadow IT Detection Gateway
suggestions.desc: Apply the malware detection logic to a Raspberry Pi-based home gateway. Focus on detecting 'phone-home' signals from cheap IoT devices that may be using non-standard encryption or deprecated RC4 suites to exfiltrate data.

suggestions.title: Browser-Based Cryptographic Health Monitor
suggestions.desc: Develop a Chrome extension that analyzes the encryption standards of all active background connections. Provide users with a 'security score' and alert them if background scripts are communicating via vulnerable or suspicious encrypted tunnels.

title: Video Compression Optimization
authors: Wenchan Jiang
abstract: High efficiency Video Coding (HEVC) has been deemed as the newest video coding standard of the ITU-T Video Coding Experts Group and the ISO/IEC Moving Picture Experts Group []. HEVC has the potential to deliver better performance than earlier standards such as H.264/AVC. The reference software (i.e., HM) have included the implementations of the guidelines in appliance with the new standard. The software includes both encoder and decoder functionality. Machine learning (ML) works with data and processes it to discover patterns that can be later used to analyze new trends. ML can play a key role in a wide range of critical applications, such as data mining, natural language processing, image r

suggestions.title: P4-Programmable Hardware Mitigation
suggestions.desc: Move the defense strategy from software-defined controllers to the data plane using P4. Implement a line-rate packet filter on a programmable switch (or BMv2 simulator) that drops overflow-inducing packets before they reach the control plane.

suggestions.title: Multi-Tenant SDN Isolation Framework
suggestions.desc: Research how to partition flow table resources in a multi-tenant cloud environment to prevent a 'noisy neighbor' overflow attack. Build a hypervisor-layer manager that enforces strict flow entry quotas per tenant.

suggestions.title: Zero-Trust SD-WAN Security Layer
suggestions.desc: Integrate the overflow defense into an SD-WAN architecture. Use a centralized controller to distribute 'trust scores' to edge routers, dynamically adjusting flow table priority based on the source's historical behavior.

suggestions.title: SDN Intrusion Prevention Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Create a commercial monitoring UI for network admins that visualizes flow table occupancy in real-time. Implement an 'Auto-Mitigate' button that triggers a script to aggregate flow rules (wildcarding) when a potential overflow attack is detected.

similar: legacy:GR-11|Fall 2016|next generation of high efficiency video coding optimization with deep learning

title: Fog-Cloud Storage Audit Service
authors: Yeojin Kim, Donghyun Kim, Junggab Son, Wei Wang, YoungTae Noh
abstract: The paper introduces a new fog-cloud storage architecture which will offer much higher throughput compared to the traditional central cloud storage architecture. The proposed architecture provides transparency in a sense that an end user device does not know the existence of fog storage, and only needs to upload its request toward the central cloud. Also, it provides a stronger audit scheme which is naturally coupled with the initial data upload process and does not suffer from the replay attack using old proof of data soundness.

suggestions.title: OCR and AI Summary Pipeline
suggestions.desc: Integrate Tesseract OCR and the OpenAI API to automatically transcribe handwritten uploaded notes. Add a feature that generates a 5-bullet summary and a 'key terms' glossary for every shared document to improve searchability.

suggestions.title: Collaborative Knowledge Graph Construction
suggestions.desc: Build a tool that links shared notes from different students based on shared concepts. Use Neo4j to visualize these connections, creating a 'map' of the course that allows students to discover related topics across different lecture dates.

suggestions.title: Incentivized Academic Marketplace
suggestions.desc: Implement a gamified reward system using a private blockchain or simple database credits. Students earn 'tokens' for uploading high-quality notes (verified by peer upvotes) which can then be redeemed for tutoring sessions or premium study materials.

suggestions.title: Learning Analytics and Gap Detection
suggestions.desc: Develop an algorithm that analyzes the aggregate content of shared notes to identify 'missing' topics from the syllabus. Provide professors with a dashboard showing which concepts students are struggling to document effectively.

title: Sharing Lecture Contents via Cloud
authors: Nidihi Patel, Jing (Selena) He
abstract: Sharing of Lecture Contents via Clouds

suggestions.title: On-Device Quantization and Speedup
suggestions.desc: Optimize the deep learning model using TensorFlow Lite's Post-Training Quantization to run at 60FPS on mid-range Android devices. Benchmark the battery impact of running the inference on the mobile GPU versus the NPU.

suggestions.title: Smart Digital Signage for Retail
suggestions.desc: Connect the Android app to a large display system that changes advertisements based on the estimated age and gender of the person standing in front of it. Use a privacy-first approach where face data is processed locally and never stored.

suggestions.title: Fairness and Bias Mitigation Study
suggestions.desc: Evaluate the model's accuracy across different ethnicities and lighting conditions. Implement a 'de-biasing' layer in the training pipeline (using datasets like FairFace) to ensure the application provides equitable results for all users.

suggestions.title: Anonymous Crowd Analytics Platform
suggestions.desc: Turn the app into a tool for event organizers to track crowd demographics at entry points. Build a centralized dashboard using Firebase that aggregates demographic counts without storing personally identifiable images.

legacy:GR-03|Fall 2016|an internet-of-things based system to share lecture notes via clouds

title: Data Analysis Approaches
authors: Arialdis Japa, Yong Shi
abstract: This project analyzes various data sets with different approaches. It starts with literature survey, followed by proposed algorithm to analyze big data sets.

suggestions.title: Multi-Omics Data Fusion
suggestions.desc: Extend the clustering model to incorporate not just genomic data, but also transcriptomic and proteomic datasets. Use a Multi-view Clustering approach to find more robust subtypes that are consistent across different biological layers.

suggestions.title: Explainable Biomarker Discovery
suggestions.desc: Apply SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) to the clustering results to identify which specific genes are the most influential in defining each subtype. Build a web tool where researchers can click on a cluster to see its 'genetic signature'.

suggestions.title: Personalized Drug Response Predictor
suggestions.desc: Map the identified cancer subtypes to known drug sensitivity databases (like GDSC). Develop a predictive model that suggests the most effective FDA-approved chemotherapy or targeted therapy based on a patient's subtype classification.

suggestions.title: Clinician-Facing Diagnostic Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Design a React-based frontend that allows doctors to upload a patient's CSV genomic profile and receive a subtype report. Include visualizations like UMAP or t-SNE plots to show where the patient sits relative to the historical cluster data.

title: Credit Card Fraud Detection
authors: Amruta Mangaonkar, Mingon Kang
abstract: As part of project, we will build a machine learning model and train it to predict if a credit card transaction is fraud or not. The data-set used for training contains transactions made by credit cards in September 2013 by European card holders.

suggestions.title: Neuro-Symbolic Logic Integration
suggestions.desc: Combine the neural network with a symbolic logic engine (like Prolog) to handle rule-based constraints in puzzles. Research how this hybrid approach improves the AI's ability to explain its reasoning steps to a human user.

suggestions.title: Chain-of-Thought LLM Fine-Tuning
suggestions.desc: Fine-tune an open-source LLM like Mistral-7B on a specialized dataset of 'insight puzzles.' Implement a 'reflection' loop where the AI must check its own intermediate steps for logical consistency before finalizing an answer.

suggestions.title: Automated Software Logic Debugger
suggestions.desc: Apply the puzzle-solving engine to find logical contradictions in complex software configurations (e.g., Kubernetes YAMLs or IAM policies). Treat the configuration as a 'puzzle' and use the AI to synthesize a sequence of steps to reach a secure state.

suggestions.title: AI Logic Tutor for K-12
suggestions.desc: Build an educational platform that generates 'insight puzzles' at varying difficulty levels. Use the AI to provide 'hints' that don't give the answer away, but instead point the student toward the algorithm they need to synthesize.

title: Malware Detection
authors: Euiseong Ko
abstract: The recent years have witnessed that significant damage has been made by critical malware such as ransomware. So far, many efforts have been made to detect malware and prevent it from damaging users by monitoring network packets. Unfortunately, such approach is hardly applicable to detect the advanced malware, which utilizes encryption to hide its presence and malicious intent. Cryptoanalysis of each packet flowing over a network might be one feasible solution for the problem. However, this approach is time- consuming and not accurate, and therefore not practical. In this paper, we first conduct a comprehensive analysis to discover unique signatures of RC4 that its ciphertexts exhibit unique

suggestions.title: Edge NPU and TPU Performance Benchmarking
suggestions.desc: Expand the study to include specialized hardware like the Apple Neural Engine (ANE) or Coral Edge TPU. Compare the energy efficiency (in Joules per inference) of these dedicated chips against standard laptop CPUs and GPUs.

suggestions.title: Energy-Delay Product (EDP) Analysis
suggestions.desc: Go beyond simple time measurements to calculate the EDP for different neural network architectures (CNNs vs. Transformers). Use a hardware power meter to capture granular energy data and identify the 'sweet spot' for model complexity.

suggestions.title: Cloud Instance Selection Optimizer
suggestions.desc: Create a Python script that profiles a user's specific ML model and recommends the cheapest AWS/Azure instance type based on the performance-to-cost ratio. Integrate live pricing APIs to ensure recommendations are up to date.

suggestions.title: Sustainable AI Training Scheduler
suggestions.desc: Develop a middleware that schedules ML training tasks based on the current carbon intensity of the power grid. Automatically toggle between CPU (low power) and GPU (high speed) to minimize the carbon footprint of the training session.

title: Malware Image Classification by ML
authors: Jhu-Sin(Samuel) Luo
abstract: Malware classification is a critical part in the cybersecurity. Traditional methodologies for the malware classification typically use static analysis and dynamic analysis to identify malware. In this paper, a malware classification methodology based on visualizing the malware and extracting local binary pattern (LBP) features is proposed. Firstly, malware images are reorganized into 3 by 3 grid which is mainly used to extract LBP feature. Secondly, LBP is implemented on the malware images to extract features in that it is useful in pattern or texture classification. Finally, Tensorflow, a library for machine learning, is applied to classify malware image with the LBP feature. Performance co

suggestions.title: Hilbert-Curve Malware Visualization
suggestions.desc: Replace the 3x3 grid with a Hilbert Curve mapping to convert 1D binary code into a 2D image. This space-filling curve preserves the locality of code blocks much better than a simple grid, potentially leading to higher LBP feature accuracy.

suggestions.title: Adversarial Robustness in Malware Imaging
suggestions.desc: Investigate how 'obfuscation' techniques like binary packing or junk code insertion change the LBP visual signature. Build a robust classifier that can detect the underlying malware family even when the binary has been 'salted' to change its visual appearance.

suggestions.title: Visual Sandbox Threat Scanner
suggestions.desc: Build a desktop application that allows security analysts to drag and drop suspicious executables to see their 'visual fingerprint' instantly. The app should display the image, the LBP histogram, and the top 3 predicted malware families.

suggestions.title: Unsupervised Malware Family Clustering
suggestions.desc: Implement an unsupervised learning model (like HDBSCAN) on the LBP features to identify and cluster new, previously unknown malware variants. This could help cybersecurity teams identify 'Zero-Day' campaigns that share visual similarities with known threats.

title: MOEA in Image Segmentation
authors: Wajira Abeysinghe
abstract: Multi Objective Evolutionary Algorithm(MOEA) is using for optimizing multiple objectives in Image Segmentation.

suggestions.title: Hybrid MOEA-UNet Semantic Segmentation
suggestions.desc: Combine the MOEA's global optimization strengths with the local feature extraction of a U-Net architecture. Use the MOEA to optimize the hyperparameters or the loss function weights of the neural network to achieve better boundary definition in complex images.

suggestions.title: CUDA-Accelerated Parallel MOEA Engine
suggestions.desc: Implement the image segmentation MOEA using NVIDIA's CUDA to handle the heavy fitness evaluations of thousands of pixels in parallel. This would transform the current approach from a slow offline process into a high-performance tool capable of processing high-resolution imagery.

suggestions.title: Autonomous Satellite Change-Detection Tool
suggestions.desc: Apply the MOEA to segment multi-temporal satellite imagery from the Sentinel-2 dataset. Build a product that automatically detects and segments urban sprawl or deforestation by optimizing for both color consistency and spatial connectivity across different time steps.

suggestions.title: Medical Histology Slide Analyzer
suggestions.desc: Adapt the MOEA to segment nuclei and glandular structures in pathology slides from the Camelyon16 dataset. Use objectives like shape regularity and texture contrast to help pathologists identify cancerous regions more accurately than single-objective methods.

similar: legacy:GRCS-28|Fall 2017|mrf and ba in 3d image segmentation

title: Security Risks in EHR Applications
authors: Maryam Farhadi, Dr. Hisham Haddad, Dr. Hossain Shahriar
abstract: The project is to study electronic medical record application (Known as OpenEMR) and assess security and privacy risks associated with OpenEMR. This work is part of a thesis project, during which we will examine a number of common open source OpenEMR applications for the presence of security and privacy vulnerabilities and their compliance with the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act (HIPAA) regulations.

suggestions.title: Automated HIPAA Compliance Scanner for OpenEMR
suggestions.desc: Build a static analysis tool that scans OpenEMR plugins and custom modules specifically for HIPAA-violating patterns, such as unencrypted PII logging or weak session management. Integrate this into a CI/CD pipeline using GitHub Actions.

suggestions.title: OpenEMR Security Hardening Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Create a containerized (Docker) 'Secure OpenEMR' distribution that comes pre-configured with the security fixes identified in this project. Include a dashboard for clinic admins to monitor real-time access logs and detect brute-force attempts.

suggestions.title: IoT-to-OpenEMR Secure Gateway
suggestions.desc: Develop a secure middleware that bridges wearable medical devices (like heart rate monitors) with OpenEMR. Focus on implementing the project's security findings to ensure that data in transit from the device to the patient record is encrypted and authenticated.

suggestions.title: Zero-Knowledge Proofs for EMR Access Control
suggestions.desc: Research and implement a Zero-Knowledge Proof (ZKP) layer for OpenEMR. This would allow a doctor to verify they have the necessary permissions to view a record without the system needing to expose the underlying sensitive access control lists (ACLs).

title: Training at the Poles
authors: Michael Kranzlein
abstract: Analyses the effects of omitting non-polar training data for review sentiment polarity classification using SVMs

suggestions.title: Transformer-Based Polar Pruning Comparison
suggestions.desc: Replicate the SVM 'polar pruning' experiment using a Pre-trained Transformer like DistilBERT. Investigate if modern attention mechanisms are more or less sensitive to 'neutral' data noise compared to classical SVMs.

suggestions.title: Active Learning for Dynamic Review Pruning
suggestions.desc: Implement an Active Learning loop that uses the SVM's uncertainty to identify which 'non-polar' samples actually provide value. Instead of omitting all non-polar data, use this tool to find the 10% of neutral reviews that help define the classification boundary.

suggestions.title: Stock Market Sentiment Noise Filter
suggestions.desc: Apply this polar classification logic to the 'StockTwits' or Reddit 'WallStreetBets' datasets. Build a system that ignores 'neutral' market updates and focuses specifically on high-conviction polar sentiments to predict short-term price movements.

suggestions.title: CleanSense: A Minimalist Review Aggregator
suggestions.desc: Develop a browser extension that uses the polar-trained SVM to filter product reviews on Amazon. It should hide the 'fluff' and 'neutral' filler text, presenting the user with only the most decisive positive and negative feedback for faster decision making.

title: Malware Detection Using Gspan
authors: Nusrat Asrafi
abstract: The project is about malware detection using Frequency Based Graph Mining and Machine Learning Algorithm.We used Gspan algorithm to malware behavior data for finding the pattern and Applying machine learning for detecting malware.

suggestions.title: GNN-Enhanced Malware Behavior Analysis
suggestions.desc: Integrate Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) with the Gspan-discovered patterns. Use Gspan to extract frequent subgraphs and feed them into a GNN (like GraphSAGE) to learn high-level embeddings of malware execution trees for higher detection accuracy.

suggestions.title: Android Runtime Behavior Guard
suggestions.desc: Port the Gspan detection logic to monitor Android system calls. Create an app that builds real-time call graphs and alerts users when an application's behavior matches the 'frequent patterns' of known mobile malware families like Joker or Emotet.

suggestions.title: Adversarial Graph Perturbation Defense
suggestions.desc: Research how malware can 'obfuscate' its graph structure to hide from Gspan. Develop a robust version of the Gspan algorithm that can detect malware even when the attacker injects 'noise' nodes and edges designed to break frequent subgraph patterns.

suggestions.title: ThreatIntel Visualizer for SOC Teams
suggestions.desc: Build a web-based visualization platform that uses Gspan to cluster similar malware samples into 'families.' This tool would allow Security Operation Center analysts to see a visual map of how a new threat relates to historical attacks based on shared graph patterns.

title: Creating Artificial Cognition
authors: Oscar Garcia, Jing (Selena) He
abstract: Creating Artificial Cognition: A Neural Network to Solve Synthesis Insight Problems

suggestions.title: Reinforcement Learning for Insight Discovery
suggestions.desc: Extend the neural network by adding a Reinforcement Learning (RL) layer. Train the agent to 'play' with synthesis problems (like the Duncker's Candle Problem) where it receives rewards for novel combinations that lead toward a solution.

suggestions.title: Neural Sleep-Cycle Optimization
suggestions.desc: Implement a 'consolidated learning' phase inspired by human REM sleep. After the network learns a set of synthesis problems, run a weight-pruning and re-organization algorithm that mimics synaptic pruning to see if it improves the model's 'creative' output on new problems.

suggestions.title: Automated Software Bug Synthesis
suggestions.desc: Apply the artificial cognition model to code synthesis. Build a tool that attempts to solve 'insight problems' in programming—tasks where a standard iterative approach fails and a 'creative' refactor of the logic is required to fix a complex bug.

suggestions.title: The AI Creative Design Co-Pilot
suggestions.desc: Build a plugin for architectural or game design software. The tool uses the cognition model to suggest unexpected but functional design elements (e.g., room layouts or puzzle mechanics) based on a set of 'insight constraints' provided by the user.

similar: legacy:GRCS-16|Spring 2017|engineering an ai that can solve insight puzzles

title: Roof Image Classification
authors: Rehnuma Afrin, Mingon Kang, Chih-Cheng Hung
abstract: We are using two types of roof images. We will align the images before training them. Finally we will apply Convolutional Neural Network(CNN) based classification of the images in CAFFE.
supervisor: Dr. Mingon Kang, Dr. Chih-Cheng Hung

suggestions.title: Spatial Transformer Network (STN) Integration
suggestions.desc: Replace the manual image alignment step with a Spatial Transformer Network inside the CNN. This allows the model to learn its own optimal alignment/rotation during the training phase in PyTorch or TensorFlow, making the pipeline more robust to varied aerial angles.

suggestions.title: Solar Potential and Efficiency Estimator
suggestions.desc: Combine the roof classification results with a solar irradiance API (like Google Sunroof). Create a web app that identifies roof types (flat vs. sloped) and materials to provide homeowners with an automated estimate of solar panel ROI for their specific roof.

suggestions.title: Post-Disaster Damage Assessment Bot
suggestions.desc: Fine-tune the CNN on a dataset of damaged vs. undamaged roofs (e.g., post-hurricane imagery). Build a tool for emergency responders that automatically tags roofs as 'collapsed,' 'shingle damage,' or 'intact' from drone or satellite feeds.

suggestions.title: Multi-View 3D Roof Reconstruction
suggestions.desc: Use the classified images as input for a Structure from Motion (SfM) pipeline. By combining the CNN's classification with 3D photogrammetry, build a research project that can accurately estimate the pitch and surface area of a roof from multiple aerial viewpoints.

similar: legacy:GR-08|Fall 2016|gender/age classification from facial image using deep learning

title: Compressed Sensing of MRI
authors: Srivarna Settisara Janney, Dr. Sumit Chakravarty
abstract: Applying Compressed Sensing to Medical images(MRI) offers potentially significant scan time reductions, with benefits for patients and retain good quality images for accurate diagnoses by doctors. We are using weighted tree wavelet based sparsity matrix to reconstruct images with fewer errors in less time.

suggestions.title: GPU-Accelerated ADMM Reconstruction
suggestions.desc: Implement the weighted tree wavelet reconstruction using the Alternating Direction Method of Multipliers (ADMM) on a GPU. This would drastically reduce the processing time, moving toward the goal of real-time MRI visualization during scans.

suggestions.title: Deep Learning vs. Wavelet Sparsity
suggestions.desc: Compare the current weighted tree wavelet approach against a Deep Learning model like a GAN-based reconstruction (e.g., DAGAN). Evaluate both methods on the fastMRI dataset to determine which better preserves high-frequency details needed for diagnosis.

suggestions.title: Low-Field Portable MRI Optimizer
suggestions.desc: Apply this compressed sensing technique specifically to data from low-field portable MRI machines. These machines are noisier and slower; using the weighted tree wavelet sparsity could enable usable imaging in remote or emergency field settings.

suggestions.title: Cloud-Based MRI Reconstruction API
suggestions.desc: Develop a REST API where sparse MRI data can be uploaded and reconstructed using high-performance cloud clusters. This would allow smaller clinics with limited local computing power to benefit from advanced compressed sensing algorithms.

title: Identifying GBM Cancer Subtypes
authors: Tejaswini Mallavarapu, Mingon Kang
abstract: Glioblastoma multiforme (GBM) is the most fatal malignant type of brain tumor with a very poor prognosis and with a median survival of around one year. Identifying tumor subtypes may play important roles in determining the survival rates in GBM. We developed pathway-based clustering method using Restricted Boltzmann Machine (RBM), called R-PathCluster, for identifying unknown subtypes with pathway markers of gene expressions and performance of R-PathCluster is assessed several clustering methods such as k-means, hierarchical clustering, and RBM models with different input data.R- PathCluster showed the best performance in clustering longterm and short- termsurvivals, although its clustering

suggestions.title: Multi-Omics R-PathCluster Integration
suggestions.desc: Expand the R-PathCluster method to include not just gene expression, but also DNA methylation and proteomics data from the TCGA-GBM dataset. Use a Multi-Modal RBM to find subtypes that are consistent across different biological layers.

suggestions.title: GBM Survival Prediction Decision Support
suggestions.desc: Build a clinical dashboard that takes a patient's gene expression profile, identifies their subtype using the R-PathCluster model, and outputs a personalized survival probability curve and suggested clinical trial pathways based on historical data.

suggestions.title: Variational Autoencoder (VAE) Latent Mapping
suggestions.desc: Compare the Restricted Boltzmann Machine (RBM) performance against a Variational Autoencoder for pathway clustering. Visualize the 'latent space' of GBM subtypes using t-SNE to see if the VAE identifies clearer, more distinct boundaries between tumor types.

suggestions.title: Cross-Cancer Subtype Discovery
suggestions.desc: Apply the R-PathCluster methodology to other aggressive cancers with high heterogeneity, such as Triple-Negative Breast Cancer (TNBC) or Pancreatic Cancer. Test if the pathway-based markers used for GBM are effective in identifying subtypes in these domains.

title: Secure protocol in Power Grids
authors: Uday Bhaskar Boyanapalli
abstract: Power Grids are using Open protocol, such as DNP3 which will is vulnerable to cyber attacks. This communication protocol is secured by Securing communication protocol in the Power grid grid network. For this project I have built a Power grid communication as Master and Outstation on DNP3 protocol on Raspberry Pi(RPi) and did a penetration testing. For securing the communication, I am building the Public key Infrastructure(PKI) on the DNP3 protocol.

suggestions.title: TPM-Based Key Management for RPi Master
suggestions.desc: Integrate a Trusted Platform Module (TPM) or a Hardware Security Module (HSM) with the Raspberry Pi. Use this to securely store the PKI private keys, ensuring that even if the Master station is physically compromised, the grid's cryptographic identity remains safe.

suggestions.title: Latency Analysis of PKI in Real-Time Control
suggestions.desc: Measure the impact of PKI handshake overhead on DNP3 communication latency. Propose and test a 'lightweight' certificate validation scheme or a session-key caching mechanism that maintains security without delaying critical power grid control commands.

suggestions.title: GridIDS: PKI-Aware Intrusion Detection
suggestions.desc: Develop an Intrusion Detection System (IDS) that monitors the DNP3 traffic for 'Man-in-the-Middle' attacks. The IDS should specifically flag invalid certificates, expired keys, or unauthorized DNP3 Master stations attempting to join the network.

suggestions.title: Secure OTA Firmware Updates for Grid Sensors
suggestions.desc: Build a system that uses the established PKI to sign and verify over-the-air (OTA) firmware updates for the Raspberry Pi Outstations. This ensures that only authentic code can be deployed to the power grid controllers, preventing 'Stuxnet-style' attacks.

title: MRF and BA in 3D Image segmentation
authors: VAIBHAV PANDEY
abstract: This research project introduces a new 3D segmentation technique that utilizes bees algorithm as optimization tool in the Markov Random Field (MRF) model. For image segmentation, image labeling is performed, wherein each pixel is associated with a label pertaining to a class or region. The problem here is how to choose a label for a pixel. To overcome this problem, a probabilistic image model is built using MRF, wherein most likely labeling is selected for each pixel based on the neighborhood pixels.

suggestions.title: Distributed Bees Algorithm on Apache Spark
suggestions.desc: Scale the Bees Algorithm optimization by distributing the 'scout' and 'forager' bee tasks across a cluster using Apache Spark. This would allow for the segmentation of massive 3D volumetric datasets (like full-body MRI) that are too large for a single machine.

suggestions.title: LiDAR Point Cloud Segmentation for AVs
suggestions.desc: Adapt the MRF-BA technique to segment 3D LiDAR point clouds for autonomous vehicles. Use the MRF to maintain spatial consistency between points and the Bees Algorithm to optimize the classification of objects like pedestrians, vehicles, and road boundaries.

suggestions.title: 4D Spatio-Temporal MRF Segmentation
suggestions.desc: Introduce a temporal dimension into the MRF model to handle 4D data (3D volumes over time). This would be applied to cardiac ultrasound or moving MRI, where the Bees Algorithm optimizes labels that must be consistent in both 3D space and across time frames.

suggestions.title: Voxel-Based Surgical Planning Tool
suggestions.desc: Create a desktop application for surgeons that uses this 3D segmentation technique to calculate organ and tumor volumes. Include a feature to export the segmented regions as STL files for 3D printing anatomical models used in pre-surgical rehearsals.

project_key: legacy:GRCS-28|Fall 2017|mrf and ba in 3d image segmentation

title: Secure Mobile Software Development
authors: Xianyong Meng, Kai Qian
abstract: In this project we present a static security analysis approach with open source FindSecurityBugs plugin for Android Studio IDE. We categorized the common mobile vulnerability for developers based on OWASP mobile security recommendations and implemented and developed detectors with FindSecurityBugs to meet the Secure Mobile Software Development(SMSD) needs in industry and education field.

suggestions.title: CI/CD Pipeline Integration for Automated Compliance
suggestions.desc: Move the FindSecurityBugs analysis out of the local IDE and into a GitHub Actions or GitLab CI workflow. Configure the pipeline to generate SARIF reports that automatically block pull requests containing OWASP Mobile Top 10 vulnerabilities, ensuring code security is enforced before merging.

suggestions.title: AI-Powered Security Remediation and Auto-Patching
suggestions.desc: Integrate a Large Language Model (LLM) API, like GPT-4o or Llama 3, to analyze the specific vulnerabilities flagged by the FindSecurityBugs plugin. Build a feature that provides context-aware code suggestions or 'one-click fix' patches directly within Android Studio to resolve insecure data storage or weak cryptography.

suggestions.title: Cross-Platform Extension for Flutter and React Native
suggestions.desc: Expand the detector's scope by mapping the existing Java/Kotlin security rules to hybrid mobile frameworks. Use the project’s methodology to create custom security linting rules for Dart or JavaScript, addressing common cross-platform flaws like insecure bridge communication or hardcoded API keys.

suggestions.title: Benchmarking Against Commercial Static Analysis Tools
suggestions.desc: Conduct a formal research study comparing the custom FindSecurityBugs detectors against commercial solutions like Snyk or Checkmarx using a dataset of intentionally vulnerable apps like OWASP GoatDroid. Use the results to tune the plugin’s precision and recall, minimizing false positives for professional developers.

similar: legacy:GRCS-30|Spring 2017|algorithm for secure android permission framework

title: Predict and Prevent College Dropout
authors: Nelson Zange TSAKU
abstract: Predictive model to predict college droupout at Kennesaw State University, specifically in the College of Computing and software engineering

suggestions.title: Permission Drift Anomaly Detection
suggestions.desc: Incorporate a machine learning model (e.g., Random Forest) that monitors how app permissions evolve over time. Alert users when an app's requested permissions 'drift' significantly from its original purpose.

suggestions.title: Child-Safety Smart Permission Filter
suggestions.desc: Build a parent-facing dashboard that uses the algorithm to automatically block apps that request high-risk permissions (camera, microphone) without a clear functional justification based on the app's category.

suggestions.title: Malware Fingerprinting via API Sequence Analysis
suggestions.desc: Research the sequence of API calls associated with specific malware families. Use the permission framework to create 'behavioral fingerprints' that can identify new malware variants that bypass signature-based scans.

suggestions.title: Privacy Audit & Compliance Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Develop a commercial tool for developers to audit their own apps for 'over-permissioning.' Provide a certificate of 'Least Privilege Compliance' that can be used to build user trust on app stores.

title: Panorama image auto scroll
authors: Kritika Garg, Mingon Kang
abstract: Title: Android application: Panorama image auto scroll with face movement. Description: There will be two layouts, one for camera which will detect the face and second one for Panorama Image. Initially, Panorama Image will be on its center view. After that If the face will move to the left then image will scroll to the right and will show the left view of the image. Similarly, If the face will move to the right then image will scroll to the left and will show the right view of the image. It will also display the age and gender of the face while detecting.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Streaming via HPCC Systems Roxie
suggestions.desc: Move from batch processing to real-time event discovery. Use the Roxie engine in HPCC Systems to ingest live Twitter or news feeds via Kafka and update the sentiment-change dashboard every 60 seconds.

suggestions.title: Financial Market Volatility Predictor
suggestions.desc: Apply the system to transcripts of CEO earnings calls. Detect sudden sentiment shifts in corporate leadership and correlate them with stock price movements using the QuantLib library.

suggestions.title: Cross-Lingual Sentiment Analysis for Global News
suggestions.desc: Integrate multilingual BERT models to detect sentiment changes across different languages. This allows the HPCC system to discover global events (like geopolitical shifts) by analyzing non-English datasets simultaneously.

suggestions.title: PR Crisis Early Warning System
suggestions.desc: Build a B2B product for PR agencies that monitors brand mentions. Use the sentiment-change logic to send high-priority alerts when a negative sentiment trend begins to 'snowball,' indicating a potential viral crisis.

title: Object recognition using Visual C++
authors: Anil Kumar Sreedharala
abstract: This project will utilize Neural Network (NN) and Deep Learning to improve the mortgage/loan application and approval processes Internships/Student Chapters (8)

suggestions.title: High-Performance Inference with TensorRT and ONNX
suggestions.desc: Optimize the Visual C++ backend by porting the neural network to the ONNX Runtime or NVIDIA TensorRT for hardware-accelerated inference. This will allow for the near-instantaneous processing of high-resolution mortgage documents and identity verification images on local bank servers.

suggestions.title: Automated Signature Verification and Tamper Detection
suggestions.desc: Extend the object recognition model to perform forensic analysis on loan documents by detecting inconsistencies in signatures or digital manipulation in scanned PDFs. Use OpenCV in C++ to identify pixel-level artifacts that suggest a document has been altered or fraudulently combined.

suggestions.title: On-Device Privacy-Preserving Document Processing
suggestions.desc: Implement the recognition engine using the Android NDK or iOS C++ wrappers to process mortgage applications locally on a user's phone. This ensures that sensitive PII (Personally Identifiable Information) is never transmitted to the cloud until it is redacted or encrypted, meeting strict financial privacy regulations.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Document Quality and Completeness Feedback
suggestions.desc: Build a live camera interface that uses the C++ recognition engine to provide real-time feedback to applicants. The system should detect if a mortgage document is missing a page, has blurry text, or if a required field is left blank, reducing the loan approval cycle time by eliminating 'bad' submissions.

title: Travelport Internship Opportunity
authors: Victoria Williams
abstract: This summer and fall I interned at Travelport as a Soft. Systems Engineer. So far, I have completed two main projects, a cloud data aggregation mechanism and an adapted Agile Scrum framework.

suggestions.title: AI-Driven Internship Skill-Gap Analyzer
suggestions.desc: Use NLP (SpaCy) to compare the student's internship tasks with current job market requirements from LinkedIn. Build a dashboard that suggests specific technical projects to bridge the gap between their experience and their dream role.

suggestions.title: Automated Technical Documentation Generator
suggestions.desc: Create a tool that parses an intern's daily Git commits and Jira logs to automatically generate a structured 'End of Internship' technical report. Use an LLM to summarize complex code changes into high-level business value statements.

suggestions.title: Longitudinal Performance Tracking System
suggestions.desc: Develop a data model to track how specific internship domains (e.g., Cloud vs. Frontend) correlate with a student's success in senior-level CS electives. Use this to provide data-backed internship advice to incoming sophomores.

suggestions.title: Company Culture Sentiment Mapping
suggestions.desc: Apply sentiment analysis to internship reviews and reflection papers across the university. Build a visualization tool that maps companies based on 'innovation vs. maintenance' and 'mentorship quality' to help students pick better placements.

title: ServiceNow Projects at Aaron's Inc.
authors: Benjamin Kaguwo

suggestions.title: Internal Knowledge Graph for Onboarding
suggestions.desc: Based on the internship experience, build a Neo4j knowledge graph that maps company projects to specific technical stack dependencies. This would help future interns visualize how different internal tools and microservices interact.

suggestions.title: Corporate Mentorship Matching Engine
suggestions.desc: Build a platform that matches current interns with alumni who previously interned at the same company. Use a recommendation algorithm based on shared tech stacks and career interests to facilitate high-value networking.

suggestions.title: Internship-to-Portfolio Conversion Pipeline
suggestions.desc: Create a secure framework for interns to document their work without violating NDAs. The tool would help users abstract their specific company projects into generalized 'architectural patterns' they can safely show on a personal portfolio.

suggestions.title: Remote vs. In-Person Productivity Study
suggestions.desc: Using the internship experience data, conduct a comparative study on 'speed-to-contribution' for remote versus in-person interns. Analyze the frequency of code reviews and Slack interactions to determine which environment fosters faster learning.

title: Technical Support Engineer at ServIT
authors: Tommy Mallis

suggestions.title: Codebase Navigation Assistant for New Hires
suggestions.desc: Reflecting on the 'learning curve' of an internship, build a VS Code extension that uses LLMs to explain the specific architectural patterns used in a company's private repository. Focus on 'where to find what' for first-week productivity.

suggestions.title: Micro-Internship Marketplace for CS Majors
suggestions.desc: Develop a platform where students can take on 2-week 'sprint' projects for startups. Use the internship experience to define the contract boundaries, ensuring the projects are achievable within a semester alongside coursework.

suggestions.title: Collaborative Internship Roadmap Planner
suggestions.desc: Build a tool where students can collaboratively map out the 'hidden curriculum' of specific internships (e.g., which internal tools to learn first). Use a wiki-style interface with version control to keep the advice current.

suggestions.title: Quantifying Soft Skill Growth in Technical Roles
suggestions.desc: Create a framework to measure the improvement of 'soft skills' (communication, time management) during a CS internship. Use self-assessment data combined with peer-review metrics to create a 'Professional Growth' score.

title: HP Internship
authors: Jere'l McElroy

suggestions.title: Internship Relocation & Housing Assistant
suggestions.desc: Build a community-driven app that helps CS interns find short-term housing near major tech hubs. Include features for finding 'intern roommates' and mapping commuting times to specific tech campuses.

suggestions.title: Blind Interview Coding Platform
suggestions.desc: To improve internship access, build a platform that focuses on 'Project-Based' screening rather than LeetCode. The system should allow candidates to submit a PR to a sample repo, which is then graded anonymously by the company.

suggestions.title: Accessibility Audit for Internship Postings
suggestions.desc: Create an NLP tool that scans internship job descriptions for biased or non-inclusive language. Provide suggestions to recruiters on how to rewrite postings to attract a more diverse applicant pool based on industry DE&I standards.

suggestions.title: Impact of Paid vs. Unpaid Internships on CS Retention
suggestions.desc: Conduct a research study using university data to see if the financial structure of a first internship impacts a student's likelihood to stay in the CS major. Use statistical modeling to control for socioeconomic factors.

title: Ventiv Technology Internship
authors: Michael Olivier

suggestions.title: Automated Resume-to-Internship Matcher
suggestions.desc: Build a system using vector embeddings (OpenAI's text-embedding-ada-002) to match student resumes with the most relevant internship postings based on project technicalities rather than just keywords.

suggestions.title: Virtual Internship Simulation Platform
suggestions.desc: Create a web-based 'simulated' internship environment for freshmen. Use Docker to spin up a pre-configured dev environment where they must solve 3-4 tickets in a 'mock' enterprise codebase to prepare for the real thing.

suggestions.title: Tracking the Evolution of Intern Tech Stacks
suggestions.desc: Analyze 5 years of internship reports to identify which technologies are rising or falling in industry use. Create a 'Tech Radar' visualization specifically for university curriculum committees to align courses with industry trends.

suggestions.title: Junior-to-Senior Peer Mentorship Portal
suggestions.desc: Develop a platform that connects students who just finished an internship (Seniors) with those currently applying (Juniors). Facilitate 1-on-1 'mock interviews' tailored specifically to the companies the seniors just worked for.

title: SVK Systems IT Admin and Data Analysis Internship
authors: Harika Parvathareddy

suggestions.title: Automated Technical Portfolio Generator
suggestions.desc: Build a CLI tool or web app that scrapes your internship project repositories and Jira logs to automatically generate a structured, Markdown-based portfolio. Use the GitHub API to highlight specific commits and technologies mastered during the 15-week term.

suggestions.title: Internship Knowledge Transfer Wiki
suggestions.desc: Develop a localized documentation framework using Docusaurus or Obsidian to map out the specific tech stack and architectural patterns encountered. Create a 'First 30 Days' guide for future KSU interns to accelerate their onboarding at that specific company.

suggestions.title: Skill-Gap Analysis Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Create a data visualization tool that compares the technologies used in the internship (e.g., Spring Boot, AWS) against the university curriculum. Identify 'missing' topics and build a roadmap for elective courses that align with current industry demands.

suggestions.title: Corporate CI/CD Pipeline Audit
suggestions.desc: Conduct a formal study of the deployment workflows experienced during the internship. Design a replica Jenkins or GitHub Actions pipeline that implements the security and testing standards used in production to show future employers your mastery of enterprise DevOps.

project_key: legacy:OTHER-06|Fall 2017|svk systems it admin and data analysis internship

title: Keystone Games Internship
authors: Davison Schuitema

suggestions.title: Modular Procedural Generation Engine
suggestions.desc: Build a C# toolset within the Unity or Unreal editor to procedurally generate level layouts based on the assets developed during the internship. This reduces manual design time and allows for infinite replayability by using Perlin noise and graph-based room placement.

suggestions.title: Cross-Platform CI/CD Pipeline for Indie Studios
suggestions.desc: Implement an automated build and deployment pipeline using GitHub Actions and Unity Cloud Build. The goal is to automate the testing of game builds on Windows, WebGL, and Android simultaneously to streamline Keystone's QA process.

suggestions.title: Behavior Tree-Based Adaptive Enemy AI
suggestions.desc: Extend existing NPC logic by implementing a Behavior Tree or Utility AI system that reacts to player skill levels. Use C# to create nodes for flanking, retreating, and coordinating attacks to make the game's combat feel more dynamic and professional.

suggestions.title: Universal Accessibility Overlay Tool
suggestions.desc: Develop a middleware UI system for the project that provides high-contrast modes, screen-reader support, and customizable input remapping. Focus on meeting the Game Accessibility Guidelines (GAG) to broaden the game's potential user base.

title: UPS Internship - Customer Engagement Platform
authors: Kyle Mayes
abstract: Undergraduate Research and HS (17) * Candidates for the best undergraduate research project award

suggestions.title: Predictive Churn Engine using Logistic Regression
suggestions.desc: Integrate a Python-based machine learning model into the engagement platform to identify customers at risk of leaving. Use the Scikit-learn library to analyze shipment frequency and customer support ticket history to trigger automated retention discounts.

suggestions.title: Serverless Microservices for Real-time Logistics
suggestions.desc: Refactor the platform's backend using AWS Lambda or Google Cloud Functions to handle spike traffic during peak shipping seasons. This migration ensures the platform remains responsive when thousands of customers track packages simultaneously.

suggestions.title: NLP-Powered Feedback Sentiment Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Apply Natural Language Processing (NLP) using the NLTK or spaCy libraries to categorize customer comments. Build a React dashboard that visualizes 'sentiment trends' across different delivery regions, helping UPS managers spot local service issues.

suggestions.title: Blockchain-Based Proof of Delivery
suggestions.desc: Develop a small-scale Hyperledger Fabric or Ethereum-based tracking layer for the platform. This provides an immutable 'Chain of Custody' for high-value shipments, ensuring that the engagement data and delivery signatures are tamper-proof.

title: Smart Smoke Detector
authors: Ashwin Kannan, JIng (Selena) He
abstract: Smart Smoke Detector

suggestions.title: Dual-Sensor Fusion for False Alarm Reduction
suggestions.desc: Integrate an MQ-2 smoke sensor with a DHT22 temperature and humidity sensor using an ESP32. Develop an algorithm that cross-references sudden heat spikes with smoke density to distinguish between actual fires and harmless cooking vapors.

suggestions.title: Mesh Networked Evacuation Pathfinding
suggestions.desc: Use ESP-NOW or Zigbee to link multiple detectors throughout a building. When one unit detects smoke, the system should light up RGB LED strips along the floor to guide occupants away from the fire source using the shortest safe path.

suggestions.title: Pusher-Based Emergency Notification System
suggestions.desc: Build a mobile companion app that receives high-priority push notifications via Firebase Cloud Messaging (FCM). Include a feature that automatically texts the user's GPS coordinates and a 'Fire Detected' alert to local emergency contacts when the alarm is triggered.

suggestions.title: Acoustic Signature Fire Detection
suggestions.desc: Add a microphone module and use a TinyML model (like Edge Impulse) to recognize the specific sound frequency of crackling fire or shattering glass. This acts as a secondary verification method for the traditional ionization or photoelectric sensors.

title: EEG Readings to Satisfaction
authors: Aaron Smith
abstract: My research investigates the potential link between Electroencephalography and general satisfaction of a consumer in a market scale. This is done using a EEG reader interface to quantify the satisfaction of the consumer, and in turn, develop an opinion of the quality of the product without asking the consumer a question (providing a more reliable source of consumer satisfaction).

suggestions.title: Deep Learning EEG Classification via CNNs
suggestions.desc: Move beyond basic frequency analysis by training a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) on the raw EEG signal data. Use the OpenBCI dataset to classify 'satisfied' vs. 'unsatisfied' states with higher accuracy than simple thresholding.

suggestions.title: Adaptive UI for Dynamic Cognitive Load
suggestions.desc: Build a web interface that simplifies its layout or changes its color palette in real-time based on the user's EEG readings. If the sensor detects high frustration or cognitive load, the UI should hide non-essential elements to improve the user experience.

suggestions.title: Real-time Artifact Removal Pipeline
suggestions.desc: Implement an Independent Component Analysis (ICA) filter using Python's MNE library to clean the EEG data. This will remove 'noise' caused by eye blinks or muscle movements, making the consumer satisfaction data far more reliable for market research.

suggestions.title: Gamified Neuromarketing A/B Testing Platform
suggestions.desc: Create a tool for UI designers that synchronizes screen recordings with EEG satisfaction timestamps. Designers can watch exactly where a user’s 'pleasure' levels dipped while navigating an app, providing a data-driven alternative to traditional surveys.

title: VR Applications in Training
authors: Anthony Schell
abstract: A research project for my User Interface Engineering course outlining the potential benefits and drawbacks of virtual technology in the workplace. Specifically, the experiment targets the training of employees compared to the experience done with another human being, and data collected on the efficiency of the training as well as the experience each employee had as a result of the training.

suggestions.title: Haptic Feedback Integration for Manual Training
suggestions.desc: Enhance the VR training simulation by integrating haptic gloves or controllers that provide resistance when handling virtual tools. This allows you to measure 'muscle memory' acquisition compared to traditional hands-on human training.

suggestions.title: Biometric Stress Monitoring in Simulations
suggestions.desc: Connect a heart-rate monitor or GSR sensor to the trainee while they are in the VR environment. Analyze whether VR-induced stress levels correlate with lower efficiency compared to human-led training, providing deeper psychological insights.

suggestions.title: Multi-User Collaborative VR Classroom
suggestions.desc: Transform the single-user experience into a networked environment using Photon Unity Networking (PUN). This allows a human instructor to enter the VR space with the trainee, combining the benefits of virtual simulation with real-time human mentorship.

suggestions.title: Automated Skill-Check Analytics Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Build a backend system that tracks every virtual interaction (time to completion, number of errors, gaze tracking). Export this data into a PowerBI or Tableau dashboard to give managers a quantified 'readiness score' for each employee.

title: Analysis of Allstate Claims
authors: Joshua Saxton
abstract: Big Data analysis using some machine learning algorithms to help Allstate manage different claim data and predict which factors influence a loss in their insurance business.

suggestions.title: XGBoost-Powered Loss Severity Prediction
suggestions.desc: Implement a Gradient Boosting model (XGBoost or LightGBM) to predict the specific dollar amount of a loss rather than just the likelihood. Use hyperparameter tuning to optimize for the 'Allstate Claims Severity' dataset typically found on Kaggle.

suggestions.title: Geospatial Risk Analysis and Mapping
suggestions.desc: Integrate the claim data with Mapbox or ArcGIS APIs to visualize high-risk clusters. By overlaying weather patterns or crime statistics onto the Allstate data, you can identify hidden environmental factors influencing insurance losses.

suggestions.title: Automated Claim Image Recognition
suggestions.desc: Develop a Computer Vision model using TensorFlow to analyze photos of vehicle damage. Link this to the claims data to automatically categorize the 'severity' of a claim before a human adjuster even opens the file, speeding up the payout process.

suggestions.title: Fairness and Bias Auditing Toolkit
suggestions.desc: Apply the AI Fairness 360 toolkit to the Allstate model to detect if certain demographic or geographic factors are unfairly penalizing specific groups. This adds an ethical layer to the data science project essential for regulated industries.

title: A Threat to Safety and Privacy
authors: Mohamed Kabad, Shainu Vazhathil, Matt Hull, Aniruddh Kathiriya, Shivani Patel, Mark Hutto
abstract: Our research project is about the safety and security requirements of drone usage, specifically the DJI Phantom 4, while focusing on the main processes used to.

suggestions.title: Wi-Fi Packet Analysis for Hijacking Prevention
suggestions.desc: Use a Kali Linux environment and Aircrack-ng to analyze the DJI Phantom 4’s communication protocols (Lightbridge/OcuSync). Document potential vulnerabilities in the command-and-control link and propose an encrypted handshake fix.

suggestions.title: Dynamic Geofencing with OpenStreetMap API
suggestions.desc: Develop a custom flight controller layer that pulls real-time data from the OpenStreetMap API to enforce 'No-Fly Zones' around temporary public events. This would prevent the drone from entering sensitive areas even if the DJI firmware is outdated.

suggestions.title: Privacy-Preserving Edge Video Blurring
suggestions.desc: Integrate an NVIDIA Jetson Nano onto the drone to perform real-time face and license plate blurring using OpenCV. This ensures that the drone complies with privacy laws by never storing identifiable human data on its SD card.

suggestions.title: Acoustic Drone Intrusion Detection System
suggestions.desc: Build a ground-based Raspberry Pi station with a directional microphone that uses a Machine Learning model to recognize the specific acoustic signature of a DJI Phantom 4. This acts as a security system to alert property owners of unauthorized drones.

project_key: legacy:URCS-05|Fall 2017|a threat to safety and privacy

title: Research on DJI Phantom 4
authors: Wei Chen
abstract: The main purpose of this study is to discover the new unmanned aerial vehicles called DJI Phantom 4 Advanced Quadcopter Drone by the means of studying the academic journals and real experience of using this device. DJI Phantom 4 Advanced Quadcopter Drone is one of the most popular unmanned aerial vehicles that provide people an amazing experience of enjoying the fantastic view brought by drones. Its powerful features allow people to explore more amazing things of this world. The future of DJI Phantom 4 Advanced Quadcopter Drone is very promising as this kind of technology will keep creating more good features beyond our imaginations; however, it also brings concerns to public that whether th

suggestions.title: Custom Telemetry Web Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Develop a Python-based Flask web server that parses DJI SDK telemetry data (altitude, battery, GPS) in real-time. Visualize this on a dashboard using D3.js to provide more detailed flight analytics than the standard DJI Go 4 app.

suggestions.title: Photogrammetry 3D Reconstruction Pipeline
suggestions.desc: Use the DJI Phantom 4 to capture high-resolution overlapping images and process them using OpenDroneMap (ODM). The goal is to create a high-fidelity 3D digital twin of a campus building, testing the limits of the Phantom 4’s camera sensor.

suggestions.title: Gesture-Based Flight Control with CV
suggestions.desc: Combine the drone's camera feed with a MediaPipe-based hand tracking model. Build a system where a person on the ground can 'steer' the drone or trigger photos using specific hand gestures, enhancing the 'fantastic experience' noted in the abstract.

suggestions.title: Automated Search and Rescue Object Detection
suggestions.desc: Deploy a YOLOv8 (You Only Look Once) model on the drone's video feed to automatically detect and highlight 'humans' or 'vehicles' in difficult terrain. This turns the DJI Phantom 4 into a tool for first responders.

title: Impact of Drone on Agriculture
authors: John Lee, Abdullah Alamri, Caylor Sirk, Franceso Benjamin, Ashley Archibald, Daylon Janis & Yekta Yalcin
abstract: The goal of this paper is to explore the impact of unmanned aerial system drones in agriculture. This paper provides detailed understanding on agriculture drone under logical headings, which will help the reader in interpreting the future scope of using drones in the agriculture sector. We specifically zoom in on the aid that drones give other than just flying. Drones can survey large areas of land without the inherent safety risks and high costs involved with the use of much larger manned aircraft, and the use of these UAV Systems can provide real time imagery and sensor data from farm field areas, which cannot be quickly accessed on foot or by vehicle.

suggestions.title: Multispectral NDVI Crop Health Analysis
suggestions.desc: Simulate multispectral imaging by applying specific color filters (NIR) to the drone footage. Use Python to calculate the Normalized Difference Vegetation Index (NDVI) to identify 'stressed' crops before they show visible signs of disease.

suggestions.title: Autonomous Swarm Coordination for Surveying
suggestions.desc: Develop a simulation in Gazebo or MAVLink where multiple drones divide a large field into a grid. Use a 'Divide and Conquer' algorithm to ensure 100% coverage in half the time it takes for a single drone to survey the area.

suggestions.title: Precision Spraying Path Optimization
suggestions.desc: Create a path-planning algorithm that takes a 'health map' as input and generates a flight route focused only on the diseased sections of the field. This minimizes pesticide waste and reduces the environmental footprint of the farm.

suggestions.title: Cloud-Based Harvest Yield Predictor
suggestions.desc: Build a web platform that ingests drone-captured aerial images and uses a regression model to predict crop yield per acre. Farmers could upload their flight data to get a 'bushel-per-acre' estimate based on plant count and greenness.
