title: IoT-based Motion Control System
authors: Deja Tyla Jackson, Zoe Cesar, Jacob Martinez
abstract: Our project involves an IoT based Motion Control System. This system is a Proof-of-Concept Implementation on Robotics using Internet-of-Things (IoT) Technologies. Technologies involved include an Arduino 101, Raspberry Pi 3 controlled robot and cloud server.

suggestions.title: MQTT-Driven Low Latency Control
suggestions.desc: Replace the standard cloud server with a dedicated MQTT broker (like Mosquitto) running on the Raspberry Pi. This reduces the 'lag' between the web command and the robot's movement, allowing for more precise navigation.

suggestions.title: Digital Twin Real-time 3D Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Create a Three.js-based web dashboard that shows a 3D model of the robot mirroring its physical orientation. Use the Arduino 101’s onboard IMU to stream pitch, roll, and yaw data to the digital twin via the Raspberry Pi.

suggestions.title: Edge-AI Obstacle Avoidance System
suggestions.desc: Integrate an ultrasonic sensor and a lightweight TensorFlow Lite model on the Raspberry Pi. The robot should be able to override cloud commands if its local sensors detect an imminent collision, demonstrating 'Edge-to-Cloud' hybrid control.

suggestions.title: Decentralized Robot Swarm Management
suggestions.desc: Extend the IoT platform to support multiple Raspberry Pi-controlled robots simultaneously. Implement a 'Leader-Follower' logic where one robot follows the movement commands of another using local Bluetooth or Wi-Fi peer-to-peer communication.

similar: legacy:UC-27|Spring 2018|iot voice and gesture control system

title: Applications of AugmentReality in Education Using HoloLens
authors: Quinten Whitaker, Trevor Bradford, Ronald Brooks, Tyler Crawley, David Howard
abstract: In this study we look at the history of augmented reality up until where it is today, and then we look into the potential future applications and implications primarily within the realm of education. We explore one particular medical study that surveyed the realism of holographic simulation to visualize internal body parts. We also explore an implementation, loading a hologram into a virtual environment to understand the technology better.

suggestions.title: Interactive Anatomy with HoloLens Spatial Mapping
suggestions.desc: Extend the medical study by using the Mixed Reality Toolkit (MRTK) to implement spatial mapping. This allows medical students to 'place' holographic organs onto real-world surfaces or mannequins, enabling a more tactile learning experience where the hologram reacts to physical room geometry.

suggestions.title: Collaborative Multi-User Surgical Training
suggestions.desc: Use Photon Unity Networking (PUN) to synchronize the HoloLens environment across multiple headsets. This would allow a lead surgeon and a student to view, rotate, and annotate the same internal body part hologram in real-time for synchronized distance learning.

suggestions.title: Real-time DICOM to Hologram Pipeline
suggestions.desc: Build a bridge using the 'fo-dicom' library to convert actual patient CT/MRI scans (DICOM format) directly into 3D meshes for the HoloLens. This takes the project from generic models to personalized medical visualization for patient-specific pre-operative planning.

suggestions.title: Eye-Tracking Analytics for Educational Assessment
suggestions.desc: Utilize the HoloLens 2 eye-tracking API to generate heatmaps of where students focus during a simulation. By analyzing which anatomical structures are ignored versus inspected, educators can quantitatively measure a student's diagnostic priorities.

project_key: legacy:URCS-09|Fall 2017|applications of augmentreality in education using hololens

title: Alternative Weighted Fuzzy C Means
authors: Michael Wong, Eric Tran
abstract: With our project, we are performing image segmentation with our new algorithm AWFCM(alternative weighted fuzzy c means)

suggestions.title: GPU-Accelerated AWFCM for 4K Imaging
suggestions.desc: Port the AWFCM algorithm from CPU-based Python/MATLAB to CUDA or OpenCL to handle high-resolution image segmentation. This would allow the algorithm to process large-scale 4K medical imagery or satellite data in seconds rather than minutes.

suggestions.title: Multi-spectral Land Use Classification
suggestions.desc: Apply the AWFCM algorithm to the EuroSAT or Sentinel-2 satellite datasets. Use the algorithm's fuzzy weighting to segment complex land types—like the fuzzy boundary between a forest and a grassland—which traditional hard clustering often misidentifies.

suggestions.title: CNN-Initialized Fuzzy Membership
suggestions.desc: Research a hybrid model where a shallow Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) provides the initial membership weights for the AWFCM. This 'warm start' approach can significantly reduce the number of iterations needed for the algorithm to converge on complex images.

suggestions.title: Real-time AR Background Removal Tool
suggestions.desc: Implement the AWFCM algorithm as a mobile library for real-time video segmentation. The goal is to create a background-blur or object-removal tool that performs more accurately than standard filters by leveraging the fuzzy weighting for hair and edge transparency.

title: Automated Macro Malware Detection
authors: Ruth Bearden
abstract: This project demonstrates the effectiveness of automating Microsoft Office macro malware using machine learning classifiers.

suggestions.title: Sequence-Aware Detection with LSTM Networks
suggestions.desc: Move beyond static classifiers by using Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks to analyze the sequential execution flow of VBA macros. This allows the system to detect 'time-bomb' malware that hides its malicious intent behind long strings of benign commands.

suggestions.title: Real-time Outlook Attachment Guard
suggestions.desc: Develop a VSTO (Visual Studio Tools for Office) add-in for Microsoft Outlook that intercepts .docm and .xlsm files. The add-in would run the project's ML model locally to scan attachments and warn users before they ever open a potentially infected document.

suggestions.title: Adversarial Macro Generation and Retraining
suggestions.desc: Build a Generative Adversarial Network (GAN) that attempts to create 'evasive macros'—malicious code that 'looks' benign to your current model. Use these generated samples to retrain and harden the original classifier against modern obfuscation techniques.

suggestions.title: Hybrid Sandbox-ML Analysis Engine
suggestions.desc: Integrate the ML classifier with Cuckoo Sandbox to combine static code analysis with dynamic behavioral features. The end result would be an ensemble model that looks at both what the macro 'says' (source code) and what it 'does' (network calls) in a safe environment.

title: Develop UI for Drone on the Apps
authors: Kelly Duong, Solyana Ayele, Somone Letman, Justin Moon, Allan Gao, Zach Black & Erik Baker
abstract: Professional videotaping is a costly and time consuming process. With the availability of inexpensive and powerful drones, it is possible to let drones automatically follow a user for videotaping. The objective of this study is to describe the techniques used to create a drone application that can follow its subject without the use of GPS, but instead through the utilization of a mobile device such as a smartphone or tablet computer.

suggestions.title: Predictive Subject Tracking with Kalman Filters
suggestions.desc: Incorporate a Kalman Filter into the tracking logic to estimate the subject’s future position. This would solve the 'lost target' problem when a subject briefly moves behind a tree or person, allowing the drone to maintain its trajectory without GPS.

suggestions.title: Gesture-Based Cinematic Commands
suggestions.desc: Use the MediaPipe framework on the mobile device to recognize hand gestures via the drone's camera stream. A user could hold up a 'peace sign' to trigger a 360-degree orbit or a 'stop' sign to land, creating a hands-free professional filming experience.

suggestions.title: On-Device Edge-AI with TensorFlow Lite
suggestions.desc: Deploy a YOLOv8-tiny model optimized with TensorFlow Lite onto the smartphone to handle subject detection. This reduces the latency of the 'follow' command by processing frames locally on the phone's NPU instead of relying on a slow cloud or heavy CPU task.

suggestions.title: Multi-Target Re-Identification (Re-ID) UI
suggestions.desc: Update the UI to detect and label multiple people in the frame using bounding boxes. Allow the user to 'tap-to-lock' onto a specific person; the system should use a Re-ID algorithm to remember that specific person's features even if they leave and re-enter the frame.

project_key: legacy:URCS-12|Fall 2017|develop ui for drone on the apps

title: VR Locomotion and its Effects
authors: Mark Chamberlain, Alex Kimbell, William Dingler, Vojtech Martinek, Ryan Drumm, Troy Wu
abstract: With the recently growing phenomenon of Virtual Reality(VR) as a popular form of entertainment, researchers are now placing their efforts in developing ways to use Virtual Reality practically as well as for leisure. VR opens new means through which real activities can be simulated, opening opportunities not only for entertainment but for training and education. In recent years there has been an explosion in the production and design of Virtual Reality software and gaming apparatus, and with any development thorough examination is required.

suggestions.title: Haptic-Visual Motion Sickness Mitigation
suggestions.desc: Research the effectiveness of 'vignetting' (narrowing the field of view) versus 'static rest frames' (adding a cockpit overlay) during locomotion. Implement both in Unity and conduct a user study measuring Nausea Scores to determine the best comfort settings for new VR users.

suggestions.title: Decoupled Head-Body Walking System
suggestions.desc: Develop a locomotion system that uses a waist-mounted smartphone (as a secondary IMU) to determine walking direction. This allows the user to look around freely with the VR headset while their physical walking direction is controlled by their hip orientation, increasing immersion.

suggestions.title: Redirected Walking in Finite Spaces
suggestions.desc: Create a 'Redirected Walking' algorithm that subtly rotates the virtual world when the user turns their head. The goal is to trick the user into walking in a circle in real life while they feel they are walking in a straight line through an infinite virtual hallway.

suggestions.title: Biometric-Adaptive Locomotion Speed
suggestions.desc: Connect a smartwatch (via Bluetooth/OSC) to the VR simulation to monitor the user's heart rate. If the heart rate spikes (indicating motion sickness or stress), the system automatically slows down locomotion speed or increases the comfort vignette dynamically.

title: Unmanned Security Drones
authors: Tyrone Marshall, Jayson Swartz, Justin Comer, Tong Chen, Janelle Bright, Micah Veale
abstract: The Primary objective of this research is to the use of small drones for personal security uses. We will create a prototype GUI that is connected to a Bebop 2 drone. Features will include are the ability to take a picture though the drone, see live feedback to the drone, and make the drone turn around. In addition to the app, we’ll determine how effective a security drone will be, and we will send a survey to the public to see if the public will want to buy a security drone

suggestions.title: Facial Recognition Perimeter Guard
suggestions.desc: Integrate the OpenCV library with the Bebop 2's live stream to implement an automated 'Whitelisting' feature. The drone could be programmed to ignore residents but send an immediate push notification to the GUI if an unrecognized face is detected near the door.

suggestions.title: Autonomous Scheduled Security Patrols
suggestions.desc: Use the MAVLink protocol to program a 'Patrol Path' for the drone around a property. Build a feature where the drone takes off every hour, follows 3D waypoints, records video, and autonomously returns to its charging pad using ArduPilot-like logic.

suggestions.title: Acoustic-Triggered Interception System
suggestions.desc: Connect the drone GUI to a network of IoT microphones (like Raspberry Pis with sound sensors). When a 'glass break' or 'loud shout' is detected, the drone automatically takes off and navigates to the GPS coordinate of the sound sensor to provide a live video feed.

suggestions.title: Encrypted Telemetry and Anti-Hijack Protocol
suggestions.desc: Implement AES-256 encryption for the control signals and video stream between the Bebop 2 and the GUI. This research project would focus on preventing 'Man-in-the-Middle' attacks where an intruder could otherwise take control of the security drone.

title: Analysis of Brain Control Drones
authors: Adnan Rashied, Jason Walters, Cheyenne Sancho, Josh Cooper, Ahmad Alissa, Eric Rawls & Kade Randall
abstract: The main objective of this study is to find efficient ways to utilize brain control headsets in conjunction with unmanned aerial drones. This study will research how effective the EPOC+ is by challenging users of different genders and ages to complete tasks using mental commands or facial expressions to control a Parrot AR-Drone 2.0.

suggestions.title: Artifact Removal using Independent Component Analysis (ICA)
suggestions.desc: Improve the EPOC+ control precision by implementing an ICA filtering layer to remove EOG (eye blink) and EMG (muscle twitch) artifacts from the EEG signal. This ensures the drone is controlled by actual 'mental effort' rather than accidental facial movements.

suggestions.title: P300-Based 'Point-and-Fly' Interface
suggestions.desc: Build a visual interface where different objects in the room (e.g., 'Door', 'Chair') flash on a screen. By detecting the P300 Event-Related Potential in the user's brainwaves when their target flashes, the drone can autonomously fly to that specific location.

suggestions.title: Hybrid BCI and Eye-Tracking Fusion
suggestions.desc: Combine the EPOC+ headset with a Tobii eye-tracker. In this setup, the user's gaze determines the 2D direction of flight, while the EEG 'Mental Command' (e.g., 'Lift') is used solely to control the altitude or to confirm an action, reducing the cognitive load on the user.

suggestions.title: Gamified BCI Flight Simulator for Rehabilitation
suggestions.desc: Develop a Unity-based training game for people with motor impairments. Users must navigate a virtual Parrot drone through hoops using only the EPOC+ commands; the system would gradually increase the 'sensitivity' of the EEG commands as the user improves their mental focus.

title: Food For Thought
authors: Mizzani Walker-Holmes, Jihwan Oh, Miriam Chapellka, Dorris Scott
abstract: This project explores public opinion on the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) in news and social media outlets, and tracks elected representatives’ voting records on issues relating to SNAP and food insecurity. We used machine learning, sentiment analysis, and text mining to analyze national and state level coverage of SNAP in order to gauge perceptions of the program over time across these outlets. Preliminary results indicate that the majority of news coverage is negative, more partisan news outlets have more extreme sentiment, and that clustering of negative reporting on SNAP occurs the South. Our final results and tools will be displayed in an on-line application that the…

suggestions.title: Temporal Correlation of News and Legislation
suggestions.desc: Use the ProPublica Congress API to correlate legislative voting dates with spikes in negative sentiment. This would determine if negative news cycles act as a leading indicator for restrictive SNAP policy changes or if the news is reacting to the legislation itself.

suggestions.title: Interactive Geographic Sentiment Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Create a Dash/Plotly web application that maps SNAP sentiment by state and county. By scraping localized Twitter data and local news RSS feeds, the tool could identify 'food insecurity awareness deserts' where public discourse is significantly lower than the actual need.

suggestions.title: Aspect-Based Sentiment Analysis (ABSA)
suggestions.desc: Fine-tune a BERT-based model to perform aspect-based analysis, moving beyond 'positive/negative' to specific themes like 'fraud,' 'eligibility,' or 'funding.' This would reveal which specific parts of the program are driving the 'majority negative' news coverage identified in the abstract.

suggestions.title: Cross-Platform Sentiment Gap Analysis
suggestions.desc: Compare the sentiment of mainstream news (using NewsAPI) against peer-to-peer discussions on Reddit (r/povertyfinance). This project would highlight the gap between how journalists report on SNAP versus the actual 'lived experience' sentiment of the program's beneficiaries.

title: Guidelines to avoid analyst mistakes
authors: Ruth Petit - Bois
abstract: In a previous study involving student analysts conducting requirements elicitation interviews, several common mistakes were identified and catalogued after a thorough analysis was made by the study’s contributors. The analysis revealed that there were 9 major points of contention that prevented the student analysists from resolving all ambiguities from their requirements gathering interview. The issues are as follows: (1) Wrong Opening, (2) Ambiguity Not Leveraged, (3) Implicit Goals, (4) Implicit Stakeholders, (5) Limitation in Terms of Resources Not Considered, (6) Non- Functional Requirements Not Considered, (7) Interrogation-like Meetings, (8) Problems Phrasing Questions, & (9) Wrong Clo

suggestions.title: Generative AI Interview Simulator
suggestions.desc: Build a web-based training tool using the GPT-4 API where the AI acts as a 'difficult stakeholder.' The AI should be prompted to use the 9 identified 'mistake-triggering' behaviors (like being ambiguous), requiring the student to use the guidelines to pass the simulation.

suggestions.title: Real-time Requirements 'Linter' for Zoom
suggestions.desc: Create a plugin that uses speech-to-text to monitor live elicitation interviews. It would use NLP to flag in real-time when an 'Implicit Goal' or 'Ambiguity' occurs, nudging the analyst to ask a follow-up question based on the 9 major points of contention.

suggestions.title: Automated Knowledge Graph Extraction
suggestions.desc: Develop a tool that parses interview transcripts and maps them into a Neo4j Knowledge Graph. The graph would visually highlight 'Implicit Relationships' (Point 9) that were mentioned but not explicitly defined, helping analysts see gaps in their requirements gathering.

suggestions.title: Quantitative Validation of the 9-Point Checklist
suggestions.desc: Perform a controlled study where one group of students is given the 'Guideline Checklist' and another is not. Use a standard 'Requirement Completeness Score' to measure the final SRS documents, providing empirical proof that following these guidelines reduces software bugs.

title: OSINT sentiment and risk analysis
authors: Alexander Dushane (SWE), James Luttrell (BSSWE), Chandler Brown (BSSWE), Thomas James (BSSWE), Marco Alfaro, (BSSWE), Brett Bouthilette (BSSWE)
abstract: This project is a proof of concept for analyzing sentiment and work history of a given companies employees to help determine potential security risks.

suggestions.title: Procedural Ruin Generation System
suggestions.desc: Develop a C++ script for Unreal Engine that procedurally generates the 'forgotten world' ruins to ensure no two playthroughs are the same. Use Houdini Engine or Perlin noise to create crumbling architecture that remains navigable for third-person combat.

suggestions.title: Haptic Feedback Integration for VR Combat
suggestions.desc: Enhance the combat immersion by integrating the bHaptics API or OpenXR haptic standards. Map enemy strikes and magical will-based attacks to specific vibration patterns on a haptic vest or VR controllers.

suggestions.title: AI-Driven Dynamic Difficulty Adjustment
suggestions.desc: Implement a machine learning agent using Unity ML-Agents that monitors player health and puzzle-solving speed. The system should adjust enemy aggressiveness in the forgotten world to maintain a 'flow state' for the player.

suggestions.title: Forgotten Will Multiplayer Co-op Expansion
suggestions.desc: Refactor the combat system to support two-player peer-to-peer networking. Design 'Will-Link' puzzles that require both players to use their abilities simultaneously to unlock specific areas of the mystery.

title: Social Market Economy Index Website
authors: Drew Cofer (BAS Information Technology), Tyler Cox (BS Information Technology), Jerome Streete (BAS Information Technology), Josh King (BS Information Technology), Johnathan Salter (BS Information Technology)
abstract: For this project we were tasked with creating a website that was able to display the Social Market Index Economy data. We were given 15 different data points for 216 countries and tasked to have that information display on a world heatmap. The heatmap will show the name of the country and the datapoint for the variable you are viewing. If you choose to see more information about the country you are able to to click on the country in the map and it will redirect. Alternatively, you can also select the country from the dropdown menu which will redirect to the country information page. On the country information page you are able to select a country from the dropdown menu and it will bring up m

suggestions.title: Spatial Audio Reactive Neon Environments
suggestions.desc: Use the FMOD audio engine to make the neon lights pulse and change colors in sync with the game’s soundtrack and weapon fire. This technical layer deepens the arcade aesthetic while providing visual cues for incoming enemy waves.

suggestions.title: Cross-Platform VR-Mobile Spectator App
suggestions.desc: Build a companion app in Flutter where non-VR players can act as 'Commanders.' Mobile users can drop power-ups or spawn extra enemies into the VR arena in real-time using a Socket.io connection.

suggestions.title: Neon Arcade Global Leaderboard and API
suggestions.desc: Develop a backend using Node.js and MongoDB to track high scores, accuracy, and kill streaks. Create an in-game VR 'Hall of Fame' where players can see 3D holograms of the current top-ranked avatars.

suggestions.title: Latency Compensation for High-Speed VR Projectiles
suggestions.desc: Implement a client-side prediction and server reconciliation system specifically for the fast-paced shoot-em-up mechanics. This ensures that in a multiplayer VR environment, neon projectiles feel responsive even with 100ms+ latency.

title: Roommates 40 Plus website
authors: Stephanie Gray (BSIT), Ashly Rumery (BSIT), Jacob Christensen, (BSIT) , Julia Huth, (BSIT)
abstract: Many people in their 40s and older find themselves needing to share the cost of living due to losing their jobs or being laid off. Many times, these people need to share living expenses and one way to do that is to find a roommate. Roommates40Plus.comis a website that allows people to submit forms based on if they're looking for a roommate or renting a room with people needing a place to live. Learn More

suggestions.title: Dynamic Color-Blindness Shader Library
suggestions.desc: Create a suite of post-processing shaders that simulate Protanopia, Deuteranopia, and Tritanopia for the non-colorblind player. This forces the cooperative partners to communicate more effectively to solve puzzles that the other literally cannot see.

suggestions.title: Accessibility-First Level Design Tool
suggestions.desc: Build an editor extension in Unity that automatically checks level geometry for 'color-reliance' issues. The tool should flag areas where a puzzle is impossible to solve without specific color perception and suggest secondary cues like shapes or patterns.

suggestions.title: Biometric Stress Analysis in Co-op Play
suggestions.desc: Integrate heart rate monitor data to track player frustration levels during difficult color-based puzzles. Use this data to research how accessibility constraints impact cooperative communication and team bonding.

suggestions.title: Roses are Red: Mobile AR Expansion
suggestions.desc: Port the puzzle mechanics to an Augmented Reality (AR) setting using ARCore. Players must move around a physical room to align 'color beams' visible only through their phone screens to solve spatial puzzles.

title: Hydrosyl - Your Smart Water Bottle
authors: Ganama Hawaou (BSCS), Jordan Simo Kaptue (BSCS), Victor Youdom Kemmoe (BSCS) , Kyle Manthe (BSCS) , Marlon Jones (BSCS)
abstract: Propose in a creative way, to the human being a way to control and have a better view of his/her water intake by implementing a SMART WATER BOTTLE linked to a mobile app. Learn More

suggestions.title: Physics-Based Predictive Catching System
suggestions.desc: Develop a sophisticated collision detection system that allows players to 'parry' or 'catch' dodgeballs based on momentum vectors. Use Unity's PhysX engine to calculate projectile trajectory and grant rewards for 'perfect timing' catches.

suggestions.title: Punk Aesthetic Character Customization Engine
suggestions.desc: Build a modular cosmetic system using vertex painting and layered materials. Allow players to design their own 'punk' gear, where textures and spikes react dynamically to the character's movement and ball impacts.

suggestions.title: Reinforcement Learning for Dodgeball Bots
suggestions.desc: Train AI opponents using Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) to learn dodging and flanking tactics. The goal is to create bots that can pass the ball to each other and coordinate 'pincer' moves against human players.

suggestions.title: Ball PunKS Tournament Broadcasting Suite
suggestions.desc: Create a dedicated 'Director Mode' for streamers that includes cinematic cameras, real-time 'heat maps' of ball travel, and automated replay generation for 'best knockouts' using the game's physics data.

title: Murta: A Modern RPG
authors: Johnny Barnes (BSCGDD), Timon Wood (BSCGDD), Chuka Amaeze (BSCGDD)
abstract: We wanted to create a role-playing game set in modern times. Typical RPGs are fantasy, so we wanted to explore the possibility of blending an atypical setting to a beloved genre. Learn More

suggestions.title: Modular Space Colony Destruction System
suggestions.desc: Implement a mesh-slicing or pre-fractured destruction system for the sci-fi environment. Players should be able to destroy cover or breach walls within the colony to create new tactical pathways during combat.

suggestions.title: Zero-G Combat Mechanics Integration
suggestions.desc: Expand the sci-fi setting by adding low-gravity zones where combat physics transition from traditional 3D movement to 6DOF (Six Degrees of Freedom). Use thruster-based movement and recoil-driven navigation.

suggestions.title: Vesta Faction Narrative Engine
suggestions.desc: Build a branching dialogue and reputation system using Ink or Yarn Spinner. Player choices during the 'Absolution' storyline should determine which sci-fi factions help or hinder them in later combat stages.

suggestions.title: Neural Network-Based Enemy Stealth AI
suggestions.desc: Develop enemies that adapt to the player’s combat style using a simple neural network. If a player relies on long-range sniping, the AI should learn to utilize the space colony’s vents and corridors for flanking.

title: War is Hell
authors: Konrad Drapela (BSCGDD)
abstract: Capstone project game.

suggestions.title: AR Security Overlay for Mobile Operatives
suggestions.desc: Upgrade the mobile component to use Augmented Reality (AR). The mobile player could 'scan' physical QR codes in their room to reveal digital blueprints or hackable nodes that help the VR player navigate the virtual facility.

suggestions.title: Asymmetric Encrypted Communication Channel
suggestions.desc: Develop an in-game 'Hacker Chat' using WebSockets with end-to-end encryption. The mobile player must 'decode' messages from the VR player to bypass firewalls, mimicking real-world cybersecurity protocols.

suggestions.title: Procedural Stealth Facility Architect
suggestions.desc: Create an algorithm that generates unique infiltration maps for every session. The generator must ensure that every 'hackable' mobile node corresponds to a physical obstacle or security gate in the VR player's view.

suggestions.title: Hacktical Advantage: 4v1 Breach Mode
suggestions.desc: Scale the multiplayer to support one VR 'Infiltrator' and four mobile 'Hackers.' Each mobile player is assigned a specific role (Surveillance, Power, Decryption, distractions) to coordinate a massive facility breach.

title: Environment Art
authors: Timon Wood (BSCGDD)
abstract: Portfolio Learn More

suggestions.title: Color-Mixing Elemental Magic System
suggestions.desc: Develop a logic engine where players can combine primary 'color' spells (Red + Blue = Purple) to create hybrid effects. For example, mixing fire (red) and water (blue) magic creates a steam-based 'Choromage' AOE attack.

suggestions.title: Isometric Pathfinding for Dynamic Environments
suggestions.desc: Implement an A* pathfinding system that handles a changing open world where the player can 'paint' new paths or block enemies using color magic. The environment should react to the player's 'color footprint' by blooming or decaying.

suggestions.title: Choromage: Open World Creature Breeding
suggestions.desc: Add a system where fantasy creatures in the open world have 'color-coded' DNA. Players can use magic to influence the evolution of these creatures, breeding them for specific mounts or combat pets based on their hue.

suggestions.title: Global Lighting and Color Shifting Engine
suggestions.desc: Research and implement a global illumination system where the world's time-of-day affects magic potency. Sunset (orange/red) could buff certain spells while debuffing others, requiring strategic exploration.

title: Shard Infinite - Capstone Game
authors: Alex Sala (CGDD)
abstract: Shard Infinite is a procedurally generated adventure game which utilizes custom visual, audio, and encounter generation systems to create unique experiences for players. Constructed in the Unity game engine, Shard Infinite represents an exploration of how procedural content can be used beyond the bounds of simply changing the location in which play occurs. Learn More

suggestions.title: Suction-Based Physics Combat Mechanics
suggestions.desc: Transform the racing game into a combat racer by allowing Roumbas to suck up debris from the track and fire it as projectiles. Use Unity's physics engine to calculate mass-based damage and suction force.

suggestions.title: Global Online Matchmaking and Ranking
suggestions.desc: Integrate Steamworks or Epic Online Services to transition from local party play to global multiplayer. Develop a 'Dusty League' ranking system where players earn cosmetic vacuum attachments for winning races.

suggestions.title: Smart-Home Hazard Map Generator
suggestions.desc: Build a level editor that uses computer vision to turn photos of real rooms into racing tracks. Players can upload a photo of their living room, and the game generates a 'Rumblin' Roumbas' map with furniture as obstacles.

suggestions.title: Reinforcement Learning for 'Perfect Path' Cleaners
suggestions.desc: Train AI racers using ML-Agents to find the most efficient cleaning path while racing. Research how these 'optimal cleaning' algorithms can be used to challenge human players in a 'high-score cleaning' game mode.

title: Choromage
authors: Steffen Lim (CGDD), Anne McCranie (CGDD), Dylan Gillespie (CGDD)
abstract: A story-based open-world adventure RPG with moral ambiguity. Learn More

suggestions.title: Browser-Based ECL Code Playground
suggestions.desc: Develop an interactive code editor using Monaco (the VS Code engine) that allows users to write and test HPCC Systems' ECL (Enterprise Control Language) code directly on the website. Use a backend container to compile and return results.

suggestions.title: HPCC Cluster Performance Visualizer
suggestions.desc: Create a D3.js data visualization tool that shows how big data is partitioned across a Thor or Roxy cluster. This helps students visualize the 'high-performance' aspect of LexisNexis technology beyond just writing code.

suggestions.title: Personalized ECL Learning Path API
suggestions.desc: Build a recommendation engine that analyzes a user's quiz performance and suggests specific HPCC tutorial modules. Use a 'knowledge graph' to map prerequisite skills for complex big data tasks.

suggestions.title: Gamified HPCC Certification Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Implement a full user authentication and badging system where LexisNexis employees can earn 'ECL Master' certifications. Include a leaderboard for completing coding challenges and a forum for peer-to-peer mentoring.

title: Project Loot RPG
authors: Taylor Adams (BSCGDD)
abstract: Project Loot RPG is a mobile role-playing game that focuses on gathering loot such as hats and weapons. The game has an in-depth system that allows players to swap the materials their weapons and hats are made from, as well as combine them to create more powerful equipment.

suggestions.title: AI-Driven Compatibility Scoring
suggestions.desc: Replace basic filtering with a machine learning recommendation engine using Scikit-learn to analyze user lifestyle habits (sleep patterns, cleanliness, guest frequency) and generate a 'compatibility percentage' for potential matches.

suggestions.title: Trust-Verified Identity Vault
suggestions.desc: Implement a secure identity verification layer using the Stripe Identity API or Onfido to conduct background checks and ID verification, specifically catering to the safety concerns of the 40+ demographic.

suggestions.title: Senior-Centric Accessibility Audit
suggestions.desc: Conduct a formal UX research study focusing on WCAG 2.1 AAA compliance and implement specialized UI components for older adults, such as high-contrast modes, dynamic font scaling, and voice-assisted navigation.

suggestions.title: Community-Based Resource Exchange
suggestions.desc: Extend the platform to include a 'Skill & Tool Sharing' module where roommates can list shared resources or chores, using a React-based dashboard to manage household agreements and communal expenses.

title: Vesta: Absolution
authors: Christopher Budden (BSCGDD), Brian Patterson (BSCGDD), Austin Huffman (BSCGDD)
abstract: 3D action game that evokes the charm and thrill of classic arcade games

suggestions.title: Pathology Simulation Module
suggestions.desc: Develop 'Disease Scenarios' where the user can witness the effects of Sickle Cell Anemia or Malaria on red blood cells first-hand. Use Unity's particle system to visualize how different pathogens interact with the bloodstream environment.

suggestions.title: Multiplayer Bio-Defense Minigame
suggestions.desc: Add a cooperative multiplayer layer using Unity Netcode where users play as white blood cells defending against an infection. This turns the educational experience into a high-stakes 'tower defense' game inside the body.

suggestions.title: Physically-Based Fluid Dynamics Integration
suggestions.desc: Replace basic animations with a Compute Shader-based fluid simulation to create realistic blood flow patterns around arterial bends and obstructions. This would involve implementing a simplified Navier-Stokes solver for real-time performance.

suggestions.title: Biometric Syncing for Immersive Biofeedback
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Polar H10 or Apple Watch API to sync the VR world’s pulse and flow speed with the user’s real-time heart rate. This creates a powerful biofeedback tool for teaching students about the relationship between activity and circulatory speed.

title: Automated Logic Translation Tool
authors: William Gambrell (BSSWE), Emad Vaid (BSSWE), Benjamin Buzeta (BSSWE), Bobby Rattanaxay (BSSWE), Halima Diallo (BSSWE)
abstract: Automated Logic Tool

suggestions.title: Layer 2 Gas Optimization
suggestions.desc: Migrate the smart contract infrastructure to a Layer 2 solution like Polygon or Arbitrum to reduce transaction costs for room check-ins, making the system financially viable for high-turnover hotels.

suggestions.title: NFT-Based Loyalty Rewards
suggestions.desc: Transform room keys into dynamic NFTs (ERC-721) that evolve based on stay frequency, granting holders automated access to VIP lounges or late check-out perks via smart contract triggers.

suggestions.title: Timed Access for Coworking Spaces
suggestions.desc: Adapt the NFC-blockchain system for flexible office rentals by implementing 'Time-Locked' smart contracts that grant access only during specific hourly windows booked via a web interface.

suggestions.title: Multi-Sig Emergency Override
suggestions.desc: Develop a decentralized security protocol using multi-signature wallets that allows hotel staff to regain room access in emergencies without compromising the privacy and immutability of the guest's digital key.

title: IoT - Melt Pump Work-Order System
authors: Shamita Hattangady (BSSWE), Aisha Siddiqui (BSSWE), Mark Zeagler (BSSWE), Michael Bowman (BSSWE), Drashtee Parmar (BSSWE)
abstract: Shaw Industries has a need to further their strategy and implementation of collecting IoT produced data. There is an assorted array of systems where IoT data is captured but it is not aggregated in a way that allows all interested parties to examine the data (data democratization). There are four melt pumps in Plant 15's extrusion department and two extrusion backing lines with two different applicators on each line. These melt pumps are critical to the lines because if they are unable to keep pace, the line must be slowed down to avoid a negative impact on quality.

suggestions.title: Cross-Vendor GPU Portability
suggestions.desc: Port the existing CUDA kernels to Vulkan or OpenCL to enable high-performance sequence analysis on non-NVIDIA hardware, such as AMD GPUs and Intel Arc integrated graphics.

suggestions.title: Tensor Core-Accelerated Smith-Waterman
suggestions.desc: Optimize the Smith-Waterman alignment algorithm specifically for NVIDIA Tensor Cores to achieve 10x throughput in local sequence alignment tasks compared to standard CUDA cores.

suggestions.title: Nextflow Pipeline Integration
suggestions.desc: Wrap the CUDA-accelerated tools into a containerized Nextflow or Snakemake workflow, allowing researchers to deploy GPU-heavy analysis across cloud-based HPC clusters like AWS Batch.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Genomic Visualization
suggestions.desc: Build a React-based frontend that uses WebGL to visualize the output of the CUDA analysis in real-time, allowing bioinformaticians to interactively explore DNA sequence matches as they are processed.

title: Lighthouse - Personal Safety Hub
authors: Joseph Chamberlain (BSSWE), Giuseppe Scoppino (BSSWE), William Silloway (BSSWE), Stephens Jean-Jacques (BSSWE), Daniel Pratt (BSSWE)
abstract: Lighthouse is an application designed to provide a greater sense of safety and security for yourself and those close to you. It strives to innovate on the way in which we approach personal safety and accountability. This was accomplished through the development of a Hub that allows users to check-in and also create a check-in schedule. A user provides one or more contacts who, in the case of missed check-in or an emergency, will be notified regarding the user's status. Integrations with the iOS application and Google Home enable users to interface with the Hub seamlessly and quickly create check-in schedules, receive notifications regarding upcoming check-ins, and check-in. The product helps
supervisor: Dr. Reza Parizi, Prof. Rachel Foster

suggestions.title: Temporal Flow Consistency
suggestions.desc: Integrate Optical Flow algorithms (like Farneback) to ensure that the 'Magic Eraser' mask remains stable across frames, eliminating the flickering effect common in frame-by-frame AI object removal.

suggestions.title: Privacy-First Dashcam Redactor
suggestions.desc: Repurpose the Mask R-CNN pipeline to automatically detect and blur faces and license plates in public dashcam footage, creating a tool for journalists and privacy-conscious content creators.

suggestions.title: Generative Inpainting Upgrade
suggestions.desc: Replace simple pixel-filling with a Stable Diffusion-based Inpainting model (using ControlNet) to realistically reconstruct complex backgrounds behind the removed objects.

suggestions.title: Browser-Based Inference via TensorFlow.js
suggestions.desc: Optimize the model for client-side execution using TensorFlow.js and WebGL, allowing users to perform video object removal directly in the browser without uploading heavy files to a server.

title: Blackfire (Capstone Game)
authors: Keiler Schroeder (CGDD), Jetta Koves (CGDD), Ryan Kessler (CGDD)
abstract: Blackfire is a turn-based strategy game where two players vie for control of the Orb of Blackfire. Each player has a team of three unique characters that each have different strengths and abilities. The players must use strategic positioning and the unique characteristics of their units to gain control of the Orb and escape with it. Learn More

suggestions.title: FHIR Standard Interoperability
suggestions.desc: Develop a module that implements the HL7 FHIR (Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources) API, allowing this OpenMRS instance to exchange patient data with modern mobile health apps and Apple HealthKit.

suggestions.title: Low-Connectivity Offline Sync
suggestions.desc: Build a Progressive Web App (PWA) using PouchDB that allows clinicians in rural areas to record patient data offline and sync it with the OpenMRS MySQL backend once a connection is re-established.

suggestions.title: BioBERT-Powered Clinical Note NLP
suggestions.desc: Integrate a BioBERT model to automatically scan free-text clinical notes in OpenMRS and suggest ICD-10 diagnostic codes, reducing the manual administrative burden on healthcare providers.

suggestions.title: Embedded Telemedicine Portal
suggestions.desc: Integrate a WebRTC-based video conferencing module directly into the OpenMRS dashboard, enabling doctors to conduct remote consultations while simultaneously viewing the patient's electronic health record.

title: Automatic Project Scheduling
authors: Hunter Allen (BSIT), Jae Park (BSIT), Louis Cheng (BSIT), Jim Nguyen (BSIT), Alejandro Lopez(BSIT), Jay Norris (BSIT)
abstract: Python GUI program that takes an Excel Spreadsheet as an argument and returns an Output Excel Spreadsheet. The program uses the Munkres python module to implement the Hungarian Algorithm that takes a matrix as an input and returns a list of index pairs.

suggestions.title: Edge Intelligence with Greengrass
suggestions.desc: Deploy AWS IoT Greengrass to the weather station to enable local data processing and automated emergency alerts (like flood warnings) even during periods of internet connectivity loss.

suggestions.title: Precision Agriculture Extension
suggestions.desc: Add soil moisture and PH sensors to the hardware stack and use AWS SageMaker to build a predictive irrigation model that provides farmers with daily water-usage recommendations.

suggestions.title: Digital Twin Visualizer
suggestions.desc: Use AWS IoT TwinMaker to create a 3D digital twin of the sensor environment, allowing users to visualize thermal gradients and wind patterns in a virtualized replica of the physical location.

suggestions.title: LoRaWAN Forest Fire Detection
suggestions.desc: Transition the communication layer to LoRaWAN using AWS IoT Core for LoRaWAN, extending the station's range to miles for low-power, long-range environmental monitoring in remote forests.

title: Quantum Computing Website
authors: Jessica Brummel (BSCS), Zach Dillard (BSCS) Ian Galler (BAACS), John Jacobs (BSCS), James Vice (BSCS)
abstract: development of a website to present an understanding of quantum computing and its emerging field of study; with demonstration and walkthrough of Qutip quantum computing simulator. Learn More

suggestions.title: Automated CI/CD AppImage Pipeline
suggestions.desc: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that automatically triggers a rebuild and test of the AppImages whenever the upstream bioinformatics tools (like BLAST or ClustalW) release an update.

suggestions.title: Virtualization Overhead Benchmarking
suggestions.desc: Conduct a performance study comparing these AppImages against Docker containers and native installations to quantify the I/O and CPU overhead for large-scale genomic sequence processing.

suggestions.title: Centralized 'Bio-Store' Portal
suggestions.desc: Build a web-based repository using a custom API that catalogs these AppImages, providing users with one-click downloads, version history, and user-submitted compatibility ratings for different Linux kernels.

suggestions.title: Immutable NixOS Lab Environment
suggestions.desc: Develop a NixOS configuration that uses these AppImages to create a 'Disposable Lab Environment' that can be instantly reproduced on any machine, ensuring consistent research results across different labs.

title: Deep Learning License Plate Reader
authors: Armando Mercado (BSCS), Carlos Lopez (BSCS), Daylon Janis (BSCS), Georgi Valkov (BSCS), Rob Douma (BSCS)
abstract: A real time license plate reader using Machine Learning thorough TensorFlow to read license plates from a video feed and look up the result in a database for make, model, and parking information. Learn More

suggestions.title: Multi-Band Neural Analytics
suggestions.desc: Expand the EEG processing to analyze Alpha, Beta, and Theta wave ratios in real-time, providing users with more granular insights into 'Deep Focus' vs. 'Calmness' using the Muse SDK.

suggestions.title: EEG-Controlled Unity Game
suggestions.desc: Integrate the web app with a Unity-based WebGL game where the player’s character moves or gains power based on the 'Attention' score detected by the EEG headband.

suggestions.title: Longitudinal Lifestyle Correlation
suggestions.desc: Implement a data logging feature that uses AWS Timestream to correlate EEG focus scores with user-logged variables like sleep hours and caffeine intake to identify personal focus triggers.

suggestions.title: Therapist Remote Monitoring Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Build a HIPAA-compliant portal for clinicians to view patient focus trends over time, allowing them to adjust ADHD treatment plans based on objective neurofeedback data.

title: Hand Gesture Recognition for VR
authors: Andrew Savas (BSCS, BSCGDD), Garrett Eddy (BSCS, BSCGDD)
abstract: We give a summary of how we can recognize hand postures using Leap Motion and give examples on how this hand recognition increases the number of interactions possible in VR Environments. A demo is available to interact with where the user can test the hand posture recognition to see the speed/accuracy we can determine the hand posture as well as seeing some possible interactions.

suggestions.title: VisionOS Spatial Vision Board
suggestions.desc: Port the iOS application to VisionOS for Apple Vision Pro, allowing users to walk through an immersive 3D gallery of their goals and interact with vision board elements using hand gestures.

suggestions.title: Generative AI Image Synthesis
suggestions.desc: Integrate the DALL-E 3 API so users can use Siri to describe a goal (e.g., 'Siri, add a picture of a solar-powered cabin in the mountains') and have the image generated and placed on the board automatically.

suggestions.title: Semantic Goal Clustering
suggestions.desc: Use Apple’s Natural Language framework to analyze the text on vision boards and automatically cluster them into life categories like 'Financial,' 'Wellness,' or 'Career' for better organization.

suggestions.title: Affiliate-Linked Action Items
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Pinterest API or Amazon Associates API to automatically find physical products that match the images on a user's vision board, turning visualization into a direct shopping experience.

title: Cardian - the Car Security System
authors: An Vu (BSCS), Roger Slattery (BSCS), Tori McCullah (BSCS) , Micah Veale (BSCS), Jade Corn (BSCS)
abstract: Cardian is a vehicle monitoring and notification system. This system sends push notifications with surrounding photos when activity is detected at a user's car. The system's design is to provide vehicle owners with a better sense of safety for their property. Learn More

suggestions.title: Universal Plugin Architecture for Music APIs
suggestions.desc: Refactor the backend using the Adapter design pattern to create a standardized interface for music services. This would allow the community to easily add support for Tidal, YouTube Music, and Deezer by simply implementing a 'Provider' class for each service's API.

suggestions.title: Cross-Platform Collaborative Syncing
suggestions.desc: Implement a 'Virtual Playlist' system where two users on different platforms (one Spotify, one Apple Music) can contribute to a single shared list. The app would use WebHooks to detect changes on one platform and automatically mirror the update to the other in real-time.

suggestions.title: Acoustic Fingerprinting for Missing Tracks
suggestions.desc: Integrate the AcoustID or Librosa library to handle tracks that fail metadata matching (title/artist). If a song isn't found by name, the app can analyze the audio characteristics to find the exact match on the target service, even if the metadata is formatted differently.

suggestions.title: Streaming Library Availability Analytics
suggestions.desc: Build a data analysis dashboard that tracks and visualizes 'transfer success rates' across platforms. This would provide insights into which genres or record labels are most frequently missing from certain services, offering valuable data on licensing gaps in the industry.

title: Chematomic
authors: Colton Trau (BSCGDD)
abstract: Chematomic is an Educational Action game where characters have a lot of chemistry. You play as chemist nanobots Adam and Mike on a subatomic adventure to save Utopian City from a threat too small for us to handle. Learn More

suggestions.title: Multimodal Acoustic Emotion Detection
suggestions.desc: Enhance the text-based ML by adding audio analysis using Librosa to detect emotional cues like pitch, jitter, and intensity from live requirements engineering interview recordings.

suggestions.title: Stakeholder Conflict Scoring
suggestions.desc: Develop an algorithm that identifies 'emotional dissonance'—flagging requirements where two different stakeholders express contradictory emotional sentiments (e.g., one is excited, one is frustrated).

suggestions.title: Interviewer Empathy Coach
suggestions.desc: Build a real-time dashboard that provides feedback to the requirements engineer during the interview, suggesting when to ask 'empathy-driven' follow-up questions based on the stakeholder's emotional state.

suggestions.title: Sentiment-Aware Jira Plugin
suggestions.desc: Create a Jira plugin that sentiment-analyzes user stories and comments to flag 'Toxic Requirements' or 'Vague User Feedback' that might lead to project scope creep or team friction.

title: Dig Royale - UE4 Steam Multiplayer
authors: Christian Murphy (CGDD)
abstract: Dig Royale is a online multiplayer game made entirely by BluePrints (visual coding) in Unreal Engine 4. It works with a peer to peer network through the UE4 Steam plugin. Learn More

suggestions.title: Predictive Brewing and Maintenance
suggestions.desc: Apply Scikit-learn to the temperature and weight data to predict when the heating element is likely to fail or when the pot requires descaling. Use the historical data to optimize the 'Keep Warm' cycle for energy efficiency.

suggestions.title: Automated Inventory and Supply Chain Integration
suggestions.desc: Add an RFID reader or a barcode scanner to the Raspberry Pi to track coffee bag usage. Use the Amazon Dash Replenishment API or a custom webhook to automatically reorder beans when the load cell detects the inventory is low.

suggestions.title: Office Productivity Suite Integration
suggestions.desc: Build a Slack and Microsoft Teams bot that allows employees to 'subscribe' to specific roasts and receive a direct message when a fresh pot is brewed. Allow remote brewing triggers via authenticated chat commands.

suggestions.title: Water Quality and TDS Monitoring
suggestions.desc: Integrate a Total Dissolved Solids (TDS) sensor to analyze water hardness and its effect on extraction quality. Build a data visualization dashboard showing the correlation between water purity and the 'Perfect Cup' rating as logged by users.

title: The CakeBook
authors: Nathanael Curtis (SWE), Kim Hertz (SWE), Victoria Williams (SWE), Thomas Glover (SWE), Danielle Brooks (SWE)
abstract: How to improve the workflow of ordering bakery items at Gabriel's Bakery, a local business.

suggestions.title: FinBERT Sentiment Optimization
suggestions.desc: Replace general-purpose NLP models with HuggingFace’s FinBERT, specifically trained on financial communication. Fine-tune the model using the 'Crypto-Sentiment' dataset from Kaggle to better capture crypto-specific slang and market nuances.

suggestions.title: Multi-Exchange Arbitrage Bot
suggestions.desc: Integrate the sentiment signals with the CCXT library to monitor price discrepancies between Binance and Coinbase. Build a bot that executes trades when high-sentiment spikes coincide with price lags between exchanges.

suggestions.title: Multimodal Price Predictor
suggestions.desc: Combine the Twitter sentiment scores with technical indicators (RSI, MACD) and on-chain metrics like Whale Alerts using an Ensemble Learning approach. This reduces false positives caused by social media 'noise' that doesn't reflect actual market liquidity.

suggestions.title: NFT Collection Hype Tracker
suggestions.desc: Pivot the system to analyze Discord and OpenSea activity for specific NFT collections. Use the sentiment engine to predict 'floor price' movements based on community engagement levels rather than just historical sales.

title: Ninjattack
authors: Bradley Engwer (BSCGDD)
abstract: Ninjattack is an elegant hack 'n' slash game for mobile devices.

suggestions.title: Persistent Character Growth via NFC Rewriting
suggestions.desc: Implement NTAG215 write capabilities to store player stats and experience directly on the physical cards. This enables a 'carry-over' mechanic where a physical card effectively becomes a 'memory card' for a character, allowing it to level up across different game sessions.

suggestions.title: Mobile Companion App with CoreNFC
suggestions.desc: Develop an iOS/Android companion app using CoreNFC or Android NFC API that allows players to scan their Wildling Rumble cards on their personal phones. This removes the need for a dedicated hardware reader and allows for 'hidden' actions, like checking secret stats privately.

suggestions.title: Augmented Reality (AR) Unit Visualization
suggestions.desc: Integrate Vuforia or ARKit to recognize the physical NFC cards through a camera view. When a player scans a card, a 3D animated model of the character would appear on top of the physical card, bridging the gap between digital combat and the physical tabletop.

suggestions.title: Multi-Device Synchronous Tabletop State
suggestions.desc: Use WebSockets (Socket.io) to synchronize the game state across multiple tablets or laptops around a table. Scanning a card on one player’s device would instantly update the 'world view' on a central screen, creating a shared digital-physical play space.

title: IP Address Score Using Blockchain
authors: Andrew Evans (SWE), Zach Starnes (SWE), Cheyenne Sancho (SWE), Damonte Bowie (SWE), Peter Twene (SWE), Mirna Ouied (SWE)
abstract: Developed a Blockchain proof-of-concept on the IBM Cloud Platform using Hyperledger Fabric to demonstrate the modification, transfer, addition and deletion of assets.

suggestions.title: Dynamic Competitive Pricing Engine
suggestions.desc: Build a real-time price monitoring tool using Selenium to scrape Amazon and Costco daily. Implement a pricing algorithm that suggests the 'sweet spot' markup for smaller wholesalers to remain competitive while maintaining margins.

suggestions.title: Customer Segmentation Clustering
suggestions.desc: Apply K-Means or DBSCAN clustering to the purchase data to identify distinct buyer personas, such as 'Bulk Resellers' vs 'Individual Consumers.' Use these clusters to generate personalized inventory recommendations for different business types.

suggestions.title: Automated ETL Pipeline with Airflow
suggestions.desc: Transition the static data analysis to a live cloud pipeline using Apache Airflow. Automate the extraction of wholesale data into an AWS Redshift warehouse to provide a continuously updating dashboard instead of a one-time report.

suggestions.title: Supply Chain Vulnerability Map
suggestions.desc: Integrate geographical data to map out the distribution centers of both companies. Use this to analyze regional supply chain resilience, identifying areas where a localized disaster would disrupt Costco or Amazon deliveries more severely.

title: GTRI Communications Project
authors: Shaidi Grell (BASIT), Elijah Watson (BASIT), Solomon Bush (BSIT), Christopher Fox (BSIT)
abstract: As GTRI staff, we have been tasked with finding an alternative to Hipchat as a chat service. We have found likely candidates, narrowed our results down to three candidates, and ran in-depth tests on one likely replacement. Learn More

suggestions.title: Headless Browser Vulnerability Scan
suggestions.desc: Integrate Playwright or Selenium into the WASP core to scan modern Single Page Applications (React/Vue). This allows the tool to detect vulnerabilities in Client-Side Rendering that traditional static scanners often miss.

suggestions.title: DevSecOps CI/CD Integration
suggestions.desc: Develop a GitHub Action that triggers a WASP scan on every Pull Request. Set up a gateway policy that automatically blocks deployments if the scan detects any 'Critical' OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities.

suggestions.title: LLM-Powered Exploit Payload Generator
suggestions.desc: Use the OpenAI API to generate context-aware XSS and SQL injection payloads based on the specific HTML attributes found by the scanner. This moves the project from generic pattern matching to intelligent, adaptive penetration testing.

suggestions.title: Security Reporting as a Service
suggestions.desc: Build a web-based management portal where users can schedule scans and receive professional PDF security audits. Use a subscription-based model and integrate Stripe for a ready-to-market SaaS cybersecurity tool.

title: AI for Finance
authors: Kushum Thapa (BS Information Technology), Nathanael Leman (BS Information Technology), Mark Malaney (BS Information Technology) Isaac Efik, (BS Information Technology ), Mizzani Walker-Holmes, (BS Information Technology)
abstract: This project addresses concerns raised by a Sponsor regarding inefficiencies in managing CCSE capstone projects. Key pain points include organization, project management, and communication among stakeholders. The proposed solution involves implementing Customer Relationship Management (CRM) software. Upon gathering requirements from the Sponsor, including contact information capture, workflow management, document control, and customization capabilities, we evaluated several CRM platforms. A total of thirty-two CRMs were reviewed and Vtiger, OroCRM, and SuiteCRM were selected for testing. SuiteCRM was chosen for its comprehensive features and user-friendliness. The second step of the project involved testing SuiteCRM functionalities on a dedicated server, leading to its selection for deployment preparation. Final steps include documentation development, training material creation, and incorporating user feedback for optimal system configuration.

suggestions.title: Automated Capstone Lifecycle Middleware
suggestions.desc: Develop a middleware service using Node.js or Python that connects the proposed CRM with GitHub and Jira APIs to automatically provision project repositories and kanban boards once a sponsor proposal is approved.

suggestions.title: Student-Sponsor Talent Matching Portal
suggestions.desc: Build a front-end interface that pulls student skills and interests from the CRM to create a 'matching' algorithm, allowing sponsors to see which student teams best align with their project requirements.

suggestions.title: Predictive Project Risk Analysis
suggestions.desc: Implement a sentiment analysis module using Python's NLTK to analyze communication logs and project status updates within the CRM, flagging projects that exhibit signs of stakeholder misalignment.

suggestions.title: Sponsor Retention & Engagement Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Create a data visualization suite using PowerBI or Tableau that tracks longitudinal sponsor engagement data, helping the college identify which industries provide the most successful long-term partnerships.

title: SCADA Network Design Analysis
authors: Brittney Miller (BSIT), Cedeno Carter, Tyrone Gardner
abstract: Industrial Control Systems (ICS) are other systems, networks, devices and controls that are used to automate, operate, and collect data on industrial processes. These systems include are Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA) systems, Distributed Control Systems (DCS), and other control system configurations such as Programmable Logic Controllers (PLC). ICS systems are used in nearly every industrial and critical infrastructure sector today such as power grids, megafactories, water treatment systems, and nuclear power plants. Therefore these systems have become targets for malicious attacks. The purpose of this specification is to define the typical network architecture of critical

suggestions.title: Multi-Objective Route Optimization
suggestions.desc: Implement a Genetic Algorithm to optimize travel routes based on multiple constraints like 'scenic beauty,' 'low traffic,' and 'minimal walking.' Use the Google Places API to fetch 'busyness' data to help users avoid crowds.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Group Itinerary Synchronization
suggestions.desc: Add a collaborative feature using WebSockets (Socket.io) that allows groups of travelers to vote on activities and see each other's live locations on the shared map. This ensures everyone stays on the 'What's Next' schedule.

suggestions.title: Offline-First Exploration Mode
suggestions.desc: Integrate MapBox offline maps and SQLite local storage to allow users to navigate their custom lists in areas with no cellular data. Build a sync engine that pushes updates to the cloud once a connection is re-established.

suggestions.title: Sentiment-Based Activity Discovery
suggestions.desc: Develop a recommendation engine that scrapes TripAdvisor and Yelp reviews and uses BERT-based NLP to categorize locations by 'vibe' (e.g., 'cozy,' 'chaotic,' 'historic'). Match these to the user's current mood input.

title: AI and Chess Variant IOS Game
authors: Nick Curtin (BSCS), Illya Blalakin (BSCS), Brian Iruka (BSCS), Reece Perry (BSCS), Pouya Ranjbar (BSCS), Dozie Enworom (BSCS)
abstract: This project allows humans to play against an AI or another person in a game of FuzzyLogic Chess. This game is created for the iOS platform. Both players will only be allowed to make legal moves in accordance to the rules of FuzzyLogic Chess. The AI player plays competitively by scanning the board as input and updating data structures and variables accordingly, and then using that data to consider all possible moves and make the best move that it can.

suggestions.title: WireGuard Kernel-Level Migration
suggestions.desc: Upgrade the core protocol from OpenVPN to WireGuard using the 'wg-ctrl' library to achieve 3x faster throughput and lower latency while maintaining the easy-to-use SDN configuration interface.

suggestions.title: Terraform-Driven Multi-Cloud Mesh
suggestions.desc: Incorporate Terraform providers for AWS, Azure, and GCP into the WeaveVPN dashboard to allow users to deploy VPN nodes across different cloud providers with a single click, creating a resilient multi-cloud mesh.

suggestions.title: Zero Trust Identity Integration
suggestions.desc: Implement OIDC (OpenID Connect) via Keycloak or Auth0 so that VPN access is granted based on user identity and MFA status rather than just pre-shared keys, moving toward a Zero Trust Architecture (ZTA).

suggestions.title: eBPF-Based Latency-Aware Routing
suggestions.desc: Use eBPF (Extended Berkeley Packet Filter) on the Linux kernel to monitor the latency between SDN nodes and automatically reroute traffic through the fastest available path in the virtual network.
