title: Approximate String Matching
authors: Aaron Roberts (BSCS), Lea Nooyen (BSCS), Jonathan Miu (BSCS), Daniel Childers, Chase Wood
abstract: The concept of approximate string matching or "fuzzy" matching is an important any industry that make use of data mining or aggregation. Some example industries include data driven marking, genetic sequencing, plagiarism detection and spam detection just to name a few. There are many different algorithms used to fuzzy match data sets. For this project we chose to look at the Levenshtein Distance algorithm implemented in three languages: Python, C++ and C#. By running benchmarks on static datasets using implementations in the different languages we found that a C-based Python approach to the Levenshtein Distance algorithm was the most efficient. We then designed a web application that allows…

suggestions.title: Layer 2 Scalability with Polygon
suggestions.desc: Migrate the loyalty program to a Layer 2 blockchain like Polygon to minimize transaction fees (gas) for small point redemptions. Implement an Ethers.js frontend to allow users to view their token balance in real-time.

suggestions.title: Tokenized Store Governance (DAO)
suggestions.desc: Implement a Decentralized Autonomous Organization (DAO) structure where loyalty point holders can vote on store decisions, such as 'Flavor of the Month' or 'Extended Hours.' Use Snapshot.org for off-chain voting to save costs.

suggestions.title: Merchant Analytics and Retention Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Build a React-based dashboard for business owners that visualizes token velocity and customer retention metrics pulled from the AWS backend. Use AWS QuickSight to generate automated reports on loyalty program ROI.

suggestions.title: Zero-Knowledge Proofs for Privacy
suggestions.desc: Use SnarkyJS to implement Zero-Knowledge Proofs, allowing customers to prove they have reached a specific loyalty tier for a discount without revealing their entire purchase history or identity to the merchant.

title: Asset Tracker - StructureWorks
authors: Connor Kendrick (BSCS), Lane Lake (BSCS), Mark Chamberlain (BSCS), Emily Cox (BSCS), Kade Randall (BSCS)
abstract: Our group was contracted by StructureWorks to implement an application defined by the specification document given to us. The purpose of this project was to create a web application that allows a company to track trailers and other equipment, whether in the company's plant or a remote work site. This tool displays where all assets are located using the Google Maps API, and provides other functionalities such as defining geofence boundaries, and sending out notifications when assets enter or leave these geofences.

suggestions.title: Privacy-First Local Voice Interface
suggestions.desc: Integrate a local Large Language Model (LLM) using llama.cpp on a Raspberry Pi 5 to allow users to set alarms and check weather via voice without sending data to the cloud. Use Whisper for high-accuracy local speech-to-text.

suggestions.title: Circadian Lighting and Blind Control
suggestions.desc: Connect the alarm clock to the Philips Hue API and Zigbee-controlled smart blinds to create a 'natural sunrise' effect. The system should gradually increase light intensity and open blinds 20 minutes before the calculated wake time.

suggestions.title: Smart Calendar Buffer Logic
suggestions.desc: Connect to Google/Outlook Calendar APIs to analyze the user's first meeting location. The clock should calculate commute times using the TomTom Traffic API and automatically wake the user earlier if an accident is detected on their route.

suggestions.title: Non-Contact Sleep Quality Analysis
suggestions.desc: Incorporate a 24GHz mmWave radar sensor to track respiration and body movement during sleep without the need for wearables. Use these patterns to trigger the alarm specifically during the 'Light Sleep' phase to minimize grogginess.

title: Muzer - Personal Music Server
authors: Stanley Gilstrap (BSCS), Stephen Chatham (BSCS), Alex Blaes (BSCS), Nick Pounders (BSCS), Kevin Kendrick (BSCS)
abstract: Able to select songs from home PC and stream them on a remote device.

suggestions.title: Adaptive Bitrate Streaming with FFmpeg
suggestions.desc: Implement dynamic transcoding on the server side using the FFmpeg library to detect network bandwidth and adjust audio quality in real-time, similar to how Spotify handles varying signal strengths.

suggestions.title: Real-time Synchronized Social Listening
suggestions.desc: Use WebSockets (Socket.io) to allow multiple remote users to join a single session and listen to the same stream simultaneously with sub-millisecond sync, enabling 'watch parties' for private music collections.

suggestions.title: AI-Driven Metadata Enrichment and Normalization
suggestions.desc: Integrate the MusicBrainz API and Librosa to automatically tag untagged MP3s and apply EBU R128 loudness normalization so that tracks from different eras play at a consistent volume level.

suggestions.title: Hybrid Cloud-Edge Storage Integration
suggestions.desc: Extend the server to support S3-compatible storage (like MinIO or AWS) so users can offload large FLAC libraries to the cloud while keeping the playback logic on their local home PC.

title: OwlPath
authors: Charlie McDermitt (BSCS), Corey Harris (BSCS), Emmaline English (BSCS), Ryne Gipson (BSCS), Bert Sloan (BSCS)
abstract: Big Munchin' is a video game designed to help teach portioning skills and promote healthy eating habits. The overall metabolic health of individuals in America is comparatively low to other countries. Metabolic health is bolstered by several factors including exercise and a proper diet consisting of essential vitamins, proteins, and other important biomolecules.The increased cost of healthy nutrient-rich foods and a lack of proper nutritional education have hindered the overall metabolic health of modern Americans. Engaging individuals in learning more about nutritional health can be a difficult task made easier by an engaging experience that stays in the user’s minds. Big Munchin can be a vehicle for increasing Americans' overall awareness of nutritional health while also ensuring they better understand what foods can be beneficial for achieving those goals.

suggestions.title: Real-World Nutrition API Integration
suggestions.desc: Replace static food data with a live connection to the Edamam or Nutritionix API, allowing players to scan real-world product barcodes to see how those specific items would impact their in-game metabolic health.

suggestions.title: Cooperative Metabolic Boss Battles
suggestions.desc: Introduce a multiplayer mode where players must collaboratively 'balance a plate' to defeat bosses representing metabolic disorders, requiring team-based synchronization of proteins, carbs, and vitamins.

suggestions.title: Wearable-Sync Activity Buffs
suggestions.desc: Use the Google Fit or Apple HealthKit API to sync the player's real-world physical activity, granting in-game metabolic bonuses or stamina boosts based on their actual daily step counts.

suggestions.title: Educational Progress Tracking for Schools
suggestions.desc: Build a web-based dashboard for teachers that aggregates anonymized student data to show which nutritional concepts (e.g., portioning, vitamin types) the class is struggling to master.

title: Android "Identify" App
authors: John McKinney (MSSWE), John Sineath (MSSWE), Mark Kordahi
abstract: The project developed a stand-alone Android application named "Identify" which utilizes deep learning CNN and R-CNN for the detection and classification of visual objects by analyzing either still images (photographs) or images seen thru a live camera lens.

suggestions.title: Zero-Trust IoT Mesh Implementation
suggestions.desc: Secure the home automation network by implementing an MQTT broker with TLS encryption. Require per-device certificates to ensure that even if one smart device is compromised, the rest of the Android-controlled network remains safe.

suggestions.title: Energy-Efficient Automation Logic
suggestions.desc: Integrate the OpenWeather API to create 'Smart Eco-Modes.' Build logic that automatically adjusts smart blinds and thermostats based on external temperature and forecasted sunlight to minimize HVAC energy consumption.

suggestions.title: Privacy-First Local Voice Control
suggestions.desc: Add a voice command module using an on-device engine like Rhasspy or Snips. This allows users to control their home via the Android app using voice without sending sensitive audio data to Amazon or Google servers.

suggestions.title: Occupancy Pattern Prediction
suggestions.desc: Apply a Recurrent Neural Network (RNN) to the device usage logs to learn the user’s daily routine. The app can then proactively suggest lighting or temperature changes before the user even enters a room.

similar: legacy:GC-05|Fall 2018|cnn - identify malign moles on skin

title: Satellite Image Land Classification
authors: Raymond Martin (MSCS)
abstract: This is a multiclass classification project surveying several methods. The dataset is the SAT-6 satellite image airborne dataset. It contains 404,000 samples and 3136 features per sample (RGB and infrared, 28x28 pixels). Each sample composes one of six labeled types of land (e.g. 'water', 'road'...). The project is done entirely in Apache Spark (using pyspark) on the KSU Spark server.

suggestions.title: Real-Time User Interest Recommender
suggestions.desc: Use the implemented clustering algorithms to group users based on their clickstream data in real-time. Connect this to a Flask API that serves personalized 'Recommended for You' content based on the active cluster the user belongs to.

suggestions.title: Interactive 3D Cluster Visualization
suggestions.desc: Utilize Three.js and t-SNE (t-Distributed Stochastic Neighbor Embedding) to create an interactive web dashboard. Users can explore high-dimensional web data clusters in a 3D space to visually identify outliers and patterns.

suggestions.title: Bot Traffic Anomaly Detector
suggestions.desc: Repurpose the clustering logic to detect non-human behavior in server logs. By clustering requests based on timing and IP headers, the system can automatically flag botnets or scrapers that deviate from human-user clusters.

suggestions.title: Distributed Clustering with PySpark
suggestions.desc: Scale the project to handle big data by porting the clustering algorithms to a Spark environment. Test the system’s performance on a massive dataset, such as the Common Crawl web index, using AWS EMR for distributed processing.

similar: legacy:UR-01HS|Spring 2016|convolutional neural networks for image classification with nao robot

title: Toxic Comment Classification
authors: Nusrat Asrafi (MSCS)
abstract: The aim of the project is to build a multi-headed model that's capable of detecting different types of toxicity like threats, obscenity, insults, and identity- based hate . I am using a dataset of comments from Wikipedia talk page edits. Improvements to the current model will hopefully help online discussion become more productive and respectful. I want to create a model which predicts a probability of each type of toxicity for each comment.

suggestions.title: ARCore Augmented Reality Tag
suggestions.desc: Enhance the 'laser' aspect by using Google ARCore to render virtual beams and shields in the phone's camera view. Players can 'see' their shots and hit markers superimposed on the real world during gameplay.

suggestions.title: Dead Reckoning for GPS dead-zones
suggestions.desc: Implement a sensor fusion algorithm that uses the phone’s accelerometer and gyroscope to estimate player movement (Dead Reckoning). This ensures the game remains playable even when GPS signal drops between tall buildings.

suggestions.title: Gamified Urban Fitness League
suggestions.desc: Transform the game into a fitness platform where points are earned for distance run and calories burned during a match. Integrate with Google Fit to track long-term stats and create a leaderboard based on athletic performance.

suggestions.title: Low-Latency WebRTC Multiplayer
suggestions.desc: Replace standard HTTP requests with WebRTC data channels for peer-to-peer communication. This reduces the latency of 'tag' registration to near-instantaneous levels, critical for a fast-paced action game.

title: Predict Credit Risks
authors: Liyuan Liu (Ph.D. in Analytics and Data Science), Yiyun Zhou (Ph.D in analytics and data science
abstract: In general, credit risk datasets are label imbalance datasets, and logistics regression is one of the standard credit risk model in the industry for many years. In this project, I aim to conduct an experimental analysis on German Credit Risk Dataset to predict banking loan default using multiple strategies to deal with the imbalanced data, such as random oversampling, random under-sampling, SMOTE, SMOTEENN, weight-based method. After that, to examine the effects of comparing multiple strategies and different machine learning algorithms, I employ multiple machine learning algorithms: decision tree (DT), random forest(RF), logistic regression(LG), neural networks(NN), and support vector machin

suggestions.title: Edge TPU Hardware Acceleration
suggestions.desc: Integrate a Google Coral USB Accelerator into the Raspberry Pi to offload deep learning tasks. This allows the system to move from low-FPS TensorFlow Lite models to high-speed, real-time object detection at 30+ FPS.

suggestions.title: Auto-Restocking Subscription Bot
suggestions.desc: Connect the inventory detection system to a web scraper or the Walmart IO API. When the camera detects that a specific item (e.g., milk) is missing from the shelf for more than 24 hours, the system can automatically add it to an online shopping cart.

suggestions.title: Multi-Pi Warehouse Sync
suggestions.desc: Build a central dashboard using Django that aggregates inventory data from multiple Raspberry Pis located in different aisles. Implement a 'Global Inventory View' that tracks total stock levels across an entire facility.

suggestions.title: Few-Shot Learning for New Items
suggestions.desc: Implement a Few-Shot Learning model that allows users to 'train' the system on a new item by simply holding it in front of the camera for 10 seconds. This removes the need for large pre-labeled datasets for every unique inventory item.

title: CNN - Identify malign moles on Skin
authors: sanjoosh akkineni (PhD Student, Analytics and Data Science)
abstract: The idea is to develop a simple CNN model , and evaluate the performance to set a baseline. Data I have used is a set of images from the International Skin Imaging Collaboration: Melanoma Project ISIC. Following steps to improve the model are: Data augmentation: Rotations, noising, scaling to avoid over fitting Transferred Learning: Using a pre-trained network construct some additional layer at the end to fine tuning our model. (VGG-16, or other) Full training of VGG-16 + additional layer.

suggestions.title: Predictive Food Scarcity Forecasting
suggestions.desc: Integrate Meta’s Prophet library to perform time-series forecasting on the historical GIS data. Add a 'Future' toggle to the heatmap that shows predicted food security levels over the next 5 years based on current climate trends.

suggestions.title: Policy Impact Simulation Tool
suggestions.desc: Add an interactive 'What If' panel where users can adjust variables like 'Grain Export Tax' or 'Rainfall Decrease.' Use a simple linear regression model to visualize how these hypothetical policy changes would shift the heatmap colors.

suggestions.title: Vector-Tile Mapbox Migration
suggestions.desc: Migrate the visualization to Mapbox GL JS to enable smooth zooming from global views down to individual province-level data. Use vector tiles to overlay secondary datasets like infrastructure density or population growth over the food security map.

suggestions.title: Hyper-Local Food Desert Mapper
suggestions.desc: Pivot the data focus from global countries to a city-level view. Use the USDA Food Environment Atlas dataset to map 'food deserts' in a specific metro area, highlighting neighborhoods with limited access to fresh produce.

title: Cancer Detection-Atrous Convolution
authors: Karl Kevin Tiba Fossoh Major: MSCS
abstract: For the past 50 years, Pathologists have had significant tedious moments providing accurate quantifications (e.g. tumor extent, nuclei count) and reduced variability between related research findings. In this work, we: - create algorithms to extract data - build models to ingest the data, - detect and classify key classes, following tested metrics through tuning and tweaking. Such an automated approach have shown to be beneficial in the context of significantly reducing such tedious processes while helping pathologists to reduce variability amongst themselves. Learn More

suggestions.title: Offline-First Progressive Web App
suggestions.desc: Convert the portal into a PWA with Workbox to allow technicians to log maintenance tickets in underground parking decks where WiFi is unavailable. The app should automatically sync data to the database once a connection is restored.

suggestions.title: Computer Vision Plate Detection
suggestions.desc: Integrate OpenALPR (Automatic License Plate Recognition) using the parking lot cameras. Link this to the maintenance portal so that if a vehicle is parked in a 'Maintenance Zone' without a permit, an automated ticket is generated for the staff.

suggestions.title: Maintenance Route Optimizer
suggestions.desc: Implement a route optimization algorithm using the Google Maps Directions API. The portal can then provide technicians with the most efficient walking or driving path to address all 'High Priority' tickets across the Georgia Tech campus.

suggestions.title: IoT Kiosk Health Monitoring
suggestions.desc: Connect the portal to the physical parking kiosks via an API. Use basic anomaly detection to flag kiosks that haven't processed a transaction in over an hour, automatically creating a maintenance ticket for a 'Potential Hardware Failure'.

title: Outcome Prediction in Intensive Car
authors: Lauren Staples (PHD in Analytics and Data Science), Ryan Rimbey (MS- Applied Statistics)
abstract: This project uses an open-access database called Medical Information Mart for Intensive Care database (MIMIC3) developed from Beth Israel Deacon Hospital in 2012. The goal of this project is to predict outcomes (in this case, death within 30 days of hospital discharge) in an Intensive Care Unit Setting, with demographic data and billing/claims data. This project achieved 79% accuracy with logistic regression and 10-fold cross validation on a balanced dataset (equal number of deaths and non-deaths). This project uses a unique method of dimension reduction in handling categorical billing data codes (International Classification of Diseases, ICD-9) that achieves the same model evaluation charac

suggestions.title: Student Study-Space Locator
suggestions.desc: Create a public-facing React dashboard that uses WiFi usage density to tell students which campus buildings are currently quietest. Provide a 'Real-Time Capacity' percentage for popular spots like the KSU library based on connected device counts.

suggestions.title: Grafana & InfluxDB Observability Stack
suggestions.desc: Replace the basic dashboard with an InfluxDB time-series database and a Grafana front-end. This allows for professional-grade monitoring of packet loss, signal-to-noise ratios, and jitter over months of campus operation.

suggestions.title: Rogue Access Point Detection
suggestions.desc: Extend the Raspberry Pi monitoring logic to scan for 'Evil Twin' access points. Use Scapy to analyze beacon frames and alert IT staff if an unauthorized device is broadcasting a spoofed KSU SSID to harvest student credentials.

suggestions.title: Predictive Network Congestion Alerts
suggestions.desc: Apply an LSTM (Long Short-Term Memory) network to historical WiFi traffic data. Build a notification system that alerts the KSU network team via Slack when congestion is predicted to exceed 90% during peak events like finals week.

title: CapsNet Traffic Light Recognition
authors: Keshav Shenoy (HS Intern)
abstract: This research constructed a Capsule Neural Network and a Convolutional Neural Network to classify traffic light images by signal and looked at the differences between the two in accuracy of classification.

suggestions.title: Temporal Consistency for Video Segmentation
suggestions.desc: Move from single-frame Mask R-CNN to a video-based approach using ConvLSTM or Mask2Former. This ensures that instruments are tracked smoothly across frames without 'flickering' or losing the mask during fast surgical movements.

suggestions.title: Intraoperative 'No-Go' Zone Alerts
suggestions.desc: Develop a real-time monitoring layer that defines critical tissue areas (like major arteries) as 'No-Go' zones. If the Mask R-CNN detects a surgical tool entering a predefined proximity to these areas, it triggers an audible or visual alert for the surgeon.

suggestions.title: Edge Deployment on NVIDIA Jetson
suggestions.desc: Optimize the Mask R-CNN model using TensorRT and quantization (INT8) to run at 30+ FPS on an NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano. This makes the system portable and usable in operating rooms without requiring a full server rack.

suggestions.title: Automated Post-Surgical Report Generation
suggestions.desc: Use the segmentation data to quantify the duration of the surgery, the frequency of tool usage, and the amount of tissue handled. Generate an automated PDF report summarizing the technical efficiency of the procedure for surgical training and review.

title: Phishing Analysis using ML
authors: Suvan Paturi (HS Intern)
abstract: Various machine learning algorithms were applied to phishing website datasets to assess their performance by comparing calculated accuracy, false positive, and false positive rates for each algorithm. The testing was conducted using HPCC Systems ECL and the dataset used was obtained from the University of Huddersfield's ML Repository.

suggestions.title: Offline LLM Tutor with Quantized Models
suggestions.desc: Integrate a small-parameter Large Language Model (like Phi-2 or Llama-3-8B) quantized for mobile. Build a voice-to-text interface that allows children to ask questions about the video lessons without needing an internet connection.

suggestions.title: Handwriting Assessment via Stroke Analysis
suggestions.desc: Develop a computer vision module for the 'sand tray' feature that analyzes the child's finger or stylus strokes. Use a lightweight CNN to give real-time feedback on letter formation and track literacy progress over time.

suggestions.title: Gamified Adaptive Learning Path
suggestions.desc: Create a software layer that adjusts the difficulty of the video lessons and whiteboard activities based on the child's performance. Use a Reinforcement Learning agent to maximize student engagement by offering rewards (digital badges) for completing challenging tasks.

suggestions.title: Solar-Powered Ruggedization for Remote Use
suggestions.desc: Design a 3D-printable, ruggedized casing for the tablet that includes an integrated solar charging panel. Create a 'mesh networking' feature where multiple tablets can sync data and updates when one device occasionally reaches a high-connectivity zone.

title: MS Office Macro Malware Detection
authors: Ruth Bearden (BSCS)
abstract: Microsoft Office documents can contain macros, scripts designed to automate menial tasks or improve a document's user-interface. Malicious attackers, however, use macros as a means to download malicious payloads from the internet onto a host computer. Due to the ever-improving document engineering techniques these attackers employ, it is becoming increasingly difficult to visually identify a document containing macro malware, and users are susceptible to activating malicious macros. The goal of this research is to find the best way to detect macro malware automatically using machine learning. This project utilizes two kinds of data extracted from a sample of malicious and benign (safe) docum

suggestions.title: Indoor SLAM with Depth Sensing
suggestions.desc: Enhance the drone's navigation by implementing ROS (Robot Operating System) with a GMapping or Cartographer SLAM package. Use a T265 Realsense camera to allow the drone to build a 3D map of a room and take photos from optimal geometric angles.

suggestions.title: Automated Real Estate Tour Guide
suggestions.desc: Program the drone to follow a pre-defined path through a house, using OpenCV to detect 'points of interest' like fireplaces or large windows. The drone should automatically center these features in the frame and take high-dynamic-range (HDR) photos.

suggestions.title: CNN-Based Aesthetic Scoring
suggestions.desc: Integrate a pre-trained Neural Image Assessment (NIMA) model onto the drone's edge processor. The drone should take multiple photos of a scene and only keep/upload the ones with the highest predicted aesthetic and technical quality scores.

suggestions.title: Autonomous Event Photographer
suggestions.desc: Modify the drone's AI to include face detection and group framing. The drone can be deployed at indoor events to hover and capture 'candid' shots by detecting smiles and eye contact using the MediaPipe library, ensuring everyone is in the frame before snapping.

title: Text-based Speaker Segmentation
authors: Steffen Lim (BSCGDD), Sams Khan (BSCS)
abstract: Imagine a text to speech algorithm that can adjust it's voice based on contextual linguistic identifiers. Learn More Graduate Research Projects (10) * Candidates for the best graduate research project award

suggestions.title: Deep Reinforcement Learning for Real-Time Control
suggestions.desc: Replace the predictive simulation with a Deep Q-Network (DQN) that controls HVAC setpoints in real-time. Use the 'OpenAI Gym' environment to simulate the West Point building and train the agent to balance occupant comfort (CO2 and Temp) against peak energy prices.

suggestions.title: Campus-Wide Microgrid Load Balancing
suggestions.desc: Extend the peak shaving model to include other energy-intensive campus assets like electric vehicle (EV) charging stations and dorm lighting. Create a coordinated strategy where HVAC load is decreased when EV demand spikes to keep the total campus draw below a critical threshold.

suggestions.title: Commercial Smart Thermostat API for Rebates
suggestions.desc: Develop a SaaS wrapper for the optimization model that connects to smart thermostats (like Ecobee or Nest) via API. Target small business owners by automating their participation in 'Demand Response' programs, earning them utility rebates during peak hours.

suggestions.title: Occupancy Sensing via Thermal Imaging Fusion
suggestions.desc: Research the impact of real-time occupancy data on the peak shaving model. Integrate low-cost PIR or thermal sensors to feed 'human heat load' data into the HVAC simulation, allowing for more aggressive energy savings in empty zones.

title: VR/AR App for Non-Visual Navigation
authors: Karis Kim (MSIT), Devan Patel (BSCGDD), Nick Murphy (MSCS)
abstract: People with visual impairments often require repetitive on-site training to memorize routes that they need to reach desired destinations. This study examined the feasibility of virtual environments as an Assistive Technology tool to supplement tradition training for daily use and pathway recall. Participants at the Center for the Visually Impaired in Atlanta were outfitted with testing equipment and asked to navigate pure virtual and mixed reality environments based on the equipment's verbal feedback. Data on the participant's movement, orientation, and confidence while using the system were collected to gain insights on the viability of virtual reality-based Assistive Technology for orienta

suggestions.title: LiDAR-to-Spatial-Audio Engine
suggestions.desc: Using the LiDAR sensor on modern iPhones, develop a system that translates point-cloud depth data into 3D HRTF (Head-Related Transfer Function) audio cues. Objects closer to the user produce higher frequency or louder localized clicks.

suggestions.title: Public Transit API Navigation Integration
suggestions.desc: Integrate the UI with GTFS (General Transit Feed Specification) data. Use spatial audio to 'pull' the user toward the correct bus stop or train platform, providing verbal 3D-audio cues for upcoming arrivals.

suggestions.title: Haptic-Audio Fusion Wearable
suggestions.desc: Create a companion wearable device (belt or vest) using ESP32 and vibrating motors. Synchronize the 3D audio cues with haptic pulses to provide a multi-sensory 'map' of the environment for improved directional confidence.

suggestions.title: Voice-First Wayfinding Assistant
suggestions.desc: Implement an LLM-based voice interface using OpenAI Whisper for speech-to-text. Users can ask, 'Where is the nearest exit?' and the system responds with spatialized audio beacons that lead them through the building.

title: Customer Review Analytics
authors: Jhanvi Vyas (MSIT)
abstract: Analyzed the results obtained from experiments to analyze the impact of customer review on today's generation.

suggestions.title: Differential Privacy for Student Bio-Data
suggestions.desc: Implement a Differential Privacy layer on the data ingestion pipeline to ensure that individual student stress levels cannot be deanonymized while still allowing the teacher to see aggregate classroom trends.

suggestions.title: Teacher Dashboard with Real-Time Heatmaps
suggestions.desc: Develop a React-based web dashboard that visualizes the classroom's 'engagement level' as a heatmap. Use WebSockets to provide the teacher with immediate feedback on which parts of a lecture are causing boredom or confusion.

suggestions.title: Smart Lighting and HVAC Feedback Loop
suggestions.desc: Connect the IoT sensors to a smart building controller (BACnet). Automatically adjust the classroom lighting color temperature or lower the room temperature if the sensors detect a significant spike in aggregate student sleepiness.

suggestions.title: Wearable SDK Integration (Apple/Fitbit)
suggestions.desc: Expand the sensor suite to include data from student smartwatches. Use the Heart Rate Variability (HRV) and SpO2 data from the HealthKit/Fitbit SDKs to calibrate the desk sensors and improve accuracy of the stress models.

title: Analysis of Top Grossing Apps
authors: Qingliang Yang (MSIT)
abstract: The online software distribution channels such as App Store has offered developers a powerful distribution mechanism. App store help users discover apps by providing categories and rankings. The ratings and reviews left by users in these online App store have the potential to influence new users. The motivation for this project is to find some clues to get high ratings to ensure that an app has a viable future. I think it is meaningful to the application company and developers.

suggestions.title: Streaming Cluster Integration with Apache Flink
suggestions.desc: Evolve the static MapReduce clustering into a dynamic stream processing application using Flink to handle real-time data clusters that evolve over time.

suggestions.title: Density-Based Big Data Clustering (DBSCAN)
suggestions.desc: Implement the DBSCAN algorithm for the MapReduce framework to compare its ability to find non-spherical clusters against traditional K-Means on the Hadoop cluster.

suggestions.title: Global Social Media Topic Community Detection
suggestions.desc: Use the Hadoop clustering implementation to analyze terabytes of Twitter/X data, grouping users into 'topic communities' based on the co-occurrence of hashtags.

suggestions.title: B2B Customer Segmentation Service
suggestions.desc: Build a specialized service for e-commerce platforms that uses the MapReduce clustering to segment millions of customers into high-value personas based on purchase frequency and volume.

title: How Big Data Can Improve Healthcare
authors: Shayan Shamskolahi (MSIT)
abstract: This project explores the possibilities that big data offers in improving the healthcare industry. More specifically, it seeks to provide an example where big data can assist decision-makers in improving the quality and effectiveness of healthcare in hospitals across the U.S. In conclusion, the project will discuss the importance of accessing public data (specially healthcare datasets) and the role that it can play in public health.

suggestions.title: Near-Infrared (NIR) Liveness Detection
suggestions.desc: Incorporate a Raspberry Pi NoIR camera to detect the 'liveness' of the eye. This prevents spoofing attacks using high-resolution photos or video screens, as real human eyes reflect infrared light uniquely compared to screens.

suggestions.title: Decentralized Biometric ID with Blockchain
suggestions.desc: Integrate the iris recognition system with a Self-Sovereign Identity (SSI) framework. Use Zero-Knowledge Proofs to allow a user to authenticate their identity without ever transmitting their raw biometric data to a server.

suggestions.title: Emotional and Cognitive State Analysis
suggestions.desc: Research the use of pupillary response (task-evoked pupillary response) to detect user cognitive load or emotional arousal. This could be used for 'adaptive security' where higher authentication is required if the user appears under duress.

suggestions.title: Multi-Factor Iris + Face Fusion
suggestions.desc: Develop a dual-model authentication system that uses YOLOv8 for face detection and a specialized CNN for iris segmentation (like DeepIris). This improves reliability in various lighting conditions where iris-only detection might fail.

title: Imbalanced Dataset
authors: Wajira Abeysinghe (MSCS)
abstract: Imbalanced dataset is available in many fields such as in credit card fraud detection, classification usually performs on the large number of normal transactions, to detect small percentage of fraud transactions.

suggestions.title: Explainable AI (XAI) for Claim Adjusters
suggestions.desc: Integrate SHAP or LIME into the prediction model to provide 'reasoning' for each severity score. This helps insurance adjusters understand why a specific claim was flagged as high-cost, increasing trust in the automated system.

suggestions.title: Multi-Modal Damage Assessment
suggestions.desc: Expand the machine learning model to include computer vision. Use a CNN to analyze uploaded photos of vehicle damage and fuse those features with the tabular data to produce a more accurate severity estimate.

suggestions.title: Fraud Detection using Graph Neural Networks
suggestions.desc: Implement a GNN to identify suspicious relationships between claimants, doctors, and repair shops. Cross-reference the severity predictions with historical fraud patterns to flag potential 'staged' accidents.

suggestions.title: Federated Learning for Cross-Carrier Training
suggestions.desc: Develop a Federated Learning framework that allows multiple insurance companies to train a shared severity model without sharing sensitive customer data, resulting in a more robust and generalized model.

title: Machine Learning for Fintech
authors: Karl Kevin Tiba Fossoh (MSCS)
abstract: Knowing the importance of the detection of fraud in our banking system, especially to protect users from unlawful transactions, we decided to go for a diversified analysis of a transaction report dataset. This dataset enabled us to better understand what are the key parameters to identify for fraud detection along. The accuracy of those different classifications is based on different methods, each providing an insight on what parameters and data affect the most the detection

suggestions.title: Adversarial Defense for Malware Vision
suggestions.desc: Implement 'adversarial training' in the TensorFlow model to detect malware that has been specifically obfuscated to bypass image-based LBP feature detection.

suggestions.title: Transfer Learning with ResNet-50
suggestions.desc: Replace the custom TensorFlow classifier with a ResNet-50 model pre-trained on ImageNet to see if general visual features improve the classification of malware LBP images.

suggestions.title: Live Malware-to-Image Scanning API
suggestions.desc: Develop a SaaS API that allows security teams to upload binaries, converts them to LBP images on-the-fly, and returns a 'threat signature' classification in milliseconds.

suggestions.title: Mobile Malware Detection for Android
suggestions.desc: Optimize the LBP-based detection model for mobile deployment (TensorFlow Lite) to scan Android APK files for malicious patterns directly on the device.

title: ML for IDS benchmark in HPCC System
authors: Alexander Federico (MSCS)
abstract: The research looks into the performance of machine learning algorithms and how accurate the machine learning model can classify whether a packet is an intrusion or not.

suggestions.title: Adversarial Robustness Testing against Feature-Statically Evaded Malware
suggestions.desc: Investigate how the model behaves when malware authors intentionally manipulate the 9 selected features using GANs to bypass detection. Build a testing suite that generates 'adversarial malware' to evaluate the stability of the RF and SVM classifiers.

suggestions.title: Explainable Malware Diagnostics with SHAP and LIME
suggestions.desc: Integrate SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) to visualize exactly why the neural network flagged a specific file. Create a dashboard for security analysts that highlights which of the 9 features contributed most to a 'malicious' verdict.

suggestions.title: Real-time Windows Kernel Driver Implementation
suggestions.desc: Port the Python-based detection logic into a C++ Windows kernel driver that monitors file execution in real-time. Use a lightweight inference engine like ONNX Runtime to maintain the 99.6% accuracy without significant CPU overhead.

suggestions.title: Cross-Platform Feature Mapping for Android APKs
suggestions.desc: Extend the feature selection methodology to the Android domain by extracting intents, permissions, and API calls. Test if a similarly small subset of features (under 10) can achieve comparable accuracy on the Drebin or Contagio datasets.

title: Youtube8M Video classification
authors: Karl Kevin Tiba Fossoh (MSCS), Maxwell Lavin (MSCS)
abstract: The main motivation of the research was the capacity to summarize digital entities such as video, sounds, and images to a simple textual representation. One of the reason was to provide the ability to people to get fast and efficient summarization of long content without any risk of losing detail, information or precision. One simple approach was to try to work with videos. In effect, videos are a set of images frames associated with sound frames. The capacity to dissociate all of these features gives us a wide array to analyze each of them can affect the neural network designed to provide a classification and a captioning of the video, but also how each of these elements could be associated

suggestions.title: On-Device Inference with TensorFlow Lite and Face Mesh
suggestions.desc: Optimize the deep learning model for mobile deployment using quantization and pruning to run on mid-range smartphones. Use MediaPipe Face Mesh to ensure classification is only performed on high-quality facial crops, reducing false positives from background noise.

suggestions.title: Mitigating Demographic Bias in Classification
suggestions.desc: Evaluate the model's accuracy across different ethnicities using the FairFace dataset. Implement adversarial debiasing or re-weighting techniques during training to ensure the age and gender estimation performs equitably across all protected groups.

suggestions.title: Retail Analytics Heatmap and Demographic Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Develop a system that connects to a store's IP camera to track the age and gender distribution of customers over time. Use OpenCV for multi-face tracking and visualize the data in a React-based dashboard to help retailers optimize product placement.

suggestions.title: Privacy-Preserving Edge Classification with Local Blurring
suggestions.desc: Modify the pipeline to perform age/gender estimation on the 'edge' and immediately discard the raw image. Implement a system where only the metadata (e.g., 'Male, 25-30') is sent to the server, while the facial image is blurred or deleted to comply with GDPR/CCPA.

title: KNN Optimization with Vector Models
authors: Arialdis Japa (MSCS), Daniel Brown (MSCS)
abstract: Optimization to the traditional implementation of the KNN algorithm by using vector space models.

suggestions.title: Edge-Deployed Inference on Jetson Nano
suggestions.desc: Convert the CNN and Capsule Network models to ONNX format and deploy them onto NVIDIA Jetson Nano hardware for real-time, low-power animal detection at the camera source.

suggestions.title: Multi-Species Interaction Tracker
suggestions.desc: Develop a temporal analysis layer that tracks multiple detected animals across video frames to identify and flag interspecies interactions like predator-prey encounters.

suggestions.title: Capsule Network Robustness Study
suggestions.desc: Conduct a comparative study on how Capsule Networks handle 'occlusion' (animals hidden by trees or brush) compared to standard CNNs using synthetic wilderness datasets.

suggestions.title: Rare Species Alert & Geo-Tagging System
suggestions.desc: Build a cloud-based dashboard that automatically geo-tags rare species sightings and sends real-time push notifications to wildlife conservationists when an endangered animal is detected.

title: Animal Identification deep learning
authors: Suvan Paturi (HS Intern)
abstract: How can we outperform the best animal identification model on the SS project? Can capsule network performbetter than CNNs in large and complex datasets? Can we build a capsule net that outperforms the best capsule net model on large and complex dataset?
