suggestions.title: Near-Miss Detection with Edge Computing on NVIDIA Jetson
suggestions.desc: Deploy the traffic conflict model to an NVIDIA Jetson Nano device for local, real-time processing at the intersection. Develop a 'Near-Miss' alert system that triggers a high-priority log whenever the Post-Encroachment Time (PET) between two vehicles drops below a safety threshold.

suggestions.title: Multi-Modal Sensor Fusion for Low-Visibility Safety
suggestions.desc: Integrate LiDAR or Thermal camera data with the live video feed to maintain safety monitoring during night-time or heavy fog. Use a Kalman filter to fuse object tracks from different sensors, ensuring continuous monitoring when visual cameras fail.

suggestions.title: Predictive Trajectory Modeling for Pedestrian Safety
suggestions.desc: Expand the system to predict the future path (0.5 to 2 seconds) of pedestrians near crosswalks using LSTMs. Analyze traffic conflicts between turning vehicles and pedestrians to proactively suggest timing changes for signalized intersections.

suggestions.title: Digital Twin Traffic Safety Synchronization
suggestions.desc: Stream the extracted traffic conflict data into a 3D Digital Twin environment using Unity or Carla Simulator. This allows city planners to virtually 'replay' high-risk events from any angle to identify structural road design flaws contributing to accidents.

title: Monster Melee
authors: Sonya Rivers (BSCGDD) Dani Butzen (BAACS) Amariae Hawkins (BSCGDD),
abstract: Monster Melee is a fighting game in which the player's luck rather than skill determines the outcome of every match. Each round Players AND enemies are given random items which either boost or bust their attack and defense abilities. It's the luck of the draw to see who comes out on top!

suggestions.title: Procedural Ruin Generation System
suggestions.desc: Develop a C++ script for Unreal Engine that procedurally generates the 'forgotten world' ruins to ensure no two playthroughs are the same. Use Houdini Engine or Perlin noise to create crumbling architecture that remains navigable for third-person combat.

suggestions.title: Haptic Feedback Integration for VR Combat
suggestions.desc: Enhance the combat immersion by integrating the bHaptics API or OpenXR haptic standards. Map enemy strikes and magical will-based attacks to specific vibration patterns on a haptic vest or VR controllers.

suggestions.title: AI-Driven Dynamic Difficulty Adjustment
suggestions.desc: Implement a machine learning agent using Unity ML-Agents that monitors player health and puzzle-solving speed. The system should adjust enemy aggressiveness in the forgotten world to maintain a 'flow state' for the player.

suggestions.title: Forgotten Will Multiplayer Co-op Expansion
suggestions.desc: Refactor the combat system to support two-player peer-to-peer networking. Design 'Will-Link' puzzles that require both players to use their abilities simultaneously to unlock specific areas of the mystery.

title: Forgotten Will + *
authors: Christopher Plummer (BSCGDD) Luke Crowley (BSCGDD)
abstract: Forgotten Will is an action adventure title for PC that lets players explore and do battle in a charming poly aesthetic world full of quirky characters and methodical game-play.

suggestions.title: Spatial Audio Reactive Neon Environments
suggestions.desc: Use the FMOD audio engine to make the neon lights pulse and change colors in sync with the game’s soundtrack and weapon fire. This technical layer deepens the arcade aesthetic while providing visual cues for incoming enemy waves.

suggestions.title: Cross-Platform VR-Mobile Spectator App
suggestions.desc: Build a companion app in Flutter where non-VR players can act as 'Commanders.' Mobile users can drop power-ups or spawn extra enemies into the VR arena in real-time using a Socket.io connection.

suggestions.title: Neon Arcade Global Leaderboard and API
suggestions.desc: Develop a backend using Node.js and MongoDB to track high scores, accuracy, and kill streaks. Create an in-game VR 'Hall of Fame' where players can see 3D holograms of the current top-ranked avatars.

suggestions.title: Latency Compensation for High-Speed VR Projectiles
suggestions.desc: Implement a client-side prediction and server reconciliation system specifically for the fast-paced shoot-em-up mechanics. This ensures that in a multiplayer VR environment, neon projectiles feel responsive even with 100ms+ latency.

title: Hacktical Advantage + *
authors: Matthew Virga (BSCGDD) Sean O'dea (BSCGDD) Caleb Mauldin (BSCGDD),
abstract: Hacktical Advantage is a video game featuring procedural generation, cooperative gameplay between two players over a network, VR, and all house made 3D modeled assets. This game features lots of gameplay, is playable on many different platforms, has a single player mode, and on its publishing, date will allow for lots of cross platform playability. Hack to your advantage and have fun!

suggestions.title: Dynamic Color-Blindness Shader Library
suggestions.desc: Create a suite of post-processing shaders that simulate Protanopia, Deuteranopia, and Tritanopia for the non-colorblind player. This forces the cooperative partners to communicate more effectively to solve puzzles that the other literally cannot see.

suggestions.title: Accessibility-First Level Design Tool
suggestions.desc: Build an editor extension in Unity that automatically checks level geometry for 'color-reliance' issues. The tool should flag areas where a puzzle is impossible to solve without specific color perception and suggest secondary cues like shapes or patterns.

suggestions.title: Biometric Stress Analysis in Co-op Play
suggestions.desc: Integrate heart rate monitor data to track player frustration levels during difficult color-based puzzles. Use this data to research how accessibility constraints impact cooperative communication and team bonding.

suggestions.title: Roses are Red: Mobile AR Expansion
suggestions.desc: Port the puzzle mechanics to an Augmented Reality (AR) setting using ARCore. Players must move around a physical room to align 'color beams' visible only through their phone screens to solve spatial puzzles.

title: Re:Birth
authors: Ronald Ison (BSCGDD) Matthew Keller (BSCGDD) Evan Williams (BSCGDD),
abstract: The game is an action game that takes place in a 3D environment. The player is a cybernetic test subject that was made in a lab. A sudden error caused by an unknown person breaks the player out of its containment capsule. The player doesn’t know where or what he is. The game will take the player through interior environments of the facility as well as exterior landscapes. The game will have the player using combos to take down various enemies.

suggestions.title: Physics-Based Predictive Catching System
suggestions.desc: Develop a sophisticated collision detection system that allows players to 'parry' or 'catch' dodgeballs based on momentum vectors. Use Unity's PhysX engine to calculate projectile trajectory and grant rewards for 'perfect timing' catches.

suggestions.title: Punk Aesthetic Character Customization Engine
suggestions.desc: Build a modular cosmetic system using vertex painting and layered materials. Allow players to design their own 'punk' gear, where textures and spikes react dynamically to the character's movement and ball impacts.

suggestions.title: Reinforcement Learning for Dodgeball Bots
suggestions.desc: Train AI opponents using Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) to learn dodging and flanking tactics. The goal is to create bots that can pass the ball to each other and coordinate 'pincer' moves against human players.

suggestions.title: Ball PunKS Tournament Broadcasting Suite
suggestions.desc: Create a dedicated 'Director Mode' for streamers that includes cinematic cameras, real-time 'heat maps' of ball travel, and automated replay generation for 'best knockouts' using the game's physics data.

title: Shards of the Demon
authors: Zachary Garrigan (BSCGDD) Daniel Lawrence (BSCGDD) Eric Stover
abstract: Shards of the Demon is a 3rd person action game where the player hunts down as many mini bosses as possible before the time expires. The more mini bosses the player kills the stronger the final boss will become. When the time expires the player will teleport into the demon's lair where the final showdown will take place.

suggestions.title: Modular Space Colony Destruction System
suggestions.desc: Implement a mesh-slicing or pre-fractured destruction system for the sci-fi environment. Players should be able to destroy cover or breach walls within the colony to create new tactical pathways during combat.

suggestions.title: Zero-G Combat Mechanics Integration
suggestions.desc: Expand the sci-fi setting by adding low-gravity zones where combat physics transition from traditional 3D movement to 6DOF (Six Degrees of Freedom). Use thruster-based movement and recoil-driven navigation.

suggestions.title: Vesta Faction Narrative Engine
suggestions.desc: Build a branching dialogue and reputation system using Ink or Yarn Spinner. Player choices during the 'Absolution' storyline should determine which sci-fi factions help or hinder them in later combat stages.

suggestions.title: Neural Network-Based Enemy Stealth AI
suggestions.desc: Develop enemies that adapt to the player’s combat style using a simple neural network. If a player relies on long-range sniping, the AI should learn to utilize the space colony’s vents and corridors for flanking.

title: Arclight, the Tales of Auria *
authors: Zared Redding (BSCGDD) Davis Schuitema (BSCGDD) Sonya Rivers
abstract: A lighthearted, fantasy-adventure game set in the world of Auria. Join Iro, the main character, on his quest to become a proper mage. Lufore, the Mages College, located in Fludore, is looking to find the Archmage's next apprentice. Can Iro prove himself to be a top contender?

suggestions.title: AR Security Overlay for Mobile Operatives
suggestions.desc: Upgrade the mobile component to use Augmented Reality (AR). The mobile player could 'scan' physical QR codes in their room to reveal digital blueprints or hackable nodes that help the VR player navigate the virtual facility.

suggestions.title: Asymmetric Encrypted Communication Channel
suggestions.desc: Develop an in-game 'Hacker Chat' using WebSockets with end-to-end encryption. The mobile player must 'decode' messages from the VR player to bypass firewalls, mimicking real-world cybersecurity protocols.

suggestions.title: Procedural Stealth Facility Architect
suggestions.desc: Create an algorithm that generates unique infiltration maps for every session. The generator must ensure that every 'hackable' mobile node corresponds to a physical obstacle or security gate in the VR player's view.

suggestions.title: Hacktical Advantage: 4v1 Breach Mode
suggestions.desc: Scale the multiplayer to support one VR 'Infiltrator' and four mobile 'Hackers.' Each mobile player is assigned a specific role (Surveillance, Power, Decryption, distractions) to coordinate a massive facility breach.

title: OSTICE: Secure Testing Environment *
authors: Tae Soo Chun (BSCS) Jonathan Cardwell (BSCS) Jack Stringer (BSCS),
abstract: Secure coding environment, that allows users to take code based examinations without the opportunity for academic dishonesty.

suggestions.title: Color-Mixing Elemental Magic System
suggestions.desc: Develop a logic engine where players can combine primary 'color' spells (Red + Blue = Purple) to create hybrid effects. For example, mixing fire (red) and water (blue) magic creates a steam-based 'Choromage' AOE attack.

suggestions.title: Isometric Pathfinding for Dynamic Environments
suggestions.desc: Implement an A* pathfinding system that handles a changing open world where the player can 'paint' new paths or block enemies using color magic. The environment should react to the player's 'color footprint' by blooming or decaying.

suggestions.title: Choromage: Open World Creature Breeding
suggestions.desc: Add a system where fantasy creatures in the open world have 'color-coded' DNA. Players can use magic to influence the evolution of these creatures, breeding them for specific mounts or combat pets based on their hue.

suggestions.title: Global Lighting and Color Shifting Engine
suggestions.desc: Research and implement a global illumination system where the world's time-of-day affects magic potency. Sunset (orange/red) could buff certain spells while debuffing others, requiring strategic exploration.

title: Recreating Image Scaling Attacks + *
authors: Tara Vaghefi (BSCS) Michael Handlin (BSCS) Torie Umbdenstock (BSCS),
abstract: When a image is uploaded to a website, the source image is processed through a scalar and the image can change without the user knowing it. These websites use general scaling algorithms, however common scaling algorithms do not consider malicious inputs that may intentionally cause different visual results after scaling.

suggestions.title: Suction-Based Physics Combat Mechanics
suggestions.desc: Transform the racing game into a combat racer by allowing Roumbas to suck up debris from the track and fire it as projectiles. Use Unity's physics engine to calculate mass-based damage and suction force.

suggestions.title: Global Online Matchmaking and Ranking
suggestions.desc: Integrate Steamworks or Epic Online Services to transition from local party play to global multiplayer. Develop a 'Dusty League' ranking system where players earn cosmetic vacuum attachments for winning races.

suggestions.title: Smart-Home Hazard Map Generator
suggestions.desc: Build a level editor that uses computer vision to turn photos of real rooms into racing tracks. Players can upload a photo of their living room, and the game generates a 'Rumblin' Roumbas' map with furniture as obstacles.

suggestions.title: Reinforcement Learning for 'Perfect Path' Cleaners
suggestions.desc: Train AI racers using ML-Agents to find the most efficient cleaning path while racing. Research how these 'optimal cleaning' algorithms can be used to challenge human players in a 'high-score cleaning' game mode.

title: Telemedicine My Way *
authors: Coogan Koerts (BSCS) Alex Towers (BSCS) Macy Parker (BSCS), Vishal
abstract: A Telemedicine web application that allows patients to connect with doctors over the internet. It allows users to register, connect with a doctor, send messages back and forth to doctors, upload & reference reports, schedule appointments & have video calls with their physicians. This application was built from the ground up using Angular v8, Dotnet Core 2.2, Entity Framework Core & a SQL Database. This application has also been hosted on a LAMP server at www.telemedicinemyway.com.

suggestions.title: Browser-Based ECL Code Playground
suggestions.desc: Develop an interactive code editor using Monaco (the VS Code engine) that allows users to write and test HPCC Systems' ECL (Enterprise Control Language) code directly on the website. Use a backend container to compile and return results.

suggestions.title: HPCC Cluster Performance Visualizer
suggestions.desc: Create a D3.js data visualization tool that shows how big data is partitioned across a Thor or Roxy cluster. This helps students visualize the 'high-performance' aspect of LexisNexis technology beyond just writing code.

suggestions.title: Personalized ECL Learning Path API
suggestions.desc: Build a recommendation engine that analyzes a user's quiz performance and suggests specific HPCC tutorial modules. Use a 'knowledge graph' to map prerequisite skills for complex big data tasks.

suggestions.title: Gamified HPCC Certification Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Implement a full user authentication and badging system where LexisNexis employees can earn 'ECL Master' certifications. Include a leaderboard for completing coding challenges and a forum for peer-to-peer mentoring.

title: Lane Restoration Road Mask *
authors: Michelle Campbell (BSCS) Ahmad Frazier (BSCE) Yihong Li (MSCS),
abstract: The detection of lane marking is vital to the successful operation of an autonomous car. Without continuous maintenance, the road surface quality depreciates over time and use, making the lane markers difficult to discern. This experiment endeavors to apply traditional lane detection and object tracking/detection algorithms to create a virtual space of the car's environment. The data from the virtual space is used to calculate and generate an appropriate lane line mask. Finally, the original image and the road mask are merged into one image to verify the accuracy of the road map. This experiment is implemented in the CARLA simulator using Python.

suggestions.title: AI-Driven Compatibility Scoring
suggestions.desc: Replace basic filtering with a machine learning recommendation engine using Scikit-learn to analyze user lifestyle habits (sleep patterns, cleanliness, guest frequency) and generate a 'compatibility percentage' for potential matches.

suggestions.title: Trust-Verified Identity Vault
suggestions.desc: Implement a secure identity verification layer using the Stripe Identity API or Onfido to conduct background checks and ID verification, specifically catering to the safety concerns of the 40+ demographic.

suggestions.title: Senior-Centric Accessibility Audit
suggestions.desc: Conduct a formal UX research study focusing on WCAG 2.1 AAA compliance and implement specialized UI components for older adults, such as high-contrast modes, dynamic font scaling, and voice-assisted navigation.

suggestions.title: Community-Based Resource Exchange
suggestions.desc: Extend the platform to include a 'Skill & Tool Sharing' module where roommates can list shared resources or chores, using a React-based dashboard to manage household agreements and communal expenses.

title: IoT Threat Discovery + *
authors: Gregg Hammer (BSSWE) Paris Walters (BSSWE) Dewong Lucas (BSSWE),
abstract: Most companies have no clue what Internet of Things assets (IoT) exist on their network and how they’re communicating with the Internet. The common approach to IoT and asset discovery on most networks is commonly performed using one of a couple of methods: active discovery via NMAP or passive discovery via a SPAN port. While these are the most common and easiest ways to enumerate a network, they are also heavily error prone and unreliable in most implementations. Additionally, they pose additional problems in that for active discovery to work the device must be pingable, and for passive discovery to work a device must actively send IP packets. This often is not the case with IoT devices. IoT

suggestions.title: Pathology Simulation Module
suggestions.desc: Develop 'Disease Scenarios' where the user can witness the effects of Sickle Cell Anemia or Malaria on red blood cells first-hand. Use Unity's particle system to visualize how different pathogens interact with the bloodstream environment.

suggestions.title: Multiplayer Bio-Defense Minigame
suggestions.desc: Add a cooperative multiplayer layer using Unity Netcode where users play as white blood cells defending against an infection. This turns the educational experience into a high-stakes 'tower defense' game inside the body.

suggestions.title: Physically-Based Fluid Dynamics Integration
suggestions.desc: Replace basic animations with a Compute Shader-based fluid simulation to create realistic blood flow patterns around arterial bends and obstructions. This would involve implementing a simplified Navier-Stokes solver for real-time performance.

suggestions.title: Biometric Syncing for Immersive Biofeedback
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Polar H10 or Apple Watch API to sync the VR world’s pulse and flow speed with the user’s real-time heart rate. This creates a powerful biofeedback tool for teaching students about the relationship between activity and circulatory speed.

title: Idea Creator: Advanced Mind Mapping + *
authors: Samuel Mullinix (BSSWE) Justin Kelley (BSSWE) Clay Visscher (BSSWE),
abstract: This project is a web based software interface that allows for the collaborative exchange of ideas in order to facilitate the creation of new and interesting products, a concept otherwise known as "Mind Mapping". With this application, users can work collaboratively to expand upon the ideas of others and extrapolate new and exciting solutions. Since Idea Creator supports this overarching idea creation process, formally known as Innovation Engineering, it will provide a much needed niche functionality to create areas of new opportunity. Additionally, with the help of a lexical cognitive synonyms database, users can receive machine generated related concepts that will assist with the mind mapp

suggestions.title: Layer 2 Gas Optimization
suggestions.desc: Migrate the smart contract infrastructure to a Layer 2 solution like Polygon or Arbitrum to reduce transaction costs for room check-ins, making the system financially viable for high-turnover hotels.

suggestions.title: NFT-Based Loyalty Rewards
suggestions.desc: Transform room keys into dynamic NFTs (ERC-721) that evolve based on stay frequency, granting holders automated access to VIP lounges or late check-out perks via smart contract triggers.

suggestions.title: Timed Access for Coworking Spaces
suggestions.desc: Adapt the NFC-blockchain system for flexible office rentals by implementing 'Time-Locked' smart contracts that grant access only during specific hourly windows booked via a web interface.

suggestions.title: Multi-Sig Emergency Override
suggestions.desc: Develop a decentralized security protocol using multi-signature wallets that allows hotel staff to regain room access in emergencies without compromising the privacy and immutability of the guest's digital key.

title: IT Capstone NCR/USSS *
authors: Alex Philavong (BSIT) Oswaldo Armas (BSIT) Seth Carroll (BSIT), Lawson
abstract: Anti-Skimming solutions for NCR and USSS. Over the time of the semester, we created and tested 3 possible anti-skimming solutions.

suggestions.title: Cross-Vendor GPU Portability
suggestions.desc: Port the existing CUDA kernels to Vulkan or OpenCL to enable high-performance sequence analysis on non-NVIDIA hardware, such as AMD GPUs and Intel Arc integrated graphics.

suggestions.title: Tensor Core-Accelerated Smith-Waterman
suggestions.desc: Optimize the Smith-Waterman alignment algorithm specifically for NVIDIA Tensor Cores to achieve 10x throughput in local sequence alignment tasks compared to standard CUDA cores.

suggestions.title: Nextflow Pipeline Integration
suggestions.desc: Wrap the CUDA-accelerated tools into a containerized Nextflow or Snakemake workflow, allowing researchers to deploy GPU-heavy analysis across cloud-based HPC clusters like AWS Batch.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Genomic Visualization
suggestions.desc: Build a React-based frontend that uses WebGL to visualize the output of the CUDA analysis in real-time, allowing bioinformaticians to interactively explore DNA sequence matches as they are processed.

title: Salesforce CRM Exploration
authors: Erik Heissner (BSIT) Anderson Kongoda (BSIT) Barry Wisdoman (BSIT),
abstract: An exploration of uses for Salesforce CRM for the College of Continuing Professional Education to enhance the value of service.

suggestions.title: Temporal Flow Consistency
suggestions.desc: Integrate Optical Flow algorithms (like Farneback) to ensure that the 'Magic Eraser' mask remains stable across frames, eliminating the flickering effect common in frame-by-frame AI object removal.

suggestions.title: Privacy-First Dashcam Redactor
suggestions.desc: Repurpose the Mask R-CNN pipeline to automatically detect and blur faces and license plates in public dashcam footage, creating a tool for journalists and privacy-conscious content creators.

suggestions.title: Generative Inpainting Upgrade
suggestions.desc: Replace simple pixel-filling with a Stable Diffusion-based Inpainting model (using ControlNet) to realistically reconstruct complex backgrounds behind the removed objects.

suggestions.title: Browser-Based Inference via TensorFlow.js
suggestions.desc: Optimize the model for client-side execution using TensorFlow.js and WebGL, allowing users to perform video object removal directly in the browser without uploading heavy files to a server.

title: Mobile Health Security Risks +
authors: Gwyn Haley (BSIT) Marcus Parham (BSIT) Yusuf Yurt (BSIT), Chrysanthus
abstract: There has been numerous mobile health apps developed that can assess our health status, gather and process data, and integrate with EMR systems. Some apps are capable of analyzing images to accurately predict or monitor health conditions such as blood glucose level. This project will investigate (i) the typical apps that can identify health parameters, (ii) types of data they process, (iii) how errors, noises or attack data can alter their properties, (iv) how to safeguard the apps and sensors to prevent the privacy and security of individuals.

suggestions.title: FHIR Standard Interoperability
suggestions.desc: Develop a module that implements the HL7 FHIR (Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources) API, allowing this OpenMRS instance to exchange patient data with modern mobile health apps and Apple HealthKit.

suggestions.title: Low-Connectivity Offline Sync
suggestions.desc: Build a Progressive Web App (PWA) using PouchDB that allows clinicians in rural areas to record patient data offline and sync it with the OpenMRS MySQL backend once a connection is re-established.

suggestions.title: BioBERT-Powered Clinical Note NLP
suggestions.desc: Integrate a BioBERT model to automatically scan free-text clinical notes in OpenMRS and suggest ICD-10 diagnostic codes, reducing the manual administrative burden on healthcare providers.

suggestions.title: Embedded Telemedicine Portal
suggestions.desc: Integrate a WebRTC-based video conferencing module directly into the OpenMRS dashboard, enabling doctors to conduct remote consultations while simultaneously viewing the patient's electronic health record.

title: Data Analytics for CPE KSU *
authors: Faviola Guzman (BSIT) Jose Calderilla (BSIT) Brandon Woodard (BSIT),
abstract: The objective of this project is to find students’ journeys, trends, geographic locations and student types that are currently enrolled in the Professional Certificates to be able to market future students. The team will present a Tableau interactive dashboard, develop a salesforce executive dashboard that will exceed the expectations of the stakeholders and create SAS Studio datasets, graph and modeling, and forecasting reports. The information will be compiled together and presented in a final presentation that will include the Tableau interactive dashboard, salesforce dashboard, SAS reports, and a final report. The scope of the project includes planning, discovery, design, development, an

suggestions.title: Automated CI/CD AppImage Pipeline
suggestions.desc: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that automatically triggers a rebuild and test of the AppImages whenever the upstream bioinformatics tools (like BLAST or ClustalW) release an update.

suggestions.title: Virtualization Overhead Benchmarking
suggestions.desc: Conduct a performance study comparing these AppImages against Docker containers and native installations to quantify the I/O and CPU overhead for large-scale genomic sequence processing.

suggestions.title: Centralized 'Bio-Store' Portal
suggestions.desc: Build a web-based repository using a custom API that catalogs these AppImages, providing users with one-click downloads, version history, and user-submitted compatibility ratings for different Linux kernels.

suggestions.title: Immutable NixOS Lab Environment
suggestions.desc: Develop a NixOS configuration that uses these AppImages to create a 'Disposable Lab Environment' that can be instantly reproduced on any machine, ensuring consistent research results across different labs.

title: OpenVPN SDN Management System +
authors: Cory Simmons (BSSWE) Quanny Hampton (BSSWE) Jessica Muh (BSSWE),
abstract: The OpenVPN SDN Management System is a day-to-day virtual private network provider that will give its users a sense of internet security and privacy. By creating secure VPN tunnels, monitored by controlled endpoint firewall rules and maintaining a uni/bi-directional connectivity between different infrastructures, resources/packages may be requested and received on multiple different routers.

suggestions.title: Multi-Band Neural Analytics
suggestions.desc: Expand the EEG processing to analyze Alpha, Beta, and Theta wave ratios in real-time, providing users with more granular insights into 'Deep Focus' vs. 'Calmness' using the Muse SDK.

suggestions.title: EEG-Controlled Unity Game
suggestions.desc: Integrate the web app with a Unity-based WebGL game where the player’s character moves or gains power based on the 'Attention' score detected by the EEG headband.

suggestions.title: Longitudinal Lifestyle Correlation
suggestions.desc: Implement a data logging feature that uses AWS Timestream to correlate EEG focus scores with user-logged variables like sleep hours and caffeine intake to identify personal focus triggers.

suggestions.title: Therapist Remote Monitoring Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Build a HIPAA-compliant portal for clinicians to view patient focus trends over time, allowing them to adjust ADHD treatment plans based on objective neurofeedback data.

title: KSU Research Portal + *
authors: Ava Podrazhansky (BSCS) Cody Harrison (BSCS) Adnan Rashied (BSCS),
abstract: The Kennesaw State University Research Portal was designed to assist undergraduate students in finding research opportunities. Both professors and students can create projects and use the web app to find others to join the project. Users can also search for projects and request to join projects of interest. The web app was developed using React-Redux, Node JS, and Firebase.

suggestions.title: VisionOS Spatial Vision Board
suggestions.desc: Port the iOS application to VisionOS for Apple Vision Pro, allowing users to walk through an immersive 3D gallery of their goals and interact with vision board elements using hand gestures.

suggestions.title: Generative AI Image Synthesis
suggestions.desc: Integrate the DALL-E 3 API so users can use Siri to describe a goal (e.g., 'Siri, add a picture of a solar-powered cabin in the mountains') and have the image generated and placed on the board automatically.

suggestions.title: Semantic Goal Clustering
suggestions.desc: Use Apple’s Natural Language framework to analyze the text on vision boards and automatically cluster them into life categories like 'Financial,' 'Wellness,' or 'Career' for better organization.

suggestions.title: Affiliate-Linked Action Items
suggestions.desc: Integrate the Pinterest API or Amazon Associates API to automatically find physical products that match the images on a user's vision board, turning visualization into a direct shopping experience.

title: College of Professional Education D
authors: Robert Cox (BASIT) Andy Nicolaus (BASIT) Brandon Hubner (BSIT), Christina
abstract: The objective of this project is to use data analytics to gather valuable demographic and marketing data from students such as geographic locations, student types, and etc. that will be used to make business and marketing decisions for the College of Professional Education in reaching potential students.

suggestions.title: Universal Plugin Architecture for Music APIs
suggestions.desc: Refactor the backend using the Adapter design pattern to create a standardized interface for music services. This would allow the community to easily add support for Tidal, YouTube Music, and Deezer by simply implementing a 'Provider' class for each service's API.

suggestions.title: Cross-Platform Collaborative Syncing
suggestions.desc: Implement a 'Virtual Playlist' system where two users on different platforms (one Spotify, one Apple Music) can contribute to a single shared list. The app would use WebHooks to detect changes on one platform and automatically mirror the update to the other in real-time.

suggestions.title: Acoustic Fingerprinting for Missing Tracks
suggestions.desc: Integrate the AcoustID or Librosa library to handle tracks that fail metadata matching (title/artist). If a song isn't found by name, the app can analyze the audio characteristics to find the exact match on the target service, even if the metadata is formatted differently.

suggestions.title: Streaming Library Availability Analytics
suggestions.desc: Build a data analysis dashboard that tracks and visualizes 'transfer success rates' across platforms. This would provide insights into which genres or record labels are most frequently missing from certain services, offering valuable data on licensing gaps in the industry.

title: Mobile Health - SpO2
authors: Joseph Lackey (BSIT) Lara Oloruntoba (BASIT) Anthony Wimbush (BASIT),
abstract: This project focuses on the accuracy, security, and availability of mobile apps that monitor SpO2 levels. Mobile phone apps are vetted and tested to gather data. These apps are then analyzed and compared against one another to determine a recommendation for a full prototype to monitor SpO2 levels.

suggestions.title: Multimodal Acoustic Emotion Detection
suggestions.desc: Enhance the text-based ML by adding audio analysis using Librosa to detect emotional cues like pitch, jitter, and intensity from live requirements engineering interview recordings.

suggestions.title: Stakeholder Conflict Scoring
suggestions.desc: Develop an algorithm that identifies 'emotional dissonance'—flagging requirements where two different stakeholders express contradictory emotional sentiments (e.g., one is excited, one is frustrated).

suggestions.title: Interviewer Empathy Coach
suggestions.desc: Build a real-time dashboard that provides feedback to the requirements engineer during the interview, suggesting when to ask 'empathy-driven' follow-up questions based on the stakeholder's emotional state.

suggestions.title: Sentiment-Aware Jira Plugin
suggestions.desc: Create a Jira plugin that sentiment-analyzes user stories and comments to flag 'Toxic Requirements' or 'Vague User Feedback' that might lead to project scope creep or team friction.

title: Server Room Monitor *
authors: Jiaming Li (BSCS) Jiahao Chen (BSCS) Shikun Lyu (BSCS), Daniel Omuto
abstract: Server Room Monitor is a temperature, humidity and vibration detector implemented by a Raspberry Pi 3B+. It can send notification email and text message to administrators when the condition in server room is abnormal. It can also send graphed temperature and humidity data to the administrators for analyzing and controlling the condition in server room. A web-based GUI was designed to display real time data and collect user information.

suggestions.title: Predictive Brewing and Maintenance
suggestions.desc: Apply Scikit-learn to the temperature and weight data to predict when the heating element is likely to fail or when the pot requires descaling. Use the historical data to optimize the 'Keep Warm' cycle for energy efficiency.

suggestions.title: Automated Inventory and Supply Chain Integration
suggestions.desc: Add an RFID reader or a barcode scanner to the Raspberry Pi to track coffee bag usage. Use the Amazon Dash Replenishment API or a custom webhook to automatically reorder beans when the load cell detects the inventory is low.

suggestions.title: Office Productivity Suite Integration
suggestions.desc: Build a Slack and Microsoft Teams bot that allows employees to 'subscribe' to specific roasts and receive a direct message when a fresh pot is brewed. Allow remote brewing triggers via authenticated chat commands.

suggestions.title: Water Quality and TDS Monitoring
suggestions.desc: Integrate a Total Dissolved Solids (TDS) sensor to analyze water hardness and its effect on extraction quality. Build a data visualization dashboard showing the correlation between water purity and the 'Perfect Cup' rating as logged by users.

title: Cyber Forensic Discovery with A.I. + *
authors: Anthony Sarkis (BSSWE) Evan Bennett (BSSWE) Gianni Orlando (BSSWE),
abstract: An automated computer forensics tool that can gather event log data from a computer that has been infected with a virus or malware, all without leaving a trace in the filesystem, and uses that data to look for any indications of a compromise using an Artificial Intelligence.

suggestions.title: FinBERT Sentiment Optimization
suggestions.desc: Replace general-purpose NLP models with HuggingFace’s FinBERT, specifically trained on financial communication. Fine-tune the model using the 'Crypto-Sentiment' dataset from Kaggle to better capture crypto-specific slang and market nuances.

suggestions.title: Multi-Exchange Arbitrage Bot
suggestions.desc: Integrate the sentiment signals with the CCXT library to monitor price discrepancies between Binance and Coinbase. Build a bot that executes trades when high-sentiment spikes coincide with price lags between exchanges.

suggestions.title: Multimodal Price Predictor
suggestions.desc: Combine the Twitter sentiment scores with technical indicators (RSI, MACD) and on-chain metrics like Whale Alerts using an Ensemble Learning approach. This reduces false positives caused by social media 'noise' that doesn't reflect actual market liquidity.

suggestions.title: NFT Collection Hype Tracker
suggestions.desc: Pivot the system to analyze Discord and OpenSea activity for specific NFT collections. Use the sentiment engine to predict 'floor price' movements based on community engagement levels rather than just historical sales.

title: Computer Vision for Fall Detection *
authors: Ryan Taylor (BSIT) Elise Maloney (BSIT) Dion Agbontaen (BSIT), Marquez
abstract: The goal of this project is to aid elderly patients in nursing homes by building a fall detection device. We are using a Raspberry Pi with a webcam to capture footage that is then sent to a server for processing two machine learning algorithms.The first algorithm is OpenPose, which takes an image as an input and outputs 25 X,Y coordinates of various body parts. These coordinates are then passed to our own XGBoost classifier that we trained to detect falls and non-falls. If a fall is detected, the Raspberry Pi sends an email and a text message to the nursing home staff.

suggestions.title: Persistent Character Growth via NFC Rewriting
suggestions.desc: Implement NTAG215 write capabilities to store player stats and experience directly on the physical cards. This enables a 'carry-over' mechanic where a physical card effectively becomes a 'memory card' for a character, allowing it to level up across different game sessions.

suggestions.title: Mobile Companion App with CoreNFC
suggestions.desc: Develop an iOS/Android companion app using CoreNFC or Android NFC API that allows players to scan their Wildling Rumble cards on their personal phones. This removes the need for a dedicated hardware reader and allows for 'hidden' actions, like checking secret stats privately.

suggestions.title: Augmented Reality (AR) Unit Visualization
suggestions.desc: Integrate Vuforia or ARKit to recognize the physical NFC cards through a camera view. When a player scans a card, a 3D animated model of the character would appear on top of the physical card, bridging the gap between digital combat and the physical tabletop.

suggestions.title: Multi-Device Synchronous Tabletop State
suggestions.desc: Use WebSockets (Socket.io) to synchronize the game state across multiple tablets or laptops around a table. Scanning a card on one player’s device would instantly update the 'world view' on a central screen, creating a shared digital-physical play space.

title: C# to Java Cross-Compiler *
authors: Harriet Haisty (BSCS) Bayleigh Correll (BSCS) Adam Gonglach (BSCS),
abstract: This project is a web-based C# to Java cross-compiler. It takes in a user's source .cs or .txt file, runs it through a backend translator, and then produces the converted code in an embedded code editor with correct spacing and syntax highlighting. The front end of the application is written in JavaScript, CSS, and HTML, with elements of AJAX. The backend is written entirely in Java. The object of this project is to convert C# code into Java code that can run on any machine.

suggestions.title: Dynamic Competitive Pricing Engine
suggestions.desc: Build a real-time price monitoring tool using Selenium to scrape Amazon and Costco daily. Implement a pricing algorithm that suggests the 'sweet spot' markup for smaller wholesalers to remain competitive while maintaining margins.

suggestions.title: Customer Segmentation Clustering
suggestions.desc: Apply K-Means or DBSCAN clustering to the purchase data to identify distinct buyer personas, such as 'Bulk Resellers' vs 'Individual Consumers.' Use these clusters to generate personalized inventory recommendations for different business types.

suggestions.title: Automated ETL Pipeline with Airflow
suggestions.desc: Transition the static data analysis to a live cloud pipeline using Apache Airflow. Automate the extraction of wholesale data into an AWS Redshift warehouse to provide a continuously updating dashboard instead of a one-time report.

suggestions.title: Supply Chain Vulnerability Map
suggestions.desc: Integrate geographical data to map out the distribution centers of both companies. Use this to analyze regional supply chain resilience, identifying areas where a localized disaster would disrupt Costco or Amazon deliveries more severely.

title: App Refactor and IoT Integrations +
authors: Edgar Pineda (BSSWE) Tanner Jones (BSSWE) Johnny Santana (BSSWE),
abstract: We have been tasked to refactor and improve an existing architecture for an application and to implement new integrations such as WatchOS, Alexa Skills, and SMS abilities.

suggestions.title: Headless Browser Vulnerability Scan
suggestions.desc: Integrate Playwright or Selenium into the WASP core to scan modern Single Page Applications (React/Vue). This allows the tool to detect vulnerabilities in Client-Side Rendering that traditional static scanners often miss.

suggestions.title: DevSecOps CI/CD Integration
suggestions.desc: Develop a GitHub Action that triggers a WASP scan on every Pull Request. Set up a gateway policy that automatically blocks deployments if the scan detects any 'Critical' OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities.

suggestions.title: LLM-Powered Exploit Payload Generator
suggestions.desc: Use the OpenAI API to generate context-aware XSS and SQL injection payloads based on the specific HTML attributes found by the scanner. This moves the project from generic pattern matching to intelligent, adaptive penetration testing.

suggestions.title: Security Reporting as a Service
suggestions.desc: Build a web-based management portal where users can schedule scans and receive professional PDF security audits. Use a subscription-based model and integrate Stripe for a ready-to-market SaaS cybersecurity tool.

title: ELSYS Software Center + *
authors: Bryan Firestone (BSIT) Sarah Brown (BSIT) Hunter Brumelow (BSIT), Justin
abstract: This project addresses concerns raised by a Sponsor regarding inefficiencies in managing CCSE capstone projects. Key pain points include organization, project management, and communication among stakeholders. The proposed solution involves implementing Customer Relationship Management (CRM) software. Upon gathering requirements from the Sponsor, including contact information capture, workflow management, document control, and customization capabilities, we evaluated several CRM platforms. A total of thirty-two CRMs were reviewed and Vtiger, OroCRM, and SuiteCRM were selected for testing. SuiteCRM was chosen for its comprehensive features and user-friendliness. The second step of the project involved testing SuiteCRM functionalities on a dedicated server, leading to its selection for deployment preparation. Final steps include documentation development, training material creation, and incorporating user feedback for optimal system configuration.

suggestions.title: Automated Capstone Lifecycle Middleware
suggestions.desc: Develop a middleware service using Node.js or Python that connects the proposed CRM with GitHub and Jira APIs to automatically provision project repositories and kanban boards once a sponsor proposal is approved.

suggestions.title: Student-Sponsor Talent Matching Portal
suggestions.desc: Build a front-end interface that pulls student skills and interests from the CRM to create a 'matching' algorithm, allowing sponsors to see which student teams best align with their project requirements.

suggestions.title: Predictive Project Risk Analysis
suggestions.desc: Implement a sentiment analysis module using Python's NLTK to analyze communication logs and project status updates within the CRM, flagging projects that exhibit signs of stakeholder misalignment.

suggestions.title: Sponsor Retention & Engagement Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Create a data visualization suite using PowerBI or Tableau that tracks longitudinal sponsor engagement data, helping the college identify which industries provide the most successful long-term partnerships.

title: Do Not Disturb +
authors: Suzanna Carter (BSSWE) Cody Mullin (BSSWE) Joseph Nguyen (BSSWE),
abstract: The purpose of this system is to allow conferences to run more effortlessly and efficiently. The software will allow users to know when the conference is about to end and see whether the room is available or not. It will accomplish that goal by having a kiosk outside of the conference room as well as a notification in the meeting room close to the ending time.

suggestions.title: Multi-Objective Route Optimization
suggestions.desc: Implement a Genetic Algorithm to optimize travel routes based on multiple constraints like 'scenic beauty,' 'low traffic,' and 'minimal walking.' Use the Google Places API to fetch 'busyness' data to help users avoid crowds.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Group Itinerary Synchronization
suggestions.desc: Add a collaborative feature using WebSockets (Socket.io) that allows groups of travelers to vote on activities and see each other's live locations on the shared map. This ensures everyone stays on the 'What's Next' schedule.

suggestions.title: Offline-First Exploration Mode
suggestions.desc: Integrate MapBox offline maps and SQLite local storage to allow users to navigate their custom lists in areas with no cellular data. Build a sync engine that pushes updates to the cloud once a connection is re-established.

suggestions.title: Sentiment-Based Activity Discovery
suggestions.desc: Develop a recommendation engine that scrapes TripAdvisor and Yelp reviews and uses BERT-based NLP to categorize locations by 'vibe' (e.g., 'cozy,' 'chaotic,' 'historic'). Match these to the user's current mood input.

title: Glory in Death *
authors: Tyler Hocker (BSCGDD) Tim Hawkins (BSCGDD) Mary Ivester (BSCGDD),
abstract: Glory in Death is a stunning, heart-pounding arena fighter where players take control of one of our two valiant adventurers, Aura and Halfred, as they battle for their survival against hordes of vicious orcs and goblins. Players will be able to defend themselves with a combination of deadly weapon attacks and special powers unique to each character. As the player progresses they will be able to level up their characters and better equip them to take on stronger and stronger enemies.

suggestions.title: Procedural Dungeon Architecture via Perlin Noise
suggestions.desc: Replace static arenas with a procedural generation system using Perlin Noise or Cellular Automata in Unity/Unreal to create infinite, unique combat layouts every time the player starts a run.

suggestions.title: Reinforcement Learning Enemy AI
suggestions.desc: Utilize Unity ML-Agents to train the orc and goblin enemies to adapt to the player’s combat style, teaching them when to retreat, flank, or use special abilities based on Aura and Halfred’s health.

suggestions.title: Asynchronous Global Leaderboard and Ghost Racing
suggestions.desc: Implement a Firebase-backed leaderboard that stores player 'ghost' data (replays of their best runs), allowing users to compete against the recorded movement patterns of top players.

suggestions.title: Deterministic Lockstep Multiplayer
suggestions.desc: Transform the single-player experience into a co-op game using the Mirror networking library, implementing client-side prediction to ensure smooth combat despite network latency.

title: Blast Off! + *
authors: Emily Strube (BSCGDD) Harrison Hopkins (BSCGDD) Terry Taylor (BSCGDD),
abstract: Hate feeling grounded? Gravity got you down? Then try "Blast Off!" by KeySmash Studios! "Blast Off!" is a game about traveling further into space than anybody before you. Start as a humble rocket enthusiast and work your way up to the stars! Each flight grants you the money you will need to upgrade and buy rockets to get yourself further and further into the final frontier! Escape Earth and embrace the great unknown, with "Blast Off!"

suggestions.title: Canvas LMS Data Auto-Population
suggestions.desc: Utilize the Canvas API to automatically pull student performance data, grades, and learning outcome assessments directly into the FCAR form. This eliminates the manual data entry bottleneck for faculty and ensures 100% data integrity from the source.

suggestions.title: Cross-Semester Learning Outcome Trend Analysis
suggestions.desc: Build an analytics engine that aggregates data from multiple FCARs over several years. Use Chart.js to visualize how specific ABET Student Outcomes (SOs) are trending, allowing the department head to identify if a curriculum change in 2022 actually improved student performance in 2024.

suggestions.title: NLP-Based Qualitative Sentiment Summarizer
suggestions.desc: Apply Natural Language Processing to the 'Faculty Reflections' and 'Proposed Actions' sections of the reports. The system could generate a summary report of common challenges (e.g., '50% of faculty noticed students struggled with Calculus III concepts') to inform department-wide changes.

suggestions.title: Dockerized Multi-Tenant Accreditation SaaS
suggestions.desc: Refactor the local Linux application into a containerized Docker architecture. This allows the tool to be offered as a 'Lab-in-a-Box' for other university departments beyond CCSE, supporting custom accreditation standards beyond just ABET.

title: Bank Deposit Subscription Report
authors: Hui Xia (MSCS)
abstract: In response to the demand of direct marketing strategy, the model in this project for bank direct marketing is to predict whether customers will subscribe the deposits with the bank by identifying potential customers. Based on the model analysis, bank can manage the marketing methods such as number of contacts, interval of contacts or selection of recipients. Additionally, bank can alleviate the risks of adverse effect of marketing from the groups who do not want to receive advertising by not contacting them. Hence, this model which can forecast the likelihood of deposit subscriptions can be profitable to the banks for improving their marketing campaign strategies.

suggestions.title: Zero-Trust IoT Mesh Implementation
suggestions.desc: Secure the home automation network by implementing an MQTT broker with TLS encryption. Require per-device certificates to ensure that even if one smart device is compromised, the rest of the Android-controlled network remains safe.

suggestions.title: Energy-Efficient Automation Logic
suggestions.desc: Integrate the OpenWeather API to create 'Smart Eco-Modes.' Build logic that automatically adjusts smart blinds and thermostats based on external temperature and forecasted sunlight to minimize HVAC energy consumption.

suggestions.title: Privacy-First Local Voice Control
suggestions.desc: Add a voice command module using an on-device engine like Rhasspy or Snips. This allows users to control their home via the Android app using voice without sending sensitive audio data to Amazon or Google servers.

suggestions.title: Occupancy Pattern Prediction
suggestions.desc: Apply a Recurrent Neural Network (RNN) to the device usage logs to learn the user’s daily routine. The app can then proactively suggest lighting or temperature changes before the user even enters a room.

title: Fashion Item Detection
authors: Eun Hye Kim (MSCS) Seunghyeon Shin (MSCS) Yong Min Ju (MSCS), Luke
abstract: Fashion Item Detection project is to detect fashion items in an image using image hierarchical segmentation, define types of fashion items and their features. We used a practical deep fully convolutional neural network architecture for semantic pixel-wise segmentation called SegNet. This architecture has an encoder and decoder. The encoder is identical to the convolutional layers in the VGG 16 network. The decoder is to map the low- resolution input feature maps to full input resolution feature maps to classify in pixel-wise. The advantage of SegNet is that the decoder upsamples a lower resolution input feature map by performing non-linear upsampling using pooling indices by eliminating the…

suggestions.title: Real-Time User Interest Recommender
suggestions.desc: Use the implemented clustering algorithms to group users based on their clickstream data in real-time. Connect this to a Flask API that serves personalized 'Recommended for You' content based on the active cluster the user belongs to.

suggestions.title: Interactive 3D Cluster Visualization
suggestions.desc: Utilize Three.js and t-SNE (t-Distributed Stochastic Neighbor Embedding) to create an interactive web dashboard. Users can explore high-dimensional web data clusters in a 3D space to visually identify outliers and patterns.

suggestions.title: Bot Traffic Anomaly Detector
suggestions.desc: Repurpose the clustering logic to detect non-human behavior in server logs. By clustering requests based on timing and IP headers, the system can automatically flag botnets or scrapers that deviate from human-user clusters.

suggestions.title: Distributed Clustering with PySpark
suggestions.desc: Scale the project to handle big data by porting the clustering algorithms to a Spark environment. Test the system’s performance on a massive dataset, such as the Common Crawl web index, using AWS EMR for distributed processing.

title: privacy and trust in VANET
authors: Seunghyeon Shin (MSCS)
abstract: Vehicular Ad Hoc Network is a subclass of mobile network that provides enhanced driving experience through the provision of roadside assistance and safety information to participating nodes. There exists multiple types of malicious threats that target the privacy of nodes, manipulating messages and many more. In this research we propose an improved VANET scheme for enhanced privacy and trust management.

suggestions.title: ARCore Augmented Reality Tag
suggestions.desc: Enhance the 'laser' aspect by using Google ARCore to render virtual beams and shields in the phone's camera view. Players can 'see' their shots and hit markers superimposed on the real world during gameplay.

suggestions.title: Dead Reckoning for GPS dead-zones
suggestions.desc: Implement a sensor fusion algorithm that uses the phone’s accelerometer and gyroscope to estimate player movement (Dead Reckoning). This ensures the game remains playable even when GPS signal drops between tall buildings.

suggestions.title: Gamified Urban Fitness League
suggestions.desc: Transform the game into a fitness platform where points are earned for distance run and calories burned during a match. Integrate with Google Fit to track long-term stats and create a leaderboard based on athletic performance.

suggestions.title: Low-Latency WebRTC Multiplayer
suggestions.desc: Replace standard HTTP requests with WebRTC data channels for peer-to-peer communication. This reduces the latency of 'tag' registration to near-instantaneous levels, critical for a fast-paced action game.

title: Refinement Neural Network Smart Car + *
authors: Bhavana Pateriya (MSCS) Sowmya Kari (MSCS) Neha Rajendra Gosavi
abstract: In recent years, smart cars have drawn much interest as a topic of research for both academia and industry. For a car to be a truly smart, it must make sense of the environment through which it is driving. The process of path planning and autonomous vehicle guidance depends on three things: localization, mapping, and tracking objects. We have focused on tracking of the objects. The proposed algorithm is developed using information from a camera. The images from the camera is used to detect and classify the object. The objects in the image are detected using Deep Neural Networks (DNN).The current DNN detectors of state-of-the-art can be divided into two categories: the two-stage approach, and

suggestions.title: Predictive Food Scarcity Forecasting
suggestions.desc: Integrate Meta’s Prophet library to perform time-series forecasting on the historical GIS data. Add a 'Future' toggle to the heatmap that shows predicted food security levels over the next 5 years based on current climate trends.

suggestions.title: Policy Impact Simulation Tool
suggestions.desc: Add an interactive 'What If' panel where users can adjust variables like 'Grain Export Tax' or 'Rainfall Decrease.' Use a simple linear regression model to visualize how these hypothetical policy changes would shift the heatmap colors.

suggestions.title: Vector-Tile Mapbox Migration
suggestions.desc: Migrate the visualization to Mapbox GL JS to enable smooth zooming from global views down to individual province-level data. Use vector tiles to overlay secondary datasets like infrastructure density or population growth over the food security map.

suggestions.title: Hyper-Local Food Desert Mapper
suggestions.desc: Pivot the data focus from global countries to a city-level view. Use the USDA Food Environment Atlas dataset to map 'food deserts' in a specific metro area, highlighting neighborhoods with limited access to fresh produce.

title: Battling Bots with Random Forests + *
authors: Derek Willingham (MSCS) Christopher Sefcik (MSCS) Colton Thompson
abstract: Bots and fake profiles are the scourge of modern day social media. We investigate the benefits machine learning, more specifically Random Forests classifiers, can have for combating these dastardly robots.

suggestions.title: Offline-First Progressive Web App
suggestions.desc: Convert the portal into a PWA with Workbox to allow technicians to log maintenance tickets in underground parking decks where WiFi is unavailable. The app should automatically sync data to the database once a connection is restored.

suggestions.title: Computer Vision Plate Detection
suggestions.desc: Integrate OpenALPR (Automatic License Plate Recognition) using the parking lot cameras. Link this to the maintenance portal so that if a vehicle is parked in a 'Maintenance Zone' without a permit, an automated ticket is generated for the staff.

suggestions.title: Maintenance Route Optimizer
suggestions.desc: Implement a route optimization algorithm using the Google Maps Directions API. The portal can then provide technicians with the most efficient walking or driving path to address all 'High Priority' tickets across the Georgia Tech campus.

suggestions.title: IoT Kiosk Health Monitoring
suggestions.desc: Connect the portal to the physical parking kiosks via an API. Use basic anomaly detection to flag kiosks that haven't processed a transaction in over an hour, automatically creating a maintenance ticket for a 'Potential Hardware Failure'.

title: Text Semantics Representation + *
authors: Karl Kevin Tiba Fossoh (MSCS)
abstract: The capacity to convert text into images is possible through the usage of generative adversarial networks. The problem is the experimental nature of the procedure and the little research made to understand how text can be improved to generate better images. The goal of this research was to provide an in-depth analysis of the impact of coherence and summarization models applied to complex texts to improve the end result of GAN models.

suggestions.title: Student Study-Space Locator
suggestions.desc: Create a public-facing React dashboard that uses WiFi usage density to tell students which campus buildings are currently quietest. Provide a 'Real-Time Capacity' percentage for popular spots like the KSU library based on connected device counts.

suggestions.title: Grafana & InfluxDB Observability Stack
suggestions.desc: Replace the basic dashboard with an InfluxDB time-series database and a Grafana front-end. This allows for professional-grade monitoring of packet loss, signal-to-noise ratios, and jitter over months of campus operation.

suggestions.title: Rogue Access Point Detection
suggestions.desc: Extend the Raspberry Pi monitoring logic to scan for 'Evil Twin' access points. Use Scapy to analyze beacon frames and alert IT staff if an unauthorized device is broadcasting a spoofed KSU SSID to harvest student credentials.

suggestions.title: Predictive Network Congestion Alerts
suggestions.desc: Apply an LSTM (Long Short-Term Memory) network to historical WiFi traffic data. Build a notification system that alerts the KSU network team via Slack when congestion is predicted to exceed 90% during peak events like finals week.

title: MBUSA Internship
authors: Karl Kevin Tiba Fossoh (MSCS)
abstract: This internship took place during the fall semester and is to be completed before the end of December 2019. It took place within the IT Innovation Hub of MBUSA and had as the main focus to create new and disruptive technologies while following agile methodologies and reputed user-centered design techniques.

suggestions.title: YOLO-based Real-time Tire Wear Detection
suggestions.desc: Extend the aircraft classifier to not only identify the plane type but also detect visible tire defects or excessive wear using YOLOv8. This provides a secondary safety check for tug operators during the 'walk-around' phase of towing.

suggestions.title: AR HUD for Tug Alignment
suggestions.desc: Develop an Augmented Reality (AR) interface for tug drivers that overlays a 'safe-zone' path based on the detected aircraft type. The system should calculate the specific turning radius and nose-gear weight limits of the identified plane to prevent structural damage.

suggestions.title: Synthetic Training via Simulators
suggestions.desc: Use Microsoft Flight Simulator or X-Plane to generate thousands of high-fidelity images of various aircraft tires under different lighting and weather conditions. Compare the accuracy of a model trained on these synthetic images versus one trained on the limited real-world airport dataset.

suggestions.title: Automated Logbook & Maintenance Integration
suggestions.desc: Link the CV classifier to an airport's maintenance database (MRO). When a tug vehicle identifies an aircraft, it should automatically pull up the tire pressure history and log the tow event, ensuring that airframe cycles are tracked without manual data entry.

title: Table Order Management System + *
authors: Vincent Ogonor (MSSWE) Abdulaziz Houbani (MSSWE) Robert Hennings
abstract: This software is one of our current projects in development for a client. It is aimed at restaurants to allow them to manage, update and keep track of orders. Our software Solution for restaurants would allow you to place an order through a tablet (iPad mini). With this software, Pay-at-the-table gives customers a sense of security, knowing that they keep control of their credit card and its financial information. It reduces trips back and forth to the POS terminal, which in turn decreases the time it takes to close the sale. This lets tables wrap up their meal and get back to their day quicker and reduces friction due to split checks or servers multitasking while payments are being made. Ou

suggestions.title: Dynamic Retail Pricing Engine
suggestions.desc: Build a pricing optimization tool that uses the predicted customer behavior to suggest real-time discounts. If the model predicts a high likelihood of a customer purchasing a high-margin item, the engine could suggest 'bundle' pricing for lower-performing inventory items to increase the average basket size.

suggestions.title: Graph-Based Market Basket Analysis
suggestions.desc: Implement a Graph Neural Network (GNN) to map the relationships between product categories during Black Friday. This can identify 'bridge products' that, when purchased, lead to significantly higher spending in unrelated categories, helping retailers optimize floor layout.

suggestions.title: Black Friday 'Panic' Sentiment Correlation
suggestions.desc: Scrape social media data (Twitter/X) from the same timeframe as the Black Friday dataset. Use NLP to correlate regional sentiment and 'deal-hunting' trends with actual sales volumes to predict how social hype impacts purchasing spikes in real-time.

suggestions.title: Customer Lifetime Value (CLV) Forecasting
suggestions.desc: Extend the analysis to predict which Black Friday 'one-time buyers' are likely to become repeat customers. Use a Beta-Geometric/Negative Binomial Distribution (BG/NBD) model to identify the demographic markers of high-value loyalists vs. discount-only shoppers.

title: Azure API for Data Analysis Power BI + *
authors: kavyasree Bezugam (MSCS) Nikitha Gaddamidi (MSCS)
abstract: We create Azure APIs for feeding the data of the client into CosmoDB and Logic app to send the analyzed data with required fields to power BI for analyzing the data. The Power BI dashboard built for the client gives the detail reports to enhance his business and track his business. Example if we have a Education organization like Kennesaw state university we would built dashboard for analyzing Area of improvements.

suggestions.title: Explainable AI (XAI) for Loan Transparency
suggestions.desc: Apply SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) or LIME to the Santander prediction model to identify which specific transaction features most influence a 'future transaction' prediction. This helps in building a transparent banking model that complies with 'Right to Explanation' regulations.

suggestions.title: Imbalanced Data Synthesis with CTGAN
suggestions.desc: Address the typical class imbalance in financial transaction datasets by using Conditional GANs (CTGAN) to generate high-fidelity synthetic 'positive' transaction instances. Compare the resulting model's AUC-ROC score against standard oversampling techniques like SMOTE.

suggestions.title: Real-time Transaction Scoring API
suggestions.desc: Deploy the trained model as a containerized Flask or FastAPI microservice. Build a front-end simulation that streams dummy transaction data and visualizes the 'Probability of Future Transaction' in real-time, simulating a bank's CRM alert system for account managers.

suggestions.title: Automated Feature Engineering with TSFresh
suggestions.desc: Use the TSFresh library to automatically extract hundreds of time-series features (like rolling means, peaks, and Fourier coefficients) from the transaction data. Evaluate if these engineered temporal features provide a significant lift in precision compared to the original Kaggle features.
