title: Wi-Fi Availability Monitoring
authors: Ruth Barraza (MSIT) Matthew Reese (MSIT) Tammara Johnson (MSIT), Tonya
abstract: Wireless has become a critical IT service, however the ability of providers to monitor the service has not matured at the same rate. The emergence of low-cost wireless devices has created an opportunity for building a system that aggregates data from many distributed data collectors.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Occupancy Integration via Computer Vision
suggestions.desc: Upgrade the static allocation algorithm by integrating a live video feed processed with YOLOv8. Use the visual data to update the parking graph in real-time, allowing the algorithm to reroute vehicles dynamically if a spot is unexpectedly blocked or taken.

suggestions.title: Dynamic Demand-Based Pricing Engine
suggestions.desc: Develop a microservice that adjusts parking rates based on the algorithm's density metrics and peak hour predictions. Use historical parking data to build a pricing model that incentivizes distribution to underutilized zones, similar to Uber's surge pricing.

suggestions.title: EV-Aware Optimization with Charging Constraints
suggestions.desc: Extend the graph model to prioritize Electric Vehicles (EVs) for spots with charging infrastructure while minimizing cable-length conflicts. Incorporate a 'wait-list' logic that re-allocates chargers as soon as a vehicle reaches a target battery percentage.

suggestions.title: Multi-Floor 3D Pathfinding for Complex Structures
suggestions.desc: Transform the 2D allocation into a 3D environment using A* or Dijkstra’s algorithm tailored for multi-story garages. Include variables for ramp steepness and ceiling height to accommodate specific vehicle types like delivery vans or SUVs.

title: The Conference Room of the Future + *
authors: Sai Kasthala (MSIT) Shayan Shamskolahi (MSIT) Ashim Thapa (MSIT),
abstract: Provide conference room of the future.... may include AI voice capability and automation that can control the technology and lighting/monitors in the room. May include a kiosk type reservation system that is associated with each conference room that can display if the room is in use, allow reservation of that room, and checking the availability. It may also include IoT type sensory to detect utilization of the room, and if it is under or over capacity, may notify the meeting organizer and make suggestions for alternatives (doing lookups of other available conference rooms in the facility). Automated alerts could also notify participants and organizers that the meeting is coming to a close so

suggestions.title: Multi-Speaker Diarization for Group Discussions
suggestions.desc: Incorporate a speaker diarization library like Pyannote.audio to distinguish between a professor and student questions. This allows the system to label the transcript appropriately, preventing confusion during fast-paced classroom interactions.

suggestions.title: STEM-Specific Vocabulary Fine-Tuning
suggestions.desc: Fine-tune the underlying LLM (like Whisper or GPT-4o) using university-specific datasets of technical jargon from engineering or medical lectures. This ensures that complex terms like 'stochastic' or 'laparoscopic' are translated accurately rather than phonetically.

suggestions.title: AR Glasses Captioning Overlay
suggestions.desc: Build a mobile interface that streams the translated text to Nreal or Vuzix AR glasses. This provides a 'heads-up' display for hearing-impaired students, allowing them to watch the professor and read captions simultaneously without looking down at a screen.

suggestions.title: Low-Resource Dialect Support for International Students
suggestions.desc: Extend the system to support low-resource languages or specific regional dialects using Zero-Shot Machine Translation. Use the FLORES-200 dataset to evaluate and improve translation accuracy for minority languages often ignored by mainstream tools.

project_key: legacy:GC-12|Fall 2019|the conference room of the future + *

title: Phishing Awareness Training App *
authors: Travis Brown (MSIT) Sammy Box (MSIT) Nicholas Moore (MSIT), Quincy
abstract: The Phishing Awareness Training application, created for KSU UITS, is a web application designed to increase awareness around common phishing threat indicators. The application presents users with sample phishing emails, lessons, and assessment questions, with feedback, to test knowledge gained from the training.

suggestions.title: Reinforcement Learning for 'Buy vs. Wait' Signals
suggestions.desc: Apply Q-Learning or Deep Q-Networks (DQN) to the existing predictive model to generate a binary 'Buy Now' or 'Wait' recommendation. The agent would be rewarded for maximizing savings compared to the average price of the route.

suggestions.title: Digital Nomad Multi-City Route Optimizer
suggestions.desc: Expand the tool to predict prices for 'Open-Jaw' or multi-city itineraries where the sequence of cities affects the total cost. Use a genetic algorithm to find the cheapest dates and sequence for a 3-4 city trip based on the price prediction curves.

suggestions.title: Price Drop Alert & Refund Monitoring Service
suggestions.desc: Build a monitoring bot that tracks purchased tickets and alerts the user if the price drops below a certain threshold. Integrate with airline 'Price Match' policies or the 24-hour cancellation window to automate the refund/rebooking process.

suggestions.title: External Factor Correlation Analysis
suggestions.desc: Integrate non-price features into the model like global fuel price indices, major sporting events (via SerpApi), and weather patterns. Research how much 'External Volatility' impacts prediction accuracy compared to simple historical price trends.

title: Determining Human Emotions *
authors: Kevin Markley (MSCS)
abstract: Comparing run-time performances and accuracy of several AI classifiers, in similar implementations, in determining human emotions from facial images.

suggestions.title: Explainable AI (XAI) for Customer Retention
suggestions.desc: Implement SHAP or LIME within the Spark pipeline to provide specific reasons 'why' a customer is predicted to churn. The end result is a dashboard for account managers that suggests personalized retention offers (e.g., 'Discount offered due to high call-failure rate').

suggestions.title: Real-Time Streaming Prediction with Kafka
suggestions.desc: Convert the batch-processing model into a streaming architecture using Spark Structured Streaming and Apache Kafka. This allows the business to detect churn signals (like a failed payment or multiple support tickets) and trigger an automated response within minutes.

suggestions.title: Sentiment-Infused Feature Engineering
suggestions.desc: Use NLP to analyze customer support logs or Twitter mentions and add a 'Sentiment Score' as a feature in the ML model. This tests the hypothesis that emotional frustration is a stronger predictor of churn than technical usage metrics.

suggestions.title: Federated Learning for Cross-Industry Churn
suggestions.desc: Research a federated learning approach where multiple telecom companies can train a global churn model without sharing sensitive PII (Personally Identifiable Information). Use a framework like OpenMined to ensure data privacy while improving model robustness.

title: Detecting Pedestrians - Skin Tones + *
authors: Rehma Razzak (MSCS) Jackson Randolph Randolph (MSCS)
abstract: Because many applications of data science can in-inadvertently create bias, it is also possible that this bias can be present in pedestrian detection, especially on racial lines.We explore the different techniques (using supervised learning)that can be used to detect pedestrians in order to figure out which ones may be the least biased and we explore ways(using unsupervised learning) to automatically measure skin-tone in pedestrian data sets in order to benchmark potential algorithms. We concluded that a You Only Look Once Convolutional Neural Network should be the most robust method of pedestrian detection, and that it was likely a skin tone classifier could be created using deep feature ex

suggestions.title: Multi-Omics Data Integration
suggestions.desc: Extend the Random Lasso implementation to handle heterogeneous datasets, combining genomics, proteomics, and metabolomics data. The goal is to identify regulatory links that span across different biological layers using a unified Spark job.

suggestions.title: GPU-Accelerated Spark with RAPIDS
suggestions.desc: Benchmark the Spark CPU implementation against a GPU-accelerated version using the NVIDIA RAPIDS library. Compare the cost-efficiency and training speed on high-dimensional datasets like the UK Biobank to determine the scalability limits of each architecture.

suggestions.title: SaaS Platform for High-Dimensional Bioinformatics
suggestions.desc: Develop a cloud-native web portal where researchers can upload CSV/VCF files and run the parallel Lasso algorithm. Use Kubernetes to spin up transient Spark clusters on-demand, providing a simple UI for non-CS researchers to view gene networks.

suggestions.title: Drug Sensitivity Prediction for Personalized Medicine
suggestions.desc: Apply the parallelized Random Lasso model to the Genomics of Drug Sensitivity in Cancer (GDSC) dataset. Predict which gene expressions most strongly correlate with drug resistance, helping identify potential biomarkers for cancer treatment.

title: Robotic Process Automation + *
authors: Arunima Choudhary (MSIT) Danielle Bacud (MSIT) Demi Evangelatos (MSIT),
abstract: The project sponsor, McKenney’s Inc is looking to automate repetitive tasks. They have enlisted the capstone team to work on the UiPath platform to create ‘robot’ (Robotic Process Automation/RPA) that will automate these tasks. The project was divided into five phases: a. Training on the UiPath platform led by Arunima Choudhary. b. Work with McKenney’s team to gather requirements for each ‘robot’. c. Work with McKenney’s Robotic Automation team to create an environment that capstone team can use to create and test robots outside of production. d. Capstone team worked to create the robots. e. Test and publish the robot in conjunction with the Robotic Automation team.

suggestions.title: Last-Mile Drone Hub Optimization
suggestions.desc: Modify the placement algorithm to account for drone flight ranges and local airspace regulations. The result should be a hybrid warehouse model that identifies 'Mother Hubs' for trucks and 'Satellite Hubs' for autonomous drone delivery.

suggestions.title: OpenStreetMap Integration for Realistic Routing
suggestions.desc: Replace simple Euclidean distances with real-world road network data using the OSMnx library. Account for bridge height limits, truck-restricted roads, and real-time traffic congestion patterns to find truly viable warehouse locations.

suggestions.title: Sustainability-Focused Multi-Objective Optimization
suggestions.desc: Redesign the fitness function to balance logistics costs with carbon footprint. Use the NSGA-II algorithm to find a Pareto-optimal set of locations that minimize both delivery time and total CO2 emissions for the fleet.

suggestions.title: Disaster-Resilient Supply Chain Mapping
suggestions.desc: Add a 'Risk Layer' to the GIS model that includes historical data on floods, hurricanes, and seismic activity. The algorithm would then suggest placements that maximize proximity to customers while staying outside high-risk environmental zones.

title: Utilizing CNN on EMG signals
authors: Maxwell Lavin (MSCS)
abstract: Through the utilization of CNN, the processing and classification of EMG signals related to human hand motion is examined.

suggestions.title: LiDAR-to-Spatial-Audio Engine
suggestions.desc: Using the LiDAR sensor on modern iPhones, develop a system that translates point-cloud depth data into 3D HRTF (Head-Related Transfer Function) audio cues. Objects closer to the user produce higher frequency or louder localized clicks.

suggestions.title: Public Transit API Navigation Integration
suggestions.desc: Integrate the UI with GTFS (General Transit Feed Specification) data. Use spatial audio to 'pull' the user toward the correct bus stop or train platform, providing verbal 3D-audio cues for upcoming arrivals.

suggestions.title: Haptic-Audio Fusion Wearable
suggestions.desc: Create a companion wearable device (belt or vest) using ESP32 and vibrating motors. Synchronize the 3D audio cues with haptic pulses to provide a multi-sensory 'map' of the environment for improved directional confidence.

suggestions.title: Voice-First Wayfinding Assistant
suggestions.desc: Implement an LLM-based voice interface using OpenAI Whisper for speech-to-text. Users can ask, 'Where is the nearest exit?' and the system responds with spatialized audio beacons that lead them through the building.

title: Data Mining by Genetic Programming + *
authors: Mahsa Shokri Varniab (MSCS)
abstract: This paper proposes an approach which uses optimized genetic programming (GP) with a new fitness function for multiclass dataset classification using 10-fold Cross Validation. In place of defining static thresholds as boundaries to differentiate between multiple classes, our work presents a method of classification where a GP system learns the relationships among experiential data and models them mathematically during the evolutionary process. We propose an optimized GP classifier based on a combination of pruning subtrees and a new fitness function. An orthogonal least squares algorithm is also applied in the training phase to create a robust GP classifier. Our approach has been assessed on

suggestions.title: Differential Privacy for Student Bio-Data
suggestions.desc: Implement a Differential Privacy layer on the data ingestion pipeline to ensure that individual student stress levels cannot be deanonymized while still allowing the teacher to see aggregate classroom trends.

suggestions.title: Teacher Dashboard with Real-Time Heatmaps
suggestions.desc: Develop a React-based web dashboard that visualizes the classroom's 'engagement level' as a heatmap. Use WebSockets to provide the teacher with immediate feedback on which parts of a lecture are causing boredom or confusion.

suggestions.title: Smart Lighting and HVAC Feedback Loop
suggestions.desc: Connect the IoT sensors to a smart building controller (BACnet). Automatically adjust the classroom lighting color temperature or lower the room temperature if the sensors detect a significant spike in aggregate student sleepiness.

suggestions.title: Wearable SDK Integration (Apple/Fitbit)
suggestions.desc: Expand the sensor suite to include data from student smartwatches. Use the Heart Rate Variability (HRV) and SpO2 data from the HealthKit/Fitbit SDKs to calibrate the desk sensors and improve accuracy of the stress models.

title: Tiered Financial Fraud Detection *
authors: Charles Gardner (MSCS)
abstract: This study focuses on financial fraud detection via the creation of a three-tiered anomaly detection system. The system is constructed by tuning multiple random forest classifiers, each optimized utilizing a different fitness function. This process is done using a randomized grid search that optimizes the random forest parameters to match the fitness function. Once complete, the models are compared to form three-tiers of detected frauds with each tier containing a different level of precision. Separating detected frauds into different tiers allows for both high precision and high recall values. With this strategy, 96% of frauds are classified correctly while still maintaining a high precisio

suggestions.title: Streaming Cluster Integration with Apache Flink
suggestions.desc: Evolve the static MapReduce clustering into a dynamic stream processing application using Flink to handle real-time data clusters that evolve over time.

suggestions.title: Density-Based Big Data Clustering (DBSCAN)
suggestions.desc: Implement the DBSCAN algorithm for the MapReduce framework to compare its ability to find non-spherical clusters against traditional K-Means on the Hadoop cluster.

suggestions.title: Global Social Media Topic Community Detection
suggestions.desc: Use the Hadoop clustering implementation to analyze terabytes of Twitter/X data, grouping users into 'topic communities' based on the co-occurrence of hashtags.

suggestions.title: B2B Customer Segmentation Service
suggestions.desc: Build a specialized service for e-commerce platforms that uses the MapReduce clustering to segment millions of customers into high-value personas based on purchase frequency and volume.

title: DL Network Anomaly Detection + *
authors: Daniel Karasek (MSCS) Jeehyeong Kim (Doctoral Researcher) Victor
abstract: Network anomalies are correlated to activities that deviate from regular behavior patterns in a network, and they can be undetectable until their actions define them as malicious and an attack has occurred. Current work in network anomaly detection includes network-based and host-based intrusion detection systems. However, these schemes suffer from high false detection rates due to the Bayesian base rate fallacy. To overcome such a drawback, this paper proposes a superior behavior-based anomaly detection system (SuperB) that uses time series as a parameter for reducing false detections. It trains a neural network with time series network traffic patterns collected from legitimate users, whic

suggestions.title: Near-Infrared (NIR) Liveness Detection
suggestions.desc: Incorporate a Raspberry Pi NoIR camera to detect the 'liveness' of the eye. This prevents spoofing attacks using high-resolution photos or video screens, as real human eyes reflect infrared light uniquely compared to screens.

suggestions.title: Decentralized Biometric ID with Blockchain
suggestions.desc: Integrate the iris recognition system with a Self-Sovereign Identity (SSI) framework. Use Zero-Knowledge Proofs to allow a user to authenticate their identity without ever transmitting their raw biometric data to a server.

suggestions.title: Emotional and Cognitive State Analysis
suggestions.desc: Research the use of pupillary response (task-evoked pupillary response) to detect user cognitive load or emotional arousal. This could be used for 'adaptive security' where higher authentication is required if the user appears under duress.

suggestions.title: Multi-Factor Iris + Face Fusion
suggestions.desc: Develop a dual-model authentication system that uses YOLOv8 for face detection and a specialized CNN for iris segmentation (like DeepIris). This improves reliability in various lighting conditions where iris-only detection might fail.

title: Designing Virtual Customers *
authors: Sourav Debnath (MSSWE)
abstract: Automated virtual agents are a great way to provide information without the need of human intervention. For this reason, they can be used in role- playing activities to simulate human conversations, particularly they can be used to train novice analysts on how to conduct requirements elicitation interviews for software engineering projects. In our research we show the design architecture of an online virtual agent, VIrtual CustOmer (VICO) and how it can be developed using existing technologies. We will showcase an initial prototype of this system and show results from an exploratory study.

suggestions.title: Explainable AI (XAI) for Claim Adjusters
suggestions.desc: Integrate SHAP or LIME into the prediction model to provide 'reasoning' for each severity score. This helps insurance adjusters understand why a specific claim was flagged as high-cost, increasing trust in the automated system.

suggestions.title: Multi-Modal Damage Assessment
suggestions.desc: Expand the machine learning model to include computer vision. Use a CNN to analyze uploaded photos of vehicle damage and fuse those features with the tabular data to produce a more accurate severity estimate.

suggestions.title: Fraud Detection using Graph Neural Networks
suggestions.desc: Implement a GNN to identify suspicious relationships between claimants, doctors, and repair shops. Cross-reference the severity predictions with historical fraud patterns to flag potential 'staged' accidents.

suggestions.title: Federated Learning for Cross-Carrier Training
suggestions.desc: Develop a Federated Learning framework that allows multiple insurance companies to train a shared severity model without sharing sensitive customer data, resulting in a more robust and generalized model.

title: Development of Digital Currency
authors: Feiyang Qu (MSCS)
abstract: In this project, we will understand the development trend and future of digital currency through analysis of patent data related to digital currency. Specifically, we use natural language processing (NPL), such as 'Topic model' to classify text in raw patent data. The processing will help us to understand which patent topics related to digital currency are concerned at different time periods. Co-advisor: Dr. Meng Han

suggestions.title: Adversarial Defense for Malware Vision
suggestions.desc: Implement 'adversarial training' in the TensorFlow model to detect malware that has been specifically obfuscated to bypass image-based LBP feature detection.

suggestions.title: Transfer Learning with ResNet-50
suggestions.desc: Replace the custom TensorFlow classifier with a ResNet-50 model pre-trained on ImageNet to see if general visual features improve the classification of malware LBP images.

suggestions.title: Live Malware-to-Image Scanning API
suggestions.desc: Develop a SaaS API that allows security teams to upload binaries, converts them to LBP images on-the-fly, and returns a 'threat signature' classification in milliseconds.

suggestions.title: Mobile Malware Detection for Android
suggestions.desc: Optimize the LBP-based detection model for mobile deployment (TensorFlow Lite) to scan Android APK files for malicious patterns directly on the device.

title: Blockchain for fake news detection
authors: Farid Khan (MSCS)
abstract: Fake news is the spread of misinformation through internet and social media. Multiple examples display the negative impact of fake news on our economy, politics and society. It is an open problem with no standard solutions. Journalist and news agencies are in a desperate need for a solution that can provide identification and verification for news content. A solution is to design a blockchain that can provide proof of concept for news publishers. Three fundamental properties of blockchain are: hash function, consensus mechanism and smart contract. This makes the system tamper- proof, provides authentication.The news agency will sign up to blockchain platform. Smart contract are designed to e

suggestions.title: Adversarial Robustness Testing against Feature-Statically Evaded Malware
suggestions.desc: Investigate how the model behaves when malware authors intentionally manipulate the 9 selected features using GANs to bypass detection. Build a testing suite that generates 'adversarial malware' to evaluate the stability of the RF and SVM classifiers.

suggestions.title: Explainable Malware Diagnostics with SHAP and LIME
suggestions.desc: Integrate SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) to visualize exactly why the neural network flagged a specific file. Create a dashboard for security analysts that highlights which of the 9 features contributed most to a 'malicious' verdict.

suggestions.title: Real-time Windows Kernel Driver Implementation
suggestions.desc: Port the Python-based detection logic into a C++ Windows kernel driver that monitors file execution in real-time. Use a lightweight inference engine like ONNX Runtime to maintain the 99.6% accuracy without significant CPU overhead.

suggestions.title: Cross-Platform Feature Mapping for Android APKs
suggestions.desc: Extend the feature selection methodology to the Android domain by extracting intents, permissions, and API calls. Test if a similarly small subset of features (under 10) can achieve comparable accuracy on the Drebin or Contagio datasets.

title: Parallel FDG Clustering with Spark *
authors: Daniel Brown (MSCS)
abstract: We previously proposed and tested the Fast Density-Grid clustering algorithm. This project discusses the work and results of designing and testing a parallel implementation of this algorithm using Apache Spark.

suggestions.title: On-Device Inference with TensorFlow Lite and Face Mesh
suggestions.desc: Optimize the deep learning model for mobile deployment using quantization and pruning to run on mid-range smartphones. Use MediaPipe Face Mesh to ensure classification is only performed on high-quality facial crops, reducing false positives from background noise.

suggestions.title: Mitigating Demographic Bias in Classification
suggestions.desc: Evaluate the model's accuracy across different ethnicities using the FairFace dataset. Implement adversarial debiasing or re-weighting techniques during training to ensure the age and gender estimation performs equitably across all protected groups.

suggestions.title: Retail Analytics Heatmap and Demographic Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Develop a system that connects to a store's IP camera to track the age and gender distribution of customers over time. Use OpenCV for multi-face tracking and visualize the data in a React-based dashboard to help retailers optimize product placement.

suggestions.title: Privacy-Preserving Edge Classification with Local Blurring
suggestions.desc: Modify the pipeline to perform age/gender estimation on the 'edge' and immediately discard the raw image. Implement a system where only the metadata (e.g., 'Male, 25-30') is sent to the server, while the facial image is blurred or deleted to comply with GDPR/CCPA.

title: Smart Wine Cellar *
authors: Hongkyu Lee (MSCS)
abstract: A Software Framework for Smart Wine Celler, which is capable of learning a user's taste of wine. As user input the wine he/she bought, based on the chemical property of the wine such as acidity, sulfate, alcohol percentage, The wine cellar software learns and predicts which combination of the various chemical property will more fit to user's penchant. I used a wine-dataset that includes chemical data and rating(how good the wine is) of more than 4000 red wines. With this dataset, I used one of the reinforcement learning algorithm, well known as Deep Q Learning, for predicting user's taste.

suggestions.title: Edge-Deployed Inference on Jetson Nano
suggestions.desc: Convert the CNN and Capsule Network models to ONNX format and deploy them onto NVIDIA Jetson Nano hardware for real-time, low-power animal detection at the camera source.

suggestions.title: Multi-Species Interaction Tracker
suggestions.desc: Develop a temporal analysis layer that tracks multiple detected animals across video frames to identify and flag interspecies interactions like predator-prey encounters.

suggestions.title: Capsule Network Robustness Study
suggestions.desc: Conduct a comparative study on how Capsule Networks handle 'occlusion' (animals hidden by trees or brush) compared to standard CNNs using synthetic wilderness datasets.

suggestions.title: Rare Species Alert & Geo-Tagging System
suggestions.desc: Build a cloud-based dashboard that automatically geo-tags rare species sightings and sends real-time push notifications to wildlife conservationists when an endangered animal is detected.

title: OS meets Homomorphic Encryption *
authors: Victor Youdom Kemmoe (MSCS)
abstract: With devices more and more connected, it is easy for an attacker to target several individuals in one attack. Most of the time, the first line of defense between an attacker and our data is the Operating System running on our device. It this project, we aim to develop a new OS security solution by using Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE). With Fully Homomorphic encryption, data can be operated without being decrypted. Conventionally, data stored on a computing system are encrypted only at rest (when they are stored in hard drives) and decrypted to be used (when they are transferred in the main memory). Therefore, if an attacker has a "listener" on your system, he will be able to grab the dec

suggestions.title: Near-Miss Detection with Edge Computing on NVIDIA Jetson
suggestions.desc: Deploy the traffic conflict model to an NVIDIA Jetson Nano device for local, real-time processing at the intersection. Develop a 'Near-Miss' alert system that triggers a high-priority log whenever the Post-Encroachment Time (PET) between two vehicles drops below a safety threshold.

suggestions.title: Multi-Modal Sensor Fusion for Low-Visibility Safety
suggestions.desc: Integrate LiDAR or Thermal camera data with the live video feed to maintain safety monitoring during night-time or heavy fog. Use a Kalman filter to fuse object tracks from different sensors, ensuring continuous monitoring when visual cameras fail.

suggestions.title: Predictive Trajectory Modeling for Pedestrian Safety
suggestions.desc: Expand the system to predict the future path (0.5 to 2 seconds) of pedestrians near crosswalks using LSTMs. Analyze traffic conflicts between turning vehicles and pedestrians to proactively suggest timing changes for signalized intersections.

suggestions.title: Digital Twin Traffic Safety Synchronization
suggestions.desc: Stream the extracted traffic conflict data into a 3D Digital Twin environment using Unity or Carla Simulator. This allows city planners to virtually 'replay' high-risk events from any angle to identify structural road design flaws contributing to accidents.

title: Adversarial ML for QoS Tampering + *
authors: Jhilakshi Sharma (MSCS)
abstract: This project focuses on exploiting Software-Defined Networks (SDNs) through Offensive Machine Learning, specifically by using Adversarial Examples. The project is currently focused on calculating the effectiveness of Adversarial Examples against SDNs using Convolutional Neural Networks for Quality-of-Service (QoS) classification of network packets. The aim is to test whether we can exploit the mechanism to obtain higher/lower QoS for packets than they deserve to get.

suggestions.title: Hybrid Neural Post-Processing Filter for HEVC
suggestions.desc: Build a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) that acts as an in-loop filter to remove blocking artifacts specifically for low-bitrate HEVC streams. Implement the filter using PyTorch and benchmark its PSNR (Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio) against the standard HEVC deblocking filter.

suggestions.title: Perceptual Loss Optimization using MS-SSIM
suggestions.desc: Retrain the optimization network using a Multi-Scale Structural Similarity (MS-SSIM) loss function instead of standard Mean Squared Error. Research how prioritizing human visual perception over raw mathematical error improves subjective video quality in high-motion scenes.

suggestions.title: Ultra-Low Latency Optimization for Cloud Gaming
suggestions.desc: Tailor the HEVC deep learning model specifically for interactive streams where latency is critical. Develop a 'Region of Interest' (ROI) encoder that uses neural networks to identify and prioritize high-detail areas (like game HUDs or player characters) to save bandwidth.

suggestions.title: Content-Adaptive Bitrate Control for Mobile Streaming
suggestions.desc: Create a classification head that identifies the video genre (e.g., sports, animation, talking head) and dynamically adjusts HEVC parameters. Use this to reduce data consumption on mobile devices by applying more aggressive compression to static scenes without losing clarity.

title: Fake News Detection by Support Vect *
authors: Jiahao Chen (BSCS) Shikun Lyu (BSCS)
abstract: Fake news detection research emerges in a couple of years and is a relative new difficult research area. In this project, we attempt to attack this problem by using FakeNewsTracker, doc2vec, and Support Vector Machine. Our results indicate that Support Vector Machine is a suitable method to classify fake news from real ones with an acceptable accuracy. Future research directions will be addressed.

suggestions.title: Temporal Consistency for Video Segmentation
suggestions.desc: Move from single-frame Mask R-CNN to a video-based approach using ConvLSTM or Mask2Former. This ensures that instruments are tracked smoothly across frames without 'flickering' or losing the mask during fast surgical movements.

suggestions.title: Intraoperative 'No-Go' Zone Alerts
suggestions.desc: Develop a real-time monitoring layer that defines critical tissue areas (like major arteries) as 'No-Go' zones. If the Mask R-CNN detects a surgical tool entering a predefined proximity to these areas, it triggers an audible or visual alert for the surgeon.

suggestions.title: Edge Deployment on NVIDIA Jetson
suggestions.desc: Optimize the Mask R-CNN model using TensorRT and quantization (INT8) to run at 30+ FPS on an NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano. This makes the system portable and usable in operating rooms without requiring a full server rack.

suggestions.title: Automated Post-Surgical Report Generation
suggestions.desc: Use the segmentation data to quantify the duration of the surgery, the frequency of tool usage, and the amount of tissue handled. Generate an automated PDF report summarizing the technical efficiency of the procedure for surgical training and review.

project_key: legacy:UR-01|Fall 2019|fake news detection by support vect *

title: Machine Learning Network Intrusion + *
authors: Jayden Johnson (HS Intern)
abstract: This project studies and compares the accuracies of different machine learning algorithms in network intrusion detection. Machine learning algorithms implemented in this project include Regression Forests, Classification Forests, and Boosted Regression Forests. Performance evaluations on these algorithms via latest intrusion detection dataset are conducted and analyzed.

suggestions.title: Offline LLM Tutor with Quantized Models
suggestions.desc: Integrate a small-parameter Large Language Model (like Phi-2 or Llama-3-8B) quantized for mobile. Build a voice-to-text interface that allows children to ask questions about the video lessons without needing an internet connection.

suggestions.title: Handwriting Assessment via Stroke Analysis
suggestions.desc: Develop a computer vision module for the 'sand tray' feature that analyzes the child's finger or stylus strokes. Use a lightweight CNN to give real-time feedback on letter formation and track literacy progress over time.

suggestions.title: Gamified Adaptive Learning Path
suggestions.desc: Create a software layer that adjusts the difficulty of the video lessons and whiteboard activities based on the child's performance. Use a Reinforcement Learning agent to maximize student engagement by offering rewards (digital badges) for completing challenging tasks.

suggestions.title: Solar-Powered Ruggedization for Remote Use
suggestions.desc: Design a 3D-printable, ruggedized casing for the tablet that includes an integrated solar charging panel. Create a 'mesh networking' feature where multiple tablets can sync data and updates when one device occasionally reaches a high-connectivity zone.

title: Geospatial Cyber Threat Hunting *
authors: kristopher barnette (BSIT) Rabab Turabi (BSIT) Lain Alexander (BSIT),
abstract: This research project will be using Splunk to create custom Dashboards to identify malicious traffic attempting to enter a network. The intrusion attempts will be analyzed to return Geospatical information "GEOINT". This information will then be used to see analyze attack methods used in specific locations.

suggestions.title: Indoor SLAM with Depth Sensing
suggestions.desc: Enhance the drone's navigation by implementing ROS (Robot Operating System) with a GMapping or Cartographer SLAM package. Use a T265 Realsense camera to allow the drone to build a 3D map of a room and take photos from optimal geometric angles.

suggestions.title: Automated Real Estate Tour Guide
suggestions.desc: Program the drone to follow a pre-defined path through a house, using OpenCV to detect 'points of interest' like fireplaces or large windows. The drone should automatically center these features in the frame and take high-dynamic-range (HDR) photos.

suggestions.title: CNN-Based Aesthetic Scoring
suggestions.desc: Integrate a pre-trained Neural Image Assessment (NIMA) model onto the drone's edge processor. The drone should take multiple photos of a scene and only keep/upload the ones with the highest predicted aesthetic and technical quality scores.

suggestions.title: Autonomous Event Photographer
suggestions.desc: Modify the drone's AI to include face detection and group framing. The drone can be deployed at indoor events to hover and capture 'candid' shots by detecting smiles and eye contact using the MediaPipe library, ensuring everyone is in the frame before snapping.

title: Adversarial Attacks on Applied CNN + *
authors: Morris Wan (HS intern)
abstract: Convolutional Neural networks (CNNs) have become more and more prevalent in applications such as image recognition and autonomous driving. However, recent literature shows that a lot of these deep-learning algorithms are susceptible small perturbations to their input known as adversarial attacks. In this project, adversarial attack algorithms and defenses were implemented and evaluated using real traffic lights data and model.

suggestions.title: Deep Reinforcement Learning for Real-Time Control
suggestions.desc: Replace the predictive simulation with a Deep Q-Network (DQN) that controls HVAC setpoints in real-time. Use the 'OpenAI Gym' environment to simulate the West Point building and train the agent to balance occupant comfort (CO2 and Temp) against peak energy prices.

suggestions.title: Campus-Wide Microgrid Load Balancing
suggestions.desc: Extend the peak shaving model to include other energy-intensive campus assets like electric vehicle (EV) charging stations and dorm lighting. Create a coordinated strategy where HVAC load is decreased when EV demand spikes to keep the total campus draw below a critical threshold.

suggestions.title: Commercial Smart Thermostat API for Rebates
suggestions.desc: Develop a SaaS wrapper for the optimization model that connects to smart thermostats (like Ecobee or Nest) via API. Target small business owners by automating their participation in 'Demand Response' programs, earning them utility rebates during peak hours.

suggestions.title: Occupancy Sensing via Thermal Imaging Fusion
suggestions.desc: Research the impact of real-time occupancy data on the peak shaving model. Integrate low-cost PIR or thermal sensors to feed 'human heat load' data into the HVAC simulation, allowing for more aggressive energy savings in empty zones.

title: Vertical Shift, A VR Esport
authors: Sean Feeser (BSCGDD), Ben Snowden (BSCGDD), Jacob
abstract: Vertical Shift is a multiplayer VR game where you fly through a city using grappling hooks, jetpacks, wings, and other innovative locomotions. The game features hand-crafted maps for the players to swing around and fly through, emphasizing the unique variety of gameplay mechanics. The game types emulate competitive sports, such as Tag, Capture the Flag, and Racing. We aim to create a new and engaging experience that will keep players coming back to play with their friends.

suggestions.title: Procedural Ruin Generation System
suggestions.desc: Develop a C++ script for Unreal Engine that procedurally generates the 'forgotten world' ruins to ensure no two playthroughs are the same. Use Houdini Engine or Perlin noise to create crumbling architecture that remains navigable for third-person combat.

suggestions.title: Haptic Feedback Integration for VR Combat
suggestions.desc: Enhance the combat immersion by integrating the bHaptics API or OpenXR haptic standards. Map enemy strikes and magical will-based attacks to specific vibration patterns on a haptic vest or VR controllers.

suggestions.title: AI-Driven Dynamic Difficulty Adjustment

Different buildings (offices, police stations, houses, etc) raise and lower these resources as they are constructed. abstract: We present a combined approach using computer vision and radio frequencies to detect and confirm the presence of an Unmanned Aerial Vehicle to avoid false positives classifications in the real-time classification of Unmanned Aerial Vehicles. These false positive results arise from similarities between either bio-life creatures and drones or sub-optimal visual conditions challenging the classification of these drones due to the difficulty in distinguishing between background. When detecting through optimal or sub-optimal visual co

title: Bioinformatics Software Packaging

abstract: The field of bioinformatics permeates almost every aspect of our lives. From the effort to cure cancer to creating disease resistant plants that we eat every day, we see the effects throughout our daily lives. AppImage is a packaging solution designed for use in Linux environments. It consolidates all source, binaries, and dependencies for a given application. It then wraps them into a singular package, and produces a self-contained application that is distribution agnostic. This allows both terminal and graphical user interface (GUI) applications to function without any additional installations or configurations on almost any Linux distribution.

title: CCSE CRM database project
authors: Sean Carroll (BASIT), Matthew Luu (BASIT), Adrian Bonaparte (BSIT), Carl
abstract: Medication nonadherence is generally defined as a patient’s inability to take their medications correctly as prescribed by their doctors. Medication nonadherence adversely affects patient outcomes and increases healthcare costs. Prior research found that health system-, condition-, patient- (older age is one factor), therapy- and social/economic-related factors have been identified to show effect on non-adherence. Our goal is to analyze the NHIS data to understand the sociodemographic and health causes of medication nonadherence, as well as answer the following questions about our selected topic: What variables are the most relevant drivers of nonadherence? Does the direction and strength of the associations between these variables and medication nonadherence vary over time? If so, how? Are trends getting better or worse? Do the results of your analysis suggest that social inequality factors may be linked to medication nonadherence? If so, how? What are the implications of your analysis for various stakeholders? How does this vary depending on whether medication nonadherence is intentional or unintentional?Advisors(s): Advisor/Instructor: Ying Xie | yxie2@kennesaw.edu Project Owner/Sponsor: Dr. Chi Zhang | czhang4@kennesaw.eduTopic(s): Data/Data AnalyticsIT4893 - IT Capstone (W01)

title: Student Engagement-Capturing Info
authors: Tyler Addison (BSIT), Jacob Yoder (BSIT), Norsi Onyekaba (BSIT), Tyler
abstract: When considering a student's attentiveness while taking online courses, it is known that they tend to lose focus or get distracted at some point during the lecture. It is said that as humans we are supposed to learn in active environments. Watching a lecture from a screen is considered a passive task. Combining that with another factor like being tired decreases attention even more. Conducting active and passive attention-based trials will reveal varying results in different states of attentiveness. This project compares active and passive attention trial results in two states, wide awake and tired. This has been done in order to answer the questions: Do subjects perform better (as in maintain concentration and attentiveness) on active tasks while both tired and awake? And do they perform worse on passive tasks when tired and better when awake? The data analyzed was collected from electroencephalogram (EEG) waves, and then later processed through a 3D Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) to produce results. Three passive attention trials and three active attention trials were performed on seven subjects, while they were wide awake and again when they were tired.Advisors(s): Dr. Ying XieTopic(s): Data/Data AnalyticsIT 4983
supervisor: Dr. Ming Yang, Prof. Dawn Tatum, Ms. Adriana (Project Manager)

title: The Clarity Project
authors: Jacob Barnett (BSIT), Henry Colomb (BASIT), Amari McGee (BSIT), Christian
abstract: These past few years have introduced the most important time in history to study new faucets of online learning. Due to COVID's impact, online learning became a staple in millions of homes across the country. Guided by the research question, “Can a machine learning model be created and trained to detect student concentration level based on eye and facial data?”, we set out to contribute to society’s understanding of online learning under the guidance of Dr. Ying Xie and Dr. Linh Le. Our process involved recording ourselves participating in online classes to garnish eye and facial data. Each student recorded data where they were staring directly at the screen and focused while also recording data from when the student was distracted and looking elsewhere. Next, deepfake technology was used to swap our faces with celebrity images to protect our privacy while also generating a large pool of data. Finally, we created, trained, and tested different machine learning models to try and find settings best suited for our needs. Preliminary results indicated our top constructed models hovering around 65-75% accuracy. This was all completed using open source software, and we believe that if access was granted to even more accurate eye tracking/deepfake technologies, the accuracy of the model would increase even further. Overall, our results indicate that it would be possible to develop a model that could indicate whether a student was concentrated or distracted based on eye and facial data while implementing a process that could protect the student’s identity, which could be useful data for either the student to review their own performance in class or for a teacher to see at what point students become distracted during an online session.Advisors(s): Dr. Ying Xie (Co-sponsor) Dr. Linh Le (Co-sponsor)Topic(s): Data/Data AnalyticsIT 4983

title: Enlightenment
authors: Jim Sizemore (BSCGDD), Logan Thompson (BSCGDD), Matt Dwyer
abstract: Enlightenment is an educational video game developed for the PC using Unreal Engine 4. The game is designed for students ages 10-12 and teaches about light and its properties, such as reflection, refraction, transparency, and opaqueness. Enlightenment was developed in conjunction with a local 4th-grade classroom, which provided user testing, feedback, and gameplay ideas.

suggestions.title: FinBERT Sentiment Optimization
suggestions.desc: Replace general-purpose NLP models with HuggingFace’s FinBERT, specifically trained on financial communication. Fine-tune the model using the 'Crypto-Sentiment' dataset from Kaggle to better capture crypto-specific slang and market nuances.

suggestions.title: Multi-Exchange Arbitrage Bot
suggestions.desc: Integrate the sentiment signals with the CCXT library to monitor price discrepancies between Binance and Coinbase. Build a bot that executes trades when high-sentiment spikes coincide with price lags between exchanges.

suggestions.title: Multimodal Price Predictor
suggestions.desc: Combine the Twitter sentiment scores with technical indicators (RSI, MACD) and on-chain metrics like Whale Alerts using an Ensemble Learning approach. This reduces false positives caused by social media 'noise' that doesn't reflect actual market liquidity.

suggestions.title: NFT Collection Hype Tracker
suggestions.desc: Pivot the system to analyze Discord and OpenSea activity for specific NFT collections. Use the sentiment engine to predict 'floor price' movements based on community engagement levels rather than just historical sales.

title: Estimating Rasch Model Parameters
authors: Amy Hadzic (BSSWE), Thaide Huichapa (BSSEW), Stephan Joseph (BSSEW),
abstract: The purpose of the project was to estimate the difficulty parameters found in the Rasch model utilizing linear regression implemented using machine learning. Our software allows users to accurately and efficiently find the difficulty parameter of each item in a fraction of the time it would take to derive the parameters utilizing traditional computational methods, all with an accuracy of over 98%.

suggestions.title: Persistent Character Growth via NFC Rewriting
suggestions.desc: Implement NTAG215 write capabilities to store player stats and experience directly on the physical cards. This enables a 'carry-over' mechanic where a physical card effectively becomes a 'memory card' for a character, allowing it to level up across different game sessions.

suggestions.title: Mobile Companion App with CoreNFC
suggestions.desc: Develop an iOS/Android companion app using CoreNFC or Android NFC API that allows players to scan their Wildling Rumble cards on their personal phones. This removes the need for a dedicated hardware reader and allows for 'hidden' actions, like checking secret stats privately.

suggestions.title: Augmented Reality (AR) Unit Visualization
suggestions.desc: Integrate Vuforia or ARKit to recognize the physical NFC cards through a camera view. When a player scans a card, a 3D animated model of the character would appear on top of the physical card, bridging the gap between digital combat and the physical tabletop.

suggestions.title: Multi-Device Synchronous Tabletop State
suggestions.desc: Use WebSockets (Socket.io) to synchronize the game state across multiple tablets or laptops around a table. Scanning a card on one player’s device would instantly update the 'world view' on a central screen, creating a shared digital-physical play space.

title: GGUM model ML parameter estmation
authors: Steven Garcia (BSSWE), James Lee (BSSWE), Aaron Badgett (BSSWE),
abstract: Meeting with the client regularly, we’ve established one of the many solutions to their requirements. The client, WoodLine Interiors, needs a well-designed system that can solve product management, client communication, and project management. The main issue that the company is facing is in the area of managing multiple projects and tracking project progress. Their issue is commonly addressed by a large number of companies that create software solutions, but it’s not personalized for their appropriate needs in the field of cabinetry. As a result our solution created the perfect stages and states of the project so there is no more confusion to where the project is situation at.Advisors(s): supervisor : Dr. Reza Parizi project owner : Woodline Interiors (client)Topic(s): Software EngineeringSWE 4724

suggestions.title: Dynamic Competitive Pricing Engine

suggestions.title: Customer Segmentation Clustering
suggestions.desc: Apply K-Means or DBSCAN clustering to the purchase data to identify distinct buyer personas, such as 'Bulk Resellers' vs 'Individual Consumers.' Use these clusters to generate personalized inventory recommendations for different business types.

suggestions.title: Automated ETL Pipeline with Airflow
suggestions.desc: Transition the static data analysis to a live cloud pipeline using Apache Airflow. Automate the extraction of wholesale data into an AWS Redshift warehouse to provide a continuously updating dashboard instead of a one-time report.

suggestions.title: Supply Chain Vulnerability Map
suggestions.desc: Integrate geographical data to map out the distribution centers of both companies. Use this to analyze regional supply chain resilience, identifying areas where a localized disaster would disrupt Costco or Amazon deliveries more severely.

title: Idea Creator with Survey Tool
authors: Bailey Cash (BSSWE), Anthony Lee (BSSWE), Ayo Awodu (BSSWE), Evan
abstract: This project is a continuation of the Idea Creator platform (idea- creator.com). Phase I included a project creation tool with the ability to define, constrain, ideate, and add research to a project, with a central focus on a word mapping tool. This phase is already live and was completed by a previous Capstone group. Phase II elaborates on Phase I by adding: - Fine tuning of Phase I elements - A concept builder tool to elaborate on established ideas - The ability to upload prototypes on the idea (in .pdf, .ppt, and other common file types) - The ability to create surveys on these prototypes, deploy them to test the ideas. - The ability to view survey results in a visual way through the aggr

suggestions.title: Headless Browser Vulnerability Scan
suggestions.desc: Integrate Playwright or Selenium into the WASP core to scan modern Single Page Applications (React/Vue). This allows the tool to detect vulnerabilities in Client-Side Rendering that traditional static scanners often miss.

suggestions.title: DevSecOps CI/CD Integration
suggestions.desc: Develop a GitHub Action that triggers a WASP scan on every Pull Request. Set up a gateway policy that automatically blocks deployments if the scan detects any 'Critical' OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities.

suggestions.title: LLM-Powered Exploit Payload Generator
suggestions.desc: Use the OpenAI API to generate context-aware XSS and SQL injection payloads based on the specific HTML attributes found by the scanner. This moves the project from generic pattern matching to intelligent, adaptive penetration testing.

suggestions.title: Security Reporting as a Service
suggestions.desc: Build a web-based management portal where users can schedule scans and receive professional PDF security audits. Use a subscription-based model and integrate Stripe for a ready-to-market SaaS cybersecurity tool.

title: ParkinsonsVR
authors: Cody Ducleroir (BSCGDD), Advisor: Dr. Joy Li
abstract: This project addresses concerns raised by a Sponsor regarding inefficiencies in managing CCSE capstone projects. Key pain points include organization, project management, and communication among stakeholders. The proposed solution involves implementing Customer Relationship Management (CRM) software. Upon gathering requirements from the Sponsor, including contact information capture, workflow management, document control, and customization capabilities, we evaluated several CRM platforms. A total of thirty-two CRMs were reviewed and Vtiger, OroCRM, and SuiteCRM were selected for testing. SuiteCRM was chosen for its comprehensive features and user-friendliness. The second step of the project involved testing SuiteCRM functionalities on a dedicated server, leading to its selection for deployment preparation. Final steps include documentation development, training material creation, and incorporating user feedback for optimal system configuration.

suggestions.title: Automated Capstone Lifecycle Middleware
suggestions.desc: Develop a middleware service using Node.js or Python that connects the proposed CRM with GitHub and Jira APIs to automatically provision project repositories and kanban boards once a sponsor proposal is approved.

suggestions.title: Student-Sponsor Talent Matching Portal
suggestions.desc: Build a front-end interface that pulls student skills and interests from the CRM to create a 'matching' algorithm, allowing sponsors to see which student teams best align with their project requirements.

suggestions.title: Predictive Project Risk Analysis
suggestions.desc: Implement a sentiment analysis module using Python's NLTK to analyze communication logs and project status updates within the CRM, flagging projects that exhibit signs of stakeholder misalignment.

suggestions.title: Sponsor Retention & Engagement Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Create a data visualization suite using PowerBI or Tableau that tracks longitudinal sponsor engagement data, helping the college identify which industries provide the most successful long-term partnerships.

title: Passive location detection with BLE
authors: Andrew Dameron (BSSWE), Nick Belvin (BSSWE), Marco
abstract: In the commercial HVAC controls business, it is valuable to know how many people are in each room. This can help to provide adequate fresh air ventilation and impact temperature control strategies. Our new line of HVAC room controllers have Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) radio receivers. Since most smart phones and smart watches support BLE, we believe that it is possible to estimate the number of people in a room from a combination of the unique BLE UUID and the signal strength information available through standard BLE programming APIs. In order to accurately estimate people counts, it will probably be necessary to have receivers in each room, know the size of the rooms, allow them to communic
supervisor: Dr Reza Parizi Project Mentor: Steve Appling

suggestions.title: Multi-Objective Route Optimization
suggestions.desc: Implement a Genetic Algorithm to optimize travel routes based on multiple constraints like 'scenic beauty,' 'low traffic,' and 'minimal walking.' Use the Google Places API to fetch 'busyness' data to help users avoid crowds.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Group Itinerary Synchronization
suggestions.desc: Add a collaborative feature using WebSockets (Socket.io) that allows groups of travelers to vote on activities and see each other's live locations on the shared map. This ensures everyone stays on the 'What's Next' schedule.

suggestions.title: Offline-First Exploration Mode
suggestions.desc: Integrate MapBox offline maps and SQLite local storage to allow users to navigate their custom lists in areas with no cellular data. Build a sync engine that pushes updates to the cloud once a connection is re-established.

suggestions.title: Sentiment-Based Activity Discovery
suggestions.desc: Develop a recommendation engine that scrapes TripAdvisor and Yelp reviews and uses BERT-based NLP to categorize locations by 'vibe' (e.g., 'cozy,' 'chaotic,' 'historic'). Match these to the user's current mood input.

title: WeaveVPN: OpenVPN Without Headaches
authors: Christian Strahl (BSSWE), Alan Castle (BSSWE), Keandre
abstract: Virtual Private Networks (VPN) are an important tool in networking infrastructure and OpenVPN is the most prolific and robust open source framework for VPN connectivity. In theory, OpenVPN allows the set up of a Software Defined Network (SDN), connecting many different VPNs together. However in practice this is difficult to accomplish. WeaveVPN is a web based service for dynamically setting up and configuring an SDN, quickly, easily, and securely.

suggestions.title: WireGuard Kernel-Level Migration
suggestions.desc: Upgrade the core protocol from OpenVPN to WireGuard using the 'wg-ctrl' library to achieve 3x faster throughput and lower latency while maintaining the easy-to-use SDN configuration interface.

suggestions.title: Terraform-Driven Multi-Cloud Mesh
suggestions.desc: Incorporate Terraform providers for AWS, Azure, and GCP into the WeaveVPN dashboard to allow users to deploy VPN nodes across different cloud providers with a single click, creating a resilient multi-cloud mesh.

suggestions.title: Zero Trust Identity Integration
suggestions.desc: Implement OIDC (OpenID Connect) via Keycloak or Auth0 so that VPN access is granted based on user identity and MFA status rather than just pre-shared keys, moving toward a Zero Trust Architecture (ZTA).

suggestions.title: eBPF-Based Latency-Aware Routing
suggestions.desc: Use eBPF (Extended Berkeley Packet Filter) on the Linux kernel to monitor the latency between SDN nodes and automatically reroute traffic through the fastest available path in the virtual network.

title: Campus Cop
authors: Charishma Macherla (MSCS), Shwetha Adoni Prasad (MSCS), Neeraj
abstract: The drone provides live streaming of scenes on college campus (best suited for secured government institution for monitoring threat) The AWS face ReKognition is used to analyse live streaming of videos. It helps in recognizing unauthorized person, describing a potential suspect, suspicious activities. Alexa is integrated with the lambda function and lambda function calculates and reads the result of face recognition.

suggestions.title: Zero-Trust IoT Mesh Implementation

suggestions.title: Energy-Efficient Automation Logic
suggestions.desc: Integrate the OpenWeather API to create 'Smart Eco-Modes.' Build logic that automatically adjusts smart blinds and thermostats based on external temperature and forecasted sunlight to minimize HVAC energy consumption.

suggestions.title: Privacy-First Local Voice Control
suggestions.desc: Add a voice command module using an on-device engine like Rhasspy or Snips. This allows users to control their home via the Android app using voice without sending sensitive audio data to Amazon or Google servers.

suggestions.title: Occupancy Pattern Prediction

title: Backdoor Attacks
authors: Bhavana Pateriya (MSCS), Sowmya Kari (MSCS), Priyanka Velu (MSCS),
abstract: Because of so many layers, Deep Neural networks (DNN) become like a black box which is difficult for us to understand and hence DNNs are vulnerable to hacks and deception. We know how a DNN will behave on a set of trained data but what about untested data? We have no idea about that. By identifying patterns that these systems use to function, attackers can modify inputs to DNNs in such a way that the DNN finds a match that we would not recognize. Such a manipulation is termed as “Trojans”. These trojans are hidden patterns that have been trained into DNN model that produce unexpected behavior which can’t be detected unless there is a trigger in input. We present a methodology capable of dete

suggestions.title: Real-Time User Interest Recommender
suggestions.desc: Use the implemented clustering algorithms to group users based on their clickstream data in real-time. Connect this to a Flask API that serves personalized 'Recommended for You' content based on the active cluster the user belongs to.

suggestions.title: Interactive 3D Cluster Visualization
suggestions.desc: Utilize Three.js and t-SNE (t-Distributed Stochastic Neighbor Embedding) to create an interactive web dashboard. Users can explore high-dimensional web data clusters in a 3D space to visually identify outliers and patterns.

suggestions.desc: Repurpose the clustering logic to detect non-human behavior in server logs. By clustering requests based on timing and IP headers, the system can automatically flag botnets or scrapers that deviate from human-user clusters.

abstract: Project Name: Amazon product Reviews and Discovering Sentiment using Byte LSTM on AWS. Using this Byte code LSTM model, It can classify the reviews as positive or negative. They are also very data efficient. When using only a handful of labeled examples, our approach matches the performance of strong baselines trained on full dataset. We also demonstrate the sentiment unit has a direct influence on the generative process of the model. We are training out Amazon review data on AWS model so that data can be trained in the faster manner. It's absolutely necessary for any o

suggestions.title: ARCore Augmented Reality Tag

suggestions.title: Dead Reckoning for GPS dead-zones
suggestions.desc: Implement a sensor fusion algorithm that uses the phone’s accelerometer and gyroscope to estimate player movement (Dead Reckoning). This ensures the game remains playable even when GPS signal drops between tall buildings.

suggestions.title: Gamified Urban Fitness League
suggestions.desc: Transform the game into a fitness platform where points are earned for distance run and calories burned during a match. Integrate with Google Fit to track long-term stats and create a leaderboard based on athletic performance.

suggestions.title: Low-Latency WebRTC Multiplayer
suggestions.desc: Replace standard HTTP requests with WebRTC data channels for peer-to-peer communication. This reduces the latency of 'tag' registration to near-instantaneous levels, critical for a fast-paced action game.

title: Smart- Pay: No Cashier Pay System
authors: Farid Khan (MSCS), Chris (MSCS) Advisor: Dr. Mohammed Aledhari
abstract: The advances in machine learning has opened a new spectrum of applications. In retail stores we find cashier systems that are either labor intensive or time- consuming. We propose Smart- Pay as a novel cashier less payment system. Smart - Pay uses machine vision, smart devices and mobile system to automatically charge the user at point of sale. Such systems can bring high productivity and efficiency for the user. We expect that through further testing and higher accuracy results, such systems can gain broad deployments and application in areas related to retail, marketing and entertainment industry.

suggestions.title: Edge TPU Hardware Acceleration
suggestions.desc: Integrate a Google Coral USB Accelerator into the Raspberry Pi to offload deep learning tasks. This allows the system to move from low-FPS TensorFlow Lite models to high-speed, real-time object detection at 30+ FPS.

suggestions.title: Auto-Restocking Subscription Bot
suggestions.desc: Connect the inventory detection system to a web scraper or the Walmart IO API. When the camera detects that a specific item (e.g., milk) is missing from the shelf for more than 24 hours, the system can automatically add it to an online shopping cart.

suggestions.title: Multi-Pi Warehouse Sync
suggestions.desc: Build a central dashboard using Django that aggregates inventory data from multiple Raspberry Pis located in different aisles. Implement a 'Global Inventory View' that tracks total stock levels across an entire facility.

suggestions.title: Few-Shot Learning for New Items
suggestions.desc: Implement a Few-Shot Learning model that allows users to 'train' the system on a new item by simply holding it in front of the camera for 10 seconds. This removes the need for large pre-labeled datasets for every unique inventory item.

These operations are going to be role-based and will be restricted based on the account login. Having a repository for faculty and staff will help them better equipped to manage the contract for hire, capstone, and research projects within the department. The application will also have the ability to print re

suggestions.title: Offline-First Progressive Web App

suggestions.desc: Integrate OpenALPR (Automatic License Plate Recognition) using the parking lot cameras. Link this to the maintenance portal so that if a vehicle is parked in a 'Maintenance Zone' without a permit, an automated ticket is generated for the staff.

Over the past few years, the department has faced challenges getting students to attend or get involved in events such as the Hack-A-Thon as well as attend speaking events and project presentations from company representatives. The lack of attendance from students puts a poor image of KSU to these companies which doesn't encourage them to attend. The previous solutions the department had tried were providing food and drinks, posting on their social media pages such as Facebook. So our Capstone project this semester is to provide

suggestions.desc: Replace the basic dashboard with an InfluxDB time-series database and a Grafana front-end. This allows for professional-grade monitoring of packet loss, signal-to-noise ratios, and jitter over months of campus operation.

suggestions.desc: Extend the Raspberry Pi monitoring logic to scan for 'Evil Twin' access points. Use Scapy to analyze beacon frames and alert IT staff if an unauthorized device is broadcasting a spoofed KSU SSID to harvest student credentials.

suggestions.desc: Use Microsoft Flight Simulator or X-Plane to generate thousands of high-fidelity images of various aircraft tires under different lighting and weather conditions. Compare the accuracy of a model trained on these synthetic images versus one trained on the limited real-world airport dataset.

abstract: The purpose is to analyze the connection between Social Media and Cybersecurity. Our goal is to pull Twitter data labeled/tagged as “Breaking News”, find any/all tweets that are relevant to Cybersecurity, then analyze the data; percentage of tweets that are Cybersecurity, how these tweets are spread (retweeted), other tags associated with the tweets, peak times when tweets are created/retweeted, and any additional information that could be determined to help provide insight on how Cybersecurity alerts function within Social Media. Once we pull the data, we will use python to analyze and complete most of the work. We will create a website to publish our findings, along with out project plan,…

If the model predicts a high likelihood of a customer purchasing a high-margin item, the engine could suggest 'bundle' pricing for lower-performing inventory items to increase the average basket size.

suggestions.desc: Implement a Graph Neural Network (GNN) to map the relationships between product categories during Black Friday. This can identify 'bridge products' that, when purchased, lead to significantly higher spending in unrelated categories, helping retailers optimize floor layout.

suggestions.title: Customer Lifetime Value (CLV) Forecasting

suggestions.desc: Use the TSFresh library to automatically extract hundreds of time-series features (like rolling means, peaks, and Fourier coefficients) from the transaction data. Evaluate if these engineered temporal features provide a significant lift in precision compared to the original Kaggle features.

title: Technology Training ITIL Assessment

abstract: The Technology Training team offers a variety of service for faculty, staff, and students. The survey has been updated for logistics, but not content. The surveys are viewed, but no formal process is in place to map the data. Problem statement: The team is always looking for ways to improve the all services to be more efficient while continuing to support the needs of the campus. However, due to time and resource constraints, the team has little bandwidth available to assess and make improvements. Objectives: 1

title: Git Contribution Measurement TooLMS
authors: Israel Chen (MSSWE), Sathya Chekuri (MSSWE), Paul Thim (MSSWE),
abstract: The project solves a novel problem in academia. In software engineering education, many curricula often require collaborative-group projects to provide students hands-on experience. However, instructors evaluating the team's performance often have to rely on their subjective judgment of the product, documentation, and team member testimonials. This creates a black-box nature in terms of how accurate a member's contribution is evaluated for the team project. As software development moves towards automated DevOps (with less and less documentation), it is critical, now more than ever, to engineer a novel solution for this problem. This project's vision is to utilize decentralized collaborative…

suggestions.title: Reinforcement Learning for 'Buy vs. Wait' Signals
suggestions.desc: Apply Q-Learning or Deep Q-Networks (DQN) to the existing predictive model to generate a binary 'Buy Now' or 'Wait' recommendation. The agent would be rewarded for maximizing savings compared to the average price of the route.

suggestions.title: Digital Nomad Multi-City Route Optimizer
suggestions.desc: Expand the tool to predict prices for 'Open-Jaw' or multi-city itineraries where the sequence of cities affects the total cost. Use a genetic algorithm to find the cheapest dates and sequence for a 3-4 city trip based on the price prediction curves.

suggestions.title: Price Drop Alert & Refund Monitoring Service
suggestions.desc: Build a monitoring bot that tracks purchased tickets and alerts the user if the price drops below a certain threshold. Integrate with airline 'Price Match' policies or the 24-hour cancellation window to automate the refund/rebooking process.

suggestions.title: External Factor Correlation Analysis
suggestions.desc: Integrate non-price features into the model like global fuel price indices, major sporting events (via SerpApi), and weather patterns. Research how much 'External Volatility' impacts prediction accuracy compared to simple historical price trends.

title: credit card approval analysis
authors: Hui Xia (MSCS) Advisor: Dr. Selena He
abstract: Nowadays, there has been an increasing number of people applying and using credit card. Along with this, there has been concerns on credit card default. Credit cards can be a convenient means for payment, a useful tool for learning financial responsibility, a resource in case of emergencies, a way to establishing good credit history and to gain greater access to credit in the future. Excessive credit card debt and late payments can damage user’s credit ratings and make it more difficult for them to obtain credit down the road. Inexperience with credit and a lack of personal financial knowledge are likely to place some users at greater financial risk for having large, and perhaps unmanageable

suggestions.title: LiDAR-to-Spatial-Audio Engine
suggestions.desc: Using the LiDAR sensor on modern iPhones, develop a system that translates point-cloud depth data into 3D HRTF (Head-Related Transfer Function) audio cues. Objects closer to the user produce higher frequency or louder localized clicks.

suggestions.title: Public Transit API Navigation Integration
suggestions.desc: Integrate the UI with GTFS (General Transit Feed Specification) data. Use spatial audio to 'pull' the user toward the correct bus stop or train platform, providing verbal 3D-audio cues for upcoming arrivals.

suggestions.title: Haptic-Audio Fusion Wearable
suggestions.desc: Create a companion wearable device (belt or vest) using ESP32 and vibrating motors. Synchronize the 3D audio cues with haptic pulses to provide a multi-sensory 'map' of the environment for improved directional confidence.

suggestions.title: Voice-First Wayfinding Assistant
suggestions.desc: Implement an LLM-based voice interface using OpenAI Whisper for speech-to-text. Users can ask, 'Where is the nearest exit?' and the system responds with spatialized audio beacons that lead them through the building.

title: Group Key Exchange without TTP
authors: Youdom Kemmoe Victor (MSCS), Yongseok Kwo Kwon, Seunghyeon Shin
abstract: This project aims at devising a security protocol that will allow a set of IoTs to establish a common cryptographic group key that they can use to encrypt/ decrypt messages exchanged. Nowadays, IoTs (smartwatches, connected weighing scale, connected fridges, etc.) handle more of our private data, such as our sleep pattern. And they interact with each other by exchanging messages. Current approaches based on the client-server model are flawed. Companies handling those servers (Trusted third parties, TTP) may go out of business, which can render our IoTs obsolete, or they can sell data transacting through those servers without owners' consent. Hence, the need for a protocol that does not rely…

suggestions.title: Differential Privacy for Student Bio-Data
suggestions.desc: Implement a Differential Privacy layer on the data ingestion pipeline to ensure that individual student stress levels cannot be deanonymized while still allowing the teacher to see aggregate classroom trends.

suggestions.title: Teacher Dashboard with Real-Time Heatmaps
suggestions.desc: Develop a React-based web dashboard that visualizes the classroom's 'engagement level' as a heatmap. Use WebSockets to provide the teacher with immediate feedback on which parts of a lecture are causing boredom or confusion.

suggestions.title: Smart Lighting and HVAC Feedback Loop
suggestions.desc: Connect the IoT sensors to a smart building controller (BACnet). Automatically adjust the classroom lighting color temperature or lower the room temperature if the sensors detect a significant spike in aggregate student sleepiness.

suggestions.title: Wearable SDK Integration (Apple/Fitbit)
suggestions.desc: Expand the sensor suite to include data from student smartwatches. Use the Heart Rate Variability (HRV) and SpO2 data from the HealthKit/Fitbit SDKs to calibrate the desk sensors and improve accuracy of the stress models.

title: Improving Solar Panel Efficiency
authors: Sumedha Inamdar (MSCS), Advisor(s): Dr. Sandeep Das, Dr. Sumit
abstract: Improving Solar Panel Efficiency by predicting the Maximum Power Point Tracking. With the global warming and environmental pollution increasing, it is of high importance that we adapt to clean and sustainable energy. Solar energy is the first that strikes our mind because of its clean nature and abundancy. Harvesting solar energy has some challenges associated with it such as improving the efficiency of photovoltaic (PV) module and making sure that the solar array operates at maximum power point (MPP). Maximum power point tracking (MPPT) techniques play a major role in efficiency improvement of the PV system. There have been various tracking techniques/ algorithms built to make the PV array…

suggestions.title: Streaming Cluster Integration with Apache Flink
suggestions.desc: Evolve the static MapReduce clustering into a dynamic stream processing application using Flink to handle real-time data clusters that evolve over time.

suggestions.title: Density-Based Big Data Clustering (DBSCAN)
suggestions.desc: Implement the DBSCAN algorithm for the MapReduce framework to compare its ability to find non-spherical clusters against traditional K-Means on the Hadoop cluster.

suggestions.title: Global Social Media Topic Community Detection
suggestions.desc: Use the Hadoop clustering implementation to analyze terabytes of Twitter/X data, grouping users into 'topic communities' based on the co-occurrence of hashtags.

suggestions.title: B2B Customer Segmentation Service
suggestions.desc: Build a specialized service for e-commerce platforms that uses the MapReduce clustering to segment millions of customers into high-value personas based on purchase frequency and volume.
