title: Intrusion Detection in IoT Network
authors: Madhuri Gurunathrao Desai (MSCS), Advisor: Dr. Yong Shi
abstract: I am concentrating on Intrusion Detection in the Internet of Things. IoT devices, which are always connected to the internet, are more susceptible to botnet attacks. To prevent such attacks, machine learning models can be built to classify normal network traffic from the once caused by botnets. Also, dimensionality reduction can be used to reduce the time taken to train data. During my tenure, I plan to research on how we can detect the intrusion traffic caused in the Internet of things (IoT) network by botnets. I am planning to use two different DR algorithms – PCA and F-Score, and two different classification methods – Decision Tree and Random Forest. With this, I plan to measure which cla

suggestions.title: Near-Infrared (NIR) Liveness Detection
suggestions.desc: Incorporate a Raspberry Pi NoIR camera to detect the 'liveness' of the eye. This prevents spoofing attacks using high-resolution photos or video screens, as real human eyes reflect infrared light uniquely compared to screens.

suggestions.title: Decentralized Biometric ID with Blockchain
suggestions.desc: Integrate the iris recognition system with a Self-Sovereign Identity (SSI) framework. Use Zero-Knowledge Proofs to allow a user to authenticate their identity without ever transmitting their raw biometric data to a server.

suggestions.title: Emotional and Cognitive State Analysis
suggestions.desc: Research the use of pupillary response (task-evoked pupillary response) to detect user cognitive load or emotional arousal. This could be used for 'adaptive security' where higher authentication is required if the user appears under duress.

suggestions.title: Multi-Factor Iris + Face Fusion
suggestions.desc: Develop a dual-model authentication system that uses YOLOv8 for face detection and a specialized CNN for iris segmentation (like DeepIris). This improves reliability in various lighting conditions where iris-only detection might fail.

similar: legacy:GR-041|Fall 2020|iot botnet and nw intrusion detect

title: OutlierDetection using RandomForest
authors: Divya Pramasani Mohandoss (MSCS), Advisor: Dr. Yong Shi
abstract: In this project we designed a model to detect the Outliers . As technology changes constantly, it is becoming difficult to track the behavior and pattern of Data. To come up with the solution we used machine learning, it becomes feasible to automate this process and to save some of the intensive amounts of labor that is put into detecting Outlier manually.

suggestions.title: Explainable AI (XAI) for Claim Adjusters
suggestions.desc: Integrate SHAP or LIME into the prediction model to provide 'reasoning' for each severity score. This helps insurance adjusters understand why a specific claim was flagged as high-cost, increasing trust in the automated system.

suggestions.title: Multi-Modal Damage Assessment
suggestions.desc: Expand the machine learning model to include computer vision. Use a CNN to analyze uploaded photos of vehicle damage and fuse those features with the tabular data to produce a more accurate severity estimate.

suggestions.title: Fraud Detection using Graph Neural Networks
suggestions.desc: Implement a GNN to identify suspicious relationships between claimants, doctors, and repair shops. Cross-reference the severity predictions with historical fraud patterns to flag potential 'staged' accidents.

suggestions.title: Federated Learning for Cross-Carrier Training
suggestions.desc: Develop a Federated Learning framework that allows multiple insurance companies to train a shared severity model without sharing sensitive customer data, resulting in a more robust and generalized model.

title: Smart Contract for Learning
authors: Farid Khan (MSCS), Advisor: Dr. Xiaohua Xu
abstract: There is a growing demand for education content on the internet. Further the industry requires technical and specific IT skillset to handle the growing use of technology. We propose a smart contract mechanism to provide transaction of digital education content (DEC). The system provides authentication, security and anonymity. It doesn't require a third party to authenticate the transaction or the e-content. These models can be used to train employees or public learning through decentralized network.

suggestions.title: Adversarial Defense for Malware Vision
suggestions.desc: Implement 'adversarial training' in the TensorFlow model to detect malware that has been specifically obfuscated to bypass image-based LBP feature detection.

suggestions.title: Transfer Learning with ResNet-50
suggestions.desc: Replace the custom TensorFlow classifier with a ResNet-50 model pre-trained on ImageNet to see if general visual features improve the classification of malware LBP images.

suggestions.title: Live Malware-to-Image Scanning API
suggestions.desc: Develop a SaaS API that allows security teams to upload binaries, converts them to LBP images on-the-fly, and returns a 'threat signature' classification in milliseconds.

suggestions.title: Mobile Malware Detection for Android
suggestions.desc: Optimize the LBP-based detection model for mobile deployment (TensorFlow Lite) to scan Android APK files for malicious patterns directly on the device.

title: Analysis of Mental Illness with VR
authors: Christopher Baxter (MSSWE), Advisor: Dr Joy Li
abstract: Virtual Reality, while a hot trend in the entertainment industry, is also becoming a large area of study in the field of mental health. DaveG is a serious game based in a VR environment designed to provide mental health professionals with a visual tool to help augment their current practices of diagnosing mental illness. Through the use of mood induction, DaveG allows a professional to observe a subject's reactions to mental stimuli, as well as physical activities, in order to assess their mental state.

suggestions.title: Adversarial Robustness Testing against Feature-Statically Evaded Malware
suggestions.desc: Investigate how the model behaves when malware authors intentionally manipulate the 9 selected features using GANs to bypass detection. Build a testing suite that generates 'adversarial malware' to evaluate the stability of the RF and SVM classifiers.

suggestions.title: Explainable Malware Diagnostics with SHAP and LIME
suggestions.desc: Integrate SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) to visualize exactly why the neural network flagged a specific file. Create a dashboard for security analysts that highlights which of the 9 features contributed most to a 'malicious' verdict.

suggestions.title: Real-time Windows Kernel Driver Implementation
suggestions.desc: Port the Python-based detection logic into a C++ Windows kernel driver that monitors file execution in real-time. Use a lightweight inference engine like ONNX Runtime to maintain the 99.6% accuracy without significant CPU overhead.

suggestions.title: Cross-Platform Feature Mapping for Android APKs
suggestions.desc: Extend the feature selection methodology to the Android domain by extracting intents, permissions, and API calls. Test if a similarly small subset of features (under 10) can achieve comparable accuracy on the Drebin or Contagio datasets.

project_key: legacy:GR-07|Spring 2020|analysis of mental illness with vr

title: Comprehensive Profile Modeling
authors: Honglai Peng (MSCS), Advisor(s): Dr. Selena He, Dr Meng Han
abstract: In this project, we will create a new model which can accomplish the tasks of face detection, gender classification, age classification, emotion classification and more simultaneously in a single integrated module.

suggestions.title: On-Device Inference with TensorFlow Lite and Face Mesh
suggestions.desc: Optimize the deep learning model for mobile deployment using quantization and pruning to run on mid-range smartphones. Use MediaPipe Face Mesh to ensure classification is only performed on high-quality facial crops, reducing false positives from background noise.

suggestions.title: Mitigating Demographic Bias in Classification
suggestions.desc: Evaluate the model's accuracy across different ethnicities using the FairFace dataset. Implement adversarial debiasing or re-weighting techniques during training to ensure the age and gender estimation performs equitably across all protected groups.

suggestions.title: Retail Analytics Heatmap and Demographic Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Develop a system that connects to a store's IP camera to track the age and gender distribution of customers over time. Use OpenCV for multi-face tracking and visualize the data in a React-based dashboard to help retailers optimize product placement.

suggestions.title: Privacy-Preserving Edge Classification with Local Blurring
suggestions.desc: Modify the pipeline to perform age/gender estimation on the 'edge' and immediately discard the raw image. Implement a system where only the metadata (e.g., 'Male, 25-30') is sent to the server, while the facial image is blurred or deleted to comply with GDPR/CCPA.

title: Text Based Rating Prediction
authors: Bhawana Sharma (MISIT), Samantha Russo (MSIT), Advisor: Dr. Ying Xie
abstract: This project aims to predict user rating based on ordinal scale for Amazon products review text. For our prediction we are using a novel loss function called Ordinal Hyperplane Loss (OHPL) solution proposed by Dr. Bob Vanderheyden and Dr. Ying Xie. The proposed solution enables deep learning techniques to be applied to the ordinal classification problem on both structured and unstructured data.

suggestions.title: Transformer-Powered Ordinal Hyperplanes
suggestions.desc: Replace the standard feature extraction with a pre-trained RoBERTa or DistilBERT model, feeding the embeddings into the OHPL layer to see how deep linguistic context improves rating accuracy over traditional CNNs.

suggestions.title: Explainable AI (XAI) Hyperplane Visualization
suggestions.desc: Develop a Streamlit dashboard that uses SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) to visualize which words are pushing a review across a specific 'hyperplane' (e.g., what specific phrase moved a 3-star review to 4-star).

suggestions.title: Multimodal Review Analysis
suggestions.desc: Combine the text-based OHPL with an image-processing branch (using ResNet) to analyze Amazon reviews that include photos, predicting ratings based on both the text sentiment and visual quality of the product.

suggestions.title: Cross-Domain Transfer Learning Suite
suggestions.desc: Test the robustness of the OHPL solution by training on Amazon reviews and performing zero-shot or few-shot inference on IMDb movie ratings or Yelp restaurant reviews to measure model generalization.

title: AI Melanoma Detection
authors: Ava Podrazhansky (BSCS), Rebekah Roepke (BSCS), Jennifer
abstract: This project examines methods of data cleaning and different machine learning models to predict early cases of melanoma.

suggestions.title: Temporal Consistency for Video Segmentation
suggestions.desc: Move from single-frame Mask R-CNN to a video-based approach using ConvLSTM or Mask2Former. This ensures that instruments are tracked smoothly across frames without 'flickering' or losing the mask during fast surgical movements.

suggestions.title: Intraoperative 'No-Go' Zone Alerts
suggestions.desc: Develop a real-time monitoring layer that defines critical tissue areas (like major arteries) as 'No-Go' zones. If the Mask R-CNN detects a surgical tool entering a predefined proximity to these areas, it triggers an audible or visual alert for the surgeon.

suggestions.title: Edge Deployment on NVIDIA Jetson
suggestions.desc: Optimize the Mask R-CNN model using TensorRT and quantization (INT8) to run at 30+ FPS on an NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano. This makes the system portable and usable in operating rooms without requiring a full server rack.

suggestions.title: Automated Post-Surgical Report Generation
suggestions.desc: Use the segmentation data to quantify the duration of the surgery, the frequency of tool usage, and the amount of tissue handled. Generate an automated PDF report summarizing the technical efficiency of the procedure for surgical training and review.

title: Automatic Fake News Detection
authors: Jordan Hasty (BSCS), Advisor: Dr. Dan Lo
abstract: This project implements a fake news detection model to identify deliberate disinformation in news articles. The model utilizes machine learning and natural language processing techniques to analyze news articles and related social media posts to determine the probability that a given news article contains fake news.

suggestions.title: Offline LLM Tutor with Quantized Models
suggestions.desc: Integrate a small-parameter Large Language Model (like Phi-2 or Llama-3-8B) quantized for mobile. Build a voice-to-text interface that allows children to ask questions about the video lessons without needing an internet connection.

suggestions.title: Handwriting Assessment via Stroke Analysis
suggestions.desc: Develop a computer vision module for the 'sand tray' feature that analyzes the child's finger or stylus strokes. Use a lightweight CNN to give real-time feedback on letter formation and track literacy progress over time.

suggestions.title: Gamified Adaptive Learning Path
suggestions.desc: Create a software layer that adjusts the difficulty of the video lessons and whiteboard activities based on the child's performance. Use a Reinforcement Learning agent to maximize student engagement by offering rewards (digital badges) for completing challenging tasks.

suggestions.title: Solar-Powered Ruggedization for Remote Use
suggestions.desc: Design a 3D-printable, ruggedized casing for the tablet that includes an integrated solar charging panel. Create a 'mesh networking' feature where multiple tablets can sync data and updates when one device occasionally reaches a high-connectivity zone.

title: Mental Illness Detection Chatbot
authors: Ava Podrazhansky (BSCS), Hao Zhang (BSCS), Advisor: Dr. Meng Han, Dr.
abstract: This is a mobile application with a chatbot that detects a user's mental illness and also aims to improve users' moods over time. The application tracks the user's mood changes over time and gives the user with a summary on a profile screen.

suggestions.title: Indoor SLAM with Depth Sensing
suggestions.desc: Enhance the drone's navigation by implementing ROS (Robot Operating System) with a GMapping or Cartographer SLAM package. Use a T265 Realsense camera to allow the drone to build a 3D map of a room and take photos from optimal geometric angles.

suggestions.title: Automated Real Estate Tour Guide
suggestions.desc: Program the drone to follow a pre-defined path through a house, using OpenCV to detect 'points of interest' like fireplaces or large windows. The drone should automatically center these features in the frame and take high-dynamic-range (HDR) photos.

suggestions.title: CNN-Based Aesthetic Scoring
suggestions.desc: Integrate a pre-trained Neural Image Assessment (NIMA) model onto the drone's edge processor. The drone should take multiple photos of a scene and only keep/upload the ones with the highest predicted aesthetic and technical quality scores.

suggestions.title: Autonomous Event Photographer
suggestions.desc: Modify the drone's AI to include face detection and group framing. The drone can be deployed at indoor events to hover and capture 'candid' shots by detecting smiles and eye contact using the MediaPipe library, ensuring everyone is in the frame before snapping.

title: Vehicle License Plate Recognition
authors: Zekai Fei (BSCS), Advisors: Dr. Selena He and Dr. Meng Han
abstract: The project includes a set of programs that can automatically detect vehicle license plates and recognize the characters from the plates. The project works for most images and is working on detecting license plates from videos.

suggestions.title: Deep Reinforcement Learning for Real-Time Control
suggestions.desc: Replace the predictive simulation with a Deep Q-Network (DQN) that controls HVAC setpoints in real-time. Use the 'OpenAI Gym' environment to simulate the West Point building and train the agent to balance occupant comfort (CO2 and Temp) against peak energy prices.

suggestions.title: Campus-Wide Microgrid Load Balancing
suggestions.desc: Extend the peak shaving model to include other energy-intensive campus assets like electric vehicle (EV) charging stations and dorm lighting. Create a coordinated strategy where HVAC load is decreased when EV demand spikes to keep the total campus draw below a critical threshold.

suggestions.title: Commercial Smart Thermostat API for Rebates
suggestions.desc: Develop a SaaS wrapper for the optimization model that connects to smart thermostats (like Ecobee or Nest) via API. Target small business owners by automating their participation in 'Demand Response' programs, earning them utility rebates during peak hours.

suggestions.title: Occupancy Sensing via Thermal Imaging Fusion
suggestions.desc: Research the impact of real-time occupancy data on the peak shaving model. Integrate low-cost PIR or thermal sensors to feed 'human heat load' data into the HVAC simulation, allowing for more aggressive energy savings in empty zones.

title: App Ordinal Classification
authors: Kayla Dougal (BSIT), Jean Rodriguez (BSIT), Jovanny Duran
abstract: Our project’s objective is to take a complex dataset such as an image dataset and apply OHPL as the decision layer to categorize the images into the different levels. The dataset chosen was for Diabetic Retinopathy and the classifications ranged from normal to proliferative.

suggestions.title: Saliency Mapping for Clinical Verification
suggestions.desc: Apply Grad-CAM (Gradient-weighted Class Activation Mapping) to the OHPL decision layer to highlight which parts of the retina the AI is prioritizing, allowing doctors to verify the model’s reasoning for specific severity levels.

suggestions.title: Mobile-Edge Screening with TFLite
suggestions.desc: Optimize the OHPL model using TensorFlow Lite for deployment on mobile devices, enabling healthcare workers in rural areas to perform instant Diabetic Retinopathy screenings without an internet connection.

suggestions.title: Active Learning for Rare Classifications
suggestions.desc: Implement an active learning loop using 'modAL' that identifies 'Proliferative' images where the model has low confidence and flags them specifically for human expert annotation to improve the dataset.

suggestions.title: Temporal Progression Prediction
suggestions.desc: Extend the dataset to include longitudinal patient data, using a Recurrent Neural Network (RNN) combined with OHPL to predict the probability of a patient moving from 'normal' to 'proliferative' in the next 12 months.

title: Edge Computation for SDR-SDN
authors: Samuel Helwig (BASIT), Brandon Gocool (BASIT), Olutayo Taiwo (BASIT),
abstract: Current implementations of Internet systems are very hard to be upgraded. The ossification of existing standards restricts the development of more advanced communication systems. New research initiatives, such as virtualization, software-defined radios, and software-defined networks, allow more flexibility for advanced computations like Edge Computation. We propose that the convergence of these overlying and complementary technologies. In recent years, independent research initiatives have proposed software-based architectures and devices to make networks more flexible. Network virtualization allows a single real machine to run several virtual machines. Software-defined radios (SDRs) impleme

suggestions.title: O-RAN Compliance and Slicing
suggestions.desc: Adapt the SDR-SDN architecture to follow O-RAN (Open Radio Access Network) standards, implementing network slicing to prioritize low-latency Edge Computation for emergency services versus general traffic.

suggestions.title: FPGA-Accelerated Signal Processing
suggestions.desc: Integrate Xilinx RFSoC or Zynq FPGAs to offload high-speed FFT and modulation tasks from the CPU, significantly reducing the power consumption and latency of the Edge Computation nodes.

suggestions.title: Blockchain-Based Spectrum Sharing
suggestions.desc: Develop a decentralized ledger using Hyperledger Fabric that allows different SDR nodes to bid for and lease frequency spectrum in real-time, preventing interference in crowded urban Edge environments.

suggestions.title: Deep Learning Interference Mitigation
suggestions.desc: Train a convolutional autoencoder on raw I/Q signal data to identify and filter out intentional jamming or accidental interference within the software-defined radio environment in real-time.

title: Using Artificial intelligence to Prescribe Medicine
authors: Xander Bush and

suggestions.title: Knowledge Graph for Drug-Interaction Safety
suggestions.desc: Integrate a Neo4j knowledge graph using the DrugBank and SIDER datasets to ensure the AI's prescriptions are automatically checked for contraindications and adverse drug-drug interactions.

suggestions.title: Personalized Genomic Prescription Tuning
suggestions.desc: Incorporate Biopython to parse pharmacogenomic data (SNPs), allowing the AI to adjust dosage levels based on a patient's genetic markers for drug metabolism (e.g., CYP450 enzyme variants).

suggestions.title: Explainable Medical Logic via SHAP
suggestions.desc: Implement a SHAP (SHapley Additive exPlanations) dashboard that provides doctors with a visual 'why' for every prescription, listing the top clinical features (weight, lab results, age) that influenced the AI’s choice.

suggestions.title: Closed-Loop Dosage Monitoring App
suggestions.desc: Build a React Native mobile companion app that uses the AI's output to track patient adherence and uses a feedback loop (patient-reported symptoms) to suggest dosage micro-adjustments to the physician.

title: Service Industry Sentience
authors: Charles Peery

suggestions.title: Real-Time Feedback Streaming with Kafka
suggestions.desc: Scale the existing sentiment analysis model by integrating it with Apache Kafka and the Yelp Fusion API. This would allow the system to process thousands of live reviews per second, providing service managers with an instant dashboard of customer satisfaction trends across multiple locations.

suggestions.title: Service Staff Burnout Predictor
suggestions.desc: Repurpose the 'sentience' NLP engine to analyze internal communication logs or employee surveys instead of customer reviews. By identifying shifts in professional tone and sentiment over time, you can build an early-warning system for HR to detect departments at high risk of turnover.

suggestions.title: Multimodal Sarcasm and Emotion Detection
suggestions.desc: Extend the text-based analysis by incorporating audio from customer service recordings using the Librosa library. Researching the intersection of vocal pitch and textual sentiment will help the system distinguish between literal praise and sarcastic dissatisfaction, a common hurdle in service industry data.

suggestions.title: Automated Managerial Response Generator
suggestions.desc: Integrate a Large Language Model (LLM) like GPT-4 to automatically draft personalized, context-aware responses to the analyzed feedback. The system would categorize the sentiment, identify the specific complaint (e.g., long wait times), and generate a professional resolution for manager approval.

title: Wellstar Health Systems Internship
authors: Joyce Anderson

suggestions.title: FHIR-Standardized Interoperability Layer
suggestions.desc: Take the internal data tools developed during the Wellstar internship and wrap them in an HL7 FHIR (Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources) API. Using a framework like HAPI FHIR, you can ensure the system can safely exchange patient or operational data with other hospital EHR systems.

suggestions.title: Predictive Emergency Room Triage Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Using the operational workflow data from the project, build a machine learning model to predict ER wait times and patient inflow based on historical Wellstar data. Display this via a React-based dashboard to help hospital administrators reallocate staff before peak congestion hours.

suggestions.title: Privacy-Preserving Federated Learning Framework
suggestions.desc: Implement a federated learning approach using PySyft to allow AI models to be trained on sensitive Wellstar patient data without the data ever leaving local servers. This addresses the critical HIPAA compliance and security concerns inherent in healthcare IT internships.

suggestions.title: Computer Vision for Supply Chain Monitoring
suggestions.desc: Extend the healthcare operational focus by building a computer vision module using OpenCV to track medical inventory levels in real-time. By analyzing video feeds of supply closets, the system can automatically trigger purchase orders when essential items like PPE or bandages fall below a certain threshold.

title: GRP-09-169 Using Logic to Explain and Formally Verify the Behavior of ReLU Neural Networks
authors: Nguyen, Nguyen Thi Binh
abstract: Homeschooling in the United States has expanded rapidly, reaching about 3.4 million students (6.26% of K-12 school-age population) in 2024-2025, with accelerated growth following COVID-19. Understanding the reasons behind these decisions is important for informing education policy, resource allocation, and the design of schooling systems that better meet families’ needs. Most of the research on homeschooling relies on statistical methods such as logistic regression or probit models to identify significant factors associated with homeschooling decisions. In this work, we approach this research question from a different perspective by leveraging Explainable AI techniques to provide deeper insights into the homeschooling decisions.

suggestions.title: Investigate Factors Influencing Homeschooling Decisions Using NLP
suggestions.desc: Utilize Natural Language Processing (NLP) to analyze social media posts, forums, and blogs discussing homeschooling. Gather data from platforms like Twitter or Reddit and apply sentiment analysis to identify key themes and sentiments behind the choice of homeschooling.

suggestions.title: Develop a Visualization Tool for Homeschooling Trends
suggestions.desc: Create an interactive web application using D3.js or Plotly to visualize homeschooling trends across different U.S. states. Incorporate data from the National Center for Education Statistics (NCES) to display statistical insights and facilitate user interaction.

suggestions.title: Build a Resource Allocation Dashboard for Homeschooling
suggestions.desc: Design a dashboard that aggregates resources, support groups, and curriculum options for homeschooling families. Use Google Maps API to allow users to find local resources and integrate feedback systems for users to rate their experiences.

suggestions.title: Analyze Impact of COVID-19 on Homeschooling Decisions
suggestions.desc: Leverage public datasets from education departments to analyze how COVID-19 has influenced the rise in homeschooling. Use statistical analysis tools like R or Python's pandas to draw correlations between COVID-19 case rates and homeschooling enrollment.

title: GRP-150-192 From Construction Floor-Plan to Robot-Ready Navigation Map: A Dual-Memory Multi-Agentic  AI That Learns from Its Own Successes and Failures
authors: Akhi, Amatul
abstract: Construction floor plans contain rich architectural information, but they are not directly usable for robotic navigation in IoT-enabled smart buildings. They often require manual processing to remove irrelevant annotations and extract navigable layouts. Existing methods either depend on fixed image-processing pipelines that do not generalize well across different floor plan styles or on data-intensive learning models that require large annotated datasets. We propose a dual-memory multi-agent framework that treats floor plan-to-map conversion as a sequential, experience-driven decision process under data scarcity. The framework uses three cooperative agents for perception, decision-making, and evaluation, which interact through a shared persistent memory represented as a relational experience graph. Each memory entry stores a visual embedding that captures floor plan characteristics and a logical embedding that records processing decisions, outcomes, and failure patterns. This design enables similarity-based retrieval, reuse of successful configurations, and explicit avoidance of past failures. Experiments on benchmark construction floor plans, open-source residential floor plans, and real-world robot navigation show strong structural preservation, stable cross-domain generalization with frozen memory, and reliable physical deployment. Our system preserves navigation-critical topology while avoiding structural corruption, demonstrating data-efficient and transferable performance across diverse floor plan styles.

suggestions.title: Integrate Computer Vision for Floor Plan Interpretation
suggestions.desc: Implement a computer vision model using TensorFlow or OpenCV to automatically detect and extract relevant features from various floor plan styles. Train the model on a dataset of annotated floor plans to improve the accuracy of navigation map generation.

suggestions.title: Evaluate Multi-Agent Collaboration in Navigation Tasks
suggestions.desc: Conduct research on the effectiveness of multi-agent systems by simulating different agent strategies for navigating floor plans. Use environments like Unity or Gazebo to analyze performance metrics and decision-making efficiency.

suggestions.title: Extend Navigation Maps for Different Building Types
suggestions.desc: Expand the framework to include support for different building types, such as residential or commercial spaces. Collect datasets of diverse floor plans and create a robust model that can adapt to architectural variations.

suggestions.title: Develop a User Interface for Custom Floor Plan Input
suggestions.desc: Create a user-friendly web interface that allows users to upload their own floor plans and receive robot-ready navigation maps. Use frameworks like Flask or React to build the frontend and backend systems for processing the input.

title: GC-113-125 Carter’s Lake Visitor Center Boating Safety Game
authors: Blake, Hunter, Brown, Chancelor, Robbins, Lauren, Bryant, Will, Bryant, Kendrick
abstract: The Boating Safety Game is an educational, kiosk-based touchscreen game created for the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers and the Carters Lake Visitor Center. It is designed to improve the knowledge and engagement of boating safety concepts for visitors, particularly for students and youth. The project was developed using multiple game scenarios meant to reinforce safe boating practices through tutorial scenes, top down navigation, life jacket and required item selection tasks, and player motivation through quizzes, feedback, scores, and a star ranking system. The game’s design emphasizes accessibility and retention through simple touchscreen interaction, guided instruction, and repeated feedback on player choices. Together, these elements demonstrate a structured and engaging approach to teaching core boating safety concepts in a format aligned with the educational and operational needs of the visitor center.

suggestions.title: Enhance the Game with Augmented Reality Features
suggestions.desc: Integrate Augmented Reality (AR) elements using ARKit or ARCore to make the boating safety game more immersive. Allow players to visualize safety equipment in their real-world environment, enhancing engagement and learning.

suggestions.title: Assess Learning Outcomes from Game Interactions
suggestions.desc: Conduct a study to evaluate the effectiveness of the boating safety game on improving knowledge retention. Use surveys and pre/post-tests to measure changes in user understanding and engagement with boating safety concepts.

suggestions.title: Localize the Game for Different Regions
suggestions.desc: Adapt the game to include local boating regulations and safety practices for different states or regions. Collaborate with local authorities to ensure the accuracy of the information and enhance the game's educational value.

suggestions.title: Create a Mobile Version of the Boating Safety Game
suggestions.desc: Develop a mobile application version of the game for iOS and Android using Unity or Flutter. Ensure that it retains the educational elements while being accessible for users on the go.

title: UR-166-204 Multi-user STEM Learning Experience Powered  by LLM Conversational Agents
authors: Haynes, Devon, Boecker, Chance, Haggard, Oliver
abstract: While Extended Reality (XR) provides experiential and interactive foundations for STEM education, current storytelling and narrative-driven applications often lack responsive nonplayer characters (NPCs), limiting interactive potential through pre-scripted stories. Additionally, despite the growth of Large Language Model (LLM) integration in XR, limited research explores the combined use of multi-user XR systems and conversational Artificial Intelligence (AI) to facilitate real-time, adaptive instruction. This project seeks to address these gaps by 1) Developing a narrative-driven STEM learning XR prototype that incorporates synchronous multi-user interaction and an embedded LLM-driven conversational agent and 2) Exploring the effectiveness of combining these technologies to improve learning engagement and educational outcomes.

suggestions.title: Incorporate Real-Time Data into LLM Interactions
suggestions.desc: Enhance the LLM by integrating real-time data APIs to provide up-to-date information on STEM topics during user interactions. Use APIs like Wolfram Alpha or custom databases to enrich the conversational experience.

suggestions.title: Evaluate User Engagement in Multi-User XR Environments
suggestions.desc: Conduct user studies to assess how well the conversational agents maintain engagement in a multi-user XR setting. Analyze user feedback and interaction logs to identify areas for improvement in agent responsiveness.

suggestions.title: Develop Customizable NPCs for Different Learning Scenarios
suggestions.desc: Create a framework that allows educators to design their own NPCs with specific educational goals. Provide a user-friendly interface for customizing dialogue and interactions based on various STEM subjects.

suggestions.title: Create a Repository of STEM Learning Scenarios
suggestions.desc: Build a library of diverse STEM learning scenarios that educators can utilize with the LLM agents. Include example scripts and interaction guidelines to facilitate effective teaching in XR environments.

title: GRM-159-214 Transportation Energy and Emission Modeling and Analysis Tool (TEEMAT)
authors: Neno Aloyem, Laeticia
abstract: The Transportation Energy and Emission Modeling and Analysis Tool (TEEMAT) is a web-based decision-support framework for evaluating the environmental impacts of EV adoption across U.S cities. TEEMAT integrates a feedforward neural network trained on MOVES 4.0 for tract-level vehicle emissions (CO2, NO?, PM2.5), a macroscopic traffic and activity-based model capturing congestion-driven emission spikes, and a Meta-Prophet model trained on NREL Cambium data for grid emissions (CO2, CH4, N2O). Results show that while EV adoption reduces tailpipe emissions, rising travel demand and congestion-induced low speeds can significantly offset these gains underscoring that meaningful decarbonization requires coordinated transportation and energy grid strategies.

suggestions.title: Implement a Real-Time Emission Tracking System
suggestions.desc: Develop a system that provides real-time tracking of vehicle emissions in different cities using IoT sensors. Integrate the collected data with TEEMAT to update the environmental impact analysis dynamically.

suggestions.title: Analyze the Impact of EV Charging Infrastructure on Emissions
suggestions.desc: Investigate how different EV charging infrastructures influence overall emissions in urban settings. Use statistical modeling to correlate charging station density with emission reductions, leveraging datasets from local municipalities.

suggestions.title: Create Visual Dashboards for Emission Data Analysis
suggestions.desc: Design interactive dashboards using Tableau or Power BI to visualize emission trends associated with EV adoption. Include filters for different cities and time frames to aid policymakers in decision-making.

suggestions.title: Expand TEEMAT for Public Transportation Analysis
suggestions.desc: Adapt TEEMAT to evaluate the environmental impact of public transportation systems, including buses and trains. Incorporate additional datasets and modify the modeling approach to consider public transport emissions.

title: GRM-010-170 AI-Enabled Water Quality Framework for E. coli Prediction and Forecasting
authors: Devaraj, Sangeetha, Mhatre, Jui
abstract: Water quality monitoring is essential for public health and environmental sustainability, yet existing monitoring infrastructures remain sparse, fragmented, and incomplete. Data from the United States Geological Survey (USGS) indicate that while over 1.5 million sites are cataloged in the USGS Water Data for the Nation, only a small fraction are actively reporting water quality measurements, with significant reductions observed in recent years. Moreover, critical parameters such as pH, water temperature, dissolved oxygen, turbidity, and microbial indicators like Escherichia coli are inconsistently measured, with widespread missing and irregular data. This work presents an AI-enabled water quality data framework designed to address these limitations by integrating heterogeneous environmental datasets, reconstructing missing observations, and enabling predictive analytics. The proposed framework incorporates spatio-temporal modeling and location-aware representations to capture upstream–downstream dependencies and environmental influences such as rainfall and watershed dynamics. Building on this foundation, we develop predictive models for E. coli concentration and contamination risk forecasting, transforming sparse and incomplete monitoring data into actionable insights. Building on this foundation, we transform sparse monitoring data into actionable insights through continuous forecasting and binary risk classification. Our proposed regression model captures the underlying environmental physics, explaining 71% of the variance (R2=0.71) in same-day E. coli loads. While continuous 24-hour advanced forecasting is limited by irregular testing data (R2=0.40), our novelty lies in deploying a binary safety classifier that successfully forecasts actionable public health hazards one day in advance with an overall accuracy of 74%.

suggestions.title: Integrate Real-Time IoT Sensors for Continuous Monitoring
suggestions.desc: Extend the framework by incorporating IoT sensors for real-time water quality data collection. Use platforms like Arduino or Raspberry Pi with sensors that measure pH, turbidity, and temperature, sending real-time data to a cloud service such as AWS or Azure for immediate analysis and forecasting.

suggestions.title: Comparative Analysis of E. coli Prediction Models
suggestions.desc: Conduct a study comparing the performance of various machine learning models (e.g., Random Forest, SVM, and Neural Networks) on E. coli prediction using historical water quality datasets. Utilize datasets from USGS and analyze which models yield the most accurate forecasts under different environmental conditions.

suggestions.title: Develop a Mobile Application for Public Access to Water Quality Data
suggestions.desc: Create a mobile application that allows users to access real-time water quality data and E. coli forecasts in their area. Implement features like alerts for unsafe water conditions and educational resources about water safety, leveraging APIs to fetch data from the developed framework.

suggestions.title: Create a Community Engagement Platform for Water Quality Reporting
suggestions.desc: Build an online platform where community members can report local water quality issues and receive feedback based on the AI framework's predictions. Use social media integration and gamification to encourage participation and awareness, potentially partnering with local health departments.

title: GRP-114-127 Gamma-Sieve: Structural De-obfuscation of Financial Regime Manipulation via Heterophilic Graph Neural Networks
authors: Regan, Christopher
abstract: Market manipulation increasingly exploits fragmentation — dispersing orders across dozens of accounts, venues, and sub-second timing windows — to evade rule-based surveillance. We present Gamma-Sieve, a heterophilic graph neural network approach that constructs heterogeneous transaction graphs (four node types, ten edge types) from market microstructure data, applying CARE-GNN with RL-gated edge filtering and TFE-GNN with spectral triple-frequency decomposition. At production scale, heterophilic GNNs outperform a bidirectional LSTM baseline by +16% AUC on fragmented coordination attacks. However, evaluation on real NASDAQ equity data (LOBSTER Level 3) reveals a critical domain-shift challenge: GNN false positive rates of 42–88% on legitimate trading, caused by structural overlap between adversarial fragmentation and real market-making coordination. Fragmentation calibration resolves this for CARE-GNN, reducing FPR from 7.5% to 2.4%. A cross-attack experiment further qualifies the structural advantage: GNNs catastrophically fail on unseen single-agent attacks while the LSTM detects them perfectly, demonstrating that robust deployment requires multi-architecture ensembles. These results establish graph topology as essential for detecting dispersed coordination while revealing the practical necessity of domain calibration and attack-diverse training for real-world deployment.

suggestions.title: Enhance Gamma-Sieve with Time-Series Analysis
suggestions.desc: Integrate time-series analysis into the Gamma-Sieve framework to allow for temporal prediction of market manipulation tactics. Utilize libraries like TensorFlow or PyTorch to create models that can predict future manipulative behaviors based on historical transaction data.

suggestions.title: Evaluate the Impact of Market News on Manipulative Behavior
suggestions.desc: Investigate how external news events influence market manipulation by analyzing news sentiment alongside transaction data. Use natural language processing (NLP) techniques to assess sentiment and correlate it with manipulation patterns identified by Gamma-Sieve.

suggestions.title: Develop a Dashboard for Real-Time Market Manipulation Detection
suggestions.desc: Create an interactive dashboard that visualizes real-time transaction data and highlights potential manipulation patterns identified by Gamma-Sieve. Use tools like D3.js or Tableau for visualization to aid financial analysts in decision-making.

suggestions.title: Collaborate with Financial Institutions for Practical Applications
suggestions.desc: Partner with financial institutions to implement Gamma-Sieve in their surveillance systems. Conduct pilot projects to demonstrate the effectiveness of your model and gather feedback for further improvements, potentially leading to commercial applications.

title: GRP-07-163 SMISHGUARD: An AI-Powered Framework for SMS Phishing Detection and Alert System for Vulnerable Users
authors: Das, Jiban Krisna
abstract: This research introduces SMISH-GUARD, a multi-layer framework for adaptive SMS phishing (smishing) detection that integrates language-aware semantic modeling, graph-theoretic campaign reasoning, and cost-sensitive decision calibration within a unified architecture. The framework integrates dual transformer encoders for multilingual semantic understanding with a heterogeneous temporal graph layer that captures relational attack signals such as shared URLs, sender reuse, and campaign propagation patterns. A cost-sensitive decision optimization module is further incorporated to translate probabilistic model outputs into risk-aware alert policies that explicitly balance false-positive inconvenience against the higher societal and financial cost of missed smishing attacks. The study evaluates four integrated datasets comprising 51,528 SMS messages and 16 phishing attack subtypes under realistic class imbalance and multilingual conditions. The framework achieves PR-AUC of 0.943 and ROC-AUC of 0.950, while maintaining high recall for critical phishing subtypes. The ablation study confirms that each architectural component—including semantic modeling, graph-based risk propagation and decision calibration that contributes independently to overall robustness. Multilingual experiments also indicate promising generalization across linguistic contexts. The framework emphasizes deployment realism, incorporating probabilistic calibration, adversarial robustness considerations, and explainable decision thresholds suitable for adaptive on-device warning systems. The findings suggest that effective smishing defense requires not only accurate language models but also mathematically grounded decision policies and structurally aware representations of attacker behavior. SMISHGUARD establishes a principled foundation for next-generation smishing defense systems that combine semantic intelligence, relational reasoning, and human-centered risk optimization to mitigate evolving SMS threat.

suggestions.title: Implement Multilingual Support for Broader User Reach
suggestions.desc: Expand the SMISH-GUARD framework to support more languages by training the dual transformer encoders with multilingual datasets, such as the multilingual version of BERT. This will enhance the detection capabilities for smishing across diverse populations.

suggestions.title: Investigate the Evolution of Smishing Tactics Over Time
suggestions.desc: Conduct research to analyze how smishing tactics have evolved, using historical SMS datasets. This can involve studying the changes in language patterns, sender behaviors, and campaign propagation to inform better detection strategies.

suggestions.title: Create a User-Friendly Mobile App for Smishing Alerts
suggestions.desc: Develop a mobile application that alerts users in real-time about potential smishing messages based on the detection framework. Include features like educational resources on identifying smishing attempts and reporting suspicious messages directly from the app.

suggestions.title: Engage with Telecommunication Companies for Wider Deployment
suggestions.desc: Work with telecom providers to integrate the SMISH-GUARD framework into their networks for proactive smishing detection. This could involve developing APIs for seamless communication between the framework and telecom systems, enhancing user safety at a larger scale.

title: UC-138-166 The Allies Connect Platform: Improving Access to Community Resources Through Technology?  ?
authors: Holland, Sarah, Anand, Neha, Andoh, Aldrick, Diop, Yacine, Rogers, Alex
abstract: Finding help shouldn’t be difficult, but for many people, it is. Important information about food, shelter, and local support is often scattered across different websites, social media pages, and documents, making it hard to find what’s needed, especially in urgent situations. The Allies Connect platform was created to bring that information into one place. It is a centralized, mobile-friendly platform that allows users to: • Search for resources • Register for events • Connect with nonprofits At the same time, the platform also provides organizations with simple tools to keep their information accurate and up to date. By focusing on clarity and ease of use, Allies Connect helps people find the support they need more quickly and confidently.

suggestions.title: Integrate Machine Learning for Resource Recommendation
suggestions.desc: Implement a machine learning algorithm that recommends community resources based on user preferences and past behaviors. Use collaborative filtering techniques and data from user interactions to personalize the experience on the Allies Connect platform.

suggestions.title: Analyze User Interaction Patterns for Improved Resource Accessibility
suggestions.desc: Conduct research on how users interact with the platform, identifying barriers to resource access. Use analytics tools to gather data on usage patterns, which can inform design adjustments to improve user experience and resource discovery.

suggestions.title: Develop a Chatbot for Instant Resource Queries
suggestions.desc: Create an AI-powered chatbot that can assist users in finding resources quickly through natural language processing. The chatbot can be integrated into the Allies Connect platform, providing 24/7 support for users seeking urgent assistance.

suggestions.title: Partner with Local Nonprofits for Enhanced Resource Listings
suggestions.desc: Engage with local nonprofit organizations to continually update and expand the resource database on the Allies Connect platform. This collaboration can ensure accurate and timely information, while also fostering community relationships and support.

title: UC-144-182 Paracosm
authors: Friday, Va'Quez
abstract: Paracosm is a gothic horror game revolving around a tattoo artist, named Villain, who discovers that his art has suddenly come to life. Despite this phenomenon, Villain insists on finishing his tasks before three Am, due to his superstitious nature. What he doesn’t know yet is that if he doesn’t finish by that time, then he will be forever stuck in his shop with no escape. He will also learn that not all of his lively drawings are friendly, and that the not-so friendly drawings of his will stop at nothing to make sure he fails-Knowing that if he fails, he will be forced to continue bringing his imaginary world to life and be bound to it for eternity.

suggestions.title: Incorporate Procedural Generation for Unique Game Levels
suggestions.desc: Add procedural generation to create unique levels or scenarios in Paracosm, ensuring each gameplay experience is different. Use algorithms like Perlin noise for environment generation and random seed generation for tattoo designs that influence gameplay.

suggestions.title: Study Player Behavior Under Time Pressure in Horror Games
suggestions.desc: Investigate how time pressure affects player performance and decision-making in horror games like Paracosm. Collect data on player choices and stress levels to analyze gameplay dynamics and refine game design for better engagement.

suggestions.title: Create a VR Experience for Immersive Gameplay
suggestions.desc: Develop a virtual reality version of Paracosm to enhance immersion, allowing players to experience the gothic horror environment more vividly. Use platforms like Unity or Unreal Engine with VR support to create an engaging experience.

suggestions.title: Launch a Community-Based Storytelling Feature
suggestions.desc: Introduce a feature where players can create and share their own stories or tattoos within the game. This could foster a community of creators and increase user engagement, potentially leading to user-generated content and expansion of the game universe.

title: UC-123-140 P15-T2 Boating Safety Game US Army Corps | Boating MVP
authors: Hamza, Maryam, Hamza, Saleh, Vanwinkle, Will, Caffrey, Trevor, Akinsunmi, Tobi
abstract: This project is an interactive 2D educational boating safety game developed in Unity to teach students essential water navigation and life jacket safety practices in an engaging and immersive format. Designed in collaboration with a real-world sponsor, the game simulates a dynamic boating environment where players navigate obstacles, identify hazards, and make safety decisions under time constraints. The experience integrates instructional modules, guided character narration, and a final quiz phase that reinforces knowledge through immediate feedback, scoring, and achievement-based rewards. Players learn critical concepts such as proper life jacket fit, safe boating procedures, hazard identification, and shallow water awareness. The project combines game development principles, user interface design, audio feedback systems, and educational assessment strategies to create a scalable learning tool suitable for kiosk deployment at visitor centers. By blending gamification with real safety standards, the application aims to increase engagement, retention, and real-world boating preparedness among youth audiences.

suggestions.title: Integrate Augmented Reality for Enhanced Learning
suggestions.desc: Enhance the boating safety game by integrating AR features using ARKit or ARCore. Players could use their mobile devices to visualize real-world boating scenarios and hazards overlayed onto their environment, creating a more immersive learning experience.

suggestions.title: Develop a Multiplayer Mode for Team-Based Learning
suggestions.desc: Implement a multiplayer mode where players work in teams to navigate challenges and make safety decisions. This could foster collaboration and communication skills while reinforcing boating safety practices in a social context.

suggestions.title: Analyze Learning Outcomes through User Data
suggestions.desc: Collect and analyze gameplay data to assess how well the game improves users' knowledge of boating safety. Use statistical methods to evaluate the effectiveness of different game modules and refine content based on player feedback.

suggestions.title: Expand to Virtual Reality for Realistic Simulations
suggestions.desc: Create a VR version of the boating safety game that allows players to experience realistic scenarios, such as navigating through stormy weather or rescuing someone in distress. Utilize Unity's XR toolkit for development and test it with VR headsets.

title: GRM-156-153 Finding Top-K assignments for Multi-Hypothesis Tracking
authors: Hood, Tyler, Gurung, Rakshak
abstract: Multi-Hypothesis Tracking (MHT) is a framework for solving the data association problem in multi-target tracking by maintaining multiple possible assignments between observations and targets over time. Rather than committing to a single solution, MHT explores a set of competing hypotheses, allowing it to handle noise, missed detections, and ambiguous measurements. In practical systems such as radar, LiDAR, and vision-based tracking, MHT is commonly implemented using algorithms like Murty’s algorithm to generate multiple high-quality assignment solutions from the Hungarian algorithm. In this work, we instead propose an assignment-tree-based approach, where hypotheses are incrementally constructed and prioritized using a structured search strategy. This allows for more flexible exploration and pruning of the hypothesis space compared to traditional k-best enumeration methods.

suggestions.title: Implement Deep Learning for Improved Tracking Accuracy
suggestions.desc: Incorporate deep learning techniques, such as convolutional neural networks (CNNs), to enhance the data association process in Multi-Hypothesis Tracking. Experiment with datasets from radar or LiDAR systems to validate performance improvements.

suggestions.title: Benchmark MHT Algorithms Against Real-World Scenarios
suggestions.desc: Conduct a comparative study of various MHT algorithms, including Murty’s and the Hungarian algorithm, using real-world datasets from autonomous vehicle tracking. Analyze their effectiveness in different tracking environments and conditions.

suggestions.title: Develop a Visual Interface for MHT Tracking Solutions
suggestions.desc: Create a user-friendly visual interface that allows users to simulate and visualize the MHT process. Use Python with libraries like Matplotlib or Plotly to graphically represent the tracking assignments and hypotheses.

suggestions.title: Integrate MHT with Drones for Wildlife Monitoring
suggestions.desc: Extend the MHT framework to be used in drone applications for wildlife tracking. Collaborate with ecologists to track animal movements and behaviors, utilizing real-time data feeds from drone sensors.

title: UC-122-135 Mutatio Mentis
authors: Alderman, Isaac, Mizell, Braden, Sutton, Collin
abstract: Mutatio Mentis is a first person, narrative heavy, puzzle-lite RPG that follows the story of a renaissance era plague doctor and their attempt to alter the minds of three subjects; a gardener, a street urchin, and a priest. The narrative is set in 1637 Florence, Italy, in the wake of the Great Plague of Milan, and draws heavily from renaissance culture. Each of the three subjects have progressively more complex personal conflicts, which present through the gameplay aesthetics of each act, as the gameplay changes to reflect the problems of each subject. Our focus is on tackling mental and emotional health through empathy and nuance rather than diagnosis. To this end, different aspects of each subject’s psyche take physical form in the dreamscape environments, allowing the player to speak and interact with them directly, influencing the state of the subject themself, and, in turn, the environment around the player. The game features many puzzles, with solutions ranging from alchemy, exploration, and dialogue, to spatial and logical reasoning. The primary goal of play is to reach the core of each subject’s mind, allowing the opportunity to change their fundamental outlook on life, if the player so chooses. Either way, the consequences will follow the player until the end. Our aim with Mutatio Mentis is to make our players think carefully about how their actions might affect each patient they treat, and find their own answer to the question of how much they should influence each one.

suggestions.title: Incorporate Voice Acting for Enhanced Narrative Depth
suggestions.desc: Add voice acting to the narrative elements of Mutatio Mentis to create more immersive character interactions. This could be accomplished using tools like Audacity for audio editing and Unity's audio system for integration.

suggestions.title: Study the Impact of Historical Context on Gameplay Experience
suggestions.desc: Conduct research on how accurately representing the historical context of 1637 Florence affects player engagement and understanding of mental health issues. Use surveys and gameplay analytics to gather data on player experiences.

suggestions.title: Create an Educational Module on Renaissance Medicine
suggestions.desc: Develop an educational module that players can access, providing insights into the medical practices of the renaissance era. This could include interactive timelines and resources to enhance the learning experience related to the game's themes.

suggestions.title: Explore Multiplayer Puzzle Solving for Community Engagement
suggestions.desc: Transform the single-player RPG into a multiplayer experience where players can collaboratively solve narrative-based puzzles. This could enhance social interaction and community engagement while tackling complex subjects.

title: GC-155-130 Multimodal Speech-Based Dementia Detection
authors: Hood, Tyler, Blanco, Carlos, Shetty, Prajwal
abstract: Early detection of dementia is important for timely intervention, but traditional diagnostic procedures remain costly, time-consuming, and difficult to scale. Speech-based analysis offers a promising non-invasive alternative because cognitive decline often affects fluency, articulation, and other acoustic properties of speech. In this work, we present a multimodal dementia detection framework that combines self-supervised speech representations from wav2vec2 with demographic metadata, including age, gender, and ethnicity. We first compare the multimodal approach against a strong audio-only baseline under a controlled experimental setup. We then extend the analysis with a systematic ablation study and repeated-run statistical evaluation to measure the contribution of individual metadata features. Results show that the multimodal model consistently outperforms the audio-only baseline, with the largest gain primarily driven by age. In contrast, gender and ethnicity provide only marginal independent benefit. Across repeated experiments, the multimodal configurations also show stable performance, supporting the robustness of the observed improvements. These findings suggest that self-supervised speech embeddings capture meaningful dementia-related information, while selected demographic context, especially age, can provide complementary predictive value. Overall, this work strengthens the case for multimodal learning as a practical direction for scalable speech-based dementia screening.

suggestions.title: Incorporate Advanced NLP Techniques for Speech Analysis
suggestions.desc: Enhance the existing framework by integrating advanced natural language processing (NLP) techniques, such as sentiment analysis and topic modeling, to analyze speech patterns more effectively. Use libraries like Hugging Face's Transformers for implementation.

suggestions.title: Evaluate the Impact of Demographic Variables on Detection Accuracy
suggestions.desc: Conduct a study to assess how demographic variables, such as age and gender, influence the accuracy of dementia detection. Use large-scale datasets to identify trends and adapt the model accordingly.

suggestions.title: Develop a Mobile App for Real-Time Dementia Screening
suggestions.desc: Build a mobile application that allows users to conduct speech-based dementia screenings in real-time. Use the multimodal framework to provide instant feedback and recommendations based on user responses.

suggestions.title: Collaborate with Healthcare Professionals for Clinical Testing
suggestions.desc: Partner with local healthcare providers to test the multimodal dementia detection framework in clinical settings. Gather feedback and refine the model based on real-world applicability and user experiences.

title: UC-097-186 Nudox - Compiler Based Information Retrieval
authors: Slabysh, Mikita
abstract: Nudox is a language-agnostic, version-aware documentation and search platform backed by compiler-level analysis. By lowering source code to intermediate representations, Nudox extracts structural metadata, like function signatures, types, and modules independent of the source language. At the core of the platform is a custom search engine built around versioned knowledge: queries resolve to graph nodes and expand outward along structural edges using semantic heuristics, surfacing contextually relevant symbols rather than flat text matches. The result is a canonical, automatically generated source of truth that tracks how a codebase evolves across commits.

suggestions.title: Enhance Search Engine with AI-Powered Code Suggestions
suggestions.desc: Integrate AI-powered code suggestion features into Nudox, utilizing machine learning models like OpenAI Codex to provide contextual code snippets as users search for documentation and information.

suggestions.title: Conduct User Studies on Documentation Usability
suggestions.desc: Perform user studies to evaluate the usability and effectiveness of the Nudox platform. Gather qualitative and quantitative data to understand how developers interact with the search engine and improve its design.

suggestions.title: Implement a Community Contributions Feature for Documentation
suggestions.desc: Add a feature allowing users to contribute documentation and examples to Nudox, creating a collaborative environment. Build a moderation system to ensure quality and relevance of community submissions.

suggestions.title: Develop a Browser Extension for Live Code Documentation Access
suggestions.desc: Create a browser extension that allows developers to access Nudox's documentation directly within their IDEs or code editors. This could streamline the documentation search process and improve developer productivity.

title: UC-121-133 Head in the Clouds
authors: Osborne, Hunter, Day, Jane, Bell, Chase
abstract: Head in the Clouds is a video game that puts the player in the shoes of a child with ADHD (Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder). Rain, the protagonist, is told by their mother to take out the trash, but keeps getting distracted and daydreaming instead. The player must beat platforming challenges to get Rain back on task. The narrative and gameplay is meant to represent the difficulties of having ADHD.

suggestions.title: Integrate Real-Time Feedback on Distraction Levels
suggestions.desc: Enhance the gameplay by adding a real-time analytics engine that tracks player actions and provides feedback on how distracted they are. Use Unity Analytics or Google Analytics to collect data and visualize it in-game, allowing players to see their focus levels and improve their performance.

suggestions.title: Conduct User Studies on ADHD Representation
suggestions.desc: Design a study to gather feedback from players with ADHD on how accurately the game represents their experiences. Analyze the data to identify areas for improvement and publish the findings to contribute to the understanding of ADHD in gaming.

suggestions.title: Develop an Educational Module for Parents and Teachers
suggestions.desc: Create an extension that offers educational resources for parents and teachers about ADHD, integrating gameplay insights. This could include tips on managing distractions and understanding the challenges faced by children with ADHD, thus broadening the game's impact.

suggestions.title: Expand to a Cross-Platform Experience with Multiplayer Features
suggestions.desc: Transform the game into a cross-platform multiplayer experience where players can team up to overcome challenges while managing distractions. Utilize Photon or Unity's multiplayer services to implement real-time collaboration and competition among players.

title: UR-171-118 AidFlow: A Predictive Financial Aid Transparency System for Students
authors: nakkana, chaathurya
abstract: Students frequently experience delays and confusion regarding financial aid refunds due to unclear system statuses and lack of communication. This project introduces AidFlow, a predictive financial aid transparency system that translates complex financial data into clear explanations, predicts refund timelines, and provides actionable guidance. A rule-based model and system pipeline were developed to simulate real-world scenarios and improve student understanding and decision-making.

suggestions.title: Implement Machine Learning for Predictive Analytics
suggestions.desc: Enhance AidFlow by integrating machine learning algorithms to predict financial aid disbursements more accurately. Use historical financial data, possibly sourced from the U.S. Department of Education, to train models that improve prediction accuracy and user recommendations.

suggestions.title: Analyze User Interaction Data for System Optimization
suggestions.desc: Conduct a research project analyzing user interaction with the AidFlow system to identify pain points. Utilize A/B testing and user surveys to refine the user interface and enhance the clarity of information presented.

suggestions.title: Create a Mobile App Version of AidFlow
suggestions.desc: Develop a mobile application that provides financial aid updates and predictions on-the-go. Utilize React Native or Flutter to ensure cross-platform compatibility and to engage students where they are most active.

suggestions.title: Integrate Chatbot Functionality for Instant Support
suggestions.desc: Add a chatbot powered by NLP tools like Dialogflow or Microsoft Bot Framework to AidFlow, providing students with immediate answers to common financial aid questions. This could streamline communication and reduce confusion among users.

title: GRM-175-233 From Leakage to Reliability in Dementia Detection
authors: Tubbs, Crystal
abstract: Automated dementia detection from speech offers a scalable approach to cognitive screening, but its reliability depends on rigorous experimental design. In this work, we reconstructed a Wav2Vec2-based dementia classification pipeline and identified critical methodological flaws, including speaker leakage, nondeterministic preprocessing, and invalid test partitions. We rebuilt the dataset using strict speaker-level separation, deterministic segmentation, and validation checks to ensure reproducibility. The corrected baseline achieved an accuracy of 44.74 percent and macro F1 score of 0.4439, reflecting a more realistic performance estimate than prior inflated results. This work establishes a scientifically valid foundation for evaluating augmentation strategies such as SpecAugment in future phases.

suggestions.title: Implement Advanced Audio Features for Better Classification
suggestions.desc: Enhance the Wav2Vec2 pipeline by incorporating advanced audio features such as pitch, tone, and speech rate analysis. Use libraries like librosa to extract these features and further improve dementia classification accuracy.

suggestions.title: Explore Cross-Linguistic Variability in Speech Patterns
suggestions.desc: Conduct research on how dementia manifests in different languages by applying the classification pipeline to multilingual datasets. This could reveal insights into cultural differences in speech and improve the model's generalizability.

suggestions.title: Develop a User-Friendly Web Interface for Clinicians
suggestions.desc: Create a web application that allows clinicians to upload speech samples and receive dementia classification results. Use Flask or Django to build the backend, ensuring that the interface is intuitive for healthcare professionals.

suggestions.title: Integrate Real-Time Feedback for Speech Therapists
suggestions.desc: Expand the project to provide real-time feedback to speech therapists based on their clients' speech patterns. This could involve developing an API that therapists can use to monitor progress and adjust treatment plans accordingly.

title: GRM-095-230 A Multimodal LLM Framework for Automated Construction Blueprint Analysis with Real-Time Decision Support
authors: Arshad, Muhammad Awais
abstract: This study addresses the high hallucination rates of Vision-Language Models (LLMs) when analyzing complex, hybrid construction blueprints. We developed a dual-input pipeline that pairs high-resolution images with a four-layer JSON "Digital Twin" (vector text, raster OCR, geometry) to mathematically ground the LLM's visual interpretation. Key Engineering Achievement: We processed a massive 137-sheet civil engineering project with zero errors. By introducing a multi-tier JSON pruning strategy, we cut token usage by up to 70% and processed the entire batch from $13-$15 to just $0.93. Decision Support Extension: We integrated real-time traffic data (TomTom) and GDOT procedural policies to transform the pipeline from a simple document reader into a strategic project management advisor.

suggestions.title: Enhance JSON Pruning with AI-Based Optimization
suggestions.desc: Build on the multi-tier JSON pruning strategy by implementing AI algorithms that dynamically optimize the pruning process based on the complexity of the blueprints. This can significantly improve processing efficiency and reduce costs further.

suggestions.title: Assess the Impact of Visual Grounding on Design Accuracy
suggestions.desc: Conduct research to evaluate how the visual grounding of LLMs affects the accuracy of construction blueprint analysis. This study could involve user testing and data collection to quantify improvements in decision-making based on the dual-input pipeline.

suggestions.title: Create a Virtual Reality Experience for Blueprint Analysis
suggestions.desc: Develop a VR application that allows users to interact with construction blueprints in a three-dimensional space. This could enhance understanding and facilitate more effective design decisions, leveraging tools like Unity and the Oculus SDK.

suggestions.title: Integrate a Collaboration Tool for Project Stakeholders
suggestions.desc: Expand the framework to include a feature that allows multiple stakeholders (engineers, architects, contractors) to collaborate on blueprint analysis in real time. This could involve adding a shared workspace with annotation tools using WebRTC or socket.io.

title: UC-087-236 Early Prediction of Player Performance
authors: Freeman, Grady, Truong, Hien, Mayo, Jackson
abstract: This study examines whether early-season performance metrics can support player evaluation under the NCAA’s shortened transfer window. Using data from Conference USA and the Mid-American Conference, we modeled offensive (UASE) and defensive (DAR) efficiency with multiple predictive methods. Across both full-season and 9-game datasets, DAR was more predictable, with higher R² and lower RMSE values. Linear Regression consistently performed best for DAR, while KNN and Random Forest performed best for UASE depending on the dataset. Results show that meaningful performance patterns can be identified early in the season, even with limited data. These findings suggest analytics can help programs make faster, more informed decisions for recruiting, roster planning, and player retention.

suggestions.title: Develop a Comprehensive Player Performance Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Create a dashboard that visualizes player performance metrics over time, allowing coaches to track players' progress. Use data visualization libraries like D3.js or Chart.js to build an interactive web application that presents this data effectively.

suggestions.title: Investigate the Impact of Transfer Windows on Team Dynamics
suggestions.desc: Conduct a research study that analyzes how the NCAA's transfer windows affect team performance and player retention. Use statistical methods to correlate player performance metrics with team success before and after transfers.

suggestions.title: Create a Mobile App for Real-Time Player Performance Tracking
suggestions.desc: Develop a mobile app that allows coaches and players to input real-time performance data during games. Use Firebase for backend support and provide analytics to help improve strategies based on live data.

suggestions.title: Integrate Predictive Analysis with Game Strategy Software
suggestions.desc: Expand the project by integrating predictive player performance metrics with existing game strategy software. This could help coaches make informed decisions during games and improve overall team performance.

title: GRM-083-218 WISE: Whitebox Importance-based Subnetwork Extraction and the Privacy-Preserving Properties of Model Compression
authors: Pederson, Mason
abstract: WISE (Whitebox Importance-based Subnetwork Extraction) is a structured compression algorithm which extracts task-specific subnetworks by instrumenting a pretrained networks with learned gates on transformer components and optimizing on task loss and L0 sparsity regularization. WISE maintains high task performance at high sparsity levels (81-88% accuracy at 85%) where other SOTA methods collapse to near random chance. We present the first evaluation of model compression along privacy dimensions: attribute inference resistance, training data memorization, and extraction attack vulnerability. Structured compression via learned gates produces subnetworks with favorable privacy-utility balance without any explicit privacy mechanism. WISE masks also transfer to fresh models with recovery ratios exceeding 1.0, supporting the Lottery Ticket Hypothesis

suggestions.title: Investigate Privacy-Preserving Architectures for WISE
suggestions.desc: Explore alternative architectures like federated learning or differential privacy to enhance the privacy properties of WISE. Conduct experiments to evaluate how these methods impact the performance and privacy guarantees when applied to the model compression algorithm.

suggestions.title: Develop an Interactive Tool for WISE Parameter Tuning
suggestions.desc: Create a web-based application that allows users to visualize and adjust parameters for WISE in real-time. Utilize frameworks like Flask or Django combined with libraries such as Plotly for visualizations to help users understand the trade-offs between sparsity and task performance.

suggestions.title: Apply WISE to Edge Device Model Deployment
suggestions.desc: Implement the WISE algorithm on edge devices to assess its efficiency in resource-constrained environments. Use datasets applicable to edge computing applications, such as sensor data from IoT devices, to evaluate how WISE affects real-time decision-making and privacy.

suggestions.title: Collaborate with Cybersecurity Firms for Real-World Validation
suggestions.desc: Partner with cybersecurity companies to apply the WISE algorithm in their model deployment processes. Focus on ensuring that the implemented models provide both high task performance and robust privacy protections against common threats in the industry.

title: GRP-165-217 Evaluation of Multi-Platform Simulation Environments for Diverse Robotic Manipulation Tasks
authors: liu, Zhiguo
abstract: Robotic development often requires transitioning between different simulation environments to meet specific task requirements. This project presents a comparative evaluation of four major simulation platforms—Gazebo, MuJoCo, CoppeliaSim, and Isaac Sim—through the successful reproduction of diverse manipulation tasks. By implementing system integration, dual-arm coordination, sequential logic, and reinforcement learning across these engines, this study identifies the functional strengths and practical engineering constraints of each environment. The results provide a qualitative guide for selecting simulation tools based on task-specific needs, such as middleware compatibility versus physical fidelity.

suggestions.title: Create a Cross-Platform Benchmarking Tool for Robot Simulators
suggestions.desc: Develop a benchmarking tool that can automatically test and compare various robotic manipulation tasks across the four simulation environments (Gazebo, MuJoCo, CoppeliaSim, Isaac Sim). Use ROS2 for integration and create a unified reporting mechanism to visualize performance metrics.

suggestions.title: Analyze Task-Specific Performance Metrics for Robotic Tasks
suggestions.desc: Conduct a detailed analysis of how different simulation environments affect the performance of specific robotic manipulation tasks. Collect data on success rates, execution times, and resource usage, and publish a comparative study detailing the findings.

suggestions.title: Implement a Dual-Arm Coordination Task Across Platforms
suggestions.desc: Design and implement a complex dual-arm coordination task that can be replicated across all four simulation platforms. Document the challenges and solutions encountered in each environment, providing valuable insights for future robotic development.

suggestions.title: Host a Workshop on Multi-Platform Robotics Development
suggestions.desc: Organize a workshop aimed at teaching peers how to effectively transition between different simulation environments for robotic tasks. Include tutorials, hands-on sessions, and collaborative projects that leverage the findings from your comparative evaluation.

title: UR-084-219 Towards Bounding the Behavior of Neural Networks
authors: Nwankwo, Emmanuel
abstract: Modern neural networks are typically considered black-box systems: while they are able to achieve state-of-the-art performance in many domains, it is difficult to elicit the reasons behind their decisions. From this, a sub-field of artificial intelligence called eXplainable Artificial Intelligence (XAI) arose to fill this gap.
