One approach to XAI is based on the symbolic compilation of a neural network's behavior to a logical formula. However, such approaches are limited in scalability, due to the fundamental difficulty of the problem. This research instead proposes an incremental and anytime approach to explaining the behavior of a neural network, for image recognition. Our approach is based on a recently published result by a KSU undergraduate, that proposed an incremental and anytime approach to explaining the behavior of an individual (threshold) neuron. To visualize the behavior of an individual neuron, we propose to enumerate prototypical examples of images that activate the neuron. We propose to visualize the behavior of a neural network by appropriately aggregating the prototypical examples of its neurons. Preliminary results suggest that such aggregate visualizations reveal interpretable patterns that can reveal the reasoning behind a neural network's decisions. Further, our approach can provide such insights in a more efficient, incremental fashion, compared to prior compilation-based methods which are by nature exhaustive.

suggestions.title: Scale Incremental XAI Bounds to Frontier Transformer Models
suggestions.desc: Extend the incremental bounding pipeline to modern open-weight transformer families (for example Llama 3.1/3.2 and Mistral) and measure how bound quality changes with parameter scale and context length. Track runtime, memory cost, and bound tightness to identify practical limits for real-world interpretability workflows.

suggestions.title: Build a Visualization Tool for Logical Formula Generation
suggestions.desc: Create a user-friendly visualization tool that displays the logical formulas derived from the behavior of neural networks. Use libraries like D3.js for interactive visualizations to help users understand complex relationships and decision-making processes in neural networks.

suggestions.title: Apply the Method to Real-World Applications in Healthcare
suggestions.desc: Test the proposed method on healthcare-related neural networks, such as those used for medical diagnosis. Assess how effectively the model can explain its predictions and identify potential biases or ethical considerations in medical applications.

suggestions.title: Collaborate with AI Ethics Organizations for Impact Assessment
suggestions.desc: Work with organizations focused on AI ethics to evaluate the social implications of your incremental XAI techniques. Aim to publish a joint paper that addresses both technical advancements and ethical considerations in AI model interpretability.

title: GRP-146-136 Platonic Policy Representations: Navigating Learned Manifolds for Rapid Adaptation
authors: Redovian, Cameron
abstract: Adapting reinforcement learning policies to changing dynamics is typically addressed by domain randomization, which trains a single robust policy at the cost of specialization, or by meta-RL methods, which enable rapid adaptation but require online inference or optimization. We propose a different mechanism: extending the Platonic Representation Hypothesis (Huh et al., 2024) and vec2vec (Jha et al., 2025) to policy space, we show that diverse task-competent policies trained under varying dynamics admit a shared, low-dimensional manifold structure that is learnable from trajectory embeddings. Platonic Policy Representations (PPR) learns this manifold via geometric preservation losses, then navigates it for rapid adaptation: a hypernetwork generates complete policy weights from any manifold position in a single forward pass, while a dynamics-to-manifold predictor guides deliberate exploration of qualitatively different behavioral strategies for novel dynamics configurations. On continuous control tasks with varying gravity, mass, and friction, PPR achieves 12-235% improvements over domain randomization baselines, with gains scaling with action space dimensionality: Ant (+235%, 8-dim), Walker2d (+153%, 6-dim), Hopper (+59%, 3-dim), and LunarLander (+12%, 2-dim). These results demonstrate that policy adaptation can be recast as geometric navigation of a learned manifold, offering a complementary paradigm to existing adaptation approaches.

suggestions.title: Develop a Visualization Tool for Policy Adaptation Dynamics
suggestions.desc: Create a visualization tool that represents the learned low-dimensional manifold structure of diverse task-competent policies. Use techniques such as t-SNE or PCA to illustrate how policies adapt to changing dynamics, helping users understand the underlying structure.

suggestions.title: Investigate the Effectiveness of Platonic Representations in Real-Time Adaptation
suggestions.desc: Conduct experiments to evaluate how well the Platonic Representation Hypothesis holds in real-time scenarios. Use environments like OpenAI's Gym and focus on rapid adaptation tasks, documenting the performance improvements compared to traditional methods.

suggestions.title: Implement Rapid Policy Adaptation in Autonomous Vehicles
suggestions.desc: Apply the proposed mechanism to develop a rapid policy adaptation system for autonomous vehicles navigating dynamic environments. Simulate various traffic scenarios and assess the effectiveness of the learned policies in real-world applications.

suggestions.title: Host a Competition on Reinforcement Learning Adaptation Techniques
suggestions.desc: Organize a competition where participants must develop their own rapid adaptation techniques based on the Platonic Representation Hypothesis. Provide datasets and scenarios for evaluation, fostering collaboration and innovation in policy adaptation research.

title: UC-137-237 The House Watches
authors: Martinez, Lisbeth, Rousell, Lauren, McBride, Jaime, Quiroz, Karizma, Kleine, Aidan
abstract: The House Watches is a horror-puzzle game about a boy and a dog trying to reunite after mysterious supernatural creatures, called duendes, invade their home. The game consists of two levels, each with a different mode of gameplay; level one is a more traditional item-collection horror experience, and level 2 is based around puzzles that must be completed in a limited time frame. The House Watches includes original art, music, and models, as well as a dynamic gameplay system that increases the difficulty of each level over time.

suggestions.title: Expand Game Levels with Procedural Generation Techniques
suggestions.desc: Enhance 'The House Watches' by implementing procedural generation for levels, utilizing algorithms like Perlin noise or cellular automata. This will provide players with unique experiences each time and increase the game's replayability.

suggestions.title: Analyze Player Behavior in Dynamic Difficulty Adjustment
suggestions.desc: Conduct a user study to analyze how dynamic difficulty adjustments affect player engagement and performance. Collect data on player behavior and preferences to inform future game design decisions and improve the gameplay experience.

suggestions.title: Integrate Virtual Reality Elements into Gameplay
suggestions.desc: Explore the incorporation of VR elements into 'The House Watches' to enhance immersion. Use platforms like Unity with Oculus SDK to create VR-specific interactions and puzzles that align with the horror-puzzle theme of the game.

suggestions.title: Collaborate with Local Artists for Game Art Expansion
suggestions.desc: Partner with local artists to expand the original art assets of 'The House Watches' through a community-driven initiative. This could include designing new creatures, environmental assets, or soundtracks, enriching the game's aesthetic and narrative.

title: UC-139-175 Agentic debugger and documenter
authors: Omodemi, Olaoluwa, Le, jade, Dietz, Preston
abstract: Modern software development teams routinely introduce subtle bugs — mutable default arguments, bare exception handlers, insecure eval()/exec() calls, resource leaks, hardcoded secrets — that escape manual review but accumulate into technical debt and security risk. Existing linters identify problems but leave remediation to the developer. This project investigates whether a coordinated multi-agent system, combining rule-based static analysis with generative LLM reasoning, can autonomously detect, fix, and document such issues with no developer involvement beyond providing the input file.

suggestions.title: Integrate Real-time Collaboration with Multi-Agent Debugging
suggestions.desc: Extend the agentic debugger to support real-time collaboration among multiple developers. Use WebSockets or a similar technology to allow developers to see live updates and suggestions from the agents, enhancing teamwork and reducing the time to fix bugs.

suggestions.title: Evaluate the Impact of Agentic Debugging on Developer Productivity
suggestions.desc: Conduct a study to measure the productivity and satisfaction of developers using the agentic debugger compared to traditional debugging methods. Collect qualitative and quantitative data on bug resolution times and developer feedback to analyze the effectiveness of the system.

suggestions.title: Implement a Plugin for Popular IDEs
suggestions.desc: Create a plugin for popular Integrated Development Environments (IDEs) like Visual Studio Code or IntelliJ that integrates the agentic debugger. This would allow developers to access debugging features directly within their workflow, making the tool more accessible and user-friendly.

suggestions.title: Develop a Customizable Rule Set for Specific Domains
suggestions.desc: Build a feature that allows teams to define and customize rules for the agentic debugger based on their domain-specific needs, such as gaming, finance, or healthcare. Utilize a user-friendly interface for rule management to enhance adoption and effectiveness.

title: GRP-03-141 Stress-Testing Parkinson’s Disease Screening: A Cross-Modal Analysis of Drawing and Speech Models
authors: Razzak, Rehma
abstract: Medical AI systems are increasingly deployed in clinical settings, yet most published models report only clean accuracy, dataset details, and training procedures—while omitting security-critical evaluations such as robustness to perturbations, adversarial vulnerability, and failure modes under realistic noise. This project addresses that gap by building a cross-modal robustness assessment for Parkinson’s disease (PD) screening models across handwriting trajectories, speech-derived acoustic features, and an LLM-based preprocessing layer. Despite strong clean performance (visual subject-level ROC AUC ˜ 0.99; audio ˜ 1.0), the visual pipeline proved highly brittle to realistic acquisition distortions. Downsampling and point-dropout caused near-chance collapse, while pressure noise and XY jitter produced monotonic degradation and threshold instability. Simple defenses improved robustness in corruption-specific ways—augmentation was the strongest general-purpose method, and resampling+augmentation best mitigated sampling-density failures—yet all defenses reduced clean accuracy. Small adversarial perturbations (e ˜ 0.2–0.3) reliably flipped predictions. Speech-feature models were naturally robust to random corruptions and generalized well across utterance types, but adversarial attacks again caused sharp collapse at small e, revealing a shared vulnerability across modalities. An LLM layer introduced additional instability: Llama-3 was format-stable but less feature-grounded, while Gemma-3 was more accurate but more brittle. Overall, the project demonstrates that high clean accuracy does not imply real-world reliability, and that security evaluation must become a standard component of medical AI development.

suggestions.title: Incorporate Adversarial Training for Improved Model Robustness
suggestions.desc: Enhance the existing PD screening models by integrating adversarial training techniques. Use adversarial examples generated from the existing datasets to make the models more resilient against noise and perturbations, thus improving their reliability in clinical settings.

suggestions.title: Analyze the Impact of Noise Levels on Model Performance
suggestions.desc: Conduct a detailed statistical analysis of how different levels of noise affect the performance of both handwriting and speech models used in PD screening. This could involve simulating various noise scenarios and measuring performance metrics to identify thresholds for acceptable accuracy.

suggestions.title: Create a User-friendly Visualization Dashboard for Model Evaluation
suggestions.desc: Develop a web-based dashboard that visualizes the robustness assessments of the PD screening models. Include interactive graphs and metrics to help clinicians easily understand model performance under different conditions, enhancing decision-making in patient screenings.

suggestions.title: Collaborate with Clinical Institutions for Real-World Testing
suggestions.desc: Partner with healthcare institutions to test the robustness of the PD screening models in real-world scenarios. Collect feedback and performance data to refine the models further and validate their effectiveness in diverse clinical environments.

title: GRP-125-144 Mapping the Affordances of Human-AI Interaction: A Large-Scale Text Mining and Statistical Analysis of LLM Usage Patterns
authors: Vallepu, Anil
abstract: People increasingly communicate with AI for schoolwork, office tasks, and daily needs. This study investigates the affordances of human-AI interaction using modern text mining and statistical analysis on the WildChat-1M dataset of over 1.1 million real-world ChatGPT user conversation logs. We apply BERTopic to extract latent interaction topics, compute a probabilistic topic-document matrix P(T|D), and perform rigorous statistical testing including Welch’s T-test and ANOVA to compare affordance patterns between GPT-3.5 and GPT-4.0 Results reveal that creative writing, Coding, message drafting and many interesting topics are the dominant affordances, while a spatio-temporal trend analysis maps how interaction patterns evolve globally over time.
similar: legacy:GR-20|Spring 2016|sentiment analysis and text mining of twitter data using r

suggestions.title: Develop a Real-Time Interaction Analytics Tool
suggestions.desc: Create a tool that analyzes user interactions with AI in real-time, providing feedback on usage patterns and effectiveness. Utilize streaming analytics frameworks like Apache Kafka and Apache Flink to process and visualize interaction data instantly for continuous improvement.

suggestions.title: Compare User Sentiment Across Current Model Tiers
suggestions.desc: Run a sentiment and satisfaction comparison across current model tiers (for example GPT-4o, GPT-4o-mini, and open-weight alternatives served via vLLM) using the same prompt sets and task categories. Pair VADER-style sentiment with task success and turn-count metrics to map which affordances drive better outcomes by model class.

suggestions.title: Create a Comparative Study of AI in Educational Settings
suggestions.desc: Examine how students interact with AI tools for various educational tasks, comparing their usage patterns and outcomes. Collect data from educational institutions and analyze how these interactions differ from office tasks, focusing on effectiveness and user experience.

suggestions.title: Develop a Recommendation System Based on Interaction Patterns
suggestions.desc: Build a recommendation engine that suggests optimal AI models or interaction strategies based on user behavior and the affordances identified in your analysis. Implement collaborative filtering or content-based filtering techniques to enhance user experience.

title: GC-141-167 Smart Resume Screening & Interview Preparation Assistant
authors: Olien, Loreli, King, Tomas, Chen, Hanzhi, Gray, Kawanda, Frazier, Brittany
abstract: The hiring process often relies on manual resume review and keyword matching, which can lead to inconsistent and biased candidate evaluations. This project introduces a Smart Resume Screening and Interview Preparation Assistant designed to improve transparency and consistency in early-stage candidate evaluation. The system allows recruiters to upload resumes and job descriptions, then uses embedding-based semantic matching and large language models to assess candidate alignment across skills, experience, education, and projects. The application generates structured rankings, explainable insights, and tailored interview questions. This project focuses on developing a functional prototype that demonstrates how AI can enhance decision support while maintaining human control in hiring workflows.

suggestions.title: Integrate Video Interview Analysis Using NLP
suggestions.desc: Extend the Smart Resume Screening Assistant by incorporating video interview analysis. Utilize NLP techniques to evaluate candidate responses during interviews, providing recruiters with insights on communication skills and personality traits.

suggestions.title: Examine Bias in AI-driven Resume Screening
suggestions.desc: Conduct research to identify potential biases in the AI model used for resume screening. Analyze the dataset for demographic disparities and assess how these biases might affect candidate evaluations and suggest improvements to mitigate them.

suggestions.title: Create a Mobile App for Job Seekers to Prepare for Interviews
suggestions.desc: Develop a mobile application that allows job seekers to upload their resumes and receive tailored interview preparation resources. Use the same AI technology to suggest common interview questions based on the job description and provide feedback on answers.

suggestions.title: Implement Continuous Learning to Adapt to Job Market Changes
suggestions.desc: Build a feature that enables the AI system to continuously learn from new job postings and candidate feedback, adapting its recommendations over time. This could involve using reinforcement learning techniques to improve the screening accuracy dynamically.

title: UC-086-235 Student Performance Pattern Mining
authors: Arevalo Colocho, Cesar
abstract: This project applies data mining techniques to explore patterns in a student performance dataset. The analysis focuses on discovering natural groupings of students and frequent associations among academic, social, and lifestyle attributes. Clustering and association rule mining are used to identify meaningful structures in the data, emphasizing pattern discovery and interpretation rather than outcome prediction.

suggestions.title: Visualize Student Performance Patterns with Interactive Dashboards
suggestions.desc: Create interactive dashboards using visualization libraries like D3.js or Plotly to represent the clusters and associations found in the student performance dataset. This will allow educators to explore patterns visually and gain insights into student behavior.

suggestions.title: Investigate the Correlation Between Social Attributes and Academic Success
suggestions.desc: Conduct a comprehensive study to analyze how social attributes influence academic performance, using the dataset to identify significant correlations. Employ statistical methods like regression analysis to quantify these relationships.

suggestions.title: Develop an Early Warning System for At-Risk Students
suggestions.desc: Build a predictive model that identifies students at risk of underperforming based on the discovered patterns. Implement a recommendation system that suggests interventions for educators to support these students before it's too late.

suggestions.title: Enhance the Dataset with Additional Attributes for Deeper Insights
suggestions.desc: Expand the existing student performance dataset by incorporating additional variables such as mental health indicators or extracurricular activities. This will allow for a more holistic analysis of factors affecting student success.

title: GRM-179-195 A Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) System for Bible Question Answering Using Scriptural Text and Commentary
authors: Koya, Maryam, Mishra, Pragya
abstract: This project develops and evaluates a question-answering (QA) system designed to address theological and interpretive questions about the New Testament. It uses a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) framework that integrates a pretrained large language model with a structured knowledge base consisting of public-domain Berean Standard Bible (BSB) New Testament and New Testament commentaries. User queries are embedded to retrieve semantically relevant passages, which are then supplied as contextual input for answer generation. The system is evaluated based on retrieval quality, answer faithfulness, and comparison to ground truth. Performance is benchmarked against a baseline BM25 keyword retrieval system without commentary, demonstrating that commentary-augmented semantic retrieval improves interpretive accuracy and reduces hallucinated responses.

suggestions.title: Integrate Voice Recognition for Bible Queries
suggestions.desc: Enhance the retrieval-augmented generation system by incorporating a voice recognition module using Google's Speech-to-Text API. This will allow users to ask questions vocally, making the system more accessible and interactive.

suggestions.title: Evaluate User Engagement with Different User Interfaces
suggestions.desc: Conduct a study comparing user engagement and satisfaction across various interface designs (e.g., chatbot, web app) for the QA system. Use A/B testing to assess which design leads to better retrieval and answer quality.

suggestions.title: Create a Mobile App for Bible Study Groups
suggestions.desc: Develop a mobile application that utilizes the question-answering system to facilitate discussions in Bible study groups. Include features like shared query history, bookmarks, and the ability to annotate scripture passages based on answers.

suggestions.title: Expand to Other Religious Texts
suggestions.desc: Adapt the RAG framework to include other religious texts such as the Quran or the Bhagavad Gita. This would involve curating a structured knowledge base for these texts and evaluating the system's performance across multiple religions.

title: GC-161-210 Hybrid Path Planning using Genetic Algorithm
authors: Tigani, Caitlin, Joyee, Ramisa Fariha, Mohammad, Wasif
abstract: This research investigates whether uninformed search (BFS) or informed search (A*) is more effective when combined with a Genetic Algorithm for maze path planning. We design and implement four algorithms: baseline BFS and A*, hybrid GA+A*, and hybrid GA(BFS+A*). Our findings show that while A* alone performs optimally, integrating it with GA can produce alternative quality solutions, though with computational trade-offs. The study demonstrates that GA+A* provides the best balance between solution quality and runtime efficiency.

suggestions.title: Implement a Real-Time Path Planning Visualization Tool
suggestions.desc: Build a web-based visualization tool using D3.js that demonstrates the path planning algorithms in real-time. Users can input maze configurations and observe how each algorithm navigates the maze, comparing efficiency and solution paths.

suggestions.title: Analyze the Effect of Different Maze Configurations on Algorithm Performance
suggestions.desc: Conduct experiments to evaluate how various maze configurations (e.g., density, size) affect the performance of BFS, A*, and hybrid GA algorithms. Document findings and create a report on optimal configurations for each algorithm.

suggestions.title: Apply Path Planning to Autonomous Vehicle Navigation
suggestions.desc: Extend the path planning algorithms to be usable in a simulated autonomous vehicle environment. Use the OpenAI Gym or CARLA simulator to test and visualize how the algorithms navigate through dynamic vehicular environments.

suggestions.title: Develop an Educational Game Based on Path Planning Algorithms
suggestions.desc: Create a game that teaches users about different path planning algorithms through interactive challenges. Players can use BFS, A*, or GA to solve mazes and earn points based on efficiency, encouraging learning through gameplay.

title: UC-177-226 Scrapper Kinetics LLC
authors: Rudenko, Mikhail, Torimoto, Andrew, Lock, Jimmy, Cheng, Marco
abstract: Scrapper Kinetics LLC is a multiplayer and multimodal physics puzzle game. Where players get to choose between playing in VR or Desktop mode, and then, with up to 7 friends (8 players total), try to make a profit in the harsh dead space hulks they have been hired to scrap. We made the game as a test to see how easy it is to have completely different devices interact in the same play space.

suggestions.title: Enhance Cross-Platform Interaction with WebRTC
suggestions.desc: Implement WebRTC to allow real-time communication between VR and desktop players in Scrapper Kinetics LLC. This will facilitate features like voice chat and collaborative puzzle-solving, enhancing the multiplayer experience.

suggestions.title: Study Player Interaction Patterns Across Devices
suggestions.desc: Conduct a research study analyzing how players interact differently in VR versus desktop modes. Collect data on player strategies, preferences, and success rates to optimize gameplay mechanics for each platform.

suggestions.title: Create Customizable Game Modes for Diverse Player Experiences
suggestions.desc: Develop additional game modes that cater to different player skills or preferences, such as timed challenges or cooperative tasks. This will increase replayability and attract a broader audience to the game.

suggestions.title: Explore Educational Applications of Physics Puzzles in VR
suggestions.desc: Investigate how the physics-based puzzles in Scrapper Kinetics LLC can be adapted for educational purposes, teaching concepts like force, motion, and problem-solving in a virtual classroom setting.

title: GRM-170-221 Mind Modeling for Neuroadaptive VR Labs
authors: Nagulapally, Harish Chary
abstract: Students with motor impairments lack equal access to hands-on STEM laboratory experiences. This study investigates whether EEG signals and eye-gaze data contain distinguishable patterns linked to specific hand motor functions both physical and imagined. Identifying this relationship is the critical first step toward a BCI-driven VR system for accessible STEM education.

suggestions.title: Develop a BCI Interface for VR Control
suggestions.desc: Create a Brain-Computer Interface (BCI) that allows users to control VR simulations with EEG signals. Utilize the OpenBCI platform and integrate it with existing VR frameworks to develop an accessible STEM education tool.

suggestions.title: Investigate the Accuracy of EEG Patterns for Motor Function Recognition
suggestions.desc: Conduct a detailed study on the accuracy of different EEG signal processing techniques for recognizing distinct hand movements. Compare traditional signal processing methods with machine learning approaches for pattern recognition.

suggestions.title: Create a VR STEM Lab for Students with Motor Impairments
suggestions.desc: Design a virtual STEM lab that simulates common experiments and activities, allowing students with motor impairments to engage in hands-on learning through imagined or minimal physical interaction, leveraging the BCI system.

suggestions.title: Collaborate with Occupational Therapists to Tailor VR Experiences
suggestions.desc: Work with occupational therapists to customize the VR system for rehabilitation purposes, creating specific tasks that help improve motor skills and cognitive functions for students with disabilities.

title: GRM-176-225 MemoryEIL: An Enhanced Memory Layer Architecture for Heterogeneous Robots
authors: Shen, Yukang
abstract: Embodied agents still struggle to generalize across robot types, tasks, and environments because most policies remain tightly tied to robot-specific observations and action spaces. While recent VLA and planning methods improve task performance, they still lack a shared memory layer for storing and reusing experience across heterogeneous robotic systems. We propose MemoryEIL, a predicate-based memory layer that converts multimodal observations and execution traces into structured graph memories while preserving raw embeddings for fine-grained retrieval and disambiguation. MemoryEIL separates short-term task belief from long-term experience and plugs retrieved memories into either VLA policies or differentiable TAMP planners. Preliminary results show better retry behavior, fewer repeated failures, and more reliable execution than planning without memory.

suggestions.title: Implement a Distributed Memory System for Robots
suggestions.desc: Extend MemoryEIL to support a distributed memory architecture, allowing multiple robots to share experiences in real-time. Utilize cloud services like AWS IoT for managing connections and data storage across various robots.

suggestions.title: Explore the Impact of Experience Sharing on Task Performance
suggestions.desc: Conduct research to evaluate how sharing experiences across different robot types affects their learning speed and task performance. Design experiments that measure efficiency before and after implementing the MemoryEIL system.

suggestions.title: Develop a Multi-Robot Coordination Application
suggestions.desc: Create an application that utilizes MemoryEIL to manage coordination tasks among heterogeneous robots, such as search and rescue missions or warehouse automation, demonstrating the benefits of shared memory in complex scenarios.

suggestions.title: Investigate Ethical Implications of Shared Robotics Memory
suggestions.desc: Research the ethical considerations of implementing a shared memory layer in robots, focusing on data privacy, decision-making autonomy, and potential biases in experience sharing among different robot types.

title: GRM-132-159 Integrating Causal Inference with Graph Neural Networks for Alzheimer’s Disease Analysis
authors: Peddi, Pranay Kumar
abstract: Deep graph learning has advanced Alzheimer’s disease (AD) classification from MRI, but most models remain correlational, confounding demographic and genetic factors with disease-specific features. We present Causal-GCN, an interventional graph convolutional framework that integrates do-calculus-based back-door adjustment to identify brain regions exerting stable causal influence on AD progression. Each subject’s MRI is represented as a structural connectome where nodes denote cortical and subcortical regions and edges encode anatomical connectivity. Confounders such as age, sex, and APOE4 genotype are summarized via principal components and included in the causal adjustment set. After training, interventions on individual regions are simulated by severing their incoming edges and altering node features to estimate average causal effects on disease probability. Applied to 484 subjects from the ADNI cohort, Causal-GCN achieves performance comparable to baseline GNNs while providing interpretable causal effect rankings that highlight posterior, cingulate, and insular hubs consistent with established AD neuropathology.

suggestions.title: Expand Causal-GCN for Genetic Variant Analysis
suggestions.desc: Enhance the Causal-GCN model to include genetic variants as additional nodes in the graph. Use datasets like the Alzheimer’s Disease Neuroimaging Initiative (ADNI) to assess how genetic factors, such as variants in the APOE gene, interact with structural connectome features in predicting AD progression.

suggestions.title: Integrate Temporal Dynamics into Causal-GCN
suggestions.desc: Modify the Causal-GCN framework to account for temporal changes in brain connectivity over time. Implement recurrent graph neural networks (RGNNs) and use longitudinal MRI datasets to analyze how early and late-stage AD differ in their causal influences.

suggestions.title: Create a Visualization Tool for Causal Influence Mapping
suggestions.desc: Develop an interactive visualization tool that allows researchers to explore the causal influences identified by Causal-GCN. Use libraries like D3.js to create visual representations of the graph, highlighting key brain regions affecting AD progression.

suggestions.title: Collaborate with Clinicians for Real-World Applications
suggestions.desc: Work with Alzheimer’s disease clinicians to deploy the Causal-GCN model in a clinical setting. Design a study to validate the model's predictions against patient outcomes, helping to refine the approach based on real-world data.

title: UC-142-177 Multiplayer SPSU Tub Racing Video Game
authors: Pitts, William, Urvan, William, Powell, Thomas, Young, Joshua
abstract: Multiplayer Bathtub Racing revives a well-known Southern Polytechnic State University tradition through a digital multiplayer experience built in Unity. The project extends a prior single-player tub racing game by adding online multiplayer

suggestions.title: Add Customizable Racing Tracks with Unity
suggestions.desc: Implement a feature that allows players to create and share their own racing tracks within the game. Utilize Unity's Tilemap system to facilitate track design and enable a community-driven content creation platform.

suggestions.title: Host a Tub Racing Tournament with Leaderboards
suggestions.desc: Organize an online tub racing tournament, complete with live leaderboards and rewards for top players. Use cloud services like Firebase to manage player data and score tracking in real-time.

suggestions.title: Incorporate Augmented Reality Features
suggestions.desc: Explore the integration of augmented reality (AR) elements into the tub racing game using AR Foundation in Unity. This could allow players to race in real-world settings, increasing engagement and immersion.

suggestions.title: Analyze User Engagement Through Game Mechanics
suggestions.desc: Conduct a study to analyze how different game mechanics impact user engagement and satisfaction. Use analytics tools to track player behavior and preferences, providing insights for future game development.

title: GRM-094-176 Influence of Speech Disfluencies and Prompt Optimization on LLM-Based Alzheimer's Detection
authors: Arshad, Muhammad Awais
abstract: This study evaluates how speech disfluencies and prompting strategies impact LLM-based Alzheimer’s Disease (AD) detection. We compared transcripts with preserved disfluencies (ADReSS) against clean transcripts (ADReSSo) using four state-of-the-art LLMs. Key Discovery: Complex prompts induce a "mirror-image" classification bias, where DeepSeek models severely over-classify AD, and GPT-5.2 over-classifies Cognitively Normal (CN) individuals. Optimization Fix: Applying DSPy MIPROv2 effectively mitigated bias in simpler prompts, while TextGrad successfully optimized complex, multi-step prompts.

suggestions.title: Explore Cross-Language Disfluency Effects
suggestions.desc: Investigate how speech disfluencies impact LLM-based Alzheimer’s detection across different languages. Utilize multilingual datasets and LLMs trained on diverse linguistic corpora to assess variances in detection accuracy.

suggestions.title: Develop a Real-Time LLM-Based Screening Tool
suggestions.desc: Create a web application that utilizes the optimized prompting strategies to provide real-time Alzheimer’s screening based on user speech input. Leverage Hugging Face's Transformers API to integrate LLMs for analysis.

suggestions.title: Implement an Educational Tool for Caregivers
suggestions.desc: Design an educational tool for caregivers that uses the findings from the study to train them on recognizing speech patterns indicative of Alzheimer’s. Incorporate interactive elements and video examples for effective learning.

suggestions.title: Collaborate with Speech Therapy Professionals
suggestions.desc: Engage with speech therapists to validate the effectiveness of the LLM-based detection methods in clinical settings. Develop protocols for using the tool in therapy sessions to assess and track client progress.

title: UC-143-178 Georgia Laws of Life CRM Implemenetation
authors: Sternon, Nicholas, Flores, Josh, Scales, Jessica, Bhuiyan, Opurbo, Patel, Shiv
abstract: This project focuses on implementing a Customer Relationship Management (CRM) system for Georgia Laws of Life using the Little Green Light (LGL) platform. The organization previously relied on spreadsheets, which caused issues such as duplicate records, inefficient reporting, and difficulty managing relationships. To address this, the team analyzed existing workflows and developed a structured data model. The system was configured, and sample data including constituents, donations, schools, and contracts was successfully imported to validate the design. The results show that the CRM system improves data organization, enhances relationship tracking, and provides a more efficient and scalable solution for managing organizational data.

suggestions.title: Enhance CRM with AI-Powered Analytics
suggestions.desc: Integrate AI-powered analytics into the Little Green Light CRM system to predict donor behavior and optimize outreach strategies. Use machine learning libraries like Scikit-Learn to analyze historical donation data.

suggestions.title: Create a Mobile App for Donor Engagement
suggestions.desc: Develop a mobile application that provides a platform for donors to track their contributions and receive updates from Georgia Laws of Life. Implement push notifications to keep users engaged with organizational activities.

suggestions.title: Evaluate CRM Impact on Donor Retention
suggestions.desc: Conduct a study to assess how the implementation of the CRM system affects donor retention rates. Use statistical analysis tools to compare pre- and post-implementation data and identify key success factors.

suggestions.title: Integrate Social Media Management Features
suggestions.desc: Enhance the CRM by adding features for managing social media campaigns. Use APIs from platforms like Facebook and Twitter to track engagement metrics and automate posting schedules.

title: GRP-01-196 Topological Drift Predicts Epidemic Instability
authors: Fanning, Charles
abstract: We study whether changes in contact-network topology predict transitions into high-risk epidemic periods across several classical empirical proximity network datasets. We use temporal graph learning with persistent homology-based topological signals and evaluate large-outbreak risk using SIR simulations to test whether topological drift serves as an early warning signal for epidemic instability.

suggestions.title: Investigate Alternative Topological Measures
suggestions.desc: Explore additional topological measures beyond persistent homology, such as Betti numbers or Euler characteristics, to see how they correlate with epidemic risk. Apply these measures to existing proximity network datasets to enhance predictive capabilities.

suggestions.title: Develop a Simulation Tool for Epidemic Forecasting
suggestions.desc: Create a simulation tool that allows users to input different contact network topologies and visualize epidemic dynamics. Use Python libraries like NetworkX for network modeling and Matplotlib for visualization.

suggestions.title: Case Study on Epidemic Response Planning
suggestions.desc: Conduct a case study analyzing past epidemics to validate the topological drift predictions. Collaborate with public health officials to provide insights on how to utilize these findings in future epidemic response strategies.

suggestions.title: Integrate Machine Learning for Predictive Modeling
suggestions.desc: Incorporate machine learning algorithms to improve the accuracy of epidemic instability predictions based on topological drift. Use frameworks like TensorFlow or PyTorch to develop predictive models trained on historical epidemic data.

title: UC-162-194 Smart Soil Analyzer
authors: Florez Garcia, Samuel, Johnson, Edward, Gamino, Aaron, Burton, Tassha, Kinney, Wyatt
abstract: The Smart Soil Analyzer is a machine learning-based application designed to maximize agricultural efficiency and sustainability. Our team developed a predictive system using a K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN) classifier trained on a comprehensive crop recommendation dataset. The tool allows users to input key environmental and soil metrics, including Nitrogen (N), Phosphorus (P), Potassium (K), temperature, humidity, pH levels, and rainfall. By processing these variables, the model accurately predicts the most suitable crop for the specific land conditions. This solution provides farmers with data-driven insights to optimize yields, reduce fertilizer waste, and combat soil degradation through precise crop matching.

suggestions.title: Integrate IoT Sensors for Real-Time Soil Monitoring
suggestions.desc: Enhance the Smart Soil Analyzer by integrating IoT sensors to collect real-time soil data such as moisture levels and nutrient concentrations. Use platforms like Arduino or Raspberry Pi along with cloud services like AWS IoT or Google Cloud IoT to transmit data, allowing for dynamic crop recommendations based on live environmental changes.

suggestions.title: Expand Crop Recommendation Dataset with Climate Change Variables
suggestions.desc: Investigate how climate change impacts crop viability by expanding the existing dataset to include long-term climate data. Use machine learning techniques to analyze correlations and adapt the KNN model to suggest crops that are resilient to changing climate conditions.

suggestions.title: Develop a Mobile App for Farmer Accessibility
suggestions.desc: Create a mobile application that allows farmers to easily input their soil metrics and receive crop recommendations on-the-go. Utilize frameworks like React Native or Flutter to ensure cross-platform compatibility, enhancing accessibility and utility for rural farmers.

suggestions.title: Partner with Agricultural NGOs for Field Testing
suggestions.desc: Collaborate with agricultural NGOs to deploy the Smart Soil Analyzer in real-world farming scenarios. Gather feedback and performance data to refine the predictive model, while also providing farmers with valuable insights to improve agricultural practices.

title: GRM-157-180 Precision Engineering: Using AI to Design Nanoparticles that Target Malignant Cells
authors: Gurung, Rakshak, Tkabladze, Nino
abstract: The challenge of predicting nanoparticle distribution remain a significant hurdle in nanomedicine. This research presents a computational framework for the inverse design of nanoparticles, utilizing ML models to optimize drug delivery systems for tumor targeting. By analyzing the relationship between nanoparticle compositions and biological accumulation, the model identifies optimal configurations to maximize therapeutic efficacy. The results demonstrate that AI-driven inverse design can significantly streamline the development of precision nanocarriers, reducing the need for exhaustive experimental trials.

suggestions.title: Implement Reinforcement Learning for Nanoparticle Optimization
suggestions.desc: Utilize reinforcement learning algorithms to further enhance the nanoparticle design process by allowing the model to learn from simulated environments and outcomes. This could lead to more efficient and innovative configurations for targeting malignant cells.

suggestions.title: Conduct In-Vitro Studies to Validate AI Predictions
suggestions.desc: Design and conduct in-vitro experiments to test the efficacy of the AI-optimized nanoparticles. Analyze the biological accumulation of nanoparticles in tumor cells versus healthy cells, providing empirical data to refine the machine learning model.

suggestions.title: Create a Visualization Tool for Nanoparticle Behavior
suggestions.desc: Develop a web-based visualization tool that illustrates how nanoparticles behave within biological systems, displaying their distribution and interaction with malignant cells. Use libraries like D3.js or Three.js to create interactive, educational graphics that can aid in research presentations.

suggestions.title: Collaborate with Medical Institutions for Clinical Trials
suggestions.desc: Partner with medical institutions to implement clinical trials using your optimized nanoparticles. This collaboration can provide real-world data and insights, further validating the AI-driven design approach while contributing to advancements in nanomedicine.

title: GRP-120-138 WALL-E: Wide-Area Aerial Live Learning for Emergency Disaster Evaluation
authors: Shrestha, Shiva
abstract: WALL-E is an AI-powered, custom-built quadcopter that surveys disaster zones in real time, classifying building damage and generating a GPS-tagged damage map with no internet required. The system uses an RGB camera for live AI inference and a FLIR thermal camera for additional situational awareness. A custom YOLO based model runs entirely onboard the Jetson Orin Nano, logging every detection via GPS for immediate command use. Future work will expand detection capabilities to include human presence identification.

suggestions.title: Enhance Damage Detection with Deep Learning Techniques
suggestions.desc: Implement deep learning techniques, such as a modified version of the YOLO model, to improve the accuracy of damage detection in buildings. Use datasets from previous disaster evaluations to retrain the model for higher precision in distinguishing various levels of damage.

suggestions.title: Analyze Human Presence Detection Algorithms
suggestions.desc: Investigate advanced human presence detection algorithms using thermal and RGB data. Research different machine learning methods, such as convolutional neural networks, to enhance the accuracy and reliability of identifying individuals in disaster zones.

suggestions.title: Create a Web Dashboard for Real-Time Damage Assessment
suggestions.desc: Develop a web dashboard that displays real-time data from the WALL-E system, including damage maps and detected human presence. Utilize technologies like Flask or Django for backend development and integrate mapping APIs like Google Maps for visual representation.

suggestions.title: Collaborate with Emergency Response Teams for Field Testing
suggestions.desc: Work alongside emergency response teams to deploy the WALL-E drone during disaster simulations or real events. Gather feedback and data to improve system performance and usability, while providing valuable insights to aid in disaster management.

title: GRP-180-147 Quantum Augmented Microgrids (QuAM) Simulator
authors: Jha, Nitin, Paudel, Prateek
abstract: Small modular nuclear reactors (SMRs) are redefining the energy generation landscape by enabling the deployment of modular, scalable, and pre-built power units that can be used to build distributed autonomous microgrids for critical infrastructure and burgeoning AI factories. Often, these microgrids are linked together to provide a resilient, decentralized power generation infrastructure. Consequently, the cybersecurity of microgrids is of critical importance. In this work, we propose a quantum augmented network framework for resilient microgrids. We integrate the ideas of secure quantum networking, quantum anonymous notification, and quantum random number generation to strengthen the integrity, confidentiality, and privacy of microgrid networks. To substantiate the possible benefits of using quantum augmented microgrids, we simulate a practical high-impact classical attack: a traffic analysis and priority-action spoofing campaign that can (1) deanonymize the anonymous notification for a high-priority action, (2) force excessive key usage, and (3) induce harmful allow/block operations at the control level. We quantify how these attacks affect information leakage, spoof acceptance, key sufficiency, and operational outcomes such as latency, deadline misses, unserved energy, etc. This quantum augmented microgrid (QuAM) framework lets us evaluate trade-offs between privacy, availability, and the operational cost of mitigations (cover traffic, verification delays, and key-rotation policies), further paving the path for the study of more nuanced attacks that arise due to the use of quantum-classical integrated frameworks.

suggestions.title: Implement Quantum Key Distribution for Secure Microgrid Communication
suggestions.desc: Integrate quantum key distribution (QKD) protocols into the microgrid framework to enhance communication security. Explore existing QKD libraries and simulate secure information exchange between nodes in the microgrid using Quantum Internet technologies.

suggestions.title: Explore Cybersecurity Threats Unique to Modular Nuclear Reactors
suggestions.desc: Conduct a comprehensive analysis of cybersecurity threats specifically targeting small modular reactors in microgrids. Develop a risk assessment framework to identify vulnerabilities and potential attack vectors, enhancing the resilience of the proposed system.

suggestions.title: Develop a Simulation Environment for Microgrid Cybersecurity Testing
suggestions.desc: Create a simulation environment that mimics a microgrid powered by SMRs, allowing for testing of different attack scenarios. Use tools like OMNeT++ or MATSim to simulate network behavior and evaluate the effectiveness of various cybersecurity measures.

suggestions.title: Collaborate with Energy Providers for Real-World Implementation
suggestions.desc: Partner with energy providers to implement the quantum-augmented microgrid framework in pilot projects. Gather data on performance and security outcomes, while providing a practical testbed for your theoretical model.

title: GC-119-134 Pipeline for VR Embodied Lecture Authoring and AI Gesture Refinement
authors: Aiyatham Prabakar, Rishi Kiran
abstract: While Virtual Reality (VR) offers immersive educational opportunities, its pedagogical success relies heavily on a genuine sense of "instructor presence". This project presents a hybrid pipeline that automatically refines presenter 3D avatar gestures using semantic AI. Our non-VR recording system captures high-fidelity facial tracking and MediaPipe for upper-body pose estimation via standard RGB video. For emotion recognition, a local Large Language Model analyzes audio transcripts to generate a timestamped emphasis track. This semantic engine, intelligently exaggerating gestures during critical lecture moments. The captured motion and AI-enhanced gestures are synthesized and replayed on a virtual lecturer within an VR environment for evaluation.

suggestions.title: Integrate Haptic Feedback for Enhanced Instructor Presence
suggestions.desc: Develop a haptic feedback system that allows users to feel the gestures of the 3D avatar during VR interactions. Utilize hardware like the Novint Falcon or haptic gloves to create a more immersive experience that enhances the sense of instructor presence.

suggestions.title: Investigate the Impact of Gesture Variability on Learning Outcomes
suggestions.desc: Conduct research on how different types of avatar gestures affect learner engagement and comprehension in VR environments. Use A/B testing with various gesture styles to quantify the impact on educational effectiveness.

suggestions.title: Create a VR Training Module for Soft Skills Development
suggestions.desc: Design a VR training module focused on developing soft skills such as public speaking or negotiation. Leverage the AI gesture refinement system to enhance the avatar's presentation style, providing users with realistic practice scenarios.

suggestions.title: Partner with Educational Institutions for Pilot Programs
suggestions.desc: Collaborate with educational institutions to pilot the VR lecture authoring system in real classroom settings. Gather feedback from both instructors and students to refine the technology and demonstrate its potential impact on learning outcomes.

title: GC-178-191 Communication App: AI-Assisted AAC Platform?
authors: Wills, Alex, Koya, Maryam
abstract: The Communication App is an accessibility-focused mobile application designed to support individuals with speech impairments, strong or unrecognized accents, and neurodivergent communication needs. The system leverages AI assisted speech-to-text (STT) and text-to-speech (TTS) technologies to enable real-time and seamless communication between users.This project aims to bridge communication gaps by providing a customizable, adaptive platform that learns user speech patterns over time. The application integrates cloud-based services, secure communication protocols, and an intuitive user interface to ensure usability, performance, and accessibility.
similar: legacy:UR-041|Spring 2021|the accessibility of the mobile gaming platform for the visually impaired

suggestions.title: Integrate Emotion Recognition for Enhanced Communication
suggestions.desc: Build a feature that uses emotion recognition through facial expressions or voice tone analysis to better understand user sentiments. Utilize TensorFlow or OpenCV for real-time detection and integrate it into the existing AAC platform to provide more empathetic responses.

suggestions.title: Implement Multi-Language Support for Global Accessibility
suggestions.desc: Extend the application to support multiple languages by incorporating Google Cloud Translation API or Microsoft Translator. This will cater to non-English speaking users and promote inclusivity across different regions.

suggestions.title: Conduct User Studies on Communication Effectiveness
suggestions.desc: Design a research study to evaluate the effectiveness of the app in real-world scenarios. Gather qualitative and quantitative data on user satisfaction and communication improvements, potentially publishing the findings in an accessibility journal.

suggestions.title: Create a Customizable Voice Pack Marketplace
suggestions.desc: Develop a marketplace where users can purchase or download customizable voice packs for TTS functionalities. Collaborate with voice actors and use platforms like Unity Asset Store to create a vibrant community around personalized communication.

title: GC-154-201 Evaluation of Generative AI Responses to Pharmacy Prompts
authors: Syed, Abrar
abstract: Generative AI (GenAI) is increasingly used in pharmacy for drug information and decision support, yet accuracy remains variable. We systematically reviewed studies that reported full prompts and model responses to evaluate correctness across pharmacy-relevant tasks. GenAI performed well on basic drug facts but was inconsistent for patient-specific recommendations and interaction checking, with occasional hallucinations. Findings support cautious, supplementary use in pharmacy practice and education.

suggestions.title: Analyze Prompting Techniques for Improved AI Responses
suggestions.desc: Investigate different prompting techniques that could lead to more accurate GenAI responses in pharmacy tasks. Conduct experiments with various prompt formats and analyze the results to develop best practices for pharmacy applications.

suggestions.title: Build a Feedback Loop System for AI Learning
suggestions.desc: Create an interactive platform where pharmacists can provide feedback on AI-generated responses. Use this feedback to retrain the GenAI model, improving its accuracy over time and making it more reliable for patient-specific recommendations.

suggestions.title: Develop a Mobile App for Pharmacy Professionals
suggestions.desc: Design a mobile application that integrates the evaluated GenAI model to assist pharmacists on-the-go. Include features like drug interaction checking and personalized patient advice, ensuring pharmacists can access reliable information anywhere.

suggestions.title: Host Workshops on AI in Pharmacy Education
suggestions.desc: Organize workshops or seminars to educate pharmacy students about the use of GenAI in their field. Collaborate with local pharmacy schools to facilitate discussions on ethical considerations and practical applications of AI.

title: UC-099-189 Spectre
authors: Tobal, Alexander, Higgins Jr, Chris, Reeves, Jaylin, Leichter, Logan
abstract: Spectre consists of four levels, where players complete various objectives and fight off ghosts while doing so. Our tutorial level introduces players to the mechanics, such as shooting, rear view mirror shooting, walking and jumping. With the rest of the levels focusing on completing objectives in order to progress. The final level culminates in a boss fight, ending the journey. While players explore and complete objectives, enemies drop a currency that players can spend to obtain upgrades. Getting hit by enemies not only reduces the players’ health but also applies debuffs to them making players more cautious of their surroundings. These debuffs include lower movement speed, locking out jumping, deactivating the rearview mirror, etc.

suggestions.title: Introduce Multiplayer Capabilities for Spectre
suggestions.desc: Expand the game to include a multiplayer mode where players can cooperate or compete against each other. Use Photon Unity Networking to implement real-time gameplay and interactions, enhancing user engagement.

suggestions.title: Create an Educational Version of Spectre for Learning Mechanics
suggestions.desc: Develop a version of Spectre aimed at teaching game design principles, focusing on the mechanics of gameplay, level design, and user experience. Include tutorials and developer commentary to guide students through the design process.

suggestions.title: Analyze Player Behavior and Game Balance
suggestions.desc: Conduct a study to analyze player behaviors and preferences within the game. Use analytics tools like Unity Analytics to gather data, and publish findings that could inform future game design decisions and balance adjustments.

suggestions.title: Launch a Game Development Workshop Series
suggestions.desc: Host a series of workshops aimed at teaching game development using Unity and C#. Focus on various aspects such as level design, AI enemy behavior, and monetization strategies, targeting both beginners and intermediate developers.

title: UC-082-216 AI Driven Guest Support for VacationsForYou?
authors: Grogan, Cassidie, Elison, Kendal, Dulcio, Benjamin, Begashaw, Ezra, Faltz, Wilfred
abstract: The AI Guest Support Assistant is a proof-of-concept, web-based chatbot designed to streamline guest support for a high-volume vacation rental operation. The system leverages a Large Language Model (LLM) combined with a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) approach to deliver accurate, context-aware responses to common guest inquiries, such as reservation details and check-in times. Built using a React frontend and a FastAPI backend, the platform integrates securely with the StreamlineVRS property management system. The solution aims to reduce call center workload by automating repetitive inquiries while maintaining a clear escalation path for more complex requests. This project evaluates the feasibility, usability, and effectiveness of AI-assisted customer support in a controlled environment.

suggestions.title: Integrate Voice Recognition for Natural Language Processing
suggestions.desc: Enhance the chatbot by adding voice recognition capabilities using the Web Speech API. This will allow guests to interact with the assistant using voice commands, making it more user-friendly and accessible.

suggestions.title: Expand to Support Unique Guest Requests and Customization
suggestions.desc: Develop a feature that allows guests to submit unique requests or customize their stay preferences. Use this data to tailor responses and improve guest satisfaction, potentially integrating with a backend database to store user preferences.

suggestions.title: Evaluate Chatbot Performance Against Human Agents
suggestions.desc: Conduct a comparative study to evaluate the chatbot's performance versus traditional human support agents. Measure response accuracy, user satisfaction, and issue resolution time, and publish the results to inform future AI implementations.

suggestions.title: Create a Knowledge Base for Common Guest Inquiries
suggestions.desc: Build a comprehensive knowledge base that the AI can reference for common guest inquiries. This could include FAQs, property-specific information, and local attractions, improving the quality of responses and reducing the workload on human staff.

title: UR-133-165 Quantum Machine Learning for Science and Engineering
authors: Barnes, Barclay, Zharikov, Anna, Hamzi, Meriem
abstract: Quantum machine learning (QML) has emerged as a promising method for overcoming the computational limitations of classical machine learning when analyzing large and complex data sets. This project investigates the application of QML algorithms to real-world science and engineering problems, with a focus on civil and environmental engineering datasets. We develop and evaluate a Python-based system, implemented in Google Colab, that integrates multiple quantum computing frameworks, including PennyLane, TensorFlow Quantum, and Qiskit, to implement and compare several QML models against their classical counterparts. The proposed system explores a range of algorithms such as Quantum Neural Networks, Quantum Support Vector Machines, Quantum Principal Component Analysis, and Quantum Logistic Regression across at least five engineering use cases, including structural health monitoring, traffic flow prediction, air quality, flood forecasting, and pollution modeling. Performance is evaluated using accuracy, computational efficiency, and scalability. The results highlight scenarios in which QML demonstrates a potential advantage over a classical approach, while also identifying the current limitations that come with near-term quantum devices. This work contributes to a modular framework for learning and applying quantum machine learning and provides insight into its practical viability and application within science and engineering applications.

suggestions.title: Implement Hybrid Quantum-Classical Algorithms
suggestions.desc: Explore hybrid quantum-classical machine learning algorithms to improve performance on specific civil engineering datasets. Utilize frameworks like PennyLane and TensorFlow Quantum to create and test these algorithms against classical benchmarks.

suggestions.title: Conduct a Comparative Study of QML Techniques
suggestions.desc: Perform a detailed analysis comparing different QML models on various datasets. Evaluate metrics like accuracy, speed, and scalability, and publish a comprehensive report that highlights the strengths and weaknesses of each approach.

suggestions.title: Develop a Quantum-Enhanced Simulation Tool for Engineering
suggestions.desc: Create a simulation tool that leverages QML to solve complex engineering problems, such as material stress analysis or environmental impact assessments. This tool could be valuable for civil engineers looking to incorporate advanced analytics.

suggestions.title: Host a Workshop on Quantum Computing in Machine Learning
suggestions.desc: Organize a workshop series focused on the intersection of quantum computing and machine learning. Invite experts in the field to discuss practical applications and provide hands-on sessions with tools like Qiskit and PennyLane.

title: GRM-012-173 Can You Trust AI Code? Understanding and Detecting Breaking Changes using LLMs
authors: Ferdous, K M, Chowdhury, Kowshik
abstract: AI-generated code is increasingly prevalent in software engineering practices, yet its reliability in preserving backward compatibility remains underexplored. This paper presents a unified study of (i) how often AI-generated code introduces breaking changes and (ii) whether large language models (LLMs) can detect such changes from commit-level diffs with explanations. We analyze 7,191 agent-generated and 1,402 human-authored pull requests from Python repositories using an AST-based approach to identify potential breaking changes. Our results show that AI agents introduce fewer breaking changes overall than humans (3.45% vs. 7.40%) in code generation tasks. However, agents show higher risk in maintenance tasks, where refactoring and chore changes introduce breaking changes at rates of 6.72% and 9.35%, respectively. To mitigate this risk and evaluate the effectiveness of the LLM-based AI agent, we developed an AI agent, PyCoReX, that can detect breaking changes from code commits. Our agent achieves a baseline F1-score of 0.82. Our findings show that commit-level LLM-based detection can support earlier and more reliable identification of breaking changes, improving the safety of agent-assisted software development.

suggestions.title: Implement a Real-Time Monitoring Tool for Breaking Changes
suggestions.desc: Build a web application that continuously monitors GitHub repositories for AI-generated code changes. Use GitHub API to fetch pull requests and integrate LLMs to analyze the diffs for breaking changes, providing developers with alerts and explanations in real-time.

suggestions.title: Conduct a Comparative Study on Breaking Changes Across Languages
suggestions.desc: Extend the analysis to include multiple programming languages such as Java and JavaScript. Collect and analyze a larger dataset of pull requests to see if the trends observed in Python hold true across languages, focusing on the type and frequency of breaking changes introduced by AI.

suggestions.title: Develop a CI/CD Plugin for LLM-Powered Code Quality Checks
suggestions.desc: Create a plugin for popular CI/CD services like Jenkins or GitLab that utilizes the findings to automatically check for potential breaking changes in AI-generated code during the build process. This could help teams better manage their deployment pipelines.

suggestions.title: Create a Training Dataset for AI Code Review Tools
suggestions.desc: Compile a dataset based on the findings that includes examples of breaking changes and their explanations. Use this dataset to train models that can assist in code reviews, helping developers understand the implications of AI-generated code.

title: GRP-163-229 Emotion Elicitation via Empathic AI (E-AI) Agent Teacher in VR Classroom
authors: Paul, Arpita
abstract: How emotions influence learning experiences in virtual reality (VR) remains unclear. To address this gap, we investigated the impact of emotionally elicited lectures on learners’ experiences and outcomes in a pedagogical VR classroom using a between-subjects design with two emotional conditions (positive vs. negative). Emotional cues were delivered by a virtual agent (VA) teacher through bodily gestures and verbal expressions during lecture delivery. In a user study (N=34), we collected multimodal data, including neurophysiological measures such as electroencephalography (EEG), galvanic skin response (GSR), heart rate (HR), heart rate variability (HRV), skin temperature, and eye gaze, along with self-reported emotion, learning experience, and quiz performance. The results showed significant differences in neurophysiological responses, particularly in EEG, GSR, HR, and temperature, across emotional conditions. However, no significant differences were observed in subjective emotional responses, learning experiences, or quiz performance. These findings suggest that emotionally expressive virtual agents can elicit measurable neurophysiological responses during VR learning, even when participants are not consciously aware of these changes, highlighting the importance of considering implicit emotional responses when designing affect-aware VR learning environments.

suggestions.title: Develop a VR Classroom Simulation with Customizable Emotional Agents
suggestions.desc: Create a VR application that allows educators to customize the emotional responses of virtual agents based on different teaching scenarios. Use Unity and Oculus SDK to build this immersive experience, enabling educators to experiment with various emotional cues.

suggestions.title: Analyze Long-Term Effects of Emotionally Elicited Learning
suggestions.desc: Conduct a longitudinal study to assess how emotionally elicited lectures impact learning retention over time. Use EEG and other biometric data to measure long-term engagement and comprehension in different educational settings.

suggestions.title: Implement Emotion-Driven Adaptive Learning Paths in VR
suggestions.desc: Design a system in the VR classroom that adapts the learning path based on the learner's emotional responses. Use machine learning to analyze real-time data from users and adjust the content delivery accordingly to enhance engagement and learning outcomes.

suggestions.title: Create a Toolkit for Educators to Measure Emotional Impact in VR
suggestions.desc: Develop a toolkit that includes metrics and guidelines for educators to measure emotional impact in VR learning environments. This toolkit can help in designing lectures that leverage emotional engagement effectively.

title: UC-167-222 SECU Horizon
authors: Duarte, Julian, Boecker, Chance, Martin, Adam, Collins, Rylan, Haggard, Oliver
abstract: Secu Horizon is a fast-paced, stealth-based 3d action platformer, where you run around in a dystopian city. The main mechanic of the game revolves around a knife projectile that the player throws and teleports to. The game will have a strong mix of smooth platforming, soft stealth sections, and bullet time combat, as the player throws around the knife to defeat enemies. The feeling that this game will invoke is that of being an unstoppable rebel ninja, in the cold dead of the night.

suggestions.title: Integrate AI-Driven Enemy Behavior in Secu Horizon
suggestions.desc: Enhance the game by implementing AI-driven enemy behavior that adapts to player strategies. Use machine learning algorithms to analyze player actions and adjust enemy tactics in real-time, making the gameplay more dynamic.

suggestions.title: Study Player Engagement Metrics in Stealth Games
suggestions.desc: Conduct a study analyzing player engagement and emotions while playing Secu Horizon. Use telemetry data to gather insights on player behavior, focusing on how stealth mechanics influence overall enjoyment and game completion rates.

suggestions.title: Create a Level Editor Tool for User-Generated Content
suggestions.desc: Develop a level editor that allows players to create and share their own levels within Secu Horizon. This tool can increase player engagement and provide a platform for community-driven content, fostering a dedicated player base.

suggestions.title: Launch a Multiplayer Mode with Cooperative Gameplay Elements
suggestions.desc: Expand Secu Horizon into a multiplayer experience where players can cooperate to complete missions. Implement shared objectives and unique character abilities that require teamwork, providing a new dimension to the stealth gameplay.

title: GRM-174-228 Mitigating Prompt-Induced Variability in LLM Outputs
authors: Tubbs, Crystal
abstract: Large language models in enterprise settings often produce structurally invalid outputs when users communicate informally, creating silent failure modes that pass unnoticed in downstream systems. This study investigates how prompt variation alone impacts schema compliance and output reliability. We evaluate three architectures across two tasks and four prompt styles, isolating the effect of interaction style on model behavior. Results show that baseline systems achieve 100% compliance under structured prompts but fail completely under ambiguous and casual inputs. A minimal reliability pipeline consisting of generation, self critique, and schema validation restores 100% compliance across all conditions at a predictable computational cost.

suggestions.title: Develop a User Interface for Prompt Structuring
suggestions.desc: Create a user-friendly web application that guides users in crafting structured prompts for LLMs. Use frameworks like React or Angular to build the interface, incorporating examples and best practices for various use cases.

suggestions.title: Investigate Domain-Specific Prompt Effects on LLM Reliability
suggestions.desc: Conduct a study exploring how prompts tailored to specific domains (e.g., healthcare, finance) impact LLM outputs. Gather data on schema compliance and analyze how domain-specific language influences model performance.

suggestions.title: Implement a Feedback System for Prompt Improvement
suggestions.desc: Build a system that allows users to provide feedback on LLM responses based on different prompts. Use this feedback to refine the model and generate insights on common pitfalls in prompt design, improving output reliability.

suggestions.title: Create an Educational Resource on Prompt Engineering
suggestions.desc: Develop an online course or resource center that teaches prompt engineering techniques for different applications of LLMs. Include interactive examples, quizzes, and community forums to engage learners and share experiences.

title: UC-128-145 KSU ESports Discord Server Bot?
authors: Gammill, Austin, Thomas, Foster, Truong, Lam, Olubajo, Jeffrey, Ahmed, Ismail
abstract: The KSU Esports program has requested us to develop further upon the Discord Server Bot that they are currently using. This prior implementation was developed by Capstone students last year. Our project’s goal was to build upon their work, polish existing features, commands, UI/UX, and fix known bugs. We have worked on improving the bot’s matchmaking algorithms, tournament seeding logic, API integration, database persistence layer, stability, and statistics tracking. Furthermore, we have developed the UI/UX to be more user-friendly, added support for additional games, and overhauled the bot’s database logic.

suggestions.title: Build a Matchmaking Algorithm for Team-Based Games
suggestions.desc: Enhance the Discord bot by implementing a sophisticated matchmaking algorithm that considers player skill levels, game preferences, and historical performance. Use APIs from games like League of Legends or Valorant for player data integration.

suggestions.title: Analyze User Engagement Metrics in Discord Communities
suggestions.desc: Conduct a research project that gathers and analyzes user engagement metrics within the KSU Esports Discord community. Use analytics tools to assess the impact of bot features on user interaction and community growth.

suggestions.title: Integrate Voice Commands for Bot Interaction
suggestions.desc: Develop a voice command feature for the Discord bot that allows users to interact with it using natural language. Use a speech recognition API like Google Cloud Speech-to-Text to implement this functionality for enhanced accessibility.

suggestions.title: Create a Tournament Management System within Discord
suggestions.desc: Build a comprehensive tournament management system that integrates with the Discord bot, allowing users to create, manage, and participate in tournaments directly from Discord. Include features like bracket generation, match scheduling, and live updates.

title: GC-168-220 Active Directory to Cloud Security Data Pipeline
authors: Butler, Michael, Shamshad, Shahiba, Touil, Mounia, Afantchao, Koko
abstract: This project builds an automated pipeline that extracts identity and asset data from on-prem Active Directory, stages it in Google BigQuery, and securely sends normalized data to Lucid through Google Cloud Run. Using PowerShell scripts, the system collects users, groups, computers, DNS, DHCP, and related metadata without changing the source environment. BigQuery serves as the staging layer for validation and processing, while Cloud Run transforms and transfers the latest data to Lucid for visualization. The goal is to provide a repeatable, traceable, and reliable workflow that improves visibility into identity relationships for security investigations, auditing, and validation in a controlled sandbox environment.

suggestions.title: Integrate with AWS for Multi-Cloud Data Pipelines
suggestions.desc: Extend the existing pipeline by implementing AWS services such as Lambda and S3 to create a multi-cloud environment. This will allow the data pipeline to pull from and push to AWS resources, enhancing flexibility and scalability.

suggestions.title: Visualize User Behavior with Enhanced Analytics
suggestions.desc: Create a dashboard that visualizes user behavior data collected from Active Directory. Use frameworks like D3.js or Plotly to present insights such as login patterns and resource access frequencies, aiding organizations in security audits.

suggestions.title: Evaluate Security Risks of Cloud Data Transfers
suggestions.desc: Conduct a study on the security implications of transferring sensitive identity data from Active Directory to cloud platforms. Analyze potential attack vectors and propose mitigations based on real-world scenarios.

suggestions.title: Develop a User Access Review Tool
suggestions.desc: Build a tool that uses the data pipeline to automate user access reviews, providing recommendations for access rights based on user behavior analysis and compliance requirements. This tool could significantly reduce administrative overhead.

title: GRM-134-128 NeuroVision: Mapping Brain Signals to Language and Visual Meaning
authors: Yellu, Siri
abstract: NeuroVision presents a unified framework for mapping electroencephalography (EEG) signals to language and visual meaning. Extracting semantic information from EEG remains a fundamental challenge due to its low signal-to-noise ratio, high dimensionality, and inter-subject variability. To address these challenges, we propose a multimodal representation learning framework that aligns EEG signals with both textual and visual embeddings through temporal modeling, spatial brain-region decomposition, and contrastive learning. The framework integrates self-supervised pretraining with supervised multimodal alignment to learn robust and transferable representations. Experimental results demonstrate BLEU-1 of 0.1106 and ROUGE-1 of 0.1493 for EEG-to-text generation, alongside a 52% improvement in retrieval performance (R@1: 1.38%) and high cross-modal consistency (0.9998). These results provide strong evidence that EEG signals encode modality-independent semantic structure, advancing general brain-to-meaning decoding and enabling scalable, next-generation brain–computer interface systems.

suggestions.title: Create a Dataset for EEG and Visual Stimuli
suggestions.desc: Compile a new dataset of EEG responses paired with visual stimuli and language tasks to further train the NeuroVision framework. This dataset could help in understanding the nuances of brain signal interpretation across different contexts.

suggestions.title: Implement Real-time EEG Signal Processing
suggestions.desc: Enhance the framework to process EEG signals in real-time using tools like Python's MNE library. This could allow for immediate feedback and interaction based on brain signal responses during live sessions.

suggestions.title: Develop an Educational Tool for Language Learning
suggestions.desc: Create an application that uses the NeuroVision framework to assist in language learning by mapping EEG responses to language comprehension tasks. This tool can provide personalized feedback to improve learning outcomes.

suggestions.title: Explore Cross-Modal Applications in Neuromarketing
suggestions.desc: Investigate the application of the NeuroVision framework in neuromarketing to understand consumer reactions to advertisements. This could lead to insights on how visual and linguistic cues affect purchasing decisions.

title: UC-158-205 ScrappyFin — IND, Simulated Digital Wallet &? Fraud Detection Platform
authors: Davis, Logan, Galvan, Dante, Jordan, Zahaira, Bracey, Avery, Pham, Chris
