abstract: ScrappyFin is an educational FinTech platform developed in partnership with The Home Depot to simulate a digital wallet system and demonstrate fraud detection techniques. Users can create virtual wallets and perform synthetic transactions in a controlled environment, enabling analysis of financial behavior without real risk. The platform combines rule-based logic with machine learning models to identify suspicious activity, such as unusual transaction amounts or patterns. An admin dashboard provides clear explanations for flagged transactions. ScrappyFin showcases how modern financial systems detect risk while serving as a practical learning tool for software engineering and data-driven applications.

suggestions.title: Integrate Blockchain for Transaction Security
suggestions.desc: Add a blockchain layer to ScrappyFin to enhance transaction security and transparency. Use Ethereum smart contracts to validate transactions and provide an immutable record of synthetic financial activities.

suggestions.title: Gamify Financial Literacy Education
suggestions.desc: Turn ScrappyFin into a gamified platform where users earn rewards for completing financial literacy challenges. Incorporate quizzes and interactive scenarios to engage users while they learn about finance and fraud detection.

suggestions.title: Analyze Machine Learning Model Efficacy
suggestions.desc: Conduct a comparative study of various machine learning algorithms used in the fraud detection module. Evaluate their performance on different synthetic datasets to determine which models deliver the best accuracy and efficiency.

suggestions.title: Create a Community for Fraud Awareness
suggestions.desc: Develop a forum or community space within ScrappyFin where users can share experiences and strategies related to fraud prevention. This could enhance user engagement and provide a platform for collaborative learning.

title: UC-117-213 Haunted Owl Hotel – A 3D Horror Maze Chase Game
authors: Griffin, Carter, Egl, Rin, Redmon, Kcyana, Portillo, Jose, Nesbit, Alana
abstract: “Haunted Owl Hotel” is a horror Pac-Man-inspired, 3D maze chase game. You play as a cute owl named Sappy trying to escape the scary hotel, but suddenly your elevator breaks down. Navigate the spooky halls to collect the candles left behind on each floor to reactivate the elevator, but be careful, after grabbing each candle, the darkness left behind will follow you. Ghosts lurk around every corner hoping to make you their next victim. Descend through each floor without losing all 3 lives to escape the haunted owl hotel and win the game.

suggestions.title: Augment Gameplay with AR Features
suggestions.desc: Integrate Augmented Reality (AR) elements into the Haunted Owl Hotel game using ARKit or ARCore. Players could use their mobile devices to see ghosts or clues in their real environment, enhancing immersion.

suggestions.title: Develop a Level Editor for Custom Content
suggestions.desc: Create a level editor tool that allows players to design and share their own mazes and challenges within the game. This could foster community engagement and encourage user-generated content.

suggestions.title: Analyze User Stress Responses During Gameplay
suggestions.desc: Conduct research on how different game mechanics influence player stress and engagement levels. Use biometric data (like heart rate monitors) to assess player reactions during gameplay.

suggestions.title: Host a Game Development Workshop
suggestions.desc: Organize a workshop where participants can learn to create their own 3D maze chase games using the existing codebase of Haunted Owl Hotel. This could serve as both an educational opportunity and a way to attract more players.

title: GRP-0100-193 Are TGNN-Based Intrusion Detection Results Trustworthy?  A Dataset Audit and Evaluation Framework
authors: Chowdhoury, Faysal, Suer, Sait, Zhang, Yinning
abstract: Temporal Graph Neural Networks (TGNNs) have reported near-perfect accuracy in Network Intrusion Detection (NID). However, this research reveals these results are often artifacts of dataset flaws rather than genuine model capability. Through a systematic audit of five benchmark datasets, we identify critical issues: node identity leakage, feature extraction artifacts, train/test contamination, and temporal sparsity. We demonstrate that models often learn to recognize specific attacker IP addresses instead of generalizing attack behavior. We propose a standardized evaluation framework featuring leakage-aware relabeling and attack-aware chronological splitting to provide a more reliable basis for future TGNN-NID research.

suggestions.title: Develop a Benchmark Dataset for NID Evaluation
suggestions.desc: Create a standardized benchmark dataset for Network Intrusion Detection that addresses the identified flaws in current datasets. Include a diverse range of attack scenarios to improve model training.

suggestions.title: Implement Real-Time Intrusion Detection Prototyping
suggestions.desc: Build a prototype that applies the proposed evaluation framework in real-time network scenarios. Use tools like Wireshark and custom scripts to simulate and detect intrusions while validating model performance.

suggestions.title: Analyze Temporal Patterns in Network Attacks
suggestions.desc: Investigate temporal patterns of network intrusions using the TGNN framework. Create visualizations that illustrate how attacks evolve over time to provide insights for security teams.

suggestions.title: Engage with Security Teams for Practical Applications
suggestions.desc: Collaborate with cybersecurity firms to apply the evaluation framework in real-world scenarios, assessing its utility for improving their intrusion detection systems through practical feedback.

title: GC-126-148 Allies Connect- Georgia's Nonprofit and Volunteer Coordination Platform
authors: Calhoun, Molly, Banks, Takeshia, Castro, David, Hanrahan, Ryan, Davis, Tarik
abstract: Georgia's nonprofit services face an issue of discoverability. While many nonprofits have the resources to help their community members succeed, they have trouble actually connecting to members of the community that need their support. Connecting with these resources is challenging for community members because their avenues of communication are spread across the internet. Some have their own websites, some have a Facebook page where they post events, some rely on word of mouth and fliers, and others rely on phone chains to keep their community members informed. This means that community members seeking support need to be able to access all these separate services. Allies Connect aims consolidate some of these communication methods by creating a website where all of Georgia's nonprofit services can host their community information, and events. Allies connect provides easy access to a visual map that displays all resources and community events in an area, and allows users to easily find and contact the resources they need. Additionally, Allies Connect features a robust volunteer management system that allows other community members to get involved and support others in their community.

suggestions.title: Integrate Real-Time Chat for Volunteer Coordination
suggestions.desc: Enhance the Allies Connect platform by integrating a real-time chat feature using WebSocket technology. This would allow community members to communicate directly with nonprofits and other volunteers, facilitating instant support and coordination for events and services.

suggestions.title: Implement a Geolocation-Based Resource Finder
suggestions.desc: Develop a feature that utilizes geolocation APIs to help users find nearby nonprofit services and volunteer opportunities. By leveraging Google Maps API, you could visualize the nearest resources on a map, making it easier for users to access help within their community.

suggestions.title: Conduct User Experience Research on Nonprofit Discoverability
suggestions.desc: Perform a study to understand how users currently find and engage with nonprofits. Use surveys and interviews to gather data, then analyze the results to inform further development of the platform, ensuring it meets the real needs of community members.

suggestions.title: Create a Gamification Layer for Volunteer Engagement
suggestions.desc: Introduce a gamification aspect to the platform, rewarding users for volunteering and engaging with nonprofits. This could involve tracking hours volunteered and offering badges or points that can be redeemed for local business discounts, increasing user participation.

title: GC-173-232 A Surrogate Accountability Framework for Agentic AI Systems
authors: Tubbs, Crystal
abstract: Agentic AI systems introduce new accountability challenges because autonomous agents can act, adapt, and execute decisions without continuous human oversight. This research develops the Surrogate Accountability Framework (SAF), an architectural approach for embedding external oversight, traceability, and control directly within agentic workflows. To operationalize SAF, a working system, Chrysalis, was designed and implemented as a real time governance layer that monitors agent behavior, evaluates decision pressure, and enforces constraints through validation and intervention mechanisms. By shifting accountability from post hoc evaluation to continuous system level enforcement, this approach reduces the risk of compounding errors and enables interpretable, actionable control signals. The results demonstrate a practical pathway for deploying agentic AI systems with enforceable accountability in real world environments.

suggestions.title: Develop a Visualization Tool for Agent Behavior Analysis
suggestions.desc: Create a dashboard using D3.js or Plotly to visualize the decision-making processes of agentic AI systems under the Surrogate Accountability Framework. This tool could analyze historical data and provide insights into how agents react in various scenarios, enhancing transparency.

suggestions.title: Evaluate Ethical Implications of Accountability in AI
suggestions.desc: Conduct a comprehensive literature review on the ethical concerns surrounding autonomous agents and accountability. The findings could contribute to a white paper that discusses the implications of the Surrogate Accountability Framework in real-world applications.

suggestions.title: Test SAF with Real-Time Monitoring of Autonomous Vehicles
suggestions.desc: Implement the Surrogate Accountability Framework in a simulated environment for autonomous vehicles. Utilize ROS (Robot Operating System) to develop a prototype that monitors vehicle decisions, providing insights into accountability in real-time scenarios.

suggestions.title: Create a Community Toolkit for Implementing SAF
suggestions.desc: Develop a comprehensive toolkit that includes guidelines, sample code, and best practices for organizations looking to implement the Surrogate Accountability Framework in their AI systems. This could empower developers and researchers to adopt SAF in various applications.

title: GRM-153-198 SafeCircle: AI and Micro-Radar-Based Remote Monitoring for Patients with AD/ADRD
authors: RAHMAN, AWAN-UR-, Borty, Soarov, Ankolu, Gowtham
abstract: Alzheimer's disease and related dementias (AD/ADRD) are irreversible and degenerative neurological conditions that severely impacts neurons, resulting in cognitive decline and memory loss. This study explores a mHealth system, including a SafeCircle iOS prototype, a novel solution that combines artificial intelligence with cutting-edge micro-radar technology. The platform offers a variety of features, including management of patient and caregiver profiles, real-time alerts in case of emergencies, emergency contact lists, one-touch SOS support, sharing of live locations, and recording of unusual events in video. It is a responsive and reliable care assistant that optimizes patient safety while reducing caregiver burden.

suggestions.title: Expand SafeCircle with Wearable Device Integration
suggestions.desc: Integrate wearable health devices (like Fitbit or Apple Watch) into the SafeCircle platform to provide real-time health monitoring. This would enable the app to receive vital signs directly from wearables, improving the accuracy of health alerts for patients with AD/ADRD.

suggestions.title: Develop a Caregiver Dashboard for Enhanced Monitoring
suggestions.desc: Create a caregiver-specific dashboard that aggregates patient data and alerts, allowing caregivers to manage multiple patients more efficiently. This dashboard could utilize data visualization libraries like Chart.js to present trends in patient health over time.

suggestions.title: Analyze User Feedback to Improve App Features
suggestions.desc: Conduct a user study to gather feedback from both patients and caregivers on the SafeCircle app's functionality. Use qualitative methods to analyze how the app meets their needs and identify areas for improvement, guiding future updates.

suggestions.title: Implement AI for Predictive Health Alerts
suggestions.desc: Enhance the SafeCircle platform by incorporating machine learning algorithms that predict health emergencies based on historical data. Use TensorFlow to build predictive models that can proactively alert caregivers before a critical health event occurs.

title: GRM-06-150 Comparative Analysis of Deep Learning Architectures for Inpatient Mortality Prediction Using Time-Series Vital Signs
authors: Meurer, Jonathan, John, Andrew Philip, Udhaya, Pranay, Robinson, Nyah, Shrivastva, Dhruv
abstract: This project evaluates deep learning architectures for predicting inpatient mortality using time-series vital signs derived from the MIMIC-III dataset. A baseline Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) model was reproduced and extended with Gated Recurrent Units (GRU), Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), and Transformer architectures. Vital signs including heart rate, respiratory rate, temperature, and systolic blood pressure were processed into fixed-length time windows for model input. Results indicate that GRU achieved the highest performance, while Transformer models underperformed due to dataset limitations. This study demonstrates the impact of model architecture on clinical time-series prediction tasks.

suggestions.title: Integrate Explainable AI for Model Decision Insights
suggestions.desc: Add explainability features to your mortality prediction models using libraries like LIME or SHAP. This will help healthcare providers understand the factors contributing to predictions, enhancing trust and usability in clinical settings.

suggestions.title: Comparative Analysis of Model Interpretability Techniques
suggestions.desc: Conduct a study comparing various interpretability techniques across the deep learning architectures implemented in your project. Present findings in a research paper, highlighting which techniques work best for time-series data in healthcare applications.

suggestions.title: Create a User-Friendly Interface for Clinicians
suggestions.desc: Develop a web-based interface that allows clinicians to input patient vital signs and receive mortality predictions from your models. Use Flask or Django for the backend and React for the frontend to create an intuitive experience for healthcare professionals.

suggestions.title: Collaborate with Hospitals for Real-World Testing
suggestions.desc: Partner with local hospitals to test your mortality prediction models in a real-world setting. This collaboration could involve setting up pilot programs to evaluate model effectiveness and gather valuable feedback for further refinement.

title: UC-136-132 Gamma Guardian: Teaching about HLH
authors: Rousell, Lauren, Leichter, Logan, Portillo, Jacob, Lock, James
abstract: Gamma Guardian is an educational strategy game for children ages 6 to 12 that introduces Hemophagocytic Lymphohistiocytosis (HLH) and immune-system balance through interactive gameplay. The game places players inside the human body, where they use touch controls, antibody shields, and immune-response management to defend against interferon gamma, bacteria, and pathogens. Development used Unity 6.3 and followed a level-based design with educational feedback, AI-driven enemies, and a simple visual style to support learning. The result is a fully functional game with five levels, integrated educational content, and multi-platform support, exceeding the original goal of producing only a demo.

suggestions.title: Enhance Game Mechanics with Multiplayer Functionality
suggestions.desc: Extend Gamma Guardian by adding a multiplayer mode where children can collaborate to tackle challenges together. Implementing Unity’s multiplayer features will create an engaging experience, fostering teamwork and social interaction while learning about HLH.

suggestions.title: Conduct Educational Impact Assessment of the Game
suggestions.desc: Design and execute a study to assess the educational impact of Gamma Guardian on children's understanding of HLH. Use pre- and post-game surveys to measure knowledge retention and engagement, providing insights for future educational games.

suggestions.title: Develop a Teacher's Guide for Classroom Integration
suggestions.desc: Create a comprehensive guide for teachers that outlines how to integrate Gamma Guardian into the classroom. This guide could include lesson plans, discussion questions, and activities that align with educational standards, making it a valuable resource for educators.

suggestions.title: Introduce Augmented Reality Features for Immersive Learning
suggestions.desc: Expand the game with augmented reality features that allow children to visualize the immune system in real-time using their mobile devices. Leveraging ARKit or ARCore, this could enhance engagement and deepen understanding of complex biological concepts related to HLH.

title: GRM-05-158 Spark MLlib vs Python Frameworks
authors: Klein, Austin D
abstract: The original study evaluated Apache Spark MLlib on large datasets showing that it was able to outperform Weka in speed while also achieving similar accuracy scores. Our research builds upon this work by extending the analysis to compare Apache Spark MLlib with PyTorch. We will measure training time, speed, and accuracy to compare the results of each approach on similar hardware specifications as the original test. Original tests show promise, though we have only implemented one of the datasets. We will continue to implement the following datasets and improve metrics.

suggestions.title: Benchmark Spark MLlib with Additional Datasets
suggestions.desc: Expand the comparison of Apache Spark MLlib and PyTorch by implementing and benchmarking additional datasets like the UCI Machine Learning Repository datasets. Utilize tools like Pandas and NumPy for preprocessing and visualize results using Matplotlib or Seaborn.

suggestions.title: Investigate Hyperparameter Optimization Techniques
suggestions.desc: Explore various hyperparameter optimization methods such as Bayesian optimization or grid search for both Spark MLlib and PyTorch models. Document how these optimizations impact training time and accuracy across different datasets.

suggestions.title: Real-Time Data Processing with Spark Streaming
suggestions.desc: Integrate Spark Streaming to process real-time data streams and evaluate the performance of MLlib and PyTorch in a live setting. Implement a simple web application using Flask to visualize model predictions in real-time.

suggestions.title: Develop a Comparative Analysis Tool
suggestions.desc: Create an interactive web-based tool that allows users to upload datasets and compare the performance of Spark MLlib and PyTorch in terms of speed and accuracy. Leverage frameworks like Streamlit or Dash for the frontend.

title: UC-011-171 Beyond Postseason Awards: Predicting Accolades via Real-Time Control Chart Signals in the NCAA Transfer Era
authors: Bowen, Daniel, Cyclewalla, Shayaan
abstract: In the era of the NCAA transfer portal, collegiate basketball coaches face the critical challenge of identifying and targeting elite recruits within a condensed 15-day window. This study investigates the predictability of elite player performance by analyzing postseason award winners within the Coastal Athletic Association (CAA). Utilizing game-by-game data on player efficiency, usage percentages, and Player Efficiency Ratings (PER), we implemented an Exponentially Weighted Moving Average (EWMA) control chart—a technique from the Statistical Process Control (SPC) family—to monitor performance signals. Our results indicate that the EWMA model successfully identifies future award-winning players after an average of only 8.58 games. By providing a 22-game lead before the season’s conclusion, this model offers coaches a significant strategic advantage, allowing for the early identification of potential transfer targets before the portal officially opens. These findings suggest that SPC-based modeling is a highly effective tool for predictive sports analytics and carries broad applications for future collegiate recruitment strategies.

suggestions.title: Enhance Performance Metrics with Machine Learning Models
suggestions.desc: Utilize machine learning models such as Random Forest or XGBoost to predict player accolades based on historical performance data. Use datasets from NCAA statistics to train and validate your models.

suggestions.title: Investigate Factors Influencing Player Performance
suggestions.desc: Conduct a deeper analysis of the various factors that influence player performance, such as team dynamics or coaching styles. Use qualitative data from interviews or surveys with coaches and players to supplement your analysis.

suggestions.title: Develop a Recruitment Dashboard for Coaches
suggestions.desc: Build a web application that provides coaches with real-time analytics on potential recruits using your EWMA model. Integrate APIs like SportsRadar to fetch live player statistics and visualize insights using Plotly.

suggestions.title: Create a Community Database of Player Performance
suggestions.desc: Establish a community-driven database where coaches can contribute and access player performance data. Implement data validation and a user-friendly interface to encourage participation and data sharing.

title: UC-164-215 HootNest: AI-Powered KSU Student Assistant
authors: Washington, Tabitha, Steele, Aspen
abstract: HootNest helps prospective Kennesaw State students get clear, reliable answers about college life. It is designed for students who may not have easy access to counselors, mentors, or campus visits. The chatbot allows students to ask the chatbot anything they need to know.

suggestions.title: Integrate Voice Recognition for HootNest
suggestions.desc: Enhance HootNest by integrating voice recognition capabilities using Google Cloud Speech-to-Text. This feature would allow students to interact with the chatbot via voice, improving accessibility and user experience.

suggestions.title: Conduct Usability Studies for HootNest
suggestions.desc: Perform usability studies to evaluate how effectively HootNest meets the needs of prospective students. Gather feedback through surveys and interviews, and use this data to iterate on the design and functionality.

suggestions.title: Expand HootNest to Include Campus Resources
suggestions.desc: Broaden HootNest's capabilities by including information about campus resources such as mental health services, academic tutoring, and financial aid. Collaborate with campus departments to ensure accuracy and comprehensiveness.

suggestions.title: Implement a Feedback Loop for Continuous Improvement
suggestions.desc: Create a system where users can provide feedback on the responses and functionality of HootNest. Use this feedback to improve the chatbot’s responses and to train it to handle more complex inquiries.

title: UC-098-187 Zero-Inflated Poisson Modeling of NCAA Postseason Awards
authors: Lane, Charles, Bresko, Kyle, Treang, Kaleb
abstract: Our project focuses on predicting postseason awards for NCAA Men's College Basketball which can be difficult to model given that less than 15% of players in a given season win awards. After evaluating basic models, we selected a Zero-Inflated Poisson (ZIP) model to account for most players receiving zero awards. We identified free-throw attempts as being the best predictor for the structural zeros present in who can win an award. The final ZIP model produced better evaluation metrics than other basic models. Accounting for structural zeros allowed us to better model how on court statistics can translate into postseason awards.

suggestions.title: Apply Advanced Statistical Models for ZIP Predictions
suggestions.desc: Experiment with more advanced statistical models, like Generalized Additive Models (GAMs), to see if they can improve the prediction of NCAA postseason awards. Compare the results to the ZIP model to determine effectiveness.

suggestions.title: Explore Other Predictive Factors for Awards
suggestions.desc: Investigate additional predictive factors such as player injuries, team performance, or coaching changes that could influence postseason awards. Collect data from multiple seasons and perform a comprehensive analysis.

suggestions.title: Develop an Interactive Awards Prediction Tool
suggestions.desc: Create an interactive web-based tool that allows users to input player statistics and receive predictions on potential postseason awards. Use Flask or Django for backend development and JavaScript for frontend interactivity.

suggestions.title: Collaborate with Sports Analysts for Insights
suggestions.desc: Partner with sports analysts or commentators to validate your findings and gain insights on player performance trends. Use their expertise to refine your model and improve the predictive accuracy of your analysis.

title: Modeling Distress and Evaluating Chatbot Safety for Suicide-Related Social Media Texts
abstract: This project addresses the urgent need for transparent chatbot safety evaluations amid rising concerns about AI-facilitated self-harm. Using public social media datasets, we simulate two tasks: (1) detecting suicidal ideation via emotion-based risk scoring, and (2) stress-testing a support-style chatbot against 888 high-risk prompts, including euphemisms and "for a story" framing. A multi-label classifier trained on GoEmotions feeds emotion profiles into a logistic regression model to generate suicidality risk scores. These scores guide a local chatbot built with Ollama's llama3, which analyzes user messages and steers responses toward safe, empathetic behavior. Evaluation shows ~90% of replies were safe or supportive. This framework links emotional signals to risk heuristics and enables reproducible safety testing under realistic self-harm scenarios.

suggestions.title: Enhance Chatbot Safety with Adversarial Testing
suggestions.desc: Perform adversarial testing on the chatbot by generating new high-risk prompts using techniques like text perturbation. Analyze how the chatbot responds to these prompts and refine its safety protocols accordingly.

suggestions.title: Explore Emotion Analysis in Social Media Texts
suggestions.desc: Conduct research on the effectiveness of different emotion analysis models, such as BERT or RoBERTa, to improve the detection of suicidal ideation in social media texts. Evaluate their performance against the GoEmotions model.

suggestions.title: Develop a Crisis Intervention Feature for the Chatbot
suggestions.desc: Implement a crisis intervention feature in the chatbot that provides users with immediate resources and contacts for mental health support when certain risk thresholds are detected. Ensure compliance with ethical guidelines in mental health.

suggestions.title: Create a Dataset for Future Research
suggestions.desc: Compile a publicly accessible dataset of social media texts related to suicidal ideation and chatbot responses. This dataset can be invaluable for future research on AI ethics and mental health interventions.

title: GC-130-160 C-Day Explorer: A Domain-Aware Platform for Discovering and Extending KSU Student Projects
authors: Jonnalagadda, Rohan, Chapaneri, Sanketh
abstract: C-Day showcases some of the strongest computing projects at KSU, but once each event ends, past work becomes scattered across semester pages, posters, PDFs, and videos, making it difficult to see long-term trends or build on prior ideas. C-Day Explorer addresses this gap with a centralized, domain-aware web platform that aggregates project records from 21 semesters of C-Day archives, KSU Digital Commons, winner pages, and YouTube presentation videos. The system organizes 1,286 projects into 11 computing domains with high abstract coverage, poster and video links, and similarity-based connections that help users quickly find related work and promising directions for extension. By turning isolated showcase artifacts into an integrated, searchable history of student innovation, C-Day Explorer helps students, judges, and faculty discover prior projects, identify patterns, and move ideas forward beyond a single showcase.

suggestions.title: Implement Machine Learning for Project Recommendations
suggestions.desc: Enhance the C-Day Explorer platform by integrating a machine learning model that analyzes user behavior and preferences to recommend relevant projects. Use Python libraries like scikit-learn and datasets from the existing project archives to train the model, allowing users to discover projects that align with their interests.

suggestions.title: Analyze Trends in Computing Domains Over Time
suggestions.desc: Conduct a longitudinal analysis of the aggregated project data to identify trends in project topics, technology usage, and student interests across semesters. Utilize data visualization tools such as Tableau or D3.js to present findings, which could offer valuable insights for curriculum development at KSU.

suggestions.title: Create a Collaboration Feature for Students
suggestions.desc: Develop a feature that allows students to express interest in collaborating on past projects. Implement a messaging system that connects students based on mutual project interests, fostering a community of collaboration and innovation at KSU.

suggestions.title: Integrate with External Project Repositories
suggestions.desc: Expand the platform's capabilities by integrating with external project repositories like GitHub or GitLab. This could allow students to showcase their code and documentation alongside their C-Day projects, making it easier for others to learn from and build upon existing work.

title: The Understudy – A 2.5D Turn-Based Story Game
abstract: "The Understudy" is a whimsy-filled 2.5D turn-based theatrical adventure where you play as the last-minute understudy, who has been suddenly thrust into the spotlight after the lead mysteriously vanishes right before showtime. Armed with nothing but masks (comedic, dramatic, and tragic) and a script you definitely didn't rehearse enough, you fight your way through a cast of dramatic acting troupe members, ranging from a painfully shy tree to a snarky jester ex to a pompous king who's very sure you don't belong on his stage. Swap masks to change your combat style, solve dialogue puzzles, and prove that even an understudy can steal the show when the curtain rises. Explore the set, confront your doubts, and discover the role you were never meant to play… but might be ready for anyway.

suggestions.title: Introduce Online Multiplayer Features
suggestions.desc: Transform 'The Understudy' into a multiplayer experience by allowing players to compete or cooperate in real-time gameplay. Use WebSockets for real-time communication and a framework like Phaser.js to handle the game mechanics and networking.

suggestions.title: Study Player Engagement Through A/B Testing
suggestions.desc: Implement A/B testing for different game mechanics, such as the effectiveness of various masks or puzzle types, to gather data on player engagement. Use analytics tools like Google Analytics or Unity Analytics to track player behavior and optimize the game experience based on findings.

suggestions.title: Create an Educational Version for Drama Classes
suggestions.desc: Develop an educational version of the game aimed at drama students to help them learn about character development and improvisation. Incorporate lesson plans and guided activities that teachers can use alongside gameplay to enhance learning outcomes.

suggestions.title: Expand Storyline with User-Generated Content
suggestions.desc: Allow players to create and share their own characters and storylines within the game. Implement a user-friendly editor and a sharing platform, enabling the community to add depth to the game's universe and engage players in content creation.

title: UC-115-161 Wayward Stray: Selix
authors: Tinoco, Arly, Ercole, Tyler, Stansel, Ivy, Lothman, Austin, Charland-Martin, Jeremi
abstract: Wayward Stray:Selix is a 3rd person platformer which places importance on exploration and discovery. Players will take the role of Selix as they explore an arid desert, fighting off enemies and discovering items hidden around the map, which reveal more about the game world and its characters. Selix, a young dragon, is exiled from the only home he’s known, forced into a strange land in search of a new place to call his own. Along the way, he finds a companion, a small dove that aids and guides his way. Exploring these uncharted areas, Selix discovers there’s more to the world than his small view, and discovers secrets about himself as well.

suggestions.title: Implement Procedural Generation for Game Environments
suggestions.desc: Enhance 'Wayward Stray: Selix' by incorporating procedural generation techniques for creating diverse and unique desert landscapes. Use algorithms such as Perlin noise to generate terrain and distribute items and enemies, ensuring each playthrough offers a fresh experience.

suggestions.title: Conduct User Experience Research on Game Mechanics
suggestions.desc: Perform user experience research to evaluate how players interact with the exploration and combat mechanics. Use surveys and playtesting sessions to gather feedback, which can guide future iterations of the game and improve player satisfaction.

suggestions.title: Develop a Companion App for Gameplay Assistance
suggestions.desc: Create a mobile companion app that provides players with hints, lore, and a map of the game world. Use React Native to develop the app, offering users an additional layer of engagement and support while they play 'Wayward Stray: Selix.'

suggestions.title: Incorporate Virtual Reality (VR) Features
suggestions.desc: Explore the possibility of adding VR support to the game, allowing players to immerse themselves in Selix's world. Utilize Unity's XR Interaction Toolkit to create a VR version that enhances exploration and interaction with the environment.

title: GRP-02-199 Topological Constraints for Protein Folding
authors: Fanning, Charles
abstract: We study whether topological loss-based constraints improve multidomain whole-chain protein structure prediction beyond the ColabFold baseline by better preserving the topologies of folded proteins. We benchmark against Wasserstein metrics with our own virtual persistence and RKHS semi-metric constraints as well as higher-order virtual persistence diagrams.

suggestions.title: Develop a User-Friendly Visualization Tool for Protein Structures
suggestions.desc: Create an interactive web tool that visualizes protein structures using libraries like PyMOL or 3Dmol.js. This tool can allow researchers to explore the topological constraints and compare them with existing protein structures, enhancing understanding and analysis.

suggestions.title: Benchmark Against Additional Protein Structure Prediction Models
suggestions.desc: Extend the research by benchmarking the proposed topological loss-based constraints against other state-of-the-art protein structure prediction models, such as AlphaFold or Rosetta. Publish findings in a peer-reviewed journal to contribute to the bioinformatics field.

suggestions.title: Create a Teaching Module on Protein Folding
suggestions.desc: Develop a teaching module that explains protein folding concepts and the impact of topological constraints. This can include interactive simulations and educational resources for use in bioinformatics or biology courses.

suggestions.title: Integrate Machine Learning for Predictive Analysis
suggestions.desc: Investigate the application of machine learning algorithms to predict protein structure based on topological features. Use TensorFlow or PyTorch to develop predictive models, which could provide new insights into protein folding mechanisms.

title: GRM-081-207 Leveraging Non-Parametric Longitudinal Rank Sum Tests (LRST) for Robust Global Treatment Effect Estimation in Alzheimer’s Disease
authors: Shahid, Imaan
abstract: Parametric approaches, such as Mixed Models for Repeated Measures (MMRM), are standard in Alzheimer’s Disease (AD) clinical trials. However, these models often falter when data violates assumptions of normality or follows non-linear trajectories—common occurrences in AD due to floor/ceiling effects on cognitive scales and heterogeneous disease progression. This study evaluates Longitudinal Rank Sum Tests (LRST) as a non-parametric alternative to maintain statistical power and robustness.

suggestions.title: Implement a Web-Based Dashboard for Clinical Trial Data
suggestions.desc: Create a web-based dashboard that visualizes the results of the LRST analysis, allowing researchers to interactively explore treatment effects in Alzheimer’s Disease trials. Use frameworks like Dash or React to build the interface and connect to databases for real-time data updates.

suggestions.title: Conduct a Meta-Analysis of Non-Parametric Approaches in AD Trials
suggestions.desc: Perform a meta-analysis comparing LRST with other non-parametric methods used in Alzheimer's Disease clinical trials. This research could provide valuable insights into the strengths and limitations of various statistical approaches in handling heterogeneous data.

suggestions.title: Develop a Simulation Tool for Treatment Effect Scenarios
suggestions.desc: Create a simulation tool that allows researchers to model different treatment effect scenarios using LRST. This tool could be used for educational purposes or to assist in designing future clinical trials for Alzheimer’s Disease.

suggestions.title: Collaborate with Clinical Researchers for Real-World Validation
suggestions.desc: Partner with clinical researchers to apply LRST to real-world Alzheimer’s Disease datasets, validating the method's robustness in diverse populations. This collaboration could lead to impactful research publications and improvements in clinical trial methodologies.

title: UC-135-129 fishGame: Mobile Hyper-casual Fishing Game
authors: Rousell, Lauren, Martinez, Lisbeth, Portillo, Jacob, Miller, Jacob
abstract: fishGame is a strategy-driven hyper-casual mobile game that combines the accessibility of traditional mobile gameplay with the progression depth of a roguelike. The project was developed over a three-month period using Unity 6.3 and related production tools. Following the completion of an alpha build, testing was conducted through gameplay sessions and a detailed follow-up survey to gather feedback on player experience, clarity, and engagement. Results indicated that fishGame was well received, with players reporting low levels of confusion and strong replayability. These findings suggest a clear interest in mobile games that offer greater depth while preserving the immediacy and simplicity associated with the hyper-casual genre.

suggestions.title: Integrate Augmented Reality Fishing Mechanics
suggestions.desc: Enhance the fishing game by adding an augmented reality (AR) feature using Unity's AR Foundation. Players could use their mobile device's camera to fish in real-world locations, providing a more immersive experience and integrating GPS to create location-based challenges.

suggestions.title: Study Player Behavior with Advanced Analytics
suggestions.desc: Implement advanced analytics tools like Google Analytics or Unity Analytics to gather deeper insights into player behavior and engagement. Analyze this data to identify which game mechanics lead to higher retention rates and propose adjustments based on player feedback.

suggestions.title: Create a Multiplayer Fishing Tournament Mode
suggestions.desc: Develop a competitive multiplayer mode where players can compete in fishing tournaments. Use cloud services like Firebase to manage real-time leaderboards and matchmaking, allowing friends to challenge each other and fostering a community around the game.

suggestions.title: Create Educational Content on Sustainable Fishing
suggestions.desc: Partner with environmental organizations to create a module within the game that educates players about sustainable fishing practices. This could include mini-games or challenges that reward players for making environmentally friendly choices while fishing.

title: UC-152-211 CCSE Capstone Meeting Intelligence Platform
authors: Crose, Calvin, Enyart, Noah, Johnson, Marcus, Jones, Jaeden, Smith, Jonah
abstract: The CCSE Capstone Meeting Intelligence Platform is a web-based monitoring system designed to assist CCSE leadership in overseeing a large variety of capstone projects. Currently, the CCSE leadership handles the overseeing of projects manually, either with advisors sitting in on student-client meetings or by reviewing recordings, which can lead to problems where potential red flags are unidentified. To help manage this, the system processes Microsoft Teams meeting transcripts and analyzes them using a Large Language Model (LLM) combined with Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) techniques. It then will identify potential project risks like scope creep, conduct concerns, and deviations from project specifications, providing automated analysis and early risk detection to help monitor capstone projects without requiring manual review of every meeting.

suggestions.title: Implement Emotion Detection in Meeting Transcripts
suggestions.desc: Extend the platform by integrating emotion detection using NLP libraries like NLTK or spaCy. Analyze the tone of the transcripts to identify emotional cues in conversations, providing CCSE leadership with insights into team morale and communication dynamics.

suggestions.title: Evaluate LLM Performance on Diverse Meeting Types
suggestions.desc: Conduct a comparative study of LLM performance across different types of meetings (e.g., technical, management, client-facing). Gather a dataset of transcripts from various meeting types and analyze how well the model identifies key points and potential red flags.

suggestions.title: Integrate Action Item Tracking and Reporting
suggestions.desc: Add a feature for tracking action items generated during meetings. Utilize a database like MongoDB to store action items and their completion statuses, providing a dashboard for CCSE leadership to monitor project progress easily.

suggestions.title: Create a Training Module for New Students
suggestions.desc: Develop a training module that uses data from the platform to help new students learn effective meeting techniques. This could include best practices for communication, as well as interactive scenarios based on real meeting data.

title: GRP-08-168 Diagnosing Faults in Electrical Power Systems of Satellites
authors: Lasley, Jared, Thi Binh Nguyen, Nguyen
abstract: Satellite systems cost hundreds of millions of dollars or more to launch. To be resistant to catastrophic failures (and total loss of investment), satellite systems are designed with redundant sub-systems and are further equipped with numerous sensors and other health-monitoring sub-systems. In this poster, we consider an approach to fault diagnosis based on probabilistic logic programming. In particular, we propose to use ProbLog to model and reason with the electrical power system (EPS) of a satellite. Once we model a system using (probabilistic) first-order logic, we can take the system state and any (unexpected) sensor readings, and through automated reasoning, we can provide ranked predictions about the most likely faults in the system. We present our approach to fault diagnosis using ProbLog, and present a case study in a simplified EPS, highlighting our ability to isolate and diagnose different categories of faults.

suggestions.title: Develop a Real-Time Fault Detection Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Create a real-time monitoring dashboard using technologies like Node.js and WebSocket to visualize the health of satellite power systems. This dashboard could highlight potential faults based on data from sensors and provide alerts for maintenance.

suggestions.title: Investigate the Impact of Environmental Factors on Faults
suggestions.desc: Study how different environmental conditions affect the reliability of satellite electrical power systems. Use historical satellite data to model correlations using statistical techniques or machine learning, potentially improving predictive maintenance strategies.

suggestions.title: Simulate Fault Scenarios for Training Purposes
suggestions.desc: Develop simulations that generate various fault scenarios in satellite power systems using ProbLog. This tool can be used for training engineers on how to diagnose and address faults, making it a valuable resource for educational programs.

suggestions.title: Explore Cross-Satellite Fault Analysis
suggestions.desc: Research the feasibility of analyzing fault patterns across multiple satellite systems. Use machine learning to identify common failure modes and suggest design improvements that could enhance the reliability of future satellite launches.

title: GC-172-139 Detection of SMS Spam using Transformer BERT Model
authors: Bonner, Nathan, Kandell, Zachary, Hayes, Michael, Quintanilla, David, Greenberg, Leon
abstract: This project evaluates automated SMS spam classification by comparing traditional machine learning against modern transformer architectures. We built a Bidirectional LSTM (BiLSTM) baseline using TF-IDF feature extraction and NearMiss-1 undersampling to handle severe class imbalances. We then compared this against a fine-tuned Hugging Face Sentence-BERT model. Preliminary results show Sentence-BERT significantly outperformed the BiLSTM baseline (99.01% vs. 95.65% accuracy). These findings demonstrate that transformer-based embeddings offer a highly accurate, scalable solution for spam mitigation without relying on aggressive data undersampling.

suggestions.title: Enhance Classification with Multi-Language Support
suggestions.desc: Extend the SMS spam detection system to support multiple languages by fine-tuning the Sentence-BERT model on multilingual datasets. Use the OPUS project or the Multilingual Amazon Reviews dataset for training, allowing the model to classify spam across different languages.

suggestions.title: Analyze the Evolution of SMS Spam Tactics
suggestions.desc: Conduct a longitudinal study on SMS spam tactics by collecting and analyzing spam message datasets over time. Use this data to refine your classifier and build a report on how spam tactics have evolved, providing insights for future defense mechanisms.

suggestions.title: Integrate User Feedback Mechanism for Continuous Learning
suggestions.desc: Build a user feedback feature that allows users to report false positives and negatives in the SMS classification. Use this feedback to continuously improve your model through active learning techniques, enhancing its accuracy over time.

suggestions.title: Collaborate with Telecommunication Companies for Real-Time Filtering
suggestions.desc: Partner with telecommunication providers to implement your spam detection model at the network level, allowing for real-time filtering of SMS messages before they reach users. This could significantly reduce the impact of spam on end-users.

title: GC-140-126 Machine Learning Models for Solar Power Output Prediction: A Comparative Study with Adaptive PSO-Based Random Forest Tuning
authors: Mahabaduge, Hasitha
abstract: Accurate prediction of solar power output is essential for energy scheduling, grid reliability, and efficient integration of renewable resources. Because photovoltaic generation is governed by changing atmospheric conditions, forecasting output is inherently a nonlinear learning problem. This study evaluates four machine learning models — Linear Regression, Random Forest, Multi-Layer Perceptron (MLP), and an Adaptive Particle Swarm Optimization-tuned Random Forest (Adaptive PSO-RF) — using irradiance, temperature, humidity, wind speed, cloud cover, and time-derived features drawn from a dataset of 6,738 observations. Random Forest achieved the strongest overall performance, with an RMSE of 1,816.24 and an R² of 0.9533. The Adaptive PSO-RF produced nearly equivalent results, confirming that the baseline Random Forest parameters were already near-optimal for this dataset. Feature interpretation was examined through both impurity-based and permutation importance. Whereas impurity-based importance erroneously ranked humidity as the dominant predictor, permutation importance yielded a physically meaningful ranking in which irradiance and hour emerged as the primary drivers. The study demonstrates that nonlinear ensemble methods substantially outperform linear regression for solar power forecasting and that careful interpretation of feature importance is necessary whenever predictors are correlated.

suggestions.title: Incorporate Weather Forecast APIs for Dynamic Predictions
suggestions.desc: Integrate weather forecast APIs, such as OpenWeatherMap or WeatherAPI, to provide real-time weather data for solar power output predictions. This integration can enhance the accuracy of your models by incorporating short-term forecasts into the prediction algorithms.

suggestions.title: Benchmark Against Newer Machine Learning Techniques
suggestions.desc: Explore and benchmark newer machine learning techniques like XGBoost or Gradient Boosting against your existing models. Analyze performance improvements in prediction accuracy and computational efficiency using the same dataset.

suggestions.title: Create a User-Friendly Prediction Dashboard
suggestions.desc: Develop an interactive web application using Flask or Django that allows users to input real-time weather data and receive solar power output predictions. This dashboard could cater to energy companies for better energy management.

suggestions.title: Evaluate Economic Impacts of Solar Output Predictions
suggestions.desc: Conduct an economic analysis of how accurate solar power output predictions can affect energy pricing and grid stability. Use data from local energy markets to assess potential financial benefits for energy providers implementing your model.

title: GRP-096-183 Early Warning Signals for Geopolitical Oil Shocks via Multi-Model NLP Sentiment Analysis
authors: Ohalete, Nzubechukwu
abstract: The Strait of Hormuz carries roughly 20% of the world’s daily oil supply. Its closure on March 4, 2026, sent Brent crude surging 36.6%, from $74.64 to a peak of $118.35/barrel. Traditional time-series models fail during such unprecedented shocks because the historical price data contains no analog. This study evaluates whether NLP sentiment analysis can detect crisis signals in news text before they appear in prices, and whether agreement patterns across models predict volatility. We score 2,249 Guardian news articles using five deterministic sentiment models across three tiers: a lexicon baseline (VADER), a general-purpose transformer (RoBERTa-CardiffNLP), and three financial-domain transformers (FinBERT, FinBERT-Tone, DistilRoBERTa-Financial). Financial-domain models detected the crisis 47 days before closure, outperforming general-purpose models by 26 days. Article volume proved the strongest volatility predictor (r=0.76, p< 0.0001), while model consensus, not disagreement, signals crisis severity, inverting our original hypothesis. LSTM and XGBoost forecasting experiments show all model variants converging near a naive persistence baseline during the crisis, confirming that point prediction of unprecedented events remains fundamentally limited and reinforcing the value of upstream textual early warning.
similar: legacy:GR-20|Spring 2016|sentiment analysis and text mining of twitter data using r

suggestions.title: Integrate Real-Time Social Media Sentiment Analysis
suggestions.desc: Expand the current model by incorporating real-time sentiment analysis from social media platforms like Twitter using the Tweepy API. This will allow for a more dynamic understanding of public sentiment and potentially identify early warning signals for geopolitical oil shocks even faster.

suggestions.title: Develop a Visualization Dashboard for Crisis Signals
suggestions.desc: Create an interactive dashboard using technologies like Dash or Tableau that visualizes sentiment scores, model predictions, and historical oil prices. This tool can help users easily identify correlations between sentiment trends and oil price movements.

suggestions.title: Explore Alternative News Sources for Sentiment Analysis
suggestions.desc: Investigate the effectiveness of sentiment analysis on different news sources (e.g., Al Jazeera, BBC) and compare how sentiment from varied geopolitical perspectives influences oil price predictions. Use a larger dataset of articles to enhance model robustness.

suggestions.title: Create a Mobile App for Oil Market Alerts
suggestions.desc: Develop a mobile application that uses the NLP sentiment analysis model to send alerts to users about potential oil price fluctuations based on real-time news sentiment. Leverage Firebase for backend services and push notifications.

title: UC-131-162 Physical 8-Bit CPU
authors: Hoerner, Samuel, Merchant, Aryan, Dislen, John, Day, Kyran
abstract: This isn’t an app - it’s the machine behind it. A fully functional 8-bit CPU, built from scratch, turning raw signals into real computation. The system combines our custom assembly-to-run, FPGA-driven control unit (CU), discrete transistor Arithmetical Logical Unit (ALU), external memory, and 5 registers to execute programs through a fetch-execute cycle. The result is a tangible computing platform that bridges low-level digital logic with high-level system behavior.

suggestions.title: Implement a GUI for Your 8-Bit CPU
suggestions.desc: Design a graphical user interface (GUI) that allows users to visualize and interact with the 8-bit CPU's operations, including real-time monitoring of the fetch-execute cycle and register states. Use Python with Tkinter or similar frameworks for development.

suggestions.title: Explore Performance Optimization Techniques
suggestions.desc: Investigate and implement various optimization strategies such as pipelining or caching to enhance the performance of the 8-bit CPU while executing assembly programs. Measure performance improvements and document findings.

suggestions.title: Develop Educational Tutorials for Learning Assembly Language
suggestions.desc: Create a series of educational resources or video tutorials that teach users how to program in assembly language on your 8-bit CPU. This can help demystify low-level programming for beginners and provide hands-on learning experiences.

suggestions.title: Build a Retro Gaming Console Using the CPU
suggestions.desc: Utilize the 8-bit CPU to create a simple retro gaming console that can run classic games. Integrate a game controller interface and develop a few original games to showcase the CPU's capabilities.

title: UR-160-200 Tortured Artist
authors: Tigani, Caitlin, Scholl, Ben, Tucker, Adam, Tucker, Anaiya
abstract: You are a photographer that wants to move out, so you take pictures of your house to give to your real- estate agent. However, as you are developing the photos you hear a noise that makes you turn on the lights, ruining your photos. Now you must retake the photos before morning, but something around the house has changed. Rooms are no longer in the right place, items are moved around, doors are locked, and an entity is watching you. Will you find the secrets within the puzzles or be left tortured?

suggestions.title: Add Procedural Generation to Game Levels
suggestions.desc: Enhance the game by implementing procedural generation for the house layout, ensuring that each playthrough offers a unique experience. Use algorithms like Perlin noise or cellular automata to create varying room configurations and item placements.

suggestions.title: Investigate AI-Driven Adaptive Difficulty
suggestions.desc: Research and implement an AI system that adjusts the game's difficulty dynamically based on player performance and behavior. This could involve analyzing player actions to modify puzzle complexity and enemy AI behavior.

suggestions.title: Create a VR Experience of 'Tortured Artist'
suggestions.desc: Develop a virtual reality version of the game using Unity and Oculus SDK, allowing players to immerse themselves in the environment and interact with objects in a more engaging manner. This will enhance the horror aspect of the game.

suggestions.title: Launch a Community Art Challenge Within the Game
suggestions.desc: Incorporate a feature that allows players to submit their own photography or artwork that can be integrated into the game as collectibles or puzzles. This could foster community engagement and creativity around the game.

title: GRM-169-137 Predicting the Stock Market's Next Move: How Neural Network Architecture Shapes Forecasting Accuracy
authors: Powell, Roderick
abstract: Three feedforward neural network (FFNN) architectures — bottleneck, parallel multi-path, and residual parallel — were trained on ten years of daily S&P 500 (SPY ETF) price and volume data to predict next-day market direction (Up/Down). All three demonstrated predictive ability above random chance. Architectural choice directly determined class prediction bias: the bottleneck concentrated errors on Up days, the parallel architecture distributed them evenly, and residual connections inverted the bias toward Down days. Model 2 (parallel) achieved the highest test accuracy (58.2%) and the most balanced class predictions among the three configurations.
similar: legacy:GRCS-01|Spring 2017|deep convolutional neural network and parallel programming

suggestions.title: Experiment with Reinforcement Learning for Stock Prediction
suggestions.desc: Implement reinforcement learning techniques to predict stock movements by training an agent on historical market data. Use libraries like TensorFlow or PyTorch to develop a model that learns from actions taken based on previous market states.

suggestions.title: Create an Ensemble Model for Improved Predictions
suggestions.desc: Build an ensemble model that combines predictions from multiple neural network architectures, such as FFNN, LSTM, and CNN, to enhance forecasting accuracy. Evaluate and compare performance against individual models.

suggestions.title: Develop a Stock Market Analysis Web App
suggestions.desc: Create a web application that visualizes stock market predictions using your best-performing model. Utilize Flask or Django for the backend and Chart.js for dynamic data visualizations to present insights and forecasts to users.

suggestions.title: Integrate News Sentiment Analysis into Stock Predictions
suggestions.desc: Combine your stock prediction models with sentiment analysis from financial news articles, allowing the model to adjust predictions based on current events. This could involve using the same techniques explored in P001 for a comprehensive approach.

title: GRP-148-223 Uncertainty-Guided Conservative Propagation for Robust Coronary Artery Segmentation
authors: Huang, Huan, Zhao, Chen
abstract: Coronary artery segmentation plays a key role in cardiovascular disease analysis, yet existing methods often produce fragmented and structurally inconsistent vessels in challenging regions. We propose an uncertainty-guided conservative propagation (UGCP) framework that improves segmentation reliability by allowing high-confidence regions to guide uncertain ones through controlled information propagation under a conservation principle. This mechanism enhances structural continuity while preventing unstable updates. Experiments on Coronary CT Angiography (CCTA) and Invasive Coronary Angiography (ICA) datasets demonstrate improved segmentation accuracy and topology preservation. Additional evaluations on other vascular datasets further suggest the generalizability of the proposed approach.

suggestions.title: Evaluate the Impact of Different Imaging Modalities
suggestions.desc: Investigate how different imaging techniques (e.g., MRI vs. CT) affect the performance of your segmentation models. Collect datasets from various sources and analyze segmentation accuracy across these modalities.

suggestions.title: Develop a User-Friendly Interface for Medical Professionals
suggestions.desc: Create a graphical user interface that allows healthcare professionals to upload coronary imaging data and visualize segmentation results in real-time. Use web technologies like React and Flask to build the application.

suggestions.title: Implement a Workflow for Automated Coronary Disease Detection
suggestions.desc: Design a complete workflow that integrates your segmentation framework with machine learning classifiers to predict coronary artery disease risk based on the segmented images. This could help streamline diagnostic processes in clinical settings.

suggestions.title: Collaborate with Medical Institutions for Clinical Trials
suggestions.desc: Partner with hospitals or research institutions to conduct clinical trials using your segmentation approach, providing valuable real-world data and insights that could refine your method and enhance its clinical relevance.

title: GRP-093-174 Transforming Everyday Smartwatch Data into Clinical Early Warnings
authors: Jahan, Nursat
abstract: Cardiovascular Disease (CVD) related most machine learning (ML) models trained on clinical data offer high accuracy but are not practical for continuous monitoring. Smartwatch-based wearables provide continuous real-time physiological data but lack clinical validation for robust risk prediction outside the clinical setting. To bridge this gap, we proposed a novel teacher-student knowledge distillation framework that transfers knowledge of complex and large EHR datasets to a small Fitbit smartwatch dataset-based prediction model. The student model achieves promising accuracy, identifying all types of derived CVD risk profile groups. Our study introduces a non-invasive continuous health monitoring framework, demonstrating that passively collected daily metrics can be transformed to clinically powerful early warnings of an individual’s long-term cardiovascular health plan.

suggestions.title: Implement a Real-Time Monitoring Dashboard for CVD Risk
suggestions.desc: Build a web-based dashboard that visualizes smartwatch data in real-time, using technologies like React for the frontend and Node.js for the backend. Integrate with APIs from Fitbit to pull data and display alerts based on the risk levels predicted by your model.

suggestions.title: Evaluate the Impact of Different Wearable Devices on CVD Prediction
suggestions.desc: Conduct a comparative study analyzing the accuracy of predictions made using data from various smartwatch brands (e.g., Apple Watch, Garmin). Use publicly available datasets on smartwatch metrics and combine them with your framework to assess how device variability affects clinical outcomes.

suggestions.title: Develop a Personalized Health Coaching App Using the Model
suggestions.desc: Create an application that provides personalized health coaching and recommendations based on the smartwatch data processed through your model. Implement features like reminders for medication, exercise tips, and alerts for irregular heart rates, enhancing user engagement.

suggestions.title: Collaborate with Healthcare Providers for Clinical Trials
suggestions.desc: Partner with local healthcare providers to carry out a clinical trial using your smartwatch prediction model. Collect data on patient outcomes over a six-month period to validate the clinical effectiveness of your approach and refine the model based on real-world results.

title: UC-149-185 AI-Assisted Media Organization and Intake System
authors: Wu, Meilun, Branham, Nevaeh, Higgins, Isaiah, Kangni, Claude, Ahmed, Fatima
abstract: This project includes the design and validation of a metadata-driven media intake and organization system implemented for the Office of the District Attorney, Cobb Judicial Circuit. The institution produces a considerable volume of photographs and video content via outreach programs and community interaction initiatives. Yet, the organization does not have an organized framework for managing media files, which results in inefficient file retrieval and data loss over time due to a lack of standardized organizational practices. In this regard, this project aims to develop a workflow-based system for the efficient organization of media files that will be powered by a local user interface and backed up by an intake process on the backend. First, the system will automate the metadata extraction process, classify all files based on events and the date range of their creation, and provide the necessary guidance on filling out the questionnaire to apply metadata consistently. Next, the optional AI-based tagging mechanism will help improve the metadata content by providing recommendations, ensuring that humans double-check the suggested changes. The system will allow integration with the Microsoft 365 SharePoint platform and its metadata management features to facilitate efficient media searching and filtering. Considering the access restrictions, the proof-of-concept was implemented in a mock SharePoint environment.

suggestions.title: Integrate AI for Automated Media Tagging and Categorization
suggestions.desc: Enhance the media organization system by incorporating machine learning algorithms, such as TensorFlow or PyTorch, to automatically tag and categorize media files based on content. Use pre-trained models for image and video analysis to improve retrieval efficiency.

suggestions.title: Analyze the Impact of Media Organization on Case Outcomes
suggestions.desc: Conduct research to explore how structured media organization affects prosecutorial efficiency and case outcomes. Gather data from the District Attorney's office and analyze it to identify correlations between media management practices and successful case resolutions.

suggestions.title: Create a User-Friendly Mobile App for Media Access
suggestions.desc: Develop a mobile application that allows authorized users to access media files from the system in a user-friendly manner. Utilize React Native to build the app and implement features like search filters and favorites to enhance user experience.

suggestions.title: Expand the System to Include Evidence Chain Management
suggestions.desc: Extend the media intake system to manage evidence chains by integrating blockchain technology for secure tracking and verification. This can help establish the authenticity of media files and ensure compliance with legal standards.

title: UR-147-188 Staged Multi-Modal Alzheimer Classification using Uncertainty Quantification
authors: Chen, Branden, Litton, Ethan, Doan, Long
abstract: Diagnosing Alzheimer’s disease often depends on costly neuroimaging techniques such as MRIs and PET scans, which are not always accessible and can place a significant financial burden on healthcare systems. Existing clinical workflows lack a reliable way to determine which patients truly require these advanced tests, resulting in either unnecessary imaging or delayed and inaccurate diagnoses. To address this challenge, we propose the Uncertainty-Driven Dual-view (UDD) model, a multi-stage framework that integrates low-cost clinical and structural data with uncertainty-aware learning. The model first generates predictions using accessible data and quantifies its confidence, referring only high-uncertainty cases for further evaluation with expensive imaging modalities. This selective escalation strategy enables more efficient use of resources while preserving diagnostic accuracy. By combining multi-modal learning with uncertainty-guided decision making, the proposed approach offers a cost-effective and scalable solution for early Alzheimer’s disease screening.

suggestions.title: Integrate Patient-Reported Outcomes for Enhanced Model Training
suggestions.desc: Enhance the UDD model by incorporating patient-reported outcomes collected through surveys or apps. This data can improve the accuracy of the model by providing additional context on symptoms and patient experiences, potentially using tools like Google Forms for data collection.

suggestions.title: Investigate Cost-Effectiveness of the UDD Model in Clinical Settings
suggestions.desc: Conduct a research study comparing the costs associated with traditional diagnostic methods versus the UDD model. Collect data from healthcare institutions to analyze potential savings and improved patient outcomes, providing valuable insight into the model's economic impact.

suggestions.title: Develop a Mobile Application for Real-Time Alzheimer Screening
suggestions.desc: Create a mobile application that utilizes the UDD model to provide preliminary Alzheimer screenings based on user-input clinical data. This app can guide users on whether to seek further diagnostic imaging, improving accessibility and early detection.

suggestions.title: Collaborate with Healthcare Institutions for Validation Studies
suggestions.desc: Partner with hospitals to validate the UDD model in real clinical settings. Gather feedback from healthcare professionals on its usability and effectiveness, and refine the model based on the data collected during the validation process.

title: UC-151-197 Nest: An AI-Powered Transition Navigator for Aging-Out Foster Youth in Georgia
authors: Sookra, Stephen, Delaney, Tylin, Bryant, Brenden
abstract: Each year, approximately 600–700 young people age out of the Georgia foster care system with no permanent family, no housing plan, and no clear guide beyond a 250-page state transition PDF. The outcomes are severe: high rates of homelessness, low college completion, and unstable employment. Nest is an AI-powered, mobile-first web application that turns this overwhelming bureaucracy into a personalized 90-day transition plan generated in under 60 seconds. Through a short conversational intake, the system collects a youth’s age, county, housing status, and education or work goals, then uses a deterministic rules engine to determine likely eligibility for key programs such as Extended Youth Support Services (EYSS), Chafee Education and Training Vouchers, former-foster-care Medicaid coverage, SNAP, and KSU’s ASCEND program. A Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipeline grounded in a curated Georgia-specific resource database explains each recommendation with citations and step-by-step actions, reducing hallucination risk. The prototype is implemented with a React/Tailwind frontend and FastAPI backend and is explicitly designed around trauma-informed UX principles so that transition-age foster youth receive not just information, but an actionable, deadline-driven plan and a “caring adult in their pocket.”

suggestions.title: Build an AI Chatbot for Instant Support and Guidance
suggestions.desc: Develop a conversational AI chatbot that provides instant support to foster youth using natural language processing libraries like Rasa or Dialogflow. This could include answering questions about resources, housing, and employment opportunities based on user inputs.

suggestions.title: Analyze Outcomes of Youth Transition Plans Generated by Nest
suggestions.desc: Conduct a longitudinal study to evaluate the success rates of youth who utilized the Nest application versus those who did not. Collect data on housing stability, employment, and education to assess the effectiveness of personalized transition plans.

suggestions.title: Integrate Local Resource Databases for Tailored Recommendations
suggestions.desc: Enhance the Nest application by integrating databases of local resources such as shelters, job training programs, and educational institutions. Use APIs to pull in real-time information and provide users with the most relevant options based on their location.

suggestions.title: Expand Nest's Scope to Include Mental Health Resources
suggestions.desc: Develop additional features within Nest to provide mental health resources and support for aging-out foster youth. Incorporate partnerships with local mental health organizations to offer coaching, counseling, and workshops that help with the transition.
